They drank merrily until they were about to be drunk, then left the shop together. The person said again, "Brother Liang has something to say. Why not tell me now?" Liang said, "Yes. There is indeed something to say. Let's ask if you are so big, do you know human ways?" The person replied with a sense of shame, "No need to talk about this, it makes one feel so angry and frustrated that one wants to die." Liang said, "Still unmarried? My new mistress is a woman with the last name Jia, widowed and beautiful. Servants mostly choose young men, probably for a purpose. If you can follow me for a visit, there will be good news!" The person did not believe it and casually replied, "Is there any reason for this? Although the mistress is beautiful, it is not something that servants can control or manage." Liang said, "You may go with me, and you will know that my words are not false."

    44

    ”某欲验其言，欣然随之。乃由歧路曲折而前，日暮犹未抵其家，某曰：“子误我事，归必受谴，可若何？”梁笑曰：“居此不复，渠又若之何？”又行二里许，至一宅第，重垣列屋，气象壮丽，则已漏下二鼓矣。梁曰：“已至主家，予先入，子且少待。”乃去。某视之，门庭修整，而阒其无人，心窃讶焉。良久，梁始出，谓某曰：“主者相召，须以礼见。”某颔之，遂同入，纡回历数门，始达主室。巨屋五楹，帘垂烛暗，惟闻琵琶声。某素耽此，方将侧耳，梁命拜之，内亦停拨。某俯伏阃外，梁入而禀白。复有顷，帘内嘤咛曰：“渠肯为我服役，亦甚善。

    Explanation

    44

    I wanted to test his words, and gladly followed him. We took a winding path ahead, and it was evening when we had not yet reached his home. I said, "You have misled me; I will surely be punished when I return, what can I do?" Liang smiled and said, "The situation remains unchanged, what can he do about it?" We continued for about two miles and arrived at a grand mansion with tall walls and rows of houses, its majestic and splendid appearance already past two drums (indicating late at night). Liang said, "We have already arrived at the master's house; I will go first, you wait a moment." He then left. I looked around, and the courtyard was neat and tidy, but there was no one around, and I secretly wondered. After a while, Liang came out and said to me, "The master has summoned us, we must greet him according to proper etiquette." I nodded, and we then entered together, taking a winding path through several gates, finally reaching the master's room. It was a large house with five bays, the curtains were drawn and the candles dim, and only the sound of the pipa could be heard. I have always been fond of this, and was about to listen closely, when Liang ordered me to bow, and inside, it also stopped playing. I knelt outside the threshold, and Liang went in and reported. After a while, inside the curtain, a soft voice said, "If he is willing to serve me, that would be very good."

    45

    但虑野性未驯，可令居西廊，候心安方可执事。”梁诺而出，即引某之衣曰：“从余去，主人留汝矣。”某思匍匐檐前，仅得片语，且严厉若役仆婢，心实不甘，不得已而起从梁行。至西侧一室，梁推扉与之入，其中黑暗如漆，都无所见。惟以手抚之，床榻温软，似有衾褥。某不悦，因诘梁曰：“若言好消息，今入活地狱矣。请引我归。”梁笑曰：“若何暴躁如是？请即安寝，好事固在后也。”言讫，竟阖其扉而去。某不能耐，视门尚虚掩，潜出，将思夜遁行。及主者之居，忽闻人语曰：“娘子肤如凝脂，虽不著寸缕，究亦莫染纤尘。”主者大笑曰：“予实不惯被服，然终日若此，不免厌见生人。”言已，鼓掌大笑曰：“裸衣国故不足道也。

    Explanation

    45

    But I worry about your wild nature not being tamed, you can be made to live in the west corridor, wait until your heart is at ease before taking up duties." Liang agreed and went out, immediately pulling my clothes and saying, "Follow me, the master has kept you." I thought about crawling under the eaves, only to get a few words, and being treated severely like a servant or maid, my heart was truly unwilling, so I had no choice but to get up and follow Liang. We arrived at a room on the west side, where Liang pushed open the door and led me inside. It was dark as pitch, and I could see nothing. Only by touching around with my hands, I felt the warm and soft bed, it seemed to have quilts and pillows. I was not pleased, and thus questioned Liang, "If you said it was good news, now I have entered the living hell. Please guide me back." Liang laughed and said, "How could you be so impatient? Please rest, good things will come later." After finishing his words, he closed the door and left. I couldn't bear it, looking at the door still slightly open, I slipped out, thinking of fleeing at night. Upon reaching the residence of the master, I suddenly heard someone say, "The lady's skin is as smooth as congealed fat, even without wearing a single thread, she is still not stained by the slightest dust." The master laughed loudly, "I am not accustomed to wearing clothes, but if I stay like this all day, I can't help but get tired of seeing people." After speaking, he clapped and laughed loudly, "The country of naked clothing is not worth mentioning."

    46

    ”某骇其语，穴窗窥之，灯明如昼，见一丽人白身立室内，腻肌如雪，妖脸若桃，纤乳酥胸，麝脐粉股，历历可辨，心乃大动，而欲入无门。旋见媪婢二人侍妇就寝。某痴立移时，遂无去志。勉强归室，暗中扪其卧具，皆以缯纩制成，绝不类寒素家物。惝恍久之，且思妇貌不置。平明即起，梁复来。问慰毕，乃曰：“家食难安。”径引之出就近村沽酒市肉，饔飧皆具，直至昏暝方归，仍宿于其室。如是者数日，某终疑讶，故晏起，而究不见旭光。及与梁出，则赤驭半天矣。心不自安，又向梁求去。梁曰：“子勿匆遽，昨已白之主人，今夕当不再虚度也。

    Explanation

    46

    The narrator was startled by the words, and peeped through a hole in the window. The lights were as bright as day, and he saw a beautiful woman standing naked in the room, her smooth skin like snow, her face like a peach, her slender breasts and soft chest, her musky navel and white thighs, all clearly visible. His heart was greatly moved, but he had no way to enter. Soon, he saw an old woman and a maid serving the woman as she went to bed. The narrator stood in a daze for a while, and then had no intention to leave. Reluctantly returning to his room, he felt the bedding in the dark, which was all made of silk and cotton, not at all like the things from a poor family. He remained dazed for a long time, and could not stop thinking about the woman's appearance. At dawn, he got up immediately, and Liang came again. After asking and comforting him, Liang said, "It's hard to be at ease with food at home." He led him directly to the nearest village to buy wine and meat, and breakfast and dinner were all prepared, until dusk when they finally returned, and he still stayed in his room. Like this for several days, the narrator finally doubted and wondered, so he woke up late, but still did not see the morning light. When he went out with Liang, the red carriage was already halfway up the sky. His heart was not at ease, and he again asked Liang to leave. Liang said, "Do not hurry, I already told the master yesterday, tonight we will not waste it again."

    47

    ”至夜，主者果令梁传命某至，仍谒于帘外，内温语谓之曰：“闻君有妙技，今夕少暇，可为予一弹。”某敬诺。梁乃设矮座于檐下，授以琵琶。某觉其器鲜泽异常，心甚喜，转弦按指，竭生平之能事，而内终寂然不见称赏。甫一阕，梁传语曰：“主人谓技若止此，无足奇。尚有佳音，可以一奏。”某因停腕发声，微闻帘内叹息，似觉称善。连歌数曲，内有欢笑声，遽命卷帘，烛光射于槛外。某微睨之，婢媪夹侍，皆衣裳楚楚，帷中坐者身无寸缕，如前夕所窥者。因大骇，窃疑其非人，而妇已命某入，且赐坐，觌面之间，绝无羞态。某于烛影之下，睹此皓皓之白，不觉动情，欲令再歌，则强弩之末矣！妇笑而起，顾谓众曰：“是儿固得陇望蜀。双目灼灼，几令予置身无地。”遽命移烛，携某就枕，众皆嬉笑而散。

    47

    By night, the master indeed sent Liang to summon me, and I still paid respects outside the curtain. The host spoke gently to me, saying, "I have heard that you possess a wonderful skill. Tonight, I have a bit of free time, and you can play for me once." I respectfully agreed. Liang then arranged a low seat under the eaves and handed me a pipa. I felt the instrument was unusually fresh and lustrous, and I was very pleased. I turned the strings and pressed the fingers, exerting all my life's abilities, but the host remained silent without praise. Just as I finished one piece, Liang conveyed the message, "The host thinks that if this is all your skill, it is nothing special. There is still good music, which can be played." I paused, then began to sing, and I faintly heard a sigh from inside the curtain, as if it were a sign of approval. I continued to sing several more pieces, and there were laughter sounds inside. The host immediately ordered the curtain to be drawn aside, and the candlelight shone on the rail outside. I glanced inside and saw maidservants and female attendants, all dressed neatly. The person sitting in the curtain was naked, just like the one I had seen the previous night. I was greatly startled and secretly suspected it was not a person, but the woman had already ordered me to enter and gave me a seat. In the presence of each other, she showed no signs of shame. Under the candlelight, I saw her pure and white skin, and I could not help but be moved. I wanted her to sing again, but I was at the end of my strength! The woman laughed and stood up, looking around and saying to the crowd, "This child already has one thing but still wants another. Her eyes shine brightly, almost making me feel uncomfortable." She immediately ordered the candle to be moved and took me to the bed. Everyone laughed and dispersed.

    48

    某解衣而就妇，其软若绵，其腻如脂，狂荡不可名状。遂以为生平之奇遇，而不暇计及乎其他。及晓，梁来引某厅外饮食，率以为常。妇亦善琵琶，尽以其技授某。然某自与妇狎，形神日耗，渐欲避之。无如柔曼当前，见猎心喜，不数旬，容若槁木矣。一日又与梁出，就肆中朝食。饭毕，见壁上琵琶，取而拨之。梁方力止其操缦，早有数人突入曰：“逋逃者乃在此耶！”某惊视之，则皆公家价，奉命缉捕者也。喧嚣间，梁已失所在。众拥某行，某力请少缓，将索梁同往见公。众中有一人诟之曰：“汝病狂耶？梁自见摈于某公，出居都外，为人傭作，不数月呕血而亡。今将三稔，若转轮亦已匍匐矣。汝犹藉渠为汝分谤耶？”某乃愕然，具以实告。众亦讶其憔悴，因随某往踪迹。

    Explanation

    48

    He undressed and approached the woman, whose skin was as soft as cotton and smooth as fat, an unrestrained and indescribable passion overwhelming him. He considered this encounter to be a rare and extraordinary experience, so engrossed that he did not think of anything else. By dawn, Liang came to lead him outside for food, a routine that became the norm. The woman was also skilled at playing the pipa, and she imparted all her skills to him. However, as he became increasingly intimate with her, his body and spirit gradually deteriorated, and he began to want to avoid the situation. Despite this, the soft and graceful woman before him aroused his desire, and within a few days, his appearance had become dull and lifeless. One day, he went out with Liang and had breakfast at an inn. After finishing the meal, he saw a pipa hanging on the wall and took it to play. Liang was trying to stop him from playing, but before he could, several people suddenly burst in and exclaimed, "The fugitive is here, isn't he!" Startled, he looked up and saw that they were all bounty hunters on official orders. Amid the commotion, Liang had already disappeared. The crowd surrounded him and forced him to move. He strongly requested a little delay, wanting to search for Liang and go together to see the official. One person in the crowd scolded him and said, "Are you crazy? Liang was expelled by a certain official, lived outside the capital, worked as a laborer for others, and died after vomiting blood within a few months. It has been nearly three years now. If you were a wheel, you would already be crawling. Are you still relying on him to share the blame?" He was startled and truthfully told them what had happened. The crowd was also surprised by his emaciated appearance and followed him to trace the situation.

    49

    至则茂草荒烟，丛冢累累，绝无宅舍。某大惊，诣左近访之，有土人笑曰：“此殆前村贾家女也。”众诘其状，答曰：“贾前巨富，生女貌甚美，酷好弦缦，尤善琵琶。及笄，与里中少年私。其父闻而怒，乘寝掩执之，少年逾窗而遁。女乞全尸，遂裸而纳之于棺，生瘗于此。时母恫之，阴以琵琶殉葬。殁已五年余，田间卧者，犹时闻弹丝声。若所遇得毋是此耶？”众乃信某言。又询梁姓，土人尚能识其冢，指而示之曰：“白杨树下一抔土，是即梁二哥矣。”众因笑谓某曰：“六三不当谢冰上人耶？”乃哗然拥之，归告主人。索公前询老成人，已料其有异，及见某，稔知遇鬼，不复加责。后某病数月，危而后痊。遂赎其券，出家于正觉寺中，法名普通。辄皛缕为人道之，闻者咸惊异焉。

    Explanation

    49

    Upon arriving, he found a scene of overgrown vegetation and eerie smoke, with numerous graves scattered about, and not a single house in sight. He was greatly alarmed and went to inquire nearby. A local resident laughed and said, "This must be the woman from the Jia family of the previous village." When asked about her appearance, the resident replied, "The Jia family was once very wealthy, and they had a daughter who was exceptionally beautiful. She had a deep love for stringed instruments, especially the pipa. When she reached the age of fifteen, she had an illicit affair with a young man from the village. Her father, upon learning of this, became furious and, while she was sleeping, seized her. The young man climbed out of the window and fled. The girl begged for a proper burial, so she was placed naked in a coffin and buried alive here. At the time, her mother secretly buried the pipa with her as a funeral offering. It has been more than five years since her death, and those who lie down in the fields still sometimes hear the sound of strings being played. Could the encounter you had not be with her?" Everyone then believed the man's account. They also inquired about the surname Liang, and the local resident still recognized the grave, pointing it out and saying, "Under the white poplar tree, there is a small mound of earth—that is Liang Second Brother." The crowd then laughed and told the man, "Shouldn't Six Three thank the ice person?" They then loudly escorted him back to inform the master. The master had already suspected something unusual and, upon seeing the man, knew he had encountered a ghost, so he no longer reprimanded him. Later, the man fell ill for several months, nearly dying before recovering. He then redeemed his debt and became a monk at Zhengjue Temple, taking the Buddhist name Putong. He often recounted this story in detail to others, and those who heard it were all surprised and amazed.

    50

    外史氏曰：女以情死，死后复仍其故辙，当不啻徐娘老去焉。余疑此特逃奴之饰语耳，来必诚有是事也。如果有之，洵所谓牡丹花下死，作鬼亦风流。夜台无父捉奸，此女正快活不少。

    Explanation

    50

    The historian says: A woman dies out of her emotions, and after death, she continues her old ways, which is not just like Xu Niang growing old gracefully. I suspect this is merely the embellished words of an escaped slave, but there must certainly be such a thing. If it is true, indeed, as they say, dying under the peony and being a ghost is still elegant. In the underworld, there is no one to catch adulterers, and this woman is indeed very content.

    51

    ○　桃花女子
    　　平阳郑生，得扶鸾之术于其友，休咎多奇中。同袍有疑事，咸取决焉。其人年仅二旬，美如冠玉，且风雅能诗，暇辄与仙人倡和。虽无所判，亦盘桓竟日，仙始退。乙卯春仲，同人咸有志于秋闱，生亦以鹗荐为望，因集于城西某观中，悬乩召仙，以卜其售否。甫焚其符，悬毫即动，大书曰：“妾，桃花女子也。郎君近时安乐否？”生素未睹此号，与众友皆愕然。乩又自动，续书一绝曰：“儿家旧住桃花岸，君子曾匀柳叶眉。蝶不寻香香觅蝶，晓风残月负多时。”众以其语不庄，咸劝生速退之。生年少情痴，竟涉冥想，且爱其诗句，强以考事相叩。其乩飚疾如风，又成七律一首曰：“些儿心事为情苗，故解星珰下碧霄。绿绮抱来谁与拨，红笺叠就或重烧。胡麻自是羞相问，灵鹊非关懒作桥。前日眉峰今浅淡，因郎蹙损待郎描。”众览诗大惊，生亦有惧色。

    Explanation

    51

    Translation:

    The Peach Blossom Maiden

    Zhen Sheng of Pingyang, a young man of only twenty, had acquired the art of spirit mediumship from a friend, and his predictions of auspicious and inauspicious events were often remarkably accurate. Fellow scholars would seek his counsel for matters of doubt. Zhen Sheng was handsome, like a jade ornament, and refined in poetry. In his spare time, he would converse with spirits. Even when there was no decision to be made, he would linger all day, only for the spirits to eventually depart. In the middle of spring in the year of the rabbit (Yi Mao), his fellow scholars all had their sights set on the autumn imperial examinations, and Zhen Sheng also hoped for success. They gathered at a temple in the western part of the city to summon spirits through the use of a spirit board, to predict their chances of success. As soon as the talisman was burned, the spirit board began to move, and it wrote in large letters: “I am the Peach Blossom Maiden. How is the Gentleman doing these days?” Zhen Sheng had never encountered this name before, and his fellow scholars were all startled. The spirit board moved again on its own, and continued by writing a quatrain: “My home was by the banks of the Peach Blossom River, and the Gentleman once trimmed his eyebrows like willow leaves. Butterflies don’t seek fragrance, but fragrance seeks butterflies. The morning wind and waning moon have been neglected for a long time.” The others, finding the words unseemly, urged Zhen Sheng to dismiss the spirit quickly. The young man, being emotionally infatuated, delved into deep thought, and he loved the spirit’s poetry. He forcefully inquired about matters related to the examination. The spirit board moved swiftly like the wind, and then wrote a seven-character regulated verse: “A little bit of emotion is the seed of feelings, so I descended from the blue sky to understand. Who will play the Lüqi (a famous zither) when it is brought out? Red paper scrolls are folded and sometimes burned again. The huama (a type of sesame) is too shy to be asked about, and the lingue (magpie) is not lazy in building bridges. The brows that were once sharp are now faint, because of the Gentleman, they have been furrowed and now await his touch to smooth them.” The others were greatly shocked by the poem, and Zhen Sheng also showed a look of fear.

    52

    乩又书曰：“诸君在此，儿女子情事不便缅陈。且科名非吾所司，请即退。”书讫，微闻麝兰气，乩遂不动。友中有知几者，规生曰：“兄不可再为此矣！此邪妖也，魔且将至。避之或可，迎之必不免。”乃撤其坛，不再令生请，众亦默然各散。生至家，虽涉疑忌，而心窃艳之，以为奇遇。翌日，复设坛私召之。乩又动，则素所邀致之鹤仙，即纯阳所驭者也。即叩以女子踪迹，仙书二十字于柈，即告退。其文曰：“安不居官好，一了便烦恼。丑者半不知，人去他来了。”意似劝隐，而实寓“女子鬼也”四字谜矣。生迷不悟，反谓仙为戏己，或者功名不显之谶耳。明日又专召女子，且祝曰：“桃花大仙如不鄙弃鲰生，请即践前约。”乩动，果复降。叩以不来之故，书曰：“昨为飞琼姊邀去茗战，实无暇。不然，中心藏之，又谁能忘之耶？”生与之酬和，句皆柔媚，能荡人心目。更多情词勾挑，直陈自荐之意。生遂为所惑，坦然不以为虞。

    Explanation

    52

    The spirit tablet wrote again: "You gentlemen are here, it is not appropriate to discuss trivial matters of women and children. Scholarship and official titles are not within my jurisdiction, please leave immediately." After writing, a faint scent of musk and orchid was detected, and the spirit tablet remained motionless. Among the friends, those who understood the signs advised Mr. 生: "Brother, you must not do this again! This is an evil spirit, and the demon is about to arrive. Avoiding it might be possible, but welcoming it is certain to lead to trouble." They then removed the altar, did not allow Mr. 生 to make requests, and everyone silently dispersed. Upon returning home, although he was somewhat suspicious, Mr. 生 secretly admired it, thinking it a strange and wonderful encounter. The next day, he secretly set up the altar again to summon it. The spirit tablet indeed descended again, and the immortal who had been summoned, a crane immortal, was the one controlled by the Celestial Master of Purity. Mr. 生 asked about the woman's whereabouts, and the immortal wrote twenty characters on a plate before announcing its departure. The text read: "Peaceful life without official position is good, once achieved,烦恼 follows. The ugly one is half unaware, when people leave, he comes." The meaning seemed to suggest seclusion, but actually contained a four-character riddle meaning "the woman is a ghost." Mr. 生 did not understand, instead thought the immortal was playing a joke on him, perhaps it was a prophecy that his fame would not be prominent. The next day, he specifically summoned the woman and prayed saying: "If the Immortal of Peach Blossom does not disdain this insignificant person, please fulfill the previous agreement." The spirit tablet descended again, and when asked about the reason for not coming, it wrote: "Yesterday, I was invited by my sister Feiqiong to a tea battle, I truly had no time. Otherwise, if I keep it in my heart, how could anyone forget it?" Mr. 生 and the spirit exchanged verses, which were all gentle and charming, able to stir the heart and mind. With more affectionate and enticing words, the spirit directly expressed the intention of self-recommendation. Mr. 生 was thus deceived and was completely unconcerned about it.

    53

    至暮始去，犹恋恋不舍，生亦忽忽如有所失。自是无日不召，无召不来，并无烦促驾之符矣。生托言藏修，足不出户，客至亦不遑晋接，昼惟与乩相对。一夕又卜，夜召之，女亦至，生意盖有所为也。径戏诘其姿容，女答以诗曰：“花作温柔玉作肌，笔尖早已涤胭脂。郎君漫问奴颜色，初放夭桃嫩柳丝。”生读之神魂飞越，顿忘其仙，贸然戏之曰：“昔曾许我画眉，迄今未窥半面，岂张京兆能向空中着笔耶？”女不致辩，又裁一律曰：“久待霜毫画，非关妾闭门。雾中花自有，泥畔絮犹存。欲种合欢树，终须杜宇魂。何时轻似叶，飞上苧罗村。”生将细询，乩已悄然。不二日，生病怔忡，絮絮如与人语，而实无所见也。然虽不再召仙，而床头枕畔时有新诗，家人窃观之，字画研丽，迥非生之手笔。其诗颇多，不甚记忆，录其最足消魂者有：“红豆抛残思欲碎，青梅剖破意徒酸。

    Explanation

    53

    Until dusk, he finally departed, still reluctant to leave, and he too felt as if something was missing. From then on, there was no day he did not summon her, and she did not fail to come, and there was no need for urgent summons. He feigned seclusion for study, never leaving his home, and even when guests arrived, he had no time to greet them, spending the day only facing the spirit board. One night, he divined again, and summoned her at night, and she also came, and he must have had a reason. He boldly questioned her appearance, and she replied with a poem: "Flowers are gentle, jade-like flesh, the brush has already washed away rouge. My lord, do not ask about my appearance, it is like a newly bloomed peach blossom and tender willow branches." He read it, and his spirit soared, suddenly forgetting that she was a spirit, and rashly teased her, saying: "In the past, you promised to draw my eyebrows, but until now, I have not seen half of your face. Could Zhang Hong really draw in the air?" She did not argue, and then composed another poem: "Waiting long for the frosty brush to draw, not because I am keeping the door closed. Flowers in the mist exist naturally, cotton remains on the mud. Wanting to plant a happy tree, eventually requiring the soul of a cuckoo. When will it be as light as a leaf, flying to the village of苎罗?" He was about to ask more questions, but the spirit board suddenly disappeared. Within two days, he fell ill, muttering as if speaking to someone, but in reality, he saw nothing. However, even though he no longer summoned the spirit, there were often new poems by his bedside, and the family secretly read them, finding the calligraphy and painting exquisite, far beyond his own handiwork. The poems were numerous, and he did not remember them all, but here are the ones most capable of melting the soul: "Red beans thrown away, thoughts are shattered, green plums cut open, intentions are only bitter."

    54

    ”“闲书情字原非恨，欲佩萱花又不忘。”“依稀似梦含羞觉，仿佛如君带笑迎。”裙边豆蔻春空结，眉上葳蕤锁不开。”“翠带近来慵自解，银缸何日倩郎吹。”凡十数联，皆绮语醉心蚀魄。盖生不死，女之笔亦不辍也。生友闻其疾，咸来问视，皆力诤其谬。生虽默默不答，心则恍惚若寤。友去，生病少瘥，阖门交庆。诘朝盥濯，忽于匜器中见一律，则又女之诗也。其词曰：“归去来兮胡不归，春风春夜掩双扉。香添帐底芙蓉艳，暖入波心鸂鶒肥。自有暮云压玉枕，何须朝露恋荷衣。温柔乡与蓬山近，莫把蓬山咫尺违。”生得诗惘然者终日，疾复剧，遂以不起。浃旬竟卒。时去召乩之日，不过蓂荚两放也。死后见梦于其友曰：“女居重泉而绐我以仙境，未免大言夸人。然相得甚欢，亦殊无所苦。幸语我家，勿以余为念。”友惊寤，转告其家人，莫不骇异，究不知女子为何代人。其诗草至今犹在，晋人亦或见之。

    Explanation

    54

    “Though the sentiment in the book is not one of resentment, I wish to wear the萱flower yet still remember.” “I wake up from a dream, blushing, as if you greet me with a smile.” The skirt's edge is adorned with the blooming of a young girl, but spring remains empty; the eyebrows are lush but locked in a frown. “The green sash has been worn lazily, and when will the silver lamp be lit by your hands?” These dozen or so couplets are all enchanting words that captivate the heart and soul. As long as the man lives, the woman’s pen does not stop. Her friends, hearing of her illness, all came to visit and tried to persuade her of her mistake. Though he remained silent, his heart was in a state of confusion and awakening. After the friends left, his illness slightly improved, and the entire household rejoiced. The next morning, while washing, he suddenly found a poem in the water container, which was another of the woman’s verses. The poem reads: “Why not return, why not return to your home, the doors closed in the spring breeze and night. The fragrance of the lotus bed adds to the beauty of the bed, and the warmth enters the heart of the鸂鶒, making it plump. There are evening clouds pressing down on the jade pillow, so why cling to the morning dew on the lotus leaves? The place of comfort is close to the mythical蓬山, so do not keep it at a distance.” He spent the day in a state of bewilderment after reading the poem, and his illness worsened again, leading to his death. After ten days, he passed away. It was only two months since the day he summoned the spirit medium. After his death, he appeared in a dream to his friend and said, “She lived in the depths of the earth and deceived me with the illusion of a heavenly realm, which was perhaps too grandiose a claim. However, we were very happy together, and I did not suffer at all. Please tell my family not to worry about me.” The friend woke up in shock and relayed the dream to his family, who were all amazed. They could not determine which era the woman belonged to. Her poetry remains to this day, and some from the Jin dynasty may have seen it.

    55

    外史氏曰：乩不必真灵，而其灵者亦未必为真仙。故凡召而辄来者，大率在鬼与仙之间而已。何则？仙必不屑至，鬼又不能至，于是灵鬼独为之。但世人习此者甚多，而生竟以之贾祸，岂心之未克自正耶？抑亦真有夙缘耶？昔余内弟亦好为此，然即世之所谓托乩，非其真也。诗皆请者代为捉刀。后向余言，召乩之时，依稀若有神助，下笔数十言，挥洒立就，其间亦多有验者。意者冥冥中，果有操纵者乎？第恐为鬼为妖，则耗人精魄，为祸不浅矣！因弃其术，不再召。由此观之，余弟达人哉！而世之耽耽于此者，其亦未见夫前车耳！

    Explanation

    55

    Historical records state: The medium is not necessarily truly spiritual, and even if it is spiritual, it may not necessarily be a true immortal. Therefore, those who come immediately when summoned are likely between ghosts and immortals. Why is this so? Immortals would not deign to come, and ghosts cannot come, so only spiritual ghosts do it. However, many people engage in this practice, and they ultimately bring about misfortune, is it because their hearts have not yet been able to correct themselves? Or is it because they truly have karmic fate? In the past, my brother-in-law also liked to do this, but it was what the world calls relying on the medium, not the true practice. The poems were all written on behalf of the medium by the summoner. Later, he told me that when he summoned the medium, it felt faintly as if with divine assistance, and he wrote freely, finishing dozens of lines quickly, and many of them proved to be true. It seems that in the unseen realm, there might indeed be someone manipulating it? But I fear that if it becomes a ghost or an evil spirit, it will consume one's spirit and energy, causing great harm! Therefore, he abandoned the practice and no longer summoned the medium. From this, my brother-in-law was truly wise! But those who are obsessed with this, they have not seen the example of those who came before!

    56

    ○　红鞋
    　　某县有甲乙二人，姻娅也，素相嘲谑，见即喋喋不休。其地有深溪，阔仅数丈，而湍急不可以涉。一日，甲乙各约数人，将往某处游。路经溪侧，隔岸边相值，一水盈盈，聊通笑语。众中亦多相识，且言且行。二人复相谑，同游者靡不粲然。乙忽拔所佩小刀，戏谓甲曰：“忤奴勿再言，言将杀汝。”甲大笑，詈曰：“畜产，尔思弑父耶？生汝不肖，杀我固宜。”语次，以膺相向，嘻嘻不已。乙亦笑挺白刃，作击刺状。众方为之捧腹，甲忽颓然倒地。亟视之，喋血朱殷，刀已陷胸矣。甲竟死，举大骇。

    Explanation

    56

    The Red Shoe

    In a certain county, there were two men, Jia and Yi, who were closely related by marriage. They had always teased each other, and whenever they met, they would talk incessantly. There was a deep ravine nearby, only a few zhang wide, but the current was so swift that it could not be crossed. One day, Jia and Yi each invited several people to go on a trip to a certain place. As they walked along the side of the ravine, they met each other on the opposite bank, with a clear stream flowing between them, and they exchanged a few words of laughter. Many of the people present knew each other, and they continued talking as they walked. The two men continued to tease each other, and all those who were traveling with them smiled. Suddenly, Yi drew the small knife he was wearing and playfully told Jia, "You scoundrel, stop talking, or I'll kill you." Jia laughed and retorted, "Beast, are you planning to kill your father? You're unworthy of being born, so it's fitting that I die." As he spoke, he pointed his chest at Yi, laughing incessantly. Yi also laughed and brandished his blade, pretending to strike. Everyone was laughing heartily when suddenly Jia fell to the ground. They quickly looked at him, and there was a pool of blood, red and dark, with the knife already piercing his chest. Jia died, and everyone was greatly shocked.

    57

    视乙，犹含笑操刃，锋锷皆赤色。两岸喧噪，行人胥惊，观者如堵墙。乙始觉其杀人，欲遁不能矣。众夺其刃，逮以见官。邑令某公，素号神明，闻即驰往枪验。验讫即命人遏溪上流，俟其涸而穷其异。果有脚迹，自此岸而达彼岸，皆往来有踪。细视之，纤细如锥，不类丈夫，亦愕然。更命人深掘其泥，甫数尺，更得一箧，启之内藏女舄一双，鲜若朱莲，崭然未朽。令顿悟，即呼乙当场语之曰：“此夙孽也。汝虽未杀伯仁，伯仁由汝而死。嬉笑兴戎，罪实自取。”乙亦俯首无词，遂论抵。先大夫每举其事数数戒人，以为嬉戏无益，且有贾祸如此者，可不慎欤！

    Explanation

    57

    Looking at Yi, he was still smiling while holding a blade, with the blade's edge all red. The shores were noisy, the passersby were all startled, and the spectators were like a wall. Yi only then realized he had killed someone, but he wanted to escape and could not. The crowd took his blade and apprehended him to see the official. The magistrate of the county, Mr. Someone, was always known as a divine being. Upon hearing, he immediately rode to the scene to inspect. After the inspection, he immediately ordered someone to block the upstream of the stream, waiting until it dried up to investigate its strangeness. Indeed, there were footprints, from this shore to that shore, all traces of back-and-forth movement. Upon closer inspection, they were as fine as a needle, not resembling a man, and they were astonished. He then ordered someone to dig deeper into the mud, after digging just a few feet, they found a chest. Opening it, they found a pair of women's shoes, fresh as a red lotus, still sharp and not yet decayed. The magistrate suddenly understood and called Yi to the scene, telling him, "This is an old karma. Although you did not kill Bo Ren, Bo Ren died because of you. Playing around caused trouble, the fault was indeed self-inflicted." Yi also bowed his head without a word, and was then punished. My late father often recounted this matter to warn others, believing that play is not beneficial, and that there are such dangers, how can one be careless?

    58

    外史氏曰：律有戏杀一条，是其杀有由于戏者。此则断无能杀，而竟以戏成之。戏真无益于人矣！犹忆某州有一妇，年未二旬，以弑夫将置极刑。官讯之，辄頳颜涕泗，惟求速死。官不忍，命入内署，令夫人从容诘问，始得其情。则夫妇俱少年，家畜牝牡二卫。一日，夫如厩，见牡者跃跃，而牝者拒以后蹄，踢不使前。夫即呼妻往观，举大笑。及归室，强妇为此状。妇不可，夫终不怿，乃勉从之。甫一腾踔，适中睾丸，遂瞥然而倒。视之，已无救矣。此尤戏杀之可笑者，因并附记于右。

    Explanation

    58

    外史氏曰：Ancient law had a provision for accidental death due to play, where death resulted from playful actions. In this case, there was no intention to kill, yet the death was caused by play. Play truly does no good for people! I recall a woman from a certain state, who was not yet twenty years old, was about to be sentenced to death for killing her husband. When questioned by the official, she blushed and wept, only seeking a quick end to her suffering. The official, moved by compassion, ordered her to be taken to the inner office and instructed his wife to question her gently. Only then did they learn the truth. The couple were both young, and they kept two male and female animals. One day, the husband went to the stable and saw the male animal jumping around, while the female animal resisted with its hind legs, kicking to prevent it from moving forward. The husband then called his wife to see, and they laughed loudly. When they returned to the room, he forced the wife to mimic the scene. She refused, but the husband remained unhappy, so she reluctantly complied. As soon as she made a leap, she happened to hit the testicles, and she suddenly fell down. When they looked, it was already too late to save him. This is especially a comical case of accidental death, and I will also record this on the right.

    59

    ○　毒饼
    　　贡生某者，失其里族，大约秦晋间人也。年近七旬，有子数人，多游胶庠。某每出，跨一黑卫，从小奚，徜徉自如，乡人皆羡之。偶如邑城，见有市信者，固田家所需也。某心忽动，托言植花为虫啮，径浼所识为证，售得两许以归。人以其语挚，故弗疑。乃某并市饴糖，椎信约数钱，共为细屑，窃面作饼饵，烦邻人妇代蒸之。既熟，携去，莫知所用。讵某置饼道周，嬉笑自返。适近村新妇归宁，复其夫家，从一小弱弟，控蹇而行。瞥见巾裹食物委路侧，取而视之，热如新出于笼，乃大喜。妇方以母家綦贫，归无所携为己耻，因命弟珍重捧赍。甫入门，即矫母命献于舅姑，举室果有喜色。饼止七枚，妇之夫他出，室中之人适符其数，乃分而啖之。

    Explanation

    59

    A Poisonous Cake

    A certain candidate for the imperial examination, whose village and clan were unknown, was probably from the region between the states of Qin and Jin. He was nearly seventy years old and had several sons who studied in schools. Whenever he went out, he rode a black guard and was accompanied by a small servant, wandering leisurely and freely, and the villagers all envied him. Once, he went to the county town and saw someone selling letters, which were indeed needed by farmers. Suddenly, he was moved and fabricated a reason, saying that his flowers planted were eaten by insects, and asked someone he knew to vouch for it, obtaining a small amount to return home. People believed his words and did not doubt him. Then, he also bought some syrup and pounded letters and coins together, grinding them into fine powder, secretly making cakes, and asked a neighbor's wife to steam them. After they were cooked, he took them away, but no one knew what they were for. He placed the cakes on the road and laughed as he returned to himself. Just then, a newly married woman from a nearby village was returning to her parents' home to visit her husband's family, accompanied by a small and weak younger brother, riding a slow horse. She suddenly saw a bundle of food left on the side of the road, picked it up and looked at it, finding it as hot as if it had just come out of a steamer, and was greatly delighted. The woman was ashamed that her mother's family was very poor and she had nothing to bring back, so she ordered her younger brother to handle it carefully. As soon as they entered the door, she pretended to act on her mother's orders and presented it to her in-laws, and the entire household showed a look of joy. The cake was only seven pieces, and her husband was out, with the number of people in the room matching the number of cakes, so they divided and ate them.

    60

    其姑不忍食，以饲妇之弟。而妇以所携无多，力诃之使去，不令分甘，姑乃食。未几毒作，阖家溃乱，又未知所由，无从救药，七人竟无一生者。夫归乃执妇送官，惨被刑楚，细弱不胜。且不令弟食，百口莫辩，遂诬服。律当寸磔，其弟亦以知情，论斩决有日矣。忽贡生某，诣官自投，兼以干糒为证。提妇验之，款式悉合。鞫之则曰：“余忽作此想，聊以为戏，初不谓其可以死人也。今闻妇之冤，不胜侧然，用敢自首。”究亦莫解其何心。官为叹息，谓是前生冤报，案遂定。以某误毒七命，法应大辟有加，子孙虽不与谋，成丁以外皆论死，某家几无噍类。妇乃得脱。后闻其邑人云：“某素健讼，以贡生把持官府，遭其害者数十人。占者谓有灭门之祸，不意将就木而犹验也。

    Explanation

    60

    The grandmother, unable to eat, fed the woman's brother. The woman, carrying little, strongly scolded him to leave, not allowing him to share her provisions, and the grandmother then ate. Soon after, the poison took effect, and the entire family was in chaos, not knowing the cause, and unable to save them. None of the seven survived. The husband returned and took the woman to the authorities, where she suffered cruel punishment, being too weak to endure. She also did not allow her brother to eat, and a hundred mouths could not defend her, so she was falsely convicted. The law should have punished her by dismemberment, and her brother, considered guilty, was sentenced to death. Suddenly, a certain candidate went to the authorities to confess, bringing dry food as evidence. The woman was examined, and the details matched. When questioned, he said, "I suddenly thought of it, just as a joke, not expecting it to kill anyone. Now hearing the woman's injustice, I am deeply moved, so I dare to confess." Still, his motives remain unclear. The official sighed, considering it a karmic retribution from a past life, and the case was thus decided. Because of his mistake, seven lives were lost, and the law should impose the death penalty. Even though his descendants did not conspire, all adults were sentenced to death, nearly wiping out the family. The woman was thus released. Later, people in the village said, "He was always involved in lawsuits, as a candidate, he manipulated the authorities, and dozens suffered because of him. Diviners said there would be a family disaster, but did not expect it to be proven even as he was about to die."

    61

    外史氏曰：突如作此想，即突然作此事，冥冥中若无鬼神，人即病狂丧心，应不至是。独惜妇以无辜，遘此冤狱，向非某之自白，不几受污名而遭惨死耶？故折狱者，不可以不慎。

    ○　翠衣国
    　　陇蜀故多鹦鹉，土人恒罗之以为玩具。成都入蒋十三，畜一佳者，驯养数年矣。一日，有癭鹆来，止于树梢，呼鹦鹉为“能言公”，隔笼与之语，询之曰：“君不游翠衣国几年矣？”答曰：“丙年离乡，丁年罹罗，今居樊中，岁又三稔，通其首尾计之，已五易春秋矣。”癭鹆又曰：“颇亦思归否？”答曰：“胡不思？君不知我，我非生而羽者也。犹忆昔年为商，贩于湖湘间，贾尝三倍，且颇善言语，恒为人解纷，人无有难之者。某岁仲春，与同伴航海，将谋重利。

    Explanation

    61

    The Chronicler remarks: If one suddenly thinks of this, then one suddenly does this. As if there were no divine judgment, one would lose one's sanity, and such a thing should not happen. Alas, the woman, being innocent, suffered this wrongful imprisonment. Had it not been for the defendant's own admission, she would not have been slandered and met a terrible end. Therefore, judges must be cautious.

    The Land of Green Garments

    The regions of Long and Shu have always been rich in parrots, and the locals often catch them to keep as pets. Jiaozhao Thirteen of Chengdu kept one particularly fine parrot, which he had been training and caring for for several years. One day, a myna bird arrived, perched on a branch, and addressed the parrot as "Master of Speech," conversing with it from across the cage. The myna bird asked, saying, "Have you not visited the Land of Green Garments for several years?" The parrot replied, saying, "I left my hometown in the year Bing, was captured in the year Ding, and now live in this cage. It has been three years since I was captured, and counting from the beginning to the end, it has been five years." The myna bird asked again, "Do you miss your homeland?" The parrot replied, saying, "Of course I do! You don't know me; I was not born with feathers. I still remember the past when I was a merchant, trading in the Hu and Xiang regions, where my profits were tripled, and I was skilled in speech, often helping others resolve disputes, and no one found it difficult. In a certain year of early spring, I sailed with my companions, planning to make a substantial profit."

    62

    舟行至一岛，碧嶂插天，蔚蓝无际。偶拉客夥数人登眺其上，愈入则其境愈佳。涉历既深，顿忘归路。岛中无一人，惟有公辈飞鸣上下，不知几千万亿。予等病不能兴，又无弋获之具可仿罗雀之风，遂饿死于岩下。他人我不能知，予则渺渺然游行至一国土，宫殿巍峨，城郭富丽。其人无贵贱，皆衣翡翠裘。予询之，人曰：‘此海中第七岛，翠衣国也。’予因谒见其王，欲图归计。王年可五旬余，亦衣翠服，能识义理，通阴阳。其国中上大夫必能诗，中大夫皆能曲，下大夫亦能言，以捷给为才，从无有不鸣者。

    Explanation

    62

    A Boat Journey to an Island

    We sailed to an island, where blue peaks pierced the sky, and the azure sea stretched boundlessly. Chancing upon a few guests, we climbed and looked around, and the deeper we went, the more beautiful the scenery became. After venturing deep into the island, we suddenly forgot the way back. The island was deserted, with only birds flying and calling up and down, their numbers unknown, perhaps in the hundreds of millions. We were ill and could not rise, and we had no hunting tools to imitate the method of trapping sparrows, so we died of hunger at the foot of the rock. I cannot know about others, but I then wandered aimlessly to a country, where the palaces were majestic and the city walls were splendid. The people, regardless of rank, all wore jade-green fur. I asked them, and they said, "This is the seventh island in the sea, the Jade-Green Robe Country." I then went to see the king, to plan a way to return. The king was about fifty years old, also wore jade-green clothing, understood principles of righteousness, and knew yin and yang. The upper officials in the country must be able to write poetry, the middle officials can all compose music, and the lower officials can also speak, considering quick and agile as talent, and there is no one who does not speak.

    63

    遂馆予为客卿，后以贵主下降。主貌姣好，亦娴歌咏，与予伉俪甚欢。明年为予制此服之，遂能飞举。时与主翱翔于茂树，倡随无间。不意为近侍所诱，将欲归视故乡。行至山中，下而取食，为人所获，羁絏于兹不能返。每思主爱，如割寸心。君今去能为我致一口音，则幸矣。”癭鹆曰：“愿为驿使，虽远弗辞。”鹦鹉乃低吟一绝曰：“双飞何日向晴皋，每为卿卿惜羽毛。最是舌尖消瘦尽，绕笼犹自语叨叨。”诗成，俯首拳足，若不胜情。

    Explanation

    63

    Thus, I was housed as a guest minister, and later, the noble lady married down to me. The lady was beautiful and also skilled in singing and reciting poetry, and we were very happy together. Next year, she made this garment for me, and thus I was able to take flight. At that time, I flew with the lady among the lush trees, following her continuously. Unexpectedly, I was induced by a servant to plan to return and visit my hometown. Traveling to the mountains, I then took food, and was captured by someone, bound here and unable to return. Every time I think of the lady's love, it feels like cutting a small piece of my heart. If you can go now and bring me a message, then it would be fortunate." The bird said, "I am willing to be a messenger, even if it is far, I will not decline." The parrot then hummed a poem softly, saying: "When will we fly together toward the bright meadow? Every time, I cherish your feathers. Most of all, my tongue is worn out, still chattering around the cage." After finishing the poem, the parrot bowed its head and clenched its feet, as if overwhelmed with emotion.

    64

    癭鹆即振翼而飞，回翔而语曰：“必不辱命，‘君勿过伤。”遂飞去。时蒋卧小窗下，院宇无人，闻其语甚为惨然，乃起辟其笼而纵之。且嘱曰：“翠衣国路远，子宜自爱，慎勿再罹罗网之灾。”语竟，鹦鹉啁癮作谢，飘然高举，渐入云汉间，不转瞬而逝。蒋以此事语其家人，多不之信，且疑其故纵，蒋竟无以自明。逾年，蒋患疾疫，病垂毙。迷惘中见有人皂衣而鸟喙，直前启曰：“君家之囚已言于翠衣国主矣。命仆奉延，即请税驾。”蒋正昏愦，莫知所指，竟毅然随之行。其人奋臂一呼，早有绿衣人十数辇，驾一肩舆，舁蒋前往。

    Explanation

    64

    The parrot flapped its wings and flew, circling and speaking, saying, "I will not disgrace the mission; 'Do not grieve too much.'" It then flew away. At that time, Jiang was lying under a small window, and the courtyard was empty. Hearing its words, he was deeply saddened and rose to open the cage and release it. He also instructed, "The journey to the Land of Green Robes is far, so you should take care of yourself and be cautious not to fall into the disaster of nets again." After finishing his words, the parrot chirped in thanks, soared gracefully, and gradually ascended into the clouds, disappearing in an instant. Jiang told his family about this event, but they did not believe him and suspected that he had deliberately released the parrot. Jiang could not prove his innocence. A year later, Jiang fell ill with a plague and was on the verge of death. In his delirium, he saw a figure dressed in black with a beak-like face, who stepped forward and said, "Your captive has spoken to the ruler of the Land of Green Robes. I am ordered to escort you, so please rest here." Jiang was in a daze and did not know what was happening, but he followed the figure resolutely. The figure raised his arm and called out, and soon, several dozen green-robed people arrived, carrying a sedan chair, and carried Jiang away.

    65

    须臾至海上，波如山立，心甚惴惴。视其舆，轻犹一叶，去水仅寻余，毫无沾湿，行且如飞。既至，有绝境，都如鹦鹉所言。即有人迎于郊外，俯伏路旁，引吭而谢曰：“主君体好生之德，罢悦耳之具，网开三面，德并二天，使折翼之禽无难旋里，嫌笼之羽竟得生还。不独乐昌之镜重圆，抑且若敖之鬼弗馁。感恩涕泣，深愧衔环，拥篲郊迎，聊酬翼卵。”言讫，伏地哀鸣，一若感激不胜者。蒋自舆中窥之，驺从甚盛，冠盖甚都，其人年二十许，翠衣翩跹，疑即曩昔所纵者。

    Explanation

    65

    In a moment, they arrived at the sea, where the waves stood as tall as mountains, and the heart was filled with great anxiety. Looking at the carriage, it was as light as a leaf, only a few feet away from the water, and it moved as if flying without getting wet. Upon reaching the destination, there was an isolated place, just as the parrot had described. Immediately, people greeted them at the outskirts, bowing down on the side of the road, raising their voices in thanks and saying, "Master, you embody the virtue of cherishing life, stopping the use of pleasing instruments, leaving three sides of the net open, and your virtue is as high as two heavens. You have made it easy for the birds with broken wings to return home and the birds that dislike being caged to be set free. Not only has the broken mirror of Luoyang been reunited, but also the ghost of the ruler of Chu is no longer in need. We are grateful and shed tears of thanks, deeply ashamed to repay your kindness, and we sweep the road and welcome you at the outskirts, slightly repaying the care of nurturing." After speaking, they bowed down and mournfully called out, as if overwhelmed with gratitude. Jiang looked out from the carriage and saw a grand entourage, with elegant hats and carriages, and a man around twenty years old, dressed in green and moving gracefully, whom he suspected to be the one he had released in the past.

    66

    乃降舆慰劳，并驾而进。入其国，人皆衣碧，语言皆带鸟音。将至路门，国主躬亲迎迓，揖而言曰：“寡人愚昧，国禁废弛，致令金闺爱婿辱于弋人。微先生释之归里，则弱女无与并栖，即不穀亦无与共治矣。”语甚癰谦。蒋目之，貌古神清，被服赫奕，因逊谢。国主揖蒋入，延至殿廷，纳之上坐。将下拜，蒋辞让至三，然后以宾主礼相见。既坐，国主又言曰，“儿女辈赖君完聚，时铭五中，无由申报。适闻病在床蓐，故遣剪舌侯奉邀，幸辱惠临，当令叩谢。”因命传语后庭，使白贵主，旋铺红毡于地。

    Explanation

    66

    He dismounted from his carriage to comfort and reward them, and they rode together. Upon entering the country, all the people wore green and spoke with a bird-like quality. As they approached the road gate, the king personally welcomed them, bowed, and said, "I am ignorant, and the national prohibitions have been neglected, leading to the disgrace of my beloved son-in-law by hunters. If it were not for your release of him, my young daughter would have no one to live with, and even I would have no one to govern with." His speech was very humble. Jiang looked at him, noting his ancient appearance and clear spirit, and his splendid attire. Jiang modestly declined. The king bowed and invited Jiang to enter, leading him to the palace courtyard and seating him in the upper seat. As Jiang was about to bow, he declined three times, and then they met with guest-host etiquette. After sitting down, the king said again, "Our children rely on you for their reunion, and we always inscribe this in our hearts, but we have no way to report it. We just heard that you are ill in bed, so we sent Lord of the Tongue to invite you. It is fortunate that you have graced us with your presence, and we will make you bow and thank you." He then ordered a message to be transmitted to the inner court, instructing the noble lady, and immediately laid a red carpet on the ground.

    67

    俄有小鬟十余，自屏后捧一丽人出，齿甚稚，翠羽之服，玉声璆然。夫妇并肩皆北面再拜，蒋不获辞，却而后受，主即退。国主命设宴于“望祢亭”，与蒋欢饮，且告曰：“此寡人跂望正平之地也。异世知心，今与君为二矣。”于是飞觞痛饮。诸大夫皆在坐，有献诗者，有歌曲者，纷沓而前，蒋亦不甚记忆。国主知蒋有恙，命取海中神露和酒饮之，恍若沃以冰雪，病遂除。宴毕，国主谢曰：“敝路褊小，土产绝稀。不腆敝赋，未足以酬大恩，聊供君之玩好，幸勿挥斥。”乃进明珠十粒，紫玉一双，约值数千緡。

    Explanation

    67

    Soon after, several young maids emerged from behind a screen, carrying out a beautiful young woman. She was very young, dressed in a garment adorned with green feathers, and the sound of jade was clear and resonant. The couple stood side by side and performed a formal bowing ritual, facing north and bowing twice. Jiang could not decline, so he stepped back and accepted the gift, after which the host withdrew. The king then ordered a banquet to be set up at the "Wang Mi Pavilion" and drank joyfully with Jiang, saying, "This is the place where I have been eagerly waiting for someone like Zhengping. A kindred spirit from a different era, now we are two." They then passed around the wine cups quickly and drank deeply. Various officials were all present, some offering poems, some singing songs, coming forward in a flurry. Jiang did not remember much of it. The king knew that Jiang was ill and ordered to fetch the divine dew from the sea to mix with the wine and drink it. It felt as if he was cooled with ice and snow, and his illness was cured. When the banquet ended, the king thanked and said, "Our humble country is small and narrow, with very few local products. Our meager offerings are not sufficient to repay your great kindness, but just to provide you with enjoyment, please do not reject them." He then presented ten pearls, a pair of purple jade, worth several thousand coins.

    68

    小鬟又传夫人命，致水心镜一围，珊瑚树盈尺，曰：“敬以报钗合镜圆之德。”贵主夫妇又私有赠遗，国主命寄于近海市肆，以券付蒋，令其自取。仍命皂衣人送之还，国主冰玉亲饯于郊，握手流连，甚不忍别。蒋思归念切，登舆而返。比至家，举家号咷，将殓尸于榇，死已二日矣。蒋推衾而起，家人大惊，询之，始得其故。出视庭柯，有癭鹆爰止未去，始悟所谓剪舌侯者，即此是也。乃设食饲之，三嗅而作。蒋疾大愈，欲诣海肆合其券，家人以为妄，力止之，遂不果行。至今蜀人呼鹦鹉曰“能言公”，其遗意云。

    　　外史氏曰：鸟之酬恩，无足深异，异在间关对语，俨有乡人话旧、知己谈心之状。而吟诗一段，尤为惨动心脾。宜乎，蒋子不忍闻而纵之归也。昔有达人，尝戒弟之畜鸟，谓其音凄楚，人家有此，多近不祥。语虽迂而实切于理。今闻此事，益信达者非无稽之谈，恻隐君子所宜深戒焉。

    Explanation

    68

    A young female servant delivered the lady's message, presenting a round waterheart mirror and a small coral tree, saying, "I respectfully offer these to repay the virtue of a happy union." The noblewoman and her husband also gave private gifts. The king ordered that a voucher be sent to a market near the sea for Jiang to redeem himself. He still ordered a person in black clothing to send him back, and the king, with his pure and beautiful presence, personally saw him off at the outskirts, holding hands and lingering, very reluctant to part. Jiang's longing to return home was intense, and he climbed into the carriage and returned. By the time he reached home, the whole family was wailing, preparing to bury his body in a coffin, as he had been dead for two days. Jiang pushed aside the quilt and got up, greatly surprising the family. When they asked him, he finally understood the reason. He went out to look at the courtyard trees and saw a myna bird that had stopped and not left. He finally realized what was meant by the "cut-tongue lord"—it was this bird. He then set out food to feed it, and it sniffed three times and then flew away. Jiang's illness greatly improved, and he wanted to go to the market near the sea to redeem the voucher. His family thought it was absurd and strongly stopped him, so he did not go. Until today, people in Shu call the myna bird "the eloquent gentleman," this is the lingering meaning.

    The historian said: Birds repaying kindness is not particularly unusual, but what is extraordinary is their conversational interaction, as if they were old acquaintances reminiscing or close friends sharing their hearts. The section about reciting poetry is especially moving and heart-wrenching. It is understandable that Jiang could not bear to hear it and let the bird go. There was once a wise man who warned his younger brother against keeping birds, saying that their mournful cries were often a sign of impending misfortune. Though the statement may seem quaint, it is actually rooted in wisdom. Hearing this story, one can see that the wise man was not speaking without reason, and it is a lesson that compassionate and wise individuals should deeply heed.

    69

    ○　痴婿
    　　幼时闻老妪常言，每及痴婿，辄为捧腹。然其事不雅驯，不足以资谈柄。及长闻某县巨家，生子而痴。其新婚之夕，种种可发人噱，而竟变化，于其妻琴瑟反以綦调，又非如聊斋之小翠，能以术易其夫者。其事在康熙初年，某家生女，貌美而性慧，少读书。父母皆雅人也，弹棋品茗，种药栽花，日惟以韵事相娱乐。暮年生女，珍惜之，不使苦读，故女仅识之无。而自孩提以至成人，久居芝兰之室。年十三，顿失怙恃，寄养于其伯兄。乃兄嫂又皆癱人，居女为奇货。邑有大姓，知其可饵，遂以百金许聘之，又不责其奁资，伯兄喜而诺。女甫及笄，竟遣之于归。大姓之子，痴人也，不第菽麦不辨，抑且牝牡不知，女在闺中亦耳其名。

    Explanation

    69

    A Foolish Son-in-Law

    When I was young, I often heard an old woman tell stories, and whenever she mentioned a foolish son-in-law, she would laugh heartily. However, these stories were not elegant or refined, and they did not provide much material for conversation. Later, when I grew up, I heard of a wealthy family in a certain county who had a son who was foolish. On the night of his wedding, there were many absurd things that could make one laugh, but ultimately, he underwent a transformation, and his relationship with his wife became extremely harmonious, unlike the story of Xiao Cui from "Liaozhai Zhiyi," who could change her husband through magic.

    This story takes place in the early years of the Kangxi Emperor's reign. In a certain family, a daughter was born who was beautiful and intelligent, but she was not encouraged to read much. Her parents were cultured and refined people, enjoying leisure activities such as playing chess, drinking tea, growing herbs, and planting flowers. In their old age, they gave birth to a daughter whom they cherished and did not force to study hard, so she could only recognize the characters for "yes" and "no." From childhood to adulthood, she lived in a refined and pleasant environment. At the age of thirteen, she suddenly lost her parents and was placed in the care of her paternal uncle. Her uncle and aunt were both disabled, and they treated her as a valuable commodity. A prominent family in the county knew of her and decided to entice her with a marriage proposal, offering one hundred gold pieces without demanding a dowry. Her uncle happily agreed. The girl, just as she reached the age of fifteen, was sent to marry into this family. The son of the prominent family was also foolish, unable to distinguish between beans and wheat, and even unable to tell the difference between male and female. The girl, while living in the inner chamber, only knew of his name.

    70

    人或为女不平，女笑而不答，惟私念曰：“苟知人事，似犹可夫，奚必过市其聪敏耶！”比及花烛之下，众目环瞩，婿出，面不垢而若垢，口不涎而若涎，五官四体举若冥顽不灵。且见女亟自引避，如逢怪异。大姓夫妇强之成礼，当堂拜，不知兴，跪不知叩，推之前，挽之后。一时贺客莫不掩口葫芦，女亦不禁失色，自嗟薄命，无可如何。婿既痴颠，女又羞涩，一切牵红合卺之文章，草草而已。

    Explanation

    70

    Some people felt sorry for the woman, but she smiled and did not respond, only thinking to herself, "If I knew how to handle human affairs, I might still be suitable as a wife. Why must I display my intelligence in public?" By the time of the wedding ceremony, with everyone's eyes watching, the groom emerged, his face not dirty but looking dirty, his mouth not drooling but looking like he was drooling. All his facial features and limbs seemed dull and unresponsive. Moreover, seeing the woman quickly withdraw, as if encountering something strange, the wealthy couple forced the ceremony to proceed. At the altar, he did not know how to rise, and when he knelt, he did not know how to bow. He was pushed forward and pulled back. At that moment, all the guests present could not help but cover their mouths in embarrassment. The woman also could not help but look pale, lamenting her unfortunate fate and feeling powerless. The groom was already foolish and mad, and the woman was shy and embarrassed. All the formalities of the wedding, such as tying the red thread and sharing the wine cup, were conducted hastily and perfunctorily.

    71

    及入洞房，犹闻其吃吃诣父曰：“面白白而衣红红，又是几阿姨？”盖其父有妾数房，年仅少长于女，故云云。父怒且笑，强叱之。既而又询其母，母曰：“为汝娶妇伴汝，何犹不知耶？”婿色似甚喜，取木牛土马之属，杂置于合欢床，而自戴面具跳舞。而前招女曰：“胡不下与予戏？”女且羞且愤，面壁弗答。婿竟呱然大啼，奔告其母曰：“妇不好弄，娶来奚为？”阖室女眷哄然大笑。女闻之，益惭，时以泪洗面。旋自解曰：“幸犹知人语，事尚可为。”乃不复悲。其姑因子痴，益怜妇，屡抚慰之。至夜，婿虽被强就枕，不知为人道，女又处子故态，不自解衣，其不成欢可想见已。

    Explanation

    71

    Upon entering the bridal chamber, the groom was still heard to stammer as he approached his father, saying, "White-faced and red-clothed, who is this aunt again?" It seems his father had several concubines, some younger and some older than the bride, hence his confusion. The father scolded him sternly but with a hint of amusement. He then consulted the mother, who replied, "I married you a wife to accompany you; how could you not know her?" The groom appeared delighted, and he fetched wooden oxen and clay horses, placing them on the bed of harmony, and began to dance with a mask on his face. He called out to the bride, "Why don't you come down and play with me?" The bride felt both embarrassed and angry, turning her back to him without answering. The groom burst into loud, infantile cries and ran to his mother, complaining, "The wife isn't playful at all; why did you marry her to me?" The entire room of female relatives burst out laughing. The bride, hearing this, felt even more ashamed and often cried. Soon after, she comforted herself, saying, "At least I can still understand human speech; there is still hope." She stopped crying. Her mother-in-law, seeing the groom's foolishness, felt even more pity for the bride and comforted her repeatedly. By night, though the groom was forced to go to bed, he did not know how to proceed with intimacy, and the bride, still in her maidenly state, refused to undress herself. It was clear that they did not achieve a harmonious union.

    72

    迨阅三朝，女心计曰：“业已倡随分定，若竟听其痴，将终身之托谓何？”因而强按羞颜，不复作儿女态。剪楮裂缯，作为飞鸢舞蝶之类，招婿与观，亦鼓掌言乐。女又取其戏具，共戴假面，与之憨跳于室中。婢妪盈前，恬不为怪。大姓夫妇见之，反喜女不知愁，并不加禁。如是者旬日，婿亦渐稔，跬步不离，俨然伉俪。女又时藏果饵以饲之，婿竟日不出室。女知机会可乘，每日整装，牵之使坐于侧，频频引镜自照，询以好否。贪色固人性，婿虽痴，年已成人，情窦渐启，亦笑而颔之，答曰：“好好！”女又询曰：“汝爱否？”笑曰：“爱爱！”女心窃喜。自是间与之戏，暇即刺绣于床，曳使并坐。辄以一足置其膝，朱莲纤嫋，诱使抚摩。又询之以好否，其答如前。女因潜择吉期。一夕，与共寝，尽去襦裤，裸而后眠。盖自燕尔以来，从未露体相向也。夜半，引使扪搎周身殆遍，柔肌腻骨，着手欲融。

    Explanation

    72

    By the third day, the woman thought to herself, "Having already established a marital bond, if I were to let him be foolish, what would become of my lifelong commitment?" Therefore, she forced herself to suppress her shyness and no longer acted like a child. She cut paper and fabric to make kites and dancing butterflies, inviting her husband-to-be to watch, and they both clapped and spoke joyfully. The woman then took his toys, and they wore masks together, jumping playfully with him in the room. Maids and nurses were present, but they were not surprised. The parents-in-law saw them and were pleased that the woman did not seem sad, and they did not forbid it. For ten days, the husband-to-be also became familiar, never leaving each other's side, as if they were a married couple. The woman sometimes hid fruits and snacks to feed him, and the husband-to-be stayed in the room all day. The woman knew the opportunity was ripe, and she dressed herself up every day, pulling him to sit beside her, frequently looking in the mirror to admire herself, and asking if he liked it. Desire for beauty is inherent in human nature; though the husband-to-be was foolish, he was already an adult, and his feelings were beginning to develop. He smiled and nodded, replying, "Yes, yes!" The woman asked, "Do you love it?" He laughed and said, "Love love!" The woman secretly rejoiced. From then on, she occasionally played with him, and when she had free time, she embroidered on the bed, pulling him to sit next to her. She would place one foot on his knee, her red lotus, slender and graceful, enticing him to touch. She again asked if he liked it, and he answered as before. The woman secretly chose a favorable date. One night, they slept together, removing all their clothes, and lay naked. Since their wedding night, they had never been naked in front of each other. At midnight, she led him to touch her body almost everywhere, her soft flesh and smooth bones melting in his hands.

    73

    女又询之如昼，婿笑而不语。再询之，始曰：“好甚。”女潜以轻躯暱就，婿果大动，不须臾，而好事成矣。侵晨，女约之曰：“勿与人言，言将挞汝。”婿果遵守勿泄。至镜台之侧，无反目之时；绣筐之旁，皆捧足之日。月余，女病吞酸。大姓夫妇以为异，使黠婢覸之，窃其事以告，则闺房之乐，竟较之寻常不痴者，殆尤甚焉。皆大悦，益爱怜女。女遂自此与婿尽弃儿戏，晨昏定省而外，即坐房中，或藏钩射覆，或博弈饮酒。婿故不解，而灵明已复，积渐能通。久之，竟与女相埒。女又教以读书，亦能辨字，非复吴下阿蒙。且自女之入也，每晨起，亲为盥濯，整巾帻，饰衣裳。数日务令一浴，摩顶放踵，刮垢磨光。婿貌本非不扬，加以人功，亦翩翩年少矣。期年，女孪生二子，锦襁绣褓，玉润珠圆。见者皆谓其肖母，而不似父。然痴婿之痴，早又乘坚策肥，周旋肆应，使自念其蚩蚩之态，当不免哑然而笑矣。

    Explanation

    73

    The woman again asked him as if it were daytime, and the groom smiled but did not speak. When she asked again, he finally said, "Very good." She secretly approached him with her light body, and the groom indeed became excited, and in no time, their affair was consummated. At dawn, the woman warned him, "Do not tell anyone, or I will beat you." The groom indeed kept the secret. At the mirror table, there was no time for quarrels; at the embroidered box, all were days of mutual support. After a month, the woman fell ill with acid regurgitation. The wealthy couple found it strange and sent a clever maid to spy on them and secretly report back. It turned out that the pleasure in the marital bed was even more intense than that of ordinary foolish people. They were all very pleased and loved and pitied the woman more. The woman then completely abandoned childish play, and except for morning and evening visits, they would sit in the room, playing hide and seek with hooks or playing games and drinking. The groom was originally not intelligent, but his spirit and intellect had returned, and he gradually became adept. Over time, he eventually matched her. The woman also taught him to read, and he could recognize characters, no longer the simpleton from Wu. Moreover, since the woman entered his life, every morning, she personally washed him, combed his hair, and dressed him. She made sure he bathed every few days, from head to toe, scrubbing away dirt and smoothing the skin. The groom's appearance was not bad to begin with, and with her care, he became a handsome young man. After a year, the woman gave birth to twin boys, in brocade swaddling clothes and embroidered swaddling cloths, like jade and pearls. Those who saw them all said they resembled their mother, not their father. However, the groom's former simplicity was long gone, and he now rode a sturdy horse and managed affairs, making him reflect on his simple and innocent appearance, and he could not help but laugh at himself.

    74

    外史氏曰：瞽者无相，则将伥伥乎奚之？人至于雌雄罔辨，痴已甚矣！非有女以相之，又何能脱凡胎而成仙骨耶？然女之于此，其柔肠不知几千百转，始肯忍耻含酸，强为此态。否则，金闺女儿，其孰无面目若是哉？余既鉴女之心，益怜女所遇之不幸。

    Explanation

    74

    The historian remarks: If a blind person lacks guidance, what will they do aimlessly? When people reach a state where they cannot distinguish between male and female, their foolishness has reached its peak! Without a woman to guide them, how can they transcend ordinary existence to achieve spiritual enlightenment? Yet, in this regard, a woman's tender emotions have undergone countless twists and turns before she is willing to endure shame and bitterness, forcing herself into this state. Who among the daughters of noble families would not be ashamed to act in such a manner? I have understood the woman's heart and feel even more pity for her unfortunate circumstances.

    75

    ○　犬婿
    　　余友邵次彭，具生花之舌，言事极其酷肖。一日谓余曰：近有一异闻，非君莫可与语，能不濯以污渌乎？”余曰：“愿闻概。”邵曰：“家有旷室，每听人之僦居，值之轩轾弗计也。岁仲春，有一妇年约二旬，貌颇妖冶，来赁屋。家人与之约，月半緡，亦不较，遂徙来居焉。家无丈夫，只一犬，狞毛狮，豺口狼牙，庞然大物也。初来人无敢近，久之觉甚驯，亦无常畜者。惟有男子入妇室，则人立而咋之，噬衣裂肤，势甚汹。家之婢媪若往，则摇尾承迎，引导使入。余以为召南之龙，卫如玉之女。居既久，婢媪皆熟识，默伺之，迹甚奇诡。夫人畜犬，不过食以残炙，饮以余沥已耳。妇则每食必呼曰：‘饭矣。’犬即昂然入。妇逊犬上坐，敬以食置其前。犬食讫，妇食。窥之者不禁疑讶，谓是犬也，何相敬如宾之若是哉？一，某仆之妇服役于内，归甚迟，将就己室，而适经彼室。

    Explanation

    75

    My Friend's Dog Husband

    My friend, Shao Ziceng, possesses a tongue that blooms like a flower, speaking of matters in an extremely vivid and lifelike manner. One day, he said to me, "I have recently heard of a strange occurrence. Only you can understand it, and I wonder if you can avoid being tainted by its turbidity." I replied, "I would like to hear it." Shao said, "Our family has a vacant house, which we rent out without regard to its quality or value. During the second month of the year, a woman in her early twenties, quite beautiful and alluring, came to rent the house. Our family agreed to a monthly rent of half a string of coins, and she accepted without haggling. She then moved in. There is no man in the house, only a dog, fierce with lion-like fur, a jackal's mouth, and wolf's teeth, a huge and imposing creature. At first, no one dared to approach it, but over time, it seemed quite tame, and no one regularly kept it. However, whenever a man entered the woman's room, the dog would stand on its hind legs and snap at him, tearing his clothes and skin, with a very fierce manner. If a servant or maid from the household went there, the dog would wag its tail in welcome, guiding them in. I thought it was like a dragon from the Shijing (Book of Songs) or a virtuous woman from the Wei section of the Shijing. After living there for a long time, the servants and maids became familiar with it, observing its behavior, which was very strange and unusual. Generally, people keep dogs by feeding them scraps and giving them leftover water. But this woman would always call out, 'It's time to eat.' The dog would then enter with a proud air. The woman would invite the dog to sit above her, respectfully placing food in front of it. The dog would finish eating first, and then the woman would eat. Those who observed this could not help but be puzzled and astonished, wondering why a dog could treat its master with such respect as if it were a guest? One day, a servant's wife was working inside the house and returned very late. She was about to go to her own room when she happened to pass by the woman's room.

    76

    闻窗有犬声，疑其未寝，穴而觇之。时正望后，月明，见妇白身偃，犬如人形俯伏其上，绝类交媾者。犬狺狺然，妇亦睥睨有。乃大骇，旋闻妇作颤声曰：‘毛毵毵，刺人肌肤，颇不可。’又曰：‘予倦矣，若何未餍耶？’遂寂然。视犬则已下榻，不觉捧腹。明日言之同辈，哄传以为笑柄。余闻之，意殊不。至冬，妇诞一子，周身皆长毫，形状如猱，因弃之不育。犬忿恚不食者竞日，事益泄。媪有与妇善者，私以谓之曰：‘若也，奈何以犬为婿耶？’妇腼然者久之，蹙额曰：‘此诚夙孽，勿言，愿以实告。予生十有五年，父母将为择婿。人既问名，忽得奇疾，若患疯痫，狂言者竟日。给以饮食，尽挥之，惟人便，父母亦坚不肯与。一日，自言曰：“若致我为畜，若乃择人而事耶？”其声音都不类予。父母疑而诘之，则曰：“我某也，汝女前世为人妇，曾与我私。既而利尽交疏，乃与夫谋，置我于死。

    Explanation

    76

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    Translation

    Hearing a dog's sound from the window, I suspected she had not slept, and peered through a hole. At the time, the moon was bright, and I looked behind. I saw the woman lying naked, and the dog, shaped like a human, was crouching on top of her, resembling a couple engaging in sexual intercourse. The dog barked fiercely, and the woman also looked around suspiciously. I was greatly frightened. Soon, the woman spoke in a trembling voice, saying: "The hair is so long and prickly, it hurts my skin, it's quite unbearable." She then said: "I am tired, why haven't you satisfied me yet?" Then it became silent. Looking at the dog, it had already descended from the bed. I couldn't help but laugh. The next day, I told my peers, and they spread it around, making it a laughing matter. I heard about it, but I felt very differently. By winter, the woman gave birth to a son, who was covered in long hair, and his shape resembled a monkey. So, they abandoned him and did not raise him. The dog, filled with anger, refused to eat all day. The matter gradually became known. The old woman had a friend who was close to the woman, and she privately told her: "You, how could you choose a dog as your husband?" The woman blushed for a long time. She frowned and said: "This is truly a past sin, don't talk about it, I wish to tell you the truth. I was fifteen years old, and my parents were going to choose a husband for me. When people asked about my name, I suddenly contracted a strange illness. It was like I had madness or epilepsy, speaking wildly all day. When given food and drink, I threw it all away. Only when I went to the bathroom, and even then, my parents firmly refused to give me anything. One day, I said to myself: 'If you turn me into an animal, should you choose people to serve?' Her voice was not at all like mine. My parents doubted and questioned her. She said: 'I am someone, your daughter was a wife in her past life, and she once had an affair with me. Later, when our interests were exhausted and our relationship grew distant, she conspired with her husband to kill me.'

    77

    我殁后，诉之冥司，冥王不以我为直，反怒我淫，谪之为犬，今且三世矣。汝女以改邪从贞，仍得为人。予大怒，又诉之冥王，判令生汝家，与汝女为匹偶，以彰果报。诘朝将诞，汝女若适他人，我必杀之。”语已，予顿仆。少顷即苏，旧疾如失。明晨，家犬生五子，其一即是犬也。父母以予故，将尽坑之，又以事涉荒诞，一毙数命，有所不忍于心，遂不果杀。次年，予甫二八，犬亦成立，跬步必随予。有执柯者至，咋之几死，因以巨练絷之。夜深断其索，入予室，登予榻，噬予衾褥，但不及肤，若以之示意者。父母惧，乃不敢以字予人。及秋，父母皆病疫，予之宿病又作，且发狂裸裎而奔，人挽之亦不能止。夜恒卧土室中，坚不出，惟犬相从。未几，父殁母愈，予又夜奔。予母往迹之，则犬俯予身，其辱不可胜言矣。母为此气结，寻亦卒。亲族知其事，遂不以人类待予，议析予产。

    Explanation

    77

    After my death, I appealed to the underworld, but the King of the Underworld did not consider me just but instead became angry at my lechery, condemning me to be a dog, and it has been three generations now. Your daughter, by turning away from evil and embracing virtue, still became human. I was greatly angered and appealed to the King of the Underworld again, who decreed that I should be born into your family and become a match for your daughter, to demonstrate the consequences of actions. On the day of birth, if your daughter marries someone else, I will kill her. After speaking, I suddenly fainted. A short while later, I revived, and my old illness was gone. The next morning, the family dog gave birth to five puppies, one of which was the dog. Your parents, because of me, planned to kill all of them, but since the matter involved absurdity, killing several lives would be too cruel, so they did not end up killing them. The following year, I was just 16, and the dog also grew up, always following me. When someone came to propose marriage, the dog nearly killed him, so he tied it with a thick rope. Late at night, it broke the rope, entered my room, climbed onto my bed, and bit my bedding, but did not touch my skin, as if to convey a message. Your parents were afraid, so they did not dare to betroth me to anyone. By autumn, both your parents fell ill with an epidemic, and my old illness flared up again. I ran around naked and mad, and no one could stop me. At night, I always slept in a dirt room, refusing to come out, with only the dog accompanying me. Not long after, your father died and your mother recovered, and I ran around again at night. Your mother went to follow me, and the dog lay on my body, the humiliation was too great to describe. Your mother was so distressed that she soon died. My relatives, knowing what had happened, no longer treated me as a human and planned to divide my property.

    78

    乃人甫及室，犬即狂噬，无有能御之者，家资赖以不散。予亦弗狂，而迷惘中亦知与犬为牝，心甚耻之。然愈后，自思亲枝且缘此不齿，况他人孰肯以予为妇，于是决意从犬。涤其垢污，美其饮食，事之如夫，今五年矣。生三胎，皆不敢育。将隐忍以终身，夫复何言？’妇语毕，泪零。媪故善谑，而诘曰：‘犬之乐亦犹人之乐欤？’妇默然良久，亦破涕曰：‘今遇痴婆子，予不忍匿矣。人之乐予不能知，乃自与犬寝处，初在病中，昏然罔觉。及愈，更蒙面受辱。久而相习，始得其乐，谅壮男子不过如是，因而爱恋弗舍，不知其他。但犬性奇妒，予若见一幼童，亦齧予身，疮痏者数日。彼亦从不另觅他雌，朝夕相守，俨然伉俪。此系不传之秘，姥勿闻之于人，益增予愧也。’媪遂笑而去。翌日，妇竟他徙，不知所往。”邵言已毕，予笑曰：“此殆君之生花舌耳，世岂真有是事哉？”邵亦相视而笑。既而思之，冥报或者不爽，良有此奇缘。因以为异，而笔之于书。

    Explanation

    78

    As soon as I entered the room, the dog began to fiercely bite, and no one could stop it; the family's wealth thus remained intact. I was not insane, but in my confusion, I also knew that I had relations with the dog, and I felt deeply ashamed. After recovering, I thought that my relatives would shun me because of this, and how could anyone else be willing to marry me? Thus, I decided to follow the dog. I cleaned its filth, provided it with good food, and treated it like a husband. It has been five years now. I gave birth to three litters, but I did not dare to raise the puppies. I will endure in silence for the rest of my life, what more can be said? The woman finished speaking, and tears streamed down her face. The old woman, who was good at joking, asked, "Is the dog's happiness also like a human's?" The woman remained silent for a long time and then broke into tears and said, "Now that I've met this foolish old woman, I can't hide it anymore. I don't know what human happiness is, but I have been sleeping with the dog. At first, while I was sick, I was in a daze and unaware. After recovering, I was even more humiliated. Over time, we became accustomed to each other, and I began to find joy in it, which I believe is no different from what a strong man would experience. Thus, I fell in love and could not let go, not knowing anything else. But the dog is extremely jealous. If I see a young child, it will also bite me, leaving scars for several days. It never seeks another female, staying with me day and night, like a married couple. This is a secret not to be passed on. Do not tell anyone, it will only increase my shame." The old woman then laughed and left. The next day, the woman moved away to another place, and no one knew where she went." After I finished telling the story, I laughed and said, "This is likely the result of your eloquent tongue. Could there really be such a thing in the world?" We both laughed at each other. Later, I thought about it, perhaps divine retribution is not mistaken, and there are indeed such strange coincidences. I wrote it down as a strange tale.

    79

    外史氏曰：槃瓠，犬也，以功竟得佳配，载之传记，虽诞而有可征。今此犬又以夙冤而获美妇，似属不经。但妇能降心事之，不以犬待犬，直以夫待犬，嫁犬从犬，妇盖有味乎其言也。呜呼，世之有以犬待夫者亦多矣，不诚犬妇之不若哉？

    Explanation

    79

    The chronicler says: "Panhu was a dog, who, through merit, eventually obtained a good match, and this is recorded in historical accounts, though it may seem mythical, there is some basis for it. Now this dog, again, due to an ancient grudge, gained a beautiful wife, which seems unbelievable. But the woman was willing to lower her heart and serve him, not treating him as just a dog, but as a husband, following the proverb 'to follow one's husband even if he is a dog.' The woman must have found meaning in her words. Alas, there are many in the world who treat their husbands like dogs, are they not worse than the dog's wife?"

    80

    ○　田凤翘
    　　韩城卢孝廉，某年下第，将归秦省。从一仆，跨二健骡，行于燕南道上。夕阳在山，犹未得所栖止。心正茫然，忽闻犬吠声，知去人家伊迩，遂疾行。然细聆之，声在树间，不由孔道，乃迂路而趋之。未及里许，日渐昏黄。比至，则屋宇不繁，草庐低矮，惟一家面水而居。谛视之，则槐荫盈门，柳丝匝地，墙头杏子累累垂熟，令人起乡关之想。客未款户，金铃复吠于门中。有老翁，年约六十许，蹒跚而出，询客何为。语以故，再三始闻，笑曰：“女孟尝固不拒客者，但虑湫隘不足以容车从，可若何？”孝廉又言之，乃曰：“俟白主人。”入闺良久，方出肃客，则已月印前溪矣。孝廉弃乘，从之入门。东向一矮屋，中甚修洁。翁延孝廉入室，仆骑均止于院中。翁谢曰：“暮夜仓卒，蓬门市远，不及备斗酒为客洗尘，幸勿怪。”言已自去。

    Explanation

    80

    ○　田凤翘
    A scholar from Hancheng, who had failed the imperial examinations in a certain year, was on his way back to the province of Qin. Accompanied by a servant and riding two strong mules, he was traveling along the southern road of the Yan region. As the sun set behind the mountains, he had not yet found a place to rest for the night. His heart was filled with uncertainty when suddenly he heard the sound of a dog barking. Realizing that a house was nearby, he quickened his pace. However, upon careful listening, he noticed the barking was coming from the trees, not from the main path, so he took a detour to investigate. Before he could walk a mile, the day was already fading into dusk. When he arrived, he saw a modest house, a low thatched cottage, the only one facing the water. Upon closer inspection, he saw that the courtyard was shaded by locust trees, the willow branches covered the ground, and ripe apricots hung from the walls, evoking a sense of nostalgia for his hometown. Before he could knock on the door, the sound of a bell attached to a dog’s collar was heard inside. An old man, about sixty years old, slowly emerged, asking the scholar what he was doing there. After explaining his situation, the old man, upon hearing it several times, smiled and said, “The lady of Mengchang would never refuse a guest, but I am concerned that the small space might not accommodate your carriage and attendants. What should we do?” The scholar explained again, and the old man said, “I will inform the master.” After a long while, the old man reappeared and invited the scholar inside, while the servant and the mules remained in the courtyard. The old man apologized, saying, “It is late in the evening, and our humble door is far from the market; we were unable to prepare even a few cups of wine to welcome you. Please do not take it amiss.” Having said this, he left.

    81

    孝廉视仆秣驹，徘徊月下。见其居偪仄，主人卧室似与客寝毗联，只隔一层篱落，而人声笑语，入耳逼清。孝廉立未久，闻细声言曰：“田家小妮子今夕不来，殊慢客。”其音似一少妇。语未竟，闻小女子声笑曰，“子非我，安知我不来？”妇亦笑曰：“正说曹操，曹操便到也。”小女子又曰：“远迢迢的陈家姨，未审来否？”妇曰：“渠亦好事者，将无来？第予家适有嘉宾，欲邀预席，但恐汝曹羞缩，皆逃去。”女子哂曰：“汝自不羞，欲捉官路作人情。我辈大家风范，岂村庄小儿所能推揣者？”妇乃大笑曰：“婢子面皮故铁包者哉？我真未及料也。”语次，忽风声隐隐，似又有老少闺人杂癴而至。凡数辈，各叙寒喧，声亦渐远，不复闻。有顷，老翁出速客曰：“家主母不揣寒陋，窃思一晤高贤，请即行。”孝廉聆其言语，颇歆动，又值客况无聊，遂与偕入。

    Explanation

    81

    The Scholar looked at the servant tending to the young horse, wandering under the moon. He saw that the house was cramped, with the master's bedroom seemingly adjacent to the guest's room, separated by only a single fence. The sounds of voices and laughter were clear and crisp to his ears. Not long after standing there, he heard a soft voice say, "The little girl from the countryside isn't coming tonight, she's really being rude to the guests." The voice sounded like that of a young woman. Before the sentence was finished, he heard a young girl's voice laugh and say, "If you weren't me, how would you know I wouldn't come?" The woman also laughed and said, "Just as we were talking about Cao Cao, here he comes." The young girl then said, "What about Aunt Chen from so far away? I wonder if she will come?" The woman replied, "She's also someone who likes to be involved, perhaps she will come. But my family just has a distinguished guest, and we want to invite her to join us, but I'm afraid you all will feel ashamed and run away." The girl sneered and said, "You're not ashamed yourself, trying to play the role of a gracious host. Our noble demeanor, how could it be guessed by someone from the village?" The woman then laughed heartily and said, "Your face is as hard as iron, I truly didn't expect this." As they continued speaking, suddenly there was a faint sound of wind, as if more women, both old and young, were arriving. Several people came, each exchanging greetings, and the sounds gradually faded away, becoming inaudible. After a while, an old man came out to invite the guests, saying, "Our hostess, despite her humble background, secretly wishes to meet a distinguished scholar. Please come in." The Scholar listened to their words, feeling quite moved, and since he was feeling bored as a guest, he decided to go in with them.

    82

    院宇不甚宽广，而花香浓郁，树影阴森，银蟾之下，布置举一一可见。左侧三楹，华美不类民家，疑即主人所寝者。右侧一草亭，颇轩敞，中设三席而虚其一，妇人四五辈，语笑甚欢。闻孝廉至，皆出迎，一衣缟素者，貌甚清丽，敛袂改容曰：“妾以先夫见背，僻陋村居。今幸君子惠临，顿光蓬荜。适田妹设有薄酌，藉花献佛，万勿疑讶。”孝廉知为主人，乃答揖曰：“不才羞等刘癵，穷如苏季，抱惭点额，狼狈西归。猥以日暮途长，惧逢虎狼暴客，轻造潭府。已荷优容，更与华筵，益惊宠召。”语已，众客亦相见毕，逊之入亭，延之首席。孝廉辞谢，而后就坐。筵前无烛，映月窥视，左席一媪二妇，年近五旬，状貌魁梧，衣杂彩之服，五色翩跹，众呼之以姨。妇容俱风格，衣亦素色，齿与主人相埒。右席则缟衣而外，只一红裳少女，美如图画。坐间恒流盼相属，意似有所欲言。

    Explanation

    82

    The courtyard was not very spacious, yet the fragrance of flowers was rich and strong, and the shadows of trees were dark and gloomy. Under the moonlight, everything was arranged and visible. On the left side were three rooms, their elegance unlike that of a common household, suspected to be the master's sleeping quarters. On the right side was a grass pavilion, quite spacious and open, with three seats set up, one left empty. A group of four or five women were talking and laughing very happily. Upon hearing that the scholar-official had arrived, they all came out to greet. One woman dressed in white, with a very beautiful appearance, gathered her sleeves and changed her expression, saying, "I am a widow, having lost my husband, and live in a humble village residence. Now I am fortunate to have a gentleman visit, and your presence has greatly honored my humble abode. My sister-in-law has just prepared a simple feast, offering flowers to the Buddha. Please do not be surprised." The scholar-official, recognizing her as the hostess, replied with a bow, "I feel ashamed to compare myself to lesser figures like Liu Bian, and as poor as Su Qin. I return in a disheveled state, feeling embarrassed and bowing my head. Due to the late hour and the long journey, I feared to encounter fierce bandits, so I lightly entered your humble home. Already having been treated with leniency, and being treated to a splendid feast, I am even more surprised by this honor." After his words, the other guests also finished their greetings, inviting him into the pavilion and seating him at the head of the table. The scholar-official declined the offer and then sat down. There were no candles at the feast, and they viewed under the moonlight. On the left side was one elderly woman and two women, nearly fifty years old, with a robust and imposing appearance, wearing colorful clothing, dancing in colorful attire. They were called 'aunt' by the others. The women all had a refined appearance, wearing plain-colored clothing, their age similar to the host's. On the right side, besides the woman in white, there was only one young girl in red, as beautiful as a painting. She constantly looked around, her eyes meeting others, as if she had something she wanted to say.

    83

    孝廉处众美之中，深自敛抑，不敢少纵。酒甫行，即觉微酣，不胜惊讶。细咀之，其酿味浓而色淡，醇酽异常。遂不多饮，略餔蔬果，以见主人之意而已。酒方再巡，媪谓众曰：“鲸吞牛饮，虽八斗亦奚以为？请效桃李园故事，各吟短篇以充觞政，客以为何如？”孝廉唯唯。因请媪首唱，媪亦倨慢不辞，口占一绝曰：“曾兆霸图侔癶凤，更符圣道笑冥鸿。红颜老去风流在，每向南阳化赤虹。”吟讫，众妇鼓掌曰：“兴殊不浅，但不觉遽露本色耳。”次及孝廉，辞让至再，乃吟曰：“一园红杏原无我，满眼夭桃信是谁。犹作广寒花下客，不须胪唱且舒眉。”众聆之，谢曰：“妾辈远逊嫦娥，何克当此？”再次及三妇，皆推不能，愿以巨觥受罚。惟红裳女子低吟曰：“长夜无灯鳞自照，断魂谁伴月为俦。凄凄一树白杨下，埋尽金闺万斛愁。”孝廉见其诗有鬼气，咄咄逼人，不禁变色而起。

    Explanation

    83

    In the midst of many beauties, the孝廉 remained deeply self-restrained, not daring to show off a little. As soon as the wine was poured, he immediately felt slightly tipsy and could not help but be surprised. Tasting it carefully, he found that the wine had a strong flavor but a light color, and was exceptionally rich and strong. So he did not drink much, ate a little of vegetables and fruits, just to show respect to the host. When the wine was served again, the old woman said to the crowd: "To drink like a whale swallows or a cow drinks, even if it is eight dou, what use is it? Please follow the example of the Peach and Plum Garden story, each recite a short poem to entertain the guests. What do the guests think about this?" The孝廉 nodded in agreement. So he asked the old woman to start, and she also acted arrogantly but did not refuse, reciting a quatrain spontaneously: "Once predicted a great plan, matched the phoenix, further aligned with the sage's path, laugh at the wild geese. The beauty of youth fades, the charm remains, often transforms into a red rainbow in Nanyang." After reciting, the crowd clapped and said: "The interest is not shallow, but it seems to reveal the true nature too quickly." Next, it was the孝廉's turn, who refused twice before reciting: "In a garden of red apricots, there was no me, in a sea of blooming peach blossoms, who am I? Still a guest under the moon in the distant palace, no need to be listed and praised, just relax and smile." The crowd listened and said in thanks: "We are far inferior to Chang'e, how can we match this?" Then it was the turn of the three women, who all refused, willing to be punished with a large cup.
