Only the woman in red softly recited: "In the long night without lamps, the fish shine on their own, who will accompany the soul as a companion to the moon? Under a lonely poplar tree, burying countless sorrows of the noble women." The孝廉 saw that the poem had a ghostly aura, intimidating, and could not help but change color and stand up.

    84

    众俱恚曰：“婢子何败人清兴？”遂皆不欢而散。孝廉出就外舍，心悸不宁。欲行则暮夜苍茫，莫知所往，欲止则踪迹诡异，深以为虞，乃和衣假寐。方转侧间，倏闻窗外弹指作声。起视之，癷见红裳女子，仓皇闪入，谓之曰：“非妾拙作，则君危矣。此地较虎狼尤恶，胡为栖栖于是！”孝廉愕然，惊询之。女亟曳其袂曰：“行矣，犹问耶？”孝廉欲顾仆马，女曰：“身存而此可徐图。”径携之只身而出。东窜里许，乃复西行，孝廉惶怖汗浃，罔知所措。至一大树下，女曰：“此即妾家，可少息，妖来自有以御之。”孝廉坌息，叩其颠末。女曰：“妾名凤翘，田姓女也。陈姊居于岐州，实一雌雉之怪。彼三人者，皆千年之蝟，专伏地底，啖人脑髓。左近之丘墓，无不罹其荼毒。

    Explanation

    84

    All were angry and said, "How you ruin people's pleasant mood!" So they all dispersed unhappily. The scholar-official went out to stay in an outer room, his heart palpitations and unease. If he wanted to go, it was dark and desolate at night, and no one knew where to go; if he wanted to stop, the strange and suspicious behavior made him deeply worried. He fell into a light sleep in his clothes. Just as he turned over, he suddenly heard a snapping sound from outside the window. He got up and looked, and saw a red-clad woman hurriedly dart in, saying to him, "If it weren't for my poor work, you would be in danger. This place is even worse than tigers and wolves, why are you wandering aimlessly here!" The scholar-official was startled and asked her in surprise. The woman quickly pulled his sleeve and said, "Let's go, still asking?" The scholar-official wanted to look after his servants and horses, but the woman said, "As long as you are safe, we can plan this later." She took him out alone. They ran eastward for a few li, then walked westward. The scholar-official was terrified and drenched in sweat, completely at a loss. They reached under a large tree, and the woman said, "This is my home, you can rest a bit. There are ways to deal with the demon." The scholar-official took a deep breath and inquired about the details. The woman said, "My name is Feng Qiao, a girl of the Tian family. Tong's sister lives in Qizhou, she is actually a female pheasant demon. Those three, all thousand-year-old hedgehogs, specialize in living underground, feeding on human brains. The tombstones nearby have all suffered their cruelty.

    85

    妾生时虔诵金刚经，殁后以之为殉，妖不敢近吾垄，因结为姊妹行，晨夕同游，实欲盗吾宝也。昨夕妾家以酒馔饷妾，渠等知之，以法摄致，强妾为东道主。不图君乃与席，妾不忍以口腹之故，使人肝脑涂地，以饱无厌之馋，曾数数目君，君竟漠然。幸得赋诗见志，耸动君听。不然，此时已莫能生矣。”孝廉闻女言，益惊。方将研诘，瞥见火光数团，越阡度陌，疾如飞隼，将至树下。女出袖中一卷，曼声娇诵，其光即摇摇不前，如有所忌惮。然相持至鸡鸣，始各散去。孝廉窜伏蓬颗，屏息不敢出声，汗蒸蒸衣襦尽湿。比及天曙，女贺曰：“君生矣。请俟日出，重诣故处，以验所言之信否。妾阴质不能昼见，今宵旅邸，梦中当来与君细叙，将有要事相商。”言讫不见。孝廉视之，蔓草寒烟，新坟三尺，犹有纸钱，以片石镇其上，因揖而谢之。仍循故道至客夕居停，则丛冢如布棋，绝无庐舍，行装辎重散委于榛莽间。亟寻其仆，则已溘然长逝。

    Explanation

    85

    I was born reciting the Diamond Sutra with sincerity, and after my death, I sacrificed myself for it. Evil spirits dared not approach my grave, so they formed a sisterhood with me, traveling together day and night, truly desiring to steal my treasure. Last night, my family offered me wine and food, and they knew about it. Using magic, they summoned me to be the host. I did not expect you to join the feast. I could not bear to cause someone to spill their blood and brains for the sake of food, to satisfy your insatiable greed. I had repeatedly looked at you, but you were indifferent. Fortunately, I managed to express my intentions through poetry, arousing your attention. Otherwise, I would no longer be able to live at this point." The scholar, hearing the woman's words, became even more surprised. Just as he was about to investigate further, he caught a glimpse of several clusters of firelight crossing fields and paths, swift as falcons, approaching the tree. The woman took out a scroll from her sleeve and recited it softly and sweetly. The light hesitated and did not move forward, as if it were afraid of something. However, they held each other until the rooster crowed, and then they finally dispersed. The scholar hid among the grass, holding his breath and daring not to make a sound, sweating profusely, his clothes and undershirt all wet. By the time dawn broke, the woman congratulated him, saying, "You are alive. Please wait until the sun rises, and we will return to the old place to verify the truth of what I said. I cannot be seen during the day because I am a female spirit. Tonight at the inn, I will come to you in a dream to discuss matters in detail, as there are important things to talk about." After speaking, she disappeared. The scholar looked around and saw overgrown grass and cold mist, a new grave three feet high, still with paper money, and a stone slab on top. He bowed and thanked her. Still following the old path, he returned to the place where the guest stayed, but there were clusters of graves scattered like chess pieces, absolutely no houses or dwellings, and his traveling gear and supplies were scattered among the bushes. He urgently searched for his servant, but he had already passed away suddenly.

    86

    囟门有小穴，其中空空然，想为群妖吸去矣。愈大骇，物色得其骡，犹幸无恙，乘之以行，旁午始抵城市。即以告人，莫不惊异，遂止孝廉于传舍，而白之官。至夜，孝廉梦女来，面酬其德，并询仆死之由。女曰：“是妖虎踞泉壤，非此莫得延年。然遇生人而餍之，恒胜于死者什倍。以君颇有福泽，无敢骤近，故假酒色以乱君。君倘酣卧，渠乃可逞。君幸从妾遁，而仆犹在梦中，其罹于毒手，又何疑哉？”孝廉又咨以驱除之方，女曰：“渠寿既长，兼具灵异，往来数百里，鬼神亦莫可如何，况人乎？”因腼然曰：“妾已与妖为仇，不复可以居此。知君失偶，愿承琴瑟之乏，从君入秦。留经作镇，亦可永获残骸，不识肯俯从否？”孝廉虽艳其姿，而惧为阴类，乃答曰：“再生之恩，何所不可？但卿生之而复死之，予心虽无憾，不亦重累卿德？是以不敢。”女沉思良久，叹曰：“言实近道，妾不敢强。

    Explanation

    86

    The fontanelle on the scholar-official's head had a small opening, and inside it felt empty, as if it had been sucked away by a group of evil spirits. The scholar-official was extremely frightened and searched for his mule, which he found still unharmed. He mounted the mule and continued his journey, finally reaching the city at midday. He immediately informed others, and all were astonished. They stopped the scholar-official at an inn and reported the incident to the authorities. That night, the scholar-official dreamed of a woman coming to him. She expressed her gratitude face-to-face and asked about the cause of the servant's death. The woman said, "This malevolent tiger spirit resides in the underworld. Without this intervention, you would not have been able to extend your life. However, it prefers to consume living beings, being ten times more satisfied by them. Since you have a lot of good fortune, it dares not approach suddenly, so it used alcohol and beauty to confuse you. If you had fallen into a deep sleep, it would have acted freely. Fortunately, you fled with me, and the servant was still in a dream, likely killed by the spirit's hand." The scholar-official then asked about methods of exorcism. The woman replied, "The spirit has a long lifespan and possesses supernatural abilities, traveling hundreds of miles. Even gods and spirits cannot do anything about it, let alone humans." She then shyly said, "I have already become an enemy of the spirit and cannot stay in this place anymore. Knowing you have lost your wife, I wish to take up the role of a wife and fulfill marital duties, following you to the Qin region. I can also remain as a guardian and protect your remains permanently. Would you agree to this?" Although the scholar-official admired her beauty, he feared she might be of the spirit world and hesitantly replied, "Your kindness in saving my life is beyond measure. But if you were to save me and then kill me, I would have no regrets, but it would be a heavy burden on your virtue. That is why I dare not." The woman thought deeply for a long time and sighed, "Your words are indeed close to the truth. I dare not force you."

    87

    ”又曰：“明旦诣官，恐有辩难，第亟呼妾名，当有裨益。”言已，孝廉顿寤。及见邑宰，果以杀仆疑之。孝廉愤激，因呼凤翘不置。宰骇然，亟退厅，引孝廉与语曰：“此吾女也，殁已两月，君何以悉其乳名？”孝廉遂述其异，并及其女之衣妆，无不吻合。宰惊喜曰：“亡女好诵金刚经，存日尝怪之，不意竟得其力。微先生言，吾不知也。”盖宰本闽人，因道远尸榇难携，又不忍失之，故即葬于任所，亦视宦为家之积习也。于是不疑仆死之枉，仅以暴卒详于上官，狱遂解。孝廉起女柩，浮厝佛寺，免令死者悬悬，宰亦从之。孝廉遂辞谢，旋里至家，其母适妊娠将产。一夕，又梦女来，谓孝廉曰：“与君终属有缘，不倡随而埙篪矣。”孝廉醒，闻母已诞生一女，知为凤翘转世。乃禀于父母，仍以字之。及长，能友其兄，不啻悌弟。孝廉年五十，犹困于公车，家又中落，其妹嫁一巨家，辄不时资助焉。

    Explanation

    87

    "Furthermore, it was said, 'Tomorrow morning, when you go to the official, there may be difficulties or debates. Just quickly call out my name; it will be beneficial.' After saying this, the scholar suddenly realized. When he met the local magistrate, the magistrate indeed suspected him of killing the servant. The scholar became angry and called out the name of Feng Qiao repeatedly. The magistrate was startled and quickly retreated to his office, leading the scholar inside and saying, 'This is my daughter, who has been dead for two months. How could you know her childhood name?' The scholar then recounted the strange events, including the appearance of his daughter's clothes and makeup, which matched perfectly. The magistrate was surprised and said, 'My deceased daughter loved to recite the Diamond Sutra. I often found it strange, but I never expected she would gain such power. Without your words, I would not have known.' The magistrate was originally from Fujian and, because the journey was far and carrying the coffin was difficult, he did not want to lose it, so he buried her in his place of service, a common practice for officials who viewed their posts as their homes. Thus, he no longer suspected the servant's death was unjust and only reported it as a sudden death to his superiors, and the case was resolved. The scholar moved his daughter's coffin and temporarily buried it in a temple, to avoid leaving the dead in limbo, and the magistrate agreed. The scholar then bid farewell and returned to his hometown. His mother happened to be pregnant and was about to give birth. One night, he dreamed that the woman came to him and said, 'We are destined to be connected. We will not lead and follow each other but will be like the xun and chi, harmonious companions.' The scholar woke up and found out that his mother had given birth to a daughter, whom he knew to be the reincarnation of Feng Qiao. He informed his parents and named her accordingly. As she grew up, she became a good sister to her brother, almost like a younger brother. By the time the scholar was fifty, he was still struggling in the imperial examinations, and his family had fallen into poverty. His sister married into a wealthy family and frequently provided assistance."

    88

    外史氏曰：世俗趋利，恒以蝟为财星，而不知其为祸最烈。即以此段观之，村居洒落，言雅色殊，令人一往而深，实已厉齿相向。脱无雌田横之义，几为女孟尝所啖，不亦殆哉？虽然，世之牟利者且不虑生焚其身，又何恤死盬其脑耶？

    ○　刘天锡

    Explanation

    88

    The Commentator said: The world is always driven by profit, often regarding the porcupine as a symbol of wealth, yet unaware that it brings the greatest calamity. As seen in this passage, the village is charming and elegant, with refined speech and appearance, which can captivate one deeply, but in reality, it is baring its teeth. Without the righteousness of a woman like Tian Heng, one could almost be devoured by a woman like Mengchang, and is this not perilous? Nevertheless, those who seek profit in the world do not even consider the risk of burning themselves alive; how much less would they worry about suffering a terrible fate?

    89

    刘嘏，字天锡，嘉善人。年仅弱冠，名噪一时，岁试辄前列，同袍者咸推重焉。崇德李氏，巨富家也，慕其文誉，以重币聘至，为子弟指南。天锡齿虽卑，而循循善诱，师道克敦，主人亦心悦诚服。嘉平将解馆归省，李氏又坚约以来岁。时天锡以家贫未偶，有母尸饔，心窃以为忧。计将归里毕姻，俟井臼有人，然后出。遂以他故力辞，而主人早窥其隐，乃毅然曰：“先生其有内顾之忧乎？然文人花烛洞房，必俟金榜，方为快事。青灯固未可厌也。倘虑太夫人在堂，无人陪侍，寒家颇多粗婢，即请遴选一二人，前往服役，予亦无所吝。”而天锡素怀壮志，诚如主人言，今因母老而计及此，初非本心。迨闻主人语，喜出望外，而中有未安，故嗫嚅不即首肯。主人因笑曰：“先生岂虑予索值哉？俟贵后相偿未晚也。”遽出侍婢数十人，俾天锡自择。天锡熟视良久，皆妖艳异常，不敢祗受。主人促之再四，乃指一玄色衣绛裙者，顾谓主人曰：“此子愿以见惠。

    Explanation

    89

    Liu Gu, courtesy name Tianxi, was from Jia Shan. At the age of twenty, he became famous for a time, consistently ranking at the top in annual examinations, and was highly respected by his peers. The wealthy family of Chongde admired his literary reputation and hired him with generous payment to serve as a guide for their young. Although Tianxi was young in age, he patiently and skillfully guided, establishing the role of a teacher well, and the master of the house was heartily convinced and convinced. Near the end of the year, he was about to leave the school to visit home, and the family firmly invited him to return the following year. At that time, Tianxi was poor and unmarried, with a mother who cooked for him, and he secretly worried about this. He planned to return to his hometown to marry, wait until someone could take care of his mother, and then leave. He thus strongly refused due to other reasons, but the master of the house had already seen his concerns and said resolutely, "Mr. Tianxi, do you have concerns about family matters? However, for a scholar, the joy of a wedding and entering the bridal chamber must wait until he passes the imperial examinations. The green lamp is not something to be despised. If you are concerned that your mother-in-law is still at home and no one is to attend to her, my humble household has many simple maids. Please select one or two to go and serve, and I will not be stingy." Tianxi had always had great aspirations, and indeed, as the master of the house said, he now considered his mother's age and this was not his original intention. Upon hearing the master of the house's words, he was delighted beyond expectation, but there was something that made him uneasy, so he hesitated and did not immediately agree. The master of the house then laughed and said, "Mr. Tianxi, do you worry that I will ask for payment? It's not too late to pay after you become successful." He quickly brought out dozens of maids and let Tianxi choose for himself. Tianxi looked at them for a long time, all of them exceptionally beautiful, and dared not accept them. The master of the house urged him again and again, and finally, he pointed to a maid wearing a dark blue robe and a red skirt and said to the master of the house, "This one is willing to be given."

    90

    ”主人哂曰：“色不甚佳，先生何取于此？当副以丽者。”即指一藕丝裳绿衣者，并以相赠。亟起入内，自检券契，尽以畀之，谓天锡曰：“吾不使先生有后忧也。”遂命二婢叩拜天锡，使以主礼事之。天锡少年持重，见二婢均有殊色，益以名教自闲，不敢少纵。辞主人启行，即另觅一舟，以载二美，言笑亦来尝轻通。

    Explanation

    90

    The host smiled and said, "The appearance is not very good, why does the gentleman value this? I should accompany it with a more beautiful one." He then pointed to a woman wearing a silk dress and a green robe, and presented them together. Quickly getting up and going inside, he checked the contracts and agreements, giving them all to Tianxi. He said, "I do not want the gentleman to have any worries in the future." Then he ordered the two maids to bow to Tianxi, to treat him with the respect of a host. Tianxi, being young but serious, saw that both maids had extraordinary beauty, and even more so, he restrained himself with the principles of propriety, not daring to let his guard down. Bidding farewell to the host and setting out, he then found another boat, to carry the two beauties. Their conversation and laughter did not reveal any light-heartedness.

    91

    归见其母，母故世家女，训子素有义方，一旦见二尤物，即诃之曰：“老妇精力尚健，提汲可以亲操，安用此纤弱者为？且汝学业无所就，便欲效寇莱公，以蒨桃自损其名耶？”天锡伏地请罪，因白主人诚意，弗克辞。母终不喜，另居二婢于室。元夕甫过，即遣天锡赴塾，不少稽，且私谓曰：“见可欲而心不乱者，惟圣人能之，吾子恐不逮此也。”二婢一名湘瑟，即衣藕丝裳者；一名琴心，则玄色衣者；俱善音律，为主人所钟爱。湘瑟尤聪慧可人意，而主人器重天锡，知非凡品，故以之持赠。天锡母茹苦自甘，饔飧皆身为之，不轻役二婢。知其素解之无，遂躬亲教读，授以内则及女四书，课若严师，咕哔终日，二婢亦甚乐之。

    Explanation

    91

    Upon returning home, Tianxi saw his mother. She was a woman of a distinguished family, who had always trained him with righteous methods. When she suddenly saw the two beautiful maids, she scolded him, saying, "I am still strong and healthy, able to fetch water myself. Why do you need such delicate and weak ones? Moreover, you have not yet achieved anything in your studies. Do you intend to follow Kou Lei Gong's example and damage your reputation with a woman like Qian Tao?" Tianxi fell to the ground and begged for forgiveness, explaining that the master had been sincere in his request and that he could not refuse. Despite this, his mother remained displeased and had the two maids separately lodged in another room. Just after the Lantern Festival, she sent Tianxi back to school, without delay, and privately told him, "One who sees something desirable and does not become disordered in heart is only a sage. I fear you cannot achieve this." The two maids were named Xiangse and Qin Xin. Xiangse wore a dress of lotus silk, while Qin Xin wore a black robe. Both were skilled in music and were favored by the master. Xiangse was particularly intelligent and pleasing to the eye, and the master valued Tianxi greatly, knowing him to be an extraordinary person, so he gave her as a gift. Tianxi's mother endured hardship willingly, preparing meals herself, and did not lightly burden the two maids. Knowing that they were not well-educated, she personally taught them the Neizheng and the Four Books for Women, instructing them as if they were strict teachers. They chattered all day, and the two maids also found it very enjoyable.

    92

    一夕，母忽谓之曰：“汝曹亦因贫致此，岂甘为人下者。既承贤主人惠归予家，必不忍令汝居妾媵之列。俟少娴妇道，遣嫁良人，庶毕汝终身之事。”琴心闻母言，默然不语；独湘瑟颜色惨改，若不胜忧者。及归室，私语琴心曰：“我辈奉主命遣事郎君，意固有在。妹观郎君，器宇纯粹而高明，当非久困寒毡者，因自谓此身可托矣。适闻太夫人命，意似不能相容，命之不辰，又将安往？倘舍玉堂金马，而事瓦犬土鸡，姊能之，妹实不能也。

    Explanation

    92

    One night, the mother suddenly told them, "You both have come to this situation due to poverty. How could you willingly be beneath others? Since we have been kindly taken into our master's family, I will not let you become concubines. Wait until you have learned the proper conduct of a wife, and I will arrange a good marriage for you, so that you may fulfill your life's duties." Qinxin heard her mother's words and remained silent; only Xiangse's face turned pale with worry, as if she could not bear the distress. When they returned to their room, Xiangse whispered to Qinxin, "We followed the master's command and served the young man, with a purpose in mind. Sister, I observed the young man, and he has a noble and bright demeanor, not one who has long been trapped in poverty. Therefore, I believed I could trust him. But now that I have heard the decision of the esteemed lady, it seems she cannot accept me. With such bad luck, where else can I go? If I give up a prestigious and luxurious life to engage in lowly and menial tasks, I can do it, but I truly cannot."

    93

    ”琴心亦潸然泣下曰：“然。”遂相对癹悒，竟夕僝僽。无何，二婢皆病矣。刘母罔知其由，亟为延医诊视，云系忧郁，内伤其情。服药如水沃石，未三月，琴心小愈，而湘瑟遂卒。临逝之日，启刘母曰：“湘瑟以卑贱之躯，得侍太夫人左右，饮食教诲，恩戴高天。今不幸而长辞，亦其命也。虽然，太夫人则未谅湘瑟之心，是以不克遂湘瑟之愿。湘瑟殁后，若能葬于刘氏茔侧，使得如骥尾之蝇，则素心遂矣。”又顾琴心曰：“姊自趋趁，不可同效无益之死，有负主人严命也。”言已而瞑。

    Explanation

    93

    Qin Xin also wept tears and said, "Indeed." Thus they sat opposite each other in sorrow, enduring all night. Soon after, both maids fell ill. Liu's mother did not know the cause and hastily called a doctor to examine them. The doctor said it was due to depression and internal emotional injury. The medicine was as ineffective as water poured on stone; within three months, Qin Xin recovered slightly, but Xiang Se died. On the day of her death, Xiang Se opened her mouth to Liu's mother and said: "Xiang Se, with her humble body, was able to serve the respected elder lady, receiving her care and teachings, and feeling her grace as high as the heavens. Now, unfortunately, she has to part forever, it is also her fate. However, the respected elder lady does not understand Xiang Se's heart, so Xiang Se's wish cannot be fulfilled. After Xiang Se's death, if she could be buried beside the Liu family's tomb, so that she might follow like a fly on the tail of a horse, then her pure heart would be at peace." She then looked at Qin Xin and said: "Sister, you should go your own way; you cannot die in vain like me, which would be a betrayal of the master's strict command." She spoke these words and then closed her eyes.

    94

    琴心恸绝复苏，刘母亦甚哀之，为市榇以殓。如其遗嘱，葬以坟园隙地。事毕，将浼人驰告于子，而虑分其文心，遂不果。天锡居主人家，馆谷较前倍丰，课业亦较前倍进。宾无权舆之叹，主无倦教之忧，相视莫逆，俨若一家。时值新秋，淫霖数日。一夕，生徒散后，月色微明，天锡倚栊凝眺，偶吟子美对月一联曰：“香雾云鬟湿，清辉玉臂寒。”因笑曰：“此老兴殊不浅。”语未毕，身后有人低谓曰：“郎君亦怜此苦况耶？”天锡愕然，回身瞻视，则湘瑟蹙黛立于灯前。

    Explanation

    94

    After Qin Xintong's revival from his deep sorrow, Liu's mother was also very saddened by his plight. She arranged for a coffin to be purchased for his burial, following his last wishes, and he was laid to rest in an unused plot in the cemetery. After the burial, there was a plan to send someone to inform his son, but they were concerned about dividing his literary spirit, and thus it did not happen. Tian Xi then lived in the host's home, where the provisions were much more abundant than before, and his studies also progressed much more than before. The guest had no complaints about the arrangement, and the host had no worry about tiring of teaching. They looked at each other with mutual understanding, as if they were one family. It was the beginning of autumn, and it rained continuously for several days. One evening, after the students had left, the moonlight was faintly bright. Tian Xi leaned against the window frame and gazed out, and he happened to recite a couplet by Du Fu about the moon: "The fragrant mist moistens the cloud-like hair, the bright moonlight chills the jade-like arm." He then smiled and said, "This old man's sentiment is quite profound." Before he finished speaking, someone behind him whispered, "Do you also pity this miserable situation, young master?" Tian Xi was startled and turned around to look, and there stood Xiang Se, with her brows furrowed, in front of the lamp.

    95

    天锡大惊曰：“汝何以至此？岂怀旧主人之恩，逋逃以归耶？”湘瑟直前裣発，愁态可掬，逡巡而对曰：“非敢如是也。”因述己与琴心致疾之由，并已身死之故。且言今在冥途，终以未得事君为恨。感蒙地下主者，怜婢子苦衷，俾得自由，乃随风至此，以了生前未遂之愿。盲已涕泗，拜伏于地。天锡益惊，且悲其死，命之起。温颜以却之曰：“闻若之言，情实可悯，致汝殀谢，实予之辜。但予有慈命在身，且承先人后，不敢以钟情致于不孝。幸为予谅，往生他方，予归当临穴吊汝。

    Explanation

    95

    Tian Xi was greatly startled and said, "How did you come to be here? Could it be that you cherished the kindness of your former master and fled back to him?" Xiang Se stepped forward and wiped away her tears, her sorrowful expression easily visible. After a moment's hesitation, she replied, "I dare not act in such a manner." She then recounted the reasons for her illness and death, as well as the circumstances leading to it. She also mentioned that she was now in the afterlife, regretting that she had not been able to serve her master. She expressed gratitude to the ruler of the underworld for pitying her plight and granting her freedom, allowing her to come here with the wind to fulfill the unfulfilled wishes of her life. Blind tears streamed down her face as she prostrated herself on the ground. Tian Xi was even more startled and grieved at her death, ordering her to rise. With a gentle expression, he dismissed her, saying, "Hearing your words, your situation is truly pitiable, and it is indeed my fault that you died prematurely. However, I have a filial duty to uphold and must inherit the legacy of my ancestors, so I dare not let my affection lead to filial impiety. Please forgive me, and go to be reborn elsewhere. When I return, I will visit your grave to mourn you."

    96

    ”湘瑟闻此，即正色答曰：“郎君何过疑婢子？即婢子在生，犹不敢以贱躯上辱君子，况今已归泉下，实为幽阴之质，而敢别存邪念哉？兹之来，亦期奉侍琴书，少释隐憾，万勿虑及其他，令婢子益增惭赧。”天锡见遣之弗去，又深感其情，遂留之。湘瑟执事惟谨，天锡读则瀹茗剪烛，曼立于旁；吟则磨墨濡毫，敛袂以待；与之语，即肃容以对；不与之语，即含笑无言；自始至终，了无倦色。夜将半，天锡欠伸，湘瑟拂榻布衾，以待天锡卧。

    Explanation

    96

    Upon hearing this, Xiang Se replied with a serious expression: "Why, sir, do you doubt me so much? Even if I were alive, I would not dare to offend a gentleman with my humble body. How much more so now that I have passed away and am a spirit of the underworld? Could I possibly harbor any other improper thoughts? I have come this time to serve by playing the qin and reading books, to alleviate some hidden regrets. Please do not worry about anything else, for it would only make me even more embarrassed." Tian Xi saw that she would not leave, and deeply felt her sincerity, so he kept her. Xiang Se performed her duties with great care. When Tian Xi read, she would boil tea and trim the candle, standing gracefully beside him; when he composed poetry, she would grind ink and dip the brush, folding her sleeves and waiting; when he spoke to her, she would respond with a serious expression; when he did not speak to her, she would smile silently. From beginning to end, she showed no sign of fatigue. As it was nearly midnight and Tian Xi yawned and stretched, Xiang Se smoothed the bed and spread the quilt, preparing for him to rest.

    97

    己亦不眠，为之整几上未掩之编，添炉中将烬之篆。事已，默坐于侧，寂无欬声。天锡甫转侧，即来问讯，兼以纤手扶其衾，爱护若婴儿，似虑夜风侵者。天锡深感之，遣使别榻就寝，答曰：“夜台固无寐者，君姑高枕，勿以婢子为虑。”天锡亦不之强，而竟夜勤恳，可谓忠爱之至矣。侵晨，天锡犹未起，几屏琴剑早无纤尘。及起，又为之整卧具，奉裳衣，视天时之寒暖而进之。未及辟户，肃然告退，曾不知其安往。主家亦有侍者，入见馆中精洁，窃疑先生所为，心衔其德，而未悉其手不烦麾也。

    Explanation

    97

    The subject also did not sleep, tidying up the unbound scrolls on the table and adding to the nearly extinguished incense in the stove. After completing these tasks, they sat silently by the side, with no sound of coughing. As soon as Tianxi turned over, the person came to inquire, using delicate hands to support the quilt, cherishing the subject like an infant, as if fearing the night wind would intrude. Tianxi deeply felt this and sent someone to another bed to rest, but the person replied, "The night hall naturally has no sleepers. You may rest easy, do not worry about the servant." Tianxi did not insist, but the person remained diligent all night long, which can be called the utmost in loyalty and love. At dawn, the table, screen, and weapons were free of the slightest dust. When Tianxi got up, the person presented clothes, providing according to the cold and warmth of the weather. Before opening the door, they departed respectfully, without knowing where they went. The master also had attendants, who entered and saw the cleanliness of the room, secretly suspecting the work of the master, bearing in mind the virtue, but not knowing that their hands did not tire.

    98

    及夕又至，习以为常，天锡不言，人亦莫识其踪迹。暇困诘其里族，对曰：“婢子亦嘉善人，与郎君同乡里。父以赌荡产，售婢子于外邑。蒙主人豢养，今已五易裘葛矣。家本贾姓，初无名者，瑟则主人所字也。”天锡念为桑梓，益加爱重。一日值重阳，主人设宴，天锡沉醉而归。室无他人，惟湘瑟在侧，乃乘兴谓之曰：“知汝善歌，胡不为予一奏？”湘瑟正容对曰：“婢子非故为藏拙，实不敢以声色惑郎君，致背太夫人之慈训耳。不然，已为之多时矣。”天锡遂不复言，即就枕。

    98

    By evening, he returned again, and this became a routine. Tianxi remained silent, and no one could identify his tracks. In his spare time, he asked about her hometown and clan, to which she replied, "I am also a good person from Jia Village, and I am from the same hometown as the young master. My father lost everything through gambling and sold me to another town. I was raised by the master, and now I have changed five times between fur and linen clothes. Originally, I belonged to the Jia family, but I had no name. Sese is the name given by the master." Tianxi, thinking of her as someone from his hometown, became even more beloved and respected. One day, on the Chongyang Festival, the master held a banquet, and Tianxi returned drunk. The room was empty, with only Xiangse by his side. He spoke to her in the mood of the moment, saying, "I know you are good at singing. Why don't you play a tune for me?" Xiangse replied with a serious expression, "I am not deliberately hiding my lack of skill. I truly dare not use music and beauty to tempt the young master, to go against the kind teachings of the young master's mother. Otherwise, I have been doing it for a long time." Tianxi said no more and laid down to sleep.

    99

    翌日，语湘瑟曰：“予昨被酒，见汝几不自持。微若言，能不陷为轻薄子耶？”益优礼之。天锡自得湘瑟，侍从有人，灯底谈心，床头论古，虽未及私，较前颇不岑寂，只恨其不复生。岁杪又解馆归，以学使者将下车，遂辞不再就塾。主人乃大张绮席，饯之于萧斋，召优演剧，欢饮终宵。以是未得与湘瑟言别，亦不复出见，心恒怏怏。及归见母，询知湘瑟果卒，琴心甫扶杖而起。心感二婢，而不敢言于母。乃母因湘瑟之卒，不胜浩叹，遂谓天锡曰：“汝宜奋志，倘得月桂一枝，即以琴心备小星亦无不可。”天锡与琴心闻之，皆窃喜。

    Explanation

    99

    The next day, he spoke to Xiangse, saying, "I was drunk yesterday and almost lost control of myself in your presence. Without your words, I could have fallen into being a frivolous person." He treated her even more respectfully. Tianxi, having gained Xiangse, now had companions. They would talk heart-to-heart under the lamp and discuss ancient matters by the bed. Though they did not engage in private matters, the situation was much less lonely than before. He only regretted that she was no longer alive. At the end of the year, he left the school and returned home, as the examiner was about to arrive, so he refused to return to the school. The host then lavishly prepared a banquet in a quiet study to bid him farewell, inviting performers to put on a play and drinking joyfully all night. As a result, he did not get a chance to say goodbye to Xiangse and did not see her again, always feeling discontented. Upon returning home and seeing his mother, he found out that Xiangse had indeed passed away. Qinxin just got up with the help of a cane. Tianxi felt gratitude towards the two maids but did not dare to tell his mother. His mother, upon hearing of Xiangse's death, could not help but sigh deeply and then said to Tianxi, "You should strive hard. If you achieve success, even if you take Qinxin as a secondary wife, it would not be impossible." Tianxi and Qinxin, upon hearing this, both secretly rejoiced.

    100

    因念湘瑟不置，既以杯酒浇其墓，兼作悼亡诗以哭之曰：“花月两无情，情痴仅见卿。不随流水去，忽傍彩云生。人既留余恨，天应鉴积诚。倘能回玉貌，来伴许飞琼。”自是幽斋独坐，辄望其来，而竟不至。比及秋闱战胜，将谒房考谢公，即崇德令也。夜宿舟中，忽梦湘瑟靓妆来谢曰：“感郎君临吊，铭刻五中。近又折桂天边，益惬所愿。然妾向者欲图其侧而不能，今后将处君正室矣。”言已不见，天锡深以为异。

    Explanation

    100

    Continuing to think of Xiang Se without setting aside the thought, I had already poured a cup of wine over her tomb and composed a poem of mourning to cry out for her, saying: "Flowers and moon are both without feeling, but my foolish passion was only seen by you. Not following the flowing water away, suddenly appearing beside colorful clouds. People have left behind lingering resentment, heaven should witness my accumulated sincerity. If you could return your beautiful appearance, to accompany me as Xu Feiqiong." Since then, sitting alone in the secluded study, always hoping for her to come, but she never came. When I passed the autumn examination, I was about to visit the examiner, Mr. Xie, who is the magistrate of Chongde. Staying in a boat at night, I suddenly dreamed of Xiang Se coming in her beautiful makeup to thank me, saying: "Grateful for your presence at my funeral, deeply engraved in my heart. Recently, picking the osmanthus from the sky, even more satisfying my wishes. But I could not join you earlier, now I will be in your main room." She spoke and then disappeared, and Tian Xi deeply found it strange.

    101

    及入见谢令，适有巨绅某公亦在座，询及榜名，愕然曰：“咄咄怪事。”令与天锡请其故，笑曰：“事涉不经，不可以闻之儒者。”坚叩之，始曰：“老夫昨得女孙，一生即能言，谓人曰：‘嘉善刘嘏，吾夫也。幸与之约，勿令寒盟。’老夫恶其不祥，命溺之。其父母不忍，力劝乃止。今遇刘君，姓氏吻合，讵非异闻？今亦姑付之一笑。”乃天锡惊喜，亟请间倩师执柯。令与某公皆不欲，曰：“君能为韦固耶？俟此呱呱者成立，则鬓已皤矣。”天锡固请，始笑而从之，犹以为桐圭之戏。

    Explanation

    101

    Upon entering to see the magistrate, Tianxi just then found a prominent gentleman also present. When they inquired about the name on the list, the gentleman exclaimed, "A strange and astonishing matter!" The magistrate and Tianxi asked him for the reason, and he laughed, saying, "The matter involves something extraordinary, and it is not suitable to tell scholars." They persistently asked him, and he finally said, "I had a granddaughter yesterday, who could speak from birth. She said to people, 'Jiashan Liu Gu is my husband. I hope you will make an agreement with him, and do not let the alliance be cold.' I disliked it as an omen and ordered her to be drowned. Her parents could not bear it and strongly advised me to stop. Now meeting Mr. Liu, whose surname matches, is it not a strange story? Now, let's just laugh it off." Thus, Tianxi was surprised and delighted, and immediately asked to have a matchmaker arrange the marriage. The magistrate and the gentleman both did not want to, saying, "Can you be like Wei Gu? Wait until this infant grows up, then your hair will be white." Tianxi insisted, and they finally smiled and agreed, still thinking it was a game of tongs and gongs.

    102

    天锡遄归告母，请使委禽，母艴然曰：“汝非失心，何竟至此？且予老矣，亟于抱孙，谁能徐徐待之？”天锡力请再三，愿以琴心摄阃政，誓不他婚。母不得已，乃听焉。纳采至公家，咸嬉笑以为异事。明年，天锡登上第，历官馆阁。岳家为之语曰：“三岁为妇，即受荣封，大奇！大奇！”天锡既以琴心主中馈，家人亦事之如内子。凡十有五年，女甫十七，即于归，时天锡已去强仕不远矣！亲迎成礼，女貌既婉丽，而语言态度无不酷肖其前生，益征其为湘瑟无疑。女遇琴心独有恩，不以妾视之。虽未及事姑，而岁时祭祀，涕泣若不胜情，亦不解其何以然。

    Explanation

    102

    Swiftly returning home to inform his mother, Tian Xi asked her to send a gift of poultry to propose marriage. His mother was displeased and said, "You are not out of your mind, yet why have you come to this? Moreover, I am old, and I am eager to hold a grandson; who can wait patiently for this?" Tian Xi pleaded earnestly again and again, vowing that he would manage household affairs with the grace of a skilled qin player and swear never to marry another. His mother, unable to refuse, finally agreed. When the gift of poultry was sent to his family, they all laughed and joked, considering it an unusual event. The following year, Tian Xi achieved the highest rank in the imperial examination and served in a high-ranking official position in the imperial library. His family remarked, "She has been a wife for three years and immediately received an honorific title; how extraordinary! How extraordinary!" Tian Xi, with the grace of a skilled qin player, managed household affairs, and his family treated her with the respect due to a wife. For fifteen years, the woman, who was seventeen, married him. By then, Tian Xi was not far from the age of forty. The wedding ceremony was completed with personal welcome, and the bride was both graceful and beautiful, her words and demeanor closely resembling those of her previous life, further proving that she was indeed Xiang Se. She showed kindness and favor to the qin player, not treating her as a concubine. Although she had not yet served her mother-in-law, she performed rituals during festivals and seasonal times, crying as if overwhelmed by emotion, and no one could understand why.

    103

    一日，忽谓天锡曰：“睹君状貌，恍若熟识，绝似何地相遇者。但妾自幼至长，未离闺闼，此中实有天缘耶？”天锡笑曰：“卿试思之，当亦有故。”女沉吟良久，恍然曰：“是矣。闻妾甫生即能语，先祖深恶之，将置妾于死地。父母惧，饮以犬血，遂不复言，迄今犹仿佛一二。”天锡因缅述其颠末，且戏之曰：“若以婢学夫人，何幸如之？”女始顿悟，回忆本来，一一胥如梦寐。乃命人厚其棺槨，迁葬湘瑟于南山之阳。后李氏之子曰佩绶、佩绂，皆藉天锡教授，并登科第。天锡今已五旬，夫人仅二十余，倡随无间，不啻少年琴瑟焉。里人王绍濂为作传，以行于世。

    Explanation

    103

    One day, she suddenly said to Tianxi, "Seeing your appearance, I feel as if we had met before, exactly like meeting somewhere. But I have never left the inner chambers since childhood, so could there be a karmic connection here?" Tianxi smiled and said, "You should think about it; there must be a reason." The woman pondered for a long time and suddenly exclaimed, "Yes. I heard that I could speak immediately after birth, and my ancestors deeply disliked it, intending to put me to death. My parents, fearing for my life, fed me dog's blood, and I stopped speaking, though I still vaguely remember a little." Tianxi then recounted the entire process and teased her by saying, "If you were to learn from a lady, how fortunate it would be!" The woman suddenly realized and recalled her original state, finding everything as if in a dream. She then ordered that her coffin be made more elaborate and reburied her at the south side of the southern mountain. Later, the sons of the Li family, named Peishou and Peifu, both passed the imperial examinations through Tianxi's instruction. Tianxi is now fifty years old, while his wife is only in her twenties, living together in harmony, no different from young lovers. A local named Wang Shaolian wrote a biography of them, which was disseminated in the world.

    104

    外史氏曰：婢之奇，至湘瑟止矣。死生不易其心，惟以不得事夫子为恨，千古侍儿，应推第一。而天卒报之以花封，志亦毕偿于再世。为善降之百祥，岂虚语哉？独惜主人如李氏，而竟不传其名，令人弗克识荆，是亦天壤间一恨事。

    ○　卷二
    　　桃叶仙
    　　尚延采，天津人，才长而短于视，跬步之间仅能约略人形。同人因呼为次公，盖戏以狂者进取主义焉。一日访友，得子建《洛神赋》于其案头。读之，色喜曰：“世固有佳丽若此哉？”友笑曰：“但恐吾兄见之，亦与嫫母无异。”尚亦笑曰：“君过矣。予纵未知色，岂遂不闻其香？”因相与大粲。越二年，南游吴楚，侨寓秣陵，载酒于秦淮等处，日寻名胜。偶过桃叶渡，忽忽有感于怀。

    Explanation

    104

    The historian says: The marvel of a female servant ends with Xiang Se. Through life and death, her heart did not change, and she only regrets not being able to serve her master. Among all servants throughout history, she should be ranked first. But heaven finally rewarded her with a flower seal, and her wish was also fulfilled in another life. Good deeds bring a hundred blessings, is it not empty talk? Only regret that the owner, like Li, does not pass on his name, leaving people unable to recognize him. This is also a regrettable matter in the world.

    ○　Volume Two
    　　Peach Leaf Fairy
    　　Shang Yanchai, from Tianjin, is tall but short-sighted, barely able to discern human shapes within a few steps. His friends jokingly called him "Second Gong" because he is a bit rash and has an enterprising spirit. One day, he visited a friend and found Cao Zhi's "Ode to the Luo River Goddess" on his desk. Reading it, he was delighted and said, "There is indeed such a beautiful woman in the world!" His friend laughed and said, "I fear that even you, my friend, would see her as no different from Nuwa." Shang also laughed and said, "You are mistaken. Even if I do not know what she looks like, I can certainly smell her fragrance." They both laughed heartily. Two years later, he traveled south to Wu and Chu, staying temporarily in Moling (Nanjing), and searched for scenic spots along the Qinhuai River. One day, passing by Taoye Ferry, he was suddenly filled with a strange feeling.

    105

    比归邸中，天已薄暮，闭门高卧，辄诵王献之旧作弗辍。俄闻窗前低咏曰：“故人不相识，独坐为谁颦。”音甚娇婉，如闺人。尚心微动，启户视之，时正月望，清光如水，院中杳无人迹。心疑为鬼，亟阖其扉，拂榻就寝，屏息不敢出声。无何，环珮珊珊，寝门竟自辟矣。尚于枕上骇瞩，苦无所见，然而香气遄发，嗅之而骨为之靡。未几，闻小语曰：“王郎睡乎？”近在咫尺，始能少识肌容。纤腰弱态，素面红裙，二十许少妇也。第为目力所限，犹未深辨妍媸，而口脂遥吹，亦既心醉。

    Explanation

    105

    Upon returning to the guesthouse, the sky was already dimming, and he closed the door and lay down, continuously reciting Wang Xizhi's old works without stopping. Soon, he heard a low recitation before the window: "An old friend does not recognize me, who am I frowning for while sitting alone?" The voice was very tender and gentle, like that of a woman in the inner chambers. The narrator's heart was slightly moved, and he opened the door to look. It was the full moon of the first month, the clear light like water, and the courtyard was empty of any human trace. The heart suspected it was a ghost, and he quickly closed the door, cleaned the bed, and went to sleep, holding his breath and daring not to make a sound. Soon, the sound of jade ornaments was heard, and the bedroom door opened by itself. The narrator, lying on his pillow, was startled but saw nothing, yet the fragrance wafted over, and the scent of her lip balm intoxicated him. Not long after, he heard a soft voice say, "Is Wang Lang asleep?" It was near at hand, and he could just barely recognize her appearance. She had a slender waist and a delicate figure, a plain face, and a red skirt, a young woman in her twenties. But limited by the narrator's vision, he still could not fully discern her beauty, yet the scent of her lip balm wafted over, and the narrator was already intoxicated.

    106

    乃不畏缩，起而曳之使坐曰：“别来无恙？何怨我不相识耶？”女笑曰：“穷措大强作解事，子知我为谁？乃鬼狐来取汝命耳。”尚竟坦然，惟以目抵女面，睫毛几刺其颊。且诵二句曰：“施朱太赤，敷粉太白，古人不我欺矣。”女颇不耐其视，曰：“人皆具眼，一目了然。奈何令眸子逼人至此？”于是两相谐谑，欢然止宿。明旦，始别去。乃谓尚曰：“君实献之后身，妾即桃叶，虽鬼而已仙矣。情缘未断，因以相投。君能与妾久处，当令君寿。慎勿泄之他人，使造言者疑我也。”尚喜得丽质，生死俱所不辞，遂不惧其鬼，而反昵之。

    Explanation

    106

    Then, not shrinking back, he stood up and pulled her to make her sit, saying, "How have you been? Why blame me for not recognizing you?" The woman laughed and said, "A poor scholar pretending to understand, do you know who I am? I am a ghost or fox spirit here to take your life." Shang remained calm, only pressing his eyes against her face, his eyelashes almost pricking her cheek. He then recited two lines: "Applying rouge is too red, applying powder is too white, ancient people did not deceive me." The woman was not pleased with his gaze and said, "Everyone has eyes, one glance and it is clear. Why make your eyes intimidate to this extent?" Thus, they teased each other joyfully and stayed overnight. The next morning, they parted ways. Then she told Shang, "You indeed offered your reincarnation, I am called Peach Leaf, even though I am a ghost, I am already an immortal. Because the emotional bond is not broken, we meet. If you can stay with me for a long time, I will make you live a long life. Carefully do not reveal it to others, let those who fabricate words doubt me." Shang was happy to get a beautiful appearance, not hesitating in both life and death, thus, he was not afraid of the ghost and instead became close to it.

    107

    虽至契密友之前，未尝微露其概。女昼去夕来，日益缱绻。即有客乘夜过访，而女之至也无形，女之避也无迹。私语喁喁，属垣者褎如充耳；笑声吃吃，窥户者阒其无人。其行踪诡秘如此，尚益信其真仙。无何，而尚疾矣。女时来问视，汤药必亲，俨然伉俪，而疾亦小愈，独向之痴情不断，每值其来，欲与之合，女怍然辞曰：“妾误君几危，犹忍以床笫相惑耶？”尚不听，强之共寝，明日而疾又大作。女叹曰，“予害夫子，将不可复为人矣。”尚正色曰：“即使今为卿死，已愈于徒生，何憾为？”女终引为己过。幸尚之艰于远视，遂匿迹韬声，虽日侍尚之左右，而不使一见之。

    Explanation

    107

    Even in the presence of close and intimate friends, she never revealed even a hint of it. The woman would leave during the day and come at night, and their affection grew increasingly tender. Even if there were visitors who came at night, the woman's arrival was invisible, and her departure left no trace. Their whispers were so soft that those who were listening would pretend not to hear; their laughter was so light that those who were watching would think there was no one there. Her movements were so mysterious that Shang believed even more strongly that she was a true fairy. Not long after, Shang fell ill. The woman would often come to visit, personally preparing the medicine, and they seemed like a married couple, and his illness slightly improved. But his infatuation with her remained unbroken, and every time she came, he wanted to be with her, but she blushed and said, "I almost caused you to die, how could I still tempt you with intimacy?" Shang did not listen, forcing her to sleep with him, and the next day his illness worsened. The woman sighed and said, "I have harmed Master, and I will no longer be human." Shang said sternly, "Even if I die for you now, it is better than living in vain, what is there to regret?" The woman finally took it as her own fault. Fortunately, Shang had difficulty seeing far away, so she remained hidden and silent, even though she served him day and night, he never saw her.

    108

    尚因疑其薄情而恨恨不已。无如疾益不起，同寓之友咸忧之，女亦数夕弗至。盖自尚之疾也，女为情所系，为忧所迫，渐不能隐其形。尚虽不睹，而人反时一见之，甫悉其病源。与之契者，皆苦口以诤，尚犹坚讳曰无。适钟山有一道士，素持敕勒之术，驱遣最灵。众乃相与为谋，不令尚知，造请焉。道士慨然与偕来，至则曰：“妖气甚深，非符咒所能祛。”乃度地为坛，四面皆张猎网。

    Explanation

    108

    Shang suspected her of being cold-hearted and resented her bitterly. Unfortunately, his illness grew worse and he could not recover. His friends who lived in the same lodging were all worried about him, and the woman did not come for several nights. Since Shang fell ill, the woman was tied down by emotions and pressured by worries, gradually she could no longer hide her form. Shang did not see it, but people occasionally saw it, and just discovered the cause of his illness. Those who were close to him all earnestly advised him, but Shang still stubbornly denied, saying there was nothing. Happened to be in Zhongshan, there was a Taoist priest who always practiced the method of exorcism, which was most effective. The group then conspired together, without letting Shang know, and invited him. The Taoist priest readily came along with them. Upon arriving, he said, "The evil spirits are very deep, not able to dispel with charms and spells." He then measured the ground and built an altar, spreading hunting nets on all sides.

    109

    道士随禹步作法，且戟手而指曰：“速速！”良久，有黑气一团，微挟赤光，自东南而来，飚疾如风，径投网内。众视之，则一白狐，毛雪色，口啣小草，闪灼有光，向之所见其赤者，盖即此也。道士不暇责问，急掣剑欲斩之。狐匍伏乞命，以喙向病室而嗥，一若悲不自胜者。道士验其草为芝，乃掷剑叹曰：“世之膜视其夫者，固此畜之不若也。吾几害天下之节义矣。”亟命撤其网，狐遂展转仍化为女。众环瞩焉，见其妖冶异常，因啧啧曰：“无怪乎尚三之见惑也。”女诣道士请命，且自白曰：“儿前身实系王家桃叶，缘夙孽堕落为狐。修持数百年，既已悟道，前一见尚，恋恋绨袍，遂狂惑忘其异类。

    Explanation

    109

    The Taoist priest followed the Yu steps in performing rituals, raising his fist and pointing while commanding, "Hurry up!" After a long time, a mass of black vapor with a faint red glow appeared, swiftly moving like a storm from the southeast and directly entering the net. The crowd looked and saw a white fox, with snow-white fur, holding a small grass that shimmered with light. The red glow they had seen earlier was from this very grass. The priest, without time to question, hastily drew his sword to strike it down. The fox crawled and begged for mercy, turning its mouth toward the sick room and howling as if overwhelmed by sorrow. The priest identified the grass as an orchid and, throwing his sword aside, sighed, "Those who treat their husbands as if they were mere skin are no better than this creature. I almost harmed the loyalty and righteousness of the world." He immediately ordered the net to be removed, and the fox, turning and twisting, transformed back into a woman. The crowd watched in awe, seeing her beauty and charm as extraordinary, and murmured, "No wonder Shang San was deceived." The woman approached the priest to plead her case, explaining, "In my previous life, I was indeed the peach leaf of the Wang family, but due to my past sins, I fell and became a fox. I practiced for several centuries and had already attained enlightenment, but when I encountered Shang San, I was deeply attached to a coarse robe and thus became delirious, forgetting my true nature as a fox."

    110

    不意尚一病沉疴，竟乃不救。儿筹思无策，昨自灵山觅得此草，实欲前来就药，行至中途，即被擒获。儿以妖妄惑人，死乃其分。祈师以此物疗郎痼疾，儿区区之念既遂，殁亦无憾。”言次，词色俱惨。众中多有涕零者，反向道士为女乞怜。道士乃呼女而进之曰：“汝来前，夫人之心如水，溢则为灾。尚虽不由汝死，病实因汝而生，乌得无罪？予今鉴汝寸诚，不加汝谴。况尚疾得此亦愈，痊后仍勉事君子，戒以寡欲清心，不独可同成地仙，亦可以完汝素志。”语已，太息径去。

    Explanation

    110

    Unfortunate, young Master has fallen ill with a deep-rooted ailment, and ultimately could not be saved. I had no ideas or strategies, and yesterday I searched for this herb on the sacred mountain of Ling, intending to come here to seek medicine. I was captured midway. I deceived and misled others, and death is my fate. I pray that the teacher use this herb to treat the young master's chronic illness. If my humble wish is fulfilled, I will die without regret." As she spoke, her words and appearance were sad. Many in the crowd shed tears, and they turned to the Taoist priest to beg for mercy for the girl. The Taoist priest then called the girl forward and said, "Come forward. The lady's heart is like water; if it overflows, it becomes a disaster. Though young Master did not die because of you, his illness was indeed caused by you. How can you not be guilty? I now recognize your sincere heart and will not punish you. Moreover, young Master's illness will also be cured by this herb. After he recovers, he should still strive to serve the gentleman, reduce his desires, and purify his mind. Not only can you both become immortals, but you can also fulfill your long-held aspirations." After speaking, he sighed deeply and left directly.

    111

    众延女入室，煎草医尚，一饮而瘳。左近赢發之夫，沾其余沥者，亦活十数人。女遂自此昼见，同寓者咸得晤言。女善书，颇得钟王家法，丐其一幅一扇，无不宝以终身。乃尚自愈后，虽益爱重女，情好倍笃，然不敢过于驰骋，遂益坚强。居半年，尚返故里，女亦与偕，但不复露形，而倡随则未之有异也。尚不自讳，恒曰：“不见可欲，使心不乱。予初视如云如荼者，直如无物。乃迫一见之，几不能生。彼目之灼灼者，可不戒哉？”闻者深颔其论。后尚至五旬，道士忽来，两人阖户饮。及夕，竟失其所在，盖与女皆仙去也。

    Explanation

    111

    The people invited the woman into the room, where they boiled herbs to treat the illness. After drinking the medicine, the patient was cured. Nearby weak men who came into contact with the remaining liquid of the medicine were also cured, saving dozens of people. The woman then began to appear during the day, and those who lived together with her could all converse with her. She was skilled in calligraphy, and her style was quite well in line with the calligraphy of Zhong Yao and Wang Xizhi. Anyone who requested a piece of her calligraphy or a fan would treasure it for life. After his recovery, the man loved and respected the woman even more, and their affection grew stronger. However, he dared not go too far, and thus became even stronger. After staying together for half a year, the man returned to his hometown with the woman, who no longer revealed her form, but their relationship remained unchanged. The man did not hide this experience and often said, "Not seeing what one desires makes the heart not chaotic. I initially saw them as clouds and thorns, as if they were nothing. But when forced to see them, almost could not survive. Those eyes that burn brightly, can one not be cautious?" Those who heard his argument deeply nodded in agreement. Later, when the man reached fifty years old, a Taoist suddenly arrived, and the two shut the door to drink. By evening, they suddenly disappeared, and it turned out that both the man and the woman had ascended to immortality.

    112

    外史氏曰：太上忘情，特不自溺于情中。而于人之有情者，未尝不心焉许之。观道士见女啣芝，即开密网，谆谆以节义为言，非因其情而怜之乎？至于众人，未必皆钟情之辈，乃为女所感，泣下沾襟，反为之居间而排难。情之动人，一至于此！然非尚之深于情，女之痴于情，吾知忘情者终属无情，不及情者又何知有情也耶？
    　　随园老人曰：近察秋毫，远昧舆薪，世之短视者固多也。惟此得遇丽人，香艳千古，不惟可以解嘲，抑更可以解酲。浩歌子直世间第一解人。

    Explanation

    112

    外史氏曰：The highest level of detachment from emotions is to not drown oneself in them. Yet, for those who are emotionally engaged, one has always been willing to acknowledge them in one's heart. Observing the Taoist priest seeing the woman holding the herb, he immediately opened the trap and earnestly spoke of righteousness and propriety. Was this not out of compassion for her emotional state? As for the common people, not all are emotionally attached, yet they were moved by the woman and cried, their sleeves wet. They even intervened to resolve difficulties on her behalf. The power of emotions to move people can be profound! However, if not for the deep emotional attachment of the woman and her emotional intensity, I know that those who claim to be detached are truly emotionless, and those who do not understand emotions cannot truly know them.

    随园老人曰：Being able to see fine details but missing the bigger picture is a common flaw among short-sighted people. Only by encountering such a beautiful woman, whose charm and allure have lasted through the ages, can one not only relieve a joke but also relieve mental or emotional distress. The great singer is indeed the first to truly understand the world.

    113

    ○　冯埙
    　　冯埙，字怀仲，浙西人，友于綦笃，无忝厥名。弟堃，素无赖，每傲慢之，埙恒笑而不言。戚族多不平，谓之曰：“渠弟而若兄，何乃无礼如是？”埙曰：“予少失怙恃，同胞者惟此一人，若以微隙而昆季分离，庸保长逝者不掩泪泉下乎？予不忍于吾亲非为弟也。”人以是高之。未几堃之发妻卒，继妻某氏，性极悍，每每以事唆其夫。堃益视兄为陌路，箕帚耰锄，恣其诟谇。埙之妇渐不能堪，亦愤诉于夫前，埙怒曰：“若亦效长舌者耶？牝鸡司晨，惟象之索。予寒门不堪有此。”遂与弟计，欲出其妻。堃始犹谏阻。及纳妇言，反向兄前媒孽嫂短，且借端与之哄。每谓兄曰：“若留嫂，盍析吾居！”于是埙去妇之志遂决。妇故名家女，誓不二夫，长跽夫子前流涕不去。堃又以言激埙曰：“我固谓儿女情长，则英雄气短也。”埙益恚，竟出其妇，堃与妻愈益得志。

    Explanation

    113

    ○　冯埙
    　　冯埙，字怀仲，浙西人，兄弟情谊极为深厚，无愧于其名。他的弟弟冯堃，一向是个无赖，常常对他傲慢无礼，冯埙总是笑着不说话。亲戚们大多不平，对他说：“你的弟弟这样无礼，你为什么还要忍让？”冯埙说：“我从小失去父母，唯一的同胞兄弟就是他，如果因为一点小事就让兄弟分离，难道不怕已故的父母在九泉之下哭泣吗？我不忍心为了弟弟而违背对父母的孝道。”人们因此更加敬重他。不久，冯堃的原配妻子去世，继妻某氏性格极为凶悍，常常挑拨冯堃。冯堃更加视兄长为路人，对冯埙的家事任意指责。冯埙的妻子渐渐无法忍受，也愤怒地向丈夫诉说，冯埙怒道：“你也学着多嘴吗？牝鸡司晨，只有象的征兆。我家贫寒，不能容忍这样的事情。”于是与弟弟商量，打算休掉妻子。冯堃起初还劝阻。等到妻子的话被采纳，反而在兄长面前诋毁嫂子，还找借口与她争吵。常常对兄长说：“如果你要留嫂子，为什么不分开住！”于是冯埙决定休掉妻子。妻子本是名门闺秀，发誓不再嫁人，长跪在丈夫面前流泪不止。冯堃又用言语激怒冯埙说：“我总是说儿女情长，英雄气短。”冯埙更加愤怒，最终休掉了妻子，冯堃与妻子更加得意。

    114

    埙亦不再言娶，茕茕一身，尽以家事予弟，坤遂少安。然自埙出妻，饮食衣服皆仰给于堃之妇，日渐凉薄。家故素封，堃与妇日餍粱肉，而以粗粝奉兄，埙亦隐忍弗言。堃以天性不安于中，遂与兄计，亦欲出其妻。埙闻之叹曰：“家门不幸，而有离异之名。一之已甚，其可再乎？不可因吾而坏弟之琴瑟，吾盍去之。”遂襆被夜行，悄然他往。堃本故为此语以饰己过，埙既远去，益中奸谋，夫妇乃私相庆幸。居无何，灶突火起，延及室庐，泯焉荡焉，尽其所有。夫妇日益贫乏，不可谓祝融回禄伥伥然目无瞳子也。先是埙仓卒去乡，茫无定向，因思舅氏某公，新任江右，遂决计谒之。独行数十里，力少倦，息于道周。俄有伟丈夫，须髯如戟，驺从甚盛，疾驰而过其前。见埙，即下曰：“故人别来无恙？”埙视之，若不相识，乃起而揖曰：“契阔日久，偶失记忆，望以姓氏见示。

    Explanation

    114

    Xun no longer spoke of marriage, living alone and entrusting all family affairs to his younger brother, which brought Kun some reassurance. However, after Xun divorced his wife, his food, clothing, and daily necessities were provided by Kun's wife, and the relationship gradually became cold and indifferent. The family had always been wealthy, and Kun and his wife daily enjoyed fine food, while providing coarse food to their elder brother, whom Xun bore in silence. Kun felt uneasy within himself due to his nature and consulted with his elder brother, also considering divorcing his wife. Upon hearing this, Xun sighed and said, "Our family's misfortune has led to this name of divorce. If one misfortune is already excessive, can there be more? I cannot allow myself to ruin my younger brother's marital harmony, so why not leave?" He then packed his bedding and quietly left at night, going elsewhere. Kun originally said this to cover up his own fault. After Xun had gone far away, Kun became more intent on his deceitful plans, and the couple privately rejoiced. After a short while, a fire broke out at the stove, spreading to the house, completely destroying everything they owned. The couple became increasingly impoverished, and one cannot say it was the work of Zhubo (fire god) or Hui Lu (fire demon) acting blindly. Before this, Xun had hastily left his hometown, without any clear direction, and thought of his official uncle, who had recently been appointed to Jiangyou. He decided to visit him. Walking alone for dozens of miles, he was a bit tired and rested by the roadside. Suddenly, a tall and imposing man appeared, with a beard like a halberd and a large retinue, rushing past in front of him. Seeing Xun, he immediately dismounted and said, "Old friend, have you been well since we last met?" Xun looked at him as if they didn't know each other and stood up to bow, saying, "We have been apart for a long time, and I have momentarily forgotten. Please show me your name."

    115

    ”客大笑曰：“不复念我耶？予与兄实同桑梓。窃已识荆，今且略置姓氏，有一事亟欲咨君。”因即树下设氍毹，与埙并坐，而叩之曰：“昨自故乡来，闻兄有弃妇之事，信有之乎？”曰：“然。”客曰：“审是，则君以孝悌自居而罹三大罪矣。”埙愕然，亟请其故。客笑曰：“君之父母以弟付君，不能导以和顺，而任其灭性陵兄，将来不可救药，一罪也。君之父母为君授室，执妇道数年，未闻蒸梨之小过，今因昆弟而弃夫妻之伦，二罪也。君之父母望君生儿，以延祖脉，乃竟出妻不娶，莫续鸾胶，即令君弟有嗣，而君俨然无后，非三大罪而何？”埙闻客言，流汗浃背，乃强与之辩曰：“兄知其一，未解其二。自来兄弟之祸，无逾于阋墙。仆惧大伤父母心，岂反以此为口实？且兄弟，手足也。妻子，衣服也。宁为手足去衣服，忍为衣服间手足？仆尝遍观人世，家之不和多以妇故。

    Explanation

    115

    The guest laughed loudly and said, "Do you not remember me anymore? I and your brother are from the same hometown. I have already had the pleasure of meeting you; let’s temporarily put aside our surnames. There is one urgent matter I wish to consult you about." He then set up a carpet under the tree and sat down with Xun, asking him, "I came from my hometown yesterday and heard that you have divorced your wife. Is it true?" Xun replied, "Yes." The guest said, "If it is indeed so, then you have committed three great sins by claiming to be filial and fraternal." Xun was startled and urgently asked for the reason. The guest laughed and said, "Your parents entrusted your younger brother to you, but you could not guide him with harmony and gentleness, allowing him to destroy his nature and disrespect his elder brother. In the future, it will be beyond cure, the first sin. Your parents arranged your marriage, and you observed the duties of a wife for several years, but I have not heard of any minor faults like cooking pears. Now, because of your younger brother, you have abandoned the relationship between husband and wife, the second sin. Your parents hoped that you would have children to continue the family lineage, but you ended up divorcing your wife and not remarrying, no one can mend the broken bond. Even if your younger brother has descendants, you will still have no descendants, is this not the third great sin?" Xun heard the guest's words and sweat poured down his back. He then tried to argue with him, saying, "You know one thing but do not understand the second. Since ancient times, the disaster of brothers is worse than quarreling within the family. I fear greatly hurting my parents' hearts, how could I use this as an excuse? Moreover, brothers are like hands and feet. A wife and children are like clothes. Would I rather lose my hands and feet than my clothes? Would I rather let clothes come between my hands and feet? I have often observed the world, and family disharmony often arises from the wife."

    116

    世少贤女子，仆因鳏而不续。弟子即兄子，渠儿即我儿，何必徒多扰扰哉！”客又笑曰：“君之言何其不达也！郑庄纵段，君子讥之；鲁隐让桓，儒林谬之；周公右王，而管蔡为戮；以兄弟之有贤不肖也。若喻妻子以衣服固已然，因手足之故，而裸以为饰，即圣人亦无取焉。且使尊夫人得罪于舅姑，君又何以处之？抑更有加于此乎，将如此而止乎？原情定律，君必有以教之。”埙语塞。客又曰：“君谓弟之子为己子，语更大谬不然。父母之生子不虞其多者，非谓含饴弄孙，一夔已足也，意将繁衍椒聊，多多益善耳。如兄以此事委之弟，当年亦何必有兄？况子嗣皆关天命，君幸而为伯道，固可矣，君之弟不幸而为中郎，又将如之何？”言已，埙乃大悟曰：“噫嘻！是仆之罪也。”亟起再拜。客使还坐而诘之曰：“君之意，将图其旧姻乎？抑别求新特乎？”答曰：“旧人虽在，吾未如何。新者其可哉！”客曰：“诺。

    Explanation

    116

    In the world, there are few virtuous women, and I remain unmarried due to being a widower. My nephew is like my own son, and his child is also my child. Why should we cause unnecessary trouble? The guest laughed and said, "Your words are so lacking in understanding! Zheng Zhuang allowed Duan to act freely, and the sages criticized him; Duke Yin of Lu yielded the throne to Duke Huan, and the scholars misunderstood him; Zhou Gong supported King Cheng, while Guan and Cai were punished; this is because of the difference in virtue among brothers. If you compare your wife and children to clothes, which are already a given, and then, due to the relationship of siblings, you leave them naked as decoration, even a sage would not approve. Moreover, if your respected wife offends her in-laws, how will you handle it? Or is there something worse than this, and will you stop at this? Based on the situation, you must have something to teach." The guest's words left me speechless. He continued, "You say that your brother's son is like your own, which is even more absurd. Parents do not expect to have many children; it is not to say that one is sufficient, but rather to have many descendants, and the more, the better. If your brother entrusted this matter to you, why did he need to have a brother in the first place? Moreover, descendants are related to fate. You are fortunate to be like Berdao, so it is fine, but if your brother is unfortunate to be like Zhonglang, what can you do?" After he finished speaking, I suddenly realized, "Ah! This is my fault." I quickly got up and bowed twice. The guest asked me to sit down and questioned, "Do you intend to remarry your former wife? Or do you plan to find a new one?" I replied, "Even if my former wife is still alive, I have no intention of doing anything. Is it possible to find a new one?" The guest said, "Yes."

    117

    家有小妹，颇贤淑，仰君高义，即以之奉攀。”埙讶曰：“异哉！萍水相逢，未过数面，突以千金之媛相属，使仆闻之滋惧矣！且仆单寒羁旅，今尚无家，得无为门楣之辱耶？”客曰：“不然。君笃伦常，仆重品望，岂敢以门第相高哉？请即同行，无须谦退。”乃以从骑授埙，并辔而前。途次自言其官阀，则黄姓，椿名，其父即现任山阳令也。行至薄暮，始抵其家，高门巍焕，仆役十数，俨有世家风。公子肃客，下骑同入，即命小奚传报曰：“速启夫人，薄情郎已邀至矣。”埙闻而心疑，亦未遑问。入则华堂夏屋，备极富丽。有妇人年可五旬，冠帔尊贵，迎于檐际，熟视而笑曰：“真吾家佳婿也。”埙知为夫人，参谒如礼，夫人辞让而后受之。少坐，即令易衣，且言曰：“今夕日吉时良，可成好事。

    Explanation

    117

    My wife has a younger sister who is very virtuous and gentle. She admires your noble character and wishes to offer her as a bride to you." Xun was surprised and said, "Strange! We have only met by chance, and have not even exchanged more than a few words, yet you suddenly offer a wealthy and beautiful woman as a wife, which makes me feel even more fearful! Moreover, I am poor and living away from home, and I do not yet have a family. Could this bring shame to my family?" The guest replied, "Not so. You are deeply committed to moral principles, and I value your reputation. How dare I consider your social status as superior? Please proceed without hesitation." He then gave his horse to Xun and rode together. Along the way, he introduced himself and his family background, saying he was surnamed Huang and named Chun, and that his father was the current magistrate of Shanyang. They arrived at his home late in the evening, where the entrance was grand and splendid, with about ten servants, and the household had a distinguished family atmosphere. The young master received the guest politely, dismounted together, and entered the house. He immediately ordered a servant to announce, "Quickly inform the wife that the guest has arrived." Xun heard this and felt suspicious but did not have time to ask. Inside, there were luxurious halls and splendid houses. A woman, about fifty years old, dressed in noble attire, greeted them at the eaves and, after looking at Xun, smiled and said, "He is truly a fine match for our family." Xun knew she was the wife and bowed and greeted her according to proper etiquette. The wife politely declined and then accepted him. After sitting for a while, she ordered him to change into different clothes and said, "Tonight is an auspicious time, and it is suitable for a good event."

    118

    ”埙怪其急，方欲起辞，俄而箫鼓喧于堂下，旋有娇鬟数人扶新妇出，与成礼，送入青庐。及埙启袱视之，烛光之下，眉目宛然，实即故妇某氏也。大骇，亟询之，妇惟挥涕不语。有顷夫人至，乃代白其由。盖妇自被逐而出，父母即遣之他适。妇以柏舟自誓，矢志不从，以是触怒父母，将强之，妇遂遁迹尼庵，欲薙其发。适遇黄夫人怜其志节，收养于家。公子故豪侠尚义，有郭解之风，因为物色冯生，使得完聚。夫人言之历历，埙亦抱惭不胜。语次，妇始谓埙曰：“君以家之不和，遣之使归，今何以仍不见容于悌弟，而亦担簦以出耶？妾诚不足惜，但思奉事舅姑十年，未蒙诃责，窃自谓此生无忝矣。忽一旦弃之如遗，竟同覆水，使为枭为鸱者，得以快心而满志，则情实不甘耳！”言之潜然，涕泣不能仰，阖室咸为之不平，埙更默然内愧。夫人乃解之曰：“儿勿气苦，薄幸人诚不足道，但今是予家赘渠，往事皆不须齿及。”妇因雪涕对曰：“母毋再言婚媾事。

    118

    Xun was surprised by the urgency, and was just about to rise and take his leave when, all of a sudden, the sound of flutes and drums erupted from downstairs. Soon after, several beautiful women escorted the bride out, and the wedding ceremony was conducted, after which she was sent into the blue tent. When Xun opened the wrapping to see, under the candlelight, her features were distinctly familiar—she was indeed his former wife, Lady Mou. He was greatly shocked and hurriedly asked her, but she only wept and remained silent. After a while, Lady Huang arrived and explained the situation on her behalf. It turned out that Lady Mou had been expelled from the family and her parents had arranged for her to remarry. She had vowed to remain chaste, refusing to comply, which angered her parents who tried to force her. She then fled to a nunnery, intending to shave her head. Fortunately, Lady Huang, moved by her resolve, took her in. The young master, known for his chivalry and sense of justice, reminiscent of Guo Jie, had sought out Feng Sheng to ensure their reunion. Lady Huang recounted the story in detail, and Xun felt deeply ashamed. As they spoke, the wife finally addressed Xun, saying, "You sent me away because of the family's discord. Now why are you still not accepted by your younger brother, and why are you carrying your belongings away? I may not be worth much, but I have served your in-laws for ten years without being scolded, and I believed I had lived up to my duties. Suddenly, you cast me aside like waste, treating me as if I were spilled water. If those who act like hawks and owls can find joy and satisfaction in this, then I truly cannot accept it!" She spoke with tears streaming down her face, unable to look up, and everyone in the room felt indignant on her behalf. Xun felt even more ashamed and silent. Lady Huang then comforted her, saying, "My child, do not be upset. Such a fickle person is not worth mentioning. But now that he has come to our home, past events need not be discussed." The wife wiped away her tears and replied, "Mother, please do not bring up the matter of marriage again."

    119

    儿已被弃，不敢作他想，但得郎来，一证其是非，殁亦无憾。今藉大兄力，既得表明衷曲，请死于郎前，以终妾靡他之志。”语至此，词气俱烈，即出袖中短刃，欲自刭。夫人使婢媪持之以力，公子遽自外入，止之曰：“妹勿尔尔。予觅冯郎来，岂反以送若之命耶？”乃谓埙曰：“诗有云，刑于寡妻，至于兄弟。古人处家室之间，良亦有道。今一有不睦，遽出其身，宜其怨也。君欲效买臣故事，仆不敢强，若犹有伉俪之情，愿早圆乐昌之镜。”埙闻夫人言，既已自悔，继见妇状，不禁惨然，惟唯唯陨涕而已。公子与夫人又居间排解，遂为夫妇如初。明日夙兴，乃双双入谢。夫人馆于别院，外事戒令弗通，以故堃家遇灾，埙竟不与闻。年余，公子之父以卓异荐升某州，遣役迎其眷。夫人因置酒话别，赠以五百金，夫妇皆垂泪而谢。公子嘱埙曰：“倘不如意，可携妹至任所相访也。”不数日遂行。

    Explanation

    119

    The child had already been abandoned and dared not think of anything else; she only wished for Young Master Feng to come, to prove the right and wrong of the situation, and even if she died, she would have no regrets. Now, with the help of my elder brother, I have been able to express my inner feelings. I ask to die in front of Young Master Feng, to fulfill my unwavering loyalty. As she spoke these words, her tone was intense, and she immediately took out a short knife from her sleeve, intending to commit suicide. The lady had the maidservants hold her back with force, and the young master quickly entered from outside, stopping her and saying, "Sister, do not do this. I have come to find Young Master Feng, how could I be the one to end your life?" He then said to Xun, "The Book of Songs says, 'Set an example for your wife, and it will extend to your brothers.' Ancient people in their households had their ways. Now, with even a slight discord, one immediately leaves, it is natural for her to be resentful. You wish to follow the story of Bi Gan, I dare not force you, but if you still have feelings for each other as husband and wife, I hope you can quickly reunite like the Le Chang mirror." Xun, upon hearing the lady's words, had already regretted his actions. Seeing the woman's state, he could not help but look sad, only nodding and shedding tears. The young master and the lady then mediated and resolved the situation, and the couple returned to their original state. The next day, they rose early and entered together to express their gratitude. The lady lived in a separate courtyard, and external affairs were forbidden to communicate, so when the Kuang family encountered disaster, Xun was finally not informed. After a year, the father of the young master was recommended and promoted to a certain state, and he sent servants to welcome his family. The lady then held a banquet to bid farewell, giving them five hundred gold coins, and both the husband and wife shed tears and expressed their gratitude. The young master instructed Xun, "If it is not to your liking, you can bring your sister to my place of duty to visit." They departed a few days later.

    120

    埙同妇归里，其家已无立锥，埙甚骇然，因出金置产，召弟同居。堃见兄嫂偕来，亦觉忸怩。堃妇私谓其夫曰：“我固逆料阿伯之有余蓄也，渠实不能舍姆，故托言他往，携金以就之。观其偕归，事可知已。不然黄家纵巨富，讵肯以重金畀路人？”堃以为然，遂播诸里党。渐入埙耳，始恚曰：“我念弟而归，今反以私妇谤我耶？予不可再居。”于是乃以余金付弟，买舟载妇，径诣黄衙。公子引谒其父，待以婿礼，使与公子司衙务，丝毫莫欺，甚重之。在任五稔，黄公致仕归，持析宦囊之半，约二三千金与之，曰：“婿弃家佐我，我不忍见婿无家也。”埙又骤富。甫归乡，弟即来谒，夫妇又褴褛如丐矣。询以前金，则曰：“屡遭盗劫，炮烙几死。今幸有命见兄，其他尚可问哉？”言已，涕泣请罪。埙仍怜而收之，夫妇遂不敢妄为私议，而卒无子。惟埙之妇二男，继其宗祧。益服黄公子之论，往来如姻娅焉。

    120

    Xun returned to his hometown with his wife. Their family had already fallen into such extreme poverty that they had no place to stand, let alone live. Xun was greatly shocked by this situation. He then spent money to buy property and called his younger brother to live together. Kun saw his elder brother and sister-in-law return, and he felt embarrassed. Kun’s wife privately told her husband, “I had indeed anticipated that Uncle would have some savings. He couldn’t bear to part with Aunt, so he claimed to go elsewhere, carrying money to join him. Seeing them return together, it’s clear what happened. Otherwise, the Huang family, though extremely wealthy, would not give a large sum of money to a stranger.” Kun agreed with her, and the rumors spread among the villagers.

    The rumors gradually reached Xun’s ears, and he became angry, saying, “I returned home thinking of my brother, but now they are accusing me of wrongdoing behind my back! I cannot stay here any longer.” Thus, he gave the remaining money to his brother, bought a boat, and took his wife to visit the Huang family’s office. The Huang’s son introduced him to his father, treating him with the respect due a son-in-law, and appointed him to manage the office affairs, entrusting him with no deceit, and highly valuing him. After serving for five years, when the Huang family’s patriarch retired and returned home, he divided half of his official’s bag, about two or three thousand gold pieces, and said to Xun, “You left your family to help me, and I could not bear to see you without a home.” Xun suddenly became wealthy again. As soon as he returned to his hometown, his brother came to visit, but his wife and he were once again in tattered clothes, living like beggars. When asked about the previous money, he said, “We have been repeatedly robbed, and I was nearly killed by the cruel punishment. Now I am fortunate to have the chance to see my brother, but what else can be asked?” After speaking, he wept and begged for forgiveness. Xun still pitied him and took them in. The couple then dared not speak ill of each other, but they eventually had no children. Only Xun’s wife had two sons who continued the family line. They increasingly believed in the Huang’s son’s views and visited each other like close relatives.

    121

    外史氏曰：世人多重妻而轻弟，怀仲独能矫之，可谓中流砥柱矣。然为弟出妻，又不复聚，则近于不可。观黄公子侃侃正论，原非夺理之词，而救痼疾以药石，冯氏先人冥漠中当为额手，又不止断者能续，作一段佳话已也。

    Explanation

    121

    The commentator said: In the world, people often value wives more than brothers, but Huai Zhong alone was able to correct this, truly serving as a pillar in the midst of the stream. However, divorcing his wife for the sake of his brother and not remarrying again approaches being unacceptable. Observing Huang Gongzi's clear and forceful argument, it was not an unjustified claim, and correcting a long-standing problem with medicine and acupuncture, the ancestors of the Feng family would surely be relieved in the afterlife. It is not just about mending what is broken, but also creating a fine story.

    122

    ○　昔昔措措
    　　湖南邹士钰，少有四方之志，年仅二句余，足迹遍天下。某年春，又将负贩入黔。所亲以蛮烟瘴雨之乡，山川险阻，劝以勿往。邹不信，且忾然曰：“大丈夫死生有命，岂区区者之能为耶？”束装径行。比及思南等处，淫霖为患，跋履维艰，未免有悔于心。一日，迷踪深山，峭壁巉岩，人行鸟道，良久始逾。绝险远望，村墟似有炊火，而深潭当路，犹须循岸而行，疲板，遂憩于树下。俄见对江有木筏，一人以长竹为棹，荡漾而来，邹大喜，以为接引者至矣。及少近而视之，其人披一短蓑，周身似无寸缕，不知乌之雌雄。立而姑招以手，乘槎者亦甚欣欣，其疾如驶，无何，即识其面，兼睹其身，则螺髻双垂，体洁如玉，一少年女子也。大骇，诧为异物。

    Explanation

    122

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    123

    方将奔避，而女子早已登岸，操苗音，且笑且歌，绝不羞涩，始悟为蛮俗，未免少见而多怪，因从之登桴。女子鼓楫而行。共济同舟，逢此绝艳，邹亦不禁心动，间以手戏之，女子漫无避忌，尔许时始渡迷津，解钱付之，女子不受，惟吃吃憨笑；且舍所业，与之偕行，土语胡卢，若将导引。邹亦喜得指南。曲折里余，始达村口。时已天气昏暝，莫辨物色。女子引之至一处，状类神庙，亲款其扉，语邹曰：“此犹可居。郎君非我族类，慎勿他往也。”其言则华音，绝无啁哳之韵。邹心异之。女子即反身自去。邹入祠，中惟一楹，聊可栖息。黑暗中都无所见，亦不知所祀为何神。方假寐，而枵腹苦饥，旋闻女子呼之，则樽酒盘飧，陈于庙阶，且酒炙俱温，啜之亦甚适口。

    Explanation

    123

    As Zou was about to flee, the girl had already reached the shore, speaking in the Miao language, laughing and singing, and showing no embarrassment. He then realized this was a custom of the southern tribes and could not help but be surprised by what was rare and strange. He followed her onto the boat. The girl rowed them along. Sharing the same boat, encountering such exceptional beauty, Zou could not help but be moved, occasionally touching her playfully. The girl showed no hesitation and, after some time, they finally crossed the bewildering ford. He tried to pay her, but she refused, only laughing foolishly and happily. She left her work and accompanied him, speaking in a local dialect or gibberish, as if leading him somewhere. Zou was also glad to have a guide. After winding through several hundred meters, they reached the village entrance. By then, it was already twilight, and he could not distinguish the surroundings. The girl led him to a place that looked like a temple, gently knocking on the door and saying to Zou, "This place is still suitable for staying. Sir, you are not of my kind, so be careful not to go elsewhere." Her words were in the refined Han language, with no harsh or discordant tones. Zou found this strange. The girl then turned and left on her own. Zou entered the shrine, which had only one column, providing barely enough space to rest. In the darkness, he could see nothing and did not know what deity was being worshipped. He took a short nap, but was suffering from hunger with an empty stomach. Soon, he heard the girl call him, and there were wine vessels and dishes placed on the steps of the temple. The wine and roasted meat were both warm, and he found them very palatable.

    124

    邹因深感其情，兼疑其为结欢之计，势必复来，私心固不忍拂，遂亦不拒，乃兀坐以俟之。漏已数下，迹竟杳然。忽夜雨大作，淅沥有声，且闻人语曰：“措措儿引一佳客来，何不见？”其音倍娇脆，全非苗语。已而荧煌内射，似有灯光，乃起自门隙觇之，则又一女，以斗笠罩首，仅及其肩，白身亦无衣袴，惟持尺木若燃藜，冒雨而行。邹不禁骇而且笑，以为身入蛮乡，日与裸裎者相值，情何以堪！正窥望间，此女早至户外，竟排闼入矣。邹不能引避，直前见之。女子凝睇微笑，似有惭色，徐曰：“突如其来，致以丑形为人见，令予颇瑟缩不安。”邹又揖之。女子敛羞为喜，相与席地坐，而置其木于前。

    Explanation

    124

    Zou was deeply moved by her feelings and suspected it was a plan to gain favor. He also did not refuse and sat alone waiting for her. The night had passed, and no sign of the person was seen. Suddenly, a heavy rain began, with a sound of淅沥. Someone said, "Mei Mei brought a good guest, why didn't you see?" The voice was particularly sweet and delicate, not the language of the locals. Soon, light shone from inside, as if there was a lamp. Zou could not help but be startled and laugh, thinking, "Being in a remote place, daily encountering naked people, how can I bear it!" While he was peeking, the girl arrived at the door and pushed it open and entered. Zou could not avoid her and went forward to see her. The girl smiled and looked at him, seeming slightly embarrassed, and said slowly, "I came suddenly, causing me to be seen in my ugly form, making me feel quite uneasy." Zou bowed to her. The girl, suppressing her embarrassment, became happy and sat down together on the ground, placing her wooden stick in front of her.

    125

    邹始得瞻仰庙像，则一女神，亦绝无装饰，披发露体，殊与所见者无异，疑其地从无布帛，不知缝纫，故创教者如此也。因叩女子姓氏并所居何地、所祀何神。女子自言为昔昔，与所谓措措者俱金蚕神之侍女，即祠内所塑者也。蚕有雌雄，此神遂分男女，凡妇人行蛊者，胥是神主之。地名强硐，所居皆生苗，距海只三日程。昨夕所过之津，即所名瘴水也。语之綦详。邹又曰：“卿既苗类，何以能汉语？即措措亦何以然？”昔昔始叹曰：“妾与措措，均非此间人，实中土之所产也。本以须眉来黔贸易，一如君之今日。不意偶中恶蛊，死不甘心，诉之于蚕神，蚕神怜之，俾与措措俱生于苗家，化身为女，又誓不蛊人，蚕神遂收之位下，用为侍从。自蚕神扰于毒龙，数往水府，妾等乃漫无统束，故得游戏至此。”邹因即叩其由。

    Explanation

    125

    When Zhu first saw the temple image, it was a goddess, completely without decoration, with loose hair and exposed body, which was no different from what he had seen before, leading him to doubt that the place had ever had cloth or knew how to sew, hence the founder of the religion represented it this way. He then asked the woman about her name, where she lived, and what god she worshipped. The woman said her name was Xi Xi, and together with the so-called Cuocuo, they were attendants of the Golden Silkworm God, who was the one molded in the temple. Silkworms have male and female, so this god is divided into male and female, and all women who practice witchcraft are under this god's authority. The place is called Qiangdong, and the inhabitants are all indigenous, only a three-day journey from the sea. The ford they crossed last night is called Zang Water. She spoke in great detail. Zhu then asked, "Since you are of the indigenous people, how can you speak Chinese? And Cuocuo, how is it that she can?" Xi Xi sighed and said, "We and Cuocuo are not from here; we are truly from the Central Plains. We originally came to trade in Guizhou with beards, just like you today. Unintentionally, we were caught by a bad spell, and we were unwilling to die. We appealed to the Silkworm God, who pitied us, and made us and Cuocuo be born into the indigenous family, transforming us into women, and we also swore not to harm people with spells. The Silkworm God then took us under his protection and made us his attendants. Since the Silkworm God has been troubled by the venomous dragon and has gone to the Water Palace several times, we have been left without supervision, therefore, we have been able to play around here." Zhu then inquired further into the matter.

    126

    昔昔未及答，而措措早已入室，笑曰：“姊对生人倾吐底里，得毋使客笑我？”昔昔微晒曰：“婢子太不谙事！郎君实有厚福，将藉以返故乡，何讳焉？”二女遂同坐，语邹曰：“毒龙悦色，其体纯阳，知蚕神之美，数数嬲之，食其种类殆尽。蚕神惧，不得已而亲诣海藏，自献其身。缘与龙为欢，不甚主人世事，故蛊毒亦不甚验。”邹因笑曰：“据卿言，神有雌雄，当成配偶。今毒龙恣意贪淫，神之雄者能无怒耶？”昔昔亦笑曰：“郎诚解人，妾辈所虑者正在此。彼一妖，不敢与龙为仇，将以妾等泄其欲。深惧其毒，故避之不遑。”邹曰：“然则奈何？”措措忽正容而答曰：“昨夕渡君，良非无意。妾等实人身，犹可奉君箕帚。今拟相从而归，为君立业。苗疆非善地，愿君毋思深入也。”邹闻之，俯而自汁，本已倦游，且二美同心，爰适所愿，但不免尚怀首鼠。

    Explanation

    126

    When Xixi had not yet responded, Cuocuo had already entered the room, smiling and saying, "Sister, you pour out your innermost thoughts to a stranger—how can it make the guest laugh at me?" Xixi smiled faintly and said, "You are too naive,婢子! Young Master truly has great fortune, which he will use to return to his hometown. Why hide it?" The two women then sat together and spoke to Zou, saying, "The poisonous dragon is pleased by appearance, its body purely masculine. It knows the beauty of the silkworm deity and repeatedly torments it, almost entirely consuming its kind. The silkworm deity fears, and unwillingly personally visits the ocean depths, offering its body. Because it enjoys the dragon's company, it does not control worldly affairs, so the toxicity is also not very effective." Zou then smiled and said, "According to your words, deities have male and female aspects and should form spouses. Now, the poisonous dragon indulges in lust. Can the male deity remain angry?" Xixi also smiled and said, "Young Master truly understands. What we worry about is precisely this. That demon dare not make enemies with the dragon; it will satisfy its desire with us. Deeply fearing its poison, we avoid it without pause." Zou asked, "Then what should we do?" Cuocuo suddenly spoke seriously and said, "Last night, we crossed you, indeed not without intention. We indeed have human bodies and can still serve you with household chores. Now, we plan to follow you back, to establish a career for you. The Miao region is not a good place. I hope you do not think about going deep into it." Zou, upon hearing this, bowed his head and sighed, already tired of traveling, and with the two beauties sharing the same heart, he was about to fulfill his wishes, but still harbored some hesitation.

    127

    无何而天已昧爽，昔昔亟起立曰：“可不可，一言而决断，妾辈亦将束装他往。君勿过于踌躇，翻以误我。”邹实不能舍，遂欣然曰：“诺。”二女皆大笑曰：“君盍少俟，容妾整顿，即可行。”言已偕出。不半时而旋，则俱易男儿装，窄衣秃袖，状类雄苗。相偕出庙门，又各肩一臣簏，顾谓邹曰：“持此去吃着不尽，无事作汗漫游矣。”遂行。仍循故道，以筏渡水，而翼邹登陟，其疾如飞。过岭数十，回顾而太息曰：“神纵知之，亦莫予追也已！”是夕，宿于旅舍，遂成欢好。二女皆柔媚，邹益乐之。数日，经一苗寨，措措与昔昔耳语，俱笑不能仰。邹询之，昔昔曰：“君勿言，今夕当有乐地，且以之泄吾幽愤。”邹益不解。措措乃先行，至一家，门庭轩敞，以为苗之素封。措措以一物置其门槛上，视之，状如蛰虫，蠖屈不伸；少顷，蠕蠕自动，旋即能飞，忽不见。

    Explanation

    127

    Soon after, the sky had grown dim and bright, and Xi Xi quickly stood up and said, "Whether it is permissible or not, a single word will decide it. We, the ladies, will also pack up and go elsewhere. Do not hesitate too much, for it might confuse me." Zou truly could not bear to part with them, so he gladly said, "Yes." The two women both laughed and said, "Why not wait a little, let us get ready, and we can go." They then left together. In no time, they returned, having all changed into male attire, with narrow sleeves and a look resembling young boys. They walked out of the temple gate together, each carrying a bamboo basket, and looked at Zou, saying, "With this, you will have more than enough to eat, and you can wander aimlessly without a care." They continued on their way, still following the old path, crossing the water with a raft, and helping Zou climb the hills, moving as swiftly as flying. After crossing dozens of mountains, they looked back and sighed, "Even if the gods knew, they would not be able to catch us!" That night, they stayed at an inn and became intimate. The two women were both gentle and charming, and Zou found them even more enjoyable. After several days, they passed through a Miao village, where Cuo Cuo and Xi Xi whispered to each other, laughing so hard they couldn't look up. Zou asked them, and Xi Xi said, "Do not say anything, tonight will be a place of joy, and we will use it to vent our hidden anger." Zou did not understand. Cuo Cuo then went ahead, arriving at a house with a spacious courtyard, thinking it belonged to a wealthy Miao family. Cuo Cuo placed an object on the threshold, which looked like a hibernating insect, curled up and unable to stretch. After a short while, it began to move, then flew away, suddenly disappearing.

    128

    邹正愕然，措措又嘱曰：“慎毋泄吾事。从妾指挥，且倍于曩夕之乐。”邹姑颔之。俄而门皆洞开，老者、少者，男妇杂逐而出，见即叩首无算，状甚觳觫。其人皆熟苗，亦尝种蛊之家也。邹顿悟，坦然屹立。昔昔厉声曰：“神实怒汝，可治筵以享吾客，吾当为汝解纷。”其中有类家长者，即应之如响，延入。与二女俱入，中庭设酌，肴核纷陈，阖家奔走恐后。微酣，昔昔命以歌，举不敢辞。少妇数人，联袂而前，虽不解所讴何语，而音韵清婉，颇可悦心。措措又择一笄女，年仅破瓜，戏令去衣把盏。女微有愠色，昔昔即怒而起。主人甚惧，长跪乞哀，昔昔叱之如犬彘，且命曰：“自妪以外，有不去裩者，死不恕！”举室震恐，无敢违。未几，白鸟翣翣，环于座侧，肉屏风不能过焉。措措又牵少女，使于邹侧并坐。邹已醉，不禁其狂，交吻扪身，靡所不至。

    Explanation

    128

    邹正愕然，措措又嘱曰：“慎毋泄吾事。从妾指挥，且倍于曩夕之乐。”邹姑颔之。俄而门皆洞开，老者、少者，男妇杂逐而出，见即叩首无算，状甚觳觫。其人皆熟苗，亦尝种蛊之家也。邹顿悟，坦然屹立。昔昔厉声曰：“神实怒汝，可治筵以享吾客，吾当为汝解纷。”其中有类家长者，即应之如响，延入。与二女俱入，中庭设酌，肴核纷陈，阖家奔走恐后。微酣，昔昔命以歌，举不敢辞。少妇数人，联袂而前，虽不解所讴何语，而音韵清婉，颇可悦心。措措又择一笄女，年仅破瓜，戏令去衣把盏。女微有愠色，昔昔即怒而起。主人甚惧，长跪乞哀，昔昔叱之如犬彘，且命曰：“自妪以外，有不去裩者，死不恕！”举室震恐，无敢违。未几，白鸟翣翣，环于座侧，肉屏风不能过焉。措措又牵少女，使于邹侧并坐。邹已醉，不禁其狂，交吻扪身，靡所不至。

    Translation:
    Zhou Zheng was startled, and Cuocuo further instructed him: "Be careful not to reveal my affairs. Follow my commands, and the pleasure will be even greater than last night's." Zhou Zheng nodded in agreement. Soon, all the doors were wide open, and a mix of elderly and young men and women rushed out, bowing countless times upon seeing them, their expressions filled with fear and trembling.
