These people were all familiar with the customs of the region and had once been involved in the practice of raising insects. Zhou Zheng suddenly understood, standing calmly and firmly. Xi Xi shouted sternly: "The gods are truly angry with you. Prepare a feast to entertain my guests, and I will help you resolve the situation." Among them was someone resembling a patriarch, who immediately responded as if to an echo, inviting them in. Along with the two women, they entered the courtyard, where a feast was set up, with an array of dishes laid out, and the entire household rushed to serve. Slightly intoxicated, Xi Xi commanded them to sing, and none dared to refuse. Several young women linked arms and stepped forward, though their words were incomprehensible, their melodies were clear and graceful, quite pleasing to the heart. Cuocuo then chose a young maiden who had just turned sixteen, playfully ordering her to undress and serve wine. The girl showed a slight expression of displeasure, and Xi Xi immediately stood up in anger. The host was greatly frightened, kneeling long and pleading for mercy, but Xi Xi scolded them like dogs and pigs, and commanded: "Except for the elderly women, anyone who does not remove their undergarments will not be spared!" The entire household was shaken with fear, and none dared to disobey. Soon, white birds flapped their wings and circled around the side of the seats, their feathers so dense that even the flesh screens could not pass through. Cuocuo then pulled another young girl and made her sit beside Zhou Zheng. Zhou Zheng was already drunk, unable to control his wild behavior, kissing and touching her, with no part of her body left untouched.

    129

    措措与昔昔皆鼓掌，遽令荐寝于邹，主人不敢逆。昔、措亦同处室内。待旦而行，其家反拜送之，过于奴隶。出村，昔昔始告邹曰：“此苗蛊数人，致赀巨万，今若此，亦足以报矣。”邹亦嘻笑不绝。自是，每过蛊家，辄嬲之如前。比及楚界，昔昔曰：“今至文物之邦，不可仍前草草。”出箧中异宝，市其一二，得钱万缗，乃买舟治装，二女始易弁而笄；色既殊尤，衣以文锦，又觅婢仆数人，烜赫不殊富室。邹本无妻孥，乃以昔昔主家政，而措措副之，亦彼此无妒心。至其簏中，皆珍异，取以易赀，金钱万计，市产构居，一切资用不乏。邹既饶裕，兼对丽人，不再作远游想。期年，各举一男。邹益欣快。忽一夕，昔昔谓邹曰：“蚕神与龙媾，不胜其淫毒，昨已命尽床第。其雄者虽在，已无能为。自今南适苗土者，可以安枕无患已。”邹姑且信且疑，或以语人，亦无有考据其言者。

    Explanation

    129

    Cuocuo and Xixi both clapped their hands, and immediately ordered to recommend sleep with Zou, the host dared not refuse. Xixi and Cuocuo also stayed in the same room. They waited until dawn to depart, and their family returned to bow and see them off, more than even slaves. After leaving the village, Xixi began to tell Zou, "These several people from the Miao sorcery, they brought wealth in the tens of thousands, now like this, it is also enough to repay." Zou also laughed incessantly. From then on, every time they passed by the sorcery family, they would torment them as before. By the time they reached the border of Chu, Xixi said, "Now that we have arrived in a land of culture and civilization, we cannot still act so carelessly." They took out rare treasures from their chests, sold some of them, and obtained a hundred thousand strings of coins, then bought a boat and prepared their belongings. The two women began to change from hats to hairpins; their appearance was already extraordinary, dressed in brocade, and they also found several servants, shining as brightly as a wealthy household. Zou originally had no wife or children, so he made Xixi the head of the household and Cuocuo the deputy, and there was no jealousy between them. When they opened the chests, they contained all kinds of treasures, which they exchanged for money, amounting to tens of thousands of coins, bought property, and built a residence, with all resources being plentiful. Zou, being well-off and surrounded by beautiful women, no longer had thoughts of faraway travels. After a year, each gave birth to a son. Zou became even more delighted. One evening, Xixi told Zou, "The silkworm goddess and the dragon mated, unable to bear their lust, yesterday they exhausted themselves in bed. Although the male one is still there, it has no power. From now on, those going south to the Miao lands can sleep soundly without worry." Zou half believed and half doubted, and when he told others, no one could verify his words.

    130

    外史氏曰：诸蛊之毒，无逾金蚕，虽苗人之贪狠为之，而济其恶者，实维是物。毒龙具大法力，嬲而奸之，且因以毙之，虽近于附会，洵快人心。况载记云：种蛊之家，其妇女多为神淫。则昔、措之所为，良亦有征可信。若二女之不以苟合，不以色妒，是居浊乱之乡，犹具清明之气，信乎其为不昧本来者矣！

    Explanation

    130

    外史氏曰：诸蛊之毒，无逾金蚕，虽苗人之贪狠为之，而济其恶者，实维是物。毒龙具大法力，嬲而奸之，且因以毙之，虽近于附会，洵快人心。况载记云：种蛊之家，其妇女多为神淫。则昔、措之所为，良亦有征可信。若二女之不以苟合，不以色妒，是居浊乱之乡，犹具清明之气，信乎其为不昧本来者矣！

    Translation:

    The historian said: Among the various poisons of the "蛊," none surpasses that of the golden silkworm, though it was created by the greedy and cruel practices of the Miao people, and it is this very creature that aids in their evil deeds. The poisonous dragon possesses great magical power, tormenting and deceiving them, and ultimately leading to their destruction. Although this story is somewhat speculative, it truly satisfies the heart. Moreover, historical records state: In families that practice "蛊," their women are often sexually violated by spirits. Thus, the actions of "昔" and "措" indeed have evidence and can be trusted. As for the two women who do not engage in casual relationships and do not harbor jealousy over appearance, living in a chaotic and corrupt place, they still possess a clear and pure spirit. Indeed, they have not lost their true nature.

    131

    ○　温玉
    　　孝廉陈凤梧，风流蕴藉人也。祖居绍兴，寄籍宛平。弱冠即掇巍科，人皆以神童目之。家在京城甘水桥，宅后有小楼三楹，即乃尊侍御公休沐处也。孝廉初犹登啸，及公捐馆，不忍手泽之存，遂闭之，已数稔矣。一夕月明，孝廉访戴归甚迟，家人皆已熟寐，惟留小童应门。孝廉入，因贪月色，不忍就枕，乃自检松柴，命童涤器，将以煎茗。俄闻笛声袅袅然，如怨如慕，如泣如诉，耸然听之，宛在楼上。孝廉骇然，毛发尽矗，凛乎不敢独醒，遽归寝。平明即起，将往踪迹，家人知而力阻，弗听。至则癿蛸在户，轩槛积尘。甫启扃，有巨物夺门而去，孝廉股栗。

    Explanation

    131

    ○　温玉
    　　孝廉陈凤梧，风流蕴藉人也。祖居绍兴，寄籍宛平。弱冠即掇巍科，人皆以神童目之。家在京城甘水桥，宅后有小楼三楹，即乃尊侍御公休沐处也。孝廉初犹登啸，及公捐馆，不忍手泽之存，遂闭之，已数稔矣。一夕月明，孝廉访戴归甚迟，家人皆已熟寐，惟留小童应门。孝廉入，因贪月色，不忍就枕，乃自检松柴，命童涤器，将以煎茗。俄闻笛声袅袅然，如怨如慕，如泣如诉，耸然听之，宛在楼上。孝廉骇然，毛发尽矗，凛乎不敢独醒，遽归寝。平明即起，将往踪迹，家人知而力阻，弗听。至则蟏蛸在户，轩槛积尘。甫启扃，有巨物夺门而去，孝廉股栗。

    ○　温玉
    　　Scholar-official Chen Fengwu, a man of elegance and refinement, was born in Shaoxing but registered in Wuan. At the age of twenty, he achieved high ranks in the imperial examinations, and everyone regarded him as a child prodigy. His family lived in the Ganshui Bridge area of the capital, and behind his residence stood a small pavilion with three rooms, which was the place where his father, the High Court Official, rested during his holidays. Initially, the scholar-official enjoyed the solitude of the pavilion, but after his father's passing, he could not bear the lingering presence of his father's touch and closed it off, which remained sealed for several years. One night, under the bright moonlight, the scholar-official returned very late from visiting Dai. All the family members were asleep, and only a young servant was left to answer the door. Upon entering, captivated by the moonlight, he decided not to go to bed and instead gathered pine wood, instructed the young servant to clean the utensils, and prepared to brew tea. Suddenly, he heard a flute playing, which was mournful and longing, and it seemed to be coming from upstairs. The scholar-official was so startled that his hair stood on end, and he felt a chill that made him不敢独醒 (not dare to wake up alone), so he quickly went back to sleep. The next morning, he woke up early and decided to investigate the source of the flute sound, but his family tried to stop him, but he did not listen. When he arrived, he found spiders on the window and dust on the railings. As soon as he opened the door, a large object rushed out, and the scholar-official trembled with fear.

    132

    仰视之，六翮凌空，则雕也。盖公没五年，此楼未辟，物遂巢其中。然门窗封识，亦不知其何自而入，良足诧异。孝廉神少定，然后入观典册，茫无人迹，又绝无可怪，惟怆怀洒泪而已，乃仍阖之而归。至夜静伺之，漏下三鼓，其声又作。谛听焉，音甚靡曼，则非复向之呜咽者矣。翌日，孝廉具衣冠，敬诣楼下，祝曰：“仙乎！鬼乎！何骇人听闻至此也？倘有妙音，请容面领，毋过吝。”言已返室，察头即有速客柬。启视之，字迹婉丽，一则温玉，一则柔娘，益皆闺中女字也。乃大骇，以问家人，举茫然莫知所自。阖室惊疑，靡不惴惴。及昏，孝廉欲赴其约，太夫人惧，诃止之。

    Explanation

    132

    Looking up, he saw the six feathers piercing the sky, and it was an eagle. Five years after Master Gai's death, this building remained unused, and the creature had nested there. However, the doors and windows were sealed, and no one knew how it had entered, which was indeed quite surprising. The scholar-official calmed his spirit slightly, then entered to examine the records and documents. There was no trace of human presence, and nothing else was remarkable; he was only filled with sorrow and shed tears, and then returned home, closing the doors behind him. By night, he quietly waited, and at the third drum strike, the sound came again. Listening carefully, the sound was very delicate and soft, not the mournful wailing of before. The next day, the scholar-official put on his formal attire and respectfully went to the foot of the building, saying, "Spirit? Ghost? Why is it so shocking to hear about? If there are any beautiful sounds, please allow me to experience them, do not be overly stingy." After speaking, he returned to his room, and upon checking, there was a note from a guest. Upon opening it, the handwriting was graceful and beautiful, one from Wen Yu and another from Rou Nang, all characters from the inner chambers. He was greatly alarmed and asked his family, but everyone was confused and did not know where it came from. The whole household was surprised and suspicious, no one was not anxious. By evening, the scholar-official wanted to meet the invitation, but his mother was afraid and scolded him to stop.

    133

    因假寐，俟人俱寝，悄然独往。未至，早有小青衣，妖冶非常，迎于门际，笑曰：“知音人诚巨胆哉？二位娘子固俟之久矣。”遂导之同行。旋闻椒兰馥郁，来自半天。遥睇楼头，二美軃袖凭栏，意似徙倚无聊。月下遇之，香雾云鬟，清辉玉臂，不欲垂怜而不能也。因拾级而登，直前揖曰：“庸耳俗肠，未娴音律，荷蒙召以雅集，能无愧乎？”一美人微哂曰：“既非顾曲周郎，奈何谆请妙部？此言亦谁能信之？”语次，睨其貌，一则玉润珠圆，嫣然百媚；一则花愁柳怨，笑可倾城。均衣五铢衣，束百宝裙，环佩珊珊，实天人也。孝廉自诧为奇遇，因曰：“客夕清音，遥聆之若出两人，而各擅所长。

    Explanation

    133

    Taking a brief nap, he waited until everyone had fallen asleep, then quietly went alone. Before he arrived, a young woman dressed in green, extremely alluring, was already there, greeting him at the door with a smile. "A true friend indeed has great courage!" she said. "The two ladies have been waiting for you for a long time." She then led him inside. Soon, he smelled the fragrant aroma of pepper and orchid, coming from high above. Looking up to the top of the building, he saw two beautiful women with their sleeves hanging down, leaning on the railing, seeming aimless and bored. Under the moonlight, he met them, their hair like clouds in fragrant mist, their arms glowing like jade. He did not want to show pity but could not help it. He climbed the stairs step by step, went forward and bowed, saying, "I have common ears and a vulgar heart, not skilled in music. Grateful to be honored with this refined gathering, how could I not feel ashamed?" One of the beauties smiled slightly and said, "Not being a Zhou Yu who appreciates music, why were you repeatedly invited to the best performers? Who would believe these words?" As they spoke, he glanced at their appearance: one was as smooth as jade and round as pearls, gracefully charming in every way; the other had a captivating smile, like flowers and willows were sad and resentful. Both were wearing a five-zhu robe and a hundred-jewel skirt, their ornamental pendants jingling, truly like heavenly beings. The scholar-official was surprised by the rare encounter and said, "The clear and melodious sounds heard from afar seemed to come from two people, each excelling in their own strengths."

    134

    愿即于此赐教，使得餍饫，其可欤？”其未言者亦哂曰：“匆遽若此，岂登徒子之床头犹有所系恋耶？”乃出袖中玉笛为奏一曲，即前一夕所闻者。恍乎若鹤之清唳，雁之哀鸣，凄凄然使人泪下。未阕，彼美一人即举翠袂挥之曰：“妹勿作此断肠声，反令佳客不快。”遂命青衣以笙来，倚槛和之，声如和鸣之凤，共哕之鸾，哀者以喜，怨者以舒，盖即隔夜所聆者，而缠绵过之矣。曲既终，乃展姓氏，始识吹笙者即温玉，而柔娘则作折柳之曲者也。孝廉与温玉晤语，谈及古今声伎，无不叩之即鸣。独柔娘低鬟掩袂，对月无言，一似重有忧者。孝廉疑而诘之，玉曰：“痴妮子故常作此态，君勿深怪可也。

    Explanation

    134

    "I would be grateful to receive your guidance here, so that I may fully indulge in learning. Would that be acceptable?" Another person who had not spoken before laughed and said, "Why are you in such a hurry? Could it be that you still have some attachment to the bed of a certain person?" She then took out a jade flute from her sleeve and played a tune, which was the same as the one heard the previous night. The sound was clear and mournful, like the cry of a crane and the lament of a goose, evoking tears. Before the piece ended, a beautiful woman raised her jade sleeve and said, "Do not play such a heart-wrenching tune, for it will make our esteemed guests uncomfortable." She then ordered a servant girl to bring a sheng (a type of mouth organ), and together they played by the railing, creating a harmonious sound like the songs of phoenixes and peacocks. The sad turned to joy, and the resentful became more at ease. This was the same melody heard the previous night, but even more emotionally engaging. After the piece finished, they revealed their names, and it was discovered that the sheng player was Wen Yu, while Rou娘 was the one who played the 'Farewell to the Willow' tune. The scholar-official conversed with Wen Yu, discussing various aspects of music and entertainment from ancient and modern times, and every topic they touched upon elicited a response. However, Rou娘 lowered her head and covered her sleeves, silent under the moonlight, as if deeply troubled. The scholar-official, puzzled, asked her about it, and Wen Yu replied, "This foolish girl often acts this way. Please do not take it too seriously."

    135

    ”夜既半，青衣促归，玉顾孝廉曰：“有客无酒，良夜不欢。君能作东道主，当过萧斋奉访耳。”孝廉敬诺，约以明夕。遂下胡梯，冉冉过楼东而去，不知其所往。孝廉亦潜归卧室，母妻皆不觉。早起，讳不宣言。旁午，入斋伪作拈毫状。至暮，托言文会未竣，遂不入内。命僮襆被来，设榻布衾，且密置酒馔，秉烛俟之，惟虞其爽约。直至漏下二鼓，双美偕来，笑语生春，非复日前之羞涩矣。入室杂坐，履舄交加。孝廉将自起暖酒，温玉以目视青衣曰：“不可重劳主人。”因命以代之。酒甫三行，少酣春色，孝廉起，请尽昨夕未尽之妙。玉辞曰：“耳目较近，鸣则惊人。”遂不复作。

    Explanation

    135

    By midnight, the servant urged the scholar to return. Wen Yu glanced at the scholar and said, "With guests but no wine, a good night is not enjoyable. If you can be the host, I shall visit your humble study tomorrow." The scholar respectfully agreed, promising to meet the next day. He then descended the ladder, slowly passing by the eastern side of the building and disappearing, his destination unknown. The scholar also secretly returned to his bedroom, and neither his mother nor his wife noticed. In the morning, he avoided mentioning it. At noon, he entered his study, pretending to write. By evening, he excused himself by saying the literary gathering was not yet over and did not enter the inner chambers. He ordered the servant to pack bedding and set up a bed with a cloth quilt, and secretly placed wine and food, holding a candle while waiting for them, only fearing he might break the appointment. It was not until two drums after midnight that the two beauties arrived together, laughing and talking, bringing a spring-like warmth, no longer shy as before. They entered the room and sat in a mixed manner, their shoes overlapping. The scholar was about to get up to warm the wine when Wen Yu looked at the servant with a glance and said, "We should not burden the host again." She then ordered the servant to do it instead. After a few rounds of wine, the scholar suggested they continue what they started the previous night. Wen Yu declined, saying, "If we are closer, I will be more impressive." They did not engage in further activities.

    136

    惟浮白欢饮，射复藏钩，以祈尔爵而已。无何，玉山尽颓，眉目荡然。玉乃离席谓柔曰：“妹盍留此，予且归。”柔作羞态曰：“侬不惯与生人卧，此事终须逊姊。”玉笑曰：“汝先吹笛，以邀风月，何人乃敢僭耶？”遂凭青衣肩，踉跄而返。孝廉与柔解衣，柔低曰：“妾尚葳蕤，郎君幸勿狂暴也。”孝廉笑曰：“敬如卿命。”既合，柔不胜凿枘之苦，猩血流丹，娇啼宛转。孝廉熟玩之，肌不丰而若无骨，态不胜而若争妍，皀席之间，极人世之乐焉。诘旦，柔揽衣先起，谓孝廉曰：“郎君尚有新人，妾请明日来。”遂跚跚自去。孝廉因诈称抱恙，不返内寝，太夫人与其细君，咸来问视。

    Explanation

    136

    Only drinking freely and joyfully, playing games of guessing hidden objects and playing with hooks, wishing you good fortune. Soon, the jade mountain collapsed completely, and the person's appearance was disarrayed. The person left the seat and said to Rou, "Why not stay here, I will go." Rou made a shy expression and said, "I am not used to sleeping with strangers, this matter must be conceded to the elder sister." The person laughed and said, "You blew the flute first to invite the wind and moon, who would dare to overstep?" And leaned on the shoulder of the attendant, staggering back. The scholar-official and Rou undressed, and Rou whispered, "I am still fresh and fragrant, please do not be rough, sir." The scholar-official laughed and said, "I will follow your command." After they joined, Rou was unable to bear the discomfort of the fit, her blood flowed red, and she cried softly. The scholar-official enjoyed her, her body was not full but seemed to have no bones, her appearance was not strong but seemed to compete in beauty, on the bed, experiencing the utmost joy of the world. The next day, Rou pulled on her clothes and got up first, and said to the scholar-official, "You still have a new person, I will come tomorrow." And left on her own. The scholar-official pretended to be ill, did not return to the private chambers, and the mother-in-law and the wife all came to inquire and visit.

    137

    孝廉以心神不爽，愿屏烦嚣，谢去之，人故莫之疑也。将夜，仍藏斗酒，以待玉来。跂望者久之，宵深果至，则仅玉一人，并青衣亦不复随。灯下促膝，觥筹交错，玉之倜傥尤胜于柔。酒未酣，孝廉之情已荡，促之就寝。玉笑而起曰：“清狂如此，宜其渴病难医也。”因而银釭笑吹，罗襦偷解，狎之。虽故处子，而枕席之情，颇为流动，抑且温软如拥絮，柔腻若涂脂，容则满月生辉，态亦名花敛蕊。甫两夕，而二难并荐。欢娱已久，曙色盈眸，玉将起，乃语孝廉曰：“两斧伐孤木，君知之乎？妾去而妹已来，妹行而妾又至，君以一身当之，鲜不败也。为君计，今且就内寝，约五夕，妾等一会。

    Explanation

    137

    The person of integrity, feeling unwell in mind and spirit, wished to avoid the noise and disturbance of the world and declined the company of others, and people did not doubt him. As night fell, he still hid a jug of wine, waiting for Yu to come. He waited anxiously for a long time, and indeed she arrived deep into the night, only Yu alone, and her maid did not follow. Sitting close together by the lamp, wine cups and chopsticks crisscrossing, Yu's free-spirited nature was even more so than gentle. Before the wine was fully drunk, the feelings of the person of integrity had already been stirred, and he urged her to go to bed. Yu laughed and got up, saying, "So purely wild, it is appropriate that his thirst for such experiences is hard to cure." She then laughingly blew out the silver lamp and secretly undressed her silk robe, engaging in intimate relations. Although she was a virgin, the feelings on the bed were quite varied, and she was also warm and soft like a blanket, soft and smooth like butter, her appearance shone like a full moon, and her demeanor was like a famous flower closing its petals. Just two nights, and both difficulties were presented together. Having enjoyed for a long time, the dawn light filled his eyes, and Yu was about to get up, then said to the person of integrity, "Two axes cutting a single tree, do you know this? I leave and my sister comes, my sister leaves and I come again, you bear it alone, it is rare not to fail. For your sake, for now, go to the inner room, for about five nights, we will meet again."

    138

    如此则君之精力已复，即妾等之情好，不更能于永固乎？”孝廉感其爱己深，颔之。玉既去，孝廉亦起，将返内室而恍惚如有失。良久，乃始能记忆，忾然曰：“柔约今夕来，我何可负渠也？”因复卧。少顷，母妻踵至，仍以病为辞，留宿于外。然心为酒色所蛊，饮食亦锐减，人遂深信共疾，太夫人将为延医，坚谢不愿。柔至夜复来，缱绻倍胜于前，昔犹娇而怯，今则柔且媚矣。临别问：“玉姊来否？”孝廉以首示之意，柔辗然颇有喜色。及夕复至，笑语曰：“妾今来代彼当夕矣！”孝廉因告以五夕之约。柔忽娇嗔曰：“妖婢竟乔献殷勤耶？妾告君，渠非我仙侣，实狐也。必别有桑濮行，散以此绐君。

    138

    In this way, your energy and strength have been restored, and our affection, cannot it be made even more enduring?" The scholar-official felt deeply loved by her and nodded in agreement. After Yu left, the scholar-official also got up, intending to return to his inner chamber, but he felt as if something was lost. Only then could he remember, and he sighed, saying, "Rouyu came tonight; how could I betray her?" Then he lay down again. Soon after, his mother and wife followed, still using illness as an excuse, staying overnight outside. However, his heart was bewitched by wine and beauty, and his food intake also sharply decreased. People began to deeply believe that he was sick, and Grandmother was going to call in a doctor, but he firmly refused. Rouyu came again at night, and the affection was even more intense than before. Before she was shy and timid, now she is gentle and charming. Just before leaving, she asked, "Has Yu come?" The scholar-official nodded to indicate, and she smiled with a hint of joy. That night she came again, saying with a smile, "I am here to take her place tonight." The scholar-official then told her about the five-night agreement. Suddenly, Rouyu pouted and said, "The sly maid is actually showing off her attentiveness? I tell you, she is not my immortal companion; she is actually a fox. She must have other improper conduct, and she is deceiving you with this."

    139

    不然，岂有相爱而能遽舍者？”言已，欢洽如常。将去且嘱曰：“君勿漏言，反似妾妒也。”至夕，玉仍杳然，孝廉亦疑之，而不知其为逊柔故。自是柔之至无虚夕，而孝廉遂憔悴不胜矣。迨至旬终，玉始夜至，入室即惊曰：“君岂未悬此榻耶？不然，何形神之惫也？”孝廉以爱柔故，坚讳之。及寝，玉觉有异，益诘之。乃言柔来太屡，且渠谓若为狐，嘱予勿泄。玉闻之大恚曰：“误与鬼子同事，几予我以杀郎名。渠乃某家小女，殁已多年，当明末时闯逆寇都自缢而死者，因乱中，槁葬于君之后楼下。尊大人在日，福德俱隆，渠乃深自潜藏。今以楼虚，遂据之。

    Explanation

    139

    If love exists, how could there be immediate separation? After saying this, everything remained as harmonious as usual. As she was about to leave, she warned, "Do not reveal this, it would seem like jealousy on my part." By evening, Jade was still elusive, and the scholar was also suspicious but did not know the reason was due to the shyness and gentleness of the other character. Since then, the presence of the gentle one was constant without a single night passing, and the scholar became emaciated and unable to bear it. By the end of the week, Jade finally arrived at night. Upon entering the room, she was surprised and asked, "Have you not been using this bed? Otherwise, how could you be so exhausted?" The scholar, loving the gentle one, firmly kept it secret. When they went to sleep, Jade sensed something unusual and asked further. She then said that the gentle one came too frequently, and she told her that the scholar was a fox, and instructed her not to reveal it. Jade heard this and became very angry, saying, "I mistakenly associated with a ghost, almost giving the ghost the name of a murderer. She was a young girl from a certain family who has been dead for many years. During the chaos of the late Ming period, she hanged herself, and was hastily buried under the scholar's house. Your father, while alive, had great virtue and fortune, and she deeply hid herself. Now that the building is empty, she has taken up residence there."

    140

    妾与之音律相知，以时往来，故得相偕见君也。”已而笑曰：“渠亦为情所皁耳！虽然，郎君则枯瘠矣。俟明夕来，妾当为君止之。”鸡鸣，遂去。孝廉确知为鬼狐，始悚惧欲徙，而惭赧未及言。是夜柔与玉果偕来，玉诋柔曰：“妹谓我为狐，妹独非鬼耶？何事人以色，而不爱人以德也。”柔赧然，无辞以对。玉剌剌不休，柔则低蛾敛黛，愁态可掬。盖柔自遇孝廉，不再如向之幽怨，今复见之，不觉悱恻，乃解之曰：“渠实爱我，子何相尤之深耶？”玉艴然，红涨于面曰：“君既左袒，妾不能与人分谤也。”拂衣迳出。柔虽仍留，亦不尽欢而罢。阅一日，而孝廉之真疾作，形色支离，神气困惫。

    Explanation

    140

    I have a rapport with him in music and rhythm, and we visit each other at times, so we were able to meet you together. She then laughed and said, "He is also troubled by emotions! However, the scholar-official is emaciated. Wait until tomorrow night, and I will stop it for you." At dawn, she left. The scholar-official definitely knew they were ghosts and fox spirits, and he became terrified and wanted to move away, but he was embarrassed and didn't have time to speak. That night, Rou and Yugu came together. Yugu criticized Rou, saying, "You call me a fox, but aren't you a ghost yourself? Why do you value people by their appearance rather than their virtue?" Rou blushed and was unable to respond. Yugu spoke incessantly, while Rou lowered her eyebrows and furrowed her brows, showing a look of sorrow that was easily visible. Since meeting the scholar-official, Rou no longer felt the previous sorrow and resentment, but now seeing him again, she couldn't help but feel a tender and melancholic emotion. She explained, "He truly loves me, why do you blame me so much?" Yugu became angry and said, "Since you have taken sides, I cannot share the blame with others." She brushed off her clothes and left directly. Although Rou stayed, she did not fully enjoy the meeting and ended it. After one day, the scholar-official's real illness began, his appearance disheveled, and his spirit and energy exhausted.

    141

    太夫人坚徙之内寝，而柔与玉俱绝迹。病既弥留，阖家忧戚。孝廉正当沈顿，忽梦玉挥泪而来，语之曰：“君不纳妾言，几殆矣。然禄籍固未绝也，妾为君疾采药嵩山，触怒岳神，堕崖而死。今与柔妹同在冥途，可胜慨哉！”言之惨然，孝廉亦大恸。玉又曰：“某医精于长桑，亟延之，二竖子或可驱也。”言已，孝廉即惊觉，如其所教，物色之，果得和缓。委以治疗，病乃痊。孝廉既愈，恒感玉之德，而悲其死之惨，且更思柔不置也。独处辄冀其来，竞杳然。又明年，孝廉之内人以产厄卒。鳏居岑寂，益思念之。

    Explanation

    141

    The mother-in-law firmly moved them to the inner chambers, while both women, Ruo and Yu, disappeared from sight. As the illness persisted, the entire family was filled with sorrow. The scholar-official, in the depths of his depression, suddenly had a dream where Yu appeared weeping, saying to him, "You did not heed my words, and nearly perished. However, your official lineage remains unbroken. I gathered medicinal herbs for you on Mount Song, offended the mountain god, and fell off a cliff, dying. Now, I am with Ruo, my sister, in the afterlife, how can I not feel profound sorrow!" Her words were heartrending, and the scholar-official was deeply moved. Yu then said, "There is a physician who is skilled in the methods of Chang Sang; urgently invite him, and perhaps he can drive away the illness." After speaking, the scholar-official awoke, and followed her advice, seeking out the physician, who turned out to be He Huan. He entrusted him with the treatment, and the illness was cured. After recovering, the scholar-official always felt grateful for Yu's virtue, mourned her tragic death, and constantly thought of her without ceasing. Whenever he was alone, he hoped for her return, but there was no sign of her. The following year, his wife died in childbirth. Left alone in solitude, he thought of her even more.

    142

    长夜凄清，寝不能寐，倏见向之青衣，癷然径入，告孝廉曰：“玉娘子传语郎君，三日后相俟于门外，见有殡女者，如是如是，则鸾胶可续也。”孝廉诘其详，答曰：“娘子死，诉之岳帝，廉得其实，怜娘子之节，许令更生。因与君旧缘未断，故将夺舍复圆耳。”孝廉因并询柔之近况，青衣曰：“彼愧见郎君，且冥司发牒，行将往生他处矣。”孝廉方将研诘，遽敛其袂而退。越三朝，俟之门外，果有扶柩而过者，覆以红毹，送者皆衣青衣，绝无缟素。察其女也，逆而语之曰：“人故未死，何葬耶？”众愕然，而柩重遂不能举。旋闻棺中嘤咛而言曰：“我已活，将复闷死矣！”乃大惊。女之父为某部郎，止此弱息，既笄而殀，深痛之，殁不忍殓，冀其复生。今闻此，喜出望外，不以为异也，第恨通衢无可止之所。

    Explanation

    142

    A Long Night of Desolation

    The night was long and desolate, and the scholar-official could not sleep. Suddenly, he saw the former green-clad servant stealthily enter and deliver a message: "Madam Yu says to the young master, meet me outside the door three days later. If you see a funeral procession for a woman, do it like this, do it like this, and the phoenix glue can be reconnected." The scholar-official asked for details, and the servant replied: "Madam Yu died and appealed to the Lord of Mount Hua. The Lord discovered the truth and pitied Madam Yu's virtue, allowing her to be reborn. Since the old bond between you and her has not been severed, she will take over her body and reunite with you." The scholar-official then asked about the condition of another woman, and the servant said: "She is ashamed to see the young master, and the underworld has issued a decree, so she will soon be reborn elsewhere." The scholar-official was about to ask more questions, but the servant quickly withdrew.

    Three days later, the scholar-official waited outside the door, and indeed, a funeral procession passed by, covering the coffin with a red cloth. The mourners were all dressed in green, and there was no one wearing white mourning clothes. Recognizing the woman, the scholar-official confronted her and said: "You are still alive, why are you being buried?" The crowd was startled, and the coffin was too heavy to lift. Suddenly, a voice from inside the coffin said, "I am alive again, but I will suffocate if not helped!" The scholar-official was greatly surprised.

    The woman's father was a government official, and he had only one daughter who died young. He was deeply grieved and did not want to bury her, hoping she would come back to life. Upon hearing this, he was overjoyed beyond expectation, not considering it strange, but only regretting that there was no place to stop in the busy street.

    143

    正仓惶间，柩中呼声倍急，孝廉乃前致词曰：“若无地息肩乎？此大好事，敝庐固可也。”部郎大喜，深感之，遂舁女榇入孝廉之门。家人咸惊怪，孝廉坚执其可。甫开棺，而女已瞿然起矣。窃睨之，貌甚姣好，虽瘦怯，与玉无殊。部郎又请假外舍，俾其小憩，孝廉无难色，径启书斋，命众扶入。部郎益衔其德，展问殷勤，知为世家子，且登桂榜，顿思以女字之，而恐其有偶。私询僮仆，知方鼓盆，益大悦。因变薤露之歌，为伐柯之咏，而凤卜以谐。孝廉喜，治筵相款，且命焚其榇于郭，以彰其奇，观者如堵焉。至暮，以香车送女归，择吉纳采，而旧弦复续。

    Explanation

    143

    In a state of panic, the coffin suddenly emitted louder cries. The scholar stepped forward and addressed the coffin, saying, "If there is no place for you to rest your shoulders? This is a great opportunity, my humble dwelling is certainly suitable." The deputy minister was greatly pleased and deeply grateful, so he ordered the coffin of the girl to be carried into the scholar's home. The family all were surprised and puzzled, but the scholar firmly insisted that it was possible. As soon as the coffin was opened, the girl suddenly got up. Peeking at her, she looked very beautiful, though she was thin and timid, she was no different from a jade. The deputy minister again took leave and went to the outer room to let her take a short rest. The scholar showed no difficulty, directly opened the study, and ordered everyone to help her in. The deputy minister was even more grateful for his kindness, asked about her condition with great care, and learned that she was a daughter of a prominent family and had passed the imperial examination, so he suddenly thought of marrying her off, but was afraid she might already be married. He privately asked the servants, and learned that she had just lost her husband, which made him even happier. He then changed the song of 'Xie Lu' into a song of 'Fakao', and the phoenix divination was favorable. The scholar was happy, prepared a feast to entertain, and ordered the coffin to be burned at the city gate to show its extraordinariness, and the spectators were like a wall. By dusk, he sent the girl back in a fragrant carriage, chose a good day to accept the betrothal gifts, and the old strings were renewed.

    144

    迨至亲迎之夕，甫启袱，女即凝睇如旧相识，而未敢遽泄。及宵，乃欷歔言曰：“妾为君两夕之欢，犯至险而陨越，君亦怜之乎？”孝廉曰：“然。藏之寸心，无时可忘，以子之灵，固早鉴之矣。”玉笑曰：“使柔妹更生，君视之当胜妾什倍。”孝廉亦笑曰：“子未忘情已事耶？”遂相携就寝。玉曰：“两以女身事君，人之受屈惟一，妾之痛楚有二矣。”既交，渥丹浃席，女之畏缩倍于前，而款洽则无异于旧焉。夙兴即起，谓孝廉曰：“妾今日乃可庙见矣。以前真所谓‘妾身未分明，何以见姑嫜’也。”乃盥濯入朝，太夫人因其娩婉，深喜之。嗣是燕尔情浓，夕无虚度，孝廉戏诘之曰：“若不虞我再病耶？”玉赧颜答曰：“今非昔比也。

    Explanation

    144

    When the time came for the wedding ceremony, as soon as the wrapping was opened, the bride gazed at him with a look of familiarity, as if meeting an old acquaintance, yet she hesitated to reveal her identity. By nightfall, she sighed and said, "For the two nights of joy I shared with you, I risked great danger and nearly perished. Do you still care for me?" The Scholar replied, "Indeed. I have kept you in my heart, never forgetting you. By your spirit, I have known this long ago." The bride smiled and said, "If I were reborn, you would find me even more desirable than I am now." The Scholar laughed and said, "Have you not forgotten the past?" They then went to bed together. The bride said, "I have served you twice as a woman, and though people may suffer only once, my pain has been twofold." After they were intimate, her face was flushed with pleasure, and though she was more hesitant than before, their affection remained as it was. Early the next morning, she rose and said to the Scholar, "I can now be introduced to the ancestors at the temple. Before, I truly felt as though my identity was not clear, and I did not know how to face my in-laws." She then washed and prepared to enter the temple, where her mother-in-law was deeply pleased by her grace and humility. From then on, their love deepened, and their evenings were no longer wasted. The Scholar playfully asked her, "Did you not expect me to fall ill again?" The bride blushed and replied, "Things are different now."

    145

    鬼与狐皆异类，五夕且不堪，况源源而来乎？今以人身侍巾栉，则坎离交济，虽少过不伤也。”孝廉倍颔其论。一夕，忽语孝廉曰：“曩者之谶验矣。妾昨梦柔来诀别，渠羞与郎见，故命妾报知，渠已托生某家。十五年后，可至广陵相访也。”孝廉时已得陇，不复望蜀，惟诘曰：“自经者亦能转轮耶？”答曰：“渠有德而无罪，且幽沉已久，合生男身。因思君故，特恳为女耳。”孝廉感其言，然亦姑置之。后孝廉屡踬春闱，竟以明经授邑宰。初任新蔡，有政声，骤擢秦州牧，十年不迁。俄以卓异升安庆守，携眷渡淮。当过邗沟，固已十有五稔矣。

    Explanation

    145

    Ghosts and foxes are all different kinds, and even five nights cannot bear their presence, let alone a continuous stream of them! If they serve in a human capacity, tending to the hairpin and comb, then the balance of yin and yang will be harmonized, and even minor faults will not cause harm." The scholar nodded in agreement with this reasoning. One night, the ghost suddenly told the scholar, "The prophecy I mentioned before has come true. Last night, I dreamed that Lady Rou came to bid farewell. She was ashamed to see you, so she asked me to inform you that she has been reborn in a certain family. In fifteen years, you may visit her in Guangling." The scholar, who had already achieved his goals, no longer aspired for more, and merely asked, "Can those who have committed suicide also be reborn?" The ghost replied, "She has virtue but no sin, and has been in the underworld for a long time. It is fitting for her to be reborn as a man. But because she thinks of you, she especially requests to be reborn as a woman." The scholar was moved by her words but set the matter aside. Later, the scholar failed repeatedly in the imperial examinations and eventually received a degree in Confucian classics, becoming a county magistrate. He first served in Xincai, where he earned a good reputation, and was quickly promoted to governor of Qin Prefecture, remaining there for ten years without further promotion. Soon, he was promoted to governor of Anqing due to outstanding performance, and he traveled with his family across the Huai River. When he passed through the Hangou Canal, fifteen years had already passed.

    146

    玉谓之曰：“苎萝村即此已是，君岂忘扬州之梦耶？”时玉已两索皆男，孝廉雅不欲，因玉固请，乃从之。为留十日，大索于瘦马家，玉皆曰非。濒行矣，有贫妪携小女郎，乞食于驿卒。玉适从太夫人游平山堂归，见之，曰：“是已。”即告孝廉，托言购婢，遂得之。玉携之归室，泫然曰：“妹何一寒至此？”女不解所谓，而星眸炯炯然，亦注视不移。玉亲为盥栉，眉目焕然。询其年，果十五，因仍以柔字之，不欲没其旧也。及夜定情，孝廉试其啼笑，宛然一柔，益狂喜，谓玉言不谬。履数任后，玉虽主中馈，而视柔若匹敌，每让一夕曰：“吾以补十五年之缺欠也。”期年，柔亦诞一子。未几，孝廉以内艰归，遂不出仕，遨游于温柔之乡以终老。

    Explanation

    146

    Jade said to him, "The village of Zhu Lu is right here, have you forgotten the dream of Yangzhou?" At that time, Jade had already two sons, and the scholar-official was naturally reluctant. Because Jade insisted firmly, he finally agreed. He stayed for ten days, searching extensively in the homes of thin horses, but Jade said they were not the ones. As he was about to leave, there was a poor old woman with a young girl, begging food from the station staff. Jade had just returned from a trip with his mother to Ping Shan Hall. Seeing her, he said, "This is the one." He immediately told the scholar-official, claiming to buy a maid, and thus obtained her. Jade brought her back to the room, saying tearfully, "Why are you so cold?" The girl did not understand what he meant, but her eyes were bright and shining, and she stared fixedly. Jade personally washed and combed her, and her eyebrows and eyes looked refreshed. He asked her age, and she was indeed fifteen. He still called her "Rou," not wanting to erase her old name. At night, they consummated their relationship. The scholar-official tested her laughter and tears, and she was exactly like Rou. He became even more ecstatic, saying that Jade's words were not wrong. After serving in several positions, Jade was in charge of the kitchen, but he treated Rou as an equal, often saying, "Let me make up for fifteen years of absence." After a year, Rou also gave birth to a son. Soon after, the scholar-official returned home due to mourning, and he then did not take up office. He wandered in the land of comfort until his old age.

    147

    外史氏曰：玉以温名，取其德也。女字以此，殆无愧乎？盖妇之美德，不过曰不妒不淫而已。女让当夕，不妒也。约以五夕，不淫也。且能为夫子死，犹欲冀夫子生。其贤如此，岳帝虽震怒，亦应霁威矣。卒之碎者复全，分者复合，温其如玉，不致徒悼于重泉，宜也。若柔娘，独无可取，惟愿为女一节，聊可解嘲。然非温玉之淑，又乌能附骥尾以传也哉？
    　　随园老人曰：人谓似聊斋《莲香传》，余亦谓似聊斋《莲香传》。然非鬼狐之迹略同，人亦乌从寻针线迹耶？事奇文奇，安在莲香后不可复有温玉？

    Explanation

    147

    The Commentary of the Outer Historian says: Jade is named for its warmth, taking its virtue. Is there any shame in calling this woman by such a name? The virtues of a good wife are nothing more than not being jealous and not being promiscuous. The woman let her turn come, showing she was not jealous. She agreed to wait for five nights, showing she was not promiscuous. Moreover, she could die for her husband and still hoped for his survival. Her virtue is like this, so even if the Lord of Mount Hua is angry, he should also calm down. In the end, the broken ones are restored, the separated ones reunite, and she is as warm as jade, not to grieve in vain in the netherworld, which is appropriate. If it comes to Rou Niang, she alone has no redeeming qualities, only wishing to be as virtuous as the woman, one can somewhat alleviate mockery. Without the virtue of Wen Yu, how could one attach oneself to the tail of a swift horse and be known?

    The Old Man of the Following Garden says: People say it is similar to the 'Lian Xiang' in Liaozhai, and I also say it is similar to the 'Lian Xiang' in Liaozhai. However, it is not because the traces of ghosts and foxes are roughly the same; how could people find the traces of needle and thread? The story is strange and the writing is strange, why can't there be Wen Yu after Lian Xiang?

    148

    ○　睡姬
    　　某贵官，娶一美妾，以色专房。而其性善睡，每每至旦弗觉，即当白昼之中，亦如三眠之柳。初未知其有异也，一日，立闲阶似与人偶语，入闺即就枕，竟至三日弗寤，贵官始疑而询之。初不肯言，及诘之再三，甫自述曰：“妾隶芙蓉城主，为讴者。以过见谪，虽居人世，而梦寐中时往供役，冀赎前愆，仍居仙境。昨为石君诞日，群仙毕集，妾以职司，不克遄返，以致见疑于公，恕之幸甚！”贵官殊不信，因曰：“若能导我一游，以实此言，则可逭。否则，敲扑不远矣！”言之色怒。妾竟坦然曰：“妾处此世，如尘栖弱草，去留惟公，死生亦惟公，决不敢以天上琼楼，为人间邀宠之地也。”贵官甚恚其言，而因爱不能割，随亦置之。居数年，妾忽病，渐且不起。贵官怜之，时就床头问视，妾忽泫然曰：“感公厚爱，诚未易报。昔公欲从妾一游芙蓉城阙，今其时矣。盍就良夜以行，以偿公之夙愿。

    Explanation

    148

    Sleeping Beauty

    A certain high-ranking official married a beautiful concubine whose beauty monopolized the household. However, she was naturally sleepy, often sleeping until dawn, and even during the day, she would sleep as deeply as a willow tree that sleeps three times. Initially, the official was unaware of her peculiarity. One day, she stood idly by the steps, as if chatting with someone, and as soon as she entered her chamber, she lay down to sleep, remaining unconscious for three days. The official began to suspect her behavior and questioned her. At first, she refused to speak, but after being pressed repeatedly, she finally confessed: "I am a servant of the Fragrant City, where I serve as a singer. I was demoted for a mistake, and though I reside in the human world, I still dream of returning to my celestial duties, hoping to redeem my past sins and remain in the heavenly realm. Yesterday was the birthday of Stone Lord, and all the immortals gathered. Due to my duties, I could not return quickly, which led to your suspicion. I beg for your forgiveness!"

    The official did not believe her story and said sternly: "If you can guide me to visit your heavenly abode once, to prove your words, then I will forgive you. Otherwise, the whip and stick are not far away!" His tone was filled with anger. The woman replied calmly: "I am but dust lodged on a weak blade of grass in this world. My departure and stay depend on you, and my life and death are also in your hands. I would never dare to use the heavenly jade towers to seek favor in the human world." The official was furious at her words but, out of love, could not bring himself to punish her. He let the matter rest.

    After several years, the concubine suddenly fell ill and eventually passed away. The official, moved by her loyalty, often visited her bedside to inquire about her condition. One day, she wept and said: "I am deeply touched by your kindness, which I cannot repay. Once you desired to visit the Fragrant City, and now is the time. Why not take this opportunity to fulfill your long-held wish and visit my heavenly abode?"

    149

    ”贵官大喜，亟询以出游之策，答曰：“公自屏人独卧，妾能导君同往，慎勿泄。”贵官颔之。及夕，宿于外斋，果梦妾靓妆如平时，而衣一五铢玄衣，杂彩烂然，迥非第中所有。且控一鹤一鸾，立请命驾。贵官勉跨其鹤，癷即凌空而起。惧且坠，瞑目而行。少顷，若蹈实地，张目顾瞻，妾与鸾鹤俱失所在。遥睹重城，金碧交映，四围皆植异树，高数丈，五色缤纷。迨近而谛观，果皆锦城所植者，香数里可闻，心益悦，遂徐步而前。方及皃阇之间，忽一披发少年乘小骊驹自门出，貌绝美而骀荡动人。贵官素嗜男色，见而悦之，因瞩之以目。少年觉之，一顾即停辔与言，询之曰：“公何为者而来此鬼域？”贵官大惊，自白其故。少年大笑曰：“石曼卿死而主此，其所治者皆鬼耳，岂蓬岛瀛州之比，亦号为神仙之境耶？”贵官犹以妾言与之辩，少年曰：“公之妾将死而归此，公未捐馆，何以偕来？即此可知矣。

    Explanation

    149

    The noble official was greatly pleased and quickly inquired about strategies for outings. She replied, "You should be alone in seclusion, and I can guide you to go together, but be careful not to reveal this." The noble official nodded in agreement. By evening, he stayed in an outer study and indeed dreamed that she was dressed up as usual, wearing a black robe weighing five zhu, adorned with colorful patterns that shone, completely different from what was in the mansion. She also controlled a crane and a phoenix, immediately requesting to be summoned. The noble official mustered the courage to mount the crane, and instantly they soared into the air. Fearing they would fall, he closed his eyes and walked. After a short while, it felt as if they were walking on solid ground. Opening his eyes to look around, he found that she and the crane and phoenix had all vanished. In the distance, he saw a magnificent city with golden and jade-like walls, surrounded by exotic trees that were several zhang tall, their colors a vibrant mix. Upon approaching and closely observing, he realized that these were indeed the trees from the city of brocade, their fragrance detectable for several li, which greatly pleased him, so he continued walking leisurely ahead. Just as he reached the outskirts, suddenly a young man with long hair appeared riding a small black horse from the gate, his appearance extremely beautiful and captivating. The noble official, who had always been fond of male beauty, was delighted by him and gave him a glance. The young man noticed and stopped to speak with him, asking, "What brings you here to this realm of ghosts?" The noble official was greatly surprised and explained the reason. The young man laughed and said, "Shi Manqing died and now rules here; those he governs are all ghosts. How can this compare to the Penglai and Yingzhou islands, or be called a realm of immortals?" The noble official still argued with her words, but the young man said, "Your wife will return here upon death, but you have not yet passed away. How did she come with you? From this, it can be known."

    150

    ”贵官顿悟，惧不能返。少年竟弃骑与语曰：“闺中人不免误公，请与某叠乘，当以公归，无足虑。”贵官因感谢，固逊少年居前，而己乃后拥之。双人匹马，背腹相交，隔衣熨贴之余，觉肌骨之柔靡既足荡心，而芗泽近闻，益为动情。贵官于此，反不欲遄归，而欲与少年俱逝。因闲诘其来此之故，答曰：“予居中山，久成仙道，亦慕此城之胜，潜来一游。讵意阴气盛炽，不可以居，故弃如敝屣而去之。”贵官信其言，而恍惚中行且数里。少年曰：“至矣！”贵官举首四顾，别更一境，重楼复阁掩映相属，山坳花木亦甚葱蒨，但妍艳而无雅致。贵官反顾而乐之，以为相去远甚。少年延入，置酒欢饮，侍者多佼童，色俱殊选。少年又白：“此方丈之山，金仙所居，非公有缘，区区者乌能至此？”酒酣，少年又进以媚技，渐至狎亵。贵官情不能遏，颇思为钻研之计。俄闻鸾声哕哕，少年色顿变，侍者亦甚仓皇。

    Explanation

    150

    The high-ranking official suddenly realized and feared he might not be able to return. The young man finally abandoned his horse and spoke with him, saying, "The lady in the inner chamber could not help but mislead you. Please ride together with me, and I will take you back. There is nothing to worry about." The official then thanked him, insisting that the young man should ride in front while he followed behind. Two people on one horse, their backs and bellies touching, felt the softness of flesh and bones pressing against each other through their clothes. The fragrance of perfume nearby stirred his heart even more. At this point, the official no longer wanted to hurry back but wished to go away with the young man. He casually asked the young man why he had come there. The young man replied, "I live in Zhongshan and have long become a Daoist immortal. I also admired the beauty of this city and secretly came to visit. I did not expect the strong and intense yin energy here, which makes it impossible to live. Therefore, I abandoned it like old shoes and left." The official believed his words and, in a dazed state, walked several miles. The young man said, "We have arrived!" The official looked around and found himself in a different realm altogether. Tall buildings and pavilions were overlapping and connected, and the hills were lush with flowers and trees, though they lacked elegance. The official looked back and found it pleasing, thinking it was far more distant. The young man invited him in, set up a feast, and they drank merrily. The attendants were handsome boys, all exceptionally beautiful. The young man added, "This is the mountain of Fangzhang, where the Golden Immortal resides. Without your good fortune, how could I reach here?" When drunk, the young man offered seductive skills, and they gradually became intimate and improper. The official could not control his emotions and thought a lot about further exploration. Suddenly, he heard the sound of the phoenix. The young man's expression suddenly changed, and the attendants were in great haste.

    151

    旋有赤色如匹练，直达中楹，则一丽人含怒自外入。视之，乃其妾也，鸾鹤犹翔其侧。贵官大惭，回顾少年，已化为石卵，大仅盈掬。妾掇之，回嗔为笑曰：“是儿亦太不自重，然亦公之福薄，今请奉君归，家人当凝望矣。”贵官抱愧，复跨鹤上升。一时屋宇都渺，怪壑悬岩，懔乎其不可留也。归至斋中，转侧而悟，且闻人噪曰：“公苏矣！”醒而诘其故，则卧已两朝夕矣。大骇，方命人视妾，而妾已使婢来邀。亟起，诣其室，妾即执手永诀曰：“本意引公入胜，不期鸾迟鹤速，以致惑于邪妖，究亦足以释公之惑矣。今将返妾故居，不得再充下陈，慎勿过悲。”因以圆石付之曰：“此公之可人所化，剖之当获宝玉，犹可朝夕把玩，不致以杀风景怨妾。”言讫，溘然而逝。贵官知其仙，厚加葬具，题之曰：“睡姬之墓。”即以石示玉人，破其璞，得明视一枚，朱眼霜毫，天然工巧。因宝之，佩以终身，斯须未尝去焉。

    Explanation

    151

    Soon there was a red streak like a ribbon, reaching directly to the central pillar, and then a beautiful woman entered with anger. Looking at her, it was her concubine, with phoenixes and cranes still flying beside her. The nobleman felt deeply ashamed, turned around to see the young man, who had already transformed into a stone egg, as large as a handful. The concubine picked it up, changed her anger to a smile, and said, "This child is also too unvalued, but your good fortune is thin. Now I will take you back, and your family will be waiting anxiously." Feeling ashamed, the nobleman again mounted the crane and ascended. The buildings all became distant, and strange ravines and cliffs appeared, so terrifying that one could not stay. Returning to his study, he turned and woke up, hearing people shouting, "Sir has awakened!" He was surprised to find that he had been asleep for two days. He was greatly shocked and immediately sent someone to check on the concubine, but she had already sent a maid to invite him. He quickly got up and went to her room, where she held his hand and said, "My original intention was to lead you into a wonderful place, but unfortunately, the phoenix was slow and the crane was fast, leading to being misled by evil spirits. In the end, it was enough to resolve your confusion. Now I will return to my former home, and I will no longer be able to serve as a concubine. Do not be overly sad." She then gave him a stone egg, saying, "This stone egg is the transformation of someone you liked. If you break it, you will find a precious jewel, with red eyes and white hair, naturally exquisite. So you won't blame me for ruining the scenery." After speaking, she suddenly passed away. Knowing she was a fairy, the nobleman provided a generous burial, inscribing it as "The Tomb of the Sleeping Concubine." He then broke the stone and found a precious gem, which he treasured, wearing it as a pendant for life, never taking it off.

    152

    外史氏曰：究是妾之狡狯，不使尘中贵人深历瑶天胜境。不然，重门击柝犹防暴客，岂世外仙居竟任狂童出入哉？至于黑甜乡中，原有乐趣，固不俟姬之导引，然后如入华胥。人患不善睡、不暇睡耳！果如姬之高枕，纵不居芙蓉城，亦平生第一快事。

    Explanation

    152

    The historian said: Examining the cunning of the concubine, she does not allow the noble person of the world to deeply experience the heavenly paradise. Otherwise, even with multiple gates and guards, one must still be wary of violent intruders; how could the abode of a hermit allow a wild child to enter and exit freely? As for the land of sweet dreams, there is inherent pleasure; one does not need the concubine's guidance to enter the realm of the Hua Xiu. The problem is not knowing how to sleep or not having time to sleep! If one can sleep as comfortably as the concubine, even if one does not live in the city of lotus flowers, it would still be the greatest pleasure of life.

    153

    ○　张仙
    　　人之艰于嗣者，多绘张仙以奉之，以其能卫厥子孙也。其像为美丈夫，锦袍角带，广颐丰髭，左挟弹，右摄丸，飘飘乎有霞举之势。仰视云中，一犬叫嗥而去，盖即俗所谓天狗也。某县一画师，尤善于此，所作者眉目如生，勃勃有神气。且遇小儿惊啼，祷之辄应，人以是神之。远近数百里奔走相求，其门若市，画师遂因是致富。县东数里许，某村有民家，娶妇而美，数年不育，乃亲诣画师求之。往返再四，甫得尺幅以归。及至家，香火奉祀，意甚虔洁。旬余，夫偶他出，妇独寝，即有伟男子衣冠甚都，直至榻前，谓妇曰：“我张仙也。夙昔鉴汝诚，思以宁馨锡汝。但汝夫痿弱，不克播种，今来代若耕耨，庶几黍稷可望，汝勿惊讶。”言已，解衣便将登床。妇睨其美，亦心动，欣然容纳。欢好达曙，始揽衣下榻，整束端严，冉冉而没。

    Explanation

    153

    A Tale of the God Zhang

    Those who have difficulty in having descendants often paint the God Zhang to worship him, because he can protect their descendants. His image is that of a handsome man, dressed in a brocade robe and a sash, with a broad chin and thick mustache. He holds a bow in his left hand and arrows in his right, appearing to fly high with the momentum of a rising cloud. Looking up at the clouds, a dog barks and runs away, which is said to be the common folk's term for the Celestial Dog. In a certain county, there was a painter who was particularly skilled at this. His paintings had lifelike eyes and eyebrows, full of spirit. Moreover, when children were startled and cried, praying to him always responded, and people regarded him as a deity. People from far and near traveled hundreds of miles to seek him out, and his door was like a market. The painter thus became wealthy due to this. Several miles east of the county, in a certain village, there was a family that married a beautiful woman. For several years, they had not been able to have children. They personally went to the painter to request one. They went back and forth several times, finally obtaining a small scroll to return home. Upon arriving home, they worshiped it with incense and fire, with great sincerity and purity. After ten days, her husband happened to be away, and the woman was alone in bed. Suddenly, a tall and handsome man with elegant attire appeared before her and went straight to the bed. He said to her, "I am the God Zhang. Having long observed your sincerity, I wish to bestow upon you a precious gift. But your husband is weak, unable to father children. Now I come to plow for you, perhaps the crops of millet and wheat can be hoped for. Do not be surprised." After speaking, he undressed and was about to get into bed. The woman looked at his beauty and her heart was moved. She happily accepted him. They enjoyed each other until dawn. Then she put on her clothes and got out of bed, putting herself back in order, and gradually disappeared.

    154

    视之，则已身入画中矣。妇益信其灵感，夫归亦秘而不告。嗣是益隆情好，夫不在即来，妇劝不拒。积渐纵恣，夫在亦来，妇遂不能讳，腼然倾吐，犹以为神之祐之也。夫察其迹，知为妖异，竟取其像而焚之。甫及帧首，有细字一行，谛视焉，则人之年庚八字也。益诧其异，毁之倍亟。越数日，闻某画师无疾暴卒。惊而侦访，其徒有与妇之夫识者，密以相告，始得其颠末。盖画师见妇，心志动摇，无能自主。画成，乃戏书其生平于轴，且祝曰：“倘有夙缘，当即于梦中相晤也。”后旬余，果梦与妇狎，自以为奇遇，萤窗间与人私述为笑，其徒以是闻之。及妇之夫焚其像，画师方在肆异中，大呼曰：“某人以火吾躯，吾无生矣！”言讫遂卒，周身草初焦灼如燎焉。妇之夫闻而大快，益以宣播，不旬日，而画师所绘编卷之像，早已尽成灰烬矣！

    Explanation

    154

    When she looked at it, she saw herself entering the painting. The woman increasingly believed in its inspiration, and upon returning, the husband kept it a secret and did not tell anyone. Afterward, their affection grew even more. When the husband was not there, the woman would come, and although the husband did not refuse her, he did not agree to their relationship. Gradually, they became unrestrained and indulgent, and even when the husband was present, the woman would come. She could no longer conceal it and spoke openly and shyly, still believing it was protected by the divine. The husband observed its traces and knew it to be an evil or supernatural phenomenon. Finally, he took the image and burned it. Just as it reached the top of the frame, there was a line of small writing. He carefully examined it, and it was a person's birth year and the eight characters (八字) of their birth. He became even more astonished by its strangeness and destroyed it even more urgently. After several days, he heard that a certain painter died suddenly without illness. Shocked, he investigated, and one of the painter's disciples knew the woman's husband. The disciple secretly informed him, and he finally learned the entire story. The painter, upon seeing the woman, was shaken in mind and will and could not control himself. When the painting was completed, he jokingly wrote his life story on the scroll and said, "If there is a predestined relationship, we will meet in a dream." After about ten days, he indeed dreamed of being intimate with the woman, thinking it was a rare and wonderful encounter. By the dim light of a lamp, he told people in secret, making them laugh. His disciples heard about it. When the woman's husband burned the image, the painter was in the midst of a strange occurrence and shouted, "Someone is burning my body with fire, I will have no life!" He finished speaking and then died. His whole body was scorched like a fire. The woman's husband heard about it and felt greatly relieved. He spread the news even more widely, and not even ten days later, the images the painter had drawn in the scroll had long been completely burned to ashes.

    155

    外史氏曰：张仙本花蕊夫人捏造者。史载夫人自入宋宫，每思后主不置，乃绘像以祀之。嗣因艺祖询及，乃诡词以对，非实有是神也。则夫画工之灵，由笔而入，似无足为神羞。抑安知非后主之风流与丹青合而为祟耶？乡人一炬，总足解醒。

    Explanation

    155

    The Historian said: Zhang Xian was originally fabricated by Consort Huayun. According to historical records, after Consort Huayun entered the Song palace, she often thought of Emperor Shizong without ceasing, and thus painted a picture to worship him. Later, when Emperor Taizu inquired about it, she gave a deceptive answer, indicating that the deity was not real. Thus, the spirit of the painter entered through the brush, and it seems not enough to be ashamed of a deity. Who knows if it is not the result of the late sovereign's charm and the painting combining to cause trouble? A local person set fire to it, which is sufficient to wake one up.

    156

    随园老人曰：以隽笔破俗情，几令世间假神道无处生活。

    ○　守一女
    　　有明中叶，山寇窃发。某村有笄女，为贼所得，悦其美，并其父母弱弟拘系之。谓不从污，将举一家尽歼之，无噍类。其父母握手啼泣，不敢默，亦不忍语也。女已有成竹在胸，独慨然曰：“儿之身，父母所生；辱儿身，即辱父母。义固万无可从，但儿不受辱，儿必死。儿死而父母弱弟无一得生，是背罔极之恩，而斩先人之祀，胥由于此。儿固含笑于九泉，以全节为荣，父母能安于地下，不以生女为怨乎？今请与之约，苟具夫妇之礼，予即事之。否则，宁死不辱，无敢以父母为念。”其父母亟白于贼，主者实一渠魁，欣然诺之。女请先释其父母与弟，贼知其绐己，坚不许。女乃太息曰：“天乎！予非不死，实不能死也。”径趋贼幕，俟与成婚，绝不作儿女子态。贼既纳女，以兼金赠其父母，使与弟俱归。

    Explanation

    156

    The Old Man of Sui Garden said: "To use a sharp pen to break through common feelings, it almost causes the false gods of the world to have no place to live."

    ○ Shouyi, the Maiden
    During the middle period of the Ming Dynasty, mountain bandits suddenly rose up. In a certain village, there was a young woman of marriageable age who was captured by the bandits. They admired her beauty and also detained her parents and younger brother. They threatened that if she refused to submit to their defilement, they would kill the entire family, leaving no survivors. Her parents held hands and wept, daring not to remain silent, yet unable to speak. The maiden had a clear plan in mind and resolutely said, "My body was born by my parents; to defile my body is to defile my parents. Morally, there is no way to comply. But if I do not submit to defilement, I will die. If I die, my parents and younger brother will not survive. This would betray the boundless kindness of my parents and cut off the ancestral sacrifices, all due to this. I will smile in the netherworld, taking the preservation of my chastity as a source of honor. Can my parents rest in peace, not regretting having given birth to me? Now I propose to make a pact with them: if they perform the rites of marriage, I will comply. Otherwise, I would rather die than submit to defilement, without considering my parents' feelings." Her parents hurriedly explained this to the bandits. The leader was actually a notorious bandit chief, and he readily agreed. The maiden requested to release her parents and younger brother first. The bandits knew she was deceiving them and firmly refused. She then sighed and said, "Heaven! I am not unwilling to die, but I cannot die." She directly went to the bandits' camp, awaiting marriage, showing no childlike or feminine behavior. The bandits accepted the maiden and gave a large sum of money to her parents, allowing them to return together with her younger brother.

    157

    及入与女别，女言笑自若，惟以一布裹，缝纫甚密，付之曰：“他日相见，可以此为质，且勿视之。”其父母遂泣而去。乃居贼中旬日，官兵大至，合围之。贼败，俱骈首受戮，所掠子女尽放之宁家，女亦归其村墟。时贼已尽平，人皆旋里，父母将与议婚，女坚执以为不可。父母笑曰：“向特不得已，岂贼也而将为之守节哉？”女曰：“不然，儿非为贼守节，实受父母之命也。父母当日果能拚生骂贼，与儿俱死，儿即淫贱，亦不忍偷生。乃既为亲而事贼，是犹奉命而嫁贼。一醮乌可再醮，重辱父母之遗乎？”即索布裹，拆视之，则骊珠在焉。因号泣曰：“以此还父母，今后无命可从也。”自是独居一室，足不出户，供木主于案，倩人题“守一”二字，以示靡他之志。父母百计俱穷，竟不能嫁之。父母卒，己亦不食而死。临殁，犹嗟叹曰：“迟矣！晚矣！”盖恨其不死于被掠之先也。

    Explanation

    157

    When she entered and parted with the girl, the girl spoke and laughed as usual, wrapping herself only in a tightly sewn cloth and handing it to her, saying, "If we meet again, this can serve as a pledge, and do not look at it." Her parents then cried and left. She stayed with the bandits for ten days, and the soldiers arrived in large numbers, surrounding them. The bandits were defeated, and all were executed together. All the children and women they had captured were released to return home, and the girl also returned to her village. At that time, the bandits had all been pacified, and everyone returned to their hometowns. Her parents planned to arrange her marriage, but the girl firmly insisted that it was not acceptable. Her parents laughed and said, "Before, it was out of necessity; how could she have been with bandits and still maintain her chastity?" The girl replied, "No, I am not maintaining my chastity for the bandits; I am following my parents' command. If my parents had been able to die rather than curse the bandits and die with me, even if I were a prostitute, I would not have wanted to live on. Now that I have been with the bandits as a relative, it is like being married to the bandits by command. Once married, how can one remarry? How can I bring further shame to my parents' legacy?" She immediately took the cloth, tore it open, and looked inside, and there was a pearl inside. She cried out and said, "With this, I return to my parents; from now on, I have no command to follow." From then on, she lived alone in a room, not leaving her room, and placed a wooden tablet on the table, asking someone to write "Chastity" on it, to show her unwavering resolve. Her parents tried everything but failed, and in the end, they could not marry her off. When her parents died, she also starved herself to death. On her deathbed, she sighed and said, "It is too late! Too late!" She regretted not dying before being captured.

    158

    外史氏曰：呜呼！名节至重，吾人何敢妄言。然以情理揆之，守一身之节，殂全家之命，烈而似近于忍。此以父母之命为词，得免父母于死，又守一不变者，差为反经合权。虽然，妇人之身，固不可如此。女子而如此，其可训不可训，则又非圣贤莫与定论矣，吾曹敢乎哉？

    ○　柳青卿
    　　文安戴敬宸，学富而貌寝，伟躯硕腹。腰大十围。且年未三旬，浓髭满颊，面皮几无隙地，人因以毛胖目之。康熙戊子，以明经登进士第，铨补耒阳令。邑人以为士元再来，闺阁中闻宰之貌，靡不窃笑而攒眉，其见陋于人如此。莅任期年，以事赴省，暮宿于某县绅家。绅家故有废楼，久扃，因宰至，始粪除之，以安其行李。戴与绅饮，夜深始登其楼，惟一仆从，余皆寝于下。戴病酒，不克遽寐，辗转间，漏下已三。癷闻异香发于榻侧，嗅之，有桂之清而兼麝之馥。戴以为楼前所植者，亦不之异。

    Explanation

    158

    The Historian said: Alas! The reputation and integrity are of utmost importance, how dare we speak lightly. However, when considering the circumstances, maintaining one's own integrity even at the cost of the entire family's life, while brave, seems close to being cruel. This is to use the command of one's parents as an excuse, to save one's parents from death, and to adhere steadfastly to one principle, which is somewhat a deviation from the norm but conforms to the principles of flexibility and pragmatism. Nevertheless, a woman's body should not be treated this way. If a woman acts in such a manner, whether it can be taught or not, only sages can make a definitive judgment; how dare we do such a thing?

    ○ Liu Qingqing
    Wen'an Dai Jingchen, learned but unattractive in appearance, had a robust and large body. His waist was ten spans wide. He was not yet thirty years old, with thick mustaches covering his cheeks, leaving almost no space on his face, and people therefore called him "Hairy Fat." In the second year of the Kangxi era (1663), he passed the imperial examination for scholars and was appointed as the magistrate of Leiyang. The townspeople believed he was the reincarnation of Shi Yuan, and upon hearing of the magistrate's appearance, the women in the town would secretly laugh and frown, showing how they regarded him as unattractive. After a year in office, he traveled to the provincial capital for official business, staying overnight at the home of a county official. The official's house had an old abandoned building that had been locked for a long time. It was only when the magistrate arrived that it was cleaned up to accommodate his belongings. Dai and the official drank together, and late into the night, they ascended the building. Only one servant accompanied him, while everyone else slept downstairs. Dai was suffering from alcohol, unable to fall asleep quickly, and as he lay tossing and turning, the night had already passed three hours. Suddenly, he smelled an unusual fragrance coming from beside his bed. He smelled it and found it had the clarity of cassia and the richness of musk. Dai thought it must be from the plants in front of the building and did not think much of it.

    159

    已而笑声吃吃，乃亟张目以睨，则少妇艳绝，掩口胡卢立于烛下。戴知为异类，更不呼仆，蹶然突起，将自身执之。妇仓皇欲遁，且匿笑曰：“尊范若此，不亦难为佳人？愿即退避三舍。”言已而趋。适足缠微松，带挂于板，竟颠踬不能脱，遂为戴所获。牵以就榻，询所从来，乃赧颜答曰：“妾柳姓，小字青卿，实狐也。服役衡山，差满将归，因喜此楼幽静，暂僦居之，不意君之涉吾地也。”语次，口脂之馨，浑如百合。戴不觉心旌动摇，强之解衣。柳笑曰：“凡狐皆能祟人，遇之辄死。以君之品，犹可末减，何反舍生就之？”言讫，以红袖掩口，仍复胡卢。戴素以貌自憎，今更不见纳于狐，益大恚。

    Explanation

    159

    After a while, there came a nervous, stifled laugh, and he quickly opened his eyes and glanced sideways. There stood a stunning young woman, her hands covering her mouth, softly chuckling by the candlelight. Dai realized she was an otherworldly being and did not call for his servant. He suddenly jumped up, intending to seize her by the body. The young woman, in a state of panic, tried to flee and, while hiding her smile, said, "If you were to treat me like this, it would be difficult for me to be a good woman. I wish you would back off a bit." After saying this, she hurriedly moved away. Just as she was about to leave, her foot bindings came loose, and her sash caught on a board, causing her to trip and fall, unable to escape. Thus, she was captured by Dai. He dragged her to the bed and asked where she had come from. She blushed and replied, "I am named Liu, and my nickname is Qingqing. I am actually a fox. I served on Mount Heng, and now that my term is up, I am about to return. I liked this quiet and secluded place, so I rented it temporarily. I did not expect you to intrude on my territory." As she spoke, the fragrance of her lip balm, like lily of the valley, filled the air. Dai could not help but feel his heart flutter, and he forced her to undress. Qingqing laughed and said, "All foxes have the ability to harm people, and anyone who encounters them will die. Given your character, I can give you a lesser punishment. Why do you want to die instead?" After saying this, she covered her mouth with her red sleeve and chuckled again. Dai, who usually hated his own appearance, felt even more angry now that he was not accepted by the fox.

    160

    逼近柳躯，亲松其扣，慨然曰：“死则死耳，与其为丑鬼以生，毋宁因妖狐而死，谁复能堪此鄙薄？”柳以纤指弹其腹曰：“身挂五斗袋，而强人欢合，君真不自量矣！”戴弗听，益力持之，罗襦才解，芗泽近闻，觉其竟体芳馥，神魂飞越。柳亦不胜羞赧，乃自登榻，引戴衾冒首而卧。戴亦情急，不能姑待，启衾而入，遂相欢好。事已，柳捋戴髯微哂曰：“于思！于思！弃甲当离。”戴亦笑曰：“于思！于思！从此弗离。”相与大噱。柳因转侧又笑曰：“杨家阿环与禄山为好，以今观之，真大难事。”俄而鸡鸣，柳着衣先起，辞戴欲行，戴挽之，坚订后约。柳曰：“仓卒呈身，此体遂为君有，去诚无所之。但君有公务，妾不敢淆。俟返琴堂，妾自趋赴，从此与胡偕老矣。”乃以香囊留赠，珍重而别。晨起，戴亦不与绅言，径趋省下，谒见院司，事竣遄归。

    Explanation

    160

    Approaching Li's body, Dai loosened her bindings and said with a sigh, "Death is death, but rather than live as a grotesque ghost, it is better to die as a beautiful fox. Who could endure such humiliation?" Li, using her slender fingers, tapped his abdomen and said, "Wearing a bag of five dou and forcing someone to join in joy, you truly do not know your own limits!" Dai did not listen and instead held her more forcefully. As the silk robe just loosened, the fragrance was near, and he felt the whole body fragrant, his soul flying away. Li, unable to bear the shame, got onto the bed, pulled the quilt over his head, and lay down. Dai, in a hurry, unable to wait, opened the quilt and entered, and they enjoyed each other's company. Afterward, Li stroked his beard and smiled slightly, saying, "Ah! Ah! Discard the armor and leave." Dai also laughed and said, "Ah! Ah! Never leave from now on." They laughed heartily together. Li turned over and laughed again, saying, "The affair between Yang Guifei and An Lushan, viewed from today, is truly a great difficulty." Soon, the rooster crowed, and Li got up first, dressed, and said goodbye to Dai, wanting to leave. Dai held her back and firmly promised a future meeting. Li said, "Hastily presenting myself, this body is now yours. Going would be nowhere to go. But since you have official duties, I dare not interfere. Wait until you return to the qintang, and I will follow. From now on, we will grow old together." She left a sachet as a gift and parted with respect. In the morning, Dai also did not speak to the official and went directly to the provincial office, paid a visit to the provincial office, finished the business, and returned quickly.

    161

    初虑柳之爽约也，重过绅家，未尝上宿，乃诵《毛诗》两联，望楼而祝曰：“无我丑兮，不寁好也。无我胡兮，不寁故也。”祝讫始行。抵署，宿于外寝，以冀其来。宵未分，而柳果至，搴帘径入，笑谓戴曰：“闺人皆畏胖，我意独怜胡。”遂引身与戴并坐，谐谑甚欢。从此夜现昼藏，渺无去志，署中人亦罕有知者。一日，忽语戴曰：“衡岳诸姊妹皆欲晤君，兼为妾贺，明夜盍一行。”戴问：“筵设何所？”答曰：“天上。”戴嗤其妄，柳正色曰：“君以妾不能登天耶？青云独步，君辈胥夸大之词，视妾之干霄直上，则浮而不实矣。”话已，复曰：“往须易好面目，如此肉累累而毛毵毵，姗笑当不可耐。”戴姑颔之，亦未深信。明夕，柳襆一物来，谓戴曰：“为君制造，费尽匠心，可着之，然后偕往。”戴展视，则人皮如蝉蜕，眉目宛然，官骸皆具，乃大骇。

    Explanation

    161

    Initially, he was concerned that Liu might break the appointment. He revisited Mr. Shen's house, but never stayed overnight. He then recited two lines from the Mao Shi (Book of Songs), and while looking at the building, he wished aloud: "Do not find me ugly, do not hasten to abandon what is good. Do not find me a barbarian, do not hasten to abandon what is old." After making his wish, he started to leave. He arrived at the office, stayed in the outer room, hoping she would come. Before midnight, Liu indeed arrived. She pulled the curtain aside and entered directly, smiling and saying to Dai: "All the women in the inner chambers fear fat, but I alone pity the barbarian." She then sat next to Dai, and they laughed and joked happily. From then on, she appeared at night and hid during the day, showing no intention of leaving, and few people in the office knew about it. One day, she suddenly said to Dai: "The sisters from Mount Heng all want to meet you, and they also want to congratulate me. Why don't you come tomorrow night?" Dai asked, "Where is the banquet set?" She replied, "In heaven." Dai scoffed at her claim, and Liu said seriously, "Do you think I can't ascend to heaven? To walk alone on the blue clouds is an exaggerated claim by you all. Compared to my ascending straight to the sky, it is hollow and false." After she finished speaking, she added, "You must change into a better appearance. With this much flesh and so many hairs, you will be laughed at." Dai nodded, but did not fully believe it. The next night, Liu brought a wrapped object and said to Dai: "It was made for you, using great skill and effort. You can wear it, and then we can go together." Dai unfolded it and saw a human skin like a cicada's shell, with eyebrows and eyes clearly visible, and all the bones and organs intact, and he was greatly shocked.

    162

    柳令谛视，以素绢制成者，无缝纫迹，如织女天衣，始信其神。柳令戴裸而着之，肤革之充饶忽焉顿缩，独至于腹，坠坠然急不能纳。柳乃笑而抚之曰：“杜甫！杜甫！无骨有肉，消瘦些儿，送汝归蜀。”戴不觉大笑，腹遽小。迨及于元，凡所谓美且鬈美且偲者，胥归于无何有之乡，即欲效毛遂处囊，颖脱而出，亦不可得。已着讫，戴于烛下索镜自临，见其面部顿清，无复扰扰，眉宗忽判，不再蓬蓬，不禁掷镜大喜。柳又进以鲜衣，百端修饰，则俨然美丈夫矣。润色甫毕，戴即请行。柳语曰：“君至彼，勿贪杯中物，适可而止，恐沉醉或泄吾事。”戴敬诺，甫出寝户，夜黑如漆，戴恍惚如行云雾中。柳时以纤腕夹持其腋，自后而吹嘘之。

    Explanation

    162

    柳令仔细观察，发现那件白绢制成的衣服没有缝线痕迹，如同织女的天衣一般神奇，这才相信其非凡之处。柳令让戴裸体穿上衣服，只见他的皮肤和肌肉突然收缩，唯有腹部依然鼓胀，显得沉重得无法塞进。柳令笑着拍了拍他说：“杜甫！杜甫！你有肉没骨，稍微消瘦一点，送你回四川。”戴不禁大笑，腹部随即缩小。到了元朝，所有曾经美丽而卷曲、美丽而智慧的人，都回到了虚无缥缈的地方，即使想模仿毛遂从囊中脱颖而出，也已不可能。穿戴完毕后，戴在烛光下照镜子，发现自己的面容突然变得清晰，不再杂乱无章，眉毛也变得分明，不再蓬乱，不由得扔下镜子大喜。柳又给他穿上新衣，经过一番修饰，他俨然成了一个英俊的男子。调整完毕，戴请求离开。柳说：“你到了那里，不要贪杯，适可而止，担心醉酒会泄露我的事情。”戴恭敬地点头，刚出门，夜色如墨，戴恍惚如行走在云雾之中。柳不时用纤细的手臂夹住他的腋下，从后面吹气。

    163

    积渐而高，身不自主，仿佛如陟层梯，不知其有几千百级。须臾至一处，朱门洞开，饰以金玉。有石兽二，高丈许，卧于左右，两楹燎巨烛，毫发毕见。榜曰：“衡帝骖鸾之馆。”盖即岳主之离宫也。柳引戴入，至其门，无人。登其堂，亦无人。转折而西，别有侧户。又入之，回廊曲槛，皆悬绛纱笼。院中花竹森秀，清芬袭人。中屋五楹，灯光外射，柳与戴犹未及阶，闻室中人语曰：“司香子延客至矣。”即有丽人四五，从帘间袅婷而出，俱宫装画衣，备极妖艳。迎迓曰：“过劳凫履，远弃琴堂，一睹壶冰，爽人眉宇，妾辈不胜景仰。”戴逊谢入室，则酒香茗气，玩好纷陈，彝鼎图书，极其典重。南向设二筵，笾豆楚列。众逊戴首坐，目相顾语，若有所疑讶，来能释然。

    Explanation

    163

    Gradually rising higher, the body could no longer control itself, as if climbing a multi-tiered staircase, unaware of how many thousands of steps there were. In a moment, they arrived at a place where the red gate was wide open, decorated with gold and jade. There were two stone beasts, each about a zhang tall, lying on either side, with two pillars bearing large candles, every hair and filament clearly visible. The sign read: "The Pavilion of the Balance Emperor Riding the Phoenix." It was the imperial retreat of the Mountain Lord. Liu led Dai in, to its door, where there was no one. To its hall, there was also no one. Turning west, there was another side door. They entered it, and found winding corridors and curved railings, all hung with red silk lanterns. In the courtyard, the flowers and bamboo were lush and fragrant, their scent pervading the air. In the middle of the house, there were five rooms, with light shining out. Liu and Dai had not yet reached the steps when they heard people inside say: "The incense master has invited guests." Immediately, there were four or five beautiful women who emerged gracefully from behind the curtain, all dressed in imperial attire and painted robes, extremely beautiful and alluring. They greeted saying: "You have traveled far and tired your feet, leaving your music room behind, and seeing the clear ice, refreshing your countenance, we are greatly in awe." Dai modestly thanked and entered the room, where the aroma of wine and tea, various treasures were displayed, bronze vessels, tripods, and books, all extremely dignified. Two tables were set facing south, with dishes and fruits arranged neatly. The others let Dai sit first, looking at each other and speaking, as if in doubt or surprise, unable to fully relax.

    164

    戴会其意，坦然即席。抚其裀，皆以异锦制就，香软殊常。众又推柳与戴联肩坐，笑曰：“数旬夫妻，今夕合卺，不可谓非晚也。”柳亦笑答曰：“暮夜仓卒，无从得酒，业以香唾代之矣。此夕当是与姊辈会亲，虽晚何伤。”众俱颜赤曰：“婢子亦大无赖。”于是履皅交错，相对举觞。酒微酣，众嗅戴身馥郁，不知其以香囊佩也，咸戏曰：“近薰者果无莸耶？”柳又笑曰：“同器者宜尔。脱遇乡辈，恐亦十年尚有臭。”众大哗曰：“汝今者亦入鲍鱼之肆矣。”因诘曰：“若言使君貌不扬，今见之，何不逮予所闻？”戴时已醺然，乃代答曰：“欲妍则妍，欲媸则媸，妍媸固任人自为耳。卿何惑乎？”柳急以目止之。

    Explanation

    164

    Dai comprehends his meaning and composes himself, seating himself calmly. Touching his cushion, he notes that it is all made of various brocades, fragrant and soft, unlike ordinary ones. The others then push Liu and Dai to sit side by side, laughing and saying, "A few weeks' husband and wife, tonight's wedding, cannot be called late." Liu also laughs and replies, "At night, in haste, unable to obtain wine, already using fragrant saliva as a replacement. Tonight, it should be meeting kin, even if late, what harm?" The others all blush and say, "The maidservant is also quite mischievous." Thus, their shoes and socks interlock, and they raise their cups to each other. With a slight intoxication, the others smell the fragrant aroma on Dai's body, unaware that he wears a scented sachet, and all tease, "Those who are near the fragrance indeed have no stench?" Liu laughs again, "Those who share the same container should be like this. If one encounters fellow villagers, it might still smell for ten years." The others loudly exclaim, "You are now entering the fishmonger's shop!" They then ask, "If you say the governor's appearance is not striking, seeing him now, how does it not match what we heard?" Dai, already tipsy, then answers on behalf, "If one wants it beautiful, it is beautiful; if one wants it ugly, it is ugly. Beauty and ugliness are left to each person to decide. Why are you confused?" Liu quickly stops him with a glance.

    165

    众中有觉者，坚以诘戴，且置巨盎于前曰：“不言，以斯为尔爵。”戴惧量不胜，始约略述之。众戏剥其革，划然有声，甫及颏下，而庐山之面目已出。众美凝睇，乱草横生，飞蓬满目，不禁捧腹绝倒。柳大有惭色，扶戴急出曰：“醉人无知，竟使人窥见底里。”戴昏然觉其身如坠，醒则卧于衙斋，革已无存，并柳亦不见。自是思之虽苦，亦绝不再来。年余，丁外艰，匆遽归里。行及宜阳道上，见柳偕一美少年，从骑十数，驰骋于茂草间，使人传语谢戴曰：“青卿致意，渠非狐，实岳帝之司香女也。自君露形，数见笑于僚辈，以故难坚旧好。今已改适郭指挥，相得甚欢，幸勿以为念。”言已，俱穿榛莽而去，家人始知其事。后戴屡为人言，且出香囊以为质。有见之者，精工芬馥，洵非尘世物也。

    165

    Among the crowd, there were those who became aware, and they firmly questioned Dai, placing a large pot in front of him and saying, "If you don't speak, I will use this pot as your cup." Dai was afraid the pot wouldn't be enough to hold his drink, so he began to vaguely describe it. The crowd playfully peeled off his skin, with a sharp sound, just reaching his chin, and the true face of Mount Lu was revealed. The crowd admired him intently, seeing wild grass growing wildly and dandelions filling his eyes, and they couldn't help but laugh and fall over. Liu had a look of shame and supported Dai, hurriedly saying, "The drunk man was unaware, and thus allowed people to see his true self." Dai suddenly felt as if he were falling, and when he woke up, he was lying in the office, with his skin no longer there, and Liu was also gone. From then on, even if he thought about it painfully, he would never come back. After a year, he encountered an external calamity and hurriedly returned to his hometown. As he was traveling on the road to Yiyang, he saw Liu with a handsome young man, with a dozen or so attendants, galloping through the lush grass. He sent someone to convey a message to Dai saying, "Qingqing conveys his regards. He is not a fox, but actually the daughter of Emperor Yu who manages incense. Since you revealed your true form, you have been laughed at by your colleagues, for this reason, it is hard to maintain old friendships. Now he has remarried to Commandant Guo, and they get along very well. Please do not worry about it." After saying this, they all disappeared into the thicket, and the family only then learned about the matter. Later, Dai often spoke to others about it and produced a sachet as proof. Those who saw it found it finely crafted and fragrant, indeed not an earthly object.

    166

    外史氏曰：夫不若人，嫫母犹以为耻，况绝代之姝哉？今世之慧女子，亦多有誉夫之癖，每一出门，辄为加意整饰。孰知大醉狼藉，最能坏人体面耶？然使良宵访戴，兴尽而返，必无此一段佳话。柳之不幸，乃戴之深幸也。

    ○　珊珊
    　　许皋鹤太史未第时，读书于溧水书院。有同舍生孙某，素同笔砚，为莫逆交。数年，肄业无所就，弃儒而商。随人航海，遂不复，疑其溺于弱水死矣。太史既贵，恒思忆之。嗣遇册封暹罗，太史充副使，远涉海外。既竣使事，归途遇飓，覆其舟。故事，凡奉使入海，正副使皆舆榇而行，以备不虞。柩前钉一金字牌，题曰“使某国某官某公之灵”，以为识。事迫，则先卧其中，束手待毙而已。太史既罹水厄，无复生望，在柩中载沉载浮，听其所之，不葬鱼腹为厚幸。忽闻人语曰：“此予之故人也，奚为至于此？”命启其棺，太史始敢展视。

    Explanation

    166

    外史氏曰： The historian said: "If one is not as good as others, even Nüwa would feel ashamed; how much more so for a woman of unparalleled beauty? In today's world, many intelligent women have a tendency to excessively praise their husbands, always dressing up carefully whenever they go out. Who would have thought that being extremely drunk and disorderly could ruin one's dignity the most? However, if on a pleasant night one visits someone and returns after having had enough, there would certainly be no such delightful story. Liu's misfortune was actually a great fortune for Dai."

    ○ 珊珊
    许皋鹤太史未第时，读书于溧水书院。 When Xu Gaohuo, the historian, was still a scholar and had not yet passed the imperial examinations, he studied at the Lishui Academy.
    有同舍生孙某，素同笔砚，为莫逆交。 There was a fellow student named Sun, who shared his ink and brushes and was a close friend.
    数年，肄业无所就，弃儒而商。 After several years, he failed to achieve success in his studies and turned to business.
    随人航海，遂不复，疑其溺于弱水死矣。 He sailed the seas with others and then disappeared, leading to the suspicion that he had drowned in the mythical River of Weakness.
    太史既贵，恒思忆之。 When Xu became a high-ranking official, he often thought of his old friend.
    嗣遇册封暹罗，太史充副使，远涉海外。 Later, he was appointed as a deputy envoy to Siam and traveled far overseas.
    既竣使事，归途遇飓，覆其舟。 After completing his mission, he encountered a hurricane on the way back and his ship was wrecked.
    故事，凡奉使入海，正副使皆舆榇而行，以备不虞。 According to tradition, whenever officials were sent on missions to the sea, both the chief and deputy envoys carried coffins with them to be ready for any unforeseen circumstances.
    柩前钉一金字牌，题曰“使某国某官某公之灵”，以为识。 A golden plaque was nailed to the coffin, inscribed with the words "The Spirit of Envoy to a Certain Country, Official, and Public Servant," to identify it.
    事迫，则先卧其中，束手待毙而已。 If the situation became dire, they would first lie inside the coffin and wait for death with their hands tied.
    太史既罹水厄，无复生望，在柩中载沉载浮，听其所之，不葬鱼腹为厚幸。 Xu was in a desperate situation, with no hope of survival. He floated in the coffin, letting fate decide his outcome, and considered it a great fortune not to be buried at the bottom of the sea.
    忽闻人语曰：“此予之故人也，奚为至于此？” Suddenly, he heard someone say, "This is my old friend; why is he in this state?"
    命启其棺，太史始敢展视。 They ordered the coffin to be opened, and Xu finally dared to open his eyes and look out.

    167

    熟视之，则孙某也，羽衣星冠，仪从甚赫，己身亦在岛屿间，无复洪涛泛滥矣。亟起离榇拜谢，孙握其手曰：“贵人犯险，良不易，请即寒家小憩。”把袂以行。入岛不数武，远望朱甍碧瓦，几埒王侯。至则高閎邃宅，阍者十余人，趋前声喏。孙引太史入，至一厅事，颜曰“钓鳌”。铺设之华，目所未睹。甫揖坐，即命酒曰：“震惊之后，非此不足以镇之。”太史称谢。须臾，苾芬旋进，珍错盈席，食之鲜美，多不能举其名。其酒绀碧色，味甚醇，孙语曰：此即东海之扶桑露也。”太史惊询之，其地属高丽国界，盖已顺流而东，几千里矣，益大骇。又询孙近况，笑而不答。既而命细君来，使者即入，旋闻环珮璆然，异香袭鼻。

    Explanation

    167

    After carefully scrutinizing it, I recognized it as Sun, who appeared in a celestial robe and star crown, with an imposing retinue. Myself was also among the islands, without the raging waves anymore. I quickly got up from the coffin to bow and thank him. Sun grasped my hand and said, "It is not easy for a noble person to take risks; please come to my humble home to rest." We walked together. After entering the island not far, from afar, the red eaves and green tiles were almost on par with those of kings and princes. Upon arrival, we found a tall and deep mansion, with more than ten doorkeepers hurrying forward to greet. Sun led the historian inside, to a hall named "Fishing for the Turtle." The lavish decorations were beyond what I had ever seen. Just after bowing and sitting, he immediately ordered wine and said, "After the shock, nothing but this could calm it." The historian expressed gratitude. Soon, fragrant dishes were quickly served, and precious delicacies filled the table, the food was so fresh and delicious that many of the dishes were unfamiliar. The wine was a deep blue color, very pure in taste, and Sun explained, "This is the dew of Fushan in the East Sea." The historian was astonished and inquired about it, learning that the place belonged to the territory of Goryeo, indicating they had traveled downstream for thousands of miles, which even more terrified him. He then asked about Sun's recent circumstances, but Sun merely smiled and did not answer. Shortly after, Sun ordered his wife to come, and the messenger entered, and soon the sound of jade pendants and the scent of exotic fragrance filled the air.

    168

    有小鬟十数，皆艳妆，自屏后引丽人出，冠飞凤之冠，蹑文鸳之履，霞衣霓裳，貌甚姣好。当筵再拜，太史欲避之，孙揽其袪曰：“以君通家之故，乃令出见，何作此蓦生态？”太史小孙二岁，因以嫂礼见之。丽人拜讫，另席坐。太史以其艳绝，不敢仰视。孙乃谓丽人曰：“阿姨年已长，未择所适。此我之故友，当时之贵人也，因封王罹难，得以至此。盍字之乎？”丽人始睨太史，意似首肯。孙又曰：“此君文章事业，当代知名，卿勿误人好事。”丽人乃起曰：“阿姊性拗，容入商之，当有以报命。”遂冉冉而去。太史闻与议姻，亟辞曰：“兄言固不当违，但身任天宪，恐挂弹章，弗敢诺。

    Explanation

    168

    There were several young maids dressed in gorgeous makeup, who led out a beautiful woman from behind a screen. She wore a phoenix crown and stepped into embroidered magpie shoes, her clothes as colorful as clouds and rainbows, and she looked very beautiful. She bowed twice in front of the audience, and the historian wanted to avoid her. The younger man grabbed her sleeve and said, "Because of our familial connection, I made her come out to see you. Why this abrupt behavior?" The historian was two years younger than the younger man and therefore greeted her with the respect due to an elder sister. After the beautiful woman had finished her bows, she sat at a different seat. The historian, struck by her unparalleled beauty, dared not look up.
