The younger man then said to the beautiful woman, "Your aunt is of an age and has not yet chosen a suitable match. This gentleman is my old friend and a distinguished person of his time, who was ennobled as a prince but suffered misfortune and came to this place. Why don't you marry him?" The beautiful woman glanced at the historian and seemed to agree. The younger man added, "This gentleman is renowned for his literary and official achievements, and you should not hinder a good match." The beautiful woman then stood up and said, "My aunt has a stubborn nature. Allow me to discuss it with her, and I will have something to report back." She then slowly and gracefully left. The historian, upon hearing that they were discussing a marriage, hurriedly declined, saying, "Your words should not be disobeyed, but I am responsible for the laws of heaven and fear being accused, so I dare not agree."

    169

    ”孙笑曰：“君误矣！此地僻处海隅，不通诸邦，兄非漂没，亦不能至此。今即欲遄归复命，实难不翼而飞。若俟风帆，十余年不止。独居寂寞，情何以堪？且予稔知兄尚无嗣，宗祀亦重事，不可固辞。”太史究以朝命为嫌。孙曰：“苏武持节，亦曾娶妇，兄独未之闻耶？”正纷议未决，丽人复出曰：“珊珊诺矣，一惟姊丈之命是听。”孙因笑指丽人，谓太史曰：“此即若人之妹也。君熟视之，较之故乡人何如耶？”太史意乃定，复以姻娅礼拜孙夫妇，咸大悦，呼之以许郎，盖从女家称之也。丽人遂同坐谈宴，至夕，孙曰：“今宵时日不吉，且许郎初经危险，神气未复，嘉礼须俟来朝。

    Explanation

    169

    Sun laughed and said, "You are wrong! This place is remote and located in a coastal area, not connected to other nations. If you were not shipwrecked, you would not have been able to come here. Now, if you wish to return quickly to report, it is indeed difficult to do so without wings. If you wait for favorable winds to sail, it will take more than ten years. Living alone in solitude, how can one bear it? Moreover, I know well that you still have no heir, and the continuation of the ancestral line is a serious matter, which cannot be firmly declined." The Grand Historian still had reservations about the imperial command. Sun said, "Su Wu, who held the staff of office, also married a wife. Have you not heard of this?" While the discussion was still ongoing, the beautiful woman reappeared and said, "Shanshan agrees, and will only follow the orders of her elder brother-in-law." Sun then laughed and pointed to the beautiful woman, saying to the Grand Historian, "This is your sister. Look at her closely, how does she compare to the people from your hometown?" The Grand Historian's mind was then decided, and he again bowed to Sun and his wife in the manner of a relative. Both were very pleased, and called him 'Xu Lang,' which is the term used by the family of the woman. The beautiful woman then sat down to talk and dine together. By evening, Sun said, "Tonight is not auspicious, and moreover, Xu Lang has just gone through danger, and his spirit has not yet recovered. The wedding ceremony must wait until tomorrow."

    170

    ”乃送太史于堂侧精室，己亦命仆襆被来，曰：“久不与故人抵足矣，今当小罄别悰。”遂联床共卧。盖孙偕伴入洋，亦遭沉舟之厄，正漂流间，遇一叟拯之，以杖挽之，旋登是岛。喜其风雅，赘之为婿。岳任姓，即东海钓鳌者也。有二女，长曰瑟瑟，幼曰珊珊。因厌尘嚣，故以瑟瑟字孙，而属其家，乃去遨游六极，不再返。孙主其家政，更从妻参道，少知去来事。是夕，历历为太史述之，直至夜分始寐。翌日，发柬召客，至者数十人，皆女之戚族，半为任姓。衣制绝古，状貌奇伟，堂中箫鼓敖曹，金玉璀璨。太史仍以原衣冠与客相见，互致企仰。

    Explanation

    170

    He then escorted the historian to a refined room beside the hall, and he also ordered his servant to pack bedding and come, saying, "I have not slept with old friends for a long time, and now should a little exhaust the emotions of parting." They then shared a bed and slept together.

    It is said that Sun entered the ocean accompanied by companions, also encountered the misfortune of a sinking ship, just while drifting, encountered an old man who rescued him, with a staff, pulled him, soon ascended this island. The old man liked his elegance, married him off as a son-in-law. His father-in-law surnamed Ren, is the one who fished for a turtle in the East Sea. He has two daughters, the elder is named Sese, the younger is named Shanshan. Because tired of the noise and clamor of the world, therefore named Sese Sun, and belonged to his family, then went to wander in the six directions, no longer returned. Sun managed his household affairs, then followed his wife in studying the Dao, knew a little about matters of going and coming. That night, clearly told the historian about it, until midnight finally slept. The next day, sent out invitations to call guests, several dozen people came, all were her relatives, half were surnamed Ren. Their clothing style was extremely ancient, appearance was extraordinary and majestic. In the hall, flute and drum music and dance, gold and jade were brilliant. The historian still met with the guests in his original attire, mutually expressed respect and admiration.

    171

    良时已届，群婢拥女出堂，璎络重遮，绾金步摇，衣翠羽氅，妆束若天仙。交拜已，送入青庐，孙自张筵款诸眷。太史与女对坐，饮合欢之杯，绾同结之心。忽婢以彩笺进曰：“阿姊以郎君清华贵客，染翰凤池，催妆无诗，合卺必当有句。愿赐数行，以志今夕之胜。”太史微笑，即取笔挥曰：“别却黄麻驾彩虹，乘槎今入斗牛宫。不须更把支机赠，自有皇华傲粉红。”腕未及停，又一婢自外入曰：“大姊有诗申贺。”即出小红笺。太史读之，其诗曰：“使星耀自九重天，金屋新看结好缘。寄语刘郎须得意，桃源还胜杏花前。”诗甚清艳，太史为之搁笔。女命婢取二作，流览良久，微哂曰：“差堪伯仲，均未足惬吾意。

    Explanation

    171

    The auspicious time had arrived, and the maidservants escorted the bride out of the hall. Adorned with heavy jewelry and a golden hairpin with a tassel, she wore a green feathered cloak, dressed as if she were a celestial being. After the mutual bowing, she was sent into the bridal chamber, and Sun himself arranged the feast to entertain the guests. The historian sat opposite the bride, drinking from a cup symbolizing marital bliss and tying together their hearts. Suddenly, a maid presented a colorful note and said, "The elder sister considers the groom a distinguished and noble guest. Since there is no poem to accompany the bride's makeup, there must be a poem for the union of the cups. I hope you will grant a few lines to commemorate this night's splendor." The historian smiled and immediately took the pen and wrote, "Farewell to the yellow hemp and ride the rainbow, boarding a raft and entering the constellation of Dou and Niu. There is no need to give more gifts of support, there is already the imperial elegance that surpasses the pink." Before his wrist could rest, another maid entered from outside and said, "The elder sister has a poem to celebrate." She presented a small red note. The historian read it, and the poem said, "The star envoy shines from the nine heavens, a new alliance is seen in the golden house. Send a message to Liu Lang to be pleased, the Peach Blossom Spring is even more beautiful than before the Apricot Flower." The poem was very clear and elegant, and the historian put down his pen. The bride ordered the maid to fetch the two poems, and after reading them for a long time, she smiled slightly and said, "They are comparable, but neither fully satisfies my wishes."

    172

    ”乃以目视婢，婢进彤管，女即书曰：“倏为彩凤衔书去，旋作文鸳觅偶来。旧是紫薇花下客，挑灯试看海棠开。”太史览诗，大笑曰：“真吾敌也，然而新红可试矣！”遽命撤烛，拥女入帷，女羞涩不克自主。已而荳蔻飘香，杜鹃啼血，绸缪娇怯，欢倍生平。晨起谢孙，孙问：“夜有佳作否？”太史以三诗示之，孙莞然曰：“君才十倍曹丕，吾岂能与许争乎？”太史亦笑曰：“弟实不敢与谐任齿。”太史居岛中六年，夫妇燕婉如一日。暇则诗篇倡和，服食皆孙供具。女诞二男一女，俱已匍匐，太史忽思归，且卧病。孙知之，慰曰：“无须惆怅，闻有使命入朝鲜，今将返棹，可附舟以归。”太史冁然，疾顿愈。

    Explanation

    172

    Then, with a glance at the servant, the servant brought a red writing tube, and the woman wrote immediately, saying: "Swiftly as a colorful phoenix carries the letter away, soon as a literary mandarin duck seeks a mate. Once a guest under the purple wisteria, light the lamp to test if the crabapple blooms." The historian read the poem and laughed, saying, "Truly my equal, but the new red can be tested!" He quickly ordered the candle to be removed and entered the curtain with the woman. The woman was shy and could not control herself. Soon, the fragrance of young orchids wafted, and the cuckoo cried with blood. Their intimate and shy relationship was filled with profound joy, more than she had ever known in her life. In the morning, she rose to thank Sun. Sun asked, "Did you have any good works last night?" The historian showed him the three poems, and Sun smiled, saying, "Your talent is ten times that of Cao Pi; how could I compete with Xu Gan?" The historian also laughed, saying, "I really dare not compare myself to you in terms of age." The historian stayed on the island for six years, and the couple remained as affectionate as ever. In their spare time, they exchanged poems, and all their needs were provided by Sun. The woman gave birth to two sons and one daughter, all of whom had begun to crawl. The historian suddenly felt homesick and fell ill. Sun knew this and comforted him, saying, "There is no need to be sad. I have heard that there is a mission returning from Korea, and you can travel with them back home." The historian smiled, and his illness suddenly improved.

    173

    孙为具小艇，载皊粮，择吉令行。太史与女诀别，执手呜咽。孙曰：“君姑锦旋视妻孥，无用依依，五年后当有会期。”太史遂登舟。至海中，船轻而不溺，一日夜遂抵海邦。适皌轩出境，逆语之。中有太史同年某，见之大惊，曰：“君之正使某公，遇救得不死。复命时，言君之尸榇无获。君固无恙耶？”太史匿其情，惟云：“漂至海岛，赖居民捞救，无力难归。今闻敕使东来，故愿望见。”众皆喜，为太史易衣，遂伺回朝。部议以太史犯难有功，擢给谏。家人始知其尚在，平其冢，焚其榇，阖门称庆。后值五年之期，果有高丽之使。家人皆不欲，太史力请于廷，又乘传以出。使事毕，暴卒于舟。既殓，棺轻于纸，异而启之，空空如也，盖随孙仙去云。

    173

    The historian prepared a small boat, loaded it with provisions, and chose an auspicious day to depart. He bid farewell to his wife, holding hands and weeping. Sun said, "You may return in glory to see your wife and children, do not cling to me; we shall meet again in five years." The historian then boarded the boat. Upon reaching the sea, the boat was light and did not sink, and within a day and night they arrived at a coastal country. Just as the imperial envoy was about to leave the country, he met the historian. Among the envoy's retinue was a colleague of the historian named Sun, who was greatly surprised to see him and said, "Your chief envoy, Mr. So-and-so, was rescued and survived. When he returned to report, he said that your coffin was not found. Are you really unharmed?" The historian concealed the truth and only said, "I drifted to an island, relying on the locals to rescue me. I lack the means to return home. I heard that the imperial envoy was coming east, so I wished to see him." Everyone was pleased and provided the historian with new clothes, and he waited for the envoy to return to the capital. The court discussed the matter and promoted the historian to the position of censor for enduring hardship with merit. The historian's family learned that he was still alive, leveled his grave, burned his coffin, and celebrated throughout the household. After five years, as predicted, there was indeed an envoy from Goryeo. The historian's family did not want him to go, but he strongly requested permission from the court and traveled by official relay. Upon completing his mission, he suddenly died on the boat. After the funeral, the coffin was lighter than paper, which was unusual, and when opened, it was empty, as if nothing was there. It is said that he followed the immortal Sun.

    174

    外史氏曰：君言不宿于家，未及反命，良不宜有此事。但封王之臣，经历险远，蹈不测之渊，凡汩没而死者，不一其人。今得闻此，亦必少慰于泉下。
    　　随园老人曰：许太史风雅绝伦，闻其使暹罗，甚持大体。迨及覆没，杳无回音。丁令威之化鹤久矣，此殆托以慰之耶！

    ○　白衣庵
    　　贵阳有熟苗，其名曰亚九，姓辜氏。勇力善斗，矫捷绝伦，俨然一苗也。乃其母则非苗，实为江左名娼，有宦于黔者，买以随任，家室妒不能容，乘官他出，赐配于苗。生一子，即亚九，故其貌不肖父而肖母。及长，色冠一方，美播遐迩。时大理某宦有名班，因谄其父以重金，罗而致之。亚九遂为优于滇，声容并妙，名擅梨园。每一讴，座客争为缠头，诸伶咸愧其不及。年十七，颇存壮志，不以柔媚自甘。一日演《泣鱼记》于乡，亚九扮龙阳君，大为假楚王所窘，不胜忿忿。至夜，乘其醉，手刃之，亡命入蜀，转折至秦。

    Explanation

    174

    Translation:

    The historian says: The lord's words did not stay at home, and he did not return to report before his mission was completed, which is indeed not appropriate for this situation. However, for a vassal who was granted the title of king, traveling through dangerous and distant places, facing unpredictable dangers, those who drowned and died were not few. Now, to hear this, he must be somewhat comforted in the netherworld.

    The elder of the Su Garden says: Xu the historian was unparalleled in elegance and refinement. It is said that when he was sent as an envoy to Siam, he maintained a dignified demeanor. Until his downfall, no word returned. The legend of Ding Lingwei, who transformed into a crane and returned after many years, suggests that this might be a way to comfort him.

    White Robe Temple

    In Guiyang, there are well-integrated Miao people. One of them is named Ya Jiu, with the surname Gu. He is brave and skilled in combat, extraordinarily agile and swift, truly a Miao person. However, his mother is not Miao; she was a famous courtesan from the south. A government official in Guizhou bought her to accompany him on his post. His wife was jealous and could not tolerate her, so when the official went out on official business, he gave her to a Miao man. They had a son, who is Ya Jiu. Thus, his appearance resembles his mother rather than his father. When he grew up, his beauty was unmatched in the region and spread far and wide. At that time, a certain official in Dali had a famous troupe. He flattered the father with a large sum of money and brought Ya Jiu to him. Ya Jiu became a renowned performer in Yunnan. His voice and appearance were both excellent, and he was famous in the theater. Every time he sang, the audience competed to give him gifts. All the other performers were ashamed of their inability to match him. At seventeen, he had great ambitions and did not content himself with being gentle and charming. One day, while performing The Weeping Fish in a village, Ya Jiu played the role of Longyang Jun. He was greatly humiliated by the false King of Chu and became very angry. That night, while the king was drunk, Ya Jiu killed him, fled to Sichuan, and eventually reached Qin.

    175

    每言曰：“大丈夫以须眉之身为巾帼之态，既已辱人，况复受狂且轻薄耶？”因是不再业歌，人亦无知其优者。资用乏绝，乞食于市，有道者见而愀然曰：“子有大难临身，何犹坦率如此？能从予往，或可以逃。”亚九故弗信，又念黄冠者鳏处，倘遇如玉之姿，必将染指而后已，遂不答，夷然自行。居无何，群丐悦其色，醉以酒，将共嬲之。亚九素有戒心，因大怒，立毙二人，乘宵遁去。及明，余丐首诸官，捕捉甚急。亚九大惧，伏于榛莽中不敢行，枵腹终日，病不能兴。至夕月上，力疾而前，忽见向之道者，贸然而来。亚九不及避，因其前知，俯伏乞命。

    Explanation

    175

    He would often say, "A true man should not adopt a feminine demeanor with his masculine body. It has already disgraced himself, and to be further subjected to the disrespectful and frivolous behavior of others is even more shameful?" Because of this, he no longer pursued his career in singing, and no one knew of his talents. His resources were exhausted, and he began begging in the marketplace. A wise man saw him and looked solemnly, saying, "You are facing a great calamity. Why are you still so open and straightforward? If you follow me, you might be able to escape." Ya Jiu did not believe him, and he also thought of the Taoist priest living alone, if he meets a beautiful woman, he will surely take advantage of her. He did not answer and calmly walked away. After a short while, a group of beggars were attracted to his appearance, got him drunk with alcohol, and planned to molest him. Ya Jiu had a usual caution, became angry, killed two people, and fled at night. By morning, the remaining beggars reported to the authorities, who captured him urgently. Ya Jiu was terrified, hid in the bushes, and could not move, going hungry all day and being too sick to get up. By evening, as the moon rose, he moved forward despite his illness and suddenly saw the wise man who came unexpectedly. Ya Jiu did not have time to avoid, because he knew the wise man in advance, and knelt down to plead for his life.

    176

    道者一目即笑曰：“忠言逆耳，反以见疑。今急而求我，无能为矣。”亚九益顿首。道者徐曰：“与若有良缘，诚难固却。”乃引与疾行，至一土窟，使入，曰：“此中食物皆备，可自制炊。俟发长尺余，当相见也。”言已，即自去，亦不强之。亚九自思，弗入亦死，苟得容身之所，幸免一时，固愈于束手待执耳，因俯躬而入。其中绝宽大，约可数楹，床榻俱以土筑成，衾褥亦具。旁有侧户，窥之，米麦堆积。乃大悦，日食以三，暇则默坐。道者亦不复来，亚九心益安，惟冀其发速长。年余，几盈尺，窟后故有池，因勤沐之。再阅一稔，发已披肩。

    Explanation

    176

    The Daoist immediately smiled and said, "Sincere advice is hard to hear and is often met with suspicion. Now that you are in a hurry and seek me, I cannot help you." Ya Jiu bowed deeply. The Daoist then said, "If there is a good fate, it is truly difficult to refuse." He then led him to walk quickly, to a cave, and made him enter. He said, "All the food is prepared here, and you can cook it yourself. Wait until your hair grows a foot long, and we will meet again." After saying this, he left on his own, without forcing him to stay. Ya Jiu thought to himself, if he did not enter, he would die, but if he could find a place to live, he might be fortunate enough to avoid immediate capture, which would be better than passively waiting to be caught. So, he bowed and entered. Inside, the space was extremely spacious, about the size of several rooms. The beds and mats were all made of earth, and the quilts and pillows were also provided. There was a side door, and looking through it, you could see rice and wheat piled up. He was greatly pleased, eating three times a day, and sitting in silence when idle. The Daoist did not come again, and Ya Jiu's heart became even more at ease. He only hoped that his hair would grow quickly. After more than a year, it almost reached a foot in length. There was a pool behind the cave, and he bathed frequently. After another year, his hair had grown to his shoulders.

    177

    又数月，道者始至，见之，笑曰：“以是云游天下，乃可无患。”即启袭出一衲衣，与之著，更付以棕垫，使从己出。是岁亚九仅二旬，而颐养之后，貌更润泽。随道者东出函关，募于城市，人见之，疑道者以女自随，纷纷私议，道者不安于心。行及睢阳，遣之曰：“予精于风鉴，仅能知人，非有其他异术也。前见子气色晦暗，知有祸临，遂以一念之仁，免子于厄。今以子为道侣，颇致惊疑，反将重为予累，子盍行。”亚九闻言，大骇，流涕不去。道者笑曰：“吾视子印堂紫气，当有奇逢。行矣，勿自误。”遂以千钱付之，使为资斧。晨出传舍，即判袂，亚九不能恋恋，亦自行。

    Explanation

    177

    Several months later, the hermit finally arrived, saw him, and smiled, saying, "If you travel the world in this way, you will be free from harm." He immediately opened his bundle and took out a monk's robe, gave it to him to wear, and also gave him a mat to sit on, telling him to follow him. That year, Ya Jiu was only twenty, but after being nurtured, his appearance became even more lustrous. He followed the hermit eastward out of the Pass of Hangu, begging in the city. People saw him and thought the hermit was traveling with a woman, whispering among themselves. The hermit felt uneasy in his heart. They traveled to Suizhou, where the hermit sent him away and said, "I am skilled in fortune-telling, but I can only know people; I have no other extraordinary skills. Before, I saw that your complexion was dark and dull, and I knew that misfortune was about to come, so with a single act of kindness, I saved you from disaster. Now that you are traveling with me as a companion, it has caused quite a bit of surprise and suspicion, and it will only become a burden for me. You should go." Upon hearing this, Ya Jiu was greatly startled and wept, refusing to leave. The hermit smiled and said, "I see that your forehead is filled with purple qi, indicating that you will have a special encounter. Go now, do not harm yourself." He then gave him a thousand coins to use as provisions. They parted ways in the morning at the inn, and Ya Jiu, unable to cling, also left.

    178

    由南而北，将及汝上，而所赠为之一空，因效道者状，坐而行乞。自朝至于日昃，绝无一文相施者，而聚观之人，且多颐指而目语。亚九反大恐，将起而去之，忽见一叟蹒跚行步过其前，数数目之。亚九视其人，年约五旬，色白皙而颏下无寸髭，颇类宦竖，因前而求其施舍。叟微笑不言，惟以手相招，意使随行，如肯布施者。亚九大喜，毅然从之。出邑东行里许，天已昏暝，叟始与之语，诘所自来，音虽苍老，实近妪，然自肩而前，辫发斑白，又垂垂于后。亚九亦莫辨雌雄，姑以权词答之。又行二里，甫抵其居。则非宅第，实兰若。

    Explanation

    178

    Ah Jiu was traveling from the south to the north, approaching the upper region of the town, when all the gifts he had were suddenly depleted. He then imitated the appearance of a Daoist hermit, sitting and begging. From morning until noon, no one gave even a single coin, and the gathering crowd mostly communicated through nods and glances. Ah Jiu was greatly frightened and was about to get up and leave, when suddenly he saw an old man limping past him, staring at him several times. The old man quite resembled a eunuch. Ah Jiu looked at the old man, who was about fifty years old, with fair skin and no beard below his chin. He seemed to be a eunuch. Ah Jiu approached him and asked for alms. The old man smiled without speaking and only gestured with his hand, inviting Ah Jiu to follow him as if he were willing to give alms. Ah Jiu was greatly pleased and resolutely followed him. They went east of the town for a few li, and it had already become dark. The old man then spoke to him, asking where he came from. His voice, though hoarse, was actually close to that of a woman. However, from his shoulder upwards, he had gray, braided hair, which hung down behind him. Ah Jiu could not tell if he was male or female, so he answered with a cautious response. They walked another two li and finally reached his residence, which was not a mansion but a temple.

    179

    月下视之，榜曰“白衣庵”，乃优尼之所栖也。亚九愕然，叟即延之同入。中庑供大士象，侧屋亦有十余。甫入门，叟即大呼曰：“又得一活宝来，可消数十长夜，汝曹真坐而安享矣。”语未已，妖尼五六辈，皆自室中出。说笑生春，第以手脱叟之帽曰：“老不羞自行觅汉，反向人有德色耶？”亚九惊顾之，乃光头如瓠，嫩发微白，其先之垂者，系假借焉。心知为淫媾之区，殊亦不惧，反大笑。老尼又语众曰：“郎尚忍饥，可速具一餐来。”众皆噭应，纷然自去。老尼迓之入秘室，已易衣相对。坐有顷，酒肴罗列。亚九饭已，团聚欢饮。睨之，诸尼色皆平等，惟一人独艳绝，亚九为之首肯。

    Explanation

    179

    Under the moonlight, he viewed it, and the signboard read "White Robe Hermitage," indicating it was the dwelling place of virtuous nuns. A Jiu was startled, and the old man invited him to enter together. In the central hall, there was an image of the Great Sage, and there were also over ten side rooms. As soon as they entered, the old man shouted loudly, "Another live treasure has come, enough to pass many long nights, you truly can enjoy yourselves sitting here." Before the words were finished, five or six nuns with a mysterious appearance came out from the rooms. They talked and laughed, bringing spring to life, and simply took off the old man's hat, saying, "Old man, you are not ashamed to find a man yourself, but instead show a look of virtue to others?" A Jiu was startled and looked at them; they had shaved heads like gourds, with slightly white hair, and the longer hair was fake. Knowing it was a place for lewd acts, he was not afraid at all, but laughed instead. The old nun then told the others, "The young man is still hungry, please prepare a meal quickly." Everyone shouted in agreement and went away hurriedly. The old nun led him into a secret room, and they had already changed clothes to face each other. They sat for a while, and wine and dishes were arranged. After A Jiu finished eating, they gathered and drank happily. Looking around, all the nuns had similar appearances, except one who was exceptionally beautiful, making A Jiu nod in approval.

    180

    因计精力无多，秃娼倘更番迭进，予身将葬于庵中，何能生返，度必临之以威，异日乃可以自立。计定，漏下已二鼓，诸尼皆起而求欢，亚九姑从之，裸衣征逐，往过来续。是夕狂淫无度，达旦始眠。亚九自觉惫甚，而决计倍亟。次日，老尼与众谋，以亚九有发，使更衣妆，诡称初来求度者。亚九亦听之，且夙曾习此，稍一回思，尽得其态，即使有人见之，亦不能料。诸尼因大悦，益相庆幸。至夕，又聚而宣淫，亚九已留意，预藏短梃，置之袖中。将寝，忽大言曰：“汝曹实类嫫母，而强思与予为欢，予诚不愿。必欲予留，惟若人差可共栖，余皆各归乃室，俟至旬日，略一波及。

    Explanation

    180

    Because I calculated that my energy is limited, if the courtesans take turns in succession, my body will be buried in the temple, how can I return alive? I suspect they will use force on me, only then will I be able to stand on my own. The plan was set, and the second drum had already struck. All the nuns arose to seek pleasure, and Ya Jiu followed them, naked and chasing after each other, coming and going continuously. That night, they indulged wildly and unrestrainedly in sexual acts until dawn before sleeping. Ya Jiu felt very exhausted but determined to double the effort. The next day, the old nun and the others plotted, as Ya Jiu had hair, to change clothes and makeup, falsely claiming to be a newcomer seeking ordination. Ya Jiu also agreed, having practiced this before, with a slight recollection, fully grasping the demeanor, even if someone saw her, they could not guess. The nuns were delighted and even more pleased. By evening, they gathered again to indulge in sexual acts, and Ya Jiu had already been paying attention, prepared to hide a short stick, placing it in her sleeve. As they were about to sleep, she suddenly spoke loudly: "You all resemble the ugly woman Momo, and forcibly trying to please me, I truly do not wish to. If you insist on keeping me, only that person is somewhat acceptable to share a dwelling with, the rest should return to their own rooms, until a ten-day period, a slight wave of interaction."

    181

    不用吾命，则挞之。”语竟，独挽艳者，麾众使退。众皆失色，大有醋意，而老尼尤不服，哓哓与争。亚九出梃一击，中其肩，仆地不能起，众始知其武勇，股栗莫敢前。亚九又持梃驱之，命负老尼亟去，竟无一人敢复留。亚九长笑数声，掩其扉，与艳者共枕，温柔旖旎，乐且未央。艳者忽叹曰：“妾与君祸不远矣。”亚九惊诘之，则曰：“此媪既妒且毒，其徒莫有违逆者。今既受君之创，必将甘心于予。平明即号召乡邻，诬妾背师行秽，有犯清规，其檀越又皆大力者，官必左袒，命将毙于杖下矣。”亚九恍然曰：“是予过也，然以一身当众淫妖，实有所不堪。”已而哂曰：“此尼有犯淫戒，诛之当无罪。

    Explanation

    181

    If you do not follow my command, I will whip you." After finishing speaking, he alone took the beautiful woman and waved his hand to make the crowd retreat. All turned pale, showing a lot of jealousy, and the old nun was particularly displeased, arguing persistently. Ya Jiu took out a stick and struck, hitting her shoulder, and she fell to the ground and could not get up. The crowd began to know his martial courage, trembling and dared not approach. Ya Jiu again took out a stick and drove them away, ordering them to carry the old nun away quickly, and finally, no one dared to stay. Ya Jiu laughed loudly several times, closed the door, and shared a bed with the beautiful woman. The atmosphere was gentle and beautiful, and they were happy and not yet finished. The beautiful woman suddenly sighed and said, "I and you are not far from disaster." Ya Jiu was startled and asked her why, and she replied, "This old woman is both jealous and poisonous, and her followers have no one who defies her. Now she has been injured by you, and she will surely be satisfied with me. At dawn, she will call upon the neighbors, accusing me of betraying my teacher and behaving immorally, violating the clear rules. Her patrons are all powerful people, and officials will surely favor her. They will order me to be beaten to death." Ya Jiu realized, "It is my fault, but to face many lustful and evil people alone is truly unbearable." He then laughed and said, "This nun has violated the sexual precepts, and to kill her would be without sin."

    182

    ”径起，周室巡视，有厨刀，适曾用以削瓜者，即仗之，拔关以出。艳者犹未料其肆暴也，俄闻娇啼号救，乃大惊，着衣而起。将出视，来逾门限，亚九早血刃而返，谓艳者曰：“除子之患，乃可以高枕无忧。”艳者骇然诘其故，亚九言：“如卿所料，群秃方聚而谋我。已有一尼启外户，似往驰报，予先刃之，次入室中，尽杀无遗。至此始快人心。”艳者闻言大怖，肌战汗淫，良久始能言。谓亚九曰：“君何凶暴至此？令人心胆俱落。明日事觉，将若何？”亚九笑曰：“与汝偕逋，夫复何虑？”艳者摇首曰：“不可也。

    Explanation

    182

    A woman jumped up and began patrolling the Zhou dynasty, noticing a kitchen knife that had just been used to cut a melon. She took it up immediately, broke open the door, and stepped out. The beautiful woman had not anticipated such violence, but soon heard a soft cry for help. She was startled and quickly got dressed. She was about to go out to see what was happening, but Suba returned early with a bloody knife and said to the beautiful woman, "I have eliminated your trouble, so now you can sleep soundly without worry." The beautiful woman was startled and asked the reason, and Suba replied, "As you suspected, the bald ones were gathering to plot against me. One nun had opened the outer door, as if to go and report, so I killed her first. Then I entered the room and killed everyone without leaving anyone alive. Only now did I feel satisfied." The beautiful woman was terrified upon hearing this, her muscles trembling and sweat pouring down. After a long while, she could speak again. She said to Suba, "How violent are you? Making my heart and courage fall. What will happen if the matter is discovered tomorrow?" Suba smiled and said, "Let's run away together, what else do we have to worry about?" The beautiful woman shook her head and said, "No."

    183

    妾与君行踪诡异，何能行？”亚九笑曰：“汝诚中智以下者。向见老尼以冠服行市上，人莫能识。今其具尚在，汝盍效之，予仍以女妆相随，矫称夫妇，又熟能辨之？”艳者顿悟，不得已而从其说，破笥出衣，尽更被服。亚九视之，固翩翩一美少年，因为之鼓掌。惟发色不类，亚九剪诸顶上，制而悬之，亟焚其旧者，曰：“勿使人窥见底里。”己故不烦改易，草草梳裹，即启行，席卷尼室所有一切金帛，尽携以去。其庵中故有蹇卫，艳者与亚九各乘其一，又以其一载辎重。比出庵门，天仅昧爽，人皆寂无知者。途次，艳者始自述：其身刘姓，父母皆汝人，幼失怙恃，遂入庵中，及笄，始披剃。

    Explanation

    183

    Our mysterious movements make it difficult to travel," the woman said. Yaji smiled and replied, "You truly lack the intelligence of an average person. I once saw an old nun wearing men's clothes in the market, and no one could recognize her. Now that the outfit is still there, why don't you imitate her? I will still follow you in female attire, pretending to be a couple. Who would be able to tell the difference?" The beautiful woman suddenly understood and had no choice but to follow his suggestion. She opened the chest and took out the clothes, changing into them completely. Yaji saw her and thought she was a handsome young man indeed, so he clapped his hands in approval. Only the hair color was different, so Yaji cut it short and made it into a topknot, hanging it up. He quickly burned the old hair, saying, "Don't let anyone see the truth." Without further ado, he hastily combed and wrapped her hair, and they set off immediately, taking all the money and silk from the nun's room and carrying it away. There were two donkeys at the temple, and the beautiful woman and Yaji each rode one, while the other carried their luggage. When they left the temple, it was just dawn, and no one knew about it. On the way, the beautiful woman began to tell her own story: She is surnamed Liu, her parents were from Ru, she lost her parents at a young age, and so she entered the temple. When she reached the age of marriage, she was ordained.

    184

    初亦恶尼之淫秽，既而不能独清，遂亦失身，然所遇则止一二也。又言老尼性淫，因色衰，不克自致，故遣我辈固结人欢。然必餍足其欲，而后分甘。其初误入者，多不能返，因知戒，不恒来。老尼又为此谲智，乔扮暮出，遇有无籍如君辈，诱之入庵，务至死而后已。前后化者已九人，与君而十矣。非君知几，当亦不免。亚九因笑曰：“若然，则尼之死，亦鬼之假手于我以泄其愤也。”遂与刘谋，日止一食。薄暮，宿于途次，己故为羞态，先入室中，饮食皆刘自传送，佣保者概不令入。人因窃笑，而不意其为规避焉。

    Explanation

    184

    Initially, he also detested the nun's licentiousness. But soon he could no longer remain pure alone, and thus he also lost his virtue. However, the encounters were only one or two. She also said that the old nun was promiscuous. Because she had lost her beauty, she could not attract herself, therefore, she sent us to secure human pleasure. However, she would only share after satisfying her own desires. Those who initially got involved could not return because they learned to avoid it, they did not come often. The old nun devised this cunning plan. She would disguise herself and go out at night, encountering people like you who had no fixed status, luring them into the temple, going to any length until they died. Before you, nine people had already been transformed; with you, it makes ten. If you had not been perceptive, you would not have escaped. Ajiu then laughed and said, "If so, then the nun's death was also a ghost using me to vent its anger." So he and Liu planned to eat only once a day. At dusk, they stayed at a place along the way. He pretended to be embarrassed and entered the room first. All the food and drink were sent by Liu. Servants and retainers were not allowed to enter. People would secretly laugh, but they did not realize it was a way to avoid the temple.

    185

    未明即行，长裙蔽足，犹有所虑，乃命刘密制女舄，尽夜而成。己遂削木为寸趾，裹以膝裤，盛以莲钩，缚束于足下。踏之步履，竟能如飞，盖其为优时，熟习之长技也。刘故未见，不胜惊奇。由是坦然前进，且不由径而行。所过镇市，追捕亦甚汹汹，言汝宁白衣庵，杀死女尼甚众，内一尼窃资而逃，必与凶身偕遁，勒限悬格，通省缉拿。见亚九与刘，亦间有疑忌，无如雌雄倒置，女既衣冠济楚，乌发拖巾，男又蝉皒蛾眉，纤距宛若，遂无自究诘。由齐入晋，谋所攸居。刘因使亚九为婿，而己仍蓄发，返其故吾。亚九不欲曰：“予昔在陕，曾为不法，迄今捕者无虚日，密迩接壤，未可以面目示人。

    Explanation

    185

    Before dawn, she set out, her long skirt covering her feet, still feeling some concern. She then ordered Liu to secretly make a pair of women's shoes, which were completed all night. She then cut wood into inch-long toes, wrapped them with knee pants, held them in a lotus hook, and tied them securely to her feet. Step with them, and one can walk as if flying, because it was a skill she was well-versed in during her time as an actress. Liu could not help but be surprised, and from then on, she proceeded calmly, taking a path not commonly used.

    In the towns and markets they passed, the pursuit was also very intense. They said that in the White Robe Temple in Ruyi, many nuns were killed. Among them, one nun stole money and escaped, and she must have fled with the main culprit. A deadline was set and a reward of 3,000 taels of silver was posted, seeking arrest throughout the province. Seeing Yaji and Liu, there was also some suspicion, but because the roles were reversed, the woman was dressed neatly, with black hair and a headscarf, the man had delicate eyebrows, and slender feet that seemed like those of a bird, so they did not investigate further.

    From Qi to Jin, planning where to settle, Liu then arranged for Yaji to marry her, and she still kept her hair, returning to her former self. Yaji did not want to and said, "I was once in Shaanxi and committed illegal acts, and since then, the police have been chasing me without a break. Being close to the border, I cannot show my face."

    186

    况若发亦难骤长，反启人疑，何如予妇而若夫，得以长此无患乎？”刘韪其言，遂不复易，卜居于绵山之下。亚九反穿其耳，加环填焉，日处闺中，绝类少妇。盖既柔媚其容，又复轻盈其态，以氍毹之袅娜，作绣阁之妖娆，不学而工，反若过之。其声容笑貌，无一而不神似。刘又携诸尼积蓄，不下千金，出则轻裘骏马，入则缓带峨冠。且幼时从师，遍历阀阅，谈笑夙所擅长，故虽弱不胜衣，人反目为书生本色。况已密留顶发，直接弁髦，纵令落帽风前，而有目者胥如无目矣。居晋数年，产业饶裕。刘已生二子，惟临蓐之月，托言卧疾，余则出入如常。

    Explanation

    186

    If the hair is also difficult to grow quickly, instead of arousing suspicion, how about giving the wife and the husband, so they can continue without trouble?" Liu agreed with the statement, and did not change his mind, choosing to live in the area below Mount Mian. The woman named Ya Jiu reversed the piercing of her ear, adding rings and filling them, living in the inner chamber every day, resembling a young woman extremely well. She was soft and charming in appearance, and light and graceful in demeanor, creating the allure of the embroidered chamber with the gracefulness of the rug. She was skilled without learning, as if surpassing it. Her voice, appearance, smile, and expression, none of which did not resemble her spiritually. Liu also carried the accumulated savings of the nuns, not less than a thousand gold coins, wearing a light fur coat and a fast horse when going out, and a loose belt and a tall hat when entering. Moreover, he had traveled through the ranks of nobility since his youth, being skilled in conversation and humor, so although he was too weak to support the weight of clothing, people regarded him as having the true nature of a scholar. The woman had secretly kept the top hair, directly connecting to the hairpin, even if the hat fell off in the wind, those with eyes were as if they had no eyes. After living in Jin for several years, their property and wealth were abundant. Liu had already given birth to two sons, only during the month of childbirth, pretending to be ill, otherwise, he went out and came in as usual.

    187

    人遂谓其母所生，而不知实父所诞也。晋人故习于俭，亚九亦从不蓄婢，家惟一二佣人，无事亦莫能入内，其踪迹可谓隐秘。乃贵阳汝宁及陕之捕役，因亚九不获，案莫能结，家属多瘐死于狱，因而天怒难回，王法莫逭。忽于丙子岁，微露其形。盖缘二子稍长，不时游戏于门，亚九不能随，又甚溺爱，遂顿忘忌讳，欲置一媪，使视其儿。适里中有售身者，以十金纳之，刘故亟谏不听也。夜则使媪寝于外，而自阖重扉，不复令入。媪故有心人，颇为疑讶。一夕，夜出便溺，瞥见中门失闭，乃窃喜，悄然径入。室中犹秉烛，因潜窥之，夫妇方豷沓交欢，儿宿于旁。时正溽暑初收，皆白身无寸缕。

    Explanation

    187

    People thus believed that he was born of his mother, but they did not know that he was actually born by his father. The people of Jin were accustomed to frugality, and Yajiu also never kept a concubine; his household only had one or two hired servants, and even if there was no business, none could enter the inner chambers, making his traces quite secret. Thus, the officials from Guiyang, Rulin, and Shanxi, because Yajiu was not captured, the case could not be concluded, and many family members died in prison, leading to the anger of heaven being hard to turn back, and the law being unavoidable. Suddenly, in the year Bingsi, his form was slightly revealed. It is because the two children grew up a bit, playing outside the door, and Yajiu could not follow, and he was very indulgent, thus suddenly forgetting about taboos, wanting to hire a woman to watch over his children. There happened to be a woman selling herself in the neighborhood, and he paid ten taels of silver for her. Liu, therefore, urgently advised but was not listened to. At night, he made the woman sleep outside, and he closed the heavy doors himself, not allowing her to enter again. The woman, being a clever person, was quite suspicious and surprised. One night, when she went out to relieve herself, she saw that the middle door was not closed, and she secretly rejoiced and quietly entered. In the room, the candle was still lit, so she secretly peered in, and saw the couple engaged in intimate relations, with the child sleeping beside them. It was the beginning of the end of the hot and humid season, and they were all naked with not a stitch of clothing.

    188

    初亦不甚介意，及细视焉，不禁骇而欲笑。于是春光尽泄，男妇攸分，媪之惑因以顿解。趋出就室，深虑见疑，明日反托疾不起，亚九与刘果不猜忌。媪后微有漏言，闻者或以为诞。里某亦耳其异，偶向所亲述之，其人颇具远识，惊曰：“此大盗也。既居此地，不首将有害。”里甲颔之，果控诸官。时介休宰彭公讳应奎，明察吏也，知其事涉重情，乃先阴拘媪至，悉得其实。翌日，遣役守候于村，俟刘出，即执之来。视其喉无雄骨，褫衣验之，嫩乳双垂。公大怒，拟以严刑，刘惧，始罄吐其故。公念亚九必勇，非绐之当不可得，乃命役径诣其家，诡言曰：“刘某犯官前，导官怒，置之狱中，必得娘子一见。

    Explanation

    188

    Initially, she did not pay much attention, but upon closer inspection, she could not help but be shocked and want to laugh. Thus, the secret was revealed, and the distinction between men and women became clear; the old woman's confusion was suddenly resolved. She rushed out to her room, deeply fearing suspicion, and the next day, she feigned illness and did not get up. A Jiu and Liu indeed did not suspect. Later, the old woman inadvertently let slip a word, and those who heard it may have thought it was a fabrication. A villager also heard of the strangeness and occasionally told it to a close friend, who had considerable foresight and exclaimed in surprise, "This is a great thief. Having lived in this place, if not reported, it will be harmful." The village head nodded in agreement and indeed reported to the officials. At that time, the magistrate of Jiehou, Mr. Peng Yingkui, a clear-sighted official, knew that the matter involved serious circumstances, so he secretly detained the old woman and fully obtained the truth. The next day, he sent officials to guard the village and waited for Liu to come out, then arrested him. Upon examining his throat, they found no male bone, and upon undressing him for examination, they saw two drooping breasts. The magistrate became very angry and intended to apply severe punishment. Liu, fearing, finally fully confessed the truth. The magistrate thought A Jiu must be brave, and if not deceived, it would not be possible to obtain. So, he ordered officials to go directly to A Jiu's home and said deceitfully, "Liu committed an offense before the officials, provoked the officials' anger, and put him in prison. He must see the lady once."

    189

    吾曹当为之疏通。”其意似索赂者。而亚九闻刘有事，大惊失措，竟自出，将细询之。役承官命，各携瓶油，见其来，预倾于地。亚九不及知，步疾而中蹶，众役遂直前擒之。戏探其裆，盈掬者实非无物，皆嘻惊以为奇。亚九欲用武，而两臂尽伤，肘骨亦折，竟不能复逞。逮至官，以事无证据，极口称冤。公乃下之狱，榜示通衢，不匝月而三捕皆至，各呈文牒，公始加刑严鞫。亚九不能支，遂伏罪。公以罪应寸磔，但死者俱以淫恶自致，详请减等论决，与刘俱弃市。狱达上宪，俱色喜，公因独膺卓异。亚九死，其子犹在晋，官为具牒，解送回乡，而亚九之母犹存焉。惟汝宁白衣庵，迄今如故，行人每指以为戒。

    Explanation

    189

    We must investigate and clear this matter." His intention seemed to be to extort a bribe. When 亚九 heard that 刘 was involved in something, he was greatly shocked and at a loss what to do, and suddenly appeared, intending to inquire in detail. The officials, following the official's orders, each carried bottles of oil and, upon seeing him come, poured it in advance on the ground. 亚九 was unaware and, walking quickly, suddenly tripped. The officials then seized him immediately. They playfully searched his crotch, and what was grasped in handfuls was not actually empty, and all were surprised and amused, thinking it strange. 亚九 wanted to use force, but both his arms were severely injured, and his elbow bones were also broken, leaving him unable to act again. When he reached the official, there was no evidence for the matter, and he cried out in great distress, claiming innocence. The official sent him to prison and posted notices in the main streets. Within a month, all three captors arrived, each presenting documents, and the official then applied harsh punishment and interrogated rigorously. 亚九 could not bear it and confessed guilt. The official decided that the punishment should be by slicing, but since the dead were all self-inflicted wickedness, he requested a detailed review and reduction of the sentence, and both 亚九 and 刘 were publicly executed. The case reached the higher authorities, who all looked pleased, and the official was uniquely recognized for excellence. After 亚九's death, his son was still in 晋, and the official prepared a document to send him back to his hometown, while 亚九's mother still lived. Only the White Robe Temple in 汝宁 remains the same to this day, and passersby often point it out as a warning.

    190

    外史氏曰：自古男女虽别，要皆蓬飞其首，以故冒为男而赝为女者，不一而足。今则不能矣。何则？雌雄之分实在于首也。发垂于后，望而知其为男；皅掩于前，见而识其为女，岂不明著哉？奈何道士无良，潜蓄凶人之发；尼师不法，全光美女之头。更有作俑之官，使混履舄于红皉：且多似瞽之夫，莫辨僧俗于白日；而亚九之诡谲得逞，刘尼之变幻难知矣。向非天夺其魄，纵集羶之蝇，可以徒死，岂逐雀之隼，皆宜无生，诸捕能无吁屈呼天耶？独是亚九之得行其智，实出于为旦之时穴处之际，故宦与道士，俱当首坐者也。

    Explanation

    190

    The Commentator said: From ancient times, men and women were distinct, yet their hairstyles were similar, like dandelion seeds floating in the air. Therefore, there were many cases of people pretending to be male while disguising themselves as female. Today, such deceptions are no longer possible. Why? Because the distinction between male and female truly lies in the hairstyle. Hair hanging down behind the head indicates a man, while hair covering the front of the face indicates a woman. How clearly defined are these distinctions! Yet, some Taoist priests, lacking virtue, secretly hide the hair of evil men; some nuns, not adhering to the law, wear the hair of beautiful women. Moreover, corrupt officials mix the shoes and sandals of the common people with those of the red-haired (a metaphor for the elite). Many are like blind men, unable to distinguish between monks and laypeople during the day. Thus, the cunning schemes of Ya Jiu succeed, and the transformations of Liu Ni are hard to understand. If it were not for heaven taking away their spirit, allowing swarms of flies to gather, they could die aimlessly, how could hawks chase sparrows, all of whom should not live? Can those who try to catch them not cry out to heaven in despair? It is precisely because Ya Jiu's schemes succeeded during the time of the morning and the cave, that eunuchs and Taoist priests both hold high positions.

    191

    ○　卷三
    　　魂灵
    　　京都有老儒，失其名，伛偻猥琐，侏儒可笑。且自耳目口鼻，以至四肢百体，无一不似有如无，人因以魂灵目之。居某巷，以授徒糊口。丙戌夏秋，都中大雨，其室为淫霖所坏，无所依栖。左近有凶宅，无人敢居，曾悬钟馗像于壁，究莫能驱，以故旷之甚久。因造请焉，欲假数椽为悬榻计。主人故识之，乃笑曰：“先生休矣！以君状貌，虽似与鬼为邻，然亦宜避而远之，何更趋而近之乎？”弗听，益固请，因许之，遂移居于其内。夜将半，果闻鬼声啾啾，毛发尽竖，惟勉诵救苦咒不辍。无何，有二人排闼径入。

    Explanation

    191

    Volume 3

    Spirit

    In the capital, there was an old scholar whose name is unknown. He was hunched over and appeared insignificant, so short that he was comical. From his ears, eyes, nose, mouth, to his limbs and entire body, he seemed almost non-existent. People called him "Spirit." He lived in a certain alley, teaching students to make a living. In the summer and autumn of 1816, the capital experienced heavy rains, and his house was destroyed by the deluge, leaving him homeless. Nearby was a haunted house, which no one dared to live in. It had a picture of Zhong Kui hung on the wall, but ultimately, it could not repel the evil spirits, and it had been empty for a long time. The old scholar went to ask the owner if he could borrow a few rooms to set up a bed. The owner knew him and said, "Forget it! Although you seem like living next to ghosts, you should avoid and stay far away from it, why do you come closer to it?" Ignoring the advice, the old scholar persisted in his request, and the owner agreed. He then moved in. When night was halfway through, he indeed heard the sound of ghosts cackling, and his hair stood on end. He persevered in reciting the mantra to save from suffering without stopping. Soon after, two people pushed open the door and entered directly.

    192

    睨之，黄瘦臃肿，不可以目。乃甫入户，即返身退却，骇曰：“咄咄怪事！”若有所辟易者。惊怖少定，更闻其语曰：“此真僬侥国疴瘘丈人，吾曹不可以复逞。”又似一人揶揄之曰：“汝不畏魁梧丈人抚剑疾视，而畏此琐琐者，是真吴牛喘月矣。虽然，吾亦胆碎，势难复留。”遂长啸而俱去。初以为佛经之力，既而知其为已，不禁引被而窃笑。竟夜安眠，声响寂然。明日出以告人，罕有信者。而居之旬余，绝无他异，乃奇之。主人欲留为久计，童蒙皆畏葸不来。爰绘其像，撒钟馗而悬之，后有居人，无不安堵。厥像至今犹在壁，山东徐明府盖曾见之。

    　　外史氏曰：人而以之镇鬼，非极英毅者不能。此独以伛偻侏儒，使数年盘踞者走而且僵，直可举天下株判投畀炎火矣。韩子云：“兼收并蓄，待无用遗。”安在僬侥国皊偻丈人，不足以收牛溲马勃之效乎？一笑。

    Explanation

    192

    Narrow-eyed, I saw a man who was emaciated and swollen, making him hard to look at. As soon as I entered the door, I immediately turned around and retreated, exclaiming, "What a strange thing!" It seemed as if he had repelled me. After my initial fright subsided, I heard him say, "This is truly a sickly old man from the land of dwarfs; we cannot take him seriously." Another person mocked him, saying, "You are not afraid of a robust old man with a sword and a stern gaze, but you fear this insignificant person, which is truly like a water buffalo panting at the moon. Although, I too am frightened, and it is impossible to stay here." They then let out a long cry and all left together. At first, I thought it was the power of Buddhist scriptures, but later realized it was just a person, and I couldn't help but laugh under the covers. I slept peacefully all night, with no sound. The next day, I told others about it, but few believed me. Living there for over ten days, there were no other strange occurrences, and I found it odd. The owner wanted to keep me there long-term, but the children were too afraid to come. I drew a picture of him, replaced the picture of Zhong Kui, and hung it up. Subsequent residents lived there without any problems. The picture is still on the wall, and Mr. Xu from Shandong province had seen it.

    The historian said: A person who can calm ghosts is not someone of extreme courage and determination. This person, however, used his hunched and short stature to make those who had been lurking there for years run away and even faint. He could have thrown all the useless things into the fire. Hanzi said, "Be inclusive and accepting, and do not leave anything useless behind." How can a hunched and short old man from the land of dwarfs not be effective in using even the most insignificant things? A smile.

    193

    ○　妒祸
    　　家有老妪，其先都中某家婢也。每言在京服役时，以其黠颇蒙主妇宠。主翁家巨富而世职，惟以无子为忧。纳一妾，既怀妊矣，主翁适以差出。濒行，顾妾谓妻曰：“此假腹之事，宜善视之。”乃主妇奇妒者也，伪应曰：“诺！”夫行，即自计曰：“此隔腹之事，吾安用之？”于是百计堕胎，幸不下。迨妾将坐蓐，密嘱此妪，无论男女辄弃之。妪素以迎合为事，及产，果乘间攘去。视之，男也，即交阍者某，使弃之于外。某初亦不平，而无如之何，适值运煤者空车驱过，委置其中。御者扬鞭去，竟不及知。主妇既得妪报，反诬妾房婢媪不加防护，致失吾儿，哭而詈之。然亦不甚究诘，转瞬而罢。反以妾为不利，甫及弥月，遣嫁出门。

    193

    ○　Dangers of Jealousy

    In one household, there was an elderly woman who had once been a maid in a certain family in the capital. She often spoke of her time serving there, mentioning that she was favored by the mistress because of her cleverness. The master’s family was wealthy and held hereditary positions, but the only worry they had was the lack of a son. The master took a concubine who became pregnant, and just as he was about to leave on official business, he looked at the concubine and said to his wife, “This is a matter of bearing a child, so you should treat her well.” However, the mistress was extremely jealous and pretended to agree, saying, “Yes!” When the husband left, she immediately thought to herself, “This is a matter of an unborn child, what use do I have for her?” She then tried every means to induce an abortion, fortunately it did not succeed. When the concubine was about to give birth, she secretly instructed the old woman to abandon the child regardless of whether it was a boy or a girl. The old woman was accustomed to pleasing others, and when the child was born, she indeed took advantage of the opportunity to take it away. Looking at it, she saw it was a boy, and immediately handed it over to a certain gatekeeper to abandon outside. The gatekeeper was initially unhappy, but he could do nothing about it. Just as a coal carrier was passing by in an empty cart, he placed the child in the cart. The driver lashed the whip and left, and did not even realize it. The mistress received the old woman’s report and then falsely accused the concubine’s maids of not protecting the child, leading to the loss of her son. She cried and cursed them, but she did not investigate thoroughly, and soon gave up. Instead, she considered the concubine to be a bad omen, and just after a month, she sent her away to be married out.

    194

    比及主翁旋归，则两望俱绝，忿极顿踣，遂以痰疾暴卒。主妇初犹不恤，洎乎丧葬事毕，别支承荫，嗣子又横暴，不善遇之，恒曰：“假腹之事媪不为，而责我隔腹之子耶？”亲族皆恶其妒，又并凌之。主妇乃大悟，密嘱前仆，使觅弃儿，竟无由可得。时已六旬余，兀坐一室，自首低垂，悔而愧，愧而哭。未几，抱恨而殁。旋闻是儿为西山素封所得，盖即煤窑主人，亦患乏嗣者。初佣人售煤而返，见车中弃儿，睡而且詈。既而熟思得策，驰归献之主人，主人大悦，厚赉之，以儿为子。及长，聪颖，遂以游泮。弃儿之仆既老，始遇煤户，访得之，然已无及矣。惟妪在吾家，年逾七十，以老疾裸而后死，两股糜烂，如被重杖，殆亦奴绝主祀之报欤？

    Explanation

    194

    By the time the master returned home, both hopes were lost. In extreme anger, he fell to the ground and died suddenly from a lung disease. Initially, the mistress did not care, but by the time the funeral arrangements were completed, another branch supported her. The heir was also cruel and violent, treating her poorly, often saying, "The old woman did not bear a child for me, so why should I care about the child in her womb?" The relatives all hated her for being jealous and further oppressed her. The mistress then greatly realized the situation, secretly instructed the former servant to find the abandoned child, but ultimately, there was no way to find it. At that time, she was over sixty years old, sat alone in a room, with her head lowered, feeling regret and shame, and crying. Not long after, she died with regret. Soon, it was heard that the child was obtained by the wealthy man of Xishan, who was the owner of the coal mine and also suffered from the lack of an heir. Initially, the hired worker returned from selling coal, saw the abandoned child in the cart, sleeping and cursing. After thinking deeply, he came up with a plan, rushed back to present it to the owner, who was very pleased and rewarded him generously, taking the child as his own son. When he grew up, he was intelligent and bright, and thus became a scholar. The servant who found the abandoned child had grown old and finally met the coal owner, finding him, but it was too late. Only the old woman was in my family, over seventy years old, dying naked due to old age and illness, her thighs rotten, as if beaten with a heavy stick. Perhaps this was the retribution for the servant cutting off the master's ancestral worship.

    195

    外史氏曰：自古妒之为祸，原不止于有此，而读此能不寒心，欲寝妒妇之皮而食其肉耶？虽然，如若人不啻亿万。苟无杵臼之义，必致若敖之馁，奈之何哉？夫纲不振而责效于银鹿樵青，恐亦过苛之论，不足以深服其心。

    ○　李念三
    　　诗言：“商人重利轻别离”，大抵晋人为然。其生也，发犹未燥即从人商贩于外，辄数年不归。其父母亦不俟子之返，即为之纳妇于家，名之曰“娶空房”。习俗不改，未知昉于何人。孟县一民家，亦沿而为之。妇年二九，美姿容，性且倜傥。于归以后，径以中馈为己任，亲顺翁姑，谐和里党，绝不儿女态。第翁以书去，初犹望子遄归，既而鳞往鸿来，则言肆主将畀以重资，使主会计，得利十分之三，因贪此不忍遽舍，故不能旋归，期亦未定何年。

    Explanation

    195

    The historian said: From ancient times, the calamity caused by jealousy has been far more severe than just the instances mentioned here. Reading this, one cannot help but feel deeply troubled and might even wish to completely destroy the jealous woman, sleeping on her skin and eating her flesh. However, such people are no less than tens of thousands. If there is no moral principle, it will inevitably lead to the famine suffered by the descendants of Ifang, what can be done about it? If the main structure is weak and one expects results from minor elements, it is likely an overly harsh criticism and insufficient to deeply convince the heart.

    ○ Li Niansan
    The poem says: "Merchants value profit and are indifferent to separation." This is generally true of people from the Jin region. They are born, their hair not yet dry, and they leave to engage in trade, often not returning for several years. Their parents, waiting for their son to return, immediately arrange for a wife to be brought into the home, calling it "marrying an empty house." The custom remains unchanged, and it is unknown who first started it. A family in Meng County also followed this practice. The wife, at the age of eighteen, was beautiful and had a bold and unrestrained personality. After her marriage, she took it upon herself to manage the household, being filial to her in-laws and harmonious with her neighbors, showing no signs of childish behavior. However, her husband left a letter saying he hoped to return quickly, but later letters indicated that the shop owner would grant him a substantial amount of capital to manage, earning a profit of one-third, and because he was greedy for this, he could not bear to leave immediately, so he could not return soon, and the time of his return was not yet determined.

    196

    妇窃闻之，心志颇灰，然在乡人，则不以为异也。里中有李念三者，不知所自来，踪迹诡异。佣于人，未尝力作，而成功反居人先。且饮食衣服，未见其经营，无少缺乏。然其貌甚粗鄙，城中女羞与为婚，以故求赘于乡，然亦无应之者。乃某家之妇，未嫁而既怨摽梅，至此又心伤杨柳，床头枕上，太息时时。翁姑因子弗归，遂不忍过责，早眠迟起，举不复诘，妇亦习以为常。一夕挑灯独绩，夜已将半，忽闻人语曰：“寝乎？”其音甚低。顾之，户竟洞开，有人悄然入。

    Explanation

    196

    I have heard of it, and her heart and will were greatly disheartened, but among the villagers, it was not considered unusual. In the village, there was a man named Li Niansan, whose origins were unknown and whose movements were strange and mysterious. He worked for others but never put in much effort, yet he succeeded before others. Moreover, his food and clothing were not seen to be managed by him, and he had no shortage of anything. However, his appearance was very coarse and unrefined, and women in the city were ashamed to marry him, so he sought to marry into the village, but no one agreed. The woman of a certain family, before she was married, already lamented the passing of her youth, and here, she was also saddened by the willow. She sighed often at her pillow. Her in-laws, because of her son's absence, did not blame her too harshly, so she went to bed early and got up late, and they did not question her anymore. The woman also became accustomed to it. One evening, she lit a lamp and worked alone, and the night was already halfway through, when suddenly, she heard someone say, "Are you sleeping?" The voice was very low. She looked, and the door was completely open, and someone quietly entered.

    197

    妇大骇，先疑为盗，无何，立于榻前，则故布衣草履，状貌可憎，即所谓李念三者。妇故熟识其人，瞿然立起，遽诘曰：“汝来奚为？”答曰：“来就寝。”妇亦大怖，几出声，强叱之曰：“此中无汝寝地，可亟归。”念三笑曰：“归固无难，但惜娘子玉貌，久虚花烛，徒担新妇之名，未识藁砧之面。愁云怨雨，渺渺无期，莺老花残，行将指日，殊令人为之长叹耳！”妇闻其言，适中隐衷，不觉潸然泣下。遂复坐，亦不挥斥令出。

    Explanation

    197

    The woman was greatly startled, first suspecting a thief, but shortly after, a figure stood before her bed, dressed in plain clothes and straw sandals, with an appearance that was ugly and repulsive. It was none other than Li Niansan, a man she well knew. Startled, she stood up and immediately questioned him, "Why have you come?" He replied, "To come and sleep." The woman was also greatly frightened, nearly screaming, and forced herself to scold him, "There is no place for you to sleep here; you should return quickly." Li Niansan smiled and said, "Returning is indeed not difficult, but I pity your beautiful appearance, which has long been without a marriage. You only bear the name of a new bride, yet do not know the face of your matchmaker. Clouds of sorrow and rain of resentment, faint and without end, old swallows and withered flowers, will soon come, truly making one sigh deeply!" The woman heard his words, which touched her hidden feelings, and unconsciously shed tears. Then she sat down again, and did not drive him away.

    198

    念三又言曰：“仆本陋貌，不堪陪侍房帷，然得识春风于荆棘之畔，犹胜抱枯蕊于空谷之中。娘子倘留意，仆愿竭尽其材，当亦无殊于两美之合也。”语次，竟逼近其身。乃妇终羞涩，情虽动而意尚游移，且系处子，无敢承迎，惟拈带微嚬而已。念三知其易与，直前持，妇觉肢体顿软，遂任其缓带松裳，相与登榻。既合，念三之身如砺石，磷磷然着肌欲破。妇殊不解，乃推之使起曰：“去，去！予宁无夫，不敢与妆为好矣！”念三笑曰：“客既登堂，忍不醉饱而去？”强之竣事，妇益不支，娇啼欲号。念三微哂曰：“他山之石犹可攻玉，汝真不可磨砻者，然亦太璞不完矣。

    Explanation

    198

    Niansan then said, "I am of humble appearance and unworthy to accompany you in the bridal chamber. However, meeting the spring breeze by the thorn bush is still better than holding a withered flower in the empty valley. If Miss would pay attention, I am willing to use all my skills, and there should be no difference from the union of two beauties." As he spoke, he advanced and approached her closely. The woman remained shy, although her feelings were moved, her mind was still hesitant, and being a maiden, she dared not accept. She only pinched her ribbon slightly. Knowing that she was easy to persuade, Niansan took her directly. The woman felt her body suddenly soften and let him loosen her belt and robe, and they climbed onto the bed together. Once they were together, Niansan's body felt like a grindstone, piercing the skin. The woman did not understand and pushed him away to get up, saying, "Go, go! I would rather have no husband than be with you!" Niansan laughed and said, "Since you have entered the hall, how can you leave without being fully satisfied?" He forced her to finish, and the woman was even more exhausted, crying softly, almost sobbing. Niansan smiled faintly and said, "A stone from another mountain can still polish jade, but you are truly unpolishable. However, you are also not a perfect unpolished jade."

    199

    ”乃抽身而起，揽衣一振，渺矣无踪。盖不独出不由户，亦无足音，妇更大惧。幸未甚至狼狈。晨起井臼如常，亦不敢泄，而心则惴惴焉，恐其复来。向晦闲立门闾，见念三匆匆经过，绝不顾瞻，遂窃喜其忘情，自以为无患。至夜将寝，有一人排闼直入，又不胜张皇。视之，则非念三，又易一人，年仅十五六，容甚秀美，被服风华。妇当惊弓之后，草木皆兵，不欲再经风雨，惟语曰：“若何人昏夜过此，宁无瓜李之嫌？请即旋返。

    Explanation

    199

    As he withdrew his body and stood up, he gathered his clothes and gave a shake, vanishing without a trace. Not only did he not go out through the door, there was also no sound of footsteps, causing the woman to become even more frightened. Fortunately, it did not reach the point of being in a great mess. In the morning, she went about her usual tasks of fetching water and grinding grain, and she dared not reveal it, but her heart was filled with anxiety, fearing that he would come back. As evening approached, she stood idly at the gate, seeing Nian San passing by in a hurry, without looking back. She secretly rejoiced that he had forgotten about her, thinking to herself that there was no longer any danger. As night approached and she was about to sleep, a person pushed open the door and entered directly, causing her to be extremely frightened. When she looked, it was not Nian San, but another person, only fifteen or sixteen years old, with a very handsome appearance, dressed in a refined and elegant manner. The woman, after being startled, saw enemies in everything, not wanting to experience such a frightening situation again, and only said, "Who are you, passing by here in the dark? Is there no suspicion of impropriety? Please return immediately."

    200

    ”其人笑曰：“名花吐艳，通国皆知，卿犹为此饰说。岂李念三未尝同榻耶？”妇默然，其人又曰：“若既尝胆，宜乎不忘其苦也。虽然，予非莽男儿，令人号泣求免者，幸无推拒。”言讫，引身与妇并，温柔软款，偎倚多情。妇之欲心已动，然以创深痛钜，究不敢轻许。其人温存至再，始与之解衣为欢，其柔腻温婉，迥殊念三。惟蓝桥玉杵，尚仍前矛，而在情荡之余，亦遂不甚艰楚。既喜其貌，兼乐其情，至此妇亦几无以自主矣！欢娱达旦，其人辞去。

    Explanation

    200

    The man laughed and said, "A famous flower blooms, known throughout the country, yet you still make excuses. Hasn't Li Niansan ever slept in the same bed with you?" The woman remained silent. The man then said, "If you have already tasted gall, it is right that you should not forget your suffering. However, I am not a crude man, someone who makes one cry and beg for mercy, please don't refuse." After speaking, he moved closer to the woman, showing warmth and care repeatedly, and they were close and affectionate. The woman's desire was aroused, but due to her deep wounds and great pain, she ultimately dared not lightly agree. The man continued to show tenderness, and finally, he undressed with her for pleasure. His touch was soft, smooth, and gentle, quite different from Niansan's. Only the phallic object, still the same as before, and in the aftermath of emotional stirring, it was not too painful. She was pleased with his appearance and delighted with his feelings, and by this point, the woman could hardly control herself! They enjoyed themselves until dawn, and then the man left.

    201

    甫下榻，粲然而笑，谓妇曰：“子姑视我孰与念三美？”时妇已倦甚，强应曰：“念三何能及君也？”迨凝睇一观，则依然念三立于前。其妇益骇，然念三早穿窗而出，犹闻其声曰：“痴妮子枉拣精肥，彼乌知既已玉碎不能瓦全耶？”妇犹不疑，而神思昏昏伏枕眠，遂闭门高卧，直至日中方起。及启户，其姑见之大惊曰：“新妇何所苦，而形神顿失？得无二竖为灾乎？”妇始心疑，已而觉私处暴痛，坟起如垆，其热炙手。然犹不敢告人，臃肿以行，困益甚。且流毒泛溢，其出如浆，遂病不能起。翁姑惧，始告厥父母，咸来问视。

    Explanation

    201

    He had just settled down when he laughed brightly and said to his wife, "Would you please compare me to Nian San in beauty?" At the time, the wife was very tired and勉强 replied, "How could Nian San compare to you?" When she fixed her gaze and looked, she saw Nian San standing before her once more. The wife was even more frightened, but Nian San had already slipped out through the window, and she still heard his voice saying, "Foolish girl, you chose the finest, yet how could you know that once shattered, jade cannot be mended into pottery?" The wife still did not suspect anything and, with her mind dazed, fell asleep leaning on her pillow. She then closed the door and slept deeply until midday. When she opened the door, her mother-in-law was shocked and exclaimed, "What troubles you, my new daughter-in-law, that you have lost your form and spirit? Is it not the two small men causing this?" The wife began to suspect, and soon felt a sudden, intense pain in her private area, which swelled like a pot and was hot to the touch. She still dared not tell anyone, walking in a bloated and swollen manner, feeling increasingly weak. Moreover, the poison spread and overflowed, seeping out like a thick liquid, and she fell ill, unable to get up. The husband's parents were frightened and finally informed her parents, who all came to inquire about her condition.

    202

    妇终耻于言，仅私告其父母曰：“误儿至此，诚不敢怨。然儿已不贞，殁后请勿葬于某氏之茔。”母唯唯而不解其故，明日遂卒。比及殓尸于棺，则腹穿肤裂，黄水汪洋，益莫得其病由。乃自妇死后，念三之毒始肆，凡遇巾帼者流闲行陇畔，即以重贿诱与之私。不从，辄强致之。遭之者，恒病即死，与妇之状略同，人始悟彼妇之疾。以此因戒其室家，不令出。如是旬余，念三忽不见。后值迅雷，有樵者归告其里人，言某山震死一蟒，已生角，角端有赤字曰“李念三”。好事者争从往视之，信然。而娶空房者，则犹不一而足也。

    　　外史氏曰：此事非常，固属娶空房者所不及料。顾吾谓，婚嫁大伦，与其娶而候归之日，何如归而后娶？红颜薄命，遭逢晋人，即无蟒之毒，亦不免于妇之叹矣。况夫娶已有日，归尚无时，耽蝇头之微利，误鸾树之佳期。丈夫志在四方，竟至此乎？篇首曰“商人重利轻别离”，良有慨乎言之也。

    202

    A Woman with a Curse

    The woman was too ashamed to speak about her condition and only privately told her parents, "I was led to this by mistake. I truly dare not complain. But I am no longer pure. After my death, please do not bury me in Mr. Such-and-Such's tomb." Her mother nodded but did not understand the reason. The next day, she died. By the time she was placed in the coffin, her belly was torn open, her skin split, and yellow fluid flowed everywhere. No one could understand the cause of her illness. It was only after her death that the poison of Nian San began to spread. Whenever she encountered women walking in the fields, she would immediately offer heavy bribes to seduce them. If they refused, she would forcibly take them. Those who encountered her would often fall ill and die, similar to the woman's condition. People began to realize the woman's illness and warned their families not to let their women go out. Like this for ten days, Nian San suddenly disappeared. Later, during a sudden thunderstorm, a woodcutter returned and told the villagers that a mountain was shaken and a python died. The python had grown horns, and at the tip of the horn was red writing that said "Li Nian San." People who were curious rushed to see it, and it was indeed so. Those who married empty houses still had many more.

    The commentator said: This matter is extraordinary and certainly beyond the expectations of those who married empty houses. But I say, marriage is a great principle. Rather than marrying and waiting for the return, why not marry after the return? Beautiful women have short lives, and encountering someone from the Jin, even without the python's poison, it would still lead to the lament of the woman. Especially when the marriage date is set but the return date is uncertain, pursuing trivial gains, missing the auspicious time of the phoenix tree. A man's ambition is to travel far and wide, could it come to this? The opening line says "merchants value profit over separation," it is indeed a profound statement.

    203

    ○　訾氏
    　　大兵初定新疆，市廛尚觉寥落。洎乎岁久，商贾云集，有号义聚者，典肆之所肇始也。肆中佣人，失其名，年仅二十许，佣于肆主有日矣。戊子秋杪，因腹急出肆大解。时当初创，城郭而外无居民，荒榛断梗之旁，随在皆可。某方踞地而遗，俄闻丛莽中有笑声。视之，则一美妇人，裳红而衣绿，亦蹲踞与之相对。且嗤曰：“予在此，渠何旁若无人？”某愕然，谓为民家妇，不及竣事而起。行后却顾，见妇亦冉冉径穿荒草而去，心始安。数日又往，妇仍先在，与某相视而笑，绝无羞缩之态。某揣其可挑，先投以言，欣然容纳，遂就城隅僻处野合而散。某归肆，至夕独卧一室，夜半，妇忽自至，悄然无声。某已为所蛊，遂不审厥由来，共欢好，倍极绸缪。

    Explanation

    203

    ○　The Zī Family
    　　When the great army first pacified Xinjiang, the commercial areas were still sparse. After some time had passed, merchants gathered in large numbers, and there was one called Yijü, the beginning of the establishment of a shop. An employee in the shop, whose name is lost, aged around twenty, had been working for the shop owner for some time. In the autumn of the year of the Earth Horse (Wùzǐ), he left the shop to relieve himself due to an urgent need. At the time, the area was just being established, and outside the city walls, there were no residents, with overgrown bushes and broken branches all around. He was squatting down to relieve himself when soon he heard laughter from among the bushes. He looked, and there was a beautiful woman, wearing red skirts and green clothes, also squatting facing him. She laughed and said, "I am here, why does he act as if I am not here?" He was startled, thinking she was a woman from a common household, and did not finish and stood up. After walking away, he looked back and saw the woman slowly disappear into the overgrown grass. His heart finally calmed down. Several days later, he went again, and the woman was still there first. She smiled at him, showing no signs of shyness or embarrassment. He thought she could be approached, so he first spoke to her, and she happily accepted. They met in a secluded corner of the city and then parted. He returned to the shop, and until evening, when he slept alone in a room. At midnight, the woman suddenly appeared, silently and without a sound. He was already entranced by her and did not inquire about her origins. They enjoyed each other's company, being very intimate.

    204

    询其姓，曰“訾氏”。叩其居址，坚不肯言，惟曰：“君得与妇相伴，亦足矣，何问其他？”及晓，披衣先起，匆匆自去，肆中亦无人知。自是无虚夕，来辄欢会，旬日后，典中诸人视某容渐枯槁，饮食亦锐减于前，颇疑之，而不意其有是也。已而遂病怔忡，少记善忘。某素司簿籍，凡典物之出入，纤悉必登。至是纷如乱丝，又多所遗漏。肆主始疑讶，将遣之行。某哀恳再四，姑留以观其后。乃未二日，故态复萌，遂决意去之。某有兄亦佣于他肆，闻之亟来乞留，言次辄泣下。肆主哀之，遂不复遣，置酒与其兄共饮。众因诘某以致病之由，初犹隐讳，及兄呵责至再，乃吐其情。众闻之骇然，以为鬼也。但查户民，从无訾姓，益更惝怳不定。至夜，乃留其兄伴弟，妇竟不来。

    Explanation

    204

    Translation

    When asked about his surname, he replied, "Zī." When questioned about his residence, he firmly refused to say, only saying, "You can be content just being with my wife; why ask about anything else?" By dawn, he got dressed and left first, departing hurriedly, and nobody in the shop knew anything about it. From then on, he never missed a night, coming to meet his wife joyfully. After ten days, the people in the shop noticed that his appearance gradually became emaciated, and his food intake also sharply decreased compared to before. They began to suspect something, but did not expect this. After that, he fell ill with palpitations, had poor memory, and was prone to forgetfulness. He had always been responsible for recording all transactions involving items, meticulously noting every detail. By then, the records became as tangled as a ball of thread, and many were omitted. The shop owner began to doubt and be surprised, intending to dismiss him. The man pleaded earnestly, and the shop owner temporarily kept him to see what would happen next. Within two days, the old behavior reappeared, and the shop owner decided to dismiss him. The man had a brother who also worked in another shop, who heard about it and came quickly to plead to keep him. While speaking, tears would fall. The shop owner felt sorry for him and no longer dismissed him, holding a banquet to drink with his brother. The others questioned him about the cause of his illness. Initially, he still concealed it, but when his brother scolded him again, he finally revealed the truth. Upon hearing it, the others were startled, thinking it was a ghost. But upon checking the household register, there was no surname Zī. They became even more bewildered and uncertain. By night, they kept his brother to keep him company, but the woman did not come.

    205

    及兄以事归，妇又夜至，相见倍加款曲，某不能拒，仍纳之。晨起，众见某神色有异，知又为鬼迷，遂共谋徙其卧具于神厨之下。所祀者，关圣帝也，妇之迹果渺然。次夕，主人虑肆后空寂，议令他人宿其中。至夜，釜鬻雷鸣，门窗风吼，竟夕不能寐。又更以一人怀刃而卧，闻声即起而捉之，都无所见。卧则笑声吃吃，兼击面杖，作揶揄状，又复不胜其扰。众中有黠者，谋使某仍卧故处，妇若至，以嗽为号，众往逐之，必得其踪迹。某不得已，从其言，众皆不寐，静以俟之。至夜，闻某大嗽，乃明炬持械，喧噪而往。及户外，犹闻调笑声，破扉径入，则唯某偃息在床，室中更无一人，益惊异之。

    Explanation

    205

    When the brother returned due to some matter, the woman came to him at night again. They met with extra warmth and politeness, and he was unable to refuse her, so he still accepted her. In the morning, the others noticed that he looked different, realizing that he had been bewitched by a ghost again. They then planned to move his bedding to beneath the kitchen altar, where Guan Yu, the god of war and loyalty, was worshipped. The woman's presence indeed disappeared. The next night, the owner, concerned about the emptiness of the place, decided to have someone else stay there. That night, the pots and pans were making a thunderous noise, and the windows and doors were howling with wind, and he was unable to sleep all night long. Then, another person was made to lie down with a knife, ready to get up and catch whoever made the sound, but nothing was seen. When lying down, there was laughter, along with hitting with a stick, making a mocking gesture, and the disturbance was unbearable. Among the group, there was a clever one who planned to have the person lie down in the usual place again. If the woman came, they would signal by coughing, and everyone would go to chase her, surely finding her trail. With no choice, the person followed their advice, and everyone stayed awake, quietly waiting. That night, when the person coughed loudly, they lit torches, armed themselves, and went in a noisy manner. When they reached the outdoor area, they still heard laughter. Breaking through the door and going in directly, they found only the person lying down on the bed, with no one else in the room. They were even more surprised and amazed.

    206

    众退，而妇返舍，愠谓曰：“予虽犯多露至此，然亦妇人也，何来妄男子，使人心胆俱落矣。”因刺刺怨恨不休。某抚慰之，乃解衣同梦，侵晨始去。某亦不再隐，诘朝悉以告众。众曰：“我辈太匆忙，反误乃事。今夜须俟其就寝，子始嗽，且力揪其衣，予等往捕之。渠白身将不能遁，怪可以获。”某又如其教。宵深，妇来，某与之款洽倍逾畴昔。迨其寝定，乃大嗽，且揽其裾于裀褥下，而軃身以压之，仪若熟寐者。及众哄然前往，闻室中作惶急声曰：“暴客又来，亟以衣予我，奈何鼾睡如死耶？”众闻而益噪，妇声益急。良久，嗤然如裂帛，则已绝裾而逸矣。众人视某，犹力揿其衣。验之，得红裳半幅于衾侧，盖以楮为之也，某因咋舌不能收。明日，传示于人，多有见之者。其兄又浼人为文，投首于城隍司，怪遂绝，某亦寻愈。

    Explanation

    206

    The group left, and the woman returned home, angrily saying, "I have come out here despite the cold, but I am also a woman. Why did this rogue man come, scaring me to death?" She continued to complain and grumble incessantly. The narrator comforted her, and they undressed and slept together until early morning when he left. The narrator no longer kept it hidden and told everyone the whole story in the morning. The others said, "We were too hasty and thus messed up the matter. Tonight, we must wait until she is asleep. Then cough, and pull her clothes forcefully. We will go and catch the strange creature. He, being unarmed, will not be able to escape, and we can catch the strange creature." The narrator followed their advice. Deep into the night, the woman came, and the narrator treated her very kindly, far more than before. When she had settled down to sleep, the narrator coughed loudly and pulled her skirt under the quilt, then lay on top of her, as if asleep. When the others rushed to the room, they heard a panicked voice from inside saying, "The violent intruder has come again. Quickly give me my clothes. Why am I snoring like a dead person?" The others heard and became even more noisy, and the woman's voice became even more urgent. After a long time, there was a sound like silk tearing, and she had already torn her skirt and escaped. The others looked at the narrator, who was still pressing her clothes. Upon inspection, they found half a red skirt by the quilt, made of paper. The narrator was so surprised that he could not speak. The next day, it was shown to others, and many saw it. Her brother then asked someone to write and submitted it to the city god's office. The strange creature was thus eliminated, and the narrator also soon recovered.

    207

    ○　假鬼
    　　余师冯佩琛先生，数从南来。道经某地，失其名，至辄迂道而过之，亦未暇诘其故。己亥仲春，自粤东罗定回辙，将赴京，复由其处，御夫则扬鞭径过，不再趋避。先生因微叩之，笑曰：“旧传斯地有女鬼，颇能为祟，故避之。比年嫁去矣，经行固无害。”先生益怪而询之，御指路侧一古冢答：“鬼居此中，衣色绯，被发吐舌，面颜无血色。每遇行旅一二人，辄出现，人恒弃其辎重而奔。如是者数年，殊不知其何怪。客岁有某者，未稔里居，中岁无妻孥，因赴淮北访所亲，少润囊橐而返。

    Explanation

    207

    ○　假鬼
    　　My master, Mr. Feng Peichen, often traveled from the south. His route passed through a certain place, whose name I have forgotten. He always took a detour to pass by, but he did not have time to ask the reason. In the second month of the year in the sixth year of the Jiahai era, he returned from Luoding in Guangdong Province and was heading to the capital. He passed through the same place again, and the driver whipped the horse and passed directly, without any attempt to avoid it. My master then gently asked about it, and the driver smiled and said, "It was said that there was a female ghost in this place, who could cause harm or mischief, so we avoided it. But in recent years, she has been married away, so passing through is no longer harmful." My master was even more curious and asked further. The driver pointed to an ancient tomb on the side of the road and said, "The ghost lives in this tomb. She is dressed in red, has long hair, and a protruding tongue, with a face that has no blood color. Whenever she encounters a traveler or two, she appears, and people always abandon their belongings and run away. This has happened for several years, but we don't know what kind of ghost she is. Last year, there was someone named... who did not know the local customs or area. In middle age, he had no wife or children. He went to Huaibei to visit relatives, returned with a little money to supplement his purse, and then came back here."

    208

    踽踽焉独行道中，顿忘是地有此异，比至，始忆之，遂股栗不能前。既而侥幸其不遇，疾驰弗顾，盖乘鬼不及知也。俄闻冢中有声，啾啾长啸，心益惴悄然。视之，一鬼自墓间，状如人所传，乃大怖，欲窜。鬼行如风雨，呜呜然相逼而来。其人即欲弃所荷，脱然而走，转念奔波千里，甫得此蝇头，一旦掷之，殊为扼腕。且鬼不过祟吾身，岂利吾有？因逡巡不能舍。鬼且咫尺，吼啸倍急，更呜咽作啼，致其人毛发胥竖，而终莫割所爱，踉跄思遁。鬼亦仅迫之，无敢前。其人急，计顿生，思以老拳尝之，宁为鬼死，不甘财亡。

    Explanation

    208

    Wandering alone on the road, he suddenly forgot that this place had such a strange phenomenon. When he arrived, he only then remembered, and he trembled with fear and could not move forward. Then he hoped he would not meet the ghost, raced away without looking back, it seems he took advantage of the ghost not knowing his presence. Soon he heard a sound from the tomb, a long, eerie howl, his heart grew even more anxious and uneasy. When he looked at it, a ghost emerged from the tomb, looking like what people had described, and he was greatly frightened and wanted to flee. The ghost moved as swiftly as wind and rain, came closer, making a mournful sound. The man immediately wanted to abandon what he was carrying, to run away quickly. But he thought, "I have traveled thousands of miles, just to obtain this small amount of wealth. If I throw it away now, it would be a great loss." Moreover, the ghost only harms my body, it doesn't benefit from my possession. So he hesitated and couldn't let go. The ghost was almost within reach, and its howls grew even more urgent, it even cried out in a mournful way, causing the man's hair to stand on end. But he still couldn't part with what he loved, he staggered and thought to flee. The ghost only pursued him but did not dare to approach. The man became desperate and suddenly had an idea, he thought to attack it with a punch, rather die at the hands of the ghost than lose this wealth.

    209

    爰出鬼之不意，直前搏之。随手而仆，一若荏弱不胜者。益得志，扬臂奋击，鬼早娇啼乞命矣。其人讶甚，谛观焉，红笺数寸，飘扬绿莎，鬼状如故，鬼舌早已弗存。其人不禁大骇，停腕诘之，则泣告曰：‘某家距此里许，身实女也。徒以老母在堂，终鲜兄弟，无已，腼颜而为此，以备甘旨之需。今已小康，但此身孑然未偶，曾默祀曰：有能识吾迹者，吾即夫之，不再作此丑态。幸与君遘，其命也夫？’其人闻言惊喜。意犹未信，遽捋其襟而验之，鸡头半垂，宛然闺质，益大悦，释之令起。

    Explanation

    209

    Seizing the ghost by surprise, he rushed forward to grab it. Immediately, it fell over, as if weak and unable to withstand. He became even more arrogant, raising his arm and striking fiercely, and the ghost had already been crying pitifully and begging for mercy. The man was greatly startled, carefully observing it, and saw a few inches of red paper floating on the green grass. The ghost's appearance remained the same, but its tongue had already been gone. He could not help but be greatly startled and stopped his hand, questioning it. It then cried and said, "My home is about a mile away from here. I am actually a woman. Just because my mother is still alive and I have no brothers, there was no other choice, so I humbly did this to provide for the needs of food and drink. Now I am doing well, but my body remains unmarried. Once I secretly prayed, saying: 'If anyone can recognize my traces, I will marry him and never do this again.' Fortunately, I met you, it must be fate." The man heard and felt surprised and happy. Still not fully believing, he quickly pulled up its collar to check, and saw the head was half-dropped, clearly a woman's body. He became even more delighted and released it to stand up.

    210

    女腼然整衣，导以同往。须臾，抵其家，茅屋低矮，篱落洒然，隐有殷实之象。初入，见一妪龙钟残疾，女告之故，冁然曰：‘固阻儿勿再出，今竟何如耶？虽然，郎君之胆，亦较升斗为巨矣。’因谓其人曰：‘老妇孤孀已久，借此女得以存活。向因无以养生，适古冢陷一巨穴，渠遂作此狡狯，今且十稔。待缘未嫁，君若琴瑟尚虚，盍赘此为吾婿，小妮子亦无颜业此矣。’其人敬诺，是夕即结为伉俪。女家颇裕，某亦心安。旬余，遂移去，不知所往。”御言次犹遥识其处，庐舍俨然。先生至都，每举以告人，靡不惊异。

    Explanation

    210

    A modestly dressed woman, she tidied her clothes and led the narrator to go with her. Soon, they arrived at her home, a low and humble thatched house with neatly arranged fences, suggesting an appearance of prosperity. Upon entering, they saw an elderly woman who was frail and disabled. The woman explained the reason for their visit, and the old woman smiled and said, "I had always forbidden the child from going out again, but now what has happened? Although, the young man’s courage is also quite remarkable." She then addressed the narrator, saying, "This old woman has been a widow for a long time and has relied on this girl to survive. In the past, because there was no way to support life, she happened to find an ancient tomb with a large cave, and she then did this trick, which has been going on for ten years. If she is waiting for an opportunity to marry, and if your marriage is still empty, why not take this as your son-in-law? The little girl will also feel ashamed to continue this." The narrator respectfully agreed, and that night, they were married. The girl’s family was quite wealthy, and the narrator also felt at ease. After ten days or so, they then moved away, and their destination is unknown. The narrator still vaguely recognizes the place, and the houses are still intact. The master went to the capital and often told people about it, none were not amazed.

    211

    外史氏曰：风声鹤唳，草木皆兵，人自仓惶，鬼何能为祟哉？而世之狡者，又故借幽冥劣相以吓嗤嗤之氓，吾不知真鬼闻之，其亦揶揄否耶？犹忆京师某巷，有鬼夜深辄出，宵行者遭之，每遗弃衣物，与此事颇相类。巷中逻卒王某，醉中见之。其首如栲栳，纸条飞鸣，周身皆白毫，约寸许。朱其目，赤其口，形状可怖。王已沉酣，莫惧，反谩骂曰：“若鬼耶应避人，汝反逐人耶？”鬼闻之折身却走，如辟易。王察其有异，疾趋而前，捽之以力，鬼亦仆。主审知为人，剥其面，褫其革，径抱以归。灯下视之，则羊裘一袭，乱毛如蝟，面具乃以汲水器为之，涂以殊墨败楮，乱粘而已。明日传视，见者俱大笑。王至今犹衣其裘，但未稔其人雌雄。

    Explanation

    211

    The Historian said: "When the wind howls and geese cry, and grass and trees seem like soldiers, people are already in a state of panic; how can ghosts cause any harm? Yet, the cunning in the world often use the image of evil spirits to frighten the ignorant. I wonder if the true ghosts, hearing of this, would mock them?" I still recall a certain alley in the capital city where a ghost was said to appear at night. Those who walked at night would encounter it and often leave behind their clothes, and this incident is quite similar. A patrolman named Wang, drunk, saw the ghost. Its head was like a winnowing basket, with paper strips fluttering and flying, and its entire body was covered in white hair about an inch long. Its eyes were red, and its mouth was also red, making it appear terrifying. Wang, already drunk, was not afraid and instead insulted it, saying, "If you are a ghost, you should avoid people; why are you chasing them?" The ghost, upon hearing this, turned and fled as if repelled. Wang noticed something unusual, rushed forward, and overpowered the ghost with force, causing it to fall. Upon closer inspection, it was discovered that the "ghost" was actually a person. Wang removed its mask and skin and carried it back home.
