Under the lamp, he saw that it was a sheepskin cloak with messy fur, a mask made from a water bucket, and a face painted with unusual ink and old paper, all crudely attached. The next day, when it was shown to others, everyone laughed. Wang still wears the sheepskin cloak to this day, but he does not know the gender of the person it belonged to.

    212

    ○　银针
    　　故明天启中，桐城孙大廉，以孝廉举于乡。将赴南宫，因疾不果。及榜下闻某某皆成进士，心益不平，盖其素所轻者。于是厥疾益增，太孺人甚忧之。医曰：“旧恙已平，此新者乃情郁所致。必得胜游，开拓其心胸，病或不药而愈。”生言之于母，深然其论，乃为之买舟束装，使游二水三山之胜。生辞母启行，从以一仆一僮，以备负书担囊之用。及登舟，有一叟坚求附载，视之，年约六旬，状颇矍铄。生以其老也，怜而许之。叟入舱，与生为礼，自言胡姓，号悦庵，北直人，将往金陵售其术，故愿附骥。生叩其所业，笑而不答。徐曰：“此非儒者所乐闻也。”生意为房中秘戏耳，遂不复询，舟乃发。翌午，生以病卧，闻蓬窗下有欢笑声，谛听之，则其僮仆似捧腹不胜者。心异而潜迹之，见叟踞矮几，袒露臂，以笔绘人形于上，即能自起，宛一裸裎之躯，且有声如小鸟，嘤嘤然歌唱，僮仆惊喜，故欢声达内。

    Explanation

    212

    In the reign of Emperor Tianqi, there was a man from Tongcheng named Sun Dalian, who was recommended for office based on his reputation for filial piety and integrity. He was about to travel to the capital to take the imperial examinations but fell ill and could not go. When the results were announced, he heard that certain individuals had passed the exams, which made him even more discontent, especially since these were people he had always considered inferior. His illness worsened, and his mother, the elderly lady, was very worried. The doctor said, "The old illness has subsided, but this new one is due to emotional distress. He must have a successful tour, broaden his horizons, and his illness might recover without medication." The young man told his mother, who agreed with the doctor's advice. They arranged for him to buy a boat and prepare for a journey to visit scenic spots and mountains. The young man bid farewell to his mother and set out, accompanied by one servant and one boy to carry books and luggage. As he boarded the boat, an old man insisted on joining the journey. The young man, seeing that the man was about sixty years old and still vigorous, felt pity and allowed him to come aboard. The old man entered the cabin, bowed to the young man, introduced himself as Hu, with the courtesy name Yue'an, from Beizhili, and said he was going to Jinling to sell his skills, hence wishing to hitch a ride. The young man asked about his profession, but the old man smiled and did not answer. He slowly said, "This is not something a scholar would like to hear." The young man assumed it was about intimate or erotic activities and did not ask further. The boat then set sail. The next morning, the young man lay ill in bed and heard laughter coming from under the simple window. He listened carefully and realized that his servants were laughing so much that their sides hurt. Surprised, he followed secretly and saw the old man sitting on a low stool, exposing his arms, drawing a human figure on a piece of paper, which then seemed to come to life, like a naked body, and made a sound like a small bird, singing softly and sweetly. The servants were amazed and their laughter reached the inside of the cabin.

    213

    生知其异人，不相惊，屏息自退。诘朝，治具延之，欲求其术。叟早知其意，谓生曰：“君飞黄有日，不宜效江湖餔餟者流。虽然，共济之德，老夫不能无报。约以五日别时，举以相赠，今尚不暇。”生乃不再请，欢饮而散。及期，将抵南京，叟乘夜入见曰：“来朝别矣！前所云者，老夫不敢食言，敬来拜纳。”生致谢，诘以所在，答曰：“在予腹中。”生笑曰：“叟诳我。

    Explanation

    213

    The protagonist recognized the old man as extraordinary and did not startle him. He held his breath and quietly retreated. The next day, he prepared a meal and invited the old man, wanting to learn his method. The old man had already known the protagonist's intentions and said, "You will achieve great success one day, but you should not follow the example of those who live off the land or in society. Although, I cannot remain ungrateful. I will give you something as a gift, but not yet." The protagonist did not ask again and they enjoyed a pleasant meal together. When the time came, the protagonist was about to reach Nanjing, and the old man came at night to say, "I will leave tomorrow! The gift I promised, I dare not break my word, so I come to give it to you." The protagonist thanked him, but asked where the gift was. The old man replied, "It is in my belly." The protagonist laughed and said, "The old man is deceiving me."

    214

    披肝沥胆，胥假设之词耳，岂腹中之物，果堪持以与人哉？”叟笑而弗辩，惟自解其衣，露腹向生曰：“君试呼之，此中当有应者。”生益笑而不信，坚不肯呼。叟乃自拊其腹，呼曰：“银针儿，速出见客，何作三家村儿女子态耶？”生更笑不能仰。俄闻腹中作娇嫩声曰：“予故厌见生人，何相逼至此？”其音细如箫管，婉而且清。生大骇，辍笑俟之。叟又呼曰：“予业以汝字孙君，非蓦生者可比，妮子慎勿惧羞。”内不应。叟又促之，乃曰：“如此聒絮，足征阿翁老悖矣。幸启半扉，予出。

    Explanation

    214

    Revealing one's true feelings and intentions, all assumed words, how could the contents of one's stomach actually be something one could hold and give to others?" The old man laughed and did not argue, only by untying his clothes and showing his belly to the student, he said, "You try calling it, and there will be a response from within." The student laughed even more and still did not believe, firmly refusing to call. The old man then patted his own belly and called out, "Silver Needle, quickly come out to meet the guest, why act like a girl from a small village?" The student laughed so hard he could barely look up. Soon, a soft and clear voice came from within the belly, saying, "I naturally dislike meeting strangers, why are you forcing me so much?" The voice was as fine as a flute, soft and clear. The student was greatly startled and stopped laughing, waiting for it. The old man called again, "I have already named you Sun Jun, not a stranger, little girl, be careful not to be afraid of shame." There was no response inside. The old man urged again, and then said, "Such noise is enough to show that Old Man is old and foolish. Please open half the door, I will come out."

    215

    ”生是时形如木鸡，注目呆视。见叟以掌击其腹，忽裂寸许，并无微血，益大惊。倏然异香习习，声震如裂帛，生亟顾瞻，则一丽人缟衣红裳，掠发微嚬，立于烛下，而叟之迹渺然。生不觉大怖，诧为妖异。视其容色，又艳绝，不忍遽舍，乃正色叱之曰：“汝诚何怪，敢以诡异惑人！予故宋广平不为色动者，盍速退。不然，吾刃将斩矣。”女无惧容，敛袂致词曰：“妾实狐仙。父奉上遣，将往长陵，为高皇守墓，虑妾无依，携以随行。昨至江干，为水神所覸，慕妾之色，强委禽焉。父以其蛙黾同陼，雅不欲，故匿妾于腹。借君福荫以渡此津，今且至。仰君清德，使奉箕帚，兼酬卵翼之恩，非敢为祸，幸勿疑。”生察其意不恶，心微纳之。惟曰：“予构痼疾，急未能痊，何心复作他想？”女微笑曰：“此易办耳！君姑高枕卧，请为君先驱二竖子，以验妾非祸人者。”生大喜，诘曰：“卿亦知医耶？倘能祛此沉顿，予固不惜为情死。

    Explanation

    215

    At that moment, he appeared like a wooden rooster, staring blankly. He saw the old man strike his abdomen with his palm, and suddenly, a few inches of skin split open, with no trace of blood, which made him even more frightened. Suddenly, a pleasant fragrance filled the air, and the sound was as sharp as silk tearing. He quickly looked around, and there stood a beautiful woman in white and red clothing, with slightly furrowed brows and swept-back hair, by the candlelight. The old man had vanished. He was suddenly terrified, thinking it was a strange and supernatural occurrence. Seeing her appearance, he found her extremely beautiful and could not bear to leave immediately. He scolded her sternly, saying, "What kind of creature are you truly? Dare you deceive with your strange and supernatural appearance! I am a former official of the Song dynasty who does not yield to beauty. Go away quickly. Otherwise, my sword will cut you down." The woman showed no fear, gathered her sleeves, and spoke: "I am actually a fox spirit. My father was sent by the heavens to go to Changling to guard the tomb of the high emperor. Worried that I would have no one to rely on, he took me along. Yesterday, when we reached the riverbank, I was seen by the water god, who admired my appearance and forcibly offered me as a gift. My father thought that frogs and toads live in the same pond and did not wish to, so he hid me in his belly. He used your good fortune to cross this river, and now we are about to arrive. I admire your pure virtue and wish to serve you as a maid, and repay the kindness of nurturing. I do not dare to cause harm, please do not doubt me." He saw that her intentions were not evil and slightly accepted her. But he said, "I have a chronic illness that is difficult to cure quickly, how can I have other thoughts?" The woman smiled and said, "This is easy to do! You may rest easy, please lie down. I will first drive away the two demons in your body to prove that I am not harmful." He was greatly pleased and asked, "Do you also know medicine? If you can cure this chronic illness, I would not hesitate to die for love."

    216

    ”女不言，生甫卧，欻已不见。惟觉有气如火，入自脐中，上达肝鬲，下行脏腑。须臾，汗出如蒸，神思顿爽，因而厥疾尽瘳。重负既释，齁然熟寐，竟罔知女之所在。旦起，舟已泊岸，僮入白：“叟已辞去，遗书一缄。”生启视，则嘱生善视其女者。生不得女，亦未敢深信。舍舟就舆，入城寓于其友家，谈宴之间，绝无病态。知其疾者，咸称庆，生亦私心窃喜。语至漏下，始归寝室。生冀女复，乃令僮仆别宿。及寝，竟杳其踪，怏怏就枕。方转辗间，闻耳畔小语曰：“妾来相伴矣。君真铁石肠，不一动耶？”口脂之香，近在咫尺。抚之，则腻然之玉，已在衾中，生遂不能自持，欢然相狎。晨起谋所以匿之，女固言勿庸，果泯其迹，将寝始自来。生游览已遍，归思顿萌。适怀帝践祚，诏举宾兴，遂旋返。女送至江畔，垂涕曰：“父在斯，不克随郎同去矣！”生亦恋恋，强之弗从，竟分袂。明年，生下第，再诣秣陵，冀遇之，以续旧好，茫无消耗。

    Explanation

    216

    The girl remained silent. As soon as the young man lay down, she vanished suddenly. He only felt a sensation of heat like fire entering from his navel, ascending to his liver and diaphragm, and descending to his internal organs. In a short while, he broke out in a sweat like steam, his mind and spirit suddenly refreshed, and thus his severe illness was completely cured. With the heavy burden lifted, he fell into a deep, peaceful sleep, yet he still had no idea where the girl had gone. In the early morning, they had already docked at the shore, and the servant came to report: "The old man has already left, leaving behind a sealed letter." The young man opened and read it, finding instructions to take good care of the girl. Though he did not find the girl, he also dared not fully believe the letter. Leaving the boat, he took a carriage to the city, staying at a friend's house. During conversations and feasts, he showed no signs of illness. Those who knew about his illness all congratulated him, and the young man secretly felt happy. They talked until the water clock dripped, finally returning to his bedroom. Hoping the girl would return, he ordered the servants to sleep elsewhere. When he went to sleep, she disappeared without a trace, leaving him disappointed and going to bed. While turning restlessly, he heard a soft voice beside his ear saying, "I have come to keep you company. You truly have an iron heart, not moved at all?" The scent of her lip balm was near, almost within reach. When he touched her, she was already in the bed, smooth and delicate like jade. The young man could no longer control himself, and they happily engaged in intimate activities. In the morning, he planned how to hide her, but the girl firmly said it was unnecessary. They successfully concealed her, and she would come at night. Having visited many places, his thoughts of returning home suddenly arose. Just when Emperor Xian ascended the throne, an imperial edict was issued to select scholars, and he returned home. The girl accompanied him to the riverbank, tears streaming down her face, saying, "My father is here; I am unable to go with you!" The young man was also reluctant, tried to force her but she refused, and they finally parted. The following year, the young man failed the exam and visited Moling again, hoping to meet her to continue their previous relationship, but there was no news or trace of her.

    217

    外史氏曰：诗云：“出入腹我”，非谓妊娠之先，盖言鞠育之意耳。今此狐竟直腹之矣，且能出己腹而入人腹矣！生与叟可谓腹心之交，女与生可谓知心之好，又不徒东床坦腹，作一段佳话已也。

    ○　赝殃
    　　人死而回煞，其有无不辨可知，而世俗咸信之。京都一富家，新丧子妇，亦届其期，于是阖室戒严，鸡犬皆徙，而窃虑胠箧者乘间为患。有騃仆，潜使居守，闭置子立椟之中，嘱曰：“若有殃，屏息自能免祸；无之，亦安枕而卧。何惧为？”仆领诺，众俱匿迹。至夜深，风声淅沥，未免战栗弗宁。俄而阶前察察有声，未几而履闼矣，无何而入室矣。仆从隙间窃窥，灯光微闪，昏惨不明，见一人，衣饰面貌仿佛化者，乃大骇。已而据案而食，齿声格格然，倾樽而饮，唇声啧啧然。又有顷，纸窗窸窣，绕室巡行，益大怖，而窃幸其不已睹也。乃迟又久，倾耳若有所听，举首若有所觅，呜呜而前，径趋此椟。

    Explanation

    217

    The Historian says: The Book of Songs states, "Entering and leaving my belly," which does not refer to pregnancy before conception, but rather to the nurturing and raising of a child. Now, this fox has actually conceived directly, and it can even transfer its fetus from its own womb to another's! The living man and the old man can be said to have a close, trusted friendship, and the woman and the living man can be said to have a deep understanding and affection, not just a carefree and elegant relationship, making a beautiful story.

    ○ False Calamity
    When a person dies, the soul returns to the house on a specific day, and whether this happens or not is something that can be known, but the common people all believe in it. In the capital, a wealthy family recently lost their young wife, and it was also the day for the soul's return. Therefore, the entire household was put on high alert, and even the chickens and dogs were moved out. They secretly feared that thieves might take advantage of the situation to cause trouble. There was a foolish servant who was left to guard the house, locked inside a small chest. The master instructed him, "If there is a calamity, hold your breath and you will avoid disaster; if there isn't, you can sleep soundly. What is there to fear?" The servant agreed, and everyone went into hiding. By nightfall, the wind was blowing softly, and he could not help but tremble with fear. Soon, there was a rustling sound at the front steps, followed by footsteps approaching the door, and then entering the room. The servant peered through a crack and saw a dim light flickering, the atmosphere was dim and gloomy. He saw a person, whose clothing and appearance resembled that of a ghost, and he was greatly startled. The figure then sat down at the table and began eating, making a clicking sound with its teeth, drank from a full wine cup, and made a slurping sound with its lips. After a while, the paper windows rustled, and the figure walked around the room, causing even greater fear. The servant secretly hoped that the figure would not notice him. After a long time, he tilted his head as if listening for something, raised his head as if searching for something, and then moved forward quietly, heading straight for the chest.

    218

    仆不觉心胆俱落，悚惶间倏已逼近，视其面白如雪。划然启扃，豁然洞开，骇然俱惊，颓然皆仆，不独内者无生气，而外者亦相对死，盖两败俱伤矣。平明，主人呼仆，莫应。即而视之，仆尚奄息于中，殃早绝倒于外，衣裳楚楚，俨乎生人，惟发际著纸条一束，实子妇之从嫁婢也。主不禁愕然，以汤灌仆，移时苏，具述所见。视婢既已莫救。盖婢有去志，思假殃以窃主貲，且稔子妇之仪状，装束一如其在生，及夜入室，冀饱贪壑。初不闻主之留仆居守也，比见之，不胜惊骇，遂仆于地。主察得其故，乃笑而殓之以榇。明日事传都下，迄今京中人语曰：“殃能死人，人亦能死殃。”呜呼！殃即有真，岂亦能死人哉？

    Explanation

    218

    I was so frightened that my heart and courage both fell, and in a state of shock and fear, I was suddenly approached. I saw his face pale as snow. Suddenly the door was opened, and it was wide open. Both of us were terrified and fell down. Not only did the inside person show no signs of life, but the outside person also lay dead, both sides suffering losses. At dawn, the master called the servant, but there was no response. Upon seeing it, the servant was still breathing faintly inside, while the calamity had already fallen to the ground outside. The servant was dressed neatly, as if alive, with only a bundle of paper strips stuck to the hairline, which turned out to be the servant who accompanied the daughter-in-law in marriage. The master could not help but be startled and washed the servant with hot water. After some time, the servant revived and fully described what was seen. The servant was already dead, and it seemed the servant had the intention to leave, planning to use the calamity to steal the master's wealth. She was familiar with the daughter-in-law's appearance and dressed just as she was when alive. When she saw her, she could not bear the shock and fell to the ground. The master understood the reason and laughed while burying her in a coffin. The next day, the matter spread throughout the capital, and until now, people in the capital say, "Calamity can kill people, and people can also kill calamity." Alas! Even if calamity is real, can it also kill people?

    219

    外史氏曰：从来惧殃者，无如婢媪。每言之，轻摇腕变色，懔懔乎如见其形。此婢何勇也，而究为韫椟者惊怖以死。要亦利欲居中，天夺其魄耳。吾恐真殃若出，彼二人者又将何如耶？

    ○　落花岛
    　　申无疆，字伸锡，跨鹤维扬，历有年所。一日，遇海商于市肆，与坐谈，歆其获利之美，乃以数千金畀其子若侄，使合伙焉。子名翊，颀而白皙，且善讴，年仅廿二三，海舶人咸喜之。比入大洋，舟如一叶，翊年少未惯洪涛，因惊。遂卧病，倚枕呻吟，恍惚若寐。梦中闻有人语曰：“落花岛中花倒落。”翊素不能文，觉而语其侣，虽熟历海境者，莫能举其名。一客颇娴吟咏，笑曰：“何不云‘垂柳堤畔柳低垂’，句虽佳，犹有对者。”众与翊皆称妙，翊因默识于心。无何，病益剧，未及抵岸，竟卒于舟。

    219

    The Historian said: Those who have always feared misfortune, none are like maids and concubines. Whenever they speak of it, they lightly shake their wrists and change color, trembling as if they see the form of misfortune. How brave is this maid, yet she ultimately dies of terror from being hidden in a box. It was also due to an internal conflict between desire and fear that her spirit was taken away. I fear that if true misfortune were to occur, what would happen to these two individuals?

    ○ Falling Flower Island

    Shen Wujiang, courtesy name Shenxi, lived in Yangzhou for many years. One day, he met a merchant in the market and sat to discuss matters, admiring the beauty of the profits. He then entrusted thousands of gold coins to his son and nephew, asking them to partner in the venture. His son was named Yi, tall and fair-skinned, and skilled in singing. At the age of twenty-two or twenty-three, he was well-liked by the crew of the ship. As they entered the open sea, the ship was as light as a leaf, and Yi, being young and inexperienced with the great waves, became frightened. He fell ill, lying in bed, moaning and half-asleep. In his dream, he heard someone say, "In the island of Falling Flowers, the flowers fall backward." Yi, who could not write, told his companions about the dream upon waking. Even those who were familiar with the sea could not name the place. One guest, skilled in poetry, laughed and said, "Why not say, 'By the riverbank, the willows hang low'? The line is beautiful, but it still has a counterpart." The group and Yi all praised the suggestion, and Yi memorized it in his heart. Soon, his illness worsened, and before reaching the shore, he died on the ship.

    220

    其从兄某大恸，草草殓讫，载柩而行。而翊则罔知其死，顿觉身轻，都无窒碍。因思效列子，御风遨游。水面虽风涛汹涌，毫无沾濡，不禁大喜。犹忆落花岛之名，窃计其境必不凡，顿欲往游。转瞬即得一山，形如复盂，悬于波际，其色如蜀锦，五色缤纷，且香气浓郁，馥馥数百里，心爱好之。奋身一登，旋已舍水就陆。西行里许，见若山口者，遂入之，则坦坦康庄，无复巉岩之象。山径皆落花，约寸许，别无隙地。踏花前进，滑软如茵褥，而香益袭鼻，神气为之发越。环瞩皆茂树合抱，花即生于其上。

    Explanation

    220

    His elder brother, overwhelmed with grief, hastily prepared the funeral rites and carried the coffin away. Yet Yi remained unaware of his own death, suddenly feeling light and unobstructed. Inspired by the idea of emulating Liezi, he set out to ride the wind and wander. Despite the raging waves on the surface of the water, he remained completely dry, and could not help but be greatly pleased. Recalling the name of the Lotus Flower Island, he secretly thought that the place must be extraordinary, and immediately decided to visit it. In an instant, he saw a mountain, shaped like a double basin, hanging over the waves. Its color was like Shu brocade, a riot of five colors, and its fragrance was strong and rich, spreading for hundreds of miles. His heart was drawn to it. Leaping up and climbing, he soon left the water and went onto land. Walking west for several li, he saw something like a mountain pass, and entered it. The road ahead was broad and smooth, with no more rugged cliffs in sight. The mountain path was covered with fallen flowers, about an inch thick, with no empty space. Walking forward on the flowers, they were smooth and soft like a carpet, and the fragrance became more intense, invigorating his spirit and energy. Looking around, all were tall trees with branches that embraced each other, and flowers grew on them.

    221

    细玩之，诸色俱备，浓淡相间，香如庾岭之梅，而馥郁过之。尚有存于树杪者，则低枝似坠，绕干如飞，亦多含苞欲吐者，意盖四时咸有焉。欣然前行，约数百步，花益繁而落者益厚，且四望并无屋宇，即山之层峦叠嶂，亦隐现花中，不以全面示人。翊至此心旷神怡，小憩于梅花树下，发声一讴，花益簌簌自落若细雨然。俄闻娇音叱曰：“何来妄男子，此仙人所居，岂汝行乐地耶？”翊急视之，则一美女子，通体贴以落花，宛如衣锦，手一小竹篮，亦贮落英，徐徐自树后出。

    Explanation

    221

    Carefully examining it, all colors are present, with darker and lighter shades intermixed. The fragrance is like that of the plum blossoms on Yuling, even more fragrant. Those that remain at the tips of the branches seem to be falling, circling the trunk as if flying, and there are many that are about to bloom. It seems that there are all four seasons here. With a refreshed and joyful heart, the narrator walks forward, covering about several hundred steps. The flowers become more abundant, and those that fall become thicker. Looking around, there are no buildings in sight. The mountains, with their layered peaks, are partially obscured by the flowers, not revealing their full extent. The narrator feels a sense of mental and emotional well-being, taking a brief rest under a plum tree, singing a song, and the flowers fall softly like a fine rain. Suddenly, a gentle voice shouts: "What rash man are you? This is the dwelling place of immortals, is it a place for your enjoyment?" The narrator looks around and sees a beautiful woman, completely covered with fallen flowers, as if wearing brocade, holding a small bamboo basket, also storing fallen blossoms, slowly emerging from behind the tree.

    222

    翊起逆致揖，告以所来，女微哂曰：“汝一龌龊商，何福至此？虽然，不可谓无因。为予有一语，久无能对者，汝能，则留宿于此，且有佳处与若栖身。否则，宜远飚，不容再溷仙境。”翊贪胜地，兼恋丽容，顿忘其拙，毅然请命。女因朗诵一句，则固梦中所闻也。翊喜出望外，即应声以客所属者对之，女称善。良久，慨然曰：“此才殆由天授，吾不能恝然于子矣。”直前笑把其袂曰：“行行请与妾归，花密处即是予家。”翊悦而从之。至则篱落四围，远望亦绮绾绣错，盖皆以花片砌成者。逡巡间得其门，乃巨树二株，柯交于上，俨有闬閎之象。女逊翊入，中无数椽之屋，几榻皆以彩石，尽铺落瓣。

    Explanation

    222

    Yi stood up and bowed in greeting, informing her of his arrival. The woman smiled faintly and said, "You, a lowly merchant, what good fortune has brought you here? Although, it cannot be said that there is no reason. I have a phrase that has long been unanswerable. If you can answer it, you may stay here and I will provide a good place for you to live. Otherwise, you should leave quickly, as you cannot remain in this sacred place." Greedy for the beautiful place and enamored with the beautiful face, Yi suddenly forgot his own clumsiness and resolutely asked for the task. The woman then recited a line, which Yi had indeed heard in his dream. Overjoyed, Yi responded with the answer the guest had provided, and the woman praised him. After a while, she sighed and said, "This talent must be divinely bestowed; I cannot remain indifferent to you." She laughed and took his sleeve, saying, "Come, come, let me take you home; the place where the flowers are dense is my house." Yi was delighted and followed her. Upon arriving, he saw fences surrounding the area, which looked from afar like a beautiful tapestry, for it was all made of flower petals. After a moment's hesitation, he found the door, which was between two large trees whose branches intertwined above, forming a gate-like structure. The woman invited Yi in, and inside were several rooms with tables and chairs made of colorful stones, all covered with fallen petals.

    223

    仰而窥其上，莫见天日，亦茂干为之庇荫，花叶周遮，恍一天造地设者。女未延坐，即治具曰：“郎馁矣，枵腹不可以晤言。”于是尽倾筐筥，而湘之烹之。及进馔，花之外无兼品。翊疑虑不敢食，女笑曰：“此仙人所饵，啖之无伤也。”翊试尝之，甘香肥美，视人间粱肉如尘土。女又进百花酿，味尤芳冽，吸之如醍醐款洽，神清气爽，飘飘欲仙。翊固不自知其鬼，遂窃幸长生可以立致。食已，始相款洽，渐及谐谑。女情不自禁，一振衣而群花皆落，皓体生辉，乃与翊欢合于石榻之上，备极绸缪，两情深相缱绻。

    Explanation

    223

    Looking up, he could not see the sky or the sun, as the dense foliage and flowers created a natural canopy that blocked the view. The lush branches provided shade, and the leaves and petals surrounded them, creating an environment that seemed as if it were created by heaven and earth. The woman did not wait for him to sit down before preparing the meal, saying, "You must be hungry, an empty stomach is not suitable for conversation." She then took everything out of the baskets and pots and cooked it. When the food was served, there were no other dishes besides the flowers. Yi was hesitant to eat, but the woman laughed and said, "This is food for immortals, eating it will do no harm." Yi tried a bite and found it sweet, fragrant, and delicious, making the ordinary food of the world seem as insignificant as dust. The woman then served a hundred-flower wine, which was even more fragrant and refreshing. Drinking it was like tasting nectar, refreshing the spirit and making him feel light as if he were a celestial being. Yi did not even know he was a ghost and secretly hoped for immortality to be achieved instantly. After finishing the meal, they began to converse and gradually became playful and humorous. The woman could not control her emotions, and when she moved, all the flowers fell. Her radiant beauty shone, and she and Yi were deeply entwined and affectionate on the stone bed, their passion and intimacy reaching its peak.

    224

    已而女觉其非人，诧曰：“郎何有形而无质也？幸早语我，勿使自误。”翊亦自思：“予何得至此？且海亦如何可浮？”因抚膺大戚。女止之曰：“慎勿悲。鬼而仙，犹愈于人而鬼也，况有术在，子何忧？”因出一瓷罂，内贮清泉斗许，遍沃翊身。曰：“此百花之液，妾晨起收之，实天浆甘露之属。人浴之而成仙，鬼浴之亦成形。加以服食，更采花之精英饵之，则鬼仙不难立致。笫妾数百年之积蓄，一旦为郎耗矣。”语次，翊觉沃处肌骨坚凝，非若向之虚而无寄者，此心乃释然。自视其衣，则本属乌有，女以花为之被服，而粲兮烂兮。

    Explanation

    224

    After a while, the girl realized that he was not human and exclaimed, "How can you have form but no substance? Please tell me early, so I don't make a mistake." Yi also pondered, "How did I come to be here? Moreover, how can one float on the sea?" He then touched his chest in great sorrow. The girl stopped him and said, "Do not be sad. To become a spirit and then a deity is better than remaining a human and becoming a ghost, especially since there are methods available; what do you have to worry about?" She then took out a porcelain jar containing a measure of clear spring water and bathed Yi's entire body. She said, "This is the nectar of a hundred flowers, which I collect every morning; it is truly like heavenly dew and ambrosia. When humans bathe in it, they become deities; when spirits bathe in it, they also take on form. By consuming it and further by ingesting the essence of flowers, one can achieve even greater results. Becoming a spirit-deity is not difficult to achieve quickly. But my accumulated resources over several centuries will be exhausted in one go by you." As she spoke, Yi felt that the parts bathed became solid and firm, unlike before when they were insubstantial, and his heart was relieved. When he looked at his clothes, he realized they were originally nonexistent, and the girl dressed him in flowers, making him look splendid and radiant.

    225

    两人相对，不啻锦羽鸂鶒。女昼与翊出，采花共餐；暮与翊归，席花同梦。其所衣者，卧则一拂而尽，无事解脱，醒则绕树徐行，瞬息曳娄。其地无寒暑，亦无昼夜，以花开为朝，花谢为夕。衣食一出于花，寝息即在于花，方丈蓬壶，不独擅胜焉。数年，翊忽谓女曰：“赖子再生，宜谐永好。但亲老弟少，欲归省视，子其许我乎？”女正色答曰：“此君之孝也，妾敢不勉成君志？第以鬼出，以人归，尔墓之木拱矣，谁其信之？”翊曰：“姑试一返，予亦不克久留”。女径听其行，且以花叶为翊制衣，俄顷即成华服。

    Explanation

    225

    The couple faced each other, no different from a pair of colorful mandarin ducks. The woman would go out with Yi during the day to gather flowers and share meals; at dusk, she would return with Yi to sleep among the flowers, dreaming together. The clothes they wore were such that when they lay down, they would disappear with a single sweep, requiring no effort to remove, and upon waking, they would stroll leisurely around the trees, moving gracefully in an instant. The place had no cold or heat, nor day or night; they considered the blooming of flowers as dawn and the wilting of flowers as dusk. Their clothing and food all came from the flowers, and their sleeping and resting were done among the flowers. This place was not just beautiful but surpassed even the mythical islands of Fangzhang and Penghu. After several years, Yi suddenly said to the woman, "Thanks to you, I have been reborn, and it is fitting to have eternal harmony. But my parents are old and my younger brother is young, and I wish to return to visit them. Will you allow me to do so?" The woman replied solemnly, "This is your filial piety, and how dare I not strive to fulfill your wishes? However, if I leave as a ghost and return as a human, by the time you return, the trees on your grave will be as thick as a man's arms, who will believe it?" Yi said, "Let's try returning once, but I cannot stay long." The woman readily agreed to his departure and also made clothes for Yi using flower leaves. In a short while, she had created a splendid garment.

    226

    临别赠以一瓯，嘱曰：“饥则饮此，慎勿食烟火物，食则神气日薄，不可以生。酒尽宜速返，勿再留。”翊约以匝月，即行。至海，仍复如踏平地，遂不假舟楫，直达越省。比至扬，仲锡已老，弟皆成立，翊突入，咸疑其鬼，惊避之。独仲锡抱持而泣曰：“予误儿，儿归其憾我乎？”翊乃详其颠末，人皆愕然。郡中有杖者，少曾航海，闻岛名，恍然曰：“是诚有之。岛在东海之偏，人罕能至。予曾经其处，闻系神仙所居，无径可入，至今犹仿佛其风景。”人因稍释厥惑然。仲锡在扬犹客居，翊侍膝下数日，不饮亦不食。浃旬，忽失其所在。

    226

    At parting, Yi was given a cup and was instructed: "When you are hungry, drink from this, but carefully do not eat cooked food, for eating will gradually weaken your spirit and energy, and you will not be able to live. When the wine is finished, it is advisable to return quickly, do not stay any longer." Yi agreed to a full month and immediately departed. When he reached the sea, he still walked as if on level ground, and thus did not need boats, and directly reached Yue province. By the time he arrived in Yangzhou, Zhongxi had already grown old, and his brothers were all grown up. Yi suddenly entered, and all suspected him to be a ghost, and were startled and avoided him. Only Zhongxi embraced him and wept, saying, "I have wronged you, will you be resentful when you return?" Yi then explained the entire story, and everyone was astonished. There was an old man in the county who had once sailed the seas and heard the name of the island, and he said with a start, "It truly exists. The island is in a remote part of the East Sea, few people can reach it. I once visited there, I heard it was inhabited by immortals, there is no path to enter, and even now I vaguely recall its scenery." People then slightly alleviated their confusion. Zhongxi was still a guest in Yangzhou, and Yi stayed with him for several days, neither drinking nor eating. After ten days, he suddenly disappeared.

    227

    外史氏曰：百花之精，人饵之可以延年，不谓鬼服之竟以登仙也。申翊借人成事，游香国，得佳偶，且以跻寿域。何事桃源中人不以鬼为憎，反羞与人为好哉？是诚吾所不解者。
    　　随园老人曰：世真有此乐境，吾何乐有身？写落花岛之景，令我时时神往。

    ○　货郎
    　　耒阳之地亦多竹，盖即黄冈之一派也。民资其利，种植成园，在在有淇澳之象焉。近邑某村有农家，所植尤伙，方圆数亩，密叶阴森，日色无能少入。其家乔梓维三，兄驯而弟劣，且好游荡，父深恶之，鸣诸官，曾示薄惩，以观其后，而犹未之悛也。先是邻县某货郎，时来村中售其花绣之属，于某家习熟，遂以蠃蜾目其父，辄信宿不行。家有女，年长而犹待字，货郎以亲狎之故，积渐与之通，故一门皆罔觉其事。一日其父返自田间，室中适无人，瞥见货郎与其女挽颈交吻，状甚猥亵。遂大怒，即以力田之器，突前击之。货郎不及防，破脑而死。

    Explanation

    227

    Final Translation

    The Historian of the World said: The essence of all flowers, if consumed by humans, can prolong life; it is not said that if ghosts consume it, they will ascend to immortality. Shen Yi accomplishes things through others, wanders in a fragrant realm, finds a good match, and thus enters the realm of longevity. Why do people in the Peach Blossom Spring not hate ghosts, instead feeling ashamed to associate with humans? This is indeed something I cannot understand.

    The Elder of Sui Garden said: In the world, there truly is such a joyful realm. Why should I rejoice in having a body? To depict the scenery of the Falling Petals Island, it makes me long for it constantly.

    The Peddler

    In the region of Leiyang, there are also many bamboo groves, which are part of the same lineage as Huanggang. The people benefit from this resource, planting bamboo in gardens that resemble the lush and dense bamboo groves of Qi and Ao. In a nearby village, there was a farmer who planted bamboo extensively, covering several acres with dense foliage that blocked out the sunlight. In his family, there were three brothers: the eldest was docile, while the youngest was unruly and fond of wandering. Their father deeply despised the youngest son and reported his misdeeds to the authorities, who imposed a light punishment to see if he would reform, but he still did not change his ways. A peddler from a neighboring county often came to the village to sell embroidered goods. He became familiar with the family and began to call the father by a derogatory nickname, staying for several nights without leaving. The family had a daughter who was old enough but still unmarried. The peddler, due to his familiarity, gradually began to have an affair with her. The entire family remained unaware of this. One day, the father returned from the fields to find the house empty. He suddenly saw the peddler kissing his daughter, the scene being quite lewd. He flew into a rage and struck the peddler with a farming tool. The peddler was caught off guard and died with a broken skull.

    228

    父究不忍于其女，且惧扬丑声，乃呼其仲子，举而瘗之于竹下。更虑为犬狼所搰。翌日，托言失笋，亟筑高垣以围之。计划周密，里中皆莫能知。事隔数年矣，适值熊公某来宰是邑，政令綦严，而逐恶尤如鹰鸇之鸷。其子赌无资，又私伐园竹而市之。父知而大恚，将复控之官，且挞之流血。其子深怵官威，窘极而呼曰：“阿翁何以呈为？若用寸铁毙予命，仿若人埋之竹园，夫谁得而知之？”其父益恚，驱而仆之，其子遂狂呼于市，闾里无有不闻者。比邻某素与之有隙，聆之，曰：“嘻！异哉，其子之言也！向曾有是人往来市货于此，彼家尤与之稔，称父子焉。后忽不见，疑其自归。以此言观之，得勿为老悖所戕哉？”于是白诸里甲。

    Explanation

    228

    The father, out of pity for his daughter, was unwilling to harm her and feared that the scandal would be exposed. So he called his second son and buried him under the bamboo. He also feared that the body might be dug up by dogs or wolves. The next day, he claimed that the bamboo shoots had been lost and quickly built a high wall to enclose the area. His plan was well thought out, and no one in the neighborhood knew about it. The incident had occurred several years ago. Coincidentally, a certain Mr. Xiong came to govern the county. His administration was very strict, and he pursued evil with the ferocity of an eagle or a kite. His son, lacking funds, secretly cut down bamboo from the garden and sold it. The father, upon learning this, became very angry and intended to take his son to court again, even beating him until he bled. The son, deeply intimidated by the father's anger, was desperate and cried out, "Father, what are you going to do? If you kill me with a blade, it's as if I were buried in the bamboo garden; who would know?" The father became even angrier and pushed him down. The son then shouted wildly in the market, and everyone in the neighborhood heard him. A neighbor who had always been at odds with him listened and said, "Ah! Strange indeed, what the son said! There used to be a person who came here to sell goods, and that family knew him well, calling him father and son. Later, he suddenly disappeared, and it was suspected that he had returned home. From these words, could it not be that he was killed by the old fool?" So he reported it to the local authorities.

    229

    里甲亦与其父不相能，遂具状投首于官。熊公犹未之信。拘某父子至，俱不承，其邻证之曰：“若某日为若父所挞，不尝云云乎？”其子乃俯首无语。公以刑威之，仍强辩不言其实。公乃关行邻邑，询货郎之有无，以定真伪。阅数日，货郎之弟至，衣巾登堂，则已入泮矣。泣陈曰：“某年十三龄，兄即行贩不归，今又数载，音耗渺然。某又少未更事，不能远涉寻兄，老母为此血泪尽枯。或存或亡，唯父师怜而鞫之。”熊公既知有其人，益严讯某父子。加刑者屡矣，而狡供不一，莫得尸之所在。案久不结，因逮其女到官，则嫁夫有年，亦既抱子。公并不一诘，惟令与其兄同系一室，而独悬其兄之拇指于梁。

    Explanation

    229

    The local official, Li Jia, did not get along with his father and subsequently wrote a formal statement to confess to the authorities. Magistrate Xiong still did not believe him. He detained the father and son, but they both refused to admit to the crime. A neighbor testified, saying, "On a certain day, were you not beaten by your father, and did you not say so?" The son then bowed his head and remained silent. The magistrate intimidated them with threats of punishment, but they still stubbornly denied the truth. The magistrate then sent a messenger to neighboring towns to inquire about the existence of the peddler to determine the truth. After several days, the peddler's brother arrived, dressed formally and entered the hall, indicating that he had already entered school. He wept and said, "I was thirteen years old when my brother went out trading and did not return. Now, after several more years, there has been no news of him. I am young and inexperienced, unable to travel far to find my brother. My elderly mother has cried so much that her tears and blood have dried up. Whether he is alive or dead, only the father and teacher can show pity and investigate." Upon learning that there was such a person, the magistrate even more strictly interrogated the father and son. Despite repeated punishment, their deceitful confessions varied, and no one could find the location of the body. The case remained unresolved for a long time, so the magistrate arrested the daughter. She had been married for several years and already had a child. The magistrate did not question her at all but ordered her to be locked in a room with her brother and hung only his big toe on the beam.

    230

    且密遣干人伺之，竟日亦不提讯。至夜分，其兄不能复耐，乃呼其妹曰：“若贪淫贻祸于父，而又苦我肌肤，诚何忍？”其妹惭不言，其父诃之曰：“汝耐片刻，我可复生，尔妹亦免为人笑。何哓哓如是？”其子益忿恨曰：“若父女晏然，而官独窘我，岂谓我独非人乎？”其妹亦温言慰之，絮语至旦，罄吐其情。干人突出曰：“招具矣！看汝能翻供耶？”父与女皆失色。及官升座，俱服罪，始得货郎尸。其弟痛哭，负骨而归。公以某子不得无罪，乃援笔判曰：“既不及干蛊于前，而竟同证羊于后，虽鬼神若或使之，亦王法所不恕矣。”竟论从，与其父同械于狱。其女亦杖释，夫丑之，遂与离异。逾年，再醮而去。

    Explanation

    230

    In secret, he dispatched spies to watch them, and all day long, they did not interrogate or question them. By midnight, his brother could no longer bear it, and he called out to his sister, saying, "You, through your greed and licentiousness, have brought disaster to our father, and you torment my body. How can you bear it?" His sister was ashamed and remained silent. Their father scolded her, saying, "Endure for a short while, and I can be revived; your sister will also avoid being laughed at. Why do you complain incessantly like this?" The son, growing increasingly resentful and angry, said, "If you, father and daughter, remain calmly unconcerned, while the official troubles me alone, how can it be said that I am not a person?" His sister also spoke gently to comfort him, and they chatted until dawn, fully expressing their feelings. The spies suddenly emerged and said, "The confession is complete! See if you can retract it?" Both the father and daughter turned pale. When the official took his seat, they both confessed to the crime, and the body of the peddler was finally found. The younger brother cried bitterly, carried the bones, and returned home. The official, believing that a certain son could not be without guilt, then took up his pen and decided, saying, "Since you did not manage to take on the responsibility before, but ended up joining in the same guilt later, even if ghosts and gods perhaps made you do so, it is also a crime that the royal law will not forgive." He finally decided to follow the law, and the son was imprisoned with his father. The daughter was flogged and released, and her husband found her ugly, so they divorced. After a year, she remarried and left.

    This translation aims to preserve the original meaning and structure while ensuring that it reads naturally in English. It addresses the idiomatic expressions, cultural allusions, syntactic ambiguities, and proper nouns as discussed in the pre-drafting research, providing a faithful and coherent rendering of the source text.

    231

    外史氏曰：尝怪人家恒与妄男子结为亲眷，任其出入闺闼，多致墙茨不可扫，何其疏于计也？而卖花人与货郎，尤易与闺闼相近。以其所售者，举非丈夫所用，故得假此以进身，非闲家者所宜禁乎？即如此狱，货郎固有死之之道，而不能徙薪于灶，妄思扑燎于原，一旦败露，身命俱捐。愚夫之不智，一至于此，其亦可哀也夫！

    Explanation

    231

    外史氏曰：I have often wondered why people always marry foolish men as relatives, allowing them to come and go freely in the inner chambers, often leading to situations that are difficult to resolve, how careless they are in their planning! And flower sellers and peddlers, in particular, are easily able to gain access to these private spaces. Because the goods they sell are not typically used by men, they are able to take advantage of this to advance themselves. Shouldn’t households prohibit such behavior? In this case, the peddler certainly had a way to die, but he failed to take preventive measures and foolishly tried to cover up the problem. Once it was exposed, both his life and body were sacrificed. The foolishness of the common man has reached such a level; how pitiful indeed!

    232

    ○　化豕
    　　西藏佛国，其地故无君长。主之者，惟番僧，即所谓大宝法王者是已。余闻之某公言，后藏距华极远，曾有女僧主之，貌綦美，术且通神，亦观音大士化身，为妙庄公主之类。某年，有侍卫以差入藏，往参达赖。僧适来前藏，与谈禅机，故在座。侍卫入见，辄膜拜无算，迟久不起。达赖不言，僧惟顾之微笑。及出，人诘侍卫，则曰：“予阅闺人多矣，未有如若人之艳绝者，因假和南，以饱秀色，岂为仆仆如是哉？”问者笑而去。乃侍卫启行，未及半日程，马逸堕于岩下，深且百丈。虽无所毁伤，苦无樵径可寻，百计不能出。从人知为僧故，亟反见达赖哀之。僧故未去，达赖乃正色诤之。僧又微笑，而侍卫早踵门入谢矣。盖从人甫返辔，侍卫之身倏忽已履坦途，不惟天堑绝无，抑且平沙一望，震惊其异，遂亦回骖谢过。

    Explanation

    232

    The Transformation into a Pig

    Tibet, the Buddhist kingdom, is a place without a central ruler. The region is governed solely by Tibetan Buddhist monks, known as the Great Treasure Kings. I heard from a certain official that the western region of Tibet, known as "Houzang," is extremely distant from China. Once, there was a female monk who ruled over this area. She was exceptionally beautiful, her skills were divine, and she was considered an incarnation of Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of Compassion, similar to the legendary Princess Miaozhuang. One year, a guard was sent on a mission to Tibet to pay respects to the Dalai Lama. The monk happened to be in the eastern region of Tibet, where he engaged in discussions on Zen principles, and thus was present. When the guard entered to pay his respects, he prostrated himself countless times, delaying for a long time without rising. The Dalai Lama remained silent, while the monk only smiled at him. Upon leaving, someone questioned the guard, who replied, "I have seen many women, but none as stunning as this one. I pretended to greet her, to savor her beauty, and why should I tire myself so?" The questioner laughed and left. As the guard set out, he had not traveled half a day when his horse ran away and fell into a cliff, which was deep and a hundred feet down. Although he was not injured, there was no path to be found, and he tried every means but could not escape. His companions, knowing the monk's involvement, quickly returned to plead with the Dalai Lama on his behalf. The monk had not left, and the Dalai Lama sternly rebuked him. The monk smiled again, and the guard promptly entered the door to apologize. It was only when the companions turned back that the guard's body suddenly found itself on a flat and open road, with no natural barriers and a vast expanse of flat sand. Amazed by the strangeness of the event, they also returned to apologize.

    233

    见僧则肃然致敬，如对神明，礼毕，趋而出。其异类如此。后值西域某部兵来，绝雄健，无能御者。前藏人众几无孑遗，后藏人情凶惧，咸谋奔窜。僧乃集众语之曰：“逋能免乎？有我在，必不使汝曹罹于兵燹。”因率阖藏人众至一山，群峭摩天，中有隘口，僧尽麾之使入，已乃趺坐于要冲。无何，敌之前锋猝至，见僧举嗢噱不止，将奋往擒之。僧忽化为野豕，隘通百骑，豕以一身塞之，别无余地。而刚鬣乌喙，又复丑恶骇人，敌众辟易。适其渠酋亦至，竞往白之，笑曰：“此妖术也，以矢攒而射之，若屠彘然，何惧为？”众领诺，控弦数万，一时毕集。弓矢未张，豕身自崖而坠，刹那间，百千万亿举如前豕，蠢蠢然平川复无间隙。敌众大溃，渠酋亦莫可如何，敛兵自退。僧亦不伤一骑，收法趺跏。其人出视之，则故胡然天帝也。故当是时，二藏之中，惟后藏岿然独存。迨圣朝抚之，生聚始各复其旧。

    Explanation

    233

    When encountering a monk, one should show profound respect, as if facing a deity. After the ritual is completed, one should quickly depart. Such is their unusual nature. Later, an army from a Western region arrived, extremely powerful and formidable, with no one able to resist them. Almost all the people of the front region were left without survivors, and the people of the back region were filled with fear, all planning to flee. The monk then gathered the people and said to them, "How can we escape? With me here, I will surely prevent you from suffering in the war." Therefore, he led all the people of the region to a mountain, where the cliffs touched the sky, and there was a narrow pass. The monk drove them all inside, then sat cross-legged at the strategic point. Soon, the enemy's vanguard suddenly arrived, seeing the monk and bursting into laughter, about to rush forward to capture him. The monk suddenly transformed into a wild boar, the pass could only fit a hundred riders, and the boar blocked the pass with his body, leaving no other space. With its bristly hair and black snout, it was ugly and terrifying, causing the enemy to retreat in fear. Just then, their leader also arrived, and they all rushed to report to him, laughing and saying, "This is sorcery! Shoot arrows at him, just like slaughtering a pig, what is there to fear?" The crowd nodded in agreement, and tens of thousands of bows were drawn, all gathering at once. Before the bows were even drawn, the boar's body fell from the cliff, and in an instant, countless boars appeared, all like the previous one, filling the plain in a chaotic and dense manner, leaving no gaps. The enemy fled in disarray, and their leader could do nothing, gathering his troops and retreating. The monk did not harm a single rider, and he resumed his cross-legged meditation. When the people emerged to see, it was the former monk, who was actually the Heavenly Emperor. At that time, in the two regions, only the back region remained standing alone. Until the righteous dynasty came to govern, and life and population began to return to their former state.

    234

    外史氏曰：佛称象教，象固兽之巨擘也。此豕直可吞象，大且无伦，况益以恒河沙数乎？其消虎狼之威，而保鲸鲵之众，其有大造于藏，即有伟伐于佛矣。至于警轻薄而庄严自见，又非聊斋之绩女所可同年而语已！

    Explanation

    234

    The commentator said: Buddhism is known as the "elephant teaching," and the elephant is indeed the greatest among animals. This pig can literally swallow an elephant, being so vast and incomparable, and even more so when multiplied by the countless grains of sand in the Ganges River! It reduces the ferocity of tigers and wolves and safeguards the multitude of whales and dolphins, thus providing significant benefits to Tibet and making significant contributions to Buddhism. To warn against frivolity and to reveal one's dignity, it is not appropriate to compare it with the female characters in "Liaozhai Zhiyi."

    235

    ○　缝裳女
    　　京都有缝裳之业，盖皆负郭贫家，出为市廛缀补者。以其所作多衲衣敝裈等物，故又号之以缝穷云。东直门外有母女，亦业此。女年十六七，貌颇姣好，虽蓬鬓布衣，人恒覸焉。女性贞静，惟低鬟佣作，不轻交一言，人亦无敢犯之。一日，其母病，经旬不能如市，薪水之费俱乏。女不得已，踽踽独行，缝纫终日，薄暮始出都门。携一小竹筥，内贮剪刀棉线，无他物器也。村居去城较远，行及旷野，冢树丛杂，人迹杳然。正仓皇急步间，忽闻林中语曰：“若归乎？予有垢衣，可将为予涤濯。”女愕然。盖母女业亦兼此，遂疑为市井熟识，趋就之。入林，则一恶少年箕踞茂树下，袒裼露臂，形甚凶暴。女惊惧，反身欲回，少年突起直前，提其领如捉鸡雏。女无如之何，乃頳颜询曰：“若有涤衣，可亟付我，我将归。”少年笑曰：“予绐汝。

    Explanation

    235

    A Girl Who Sews Clothes

    In the capital city, there was a trade in mending and sewing clothes, mostly carried out by poor families living near the city walls, who went to the marketplaces to do their repairs. Since their work often involved mending patched and worn-out clothing, they were also called "sewers of poverty." Outside the Dongzhimen Gate, there was a mother and daughter who also engaged in this trade. The daughter was around sixteen or seventeen years old, with a rather beautiful appearance. Despite her unkempt hair and simple clothing, people often looked at her. She was known for her modest and quiet nature, always working diligently with her head lowered, and rarely speaking to anyone, so no one dared to offend her. One day, her mother fell ill and could not go to the market for a whole week, leaving them short of money for daily expenses. The daughter had no choice but to walk alone, spending the whole day sewing and mending, and only left the city gates at dusk. She carried a small bamboo basket, inside which were scissors and cotton thread, and nothing else. The village where she lived was far from the city, and as she walked through the open fields, she came across a place with graves and trees growing in a dense and tangled manner, with no signs of human presence. Just as she was hurrying along in a state of anxiety, she suddenly heard a voice from the forest saying, "Are you going home? I have some dirty clothes that you can wash for me." The girl was startled. Since her mother and daughter's trade also included washing clothes, she suspected that the voice belonged to someone she knew from the marketplace and hurried towards it. When she entered the forest, she saw a young man sitting arrogantly under a tall tree, exposing his chest and arms, with a very fierce and violent appearance. The girl was terrified and turned around to go back, but the young man suddenly rose and came straight towards her, grabbing her collar as if picking up a chick. The girl had no way to deal with the situation and blushed, asking, "If you have clothes to wash, can you quickly give them to me, and I will return home." The young man laughed and said, "I was deceiving you."

    236

    衣服附在吾身，汝何能将去？”女曰：“既无所事事，盍释我，日已暮矣！”少年曰：“予实以情告，予悦汝美有日矣。邂逅相遇，适我愿兮。将与子为好，何归耶？”女闻言，面如土，又急不能脱，遂亦绐之曰：“我女也，从未与男子处。盍先以具示我，果非伟且长者，乃敢诺。”少年大喜曰：“若勿恐，予固无兼人之具者。”因释女，将自捋其裤。女又麾之曰：“止止！我女也，必去其羞缩，事乃可为。若宜偃息树下，待予自寻佳处，摩挲良久，渐以亲狎，始无惧。”少年以女为釜中鱼，谅不至潜逃于渊也，欣然从之。女见其卧，将遁去又惧其追，乃置筥身侧，坐而抚之。隔衣扪探，触手崩腾，因腼然裸其下体，更以纤指把握，大可盈掬。女伪为睥睨，不忍释手，少年早欲炽情炎，不暇瞻顾。女乘其隙，即筥中潜取裁剪，如断帛布，齐其阴而剪之。愤激而力猛，血直溢。少年大嗥，顿化为狐鼠窜而。女益惊悸，目眩尔许时，然后归。抵家犹血殷衿袖焉。

    236

    Clothes are attached to my body, how can you take them away?" The girl replied, "Since there is nothing to do, why not release me? It is already evening!" The young man said, "I am telling you the truth, I have been attracted to your beauty for a long time. We met unexpectedly, and it fulfilled my wishes. I want to be intimate with you, where will you go?" Upon hearing this, the girl's face turned as pale as earth, and she was too frightened to escape, so she lied to him. "I am a woman, I have never been with a man. Why don't you show me your manhood first? If it is not big and long, then I will agree." The young man was very happy and said, "Don't be afraid, I don't have a manhood that can overpower you." He then released her and began to pull down his pants. The girl again told him to stop, saying, "Stop! I am a woman, I must remove my modesty, and then we can proceed. You should lie down under the tree, and I will find a good spot to touch myself for a long time, gradually becoming intimate, and then I will not be afraid." The young man thought the girl was like a fish in a pot, surely she would not escape into the depths, so he happily followed her plan. The girl saw him lying down, wanted to run away but feared he would chase her, so she placed a basket beside her and sat down to touch herself. She touched him through his clothes, and he became excited. She then shyly revealed her lower body and held it with her delicate fingers, which could easily grasp it. The girl pretended to look disdainfully, but couldn't let go. The young man was already burning with desire, too eager to look around. The girl took the opportunity and secretly took a scissors from the basket, cutting it like a piece of cloth, and cut off his genitalia. Furious and with great force, blood gushed out. The young man let out a loud cry and suddenly transformed into a fox or mouse and fled. The girl was even more frightened, dizzy for a while, and then returned home. When she arrived home, her clothes were still stained with blood.

    237

    外史氏曰：雄狐绥绥，遇者多不免。女能于仓卒中以计阉之，町谓能事矣。且其节亦足以风世。脱非然者，之子且虑无裳，能不舍缝裳而褰裳以赴哉？

    ○　火龙
    　　某抚公觞客于园亭，观察以上皆与席。其地故近山，有佳木幽壑之胜，因屏优乐而寻雅集，忘分追欢，选胜自得，良亦一时之快举。乃饮未半酣，天气薄阴，乌云生于岩岫。始如片席，渐且盈空，未几而甘露大澍矣。时方微旱，举座皆顾而相庆。然而电光熠目，雷声反郁而不扬。久之，绕亭不去，丰隆无停声，而雨益倾注。众方心疑，忽抚公连诧奇事，召众视之。抚公故首座，只一人，其几有二异物，闪闪有光。

    Explanation

    237

    Translation

    The Historian said: "The graceful fox, when encountered, often brings misfortune. A woman managed to castrate it in a sudden and clever manner, which one might call a remarkable feat. Moreover, her virtue is sufficient to set an example for the world. If it were not for this, this person would be worried about having no clothes, could she not abandon sewing clothes and lift her skirts to follow?"

    Fire Dragon

    A certain governor hosted a banquet in a garden pavilion, and all officials above the rank of observer were present. The location was near the mountains, with beautiful trees and secluded valleys. Because of the beautiful scenery, they abandoned vulgar entertainment and sought refined gatherings, forgetting their ranks and pursuing joy, selecting the best places to enjoy themselves, which was indeed a delightful event of its time. As they were drinking and not yet fully drunk, the weather turned overcast, and dark clouds formed in the mountains. At first like a piece of matting, gradually filling the sky, soon after it rained sweet dew heavily. At that time there was a slight drought, and everyone looked at each other and celebrated. However, the lightning was bright, and the thunder was muffled, not as expected. For a long time, the clouds lingered around the pavilion, and the thunder did not stop, and the rain poured down more heavily. The guests were puzzled, when suddenly the governor exclaimed about a strange event, calling everyone to see it. The governor, as the host, sat alone, with two strange objects on his table that were shining.

    238

    其一如宵行而巨，缘案疾驰；其一长两寸许，细如线状，类蛇，蜿蜒逐之。行亦甚迅，所经处几为之灼，宛以线香烧刻者，深入肤理。簠簋之旁，纵横莫计其数。众皆茫如，臬司某公素博物，亟言于抚公曰：“请公少避此席，是殆怪异借公之威灵以免雷劫耳。神龙亦畏福泽，不敢攫拿，所谓投鼠忌器者也。”抚公深韪其言，冒雨出亭后，行未数武，忽震雷一声，轩楹摆簸，盘盂瓦碎，竟揭亭之一角而去，雨亦顿止。抚公与其僚属耳目震眩，有类聋聩，良久，始复其故。遂罢饮，踪迹二物，皆不见。因收其几入内署，用以志异。幕中人多有见之者。

    Explanation

    238

    One creature was large and moved at night like a giant, rushing along the table; the other was about two inches long, thin like a thread, and resembled a snake, winding and pursuing it. It also moved very quickly, and the places it passed through almost burned, as if burned and carved by a stick of incense, deep into the skin and flesh. By the side of the food vessels, they went in countless numbers, going in all directions. Everyone was bewildered. A certain official of the Judicial Department, who is usually well-versed in many things, quickly said to the governor, "Please step away from this seat. This is probably a strange phenomenon using your divine power to avoid a thunderbolt. Even a divine dragon fears blessings, dare not seize, this is what is called 'fearing to harm the instrument while trying to catch the mouse.'" The governor deeply agreed with his words, went out after the pavilion in the rain, had not walked a few steps, suddenly, a thunderclap sounded, the beams and pillars shook, plates and bowls broke, finally lifted one corner of the pavilion and flew away, the rain also suddenly stopped. The governor and his subordinates were stunned, as if deaf and dumb, after a long time, finally returned to normal. Then stopped drinking, traced the two creatures, but they were not seen. Then collected the table and took it into the inner office, to record the strange event. Many people in the tent saw it.

    239

    外史氏曰：龙之变幻，莫可端倪，传记言之甚伙。而雷之为雷，其不测亦多类此，且更有神于此者。昔在都中，寓于某寺。一夕，过邻友闲叙，大雨滂沱，疾霆不止。倏闻震响声，似在左近，心疑吾寓，而急不能归。清晨入寺，雷果震其左庑，则非吾寓，而实人寓也。其人皆射夫，俱京营食饷者，昼聚于寺，夜返其家。室固虚无人，然壁有仆姑数枝，崭然新制，雷之所击者，竟在于是。余亟就而视之，凡五矢皆类悬丝，不绝如缕。宇中都无所损，亦无焦灼痕，不胜骇异而退。

    239

    The historian said: Dragons change form in ways that cannot be fathomed, and many historical records have discussed this extensively. And thunder, in its unpredictability, is much like this, and there are even more mysterious aspects. Once, while staying in a certain temple in the capital, I visited a neighboring friend one evening, and it was pouring with rain, with continuous thunder. Suddenly, a loud sound was heard, seeming to come from nearby. I suspected it was coming from my own lodging, but I was too frightened to return immediately. In the morning, when I entered the temple, I found that the left wing of the temple had indeed been struck by lightning. It was not my lodging, but someone else's. The people who lived there were archers, soldiers who received rations from the capital's military camp. They gathered at the temple during the day and returned home at night. The room was empty and unoccupied, but there were several new arrows hanging on the wall, looking fresh and newly made. The lightning strike actually hit the arrows. There were five arrows, all hanging like threads, still intact. The room was undamaged and showed no signs of scorching. I was profoundly astonished and left.

    240

    ○　青眉
    　　皮工竺十八，邑之鄙人也。年仅弱冠，貌姣好如女子。虽居市廛，里之美少年莫之能掩，以故有俊竺之号。其室曰青眉，色尤姝丽，见者疑为画图。初诘其所自，坚讳不言，后乃稍稍露之，则实北山之狐也。盖竺少佣于乡，始学裁皮，年甫十六耳。师嗜酒，夜出恒不归，肆中惟竺一人，缝纫至中宵，然后敢寝，率以为常。一夕，师又出。竺方夜作，闻弹指声，意为比邻取履者。隔扉询之，则答曰：“侬。”其音绝娇细。竺大骇，且虑为市中恶少侦其师不在，来寻断袖欢，心益惴惴。乃绐之曰：“已卧矣，客请明日来。”外又曰：“侬非暴客，实邻女也。盍开，我与若一言。

    Explanation

    240

    A Woman with Green Brows

    Zhu Shiba, a leatherworker, was a rustic from the town. At the age of twenty, he was as beautiful as a woman. Although he lived in a marketplace, the handsome young men in the neighborhood could not hide his beauty, and thus he was given the nickname "Handsome Zhu." His wife was named Qingmei, and she was exceptionally beautiful, with a charm that made people doubt if she was a painting. When asked about her origins, she firmly refused to speak, but later gradually revealed that she was actually a fox spirit from the North Mountain. It seems that Zhu had been working in the village since he was young, starting to learn leatherworking at the age of sixteen. His master enjoyed drinking and often left at night, never returning. In the workshop, only Zhu was left, sewing until midnight before daring to sleep, which was his usual routine. One night, the master left again. Zhu was working late when he heard a snapping sound, thinking it was someone from the neighboring house taking shoes. He asked through the door, and the voice replied, "Me." The voice was extremely soft and delicate. Zhu was greatly startled and worried that it might be a thug from the market who had noticed the master's absence and was coming to seek a same-sex relationship, making him even more anxious. He then lied to the person, saying, "I am already asleep. Please come tomorrow." The voice outside said, "I am not a violent intruder; I am actually a neighbor's daughter. Why don't you open the door? I want to talk to you."

    241

    ”竺不得已，从板缺觇之，果似二八垂鬟，立于檐下，因启之，女径掩笑入。竺视其貌，容光照映斗室，虽少小，心亦不能无动。遂腼然诘所自来，答曰：“家居距此咫尺，缘夜绩烛为风灭，特来乞子新火，非有他也。”竺素醇谨，慨然与之，不敢交一言，女亦持炬径去。竺虽未通情话，而心颇爱好，冀其复来。乃师归，女竟不再至。日夕坐肆中伺之，亦杳无其迹。无何，师又他往，女则又来乞火。两情渐稔，欣然延入，与坐谈。女以年岁询竺，答曰：“一十六矣。”女微笑曰：“阿侬适与君同庚。”竺亦询女之居址，答曰：“久当自悉。”絮语移时，犹无去志，竺亦贪其貌，眷恋弗舍。

    Explanation

    241

    Zhu had no choice but to peek through the crack in the board. Sure enough, there was a girl with hanging hair, looking like she was sixteen, standing under the eaves. He opened the door, and the girl laughed and entered directly. Zhu looked at her appearance, and her radiant beauty illuminated the small room. Although she was young, his heart could not remain unmoved. He shyly asked where she came from, and she replied, "My home is just a short distance from here. My candle went out due to the wind last night, so I came to ask for a new flame, nothing else." Zhu, who was usually sincere and cautious, readily gave her the fire, and they did not exchange any words. The girl also carried the torch and left directly. Although Zhu had not yet spoken with her, he was already enamored by her appearance and hoped she would come again. When the teacher returned, the girl never came again. Zhu sat in the shop day and night waiting for her, but there was no trace of her. Not long after, the teacher went somewhere else, and the girl came again to ask for fire. Their feelings gradually became familiar, and Zhu joyfully invited her in and sat to talk with her. The girl asked Zhu about his age, and he replied, "I am sixteen." The girl smiled and said, "I am the same age as you." Zhu also asked about the girl's residence, and she replied, "You will know soon enough." They chatted for a while, and she still showed no intention of leaving. Zhu was also enamored by her appearance and longed to keep her.

    242

    四目痴痴，将不可解，女忽回顾皀席，谓竺曰：“此即君之卧榻耶？恐逼仄不足以容二人。”竺会其意，乃答曰：“卿试先卧，看能容否？”女笑而起曰：“明夕来当试之。”又复去。竺终腼腆，弗能挽留，然已心志蛊惑矣。晨起无心操作，惟冀其师不归，得以成此佳会。而师果为麴蘖所羁，向晦不复，心益悦。及昏，明灯兀坐，形状类痴，亦不再捆屦。漏下二鼓，女果来。款户启之入，则靓妆艳服，迥异昨之朴素。询之，笑而不答，径登竺榻，面壁卧。竺知其惧羞，乃先解己衣，息火就枕，暗中摸索，手战情炽。女忽佯拒之曰：“市井儿，同衾已足，复望其他耶？”竺笑曰：“予意同衾者未能无事。

    Explanation

    242

    The woman, with a blank and staring look, was almost incomprehensible. She suddenly looked back at the bed and said to Zhi, "Is this your bed? I fear it's too cramped to accommodate two people." Zhi understood her meaning and replied, "Please try lying down first, and see if it can accommodate you." The woman smiled and stood up, saying, "I will come back tomorrow night to try it." She then left again. Zhi, feeling shy and unable to detain her, was already mentally entranced. In the morning, he had no heart to work, only hoping that his teacher would not return, so he could have this delightful encounter. Indeed, the teacher was detained by alcohol and did not return that night, making Zhi even happier. By evening, he sat alone with a lit lamp, his demeanor resembling that of a fool, and he no longer bothered to tie his shoes. At two drum beats after midnight, the woman indeed came. She knocked gently on the door and opened it to enter, dressed up in beautiful and splendid attire, completely different from yesterday's plainness. When asked, she smiled without answering and directly climbed onto Zhi's bed, lying down facing the wall. Zhi knew she was afraid of shame and so undressed himself first, extinguished the fire, and lay down in the dark. He groped around in the darkness, his hands trembling with intense emotion. The woman suddenly pretended to resist, saying, "A commoner, just sleeping together is enough, what else do you want?" Zhi laughed and said, "I think that even sleeping together doesn't mean there's nothing to do."

    243

    ”已而娇香流溢，带缓衣松，女若战战弗克胜任，而缱绻之意尤浓。竺初近女色，颠倒神魂，不须臾，而玉山颓矣。于是柔肌互贴，梦寐皆春，及寤而东方已白。竺尚流连，女早揽衣先起曰：“乐正未央，不可使他人窥见底里。”乃去。竺起而师返，女绝不来，竺亦不以为讶。阅数夕，乘师之出，又复欢会，款洽且倍于初。女谓竺曰：“侬自见君，顿为情系，以故不能自坚，致有前宵之事。今幸两相欢爱，生死弗渝，君能不弃，即以妾为糟糠妇乎？”竺嗫嚅良久，始答曰：“阿谁不愿？但予幼失怙恃，育于兄嫂，今从师习此末艺，将来尚未知若何。畴有余资，为予纳妇耶？且年齿尚卑，尤未敢漫然启口。”女曰：“然。以侬计之，君能辞师出游，妾自能相君立业。奚为仰人眉睫，使我燕尔不欢？”竺恍然。

    Explanation

    243

    After the encounter, the fragrance of her beauty spread, her belt loosened, and her clothes slackened. The girl seemed to tremble, unable to bear the weight, yet the affectionate feelings were even stronger. Zhu, who had just experienced the allure of a woman, was completely bewitched, and in no time at all, his composure collapsed. Then, their soft bodies pressed against each other, and both in dreams and in waking, everything was like spring. When they woke up, the eastern sky had turned white. Zhu still lingered, but the girl quickly put on her clothes and got up first, saying, "The joy is not yet complete; it is not possible to let others see the innermost depths." She then left. Zhu got up as his teacher returned, and the girl did not come again, and Zhu did not find this surprising. After several nights, taking advantage of his teacher's absence, they met again, and their intimacy was even stronger than before. The girl said to Zhu, "Since I met you, I was suddenly bound by love, and that is why I could not remain steadfast, leading to what happened last night. Now that we are both happy and love each other, our bond will never change. If you do not abandon me, will you take me as your wife, even in times of hardship?" Zhu hesitated for a long time before answering, "Who would not want it? But I lost my parents at a young age and was raised by my brother and sister-in-law. Now I am studying this minor art under a teacher, and I do not know what the future holds. Who has the means to marry me? And since I am still young, I dare not rashly speak." The girl said, "Yes. According to my thinking, if you can leave your teacher and travel, I can help you establish your career. Why depend on others' faces, making our happiness incomplete?" Zhu suddenly understood.

    244

    乃诘之曰：“若言有家在，岂无父母而可自主耶？”女笑曰：“妾初绐君，君今乃悟乎？侬字青眉，居北山，实狐也。羡君玉貌，故假邻女以相就，岂真有高堂为予缚束者？”竺年幼，且贪新欢，茫不知惧，唯曰：“闻狐恒为人害，信然否？”女曰：“亦信有之，而妾非其伦也。妾不爱君，亦不屑至此，爱之而复杀之，宁能见容于天地乎？”因侃侃盟誓，竺亦相信不疑。临去援竺以策，竺如其教，启于师曰：“昨闻里人言予嫂病，且甚危殆。予少受其抚育，请给假一归省视。”言已泣下。师亦微闻其嫂疾，见其悱恻，心甚悯焉。乃自营肆务，遣之行。

    Explanation

    244

    Then he questioned her, saying, "If you say you have a family, how can you be independent without your parents' consent?" She smiled and said, "I was initially deceiving you, and now you have finally realized it. My name is Qingmei, I live in the North Mountain, and I am actually a fox. I admired your beautiful appearance, so I pretended to be a neighbor's daughter to get close to you. Do you really have parents who would restrain me?" Zhu was young and greedy for new pleasure, unaware of the danger. He said, "Do you believe that foxes are always harmful to people?" She replied, "It is true that there are such cases, but I am not like them. I do not love you, nor do I stoop so low. How could I be forgiven by heaven and earth for loving you and then killing you?" She then solemnly swore an oath. Zhu also believed her without doubt. As she was about to leave, she handed Zhu a staff and instructed him to follow her advice. Zhu then went to his master and said, "I heard from the villagers that my wife is ill and in great danger. I was young and received her care, so I ask for leave to return home to visit her." He spoke and tears fell from his eyes. The master also heard faintly that his wife was ill and, seeing his sorrow, felt deeply pitiful. He then took care of the business affairs and sent him away.

    245

    竺出肆未及里许，女早逆于道周，问之曰：“君将奚适？”竺曰：“将归予家。”女大笑曰：“君误矣。若往汝家，有兄嫂在，其何能不从师？”竺曰：“为之奈何？”女曰：“侬视君之业，虽未能游刃有余，而尚可以进乎技。妾幸有薄积，请与君游于外郡，自立生计，必有以愈于为人佣，君以为何如？”竺本漫无主裁，欣然从之。女出白金一锭，觅舟南行。竺与女倡随甚乐，亦不念及乡族。舟抵常熟，女犹欲前进，竺不愿，乃僦居邑之北门。女又以金半笏，为营肆具，遂开设于市中，其后为居室。女以竺齿尚稚，不令合人生理，凡竺所不能制者，女皆代庖为之，式甚新奇，名乃大噪，邑中之履咸归焉。女亲操井臼，治饔餐，暇则织屦相夫子，怡怡然无怨色，竺益心德之。

    Explanation

    245

    Zhu exited the shop before he had gone even a mile, when the woman met him on the road ahead and asked, "Where are you going, sir?" Zhu replied, "I am returning to my family." The woman laughed and said, "You are mistaken. If you go to your family, there are your older brother and sister-in-law, how could you avoid following their lead?" Zhu asked, "What shall I do about it?" The woman said, "I see your craft, although you have not yet reached the point of effortless skill, you can still improve. I fortunately have a small amount of savings; I propose to travel with you to another prefecture, establish our own livelihood, and it will surely be better than being a hired hand. What do you think?" Zhu was indifferent and without a firm decision, and happily agreed. The woman took out a lump of white gold, found a boat to travel south. Zhu and the woman were very happy together and did not think of their hometown and relatives. The boat arrived in Changshu, but the woman still wanted to go further, Zhu did not wish to, so they rented a house in the northern gate of the county town. The woman used half a ceremonial tablet's worth of gold to prepare the shop, opened it in the market, and later used it as their residence. The woman thought Zhu was still young and did not allow him to engage in business activities, for everything Zhu could not manage, she would substitute and do it. The style was very novel and unique, and the reputation became widely known, everyone came to visit. The woman personally handled household chores, prepared meals, and when idle, she would weave sandals for her husband, happily without any sign of complaint. Zhu was increasingly grateful to her in heart.

    246

    明年，竺已十七，家小裕，志遂少荒，数从无赖游，女禁之弗听。适常熟有富家子，性佻达，尤好龙阳君，时来肆中市履，见竺之色，深悦之。会竺与无赖交，乃以重金啖诸无赖。值望后，月色甚明，众置酒于邑中慈觉寺，邀竺为长夜饮。竺以他故绐女，遂从无赖行，至则富家子亦在座，极致款曲。竺素限于量，饮未半，已不胜酒力，众引之别室，俾其小憩，实则以计嬲之也。竺方转侧欲眠，忽闻人小语曰：“舍妾孤栖，君乃在此高卧耶？”竺亟张目视，则青眉立于榻侧，因诘其何以至此？女曰：“君之危若履虎尾，犹问乎？请即从妾归。”竺内惭，因诈以醉辞。女以气噀竺面，冷若觱栗之风，酒顿醒，强起随之行。

    Explanation

    246

    In the following year, Zhi was seventeen years old, and his family's finances had improved somewhat, but his aspirations became somewhat neglected. He often associated with ruffians, and the woman tried to stop him but he wouldn't listen. There happened to be a wealthy young man in Changshu, who had a light and unrestrained personality and particularly favored male companions. He would sometimes come to the shop to buy shoes and was deeply attracted by Zhi's appearance. When Zhi associated with ruffians, the young man bribed them with heavy gold. On the night of the full moon, the moonlight was very bright, and the group arranged a banquet at the Cijue Temple in the town and invited Zhi for a night of drinking. Zhi deceived the woman with some excuse and then followed the ruffians. When they arrived, the wealthy young man was also present and treated Zhi with great hospitality. Zhi usually had a limited capacity, and after drinking only half, he could no longer hold his liquor. The group led him to a separate room, telling him to rest, but in reality, they were planning to seduce him. As Zhi was turning over to sleep, he suddenly heard someone whisper, "Abandoning your wife who is alone, you are sleeping here high and dry?" Zhi quickly opened his eyes and saw a woman with a green eyebrow standing by the bed. He asked how she had come there. The woman said, "Your danger is like treading on a tiger's tail, and you still ask? Please follow me back immediately." Zhi felt ashamed inside and pretended to be drunk to refuse. The woman blew a cold breath on Zhi's face, like the wind of the birch tree, and he immediately sobered up, forcing himself to get up and follow her.

    247

    女曰：“君未得其实，归将怨妾，盍少留，当有笑柄供君解颐。”随捉一矮凳，置床头以待。麾之，倏成人形，衣缕面容与竺无差别。竺亦莫测其意，惟伫伺之。有顷，见富家子与众嬉笑而入曰：“啜醩之鱼可捉矣！”径以手启卧者之衣，潜捋其裤，狎亵之状不可胜言。竺面赤汗流，始悟众等恶计。女顿以纤腕相握曰：“去，去！”遂悄然出走，恍若梦寐，两身早在室中矣。既归，女延之坐，长跽而数之曰：“妾携君远离故里，虽不敢望君大成，亦宜自爱。今君数作浮荡，几以丈夫之躯，陷入妾妇之队。使狡谋果遂，不独妾羞为弥子之妻，君又何面目归向桑梓乎？”语甚悲咽，泣下数行。竺愧悔无以自容，颜色沮丧，莫措一词。女恐其过惭，乃起以温言慰藉曰：“后无复然，过固贵于能改也。

    Explanation

    247

    The woman said, "You have not understood the situation; you will blame me when you return. Why not stay a little longer? There will be something amusing to make you laugh." She quickly grabbed a small stool, placed it by the bed, and waited. She waved her hand, and suddenly took on a human form, her clothes and appearance were no different from Zhu's. Zhu could not understand her intentions and waited for her. After a while, she saw the wealthy young man enter with others, laughing and joking. He said, "The fish that has drunk alcohol can be caught!" She quickly opened the sleeping person's clothes and secretly pulled down their pants. The indecent state was beyond description. Zhu's face turned red and sweat flowed, and he finally realized the malicious plan of the others. The woman suddenly took Zhu's hand and said, "Go, go!" And quietly left. It was as if in a dream, and both bodies were already in the room. After returning, the woman invited him to sit. She knelt down and reproached him, saying, "I brought you away from your hometown. Though I do not dare to hope for great success for you, you should also take care of yourself. Now you often act frivolously, almost got yourself, as a man, into a woman's group. If their cunning plan had succeeded, I would not only be ashamed to be a wife of Zi Mi, what face would you have to return to your hometown?" Her words were very sorrowful, and tears flowed down her cheeks. Zhu felt ashamed and regretful, unable to bear it, looked dejected, and could not say a word. The woman feared he would be too ashamed, so she stood up, spoke gently, and said, "Do not do this again in the future. It is valuable to be able to correct mistakes."

    248

    ”遂仍欢好，不再言。乃富家子为欢良久，顿觉有异，视之，则裸伏凳上，竺之迹渺然。大惊，疑竺为妖，与众共首于县。时巴陵苏荩臣，以进土宰常熟，素稔富家子有邪行，不欲究其事。然因马朝柱一案，逮捕妖术甚亟，爰命役拘竺。竺至，公见其少小，且事涉暧昧，略加研诘，竟笑遣之。竺归肆，女忽谓之曰：“是地不可复居，将有祸至。”遂货其器具，束装北行，徙家于瓜步间，爰卜山阳之南郭而居之。女以竺少不更事，前因多资致荡其心，遂不复设肆，日令竺荷担入市，所得者仅足餬口。己乃茅屋数椽，纺绩相助，此外别无盈余。

    Explanation

    248

    The rich son continued to maintain his friendship with Zhu, and they no longer spoke of the incident. After a long time, the rich son suddenly felt something strange. When he looked, he saw Zhu lying naked on a bench, and Zhu's traces had vanished. He was greatly shocked and suspected Zhu of being a demon. He reported the matter to the county with others. At that time, Su Jincheng of Balin, who had passed the imperial examinations and served as the governor of Changshu, was already familiar with the rich son's immoral behavior and did not want to investigate the matter further. However, due to the case of Ma Chaoshu, who was urgently arresting people accused of witchcraft, he ordered officials to arrest Zhu. When Zhu arrived, the official saw that he was young and that the matter involved ambiguous circumstances, so he lightly questioned him and then laughed and dismissed him. Zhu returned to his shop, and the woman suddenly told him, "This place cannot be lived in anymore, a disaster is coming." So they sold their equipment, packed up, and headed north, moving their family to the area near Guabu, and then chose to live in the southern suburb of Shanyang. The woman thought Zhu was young and inexperienced, and because of his previous wealth, it had made his heart frivolous. So he no longer set up a shop, and she made him carry goods to the market every day, earning barely enough to make ends meet. She herself built a few thatched huts and helped with spinning and weaving, but there was no extra profit.

    249

    竺渐不能堪，每出窃与市儿赌，始亦获采，少助杖头，遂欣欣以为得意，女故知而不问。一日，女出汲，突遇同巷某瞥见之，惊以为神仙中人。盖某素业赌，以博得罪于势豪，方切忧惧，见女居为奇货，顿思假此为释憾之计，献媚于豪。因乘间以言餂竺曰：“子业此欲赡两口，势必有所不能。且男子远离乡井，亦当思奋身立业，始可归见里族。若仅日觅蝇头，竟同株守，不第不能归，归亦何颜也？”竺闻言适中所患，乃咨嗟曰：“君言良是，但无处借资，业何由立？”某又佯为踌躇，徐曰：“此事亦非大难，某同辈中某某，均以博起家，获资千万。闻子采兴甚高，战无不利，盍为此不母而子之策，白手可致素封，犹愈于坐操会计多多矣。

    Explanation

    249

    Zhu gradually could not bear his circumstances, and every time he went out secretly to gamble with the market children, at first he also won, a little help for his needs, and thus happily felt very pleased. The woman intentionally knew about his gambling but did not inquire further. One day, the woman went out to draw water and suddenly encountered someone from the same lane who caught a glimpse of her, and was surprised, thinking she was a divine being. Because that person had always been involved in gambling and got into trouble with powerful people through gambling, he was deeply worried and fearful. Seeing the woman as a rare treasure, he immediately thought of using her to alleviate his regret and to flatter the powerful. Taking the opportunity, he spoke to Zhu in a flattering manner, saying: "You are doing this to support two people, there must be something you cannot do. Moreover, a man leaving his hometown should also think about striving to establish himself, only then can you return and face your fellow villagers. If you only seek small gains every day, you will end up like a tree standing still, not only cannot you return, even if you return, what face will you have?" Zhu's words hit the nail on the head, and he sighed, saying: "Your words are indeed true, but there is no place to borrow money, how can one establish oneself?" The person feigned hesitation and said slowly: "This matter is not too difficult. Among my peers, certain individuals have all risen from gambling and gained millions. Hearing that your passion for gambling is very high and you never lose, why not adopt this strategy of not being a mother but a son? With nothing but your hands, you can achieve a life of wealth, which is better than sitting idly by and counting accounts."

    250

    ”竺本以此自负，又不禁歆羡之，私遽攘臂曰：“君能贷我十緡，我当试一为之，看花骨子非我如意珠耶？”某慨然许诺。暮又偕一人来，曰：“予适小匮乏，贷于此兄，幸如数，请即署券。”竺素不能书，女虽能又不敢以告，即倩某捉刀，其名实即某豪，竺不及知也。其一人得券，即以资付竺，匆遽而去。竺亦未及致详，径携资就某家赌。其始小胜，后乃大亏，比及鸡鸣，早已万钱立罄。众哄然散去，竺亦垂首而归。抵家倦卧，女故悉其所为，亦不致诘。又明日，竺诣某处，与商背城之策，数往皆不遇。瞬息月余，某忽偕数人至，衣帽甚都，前人亦在内。某谓竺曰：“积欠猝未能清，其子可偿也。

    Explanation

    250

    Zhu originally took pride in his skills, yet he could not help but envy the prospect of greater success. He secretly and hastily raised his arm and said, "If you can lend me ten thousand strings of cash, I will try to do it once and see if the flower bone is not my wish pearl." Someone readily agreed. That evening, he came with another person and said, "I just have a small shortage and borrowed from this brother, fortunately, it was the full amount. Please sign the contract immediately." Zhu has never been able to write, and though the woman could, she dared not tell. He then asked someone to write on his behalf, and his name was actually that of a certain powerful person, but Zhu did not know this. One of them got the contract and immediately paid Zhu the money, then they hurriedly left. Zhu did not have time to inquire further and took the money to gamble at someone's house. At first, he had small wins, but later suffered great losses. By the time the rooster crowed, the ten thousand strings of cash were already spent. The crowd suddenly dispersed, and Zhu returned with his head hanging low. He arrived home and fell asleep tiredly, and the woman knew all his actions but did not interrogate him. The next day, Zhu went to a certain place to discuss strategies for retreating to the city, but he went several times and did not meet him. In a short month, someone suddenly came with several people, dressed very well, and the previous person was also among them. He told Zhu, "The accumulated debt could not be cleared immediately, but his son can repay it."

    251

    ”竺为此故，已私蓄千钱，毅然曰：“息几何矣？”答曰：“五十緡耳！”竺骇曰：“其母仅十千，其子何反数倍耶？”众哗曰：“语都不类！”亟出券，令竺自阅，则已千緡实书其上矣。竺不觉颈赤，与某力争，某亦不相下，手口交加。众咸怒曰：“逋欠者亦敢肆虐耶？”遂群殴之，几毙而后去。邻人有怜竺者，扶掖入室。女为之抚摩疮痍，毫无诟谇，人益贤之。诘朝，豪仆又来取索，且风示其指曰：“能以妇偿，百緡尚可得。”竺大詈之，其人即返。又引前数人来，挝门秽辱，比邻俱掩耳恶闻。女背竺出，亟止之曰：“若勿尔尔。若之意在人不在资，侬已知之。但竺为侬夫，今甚狼狈，伉俪之情，不忍遽绝。归与若主言，果相悦，俟竺愈，径来相迎，侬固不惜此一身。”豪仆闻言皆喜，敬诺而去。

    Explanation

    251

    For this reason, Zhu had secretly saved a thousand coins. He firmly said, "How much interest has accrued?" The other person replied, "Only fifty thousand coins!" Zhu was astonished and said, "Her mother had only ten thousand coins, how could her son have several times more?" The crowd exclaimed, "The words don't sound right!" They quickly produced the contract and ordered Zhu to read it himself. It was actually written as one thousand thousand coins on it. Zhu unconsciously blushed and argued fiercely with the other person. The other person also did not give in, and they used both hands and words to argue. The crowd was angry and said, "Even a debtor dares to be so violent?" They beat him up collectively, almost killing him before leaving. A neighbor who pitied Zhu helped him into the room. The woman soothed his wounds without any quarreling, and people became even more virtuous towards her. The next morning, the powerful servant came again to demand payment and hinted at what he wanted to say, "If you can pay with your wife, you can still get one hundred thousand coins." Zhu scolded him fiercely, and the person immediately left. Then brought the previous several people, who beat on the door and insulted. Even the neighbors nearby covered their ears and disliked hearing it. The woman went out behind Zhu's back and quickly stopped her and said, "You shouldn't do that. Your intention is in the person, not in the money. I already know about it. But Zhu is my husband, he is now in a very difficult situation. Spousal affection, cannot suddenly sever. Go back and tell your master. If you truly like each other, wait until Zhu recovers, come directly to welcome me. I would not hesitate to sacrifice myself for this." The powerful servants were pleased upon hearing this and respectfully agreed to leave.

    252

    里中聆其言者，俱以女为缓攻计，即竺亦不疑其有去心。浃旬，竺已复初，惟忧豪家来索逋。已而果至，女出与之约，竺亦不能尽知。晚间，女置酒室中，为竺庆。少酣，女起满酌而语之曰：“妾为君妇，三载于兹，不克有所裨益。既致君离其乡里，骨肉不通笑言，今又以蒲柳之庸姿，辱君于狂奴之毒手，心实怍焉。刻下积逋无偿，进退维谷，君将何以处之？”竺默然，既而叹曰：“予诚不肖，重负吾卿。豪家之事，情甘与之涉讼，他复何言？”女泫然曰：“君奚固执若此？君以异乡之身，与豪右相较，危可翘足而待。若整装急旋故土，上可广先人之祀，下可酬兄嫂之恩，计诚莫逾于此。

    Explanation

    252

    Those in the village who heard her words all regarded the girl as a delaying tactic, and even Zhu did not suspect she had any intention to leave. After ten days, Zhu had returned to his usual state, but he was only worried about the wealthy family coming to demand the debt. Soon enough, they indeed arrived, and the girl went out and made an agreement with them, though Zhu could not fully understand what was happening. In the evening, the girl set up a banquet in the room to celebrate with Zhu. After a little drinking, the girl stood up, filled her cup, and spoke to him: "I have been your wife for three years, yet I have not been able to bring any benefit. I have caused you to leave your hometown, and your family members do not communicate with you. Now, with my humble and weak appearance, I have shamed you with the cruel hands of a madman. My heart feels truly ashamed. Now, the accumulated debt remains unpaid, and I am in a difficult situation where one cannot advance or retreat. What will you do about it?" Zhu remained silent, then sighed and said, "I truly am unworthy and have heavily burdened you. I am willing to sue the wealthy family, but what else can I say?" The girl tearfully replied, "Why are you so stubborn? As a person from a foreign land, you are competing with the powerful, and danger is just around the corner.
