If you quickly return to your hometown, you can honor your ancestors and repay your brothers and sisters-in-law. There is no better plan than this."

    253

    ”竺已喻其指，因曰：“我归子将若何？”女曰：“豪之所图者，色也，妾以色事君，即以色事豪，渠必不追吾夫矣！”竺艴然色异曰：“是何言也？予宁死不以妻抵债。”女遂不再言。及寝，又以利害说之，竺方首肯。女即起为之治装，促之行曰：“不可缓，迟疑则祸至矣。”竺尚留连，女强之出门，以手麾之，竺遂不能自由，大奔若狂，直至百里外，始复其故步。暮投旅邸，计去山阳已二日程，竺终以女为念，止不复前，将以探其耗。阅五日，果有自淮上来者，且其熟识也，见竺即尤之曰：“子诚负心，捐妻子而远遁，令其死于强暴，情何以堪？”竺故预料有此，乃大恸。诘其颠末，人曰：“尊阃至豪家，涕泣不食。夜出缢于其门，尸重不能举。

    Explanation

    253

    Zhu had understood her meaning and then said, "What will you do if I return?" The woman replied, "The powerful man desires only beauty. I served you with my beauty, and now I will serve him with my beauty. He will surely not pursue my husband anymore!" Zhu became angry and changed his expression, saying, "What are you saying? I would rather die than use my wife to pay off debts." The woman then stopped speaking. When they lay down to sleep, she again tried to persuade him with arguments of advantage and disadvantage, and Zhu finally nodded in agreement. The woman immediately got up to prepare his clothes and urged him to leave, saying, "Do not delay, hesitation will bring disaster." Zhu still lingered, but the woman forced him out the door, waving her hand at him. Zhu was then unable to control himself and ran wildly, until he reached a hundred miles away, where he finally returned to his old ways. At dusk, he stayed at an inn, calculating that he was two days' journey from Shanyang. Zhu was still thinking about the woman and stopped, not going further, intending to investigate her situation. After five days, indeed, someone came from the Huai River, and he was an acquaintance. Seeing Zhu, he immediately blamed him, saying, "You are truly unfaithful. You abandoned your wife and children and fled far away, leaving her to die at the hands of a powerful man. How can one bear such feelings?" Zhu had indeed anticipated this and then wept bitterly. He asked about the details, and the person said, "Your wife went to the powerful man's house, wept, and did not eat. At night, she hanged herself at his door. Her body was too heavy to lift."

    254

    官知之，检其怀中，得血状，具诉其冤。官将逮子，莫知所往，因置豪于法，并诱子者亦得罪，邻里咸称快。予来时，狱将具矣。”竺心又少慰，乃市楮镪，祭之于野，痛哭至呕血。病卧传舍，时时饮泣，旋复迷惘。沉顿间女忽欻然入，就榻抚视，且笑曰：“妾已得生，君何为欲死耶？”竺愕然曰：“闻卿已殉节，今至此，得勿学桂英来索王魁命乎？予诚负心，没亦无憾。”女又笑曰：“年已如许大，何犹菽麦不辨，呱呱作小儿啼哉！妾本狐仙，宁无自全之策？向之殁者，特江间一片石，岂侬亦效痴妇人作投缳鬼哉？”竺夙知其灵异，欣喜不胜。而病已甚惫，女投之以药，遂霍然。

    254

    The official investigated and searched her pocket, finding a blood-stained note, and he fully reported her grievance. The official intended to arrest the child, but did not know where he had gone. He thus punished the ruffian according to the law, and the person who lured the child also incurred guilt, and the neighbors all praised it. When I arrived, the case was about to be finalized. Zhu's heart was somewhat comforted. He then bought paper money and offered sacrifices to her in the fields, wept bitterly until he vomited blood. He lay ill in the inn, often weeping, and soon became confused. In his daze, the woman suddenly appeared, approached the bed to comfort him, and smiled, saying, "I have already been saved, why do you want to die?" Zhu was startled and said, "I heard you had sacrificed your life, now that you are here, are you not like Guiying coming to claim Wang Kui's life? I truly betrayed you, even if I die, I have no regrets." The woman smiled and said, "You are already this old, why don't you distinguish between beans and wheat, and why are you crying like a child? I am originally a fox spirit, how could I not have a way to save myself? The one who died before was just a piece of stone in the river, how could I also imitate a foolish woman to hang myself?" Zhu had always known of her supernatural abilities, and was overjoyed. But his illness was severe, and the woman gave him medicine, and he soon recovered.

    255

    女又谓竺曰：“妾不可露形于此，致人疑怪，当仍往前途候君，君亦勿久滞。”乃先行。竺至次日，亦就道，至夕与女重圆于旅次。竺谋他适，女不可曰：“前因一时孟浪，屡踬于他乡，今而知安乐莫如故土也。请即偕归，不再与君作汗漫游矣。”于是出金为竺制衣履并己之妆饰，遂返本邑。初竺之兄不见弟，欲讼其师，乡人有见竺远行者，力止之，而兄嫂恒思忆不置。一旦见竺携艳妻复，其邦族咸惊喜。竺诡言娶于他邑，人亦不疑。女以资授竺，使仍设肆于市，而迎兄嫂与居，奉养于家，曰：“为我约束狂郎。妇虽智，究难钳制夫也。”自此竺与女力作，家以日裕。余初见青眉，深异其非常人，因再三诘，竺甫肯缅陈其概。更谓余曰：“微君之文，予妻将湮没毕世矣。”余亦喜其相夫之智，持节之坚，遂援笔而为之传。

    255

    The woman again told Zhu, "I cannot show my form here, as it would cause people to doubt and be surprised. I should still go ahead to wait for you. You should not linger too long." She then went ahead. The next day, Zhu also set out on the journey, and by evening, they reunited with the woman on the road. Zhu planned to move elsewhere, but the woman said, "Before, because of a momentary rash act, I often stumbled in foreign places. Now I know that happiness is best in one's hometown. Please come back with me. I will no longer travel aimlessly with you." She then took out money to make clothes and shoes for Zhu and also for herself, and they returned to their hometown. At first, Zhu's brother did not see his younger brother and wanted to sue his teacher. Some villagers saw Zhu traveling far away and strongly stopped him. However, Zhu's brother and sister-in-law constantly thought of him and could not stop. One day, they saw Zhu return with a beautiful wife, and the entire village was surprised and delighted. Zhu lied and said he had married in another town, and people did not suspect. The woman gave Zhu money to let him set up a shop in the market and brought his brother and sister-in-law to live with them, supporting them at home. She said, "Discipline my reckless husband. Though I am intelligent, it is ultimately difficult to control my husband." From then on, Zhu and the woman worked hard, and their family became increasingly prosperous. I first met Qingmei and deeply felt that she was not an ordinary person. So I repeatedly questioned her, and Zhu only then reluctantly recounted the general outline. He then said to me, "Without your writing, my wife would have been forgotten for eternity." I also admired her wisdom in managing the household and her steadfastness in maintaining principles, so I picked up my pen and wrote a biography for her.

    256

    外史氏曰：青眉固功之首，而亦罪之魁。非其诱竺速出，何至屡濒于险？幸而归老首丘，差可自盖。然亦竺之嗜饮嗜赌，自诒伊戚，岂真妇有长舌为厉之阶哉？温柔乡不慕而慕醉乡，宜其有兔脱之厄；恩爱海不贪而贪苦海，宜其有鼠窜之危。故罪不可不专责之青眉，究亦不能末减于竺皮。

    Explanation

    256

    The historian said: Qingmei is indeed the foremost in merit, yet also the chief of sins. Had it not been for his luring Zhubi to rush out hastily, how could he have repeatedly faced danger? Fortunately, he returned to his native place in old age, which somewhat covers his past misdeeds. Yet Zhubi's addiction to drinking and gambling brought trouble upon himself. How could women with long tongues truly be the cause of his troubles? Preferring the place of drunkenness over the place of comfort and ease, it is appropriate that he faced a dangerous situation; seeking the sea of suffering instead of the sea of love, it is appropriate that he faced a perilous state. Therefore, the blame cannot be exclusively assigned to Qingmei, and ultimately, it cannot be completely mitigated for Zhubi.

    257

    ○　王秋泉
    　　王秋泉者，吾邑名医也。有某富人病且死，延秋泉，秋泉适治某贵人疾，不果往。富人念不已，中夜绵惙，谓其子曰：“吾宁得一晤王先生，虽死不恨。”子乃复走仆秋泉所，顿首敦促。会所治贵人疾良已，又数日，贵人起，治具觞秋泉，奉金币为寿。秋泉饮大醉，归至舟中，语家人曰：“今可赴富人约矣！”而富人子所遣仆早踊跃解维，代摇橹。至其家，传呼曰：“王先生至矣！”举家惊喜出迎。秋泉方酣睡，家人起诸梦中，主人已盛衣冠，鞠躬入舟肃客。秋泉谢暮夜，请得诘朝栉沐登堂。主人固请曰：“老父忍死待先生，先生幸辱临，何栉沐为？”强之入，诊脉已，与药竟出。主人盛馔揖秋泉，秋泉但摇手谢，还舟解衣卧。鸡鸣酒醒，呼其家人骂曰：“惰奴旷乃公事。

    Explanation

    257

    ○　Wang Qiuchuan
    　　Wang Qiuchuan was a renowned physician in my county. Once, a wealthy man was gravely ill and near death, so he summoned Wang Qiuchuan. At the time, Wang was treating a certain nobleman's illness and could not go. The wealthy man, unable to let go, was in distress at midnight and told his son, "I would rather meet Mr. Wang just once, even if I die, than regret it." The son then went to Wang's residence, prostrated himself, and earnestly urged him. Coincidentally, the illness of the nobleman Wang was treating had greatly improved, and after several more days, the nobleman recovered. He prepared a feast for Wang Qiuchuan, presented gold and coins as a gift, and invited him to celebrate. Wang drank to the point of drunkenness, returned to his boat, and said to his family, "Now I can go to the wealthy man's appointment!" The servant sent by the wealthy man's son was already enthusiastic and untied the boat, taking the oars. Upon arriving at the wealthy man's home, he shouted, "Mr. Wang has arrived!" The entire family was surprised and joyful, coming out to greet him. Wang was just in a deep sleep, and the family woke him from his dream. The host had already put on his finest clothes and bowed, entering the boat to greet the guest. Wang thanked him for the late night and asked to wait until the next morning to wash and dress before entering the hall. The host insisted and said, "My father is holding on to life, waiting for you, Mr. Wang. You have honored us with your presence. Why bother washing and dressing?" He forced Wang to enter, and after diagnosing the pulse, gave medicine and then left. The host prepared a feast and bowed to Wang Qiuchuan, but Wang only waved his hand to thank him and returned to the boat, undressed, and lay down. At the crow of the rooster and the waking from drunkenness, he called his family and scolded, saying, "Lazy servant, neglecting your master's affairs."

    258

    某富人迟我久，当夜赴之，何尚泊此？”家人曰：“公顷已诊脉与药，忘之？”秋泉大惊曰：“审与药乎？吾直大醉，必杀之矣。”顿足促解维归，谓不去必受辱。家人匆遽解维，主人已遣仆伺秋泉，闻去，即入报。须臾门启，望岸上烛笼数十，传语止王先生。秋泉不知所为，俄而主人踉跄至，入舟顿颡，泪下承睫，谢曰：“老父得先生刀圭，乃者熟寝，病若脱矣。先生存，父存；先生去，父且大去；惟先生终哀怜之。”秋泉自疑曰：“世岂有是事哉？必绐我。”然已无可奈何，强随之登堂，门且掩，心犹怦怦然。坐定，主人申谢再三：“先生用药何神验乃尔？”秋泉乃漫应曰：“昨已得其概，请更得审视。”遂入视，索药渣观之，私自慰曰：“幸不误。”更与数剂，起其疾，厚获而归，呼为醉先生云。

    　　外史氏曰：此事见《青鸟志》。醉梦之中，而用药之神效如此，岂其中有鬼神耶？然亦可见医术之不尽足凭，而生死之自有命也。一笑。

    ○　卷四

    Explanation

    258

    A wealthy man had kept me waiting for a long time, and I was supposed to go to him that night. Why are you still here?" asked the household. "He was given a pulse diagnosis and medicine just now, did you forget?" replied the family. Autumn Spring was greatly surprised and said, "Did you really give him medicine? I was just drunk, I must have killed him!" He stomped his feet and urged them to untie the boat and return, saying that if he didn't leave, he would be disgraced. The family hurriedly untied the boat, but the host had already sent a servant to watch over Autumn Spring. Upon hearing that he was leaving, the servant immediately went inside to report. Soon the door opened, and dozens of lanterns were seen on the shore, passing on a message to stop Master Wang. Autumn Spring was at a loss, not knowing what to do. Soon after, the host staggered in, entered the boat, knelt down, tears streaming down his cheeks, and thanked him, saying, "My father received a small amount of medicine from you, and he slept deeply, his illness seems to have disappeared. If you stay, my father will live; if you leave, my father will surely die. Only you, please have mercy on me." Autumn Spring doubted, "How can there be such a thing in the world? They must be tricking me." However, he had no choice but to reluctantly follow him up to the hall, the door almost closed, his heart still thumping. Sitting down, the host thanked him repeatedly, "How miraculous is your medicine!" Autumn Spring casually replied, "I already got a general idea yesterday, please let me take a closer look." He then went inside to examine, inspected the medicine residue, and privately consoled himself, "Fortunately, I didn't make a mistake." He gave him a few more doses, cured his illness, and returned with great profit, earning the nickname "Drunken Master."

    The historian said: This story is recorded in the "Blue Bird Record." In a state of drunkenness and confusion, the miraculous effect of the medicine was like this, is there really a ghost or deity involved? However, it also shows that medical skills are not always reliable, and life and death are determined by fate. A smile.

    259

    胎异
    　　粤东之俗，女生十二三，即结闺阁之盟。凡十人，号曰十姊妹，无论丰啬，不计妍媸，簪珥相通，衣饰相共，俨有嘤鸣之雅焉。及嫁，缓急相扶持，是非相袒护。凡翁姑之不慈，夫婿之不睦，叔伯妯娌之不相能，父母兄弟所不敢问者，惟姊妹得而问之。故闺门之内，蒂固根深，莫能摇夺。而狮吼之威，即在司牧者，亦为之屏息，矧其下者哉！某县，绅家有女及笄，字于巨族。忽病吞酸，腹亦震动，父母咸疑之。然而家禁森严，内无五尺童子，唯同盟一女弟，系贫无所依者，女白诸父母，留养于家。昼则共一绣筐，夜则同一绣榻，此外别无一人。疑不及此，遂目为疾，延医诊视之，举不能辨。无何而弥月不迟矣，且居然生子矣。众论汹汹，丑扬中冓。婿家巨族，不堪其辱，遂讼于邑宰，欲罢其婚。女家亦惭赧不能白，将致女于死以涤垢。惟姊妹行不忍，具牒于县，言女之诬。娇鸟群啼，哄堂号泣，宰亦无能判决。事闻中丞，委员同鞫，究不能定。

    Explanation

    259

    Abnormal Birth

    In the customs of Guangdong, when a girl reaches twelve or thirteen years old, she forms an alliance in the inner chambers. There are ten such girls, regardless of wealth or beauty, who share their personal adornments and clothing, creating an atmosphere as harmonious and elegant as a gathering of birds singing in unison. After marriage, they support each other in times of need and defend each other in disputes. They address issues such as the unkindness of in-laws, the lack of harmony with husbands, and the inability to get along with uncles, aunts, and sisters-in-law, which even parents and brothers dare not speak of, but only the sisters can. These bonds are deeply rooted and firmly established, and even the powerful presence of a woman, which can make even the head of the household tremble, cannot shake them.

    In a certain county, a daughter of a gentry family, who was betrothed to a prominent family, suddenly fell ill with acid reflux and abdominal pain. Her parents suspected foul play. However, the family was strict, and there were no young boys inside. Only a poor and dependent sister-in-law remained, whom the daughter requested to be kept at home. During the day, they shared a sewing basket, and at night, they slept on the same bed, with no one else present. The suspicion did not extend to this sister-in-law, so the illness was treated as a medical condition, and doctors were called to diagnose her. However, none could determine the cause. Soon, the pregnancy reached full term, and she unexpectedly gave birth to a child. The public reacted with loud and angry discussions, exposing the scandalous private matter. The husband's family, being a prominent family, could not bear the shame and sued the county magistrate, demanding the annulment of the marriage. The woman's family also felt ashamed and unable to clear her name, and they planned to kill her to cleanse the stain. However, her sisters could not bear to see this and submitted a written appeal to the county magistrate, stating that the woman was falsely accused. The sisters wept collectively in the hall, and the magistrate was unable to make a decision. The matter was reported to the provincial governor, who investigated jointly with a commission, but ultimately could not reach a conclusion.

    260

    时少司寇某公，出司臬事于粤，谙练详核，强记多闻，乃谓其属曰：“盍使稳媪相女，若系闺体，则斯狱无难立断矣。”其属窃笑，以为生子者，不副不坼，容或有之，未闻既生既育，而犹珠联璧合者也。因奉宪令，勉使验之，果皆以处子报。犹恐其妄，遂各遣其衙眷同往查勘，又俱以女体为言，始信之，而惑愈滋。因复命于公，公闻之，默然良久，遽诘曰：“胎岂有异乎？”对曰：“向曾视之，虽无生气，具体亦人。

    Explanation

    260

    A Judicial Official in Guangdong

    At the time, a Judicial Official named Mr. So-and-so was serving in Guangdong. He was experienced and meticulous, with a strong memory and broad knowledge. One day, he said to his subordinates, "Why not have the midwife examine the woman? If she is a virgin, this case will be easily and immediately resolved." His subordinates secretly laughed, thinking that a woman who has given birth, not double-faced or split, might still be a virgin, but they had never heard of a woman who has given birth and raised children, yet still has a body like pearls and jade. Following the command of the superior, they encouraged and had them verify it. Indeed, they all reported that she was a virgin. Still fearing that it might be false, they then each sent their family members to go and investigate together. Again, they all said it was a woman's body. They began to believe it, but the confusion grew more. They reported back to the official, who remained silent for a long time, then immediately asked, "Is the fetus different?" The subordinates answered, "I have seen it before, although it has no life, it is still a human form."

    261

    但四肢百体空空然，如蝉之蜕，如革之囊，一似全无骨肉者，惟此为疑耳。”公乃太息曰：“仕优弗学，几杀人子。诸君固有所不知，此二女同居，重阴交感之象也。”众请其说，公笑而不言，命吏诣库取某年部案，与众观之。中一事，若合符节，众乃顿悟。盖女年已长，情事渐知，私与女伴效其状。虽两雌无异，而真气流通，固亦有孕。第无云雨之私，究非盨缊之正，遂令硕果虽结，宛同钻核之李，职是故耳。僚属叹服，乃定案，婿家亦无异言。后数月，迎女于归，倡随无间。至今生子数人，则骨擎肤立，迥非向之仅具皮相者矣。

    Explanation

    261

    But the limbs and body felt hollow, like a cicada shedding its old skin, like a leather bag, as if completely without bones and flesh; this was the only doubt. The official then sighed and said, "Neglecting learning, almost like killing one's own child. You all do not know this, it is the image of two females interacting in a way that symbolizes a deeper, perhaps unnatural, connection." The others asked for an explanation, and the official smiled but did not speak, ordering the clerk to fetch a case file from the treasury and show it to them. One of the cases matched the situation perfectly, and they suddenly understood. It turned out that the girl, having grown up, had become aware of her feelings and secretly imitated the behavior of her female companions. Although both were females, the natural flow of vital energy was disrupted, and she did become pregnant. However, there was no conventional intimacy, and it was not the proper, conventional way. Thus, although she had formed, it was as useless as a plum with a hole through its pit. The officials were amazed and convinced, and the case was settled, with no objections from the family. After several months, the girl was brought home, and they lived in perfect harmony. To this day, she has given birth to several children, and they have bones supporting their skin, far from the previous state of having only skin and no substance.

    262

    外史氏曰：胎之异者，履迹吞卵，而后载籍所传，指不胜屈，固不止有此异也。惟以二阴之爻，竟成坎离之济，人多未之前闻，遂不免震骇耳目耳！然无公之博洽，孰敢臆决？而倡声者尝戏以语人曰：“亦有学养子而后可嫁。”得此可补贤传之未及矣！

    ○　夏姬
    　　金陵某宦，素贪鄙，生女而美，且能诗，尝郢政于郡中某夫人，以其为闺中之尊宿也。一日春暮，女偶得诗曰：“花落花开总是春，惜春何必怨花神。别余一种春光好，柳絮如花亦惹人。”吟成录小笺，遣婢诣夫人郢政。夫人阅之，蹙额曰：“是儿欲狐媚终身耶？”评跋深示不足。婢归述其语，女乃强自敛抑，取“关雎”“葛覃”诸篇，日讽咏之。期年，少洗其靡曼之习。未几，宦以夤缘起复，又出仕于晋。女将随父之官，往辞某夫人，因指壁间红白二梅图求赠言。夫人即吟曰：“南枝不比北枝寒，漫把丹青一样看。倘共红芳颦笑日，更无人倚玉栏干。”盖以高洁相望，而寓箴规之意焉。

    Explanation

    262

    外史氏曰： The historian commented: "There are many extraordinary conceptions, such as those born from divine intervention, and the records of such cases are too numerous to count. However, this is not the only unusual case. It is rare that the second line of the Yin hexagram in the I Ching (Book of Changes) leads to a balance between water and fire, a phenomenon that most people have not heard of before, and thus it startles the ears and eyes! Yet without a broad and profound understanding, who dares to make a rash judgment? Some have even joked and said to others: 'There are those who must first learn to raise a child before they can get married.' This saying can supplement the omissions in the records of the wise."

    ○ 夏姬
    In Jinling, there was a certain official who was inherently greedy and base. He had a daughter who was beautiful and also skilled in poetry. She once sought criticism on her poetry from a certain lady in the county, as she considered her a respected figure in the inner circle. One spring evening, the girl composed a poem: "The falling and blooming of flowers is always spring. Why lament spring when it is so beautiful? Another kind of spring light is delightful, and the willow fluff, like flowers, also captivates people." After finishing the poem, she wrote it down on a small note and sent a servant to the lady for criticism. The lady read it and frowned, saying, "Does this girl wish to charm men all her life?" Her critique was harsh, indicating deep dissatisfaction. The servant returned and recounted the lady's words to the girl. The girl then forced herself to be more restrained and began to recite and reflect on the poems "Guān Jū" and "Gě Tán" from the Shijing (Book of Poetry) daily. After a year, she somewhat reduced her frivolous habits. Soon after, the official was reinstated through connections and went to serve in Jin. The girl was about to accompany her father to his new post and went to bid farewell to the lady. She pointed to a painting of red and white plum blossoms on the wall and asked for a gift of words. The lady immediately recited: "The south branch is not as cold as the north branch; do not judge them the same with your brush. If you share the same days of sorrow and joy with the red blossoms, there will be no one left to lean on the jade railing." This poem expressed her high hopes for the girl's purity and integrity, while also implying moral guidance.

    263

    女闻诗默然，及归，愈知儆省。于路时佩教言，无少纵，虽江山尽多风景，而舟车之内，不一窥帘。信乎汉南游女沐周文之雅化，几同乔木之难息矣。独是女初生时，宦即梦巫大夫来谒，询之，则春秋之屈臣也。从一美妇，鸡皮犹腻，趸发将星，貌且妖冶异常，称之曰夏夫人。屈臣谓宦曰：“吾两人幽冥相聚，几二千年。今上帝有命，以此长春花持赠君家，宜善视之。”言讫，留妇将独去。美人揽其祛，意甚流连，嘤嘤娇泣。宦惊觉，声犹在耳，使人视之，则女诞生于侧室，方落蓐不久。宦闻而恶之，心知夏姬为不祥尤物，欲弃之，而犹豫未忍。比长，遂密为防闲，戚族男子，惟十五以下者方许入内。

    Explanation

    263

    The woman listened to the poem in silence. Upon returning home, she became even more aware of self-reflection and caution. On the road, she treasured the teachings, without any slackening. Despite the many beautiful landscapes along the way, she never once peeked through the curtain inside the carriage. Indeed, the woman from the south of the Han dynasty, bathed in the refined culture of the Zhou dynasty, was almost like a tall tree that is hard to stop growing. Especially when she was born, the official dreamed of a high-ranking official named Wugu coming to visit. When he asked about it, he learned that it was Qu Yuan from the Spring and Autumn period. Accompanied by a beautiful woman, whose skin was still smooth as a chicken's and whose hair resembled a star, she was exceptionally charming and was called Xia Fu Ren. Qu Yuan said to the official, "We have been together in the underworld for nearly two thousand years. Now, the Heavenly Emperor has given me this Changchun flower to present to your family. You should take good care of it." After speaking, he left the woman and was about to depart alone. The beautiful woman grabbed his sleeve, showing great reluctance, and wept softly. The official woke up in a start, the sound still in his ears. He sent someone to check, and they found that the girl had been born in a side room, having just been delivered not long ago. The official disliked it, knowing that Xia Ji was an unlucky and extraordinary woman, and he wanted to abandon her but hesitated and did not dare to. When she grew up, he secretly took precautions, allowing only men from the family who were fifteen years old or younger to enter the inner chambers.

    264

    虽托言家范，实为此女故也。及抵任所，又梦巫大夫贸然而来。延之坐，相与晤语，大夫遽启曰：“闺爱及笄，分应字我，不然恐无以安其心。”宦以幽明阻隔，意不欲许。大夫拂衣而起，微哂曰：“予亦不敢违天，特以旧爱难忘，强以相请耳。岂真向汝家坦腹哉？”径出弗顾。宦寤而愈忧，然见女颇贞静，遂以妖梦无凭，憗置之。惟女微闻其事，恚曰：“何物淫鬼，敢以邪说惑人。即令有之，岂人定竟不可胜天耶？”忿欲祝发为尼，家人力止之。乃洗脂粉，为女冠，日惟静坐一室中，即婢妪亦罕见其面。且为书致某夫人曰：“自蒙清训，益以洗心，针黹为常，吟咏并废。

    Explanation

    264

    Though ostensibly a family precept, it is actually for the sake of this daughter. When he arrived at his post, he dreamed again of a sorcerer named Doctor Wu who suddenly appeared. He invited him to sit and they conversed. The doctor suddenly said, "My love for my daughter when she reaches the age of marriage is destined for her to marry me, otherwise, I fear it will not be enough to satisfy her heart." The official, considering the barrier between the living and the dead, did not want to agree. The doctor angrily stood up and smiled slightly, saying, "I也不敢 defy the will of heaven, but because of my old love, I am forced to ask for you. How could I really want to lie down in your family?" He left without looking back. The official woke up even more worried, but he found the daughter to be very virtuous and calm, so he dismissed the dream as a mere illusion and set it aside. Only the daughter heard about it and angrily said, "What kind of lewd ghost dares to use heretical teachings to deceive people. Even if there were such a thing, how could human will ultimately not overcome fate?" Angerly, she wanted to cut off her hair and become a nun, but her family members tried to stop her. She then washed off her makeup and became a nun, sitting quietly in a room all day, and even the servants rarely saw her face. She also wrote a letter to a certain lady, saying, "Since receiving clear and pure instruction, it has further purified my heart. Sewing and embroidery became her usual activities, and reciting poetry was abandoned."

    265

    虽经过瓜扬淮泗之胜，远峰耸秀，近水澄鲜，亦若视为无物。不意突遭鬼谤，谓儿为夏姬后身。夫姬生于千载之上，儿生于千载而下，安在转轮之必俟今日哉？儿誓以女贞自守，不字终身，以仰副垂教之雅。庶几使鬼物无颜，徒逞铄金之口；闺人生色，长留抱璞之躯。”云云。夫人开缄色喜，曰：“是儿果能回心，前因固不足道也。”逾年，闻女死，且身首异处，乃大骇，亦莫知其故。又逾年，闻宦以事去官，无颜归其桑梓，寄籍于他省。女之事，实益不可知。时康熙之戊子也。及庚寅之岁，夫人之长君莅任于晋，即女父之旧治，迎母奉养，以尽子职。夫人往见，衙署半虚，加以筦键。怪问之，对曰：“此中有鬼，向晦即现形，风雨之夕为尤甚，故无敢居者。

    Explanation

    265

    Despite having experienced victories in Gu, Yang, Huai, and Si, the distant peaks majestic and the nearby waters clear and fresh, the subject treated them as if they were nothing. Unexpectedly, she was suddenly accused by a ghost, claiming that her daughter was the reincarnation of Xia Ji. Xia Ji was born a thousand years ago, while the daughter was born a thousand years later; why must the cycle of reincarnation wait until now? The daughter swore to maintain her chastity and remain unmarried for life, to honor and follow the teachings. She hoped that this would make the ghost ashamed and unable to spread false rumors, and that it would bring honor to women and preserve her pure nature. The mother opened the letter with a happy expression and said, "If the child can truly change, past actions are no longer relevant." A year later, she heard that her daughter had died, with her head and body separated, and was greatly shocked, not knowing the reason. Another year later, she heard that her husband had left his position and could not return home, so he registered in another province. The daughter's situation became even more mysterious. This event occurred during the year of Wuzi in the reign of Emperor Kangxi. By the year of Gengyin, the eldest son of the lady took office in Shanxi, the former territory of the daughter's father. He welcomed his mother to live with him to fulfill his filial duties. When the mother visited, the government office was half-empty, with locks on some areas. She asked curiously about this, and the response was, "There are ghosts in the office, which appear especially at dusk and during storms, making it uninhabitable."

    266

    ”夫人闻之，恍然曰：“得毋某家之媛乎？试启其封，吾为尔以理遣之。”长君虽悉其事，但恐祸及母，极力谏沮。夫人弗听，强辟之，携一幼婢露坐其间，举家无敢从。夜未分，即淅沥作响。有顷，风威陡作，其寒竖人毛发，久而始息。即亦无所睹，惟闻墙角有声，似微吟者，倾听之，则五言绝也。其诗曰：“舞蝶应难觅，花枝不久留。可怜今夜月，空照旧温柔。”咏叹再三，音甚凄婉。夫人稔知为女，乃笑曰：“是儿之情，殆不死矣。”因和其韵曰：“三叠音应记，双鱼今尚留。但能怀窈窕，何事泣温柔？”女聆之，惊曰：“是吾师也。”趋而前。虽隐其貌，实闻其声，欷歔曰：“夫人无恙耶？五年阔别，鬓发斑矣。

    Explanation

    266

    The lady, upon hearing this, suddenly said, "Could it be the maiden from a certain family?" She then said, "Let us open the seal, and I will handle it properly." The younger son, though fully aware of the situation, was afraid that his mother would be harmed, and he strongly advised against it. But the lady did not listen, and she forcibly opened the seal, taking a young maid with her and sitting exposed to the open air. The whole household dared not follow. Before night fell, a dripping sound began to be heard. Soon, the wind suddenly became strong, so cold that it made people's hair stand on end, and it lasted for a long time before subsiding. There was nothing to be seen, only a sound coming from the corner of the wall, as if someone was softly singing. Listening carefully, it turned out to be a five-character quatrain. The poem read, "The dancing butterfly should be hard to find, the flower branch will not stay long. How pitiful is the moon tonight, shining in vain on the old gentleness." The poem was recited again and again, with a very sad and tender tone. The lady, recognizing the young woman, smiled and said, "Her feelings are almost certainly alive." She then composed a poem in response, "The threefold melody should be remembered, the pair of fish still remain. As long as one can cherish the graceful beauty, why cry over the gentle affection?" The young woman, upon hearing this, was surprised and said, "That is my teacher." She hurried forward. Although her face was hidden, her voice was heard, and she wept, saying, "Is my lady safe? Five years of separation, your hair is already gray."

    267

    曾不念儿死之惨乎？”言次近在咫尺，小婢虽幼，见其相逼而来，大怖欲啼。夫人神色自若，微诘其致死之由，答曰：“前寄寸缄，敬陈心迹，想在照鉴之下。不图莲性难胎，藕丝易缚，书甫去，而儿之诸姑来。以其君子亦任陕中，将赴京候升，留眷属于父任所，不及携也。姑有幼子，貌甚姣好，年仅十二龄耳。以中表姊弟，至亲无嫌，时入内室，儿因爱之，与同寝食。居无何，以暴疾卒，父与姑大恸。究其病由，群婢之狡者，谮儿于父，言与之有私。父素疑此，毒加拷掠，遂诬服。幸丑声未扬，仍为儿议婚于某尉。事已可成，忽有剧盗夜入深闺，断儿首而去。殁后始知，其人盖即尉之所使耳。冥冥之中，每思报复，奈其人有剑术，不可以近。

    Explanation

    267

    "Did you not even remember the terrible death of your child?" The speaker was close by, and the young maid, though young, was greatly terrified and wanted to cry. The lady remained calm and composed, gently questioning the cause of the child's death. "Just as the letter was sent, the child's aunts arrived," the maid replied. "The father, a man of high moral standing, also held a position in Shanxi and was about to go to the capital to await promotion, leaving the family in the father's place of service, unable to take them along." The aunt had a young son, very beautiful in appearance, as cousins, they were close relatives with no suspicion, often entering the inner room. The child died suddenly from a violent illness, and the father and aunt wept greatly. The cunning maidservants slandered the child to the father, who had always suspected this, and thus tortured him cruelly, causing him to falsely confess. Fortunately, the bad reputation did not spread, and they still arranged a marriage for the child with a certain military officer, the matter was almost complete, when suddenly, a notorious bandit entered the inner chambers at night, cut off the child's head, and left. Only after death did it become known that the person was likely sent by the military officer. In the depths of the spirit, he often thought of revenge, but since the person had martial arts skills, one could not approach.

    268

    是以吁屈清夜，有骇听闻，幸勿罪。”语竟，悲楚不胜。夫人微哂曰：“若欺予哉！予未闻申生孝已，有鬼神不为代白之冤；红线隐娘，有一时枉杀无辜之事。若性如水，勉就隄防，稍遇横流，便成泛滥。寝食既与同，素丝岂无染？安得以小弱弟为词乎？彼县尉者，家有剑仙，讵容室有荡妇？想畏若父之威，婚不敢辞，意岂无怒？横加白刃于红颜，特以此耳。”夫人言未及终，女似甚惭者，徐曰：“惟然有之，夫人其何以教我？”夫人知其可遣，乃正色曰：“阅汝前书，合以近事，若真再世之夏姬矣。株林之丑，流秽篇章，今既未至于滥觞，不污清化，是亦汝之大幸。为汝计者，正宜远遁荒野，与草木为伍。

    Explanation

    268

    Thus, in the quiet night, I express my sorrow, which is shocking to hear; please do not blame me." After she finished speaking, she was overwhelmed with grief. The lady smiled slightly and said, "Are you deceiving me? I have not heard of the case of Shēng Shēng, who had a wrong that even ghosts and gods could not clear; nor have I heard of the incident of the legendary female warrior, who wrongfully killed the innocent at one time. Your nature is like water, so you should strive to build embankments; if slightly encountered with a flood, it will become a flood. Sleeping and eating are already the same, so unstained silk will surely be dyed. How can you use the excuse of a young and weak brother? That county magistrate has a sword saint in his family, how can there be a promiscuous woman in the house? I suppose he fears your father's authority, so he dare not refuse the marriage, but how could he not be angry? To brutally kill a beautiful woman, that is the reason. Before the lady finished speaking, the girl seemed very ashamed and slowly said, "Indeed, there is such a thing, what should I do, madam?" Knowing that she could be sent away, the lady spoke sternly, "Reading your previous letters, combined with recent events, if you are truly the reincarnation of Ji Xia, the disgrace of Zhu Lin, spreading filthy writings, now it has not reached the point of excess, not to taint the pure transformation, this is also your great fortune. For your sake, it is appropriate to retreat to the wilderness, to live among plants and trees."

    269

    必有夙世钟情，仍来相访，或能携汝以返，亦未可知。若犹溷迹官舍，倘遇正人，运彼慧剑，将魂消魄灭，永堕沉沦，益不可问矣。予言止此，汝宜自思。”女闻言若有所悟，悲叹许时，肃然请退。行数武，少露其形，则鹃血飞红，霓衣尽赤，俨然一无首之尸。婢遂惊倒，夫人亦目眩者久之。忽夫人之长君，突出于侧。盖虑其母之被祟，潜踪相从，及见从容谕遣，不胜叹服，故未遽出耳，乃扶夫人归寝室。明日遂绝女之迹，衙中始安堵无扰。询之吏胥，鲜能言女之奸状。后遇尉来晋谒，留心物色，从一人，虬髯虎面，瞻视非常，疑刺女者即此也。长君履任未久，尉即挂冠归，似知其事之泄者。夫人又梦女冠帔来谢云：“从巫大夫作三湘之游，复得相聚矣。”

    Explanation

    269

    There must be a predestined affection from a past life that still comes to visit, and perhaps she could be taken back, though it is uncertain. If she continues to hide in the official quarters, and if she encounters a virtuous person, that person might wield their wisdom like a sword, destroying her soul and spirit, leading her to eternal oblivion, which is even more uncertain. My words end here; you should reflect on this." The woman, upon hearing this, seemed to have some realization, sighed for a while, and respectfully requested to withdraw. After walking a few steps, she slightly revealed her form, and the blood of the cuckoo flew red, her rainbow robe turned entirely red, clearly a headless corpse. The servant girl was startled and fell over, and the lady was dazed for a long time. Suddenly, the respected elder emerged from the side. It seemed that he was secretly following to protect his mother from harm, and upon seeing the calm instruction and sending away, he was deeply impressed and did not rush out, so he helped the lady return to her bedroom. The next day, the traces of the woman disappeared, and the office became peaceful and undisturbed. When asked, the officials could rarely speak of the woman's wicked deeds. Later, when the magistrate came to pay respects, he carefully observed and identified one man with a curly beard and a tiger-like face, who looked very unusual, and suspected that he was the one who killed the woman. The elder had not been in office for long, and the magistrate immediately resigned and returned home, seemingly knowing that the matter had been revealed. The lady then dreamt that the woman appeared in a magical robe and thanked her, saying, "I traveled with the doctor of magic to the Three Xiangs and reunited again."

    270

    外史氏曰：淫根于性，犹难化诲，况为三生之夏姬耶？故始虽唯唯受教，铮铮矢节，卒不免跃十二龄之子南，过此以往，又何所底止乎？幸剑仙奋一时之威，杜无穷之祸。举凡贵介如陈灵，浮浪如孔仪，皆得侥免，岂仅先拯一将危之御叔哉？某夫人数语，尤有烈丈夫风，亦足与剑侠并传。随园老人曰：不细述于前，惟约略于后，深得龙门叙事之法。亦以事关闺帷，特为委曲之笔。诗人之忠厚，吾于此又见一斑。

    270

    The historian said: The root of lust in nature is still difficult to transform and educate, especially when it comes to the legendary Lady Xia Ji, who is said to have lived through three lives. Therefore, at first she humbly accepted instruction and firmly vowed to maintain her virtue, but in the end, she could not avoid jumping into the arms of a twelve-year-old boy named Nan. Beyond this, where would there be any limit? Fortunately, the sword sage exerted his power at that time to prevent endless disasters. All noble figures like Chen Ling and carefree individuals like Kong Yi were fortunate to be spared; it was not just to first save a dangerously ill Uncle Yu. A few words spoken by a certain lady, particularly those with the spirit of a valiant man, are also worthy to be mentioned alongside the sword sage. The elder of the Su Yuan said: Not detailed in the beginning, only briefly mentioned later, deeply grasping the narrative method of Longmen. Also, concerning matters of the inner chambers, a gentle and tactful pen is used. The loyalty and kindness of the poet, I see another glimpse of this here.

    271

    ○　郎十八
    　　客窗夜雨，滴滴愁人，闻有歌郎十八者，即而听之，音甚凄婉。初莫解其故，诘朝讯之旅人，则湖襄之近事也。其人宗姓，酉名，字蕴二，桂阳青衿，善诗赋，能文章，知名于当代，盖亦楚中之秀出者。尝昼寝，梦一丽人，柔媚娇小，语之曰：“郎十八，妾十七，夙世相逢成姻契。”近而就焉，霍然顿悟。时宗已届四旬，漠不介意。自是每梦必见之，云云如前，欲诘之，有口若瘖，旋复惊觉。闻者或以为妖，戒宗正心以祛，而竟不能绝。

    Explanation

    271

    A Night of Rain at the Guest Window

    The rain drips at the guest window, each drop saddening the heart. I heard of a singer known as "Lang Shiba" and listened to his music, which was very sorrowful and gentle. At first, I could not understand the reason for such feelings. The next morning, I asked travelers about it, and they said it was a recent event in the Lake Xiang region. The person in question is named Zong, with the given name You and the courtesy name Yun'er. He is a scholar from Guiyang, skilled in poetry and prose, renowned in his time, and considered an outstanding figure in the Chu region. Once, while sleeping during the day, he dreamt of a beautiful and charming woman. She spoke to him, saying, "Lang Shiba, I am seventeen, and we have been destined to meet in a previous life, forming a marital bond." As he approached her, he suddenly and clearly understood. At the time, Zong was already in his forties and remained indifferent. From then on, he would see her in his dreams, and the same words would be spoken. He wanted to question her but found himself unable to speak, and he would wake up in a sudden shock. Those who heard about this sometimes thought it was a supernatural phenomenon and advised Zong to purify his mind to dispel it, but ultimately, it could not be completely eliminated.

    272

    丙子仲秋，赴试省垣，棘闱矫屋之中，偶向同人话其异，一人愕然曰：“此吾女弟之谶也。女弟年十七而夭，未卒之先，辄梦人语曰：‘良缘真不偶，可惜郎十八。’醒则悒怏，未几而亡。生时颇能诗，因作长歌以禳之，此其起句也。”言之泣下。同人皆惊异，宗自以生人遇鬼，寿将不永，亦郁郁弗快，遂无心诘其全诗。秋闱事竣，宗每忧虞。乃是科竟获隽，中五经第三人，欣幸之余，顿忘前事。明年丁丑，下第归，距家仅百余里，舟行次公安，泊于小渚。

    Explanation

    272

    In the second month of autumn in the year of Bingzi, I went to the provincial examination hall. In the examination hall, I happened to talk to fellow examinees about something strange. One of them was startled and said, "This is a prophecy about my younger sister. She died at the age of seventeen. Before her death, she often dreamed that someone said, 'A good match truly does not come along. It is a pity that the young man is eighteen.' Upon waking, she was dejected, and she died shortly after. When she was alive, she was quite skilled in poetry. Therefore, she wrote a long song to ward off the omen. Speaking of which, he wept. All the fellow examinees were surprised, and I, as a living person, encountered a ghost. My life will not be long, and I was depressed and not feeling well, so I had no heart to ask for the entire poem. After the autumn examination was completed, I often worried. Finally, I succeeded and came third in the Five Classics category. In the midst of joy and relief, I forgot about the previous matter. The following year, in the year of Dingchou, I failed and returned home. I was only about a hundred miles from home, traveling by boat, and stopped at Gong'an, anchoring at a small sandbank."

    273

    月色甚明，宗乘酒酣，兴豪登岸。小步甫数武，俄见一双鬟自芦荻中出，遮道致词曰：“夫人闻主君南返，洁治一樽，粪除三径，遣婢子奉迓文轩，幸即枉临为望。”宗骇曰：“夫人伊谁？”笑对曰：“主君之内子，何不识耶？”宗大惊，窃疑其室有变。双鬟又强之，乃从之行。曲折至一处，朱门碧瓦，仿佛贵家，双鬟引导以入。门庭虽故有司阍，亦莫敢究诘，且恭谨如待主人，宗亦不问。至厅事，铺设綦华，别无宾客。双鬟顾曰：“夫人在内室，想凝盼久矣。

    Explanation

    273

    The moonlight was very bright. Zongcheng, drunk and inspired, stepped ashore. After taking just a few steps, he soon saw a pair of girls with hair buns emerging from the reeds, blocking the path and speaking, saying, "Madam heard that Master returned from the south, cleaned up a jug of wine, tidied the three paths, and sent the maidservants to welcome the literatus. We hope you will grace us with your presence." Zongcheng was startled and asked, "Who is this madam?" They smiled and replied, "She is the wife of Master. How could you not know?" Zongcheng was greatly shocked and secretly suspected that something had happened in his home. The girls urged him again, so he followed them. After winding through a place, they arrived at a spot with red gates and green tiles, which seemed like a noble family's residence. The girls led him inside. Although there were gatekeepers at the entrance, they dared not question him and were respectful as if welcoming the master. Zongcheng did not inquire further. Upon reaching the hall, it was adorned with exquisite decorations, and there were no other guests present. The girls turned and said, "Madam is in the inner room, and she has been waiting anxiously for a long time."

    274

    ”复历门二重，始达闺阃，则绣户香深，翠楼春锁，又别一佳境。双鬟搴帘延请，更宣言曰：“主君来。”宗入其室，有丽者承迎。谛视之，花貌娇羞，冠帔严整，固即梦中人也。敛皀肃客，泪含美目，惨然曰：“红颜黄土，不获早践前盟，妇职有亏，惟君子怜而恕之。”宗心知为鬼，而贪其姝丽，不甚惊。徐徐言曰：“素昧平生，从无婚媾之言，辱承见召，既已怀疑，更聆清词，益用滋惑，惟夫人明教之。”言已长揖。女延之上坐，答曰：“君之前生与妾同居此宅，实夫妇也。

    Explanation

    274

    After passing through two sets of gates, one finally reaches the inner chambers, where the embroidered doors exude a deep fragrance, and the green-tiled building seems to be sealed by spring, presenting another beautiful scene. Two young women draw the curtains and invite him in, and one of them announces, "The master is here." The man enters the room, and a beautiful woman greets him. Upon closer inspection, she has a delicate and shy appearance, her ceremonial cap and robe are neatly arranged, and she is indeed the woman from his dream. She composes herself and respectfully receives the guest, her beautiful eyes filled with tears, and she speaks sadly, "My youthful beauty now lies in the earth, and I was unable to fulfill our previous vows early. I have failed in my duties as a wife, and I ask only that you, the gentleman, show compassion and forgive me." The man realizes that she is a ghost, but he is drawn to her beauty and is not greatly alarmed. He speaks slowly, "I have never met you before, and there has been no discussion of marriage. I am honored to be invited, but I am already suspicious, and hearing your clear words only deepens my confusion. Please, madam, enlighten me." He bows deeply. The woman invites him to sit, and she responds, "In your previous life, you and I lived in this house together, and we were indeed husband and wife."

    275

    设有盟言，愿再生仍偕伉俪。今世郎十八，妾十七，好事可成。无如妾以夙孽，数合早殀，不获共君琴瑟，私心憾恨，靡有穷期。殁之后，诉于地下主者，许妾待字冥途，仍居故第，今又廿有余年矣。”语至此，宗又窃疑已死，骇然曰：“予将于窀穸纳妇乎？”女笑曰：“非然也。昨日冥官牒下，许妾转轮，与君践此旧约。适值南旋，因以奉屈，特达此情，与君子庶几早偕鸳侣，岂望君光我泉壤哉？”宗闻言，惊疑少定，乃笑曰：“卿误矣。

    Explanation

    275

    If there were a pact or vow, I would wish to be reborn and still be together as husband and wife. In this life, the young man is eighteen, and I am seventeen; good things can be accomplished. However, due to my past karma, I was destined to die young, unable to share music with you. My personal regret and sorrow are without end. After my death, I appealed to the ruler of the underworld, who allowed me to await marriage in the afterlife, still residing in the old house. It has now been more than twenty years. Hearing this, the man secretly suspected that she had died, and in shock, he asked, "Am I to marry a woman in the grave?" The woman laughed and said, "Not at all. Yesterday, the officials of the underworld issued a decree, allowing me to be reborn and fulfill this old pact with you. I am returning south, so I have invited you to join me, especially to convey this sentiment, and perhaps to be able to reunite early as a couple. Do I expect you to honor my grave?" Upon hearing this, the man's surprise and doubt subsided, and he laughed, saying, "You are mistaken."

    276

    予之马齿，将及强仕，再俟卿十有七年，花甲将届，复何颜再结花烛乎？况室人共苦同辛十数余载，脱有不讳，当守义以酬其功，忍更思人间少艾耶？”女又笑曰：“是有数在。彼月老之姻缘簿，岂人世之如意珠？且婢子既夺我原配之名，享我青春之乐，报亦惨矣！乌有久假不归者？”宗诘其详，女曰：“君今之室，即我前世之婢也。从妾媵君，以聪慧多得君怜。妾虑分房帷之爱，借小事挞之数十，渠遂气结而死。冥司因录妾过，转世当殀，所谓孽者即此也。

    Explanation

    276

    As for my age, it is approaching the peak of strength, and I have awaited you for seventeen years. With the age of sixty just around the corner, how can I face the ceremony of wedding again? My wife and I have shared hardships and toil for many years. If she passes away, I should uphold righteousness to repay her virtues and cannot bear to think of young and beautiful women." The woman laughed and said, "There is a destiny at play. The matchmaking book of the God of Love is not the perfect match for the world. And since the servant has taken my first wife's name and enjoyed the joys of youth, the retribution is also cruel! How can there be a long-term borrower who does not return?" The master asked for more details, and the woman said, "Your current wife was my servant in a past life. She followed me and gained your affection through her intelligence. I feared that she would divide our love, so I punished her with dozens of lashes for a minor offense, and she died of shock. The underworld recorded my fault and decreed that she would die young in this life. That is what is meant by 'karma.'"

    277

    渠根基浅薄，得此已足，谅不能偕白首而兼富贵耳。”宗犹豫未信，女又曰：“渠有心疾，岁时一举发，即前生遭殴之故。惟此可证，足征妾言非谬。”语未终，忽一鬟进曰：“机事宜密，夫人勿深恃主君而易言也。”女遂默然，遽命酒合欢。双鬟十数人，或歌或舞，纷沓盈前，宗亦不觉心醉。女因谓之曰：“此即君日前之乐也，好自奋勉，今生将复享之矣。”于是饮酒畅甚。方思缱绻，以慰其情，俄有苍头两辈，驰白曰：“毓婴使者入至矣。”女乃起与宗为别，欷歔不胜。

    Explanation

    277

    He had a shallow foundation, and having this is enough; surely they cannot live together until old age and share wealth and honor. The man was hesitant and did not believe it. The woman said, "He has a mental illness, which flares up every year, due to being beaten in a past life. Only this can prove it, sufficiently demonstrating that my words are not mistaken." Before she finished speaking, a young woman suddenly stepped forward and said, "Important matters should be kept secret; do not overly rely on your husband and speak casually about them." The woman then became silent and quickly ordered wine to celebrate together. Several dozen young women, some singing and others dancing, crowded in front of him, and the man was also unconsciously captivated. The woman then said to him, "This is the same joy you experienced recently. Do well and make an effort; you will experience this again in this life." They drank heartily. Just as he was thinking of being intimate to comfort his feelings, two groups of elderly men hurried to report, "The messenger who is giving birth has arrived." The woman then stood up and said goodbye to the man, sobbing and overwhelmed.

    278

    既而曰：“后晤犹远，君能伴妾一往，庶识其处，为异日觅缘之地。”宗亦欲觇其异，欣然从之。出门即有油壁小车，驾以良马，使者凡两人，面目狞恶，而侍女颇恭。女指宗语之曰：“若人即我之夫子，将随往视我门闾，幸勿阻。”使者唯唯。宗遂与女共乘，车驰如风。途次似有城郭，皆不及注目，女在车中谓宗曰：“凡人转生，必昧前因，至死乃复悟，妾此行当亦如之。他日相逢，恐不免以少长为憾，郎十八旧作，君尚能记忆否？”宗答曰：“虽得其概，未详其词。

    Explanation

    278

    After a while, she said, "The next meeting is still far off. If you can accompany me there, perhaps I can recognize the place, as a spot to seek a connection in the future." The narrator also wanted to see the unusual, and gladly followed her. Upon leaving, there was an oil-painted small carriage, driven by a fine horse, with two messengers, who had fierce and ugly faces, but the maids were very respectful. The woman pointed to the narrator and said to them, "That person is my husband. He will accompany me to see my home. Please do not hinder us." The messengers nodded in agreement. The narrator then got into the carriage with the woman, and the carriage rushed like the wind. Along the way, it seemed there were cities and walls, but none were worth pausing to look at. In the carriage, the woman told the narrator, "When people are reborn, they must forget their past causes, and only upon death do they realize again. My journey will also be like that. The next time we meet, I fear it will not be without regret over age differences. The old work of eighteen, do you still remember it?" The narrator replied, "Though I have grasped the general idea, I do not fully understand the words."

    279

    ”女乃自诵其诗，凡数十言，语极哀艳，强宗识之，遂弗忘。有顷，至一郡邑，绝类黄冈。近市有巨家，皘閎高大，车止门外，使者促女降舆。女执宗手涕泣曰：“勿相忘，此亦吾乡贵人门第，甚相当也。”言讫下车，宗亦将躬亲送之，甫离轮辕，金声大作，恍如五夜晨钟。张目顾瞻，则身卧于篷底，舟子方始扬帆也。亟呼从者询之，客夕并无登眺事，且泊舟之地，断岸千尺，宗始自悟其梦，不禁讶然。及归，室家幸无恙，因阴劝其积善，以冀大年。

    Explanation

    279

    The girl then recited her own poem, which was several dozen words long and very sorrowful and beautiful. Qiang Zong remembered it and never forgot it. Soon, they arrived at a city or town that was very similar to Huanggang. Near the market, there was a large house with a grand entrance, and the carriage stopped outside the door. The messenger urged the girl to get out of the carriage. She held Zong's hand and wept, saying, "Do not forget me. This is also a noble family in my hometown. It is very suitable." After finishing speaking, she got out of the carriage. Zong also wanted to personally see her off, but as soon as she left the carriage, a loud metallic sound was made, like the bell at dawn in the fifth watch of the night. Opening her eyes and looking around, she found herself lying at the bottom of the awning, and the boatman had just started to hoist the sail. Zong quickly called the attendant to ask about it. There was no evening excursion planned for the guests, and the place where the boat was moored was a cliff hundreds of feet high. Zong suddenly realized it was a dream and could not help but be astonished. When he returned home, his family was fortunately unharmed, and he secretly advised him to accumulate good deeds in hopes of a long life.

    280

    诘其故，直告之，乃笑而不信。自是宗试辄不利，凡五战春闱，皆败北以返，究以大桃铨补黄冈教授。时已五十有二矣，内子亦届五旬，倡随俱健，可望偕老，窃谓前梦尽妄。乃履任二年，忽丧其偶，宗心为之甚戚，誓不再续。且有子二女一，皆成立，足娱晚景，益无他志。明岁，湘南大疫，死者相继，宗之子女又俱归于冥漠之乡，孑然一身，形影相吊。初亦惨怛无所思，继念无后之大，自揣康强，乃图以续。而头童齿豁，人皆羞与为婚，亦徒怀此意而已。

    Explanation

    280

    He questioned the reason, and she told him truthfully, but he laughed and did not believe her. From then on, whenever he took the exams, he was unsuccessful. He failed five times in the spring examinations, returning defeated each time, and ultimately, he was appointed as a professor in Huanggang. He was already fifty-two years old, and his wife was also fifty. Both were healthy, and they could hope to grow old together. He secretly thought that the previous dream was all nonsense. After serving for two years, he suddenly lost his wife. He was deeply grieved and vowed never to remarry. Moreover, he had two sons and one daughter, all of whom were grown up and could bring joy to his old age. He had no other aspirations. The following year, a great epidemic broke out in southern Hunan, and people died one after another. His children also passed away and joined the realm of the dead. He was left alone, with only his shadow for company. At first, he was so grieved that he had no thoughts, but later, considering the importance of having descendants, and thinking himself still healthy, he decided to remarry. However, his hair was thinning, and his teeth were worn down, and people were ashamed to marry him, so he could only harbor this intention.

    281

    一日出郭迎上宪，乘马过一巨宅，颇似诞女之地。因诈称口渴，命役少贷壶浆，而己立马以窥之。俄有长者自门内出，见宗讶曰：“文光射室，果有异人，请即少辉蓬荜。”宗视之，则邑中梁氏，曾任副元戎。其子数人，亦多有显者，惟公致仕居家，此其别墅也。宗亟弃乘为礼，翁延宗入，笑谓宗曰：“君辈老书生，只合拥皋比训诲生徒，乃亦伛偻奔走，得毋惫甚？”宗惭其言，亦笑而谢曰：“为此一官，未能免俗，必如翁，乃可东山高卧耳。

    Explanation

    281

    One day, I went out of the city to meet a high-ranking official. Riding a horse, I passed by a large mansion that seemed to be a place where a daughter was born. I then pretended to be thirsty and ordered a servant to bring some water, while I stood still on my horse to look at it. Soon, an elderly man came out from inside the gate. Seeing me, he was surprised and said, "Your literary brilliance shines through the room; indeed, you are a remarkable person. Please come in and brighten our humble abode." Upon seeing him, I realized it was Liang, a prominent figure in the county who had once served as a deputy military commander. His sons, several of whom are also prominent figures, live there. Only he has retired and lives at home; this is his villa. I quickly dismounted from my horse to show respect. The elder invited me in and smiled, saying, "You lot of old scholars, you should only be holding a gao bi to teach your students. Yet you bow and run around, are you not tired?" I felt ashamed by his words and also laughed and thanked him, saying, "For this position, I cannot escape convention. Only like you, can one truly retire to the east mountain and live in seclusion."

    282

    ”翁因诘其家事，宗历言之，翁亦为之扼腕。旋闻上宪将至，遂辞出。翌日，有媒妁诣宗议姻，则即梁翁所使也，宗怪之，即请其故。盖翁有弱女年仅二八，甚钟爱，不轻以字人。方宗有细君之丧，翁即梦女于归，其婿即宗也。初犹笑其不类，及与宗遇，又梦如之。且梦诸子俱缧絏，惟宗盛服，独据一堂，少缓颊而桎梏尽脱。觉而异之，私念宗必通显，能拯岳家之溺，故有此使。宗诘之，媒乃具白其事，宗笑曰：“老书生讵有此一日？虽公有命，窃恐诸郎相鄙薄也。”媒又固请，议乃谐。

    Explanation

    282

    The elder then asked about the family matters, and Zong spoke in detail. The elder clenched his fist in frustration. Soon, he heard that the superior officials were coming and excused himself and left. The next day, matchmakers came to Zong to discuss marriage, which was sent by the elder. Zong found it strange and immediately asked for the reason. It turned out that the elder had a young daughter, only sixteen years old, whom he very much loved and did not easily give in marriage. When Zong had recently lost his wife, the elder dreamed that his daughter would marry, and her husband would be Zong. Initially, he laughed at the idea because it seemed unlikely, but when he met Zong, he had another dream like that. He also dreamed that all his sons were in chains, while Zong was dressed in fine clothes, standing alone in a hall, and with a little persuasion, the shackles were all removed. Upon waking, he found it strange and privately thought that Zong must have achieved prominence and could save his family from trouble, which is why he sent the matchmakers. Zong questioned them, and the matchmakers explained the matter in detail. Zong laughed and said, "An old scholar like me would never have such a day." Even though there was a public order, he feared that his sons might look down on him. The matchmakers persisted in their request, and eventually, the agreement was reached.

    283

    择吉纳聘，邑人皆笑以为翁之耄昏，而女之薄命也。乃许字未几，宗竟以上考擢县令，人始惊异。明春亲迎，宾从之赫奕，仪仗之炫耀，迥非青毡故态，益啧啧称羡不已。青庐之中，宗视女貌，宛如一同车入梦之人，始信定数。惟女以妙龄贵族，适此耆艾，颇以为羞。虽不敢怼其父母，而空闺静处，时有粉痕泪迹焉。宗知其意，乃以郎十八一诗私授侍儿，令其按节以歌之。其词曰：“郎十八，妾十七，夙世相逢成姻契。奈何金闺月易沉，朱陈未缔先相失。

    Explanation

    283

    Choosing a favorable date for the betrothal, the townspeople all laughed, thinking the old man was senile and the girl was destined for an unfortunate fate. However, after only a short time since the betrothal was agreed upon, the groom was unexpectedly promoted to the position of county magistrate based on a high evaluation, and people began to be astonished. In the following spring, he personally came to fetch the bride, with the guests and attendants appearing grand and impressive, and the ceremonial regalia displaying a splendor far beyond their humble past, leading to even more admiration and praise. In the bridal chamber, the groom looked at the bride's appearance and felt as if she were the same person he had met in a dream, finally believing in the predestined nature of their union. However, the bride, being a young noblewoman, was quite ashamed to be married to such an elderly man. Although she dared not blame her parents, she often shed tears in her empty room, leaving traces of her sorrow. The groom understood her feelings and privately gave a poem titled "郎十八" to a servant girl, instructing her to sing it in a measured and graceful manner. The poem reads: "At eighteen, I was your lover. At seventeen, I became your wife. We met in a past life and formed a marital bond. How can the moon in the golden palace easily sink, when Zhu and Chen were destined to marry but were separated by fate."

    284

    雨潇潇，云密密，巫峡阳台都未悉。纵令楚客梦中来，未必巫娥花里出。并蒂莲，合欢桔，世间草木犹亲暱。天公应是独怜花，人当美满遭妖嫉。白面郎，态飘逸，玉人何处新婚毕。红颜空向卷中求，须臾鹤发如太乙。绣帷人，倍啾唧，嫫母无盐反超轶。银瓶落井玉沉埋，不许摽梅歌迨吉。叩元穹，凭彩笔，愿将百岁易一日。但得于飞十二时，花残月缺良不恤。且调琴，并鼓瑟，孤鸿浮寄双鸳室。艳李秾桃亦自春，白头吟咏曾何必。蝶寻香，蜂成蜜，前途由来黑似漆。

    Explanation

    284

    Rain falls softly, clouds gather thickly, and the beauty of Wuxia and Yangtai remains unseen. Even if a guest from Chu comes in a dream, the goddess of the mountains may not emerge from among the flowers. Twin lotus, paired tangerines, even the plants and trees of the world show a natural affinity. Heaven alone seems to favor the flowers, while humans who are happy and fulfilled often face envy. A handsome young man, graceful and elegant, where has the beautiful person just completed their wedding? Beauty is often sought in books, but youth fades quickly, like the transformation of a young woman into an old one. People in luxurious settings are particularly restless, while plain women like Ugly Woman and Lady Wu somehow surpass others. A precious thing is lost, like a silver bottle falling into a well, and the song of the young woman waiting for her husband is delayed. Praying to heaven and using colorful writing tools, one wishes to exchange a long life for a single day of happiness. If one can have twelve hours of happiness, even if it means the rest of the time is marked by decay and loss, it is worth it. Let us play the lute and the zither together, like a lonely goose floating in a room meant for a pair of mandarin ducks. Even beautiful things like peaches and plums can bring spring, and old age can be filled with poetry and reflection. Seeking pleasure and achieving fulfillment, the future is often dark and uncertain.

    285

    鹪鹩惟望占枝头，甘心兰梦输燕姞。歌涧槃，乐衡泌，何必黄金千万镒。翠钿珠串逊卿卿，我先荆布奉巾栉。登皇朝，郎辅弼，朱轮画阁人安佚。非关薄命觊花封，侬取名兮汝取实。千百言，心专一，回天只恨无神术。雏莺乳燕果同栖，信是红裙运不窒。楼十二，桥廿四，吹箫望月反书帙。欢娱恰遇少年时，此乐何人能究诘。弹箜篌，吹觱栗，悲欢自古原不一。此中别有断肠声，娇歌未已珍珠溢。”适值家宴，婢遂奏于筵前。女以夙慧颇通文，闻未及阕，早涕泣不能仰。

    Explanation

    285

    A Small Bird's Aspirations and the Joys of Life

    The small bird (鹪鹩) merely hopes to find a place to rest on a branch, content with a modest fate, much like the virtuous Yan Ji. Singing by the stream and enjoying the coolness of the well are enough; why seek the wealth of thousands of gold pieces? Even the finest jewelry and ornaments are inferior to the affection and care of a loved one; I am willing to serve with the simplest of tasks. Achieving high office in the imperial court, with my husband as an important advisor, brings a life of luxury and ease. My happiness is not due to any misfortune or desire for material wealth; I value the name, while you value the substance. A thousand words, but the heart remains true; even with great power, one cannot change certain fates. The joy of young birds living together in harmony confirms that my life is free from obstacles and full of good fortune. In a place with twelve towers and twenty-four bridges, I play the flute while gazing at the moon, then return to my books. The joy of youth coincides with the best years of life; who could possibly question or diminish such happiness? Playing the箜篌 and blowing the觱栗, joy and sorrow have always been part of human experience. In this, there are sounds that pierce the heart; the singer's voice still rings out even as tears flow. During a family banquet, the maid performed this piece before the guests. The young woman, endowed with innate wisdom and a good understanding of literature, began to weep before the song had finished, unable to bear the emotions it evoked.

    286

    曲终呼而询之，婢不能对，宗乃从旁缅述綦详。女恍然大悟曰：“噫！妾知之矣！”因破涕为笑，欢好异常。自此悲欢不形，较之贾大夫射雉之后，尤为好合焉。阅数月，梁翁病卒，诸子俱奔丧归，颇不以宗为意，闻问弗通。迨宗以廉能累官至方面，妻族始敬礼之。独翁所梦排难一事，迄今渺然。意者德可胜妖欤？或时有未至欤？且安知非冥冥之中，鬼神为之撮合，而特假此以怵翁也？女今只二旬余，生子已能读父书，而宗之矍铄，无异昔年，则信乎缘之夙定者矣！余既得其大概，爰就芭蕉新雨而附记于斯编。

    　　外史氏曰：老夫耄矣，之子犹少，非以夙缘平其憾，鲜有不抑郁终身者。然女本为青年佳偶，赍恨重泉，究乃因白发藁砧，再游人世。死死生生，要皆妒之一字自贻伊戚也。故古今疗妒之方，应推此为第一。

    Explanation

    286

    After the music ended, he called out and asked. The maid could not answer, so the master described the situation in great detail from the side. The woman suddenly realized and said, "Ah! Now I understand!" She stopped crying and started laughing, and their relationship became exceptionally harmonious. From then on, her sadness and joy were no longer visible, and compared to the time after the Master Jia shot the pheasant, their relationship was even more harmonious. After several months, the old man from Liang died, and his sons all returned home for the funeral, showing little interest in the master and not responding to his inquiries. It was only when the master was promoted to a high position due to his integrity and ability that his wife's family began to respect him. The matter of the old man's dream of resolving difficulties remained unresolved. Could virtue overcome evil spirits? Or was it not yet the right time? How could one know that in the unseen world, the spirits did not bring them together, just using this to frighten the old man? The woman, now only twenty or so, had a son who could already read his father's books, and the master remained vigorous and lively, no different from his youth, proving that their fate was predestined from the beginning! Having grasped the essence of the story, I recorded it here under the new rain of the banana leaves.

    The historian said: The old man is now elderly, but the young man is still young. Without the predestined fate to ease his regrets, few would not live a life of depression. Yet, the woman was originally a young and beautiful wife, carrying her hatred to the underworld, but ultimately returned to the world due to the white-haired husband. Whether in life or death, all stem from the word "jealousy" bringing self-inflicted sorrow. Therefore, the best method to cure jealousy in ancient and modern times should be this very story.

    287

    随园老人曰：余每谓老夫少妻，似亦造物者之过。今乃知天之报施妒妇，必如是始快人心。第虑江泛之诗弗作，而枯杨之稊几遍天下耳！郎十八一篇，吾安得家喻而户晓之？

    ○　三生梦
    　　泾水之阳，有异人，传者失其姓字。家无长物，悬一囊，空空如也。然过旗亭酒家，携囊以入，饮必尽醉，醉即探囊与值，无少乏，人因以为异。一日，饮于某肆。既已醺然，有丐者就而行乞，敝污褴缕，状貌可憎，年且三旬而上。某忽惊曰：“玉人何憔悴至此？岂其千金一笑，犹不足供汝温饱耶？”丐者愕然，一时当垆涤器之人，靡不粲然。某亦不顾，索酌与丐共饮，且命之歌。丐辞以不能，某笑曰：“汝之腼腆犹昔，又何怪予之心醉也。”相对浮白，意甚狎昵，直至日晡而后别。又倾囊中一缗，与之曰：“聊以备花粉之需，毋固却。”丐者大悦，顿首称谢，某若有恻然不忍之容。

    Explanation

    287

    The Old Man of Sui Garden said: I often thought that an old man with a young wife seemed to be a fault of the Creator. Now I understand that Heaven's retribution against a jealous wife must be like this to satisfy the hearts of people. However, I fear that the poem of the river's overflow will not be written, while the sprouts of withered willows will nearly be everywhere! How can I make the Eighteen Chapters of Lang known house by house and door by door?

    Three Lifetimes Dream

    On the north bank of the Jing River, there was a strange person whose name and surname were unknown. His home had no valuable possessions, and he hung an empty bag. Yet, when passing by a tavern, he would carry the bag inside and drink until he was thoroughly drunk. When drunk, he would take money from the bag to pay, never lacking, and people thus found him extraordinary. One day, he drank at a certain tavern. Already slightly intoxicated, a beggar approached him to beg. The beggar was ragged and dirty, with an ugly appearance, and was over thirty years old. Suddenly, the man exclaimed, "Why is the jade person so withered and emaciated? Could it be that a smile worth a thousand gold is still insufficient to provide you with warmth and food?" The beggar was startled, and all those who were tending the stove and washing utensils smiled brightly. The man paid no heed and ordered wine, drinking with the beggar and asking him to sing. The beggar declined, saying he could not. The man laughed and said, "Your shyness is still the same as before; why should I be surprised that I am heart drunk?" They drank toasts together, their intentions very intimate, until the afternoon, and then parted ways. He also emptied a bag of one string of coins, giving it to the beggar and saying, "Just to prepare for your need for cosmetics, do not refuse it." The beggar was greatly pleased, knelt and thanked him, and the man had a look of pity and unwillingness.

    288

    丐既去，某遂寄宿肆中，亦其醉时之恒事耳。翌日晨起，有好事者争诘其由，某笑曰：“是前世之佳丽人也。诸君欲询之，当偕予往访之。”众皆踊跃相从。出肆半里许，即丐寓，则一废祠，破屋颓垣，见丐独卧于庑下。就视之，藉草枕石，病且危，众乃大骇。未及发言，丐早张目而视，见某即曰：“仙师来乎？三生一梦，微师之神力，予尚迷不知悟也。”众益错愕，交喙致诘。某揖众列坐，使丐自白，因悉得其梗概。盖丐自遇某返，心亦疑讶，然已酒力不胜，遂鼾睡如死。梦入一宅第，金玉罗列，锦绣堆积，私心颇爱好之。继见守者无人，贪念顿起，乃择其精者，随意攫取。已而悟曰：“噫！予殆盗也，人知之，必将不免。盍归乎来。

    Explanation

    288

    The beggar had already left, so I stayed overnight in a shop, which was also a usual thing for him when he was drunk. The next morning, upon waking, some curious people eagerly asked the reason. I smiled and said, "She is a beautiful woman from a previous life. If you all want to ask her, you should go with me to visit her." Everyone was eager to follow. We walked out of the shop for about half a mile, and it was the beggar's residence, an abandoned temple with ruined houses and collapsed walls. I saw the beggar lying alone under the eaves. Upon closer inspection, he was lying on grass and using a stone as a pillow, sick and in danger, and everyone was greatly shocked. Before anyone could speak, the beggar opened his eyes and looked, seeing me, he said immediately, "Is the master here? Three lives as one dream, without the master's divine power, I was still confused and unaware." Everyone became even more surprised, asking with their mouths open. I bowed and had everyone sit in a row, and had the beggar explain himself, thus, I fully understood the general outline. It is because the beggar met me again, he was also suspicious and surprised, but he was already too drunk to bear it, so he fell asleep like a dead person. He dreamed of entering a mansion, where gold and jade were arranged, and silk and brocade were piled up, he secretly loved and enjoyed it. Then he saw that there was no one guarding it, his greedy desire suddenly arose, he then chose the best ones, taking them arbitrarily. Afterward, he realized and said, "Ah! I am probably a thief, if people find out, they will surely not let me off. Why not return now?"

    289

    ”及出，则崇垣丈许，高不可逾。仓皇间，足忽去地，竟能不翼而飞，遂大喜。而回覸第内，火光荧煌，适有持炬追逐者，其声且汹汹不绝。因度屋而趋，虽隔越丈寻，视如无物。比归，则有妻有孥，方明烛相俟，非复孑然一身矣。见丐至，皆慰劳之，暖酒炙肉，奉侍殷勤。丐遂沉醉而寝。明日起，携金如市，乘马从仆，邑人皆致敬。自视其衣，高冠盛服，实大盗不同穿窬者，志益自得。由是每夕必出，出必重获而后返，人固未能相难也。年六旬，妻妾数人，子女亦皆成立，资用肥饶，尽足娱老，亦弃盗弗业矣。一夕乘醉又出，至一家，闺闼深邃，惟少妇二人，侍婢三四而已，欺其荏弱，竟突入。

    Explanation

    289

    Upon exiting, he encountered a high wall, about a zhang (approximately 3 meters) tall, which seemed impossible to climb over. In a moment of panic, he suddenly felt as though he had left the ground, and indeed, he was able to fly without wings, much to his delight. Looking back at his courtyard, he saw bright, flickering flames, and there were people holding torches chasing after him, their shouts continuous and loud. He crossed from house to house, even though the distances were measured in zhang and chi (about 3 meters and 1 meter respectively), he moved as if they were nothing. When he returned, he found that he was no longer alone; there was a wife and children waiting for him with lit candles, a far cry from his previous solitary state. Seeing the beggar arrive, they all comforted him and treated him well, warming wine and roasting meat, serving him with great care and attentiveness. The beggar became drunk and fell asleep. The next morning, he carried gold as if it were money in the market, riding a horse with servants following, and the townspeople all showed respect. Seeing himself in fine clothes and a tall hat, he realized he was no longer just a petty thief but a major criminal, and his confidence grew even more. From then on, he would go out every night, always returning with great gains, and people could not really object. By the time he was sixty years old, he had several wives and concubines, and his children and grandchildren were all grown up. He was wealthy and well-provided for, sufficient to enjoy his old age, and he had given up thievery as a career. One night, drunk, he went out again and came to a house with a deeply secluded inner room. There were only two young women and a few maidservants inside. Taking advantage of their weakness, he finally rushed in.

    290

    一妇甫就枕，一妇既解衣，见丐俱噤不能出声。丐视未寝之妇，素体莹洁，不觉色心萌动。更揭卧妇之衾，则白身如玉，恍然落叶之花，随风动摇，四肢无著，益勃然情不自禁。径登其榻，将就而狎之，妇亦无敢峻拒。忽回顾彼妇已渺，乃大疑。方起而索之，俄闻户外喧噪声，且甚伙，盖即妇所号召者也。犹恃其能，坦然无惧。既已夺门欲奔，旋见一物如寒霜，直沃厥脑，应手而仆。耳中犹闻人语曰：“盗已毙矣！谁谓我剑不利耶？”丐知已死，魂遂飘然，犹将归家视其眷属。乃丐甫及门屏，即有人鼓掌笑曰：“以盗去，固应以娼来也。”丐不自悟，心则孔悲，及发声，室中哄然曰：“儿落草矣！女也。”丐惊而四顾，身在次儿室中，其妇方坐蓐云。

    Explanation

    290

    One woman was just about to lie down to sleep, while another had already undressed. Seeing the beggar, both women were mute, unable to speak. The beggar looked at the woman who was still dressed, noticing her always pure and clean body, and unconsciously felt a lustful desire stir within him. He then uncovered the quilt of the sleeping woman, revealing her white body like jade. She seemed like a delicate flower fallen from a leaf, swaying with the wind, her limbs without support, which only intensified his uncontrollable passion. He directly climbed onto her bed, intending to approach her intimately, and the woman dared not strongly resist. Suddenly, he looked back and saw the other woman had vanished, causing him great doubt. He stood up and began searching for her when he suddenly heard noisy sounds outside the door, and there were many people, it turned out they were the women she had called. Still relying on his own abilities, he remained calm and fearless. Having already rushed out the door to escape, he suddenly saw a thing like cold frost, which directly poured into his brain, and he fell down immediately. In his ears, he still heard people talking, saying, "The thief is dead! Who says my sword is not sharp?" The beggar knew he was dead, his soul floated away, yet he still wanted to return home to see his family. Just as he reached the door screen, someone clapped and laughed, saying, "Go as a thief, naturally come as a prostitute." The beggar did not realize it, his heart was deeply sad. When he spoke, the room suddenly became noisy, saying, "The child has been born! It is a girl." The beggar was surprised and looked around, realizing his body was in the second child's room, and his wife was in labor.

    291

    心知再世，不敢自明，然在襁褓中，家事无不历历。闻其长子次子，俱为被盗者所讼，官私交迫，家室一空。二男皆以惨刑毙于狱底，次妇携其女归母家，未几再醮去，竟弃其女不复顾。丐居舅室十余年，比长，貌甚丽，而舅妗遇之苛役如仆婢。一日，有老妪踵门而入，谓妗曰：“郎云此中一株柳，俾予移栽，盍指以示我。”妗解其意，召甥相见。妪喜曰：“若舅言不诬，老妇植之，足敷半生乘凉矣。”言已将去，妗属曰：“渠非我种类，实剧盗某之女孙也，汝宜志之，勿令人以予家为笑。”妪唯唯自返。阅两日，即有肩舆来迓。丐知为娼家，坚不肯往，舅妗以鞭扑逐之，痛极始行。

    Explanation

    291

    Knowing in the heart that it was a second life, she dared not reveal it to herself. Yet, even in her swaddling clothes, she remembered all family matters clearly. She heard that both her eldest and second sons were sued by the thief, pressured by both official and private matters, and the household was completely emptied. Both sons were killed with cruel punishment in the prison. The younger wife took her daughter back to her mother's home, soon after she remarried and left, ultimately abandoning her daughter and never looking back. She lived as a beggar in her uncle's house for over ten years. When she grew up, she was very beautiful, but her uncle and aunt treated her harshly, like a servant or slave. One day, an old woman entered the door and said to the aunt, "The master said there is a willow tree here, and asked me to transplant it. Why don't you show it to me?" The aunt understood her meaning and called the nephew to meet her. The old woman happily said, "If your uncle's words are not false, I will plant it, and it will provide shade for half a lifetime." After speaking, she was about to leave, and the aunt instructed, "She is not of our kind; she is actually the granddaughter of a notorious thief. You should remember this, and do not let others laugh at our family." The old woman nodded and returned. After two days, there was a sedan chair sent to fetch her. Knowing it was a brothel, she firmly refused to go. Her uncle and aunt beat her with whips and drove her away. She only went after being severely beaten.

    292

    至则浓妆艳抹，嬉笑倚门，即不欲娼而不得已。数旬以后，名乃大噪。筝笛琵琶，素所未解者，而俨然能事，在己亦不得自由。且其色艺既精，单寒者遂莫能问鼎，而惟巨家富室得以攀折。独是温柔软款，虽胜于穿窬穴墙，而苟贱卑污，尤甚于鼠偷狗窃。既朝张而暮李，人尽可夫；更换斗而移星，客犹未寝。翠眉朝画，面如燕市之帘；锦被宵温，身同射圃之鹄。昔也倾囊鹄箧，惟贪椟内之精金；今也躏绿蹂红，谁惜床头之美玉。丐虽故盗，心颇耻之。如是者十年，以郁疾而卒。甫死，即有獠牙巨角、非兽非人者，凡数辈，絷之以去。俄顷抵一衙署，意即世之所谓冥司也。入见冥王，面黑而有光，乌珠双炯，貌耸神清，次如包龙图，衮冕尊严，高拱于殿上。

    292

    Upon her arrival, she adorned herself with heavy makeup and playful laughter, leaning against the door, though she did not wish to become a prostitute but had no choice. After several weeks, her reputation became widely known. Instruments she had never learned to play, such as the zither, flute, and lute, she appeared to master effortlessly, yet she herself could not control this transformation. Moreover, as her beauty and skills became refined, only the wealthy could afford her, while the poor were left out. Her gentleness and charm, though better than breaking into walls, still made her a lowly and despicable figure, akin to a thief. She went from one man to another, treating all as equal; frequent changes in partners, with guests still not sleeping, indicated a chaotic and unending cycle. Her eyebrows were painted in the morning, her face like a curtain in the market of Yan; her bed was warm at night, her body like a target in a shooting range. Once, she would empty her purse for a fine box, only to covet the precious gold inside; now, she trampled over greenery and red flowers, who would care for the beautiful jade at her bedside? Even beggars, who are already considered thieves, would feel ashamed of her. Living like this for ten years, she died of depression. Immediately after her death, several creatures with fangs and horns, neither animal nor human, took her away. Shortly after, they arrived at an office, which was presumably the underworld. She met the King of Hell, who had a dark face with a glow, bright black eyes, a dignified appearance, and wore a grand robe, sitting high on the throne.

    293

    少致研诘，遽曰：“是有耻心，可贷之，仍令为丐。”且命吏喻以所指。吏即引丐与语曰：“汝自今三世以前，亦丐也，行于市上，见物不无贪心，而究无术以致之，乃悒怏而死。缘此一念，堕落盗中，辗转相因，益以莫救。今得复尔本来，实出冥王大德，汝盍鸣谢。”丐颔之，方将跪拜，忽一巨鬼以骨朵自后击之，大痛失声，惊悸而寤。醒时了然洞沏，惟脑痛欲裂，遂不能起。丐既为众缅述之，莫不为之骇汗。因言邑有积贼，号曰飞手张，殁已数十年矣，取物如寄，人莫能禁，后为某宦家，以妓为饵，毙之室中。又有苏五金者，名娼也，风月十年，倾动数郡，云是盗之遗孽。以丐言验之，良不谬已，遂相与慨息不置。

    Explanation

    293

    A brief inquiry was made, and he immediately replied, "This person has a sense of shame, and can be forgiven; still allow him to remain a beggar." He also ordered the official to explain the reason to the beggar. The official then led the beggar and said, "You, before three generations, were also a beggar. Walking on the market, you had no lack of greed for things, but ultimately had no way to obtain them, and died in despair. Because of this thought, you fell into thievery, and the cycle continued, making it impossible to save. Now you have returned to your original state, truly due to the great virtue of the King of the Underworld. Why not express your gratitude?" The beggar nodded in agreement, about to kneel and bow, when suddenly a giant ghost struck him from behind with a club. He cried out in great pain, awoke in a state of shock and fear. When he woke up, he clearly and thoroughly understood, but only the headache was unbearable, and he could not get up. The beggar had already recounted this story to the crowd, and no one could help but be terrified and sweat. He then mentioned that there was a notorious thief in the town, known as Flying Hand Zhang, who had died several decades ago. He took things as if they were entrusted to him, and no one could stop him. Later, he was killed in the room of a certain official family, using a courtesan as bait. There was also Su Wu Jin, a famous courtesan, who had been active for ten years, causing a sensation in several counties, and was said to be the descendant of a thief. When the beggar's words were verified, it was indeed true, and they all sighed in astonishment.

    294

    某因谓丐曰：“两生受享，一时贫乏，幸获清白之名，远胜污辱之实。丐固无病，病亦不在丐矣。”乃大笑，引众俱去。丐亦顿痊，后人见丐于市，深自敛抑，若有所感动者。偶见行人遗数钱于道周，丐呼而止之，其人故不知为丐，一顾而笑曰：“若乞也，胡不自取而犹告我耶？”丐默然，舍之迳去。知者以为丐得道也。未几，某提共囊诣诸肆，言将有远行，遂别去。不数日，亦失丐之所在。咸宁陈仰举、上元许辅仁，皆与闻其事焉。外史氏曰：盗既为盗，固不惮于娼，以盗实危于娼也。盗思为盗，必不乐于丐，以丐远逊于盗也。不知丐苟不盗，且以洁白之行，傲然于红粉黄巾之上矣。冥王爱人以德，故其于赏罚如此。

    Explanation

    294

    I then said to the beggar: "Two lives enjoying wealth, one moment in poverty, fortunate to gain a name of purity, far surpassing the reality of disgrace. The beggar is not sick, nor is the sickness in the beggar." He then laughed heartily, leading the crowd away. The beggar suddenly recovered, and later people saw the beggar in the market, deeply restraining themselves as if moved. Chanced upon a passerby leaving some money on the side of the road, the beggar called out and stopped him. The passerby, unaware of the beggar's identity, looked at him and said, "If you're begging, why don't you take it yourself and still tell me?" The beggar remained silent, leaving it behind and going straight away. Those who knew thought the beggar had attained the Way. Soon after, I carried my bag to the shops, saying I was going on a distant journey, and then departed. In no time, I also lost track of the beggar's whereabouts. Chen Yangju of Xianning and Xu Furen of Shangyuan, both heard of the matter. The historian outside said: "A thief, being a thief, naturally does not fear prostitution, for theft is more dangerous than prostitution. A thief thinking of being a thief, would certainly not be happy with begging, for beggary is far inferior to theft. Unaware that if the beggar did not steal, he would still be pure in conduct, standing proudly above the red and yellow turbans. The King of the Underworld loves people through virtue, hence his rewards and punishments are like this."

    295

    随园老人曰：梦之为梦，所以兆其将来，今乃鉴其已往。孽镜高悬，后车因以不覆。目之曰异人，良不愧已。

    ○　固安尼
    　　固安，小邑也，素无尼，有之，自静定始。静定姓王氏，其初为某大家婢，色甚殊。主人纳之后房，宠冠同列。主人卒，遂请于主妇，披剃为尼，以报厚恩，实欲脱羁绊而远飏也。主妇喜其诚，为筑庵于近郭，所费不下千缗，极其壮丽。独供一南海大士像，因号之曰观音庵。静定既辟法门，香火众盛，又收女徒数人，日益纵恣。距庵仅半里，为法祥寺，寺僧之壮者皆与往来绸缪，无虚夕。然静定自建此庵，门户日扃，白叟黄童莫或能入，而壮夫无论已。且朝饔夕飧，凡有所须，日倩一贫妪购办，此外不再启闭。己与徒，非值斋醮，绝不轻出，人因以清净称之，鲜有疑其秽者。

    Explanation

    295

    The Elder of the Sui Yuan Garden said: Dreams are dreams, and thus they foreshadow the future; now they reflect the past. The mirror of karma hangs high, and the following carriage will not overturn. To regard him/her as a strange person, truly without shame.

    The Nun of Gu'an

    Gu'an is a small town, which had never had a nun before, until Jingding. Jingding was a woman of the Wang family, originally a maid in a wealthy household, with a very unusual appearance. Her master took her into his inner chambers and favored her above all others. After her master's death, she petitioned the mistress, requesting to be ordained as a nun to repay the deep kindness, in truth desiring to escape her bonds and fly far away. The mistress admired her sincerity and built an abbey near the town, spending no less than a thousand strings of cash, making it extremely grand and magnificent. She alone housed an image of the Bodhisattva of the South Sea and named the abbey Guanyin Abbey. After Jingding opened the door to the Dharma, the incense and fire were abundant, and she also took in several female disciples, becoming increasingly unrestrained. Only half a li from the abbey was the Fayang Temple, and the strong monks there all had intimate relations with her, without a night's rest. Yet, since Jingding built this abbey, the doors and windows were closed every day, and neither white-haired elders nor yellow-haired children could enter, let alone strong men. Moreover, for daily meals, whatever she needed, she would hire a poor old woman to buy and prepare it, and she would not open the doors again for anything else. She and her disciples would only leave the abbey for offerings and sacrifices, and they would not lightly venture out. People therefore called her "pure and clean," and few doubted her purity.

    296

    上元汪秉铎，以孝廉来宰是邑，以尼与僧居相近，心窃讶焉。询诸绅衿，又皆力白其孤洁，汪究惝恍不定，乃遣干人密侦于左近。旬余，有土工许二，醉卧庵前，嫚骂不绝，语语皆侵静定，静定亦闭门不敢答。干人以为疑。翌日，矫宰命遍召土木之工，而独阴厚许，许心感之。一日，醉以酒而诘之曰：“若某日于某庵前，何暴怒乃尔？”许笑曰：“淫婢子许我重赇，每日五贯，今竟悍不肯与，故小辱之。”干人佯讶曰：“静师素有清德，以何事而赂汝？得毋诬乎？”许怒曰：“秃娼何清何德，惟予深悉之。近寺僧，皆其所欢也。虑为人所踪迹，乃以五十缗浼予为地道，自某家坟侧直至庵中，凡四十余丈，号为方便门。

    Explanation

    296

    Master Wang of Shangyuan, having been selected as a孝廉 and appointed to govern this county, was surprised to find that a nun lived in close proximity to a monk. He quietly pondered this and inquired of the local gentry, who all strongly defended her purity. Master Wang remained uncertain and thus sent a subordinate to secretly investigate nearby. After more than ten days, a laborer named Xu Er was found drunkenly sleeping in front of the temple, continuously abusing and insulting the nun.
