The nun, in turn, closed the door and dared not respond. The agent found this suspicious. The next day, the agent pretended to be the magistrate and summoned all construction workers, secretly favoring Xu Er, who felt grateful. One day, the agent got Xu Er drunk and questioned him, saying, "On a certain day in front of a certain temple, why were you so angry?" Xu Er laughed and said, "The lewd nun promised me a large bribe, five strings of cash daily, but now she is stubborn and refuses to give it, so I slightly insulted her." The agent pretended to be surprised and said, "The nun has always been pure and virtuous. For what reason would she bribe you? Is this not a lie?" Xu Er angrily retorted, "What purity and virtue does this shaved prostitute have? Only I know her well. The monks near the temple are all her lovers. She fears being followed, so she asked me to dig a tunnel with fifty strings of cash, from the side of a certain family's tomb to the temple, a total of more than forty zhang, calling it the 'convenient gate.'"

    297

    诸僧乘夜蛇行于其中，或五或三，均来可定。秃娼又于朔望，率女弟子夜行赴寺，作大欢乐道场。深恐予有漏言，故加赂焉。渠但能欺聋聩耳，岂能欺予哉？”干人得实，即以白汪。汪召许以刑拟之，尽得其奸状。乃于是月之望，五鼓出邑，诣法祥寺行香。既至，僧始知，亟弃所乐出迎宰官。汪绐主僧曰：“汝寺之众约几何？可悉告予，予将普施舍。”主僧仓卒以数对，中实缺一二人。汪指数之，阳怒曰：“何竟诳予？是必藐我之职，有高枕不出者。”命役遍索于各房，得诸尼于卧榻，絷之以出，皆裸如也。汪笑谓主僧曰：“阻汝佳会，颇杀风景。然世尊则攒眉久矣！”主僧顿首至流血。汪命尽拘之，而尼中独不见静定。

    Explanation

    297

    The monks moved stealthily through the night, like snakes, arriving in groups of either five or three, a pattern that was predictable. The monk known as "Tusuang" would lead his female disciples on nighttime visits to the temple during the full moon and new moon, creating a grand and joyous assembly. He deeply feared that I might reveal their secrets, so he bribed me. He could only deceive the deaf and dumb, how could he deceive me? The informant reported the truth to Wang. Wang summoned someone to discuss the use of punishment and uncovered all the corrupt activities. On the night of the full moon, at the fifth drumbeat, he left the town to visit the Fa Xiang Temple for incense offerings. Upon arrival, the monks realized his presence and quickly abandoned their entertainment to welcome the official. Wang deceived the head monk, saying, "How many monks are there in your temple? Please tell me in detail, and I will provide widespread charity." The head monk hastily provided a number, but there were actually one or two fewer. Wang pointed out the discrepancy and pretended to be angry, saying, "Why did you deceive me? This must be a sign of disrespect for my authority, with someone staying in bed and not coming out." He ordered a search of all the rooms and found the nuns in their beds, binding them and taking them out, all of whom were naked. Wang smiled at the head monk and said, "Interrupting your pleasant gathering is quite a spoil of the mood. However, the Buddha has been frowning for a long time!" The head monk bowed so deeply that he bled. Wang ordered the arrest of everyone, but noted that one nun, named Jingding, was missing.

    298

    询之，则因病在庵，亦捕之至，实则私产坐蓐耳。汪鞫之，僧尼皆伏罪，惟静定不承。因出许二与之质，兼掘其地道，乃成招。既而得二尸于隧畔，则诸僧以妒相杀者。遂定重罪，流僧尼于岭表，而褫主僧与静定之衣，束以大布，使之对面拥抱如好合然，贮以巨龛，积薪而焚之。汪戏为之偈，以送之曰：“咄嗟二师，四大相依。听我一语，携手归西。由空入色，设想虽奇，刹那败露，信有天知。借此三昧，急早脱离。莫沉欲海，永证菩提。生既掌风流之教，死亦化连理之枝。噫！改换皮毛犹牝牡，秋风道上每双骑。”诵讫，阖邑为之粲然。俄顷，毗卢尽成灰烬，庵遂废。迄今固安之俗，缁流尚无雌伏者，汪公肃清之力也。

    Explanation

    298

    Inquiring into the matter, it was found that she was ill in a temple, and they were also captured and brought there. In reality, she was just giving birth in private. Wang interrogated them, and the monks and nuns all confessed, except for Jingding, who did not confess. Because he produced Xu Er as evidence, and also dug up their underground passage, they then confessed. Later, two bodies were found beside the tunnel, and it turned out that the monks had killed each other out of jealousy. So they were given severe punishment, sent the monks and nuns to the remote regions, and stripped the chief monk and Jingding of their clothes, binding them with coarse cloth and making them embrace each other face-to-face as if they were in harmony, putting them in a large niche and piling up wood to burn them. Wang jokingly wrote a verse to send them off, saying:

    Ah, two masters! The four elements depend on each other. Listen to my words. Take each other's hand and go west. From emptiness to form, the idea is strange. Exposed in an instant, indeed, heaven knows. Using this state of concentration, quickly detach. Do not sink into the sea of desire, always attain enlightenment. In life, they held the teachings of elegance. In death, they became intertwined branches. Ah, changing skin and fur still makes them male and female. In the autumn wind, they often ride together.

    After reciting, the whole city laughed. In a moment, the temple of Vairocana became ashes, and the hermitage was then abandoned. Until now, in Gu'an, there are no more female hermits. It is the power of Master Wang to cleanse.

    299

    外史氏曰：尼之为尼，至今愈不可问矣。肆欲宣淫、恬不知耻如静定者，犹存羞恶之良者也。脱非许二漏言，依然清冰洁玉，孰得而议其后哉？余友邵次彭，曾作解冤经，几数百言，今撮其略如左。经曰：“伏以白马皌经，原非用牝；奈何青狮秉教，反为求雌？夫勤面壁之深功，眼底空花易过；脱少参禅之定静，泥中败絮宜沾。如引摩登，同归极乐；难辞佛国，顿改温柔。故既形判阴阳，自合礼严男女。

    Explanation

    299

    The historian says: The nun, as a nun, has become increasingly beyond reproach. One like Jingding, who indulges in lust and promiscuity without shame, still retains some sense of shame and disgust. Had it not been for Xǔ Èr's revelation, she would still be as pure and virtuous as ice and jade, who could then criticize her? My friend Shào Cìpéng once wrote the Jiě Yuān Jīng (Sutra of Resolving Karmic Debts), which is several hundred words long. I will now summarize its main points. The sutra says: "In the Báimǎ Bìng Jīng (White Horse Sutra), the original intention was not to use the female principle; how then can the Qīngshī Bǐng Jiào (Green Lion Teaching) be used to seek the feminine? The deep practice of facing the wall is easy to pass through the illusions in one's eyes; if one lacks the calmness of Zen meditation, one should be tainted by the filth of the world. If one leads Mòdēng (a person or concept) to the Pure Land, it is difficult to escape the Buddha-land and suddenly become gentle. Therefore, since the body is divided into yin and yang, it is appropriate to strictly adhere to the rites for men and women."

    300

    乃有若而人者，非禀乾健以生，洵足超凡入圣，实抱坤柔以处，只堪献供拈花。蕴玉含香，自有仙郎描翠黛；欺桃笑柳，不应少女谢金钗。苟赖天全，奚为祝发？倘非病废，何事披缁？窃尝叩厥由来，不禁哑然言下。盖缘婆心佞佛，掌珠轻掷于空门；月老主婚，花烛忽陈于梵宇。既髠其顶，安知为尚为尼；苟秘其踪，何必非夫非妇。况近春风之面，孽海难清；且敲夜月之扉，蓬山不远。以致梅娇杏俏，好花终上道人头；暮鼓晨钟，私语频留老僧耳。冤之当解，莫此为尤。想其燕乳莺雏，随缘可度。洎乎苞含蕊吐，赍恨难消。

    Explanation

    300

    There are such people as this: not born with the strong and vigorous qualities of the heavens, truly capable of transcending the ordinary and entering the realm of the sage, but actually possessing the gentle and yielding qualities of the earth, only fit to be offered as a flower to be plucked. Holding precious gems and fragrant flowers, there is a celestial man who sketches green eyebrows; mocking the peach and laughing at the willow, a young girl should not thank the golden hairpin. If one relies on the completeness of heaven, why then take a vow of celibacy? If not due to illness or disability, why then wear the monk's robe? I have secretly inquired about its origin, cannot help but speak in surprise. It is because of the kind-hearted devotion to Buddhism, the cherished pearl was lightly cast into the void of the monastery; the god of marriage arranged the union, the wedding torches were suddenly set up in the temple. Having shaved his head, how can one know if it is still a nun; if one keeps their whereabouts secret, why not be neither husband nor wife. Especially when close to the face of spring, the sea of sin is hard to cleanse; and knocking on the door of the night moon, the fairy mountains are not far. Leading to the plum and apricot being charming, beautiful flowers eventually adorn the head of a monk; the evening drum and morning bell, private words frequently linger in the ears of the old monk. The grudge should be resolved, none more so than this. Thinking of the swallows' young and the orioles' chicks, can be guided by following the path of karma. When the bud contains and the flower blooms, carrying resentment that is hard to dispel.

    301

    人尽有情，惟我木鱼敲五夜；谁能无欲，输他锦帐价千金。因太息于蒲团，徒悲鸟道；遂流连于莲座，暗许鸿沟。于斯时也，三千揭谛恨难言，八百金刚降不住矣。迨至琉璃灯火，乘暗谈经，不免纸帐梅花。因昏入定，袈裟湿露，更带些云雨之痕。茫履寻春，不再问莺花之径。可笑者藤牵蔓引，床头结一对葫芦。堪怜者水尽烟空，月下散几双皍皏。当解之冤，信居其二。无何情深爱久，时贮甘露于钵盂；又难乞药寻方，早祛愁根于脏腑。

    Explanation

    301

    Everyone has feelings, but only I strike the wooden fish for five nights; who can be without desire, losing to the silk canopy worth a thousand gold pieces. Sighing on the zafu, only mourning the bird's path; then lingering at the lotus throne, secretly agreeing to the Hangu Pass. At this time, the three thousand gods of revelation have a grievance hard to express, the eight hundred Vajras cannot be subdued. Until the crystal lamp is lit, talking about the scriptures in the dark, cannot help but have paper screens and plum blossoms. Entering meditation due to drowsiness, the robe is wet with dew, also bearing traces of rain and clouds. Searching for spring with vague footsteps, no longer asking about the path of orioles and flowers. Pitiable is the one whose water is exhausted and smoke is empty, scattered a few pairs of pearls under the moon. The grudge that should be resolved, indeed, it is among the two. Without knowing, deep feelings and long-lasting love, store sweet dew in the alms bowl; also difficult to beg for medicine and seek a remedy, early remove the root of sorrow from the inner organs.

    302

    法躯怀甲，禄山之腹难藏；禅室添丁，贵妃之钱谁赐？于是祇园隙地，埋尽姹女婴儿；顿令灵鹫奇峰，陷在血盆阿鼻。冤乎宜解，又属其三。是故幸而瓦全，清净已非清净；不幸而玉碎，风流忒不风流。方盘荐有颈之瓜，弥勒佛难开笑口。苗条吃无情之棒，比丘尼莫按羞眉。何如斩断情丝，永清法界；反可跳出欲海，免落迷津。不望菩萨之慧刀，割开并蒂；惟求如来之宝树，辞却旁枝。佛法不度闺门，陷人坑早已打破；仙娥请回莲步，迷魂阵俱各勾除。从此后锡杖无尘，衲衣少垢，大雄宝殿方可号以大雄，不坏金身乃能终于不坏。咦！愿求一滴杨枝水，洗尽人间老婆禅。”经语尚多，此尤解颐。

    Explanation

    302

    The body of the law carries armor, yet An Lushan's ambitions cannot be contained; the Zen room adds a son, whose funds were granted by Yang Guifei? Thus, in the Garden of Jeta, all beautiful girls and infants are buried; the Vulture Peak, once a sacred place, is now tainted by the hellish depths. Alas, this injustice should be resolved, and it is the third instance. Therefore, if one is fortunate enough to survive as a whole, purity is no longer true purity; if one is unfortunate enough to break like jade, elegance is lost. The dish of melons with necks, even Maitreya Buddha finds it too serious to smile. A slender figure is struck by an unfeeling rod, and nuns should not hide their shame. How about cutting off emotional ties, forever purifying the Dharma realm? One can jump out of the sea of desire and avoid falling into the maze of confusion. One does not hope for the wisdom sword of the Bodhisattva to cut open the twin lotus, but only seeks the precious tree of the Tathagata, discarding the side branches. The Dharma does not reach the inner chambers, but the pit that traps people has already been broken; celestial maidens return their lotus steps, and all the arrays that bewilder the soul have been removed. From now on, the staff will be free of dust, and the patched robe will have little dirt. Only the Great Hero Hall can be called the Great Hero, and only an unbreakable golden body can remain unbreakable. Ah! May one seek a drop of water from the willow branch, washing away all the worldly and vulgar forms of Zen. There is still much more to the sutra, but this particularly enlightens.

    303

    ○　无常鬼
    　　易郡人吴可久，尝宵行驿路中，循表道之树，徒步而疾行。时正月明，见前有白光矗立，摇摇动荡，仿佛云影徘徊。远观之，亦不甚悉。有顷至前，则非云气，实一巨人，白衣裤，长与树杪等，往来蹀躞。路阔可并驰三军，其人跬步越之，似无余地。乃大骇，惧不敢前。初犹俟其去而后行，久之，其踱如故，且两袂綦宽，累累然有物蠕动，渐作鬼声。遂不敢复立，反身就故道，另觅曲径而归，竟亦无恙。后以之语人，或谓为无常鬼，似亦近是。

    Explanation

    303

    A Ghost of the Death Messenger

    Once upon a time, a man named Wu Kejiu from Yixian County was traveling at night on a post road, following a path marked by trees, walking quickly on foot. At that time, the moon was bright, and he saw a white light standing erect ahead of him, swaying and fluctuating, as if a cloud shadow was lingering. From a distance, he was not quite sure what it was. After a while, when he got closer, he realized it was not a cloud but a giant, wearing white clothing and pants, as tall as the treetops, pacing back and forth. The road was wide enough for three armies to ride side by side, but the giant took steps that surpassed the usual distance, as if there was no room left. Wu Kejiu was greatly frightened and dared not move forward. At first, he waited for the giant to leave before continuing, but after a long time, the giant continued pacing as before, and his sleeves were very wide, with something wriggling continuously, gradually making ghostly sounds. Wu Kejiu was so terrified that he could not stand still anymore. He turned around and returned to the original path, finding another winding path to return home, and finally came back without any harm. Later, he told others about this experience, and some said it was the ghost of the Death Messenger, which seemed to be close to the truth.

    304

    外史氏曰：野旷宵深，鬼魅现形，固所宜然。昔先大父所居之宅，多怪异。尝于中夜寤后，见窗间巨影大于丈尺之牖，而窸窣刺纸有声。叱之至再，甫徐徐缩足踏地，其响甚巨，俨掷千余斤重石，比屋皆闻。人以为魍魉，亦一异也。

    ○　苏绪
    　　汝南苏绪，字道基，风雅士也。元皇统中，以事羁迹于燕，久未得归。适遘疫疠，卧病于城北之翀霄观中。势已危笃，道众虑其死，扶而委置于庑下，苏迷惘亦不自知。恍惚中见其故友某生，匆匆来，谓之曰：“诸务告竣，可行矣！”苏正乡思迫切，欣然从之。甫出观，早有二卫絷以相俟，友挟掖使乘同行。出郭约数里，天已昏暝，友白曰：“夕阳西下，盍停骖明旦启行乎？”苏亦颔之。于是共诣一村，桃柳夹植，红碧相间，行次落英缤纷，长条摇曳，绝似暮春风景。

    Explanation

    304

    The historian says: In the vast wilderness and deep night, ghosts and demons appear, which is naturally expected. In the house where my grandfather once lived, there were many strange occurrences. Once, after waking up in the middle of the night, I saw a large shadow in the window, larger than the opening of a window, accompanied by a faint rustling sound piercing through the paper. I rebuked it twice, and just as it slowly retracted its feet and stepped on the ground, the sound was very loud, as if throwing a stone weighing over a thousand pounds, and the entire neighborhood heard it. People thought it was a ghost or demon, and it was indeed a strange event.

    ○ Su Xu
    Su Xu, from Runan, was a refined and cultured man. During the reign of the Yuan dynasty, he was trapped in Yan due to certain affairs and had not been able to return home for a long time. Just then, he contracted a plague and lay ill in the Chongxiao Temple in the northern part of the city. His condition was extremely critical, and the temple congregation feared he would die, so they helped him and placed him under the eaves. Su Xu was dazed and unaware. In a daze, he saw an old friend named Mr. Someone, who came in a hurry and said to him, "All matters have been completed; we can go now!" Su Xu, who was deeply longing for home, happily followed him. As soon as he left the temple, two guards were already waiting to restrain him, and his friend supported him and made him ride along. After traveling about three miles out of the city, it was already dusk, and his friend said, "The sun is setting; why not stop the carriage and start the journey tomorrow?" Su Xu nodded in agreement. Then they went to a village together, where peach and willow trees were planted, red and green alternating, with fallen flowers falling profusely and long branches swaying, absolutely resembling the scenery of late spring.

    305

    苏顾谓友曰：“斯何地也？时届三秋，何卉木繁盛若是？”友笑曰：“兄勿问，至时自有佳境。”须臾抵一门，状甚烜赫，如王侯巨家，僮仆出入以百计。苏觉精神陡健，回顾其友，已失所在，乃自下骑而入。甫及门侧，俄闻宅第中金鼓大作，如演弋阳剧焉。心益歆动，潜往窥之，即亦无人诃止。历门三重，直达厅事。堂上银烛高烧，宾主十余席，优人队舞而前，亦莫辨为何曲，箫管敖曹，间以笑语而已。苏鹄立谛视，宾席皆男，有黄冠者，有缁衣者，有冕者，有弁者，亦有草衣卉服者，贵贱老少，形状不一。

    Explanation

    305

    Sū Gù said to his friend, "What is this place? It is now late autumn, why are the plants and trees so lush?" His friend smiled and said, "Brother, do not ask; at the right time, there will be a wonderful place." Soon they arrived at a gate, which was very magnificent, like that of a noble or wealthy family. Servants came and went by the hundreds. Sū felt his spirit suddenly invigorated. When he looked back at his friend, he had disappeared. He then dismounted and entered. As soon as he reached the side of the gate, he soon heard a great sound of drums and gongs from inside the mansion, like a performance of a Yiyang opera. His heart grew more excited. He secretly went to look, but no one stopped him. Passing through three gates, he reached the main hall. Inside, silver candles burned brightly, and there were more than ten tables set up for guests and hosts. Dancers performed in front of them, but he could not tell what tune it was. Flutes and pipes were played, interspersed with laughter and conversation. Sū stood there like a crane, watching carefully. All the guests were men. There were those with yellow hats (likely Taoist priests), those in black robes (likely Buddhist monks), those wearing crowns, and those wearing ceremonial hats. There were also those wearing clothes made of grass and plants. They were of different statuses, ages, and appearances.

    306

    惟主席二筵，坐四美人，璎珞垂珠，半遮娇面，均衣五色宫衣。席前巡酒者，亦皆二八妖鬟，绝无三尺之童，心窃艳之。立许时，不闻驱逐，一时丽色惊眸，酒香聋鼻，不觉宿疾若失。而脾燥喉干，顿忘顾忌，忽抗声大呼曰：“如此高会，而不一波及旅人，为之主者，不亦吝甚？”语未毕，突如而前，笑谓主者曰：“有不速之客一人来！”径趋上坐，便将就席，一堂举愕然。主者微有怒色，遍召门者诘之，咸以不知跪白。宾席者俱笑曰：“此狂客实有夙缘，夫人幸勿怒。”乃命以杯箸进苏，逊之首坐，仍复欢饮。

    Explanation

    306

    In the grand hall, there were two seats, and four beautiful women sat upon them. They wore jewelry and beads that hung down, partially concealing their lovely faces, all dressed in colorful imperial robes. Before the seats, young and beautiful women served wine, and there were no children present, which secretly delighted me. After a while, no one was expelled, and suddenly, the sight of their beauty and the aroma of the wine overwhelmed my senses, making me feel as if my chronic illness had vanished. My thirst and dry throat made me forget my inhibitions. Suddenly, I shouted loudly, "Such a grand gathering, yet not a single wave of hospitality for travelers! Is the host not being quite stingy?" Before I could finish, I rushed forward, smiling at the host and saying, "There is an unexpected guest here!" I moved directly to the main seat and prepared to sit down, causing everyone in the hall to be taken aback. The host showed a slight expression of anger and called all the servants to question them, but they all claimed ignorance and knelt to report. The guests all laughed and said, "This eccentric guest truly has a predestined connection, please do not be angry, Lady." The host then ordered the guest to be served and seated at the main position, and we resumed our joyful drinking.

    307

    苏连浮三爵，俨如甘露醍醐，烦燥顿解，益大悦。方将展问官阀，俄而钲鸣若雷，铙喧如沸，优人扮鬼十数，共执一人，当场而支解之。手足割裂，脏腑狼藉，血雨腥风，扑鼻惨目。苏素未睹此，不觉大惊，以袂自障其面，汗如雨下，耳边惟闻嘶痛之声，股傈欲走。良久，张目视之，寄身廊庑，盖已霍然愈矣，始知其梦，且叹其奇。因匍匐往寻道众，饮以粥汤，不旬日，而复初。后过岳庙，见寝宫塑四美人，酷似梦中东道主，但不知所延者为何客耳。又半年，始归故乡。病疫者每乐闻其说，比诸子章髑髅云。

    Explanation

    307

    Su drank three cups of wine in succession, and it was as delightful as sweet dew and pure milk, suddenly relieving his restlessness and making him even more delighted. Just as he was about to inquire about their social status, suddenly the sound of drums and cymbals resounded like thunder, and the performers, dressed as ghosts in the dozens, seized one person and dismembered him on the spot. Hands and feet were cut apart, organs scattered in disorder, blood rained down, and the stench of wind was horrifying to the eyes. Su had never seen such a thing before and was suddenly very frightened, covering his face with his sleeve. Sweat poured down like rain, and he only heard the sound of screams and cries, his legs trembling as he wanted to run. After a long time, he opened his eyes and looked, finding himself in a corridor. He had suddenly recovered, realizing it was a dream, and marveled at its strangeness. He then crawled to find the Daoists, who gave him porridge and soup, and after a few days, he returned to his original state. Later, he passed by the Yue Temple, where he saw four beautiful women sculpted in the sleeping quarters, extremely similar to the host in his dream, but he did not know who the guest was. After another six months, he finally returned to his hometown. Those who were sick with plague often enjoyed hearing this story, which is comparable to the story of Zixia and the skull.

    308

    外史氏曰：酒色薰心，鲜能祛病，凶残触目，反可愈疾。甚矣，安不如危，而因危得安者，难以更仆数也。若仅以梦中佳遇，解其弥留，吾知其疾必增。即卢鹊罕与奏效，况神乎？神之为神，当亦深悉乎此已。

    ○　卫美人
    　　京师某公，家有老仆曰李某，性朴实，然诺不欺。从某公居积数年，少有余蓄，年且老，乃退闲自设一肆于市，以屠驴为生。一夕，宿于家，梦一美人，玄衣而白褶，粉容惨淡，珠泪凄惶，直前再拜曰：“妾卫姓，突遭强暴，命在须臾，非君莫能拯救，望垂援手，敢效衔环。”李梦中粗解所言，恻然许之。及寤，始夜分，展转不寐。五鼓始起如市，亦循其常，所疑固不在此也。

    Explanation

    308

    The Historian remarks: Being intoxicated by wine and women rarely cures illness, but witnessing fierce and cruel sights can sometimes heal one's ailments. Indeed, tranquility is often less beneficial than danger, and the number of cases where one gains tranquility from danger is too numerous to count. If one relies solely on a dream encounter to cure a critical illness, I know that the condition will only worsen. Even a legendary physician like Lu Que would rarely be effective, let alone a deity. The deity, being divine, must surely deeply understand this.

    Wei Beauty

    A certain gentleman from the capital had an old servant named Li, who was honest and never deceitful. After serving the gentleman for many years, Li had saved a little money. As he grew old, he retired and set up a stall in the market to earn a living by slaughtering donkeys. One night, he stayed at home and dreamed of a beautiful woman in a black robe with white pleats. Her face was pale, her tears of pearls filled with sorrow, and she knelt before him twice, saying, "I am of the Wei family, suddenly subjected to violence, my life is in danger, and only you can save me. I hope you will lend a hand, and I dare to repay you with a ring." Li understood her words in his dream and felt compassion, promising to help her. When he woke up, it was already midnight, and he tossed and turned, unable to sleep. At the fifth watch, he got up as usual and went to the market, continuing his daily routine. The doubt he had was not about this.

    309

    比至肆前，闻众喧笑曰：“腹膨脝，奈何无孕？”又一人曰：“果有之，正不知为谁氏种。”言次哄然。李讶之，试从板隙窥焉。烛焰摇红，镬汤蒸雾，众方鼓刃，而屠椿上缚一妇，白身挺立，绝似睡乡所见者。然已破腹屠肠，腥红满地，不可以复救。李大骇，惧祸及己，悄然复返。抵家尚战栗，日高不敢出。肆中操刀者不见主人，亟往促之。李审知无患，方肯偕来。至，见肉陈于砧上，依然是驴，始悟前梦。以未得救，秘而不言。阅三日，即阖其肆，不再业屠。其人改业后告人，每举此事以戒杀，犹以食言为憾焉。

    Explanation

    309

    By the time he arrived at the shop, he heard the crowd laughing and saying, "Her belly is swollen, yet she is not pregnant?" Another person said, "If she really is, I wonder who the father is." The laughter grew louder. Li was surprised and, trying to peer through a gap in the boards, saw flickering candlelight and steam rising from boiling water. The crowd was sharpening their knives, and on the butcher's pole was tied a woman, standing naked and upright, looking exactly like someone he had seen in a dream. However, she had already been disemboweled and her intestines were cut out, with blood covering the ground, making it impossible to save her. Li was greatly shocked, fearing that the trouble might come to him, and quietly returned home. Upon arriving, he was still trembling, and dared not go out during the day. The person in the shop who was holding the knife did not see the owner, so he went urgently to find him. Li confirmed that there was no danger, and then agreed to come along. When he arrived, he saw the meat on the chopping block, and it turned out to be a donkey. He finally understood his dream. Because he had not managed to save the woman, he kept the incident secret. After three days, he closed his shop and stopped being a butcher. After changing his profession, he told others about this incident, often mentioning it to warn against killing, still feeling regret for not keeping his promise to save the woman.

    310

    外史氏曰：香闺绣阁之人，安有披毛戴角之日？审此梦中风景，谓是阎罗老子，铁石心肠，又乌知香温玉软中放侈骄淫，苦恼皆自惹耶？吾愿天下恒河沙数美人，读此皆合猛醒。与其摇尾以求生，何如忏悔于未死。不但可免于屠，且可无焚琴煮鹤之事。奈之何迷而不悟？

    ○　苦节
    　　燕赵古称多佳人，以予所见，都城而外，其村居而野处者，苟非黄面瞿昙，即属黑头包老，鲜有一白皙可观者。抑且瘿瘘拥项，何问蝤蛴？复履撑舟，可知莲瓣，盖亦风土淳朴之故也。惟玉田一少女，盈盈十五，貌颇轶群。虽非莹光之玉，尚存夭桃之色。乃余再经其地，闻已适人，且守苦节而死，心窃悯之。诘其故，则其翁聂姓，曾业儒，迂腐执拗，为文三十载莫博一衿，因弃而归农。生一子，颇有父风，亦屡试而未售，即女之婿也。乔梓之间，互相标榜，父谓子为芝兰，子以父为姜桂。父之所可，子亦从而可之；子之所否，父亦从而否之。

    Explanation

    310

    The historian says: Women in the inner chambers of the home, how could they ever become beastly? Considering this dream-like scene, it is like the stern and unyielding Yama, the god of death, yet how could one know that within the warmth and tenderness of a woman's body, there lies indulgence, pride, and licentiousness, all of which bring their own troubles? I wish that all the beautiful women in the world, upon reading this, would wake up to the truth. Is it better to grovel for survival, or to repent before death? Not only can one avoid being slaughtered, but also avoid the destruction of beauty and the arts. How can one remain in a state of ignorance and not realize the truth?

    Hardship in Maintaining Virtue
    The regions of Yan and Zhao are historically known for their many beautiful women. From my observations, outside the capital cities, those living in villages and rural areas are either old monks with yellow faces or elderly scholars with black hair, few of whom are fair and beautiful. Moreover, they often have goiters and neck tumors, how can one ask about the beauty of their necks? Their feet are often calloused and swollen, like lotus petals, which is also due to the simple and rustic customs of the region. Only one young woman from Yutian, at the age of fifteen, stood out for her beauty. Though she was not as radiant as jade, she still had the bloom of a young peach. I passed through the area again and heard that she had married and died while maintaining her virtue, which deeply moved me. When I inquired about the reason, it turned out that her father, surnamed Nie, was once a scholar who was stubborn and inflexible. He spent thirty years writing without passing the imperial examinations, so he abandoned his studies and returned to farming. He had a son who was very much like his father, also failing in his exams multiple times, and he became her husband. Between father and son, they mutually praised each other; the father called the son "orchid and lily," and the son referred to the father as "ginger and cinnamon." Whatever the father approved of, the son also approved of; whatever the father disapproved of, the son also disapproved of.

    311

    创立臆说，讥刺短长。人有美，且鳃鳃弗许；人有恶，即剌剌不休。是以取憎于乡，同井者胥为之侧目。自女于归，家益贫薄，藜藿薪樗之采，皆女躬亲任之。媪又病废在床，不克偕往，以如玉之姿，行中林之地，保无有诱以死鹿者。幸女性贞淑，言笑不苟，而乡风近古，王法綦严，人固无能犯，而亦无敢犯焉。乃翁有妻姊某氏，其家与翁居相错也。生一女曰二姑，貌媸而性荡，施粉涂朱，巧笑轻颦，里中少年，咸以口舌相嘲弄。因与翁家为瓜葛，凡女之出作，姑必与俱，而任其轻佻，亦各执其事不之顾。时值新秋，秸秫茂密，女将往采菽荳，以供午餐。

    Explanation

    311

    She invented fanciful theories and criticized both the strengths and weaknesses of others. When someone was praised, she would be extremely reluctant to admit it; when someone was criticized, she would talk incessantly. As a result, she became disliked in the village, and those who lived nearby would all look at her with sideways glances. After she married, her family became increasingly poor and thin. She personally gathered coarse vegetables and firewood. Her grandmother was sick and bedridden, unable to accompany her. With her beauty of jade, she walked in the middle of the forest, and who could say there was no one luring her with a dead deer? Fortunately, she had a chaste and virtuous nature, speaking and laughing without being frivolous. The local customs were close to ancient traditions, and the laws of the king were extremely strict. No one could or dared to harm her. Her father-in-law had a sister-in-law named Mrs. So-and-so, whose home was near his. She had a daughter named Second Auntie, who was ugly in appearance but promiscuous in nature. She applied makeup, smiling cleverly and frowning lightly, and the young men in the village would mock her with words. Because of the family ties, whenever the girl went out to work, Second Auntie would always accompany her, allowing her to be light and frivolous, while they focused on their own tasks without paying attention to her. It was the beginning of autumn, and the corn and sorghum were lush and dense. The girl was about to go to gather beans and soybeans to provide lunch.

    312

    盖燕蓟之地，田间杂植，蔓引于梁木之上，如附乔之萝，结荚可茹，农家以为常品。女欲呼姑，既已先往，乃独行。披禾而入，采未倾筐，俄闻笑声吃吃，似在左近。因大惊，疑有恶少窥己只身，将为掩袭之计。及拨密秸视之，见姑鞠躬如磬折，隐约之间状如大解者。初不意连理之枝，仅睹其半也，因笑而相唤。两人举大骇，谓女已窥见底里。姑亦不敢应声，径穿阡陌，仓惶遁去。女犹不悟，讶其避己，尚以为匿迹禾中，聊相嬉戏，遂辍采往从之，竟不见。

    Explanation

    312

    In the region of Yan and Jì, fields were interspersed with various plants, their vines trailing on the rafters of farmhouses, like ivy clinging to a tall tree. The pods could be eaten, and farmers considered it a common crop. The girl wanted to call her grandmother, but since she had already gone ahead, she went alone. She pushed through the rice stalks to enter the field, still gathering before her basket was full, when suddenly she heard giggling, as if nearby. She was greatly startled and suspected a mischievous youth was watching her alone, planning to ambush her. When she parted the dense straw to look, she saw her grandmother bowing deeply, in the dim light it seemed like she was relieving herself. She hadn't expected that the intertwined branches would only show half, so they laughed and called out to each other. Both of them were greatly shocked, thinking the girl had seen everything. Her grandmother dared not respond, hurriedly passed through the fields, and fled in panic. The girl still didn't understand, surprised that she was avoiding her, still thinking she was hiding in the rice stalks, just playing around. So she stopped gathering and followed, but she never saw her again.

    313

    比归遇姑于微行，乃笑而谓曰：“亦太疏狂，独不畏人之见耶？”姑面色红白，心益疑惧，爰乘间谋之所欢曰：“春光泄矣，可若何？渠姑与吾母姊妹也，渠翁性乖戾，乡邻有小过、辄龂龂而道之，况姻娅而有是事乎？予父母必置予于死地矣！”言已娇泣，如丧考妣。其人齐姓，故无赖，非乡中人，家于邑城，甚饶裕，缘视佃人刈获，始来此。见姑与女偕，好丑攸分，恍辛夷之与桃李，心大动。既而闻聂父子之陗厉，而女又端重不佻，似不可猝得，遂假途于姑。原以图女，非止得陇而始望蜀者。闻言大喜，乃谓姑曰：“若惧乎？是在人谋。倘以计兼致之，则弭其口不难矣。”姑以首示之意，徐曰：‘是诚不能。渠夫年正壮，琴瑟綦调，非如我无人操缦者。

    Explanation

    313

    Upon returning, she encountered her mother-in-law during a secret outing. Smiling, she said, "You are also too reckless, don't you fear being seen?" Her mother-in-law's face turned red and white, and her heart grew more suspicious and fearful. She then took the opportunity to discuss with her lover, saying, "The spring has been revealed, what can be done now? Her mother-in-law is my mother's sister, and her husband is ill-tempered, always arguing with neighbors over minor faults. How much more so when it comes to family matters? My parents will surely kill me!" She finished speaking, sobbing tenderly, as if mourning her parents. He was surnamed Qi, a rascal, not from the village, living in the county town, and very wealthy. Because he was watching the tenant farmers harvest, he came here. Seeing the mother-in-law and daughter together, the difference in beauty was clear, like the contrast between magnolia and peach blossoms. His heart was greatly stirred. Later, he heard about the severity of the father and son, and the daughter was dignified and not frivolous, making it seem unlikely to obtain her quickly. Thus, he took a detour through the mother-in-law. Originally intending to pursue the daughter, not someone who is content with what he has. Hearing this, he was greatly pleased and said to the mother-in-law, "Are you afraid? It depends on human planning. If we plan to bring both of them together, it won't be hard to silence them." The mother-in-law nodded to indicate her agreement and said slowly, "I truly cannot. Her husband is in his prime, and their relationship is very harmonious, unlike me who is without a partner."

    314

    且言不涉邪，与之语房帷中事，辄颊赤而疾趋。女伴犹然，妄男子可知已。”齐曰：“不然。渠家甚贫，妇性如水，倘以利诱，而更以情欲动之，渠将自炫不遑，奚难耶？”姑勉从其言。齐以千钱授姑，且授以策。嗣是有货物来村者，凡闺人之所需，及一切饮食，姑必呼女共观，市以相遗。女坚不肯受，而讶其挥霍。或微问之，姑笑而不答。阅数日，齐于新筑场上，集村中牧牛儿，为蹴球之戏，琢石为之，以双丸相击为胜者。瞥见女与姑冉冉而来，时将往挑野蔬也。齐止姑，立而与语，授以腰橐，冀女见之。女早疾行而前，相去不止数武。

    Explanation

    314

    "Surely you don't mean to say that when you talk about private matters, their cheeks would flush red and they would quickly move away. The female companions are still the same, and a common man is even more so. No. His family is very poor, and women are gentle and adaptable. If you use money to entice, and further stir them with desire, he will be so eager to show off that he won't have time. What difficulty is there?" The aunt reluctantly followed her advice. Qi gave a thousand coins to the aunt and gave her a plan. Afterwards, whenever goods arrived in the village, whatever the women needed, and all kinds of food and drink, the aunt would call the daughter to see them together, buy them, and give them as gifts. The daughter firmly refused to accept them and was surprised by her extravagance. Or asked her subtly, the aunt smiled but did not answer. After several days, Qi on the newly built field gathered the village boys who herded cattle to play a game of kicking a ball, made from carved stone, the winner is determined by hitting the ball with two balls. Suddenly saw the daughter and aunt slowly approaching, they were going to pick wild vegetables. Qi stopped the aunt, stood and talked to her, gave her a waist pouch, hoping the daughter would see it. The daughter quickly walked ahead, they were more than several steps apart."

    315

    姑因故以示女曰：“若人太厚情，得之群儿者，悉以畀我，将何以酬？”女自是疑姑有私，稍稍远之。而姑与齐亦疑其先几规避，阴谋倍亟。无何，场功伊始，女奉翁媪命往舂麦。以己荏弱，不得已而借助于姑。及昏未竣，女惧翁责，晚食后乘夜复往。乃姑与齐已造谋，预伏硙室，且阖其扉。女既知姑先在，呼令辟之，寂无应者。女不得入，麦不得出，遂徘徊不能去。旋闻姑笑曰：“若具粗壮如萝卜，其乐真难言状！”女骇然，益信姑与人私，抽身欲返，而虑麦为人窃。进退彳亍之际，乃室中浓云密雨之声间杂聒耳。女羞忿畏怯，不知所出，遂不顾其麦而归。

    Explanation

    315

    The grandmother, for some reason, told the girl, "That person is too sentimental, giving everything she received from the children to me, what will she repay me with?" The girl then began to suspect that the grandmother had a secret affair and gradually distanced herself. The grandmother and Qi also suspected that she had anticipated and avoided them, and they plotted more urgently. Soon after, at the beginning of the harvest, the girl was ordered by her grandparents to go grind wheat. Because she was weak herself, she reluctantly relied on the grandmother. By dusk, it was not finished, and the girl feared the grandfather's reprimand, so after the evening meal, she went back at night. The grandmother and Qi had already conspired, and they had hidden in the mill room, closing the door. The girl knew the grandmother was already there, called out for her to open the door, but there was no response. Unable to enter, and unable to take the wheat out, she hesitated and could not leave. Soon, she heard the grandmother laugh and say, "If you are as coarse and strong as a radish, the joy is truly indescribable!" The girl was shocked and even more convinced that the grandmother had a secret affair, so she tried to leave, but worried that the wheat would be stolen. In her hesitation, the sound of dense clouds and heavy rain in the room mixed and disturbed her ears. Feeling ashamed, angry, and fearful, she did not know what to do, so she went home without caring about the wheat.

    316

    姑久之不见女入，知其坚不可动，瞰其去，遣齐逐之，已不能及。二人益惶悚，姑咎齐曰：“前日之目既不可掩，今夕之耳愈不能扪。弄丑当场，究无济于大事，子真误予矣。”齐思之良久，曰：“事急矣！非强致之，必不可挽。向晦闻聂翁饮于邻家，醉已不起，其子又在田畔，今夜亦不得归。渠家一妪，因病早久睡乡，何能为？子盍导我一往，务随吾心而后已。”姑匆遽不知所为，径从之。及抵聂家，疑惧犹未敢入。静侦之，阒其无人。姑素往来甚稔，潜启其扃入，闻病媪询曰：“若来乎？收贮宜严，须防鼠子泼撒也。”盖在恍惚中，谓其妇归，而不意其弃麦先返。

    Explanation

    316

    The old lady waited a long time without seeing the girl enter, knowing that she was resolute and could not be moved. Watching her leave, she sent Qi to chase after her, but he could not catch up. The two became even more fearful and anxious. The old lady blamed Qi and said, "The eyes from yesterday cannot be covered, and the ears tonight cannot be covered either. To embarrass oneself in public, ultimately does no good for the big matters; you truly have misled me." Qi thought about it for a long time and said, "The matter is urgent! If not forcibly brought back, it cannot be undone. It was heard that Old Man Nie drank at a neighbor's house until dusk, was drunk and did not get up, and his son was also in the fields. He would not return tonight either. An old woman from that household, because of illness, had been sleeping for a long time. What can she do? Why don't you guide me there? Do as I wish and then stop." The old lady, in a hurry and unsure of what to do, followed him directly. When they arrived at Old Man Nie's house, still hesitating to enter due to fear, they quietly observed and found the place empty and quiet. The old lady, being familiar with the place, secretly opened the door and entered. She heard the sick old woman ask, "Have you come? Storage should be strict, and must guard against mice scattering things." She was in a daze, thinking her wife had returned, without realizing that she had returned earlier to discard the grain.

    317

    姑肖女声微应之。径达女房，灯犹未烬，知已就寝，低唤曰：“嫂何舍之遄归？予亦将之来矣。”女闻姑声，意其以麦至，坦然启扉。齐先入，姑始继之。女蓦地见齐，知怀恶意，大怖欲号。姑亟以手掩其吻，齐遂极力拥持，致之于榻，将行奸。女心甚恚，突以手爪刺其颊，血迹殷然。齐亦怒，两不相下，乃命姑持纤腕，而取床头败絮，置诸樱口，哑其声。女不能支，力亦少怯。齐大悦，将解其衣，女又撑持如前。幸衵服牢系，猝不可解，而无瑕之璧，深护椟中，青蝇不得遽玷。相持既久，姑力微疲，甫一纵，而女已堕地。两人又力致之，再登再堕，漏下已三。姑惧曰：“母将觅我，我宜归。

    Explanation

    317

    The aunt imitated the girl's voice and responded softly. She directly reached the girl's room, where the lamp was still lit, indicating that the girl had already gone to bed. She spoke softly, saying, "Why did you return so quickly, sister-in-law? I was also about to come." The girl, hearing her aunt's voice, thought that she had come with wheat and opened the door calmly. Qi entered first, followed by the aunt. The girl suddenly saw Qi and realized that he had malicious intentions, which terrified her greatly. The aunt quickly covered her mouth with her hand, and Qi then forcefully held her down and placed her on the bed, intending to commit a vile act. The girl was very angry and suddenly scratched his cheek with her nails, causing blood to appear. Qi was also angry, and neither of them gave in. He then ordered the aunt to hold the girl's wrists and took some old rags from the bedside, placing them in her mouth to silence her. The girl could no longer resist and became slightly weaker. Qi was greatly pleased and was about to undress her, but the girl resisted again as before. Fortunately, her undergarments were tightly fastened and could not be undone immediately, and the unblemished jade remained well-protected in the box, beyond the reach of the green fly. After holding each other for a long time, the aunt grew slightly tired and let go, and the girl fell to the ground. The two of them then forced her again, causing her to fall and rise repeatedly. It was already past three o'clock. The aunt was afraid and said, "My mother will come looking for me; I should return."

    318

    岂此时麦犹未尽耶？”齐究不甘，不再致女于榻，将席地而就之，少遂其私。女手足力尽，无少余。齐势益猛，且探手纤腰，欲断其带。女忿气中激，反较前愈暴，乘姑松懈，十指自如，又突起以柔荑掌其面，并伤及眼眶眉棱骨。齐觉痛不可当，亟释女，反身欲行。已而恨恨曰：“婢子无情乃尔！”心实不平，又遽反踢之，以足中其肋。女亦负痛不嘶，姑乃劝之，又去女口中阿堵，温慰数言，然后与齐俱归。盖知事已决裂，聊借此修补者也。乃女虽未重伤，心则火炽，将起而就榻，又病莫能兴，困瘁支离，惨难言状。

    Explanation

    318

    Is it that the wheat is not yet harvested at this time?” Qi was still unwilling, so he did not bring the woman to the bed, but instead lay on the ground and proceeded with it, slightly satisfying his private desires. The woman was physically and emotionally drained, with no strength left. Qi's aggression grew more intense, and he reached out to her slender waist, wanting to cut her belt. The woman, suddenly ignited with anger, became even more violent than before, taking advantage of her husband's relaxation, her fingers moving freely, and she suddenly slapped her face with her soft fingers, injuring her eye sockets and eyebrow bones. Qi felt the pain was unbearable, and quickly released the woman, turning around and wanting to leave. He then spoke resentfully, “The maidservant is so heartless!” His heart was really not at peace, so he suddenly kicked her, hitting her ribs with his foot. The woman, suffering pain but not crying out, was then comforted by her wife, who removed the thing in her mouth, saying several comforting words, and then they all returned together. Knowing that the matter had been irreparably damaged, they just used this to patch things up. Although the woman was not seriously injured, her heart was burning with intense emotional distress. She was about to get up and go to bed, but the illness prevented her from getting up, and she was exhausted and emaciated, too sad to describe.

    319

    有顷，闻门前剥啄声，旋即径入，大吐狼藉，且责问夜户不闭故。则其翁醉卧比闾，邻人往呼聂子，扶掖而归；因而在田者又将入室。脱令齐与姑少延顷刻，亦邂逅相遭，谓非天哉？聂子侍父寝，遂归己室，吽吽然，犹以启闭不严为怒。及入户而视之，见女卧地上，云鬓蓬飞，花容揾土，乃失色。惊询其由，时女已能言，因缕缕叙述其事。聂闻之，勃然震怒，初亦愤不能平，及徐以手探女衣，带其褊矣，固未绝也。聂素迂，默然内念，转以生疑，反不以完赵为功，而竟以诳楚为惑。卒然问曰：“若妇人也，遭一壮男子，庸讵能免？”女初俟夫至，得诉之以鸣其冤，突闻此言，气填胸臆。

    Explanation

    319

    After a short while, he heard a light knocking at the door, and soon afterward, the person entered directly, leaving a mess behind and scolding for not closing the door at night. It turned out that his father had been drunk and was sleeping in the neighbor's house, and a neighbor went to call him, helping him back home; as a result, those in the fields were about to enter the house. If the husband and wife had just delayed for a moment, they would have met by chance, and one might say it was not fate. The protagonist waited on his father until he fell asleep, then returned to his own room, still angry, blaming the door for not being properly closed. When he entered the room and saw the woman lying on the ground, her hair disheveled, her face covered in dirt, he was shocked. He asked her what had happened, and at that time, the woman could already speak, so she narrated the events in detail. Upon hearing this, the protagonist became suddenly very angry, unable to calm down due to his anger. However, when he gently felt the woman's clothing, he found the belt was loose, not completely cut off. The protagonist was always indirect in his thinking, silently reflecting inwardly, and started to doubt. Instead of believing that the woman had been saved by a strong man, he ended up being deceived by a lie. Suddenly, he asked, "If you are a woman, how could you avoid encountering a strong man?" The woman had initially waited for her husband to arrive, hoping to tell him and clear her name, but upon hearing this, she felt her breath fill her chest and heart with intense emotion.

    320

    乃复曰：“妾果不免，君何以知之？”聂忿然作色曰：“天下惟处子可辨其贞淫，汝曹既已适人，则不能矣。衣服附在汝身，尚宜慎之，矧带居隐微之地，今乃不绝如缕，自谓能免，谁其信之？”女益恚，因曰：“若之姨妹濒我于危，我力御强暴，为若守此全躯，乃反谤我耶？”聂闻女言至此，亟摇腕止之曰：“此母之至戚也，汝何倡言若是？予亦老吏，直以斯带断之矣。”女因负屈，极口与争。聂终以带为论，且曰：“持此以示路人，渠言无疑，予又何疑哉？”其坚僻如是。女以不平之气，更被此不白之冤，情难隐忍，乃举姑之名而痛詈之，至于流涕。

    Explanation

    320

    He then said, "If I am indeed doomed, how do you know this?" Nie, suddenly angry, changed his expression and said, "In the world, only a virgin can be distinguished between chastity and licentiousness. Since you women have already been married, you cannot. Since clothes are attached to your body, you should still be cautious. Let alone the belt, which is in a hidden and subtle place; now it is barely holding on. If you claim to be able to escape, who will believe you?" The woman became even more angry and said, "Your aunt and sister put me in danger. I defended myself against violence and protected my entire body for you. Now you are defaming me?" When Nie heard the woman's words to this point, he quickly shook his wrist to stop her and said, "This is a close relative of your mother. Why are you speaking so boldly? I am an old official, and I will simply decide this case based on this belt." The woman felt wronged and argued fiercely. Nie was so stubborn and said, "Show this to passersby. They will have no doubt, and why should I doubt it?" The woman, filled with indignation and suffering from this unjust accusation, could not restrain her emotions. She then raised her grandmother's name and cursed her fiercely, even shedding tears.

    321

    聂既惧母闻，又素以爱护母党为孝，见女丑诋其短，愈大怒，即取室中短梃，将以挞之。女见其用武，不胜愤激，言益不熊少默，吁屈吁天，比邻皆闻。而醉中之翁，病中之媪，则固不能不觉也。聂因女不屈己，挞之十数。忽闻父醒，厉声诘问，乃舍女趋出，约略述之，而独隐姑之一节。翁遂大赞曰：“明鉴哉，予之子也。否则几为所蔽哉！”女闻翁妪已寤，欲出陈诉，而负伤，起立不能，因匍匐出室，自牖而泣白之。语稍侵姑，媪即发声叱之曰：“是何言耶！二姑一女子，不日将以字人，汝以片言误人终身，能无深痛我心哉？”乃作呻吟之态，谓其子曰：“牵之去，勿呕老妇。

    Explanation

    321

    Nèi, fearing that his mother would hear, and always considering showing love and care for the mother's relatives as filial piety, saw the daughter slander his shortcomings and became even angrier. He immediately took a short stick from the room and was going to beat her with it. The daughter, seeing him use force, could not bear the intense anger and frustration; her words became more and more intolerable and silent, and she cried out to heaven in distress, and the neighbors all heard. The drunk old man and the sick old woman, however, could not help but notice. Nèi, because the daughter refused to submit to him, beat her several times. Suddenly, he heard the father wake up and asked sternly. He then left the girl and rushed out, briefly recounting the incident, but only hid the part about the grandmother. The old man then greatly praised, saying, "How wise you are, my son indeed. Otherwise, you would have been deceived by her!" The daughter, hearing that the old man and old woman had woken up, wanted to go out and report it, but was injured and could not stand up. So she crawled out of the room and cried and confessed through the window. Her words slightly criticized the grandmother, and the old woman immediately shouted and scolded her, saying, "What are you saying! Two aunts and one girl, soon she will be married off. You with a single word have ruined her life, how could it not deeply hurt my heart?" She then made a groaning expression and told her son, "Take her away, do not make the old woman vomit."

    322

    ”翁又嘱曰：“吾儿大义深明，予无多赘，盍善处之，勿令儿女情长也。”言已，遂寂然。女犹哀陈，聂恐大伤父母心，曳以入室，拳杖交加，且以断带为据，令女诬服。女既遭齐踢伤，又复试聂毒手，创深于外，气结于中，遂渐不能堪。乃大呼曰：“聂某，天日在上，予不负汝，汝诚负予矣！”竟瞑目不语。视之，则已气绝矣！既而大怖，自揣无罪毙妻，律应论抵。于是狡计顿生，周视屋宇，左侧之壁将颓，又以砖石堆砌者，乃扶女尸于其下，亟出室外，推而仆之，始往白其父母。

    Explanation

    322

    The father then instructed, "My son has deeply understood the great righteousness; I have nothing more to add. Why not handle it well, and avoid excessive sentimentality." After speaking, he fell silent. The woman continued to plead her case, but Nie, fearing that this would deeply hurt his parents' feelings, dragged her into the room and beat her with fists and sticks. He also used a broken belt as evidence, forcing her to falsely confess. The woman, having suffered multiple kicks, was further subjected to Nie's cruel treatment. Her injuries were severe both externally and internally, and she gradually could no longer endure. She then cried out, "Nie, with the heavens as witnesses, I have not wronged you; you have truly wronged me!" She closed her eyes and remained silent. When they looked at her, she had already lost her breath. Fearing greatly, Nie convinced himself that he had killed his wife without cause, and according to the law, he should be punished. Thus, a cunning plan formed in his mind. He carefully examined the house and noticed that the wall on the left side was about to collapse, and it was constructed with bricks and stones. He placed the woman's body under it, quickly went outside, pushed and made it fall, and then went to report to his parents.

    323

    翁媪莫之哀，而反盛称其子之智，则其别具肺肠，可知已。昧爽，即驰报岳家，阖门悲泣。独女之父侧首曰：“秋无淫雨，何至坏垣？宜亟往视之。”及至，号啕而入。始起土，展视女尸，则为破壁所压，疮痍辨识良难。惟二目荧然，泪犹含眶，其为生前受创，证据昭然。女父亦不遽声言，痛哭一番，反慰安其婿而后去。即日就控于官，翁父子知之，亦补牍申诉。官拘邻佑研讯，有五夜梦回闻女声之悲惨者，即据实以对，盖亦聂父子所不相能之故也。官诣翁家，命检化者，无如肢体糜烂，几莫能明。仵人有谢二者，精于其术，指出木石各伤之异，并生前死后受创之殊，证以洗冤录，无不吻合。

    Explanation

    323

    The elderly couple did not mourn for her, but instead highly praised their son's intelligence, indicating that they had ulterior motives. At dawn, they hastened to report to the in-laws, and the entire family wept bitterly. The daughter's father nodded and said, "If there was no heavy rain, why would the wall be damaged? One should hasten to go and see it." Upon arriving, they entered crying loudly. They began digging and examined the body of the girl, who had been crushed by the broken wall. The wounds were difficult to identify. Only her eyes still glowed, with tears still in them, clearly showing that she was injured before her death. The daughter's father did not immediately declare, but cried bitterly once, then comforted his son-in-law and left. That same day, they filed a complaint with the authorities. The grandfather and his son also submitted a document to appeal. The official arrested neighbors and witnesses to investigate. There was someone who, five nights ago, awoke from a dream and heard the girl's sad and pitiful voice, and he then responded based on the truth, which is because the Nie father and son did not get along. The official ordered an inspection of the dead body, but the limbs were so rotten that almost nothing could be identified. There was a coroner named Xie who was skilled in his craft. He pointed out the differences between injuries caused by wood and stone, and also between injuries sustained before and after death, based on the Washing Away of Wrongs, and everything matched perfectly.

    324

    官乃以严刑鞫聂子，得其致死之由，而究不肯言逼奸之事，人亦无从知之，亦几于天庇淫人，使二凶得以漏网矣。案既定，以聂子殴妻至死，又不以罪，合论绞，下诸狱中。聂翁深痛其子，不免漏言，少露二姑之秽。其父素强悍，闻而大恚。初犹以翁为污蠛，及徐察之，见姑时立闾间，与一男子相嘲笑，则即齐也，遂大疑。夜起前往掩执，二人果并头高卧，绝类倡随。忿极，即踰窗而入，手刃其女，并杀齐。及见二尸裸然，乃益愤懑，斩为数段，携首赴县，具白其由。比见官，突然愦乱作女声涕泣曰：“妾今得假手屠仇，死无憾矣。然不可竟尔泯没也。

    Explanation

    324

    The official then interrogated Nèi Zǐ with severe punishment, discovering the reason for his death, but he ultimately refused to speak about the forced sexual assault, leaving others unable to know the truth. It almost seemed as if heaven was protecting the immoral, allowing the two criminals to escape punishment. The case was decided, charging Nèi Zǐ with killing his wife, but he was not found guilty, still sentenced to hanging and imprisoned. Nèi Wáng deeply grieved for his son, could not help but reveal some information, slightly exposing the uncle's uncleanness. His father, usually strong and tough, heard this and became very angry. At first, he thought the grandfather was dirty, but upon careful investigation, he saw the uncle standing in the gate, laughing with a man, who was Qi, and thus became greatly suspicious. He got up at night to go and catch them. The two were indeed sleeping together, very much like a couple. In great anger, he jumped through the window, killing his daughter with his own hands and also killed Qi. When he saw the two bodies naked, he became even more angry, cutting them into several pieces, took their heads to the county, and explained the reason. When he saw the official, suddenly confused, he cried out in a woman's voice, saying, "I can now take revenge on my enemy. I will have no regrets in death. But I cannot let this go unnoticed."

    325

    ”遂当庭倾吐其冤，并齐与姑谋奸之状，一一缅诉，无少遗。官役皆骇，署之内外，观者如堵。言讫顿仆，仍复其旧，而人之聆之者，靡不怒发上指已。官又提聂子鞫问，始肯言姑所为。而己实疑信参半，且因母族之恶，无敢暴白，故隐讳至今，殊不谓死者犹多此哓哓也。官闻其言，因笑曰：“汝诚愚孝，然法之所在，身命俱捐，又岂全躯事亲者所忍为乎？”聂子乃号泣伏罪。官以事涉荒诞，且齐与姑已授首，竟薄责二姑之父，存案逐出。而聂子之死罪，究以莫逭。明年，乡人请于宫，为女立祠。岁时祷祀，颇著灵佑之名。余既得其概，爰作长歌悼之。限于篇幅，不能具载。然女之大节，足与日月争光矣。是为传。

    Explanation

    325

    During the trial, he then poured out his grievances in court, detailing the plot with his aunt and his mother, recounting every detail without omission. The officials were all shocked, and inside and outside the office, the spectators were like a wall. After speaking, he suddenly collapsed and returned to his original state, and all who heard him were so angry that their hair stood on end. The official then questioned the son of the mother, and he finally admitted to the mother's actions. However, he himself was uncertain, and due to the disgrace of his mother's family, he dared not reveal the truth, so he had concealed it until now, not expecting that the dead would still be so vocal. The official heard his words and laughed, saying, "You are truly a foolish filial son, but where law is concerned, both life and body are sacrificed; how could someone who preserves their body to serve their parents bear to do such a thing?" The son of the mother then wept and confessed. The official, considering the matter to be fantastical and that the aunt and mother had already been executed, lightly blamed the father of the two aunts, kept the case on file, and expelled them. As for the son of the mother, he could not escape his death sentence. The following year, the villagers petitioned the palace to build a temple for the girl. Prayers and sacrifices were made regularly, and the temple became famous for its divine protection. I have learned the general outline and have composed a long song to mourn her. Limited by the length, I cannot fully record it. However, her great virtues are worthy of competing with the sun and moon in brilliance. This is the account.

    326

    外史氏曰：余初见是女，温柔娇小，似不宜铮铮如是。及闻其事，益慕其人。古所谓贞姬烈嫒，其必非无盐嫫母也可知。故又曰：节之苦者，不于常而于变。女而誓不二夫，不少概见。惟此诱之以利若弗见，动之以欲若弗闻，且惕之以威若弗惧者，为闺阁中之不易有也。孰谓得妇如此，竟甘心毙之而不惜？则传其事者为无罪，而闻其人者亦足以风矣。

    ○　狐妪
    　　乐部掌祠祭之事，虽巡狩而亦扈从，盖亦以所过山川，并古帝先师之祀焉。旗员某公，职隶太常，号曰赞礼郎，六品秩也。辛未大驾南巡，公与同署三人皆备其选，随驾回銮抵济上。暮宿于民家，其居停亦巨族，宅第轩敞，有厅事五楹，扃之不以寓客。公问焉，则答曰：“有仙居之，无敢扰。”叩其踪迹，实狐耳，公与僚友皆大噱。时正清和下旬，天气喧热，殊不可耐，以其庭屋高爽，竟不听主人言，破扉而入。

    326

    **Historian's Commentary: I first saw this woman, gentle and delicate, seemingly not suited to be so resolute. Upon hearing her story, I admired her even more. The virtuous and chaste women of old, it is known that they were not unattractive. Therefore, it is said: The difficulty of maintaining virtue lies not in the ordinary but in the extraordinary. A woman who swears never to have a second husband is not commonly seen. Only those who are enticed with benefits but as if not seen, stirred by desire but as if not heard, and warned with threats but as if not feared, are not easily found among women. Who would say that getting such a wife would make one willing to kill her without regret? Those who transmit her story are blameless, and those who hear of her are also inspired by her virtue.

    ○ Fox Spirit
    Music officials are in charge of sacrificial rites, and even during imperial tours, they accompany the emperor, as they also oversee the worship of mountains and rivers and ancient emperors and teachers. A certain flag officer, whose rank is under the Taichang, is known as a Zanli Lang, a sixth-rank official. During the year of the Metal Goat, when the imperial entourage toured the south, this officer and three others from his office were selected to accompany the emperor. They returned to Jining with the imperial entourage. In the evening, they stayed at a civilian's home, which was a prominent family, with a spacious mansion and a hall with five rooms, locked and not intended for guests. When asked, the host replied, "There is a fairy residing here, and we dare not disturb them." When asked about their whereabouts, it turned out to be a fox, and the officer and his colleagues all laughed. It was the end of the pleasant and warm month, and the weather was extremely hot, making it unbearable. Because the courtyard and house were high and cool, they ignored the host's words and broke open the door to enter.

    327

    视之，虽无床幔，装饰精洁，乃大悦。主人力阻再四，究不见从，呼仆假悬榻数张，并设于内，相与呼卢浮白，尽醉而卧。其二人胆少怯，不安于中，托言畏暑，陈卧具于庑下。惟公与一友，宴然就枕，一眠于中庭，一栖于西侧，盖其屋五，区而为三故也。公寝至夜分，酒力微醒，忽觉卧榻摇摇，肢体震撼。初亦不甚介意，已而床忽自起，乃大骇。睨之，有四人侏儒，短小青衣，各执床之一足，以力举之。积渐而高，几与屋梁相埒。公大惧，隐忍不言。无何，上抵屋顶。

    Explanation

    327

    He observed the room, and though there were no bed curtains, the decoration was meticulously clean, and he was greatly pleased. The host strongly opposed it repeatedly, but ultimately did not comply. He called the servant to bring several temporary beds, and set them up inside. They took turns playing dice and drinking wine, drinking themselves to intoxication and then lay down. The other two were timid and felt uneasy inside, pretending to fear the heat, and arranged their bedding under the eaves. Only the official and one friend calmly went to bed, one sleeping in the central courtyard, one on the western side, because the five rooms were divided into three areas. The official slept until midnight, slightly sober from the wine, when he suddenly felt the bed shaking, his limbs were shaken. At first, he did not pay much attention, but then the bed suddenly rose on its own, and he was greatly frightened. He glanced at it, and saw four dwarfs, small and dressed in blue, each holding one leg of the bed and lifting it with force. Gradually rising higher, almost reaching the height of the ceiling, the official was greatly frightened and bit his tongue, not speaking. Soon after, it reached the roof.

    328

    其槅均以板制成，月光之下，丹臒灿然，面之相去，其间不能以寸。公方虑舁者释手，而细语嘈杂，果商所以扑之。屋高数仞，陨越实为可忧。正仓惶间，俄见屋上有小间，豁然洞开，中一妪，年可六旬，高髻白发，衣褐色衫，挂念珠，半露其身，视公而笑。亟叱曰：“儿辈勿恶作剧！诸公皆从天子至此，风尘鞅掌者速诣故处，宁一夕亦不可忍耶？”四人闻其言，无敢违逆。榻渐低，去上渐远，良久始复其故。

    Explanation

    328

    The compartments are all made of boards, and under the moonlight, the red and black colors shine brightly. The distance between them is no more than an inch. The official was just worrying that the bearers would let go, when the soft and noisy chatter, the fruit merchants were using it to cover up. The house is several ren high, and falling is truly a cause for concern. Just as he was in a state of panic, suddenly a small room on the roof was suddenly open, and there was an old woman in the middle, about sixty years old, with a high bun and white hair, wearing a brown robe, with prayer beads, half of her body exposed. She looked at the official and smiled. She quickly scolded, "You children don't play tricks! All of you have come here from the emperor, those who are tired from the dust should quickly go back to your original place, can you not even bear one night?" The four people heard her words and dared not disobey. The bed gradually lowered, the distance from above gradually increased, and after a long time it returned to its original state.

    329

    公觉榻已至地，仅著一裤，不及觅衣，白足而奔出。出户即大呼，两友及仆辈皆惊寤，急起询之。公为具言其状，汗流浃背，众皆大笑。未几，西侧卧者亦奔出，大呼，众视之，面涂浓墨如厉鬼，益为捧腹。令其自白，则亦妪为戒止，妪侧一少女，色殊忿忿，因以掌击其面，淋漓如浆，初不意其为墨也。语次以衣自拭，惊定而笑。亟命仆掌火以入，移榻于外。甫少憩，天已达旦，遂束装而行，亦不复见主人，怀惭自去。公归，每举以告人，且曰：“非藉圣主威灵，吃跌当不小。”

    　　外史氏曰：陈蕃设榻，以待徐孺，未闻既至反悬之者。狐儿慢客，且欲扑之，无礼一至于此！赖妪以片言解纷，不至获罪于嘉客，可称贤母。足与陶公堂上之老，并垂不朽矣！

    ○　二编

    Explanation

    329

    The public awoke to find the bed had fallen to the ground, wearing only one pant, and not having time to find clothes, he ran out barefoot. Upon stepping out, he shouted loudly, and his two friends and servants all woke up in alarm, quickly rising to ask him what had happened. He explained the situation in detail, drenched in sweat, and everyone burst into laughter. Soon after, the person who had been lying on the west side also ran out, shouting, and the crowd looked at him, seeing his face smeared with thick ink, looking like a fierce ghost, which made them laugh even more. When he was asked to explain, it turned out that the old woman had tried to stop him, and beside her was a young girl who looked unusually angry, so she struck him in the face with her palm, causing the ink to spread like porridge, and he did not initially realize it was ink. While speaking, he wiped himself with his clothes, calmed down, and laughed. He immediately ordered the servant to bring fire inside and moved the bed outside. After a brief rest, the sky had already dawned, so he packed his belongings and left, without seeing the host again, feeling ashamed and departing on his own. When he returned home, he often recounted the incident to others, saying, "Without the divine power of the sage ruler, the fall would have been much worse."

    The historian commented: Chen Fan set aside a bed to welcome Xu Ru, but there is no record of him hanging the bed up once Xu Ru had arrived. The fox child was rude to the guest and even wanted to strike him, showing a level of disrespect! Thanks to the old woman's few words, the dispute was resolved, and he did not offend the distinguished guest, which can be called the virtue of a wise mother. She can be compared to the wise elder on Tao Gong's hall, both of whom will be remembered forever!

    330

    序
    　　且天地大矣，四海九州广矣，人物之形形色色、怪怪奇奇繁且赜矣。目非瞆而似瞆者，动谓以目所亲见者为真，将天地之大，四海九州之广，尽纷呈于吾目耶？耳非聋而似聋者，动谓以耳所亲闻者为实，将形形色色、怪怪奇奇之繁且赜，尽交集于吾耳耶？浸假而纷呈于吾目，殆熟视若无睹耶。浸假而交集于吾耳，殆习听若不察耶。呜呼，噫嘻！庸耳俗目之交，殆不足语以天地之大，四海九州之广，形形色色、怪怪奇奇之繁且赜邪。长白浩歌子有《萤窗异草》一书出焉。其思入窈冥者，可断以理之所必无也；其言归讽谕者，可信为情之所或有也。初编既印行问世，而泉唐友人又函示二编。吾不知见之者咋舌凡几辈耶；吾不知闻之者蹙额凡几人耶；又不知谓为真、谓为实者，持亲见亲闻以语人咋舌蹙额者，更不少耶；又不知广大繁赜者，将尽泯没于咋舌蹙额之流耶。呜呼，噫嘻！光绪三年，岁次丁丑，犹清和月中，浣古沪缕馨仙史序于铸铁庵之南窗。

    Explanation

    330

    Preface

    The world is vast, and the four seas and nine provinces are expansive, with a myriad of diverse and strange phenomena. Those who, though not blind, act as if they are, often believe what they see with their own eyes is true. Will the vastness of the world and the expansiveness of the four seas and nine provinces all be presented to my eyes? Those who, though not deaf, act as if they are, often believe what they hear with their own ears is true. Will the myriad of diverse and strange phenomena all be heard by my ears? Suppose they were presented to my eyes, would I still ignore them due to familiarity? Suppose they were heard by my ears, would I still fail to understand them due to habit? Alas, indeed! The common people, with their ordinary ears and eyes, are certainly unable to comprehend the vastness of the world, the expansiveness of the four seas and nine provinces, and the myriad of diverse and strange phenomena. Long Bai Haogezǐ has produced a book titled Yingchuang Yicao (Strange Tales from the Lamp Window). Those stories that delve into the depths of the unknown can be judged as what reason dictates must not exist; those that return to admonition can be believed as what emotion or reality might have. The first edition has already been published, and a friend from Quantan has sent me the second edition. I do not know how many people are shocked or amazed by it; I do not know how many people are displeased by it; nor do I know how many believe it to be true, holding that they have personally seen or heard it and telling others, causing them to be shocked or displeased; nor do I know if the vast and complex will be lost to those who are shocked or displeased. Alas, indeed! In the year of Dingchou during the Guangxu era, in the fifth month of the lunar calendar, Xuan Shi, pen name Huluxin, wrote this preface in the south window of Zhu Tie Temple.

    331

    ○　卷一
    　　潇湘公主
    　　侯鼐，字仲鼎，南阳人，倜傥少年也，与同邑邵生相友善。邵故贫，惟仲鼎深知之，二人遂为管鲍交。学文不就，同入武庠，盖皆仲鼎之力焉。仲鼎有胞舅莅任湖襄，因署中经理乏人，驰书召之。仲鼎将往而虑邵无以资生，予以五十缗，嘱曰：“以此为弓马费，善自肄业。来年兄归，可同赴武闱，夺锦标也”。邵涕泣送之，颜色惨淡，若悲不自胜者。仲鼎为之怏然，凄凄就道，恒以邵生为念。抵舅任所，衙务纷如乱丝，仲鼎一一条理之，半载甫能就绪。因思邵，且欲为图南举，力请归省，舅不得已乃遣之。

    Explanation

    331

    Final Translation

    Chapter One
    Princess of Xiangxiang

    Hou Dai, courtesy name Zhongding, was from Nanyang. He was a free-spirited young man who became close friends with his fellow townsman Shao Sheng. Shao was poor, but only Hou Dai truly understood him, and the two became as close as Guan Zhong and Bao Shuya. They failed in their studies and together entered the military academy, which was due to Hou Dai's efforts. Hou Dai had a maternal uncle who served in Huxiang, and because the office was short of managers, he sent a letter summoning Hou Dai. As Hou Dai was about to go, he worried that Shao would have no means to support himself, so he gave him fifty strings of cash and said, "Use this money for archery and horseback riding expenses, and study diligently. Next year when I return, we can take the military examination together and win the championship." Shao wept and sent him off, his face pale and gloomy, as if he could not bear the sorrow. Hou Dai felt disappointed and left sadly, always thinking of Shao. Upon arriving at his uncle's post, the official duties were as chaotic as tangled threads, but Hou Dai sorted them out one by one, and after half a year, they were finally organized. He then thought of Shao and wanted to plan for a future success, so he strongly requested to return home to visit his family. His uncle, unable to refuse, finally sent him away.

    332

    仲鼎素轻财，与皆不受，乃以一僮一剑相随，囊橐萧然，绝不类已饱猪肝者。抵淮上，避风泊于小港。是夕，月明如水，气肃若秋。仲鼎方倚窗闲眺，适有艨艟巨舰，逆流而南，行甚缓。舟中方夜宴，箫管嗷嘈，娇歌宛转。疑为瓜步巨家，殊不介意。无何，已至舟前，忽闻人大声语曰：“月色甚佳，何为行也？亦当停泊于此。”言未竟，诺声如雷，船遂止。仲鼎聆其音，绝类邵，盖时时在心者，故一触即动。有顷，笙歌顿止，皕首严声，有人先出辟除曰：“贵主同粉侯出观江景，盍避之。”船中一时皆肃然。须臾，异香馥郁，飘越邻舟，扑入脑髓。

    Explanation

    332

    Zhong Ding had always been frugal with money, and he and his companion both refused to accept anything. They traveled with only one servant and one sword, their belongings sparse, and they did not look like someone who had just eaten a lot of pork liver. They reached the Huai River and sought shelter in a small harbor to avoid the wind. That night, the moonlight was clear and bright, like water, and the atmosphere was solemn and cool, like autumn. Zhong Ding was leaning by the window, leisurely looking around, when suddenly there was a large warship sailing upstream towards the south, moving very slowly. There was a banquet on board the ship during the night, and the sound of flutes and pipes was loud and noisy, with sweet and melodious singing. Zhong Ding thought the ship belonged to a wealthy family from Guabu and did not pay much attention to it. Soon after, the ship arrived in front of Zhong Ding's boat. Suddenly, he heard someone speak loudly: "The moonlight is beautiful, why are you moving? You should also anchor here." Before the speaker finished, there was a loud response like thunder, and the ship then stopped. Zhong Ding recognized the voice as very similar to Shao's, because Shao was always on his mind, so at the first touch, he recognized him. After a while, the music and singing suddenly stopped. A loud and strict voice said, "Someone came out first to clear the way and said, 'The noble lady and her attendant are coming out to view the river scenery. Why don't you move away?'" Everyone on the ship became very quiet. Shortly after, a strong and pleasant fragrance filled the air, spreading to the neighboring boat and even entering the brain.

    333

    旋见纱笼数对，自舱中出，望之状如列星。即有紫衣贵人，乌巾犀带，俨古之王侯，携二八娇艳，宫装如画图，容夺皎月之光，色甚姝丽。侍婢十数，皆衣锦绣，设小榻，累席重裀，二人乃并肩而坐。仲鼎遥睹良久，知为鬼神。然谛观之下，紫衣人面容举止酷肖邵生，不禁大惊曰：“吾弟其死乎？”益因之注目。未几，丽者凝睇瞥见侯舟，骇曰：“俗客在此，胡不早言，使人窥见宫仪？从者当有罪。”乃艴然，俱起入舱。未久，有人厉声询曰：“泊者何人？”舟子代答曰：“南阳侯相公，将归故乡耳。”其人惊曰：“是吾家都尉桑梓也。

    333

    Soon after, several pairs of lanterns emerged from the cabin, appearing like a row of stars when looked at. There was a high-ranking official dressed in purple robes, wearing a black headband and a belt made of rhinoceros horn, resembling ancient kings and princes. He was accompanied by two young and beautiful women, dressed in royal attire as exquisite as a painting, their beauty rivaling the light of the full moon, and they were very charming and lovely. Several dozen attendants, all dressed in brocade and embroidery, set up small couches with multiple layers of mats and thick cushions, and the two sat side by side. Zong Ding observed from afar for a long time, knowing they were spirits. Upon closer inspection, the person in purple robes had a face and demeanor very similar to Shao Sheng, and he could not help but exclaim in surprise, "Is my brother dead?" And thus, he paid closer attention. Shortly afterward, the beautiful woman cast a glance and saw the nobleman's boat, exclaiming in shock, "An ordinary guest is here, why didn't you say so earlier, allowing someone to see the royal court? The attendants should be punished." They became angry, and they all stood up and went back into the cabin. Not long after, someone asked sternly, "Who is moored here?" The boatman replied on behalf, "It is Duke NanYang, returning to his hometown." The person exclaimed in surprise, "He is from the same hometown as my family's general."

    334

    ”即入禀复。旋有貂珰二人，来诘里阀，仲鼎隔舟告。紫衣人遽出船头，大呼曰：“吾兄今日始还乎？固不意弟在此也。”仲鼎审视，果邵生，益愕然。邵请过舟，仲鼎从之。比入舱中，则异采夺目，奇芬袭衣。窗横孔雀之屏，座隐芙蓉之褥，备极人世华侈。仲鼎未及发声，邵一挥，金镛大作，瑶笙锦瑟，急管繁弦，嘈杂乎一时虽有言亦如充耳矣。邵乃施裀再拜。礼甫毕，玉磬一声，八音俱寂，即伶工亦不知其安在。乐阕，仲鼎始得展询，详诘其由。邵微笑不答，惟命肆筵相待曰：“且尽一夕之欢，勿言旧事，使我故人倍增忉怛也。

    Explanation

    334

    Zhong Ding immediately reported back. Soon, two officials came to inquire about the family background, and Zhong Ding reported from across the boat. A man in purple robes suddenly stepped onto the boat's prow and shouted loudly: "Is my elder brother returning today? I didn't expect to find you here." Zhong Ding looked closely and found it was indeed Shao Sheng, even more astonished. Shao invited him over to the boat, and Zhong Ding followed. Upon entering the cabin, the unusual brilliance was dazzling, and the strange fragrance permeated the clothes. The windows were adorned with peacock screens, and the seats were hidden under lotus-patterned cushions, displaying the utmost luxury of the world. Before Zhong Ding could speak, Shao waved his hand, and the golden bell rang loudly, followed by the sound of jade flutes, embroidered zithers, rapid pipes, and intricate strings, creating a cacophony that made any words seem like mere noise. Shao then laid out a mat and bowed twice. As soon as the ceremony was completed, a jade chime sounded once, and all the sounds fell silent, even the musicians did not know where they had gone. When the music ended, Zhong Ding could finally ask questions and inquire in detail. Shao smiled without answering, only inviting him to sit down and saying: "Let's enjoy this night's joy, don't talk about past events, it will only make my old friend more sorrowful."

    335

    ”仲鼎益疑，固询之，无如绮席张矣，珍错献矣，笾豆楚列，肴核旅陈，邵把盏为仲鼎寿，而乐音复发，其响益繁，前席者能不缄口哉？无已就坐，樽俎之品，率多不知其名，行酒皆宫监，仲鼎愈觉不安。邵又曰：“吾兄非他人，可令婢子来。”言次，外乐尽停，晶帘微启，早有丽姝十数辈，皆殊色，拨阮搊筝，敲金戛玉，各献厥技，奏于筵前。继又发绕梁之音，作惊鸿之舞，于是饮酒乐甚。而仲鼎则窃有弗愿也，乘间谓邵曰：“观止矣！尚有欲言，请辍雅奏。”邵即以素箑挥之，余音顿歇。仲鼎因促坐叩其颠末。

    Explanation

    335

    Zhong Ding grew increasingly doubtful and insisted on asking, but there was nothing to be done; the brocade mat was spread out, delicacies were presented, bowls and plates were neatly arranged, dishes and fruits were displayed in rows, Shao held a cup to toast Zhong Ding, and the music sounded again, its resonance becoming more complex. How could those seated in front not keep silent? If not, then sit down; the dishes on the table, most of which were unfamiliar, and the servers of wine were all palace attendants, making Zhong Ding feel increasingly uneasy. Shao then said, "My elder brother is not an ordinary person; let the maidservants come." As he spoke, the music outside ceased, the crystal curtain slightly opened, and soon there were ten or more beautiful women, all of extraordinary beauty, plucking the lute, strumming the zither, striking gold and jade, each displaying their skills before the banquet. Then came the lingering sound of music and the graceful dance of the startled crane, and thus the drinking and merrymaking reached a high point. However, Zhong Ding secretly had no desire for this, and taking advantage of a moment, he told Shao, "It is enough to see! There is still something I want to say, please stop the elegant music." Shao immediately waved a white fan, and the lingering sound suddenly stopped. Zhong Ding then quickly sat down and asked about the beginning and end of the matter.

    336

    邵笑曰：“兄欲闻此，当满饮三大杯，弟与兄为竟夕谈。”语已，即飞一觥来，仲鼎慨然饮之。连尽三爵，曰：“杯罄矣，可得闻乎？”邵乃尽屏左右，只留二小鬟捧觞，己与仲鼎合席而釂。始自述曰：“弟与兄别后，窃思学剑读书，与吾兄成名当世。因厌城市烦嚣，徙居表兄之别墅。其地颇多竹木，且闲旷可为瞿圃，乃以兄所赐之半，构精室二楹而居之。昼则驰马试剑，夜则简练揣摩，诚吾人藏修之所也。今岁仲春既望，月色薄晦，暮霭冥冥。弟夜坐，正将秉烛宵读，俄闻牖外小语曰：‘贵人寝未？’音似巾帼中人。

    Explanation

    336

    Shao laughed and said, "If you wish to hear this, you should drink three large cups, and I will talk with you all night." After he spoke, he immediately sent a cup, and Zhong Ding drank it with a sigh. He drank three cups in succession and said, "The cup is empty, may I now hear it?" Shao then dismissed everyone around, leaving only two young maids to hold the cups, and joined Zhong Ding at the table and drank. He began to recount, "After parting with my brother, I secretly thought of learning swordplay and reading, to achieve fame with my brother in this world. Because I was tired of the noise and bustle of the city, I moved to my cousin's villa. The place had many bamboo and trees, and was spacious and suitable for a garden. I built two elegant rooms and lived there. During the day, I rode horses and practiced swordplay; at night, I studied and pondered. Truly, this is a place for cultivation and learning. This year, on the full moon of the second month, the moonlight was dim and hazy, and the evening mist was thick. At night, I was about to read by candlelight, when suddenly, I heard a small voice outside the window say, 'Have you not yet slept?' The voice sounded like that of a woman."

    337

    启视之，则中官数辈，皆著紫罗衫，谓弟曰：‘王与后将以公主下嫁，某等奉命特来粪除。’弟惊曰：‘王何人也？素昧平生，何所见而以棣华相属？’中官曰：‘衡山大帝，贵人岂不闻耶？’弟以生人而为神婿，语近不祥，乃固辞。中官竟弗听，排闼直入，铺设一新，然后去。弟入视居室，则书剑弓矢不知归于何处，唯有锦幔低垂，绣几罗列。今舱之所陈，半皆吾家之故物。向觉所居湫隘，一旦杂陈诸物，反憎其阔。且未审从何运入，即弟亦至今不解。祸福未卜，静以俟之。又许时，中官以毡囊至，出今之所服者，代弟服之，又去。

    Explanation

    337

    He opened and looked at it, and several eunuchs, all dressed in purple silk robes, told his younger brother, "The king and queen will arrange for their daughter to marry, and we have been specially sent to prepare everything." The younger brother was startled and asked, "Who is this king? I have never met him before, and what do you see that makes you think of us as close as brothers?" The eunuchs replied, "It is the Emperor of Mount Heng. Surely you have heard of him?" The younger brother, thinking it was unusual for a mortal to become a divine son-in-law, politely declined. However, the eunuchs did not listen and forcefully entered, setting everything up anew before leaving. The younger brother then entered the room and found that his books, swords, bows, and arrows were nowhere to be seen, but the silk curtains hung low and embroidered tables were arranged. Many of the items in the room were familiar to him. Previously, the place felt cramped, but now with all these items, he found it too spacious. He was also puzzled about how they had been brought in, as he still did not understand. Good or bad fortune was uncertain, so he calmly waited for it. After some time, the eunuchs returned with a felt bag, bringing out the clothes he was now wearing, and then left.

    338

    许时，引小鬟四人来，皆五采宫衣，执金莲炬，周视室内，相语曰：‘喜无武夫气，尚可以敌体凤台人。’言毕，含笑俱返。则又一中官，坌息而来报弟曰：‘请整衣，公主至矣。”邵语至此，又屏去小鬟，低告曰：“闺房猥亵之事，本不当秽清听，然兄知我者也，故不忍于自匿。”因接叙前词曰：“主将至，弟遥睇之，年可十四五，风姿绰约，天人也。从婢益伙，驾辎軿，张翠盖，威仪甚都。甫下车，中官令弟以臣礼迎谒，弟有准色。主旁一小鬟亟摇纤腕止之曰：‘王有后命，以驸马为阳世人主臣，即觐吾王，亦行主宾礼，况与公主为伉俪乎？’弟乃不拜，以匹敌礼相见。小鬟辈拥主入室，弟始与主对坐。近觇之，玉肌花貌，窈窕端凝，且羞涩不敢仰，俯首无一言。

    Explanation

    338

    After a while, four young maidservants came, all dressed in colorful court robes and holding golden lotus torches. They looked around the room and spoke to each other, saying, "We are glad there is no rough soldierly demeanor here; it is still suitable for someone of the Phoenix Platform." After they finished speaking, they smiled and all returned. Then another official came panting to report to the younger brother, saying, "Please dress properly; the princess has arrived." When Shao said this, he dismissed the maidservants and whispered, "The private and intimate matters of the inner chamber should not be heard by the ears of the pure. However, my brother understands me, so I cannot hide them from him." He then continued, "When the princess was about to arrive, I looked at her from afar. She was about fourteen or fifteen years old, with a graceful and charming appearance, like a heavenly being. She was accompanied by many maids, riding in a carriage with a green canopy, looking very dignified and impressive. As soon as she got off the carriage, the official ordered me to greet her with the formal etiquette of a subject, but I had a hesitant look. A young maid quickly waved her slender wrist to stop me, saying, 'The king has a subsequent command: to treat the prince as a subject of the earthly ruler. Even when meeting our king, we should perform the host-guest ceremony; how much more so when married to the princess?' I then did not bow, but met her with the equal courtesy. The maids escorted the princess into the room, and I then sat opposite her. Observing her closely, she had jade-like skin and a flower-like face, was graceful and composed, and shyly dared not look up, lowering her head without saying a word.

    339

    适几上有楮颖未及收，主微目之，小鬟即进曰：“主欲与粉侯夺文坛赤帜耶，恐赳赳仅能以矛戟见长，未易办此。’主乃微哂。弟恚其言，即搦管而起，大挥一绝曰：‘倚天长剑吐虹霓，一啸何难退鼓鼙。反笑终军无气概，仅能弱冠脱鸡栖。’弟盖自言其志也。主览之，似觉目笑，且与鬟耳语数四，鬟乃告弟曰：‘主云诗则佳矣，何催妆无丽句耶？’弟赧然，因命鬟请之于主。主不辞，即命笔自题一章。弟捧读之，盖和前韵曰：‘何事王姬驾彩霓，丈夫犹自志征鼙。封侯无骨君须鉴，且掷长缨入凤栖。’弟讽诵再三，益悦服。

    Explanation

    339

    Just as the paper and brush were still on the desk, the host glanced at them, and a young female servant immediately said, "Do you wish to challenge the red flag of the literary world with the gentleman, Master? But if you are only good at using spears and halberds, it will not be easy to handle this." The host then smiled slightly. The younger brother, angered by these words, picked up the brush and stood up, writing a poem with great vigor: "A long sword leans against the sky, emitting rainbow-like light; a single shout how difficult it is to drive back the drums and gongs. I laugh at the lack of courage in Zhong Jun, who can only escape from the chicken coop at the age of twenty." The younger brother was expressing his own aspirations through the poem. The host read it and seemed to smile with his eyes, then whispered to the young female servant several times. The servant then told the younger brother, "The host says the poem is good, but why is there no elegant line for the wedding poem?" The younger brother blushed and ordered the servant to ask the host for a poem. The host did not refuse and immediately wrote a chapter with his own hand. The younger brother read it respectfully, and it followed the previous rhyme: "What is the matter with a princess riding a colorful rainbow? A man still has the ambition to fight for victory. You must understand that being ennobled without backbone, and throw the long rope to enter the phoenix's nest." The younger brother recited it repeatedly, becoming increasingly pleased and convinced.

    340

    方倡随间，小鬟以红巾拂几，使弟与主并坐。珍馔自外入，其气蒸腾若新出于鼎。鬟以小犀杯系红丝一线，如合卺然。其酒色甚赤，饮之浓酽。小鬟语弟曰：‘此酒唯合欢用之，所谓潇湘之鸂鶒红也。’主不沾吻，弟亦不能引满。无何，玉漏三滴，中官入促鬟归。鬟乃代主脱簪珥，松礼衣，肃然告退。弟与主款洽无异于常人，第主性贵不苟言笑，闺阁中盖庶几焉。好合之余，始自述其本末。盖衡山大帝第四女，初封潇湘公主，年甫十五耳。侵晨，鬟即至，起居于榻前，为主整衣易履，仍捧之登舆而去。弟室中遂无长物，一仍其旧。

    Explanation

    340

    At the time when the husband and wife were in harmony, a young maid wiped the table with a red scarf and seated the brother and the princess side by side. Delicacies entered from outside, their aroma rising as if freshly cooked in a cauldron. The maid tied a small rhinoceros horn cup with a red thread, like the wine cup used in a wedding ceremony. The wine was very red, and it was strong and sweet. The maid said to the brother, "This wine is only suitable for the wedding, known as the 'Xiang River's Mandarin Duck Red.'" The princess did not taste it, and the brother could not finish his drink either. Soon, the third drop of the jade water clock fell, and a court official entered to urge the maid to return. The maid then undid the princess's jewelry, helped her change into her nightclothes, and respectfully bid farewell. The brother and the princess were close and harmonious, just like any ordinary couple. The princess, however, was noble and did not often smile or speak, making her somewhat aloof in the inner chambers. After enjoying their time together, the princess began to recount her background. She revealed that she was the fourth daughter of the Jade Emperor of Mount Heng, initially bestowed the title of Princess of the Xiang River, and was only fifteen years old.
