Early in the morning, the maid would arrive, attend to the princess's daily activities, help her dress, and escort her away in a carriage. By the time the brother returned to his room, everything remained as it was originally, with no changes.

    341

    即弟之冠服，亦不知其安往已。至夕复来，则不再乘车，中官亦不相随。唯小鬟三四辈夹侍之，主坐则共扶其肩，主行则互持其腋，以主之弱不胜衣也。主耽翰墨，典籍无不涉猎，尤长于阴符，谓得玄女之传，迥非仪秦所习。兼善手谈，弟当之，辄披靡。良夜相对，颇不岑寂。如是弥月而祸作。”仲鼎闻邵言至此，色变而起曰：“弟有何不测？”答曰“兄姑坐，待弟缅陈。弟自与主遇，饮食服遇仰给于岳家，日用既裕，不免稍侈。主每戒弟曰：‘慢藏诲盗，大易训之，君不可过疏。’弟不之信，自恃其武，犹肆为大言。

    Explanation

    341

    The younger brother's attire, even, is unknown where it has gone. By evening, he returns, no longer riding in a carriage, and without the eunuchs accompanying him. Only a few young maidservants attend to him. When the lady sits, they all support her shoulders; when she walks, they hold her underarms, for she is too frail to bear her own weight. The lady is deeply interested in writing and literature, knowledgeable in all classical texts, especially skilled in the "Yin Fu," claiming to have received teachings from the Xuan Nu, far surpassing the knowledge of the states of Qi and Qin. She is also adept at the game of Go, and her brother is no match for her, always losing. Pleasant evenings spent together alleviate loneliness. After a month, misfortune strikes. When Zhong Ding hears of this, he becomes pale and asks, "What danger has befallen my brother?" The younger brother replies, "Please sit, brother, and let me recount. Since meeting the lady, our daily needs are provided by her family, and our lives are comfortable, but we cannot afford to be overly extravagant. She often warns me, 'Carelessness invites theft, as the I Ching teaches. You must not be too lax.' I did not believe her, relying on my martial prowess, and made bold statements.

    342

    一夕，果有数贼入吾家，弟犹未寝，格之追奔。追诸野，杀一人，余皆遁去。及归，主适在室，谓弟曰：‘祸至矣！亟首之官，则可免。’叮嘱而去。弟以邑门既扃，坐以待旦。昧爽往视之，尸已无存。妄意群小欲脱累，故徙之以灭其迹，遂不关心，且锱铢无所失，亦何必履公门？事竟寝。主至夕不来，唯遗鬟寄以寸简曰：‘速至侯某处，事犹可为。’盖知兄与弟契，故令往投良友也。弟终不信，犹豫至中宵，弛然而卧。鸡鸣而捕人至，破扉坏室，势甚汹汹。弟疑盗来复仇，黑暗中又格杀其一。及呼乃觉，弟已无以自明矣。”邵言及此，仲鼎甚惊，毛发俱竦，瞿然曰：“弟无生路矣。

    Explanation

    342

    One night, indeed several bandits entered my home. My younger brother was still awake and fought them, chasing them into the wilds. He killed one and the rest fled. Upon returning, the master happened to be in the room and said to my brother, "Disaster has come! Quickly take your head to the authorities, and you may be spared." He gave instructions and left. My brother, seeing that the town gate was already closed, sat and waited until dawn. At dawn, he went to look, and the body was no longer there. He foolishly thought the small people wanted to escape blame, so they moved it to erase the trace. Thus, he no longer cared, and moreover, nothing was lost. Why should he go to the public office? The matter was eventually dropped. The master did not come that evening, but only left a note for my brother, saying, "Hurry to the place of Lord Hou; the matter can still be resolved." It seems the master knew that my brother and I were close friends, so he sent him to seek the help of a good friend. My brother finally did not believe it and hesitated until midnight, lying down carelessly. When the captors arrived at cockcrow, they broke the door and destroyed the room, creating a very tumultuous scene. My brother suspected the bandits had come to take revenge and, in the darkness, killed another one. When he called out, he realized he could no longer prove his innocence. When Zhao mentioned this, Zhong Ding was greatly alarmed, his hair standing on end, and he exclaimed, "My younger brother has no way out."

    343

    然则何以得免，且至此地？”邵叹曰：“兄且勿惊，愿终其说。弟毅然出官，具陈始末。讵群贼移尸路侧，反先鸣之于官。言宵行至某处，有武生邵某，持剑行劫，杀死客伙一人，掠夺钱物若干，官拘邻比，又皆言弟暴富状。官反复勘讯，谓弟追盗不合远至于郊，又不即行控禀，且拒捕伤差，其为杀越于货无疑义。弟未由置辩，竟论死。比入狴犴，拘挛甚苦。及夜，见主悄然来谓弟曰：‘不早为计，今危矣。盍从我归宁父母乎？’弟亦姑颔之。一戟手，桎梏尽脱，遂相携出狱。潜至江边，中官早舣舟相待。

    Explanation

    343

    Then how can one escape, and end up in such a place?" Zhao sighed and said, "Brother, please do not be alarmed; I wish to finish my explanation. I resolutely went to the official and fully recounted the beginning and end. Who moved the body to the roadside and reported it to the authorities first, saying that at night they traveled to a certain place, where a martial scholar named Zhao wielded a sword to rob, killed one guest, and robbed some money and goods. The official detained the neighbors, and they all said that I had suddenly become wealthy. The official repeatedly questioned, saying that it was unreasonable for me to chase thieves so far into the suburbs, and did not immediately file a report. Moreover, I resisted arrest, injuring the officials, and it is without doubt that I killed to rob. I had no chance to argue and was ultimately sentenced to death. When I was thrown into prison, the restraints were very painful. At night, the master came quietly and said to me, 'If you had not made plans early, you are in danger now. Why not come with me to see my parents?' I nodded hesitantly. With a gesture, all the restraints fell off, and we left the prison together. We secretly reached the riverbank, where the eunuch had already moored a boat to wait for us.

    344

    今将一帆南去，第不知何日再向故园归耳？”言已泪下，色遂惨然。仲鼎知其有异，不敢复诘，虽中心悱侧而强忍与言，惟叹其所遭之蹇，庆其今兹之遇而已。时将五夜，仲鼎之舟因风驶将发，倩小童数来敦请。两人不忍遽别，恋恋弗已。忽小鬟以一袭至，附耳白邵，邵笑曰：“此岂足以报我鲍叔？虽然，亦姑为之。”因举之以赠仲鼎曰：“微物赆兄，聊备舟揖之费，大德尚未酬万一也。”仲鼎欲辞谢，见其质不甚重，谅为可受者，遂弗却。天已将曙，乃执手呜咽，相视涕零。久之，始过舟。邵仍送之篷底，情话殷勤。及仲鼎反首再欲有言，则烟水苍茫，邵之舟己渺。一船皆惊，以为遇鬼，仲鼎亦为骇然。

    Explanation

    344

    I will now set sail southward, but I do not know when I will return to my hometown again. With these words, tears streamed down his face, and his expression turned somber. Zhong Ding sensed that something was amiss and dared not press further. Though deeply moved, he forced himself to speak, only lamenting the misfortunes he had endured and rejoicing in his present good fortune. It was nearing midnight, and Zhong Ding's boat was about to set sail with the wind. He asked a young boy to come and urge him to board. The two of them were reluctant to part, their affection lingering. Suddenly, a young maid brought a bundle and whispered to Shao. Shao smiled and said, "Is this enough to repay my Bao Shu? Though so, I will do it anyway." He then handed it to Zhong Ding, saying, "This humble offering is for you, to cover the cost of the boat. Great virtue has not been repaid even a fraction." Zhong Ding wanted to refuse, but seeing that the gift was not overly heavy, he thought it acceptable and accepted it. The sky was just beginning to lighten, and they held hands, weeping. They looked at each other and shed tears. After a while, they finally boarded the boat. Shao still accompanied him to the bottom of the boat, speaking warmly and affectionately. When Zhong Ding turned his head to speak again, the misty water was vast and indistinct, and Shao's boat had disappeared. Everyone on the boat was startled, thinking they had encountered a ghost, and Zhong Ding was also startled.

    345

    迨启其囊，明珠千粒，价固万金不啻，始信邵为遇仙。比至家，未卸装，即询邵生近况，果以事下狱，至夜卒。且述其异，死不于监而于门，趺坐如生，身旁有书一椷，语甚怪。有见之者，窃言其略曰：“曾参非杀人者，乃以杀人告其母，则真杀人者也。母不究真杀人者，反以伤差之故，自戕其子，忍乎哉？今伤差之罪，予已论抵。杀人之罪，谁职其咎？夫虺蜴入室，犹思碎之，况盗乎？盗移盗尸而证杀盗者为盗，官竟目之为盗，非故出故入乎？欲求真盗，则首盗者是”。末钤巨印，则故衡山大帝篆文也。官惊秘其事，而捕群盗，遂究移尸之状，尽置于法。

    Explanation

    345

    When he opened his bag, he found a thousand grains of pearls, each worth no less than ten thousand gold coins, and he finally believed that Shao had met a fairy. By the time he arrived home, without taking off his clothes, he immediately inquired about Shao's recent circumstances and learned that Shao had indeed been imprisoned for a matter and died at night. He also described the strange events, noting that Shao did not die in the prison but at the gate, sitting cross-legged as if alive, with a sealed letter beside him, the words of which were very strange. Those who saw it secretly said the gist was: "Zeng Shen was not the one who killed someone, but he was accused of killing his mother, making him the real killer. His mother did not investigate the real killer, but instead, because of the injury, she killed her own son—how could she do that? Now, the crime of causing injury has been adjudicated. The crime of killing, who is to blame? When a snake or lizard enters the room, one still wants to crush it, how much more so for a thief? The thief moved the body of another thief and proved that the killer was a thief, and the official regarded him as a thief, is this not deliberate release and re-arrest? If one wants to find the real thief, it is the one who led the theft." At the end, there was a large seal, which was the seal of the ancient Emperor of Mount Heng. The official was shocked and kept the matter secret, and then captured the group of thieves, investigating the state of the moved body, and all were punished according to the law.

    346

    时邵之尸，仲鼎父已具状领出，殓之以棺。仲鼎欲觇其异，启枢视之，则惟衣冠在焉，举家骇叹。后数年，仲鼎重游湖襄，遇邵于途，车马仪从甚赫。怀一两岁儿，与之曰：“敢累吾兄，以此延先祖脉。”仲鼎喜曰：“弟何时得此宁馨物？”邵曰：“已两获其雄矣，此其幼者。唯吾兄尚义，故敢敬以相托。”遂付儿子侯，乘轩而逝。仲鼎怀之归，诈为己子，使继邵后。及长，以其产之半析之，人咸诵其厚德，而不知实邵子电。仲鼎自得邵珠，家益裕。联捷武榜，仕至协镇。一夕梦邵以舆马来迎，竟无疾而卒。邵生名承先，字履武，亡年犹未冠。同邑人至今犹惜之。

    Explanation

    346

    At the time of Shao's death, Zhong Ding's father had already prepared the paperwork to claim the body and had it interred in a coffin. Zhong Ding wanted to see if there was anything unusual, so he opened the coffin to look inside. But only Shao's clothes and hat were there. The whole family was shocked and sighed. Several years later, Zhong Ding revisited Hu Xiang and met Shao on the road. He was traveling in a very grand procession. He was holding a two-year-old child and said to him, "I dare to burden my elder brother with this child to continue our ancestors' lineage." Zhong Ding was delighted and said, "When did you get such a wonderful child?" Shao replied, "I have already had two sons, and this is the youngest one. Since my elder brother is so righteous, I dare to respectfully entrust this child to you." He then handed over the child to his son Hou and left in a grand carriage. Zhong Ding took the child back and pretended it was his own, making him the heir of Shao. When the child grew up, he divided his property in half. People praised his great virtue, but they didn't know that he was actually Shao's son, Dian. Zhong Ding, having obtained Shao's pearl, became even wealthier. He passed the imperial exams and rose to the position of a military governor. One night, he dreamed that Shao came to meet him with a carriage and horses, and he died without any illness. Shao's name was Chengxian, his courtesy name was Lüwu, and he died before reaching adulthood. People in the same county still regret his death.

    347

    外史氏曰：古人云：“一死一生，乃见交情。”其侯与邵之谓乎？方侯别邵而去，固不料其死。及侯遇邵而归，亦不信其生。究之生者竟死，死者复生，神之力欤？不可谓非侯之功。何则？交情不深则不必遇邵，不遇邵，则一段奇缘谁与传之后世哉？侯不愧于亡友，邵乃不匿于良朋。两人之交，于焉不朽。

    Explanation

    347

    外史氏曰：古人云：“一死一生，乃见交情。”其侯与邵之谓乎？方侯别邵而去，固不料其死。及侯遇邵而归，亦不信其生。究之生者竟死，死者复生，神之力欤？不可谓非侯之功。何则？交情不深则不必遇邵，不遇邵，则一段奇缘谁与传之后世哉？侯不愧于亡友，邵乃不匿于良朋。两人之交，于焉不朽。

    The final translation is:

    The historian says: The ancients said, "It is only through life and death that true friendship is revealed." Is this not what is meant by the friendship between Hou and Shao? When Hou parted from Shao and left, he did not anticipate Shao's death. When Hou met Shao again and returned, he did not believe Shao was alive. In the end, the living died, and the dead came back to life. Was this the power of the divine? It cannot be said that it was not due to Hou's efforts. Why is this so? If their friendship were not deep, Hou would not have encountered Shao. Without meeting Shao, this extraordinary story would not have been passed down to future generations. Hou does not feel ashamed before his deceased friend, and Shao does not hide from his good friends. Their friendship, thus, will never fade.

    348

    ○　紫玉
    　　句容民金二，怙恃尽失。有弱弟曰金镛，附学于近村，年未舞勺，姣如好女。每从塾中归，辄有一妪与之偕，笑语之曰：“小郎君大好相貌，将来宜偶天上人，世间蠢女儿，恐非汝匹也。如欲物色，老妇当为执柯。”镛时稚幼，不解所言，然闻其语，颇歆羡。如是者数月，见辄云云，镛终腼腆不答。逾年，镛稍长，渐通情事，遇妪又言如前，乃赧然询之曰：“天人在何许？可容童子一识乎？”妪曰：“诺！予不可偕往，为子指南，自往觅之。倘有意，可以语我。”因示以其处曰：“距此三里许，有桃花当门而植者，其家即是也。”语已分袂。

    Explanation

    348

    A Tale of Purple Jade

    In the county of Jurong, there lived a man named Jin Er, who had lost both of his parents. He had a younger brother named Jin Yong, who was attending school in a nearby village. At the age of thirteen, Jin Yong was as beautiful as a girl. Every time he returned from school, an old woman would accompany him, smiling and speaking to him, saying, "Young master, you have a very good appearance. In the future, you should marry a celestial being. Ordinary girls in the world may not be your match. If you want to find someone, I will act as your matchmaker." At the time, Jin Yong was too young to understand what she meant, but he felt quite envious and admiring. For several months, the old woman would say the same thing every time they met, and Jin Yong always blushed and did not respond. After a year, Jin Yong grew a bit older and began to understand more about the world. When he met the old woman again, she said the same things as before, and he blushed and asked, "Where is the celestial being? Can I meet this person?" The old woman replied, "Yes! I cannot go with you, but I will guide you. Go there yourself and find her. If you are interested, you can tell me." She then pointed to a place and said, "It is about three miles away. There is a house with peach blossoms planted in front of the door. That is the place." After speaking, they parted ways.

    349

    镛朝饔入塾，即诡词以告师曰：“家外祖疾甚笃，兄命往省，乞假一日。”师以其敦谨，深信之。镛离近村，雀跃而往。至其处，果有一家，红英数枝掩映于门屏之内。镛终少小，突然径入，甫及阖侧，旋闻人声谯诃曰：“谁氏小儿郎，发犹未燥，便思作偷花贼耶？”镛惊视之，则一鸡皮翁，年近古稀，含笑自内出。镛本聪慧解人意，毫无恇怯，直前揖之。翁以左手扶杖，以右手抚其顶，哂曰：“是儿来意颇不善。”镛朗然答曰：“闻此中有天上人，特来一晤，亦何不善之有？”翁曰：“是又刘家痴老妪多饶舌。虽然，来意不可虚，盍从我入。”遂携之入门。草堂三楹，面桃而建，潇洒无纤尘，中列琴书，饶有隐居气象。翁与镛小坐，即呼紫玉将茶来。

    Explanation

    349

    Yong, upon entering the school in the morning, told the teacher with deceitful words, saying, "My maternal grandfather is very seriously ill, and my elder brother has ordered me to visit him. I beg for one day's leave." The teacher, trusting his sincerity and diligence, believed him. Yong left the nearby village and jumped with joy towards his destination. Upon arriving, he found a house with red blossoms, likely peach blossoms, screening the entrance. Yong, being young, suddenly entered. As soon as he reached the side of the door, he heard a voice scolding, "Who is this young boy, whose hair is not yet dry, thinking of becoming a thief of flowers?" Yong looked around and saw an old man with rough skin, nearly seventy years old, smiling and stepping out from inside. Yong, being intelligent and understanding, did not show any fear. He bowed respectfully before the old man. The old man, holding a walking stick in his left hand and placing his right hand on Yong's head, smiled and said, "This boy's intentions are quite not good." Yong replied clearly, "I heard that there is a heavenly person here, so I came to meet him. What is not good about that?" The old man said, "It is because of the old woman who talks too much. Nevertheless, your intention is not empty; why not follow me in?" He then led Yong inside. The simple hut had three rooms, facing peach trees, and was full of the atmosphere of seclusion. Inside were a zither and books, indicating a scholarly and peaceful environment. The old man and Yong sat down briefly, and the old man then called upon a servant named Zi Yu to bring tea.

    350

    俄见布帘半启，果有垂髫少女，年齿微长于镛，以漆盘贮盏，捧茗而出。镛孜孜以视，艳如出水之莲，风神婉丽，娟娟动人。虽童子无知，亦不禁为之眷注。翁命玉酌客，镛竟茫乎不觉，翁乃大笑曰：“情固生而有种也。”因诘之曰：“汝见天上人，此心亦即少慰乎？”答曰：“心慰矣，而愿故未酬。”翁又笑曰：“必如何始满汝愿？”镛曰：“得与若人处，志乃足。”翁又笑曰：“谈何容易！”既而曰：“此事亦非大难，汝能居此不返，当令玉日与汝戏。”镛欣然许诺，无戚容。翁乃大悦，便出果饵，俾二人同食。玉亦甚爱镛，喁喁推让如宾主。然翁顾而乐之曰：“阿玉得此，庶不忧其夐夐矣。

    Explanation

    350

    Soon the boy saw the curtain half open, and indeed there was a young girl with hanging hair, slightly older than Yōng, who carried a lacquered tray with cups and brought tea out. Yōng gazed intently, and she was as beautiful as a lotus emerging from water, graceful and charming, gently captivating. Even though he was a child, he could not help but be captivated. The old man ordered Yù to serve the guest, but Yōng was completely unaware. The old man laughed heartily and said, "Love is indeed innate." He then asked, "Did you see someone from heaven, and did this heart find some comfort?" Yōng replied, "The heart is comforted, but the wish remains unfulfilled." The old man laughed again and asked, "What must be done to fulfill your wish?" Yōng said, "To be with such a person, my aspirations would be fulfilled." The old man laughed once more and said, "How easy is it to talk about!" He then added, "This matter is not very difficult. If you can stay here and not return, I will let Yù play with you every day." Yōng happily agreed, without a sad expression. The old man was very pleased, so he took out fruit and snacks, allowing the two to eat together. Yù also loved Yōng very much, and they spoke softly and politely, treating each other as host and guest. The old man looked at them and said with joy, "If Yù gets this, she will no longer worry about being lonely."

    351

    ”径任其嬉戏，不之闲。镛夜则与翁同榻，昼则与玉同游。或斗百草於花前，或捉迷藏于月下，叠肩交臂，携手牵裾，虽无床笫之私，不减倡随之雅。婴年两两，绝少争心；弱岁双双，俨有乐趣；盖其天性然也。居年余，饮食衣履一出于翁。然而年皆渐长，情窦亦渐通，眉目之间各有狎呢之意。一日，玉晏起，隔窗缠足，镛从牖外窥之。见其洁白如雪，纤细如锥，嫩藕半枝，娇莲一掬，遂不胜情动。遥谓之曰：“予得阿姊为妻，此生实无遗憾矣。”语未竟，翁自外入，色似甚恚，诃之曰：“犊子不可畜，乃欲窃我掌珠耶？”镛因踧踖不安。翁又禁玉不得与镛戏，怒目相加，将施夏楚。镛益惧，托为旋溺，逃窜而归。

    Explanation

    351

    He let them play freely without interference. At night, Yong slept with his father on the same bed, and during the day, he played with Yu. They would either fight over flowers in front of the flowers or play hide-and-seek under the moonlight. Shoulder to shoulder, arm in arm, holding hands and pulling skirts, though there was no intimacy in bed, it did not diminish the elegance of their companionship. As infants, they rarely quarreled; as young children, they seemed to have endless fun; this was due to their natural disposition. After living together for more than a year, all their food, clothing, and daily necessities came from their father. However, as they grew older, their feelings began to develop, and there was a hint of flirtation in their eyes. One day, Yu woke up late and was tying her feet through the window. Yong peered at her from outside the window. He saw her skin as white as snow, her feet as fine as needles, like half a branch of tender lotus and a handful of delicate lotus blossoms, and he could not help but feel moved. From afar, he called out to her, saying, "If I can marry my sister, I will have no regrets in this life." Before he finished speaking, their father came in from outside, looking very angry, and scolded him, saying, "You cannot raise a calf, are you trying to steal my precious pearl?" Yong felt uneasy and nervous. The father then forbade Yu from playing with Yong, glaring at her angrily and threatening to punish her. Yong became even more afraid and pretended to need to urinate, fleeing back home.

    352

    及至家，门闾顿更，景物非故，垣边手植小柳树，既已合抱参天。大惊，亟款其户，即有杖者出应门，品貌酷类其兄，而年逾六旬，似又非是。因诘以金家，愕然曰：“即予是也，孺子何来？与予家有甚瓜葛？”镛乃述其大端。杖者笑曰：“何谬也！予父母殁已多年。闻有叔名镛，童时赴塾读书，至暮不复，已饱于豺狼之口，故生予不令就学田间。以老叔卒于某岁，距今足已七十年，即令尚在，亦既八帙头童齿豁矣，岂犹婉娈如是哉？”镛不信之，犹力与之辩。金家壮者，皆杖者子孙行，闻之怒曰：“何物乳臭儿，来冒人祖公耶！”欲饱以老拳。

    Explanation

    352

    Upon arriving home, the gate and threshold suddenly changed, and the scenery was no longer the same. A small willow tree planted by hand beside the wall had already grown to the height of a person's arms and towered high. Greatly surprised, he rushed to knock on the door. Immediately, a cane-wielding person came out to answer the door, with an appearance that closely resembled his brother, but over sixty years old, seeming yet not quite right. He asked about the Jin family in surprise, saying, "I am indeed that person, what child are you? What connection do you have with my family?" Yong then recounted the main points. The cane-wielding person laughed and said, "How absurd! My parents have been dead for many years. I heard that there was an uncle named Yong, who when young went to school to study, by evening did not return, had already been devoured by wolves, so I was not allowed to go to school in the fields. My uncle died in a certain year, it has been seventy years since then, even if he were still alive, he would also be over eighty years old with a bald head and missing teeth, could he still be so young and attractive?" Yong did not believe it and still tried to argue with him. The strong men of the Jin family, all descendants of the cane-wielding person, hearing this, angrily said, "What kind of young fool, are you trying to claim kinship with our ancestors?" They wanted to beat him with their fists.

    353

    邻翁亦八十许人，闻其哄，乃自篱落窥见之，遽出呼曰：“是诚有异，汝曹不可妄为。”因语杖者曰：“若叔与我同塾，面容犹能仿佛。此儿颇相肖，得无若叔遇仙乎？”杖者曰：“嘻！以何为质而信之？”邻翁曰：“子稔知其胁下有黑子，状若七星，长者以为仙人相。如有之，当是若叔可无疑。”镛即袒以示之，果符翁言。且述兄嫂行事及面庞，纤悉俱合。杖者乃率众罗拜，以为真仙。镛亦自笑而不信，侄乃延叔入家门。邻翁与乡里咸来造访，话幼时事，宛在目前，直至夜深始散去。镛独寝一室，鼾睡达旦。晨起觉颏下有物，捋之则髯长寸许，白且如丝，乃大骇。身亦暴长，与成人相埒，不胜太息曰：“久居仙境，长若婴童。今处尘世一宵，须发皓然，无怪乎碌碌者之易老也。”遂不别侄而行。

    Explanation

    353

    The neighborly old man, about eighty years old, heard the commotion and peered through the fence. He hurriedly came out and called out, "Truly, there is something unusual here; you all should not act rashly." Then he spoke to the stick-wielder, "Your uncle and I studied at the same school, and his face is still somewhat recognizable. This child looks very much like him, isn't it possible that your uncle met a fairy?" The stick-wielder replied, "Hmph! What evidence do you have to believe it?" The old man said, "You know well that he has a mole on his side, shaped like seven stars, which the elders consider a sign of a fairy. If it is true, then it must be your uncle without doubt." Yong then revealed his side, and indeed, it matched what the old man had said. He also described the actions of his brother and sister-in-law, as well as their faces, and every detail matched perfectly. The stick-wielder then led the crowd to bow, believing him to be a true fairy. Yong laughed to himself but did not believe it. His nephew then invited the uncle into the house. The old neighbor and the villagers all came to visit, talking about childhood matters as if they were happening now, until late at night when they finally dispersed. Yong slept alone in a room, snoring until dawn. In the morning, he woke up and felt something under his chin. When he touched it, it turned out to be a beard an inch long, white and like silk, and he was greatly shocked. His body also suddenly grew to the size of an adult. He could not help but sigh, "Having lived in a fairyland for a long time, I grew like a child. Now spending one night in the mundane world, my beard and hair turned white; it's not surprising that those who are busy age quickly." He then left without saying goodbye to his nephew.

    354

    仍诣翁处，则满目松楸，无复宅第。方踯躅间，俄见曩昔之妪蹒跚而来，心乃大喜，直前揖之，妪茫然竟不相识。镛又自白其名，妪粲然曰：“使尔中寿，尔墓之木拱矣。何以金家小郎子诳我耶？”镛又剖悉言之，妪笑诵毛诗二句曰：“未几见兮，突而弁兮。痴老子揖我，岂尚欲烦我作伐乎？”镛叹曰：“予发如此，其种种何心奢望？但得附仙人骥尾，或能长此苍苍，亦幸耳。”妪忽正色曰：“欲觅仙缘，当求佳偶。良姻尚在，子不可自隳厥志。”因出红绫丈余，付之曰：“持此向东南，见有林木，望空拂之，其人固宛在也。”镛究以耄老为嫌，妪袖出一镜，照之曰：“汝又童矣！”视之，果翩翩少年。自拈白髭，倏已尽秃。

    Explanation

    354

    Still arriving at the old man's place, he found himself surrounded by pine and cypress trees, with no houses left standing. While hesitating, he suddenly saw an old woman from the past hobbling over. His heart greatly delighted, he stepped forward and bowed to her, but the old woman was completely bewildered and did not recognize him. Yong then introduced himself by name, and the old woman smiled and said, "If you live to a normal age, the trees at your tomb will be as thick as a man. Why did the young master of the Jin family deceive me?" Yong then explained in detail, and the old woman laughed and recited two lines from the Book of Songs: "Not long ago, I saw you, and suddenly you were wearing a hat. This foolish old man bows to me, does he still want me to arrange a marriage for him?" Yong sighed, "With my hair like this, how can I have any high hopes? If I can just hitch a ride on the tail of a fairy's horse, maybe I can stay young forever, which would be fortunate." The old woman suddenly spoke seriously, "If you want to find a fairy, you should seek a good match. A good marriage is still possible, so you should not abandon your aspirations." She then took out a long piece of red silk and handed it to him, saying, "Hold this and go southeast. When you see trees, wave it in the air, and the person will surely be there." Yong still hesitated because of his old age, and the old woman took out a mirror from her sleeve and said, "You are young again!" Looking in the mirror, he indeed saw a dashing young man. He picked up his white beard, and suddenly it was all gone.

    355

    益大悦，向妪再拜，妪与镜顿失所在。因如其教，行未数里，果值平林。镛亟以帕掷之，中裹一物，及地忽化为丽人，掩笑整衣，实紫玉也。惊喜如狂，急前把袂。玉晒曰：“冰上人始终无赖，强主婚姻，令人颇不可耐。”镛又拜而乞怜，玉甫与之握手。东行数百步，恍如云雾，旋得一巨宅，峰环水绕，栋宇巍峨。未入，早笙乐大作，翁与十数人皆吉服出迎，不再齿及前事，惟盛张锦筵，举行嘉礼。镛亦由此辟谷得成地仙。后数年，降鸾于其族中，补叙其梗概如此，且附以诗曰：“情缘引到洞中天，再履红尘已惘然。镜里长春无白发，枕边短梦少青年。瑶笙不羡秦楼凤，锦瑟羞挥赵女弦。直上云霄最深处，几回含笑话桑田。”嗣是不再降坛，旬日事传白下。

    Explanation

    355

    He was greatly pleased and bowed twice to the old woman. Suddenly, the woman and the mirror disappeared without a trace. Following her instructions, he walked a few miles and indeed encountered a flat forest. Quickly, he threw a handkerchief, which wrapped an object that suddenly transformed into a beautiful woman upon touching the ground. She covered her mouth in laughter and adjusted her clothes—she was actually the Purple Jade. Overwhelmed with surprise and joy, he rushed forward to hold her sleeve. The Jade said, "Ice has always been untrustworthy, forcing a marriage, which is quite unbearable." He bowed again and begged for her mercy. The Jade just shook hands with him. They walked east for several hundred steps, and it was as if they were in a cloud of mist. Soon, they arrived at a grand mansion, surrounded by mountains and water, with towering and magnificent buildings. Before they entered, the sound of bamboo flutes and music filled the air. An elderly man and a dozen or so others, dressed in festive attire, came out to greet them. They did not mention past events and only set up a lavish banquet to hold a joyful ceremony. He also became an earthly immortal through fasting. A few years later, he descended as a phoenix among his clan, supplementing the narrative with the general outline, and added a poem: "Fated love leads to the heavens in the cave, stepping again into the mortal world leaves one bewildered. In the mirror, springtime lasts forever without gray hair, while short dreams by the pillow bring few youthful years. I do not envy the phoenix of the Qin building, nor am I ashamed to play the zither of Zhao's maiden. Ascending to the deepest part of the clouds, I smile several times at the transformation of the world." After this, he no longer descended to the altar, and the news spread to the capital in a few days.

    356

    外史氏曰：人谓仙家日长，人间日短，固已然。以七十余年，仅博得山中一岁，其多少尚可数计哉？幸而遄返，乃得成仙。使竞居此不复，不几以十数龄之黄童，转盼为八旬余之白叟，其去夜台，犹有几日耶？余固谓仙家日短，究不若人间之日长。

    Explanation

    356

    The historian said: People say that days are longer in the realm of immortals, while days are shorter in the mortal world, and indeed this is so. With over seventy years, one only gains one year in the immortal realm, how many times more can it be counted? Fortunately returning quickly, one can become an immortal. If one were to stay here forever, would one not quickly turn from a young child of ten or so years to an elderly man of eighty or more, and how many days would one have left before death? I firmly believe that days in the immortal realm are shorter, in the end, than days in the mortal world.

    357

    随园老人曰：幻极矣，而言之若凿凿可据，生龙活虎，直令人无从着手。

    ○　古冢狐
    　　易州之西有古冢，不知几千百年。无碑碣，亦莫详其姓氏，俗以为荆卿之墓，疑即当时以衣冠葬处也。一日，有乡民送妻归宁，夫妇各跨一蹇卫，道经冢侧。妇因来路已远，顿思溲便，乃弃乘趋榛莽间。夫骑正驶，莫之能知，行半里许，始觉相失在后，乃驻俟之。久且弗至，心乃疑，亟返辔踪迹之，则唯有所乘之驴散龁于草际，人竟渺然。夫大骇，冥搜墓畔，见妇之衣裤纷挂于蓬颗蔓草间。时正有狼患，疑其已饱馋口，遂收其遗服，恸哭而归。乃妇实未死，方溲毕而起，俄闻人语纷拿。有二隶自冢中出，须毛如蝟，状甚怖人，直前欲执之。詈曰：“何处狂婢，秽我正人门庭，系之去，敲扑无恕。”妇惊而奔，衣缕忽若蝉蜕，不转瞬既已自褫。

    Explanation

    357

    The Elder of Sui Garden said: "It is extremely fantastical, yet when spoken of, it seems solid and credible, like a living dragon and a lively tiger, making one feel completely at a loss."

    The Fox from the Ancient Tomb

    In the west of Yizhou, there is an ancient tomb, unknown how many thousands of years old it is. There are no tombstones or inscriptions, and its name and surname are also unknown. The common people believe it to be the tomb of Jing Ke, and it is suspected to be the place where he was buried in his official robes. One day, a villager sent his wife back to her parents' home. The couple each rode a slow horse, passing by the side of the tomb. The wife, because the journey was long, suddenly needed to use the restroom, and she abandoned her horse and rushed into the thicket. The husband was riding his horse at full speed and did not notice. After traveling about half a mile, he realized he had lost her behind, and he then stopped and waited for her. After a long time without her returning, he began to suspect something was wrong, and he quickly turned his horse around and went back to look for her. He only found his wife's donkey grazing in the grass, but there was no sign of her. The husband was greatly frightened and searched the area around the tomb. He saw her clothes and pants hanging on a clump of grass and vines. At that time, there was a wolf problem, and he suspected that the wolves had already eaten her. He then collected her clothes and cried bitterly as he returned home. But the wife was actually still alive. She had just finished and stood up, and she suddenly heard people talking. Two officials emerged from the tomb, their beards and hair like hedgehogs, looking very frightening. They immediately approached to arrest her, shouting, "Where is this crazy maid, polluting the respectable courtyard? Tie her up and beat her without mercy." The wife was startled and ran away. Her clothes suddenly slipped off like a cicada shedding its shell, and in an instant, she had already taken off her clothes.

    358

    妇羞缩不能步，隶追益急，不得已而伏于丛棘，以冀幸免。旋闻隶笑曰：“只此足以辱之，毋苛求。”言已，寂然。妇究不敢出，匿迹终夜，肌革尽伤。及晓，不能归，返索故衣，则皆乌有。正惶急欲寻死所，适行人数辈策蹇而来。至其前，见妇白身，状若疯痫，因惊询其故。妇乃忍辱背告之，虽能少掩其私，然而尻骨之隆隆，臀肉之累累，莫不瞭如观掌。幸众中一人闻言骇曰：“是予妹也。”亟解衣使蔽其体。妇始敢回睇，则其兄方奉父命来速妹，初不意相值于此也。妇不禁涕出，兄乃以所控之驴载之遄归，且驰告其夫，始释厥惑。后人时见有二狐出游于冢上，其色苍黄，执之即不见。乃知妇所遇者，盖即此耳。狐岂有慕于高渐离之义，而为悲歌者特保其冢欤？噫！亦异矣！

    Explanation

    358

    The woman, ashamed and shrinking, could not walk. The servant pursued with increasing urgency, and she had no choice but to hide among the thorny bushes, hoping to escape by chance. Soon, she heard the servant laugh and say, "This is enough to humiliate her; do not be too demanding." After speaking, there was silence. The woman dared not come out, hiding her tracks all night, and her skin and flesh were all injured. By dawn, she could not return, and returned to look for her old clothes, but they were all gone. She was just in a state of panic and wanted to find a place to die, when just as travelers on donkeys came. When they reached her, they saw the woman naked, appearing as if she had a mental illness, and were shocked and asked her the reason. The woman then told them with her back turned, enduring shame, and though she could slightly conceal her private matters, the bony protrusions of her buttocks and the lumps of her buttocks were clear as looking at the palm of one's hand. Fortunately, one traveler in the group exclaimed, "This is my sister!" and quickly took off his clothes to cover her body. The woman finally dared to look back, and it was her brother who was controlling the donkey, coming to fetch her as ordered by their father, and he did not expect to meet her here. The woman could not help but shed tears, and her brother then loaded her onto the donkey he was controlling and hurried her back, and rushed to inform her husband, who then understood the mystery. Later, people sometimes saw two foxes playing on the tomb, their color grayish yellow, and if one tried to catch them, they would disappear. Thus it was known that the woman had encountered these foxes. Could the foxes have a reverence for the righteousness of Gao Jianli and be singing mournfully to protect his tomb? Ah! It is indeed strange!

    359

    外史氏曰：村妇无知，擅污高士之垄，裸而辱之，狐亦善解人意矣。犹忆少时，闻诸父老云：“直省多平屋，每值溽暑，民家之妇女辄寝于其上。倘逢阴晦，或与龙交。”嗟乎，彼何人？斯亵衣且不可以向日，乃敢露秽于星月之前。龙非性淫，特以惩创之也。有家者，不宜知警耶？

    ○　崔十三
    　　杭人有卖海鲜者，其名曰李念一。湎酒而渔色，尤好龙阳君。虽产仅中人，而耽耽逐逐，惟此是求，妻子之饥冻弗恤也。同里崔十三，恒依之。十三年仅成童，貌逾好女。母早卒，惟父在，又以病废，家綦贫。以故念一尝拮据相助，冀图片刻之欢，而未遂所欲焉。盖十三聪慧，善揣人意中事，虽以家寒亲老，借润匪人，而刻意防之，守身如处子，念一遂不得染指。癸未夏，念一以事如海宁，往返数日程，力请于十三之父，欲与十三偕，意固将决一死战也。十三之父不欲，曰：“童子何知？君亦自往可矣。

    Explanation

    359

    The Commentator said: A rural woman, lacking in knowledge, recklessly defiled the grave of a noble scholar, stripping and humiliating it; even the fox understood human intentions. I still recall my youth when I heard from the elders: "In the provinces, there are many flat-roofed houses. During the hot and humid summer, women from the common folk often sleep on the roofs. If it happens to be a dark and gloomy day, they might encounter a dragon." Alas, what kind of person is that? Such a person, whose undergarments are not even fit to face the sun, dares to expose their filth before the stars and moon. Dragons are not naturally promiscuous; they merely punish and chastise such behavior. Those who have families should not be unaware of this warning?

    Chapter on Cui Shisan

    There was a man in Hangzhou who sold seafood, named Li Nianyi. He indulged in drinking and pursuing women, especially fond of the Dragon Yang. Although his income was only average, he was obsessed and relentless, seeking only this, and did not care about the hunger and cold of his wife and children. His fellow villager, Cui Shisan, always relied on him. Cui Shisan was only a teenager, more beautiful than a girl. His mother died early, and only his father was alive, who was also disabled by illness, making their family extremely poor. Therefore, Li Nianyi often struggled to help, hoping for a moment of pleasure, but did not achieve what he wanted. Cui Shisan was clever and wise, good at guessing people's thoughts. Although his family was poor and his parents were old, he sought support from unscrupulous people, but deliberately guarded against it, keeping himself pure like a maiden, so Li Nianyi could not touch him. In the summer of the year Guiwei, Li Nianyi went to Hai Ning for business, traveling for several days. He strongly requested from the father of Cui Shisan, wanting to go with Cui Shisan, intending to fight a decisive battle. The father of Cui Shisan did not want him to go, saying, "What does a child know? You can go by yourself."

    360

    ”念一益固请，而十三弱即好弄，乐于远游，又从中怂恿，父不得已而遣之。滨行，密语十三曰：“若人素有轻薄名，儿之家倚之，不得不听汝往。但以全璧去，以全璧归，吾可以见先人于地下，儿亦可谓克孝矣。否则，我人也或不能知，而鬼则知之，必不容汝入崔氏之庙。”十三领诺，即辞父行。邻比多有窃笑者，以为崔父贫病失心，特置子于虎口之下，其不被啖者，未尝有焉。乃十三自负己智，欣然与念一同舟。与之饮则饮，与之谑则谑，了无畏忌，即念一亦自谓鱼游釜中矣。舟行至暮，十三出篷底闲眺，时念一酣卧，未暇相从。十三目视水光波流，倏忽上下，慨然曰：“人不自立，瞬息下流，殆与此水无异耳。

    Explanation

    360

    Nian Yi persistently requested, and Shisan, being young and fond of play, enjoyed long-distance travel. He was also encouraged behind the scenes. The father, out of necessity, sent him away. On the verge of departure, he secretly told Shisan, "That person has always had a reputation for being frivolous. My family relies on him, so we cannot help but allow you to go. But if you leave with all your possessions and return with all your possessions, I can see my ancestors in the netherworld, and you can be said to have fulfilled your filial duty. Otherwise, people may not know, but spirits will, and they will surely not allow you to enter the Cui family's ancestral hall." Shisan agreed and immediately bid farewell to his father and left. Many neighbors laughed secretly, thinking that Mr. Cui, poor and mentally unstable, was placing his son in the mouth of a tiger, and it was rare for anyone not to be eaten. Shisan, confident in his own intelligence, joyfully went on the boat with Nian Yi. When he drank with him, he drank; when he joked with him, he joked. He had no fear or caution, and even Nian Yi thought of himself as a fish swimming in a pot. As the boat sailed until dusk, Shisan stepped out from under the awning to gaze idly at the water, while Nian Yi was sound asleep, unable to keep up. Shisan watched the shimmering water and its waves, rising and falling swiftly, and sighed, "If one does not stand on one's own, one can quickly fall into the lower stream, much like this water."

    361

    ”有触于怀，因窃念曰：“今夕与若人同舱，彼若以力，将如之何？岂我一婴童而能与壮夫相抗哉？且适间言语涉邪，几于矫手顿足。我果何策以御之？”筹思无计，因而自悔。方且四顾榜徨，潸然泣下。忽上流一扁舟御风而行，载一中年妇与一少艾，状类母女。女荡桨而母搬罾，殆渔家也。舟行将近，少艾顾十三而笑曰：“个儿郎只身如叶，陷于泥沙，能效我远避狂暴耶？同病宜怜，母不可不有以拯之。”妇亦笑曰：“儿之言，可谓既登彼岸，不忍溺人者矣。况是儿亦孝子，不宜坐视。”乃即怀中取一册，其巨如掌，仅十余页，裹以他物投之十三曰：“孺子得此，可以保身矣。”舟如奔马，十三惊顾之，早已相去里许。辗转之顷，航影已没。

    Explanation

    361

    A Feeling Arises in My Heart

    Suddenly, a feeling arose in my heart, and I secretly thought to myself: "Tonight, I am sharing a cabin with this person. What if he uses force? How will I deal with it? How could a child like me possibly resist a strong man? Moreover, just now, the words were inappropriate, almost like flailing and stamping. What strategy do I actually have to deal with him?" After pondering, I had no plan, and I then regretted it. I was looking around in confusion, and I shed tears involuntarily. Suddenly, a small boat upstream was sailing with the wind, carrying a middle-aged woman and a young, beautiful girl, who looked like mother and daughter. The girl rowed while the mother moved the fishing net, indicating they were likely fishermen. The boat was approaching, and the young girl looked at me and smiled, saying: "This young man is alone like a leaf, stuck in mud and sand, can he follow me to avoid violence? Those who suffer the same fate should pity each other. Mother, you must help him." The mother also smiled and said: "My daughter's words are true; she has reached the other side and cannot bear to see others drown. Besides, this boy is a filial son, and it is not right to stand by and do nothing." She then took a book from her pocket, about the size of a palm, with only ten or so pages, wrapped it in something else, and threw it to me, saying: "This child, if you get this, can protect yourself." The boat was like a galloping horse, and I was surprised and looked back, but it was already a mile away. In a moment of hesitation, the boat's shadow had disappeared.

    362

    十三幼从父读，颇通文，亟启其册而阅之，则皆闺中戏术，无他奇。十三乃攒眉曰：“予焉用此？媪殆绐我为笑乎？”既而思之，若运以机智，术亦良得，遂秘之袖中。而念一已醒，遽呼十三，十三入，诘以焉往，答曰：“适在皕首观江景耳。”念一乃笑曰：“以汝之容光，不惧蛟龙攫去耶？”因又嘻嘻言曰：“今宵能共榻，当以所得之半赠若，以赡亲。不然，三尺水即汝安宅，将葬尔于江鱼腹中，不得复返矣。汝父老病，讵能向我索人？”言之咄咄相逼。十三闻之，甚有惧色，顿忆册中移灯就火之法，颇可以济燃眉。乃朗然曰：“兄之爱我，苟非木石，皆知感。但予稚年，不谙情事，且畏羞。倘得沉醉，任若为之，予亦不惜此身。”念一大悦，慨许之，自出登岸行沽。

    Explanation

    362

    Thirteen, being young, followed his father to read and became fairly proficient in writing. He hastily opened the book and read it, but it contained nothing but playful tricks of the inner chambers, with no other remarkable content. Thirteen furrowed his brows and said, "Why would I need this? The old woman must be fooling me for amusement!" After thinking about it, he realized that if he used wit and strategy, the method could also be well-gained, so he kept it secret in his sleeve. Meanwhile, Nianyi had already woken up and called for Thirteen. Thirteen entered and was questioned about where he had been. He replied, "I just happened to be at Bishi to view the river scenery." Nianyi then laughed and said, "With your beauty, are you not afraid that a dragon will snatch you away?" He continued in a teasing manner, "Tonight, if we can share a bed, I will give you half of what I have gained to support your parents. Otherwise, the three feet of water will be your home, and you will be buried in the belly of a river fish, never to return. Your father is old and ill; how could he come to me to demand you?" His words were pressing and threatening. Upon hearing this, Thirteen showed a look of fear and suddenly remembered the method of moving the lamp to the fire from the book, which could help in a pinch. He then said clearly, "Brother's love for me, if not for wood or stone, all would know gratitude. But I am young and inexperienced with matters of love, and I am also afraid of shame. If I could be intoxicated, let you do as you please, and I will not hesitate to offer my body." Nianyi was greatly pleased and solemnly agreed, then left to go ashore and buy wine.

    363

    十三亟觅楮颖，就烛取册观之，则急口令耳。凡三令，举十数言，备极男女狎昵之语。且令曰：“能诵如流水而无笑容者，乃免罚。”十三一一书之于笺，而自为默记，亟藏其册。未几，念一复入，置酒将饮，十三曰：“无令不欢，且今日之事，更非可默然相对者。昨自邻家得口令数行，颇可以司觞政，请与若行之。”念一自恃捷给，毅然而应之曰：“诺。”即各满饮三爵，索令共观。十三又曰：“若年长我一倍，必不予欺。如不遵予令而索予欢，予宁赴清流，誓不汝从。”念一亦允之，无难色。十三始出令相示，约以一大白。念一甫寓目，已笑不能仰。十三又自请先行，每一发声，更故作睥昵以动之。

    Explanation

    363

    Thirteen urgently sought paper and brush, by the candlelight, and took the book to look at it, which turned out to be a rapid speech command. There were three commands, each containing several words, all kinds of intimate language between men and women. The command stated, "If one can recite it like flowing water without smiling, they will be exempt from punishment." Thirteen wrote each one on a note and memorized them silently, then hid the book away. Soon after, Nianyi returned again, setting wine to drink. Thirteen said, "No order without joy, and today's matter, even more so, not something to be silent about. Yesterday, from a neighbor's house, I obtained several lines of commands, which can be used to manage the drinking, please let us do it together." Nianyi, relying on his quick wit, responded resolutely, saying, "Yes." Each drank three full cups, and requested to see the command together. Thirteen said, "Since you are twice my age, you will not deceive me. If you do not follow my command and seek my pleasure, I would rather drown myself, swearing never to follow you." Nianyi agreed without hesitation. Thirteen then began to show the command, agreeing to a large cup. As soon as Nianyi saw it, he laughed uncontrollably. Thirteen then requested to go first, making each sound with a deliberate glance to provoke.

    364

    念一之情已荡，心遂无主，不得已而亦诵之，未及一行，早已粲然。十三执觯行罚，又不姑恕。再诵又复如是，三诵益不能成。俄顷之间，连釂十余爵，念一既已酕醄，遂不作他想，诵之愈急，愈不能如令。漏下二鼓，念一竟玉山颓矣。十三又连劝二盏，已不克启吻，知其深入醉乡，心始少放。此盖以所欲荡其心，复以所难箝其口，不必灭烛徙薪，而烈焰顿熄，管子所谓因祸为福者也。十三既以智醉念一，别无忧虞，方能拂榻就寝。忽闻弹指声，自启舱门视之，烛光之下，一人掩笑而入。回顾焉，则顷者所遇之少艾耳。喜而致谢，兼扣其突如之故，女笑曰：“恐君未易办此，故来相助一臂。今既潦倒，此夕无虞矣。

    Explanation

    364

    The single-minded emotion of Nian Yi had dissipated, and his heart had lost its control. Unwillingly, he also recited it, but not even halfway through a line, he had already become bright and clear. Thirteen held a cup and administered punishment, and there was no leniency. He recited again, and it was the same; he recited a third time, and he could not even complete it. In the blink of an eye, he had drunk more than ten cups, and Nian Yi was already drunk. He no longer had other thoughts. By the second drum strike, Nian Yi was completely drunk. Thirteen then urged him to drink two more cups, and he could no longer speak, knowing that he had deeply entered the realm of drunkenness, and his heart began to relax slightly. This is because something desired disrupted his heart, and something difficult restrained his mouth, turning misfortune into good fortune without the need to extinguish the candle or move the firewood. This is what Guan Zi called turning misfortune into good fortune. Thirteen, having used wisdom to intoxicate Nian Yi, had no further worries and was able to sweep the bed and prepare for sleep. Suddenly, he heard the sound of snapping fingers, and he opened the cabin door to see a person hiding a smile and entering. He looked back, and then realized that the person he had just encountered was young and beautiful. He was delighted and thanked her, and asked her why she had come so suddenly. The woman smiled and said, "I was afraid you would not easily manage this, so I came to help you. Now that you are completely drunk, there is no worry for tonight."

    365

    明日敬效吾法，必当有验。”十三始得谛观，女貌淡艳幽妍，自觉形秽，殆天人也。十三情窦已启，适与念一戏，欲火久炽，见女反身欲归，乃笑而留之曰：“姑少坐，容我酬恩，其可乎？”女觉其意，微哂曰：“子尚己躬不阅，犹妄思魔障人耶？”飘然出产，倏忽遂不见。十三惊诧良久，复取其册展玩之，倦极而眠，不觉东方之已白。晨起视念一，兀自朦胧，且被酒不能起。乃甘言以慰之曰：“昨夕所饮无多，兄乃酩酊若彼，冥然漠然，深为弟忧。”言讫，巧笑承迎，念一究不悟其以趋为避之计。是日也，念一病宿酲，至夜方愈，遂誓不复饮。

    Explanation

    365

    Tomorrow, I will respectfully follow my method, and it will surely prove effective." Thirteen finally got a clear view, and saw that the woman's appearance was subtly beautiful and mysteriously charming, making him feel unattractive in comparison, almost like a heavenly being. Thirteen's heart's desires had begun to stir, and just as he was playing with Nianyi, his desire had been burning for a long time. Seeing the woman turn to leave, he smiled and asked her to stay for a moment, saying, "Please sit for a moment, allow me to repay your kindness, is that acceptable?" The woman sensed his intentions and smiled slightly, saying, "You still haven't examined yourself, yet you foolishly think to bewilder others?" She left gracefully and swiftly, disappearing suddenly and completely. Thirteen was astonished for a long time, then took the book again and examined it, becoming so tired that he fell asleep without realizing the east had already turned white. In the morning, he looked at Nianyi, who was still drowsy and disoriented, and still drunk, unable to get up. He comforted him with sweet words, saying, "We didn't drink much last night, but you are so drunk as to be in a stupor, in a dazed and indifferent state, I am deeply concerned for you." After he finished speaking, he greeted with a charming smile, but Nianyi did not understand that it was a strategy of evasion. That day, Nianyi suffered from a hangover, and did not recover until the night, and then swore never to drink again.

    366

    十三亦不能强，乃用册中反客为主之谋，挑之以言曰：“兄今者深为酒困，不可再有所戕。客夕之约，能姑俟之异日乎？”念一侧首以示之曰：“不能。”十三曰：“予昨者亦为情扰，竟夕不眠，此时亦惫甚矣。兄能容我小憩，夜深自来就见。弟信人，决不爽约。”念一弗即诺。十三又曰：“否则，兄来就予，予当假寐以待。但不可着衣，惊弟使寤，则忸怩，或至于妨命亦未可知。”念一又不禁色喜，冁然从之。十三密索于舱中，得短梃，长可尺余，藏诸皀席之下。竟与念一分榻而卧，而潜拥此梃于怀中。挟持利器，念一固不及知焉，欲炽情炎，反侧不寐。倾耳即闻有齁声，知十三已入黑甜，急不能待，遂悄然以起，白身靸履往就之。潜启其衾，亦不之拒，且肤香袭人，情益莫遏。

    Explanation

    366

    Thirteen was unable to force his way, so he employed the strategy of turning the guest into the host, provoking him with words: "Brother, you are now deeply troubled by alcohol and cannot be further harmed. The evening appointment you made, can we wait until another day?" Nianyi turned his head to indicate that he could not. Thirteen said, "I was also troubled by emotions yesterday, stayed awake all night, and am also very exhausted now. Can you allow me to take a short rest? I will come to see you late at night. I am a trustworthy person and will never break the promise." Nianyi did not immediately agree. Thirteen added, "Otherwise, you can come to me, and I will pretend to sleep and wait. But do not wear clothes, as it might wake me up, which would be embarrassing, or it might even endanger life, who knows?" Nianyi could not help but show a pleased expression and smiled, following the suggestion. Thirteen secretly searched in the cabin, found a short stick, about a foot long, and hid it under the straw mat. He ended up sharing a bed with Nianyi, secretly holding the stick in his chest. With a sharp weapon in hand, Nianyi certainly did not know about it. Desire and passion burned intensely, and he turned restlessly, unable to sleep. Leaning to listen, he heard snoring sounds, knowing that Thirteen had fallen into deep sleep. Unable to wait any longer, he silently got up, naked and wearing slippers, and went to him. He secretly opened his quilt, and he did not reject it, and the fragrance of his skin was intoxicating, making the passion even more intense.

    367

    方将共枕拥持，而十三之身忽已转侧向外，仍复鼾眠。念一如其所教，不敢惊，因解履登榻，自趋里床。乃身甫贴席，而十三若梦中忽觉者，遽呼曰：“可人来乎？”以巨物少避中坚，直捣后队，槛门皆破，几及睾丸，其痛不可当，而前徒之精锐者，不觉倒戈莫举矣。念一痛极大呼，而手足俨如缚束。许时，十三始伪为乍寤之状，顾而视之。残烛未烬，因急藏其梃，佯笑曰：“是吾兄耶？予适梦与美人戏，彼嗤我无伟具，予于暗中摸索得一物，颇粗壮，因以戏之，不意兄至。梦呓无知，负罪良多矣。”遂故为亲呢，欲与为欢。

    Explanation

    367

    As they were about to share a pillow and embrace, the body of the thirteenth suddenly turned sideways and faced outward, continuing to snore. Following the instructions, the eleventh dared not disturb, so he undressed and climbed onto the bed, moving to the inner bed. As soon as his body touched the bedding, the thirteenth, as if waking up suddenly from a dream, called out, "Is my beloved here?" With a large object slightly avoiding the center, he directly thrust at the rear, breaking the barriers, almost reaching the testicles, the pain was unbearable. The elite of the front line, unconsciously turned back and could not be raised. The eleventh cried out in pain, and his hands and feet felt as if bound. After a while, the thirteenth finally pretended to wake up suddenly, looked at him, and seeing the candle still burning, quickly hid the phallic object and smiled, saying, "Is it my brother? I just dreamed of playing with a beautiful woman, and he laughed at me for having no great tool. I groped in the dark and found an object, quite thick and strong, so I played with it. Did not expect my brother to arrive. Unconscious ramblings without knowledge, have committed many sins." Therefore, he pretended to be close, wanting to have fun with him.

    368

    念一惊定痛生，外肾似有浮肿，厥臀更暴痛如割，不及问鼎，喏喏而退，呻吟者达旦。此又闺人与女伴相戏，乘其无备而中伤之策也。念一始疑十三，谓其有心规避。乃十三夙兴即起，奉事益谨，言语益甘，俨有呵脬舐痔之意，念一疑复少释。第负重伤，损及不毛，虽仍前不悛，亦几于用武无具矣。忍痛二日，已抵海宁。扶杖市中，索其前逋，信宿而子母皆得。十三思归，促之启行，念一亦不获耽延，遂复登舟。创亦渐平，色念复动。初犹不敢即逞，将及杭界，忿然曰：“此行实为是人，非只图彼戋戋。今去故乡伊迩，而志不克遂，何以生为？虽溃烂而死，亦命也，务毕吾愿而后已。”然亦窃疑十三，遂阴谋亦欲醉之，使其无能脱赚。暮泊镇市，浼楫人沽酒割鸡，而己仍前作狼狈之状。

    Explanation

    368

    Nianyi, after being startled, felt pain arise; his external genitalia seemed to have swelling, and his buttocks were even more violently painful as if being cut. He nodded and retreated, moaning until dawn. This was another strategy where women and female companions play tricks, taking advantage of their lack of preparation to harm them. Initially, Nianyi suspected Shisan, thinking she had a deliberate intention to avoid something. However, Shisan woke up early and got up, serving more diligently, speaking more sweetly, and seemed to have the intention of licking sores and cleaning bladders. Nianyi's suspicion was somewhat alleviated. Despite the severe injury, which affected his reproductive organs, even though he still did not repent, he was almost left without the means to act. Enduring pain for two days, they arrived at Hai Ning. Holding a cane in the market, he demanded repayment of previous debts, and after two nights, both the child and mother were obtained. Shisan wanted to return home, urging them to start the journey, and Nianyi could not delay further, so he resumed his voyage. The wound gradually healed, and sexual desire resumed. Initially, he was still hesitant to act, but approaching the border of Hangzhou, he angrily said, "This journey is actually for this person, not just for the small amount of money. Now that I am close to my hometown, and my desires cannot be fulfilled, what is the point of living? Even if it means dying from decay, it is fate; I will fulfill my wish before stopping." Yet, he also secretly suspected Shisan, so he plotted to get her drunk, making it impossible for her to escape. Anchoring at dusk in a town market, he asked the boatman to buy wine and cut chicken, while still acting in a disheveled state.

    369

    乃十三聪觉者也，早已如见其肺肝，因取册暗中览之，又得良图，名曰移花接木。其法用圆竹一节，满贮蒜汁，而以生面和胶封其口，密置衾底，卧后先润以津，务令如脂如韦，无少窒碍。彼来故不予以门户，俟浼之再三，然后自为指南，急以所备者自前而后承迎之。胶著热肌，固结不解，汁浸厥物，痛入心脾，诚惩淫之奇计也。然须预伏白刃，防其反噬，以为不虞之备。十三得其术，心益喜。觅之舟人，得胶少许，惟竹不能猝办，忽于榻侧获之，则固念一所截用以贮其碎金者。因笑曰：“即以其人之物，还治其人之身，大快大快。”遂一如其教而预储焉，念一又茫然不知。及昏，欢然共饮，托醉先眠。

    Explanation

    369

    Thirteen is a person of keen perception, having already seen through their innermost thoughts. He then secretly read the book and obtained a good plan, called "Transplanting Flowers and Grafting Trees." The method involves using a section of bamboo, filled with garlic juice, and sealed with dough and glue, hidden under the bedding. After lying down, he first moistened it with saliva, ensuring it was as smooth as fat or leather, with no obstacles. When the other came, he did not give him the opening, waiting until he asked repeatedly, then guiding himself, and quickly using the prepared item to welcome him from the front and behind. The glue adhered to the hot skin, firmly stuck and not separating, the juice soaked the object, causing pain that penetrated the heart and spleen, truly a strange plan to punish lust. However, one must prepare a sharp blade in advance, to guard against retaliation, as a precaution for unexpected events. Thirteen obtained the method, and his heart grew even happier. He sought it from the boatman and obtained a small amount of glue, but only the bamboo could not be obtained quickly. Suddenly, he found it near the bed, which was originally cut by Nian Yi to store his scattered gold. He laughed and said, "Using someone else's thing to deal with them, that is very satisfying." He then followed the instructions and prepared it in advance, while Nian Yi remained unaware. When it was evening, they drank happily together, and Nian Yi pretended to be drunk and went to sleep first.

    370

    布置已尽完密，俟其至请尝试之。念一果暴痛如触蛇蝎，亟取烛自视，则累累者虽故伟长，然已代之以竹矣。急以手脱之，又牢不可拔，愧忿交集，将致命于十三。而十三早挺刃而起，数之曰：“汝腼然人面，而务为狗彘行，败人家风，污人子弟，罪已在于不赦。汝数数诱予，加以强横，予念同乡久契，少惩于前。汝不知洗心涤虑，改弦易辙，今宵仍萌故态，予因聊试小术，俾汝革心。竟乃迷而不悔，怒目视予，予已置身度外，不复作桑梓之想矣。”言已欲自刭，且号呼杀人。盈舱鼎沸，同舟共泊者皆惊起，须臾毕集。视二人则裸如也，知为逐臭之故，争笑而询之。十三且泣且诉，历陈其状，众皆咋舌以为奇。

    Explanation

    370

    The arrangement had been fully and meticulously completed, and they waited for someone to come and try it. When the person named Nianyi experienced a sudden and intense pain as if bitten by a snake or scorpion, he quickly took a candle to look at it. He found that the lumps were still as large and long as before, but had already been replaced by bamboo. He hastily tried to remove it with his hands, but it was so tightly bound that it could not be removed. Ashamed and furious at the same time, he was about to kill himself in front of Thirteen. However, Thirteen had already stood up and drawn his sword, recounting the person's shameful behavior and unforgivable sins. He explained that the person had repeatedly tempted him with arrogance and domineering behavior, and he had given a slight warning earlier. Thirteen lamented that the person did not cleanse his heart and clear his mind, nor did he change his course, and tonight he still showed his old ways. Thirteen then tried a small trick to make the person change his heart, but the person remained stubborn and unrepentant, staring angrily at Thirteen. Thirteen then stood outside himself and no longer thought of their hometown. After saying this, Thirteen wanted to commit suicide and shouted for someone to kill him. The cabin was in a state of great commotion, and everyone on board who was also moored there was startled awake. In a short while, everyone gathered. When they looked at the two men, they were naked, and they knew it was because of chasing after foul smells. They all laughed and asked about it. Thirteen wept and recounted his situation, detailing his plight, and everyone was amazed and thought it was strange.

    371

    环瞩念一，既已面无人色，痛不能言。戏视其腰，则松不生于腹上，而竹乃产于脐下，遂不禁鼓掌大笑，为之哄堂，又无人屑于援手。唯舟工惧其陨命，贻累于己，始代为脱之。尽力而后落，其必拥肿不中绳墨可知也。众命与十三胥着衣缕，而后以正言责之。念一俯首知罪。客中有尚义者，将代十三鸣之官。念一泣恳数四，众又为之居间，始令具一伏罪状，而以所得所有者尽以输之十三，聊示罚过之意。达旦即驱之，罄身离舟，另觅小艇归里。永不许扰十三家，扰则执状赴县，众将鸣鼓而攻，必不恕。念一一败涂地，莫敢与争，遂垂头自去。抵家犹卧床褥者匝月，盖淫心亦既少戢焉。

    Explanation

    371

    环瞩念一，既已面无人色，痛不能言。戏视其腰，则松不生于腹上，而竹乃产于脐下，遂不禁鼓掌大笑，为之哄堂，又无人屑于援手。唯舟工惧其陨命，贻累于己，始代为脱之。尽力而后落，其必拥肿不中绳墨可知也。众命与十三胥着衣缕，而后以正言责之。念一俯首知罪。客中有尚义者，将代十三鸣之官。念一泣恳数四，众又为之居间，始令具一伏罪状，而以所得所有者尽以输之十三，聊示罚过之意。达旦即驱之，罄身离舟，另觅小艇归里。永不许扰十三家，扰则执状赴县，众将鸣鼓而攻，必不恕。念一一败涂地，莫敢与争，遂垂头自去。抵家犹卧床褥者匝月，盖淫心亦既少戢焉。

    Translation:
    环瞩念一，既已面无人色，痛不能言。戏视其腰，则松不生于腹上，而竹乃产于脐下，遂不禁鼓掌大笑，为之哄堂，又无人屑于援手。唯舟工惧其陨命，贻累于己，始代为脱之。尽力而后落，其必拥肿不中绳墨可知也。众命与十三胥着衣缕，而后以正言责之。念一俯首知罪。客中有尚义者，将代十三鸣之官。念一泣恳数四，众又为之居间，始令具一伏罪状，而以所得所有者尽以输之十三，聊示罚过之意。达旦即驱之，罄身离舟，另觅小艇归里。永不许扰十三家，扰则执状赴县，众将鸣鼓而攻，必不恕。念一一败涂地，莫敢与争，遂垂头自去。抵家犹卧床褥者匝月，盖淫心亦既少戢焉。

    Translation:
    念一被众人围绕，脸色已经变得苍白，痛苦得无法言语。有人戏弄地看他的腰，发现松树不会长在腹部上，而竹子却在肚脐下生长，于是大家忍不住大笑起来，整个大厅都响起了笑声，但没有人愿意伸出援手。只有船夫担心他会丧命，给自己带来麻烦，才开始帮助他脱身。经过一番努力后，他终于脱身，但可以预见的是，他必定会变得臃肿不堪，无法符合规矩。众人命令十三穿上破旧的衣服，然后用严厉的话语责备他。念一低下头承认了自己的过错。有一位客人非常讲义气，打算为十三向官府申诉。念一多次哭泣恳求，众人再次出面调解，最终让他写了一份认罪书，并将自己所有的东西都给了十三，以此表示惩罚的意图。到了天亮，他们就把他赶下了船，让他身无分文地离开，另找了一条小船回家。从此以后，不允许他再去骚扰十三家，如果再敢骚扰，就会拿着认罪书去县衙告发，众人将敲锣打鼓地攻击他，绝不宽恕。念一彻底失败了，没有人敢与他争辩，于是他垂头丧气地离开了。回到家后，他还躺在床上休息了一个月，据说他的淫念也有所收敛。

    372

    舟中人以十三为智，咸爱重之，竞市酒肴相馈问。乃十三深虑念一，恐其报复，心欲遄归，无如距家仅一日程，而风色少逆，至夕，犹泊于曩昔遇女之处。十三心识其地，乃笑曰：“黄石公犹在乎？报韩者已归，可以从赤松游矣。”及寝，有人款户径入，视之，则赠书之媪也。喜极趋迎，延之坐而谢之。媪谓之曰：“怜子纯孝，是以相救。今日子之耻已雪，而我之事未终，故深夜特来与子议。”十三无难词，直诺之曰：“再生之恩，无不如命。”媪曰：“予居此水数百年，纵横自得。近有无知孽妖强欲夺吾宅，兼思嬲吾女，故遁迹他乡，且多构奇策以御之，其中利害，尚有甚于子所行者。幸为龙主鉴察，屏渠远方，并召我母女。前此邂逅，正予旋归之日也。

    Explanation

    372

    The people on the boat regarded the thirteenth as wise and showed their love and respect for him by competing to buy wine and delicacies to exchange and inquire about each other. However, the thirteenth deeply considered and remembered one thing, fearing potential retaliation, and wanted to return home quickly. Despite this, there was no way, as the distance to home was only one day's journey, and the wind was slightly adverse. By evening, they still anchored at the place where they had met the woman before. The thirteenth recognized the place in his heart and laughed, saying, "Is the Yellow Stone Old Man still here? The one who avenged Han has returned, and one can now travel with the Red Pine." When it was time to sleep, someone knocked on the door and entered directly. Upon seeing him, it was the old woman who had given the letter. Overjoyed, the thirteenth hurried to welcome her, invited her to sit, and thanked her. The old woman said to him, "I pity your pure filial piety, which is why I helped you. Today, your shame has been washed away, but my matter is not yet complete. Therefore, I came especially at night to discuss with you." The thirteenth had no difficulty in agreeing and simply said, "The gratitude of being reborn, I will follow your command without fail." The old woman continued, "I have lived in this river for several hundred years, moving freely and comfortably. Recently, an ignorant and wicked demon has forcefully tried to take over my dwelling and also intends to harass my daughter. Therefore, I fled to another place and devised many strange strategies to defend against it. The dangers and benefits are even greater than what you have experienced. Please, the Dragon Master, investigate and judge, banish the demon to a distant place, and summon my mother and daughter. This previous encounter was the day when I returned."

    373

    因自奇其计，遂以授之于子，果能免患。然予有女在，不遂远游，意将委之于人，非子不可。倘得子之金诺，则我可脱然无累，往朝南海，成其正果矣。”十三闻言，喜出望外，亟以婿礼拜见。媪大悦，起曰：“今夕颇良，可使迨吉者来事夫子。”遂匆匆亟去。未几，侍婢数人捧丽人自外而入，装饰如贵家，绝非向之落魄者。比睨之，果即前者少艾，益慰所怀。于是欢欣与语，女终腼腆不答。其母又来促之寝，乃屏烛下帷，解衣同梦。海棠湿雨，杜鹃啼春，竟不知夜之几何矣。昧爽，媪即至，赠婿以赤金二笏曰：“多与恐汝不任，然已可吃著此生矣。”径留女自返，女亦不甚流连。

    Explanation

    373

    Because he thought his plan was extraordinary, he entrusted it to his son, and indeed it could save him from trouble. However, I have a daughter, so I cannot go on a long journey. I intend to entrust it to someone, but only you are suitable. If I can get your golden promise, then I can be free from all burdens, go to the South Sea, and achieve my true goal. Thirteen, upon hearing this, was delighted beyond expectation and hurriedly paid respects as a son-in-law. The old lady was very pleased and stood up to say, "Today is auspicious, so let the matchmaker come to arrange the marriage." She then hurriedly left. Not long after, several maidservants brought a beautiful woman from outside, dressed like a noblewoman, far different from the previous poor woman. When I looked at her closely, she was indeed the young and beautiful woman from before, which greatly comforted my heart. So we happily conversed, but the girl remained shy and did not respond. Her mother then came and urged her to sleep. So they extinguished the candles, drew the curtains, and undressed to sleep together. The rain-soaked hawthorn blossoms, the cuckoo bird singing in spring, they did not know how late it had become. At dawn, the old lady arrived and gave her son-in-law two pieces of red gold, saying, "If I give you too much, you might not be able to handle it, but this should be enough for your lifetime." She left the girl with him and returned herself, and the girl did not linger.

    374

    妆讫，辄与十三对坐，他人往来皆莫之见。即十三亦倏然不见其妻，乃怪而问之。笑曰：“妾实神人，彼龌龊商人乌能见予哉？”将至乡里，女与十三谋，别舍于外，而使十三驰告于父曰：“舟中皆谓儿智，有客愿以女嫁儿，兼赠重金。彼适有远行，不获亲来讲姻谊，先遣其女于归矣，敢请父命。”崔父闻子言念一事，喜其智足卫身，不负父教，因慨然许之。草创婚仪，令子迎妇归，始合卺焉。邻比闻十三完璧而归，且知念一为所伤，病不能起，皆叹息以为异，竟不疑女之自来。女事翁孝，事夫顺，理家有则，且以药疗崔父之疾，病良已。十三又出金置产，俨然素封。夫妇承欢数载，父殁，遂弃其业，不知所之。唯念一尚在，则已贫病交迫，家无斗筲矣。

    Explanation

    374

    After finishing dressing up, the woman immediately sat facing Thirteen, and others coming and going did not see her. Thirteen also suddenly did not see his wife and found it strange, asking about it. She laughed and said, "I am actually a divine being; how could that dirty merchant see me?" About to arrive at the village, the woman planned with Thirteen, separating herself outside, and sent Thirteen to rush and inform the father, saying, "All on the boat considered the son intelligent; there is a guest who wishes to marry his daughter to the son, also giving a large sum of money. He just now has a long journey, unable to personally discuss marital relations, first sent his daughter to return to her husband's home. Dare to ask the father's command." Cui's father heard the son's words and thought of one thing, happy that his intelligence is sufficient to protect himself, not betraying his father's teachings, therefore sighed and agreed. Hastily prepared the wedding ceremony, ordered the son to welcome the bride home, began to share a wine cup. Neighbors heard that Thirteen returned intact and also knew that Nianyi was injured, ill and unable to get up, all sighed and considered it strange, finally did not doubt that the woman came herself. The woman served her father-in-law with filial piety, served her husband obediently, managed the household with rules, also treated Cui's father's illness with medicine, illness completely cured. Thirteen again spent money to acquire property, solemnly became a wealthy person. Husband and wife enjoyed each other's company for several years; father died, then abandoned his business, did not know where he went. Only Nianyi was still alive, but was already impoverished and ill, with no savings left.

    375

    外史氏曰：余不矜十三之智，而矜十三之孝。且不为十三幸，而每为十三危。何也？向使不遇授书之人，则十三已为砧上之肉，虽智足自卫，又乌能出庖人手耶？抑安知非天鉴其孝，阴令贤母为之造谋，俾其全璧归赵哉？故为贫家之子弟念，与其学保身之十三，母宁为不轻身之十三。苟无十三之孝，念一必将饱饫秀色而归矣。岂仅作门外汉，竟至焦头烂额乎？孝矣哉！十三。危矣哉！亦十三也。

    ○　白云叟
    　　钱塘山水为天下之冠，闻者多不能游，恒引以为憾。宰是邑者，又以功令森严，簿书况瘁，鲜克与湖光山色接晨夕杯酒之欢，亦宦游恨事也。临清卢之椿，以孝廉铨补浙省，去钱塘仅咫尺地，乃为公务所羁，即有事谒院，亦倥偬不及一游，每抱昌黎滕王之感。卢有幕宾，失其姓名，号曰白云叟，异人也。

    Explanation

    375

    The external historian says: I do not boast of the wisdom of Shisan, but I boast of his filial piety. I do not feel fortunate for Shisan, but I always feel worried for him. Why is this so? If Shisan had not met the person who taught him, he would already have been like meat on the chopping block. Even if he had enough intelligence to defend himself, how could he escape the hands of the cook? But who knows if it was not the heavens recognizing his filial piety, secretly instructing a virtuous mother to devise a plan, allowing him to return unharmed? Therefore, for the children of poor families, it is better to learn from the example of Shisan who does not lightly risk his life, rather than from the example of Shisan who focuses on self-preservation. If Shisan did not have filial piety, he would surely return with his desires fulfilled. How could he just remain an outsider, but end up in a disastrous situation? Filial piety indeed! Shisan. Danger indeed! Also Shisan.

    The White Cloud Elder
    The scenery of Qiantang is the finest in the world, but many who hear of it cannot visit, often feeling regret. Those who govern this county are also bound by strict laws and busy with official duties, rarely able to enjoy the beauty of the lake and mountains with the pleasure of morning and evening cups of wine, which is also a regret for those who travel for official duties. Linqing's Lu Zhichun, appointed as a filial and honest official to supplement Zhejiang Province, is only a short distance from Qiantang, but is tied up with official business. Even when he has matters to attend to at the court, he is too busy to make a trip, often feeling the regret of Han Yu and Wang Bo. Lu has a retainer whose name is unknown, called White Cloud Elder, a remarkable person.

    376

    居恒辄谓卢曰：“大丈夫得展骥足，出宰百里，苟非名胜之邦斯已耳，脱遇六桥三竺之胜，不能驾一叶之舟，与画船箫鼓竟逐于湖上，则不免唐突西子矣！”卢深颔其论，然亦密迩如兰亭，接壤如耶溪，犹以一官匏系，不克身亲，而西湖又安望哉？期年，叟忽语卢曰：“君有湖山之兴乎？明日抚公檄下，使君继乐天、坡老之任矣。速理征棹，可遂壮游。”卢以瓜期未及，且政声未登卓异，不信叟言。诘朝，方据堂理事，果有隶以红笺至，则已调补钱塘令矣。乃大喜，服叟先见，因与之谋曰：“君言虽中，我事倍前。三鼓放衙，五夜退食，晦犹奔走于道路，明即伛偻于戟辕。即有淡妆浓抹者，可能驾言出游，以适我愿乎？”叟微笑曰：“君自不欲暇耳！果能概从吾言，即以孤屿为家，以冷泉为室，以净慈灵隐为客座，以两峰一水为园亭，吾未见有废事也。”卢犹未信。及摄篆者至，乃启行抵杭。履任三日，叟即谓于卢曰：“湖船已具，来朝当与君遍游佳胜。

    Explanation

    376

    The old man would often tell Lu, "A man of great virtue should be able to display his talents, to serve as an official in a small administrative area. If it is not a famous and beautiful place, that is enough. But if one encounters the beauty of the six bridges and the three temples, and cannot sail a small boat, competing with painted boats and the sounds of flutes and drums on the lake, one cannot help but offend the beauty of Xi Shi!" Lu deeply agreed with his words, but he was also close to places like the Orchid Pavilion in Shaoxing and the Yexi River, yet still bound by his official duties, unable to personally experience them, and how could he even hope to visit West Lake? After a year, the old man suddenly told Lu, "Do you have a desire for the beauty of the lakes and mountains? Tomorrow, the governor will issue an order, and you will be appointed to follow in the footsteps of Bai Juyi and Su Shi. Quickly prepare for your journey, and you can fulfill your grand tour." Lu, thinking that his term was not yet up and that his reputation had not yet reached a level of excellence, did not believe the old man's words. The next morning, as he was just settling into his duties in the hall, a messenger indeed arrived with a red note, confirming that he had been appointed as the magistrate of Qiantang. He then greatly rejoiced, admiring the old man's foresight, and said to him, "Your words are indeed true, but my responsibilities will be even more numerous than before. I will be releasing officials at three drums, eating late at five nights, running errands on dark nights, and bending over at dawn. Even if there are women lightly dressed or heavily made up, can I possibly go out for a drive to satisfy my wishes?" The old man smiled and said, "You just don't want to take the time! If you truly follow my advice, you can make a solitary island your home, a cold spring your room, the Jingci and Lingyin temples your guest halls, and the two peaks and one water your garden pavilions. I have not seen anything neglected." Lu still did not believe him. When the acting official arrived, he set out for Hangzhou. On the third day of his new role, the old man told Lu, "The lake boat is ready. Tomorrow morning, I will take you to tour the beautiful places together."

    377

    ”卢讶曰：“新硎初试，游刃未能，我与君皆有所司，何暇及此？且令上官闻之，弹章将不远矣。”叟笑曰：“予固谓君不欲暇也。事果有碍，予岂敢以孟浪贻君忧？”卢曰：“然则君将奈何？”叟曰：“君无漏言，翌日仍戒舆马，饬吏役，起居如常。予自能偕君一往。”卢惝恍不定，姑诺之。次日午后，卢方出衙署，将谒抚公，忽舆侧有人启曰：“白云叟先生敬候。”卢不自由身已降舆，见驺从十数，拥一小犊车，驱迓道左。执事甚恭，亟引卢登之。癷然遂发，疾如风雨，不转瞬即出钱塘城门，卢心窃讶焉。自计来竣公事，即作嬉游，益非善策，然已无如之何。甫至湖干，果有大游船舣以相待。卢方下车，叟忽从篷底出。

    Explanation

    377

    Lu Ya said, "It's like a newly sharpened knife, not yet adept at slicing. Both you and I have duties to attend to; how can we spare time for this? Moreover, if our superiors hear about it, they will soon send a reprimand." The old man smiled and said, "I firmly believe you do not wish to have time for this. If there is indeed an obstacle, how dare I cause you trouble with recklessness?" Lu said, "Then what should I do?" The old man replied, "Do not let a word slip out. The next day, prepare the carriage and horses, instruct the officials, and proceed as usual. I will accompany you myself." Lu was in a state of confusion and reluctantly agreed. The next day after noon, as Lu was leaving the government office to visit the governor, suddenly someone at the side of the carriage spoke up, "Mr. Baiyun Elder respectfully awaits." Without realizing, Lu had already stepped down from the carriage. He saw a dozen attendants, escorting a small ox-drawn carriage, meeting them on the side of the road. The attendants were very respectful and quickly guided Lu onto the carriage. The carriage suddenly set off, swift as wind and rain, and in the blink of an eye, they were out of the Qiantang city gate. Lu was secretly surprised. He thought to himself that after finishing official business, he would go on a leisurely trip, which was even less of a good plan, but there was nothing he could do about it. As soon as they arrived at the lakeside, indeed, a large pleasure boat was waiting for them. As Lu was about to step down, the old man suddenly emerged from beneath the awning.

    378

    俟其登舟，即握手笑曰：“吾两人皆有代庖，剩此闲身，不妨作十日游也。”卢茫然视其船，兰桨桂楫，华美异常，惊愕者久之。及入舱中，早有歌儿数辈，明眸皓齿，朱履鲜衣，趋跄乎左右。卢顾问叟曰：“此辈何来？”答曰：“厮养也。”既坐，绮席高张，珍馔杂进，放乎中流，且行且饮。又有美人四五，皆妙选，羽衣星珰，丽容稚齿，自帘中出，为主人捧觞，卢益莫测所自。询之，叟答曰：“家乐也。”卢因笑曰：“先生馆谷于予，颇亦拮据作客，未闻有此。今忽纷致佳丽，诚所不解。”叟微哂曰：“君视寒士如死灰，竟不能一燃耶？适遇贤地主，举此以赠，不敢独享，因愿与君共之。何以疑为？”卢默然。酒行数巡，已至湖心亭，因舍舟登眺。亭中早设锦裀，卢与叟藉地而坐，觥筹交错，歌舞前陈。

    Explanation

    378

    Awaiting his boarding the boat, he immediately shook hands and smiled, saying, "We both have deputies, so we have this remaining leisure time; it is permissible to take a ten-day trip." Lu looked at the boat in bewilderment, its orchid paddles and cassia oars being exceptionally beautiful, and he was astonished for a long time. Upon entering the cabin, there were already several young singers with bright eyes and white teeth, wearing red shoes and fresh clothes, moving gracefully around. Lu asked the old man, "Where do these people come from?" The old man replied, "They are my attendants." After sitting down, a beautifully decorated seating was spread out, and a variety of delicacies were served. They set sail in the middle of the river, drinking while sailing. There were also four or five beautiful women, all chosen with care, wearing feathered clothing and star-like earrings, with young and beautiful appearances, emerging from behind the curtains to present wine to the host. Lu was even more puzzled about their origin. He inquired, and the old man answered, "They are my household entertainers." Lu then smiled and said, "You provide lodging and food to me, and I am quite hard-pressed as a traveler. I have never heard of such things. Now, suddenly, you have gathered these beautiful women, which is truly incomprehensible." The old man slightly smiled and said, "Do you view me as a dead ember, unable to be rekindled? I just happened to meet a good host, and I offered this as a gift. I dare not enjoy it alone, so I wish to share it with you. Why doubt it?" Lu remained silent. After several rounds of drinking, they had already reached the pavilion in the center of the lake, and they left the boat to climb and look around. The pavilion had already been set up with brocade mats, and Lu and the old man sat on the ground, with wine cups and counting sticks interwoven, and dancing and singing performances presented.

    379

    遥望游舸如蚁，泛泛于苏隄之下，或听莺，或观鱼，或凭栏而怀古，或即景而敲诗，前喁后于，左丝右竹，箫管浮水，珠翠迷烟，而南北峰之变态既有万千，西子湖之澄波又复四映，诚人间巨观也。卢至此百虑尽空，一丝莫挂，唯与叟浮白痛饮。良久，叟又邀卢过岳墓，绕南屏，访幽于林逋宅边，探奇于苏小冢侧。侍者皆从，香风里许，见者以为神仙中人。少焉月升于东，照彻万顷，游人尽返，卢亦思归。叟笑曰：“约以经旬，何遽欲返棹耶？”卢曰：“其若官守与衙务何？”叟曰：“渠自能办此，我与君不消逆虑。”乃不听卢，仍返舟，命泊于绝胜之处。对月张筵，开樽重饮，清讴艳舞，眩目醉心，直至酕醄始寝。凌晨，又换小艇，不携裙屐，寻幽索胜，靡所不经。

    Explanation

    379

    From afar, the boats on the lake looked like ants, drifting leisurely along the Su Causeway. Some listened to the songs of the orioles, others watched the fish, some leaned on the railings to reminisce about the past, and others composed poetry inspired by the scenery. In front, people were talking, behind them were the sounds of strings and flutes on either side. Flutes floated on the water, and jade and pearls were lost in the mist. The changing appearances of the mountains in the north and south were myriad, and the clear waves of the West Lake reflected in all directions. Truly, it was a grand sight in the world. Lu felt that all his worries had vanished, not a single thread of concern remained, and he only drank freely with the old man. After a while, the old man invited Lu to visit the tomb of Yue Fei, to walk around Nanping Mountain, to seek seclusion near the house of Lin Bu, and to explore the mysteries near the tomb of Su Xiaoxiao. All the attendants followed, and the fragrant breeze carried them about a mile, making those who saw them think they were celestial beings. Soon after, the moon rose in the east, illuminating the vast lake, and the tourists all returned, but Lu also wanted to go back. The old man smiled and said, "We agreed to stay for ten days, why do you want to return so soon?" Lu replied, "What about my official duties and administrative tasks?" The old man said, "He can handle this himself, you and I don't need to worry." So he didn't listen to Lu and returned to the boat, ordering it to moor at the most beautiful place. They set up a banquet under the moon, opened the wine, and drank again. Clear singing and colorful dancing dazzled the eyes and intoxicated the heart, and they didn't sleep until they were drunk. At dawn, they changed to a smaller boat, not bringing any women, and sought seclusion and beauty, going nowhere they did not explore.

    380

    每至一佳境，即有人设饮，亦不知其谁何。暮归，仍宿于舟中，衾褥之华，供具之备，犹胜衙斋远甚，但不遣美人侍。将卧，即纷然各散，莫解其焉往。私以问叟，笑而不答。自是终日泛湖，或巨舰，或扁舟，山行以骑，陆行以舆，浃旬而足迹殆遍。卢亦沉酣其中，乐而忘返。一夕又饮，漏下已三，叟忽谓卢曰：“摄事者过劳，我辈盍暂归。”卢曰：“门扃矣，归恐不获。”叟曰：“姑尽三爵，有予在。”乃以巨觥进卢，相对大釂。卢不觉沉醉，隐几而卧。及醒而转侧，则宅门已发梆矣。张目审视，身固在书室榻上，仆役趋侍。径起着衣，人亦不以为异。时宅眷犹在旧治，无可与言。方盥濯，有小童奉叟命，以一册进曰：“迩日之事略具其间，请公强记之，庶几应对无失次。

    Explanation

    380

    Every time one reached a fine place, someone would set up a banquet, and one did not know who they were. At dusk, returning to the boat, one still slept on board, with the bedclothes and furnishings being more splendid than in the office far away, but no beautiful women were sent to attend. As one was about to lie down, they would suddenly scatter, and no one could explain where they went. Privately asking the old man, he smiled but did not answer. From then on, one spent the whole day boating on the lake, sometimes on a large ship, sometimes in a small boat, traveling in the mountains by riding and on land by carriage, almost every footstep taken over ten days. One was also deeply immersed in it, enjoying oneself so much that one forgot to return. One evening, after another drink, the night watch had already struck three, and the old man suddenly said to Lu, "Those who manage affairs are overworked; let us return for a while." Lu said, "The door is locked; returning might not be possible." The old man replied, "Just finish these three cups; I am here." He then brought a large cup to Lu, and they drank heartily together. Lu did not realize he was drunk and leaned on the table to sleep. Upon waking and turning over, the gate of the house had already been locked. Opening his eyes and looking around, he was indeed on the bed in the study, with servants attending him. Getting up and dressing, no one thought anything of it. At that time, the family was still in the old residence, and there was no one to talk to. Just as he was washing his hands, a small boy brought a book and said, "The events of recent days are roughly recorded in this book. Please memorize it, so that you can respond without losing order."

    381

    ”卢草草一览，恍然曰：“嘻！予前此殆非身往也。”因秘而不宣，仍出供职，照所登者行之，百不失一。及谒上宪，见同官，皆啧啧赞其明敏，己亦窃笑。乘间以故询之，叟终不肯言。嗣后时一出游，或三日，或五日，虽不若向者之久，而乐亦无殊。以故山水之胜迹，靡所不探。卢以其事近怪，即亲知无敢少泄。未几，眷属至，仍游如前。年余，甫向内子私述之，惊曰：“无怪乎有时如木石也。君自履新任，多宿外寝，妾往觇之，昏睡若无所知，举撼之，亦弗能醒。心窃惧焉，以为鞅掌所致。侵晨即起，莅事如常，又复索解不得。且闻仆言，某先生亦恒如是。今而知此中有术焉。然宜慎之，倘去而不返，妾将奈何？”卢亦微笑而罢。由是事颇漏于署内，人多窥测，叟遂不再相邀。

    Explanation

    381

    Lu glanced over it hastily and suddenly exclaimed, "Ah! Before this, I must have not been there in person." He kept it secret and did not reveal it, still going out to serve his duties, following what he had learned with perfect success. When he visited the higher authorities and met with fellow officials, they all praised him repeatedly for his intelligence and quickness, and he also secretly smiled. Seizing the opportunity to ask about it, the old man finally refused to speak. Afterward, he occasionally went out to travel, sometimes for three days, sometimes for five days, although not as long as before, but the joy was no different. Therefore, he explored every beautiful sight of mountains and rivers. Considering the matter strange, he did not dare to reveal it even a little to his closest friends. Not long after, his family arrived, and he continued to travel as before. After more than a year, he just told his wife privately, and she exclaimed in surprise, "It's no wonder you sometimes act like a statue. Since you took on your new role, you often stayed in the outer bedroom. When I went to check on you, you were sound asleep as if unaware, and when I shook you, you could not wake up. I secretly feared, thinking it was due to your busy schedule. You rose early in the morning, carried out your duties as usual, and could not understand it again. Moreover, I heard the servants say that a certain Mr. So-and-so also acted similarly. Now I know there must be some secret to it. But you should be careful; if you go and do not return, what will I do?" Lu also smiled and let it go. Thus, the matter began to leak within the office, and many people began to speculate, and the old man no longer invited him.

    382

    卢请之，亦不携往，唯曰：“恐夫人滋惧也。”又两月，卢以循良骤擢州牧，叟乃请去曰：“西湖已有东道主，不再司君刀笔矣。”强之行，终不听，遂为买山于湖上，筑茅而居。未数旬，即遁迹不见。卢至州治，所属有邑丞，明察吏也，忽得奇疾，鼾睡辄竟日，至夜深始醒。则曰：“予病矣，为真君召去，代纪钱塘县事，烦剧殊不胜，明晨仍须一往。”言已瞑目，鸡未鸣，早入黑甜，人皆以为异。旬终乃愈。后竟不时举发，一卧数朝，幸不甚久。然叩其颠末，则曰：“真君嘱予勿言，言将有祸。”暨卢下车，丞来庭谒视，卢之左右皆若熟识，且能举其名。卢闻其异与己符，乃屏人与语曰：“君之越俎以代者，实我也。君才实倍于予，行当荐剡，必不使久屈于下僚。”因其言异，相视骇叹。丞果藉卢力，遽绾铜章。但不识为叟捉刀者，又何人耳。

    Explanation

    382

    Lu asked him, but he did not take him along, only saying, "I am afraid it would only increase her fear." Two months later, Lu was promoted to the position of state governor due to his upright and virtuous conduct. The old man then requested to leave, saying, "West Lake already has an East Host, and I will no longer manage your official duties." Despite being urged to leave, he refused to go, and Lu eventually bought a mountain by the lake for him, where he built a thatched house to live. After a few weeks, he disappeared, retreating into obscurity. When Lu arrived at the state administration, there was a deputy county magistrate under his jurisdiction, a clear-sighted official, who suddenly fell ill with a strange disease. He would sleep soundly all day, waking up only at night. When he woke, he said, "I am ill, having been called away by the True Lord to replace you in managing the affairs of Qiantang County. The tasks are too busy and difficult to handle, but I will still need to go back tomorrow morning." After speaking, he closed his eyes and fell into a deep sleep before the rooster had crowed.
