The two scholars laughed at his absurdity and were just about to move when they soon heard someone speaking in the pavilion. The voice sounded strange and eerie, and they were startled and said, "Who is the foolish young man peeping into someone's garden? Does he intend to be a thief?" Listening carefully, it sounded quite like a strong woman. They were greatly frightened, and the young monk quickly pulled them and said, "Go, go! The fox lady's evil has begun; we cannot stay here."

    471

    ”二生皆有惧色，亟反身循其故道，仓皇而返。于路询之，幼僧答曰：“此某贵人废园也，为妖所据，封闭不开。然遇狐夫人不在，犹可游览。今值之，则弗能矣。”二生惊愕久之。及归寓，主僧问：“往后园否？”皆隐讳不敢言。至夜，二生就主僧闲叙，漏下二鼓始散。归行至庑下，月色盈窗，遂不复索火。入室，解衣登榻就寝。主僧突如其来，隔牖询曰：“二君昼游，倘有所闻，可亟言，勿自误。”二生仍坚讳曰：“无。”僧乃去。

    Explanation

    471

    Both young men had fearful expressions and quickly turned back, retracing their old path, returning in a panic. On the way, they asked the young monk, who replied, "This is a ruined garden belonging to a noble, inhabited by a supernatural being, and is sealed off. However, if the Fox Lady is not present, one can still visit. But if one encounters her, then one cannot." The two young men were shocked for a long time. When they returned to their lodging, the main monk asked, "Did you go to the back garden?" They all concealed their answer, unwilling to speak. At night, the two young men chatted with the main monk, and it was past two drums when they dispersed. Returning to the hall, the moonlight filled the window, so they did not light a fire. Entering the room, they unfastened their clothes, climbed onto the bed, and went to sleep. Suddenly, the main monk appeared, asking from behind the window, "If you two heard anything during the day, you can quickly tell me, do not deceive yourselves." The two young men still insisted on denying, saying, "No." The monk then left.

    472

    二生枕上乃哂曰：“此髡何胆怯乃尔？世即有妖魅，敢傍我蟾宫客耶？”竟熟寐。一梦初回，苏先寤，觉怀中温软，仿佛有腻人。疑为李生，而素不谐谑。试以手扪之，肌滑不可着指，乃大惊。张目审视，则二旬少妇，既已衾中同梦矣。因撼之醒，致诘焉。妇惟曰：“敬来荐枕，何问耶？”苏已久旷，莫能致详，须臾衾枕鸣动，好事已成。虽卧榻有人，弗及知欢好。有顷，昏然沉睡。梦中闻李呼声，大诧奇事。及醒，则抱中琵琶，早不知其安往，因询李惊怪之由。李曰：“予梦初觉，枕畔闻鬓云香气，似有妇人共卧。视之，果然。

    Explanation

    472

    Two scholars, lying on their pillows, laughed and said, "What a coward this monk is! If there are any demons in the world, dare they approach us, guests of the moon palace?" They finally fell into a deep sleep. Just waking from a dream, Su woke up first and felt something warm and soft in his arms, as if there was a woman with soft skin. He suspected it was Li, but they never joked around. He tried touching it with his hand, but the skin was smooth and slippery, hard to touch. He was greatly surprised. Opening his eyes and carefully examining, he found a young woman in her twenties, who had already been sharing the bed in a dream. He shook her awake and asked her about it. She only said, "I respectfully came to offer my company, why ask?" Su had been long without a partner, and could not describe it in detail. In a moment, the bed and pillows moved, and the good deed was accomplished. Although there was someone in the bed, he did not know the details of the pleasure. After a while, he fell into a deep sleep. In the dream, he heard Li's voice, and was greatly surprised by the strange event. When he woke up, he found a lute in his arms, and did not know where it had gone. He then asked Li about his surprise. Li said, "I just woke up from my dream, and smelled the fragrance of her hair by the pillow. It seemed like a woman was lying next to me. I looked at it, and indeed, she was there."

    473

    予心微动，既而思功名事重，且女亦不解其何自而来，爰正心以却之。渠乃软款纠缠，百计求合。予正漠然不顾，忽窗隙间巨目如灯，直射室内，呼曰：‘小珍珠不可溷正人，盍归乎来！’其声即昼之所闻者。予甚恐，厉声呼兄，而两俱不见。”苏闻李言，太息曰：“予殆将死乎？不能正心如兄，已为此妖所惑，可奈何？”因缅述其事，泣数行下。李姑宽解之，仍复就枕。及明，李起盥濯，呼苏不应。近视之，眉垂目闭，已溘然长逝矣。李惶悚，急呼主僧。

    Explanation

    473

    My heart stirred slightly, and I then thought of the importance of achieving fame and success. Moreover, the woman did not understand where she came from, so I resolved to reject her with a clear mind. She, however, was persistent and gentle in her attempts to engage, using every possible means to seek union. I remained indifferent and unconcerned, but suddenly, a large eye like a lamp shone directly into the room, and it called out, "Little Pearl, you must not trouble the virtuous. Why don't you return?" The voice was the same as the one I had heard during the day. I was greatly alarmed and called out loudly to my brother, but both the woman and the supernatural entity disappeared.

    Upon hearing Li's account, Su sighed deeply and said, "Am I going to die? I cannot be as upright in my heart as my brother; I have already been bewitched by this demon. What can I do?" He then recounted the story in detail, with tears streaming down his face. Li tried to comfort him and went back to bed. By morning, Li got up to wash, but when she called out to Su, he did not respond. Upon closely examining him, she saw that his eyebrows were drooping and his eyes were closed. He had suddenly passed away. Li was greatly alarmed and urgently called the head monk.

    474

    僧至，见苏暴卒，顿足曰：“不早言，祸果及矣。然存其一，犹幸也。”李请其故。僧曰：“二君所游废园中，有妖曰狐夫人。实非狐也，以其能役狐，故以是称之。狐有小珍珠，小珊瑚等名，恒能魅人，遇之从无生者。闻其摄人精血，供养狐夫人，夫人因为之物色。若遇少年登眺，不遘狐夫人，则可以生，偶尔相遭，弗可活也。”李遂悉陈客夕事。僧曰：“君心正，当有后福。然早与予言，置经一卷于室中，则苏君亦可以无死。”因共相慨息。李市榇殓友，即日徙居南城。是岁竟以俊秀领乡荐。明年携苏柩归浙乡，人多叹惋不置云。

    　　外史氏曰：邪不干正，古语信然。况儒者紧要关头，尤在于正心一语。心既正，则三藏灵经且可不用，何妖之有乎？苟或不然，衾中之温软，既已气绝于鸿沟，枕畔之香云，难免魂消于鸟道。泉路有良朋，桂榜失吉士矣。一死一生，读书养气之功，其浅深于此可见。

    Explanation

    474

    The monk arrived and saw Su die suddenly. He stamped his feet in grief and said, "If you had spoken earlier, the disaster would not have occurred. However, the survival of one is still fortunate." Li asked the reason. The monk said, "In the abandoned garden where the two of you were, there is a fox spirit called Fox Lady. She is not a fox, but she is called Fox Lady because she can control foxes. The fox has names like 'Little Pearl' and 'Little Coral' and is always able to bewitch people; no one who meets her survives. I heard that she captures human essence to nourish Fox Lady, and Fox Lady in turn seeks out victims. If a young man encounters Fox Lady, he is doomed, but if he encounters the fox alone, he may survive." Li then fully recounted the events of the previous evening. The monk said, "Your heart is upright, and you will have future blessings. If you had spoken earlier and placed a scripture in the room, Su would not have died." They then sighed together. Li bought a coffin to bury his friend and immediately moved to the southern city. That year, he was recommended as a talented scholar. The next year, he carried Su's coffin back to his hometown in Zhejiang, and many people sighed in regret.

    The historian said, "Evil cannot overcome virtue, as ancient sayings affirm. Especially for scholars, the key moment is in maintaining a virtuous heart. If one's heart is upright, even the sacred scriptures are unnecessary, for what evil could there be? If not, the warmth and softness of the bed will have been lost across a great divide, and the fragrant clouds by the pillow will inevitably dissipate along the path of the soul. In the realm of the dead, one may find good companions, but in the world of the living, one loses a virtuous scholar. The contrast between life and death reveals the depth of one's spiritual cultivation through reading and learning."

    475

    ○　尸变
    　　涿郡有阴阳家，善妖术。凡里中素封有死者，必重币邀致之，肆筵相款，乃可晏安。否则，祸作矣。某村一富翁，有二子皆入武庠。翁以老病卒，戚族皆言某有异术，务得其来，乃可殓。盖藉以免祸，非仅为送死也。二子亦闻其人，心怵之，果以币往。某适构一宅，不欲出诸已，稔知翁家巨富，将令代为落成。见其币，以为薄，挥去之。翁子又加币亲往，某犹少之，且哂曰：“若非市井傭可以戋戋了事者，必欲予往，非百金不可。”翁子素负气，遂反唇曰：“若勿太作意，死生亦有命在，若能魇我全家殁耶？”乃悻悻而返。戚族咸以为忧。且觅术者，通邑无一人敢应其召，而事不可复挽。闻某与某契，因浼之一行，愿如其数。某闻而笑曰：“渠恃富家郎，尚气遄归，今复求我耶？予观翁死之时日，今夕子亥之交，当有尸变，故索渠重酬，为渠镇伏。既区区者而不予畀，欲我往，非三百金我亦不屑。

    Explanation

    475

    ○　尸变

    In the region of Zhuo, there was a practitioner of the principles of Yin and Yang who was skilled in magical arts. Whenever a wealthy person in the village died, they would be invited with heavy gifts to attend a feast and ensure peace. Otherwise, disaster would follow. In a certain village, a wealthy man had two sons who were both enrolled in the military academy. The man died of old age and illness, and his relatives all said that the practitioner had unusual skills and must be invited to perform the funeral rites to prevent misfortune. It was not just to bury the dead, but to avert disaster. The two sons also heard of the practitioner and were frightened, so they sent gifts to invite him. The practitioner was just building a house and did not want to leave, knowing that the man's family was very wealthy and intended to have the house completed for them. Seeing the gifts, he thought they were too small and dismissed them. The sons of the man then added more gifts and went personally to invite him, but the practitioner still thought it was insufficient and sneered, saying, "If you are not a common laborer who can settle matters with a small amount, then you must want me to come, but it will cost you a hundred gold." The sons of the man, who were always proud, retorted, "Do not be too arrogant! Life and death are matters of fate. Can you really cause the death of my entire family?" And they left angrily. The relatives were all worried. They tried to find a practitioner, but no one in the entire town dared to accept the invitation, and the situation could not be reversed. They heard that the practitioner had a friend and asked him to go instead, offering the same amount. The practitioner heard this and laughed, saying, "He relies on his wealthy sons and left in a huff. Now he wants me again? I have observed that the man will die tonight at the time of the Zi and Hai hours, and there will be a corpse transformation. That is why I asked for a large reward to pacify the situation. Since you offered such a small amount and did not give it to me, if you want me to come, it will cost you three hundred gold, and I will not deign to do it for less."

    476

    ”言已，遂揖某出，曰：“传语诸郎君，性命非若草菅也。”某归述其言，众益忧。尸在床榻，且将腐，有榇不得殓，子心凄楚。不得已而仍议加币，倩某再往。忽众中一人忿然曰：“如此贪饕，情实不甘。予荐一人，或可以蒇事。”众询之，则其人亦业堪舆术甚奇，但为某所掩，技不得售，今居左近，可邀而至。翁子实莫能降心，首从其指，遣人往。不须臾而来，状甚蓝缕，众仆偃蹇不为礼，窃以为误乃公事，必若人。荐者与之言，其人入视亡者，轮指良久，毅然曰：“今辰甚吉，故百无禁忌。”众乃纷述某言，其人笑曰：“是儿固作孽久矣！今若此，是死期亦迫也。某遇异人，得一术，今夜请尝试之。”翁子大喜，许以聘彼者酬此。其人曰：“此事予亦不较，惟冀诸郎君安逸，庶见予非夸大者。”乃索乌碗三，素毫一，丹砂钱许。时已初鼓，其人就烛下书符，碗内蜿蜒如蛇形。嘱曰：“各宜闭户寝，勿惊祸至。予自当之，不相累。

    Explanation

    476

    Having finished speaking, he then bowed and sent me out, saying, "Convey my words to the young men: life is not as cheap as grass." I returned and recounted his words, and the others became even more worried. The body was on the bed and was about to decay, with no coffin to bury it, and the son's heart was filled with sorrow. Unwillingly, they still discussed adding more money and asked me to go again. Suddenly, one person in the crowd angrily said, "So greedy, I really couldn't bear it. I recommend one person, maybe he can finish the matter." They asked him about it, and he said that the person was also very skilled in feng shui, but was overshadowed by someone else, and his skills could not be used. Now he lived nearby and could be invited to come. The old man's son really couldn't bring himself to accept the suggestion, but first followed his advice and sent someone to go. He came back very quickly, looking very ragged. The servants acted arrogantly and did not show respect, secretly thinking it was a mistake in official business, and surely it was a person. The recommender spoke to him, and the person went in to see the deceased, pointing around for a long time, and firmly said, "Today is very auspicious, so there are no taboos." They then recounted what someone said, and the person smiled and said, "This child has been doing evil for a long time! Now he is like this, his death is also near. I met a strange person and learned a technique, tonight, let's try it." The old man's son was very happy and promised to pay him as much as he paid for the other. The person said, "I don't care about this matter, I only hope that the young men can be at ease, so you will see that I am not exaggerating." He then asked for three black bowls, one white brush, and a handful of cinnabar. It was already the first drumbeat, and the person wrote a talisman under the candle, with the bowls inside twisting like snakes. He instructed, "Everyone should close their doors and sleep, do not be alarmed if disaster comes. I will take it upon myself, I will not burden you."

    477

    ”于是裸其上衣，披发跣足，且藏所余丹砂于裩际，猱升而上至屋梁。命人以碗与之，亟麾之曰：“去去！但闻予号，则渠生而我死矣。”众始骇然，悉屏其迹。其人偃息棁侧，更筹倏已两易，窃自念曰：“殆来乎？”俄而村柝将三，寂无声息，亦倦欲寐。欻见灯影幢幢，风声淅淅，骇曰：“至矣！”因凝神起坐。未几，纸衾析析自鸣，尸骸蠕蠕自动，甫转瞬而卧者已起。其人急以碗掷之，盝然震响，应声而仆，心少安。无何，尸又复作。未取碗倏尔离床，又急掷之，又仆地。窃恐其变，注目直视无敢移。忽然崛起，长啸有声，若知梁上人，怒目相属，势将攫拿。其人自揣余此一碗，弗效予亦莫能生已。急取掷之，尸遂颠。冥然许久，窃自以为无患。方转侧欲下，尸又蹶然，较前益暴。至是，其人之技穷矣。震慑间，尸已能步，直逼屋梁，距踊而前。其声呜呜若悲泣，远迩皆闻，其人亦股慄欲坠。

    Explanation

    477

    After the man had stripped off his upper garment, disheveled his hair, and gone barefoot, he skillfully climbed up to the roof beam, hiding the remaining cinnabar in his waistband. He ordered someone to give him a bowl, then quickly waved it away, saying, "Go away! If you hear my cry, then he lives while I die." The crowd was startled and all hid their tracks. The man lay down beside the beam, and the night watchman's gong had changed twice. He secretly thought to himself, "Has it come?" Soon, the village watchman's drum struck three times, and there was complete silence, so he was also tired and wanted to sleep. Suddenly, he saw the lamp shadows swaying and heard the wind rustling, and he was startled, saying, "It has arrived!" He then sat up, concentrating. Shortly after, the paper shrouds rustled, and the corpse wriggled and moved on its own. In the blink of an eye, the one who had been lying down stood up. The man quickly threw the bowl at it, making a loud clattering sound, and it fell with a thud. His heart was somewhat at ease. Soon, the corpse rose again. It suddenly left the bed, and the man threw the bowl at it again, causing it to fall once more. Fearing its transformation, he stared intently without moving. Suddenly, it stood up, let out a long howl, as if aware of the man on the beam, glaring at him with fury, ready to seize and grab. The man thought to himself that with this last bowl, if he did not act, he would not be able to survive. He quickly threw the bowl, and the corpse fell. It remained motionless for a long time, and he secretly thought he was safe. As he turned to descend, the corpse suddenly rose again, even more violently than before. By now, the man's skills were exhausted. In his state of shock, the corpse could already walk, approaching the roof beam, jumping and advancing. Its sound was mournful, like a cry of sorrow, heard far and wide, and the man shook with fear, almost falling.

    478

    少顷，尸至梁下，仰首奋身，疾如飞隼，将以手攫其裩。其人大怖，因思非我则伊，势不俱生，遂摸腰间丹砂，幸尚在，乃悉纳于吻，复自啮其舌，和血噀之。尸不能支，颓然而倒，且大呼曰：“予与若无郄，何竟死我耶？”遂无声。其人徐徐转动，而筋骸麻痹，有类不仁。已而鸡鸣，众来入视，尸已不在榻，乌碗瓦碎，散布如星，因相与咋舌。其人方下取衣着之，且命众舁尸还故处。语之曰：“亟往觇，某人死矣。

    Explanation

    478

    A short while later, the corpse arrived at the beam, raised its head and strained its body, moving as swiftly as a falcon, about to grab his pants with its hands. The man was greatly terrified and thought that either he or she must die, as they could not both live. He then felt in his waistband for the cinnabar, fortunately still there, and swallowed it all, bit his tongue, and spat out the blood. The corpse could not support itself and collapsed, and shouted, saying, "I have no quarrel with you, why did you end up killing me?" and then there was silence. The man slowly moved, but his muscles and bones were paralyzed, as if he were dead. Soon after, the rooster crowed, and the others came in to look. The corpse was no longer on the bed, the black bowl and broken pottery were scattered like stars. They all gasped in amazement. The man then went down to get dressed and ordered the others to carry the corpse back to its original place. He told them, "Hurry and check, so-and-so is dead."

    479

    ”众如其言往侦之，噭啕之声，果已达于墙外。盖某至夕不见人至，恨恨曰：“若藐我，我必报之，岂术犹有神于我者乎？”乃抱忿而寝，比及五鼓，其妻闻夫大呼云云，即翁尸之所言也，抚之，气绝，阖室惊惶。此时已就衣衾矣。侦者归述其异，咸诧叹。翁子遂举金酬某人，尸乃入木。后某之子有所闻，因以其状首之官，讼某以术杀其父，官竟不之直。某死未逾年，妻淫子赌，家产荡然。惟某以此闻于人，咸异其术，至今名噪一郡，已富埒素封矣！

    Explanation

    479

    The crowd followed his words and went to investigate. The sound of loud weeping indeed reached outside the wall. It was said that the magician, upon not seeing anyone come by at night, angrily declared, "If you despise me, I will surely retaliate. Is there still a more powerful magic than mine?" He went to bed with resentment. By the fifth drumbeat, his wife heard her husband shouting, which was exactly what the old man's corpse had said. She touched him, and he lost his breath. The whole household was in a state of panic. At that time, the body was already dressed in clothes and wrapped in quilts. The investigators returned and recounted the strange events, and all were amazed and sighed. The old man's son then presented gold to the magician as a reward, and the body was finally buried. Later, the magician's son heard something and reported it to the authorities, accusing the magician of using magic to kill his father. The official did not find him guilty. Within a year of the magician's death, his wife committed adultery, and his son gambled, ruining the family property. However, the magician's reputation spread far and wide, and he became as famous as the richest families in the prefecture, and is still renowned today.

    480

    外史氏曰：甚矣哉，人之贪也。亡者本无事，而故诈其财。百金亦云厚矣，犹靳之，渐增至三百。吾知即以三百往，彼人之心当未尽餍耳。迨至人极计生，捷足者起，不独褫其魄而毙之，且如扼其吭而夺之。贪壑未满，遗产如焚，又不止术之杀身也。

    ○　黄灏
    　　太学士黄灏，吴中之巨富也。受知于某邑宰，欲报未由，恒以为歉。宰好内，后房佳丽数人，犹未足以满其志。黄知之，多方罗致，将假比翼为衔环，而物色尚未有得。一日盛夏，巡行田亩，见一美妇人，被服罗縠行。日中丝丝，见肤莹白如玉，心窃喜，突然询曰：“视若之衣，谅非寒素之家，胡为独行草露之间？岂不畏桑濮之讥耶？”妇闻之，色似不怿。乃以美目睨黄而答曰：“何处轻薄郎，强预人家事？此非汝所宜问也。”言已，径穿沟洫而去，不复顾。黄惭而退，然窃计得此，以献我公，当不啻琼瑶之报。

    Explanation

    480

    The Historian said: How great indeed is the greed of man! The deceased had no cause for death, yet they were deceived to obtain their wealth. A hundred gold pieces is already a generous sum, yet they still hesitated, gradually increasing the amount to three hundred. I know that if they were given three hundred, the person's heart would not have been fully satisfied. Until someone came up with a plan, the swift-footed one acted, not only stripping them of their spirit and killing them, but also strangling them and taking their wealth. The greedy pit was not filled, the inheritance was as if burned, and it was not just a matter of the technique causing death.

    ○ Huang Hao
    The Grand Secretary Huang Hao was a great wealthy man from Wu. He was known by a certain county magistrate, who wished to repay him but had no way to do so, always feeling ashamed. The magistrate enjoyed the company of several beautiful women in his household, but it was not enough to satisfy his desires. Huang knew this and sought to gather them, intending to use the metaphor of "two birds flying together" and "repaying a debt with a ring" to achieve his goal, but he had not yet found anyone suitable. One hot summer day, while inspecting the fields, he saw a beautiful woman walking, dressed in fine silk. In the sunlight, her skin shone as white as jade. He secretly rejoiced and suddenly asked, "Seeing your clothes, I assume you do not come from a poor family. Why are you walking alone in the fields? Do you not fear the criticism of 'Sang and Pu'?" The woman heard this and seemed displeased. She looked at Huang with beautiful eyes and replied, "Who are you, a frivolous young man, to meddle in my affairs? This is not something you should ask." After she spoke, she walked straight through the ditches and did not look back. Huang was ashamed and withdrew, but he secretly planned to obtain her and present her to the magistrate, believing that it would be no less than a precious jade in return.

    481

    第未稔其何如人，无自而致之，怅怅而已。翌日，又遇之垄畔，则珠泪盈颊，形色仓皇，不复若昨之暇豫。黄不自禁，又揖而询之。妇始肃容曰：“予事非贵人莫能了，睹君状貌，似与贵人有素者，盍腼厚颜一陈颠末。”黄亟叩其故，妇曰：“予家距此五里，夫早殁，徒有翁在。予父母则居东乡，怜予少，欲归而嫁之。昨自母家还，以此意告翁，翁竟悍不许。且使予往告父母曰：‘若女能嫁邑尊，或不得已而听之。其他，必以讼。’予思父母一齐民耳，何能识邑尊？君果有素，幸为一言，无敢忘。”黄大喜，适符所愿。乃毅然曰：“邑尊，吾师也。

    Explanation

    481

    Just because I am not yet familiar with what kind of person he is, there is no way to bring about [a resolution], and I am only feeling disappointed. The next day, I encountered her again by the roadside, and she had tears like pearls streaming down her cheeks, looking hastily and panicked, no longer like yesterday's leisurely state. Mr. Huang could not help himself and bowed respectfully to ask her. The woman then said with a serious expression: "My matter cannot be resolved unless a noble person does it. Seeing your appearance, it seems you are familiar with noble people. Why not, with an embarrassed but bold face, tell the whole story?" Mr. Huang hurriedly asked the reason. The woman said: "My home is five miles from here. My husband died early, and only my grandfather is left. My parents live in the eastern village. They love me because I am young and want to return and marry me. Yesterday, I returned from my mother's home and told my grandfather this intention. Grandfather finally stubbornly refused. And sent me to tell my parents, saying, 'If your daughter can marry the magistrate, or if there is no other choice, accept it. Otherwise, we will use a lawsuit.' I think my parents are just common people. How can they know the magistrate? If indeed you are familiar with him, please do a favor and speak on my behalf. I will not dare to forget." Mr. Huang was greatly pleased, as it exactly matched his wish. He then said resolutely: "The magistrate is my teacher."

    482

    谨当任之，谅渠田舍翁，何能为？但邑尊綦贵，子虽美，恐不能正位，可若何？”妇因破涕为笑曰：“蒲柳之姿，得备金钗之数亦足矣，敢奢望耶？”黄益喜，力任其事，妇乃申约而去。黄即日星驰，入谒邑宰面陈。宰固渔色无厌者，闻亦欣悦，但虑娶部民妻女，有玷官箴，未敢许可。黄为之谋曰：“门生假父师之名，以镇伏乡愚；父师亦假门生之名，以成全好事。与其父母言，则门生自娶；与其夫家言，则临以父师。事纵灭裂，证者有人，无虑也。”宰喜从其计。黄甫归家，妇即来侦探，示以宰意，无不敬从。明日偕其父母来，授以百金，成券而退。

    Explanation

    482

    Huang took on the responsibility, thinking that the man was just a simple countryman and could not do much. However, the magistrate was of high rank, and although the woman was beautiful, he feared she might not be a suitable match, so what should they do? The woman stopped crying and smiled, saying, "With my humble appearance, to be able to count among the gold hairpins is enough; how dare I have such high expectations?" Huang was delighted and took on the matter with great effort. The woman made an agreement and left. Huang traveled quickly to meet the magistrate in person. The magistrate, who was insatiable in seeking beauty, was also pleased upon hearing this, but he was concerned that marrying a woman from his jurisdiction would tarnish his reputation, so he hesitated to give his consent. Huang proposed a plan: "The student can pretend to be a father-teacher to pacify the ignorant people in the village; the father-teacher can also pretend to be a student to complete a good deed. When speaking to the parents, the student will marry; when speaking to the husband's family, the father-teacher will be present. Even if things go wrong, there are witnesses, so there is no need to worry." The magistrate was pleased and agreed to the plan. Huang just returned home, and the woman came to investigate, showing him the magistrate's intentions, which she respected. The next day, she came with her parents, and Huang gave her a hundred gold pieces. They made a contract and left.

    483

    薄暮，妇即自至，携一小箧，封识甚密。然自始至终，究未与其翁晤。黄亦怙势，坦然无疑，更为妇制衣饰，约费数百缗，始择吉送之赴署。宰见其色，果异寻常，深以黄为感。及夕入房，情好綦笃。晨起视之，忽更一人，姿貌亦甚平等。宰大骇，诘之，乃泣诉曰：“妾本黄监生之姬也，昨看新人升舆，被渠捽妾同来。公入室，渠即遁去。公强妾宿，遂承恩宠，欲辩不能，惟有啜泣而已。”宰益惊，踌躇良久，亟命舆送之归。且诡言曰：“公昨染寒疾，遂虚洞房。今晨始见其异，敬以完赵，弗敢留。”黄见姬大惊，亟往视妇，则晓妆甫毕，对镜簪花，果宛在室中。

    Explanation

    483

    At dusk, the woman arrived on her own, carrying a small chest, which was sealed very tightly. However, from beginning to end, she never met her father-in-law. Huang, relying on his power, acted calmly and without doubt, and made clothes and adornments for his wife, estimating the cost at several hundred strings of cash. He then chose a propitious day to send her to the office. The official saw her appearance, which was indeed different from ordinary, and deeply felt grateful to Huang. By evening, he entered the room, and their feelings were extremely close. In the morning, he got up and suddenly saw someone else, whose appearance was also quite ordinary. The official was greatly startled and questioned her. She cried and told him, "I am originally Huang's concubine. Yesterday, I saw the new bride ascend the sedan chair, and he forcibly took me along. When you entered the room, he immediately fled away. You forced me to stay the night, thus receiving your favor. I wanted to argue but could not, only crying helplessly." The official was even more surprised and pondered for a long time. He immediately ordered a sedan chair to send her back and lied saying, "You were infected with a cold yesterday, thus the bridal chamber was empty. This morning, I first noticed the difference. Respectfully, I returned her to Zhao and dared not keep her." Huang saw his concubine and was greatly surprised. He hurriedly went to see his wife, who had just finished her morning makeup, was combing her hair in front of the mirror, and indeed she was in the room.

    484

    黄甚恚，面数之曰：“予以何术摄我爱姬？几令予不复为人？要还予金，子自去。”妇闻言略不为意，徐答曰：“聊与君戏，何怒也？公舆尚在，予今即请亲往。”遂冉冉出门，登舆而去。黄虑其再逞，遍视眷属，无少缺，因共键所居之户，固自以为无患。诘朝，方兀坐，其叔自外入，吽吽然突以首触黄，瞋目大呼曰：“汝害我，誓不与汝俱生。”黄茫然不解其故，长跽请教。叔忿定，始言曰：“予年半百，止一女，将以赘婿养此终身。

    Explanation

    484

    Huang was extremely angry and confronted her face-to-face, saying, "What method did you use to capture my beloved concubine? You almost made me lose my humanity. Return my money, and you may leave." The woman paid little attention to his words and calmly replied, "I was just playing a trick on you. Why are you angry? The sedan chair is still here. I will go in person now." She then slowly left the door, mounted the sedan chair, and departed. Huang feared that she might act again, so he looked over his family members and found nothing missing. They all locked the door to her residence and thought they were safe. The next morning, as Huang sat alone, his uncle came in from outside. He roared and suddenly hit Huang with his head, glaring and shouting loudly, "You have harmed me. I swear I will not live with you." Huang was confused and did not understand the reason, so he kneeled down and asked for an explanation. The uncle calmed down in anger and began to speak, "I am fifty years old, and I have only one daughter. I will have a son-in-law to support her for life."

    485

    汝以何妖术，送入县衙，致令狼藉而归，不堪适人，非害我而何？”黄始惊愕，心知妇所为，而姑先叩其状。叔曰：“予已就衰，起甚迟。将出门他往，忽见一舆爰止户外，启视之，则吾女也。持予泣告，言方对镜簪花，被汝强致之舆中。瞬息至一处，富丽不似民居。饲以美食，、饮以醇酒，夜则有官人来共寝处。予询其仪表，则邑尊耳。又言官人至旦，见而怪之曰：‘咄咄！此又异事。’略诘数言，即令驾舆送之还。非汝害之，又谁咎！”黄乃述妇之妖异，举家证之，叔始缄口陨涕。黄以温言劝慰，叔甫去而妇即来。入室笑曰：“黄官人，汝之如君，果无瑕之璧耶？”黄顿悟宰之诳已，忿气中激，将致命于妇。

    Explanation

    485

    "What kind of magic did you use to send me to the county office, causing me to return in a disgraceful state, unfit for marriage? What else could it be but harm to me?" Huang was initially startled, knowing in his heart what the woman had done, but temporarily inquired about the details. His uncle said, "I am already in decline and rise very late. As I was about to leave for somewhere else, I suddenly saw a carriage stop outside the door. I opened to see it, and it was my daughter. She held me and wept, telling me that she was just about to put flowers in her hair when you forcibly took her into the carriage. In an instant, we arrived at a place that was luxurious and unlike a common household. I was fed with fine food and given strong wine to drink. At night, an official came to share the bed. I asked about his appearance, and he was the county magistrate. He said until dawn, 'Dōu dōu! This is another strange matter.' After briefly questioning her a few words, he immediately ordered the carriage to take her back. If it wasn't you who caused this, who else could be blamed?" Huang then recounted the woman's strange behavior, and the whole family confirmed it. The uncle finally fell silent and shed tears. Huang tried to comfort him with gentle words, but as soon as he left, the woman came in. She entered the room and said with a smile, "Mr. Huang, you are like a flawless jade. Is that truly the case?" Huang suddenly realized that the magistrate had deceived him, and anger flared up within him. He intended to kill the woman.

    486

    妇不顾，笑入所居，取其箧以付黄曰：“持此赴县，委折尽明，予不屑絮言也。”拂衣径出，早有肩舆候于门，妇即乘之，旋失所在。黄深以为异，访诸东村，亦并无其父母。明日赍箧往邑中，将共验之。抵署，吏役喧传宰病不能视事，黄诘其由，益骇然。盖宰屡遭讹误，心觉其妖，遂扃新妇之室。宰至晚偶过其傍，忽见妇靓妆曼立，招之曰：“甫承雨露，即弃妾如遗。秋风团扇之诗，独不能为公作耶？”言次，巧笑承迎。宰不能自主，与之偕入，解衣交欢，枕席之情远胜畴昔。将曙犹拥之酣寝，陡觉臂痛如割，张目视焉，则一狞毛巨犬，盞目豺口，正在抱中。惊绝亟起，将出户，户实反扃。

    Explanation

    486

    The woman did not look back, smiling as she entered her residence, took her box, and handed it to Huang, saying, "Take this to the county office, and everything will be clearly explained. I do not care to explain further in detail." Wiping her sleeves and walking out directly, there was already a sedan chair waiting at the door. She got into it and soon disappeared without a trace. Huang found it deeply strange and visited the eastern village, but there were no signs of her parents. The next day, he carried the box to the county town to verify its contents. Arriving at the office, the officials and servants loudly spread that the magistrate was ill and could not handle affairs. Huang questioned the reason and became even more shocked. The magistrate had been deceived many times and felt that it was supernatural, so he locked the new wife's room. The magistrate, just received the rain and dew, and immediately treated me like a cast-off object, and asked if he could not write a poem about the autumn wind and a fan for her. While speaking, she smiled and welcomed him. Shocked to the point of disbelief, he quickly got up, intending to leave the house, but the door was actually locked.

    487

    犬逐之，白身绕屋而走，被创无数。幸婢媪闻其号，破扉相救。犬先夺门出，有识之者，则吏典之猎狗也。宰惊定痛生，卧不能起。黄因请入，见视于榻前，言及前事，相对赧然。黄出箧共启之，中无所有，唯尺素一幅，大书曰：“我本南山狐，偶来尘世内。蓦遇胁肩徒，强入参昴队。赚尔资百金，劳我心三昧。一污画屏姬，再戏金闺妹。受者尚无伤，令与眠盠配。以色悦长官，应得风流秽。居位思邪行，当遭韩卢吠。劝君各洗心，良言莫予怼。长歌归去来，不复语汝辈。”宰与黄览讫，皆汗下湿衣。后宰迁官，黄遂以叔妹嫁宰，以结其未结之局。嗣是，足不履公门，竟以中寿终。狐之棒喝亦灵矣！

    　　外史氏曰：献笑乞怜，已邻于妾妇之道，又复借妾妇以博其欢心，黄之不肖甚矣！狐子可儿，即以其妾与妹为赝鼎。苟具人性者，能不愧怍死耶？第狐性皆畏狗，此独能役之，则又狐之仙，而非啖鸡之狸可比也。

    Explanation

    487

    A Fox's Warning

    A dog chased it, and it ran around the house with its body exposed, suffering countless wounds. Fortunately, the maidservants heard its cries and broke open the door to rescue it. The dog first broke through the door to escape, and those who recognized it knew it was the hunting dog of the official. The official was shocked, then pain set in, and he lay unable to rise. Huang then requested to enter and examined him at the bedside. He mentioned the previous events, and they both blushed in embarrassment. Huang took out a box and opened it together. There was nothing inside, only a small piece of silk, on which was written in large characters:

    "I am originally a fox from the southern mountains, accidentally came into this worldly realm. Suddenly encountered sycophantic followers, forcibly joined the ranks of the stars. Deceived you into giving a hundred gold, tortured my heart in the process. Once defiled the lady on the painted screen, again played with the lady in the golden chamber. The recipients were not harmed, ordered them to marry with the sleeping beauty. To please the official with beauty, should be considered debauchery. Those in power who think of wicked deeds, will be barked at by the hunting dog. Advise you to cleanse your hearts, do not resent the good advice. Sing a long song of return, no longer speak to you."

    The official and Huang finished reading it, both drenched in sweat. Later, the official was promoted, and Huang then married his niece to the official, to settle the unfinished business. After this, he no longer stepped into the public offices, eventually dying at an average age. The fox's warning is also effective.

    The Outer Historian said: Offering laughter and begging for pity, already close to the ways of a concubine, and again using the concubine to win favor, Huang's lack of virtue is evident! The fox is clever, using his concubine and sister as a counterfeit. If one has human nature, how can they not feel ashamed to death? However, all foxes fear dogs, but this one can command them, making it a divine fox, unlike the cat that eats chickens.

    488

    ○　徐小三
    　　京有歈者，其名曰小三，本徐姓之子，貌韶秀，妩媚动人。其父在日，不使歌。父卒，无以赡母，乃就师学之。甫一缓颊，则缠头盈坐，名噪一时。师惜其音，跬步曾不离，深恐为浮浪者诱。以故年十五犹从师宿，非昼不得归。一日，小三之外氏以病卒，其母力请于师，始听其往。然犹亲与之偕，若怀宝者，亦极其慎重之意。外氏居郭外，距城犹十数里，小三至，已旁午，哭奠者久之。外家久不晤甥，强之信宿，师不许，小三亦不敢留，仅饱晚餐，匆遽而返。及出门，则已夕阳西下矣。其出也本以车，比及归途，马忽偾辕，遂不克乘坐。师弟徒步以前，颇蹇缓。天早就瞑，悬揣鱼钥已下，其师颦蹙曰：“门阖矣，将安归？”小三亦云：“无已，思投旅舍。”而去关犹迂，乃疾行。未及里许，夜已初鼓，时正下弦，无月色，阴晦异常。

    488

    Little San of the Xu Family

    In the capital, there was a singer named Little San, originally a son of the Xu family, who was handsome, graceful, and charming. While his father was alive, he did not allow him to sing. After his father's death, Little San had no means to support his mother, so he sought out a teacher to learn singing. As soon as he opened his mouth to sing, the audience showered him with gifts, and his fame spread widely for a time. The teacher valued his voice and never left his side, not even for a step, deeply fearing that loose characters might corrupt him. Therefore, even at fifteen, he still stayed with the teacher, and he could not return until daytime. One day, Little San's maternal uncle died of illness. His mother strongly pleaded with the teacher, and the teacher finally allowed him to go. But still, the teacher accompanied him personally, like someone carrying a treasure, also showing extreme caution. The maternal uncle lived outside the city, and it was still several miles from the city. Little San arrived, and it was already noon. He mourned and paid respects for a long time. The maternal uncle's family had not seen their nephew for a long time and insisted he stay for two nights. The teacher did not agree, and Little San also dared not stay. He only had a full dinner and hurriedly returned. By the time he left, the sun had already set. He originally went out in a carriage, but on the way back, the horse suddenly stumbled and fell, so he could no longer ride. The teacher and his disciples walked ahead, quite slow. It got dark early, and he guessed that the fish-shaped lock had been lowered. The teacher frowned and said, "The gate is closed, where will we go?" Little San also said, "There is no other choice, let's seek lodging." But the road to the inn was still far, so they hurriedly walked. Before they had walked a mile, it was already the first drumbeat of the night. It was the waning moon phase, there was no moonlight, and the weather was unusually dark and gloomy.

    489

    遥望表道之树，恍惚如人形，小三大怖，紧依其师肘下，莫敢远离。正仓皇乱步，俄见火光闪灼，穿林而来。及近视之，则一人执碗灯，青衣矮帽，绝类优人所扮之苍头。师弟皆大惊，稔知为鬼，欲避之。其人瞥见小三，若熟识，直前执之曰：“逋逃儿，亦得相遇耶？亟随我去见都尉，予为汝受创深矣！”言次径挽之行，疾如风雨。小三惧而啼，其师不及夺，亦不及追，转瞬间形声已渺，有懊丧而已。乃其人携小三行，俄顷至一处，始慰之曰：“若勿怖，此间大好，远胜汝家。”小三甫敢展视，则朱垣碧瓦，仿佛王侯。小三素游大家，亦不以为意，惟震恐逋逃一言，且虑归受鞭扑，他无所戚。行及门庭，闬闳高大，官吏蚁聚，灯火星罗，锦衣花帽之人难以仆数。见其人皆声诺，亦略不顾瞻，径引小三入。履限数重，至一堂，庭燎辉煌，仰见匾额题曰“仪凤双栖”。小三固不解为何所，第见晶帘垂地，画栋凌云，文窗玉映，烛焰生辉。有顷，帘中似有人影。

    Explanation

    489

    From afar, the protagonist saw a tree on the road, which appeared vaguely like a human figure. Frightened, he clung tightly to his teacher's arm, daring not to stray far. As he was in a state of panic and running aimlessly, he suddenly saw flickering firelight passing through the forest. Upon getting closer, he saw a person holding a bowl-shaped lamp, dressed in green clothes and a low hat, resembling a servant portrayed by actors. Both the teacher and the protagonist were greatly shocked, knowing well that it was a ghost, and they wanted to avoid it. The person caught a glimpse of the protagonist, as if familiar, and seized him, saying, "Fleeing child, can we meet again? Hurry and follow me to see the magistrate; I have been deeply hurt for you!" While speaking, he pulled the protagonist along, moving as swiftly as wind and rain. The protagonist, frightened and crying, was unable to snatch himself away, nor could his teacher chase after him. In an instant, the figure and sound vanished, leaving only disappointment. The person then led the protagonist away, and shortly reached a place, comforting him, "Do not be afraid; this place is very good, far surpassing your home." The protagonist, who was accustomed to visiting wealthy households, did not take it seriously, but he was still shocked by the word of fleeing and worried about being beaten upon returning home. As they reached the gate and courtyard, the gateways were tall and grand, with officials gathering like ants, lights and torches spread out like stars, and people in brocade clothes and colorful hats too many to count. Seeing those people, they all responded with a nod, paying little attention, and led the protagonist inside. Passing through several thresholds, they reached a hall, where the courtyard torches were bright and splendid. Looking up, he saw a signboard inscribed with "The Phoenixes Nesting Together." The protagonist did not understand what this place was, but he saw crystal curtains hanging to the ground, painted beams reaching the clouds, carved windows reflected like jade, and candle flames emitting light. After a while, he saw a human figure inside the curtain.

    490

    俄而乐作，箫管敖曹，堂上屦满。旋有一宫装妇人搴帘问曰：“歌者来乎？”其人亟对曰：“来矣！”即把小三之袂以付之，已乃趋出。小三随妇入，帘内庭中设二筵：一南向，一西向。南向坐一人，珠翠重遮，云衣半露，如庙中所塑之圣姥。西向亦一人，则金貂朱紫，状若古之勋戚。左右皆美女，凡十数人，或执乐器，或持酒具，俱肃然无声。妇人命小三俯伏参谒，侧坐者询曰：“闻汝善歌，汝能歌几何？”小三股慄不能答。正坐者遽命赐酒，少壮胆力，其音娇细，略可辨曰：“粉侯勿相吓，此子之心，尚摇摇如悬旌也。”已而果有二鬟，披发垂髫，年约十三四，一捧樽，一承榼，笑而觞之。其一衣杏红衫，淡绿裙，貌尤姣好。小三睨之，心微动，威仪之下赐爵，弗敢辞，乃跪而饮之。衣杏红衫者低语曰：“今系贵王千秋寿，曲弗可少也。”遂趋去，回眸匿笑，意颇相怜，小三益觉倾慕。

    Explanation

    490

    Soon after the music began, the sound of flutes and pipes filled the air, and the hall was crowded with guests. A woman dressed in imperial attire lifted the curtain and asked, "Has the singer arrived?" The person quickly replied, "Yes, she has!" and immediately took the sleeve of Xiao San to hand her over, then hurried out. Xiao San followed the woman inside, and within the curtain, the courtyard was set with two tables: one facing south and one facing west. Facing south sat a woman heavily adorned with pearls and jade, her cloud-like robes half-visible, resembling a statue of a revered female deity in a temple. Facing west sat another person, dressed in gold fur and purple clothing, looking like a noble or high-ranking official from ancient times. Around them were several beautiful women, about ten in total, some holding musical instruments and others carrying wine vessels, all silent and solemn. The woman commanded Xiao San to kneel and pay respects. The person sitting to the side asked, "I've heard you are good at singing. How many songs can you sing?" Xiao San trembled with fear and could not answer. The person seated upright quickly ordered wine to be served. With youthful courage and strength, the voice was soft and delicate, barely discernible as it said, "Lady, do not frighten this young man. His heart is still fluttering like a flag in the wind." Soon after, two young girls appeared, their hair loose and hanging down, around thirteen or fourteen years old. One held a wine cup, and the other a wine jug, and they smiled as they served the wine. One of them wore a light red shirt and a pale green skirt, and her appearance was particularly charming. Xiao San glanced at her, and her heart slightly moved. Under the authority of the dignitary, she was given a drink, which she dared not refuse, and knelt to drink it. The girl in the light red shirt whispered, "Today is the celebration of the Prince's birthday, and a song cannot be lacking." She then hurried away, glancing back with a hidden smile, showing a hint of affection. Xiao San felt even more attracted to her.

    491

    酒力微酣，胆气顿壮，舞蹈向上，抗喉一歌，细如啭莺，高若鸣鹤，堂上皆抚掌称妙。曲终三阕，胥寿词。中坐者益喜，嘤咛而言曰：“是儿何善解人意？”顾侍者以白金二锭赉之。小三顿首以谢，起请贵人命，因历数曲名。西向者曰：“汝任汝意，我固未之前闻。”小三性狡狯，乃择其素所擅场及家筵可讴者，随意歌之。每一曲，辄称善。时夜已过半，中座者微有倦容，乃顾侧席欠伸曰：“粉侯且自娱乐，予体惫矣！”侧坐者曰：“今因贵主诞辰，特以介眉，余兴未尽，何遽起也？”固强之终席，复歌二曲。因对主曰：“是儿若无所羁，必思归，盍筹所以留之。”主曰：“君将奈何？”答曰：“观渠聪慧，当非不知情事者。若以一婢为藕丝，则鲲鹏之翅尚可缚，况燕雀乎？惟贵主命之。”主笑曰：“是儿好大福，驸马为汝执柯矣！”因顾小三曰：“都尉将使人伴汝，汝盍自择，勿致怨月老不公。”遂命侍者环列，听其自觅佳偶。

    Explanation

    491

    The child, slightly intoxicated, felt his courage grow. He danced upward and sang with a raised voice, his voice as delicate as a nightingale and as high-pitched as a crane. Everyone in the hall clapped and praised his performance. After singing three songs, the master of the ceremony said, "This child truly understands people's intentions!" He then gave the child two pieces of white gold as a reward. The child bowed deeply to thank him and then asked the noble person for permission to list the names of the songs. The person facing west said, "You can sing whatever you want; I have never heard these songs before." The child, being clever and mischievous, chose songs that he was skilled at and suitable for family gatherings, and sang them at will. Each song was praised. By the time the night had passed half, the person in the middle showed signs of fatigue and yawned, saying, "The Princess, please enjoy yourself; I am tired." The person on the side said, "Today is the Princess's birthday, let's celebrate; our fun isn't over yet, why get up so soon?" They insisted on staying and sang two more songs. Then, addressing the princess, he said, "If this child has no restraint, he will surely want to go home; let's think of a way to keep him." The princess replied, "What do you suggest?" He answered, "Seeing his intelligence, he must know about matters. If we give him a maid as a bond, even the wings of a great bird can be bound, let alone a small one. Only the Princess can decide." The princess laughed and said, "This child has great fortune; the Prince will be your matchmaker!" She then turned to the child and said, "The Marshal will send someone to accompany you; choose for yourself, don't blame the matchmaker for being unfair." She ordered the attendants to stand in a circle, allowing the child to choose a good match.

    492

    众鬟均喜小三，腼然鹄立。小三谢讫，就众中谛观殆遍，独指一人曰：“窃欲此，未敢以为可也。”众视之，则衣杏红衫者。堂上粲然，主与驸马亦笑曰：“是儿固留意久矣。”乃命于堂侧小室设床帐，使定情焉。遂皆起，侍人以绛纱笼烛导引而去，只留杏红衫者伴小三。小三请其名，赧颜答曰：“我贵主贴身宫婢四喜也。素承恩宠，左右弗离，今以之犒子，赏亦厚矣！”言未已，向者宫装之妇又偕二婢以衾枕来，笑曰：“雏儿亦竟能成对哉？可喜！可喜！”铺设欲返，喜使小三拜之曰：“此宫中刘院君，妾之恩母也。”小三拜之如婿礼，刘大喜，含笑而去。小三为喜解衣，喜小语曰：“妾尚幼，固不谙此。”小三笑曰：“予亦试为之，谁谙耶？”相携就枕，潦草成欢，然已海棠红褪矣。

    Explanation

    492

    The young attendants all favored the young male attendant, standing respectfully and bashfully before him. After thanking them, the young male attendant looked closely at each one, nearly everywhere, and then pointed to one person, saying, "I secretly desire this, but I dare not think it possible." The others looked, and it was the one wearing a pale red robe. The lady of the house smiled brightly, and her husband, the prince, also laughed and said, "This young one has indeed been paying attention to her for a long time." She then ordered a small room to be set up with a bed and canopy beside the hall, where the young male attendant could establish a romantic relationship. Everyone then got up, and the servants led the way with red silk lanterns, leaving only the one in the pale red robe to accompany the young male attendant. The young male attendant asked for her name, and she modestly replied, "I am the young female attendant Four Joy, who has always been favored by the lady of the house. I have always been by her side, and now I am rewarded to you, which is a great honor!" Before she finished speaking, another woman in court dress arrived with two maids, bringing bedding, and said with a smile, "Even a young one can pair up! How delightful! How delightful!" As they were setting up, Four Joy instructed the young male attendant to bow to her, saying, "This is Lady Liu, the respected and influential woman in the palace, who is my benefactor." The young male attendant bowed as if he were a son-in-law, and Lady Liu was greatly pleased, leaving with a smile. The young male attendant undressed Four Joy, and she whispered softly, "I am still young and inexperienced." The young male attendant laughed and said, "I am also trying it for the first time, who truly knows?" They lay down together, hastily achieving pleasure, but Four Joy's beauty and passion had already faded.

    493

    事已，喜谓小三曰：“君知驸马为何许人？”小三曰：“余初至，乌能知之？”喜曰：“妾亦未得其详，但闻巩姓，明末人，阖家死难，今已百年。上帝怜其忠，命其司蓟北一带之祸福，此其佳城耳。子何为而至此？”小三乃大惊，涕泣欲号。喜急止之曰：“勿扬声，属垣未必无耳也。妾既从君，不敢不以实告。然果以妻视妾，亦不至久留于兹。”小三辍泪目之曰：“汝独非鬼耶？”喜曰：“然！但从君出，仍可为人。

    Explanation

    493

    After the matter was concluded, Xi said to Xiao San, "Do you know who the驸马is?" Xiao San replied, "I just arrived, how could I know?" Xi said, "I also do not know the details, but I only heard that he was surnamed Gong, a person from the late Ming dynasty, whose entire family died tragically, and it has been a hundred years since then. God, moved by his loyalty, appointed him to oversee the fortune and misfortune of the area around Jixian. This is his fine tomb. Why did you come here?" Xiao San was greatly shocked, weeping and about to cry out. Xi quickly stopped him and said, "Do not make a sound, the walls may have ears. Since I follow you, I dare not conceal the truth from you. But if you truly see me as your wife, I would not stay here for long." Xiao San wiped away his tears and looked at her, saying, "Are you not a ghost too?" Xi said, "Yes! But if you leave with me, you can still become a human again."

    494

    ”小三诘其故，答曰：“妾家距此半里许，固俨然人也。因病时疫不能汗，竟暴卒。父母不忍弃，葬于公主墓侧。驸马查妾死籍，禄未绝，然亦不可以生矣，因以药活我，留为侍婢。妾盖半生而半死者。”小三深疑其言，喜曰：“凡鬼皆无血，有亦淡然，君试以钗股刺妾，乃可信。“小三犹豫未忍，喜径拔簪自刺，潸然血出，色且赤甚。因信之，商所以偕遁。喜曰：“今尚未可，晚夕当与君计之。”遂披衣先起，嘱曰：“是地阴气盛，慎毋他出。”乃去。小三遵其教，足不窥户。

    Explanation

    494

    "Xiao San asked her why, and she replied, 'My family lives about half a li away from here. I was once a real person. Because of an epidemic illness that prevented me from sweating, I eventually died suddenly. My parents could not bear to abandon me, so they buried me beside the princess's tomb. The prince checked the records of the deceased concubine and found that my salary had not been cut off, but I could not be revived. He then used medicine to bring me back to life and kept me as a servant. I am now someone who is half alive and half dead.' Xiao San deeply doubted what she said. Xǐ said, 'All ghosts have no blood, and if they do, it is pale. You can test this by pricking me with the pin of your hairpin, then you will believe me.' Xiao San hesitated and did not want to do it, but Xǐ immediately pulled out her hairpin and pricked herself. Bright red blood flowed out. Because of this, Xiao San believed her. They discussed how to escape together. Xǐ said, 'It is not possible now, but in the evening, I will discuss it with you.' She then got up and put on her clothes, and instructed, 'This place has a strong presence of yin energy. Be careful not to go anywhere else.' And she left. Xiao San followed her instructions and did not leave the house."

    495

    有顷，闻人语势甚喧，即有谒者入报曰：“都城隍来祝寿。”内曰：“请回舆，来朝踵谢。”又报曰：“都土地来介禧。”答曰：“弗敢劳，请即返署。”以下都邑、城隍、土神、榖神之类，惟闻传呼注册而已，都亦不甚记忆，小三益战战弗宁。与之食，无敢下咽；与之饮，无敢沾吻；默坐垂涕，形如木鸡。日将晡，喜从外来，以二桃与之曰：“此人世物，尚可食。”见其余泪盈眶，面色惨淡，蹙额曰：“若勿作此态，累人敲扑也。”言讫复出。小三啖其桃，味甚甘美，即亦不饥。未几，天又昏暝，堂中早燃巨烛，刘又来引小三入，更至一堂，较客夕所见尤为轮奂，而轩敞逊之。主与驸马并坐一席，如伉俪。先令小三与喜骈肩参拜，而后命之歌。驸马觉小三面容有异，音韵非昨，讶之曰：“婢子得无泄吾事耶？”又笑曰：“予实自误，外间人固不可留。”乃与主耳语数四，急以手挥之，弗使歌。

    Explanation

    495

    After a short while, there was a loud commotion, and a court official entered to report, "The City God has come to wish you a happy birthday." The person inside said, "Please return and come back later to express your gratitude." Another report came, "The Land God has come to bring good fortune." The response was, "I do not dare to trouble you, please return to your office immediately." Below were the city officials, the City God, the Earth Deity, and the Grain Deity, and so on. They were only heard being called out and registered, and the main character did not remember them well, while the servant became increasingly frightened. When food was offered, she dared not eat it; when drink was offered, she dared not taste it. She sat silently with tears streaming down her face, looking as lifeless as a wooden chicken. As dusk approached, the prince came from outside and gave her two peaches, saying, "These are earthly things, and you can still eat them." Seeing her eyes filled with tears, her face pale and her brow furrowed, he said, "Do not act like this, it will make people punish you." After speaking, he left again. The servant ate the peaches, which were very sweet, and she was no longer hungry. Soon after, the sky grew dark, and the hall was lit with large candles. The official Liu came to lead the servant in, and they entered another hall, which was even more magnificent than what they had seen the previous evening, though it was less spacious. The main character and the prince sat together on the same seat, as if they were husband and wife. First, they were instructed to bow with their shoulders touching, and then the prince ordered the servant to sing. The prince noticed that the servant's face looked different and her voice was not as it had been before, and he asked in surprise, "Did you reveal my secrets?" He then laughed and said, "I made a mistake myself, and outsiders cannot be kept." He whispered to the main character several times and quickly waved his hand, dismissing the servant from singing.

    496

    且呼喜与小三并跪，语之曰：“予以忠节成神，非鬼也。汝曹事我，寿将不止于期颐。今既有异志，亦不汝责，盍偕归。”小三甚喜，而喜有惭色。主命婢取钗钏数事，并黄金一笏，赐之曰：“将去作人家，勿再习贱业，辱吾婢也。”二人俱稽首泣谢。驸马即令刘引之去，即交向之苍头，使送出。夫妇甫至府外，宅第顿渺。小三回顾，则一古墓巍然道左，始信喜言不谬。因与喜谋将奚适，喜曰：“此间不可复居，居必致人骇怪。

    Explanation

    496

    The master called Xǐ and Xiǎo Sān to kneel together and said to them, "I have become a spirit through loyalty and righteousness, not a ghost. If you serve me, your lives will extend beyond the age of 100. Now that you have changed your minds, I will not hold you accountable, so let us return together." Xiǎo Sān was very happy, while Xǐ showed a look of shame. The master ordered a servant girl to fetch several pieces of jewelry and a tablet of gold, and gave them, saying, "Go and live in a new home, do not continue in a lowly profession, which would disgrace my servant." Both of them bowed formally and wept with gratitude. The prince-in-law immediately ordered Liú to lead them away and handed them over to Cāngtóu to escort them out. As soon as the couple reached the gate of the mansion, it suddenly disappeared. Xiǎo Sān looked back and saw a large ancient tomb standing on the left side of the road, and began to believe that Xǐ's words were true. She then discussed with Xǐ where they should go next. Xǐ said, "We cannot stay here, as it would cause people to be alarmed."

    497

    可往附近州郡，择地安家，然后迎母。此策庶乎万全。”小三从之。因暗不能行，坐憩树下，盖犹藉庇威灵，恃以无恐也。昧旦，始趋村镇，以主前赐白金，觅车治任，载喜启行。东去二日程，至蓟州，出黄金置产建屋，颇类素封，始遣价奉迎其母。初其师因失小三，惧其母讼，不敢归。小三之母哭泣思儿，又贫病无以自给，日渐狼狈。至是得小三书，始知其在蓟立业，安然无恙。师始来复，母遂往。年余，有人自蓟入都，询以徐小三，则抱子矣。

    Explanation

    497

    Xiao San was advised to go to nearby states and counties, choose a place to settle, and then bring his mother. This plan was nearly foolproof. He followed the advice. Because it was dark and he couldn't walk, he sat and rested under a tree, still relying on the protection of his mother's spirit and feeling no fear. At dawn, he started to head towards the village, using the gold given by the master to find a vehicle and prepare the baggage, and then set off with joy. After two days' journey east, he arrived at Jizhou, where he used the gold to buy property and build a house, which was quite similar to a wealthy landowner. He then sent someone to fetch his mother. Initially, his master, fearing that his mother would sue him for losing Xiao San, did not dare to return. Xiao San's mother cried and missed her son, and being poor and sick, she could not support herself, becoming increasingly distressed. Upon receiving a letter from Xiao San, she learned that he had established himself in Jizhou and was safe and sound. The master then came back, and the mother went with him. A year later, someone who had come from Jizhou to the capital asked about Xu Xiao San, and it turned out that he had a child.

    498

    外史氏曰：明末国戚尽节者，惟巩君永固一人而已。是盖其英灵不泯，特借歌者以传之，非真学杨越公、裴晋公，作此一番豪举也。独喜之本末，颇近不经。然而疾疫传染死者，未必皆无禄之人，如是则冢中之活鬼，又不止一喜已也！可发一噱。

    ○　花异
    　　湖南汪仲盡，有花癖，园植名花数百本，皆上选。自春届秋，烂漫无已，时而浓淡相间，灿若云锦，主人亦未尝判以畛畦。一夕与友宿于裛香亭，莲漏已三，汪鼾然而友尚反侧。忽闻哄声伊迩，且娇细，绝类闺人，心讶然，披衣起，听有人语曰：“长夜无聊，愿与君之士戏，君姑凭式而观之。”又一人曰：“不腆敝赋，敢犒从者，幸勿退避三舍。”言已大哄。友从窗隙窥觇，月色甚明，见有卒伍二队。

    Explanation

    498

    Final Translation

    外史氏曰：明末国戚尽节者，惟巩君永固一人而已。是盖其英灵不泯，特借歌者以传之，非真学杨越公、裴晋公，作此一番豪举也。独喜之本末，颇近不经。然而疾疫传染死者，未必皆无禄之人，如是则冢中之活鬼，又不止一喜已也！可发一噱。

    The historian comments: Among the nobility who remained loyal until death at the end of the Ming dynasty, there was only one person, Gong Jun Yonggu. This is because his spirit and virtue remained undiminished, and the story was passed down through songs, not because he truly imitated Yang Yuegong and Pei Jingong in performing such a heroic deed. The origin and development of this particular story seem somewhat unbelievable. However, those who died from the epidemic were not necessarily all without rank or status; thus, the restless spirits in the tombs were not just one. It can provoke a laugh.

    ○ 花异

    In Hunan, there was a man named Wang Zhongjin who had a passion for flowers. He planted hundreds of varieties of famous flowers in his garden, all of the finest selections. From spring to autumn, they bloomed endlessly, sometimes with colors that were both rich and delicate, shining like brocade. The owner did not divide them by kind or type. One night, he stayed at a pavilion called Yi Xiang Ting with a friend. By the third watch, Wang was already asleep, but his friend was still tossing and turning. Suddenly, they heard a commotion nearby, and the voices were soft and gentle, very much like those of a woman. His friend was surprised and got up, putting on his clothes. He listened and heard someone say, "It's a long night, and I wish to play with your servant. Please watch from the side." Another person replied, "I have a modest offering, please accept it as a reward for your servants, and do not retreat three steps." They then made a loud noise. The friend looked through the window crack, and the moonlight was very bright. He saw two groups of soldiers.

    499

    一树赤帜，旆旌亦无杂色，望之如火；一建素幢，衣甲皆类霜雪，瞻之如荼。然详觑之，两军皆束绿裳，修眉媚脸，色艳肌香，实届闺中丽质。军中各筑高台，上居魁帅，披金鏁子细铠，内衬锦袍，亦分赤白色，尤冠乎一军，执小号旗指挥而进退之。其徒皆挺白刃，与月争辉，喧呼酣斗。园不甚修广，而俨若古战场，不觉其隘。友大骇，疑为鬼狐，连诧奇事。外闻有人声，遂纷如娇鸟各散，已而寂然。友呼汪与语，汪以为妄，友遂寝。晨起启户视之，园中之花，赤白各成一队，无复灿若云锦矣，始信友言非罔，相对骇然。友亦不信宿而去。

    Explanation

    499

    A red flag was raised, and the banners and pennants were all of a single color, appearing like fire when viewed from a distance; another white banner was raised, and the armor and clothing resembled frost and snow, appearing like tea (likely a metaphor for pale or white color) when viewed from a distance. However, upon closer inspection, both armies were dressed in green, with beautiful and charming faces, and possessed the beauty of women from the inner chambers. In the middle of each army, a high platform was built, on which the commander sat, wearing golden, finely crafted armor with a brocade robe underneath, also divided into red and white, and held a small flag to command the troops. The soldiers all held their white weapons, competing with the moonlight for brightness, and fought fiercely with loud shouts. The garden was not very large or well-maintained, but it looked like an ancient battlefield, and one did not feel its narrowness. The friend was greatly startled, suspecting it to be ghosts or fox spirits, and was amazed by the strange event. Upon hearing human voices from outside, the figures scattered like delicate birds, and then everything became silent again. The friend called out to Wang to talk, but Wang thought it was absurd, and the friend went to sleep. In the morning, upon opening the door to look, the flowers in the garden were now arranged in red and white lines, no longer shining like brocade, and the friend finally believed the friend's words were not false, and they were both amazed. The friend also left the next day.

    500

    外史氏曰：此友绝俗，全无雅态。若能屏息作壁上观，当亦不殊于垓下。惜乎小鹿心头，遂令花营顿撤。安知两队丽人不詈彼为噉猪肠儿，败人清兴。随园老人曰：花亦能读，左盲丘明将香艳千古矣！于此觉康成婢子解诵毛诗，真非人间仅事。

    ○　鬼书生
    　　故明成化中，商州民有行二者，忘其姓氏。以负贩为业，昼出夜归，独行无伴侣，豺狼鬼魅，举不关心。一夕归倍迟，时正深秋，白杨风起，道旁丛冢仿佛如有啸声，实是林中落叶与封家十八姨互相唱和耳。二方径行不顾，忽闻书韵盜唔，随风声而上下，或疾或徐，似断实联。讶之曰：“予日夕过此，未见一瓦一椽，此诵声胡为乎来哉？”因立而倾听，音响宛在左近。

    Explanation

    500

    The Historian remarks: This friend transcends the ordinary, lacking any refined demeanor. If he could hold his breath and observe from the wall, he would still be no different from the fate at Gaixia. Alas, the heart of a young man led to the sudden dissolution of the flower camp. How can one know that the two groups of beautiful women would not curse him as a pig-eating child, ruining their clear兴致. The Old Man of Sui Garden says: Even flowers can be read; Confucius (left blind) will be fragrant and splendid for a thousand years! From this, one realizes that the maid of Kong Yingda, who recites the Mao Shi, truly is not an ordinary matter.

    Ghost Scholar

    During the Chenghua period of the Ming dynasty, there was a man from Shangzhou whose name is forgotten. He made a living by buying and selling goods, traveling during the day and returning at night, always alone without companions, and neither wolves nor ghosts troubled him. One evening, he returned much later than usual. It was late autumn, and the wind rustled through the white poplars; beside the road, clusters of graves seemed to have a howling sound, which was actually the sound of leaves in the forest and the eighteen mistresses of Feng's house singing in harmony. He continued on his way without turning back. Suddenly, he heard a bookish voice, rising and falling with the wind, sometimes fast, sometimes slow, seemingly interrupted but actually continuous. Surprised, he said, "Every day I pass by here, I have never seen a single tile or beam. Where does this recitation come from?" He stopped and listened carefully, and the sound seemed to be coming from nearby.

    501

    心知非人，乃大叱曰：“天青星皎，何物死魅敢在此作声，惊我行旅！会须以老拳饱之。”语未已，其读忽辍。须臾，非烟非雾，出自冢中。因伏于草间觇之，见有人如书生，博带峨冠，约略可辨。既而闻其言曰：“宵深路僻，不宜犹有行人。适闻吠声，似怪予呫哔者，当以火烛之。”其音颇类楚咻，遽呼曰：“徐家可速将一炬来！”旋闻嘤咛响应，即有火光自墓内出，幢幢然，其色甚青而惨淡，当是鬼燐无疑。无何相近，则一少艾持碗灯尸闪而来，又宛然人世物，绝不类幽壑所吹者，心窃异焉。

    Explanation

    501

    The heart knew it was not a person, so he shouted loudly, "The night sky is clear with bright stars. What kind of dead spirit dares to make noise here, startling my journey? I will surely satisfy it with my fists!" Before the words were finished, the sound suddenly stopped. In a moment, something neither smoke nor mist emerged from the tomb. Therefore, he crouched in the grass to spy on it, and saw someone like a scholar, wearing a wide sash and a tall hat, barely recognizable. Afterward, hearing his words, "Deep night and remote path, should not still have travelers. Just heard barking sounds, seems to be surprised by my whispers, should use fire to illuminate it," his voice was quite similar to the Chu accent, and he immediately called out, "Mr. Xu, please bring a torch quickly!" Soon heard a soft response, and then firelight emerged from inside the tomb, waveringly, its color very blue and gloomy, it must be ghostly phosphorescence without doubt. Not long after, getting closer, a young man holding a bowl-shaped lamp appeared, yet it was like something from the human world, not at all like what is blown from the depths of the abyss, secretly found it strange.

    502

    书生语以故，将肆冥搜，忽闻少艾笑曰：“子欲人，斯人至矣，何反震惊如此？”书生亦笑曰：“卿言良是。草中人固不异冰上人也。”于是径趋蓬颗，揖二使出，曰：“君既不畏吾辈，何为匍匐于兹？幸勿怀疑，即出相见。”二果坦然无惧，起立拱揖。睨其貌，美如冠玉，年仅终童。少艾执烛旁侍，意颇肃恭，而衣饰之淡艳，容色之殊尤，则固目所未睹。二知非俗鬼，因谢曰：“归途过晏，忽聆清吟，窃疑揶揄我者，不意乃士林君子读书以消此长夜。

    Explanation

    502

    The scholar spoke of the past, about to search deeply, when suddenly he heard a young and beautiful maiden laugh and say, "If you desire someone, that person will come, why are you so shocked?" The scholar also laughed and said, "Your words are indeed correct. A person in the grass is just as real as a person on ice." Then he rushed directly to the tuft of grass, bowed to the two envoys and sent them out, saying, "Since you do not fear us, why are you crawling here? Please do not doubt, and come out to meet us." The two envoys were indeed calm and fearless, standing up and bowing. Glancing at their appearance, they were as beautiful as jade on a crown, only young boys. The maiden held a candle and served as an attendant, her attitude quite solemn and respectful, and her clothing subtly and delicately beautiful, her appearance exceptionally beautiful, something never seen before. The two envoys knew they were not ordinary ghosts, and thus thanked him, saying, "The return journey was late, suddenly we heard a clear and melodious song, we secretly suspected those who mocked us, unexpectingly, it is a scholar of high moral character reading to pass the long night.

    503

    予呓语唐突，万勿苛责是幸！”书生又笑曰：“我辈何人，岂能为祟？君似不免多虑。虽然，有事敬求，亦不虚此一遇。”遂揖二同坐于林下，始自白其由来。盖书生本襄阳人氏，商牧某公，其父执也。书生以小试不利，心窃愤懑，将赴北京援例以冀科名，故迂道过此，思饱猪肝以望云路。惟因少年行迈，未免裘马甚都，乃为奸人所觊觎。至此林中，天已昏暮，方将疾趋赴州治，一时伏甲尽起，盞拥而前，书生与二仆遂皆毕命路侧，某公固未及知也。贼既泻囊，大失所望，因虑捕盗者踪迹，遂潜启窀穸，纳尸圹内，方始瓜分而散。书生言至此，潸然泪下。少艾忽蹙黛曰：“若勿絮絮，使人如割寸心。

    Explanation

    503

    My rambling and presumptuous remarks, do not blame me for them, I will be very grateful!" The book scholar smiled and said, "Who are we to cause harm? You seem to be worrying unnecessarily. However, there is something I humbly request, and this meeting would not be in vain." He then invited the two to sit under the trees and began to explain his background. It turned out that the book scholar was from Xiangyang, and a merchant official, his father's close friend. The book scholar had failed a minor examination and was secretly resentful, planning to go to Beijing to seek a degree through special privilege in hopes of passing the imperial examinations. Therefore, he took a detour through here, hoping to eat pig liver and look forward to a bright future. However, because he was a young man dressed in fine clothes and riding a good horse, he was coveted by evil people. By this forest, it was already dusk, and he was about to hurry to the state administration when suddenly bandits surrounded him. The book scholar and his two servants were all killed by the roadside, and the merchant official was unaware of this. The bandits emptied their pockets, greatly disappointed, and fearing the pursuit of the authorities, they secretly opened the tomb, buried the bodies inside, and then divided and dispersed. As the book scholar spoke of this, tears streamed down. The young lady suddenly frowned and said, "If you don't stop talking, it will feel like cutting my heart into pieces."

    504

    ”书生因指之语二曰：“此子即予之地主，生前亦遭妒妇身死，先葬于兹。予与之邂逅九泉，见其深娴风雅，又复同病堪怜，甚相爱慕。两美既合，兼之同穴，但乏斧柯，抱愧鹑鹊。今得君，可以赤绳对系矣！”言已，又起长揖。二敬诺，因询其何以主盟。答曰：“余有控牒一纸，内言某与某愿为姻契，即署君名，言为作合，祈君代焚于城隍司，则无媒之嫌可免矣。外有白金二锭，系在腰橐深藏者，浼为置材一具，暮夜来此，启冢出尸，将予残骸入榇，与若人同厝于斯，予当数世不忘是恩。

    504

    The scholar then pointed and said to the second person, "This person is my landowner. In life, he was also killed by a jealous wife and was first buried here. I met him in the underworld, saw that he was deeply skilled in literature and elegance, and again, we shared similar misfortunes, which were pitiable. We greatly admired and loved each other. Two beauties have already united, and together in the same grave, but lacking an axe and handle, we felt ashamed like the magpie and the crow. Now that I have found you, we can now tie the red cord together!" Having finished speaking, he then rose and performed a long bow. The second person respectfully agreed and then asked how he could take charge. He replied, "I have a petition in one paper. Inside it says that someone wishes to form a marital bond with someone else, and it will be signed with your name, saying that it is for matchmaking. I ask you to burn it at the City God's office, thus avoiding the suspicion of lacking a matchmaker. Outside, there are two ingots of silver, tied in a deep pocket in my belt. I ask you to prepare a coffin, come here at night, open the tomb and remove the body, place my remains in the coffin, and bury them together here. I will remember this favor for many generations."

    505

    未识君肯援手否？”二一一力任，无难色。书生乃出金与牒，再拜而付之，然后与少艾和烛而灭。二亦目眩移时，四野萧森，慄不可留，遂怀金亟返。至家视其物，粲然白镪，真金也。乃笑曰：“痴鬼以此与我，将以望棺槨之美与倡随之乐乎？开圹者死人，谁不知？予不任受功，亦不任受过，得此生殖足矣，其他何恤焉？”竟私其金，不为市榇，又匿其牒，不以焚。盖当受金时，贪念已萌。旬日，金将用尽，仍出负贩。途遇州役系之去，茫然不知其由。至案则积赌某某咸在，二曾以鬼金偿其债，寻变为楮，故以妖妄讼之耳。

    Explanation

    505

    "Do you not know if you would be willing to lend a hand?" The second person took on the responsibility with ease, showing no difficulty. The scholar then presented the gold and the document, bowing twice before handing them over, and then extinguished the candle together with the young lady. The second person also experienced dizziness for a while, and the surrounding wilderness was desolate and eerie, making it impossible to stay. He then hurriedly returned home with the gold. Upon arriving home and inspecting the item, it was clearly white and shiny, truly gold. He laughed and said, "The fool gave this to me, expecting me to look forward to the beauty of a coffin and the pleasure of following a funeral procession? Those who dig graves are dead people, who doesn't know that? I cannot take credit for the deed, nor can I bear the blame. With this, I can sustain myself, and what else should I worry about?" In the end, he kept the gold for himself, did not use it to buy a coffin, and also hid the document without burning it. The desire had already taken root when he received the gold. After ten days, the gold was almost spent, and he once again went out to carry goods. On the way, he encountered a local official who detained him, and he was completely unaware of the reason. Upon arrival at the case, it was found that the person had accumulated gambling debts, and the second person had used the ghost gold to pay off the debt, which later turned into paper money, hence the lawsuit was filed on the grounds of superstition.

    506

    二初不肯承，及证佐昭然，乃不能讳，因述其金所自来。官怒其诞，将笞之。忽首者一人，张目直视，大呼曰：“死襄阳某者，即此曹某也，公可加刑于二。”官闻之骇然，盖即书生所云父执者。初知将即至，心甚喜，日使人迎候于郊，已而寂然，方以为疑，至是闻其死，故大惊。亟询其状，对曰：“详在牒中，二实执之。我城隍司之急足隶也，不能缅述，请即退。”言讫，其人顿扑。

    Explanation

    506

    The first person initially refused to admit it, but when the evidence became clear, he could no longer hide the truth and then recounted the origin of the gold. The official was angry at his deceitfulness and intended to whip him. Suddenly, one person stepped forward, opened his eyes and stared directly, and shouted loudly, "The one who died in Xiangyang, that is this Cao someone, you can impose punishment on the second one." The official heard it and was startled, for this was the fatherly friend mentioned by the scholar. The first one knew that he was about to come, and he was very pleased, sending people daily to wait for him at the outskirts. Afterward, it was quiet, and he began to wonder. Up until that point, when he learned of his death, he was greatly shocked. He quickly inquired about the circumstances, and the person replied, "The details are in the official document, the second one actually held it. I am a messenger of the City God's Office, I cannot recount it in detail, please let me withdraw." After he finished speaking, the person suddenly collapsed.

    507

    官视群小皆变色，乃佯笑曰：“鬼何为者？此二以妖术遣之耳！”命将二另锢一室，以犬血厌之，明日当严鞫，实则阴使之取牒也。众果心安，官又语之曰：“事未能白，汝曹亦不可返，盍居此俟偿尔金。”亦命散禁于室侧，而潜以健卒逻守之。夜分，二始以牒至。官视之，非婚牍，而实血状，历陈劫杀之惨，不胜勃然。亟升座，令役往械各犯，多在梦中。驱至庭，毒加拷掠，乃尽服，无一漏网者。案既定，官乃亲往起其尸，面色如生时，犹未朽。因二言，盛其葬具，与女柩同厝于一丘。既竣，始判令二归。临遣，官笑谓之曰：“善为执柯，勿再误人好事也。”庭之上下皆粲然，二乃归。自是胆力虽犹昔，义利亦稍分明矣。

    　　外史氏曰：始见书生缓于复仇，而急于图婚，未尝不心焉鄙之。及其假伪金，首真盗，谈笑复仇，而佳配之有无竟不必问，始叹图婚一举，亦假途灭虢之计耳！智岂出苟息下哉？若二以贪败乃公事，而究为撮合之山，直是庸人侥幸。

    Explanation

    507

    The official saw the crowd of small people all turn pale, and then pretended to laugh and said, "What are these ghosts doing? These two were sent away by magic!" He ordered to lock the two in a separate room, using dog blood to ward them off. Tomorrow, a strict interrogation would be conducted; in reality, it was secretly ordered to retrieve the document. The crowd indeed felt reassured, and the official then spoke to them, "The matter has not yet been clarified, so you cannot return either. Why not stay here until your gold is returned?" He also ordered them to be released from confinement near the room and secretly posted strong soldiers to guard them. At midnight, the two finally arrived with the document. The official examined it, and it was not a marriage document, but rather a blood accusation, detailing the horrors of robbery and murder. He became furious and quickly ascended his seat, ordering the servants to arrest the criminals, most of whom were in a dream-like state. He drove them to the court, applied severe torture, and all confessed, none escaping punishment. The case was settled, and the official personally went to dig up the body. The face was as it was when alive, still not decayed. Following the two's words, he prepared the burial items and buried them alongside the woman's coffin in the same grave. After completion, he finally ordered the two to return home. On the verge of sending them off, the official laughed and said to them, "Be good at matchmaking, and do not again spoil people's good deeds." Everyone in the court laughed heartily, and the two then returned home. From then on, their courage and strength remained as before, but their sense of right and wrong became clearer.

    The historian said, "Initially, I found it contemptible that the scholar was slow to avenge but eager to marry. However, when he pretended to give fake gold, actually arresting the real thief, and laughed while avenging, without even questioning whether they would get a good match, I began to admire his strategy of using a pretext to eliminate an enemy. How intelligent could they be compared to Gou Xi? If the two lost their case due to greed, but ultimately became matchmakers, it was merely an ordinary person who got lucky."

    508

    ○　于成璧
    　　于成璧，沈阳人。少时从其兄商于他省，壮岁将归授室，其兄付以千金，嘱曰：“于路多花柳，慎勿浪费，速归故乡，洞房花烛伊迩矣。”成璧果有心于狭邪，虽诺之，而实若罔闻。辞兄径行，途次已所费无算。将抵蓟门，秣驹镇市，因内急独出大解。一望田禾，郁葱畅茂，时值夏耘之际，行陌无隙地，乃就其垄而遗焉。便讫带犹未束，俄一物突起于前，状如小豕而长毫，奔逸而去。成璧以为异，遂逐之。其物由曲径而驰，绝不反顾。成璧足力不能及，约半里而止，物亦失其所在。将返故道，忽见二丽人珊珊碎步，自微行而来。一衣茜红裳绿衫，翠钿金翘，貌甚妖冶；一则荆钗裙布，雅淡修洁，而容光尤照映左右。窃疑一主一婢，未敢询也。成璧素性耽此，遂踯躅田畔，不能行。

    Explanation

    508

    于成璧，沈阳人。在年轻时，他跟随兄长到其他省份经商，到了壮年准备回家娶妻时，兄长给了他一千两银子，并告诫他说：“路上有许多娱乐场所，要小心不要浪费，尽快回到家乡，你的婚礼就在眼前了。”成璧确实对风月之事有兴趣，虽然答应了兄长的话，但实际上并不在意。告别兄长后，他径直上路，不久之后已经花费了不少钱。当他接近蓟门时，在一个可以喂马的镇上停留，因为突然需要解手而独自外出。他看到田野里的庄稼郁郁葱葱，正值夏季耕作的时候，路上行人络绎不绝，于是就在田垄间解决了问题。解决完之后，他还未系好腰带，突然有一物从前方窜出，形状像一头长有长毛的小猪，迅速逃离。成璧觉得奇怪，于是追了上去。那东西沿着曲折的小路飞奔，完全没有回头的意思。成璧追赶不及，大约走了半里路才停下，那东西也消失了。当他准备返回原路时，突然看到两个美丽的女子轻盈地走来，她们从一条小路上出现。其中一个穿着红色的裙子和绿色的上衣，戴着翠绿色的发饰和金色的发簪，容貌非常妖艳；另一个则穿着朴素的裙子和布衣，显得优雅而整洁，她的美丽尤其耀眼。成璧私下猜测她们是一主一仆，但不敢询问。成璧本性就喜欢这样的场合，于是站在田边犹豫不决，无法继续前行。

    509

    二女且行且语，至成璧逐物处，贫女忽四顾而言曰：“姊昨约桑中人实在于此，今竟不见，须知渠非尾生之流。”富女微笑不答，徐曰：“若勿饶舌。适从远处望，一似小不才鼠窜道左，丑态毕露。想又为过客所窘，言之殊令人赧然。”贫女大笑，亟摇纤腕止之曰：“不可说，追奔者尚在，闻此益令姊无处置身。”语次渐近成璧前，皆以横波流盼，良久始徐徐过之。径甚偪仄，成璧虽侧身避路，而娇香流溢，鼻尖领略已多，纵非荡子，亦情不禁矣。二女又回眸匿笑，毫不作羞涩态，愈令人魂销。美人既去，成璧犹木立不移。其御候久不至，踪迹之，见其如呆，不禁窃笑。促之归，将以启行，成璧曰：“予身适不快，即此栖息，明日行，当无不可。”御人以非止宿之时，颇有违言，再四强之而后可，遂近觅村店小住。成璧不饮亦不食，惟孑身徘徊于垄畔，冀其复遇，而竟杳无其踪。日将西下，俄见富女独来，惊喜曰：“佳人至矣。

    Explanation

    509

    The two girls walked and talked, arriving at the place where Chengbi was chasing after things. The poor girl suddenly looked around and said, "Sister, yesterday you said the person from the mulberry grove was really here, but now I can't see him. You must know that he is not of the same kind as Wei Sheng." The rich girl smiled and did not answer, but slowly said, "You should not talk so much. Just now from afar, it seemed like a small incompetent mouse scurried to the side of the road, revealing all its ugly traits. I think he was embarrassed by the passerby, and it is very embarrassing to speak of it." The poor girl laughed loudly and quickly shook her slender wrist to stop her, saying, "Do not say it, the pursuer is still there, and hearing this will make it even more difficult for my sister to find a place to stand." As they spoke, they gradually approached Chengbi. They both cast their eyes sideways with a lingering glance, and after a long while, they slowly passed by him. The path was very narrow, and Chengbi tried to move aside to avoid the path, but the fragrance of her beauty overflowed, and it was already abundantly felt on the tip of the nose. Even if he was not a licentious man, he could not help but be moved by her presence. The two girls turned their eyes and secretly smiled, showing no sign of shyness, which made him even more captivated. After the beautiful woman left, Chengbi stood like a wooden statue and did not move. His driver waited for a long time without arriving, and when he found him, he saw that he was as if in a daze and couldn't help but secretly laugh. He urged him to return, intending to start the journey, and Chengbi said, "I am feeling unwell, so I will stay here to rest, and we can leave tomorrow, which should be fine." The driver, thinking it was not the right time to stop, had some objections, but after repeated insistence, he finally agreed. So he found a nearby village inn to stay for a short while. Chengbi neither drank nor ate, but walked alone by the field, hoping to meet her again, but she remained nowhere to be found. As the sun began to set, suddenly the rich girl came alone, and he said in surprise, "The beautiful woman has arrived."

    510

    ”忽闻禾秆簌簌然，有一男子乌衣玄冠，侏儒短小，自茂密中直前拥女曰：“午以为期，日之夕矣，得无怨我负约乎？”女似不悦，以袖挥之曰：“予不我思，岂无他士？谁复念汝薄幸也！”其人笑曰：“子勿大言夸人，目前故无可褰裳者。”女即指成璧而目之曰：“彼人是哉！狂童之狂也且。”其人闻女言，掉头一视，即骇然曰：“咄咄！何逼人至是！”意甚仓皇，遽入禾中，竟不见，成璧亦无所疑。女以翠袂招之，遂举趾相就。

    Explanation

    510

    A sudden rustling was heard from the tall grass, and a man dressed in black clothes and a black hat, short and stunted, emerged from the dense vegetation and embraced the woman. "We agreed to meet at noon, and now the day is ending. Do you not blame me for breaking the appointment?" he said. The woman seemed displeased and waved her sleeve at him, saying, "If I did not think of you, would there be no other man? Who would remember your unfaithfulness?" The man laughed and said, "Do not boast so much; there are no other suitable candidates at the moment." The woman pointed to Cheng Bi and said, "That man is the one! How foolish and reckless he is!" The man, upon hearing her words, turned his head and looked at her, then exclaimed in surprise, "What are you saying! Why are you being so harsh?" He seemed panicked and quickly disappeared into the tall grass, vanishing from sight. Cheng Bi remained unperturbed. The woman gestured to him with her green sleeve, and he followed her gesture.

    511

    女先行，亦入丛禾，成璧尾之入，则别一天地。重楼复阁，画栋雕檐，非复绿蓐争茂者矣。怪而询之，女笑曰：“君但得佳处栖身，何必问堂构之所自哉？”于是展锦裀于绣榻，设鸳枕于翠衾，解衣为欢。肌柔态媚，成璧虽数过狭邪，曾不若是荡而逸也。事阕，女诘之曰：“君以何业而过此？妾观玉貌，非仅牵车服贾者。”成璧因告以归乡之故。女曰：“婚有人乎？抑择而后娶也？”成璧曰：“尚无所聘，归始谋之。”女又笑曰：“归即善谋，不过头蓬葆面黧黑者，乌足当君伉俪耶？诚能予我千金，当为君致一美妇。

    511

    The woman went ahead, also entering the dense grass, Chengbi followed and entered, then it was another world. Towering multi-story buildings, painted beams and carved eaves, no longer the place where green grasses vie for growth. She found it strange and asked about it, the woman laughed and said, "If you only find a good place to settle, why bother asking about the origin of the building?" She spread a brocade quilt on the embroidered bed, placed a pair of mandarin duck pillows on the green quilt, and undressed for pleasure. Soft skin and alluring appearance, despite having passed through many brothels, never was as unrestrained and carefree. When the matter was finished, the woman inquired, "What business do you have here?" "I see your noble appearance is not just that of a common laborer," she said. "Are you married? Or do you choose carefully before marrying?" Chengbi replied, "I have not yet arranged a marriage, I will consider it upon returning home." "Upon returning, you will make a good plan," she encouraged, "it will not be someone with unkempt hair and darkened face, how could such a person be your spouse? If you truly give me a thousand gold, I will provide you with a beautiful wife."

    512

    君囊中能足此数否？”成璧喜甚，亟叩以伊谁。答曰：“即向之女伴也。不幸早寡，孑然无依，里中恶少又数数窘之。倘得侍奉巾栉，不第渠免斯饥，即妾亦无所萦系已。”成璧心既失主，遂不较价轩轾，惟默计所携虽少，然货去衣装，不难如数，因径以首颔之。且曰：“予与卿好合方新，乃不为己谋，而反引他人，得无以此相餂乎？”女曰：“不然。妾性荡，不能困守闺中，恒多外遇。又不能为君作家，君亦安所用之？渠果相从，妾亦得以娅姻往来，依然有此一线。第不以其新而弃我故，则幸耳。

    Explanation

    512

    "Can your purse contain this amount?" Cheng Bi was very pleased and hurriedly asked who she was. She replied, "She is my former female companion. Unfortunately, she became a widow at a young age, alone and without support. The ruffians in our neighborhood often harass her. If I could serve you in personal grooming, not only will he avoid this hunger, but I will also have no worries." Cheng Bi's heart had already lost its owner, so he did not care about the price being high or low. He only silently calculated that the amount he carried was small, but selling the goods and clothes, it was not difficult to make up the amount. So he immediately nodded his head in agreement. And said, "My relationship with you is still new. But you are not thinking about yourself, and instead bring others in. Are you trying to tempt me with this?" The woman said, "No. I am a woman of loose morals, unable to stay confined in the inner chambers, frequently having extramarital affairs. And I cannot manage the household for you. What use would you have for me? If he truly follows, I will also be able to visit through in-laws, still having this thread of connection. But do not abandon me because of my newness, then it will be fortunate."

    513

    ”语已，促之使起曰：“旁观者不无怒目。君且归，能为妾留二日，则好事可谐。”成璧起而整衣，举目四望，屋宇一空。不惟衾枕俱为乌有，即所立之地，亦只町畦间，乃大骇。女早不知所往。心知其为妖异，而少年贪色，不以为虞。归途日已瞑，瞥见土人张弓持械，如有所追逐者。迫视之，则一牝狐，毛深黑，颠踬于丛莽间，猎犬群咋，势甚危急。心悯焉，乘众未至，取石投犬。犬少却，狐乃得遁，初不知其即贫女也。及猎者追及，成璧已去，遥闻众语曰：“此畜初来甚驯，屡将获之，奈何复遁耶？”成璧窃笑，亦不为意。

    Explanation

    513

    After the words were spoken, he urged him to stand up and said, "The onlookers were not without anger. You should return. If you can stay with me for two days, the good thing can be accomplished." Cheng Bi stood up and adjusted his clothes. Looking around, he saw the house was completely empty. Not only were the bedding and pillows all gone, but even the place where he stood was just a plot of land. He was greatly shocked. The girl had already disappeared. Knowing it was an otherworldly being, but the young man was greedy for beauty and did not regard it as a cause for worry. On his way back, the sun had already set. Suddenly, he saw the local people drawing bows and holding weapons, as if they were in pursuit. He looked closely at it and saw a female fox with deep black fur, stumbling in the thick undergrowth. The hunting dogs were barking, and the situation was very dangerous. He felt compassion and, while the others had not arrived, picked up a stone and threw it at the dogs. The dogs backed off slightly, and the fox then managed to escape. He did not know that it was the poor girl. When the hunters caught up, Cheng Bi had already left. He heard their voices from a distance, saying, "This beast was very tame when it first came. They often tried to capture it. Why did it escape again?" Cheng Bi smiled to himself and did not pay much attention to it.

    514
