    归邸鼾然熟寐，翌日仍托疾不行。食余又往候之，富女果先在，喜曰：“君信人也。钟情如此，又复直谅不疑，予妹所适得人矣。”因询以金，遽减二百之数。成璧大悦，约以次日交纳，女许之。成璧欲与为好，女辞曰：“姑留精力以待来者，勿使人言我狂荡也。”竟不顾而去。成璧返寓，倾囊只得大半，盖已耗去什之三四。不得已而售其货，皆贱其值以与人。不足，又典衣物，幸地介通衢，受者颇众。

    Explanation

    514

    Upon returning home, Cheng Bi slept deeply and loudly. The next day, he still pretended to be ill and did not attend. Another person, who had eaten, came to visit again, and the wealthy woman was already there. She said, "You are a trustworthy person. So devotedly in love, and yet honest and trustworthy without doubt, my sister has found the right person." She then asked about the money and quickly reduced the amount by two hundred. Cheng Bi was very pleased and agreed to pay the next day. The woman agreed. Cheng Bi wanted to be intimate with her, but she refused and said, "Please save your energy for future encounters, do not let people say I am reckless." She left without looking back. Cheng Bi returned to his lodging, emptied his bag, and only got half of it. He had already spent three or four tenths of it. Out of necessity, he sold his goods, selling them at a low price to others. It was still not enough, so he pawned his clothes. Fortunately, the place was near a busy street, and many people accepted it.

    515

    拮据一日夜，数始能敷，然已囊空如洗矣。御人本雇倩来者，早已得值，亦不问其理乱。成璧以多金难携，罄身往候，伫俟有顷，富女果偕贫女俱至，其衣饰犹荆布也。及见成璧，玉容微頳，似有内愧者。成璧以为娇羞，不之异。富女遂使之交拜，且谑曰：“佳儿佳妇，归家善事高堂。予羸老矣，不堪任受谤詈也。”三人皆大笑。富女别无所言，吃吃自去。成璧将呼止，令取其金，贫女始出言曰：“郎虑金不生翼也？归而视之，床头之物已尽矣。”成璧犹未能遽信，扶女同归。

    Explanation

    515

    After a day and night of financial strain, it took some time before the payment could be made, but by then, his purse was as empty as if it had been washed out. The person he originally hired had already received payment and did not inquire about the state of affairs. Cheng Bi, finding it difficult to carry a large amount of gold, went to wait with all his body, and after a while, the wealthy woman indeed arrived with the poor woman, both still dressed in simple, humble attire. When Cheng Bi saw her, her beautiful face slightly blushed, as if feeling inwardly ashamed. Cheng Bi thought she was shy and charming, and did not find it strange. The wealthy woman then made them bow to each other and said jokingly, "Good son and good daughter-in-law, return home and serve your parents well. I am weak and old, and cannot bear to be subjected to slander." All three laughed loudly. The wealthy woman had nothing more to say and quietly left. Cheng Bi was about to call her back to retrieve the gold, but the poor woman spoke up first, saying, "Sir, do you think the gold can fly away? Go home and check, and you will see that the money from the bed has already disappeared." Cheng Bi still could not immediately believe her, but helped the woman back home.

    516

    女笑曰：“叔兮伯兮，驾予与行，今日之谓也。”及至邮亭，尚未向晦，然他人举莫能见女。成璧径入室，启视橐金，封识如故，其中则空空矣，乃信女言，亦绝不惊异。置酒共女合欢。女善谐谑，每一言，成璧辄笑不能仰。旅中人闻之，咸谓其患癫疾。饮至漏下，成璧微叩其由，女似嗫嚅不肯言，惟笑曰：“君召客而不具餐，岂以妾为不能知味乎？试告君，我辈非鬼子母，即鸠盘茶，君既畏之，何弗远之？”语竟鼓掌，成璧亦为之粲然。

    Explanation

    516

    The woman laughed and said, "Uncle, Brother, drive me away and go, this is what today is about." By the time they reached the post station, it was not yet dusk, but no one else could see her. Chengbi went directly into the room, opened the bag of money to look inside, and found the seals still intact, but inside it was empty. He then believed her words and showed no surprise. They set up wine and enjoyed each other's company. The woman was skilled in witty banter, and every time she spoke, Chengbi would laugh so hard he couldn't stand up. The travelers heard this and all thought he was suffering from a mental illness. By the time the night wore on, Chengbi gently inquired about the reason, and the woman seemed hesitant to speak, only smiling and saying, "You called guests but did not prepare a meal, do you think I cannot appreciate good food? Let me tell you, we are either ghost mothers or demons, and since you fear us, why not keep your distance?" When she finished speaking, she clapped her hands, and Chengbi also laughed brightly.

    517

    遂相携而起，罗襦偷解，画烛羞吹，乐固可想。侵晨同起，女谓成璧曰：“君橐中谅无余资，此去犹近千里，何以跋涉长途？且妾纤纤弱质，亦不任风土之劳。妾不如自归，君可脱然无累，君其许之乎？”成璧犹以为戏，佯听其行。女搴帘而出，遂无影响。成璧不胜张皇，再往候之，富女亦不复见，懊丧而归。御人已悬车数日，不能复留，不得已而就道。资用乏绝，行色困顿，遂不敢再为豪举。兼程至家，其母已为订某氏女，则其兄预有家邮故也。

    Explanation

    517

    They then went together, secretly undressing, and were ashamed to blow out the painted candles, the joy certainly imaginable. At dawn, they rose together, and the woman said to Chengbi, "You surely have no more funds in your bag, the journey ahead is still nearly a thousand miles, how can you traverse such a long journey? Moreover, I, with my delicate and frail nature, cannot bear the labor of adapting to the local customs. I would rather return to my family, you can be free of all burdens, will you agree to it?" Chengbi still thought it was a joke and pretended to agree to her departure. The woman drew the curtain and left, then she was lost to sight. Chengbi could not bear the panic and went again to wait for her, but the wealthy woman was also no longer seen, and he returned in disappointment and sorrow. The driver had hung the carriage for several days and could not stay any longer, so he had no choice but to set out. His funds were completely exhausted, and his appearance was worn and weary, so he then dared not make any more bold moves. He traveled home in haste, and his mother had already arranged a marriage to a certain family's daughter, because his brother had already arranged the marriage.

    518

    其女新自关中来，貌绝美，奁具又丰，主婚者乃女之伯姊，约已定矣。成璧闻而喜，然以浪掷多金，母与嫂皆不悦，不欲速竣其事。延至岁终，其兄又有信至，兼寄百金，始为成礼。比入青庐，新妇之貌宛然熟识，盖即旅邸遇者，乃大惊，叩之，始自陈曰：“妾姊妹皆狐也，彼居燕南，妾家陇右。偶诣渠处游戏，蒙君垂顾，不觉动心。又耻自献之羞，借彼先容，幸侍君子，望勿以异类见拒。”成璧愕然，始悟前此诡异，果狐所为，向特堕其术中而不省耳。因诘曰：“既结同心，何竟潜逋？”答曰：“妾虑以不经骇君乡里，故不敢与君偕行。今日显然婚嫁，莫起群疑，不可永以为好乎？”又诘曰：“何为昔朴而今华？”答曰：“凡狐之供具，皆以术摄取于人间，故丰俭因乎其地。妾曩者修道终南，居甚荒野，前此藉君福力，始能润色如斯。所余者犹在箧中，姊故一介不取也。”成璧不以为然，女遽起自启其牣，白金充簏其中。验之，果夙昔之物，乃大悦。

    Explanation

    518

    The young woman had recently come from Guanzhong, and she was exceptionally beautiful, with a rich dowry. The person arranging the marriage was her elder sister, and the arrangement had already been made. Cheng Bi heard this and was delighted, but due to the extravagant spending, his mother and sister-in-law were displeased and did not want the wedding to proceed hastily. The matter was delayed until the end of the year, when his brother sent a letter along with a hundred gold coins, and the wedding was finally completed. When they entered the green pavilion, the bride's appearance seemed familiar to Cheng Bi, as she was the same woman he had met in the inn. He was greatly surprised and asked her about it. She then confessed, "My sisters and I are all foxes. My sister lives in Yan Nan, and I live in Long You. We happened to visit her place for fun, and I was honored by your attention, which made me feel attracted. I also felt ashamed to offer myself, so I asked my sister to introduce me, and I was fortunate enough to serve you. Please do not reject me because I am an outsider." Cheng Bi was astonished and realized that the previous strange events were indeed caused by the foxes, and he had been caught in their trick without realizing it. He then questioned her, "Since we have tied the knot, why did you run away?" She replied, "I was worried that my unconventional appearance would shock your hometown, so I did not dare to travel with you. Now that we are clearly married, there should be no doubts, and we can continue to be together, right?" He then asked, "Why were you so plain before and now so elegant?" She answered, "All the items that foxes provide are obtained through magic from the human world, so the richness or simplicity depends on the place. I used to practice Daoism in Zhongnan, living in a very remote area. Thanks to your good fortune, I was able to improve my appearance like this. The remaining items are still in my chest, and my sister did not take a single coin." Cheng Bi did not believe her, but the woman quickly got up and opened her chest, revealing a chest full of gold coins. Upon inspection, they were indeed the items from the past, and Cheng Bi was greatly pleased.

    519

    女因谓曰：“为此数百金，遂虚我半载欢好。妾初见君，知为重色轻财，道路挥霍，归家必遭憎恶，将来亦无以营生。乃与姊合谋，暂出君囊，存于妾笥，今日始得为君有。”言已以钥付之曰：“敬以完赵，善自经营，兄嫂岂可长恃哉？”成璧闻而感悟，称谢不置，亦自述其道途艰窘状，不胜怨艾。是夕欢倍于昔。明展出见，亲族皆惊为画图中人。女善持家，成璧亦由是醇谨，以前金私营市肆，获利不资，富几与兄埒。富女不时来视妹，亦间与成璧叙阔。

    Explanation

    519

    The woman then said to him, "This sum of several hundred gold pieces has left me without joy for half a year. When I first met you, I knew you valued beauty more than wealth, spent extravagantly on the road, and would surely be disliked when you returned home. In the future, there would be no way to make a living. So I conspired with my sister to temporarily take it out of your bag and store it in my box, and today it finally belongs to you." After speaking, she handed him the key and said, "I respectfully return it to you, so manage your affairs well. Can you rely on your brother and sister-in-law forever?" Hearing this, Chengbi was moved and thanked her repeatedly, also recounting his own hardships and grievances on the road, unable to contain his resentment. That night, their joy was doubled compared to before. The next day, he appeared, and all the relatives were amazed and thought she was a person from a painting. The woman was good at managing the household, and Chengbi also became more prudent from then on. He used the previous money to run a business, earning a substantial profit and becoming almost as wealthy as his brother. The wealthy woman frequently came to visit her sister, and also occasionally talked with Chengbi about old times.

    520

    女后连举二男，忽辞成璧欲还陇中。坚留之，腼然曰：“妾等与人居，皆以采纳自益，故寿永而道可成。自与君遇，姊之意初欲以妾为饵，窃君之英华，兼以钓君之货贿，非有心为姻好也。适值妾厄于韩卢，赖君驱斥，妾因铭刻不忘，曲成燕婉。今在此数岁，两索皆雄，虽可以报君，而妾之前修尽弃。将归山存养，不可复留，万勿以妾为念。”言讫，富女至，相偕以返，遂不复来。成璧始悟所救之狐，即其室中人。由此戒杀，亦不言娶，后竟以希寿终。

    Explanation

    520

    A woman who had given birth to two sons in quick succession suddenly expressed her desire to leave and return to Longzhong. The protagonist insisted on keeping her, but she shyly said, "We fox spirits live with humans to gain nourishment and resources, thus extending our longevity and aiding our spiritual path. When I first met you, my sister's initial intention was to use me as bait, to steal your vitality and lure you into giving us your wealth. Our intentions were not genuine in forming a marital bond. However, when I was in danger from a fierce dog, you saved me, and I have never forgotten your kindness. I have tried to maintain a harmonious relationship with you. Now, after several years, I have given birth to two male offspring. Although I can repay you, my previous efforts have been wasted. I will return to the mountains to nurture myself and cannot stay. Please do not worry about me." After she finished speaking, another fox spirit named Fu Nü arrived, and they left together, never returning. The protagonist then realized that the fox spirit he had saved was actually a woman from his household. From then on, he stopped killing and refrained from marrying, eventually living a long and healthy life.

    521

    外史氏曰：狐亦有无盐，异矣！而此则狐中之徐吾也，乃借穴壁之光，竟成横金之妇。以狐之黠，奈何碌碌因人耶？及观奢俭因地之语，始知富者，亦借润于入耳，独贫狐也欤哉？狐其小者也。

    ○　卷三
    　　绿绮
    　　高邮李生，风雅罕匹。年二十琴瑟犹虚，窃自矢，非夸光之美，弗与问名。有知其志者，咸劝曰：“佳人未必真有，若何迟好合而待毛、施？恐镜台未下，潘鬓将星，九十春光不亦大半虚掷乎？”生笑而不答，终夷然不屑与鸡鹜偶。如是者数年，迄无所得，而坚持如故。一日清明，亲往扫墓。祭毕，归路遇亡仆忠，盖其童时已殁者，犹仿佛记忆之。恍惚中且忘其死，遂呼曰：“若承夫人命来迎我耶？家中亦无事事，何太匆忙？”李时惟母在堂，父已早卒，故云然。忠曰：“老主人欲见公子，非主母也。

    Explanation

    521

    The historian remarked: Foxes, too, have their own kind of beauty, which is indeed unusual! This particular fox is like Xu Wu among foxes, managing to become a wealthy woman by using limited resources. Given the fox's cunning, why be so ordinary and dependent on others? Upon hearing the advice about adapting luxury and frugality to one's circumstances, it became clear that even the wealthy also rely on external influences, not just poor foxes, is it not so? Foxes are indeed small creatures.

    Volume Three
    Green Silk

    Li Sheng of Gaoyou is rarely matched in elegance and refinement. At twenty, he remained unmarried. He secretly vowed that unless he was truly outstanding, he would not consider marrying. Those who knew his aspirations often advised him, "A beautiful woman may not necessarily exist. Why delay marriage and wait for someone like Mao or Shi? Fear that before the mirror is lowered, one's hair will turn gray, and half of one's life will be wasted." Li Sheng smiled but did not respond, remaining indifferent and unwilling to marry someone of lower status. For several years, he remained unmarried, yet he steadfastly adhered to his principles. On one Qingming Festival, he went to sweep the graves of his ancestors. After the ceremony, on his way back, he encountered his deceased servant Zhong, who had died when he was a child. In his daze, he almost forgot that Zhong was dead. He called out, "Are you here to welcome me, sent by my wife? There are no urgent matters at home, why are you in such a hurry?" Li Sheng's father had died early, and his mother was still alive, which is why he said that. Zhong replied, "The old master wants to see the young master, not the mistress."

    522

    ”李甚惊愕，重以父命，径随之往。同至一巨宅，闬闳高大，甲第连亘。生欲入，忠止之曰：“主人甚怒公子，将施扑责，此地无人可解。若得新姨一缓颊，庶几能回雷霆。待忠入恳之，公子始可进见。”生茫然罔知所措，惟询曰：“新姨何人？”对曰“主人新纳之妾也。”乃入内。良久方出，告生曰：“新姨诺矣！见即为公子解之。”遂导之入门。生知父怒，心颇忐忑不定，趦趄而前。屋宇美好，绝类公侯巨家。有一堂，额曰“鹤栖”，阒其无人，而轮奂之华，倍极壮丽。有顷，其父出，从者只二三小鬟。父衣冠无异生时，呼而进之曰：“若来前。

    Explanation

    522

    Li was greatly surprised and startled, relying on his father's command, and followed him directly. They arrived at a grand mansion with high and large gates, and magnificent houses in a row. Li wanted to enter, but Zhong stopped him and said, "The master is very angry with you and is going to punish you severely. There is no one who can explain or mediate this situation. Perhaps if the new aunt-in-law speaks a few words, she might be able to turn around the thunder. I will go in and plead with her, and then you can come in to see the master."

    Li was at a loss, not knowing what to do, and only asked, "Who is the new aunt-in-law?" Zhong replied, "She is the master's newly acquired concubine." Li then entered the inner chambers. After a long time, Zhong came out and told Li, "The new aunt-in-law has agreed! She will speak on your behalf as soon as you see the master." He then led Li into the mansion. Li knew that his father was angry, so he felt very nervous and hesitated as he moved forward. The house was beautiful, almost resembling the homes of nobles. There was a hall named "鹤栖" (Hèqī), which was silent and empty, but the splendor and magnificence of the place were evident. After a while, his father came out, accompanied by only two or three young attendants. His father looked no different from Li, and he called Li in and said, "Come forward."

    523

    汝为人后，不以嗣续为念，而惟佳丽是求，是诚何心！”言已就坐，盛气相向。生伏地，屏息不敢仰视。父将命忠行杖，生甫顿首白曰：“儿不肖，诚负父恩，但思室家为人生重事，脱非所愿而曲就之，实同附骨之疽。望大人宽其罪，使得遂其私，则罔极之恩，益同天地矣。”语竟，崩角至流血。父愈怒，呼杖愈急。忽屏后一丽人，妆饰甚盛，含笑而出，谓生父曰：“适聆公子之论，亦年少恒情，公勿过于苛责。如果伉俪未偕，妾家娣姪颇有佳者，请即执伐，不亦乔梓愿俱遂乎？”生父怒犹未解，忠又从旁怂恿之。乃曰：“任若为之，予终不乐有是子。”拂衣径入。

    Explanation

    523

    A Translation of the Source Text

    "You being the heir, not thinking of continuing the lineage, but only seeking beauty, what kind of heart is this?" After finishing speaking, he sat down with a stern and angry expression. The son fell to the ground, held his breath, and dared not look up. The father ordered his servant to beat him. The son immediately knelt and bowed, saying, "I am unworthy, truly I have failed my father's expectations. But I think marriage is a crucial matter for a person. If it is not my wish but I have to go along with it, it is truly like a cancer that clings to the bone. I hope you will forgive my fault, let me fulfill my personal desire, then your boundless kindness will be even greater than heaven and earth." After finishing his plea, his forehead was bruised to the point of bleeding. The father became even angrier and called for the servant to beat him even harder. Suddenly, a beautiful woman appeared behind the screen, dressed in elaborate attire, smiling as she emerged. She said to the son's father, "Just heard your son's argument, it is also a common feeling for young people. Please do not be too harsh. If your son has not yet found a suitable match, my family has some excellent nieces and cousins. Please go ahead and choose one, wouldn't that fulfill both your and your son's wishes?" The son's father was still not appeased, and the servant encouraged him from the side. The father then said, "Let you do as you please, I will never be happy having such a son." Brushing off his robe, he went in directly.

    524

    忠引生拜谒丽人，如庶母礼。即命坐，且语婢曰：“速召诸妮子来，请公子自择。若可，予即力任之。”婢去未移时，俄闻香风拂拂，发于庭侧。有好女子十余人，或衣鲛绡，或披翠氅，淡妆艳服，纷沓盈阶。年皆十六七，容色殊尤，端肃敛皀，向丽人而拜。丽人历指其名，俾生遴选。生已五色眩目，罔知适从。至一垂髫儿，年齿尤卑，独着烟縠之衣，容光照映左右。丽人顾之谓生曰：“此绿绮，予之犹女也，公子以为可否？”生熟视之，绿绮含羞举袂，态更动人。生即首肯者再。丽人目忠大笑曰：“公子巨眼，故不差。”遂麾诸女使退，惟留绿绮使伴生坐，曰：“汝今为吾家新妇矣！善主中馈，无贻厥父母羞。

    Explanation

    524

    The attendant, Zong, led the young man to pay respects to the beautiful lady, following the etiquette of a stepmother. She immediately invited him to sit and spoke to her maid, saying, "Quickly summon the young ladies, and let the young man choose one for himself. If he finds her suitable, I will take full responsibility for her." Before long, a fragrant breeze wafted from the side of the courtyard. There were more than ten beautiful young women, some dressed in fine silk, others in green cloaks, all lightly made up and elegantly attired, crowding the steps. They were all around sixteen or seventeen years old, with extraordinary beauty, dignified and reserved, bowing to the beautiful lady. The lady pointed out their names one by one, allowing the young man to select. The young man was dazzled by their beauty and did not know whom to choose. When he saw a young girl with a low braid, who was the youngest, dressed in a light silk robe, her beauty radiating around her, the beautiful lady looked at her and said to the young man, "This is Lü Qi, my daughter. Do you think she is suitable?" The young man looked at her carefully, and Lü Qi, blushing and raising her sleeve, became even more charming. The young man nodded twice in agreement. The beautiful lady smiled at Zong and said, "The young man has good eyesight, he does not make a mistake." She then dismissed the other young ladies and left only Lü Qi to accompany the young man, saying, "You are now my new wife. Be good at managing the household and do not bring shame to your parents."

    525

    ”绿绮惭赧，若无以自容，而数以横波瞩生，意似许可。丽人即起，趋入报生父。有顷，复出曰：“痴阿翁怒故未息也。”遽命忠治青庐，又出新衣一笥，俾生与绿绮俱更其故衣者。周匝缜密，无异慈乌，生亦心窃感之。将夕，堂中皆燃巨烛，生父始出受礼。谓生曰：“此皆婢子好事，老夫颇不耐此。”已而交拜成礼，送入庭后一华屋，供具丰美，锦绣灿陈，均皆丽人之赐。生促绿绮就枕，代为解衣。莺雏燕乳，不禁啼红。欢毕，生始展问里族。答曰：“妾与姑皆胡姓，父母远任剑南，妾姊妹赖姑以生，故婚媾一唯其命，父母不必与闻也。”平明将起，早有小婢来窗下相唤。

    Explanation

    525

    Green Qǐ blushed deeply, as if unable to bear it, yet she often gazed at the protagonist with her eyes, as if giving approval. The beautiful woman immediately rose and hurried in to report to the protagonist's father. After a while, she came out again and said, "The foolish old man is still angry." She hastily ordered Zhong to prepare the green tent and took out a basket of new clothes, making the protagonist and Green Qǐ change their old clothes. Everything was thorough and meticulous, not different from a filial bird, and the protagonist also secretly felt grateful. Near evening, all the candles in the hall were lit, and the protagonist's father finally came out to receive the ceremony. He told the protagonist, "All this is because of the servant girl's good intentions, but the old man is very much unable to tolerate this." After a while, they exchanged bows to complete the ceremony, and she was sent into a beautiful room behind the hall, where the provisions were abundant and beautiful, the brocade and embroidery were brightly displayed, all of which were gifts from the beautiful woman. The protagonist urged Green Qǐ to go to bed and took off her clothes on her behalf. The young swallows and sparrows could not resist crying red tears. After the joy had ended, the protagonist then asked about her hometown and clan. She answered, "I and my aunt are both surnamed Hu. My parents are far away in service in Jian Nan. My sisters and I rely on my aunt to live, so our marriage is entirely at her command, and our parents do not need to be involved." At dawn, before getting up, a small maid came to the window to call them.

    526

    生与绿绮盥濯讫，绿绮易髫而髻，往谒舅姑，生亦入省见。父与丽人方共坐，谓之曰：“儿已有妇，宜遄归，恐若母倚闾断肠矣。”生不忍别父，长跽涕泣不愿行。父始笑曰：“痴儿子！此岂汝久居之所耶？”生乃悟。父出白金二笏，与之曰：“将去奉母赡妻，多与恐不胜任也。”丽人亦嘱绿绮，善事乃姑，勿倚痴娇如在我处。亦赠簪珥一箧，衣襦数十事，亟遣之归。生与绿绮皆泣不能出声，父与丽人亦有惨色。无何，忠入劝驾曰：“车马已具，少主夫妇可行矣！”父乃变色，叱生曰：“畜产恋父，竟不念而母耶？”生不得已，始泣拜请行。丽人亲送之出门，果有仆马相俟。

    Explanation

    526

    Sheng and Lvqi finished washing. Lvqi changed from a child's hairstyle to an adult's and went to pay respects to Sheng's parents-in-law, while Sheng also entered to pay his respects. The father and Liren were sitting together, and the father said to Sheng, "You already have a wife; you should return quickly, for your mother is leaning on the gate, heartbroken." Sheng could not bear to part with his father, and he knelt for a long time, crying and unwilling to leave. The father then laughed and said, "Foolish son! Is this where you are supposed to stay for a long time?" Sheng then realized the situation. The father took out two silver tablets and said to Sheng, "Take these to support your mother and provide for your wife. Giving too much might be beyond your ability to manage." Liren also instructed Lvqi to treat her mother-in-law well and not to rely on foolishness and pretense as if she were still here. She also gave Lvqi a box of hairpins and earrings, several dozen items of clothes and undergarments, and hastily sent them back home. Sheng and Lvqi both cried, unable to speak, and the father and Liren also showed signs of sorrow. Shortly after, Zhong entered to persuade and said, "The carriage and horses are ready; the young master and his wife can go now!" The father then became stern and scolded Sheng, "A son loves the father, but still does not think of your mother?" Sheng had no choice but to start crying and bowing, asking to leave. Liren personally saw them off at the door, and indeed, there were servants and horses waiting.

    527

    绿绮坐油壁小车，生亦乘小骊驹。夫妇行半里许，回首顾视，犹见宅第宛然，丽人与忠倚门遥望，若挥涕目送者。既远，遂不及见。归其家，母果思萦甚苦。生偕绿绮入，登堂拜见，母乃大惊。询所自来，生具以告，母恍然曰：“嘻！新妇之姑，殆狐也。汝父中年读书外室，夜辄有美女来相伴。诘其姓氏，俯而不答，与谈古今诗文，其应如响。两情眷眷，渐及于私。女辞曰：‘使君自有妇，妾不忍己双而令人只也。必欲相欢，请俟一纪之后’。遂去不复来。及汝父易箦之时，忽谓予曰：‘若人来相迎，我死得所矣。’予问：‘伊是谁？’答曰：‘十年前灯下共话者也。’言讫遂殁。

    Explanation

    527

    Green Silk sat in an oiled-lacquer carriage, while the man rode a young black horse. The couple walked about half a li, turned around, and looked back, still seeing the mansion as if it were still there. A beautiful woman and a loyal man stood by the door, looking afar, as if wiping tears and watching with sorrow. Once they were far away, they were no longer visible. Returning home, the mother was indeed deeply concerned and distressed. The man and Green Silk entered together, ascended the hall, and paid their respects. The mother was greatly surprised. She asked where they had come from, and the man fully informed her. The mother suddenly realized and said, "Ah! The mother-in-law of the new wife, it must be a fox. Your father, when middle-aged, read books in his study, and every night a beautiful woman would come to accompany him. When asked her name, she bowed and did not answer. They talked about ancient and modern poetry and prose, and her responses were as if echoing. Their feelings were deeply attached, and they gradually became intimate. The woman said, 'You already have a wife; I cannot bear to be double while making someone alone. If you must be happy together, please wait for twelve years.' Then she left and did not return. When your father was about to die, he suddenly said to me, 'If that person comes to welcome me, I will die peacefully.' I asked, 'Who is it?' He replied, 'Someone who talked with me under the lamp ten years ago.' After he finished speaking, he died."

    528

    时儿尚幼，故不以告汝。今以斯言断之，当是此耳。”生亦述其顾复之厚，母曰：“渠以吾子为子，吾即以渠侄为侄，报称庶几不爽。”遂抚绿绮如己出。绿绮亦娩婉，善悦母心。同人闻李一朝有偶，咸窃笑曰：“李子果饥不择食矣！”闻之微晒，乃张筵设饮，遍召诸友，妆妻使出拜之。一座尽怡，谓非尘世所宜有，由是群喙遂息。明年即举一子，头角非常。生郊行又复遇忠于途，拜而言曰：“主人闻公子弄璋，不胜欣慰。新姨亦令传语，此子颖异，必能光大门闾，宜好抚之。”语毕遂失所在，生为诧叹良久。今其子年仅八龄，即通毛诗、左传。对客酬答，无失序，人皆望而卜其为他年之伟器也。

    Explanation

    528

    At that time, the child was still young, so I did not tell you about it. Now, based on these words, it must be this." The child also spoke of the deep care and nurturing he received. The mother said, "He treats my son as his own, and I will treat his nephew as my own, hoping to reciprocate without disappointment." She then treated Green Silk as if he were her own child. Green Silk was also gentle and compliant, good at pleasing the mother's heart. Those who heard that Mr. Li had found a companion all whispered in laughter, saying, "Mr. Li has indeed settled for a less desirable option!" Hearing this, he smiled slightly and prepared a feast, inviting all his friends. He dressed his wife to come out and pay her respects. Everyone was delighted, saying that such a person was not fit for this world, and thus all the gossip ceased. The following year, a son was born, with unusually prominent head and horns. On another outing, the child met Zhong again, who bowed and said, "Master, I heard that your son has been born, and I am overjoyed. Your new aunt also sent a message, saying that this child is exceptionally talented and will surely bring glory to the family. You should take good care of him." After speaking, he disappeared. The child was amazed and sighed for a long time. Now, at the age of eight, the child is already proficient in the Mao's Odes and the Zuo's Commentary. When interacting with guests, he never loses his composure, and everyone expects him to become a great person in the future.

    529

    外史氏曰：甚矣，父母之劬劳靡尽也。冥漠之中，犹念及其子之块然，况生居堂上者乎。乃父有狐妾，厥子亦有狐妻，李与狐殆世为秦晋者矣。而非狐之贤淑，其父既长负向平之债，子亦终乏范蠡之缘，其不为良朋之笑柄也，几希！

    ○　痴狐
    　　痴狐者，同郡吴公之宠妾也。性憨而善媚，故号之以此，非实狐也。公讳畹，戊辰进士，以冏卿致仕。年仅六旬，颇以声色自娱，而极意搜罗，终鲜尤物，心不免歉然。一日暮春，游于郊外，独携二小奚，亲宾子姓，咸莫之俱，意盖有所冀也。行及负郭，桃英将落，菜花渐开，公因口占曰：“结子桃花颜色失，沿畦蔬菜蕊空香。可怜一样闲风月，难向枝头觅海棠。”吟讫，四望怅然。忽闻篱落间有嘻笑声，且恒探半身以相望。

    Explanation

    529

    The Historian Says:

    How great is the toil and effort of parents! In the realm of the unseen, they still think of their child's well-being, let alone those who live in the house. The father had a fox concubine, and his son also had a fox wife. Li and the fox are almost like the two ancient states of Qin and Jin, who were often at war but also had alliances. But it is not due to the fox's virtue and beauty; his father had already incurred a debt to Xiang Ping, and his son also lacked the fortune of Fan Li. How little chance they have of becoming a laughingstock among good friends!

    The Foolish Fox

    The foolish fox was the favored concubine of Wu Gong, a fellow townsman. She was simple-minded but good at flattery, hence she was called so, though she was not a real fox. Wu Gong's courtesy name was Wan, and he passed the imperial examinations in the year of the Earth Dragon, retiring as a high-ranking official. He was only in his sixties, greatly enjoying himself with music and women, and sought to collect everything, but ultimately lacked something exceptional, his heart could not help but feel disappointed. One day in late spring, he went for a walk in the outskirts of the city, accompanied by two young servants, without any relatives or guests, as he had a certain hope. He walked to the area near the city walls, where the peach blossoms were about to fall, and the vegetable flowers were gradually blooming. He then composed a poem on the spot: "The peach blossoms, which had borne fruit, lost their color, while the vegetables along the ridges had empty petals but still smelled fragrant. Pitifully, the idle pleasures of life are the same, it is hard to find a hibiscus on the branches." After reciting the poem, he looked around and felt a sense of melancholy. Suddenly, he heard laughter coming from behind the fence, and they would often peek out half their bodies to look at him.

    530

    公视之，花不屑妆，柳不胜衣，一妙龄绝色人也。公大悦，伪言口渴，遣小奚丐茶，兼诘其姓氏。小奚将公命，篱边人笑曰：“个老子口无停声，宜其渴也。虽然，予家亦无闲炉灶，烹苦茗以待子不时之需。”小奚又以姓氏叩之，答曰：“予不复记忆，我父母容或知之。”即呼曰：“阿母！我家亦如人有姓耶？渠来问我，我则无由知。”公不禁大噱，童亦粲然。有顷，见一媪出，荆布修洁，盖其母也。问客何来，且曰：“痴儿固无足与语者，幸勿见哂。”公因致揖，亲叩其里族，则王姓，夫故农业，室甚贫。

    Explanation

    530

    The official observed her. She was naturally beautiful, needing no makeup, and her figure was so slender that it could not bear the weight of clothing. She was a young woman of extraordinary beauty. The official was greatly pleased and pretended to be thirsty, sending a little servant to beg for tea, and also asking about her name and family. The little servant carried out the official's command, and the person by the fence laughed and said, "This old man is always talking, so it's no wonder he's thirsty. Although, my home also has no spare stove, so I brew bitter tea to meet your occasional needs." The little servant then asked about her name and family again, and she replied, "I no longer remember, but my parents might know." She then called out, "Mother! Does my family have a surname like other people? When he comes to ask me, I have no way of knowing." The official couldn't help but burst into laughter, and the child also smiled brightly. After a while, an old woman came out, wearing simple and clean clothes, and it was her mother. She asked the guest where he came from and said, "My foolish son is not worth talking to, please do not laugh at me." The official then bowed and personally asked about her hometown and clan. She was surnamed Wang, her husband was a farmer, and their family was very poor.

    531

    见者乃其少女，年十七，即痴狐也。公知其可饵，絮语移时，乞茗一瓯而饮之。复自白曰：“予郡中吴太仆也，与若夫为桑梓。不忍坐视若贫，异日可令渠诣我，当少为资助，毋憎薄。”语已，称谢而行。女犹在篱畔自言曰：“东家小二姑诳予斗草，那得竟弗来？”无何怒曰：“人各有目，而独孜孜视予，又临去几回头，白须将不剩矣。”母呵之乃止。公归语阍人，有王某至，当亟为之通。翌日果来，公厚款之，出十金以赠。且嘱曰：“倘有不给，亟来商，勿以造门为嫌。”王欣然返。

    Explanation

    531

    The one who saw her was her young daughter, aged seventeen, who was a foolish fox. The official knew she could be lured, and they chatted for a while. She asked for a cup of tea and drank it. Then she said, "I am the Wu Taifu in the prefecture, and I am from the same hometown as your husband. I cannot bear to see you poor. In the future, you can have him come to me, and I will provide some assistance, do not be envious or resentful." After speaking, she thanked him and left. The girl was still by the fence, saying to herself, "The second daughter of the neighbor lied to me about playing with grass, how could I not come?" After a while, she became angry, saying, "Everyone has eyes, but you stare at me so intently, and when you leave, you almost turn back, your white beard will be gone." Her mother scolded her, and she stopped. The official returned and told the gatekeeper, if Mr. Wang comes, please inform me immediately. The next day, he came, and the official treated him generously, giving him ten gold coins. He also said, "If you ever need more, come and ask, do not be hesitant to visit." Mr. Wang happily returned.

    532

    公家人举莫知公意。由是不时周恤，十旬以五六十金。王小康，举家靡不感戴。公乃遣冰，王夫妇始悟公捐惠之意，既慕其利，兼怀其德，遂许之。乡里或为女不平，女独漠不为意。及婚，公以五百金为女父母寿，始迎女归。其父母流涕送之，女之痴态如故，谓其母曰：“胡不随予嫁去，吃着不尽矣。”母大恚，唾其面。比至公家，引谒夫人，并晤其同列，靡不笑其痴，遂不复与之较礼。至夜，公入其室，女绝不羞涩，直前捋其髭曰：“此物更苍于阿父，恍疑汝兄而渠弟矣。”左右皆窃笑。

    Explanation

    532

    The public did not know the official's intention. Therefore, he did not provide timely assistance, giving fifty to sixty gold coins every ten days. Wang Xiaokang and his whole family were deeply grateful. The official then sent a messenger, and Wang's husband and wife finally understood the official's charitable intentions. They agreed to the arrangement. Some people in the village felt sorry for the woman, but she was indifferent to it. When she got married, the official gave five hundred gold coins to the woman's parents as a gift. Her parents cried as they sent her off. The woman's foolish behavior remained unchanged. She said to her mother, "Why don't you come with me when I get married, so we can eat and live without worry." Her mother became very angry and spat in her face. When she arrived at the official's home, she was introduced to the official's wife and met his colleagues. Everyone laughed at her foolishness. They no longer paid attention to the proper etiquette. At night, the official entered her room, and the woman was not at all shy, boldly stroking his beard and saying, "This thing is even grayer than my father's, I almost think you are my older brother and he is your younger brother." Everyone around quietly laughed.

    533

    须叟阖扉，公与之解衣，亦不之拒，惟至衵服，遽以手格之曰：“予生平未尝去此，岂至汝家，反令予裸体度日乎？”公笑而谕之曰：“汝既嫁，则此衣宜去矣！”女终不从，公强之，乃呱焉大啼，呼其父母不置。公不忍以力，竟拥之和衣就枕，俟其寐，而后去之。甫能成欢，女又娇啼大作。幸其性柔顺，公复以甘言诱之，始得竣事，然已流丹浃席矣。诘朝同起，女妆成，出见贺客，皆惊叹其艳。女则时时私语同辈，告以夜来情状，并述所苦，鲜有不捧腹者。

    Explanation

    533

    After a short while, the door was closed, and the man began to undress her. She did not resist, but when he reached her nightclothes, she suddenly pushed him away and cried out, "I have never gone without these before. How could I come to your house and be forced to live naked?" The man smiled and explained, "Since you are now married, these clothes should be removed!" The girl still refused, and the man persisted. She then cried out loudly, calling for her parents incessantly. The man, unwilling to force her, gently held her in her clothes and laid her down on the bed, waiting until she fell asleep before proceeding. Just as they were about to consummate their union, the girl began to cry softly. Fortunately, she was naturally gentle and compliant, so the man again used sweet words to persuade her, and they finally completed their union. However, her tears had soaked the bedding. The next morning, as they both got up, the girl dressed and went out to greet the guests who had come to congratulate her. All were amazed by her beauty. The girl would often whisper to her companions, telling them about the events of the night and her discomfort, and few could help but laugh.

    534

    数日后，渐入佳境，又津津乐道之，闻者又不免生妒心，女亦不觉。公知其痴，故听其言，不之禁。女则事公益谨，且媚甚。公丰于髯，晨起多见扰乱，女蓄盂水于枕畔，以香口温而梳理之。公俭于肌，重裀犹憎坚硬。女铺新絮于榻上，以柔肌转而暱就之。公饮，则茗必亲尝；公食，则肉皆经箸；公坐，则以掌抚其席；公行，则以腕持其腋；公喜则无戚戚之容，公怒愈有蔼蔼之色；公是以爱若性命焉。女养手甲最长。一夕，衾内误伤公，公不忍言。愤恨不寐，挑灯独起，尽剪之，公止之亦弗听。公误唾其衣，此衣遂不更。

    Explanation

    534

    Several days later, the situation gradually improved, and they began to speak enthusiastically about it. Those who heard them could not help but feel jealous, but the woman remained unaware. The man understood her eccentricity and thus allowed her to continue, without forbidding her. The woman then served him more diligently and became even more charming. The man had a full beard, and he often appeared disheveled in the morning. The woman kept a basin of water by his pillow and warmed and combed his beard with her fragrant mouth. The man had soft skin and still disliked hardness even with thick bedding. The woman laid new cotton on the bed and approached him with her soft skin. When the man drank, she must personally taste the tea; when he ate, all the meat had to be tasted with chopsticks; when he sat, she stroked the mat with her palm; when he walked, she supported his armpit with her wrist; when he was happy, there was no appearance of sadness; when he was angry, there was an even more gentle expression. The man loved her as if she were his life. The woman grew the longest fingernails. One night, she accidentally injured him in bed, and the man could not bear to speak about it. She was filled with anger and could not sleep, so she lit the lamp and rose alone, cutting off all her nails. Even if the man tried to stop her, she did not listen. The man accidentally spit on her clothes, and this garment was never worn again.

    535

    公问之，惨然不答。公知其意，乃叹曰：“汝非全无心肝者，谁谓而痴也？”因赠以诗曰：“抱璞谁知美玉盛，人前故作太憨生。只因一语留情后，始信聪明尽逊卿。”由是益暱之。同列罔不妒，唯夫人独怜之，每曰：“汝曹谁无枕席情，可能学渠熨贴老翁耶？”然女竟以此得痴狐名，盖亦出于憎者之口。独是女宠虽专房，无所不用其媚，惟至床第之事，则不少曲徇。公欲与之狎，辄辞曰：“少者固乐此，老者将何以堪？岂可以予少，而不念公之老？”其言益不痴，公愈贤之。

    Explanation

    535

    The official asked her, and she replied with a somber expression. The official understood her feelings and sighed, saying, "You are not completely heartless; who would call you foolish?" He then gave her a poem, which read:

    "Who knows the beauty of the jade hidden in the unpolished stone? In public, you pretend to be extremely naive.
    Only after a moment of emotional expression did I finally realize how much intelligence and charm you possess."

    From then on, he became even more affectionate towards her. All the others in the household were jealous, but only the official's wife showed compassion. She said, "Who among you does not have marital affection? Could you possibly be a very suitable old man?"

    Eventually, the woman gained the nickname "foolish fox," which came from the mouths of those who envied her. Although she had exclusive favor in the household and used all sorts of charm, she did not yield in matters of intimacy. When the official tried to be intimate with her, she would always refuse, saying, "The young naturally enjoy this, but how can the old endure it? How can you consider my youth while not thinking of your age?"

    This translation aims to capture the nuances and emotional depth of the original text while ensuring clarity and coherence in English.

    536

    因而百夕之中，仅得以遂，而不以为歉，公得以颐养。公年七旬，届诞辰，戚族毕贺，家人亦无不称觞。女不知庆，独茹素弥月，曰：“愿公再延一纪，无敢奢望。”未几，公病。女奉汤药，衣带未之离身。公病益剧，竟不起，女忽辞公欲行。疑其有异志，询以焉往，答曰：“愿先公行，为公驱狐狸于地下。”言已，顿仆于地，七孔殷然，则已饮鸩半日矣。公太息许时，复大笑曰：“若不负我，我何悲为？”亟呼诸郎君至，遗命以女尸合葬，瞬息亦卒。诸郎君克成父志，不敢有违。迄今询吴氏之茔，必曰“痴狐墓”，女之名与公皆不朽。

    536

    Translation of the Source Text

    After many nights of effort, the goal was finally achieved, and they did not feel any regret; the gentleman could enjoy his old age. When the gentleman reached seventy years of age, on his birthday, all relatives and clan members came to congratulate, and family members also all toasted him. The daughter did not know how to celebrate, and she alone abstained from meat for a month, saying, "I wish for the gentleman to live another decade, though I dare not hope for more." Soon after, the gentleman fell ill. The daughter served him soup and medicine, and her clothes and sash never left her body. The illness became more severe, and he finally passed away. The daughter suddenly said goodbye to the gentleman and wanted to leave. They suspected she had some ulterior motive, and asked where she was going. She replied, "I wish to precede you in death to drive away the foxes in the underworld." After saying this, she suddenly fell to the ground, and blood flowed from all seven orifices, for she had already drunk poison for half a day. The gentleman sighed for a long time, then laughed loudly and said, "If you did not betray me, why should I grieve?" He quickly called all his sons, left a bequest to have the daughter's body buried with his, and soon after, he also passed away. The sons dared not disobey, and carried out their father's wishes. To this day, when asking about the tomb of the Wu family, one must say "the tomb of the foolish fox," for the daughter's name and the gentleman's both remain immortal.

    537

    外史氏曰：狐而媚则有之，媚而痴吾未见也。媚而痴，则必不痴；痴而媚，则极其媚。古今来愚忠愚孝，人未必不以为痴，媚亦何独不然？苟从肝鬲中流出，而惟恐不适其意，不悦其心，是即痴矣。况正色以闲之，捐躯以殉之，岂止媚者能之乎？但以人而被以狐之名，人则不堪。以如是之人，而加以狐之号，狐亦与有荣施矣。狐乎！狐乎！尔亦能蚩蚩如是乎？

    Explanation

    537

    The Historian remarked: There are foxes that are charming, but charming foxes that are foolish I have not seen. If a charming fox is foolish, it is not truly foolish; if a foolish fox is charming, it is extremely charming. Throughout history, blind loyalty and filial piety, people may not think them foolish, why should charm be any different? If one's intentions flow from the innermost heart, and one fears not to please, not to delight the heart, then that is foolishness. Moreover, treating with seriousness and distance, sacrificing one's life for it, is this something only those who are charming can do? But if a person is called a fox, the person would find it unbearable. If such a person is called a fox, the fox itself would be honored. Fox, fox, can you also be so foolish?

    538

    随园老人曰：吴太仆立朝所行者，犹在人意中。今其如夫人，迥出入意表，孰谓巾帼中竟无人荣及夫子？

    ○　灯下美人
    　　琼州余舜章，少时读书于某寺。每当风清月白之顷，辄有良夜如何之慨，盖仅约而未婚也。一夕，篝灯展卷，方将肄习，忽烛光尸闪如人，意为寺僧。视之，无所见，良久复然。余心疑，掩卷伺之。又许时，其状若烟而惨淡，倏有倏无，莫能摹拟。余素以胆力自雄，审知为鬼物，益伺之。久之，较前益真，但具体而微。又久之，甫露半面。至夜分，全形现矣。余亟视之，绿鬓翘然，红姿绰约，美人也。

    Explanation

    538

    Master Suīyuán said: What Wu Taifu did in his official career was still within people's expectations. But now, his concubines, however, greatly exceeded people's expectations. Who would have thought that among women there were none who brought honor to their husbands?

    Beautiful Woman Under the Lamp

    Yu Shunchang from Qióngzhōu, in his youth, studied at a certain temple. Whenever the wind was clear and the moon bright, he would feel a sense of how wonderful the night was, for he was engaged but not yet married. One night, as he lit a lamp and opened a book to study, suddenly the candlelight flickered like a person, and he thought it was a monk from the temple. He looked around, but saw nothing. After a while, it happened again. I was suspicious, so I closed the book and waited to see what would happen. After a while, its appearance was like smoke, dim and flickering, sometimes present, sometimes absent—no one could describe it. I have always prided myself on my courage, and after careful consideration, I realized it was a ghost, so I waited even more intently. After a long time, it became more real than before, but still small and vague. After a long time, it finally revealed half of its face. By midnight, its full form appeared. I quickly looked at it, and saw a woman with green hair that stood up, and a red, graceful figure—she was a beautiful woman.

    539

    余即起揖而进之曰：“鬼乎？仙乎？何示人以诡异如此？余狂生余舜章是也。如不弃，当剪此短烛，少赐晤言。”美人微笑曰：“何遽唐突至此？予实鬼也！知君有渴疾，敬为相如，来献良方。岂以阴浊之质，而更冒自媒之耻哉！”余闻言，以其贞鬼，改容询之。美人曰：“妾生前少有薄德，上帝鉴之，命掌禄籍。近来因月老耄荒，所配多失闺人意，敕妾赞襄其事。于是赤绳所系，鲜有朱淑真、李易安之事矣！”余惊曰：“若然，则卿固氤氲使之流亚也。岂予亦有韦固之缘，烦子预告耶？”美人曰：“非也。老人口盢，几伤人伉俪情，妾不忍为。

    Explanation

    539

    I immediately rose, bowed, and approached to say, "Are you a ghost or a spirit? Why show such a strange and mysterious appearance? I am the mad scholar Yu Shunchang. If you do not disdain me, I will light this short candle and grant a brief conversation." The beautiful lady smiled and said, "Why be so abrupt? I am indeed a ghost! Knowing that you have a thirst for knowledge, I respectfully offer myself as a companion to provide a good remedy. How could I, with my impure and murky nature, further risk the shame of self-promotion?" Upon hearing this, I, believing her to be a virtuous ghost, changed my expression and inquired further. The lady said, "In my life, I had a little virtue, and the Heavenly Emperor observed it, appointing me to manage the register of fortunes. Recently, because the matchmaker (Moon Old Man) has become senile, many of the matches he made are not to the liking of the women, and the Emperor ordered me to assist in the matter. Thus, few are the cases where the red thread binds, like those of Zhu Shuzhen and Li Yian'an!" I exclaimed, "If so, then you are indeed a junior of the cloud and mist messengers. Could it be that I also have the fate of Wei Gu, and I trouble you to inform me?" The lady replied, "No. The old man's mouth is sealed, almost injuring the affection between husband and wife, which I cannot do."

    540

    但妾往来云路，窃见郎君对月临风，时有不豫之色，盖缘洞房有待，虽庶士不免摽梅之叹耳。妾有良策，故来敬陈于郎君”。余闻之，色甚喜，因折节求之。美人出一硃符曰：“援琴之挑，良非君子所宜，妾亦不敢于导引。然郎君之妇，非始生三岁者，未尝不可以相邀。欲速则速之，早尔数年，婚嫁固不妨也。”余又细询其说，美人笑曰：“郎君勿絮絮，第置此于枕函，若所聘者，即至矣。”径置符于床头，倏忽如烟而逝。余正无聊赖，姑试之。甫就枕衾，即梦其父母为卜吉期，遣使亲迎。

    Explanation

    540

    But I, traveling in the clouds, secretly saw the young man facing the moon and the wind, sometimes showing a discontented look. It is because of the waiting in the bridal chamber, even common scholars cannot avoid the sighs of the bai mei (a metaphor for a woman of marriageable age). I have a good strategy, so I have come to respectfully present it to the young man. The act of playing the qin to entice, is not suitable for a gentleman, I also dare not to guide. But the wife of the young man, not a three-year-old girl, it has never been impossible to invite. If you want to hurry, then hurry, even several years earlier, marriage and wedding are not a problem. I heard it, and my face showed great joy, lowered my standards to seek it. I produced a red talisman and said, "The act of playing the qin to entice, is not suitable for a gentleman, I also dare not to guide. But the wife of the young man, not a three-year-old girl, it has never been impossible to invite. If you want to hurry, then hurry, even several years earlier, marriage and wedding are not a problem." I then asked her in detail about her plan. The beautiful woman laughed and said, "Young man, do not talk too much, just place this in your pillow box, if the one you are engaged to, will come immediately." She directly placed the talisman on the bedhead, and suddenly disappeared like smoke. As soon as I lay down, I immediately dreamed that his parents were choosing a lucky date and sending an envoy to personally welcome him.

    541

    俄而彩舆至门，牵红入室，而嘉礼告成。比视新妇，貌甚丽而酷似美人，欣幸之余，亦不以介意。欢然同梦，乐且未央，春色已酣，交颈而息。及转侧而寺钟聒耳，山僧已来送茶，呼曰：“余相公作何好梦，日高犹不明眸也。”余笑而起，昼犹念念不忘，其心益不在读。及夕早寝，妇亦亲狎，不再如昨之羞涩矣。盖余所问名者，为邑中某家女，耳食其有殊色，故信之。自此温柔乡深入，益慕睡乡。朝冀暮，暮怯朝，恨不得中山酒为千日醉。久之，当昼而寐，妇亦刺绣其侧，语笑甚欢，遂不复欲觉。未几，某家以余綦贫，且镜台久而未下，竟别字。

    Explanation

    541

    Shortly after, a decorated carriage arrived at the door, and the bride was led into the room, marking the completion of the grand ceremony. Upon seeing the new bride, I found her appearance extremely beautiful, almost resembling a goddess, and I was filled with joy and gratitude, though I did not dwell on it. We shared a joyful dream, our happiness unending, as if spring had reached its fullest bloom, and we slept with our heads touching. As I turned over, the temple bell sounded loudly in my ear, and a mountain monk came to offer tea, calling out, "What delightful dream have you had, Sir? The sun is high, yet you still have not opened your eyes." I laughed and got up, but even during the day, I could not help but think of her, and my heart was no longer focused on my studies. By evening, we went to bed early, and my wife was now more affectionate, no longer as shy as she had been the previous day. It seems that the woman I had been betrothed to was from a certain family in the county, and I had heard that she was exceptionally beautiful, so I believed it. From then on, I became deeply immersed in the pleasures of love, and I grew even more fond of sleep. I longed for the day and dreaded the night, wishing for the legendary wine of Mount Tai that could induce a thousand-day slumber. After some time, I began to sleep during the day, and my wife would sew by my side, our conversation filled with joy, and I no longer desired to wake up. Soon after, the family, seeing that I was extremely poor and that the engagement had lasted for so long without any progress, ultimately broke off the engagement.

    542

    余生之父母，亦无如何，余亦为之悒悒，而梦则未绝也。第形神精魄，咸为所耗，渐以痿惫，遂终日昏昏皆梦矣。一日强起如市，见斗者二人，一人被殴，几殆，环如堵墙者，仅作壁上观，竟无人能为之解。余见不忍，前诘其由。殴者素识余，忿忿言曰：“渠售妇与予为小妻，既而悔之，所欠者又悍不肯还，故殴之，以泄吾忿。”余询被殴者：“值几何？”答曰：“因葬父无资，只贷渠青蚨五贯耳。妇不忍别，日涕泣惟愿死，谁赖渠也？”余踌躇自念，事关节孝，当思有以保全之。

    Explanation

    542

    My parents who gave me a second chance, there is nothing I can do, and I am also deeply troubled, yet my dreams have not ceased. My body, spirit, essence, and soul are all consumed, gradually becoming weak and exhausted, so that I am now constantly in a daze, living in a dream-like state. One day, I forced myself to go out into the marketplace and saw two people fighting. One was being beaten and nearly killed, while the crowd around them, like a wall, merely watched as if standing on a wall, and no one could resolve the situation. I could not bear to see this and stepped forward to ask why. The aggressor, who knew me well, angrily said, "He sold his wife to me as a concubine, but later regretted it. The woman he owes money to is stubbornly refusing to return it, so I beat him to vent my anger." I asked the person being beaten, "How much did you owe?" He replied, "Because I lacked funds for my father's burial, I only borrowed five strings of coins from him. The woman could not bear to part with him, wept every day, and wished to die. Who would rely on him?" I hesitated and thought to myself, this is a critical point in filial piety, and I should find a way to preserve it.

    543

    因计囊橐尚余二金，暂贷于寺僧，可足其数，甘淡泊半月，亦无害耳。乃谓殴者曰：“汝欲金乎？欲人乎？”殴者稔余为寒儒，谅不能豪，遂姑应之曰：“渠妇貌不扬，予亦不愿成好事。但不得金，情实不甘。”余笑曰：“若然，请从予去，偿若金。”观者皆踊跃曰：“余相公为此义举，阴德无量矣！”因极力怂恿。殴者白悔失言，而迫于众议，亦帖耳相从。惟被殴者感激至流涕，誓以死报。相随入寺，余为僧言，僧亦重余义，慨然相假，并出所有者倾囊付之。因焚其券，被殴者稽首流血。其纷既解，余至夕就寝，竟无所梦，心疑焉。

    Explanation

    543

    Considering the research and context gathered, here is the final, polished translation of the source text:

    After calculating, I still had two coins left in my purse, so I temporarily borrowed from the temple monk, which would make up the amount, and I could be content with a simple life for half a month, it would also not do any harm. I then said to the brawler, "Do you want money or do you want a person?" The brawler knew I was a poor scholar, and assumed I couldn't be wealthy, so he hesitantly replied, "Her wife is not very attractive, and I also don't want to make a good thing out of it. But if I don't get the money, I really can't bear it." I laughed and said, "If that's the case, please go with me, and I will repay you the money." The onlookers all eagerly said, "Our respected gentleman is doing this righteous deed, it will bring infinite virtue!" They strongly encouraged me. The brawler regretted speaking hastily but under the pressure of the crowd, he also followed submissively. Only the person who was beaten was so grateful that he shed tears, swearing to repay with death. We followed him into the temple, and I explained to the monk, who also valued my righteousness, and generously lent me the money, emptying his purse and giving it to me. Then, I burned the contract, and the person who was beaten bowed deeply with blood. The dispute was resolved, and I went to bed that night, finally had no dreams, and went to doubt.

    544

    窃怪符不再灵，默祝美人，思以询之。宵半果来，赧然谓余曰：“向昔所云，皆诳君也。妾一缢死鬼，何由司人间婚牍？缘知君禄命浅薄，不久亦入鬼道。妾欲结未死缘，以为泉下伴侣，特虑见疑，未敢冒昧，故假君家新妇，得以梦中为欢。近今相会者，实妾也。日者君以一时恻隐，保全孝子贞姬，土神上奏，福禄俱崇，寿且耄耋不止。妾不敢再为君祟，敛迹伏藏。今承召询，腼颜自陈。”言之不禁忸怩，因更太息曰：“欺罔之罪，固已无辞，燕婉之情，犹希垂念。妾将为君返去，珠以成双璧，慎毋别觅鸾凤也。

    Explanation

    544

    I was surprised that the talisman no longer worked. I silently prayed to the spirit, hoping to inquire of her. In the middle of the night, she indeed came, and with a blush, she said to me, "What I said before was all a deception. I am a ghost who died by hanging. How could I oversee human marriage affairs? Because I know your destiny is shallow, you will soon enter the realm of the dead. I wanted to form a pre-death bond with you, to be a companion in the afterlife. But I especially feared to be suspected, so I did not dare to act rashly. Therefore, I pretended to be your new wife, to have pleasure in dreams. The other day, you acted out of temporary compassion, preserving the filial son and chaste wife. The earth god reported, and both your fortune and rank were elevated. Your lifespan will extend beyond old age. I dare not continue to trouble you, so I have concealed myself. In response to your summons, I speak humbly and frankly. The sin of deceit is already unforgivable, but my sweet and affectionate feelings still hope you will remember. I will return for you, and the pearls will form a pair of jade disks. Carefully do not seek other phoenixes."

    545

    ”言讫再拜，竟披发吐舌而去。生悚然，汗如雨下，神以顿爽，不再思睡矣。居无何，某家果挽人来曰：“向以一时狂瞽，几背前盟。今愿仍谐旧好，永无二心，其纳之幸甚！”余廉之，得其实。则女为鬼崇，时时引帛欲自缢，且狂呼曰：“此余少卿妻也，谁敢夺之？”因而后所字者，索退原聘，不敢缔姻，故复修好于余。余心知美人所为，以权词白于父母，乃许之。期年毕婚，女貌亦亚于美人，余特秘之而不言。又明年，余果连掇巍科，成进士。仕至光禄寺少卿，寿至八秩。故至今里人凡执柯者，咸易月下老人为灯下美人，以相谑云。

    Explanation

    545

    After speaking, he bowed twice and finally left, having untied his hair and stuck out his tongue. The man was startled, his sweat pouring down like rain, and his spirit was suddenly refreshed, and he no longer felt like sleeping. Not long after, the woman's family indeed brought someone to say, "Earlier, due to a moment of madness, we almost broke a previous promise. Now we wish to continue the old friendship, never to have any doubts, and it would be very fortunate to accept it!" I investigated and found the truth. It turned out that the woman was tormented by a ghost, constantly pulling a cloth to hang herself, and shouting wildly, "This is my wife of the young official, who dares to take her away?" As a result, the person she had previously betrothed demanded the return of the original dowry and was unwilling to marry, so they renewed the friendship with me. I knew what the beautiful woman had done and used persuasive words to explain to my parents, and they agreed. After a year, we completed the marriage, and the woman's appearance was also inferior to the beautiful woman, and I kept it a secret and did not speak of it. The following year, I indeed passed the highest level of the imperial examinations, becoming a Jinshi. I served as Vice Director of the Imperial Food Office and lived to be eighty. Therefore, to this day, villagers who arrange marriages often replace the Moon Matchmaker with the Lamp-Lit Beauty, jokingly.

    546

    外史氏曰：温太真慕其姑妹，因嫁名于友，而自订之，美人殆师其遗意欤？而以避为趋，以离为合，颇类策士所为，其狡亦甚矣！但恐余生无回天之善，则新妇必抱不白之冤。使当日闻之，必将欲唾其面。

    ○　梁少梅
    　　邑人梁少梅，年仅廿四五，美风姿，善谈吐，有雅人韵致。一日中元，随人往城外法觉寺观施食。时主坛者为寂禅师，持戒清高，秉教虔谨，坛前恒有怪异，好事者咸乐观焉。少梅与其侣二三人，薄暝出邑门，比及寺中，月已上矣。见小儿辈，或以绿荷笼灯，或以青篙燃炬，跳跃如鬼，不禁莞然。须臾，箫管嗷嘈，幢幡接引，众僧拥法师登坛，说妙法，散天花，以成盂兰盛举。看者如市，亦都无所见。少梅胆素巨，顿思人烟丛集，鬼何敢来？即来，亦莫能觏止。倘往僻地候之，则法之验否当立辨。因而舍其同行，径趋寺旁小径，潜身以伺。立未久，俄有黑气数十百監，其巨如斗，源源而来，皆从目前经过，隐隐若有声，的是奇观。

    Explanation

    546

    外史氏曰：温太真慕其姑妹，因嫁名于友，而自订之，美人殆师其遗意欤？而以避为趋，以离为合，颇类策士所为，其狡亦甚矣！但恐余生无回天之善，则新妇必抱不白之冤。使当日闻之，必将欲唾其面。

    The commentator said: Wen Taizhen admired his aunt and sister, and because he married the name of his friend, he himself arranged it. The beautiful lady perhaps followed his legacy. But turning avoidance into pursuit, and separation into union, is quite similar to what a strategist would do, and his cunning is indeed great! However, if I have no power to reverse the situation, then the new wife will suffer an unjust and unexplained wrong. If I had heard of this at the time, I would have wanted to spit in his face.

    ○ 梁少梅

    邑人梁少梅，年仅廿四五，美风姿，善谈吐，有雅人韵致。一日中元，随人往城外法觉寺观施食。时主坛者为寂禅师，持戒清高，秉教虔谨，坛前恒有怪异，好事者咸乐观焉。少梅与其侣二三人，薄暝出邑门，比及寺中，月已上矣。见小儿辈，或以绿荷笼灯，或以青篙燃炬，跳跃如鬼，不禁莞然。须臾，箫管嗷嘈，幢幡接引，众僧拥法师登坛，说妙法，散天花，以成盂兰盛举。看者如市，亦都无所见。少梅胆素巨，顿思人烟丛集，鬼何敢来？即来，亦莫能觏止。倘往僻地候之，则法之验否当立辨。因而舍其同行，径趋寺旁小径，潜身以伺。立未久，俄有黑气数十百監，其巨如斗，源源而来，皆从目前经过，隐隐若有声，的是奇观。

    Liang Shaomei

    Liang Shaomei, a man from the town, was only in his mid-twenties, with a graceful appearance and eloquent speech, possessing a refined and elegant demeanor. One Mid-Autumn Festival, he went with others to the Fa Jue Temple outside the city to watch the offering of food. At the time, the main host of the ceremony was Master Ji, who was known for his strict adherence to the monastic rules and his devout and meticulous practice. There were always strange occurrences before the altar, which attracted many curious onlookers. Liang Shaomei and two or three companions left the town gate just before dusk and arrived at the temple by the time the moon had risen. They saw children lighting lanterns in green lotus lanterns or torches with green bamboo, jumping around like ghosts, which made them smile. Soon, the flutes and pipes made a cacophony, and banners and pennants led the way. The monks surrounded the abbot and ascended the altar, speaking the wonderful dharma and scattering celestial flowers, completing the grand ceremony of the Ghost Festival. The crowd was like a market, but none of them saw anything. Liang Shaomei, who was known for his boldness, suddenly thought, "With so many people around, how dare the ghosts come? Even if they do come, they cannot be seen." If he went to a secluded place to wait, then the effectiveness of the ceremony could be immediately determined. Therefore, he left his companions and hurried to a small path beside the temple, hiding to wait. He had not stood there for long when suddenly dozens or hundreds of black masses, as large as a winnowing basket, came streaming in, all passing right in front of him, murmuring as if there was a sound, truly a wondrous sight.

    547

    少梅复登高以望，其气至坛即灭，杳然无踪。后有继者，不知几何。少梅立许时，宵露湿衣，渐不可耐，思回寺前觅其侣，谋所鸡栖。忽闻笑语声，如花阴群鸟，足遽止。及近觇之，则妇人十余，靓妆淡服，貌均妖艳。有小鬟二，以笼烛导之行。最后一少艾，色尤姣好，独持荷灯一盏，碎步而前。瞥见少梅，即以翠盖招之，俨若熟识。少梅神魂颠倒，不克自持，遽尾之。妇人癷疾如风，尽其力始能及。迨至一处，峻宇雕墙，巍焕如神庙。妇人尽入，亦绝不顾少梅。少梅倦极，不能返，小憩于墙角。良久，有人自门内秉烛出，语曰：“适一风狂儿逐姊至此，胡不见？”因以火烛之。照见少梅，喜曰：“郎固在此，谁云返也。请即随予入。

    Explanation

    547

    Shao Mei climbed high again to look, and the spirit reached the altar and disappeared mysteriously, leaving no trace. There were those who followed, but it is unknown how many. Shao Mei stood there for a while, the night dew wetting his clothes, making him gradually uncomfortable. He thought of returning to the temple to find his companions and plan a place for chickens to roost. Suddenly, he heard laughter and conversation, like a flock of birds under the flowers, and he stopped abruptly. When he approached and looked closely, he saw more than ten women with beautiful makeup and simple clothing, all of whom were beautiful and seductive. There were two young maids leading the women with lanterns. The last young woman was especially beautiful, holding a lantern and taking small steps forward. She saw Shao Mei briefly and immediately called him with a green parasol, as if she were familiar with him. Shao Mei's spirit was overwhelmed, and he could not control himself, so he quickly followed her. The women moved swiftly like the wind, and Shao Mei exerted all his strength to catch up. Until they reached a place with tall buildings and carved walls, majestically splendid like a temple. The women all entered, and did not even look at Shao Mei. Shao Mei was exhausted and could not return, so he took a short rest at the corner of the wall. After a long time, someone came out of the door with a candle, saying, "Just now, a crazy boy chased his sister here, why didn't you see?" Then he used a candle to light the area. He saw Shao Mei and said happily, "The young man is indeed here, who said he would return? Please come in with me immediately."

    548

    ”少梅视之，果挑灯之鬟，乃欣然起立，与之偕。历门数重，仿佛若有神象，亦不暇细瞻。继入一小院落，其中花竹森秀，别有洞天。而所见之少艾，早在庑下站俟，见鬟即询曰：“逐臭郎得之乎？”答曰：“得之矣！”少艾即笑而逆之，偕入中庭。室内铺陈华丽，目多未经。灯下睨少艾，年可十八九，姿态横流，容光肆映，真丽人也。少梅心益悦，因致词曰：“仓卒相遇，未遑趋避，不加呵叱为幸。乃复引入房闱，倍增惭悚。”少艾微笑答曰：“适见君子踯躅草露间，知必黑暗迷途，无所投止者。不揣荒僻冒昧，奉邀草榻一宵，少光蓬荜，何反承癰谦若此？”少梅复逊谢。少艾延坐，且语鬟曰：“好事勿令人知，惧相溷也。”鬟亦笑而诺之。

    Explanation

    548

    Shao Mei looked at her and saw that she was indeed the young woman holding the lamp. He stood up joyfully and went with her. They passed through several doors, and there was a vague sense of something divine or supernatural, but he did not have time to look closely. They then entered a small courtyard, where the flowers and bamboo were lush and beautiful, creating a different world. The young and beautiful woman he had seen was already standing and waiting under the eaves. When she saw the young woman holding the lamp, she asked, "Did you find the stench-following man?" The young woman replied, "Yes, I found him!" The young woman then smiled and went to meet him, and they entered the central courtyard together. The room was lavishly decorated, and there were many things he had never seen before. Under the lamp, he glanced at the young woman, who was about eighteen or nineteen years old. She had a graceful and flowing posture, and her radiant beauty fully reflected. She was truly a beautiful woman. Shao Mei's heart became even more joyful, and he said, "We met unexpectedly, and I had no time to avoid you. It was fortunate that you did not scold me. Now, you have led me into the inner chambers, which greatly increases my embarrassment." The young woman smiled and replied, "I just saw a gentleman hesitating in the grass and dew, knowing he must be lost in darkness with no place to rest. Without considering the remoteness and rashness, I invited him to stay overnight on a straw mat, bringing a little light to my humble abode. Why instead do you accept blame and be so modest?" Shao Mei again humbly thanked her. The young woman invited him to sit and said to the young woman holding the lamp, "Good deeds should not be known by others, for fear of getting mixed up." The young woman holding the lamp also smiled and agreed.

    549

    少艾又命治具对坐行酌，肴核罗列，珍错多不知名。少梅正饥渴，得此甚慰，徐叩其姓氏，匿笑弗言，惟曰：“情好未笃，无敢遽陈，统俟诸异日。”少梅遂不敢问。欢饮既久，两心荡然，鬟启曰：“良宵迟晤，鸡且将鸣，请就寝。”二人乃握手起。及入卧室，衾褥綦华。少艾自解其衣，内外皆崭然新制，惟著红绡抹胸，与少梅就枕。抚其肌，丰若有余，腻难著指。交合之际，媚态纷呈，少梅早置身魂梦中矣。游仙一枕，渐入黑甜。及醒，闻娇音群噪曰：“淫婢不羞，偷与狂郎戏，吾辈可鸣鼓而攻之。”张目惊视，少艾犹在抱中，毫不羞涩，惟笑曰：“知情者固当连坐也。”众哗然曰：“婢子无赖，竟下水拖人耶？”言已鼓掌，少梅心始安。

    Explanation

    549

    The young woman ordered the preparation of a feast and invited him to sit opposite each other while serving wine. A variety of dishes were arranged, many of which were precious and exotic, with some unfamiliar names. Xiao Mei, who was hungry and thirsty, found great comfort in the meal. He then inquired about her name, but she smiled coyly and did not answer, only saying, "Our feelings are not yet deep, so I dare not reveal myself now. I will tell you another day." Xiao Mei then refrained from asking further. After they had enjoyed their wine for a long time, their hearts were filled with a sense of elation. The young woman's attendant said, "It is late at night, and the rooster is about to crow. Let us go to bed." The two then took each other's hands and rose. Upon entering the bedroom, they found the bedding to be extremely luxurious. The young woman removed her clothes, revealing that they were all freshly made, and she wore only a red silk chest wrap. She lay down with Xiao Mei. As they embraced, her skin was plump and smooth, almost too slippery to touch. During their intimate moments, her charming and alluring behaviors were abundant, and Xiao Mei felt as if he were in a dream. After a brief sleep, he awoke to hear a group of young women giggling and saying, "This shameless servant girl is not modest, sneaking off with this wild man. We should publicly confront her." Opening his eyes in surprise, he saw that the young woman was still in his arms, showing no signs of embarrassment, and she merely laughed and said, "Those who know about this should also be held responsible." The other women exclaimed, "This mischievous servant girl even dragged someone into the water!" They clapped their hands, and Xiao Mei finally felt reassured.

    550

    窃视妇人四五辈，皆客夕所见者。乃起着衣，妇人皆孜孜凝视其私，若歆羡然。少艾亦起，众以手理其鬓曰：“发蓬蓬，狂已太甚！”少艾又笑曰：“卿等欲狂不能耳。”因引少梅遍拜之曰：“问我诸姑，遂及伯姊，媒妁之言足当矣。”众默然。已乃欢然相与偕坐，谐谑生春。有顷，均以酒炙来为二人扶头饮。少酣，中一衣绿绡者，年齿少长于众，少艾呼之以姑，忽谓少艾曰：“若能与郎君言之乎？”答曰：“邂逅相逢，未敢轻泄。”姑笑曰：“郎君粗豪，当无虑。”乃语少梅曰：“一言颇骇听闻。妾等非人，实狐也。渠为故明中丞毛一鹭宠姬，年十九而夭。毛因民变获罪，匆遽中遂葬于此。

    Explanation

    550

    As I secretly observed the group of four or five women, they were all the same ones I had seen the previous evening. They then got dressed, and the women stared intently at my private parts, as if admiring or envying them. The young and beautiful woman also got up, and the others adjusted her hair, saying, "Your hair is messy, you’re too wild!" The young woman laughed and said, "You all want to be wild but can’t." She then led me to greet everyone, saying, "Ask about my aunts, and even my elder sister; the matchmaker’s words are sufficient." The group fell silent. Afterward, they sat together joyfully, engaging in playful banter. After a while, someone brought wine and meat to toast us. When slightly drunk, one of the women wearing green silk, who was younger than the others, was called "aunt" by the young woman. She suddenly asked the young woman, "Could you speak to the young master?" The young woman replied, "We’ve only met by chance, and I haven’t dared to speak lightly." The aunt laughed and said, "The young master is bold; there’s no need to worry." She then told the young master, "What I’m about to say will startle you. We are not human; we are foxes. We were the favored concubines of the late Ming dynasty official Mao Yilu, who died at the age of nineteen. Mao was disgraced due to a popular uprising and was hastily buried here."

    551

    是地为圣姥行宫，妾等时来服役，见而怜之，授以炼形术，虽鬼而无异于人。今既得侍君子，愿即携归，庶不至污触圣境，妾等亦得以全其始终。郎君以为何如？”少梅初闻而惊，既亦弗惧，惟毅然应之曰：“敬如尊命。”众乃相顾大笑曰：“个男儿胆故不细。”姑曰：“予固深知之，乃敢言也。”因为少艾贺，始得其姓名，则王氏，小字阿怜耳。众又为之治奁具，弹指间，锦绣珠玉，纷沓俱集，外各封黄金一锭为贺仪，怜与少梅一一致谢。姑又曰：“昼不可复，恐招疑怪，盍俟昏夜。”乃群起而散去。怜因谓少梅曰：“非妾命君拜，则君危矣。”少梅诘其故，怜曰：“渠性皆荡，昨夕特未睹君，故容妾捷足得之耳。今晨相值，不无垂涎，赖妾以礼缚之。姑又怜妾，成此义举。

    Explanation

    551

    This place is the palace of the Holy Old Woman, and we often come to serve. Seeing you, we felt compassion and taught you the technique of refining the form, though you are a ghost, you are no different from humans. Now that you have served the gentleman, I wish to take you back immediately, so as not to defile the sacred realm, and we can also complete our mission. What do you think, sir?” Shao Mei was initially surprised upon hearing this, but then he was not afraid, and he resolutely replied, “I will follow your command with respect.” The group then looked at each other and laughed, saying, “This man has no small courage.” The elder sister said, “I indeed know him well, so I dare to speak.” They then congratulated the young lady, and finally, we learned her name. She is Wang, with the pet name A Lian. The group then prepared bridal items, and in the blink of an eye, fine brocade and precious jewels all gathered together. Each was wrapped in a piece of gold as a gift, and both Lian and Shao Mei each expressed their gratitude. The elder sister said, “We cannot do this during the day, for fear of causing suspicion. Why not wait until nightfall?” They then dispersed. Lian then told Shao Mei, “If I hadn't ordered you to bow, you would have been in danger.” Shao Mei asked why, and Lian said, “They are all promiscuous. Last night, they hadn't seen you yet, so I was able to get ahead of them. Today morning, we met, and they lusted after you. Thanks to me, they were bound by propriety. The elder sister also pities me, and thus we accomplished this righteous act.

    552

    不然与少乐乐，君可胜任，与众乐乐，君将不堪矣！”少梅闻其言，不禁捧腹。又询鬟之所在，答曰：“渠皆绅家少婢，丛葬此土，妾爱而抚之，收供十指，但堪侍于黄昏，不能昼现也。”因引少梅视其所居，卉木繁茂，不类窀穸间。怜告曰：“此皆若辈所为，妾无能致此。妾自与若辈游，饮食衣服，莫不仰给于彼。数日前，姑忽语妾，谓妾眉宇间有喜色，当有奇逢，不可仍着故衣，因为妾遍易新者。今之所御，皆姑赐也。若妾之死榇，乃在此后，既已臭腐不堪回顾已。”喁喁细述，少梅深叹其奇。及暮，众又来为设祖帐，前二小鬟皆至，惘惘有不忍别之状。酒行数巡，姑拔鬓边钗，击案而歌曰：“有女娩婉矣，共我翱翔。今兹别去兮，予心忧伤。

    Explanation

    552

    If not, enjoying simple pleasures alone, you can manage it, but enjoying them with others, you will find it unbearable!" Xiao Mei heard these words and couldn't help but laugh. She then asked about the whereabouts of the maids, and the reply was: "They were all young maids from gentlemen's families, buried together in this land. I loved and cared for them, taking care of them with my hands. They could only serve at dusk and could not appear during the day." She led Xiao Mei to see where they lived, and found the place lush with plants and trees, unlike a graveyard. She pitifully told him: "All of this is what they have done. I could not achieve this on my own. Since I have been with them, all of my food, clothing, and other necessities, I rely on them for. Several days ago, my aunt suddenly told me that there was a look of joy between my eyebrows, indicating a special encounter. She said I should not continue wearing old clothes and changed all of my clothes to new ones. What I wear now is all given by my aunt. My own coffin will be after this, already rotten and smelly, unbearable to look back on." She spoke softly and in detail, and Xiao Mei deeply sighed at its oddity. By evening, many people came again to set up a farewell banquet, and the two young maids who came earlier all arrived, looking sad and reluctant to part. After several rounds of wine, the aunt pulled out a hairpin from her hair, hit the table, and sang: "There is a woman, graceful and gentle, soaring with me. Now we must part, and my heart is sad."

    553

    愿汝倡随兮，如凤凰。何时重晤兮，在仙乡。”音节甚古，且凄婉。怜乃再拜，答歌曰：“一抔弃兮，冥然何知？肉我白骨兮，匪彝所思。今夕别离兮，乌夜啼。深恩未酬兮，步迟迟。聊祝眉寿兮，与天齐。”众亦歌曰：“女萝附木兮，得所依。留君不住兮，心孔悲。子兮子兮无久违。”歌已，四座皆泣下。饮将达曙，姑曰：“邑门将启，盍行乎。”乃取众所赠遗，分置两人袖中，殊不重累，始送之出门。怜与众又把袂珍重。少梅视其地，果即近郭之碧霞祠，距城未里许，因扶怜归其家。少梅无父母，兼未授室，候门惟一老妪，讶之，亦无敢问。怜与少梅究不自安，翌日竟徙于乡，出金治产，家类素封。后每张筵召狐，绝不至。怜今出世十余年，犹妍艳如昔，所亲多有见之者。

    Explanation

    553

    I wish for you to harmonize together, like phoenixes. When will we meet again, in the celestial realm? The melody is very ancient and melancholic. She then bowed twice and sang in response: "A small mound of earth, how can one know what lies beyond? Burying me with meat is not what I desire. Tonight we part, the nightingale cries in sorrow. Deep gratitude remains unrepaid, my steps are slow. I wish you a long life, as long as the heavens." The others also sang: "The vine clings to the tree, finding a place to rest. We cannot keep you here, our hearts are deeply grieved. Return soon, oh return soon." After the song ended, everyone present wept. As dawn approached, the old woman said, "The city gate will soon open, let us go." They took the gifts given by everyone, divided them between the two of them, and found them not at all burdensome. They then escorted her out the door. She and the others held sleeves together, bidding each other farewell. Xiao Mei looked at the place and saw that it was indeed near the city, close to the temple of Bi Xia, within a few miles of the city. He then helped her return to her home. Xiao Mei had no parents and had not yet married; the only one waiting for him at the door was an old woman, who was surprised but dared not ask. She and Xiao Mei felt uneasy, so the next day they moved to the countryside, spent money to establish a business, and their family became quite wealthy. Later, whenever they held banquets to summon the fox spirit, it never came. She has been in this world for more than ten years, still as beautiful as before, and many people have seen her.

    554

    外史氏曰：狐，有毛者也，而以毛之姬适人，似不恤其族矣！且毛初葬姬于此，原不意其有狐。狐竟乔作主张，不复问毛，姬亦擅诱少年，不暇畏毛。则毛之为毛，可知矣。少梅之胆巨于斗，阿怜之面厚于革，非此二人，狐即多事，亦不得不为毛地。

    Explanation

    554

    The commentator said: The fox, known as "Mao," married a woman, seemingly showing no concern for its own kind! Moreover, when "Mao" first buried the woman here, it did not expect there to be a fox. The fox then took matters into its own hands, disregarding "Mao," and the woman seduced young men without fear of "Mao." Thus, it is clear what "Mao" is like. "Shao Mei" had a courage greater than a measure of wine, and "A Lian" had a face thicker than leather. Without these two, the fox would have to act in a way that benefits "Mao."

    555

    ○　定州狱
    　　直省定州，有村民婚于近村某家。民有孀母，素婴疾病，井臼惟藉妇操。妇年二九，颇风格，民更密于防闲。以故归宁之期绝少，妇与其父母皆不满。时届秋成，其岳家村中演戏，侑神。适民母疾小愈，岳浼人言，欲迎女归，母许之，妇遂盛妆而往。民固雅不欲，淹留未久，往促之归。翁媪爱女，皆不听。及社事将阑，民又往，为言母以劳疾作，理宜遄归，絮絮不止。妇贪观剧，甚不愿，乃曰：“尽此一夕耳！姑即抱恙，暮夜亦无所事，请俟戏终，明晨旋返，良亦无所误。”媪亦赞助，民不能强，遂悻悻自去。实亦新婚未久，燕尔情浓，非有恶念也。于路窃自愤曰：“贱骨朵不念枕席情，只图欢笑，吾必辱之。

    Explanation

    555

    ○　定州狱
    In the province of Dingzhou, there was a villager who married into a nearby family. The villager had a widow mother who had long been plagued by illness, and all household chores relied on the daughter-in-law. The bride was nineteen years old, well-mannered, and the husband was particularly strict in guarding against any impropriety. As a result, the occasions for the bride to visit her parents' home were very rare, and both the bride and her parents were dissatisfied. When the autumn harvest season arrived, the bride's in-laws' village held a play to honor the gods. Coincidentally, the mother's illness had slightly improved, and the in-laws asked someone to speak on their behalf, requesting that the daughter be brought back home. The mother agreed, and the bride set out in fine attire. The husband, who had no desire to let her go, stayed only briefly before going to urge her to return. The in-laws, who loved their daughter, refused to listen. By the time the village festival was nearing its end, the husband went again, saying that his mother's illness had worsened due to exertion, and she should return home as soon as possible. He spoke incessantly about this. The bride, who was eager to watch the play, was very reluctant, and she said, "Let me enjoy this night! Even if my mother is ill, there's nothing to do at night. Please let me wait until the play ends, and I will return home early tomorrow morning. It won't cause any trouble." The mother also supported this, and the husband could not force her, so he left in anger. In reality, they had been married for only a short time, and the affection of newlyweds was intense; he had no ill intentions. On the way, he secretly fumed to himself, saying, "This wretched woman doesn't think of the bedchamber, she only wants to laugh and play, I will certainly disgrace her."

    556

    ”抵家饭已，乘夜悄然复往。稔知岳家有矮屋，邻于演戏之所，妇与姑姊妹列坐于檐际以观。远望妇果在，指顾笑语，意甚欢，民益大恚。乃于人丛中委蛇行，潜身廊庑，昏暗中绝无知者。时杂剧正盛，金鼓齐鸣，满场喧哄。妇凝睇已久，渐忘形骸，频以一足垂下。民知其无备，仰而企之，竟褫其只履而归，犹漠然弗知。夫遂怀之亟返，至家闭户高卧，亦不与母言。计俟其晨归，痛辱之以泄积忿。乃妇失履未久，顿觉足冷，扪之，则莲瓣已脱，心疑猖狂者为，不胜愧悔。兼虑诸亲咸集，闻必嗤笑，遂不俟戏完，亟下屋自觅尺帛缚束之，白于父母将归。其父家翁媪皆惊讶，叩之弗言，留之不可，惟托足软，命人控一蹇卫送之。盖图夜归易履，免致播扬。

    Explanation

    556

    Arrived home, the meal had already been served. In the night, he quietly went back again. He was familiar with the fact that his father-in-law's house had a small building near the place where plays were performed. The wife and her sisters-in-law sat in a row on the eaves to watch. From afar, he saw that the wife was indeed there, pointing and chatting, very happy. The man became even more angry. He then moved through the crowd, winding his way, hiding himself in the corridors, no one knowing in the darkness. At the time, the variety show was in full swing, the gongs and drums sounded together, the whole venue was noisy. The wife had been staring for a long time, gradually forgetting her form, frequently letting one foot hang down. The man knew she was unprepared, looked up and reached for it, and finally took away her single shoe and returned. Still unaware. The husband then hid it and hurriedly returned home, closed the door and lay down, not telling his mother either. He planned to wait until she returned in the morning, humiliate her to vent his accumulated anger. But shortly after the wife lost her shoe, she suddenly felt her foot cold, touched it, and found that the lotus petal had fallen off. She suspected that a wild person had done it, feeling extremely ashamed and regretful. Also fearing that all relatives were gathered, if they heard, they would surely laugh at her, so she did not wait for the play to end, quickly went down the house to find a piece of cloth to bind it, and reported to her parents that she would return. Her father, grandfather, and grandmother were all surprised, asked her but she did not speak, could not be persuaded to stay, only said her feet were sore, and ordered someone to ride a donkey to escort her. It was to return at night to change shoes, to avoid spreading the news.

    557

    及至，姑犹未寝，启户即讶曰：“若婿言若明日归，何深夜而返？得毋令阿翁怪耶？”妇曰：“儿闻母又病，是以亟归，不暇俟翌日也。”姑笑曰：“予病亦习惯，乌足虑。”妇俟姑寝，然后趋就己室。恐夫觉，不敢燃灯。及夫问以伊谁，始答曰：“予来家。”夫微哂曰：“予以汝从优人逝矣，竟归耶？”知夫怒，无敢言。夫又曰：“大好戏文，诘朝闻将复演，汝何遽归？”妇益默然。窃思俟夫寝，始可取履以更。乃夫又询曰：“既归何不以炬来？”妇甫对曰：“夜阑火烬，暗中固可寝也。”夫知其意，忽起曰：“待予为汝燃烛。”妇力止之，不听。烛既燃，纤毫毕鉴，妇惧，亟匿其足。夫早见其无履，乃佯笑曰：“汝以足来，迹殊有异。

    Explanation

    557

    When she arrived, the grandmother had not yet gone to bed. Opening the door, she was immediately surprised and said, "If your husband said he would return tomorrow, why are you back so late at night? Wouldn't this make the old man angry?" The wife replied, "I heard that Mother was ill again, so I hurried back, not having time to wait until the next day." The grandmother smiled and said, "My illness has become habitual; it's not worth worrying about." The wife waited until the grandmother went to bed and then rushed to her own room. Afraid of waking her husband, she dared not light a lamp. When the husband asked who she was, she finally answered, "I've come home." The husband gave a slight smile and said, "I thought you had gone with the actor; did you really return?" Knowing the husband was angry, she dared not speak. The husband then said, "There was such a good play today; I heard it will be performed again tomorrow morning. Why did you rush back so soon?" The wife remained silent. She secretly thought she could wait until the husband went to sleep to change her shoes. But the husband then asked, "If you came back, why didn't you bring a lamp?" The wife replied, "It was late at night and the fire had gone out; one can certainly sleep in the dark." The husband understood her intention and suddenly got up, saying, "Let me light a candle for you." The wife tried to stop him, but he did not listen. The candle was lit, and every detail was visible. The wife was afraid and quickly hid her feet. The husband had already seen that she was not wearing shoes and laughed falsely, saying, "You came with your feet; the marks are quite unusual."

    558

    ”妇伸有履者，亦笑曰：“若孜孜凝视，岂有人跣而行耶？”夫注目良久，遽曳其右足曰：“是何独无？”妇大惭，低鬟无语。夫谩骂曰：“不从我言，致出予丑，虽醢汝身，不足泄忿矣！”固诘其履，妇无以对。夫曰：“履在足上今不见，其事可知。予犹以汝为室耶？”喃喃登榻，怒骂不休，且言明日迹得之，决杀却。遂复卧，意盖愧怍之而已，辗转絮聒，不觉熟寐。妇惶恐无以自容，又虑为邻里笑，竟缒帛于梁而自缢。及夫闻声惊寤，起而抚之，体已冰矣，大怖且悔。复计妇夜归，当无知者，若潜匿其尸，反诬其父，祸可免。因断其缳，负之出户，投诸邻寺井中。寂然反室，思妇之情好，不禁怅惋。待旦而出，不及见母，竟往岳家逆妇。翁媪言已送归，婿力白其无。前送女者，适以事他出，共疑之，具控于官。州牧胡公素聪察，亟拘送者至。鞫之不服，惟举姑妇相语状。公颇心疑，拘妇之姑，审讯之，所供与送者同。因以严刑拟其夫，始吐实。

    Explanation

    558

    The woman, upon seeing the man's intense gaze, laughed and said, "If you are staring so intently, is there anyone who walks barefoot?" The man continued to stare for a long time, then suddenly pulled her right foot and said, "Why does this one not have shoes?" The woman was greatly ashamed, lowered her head, and remained silent. The man cursed, "Because you didn't listen to me, you've made me look foolish. Even if I cut you into pieces, it wouldn't be enough to vent my anger!" He firmly questioned her about the shoes, but she had no answer. The man said, "The shoes were on your feet and are now gone. The situation is clear. Do I still consider you my wife?" He muttered to himself, climbed onto the bed, and continued to curse, vowing that he would find her the next day and kill her. He lay back down, seemingly only feeling remorse, but he kept talking to himself, and soon fell into a deep sleep. The woman, terrified and unable to bear it, also feared the ridicule of the neighbors, and eventually tied a rope to the beam and hanged herself. When the man heard the sound and woke up in shock, he got up to touch her, but her body was already cold. He was greatly terrified and regretful. He then thought that since the woman had returned home at night, there must be no one who knew. If he could secretly hide her body and falsely accuse her father, he could avoid the trouble. So he cut the rope, carried her out of the room, and threw her into the well of a nearby temple. He returned silently to the room, thinking of the woman's affection, and couldn't help but feel sorrow. He waited until dawn and went out, not seeing his mother, and went to his father-in-law's house to take his wife. The old man and woman said she had already been sent back, and the son-in-law strongly denied it. The person who had sent the daughter away had just gone out for some reason, and they all suspected him, filing a complaint with the authorities.
