The prefectural governor, Mr. Hu, was known for his keen insight and quickly detained the person who had sent the daughter away. When questioned, he refused to admit anything, only mentioning the conversation between the mother-in-law and daughter. The governor was suspicious and detained the mother-in-law, questioning her. Her testimony matched that of the person who had sent the daughter away. The governor then threatened the husband with severe punishment, and he finally confessed the truth.

    559

    公命加以桎梏，押往觅尸。令善泗者出诸渊泉，则闯然一髠，无所谓朱颜绿鬓者。公与吏民皆大骇，审视之、额烂身殒，乃寺僧某也。盖妇尸坠井，适挂于坎，未至没水。缚少缓，竟以更生。忽觉冷砭肌骨，不可当，且暗如昏夜，仰视之，又见天光。窃意身履冥途，故其境如此。迨以手扪之，寒泉浸溢，始悟入井，乃号呼望救。适寺僧起五鼓时，桔槔灌园。闻井中有声，疑失足误坠者，俯询之，则邻妇某氏也。僧故识其夫，亟引修绠拯之。井深九仞，妇手腻力怯，多方竟不能上。正惶急间，俄一少年贸然来，亦邻家学圃者。见僧鞠躬用力，乃笑曰：“大师何作此态？岂金绳中断，以致净瓶不出耶？”僧语以故。少年曰：“不仁哉！吾师也。

    Explanation

    559

    The official ordered shackles to be put on him and commanded him to be taken to find the body. He ordered a skilled diver to descend into the deep spring, and suddenly a bald man appeared, with no one having red cheeks and green hair. The official and the officials and common people were greatly startled. Upon close examination, they found that his forehead was rotten and his body had died; it was the temple monk. It turned out that the woman's body had fallen into the well and was stuck on a ledge, not fully submerged in water. If the rope had been slightly looser, she would have survived. Suddenly, he felt a coldness that pierced his flesh and bones, unbearable, and it was dark as night. When he looked up, he saw the light of the sky. He secretly thought he was on the path to the underworld, which explained why the environment was so strange. When he touched it with his hand, he felt the cold spring water overflowing, and he then realized he was in the well and began to shout for help. Just as the temple monk was watering the garden at the fifth watch of the night, he heard a sound in the well and suspected someone had accidentally fallen in. He leaned down to ask and found it was a neighbor's wife. The monk recognized her husband and quickly pulled her up with a long rope. The well was nine ren deep, and the woman's hands were soft and weak, so despite many attempts, she could not climb up. Just as he was in a state of panic, a young man suddenly appeared, also a neighbor who learned gardening. He saw the monk bowing and straining, and laughed and said, "Master, why are you in this state? Could it be that the golden rope has broken, causing the vase to remain stuck?" The monk explained the situation. The young man said, "How unkind! I am your disciple."

    560

    宁有慈航普渡，而高居彼岸者乎？若素能浚井，予缒汝下，渠乃可上，何计不及此？”僧曰：“然，予亦熟筹若此，时值乏人，故作此拙态。”遂浼少年执绠，已乃缒绳而下。既得妇，解绠束其纤腰，号曰：“亟提之。”少年用力，妇果出。睨之，虽衣裾沾濡，貌颇婉丽，心大动，绐之曰：“娘子以盦予我，请就高阜憩息，予出吾师。”妇解付少年。少年四望，有巨石，其大如缶，力掇之下诸井，适中僧颅，竟毙于水。少年虑其复活，再取他石连投之，寂然无声。知其已死，始止而不投，突牵妇衣曰：“去之！此处不可以复留。”妇见僧毙，知非好相识，大惧欲走。少年胁之以力，不能脱，始勉从之。曲折里许，至一土室中，少年语妇曰：“僧与予言，意颇不善，予故力救汝。今将送汝归，但衣湿恐不可耐。予他之，任汝自便，俟燥而后行。

    Explanation

    560

    Is there anything else you would like to add or modify before I provide the final translation?

    If not, here is the final translation:

    "Is there a compassionate ferryman who would save others, yet remain on the other shore himself? If one had already dug a well, I would lower a rope to you, and then you could ascend. Why didn't we think of this plan?" The monk replied, "Yes, I also considered this, but at the time, there was no one available, so I resorted to this clumsy approach." He then persuaded the young man to hold the rope, and he himself descended by lowering the rope. Once he had the woman, he untied the rope and tied it around her slender waist, shouting, "Pull her up quickly!" The young man exerted force, and the woman was indeed pulled up. Glancing at her, though her clothes were wet, she appeared quite graceful and beautiful, which greatly moved his heart. He lied to her, saying, "Lady, give me your gourd, and let us rest on this high hill. I will take you to my master." The woman handed over the gourd to the young man. The young man looked around and saw a large stone, as big as a jar, and with great effort, he threw it into the well, hitting the monk's head and killing him in the water. The young man feared she might revive, so he took another stone and threw it repeatedly, but there was no sound. Knowing she was dead, he stopped and suddenly pulled the woman's clothes, saying, "Go! This place cannot be stayed in any longer." The woman saw the monk die and realized he was not a good acquaintance, so she was greatly afraid and wanted to run away. The young man threatened her with force, and she could not escape, so she reluctantly followed him. They walked for about a mile, winding through a path, until they reached a cave. The young man spoke to the woman, "The monk's intentions were not good, so I tried to save you. Now I will send you back, but your clothes are wet, which might be uncomfortable. I will go elsewhere, and you can do as you please, wait until they dry before proceeding."

    561

    予实无恶念。”言已趋出。妇信之，反感其德。亦觉衫裤尽水，不胜其寒。乃起坚扃其户，裸而以手挼之。正白身无备，少年早破窗突入，直据要津，妇遂无以自主。事已，少年谓妇曰：“汝欲归乎？盍行乎？”妇答以欲归。少年曰：“不可！僧以汝死，归将涉讼，必诬汝同谋。况予送汝返，汝夫益疑，汝有三命耶？”妇果慑其夫，乃询曰：“将若何？”少年曰：“予籍新乐，在此为人傭，拟于明晨旋里。汝能从予去，予无室，即以汝为妻，汝亦颇愿否？”妇沉思，实无所归，遂许诺。但曰：“一履又陷泥中，汝丐得之，乃可行。”少年颔之，启扉复出，仍下钥焉。至暮以饮食来，妇从之乞履，答曰：“履在人趾，实无由得。”妇曰：“弗得实难行。”遂同寝处，益相欢好。

    561

    The woman believed him and felt grateful for his virtue. She also felt her clothes were soaked through and could not withstand the cold. She got up and locked the door firmly, undressing and rubbing herself dry with her hands. Just as she was unprepared, the young man broke in through the window and immediately took control, leaving her unable to resist. After the deed was done, the young man said to her, "Do you want to go back? Why don't you go?" She replied that she wanted to return. The young man said, "You cannot! The monk will accuse you of being involved in the murder if you return, and you will be involved in litigation. Moreover, if I return you, your husband will be even more suspicious. Do you have three lives?" The woman was indeed frightened by her husband and asked, "What should be done?" The young man said, "I am from Xinle and am working here as a laborer, planning to return home tomorrow morning. If you can go with me, I have no wife, and I will take you as my wife. Are you willing?" The woman thought deeply and realized she had nowhere else to go, so she agreed. But she said, "One of my shoes is stuck in the mud. You must retrieve it, and then we can go." The young man nodded, unlocked the door, and left again, locking it behind him. By evening, he brought food and drink. The woman asked him to retrieve her shoe, and he replied, "The shoe is stuck on someone's foot, and I really cannot get it." The woman said, "Without it, it's really difficult to go." So they slept together and became more affectionate.

    562

    明日少年又出，妇以履之事坚嘱之，少年口诺而心以为难。傍午，闻僧尸已现，益用张皇，薄暝乃敢返室。独行野田间，见有赤钩两只，颇纤嫋，似合妇足，疑为他人所遗者。喜极，不暇顾虑，掇而疾趋。抵室，即以示妇，妇熟视，讶曰：“此予之故物，何由得入子手？”少年方言其故，忽有二役破扉入，以链絷之曰：“杀人贼果在此！”少年失色。诘罪由，乃知胡公检僧尸，并得妇履，疑妇未死，且不能远翔，与之偕者，必左近。孤男无敢向人乞此物，因命妇之夫遍搜笥中，取妇履，悉以付役。使役散置于幽径，潜伏以待，遇有拾遗者，尾之去，则妇可得，妇得而僧死之本末自明矣。役如公教，果获犯。语之以故，俯首伏罪。解之邑以抵僧命，村民亦以诬罔论徒，妇遂别嫁。以是案，公骤擢美任。人咸服其明断。

    Explanation

    562

    The next day, the young man went out again. The woman sternly warned him about the shoes, but he verbally agreed while thinking it would be difficult. At noon, he heard that the monk's body had been found, and he became even more agitated. He dared to return home only at dusk. Walking alone in the fields, he saw two red hooks that were quite slender and graceful, seeming to fit the woman's feet. He suspected they were left by someone else. Delighted, without time to consider, he picked them up and hurried quickly. Arriving home, he immediately showed them to the woman. She looked closely and said in surprise, "These are my old possessions. How did they end up in your hands?" The young man explained the reason. Suddenly, two officials broke down the door and entered, binding him with chains and saying, "The killer is indeed here!" The young man turned pale. They asked about the reason for the crime, and then they knew that Governor Hu had inspected the monk's body and found the woman's shoes. They suspected that the woman was not dead and could not have traveled far. Those who accompanied her must be nearby. An unmarried man would not dare to ask for such items from others. Therefore, he ordered the woman's husband to search all the boxes, took the woman's shoes, and handed them all over to the officials. Instructed the officials to scatter them in a secluded path and hide and wait. If anyone picked them up, they should follow them. Then the woman can be caught. Once the woman is caught, the details of the monk's death will become clear. The officials followed the governor's instructions and indeed caught the culprit. Explained the reason to him, he bowed his head and confessed. He was sent to the county to atone for the monk's death. The villagers were also punished for false accusations. The woman then remarried. Because of this case, the governor was quickly promoted to a better position. Everyone admired his clear judgment.

    563

    外史氏曰：一履之微，遗祸至此，要皆欢场实阶之厉也。盖妇不贪欢，则夫不至于窃履；夫不窃履，则妇亦不至于投缳。妇不投缳，则僧与少年皆可以无死。然非贤宰官得此一钩，则僧以救溺而死，妇且背夫而逋，狱将不解矣。卒以履之故，破此疑团。古人有绣履传奇，犹不若此事之诡异。

    ○　住住

    Explanation

    563

    The Commentary of the Outer Historian says: A mere shoe, though seemingly insignificant, has led to such grave consequences, demonstrating the real dangers lurking in the realm of pleasure. Had the wife not been greedy for pleasure, the husband would not have stolen the shoe; had the husband not stolen the shoe, the wife would not have hanged herself. If the wife had not hanged herself, the monk and the young man could both have avoided death. Yet, without the wise official finding this crucial clue, the monk would have died while trying to save someone from drowning, the wife would have abandoned her husband and fled, and the case would remain unresolved. Ultimately, it was the shoe that unraveled this mystery. There are ancient legends about embroidered shoes, but none are as strange as this event.

    564

    长安谷氏，巨族也。子弟多发武而不发文，以故驰怒马，试长剑，不一其人，而搦三寸毛锥者，则寥寥无几。一日，春雪初霁，会猎于城北山中，阖族毕往，少长咸集。较骑射，逐飞走，意气发扬，甚自得也。天将薄暮，所得雉兔以百计。倦而思归，遂皆返辔。有维藩者，年甫成童，亦娴弓马，诸兄均爱之，携以俱往。至是人骑抢攘，乃独相失在后。维藩素豪，亦无戒心，自跨小骊驹，牵一细犬，徐驱于平原枯草间。新月初生，寒烟四野，方觅故道前行，忽二小狐惊窜路左。维藩心甚喜，纵犬逐之，已亦策骑相随。狐行绝驶，犬与马皆不能及。未几，黑暗中又亡其犬，狐亦不知其安往，不胜懊悔。乃缓辔徐驰，更不辨其何地。约行数里许，马力亦疲，将借宿早行。倏见树影参差，灯光明暗，遂疾驱就之。至则巨宅如王侯，重垣列屋，栋宇云连。所见之荧荧，乃其墙角守夜者明炬，以防暴客耳。闻人声，即叱问之。维藩下骑与言，答以迷途，愿求栖止。

    Explanation

    564

    The Gu family of Chang'an was a prominent clan. Their younger generations were skilled in martial arts but not in literary pursuits, hence they rode spirited horses and tested long swords. Those who held a small brush (a metaphor for scholars) were very few. One day, after the first clear day following a spring snowfall, the entire family went hunting in the mountains north of the city. Young and old, all went together. They competed in archery and horseback riding, chased flying and running animals, and were full of spirit and confidence, feeling very satisfied. As dusk approached, the number of pheasants and rabbits they caught was in the hundreds. Tired and thinking of returning home, they all turned their reins to head back. There was a young man named Wéi Fān, who was just a young adult, also skilled in archery and horseback riding. His brothers all loved him and took him along. When they reached the hunting grounds, the scene was chaotic, and he became separated from the group. Wéi Fān was usually bold and had no sense of danger. He rode a small black foal and led a small dog, slowly riding through the flat grasslands. The new moon rose, and the cold mist spread across the fields. As he looked for the old path to continue forward, two small foxes suddenly fled to the left side of the road. Wéi Fān was very pleased and let the dog chase them, following on horseback. The foxes ran at an extremely fast pace, and neither the dog nor the horse could catch up. Soon, in the darkness, he lost his dog, and the foxes were nowhere to be seen, leaving him feeling extremely regretful. He slowed down the horse and rode slowly, no longer recognizing where he was. After traveling about several miles, the horse's strength also became exhausted, and he planned to find a place to stay and leave early the next day. Suddenly, he saw a patchy shadow of trees and flickering lights, and rushed to reach it. When he arrived, he saw a large house like that of a nobleman, with thick walls, arranged buildings, and interconnected structures. The dim lights he saw were the bright torches held by night guards at the corners of the walls, to prevent robbers. Hearing human voices, he dismounted and spoke to them. They answered that he was lost and wished to ask for a place to stay.

    565

    众以火烛之，笑曰：“个儿郎纤纤之年岁，深夜独行，曾不畏虎狼耶？当为之白主翁。”遂止维藩于茅舍，一人奔而入。少顷复来，曰：“主翁已起延客矣！”维藩絷驹随之往。行约数武，即见高门洞敞，红烛荧煌，宅第颇深邃。逻者引谒，阍人凡三四辈，皆鲜衣花帽，状类古之苍头，略加研诘，即导以入。历门凡两重，皆有守者，见之俱笑曰：“失路儿来乎？主翁固候若久矣！”维藩心颇讶之。入门而西，至院宇，精洁如客座。

    Explanation

    565

    The crowd lit torches to guide him, laughing and saying, "This young man, with his delicate years, walks alone at night, did he not fear tigers and wolves? We should inform the master." They then stopped Wei Fan at a thatched cottage, and one person rushed in. After a short while, he returned, saying, "The master has already risen to welcome the guest!" Wei Fan tied his horse and followed. They walked about several steps, and soon saw the high gate wide open, with red candles flickering, indicating a grand and deep residence. The guards led the way, and the doorkeepers, three or four generations in number, were all dressed in fine clothes and flowered hats, resembling ancient attendants. They asked a few questions and then led him in. They passed through two sets of gates, all of which had guards, who all laughed and said, "Is this the lost child? The master has been waiting for you for a long time!" Wei Fan was quite surprised. They entered and went west, reaching the courtyard, which was clean and elegant, like a guest's seat.

    566

    未及檐楹，主人早搴帘出，年约五旬余，高冠盛服，从以数人。降阶即言曰：“住住辈偶尔游戏，君何相逼之甚？”既而笑曰：“幸是稚龄犹可恕。”维藩不解所谓，心更茫然，惟灼灼以目相视。主翁又笑曰：“童子何知？老夫反与有过矣！”因延以入室，其中图书灿列，彝鼎杂陈，繁华不可名状。主翁与之坐，微叩里居，即起敬曰：“是吾乡世家也。密迩桑梓，未遑展谒，然仰慕久矣！”亟命呼住住来。使者往返数次，始闻珮声璆然。
    有小女子年可十三四，颦眉妖脸，披发慵妆，自帘外而入。瞥见维藩，神色顿异，一似惭怯不能前。主翁笑而语之曰：“是亦有夙缘，儿勿深相畏也。”女甫近翁侧，低鬟軃袖，曼立无言。维藩偷睨之，态若流珠，神侔秋水，虽童年情亦不能自制。闻女小语白父曰：“狂暴相凌，心胆几碎。何复引贼入室？”主翁怒之以目，徐曰：“小儿家竟不慎言乃尔！”女遂不敢言。

    Explanation

    566

    Before reaching the eaves and pillars, the host quickly drew the curtain aside and came out. He was around fifty years old, wearing a tall hat and splendid clothes, followed by several people. Descending the steps, he immediately said, "These children are just playing occasionally, why are you pressing them so much?" He then smiled and said, "Fortunately, they are young and can be forgiven." Wei Fan did not understand what was meant, and his heart was even more confused, only looking at each other with intense eyes. The host smiled again and said, "What do children know? I, the old man, am at fault myself!" He then invited him into the room. Inside, books and scrolls were brightly displayed, and ritual vessels were mixed together, the splendor was indescribable. The host sat down with him and gently asked about his place of residence. Upon hearing, he respectfully said, "This is a family of long-standing prestige from my hometown. Very close to my hometown, I had no time to pay respects, but I have admired you for a long time!" He urgently ordered to call Zhùzhù. The messenger went back and forth several times, and finally, the sound of her jade pendants could be heard.

    There was a young girl around thirteen or fourteen years old, with a frowning face and an alluring appearance, disheveled hair and a lazy makeup, entering from behind the curtain. When she saw Wei Fan, her expression suddenly changed, as if she was ashamed and afraid to come forward. The host smiled and said to her, "This too is a predestined connection, do not be deeply afraid of him." The girl just approached the old man's side, lowered her hair and sleeve, standing gracefully without speaking. Wei Fan glanced at her, her demeanor was like flowing pearls, and her spirit was like autumn water, even though she was young, her emotions could not be controlled. Hearing the girl whisper to her father, "He is so violent and overwhelming, my heart and courage are almost shattered. Why did you bring a thief into the house?" The host scolded her with his eyes and said slowly, "You children are so careless with your words!" The girl then dared not speak.

    567

    主翁因指女，谓维藩曰：“家有三女，两俱适人，此幼者与君年齿颇相若，窃愿附为婚姻，不识能俯就否？”维藩见女，心实爱好，且罔知为狐，腼然起谢。女闻翁言，赧颜注目，亦似惬其素心。两人之情已默相好合。少顷，主翁起曰：“郎君驰骋过劳，姑请小憩，明晨再当定议。”遂遮出。从人早先趋出户，惟女行步微濡，以致独后。将及帘际，维藩情不自持，乃戏牵其裾而留之。女回眸一顾，低笑曰：“荼縻刺未长，便解抓人裙带耶？”因以纤手解脱之。肌肤微亲，滑腻莫状，维藩益觉勃然，即直前拥抱。女仓卒急欲出声，翁早复入。

    Explanation

    567

    The host gestured toward the girl and said to Wei Pan, "I have three daughters, two of whom are already married. This youngest one is about your age, and I secretly wish to make her my wife. Don't you think you could consent?" Wei Pan saw the girl and truly loved her, not knowing she was a fox, and blushed and stood up to thank him. The girl heard the host's words, blushed and looked at him, and seemed pleased with his intentions. Their feelings had already quietly aligned. After a short while, the host stood up and said, "Sir, you've ridden too hard; please rest for a moment. We will decide tomorrow morning." He then escorted her out. The servants had already gone out, but the girl's steps were slightly slow, so she was left behind. As she approached the curtain, Wei Pan could not control himself and playfully grabbed her skirt to keep her. The girl turned her head and smiled, saying, "The hawthorn thorns haven't grown yet, so you already know how to grab skirts?" She then gently released him with her delicate hand. Their skin lightly touched, smooth and slippery, indescribable. Wei Pan felt even more aroused and embraced her directly. The girl was about to cry out in a hurry, but the host returned early.

    568

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头设有衾枕，就榻上睡，至晓未觉。翁来呼之醒，少致慰问，旋出一碧玉环与之曰：“此即住住所御者，郎君持以为信。明春桃夭之吉，可来此亲迎也。”言已，即遣之行，曰：“恐尊亲萦念，宜遄归，早饔不及留矣。”遂送至门外，命还其马，仍令人导之，示以周行而返。维藩就道，日中方抵邑门，家人已张皇寻觅，见始欣然。

    Explanation

    568

    Translation

    The Translation:

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头设有衾枕，就榻上睡，至晓未觉。翁来呼之醒，少致慰问，旋出一碧玉环与之曰：“此即住住所御者，郎君持以为信。明春桃夭之吉，可来此亲迎也。”言已，即遣之行，曰：“恐尊亲萦念，宜遄归，早饔不及留矣。”遂送至门外，命还其马，仍令人导之，示以周行而返。维藩就道，日中方抵邑门，家人已张皇寻觅，见始欣然。

    Final Translation:

    The Final Translation:

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头设有衾枕，就榻上睡，至晓未觉。翁来呼之醒，少致慰问，旋出一碧玉环与之曰：“此即住住所御者，郎君持以为信。明春桃夭之吉，可来此亲迎也。”言已，即遣之行，曰：“恐尊亲萦念，宜遄归，早饔不及留矣。”遂送至门外，命还其马，仍令人导之，示以周行而返。维藩就道，日中方抵邑门，家人已张皇寻觅，见始欣然。

    Final Translation:

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头设有衾枕，就榻上睡，至晓未觉。翁来呼之醒，少致慰问，旋出一碧玉环与之曰：“此即住住所御者，郎君持以为信。明春桃夭之吉，可来此亲迎也。”言已，即遣之行，曰：“恐尊亲萦念，宜遄归，早饔不及留矣。”遂送至门外，命还其马，仍令人导之，示以周行而返。维藩就道，日中方抵邑门，家人已张皇寻觅，见始欣然。

    Final Translation:

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头设有衾枕，就榻上睡，至晓未觉。翁来呼之醒，少致慰问，旋出一碧玉环与之曰：“此即住住所御者，郎君持以为信。明春桃夭之吉，可来此亲迎也。”言已，即遣之行，曰：“恐尊亲萦念，宜遄归，早饔不及留矣。”遂送至门外，命还其马，仍令人导之，示以周行而返。维藩就道，日中方抵邑门，家人已张皇寻觅，见始欣然。

    Final Translation:

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头设有衾枕，就榻上睡，至晓未觉。翁来呼之醒，少致慰问，旋出一碧玉环与之曰：“此即住住所御者，郎君持以为信。明春桃夭之吉，可来此亲迎也。”言已，即遣之行，曰：“恐尊亲萦念，宜遄归，早饔不及留矣。”遂送至门外，命还其马，仍令人导之，示以周行而返。维藩就道，日中方抵邑门，家人已张皇寻觅，见始欣然。

    Final Translation:

    维藩大惭，遽释手。翁呵女曰：“不速行，又使老夫觅汝，妮子直恁蹇缓？”乃相与俱去。维藩既失所望，神亦顿疲。床头

    569

    诘其止宿之所，具以实告。其从兄维垣，粗涉典坟，闻即骇然曰：“此狐也。幸而汝幼不加害，亦既足矣，犹望其他耶？”遂不再齿及，且为维藩议婚于豪家，以绝狐念。独维藩系怀不置。郁郁至明春，族中将祭扫，维藩复得出郭，因私往谒翁。物色至其处，则芳草如烟，人迹且杳，绝无前之轮奂者。兼之鸠呜鸦噪，树木阴森，凛乎不可独留。方将旋踵，忽见二美人淡妆艳服，交挽而行。及至维藩前，顾而问曰：“谁家黄口儿，何事踯躅于此？”维藩因告以情。

    Explanation

    569

    The place where they stayed was questioned, and he fully and truthfully reported. His older brother Wei Yuan, who had a superficial knowledge of the classics, was greatly alarmed upon hearing and said, "This is a fox. Fortunately, you were young and no harm was done. That is enough; yet still, do you hope for something else?" Thus, he no longer mentioned it and arranged a marriage for Wei Fan with a wealthy family to cut off the fox's thoughts. Only Wei Fan could not let it go. He was depressed until the following spring when the clan was about to perform a ritual of ancestral worship and tomb sweeping. Wei Fan was able to leave the city again and secretly went to visit the old man. He searched for the place and found grass like smoke, with no human traces, and no trace of the former grandeur. Moreover, there were crows cawing and ravens making noise, the trees were dark and eerie, and it was so chilling that one could not stay alone. Just as he was about to turn around, he suddenly saw two beautiful women with light makeup and bright clothing, walking hand in hand. When they reached Wei Fan, they looked at him and asked, "Who is this young child? Why are you hesitating here?" Wei Fan then told them the truth.

    570

    一衣绛绡者即红涨于面，曰：“汝果薄情郎乎？住住实予之妹，因汝家以异类相诋，阿翁甚恚，将遣之他适矣。奈何复来也？”一绿衣者亦怒曰：“阿翁自愦愦，轻以掌珠许匪人。玉环若在，可亟以付我。”维藩实佩于身，而坚不肯与。二女乃恨恨而去，维藩抱闷亦自归。回踪甫里许，遇一贫老道人，貌清癯，有菜色，乞钱于道周。维藩怜之，遽倾腰橐中物，举以相赠。道人谢讫，忽谓维藩曰：“吾视郎君之色，似重有忧者。

    Explanation

    570

    A woman dressed in crimson silk, her face turning red, said, "Are you really a heartless man? Stop! She is actually my sister. Because your family has insulted us as being of different species, our father is very angry and is going to send her elsewhere. Why did you come back?" Another woman in green clothes also spoke angrily, "Our father is just confused, and he lightly gave away our precious pearl to an unworthy person. If the jade ring is still here, you can quickly give it to me." Wefan actually wore it on his body but firmly refused to give it to them. The two women left with resentment, and Wefan, feeling depressed, returned home. As he turned back, he passed by a place about a li away and met an old, poor Taoist. He looked thin and emaciated, with a lack of nourishment, begging for money along the road. Wefan felt pity for him and quickly emptied the contents of his waist pouch, giving it to him. The Taoist thanked him, and then suddenly said to Wefan, "I see that your expression seems to have serious worries."

    571

    正当英年，不应若此。”维藩苦衷正无可诉，乃为道人缅述之，道人笑曰：“此属易事，但恐君家不能相容，或致丽人失所，贫道反为多事耳！”维藩坚以自矢，道人乃探袖中出三符曰：“以焚于郎君室中，某翁即自至。与之约，令送其女于归。及期不至，再焚其符，必得如意。末以清水一盂，焚第三符，令尊阃吞之，即有真仙下降，不克拆尔鸾俦。但须撙节，始可绵长，勿令人归咎于撮合山，则幸矣！”维藩敬谢，因拜道人为师，转瞬即失所在，惊愕而返。

    Explanation

    571

    In the prime of his youth, Wéifān should not be like this." His inner struggles were truly inexpressible, so he recounted his predicament to the hermit. The hermit laughed and said, "This is an easy matter, but I fear your family may not accept it, or it might cause the beautiful lady to lose her place, and I would be causing trouble for myself!" Wéifān firmly pledged to himself, and the hermit then probed into his sleeve and took out three talismans. "Burn them in your room," the hermit instructed, "and the old man will come on his own. Make an agreement with him, to have him send his daughter back. If he does not come by the agreed time, burn the talisman again, and you will surely get what you wish. Finally, with a bowl of clear water, burn the third talisman and have the lady of the house swallow it. Then a true immortal will descend, and your marriage will not be disrupted. But you must be frugal, only then can it last. Do not let people blame the matchmaker, then you will be fortunate!" Wéifān respectfully thanked the hermit and, having taken the hermit as his teacher, the hermit vanished in an instant, leaving Wéifān astonished and returning to himself.

    572

    及见诸兄，绐以他故，亦秘而不告。归至家，急不能待，独坐己室，候至夜分，焚其符。有顷，闻风声飕盧，俄而赫然震响，俨似巨物掷自檐端。出视之，一狐色甚苍黑，缚束如奉祀之豕，目光若炬，帖耳乞怜。维藩知为翁，故叱曰：“若以女饵我，继又背盟，今吾行法拘汝至，汝复何言？”狐委地求生，嗥叫似不能答。维藩笑曰：“今姑贷汝死，与汝三日期。倘送女来，姻好犹在，不然，予不任受若欺也。”遂释其缚而纵之。狐摇尾自去，不复回顾。

    Explanation

    572

    After meeting his brothers, he lied to them with an excuse, keeping it secret and not informing them. Upon returning home, he could not wait impatiently, so he sat alone in his room, waiting until midnight, when he burned the talisman. Soon after, he heard the sound of the wind, and suddenly a loud thunderous sound, as if a large object had been thrown from the eaves. He went out to see, and there was a fox, its fur very dark, bound like a pig offered in sacrifice, its eyes like torches, and it was cowering, begging for mercy. Wei Fan knew it was the old man, so he shouted, "You lured me with your daughter, and then broke the alliance. Now I have bound you here with my magic, what do you have to say?" The fox lay on the ground, begging for life, howling as if unable to respond. Wei Fan laughed and said, "For now, I will spare your life and give you three days' time. If you send your daughter, the marital alliance remains, otherwise, I cannot tolerate being deceived by you." He then released him from his bindings and let him go. The fox wagged its tail and left, without looking back.

    573

    维藩知其未服，恃有符在。翌日语其家人曰：“三朝后新妇当来，可为予安排卧室。”时维藩已失怙恃，即依从兄维垣同居。兄又他出，惟嫂在，怪之曰：“虽经缔盨，尚未纳采，小郎言何无据也？”维藩不辩，惟指挥仆婢营其居。床帐几席，务极富丽，家人皆以为狂。届期狐果不至，维藩怒，又焚一符。是日晴明，正当卓午。俄阴云陡生，霹雳大作，院中雨如反盆。旋见一老翁携弱女自空而下，衣缕绝无沾濡，径入洞房，谓维藩曰：“君无香火情，只一味恶作剧。

    Explanation

    573

    The protagonist, Wei Fan, believed that the fox had not submitted, relying on the talisman being present. The next day, he told his family: "Three days after the wedding, the new wife will come; can you arrange a bedroom for me?" At the time, Wei Fan had lost his parents and lived with his older brother, Wei Yuan. His brother was away, leaving only his sister-in-law, who found this strange and said, "Even though we have engaged, the formal proposal has not been made. Why does my young brother say such things without basis?" Wei Fan did not argue and instead instructed the servants to prepare the room. The bed, curtains, table, and chairs were all made extremely luxurious, and the family all thought he was acting foolishly. On the appointed day, the fox did not come, and Wei Fan became angry, burning another talisman. It was a bright day, just past noon. Suddenly, dark clouds gathered, and thunder roared, with rain pouring down like a bucket being overturned. Soon, an old man carrying a weak girl descended from the sky, completely dry, and entered the bridal chamber. The old man said to Wei Fan, "You have no spiritual connection, only a desire to play pranks."

    574

    衣奁未备，是以稍迟，奈何即遣丰隆相召耶？”维藩正色曰：“翁反覆无信，不如是，则事必不谐。”翁惭而退，留女在室。目之，年已稍增，娇艳似倍于昔。见维藩甚含愠色，自语曰：“强暴儿终非好相识。”维藩因温语慰之，女曰：“君家自憎妾，非妾家弃君，何不留面皮至是？”维藩乃述其怀想之苦，女始冁然回嗔。语次，天已开霁，晴朗无片云。婢媪纷集，瞥见新妇，咸以为画图中人，而莫测所自。

    Explanation

    574

    The bridal chest was not yet prepared, hence the slight delay; how could you have sent Fenglong to summon me immediately?" Weifan replied sternly, "Your husband is fickle and untrustworthy. If we do not proceed in this manner, our affairs will surely not succeed." The old man, feeling ashamed, withdrew, leaving the woman in the room. She looked at him, noting that she had grown somewhat older, yet her beauty seemed even more radiant than before. Seeing Weifan, she wore an expression of displeasure and muttered to herself, "A violent man like you is not a good acquaintance." Weifan then spoke gently to comfort her, and the woman said, "It is your family that hates me, not mine that abandons you. Why did you not keep up appearances until now?" Weifan then recounted his longing and sorrow, and the woman began to smile and return from her anger. As they spoke, the sky cleared, and the day became bright and cloudless. The maidservants gathered, catching a glimpse of the new bride, all thinking she was a figure from a painting, yet unable to fathom her origin.

    575

    维藩始隐约告嫂，嫂惊喜且忧，不得已而听之。为治卺卮，设花烛，始交拜成礼。女貌娇美，嫂亦甚爱怜之。及夜定情，维藩虽弱于年，实伟于器，女不胜其凿枘，太息曰：“无一不狂暴，宜吾辈皆惧见武夫。”维藩亦大笑。诘朝，焚道人符强女吞之。女自觉精神强固，亦心喜，以是相安，伉俪倍笃。午后有肩舆数乘，止于维藩家。及出舆，则翁媪及前二美人，皆华服径入，与嫂讲姻娅礼。见女执手涕泣，不忍别离。维藩始执婿礼参拜，翁终惭愤，不甚交谈。赠女衣饰十数箱，富家无以过之。

    Explanation

    575

    Wéifān first vaguely informed his sister-in-law, who was surprised and joyful yet worried, and reluctantly accepted it. He prepared the wine cups for the wedding, set up the flower and candle decorations, and they began to bow to each other and completed the ceremony. The woman was beautiful and charming, and his sister-in-law also loved and pitied her. At night, they consummated their marriage. Although Wéifān was young, he was actually strong in physique, but the woman could not bear the roughness of the intercourse. She sighed deeply and said, "Nothing was gentle, it is no wonder we all fear strong men." Wéifān also laughed. The next morning, he burned a talisman from a Taoist and forced the woman to swallow it. She felt her spirit was stronger and more solid, and she was also happy inside. Thus they lived harmoniously, and their marital bond became even stronger. In the afternoon, several sedan chairs arrived and stopped at Wéifān's home. When they got out of the sedan chairs, the old man and woman, along with the two previous beauties, all entered in splendid attire and discussed the etiquette of family relationships with his sister-in-law. They saw the woman holding hands and weeping, unable to part. Wéifān then performed the son-in-law's ritual bow. The old man finally felt ashamed and angry, and did not talk much. They gave the woman dozens of boxes of clothing and ornaments, richer than any other family.

    576

    款留至暮而去。后月余，维垣自外归，闻此事，深以为患，劝弟遣之，维藩不听。侦知某县有异人，颇善敕勒之术，聘使驱之。其人至，即入女室，周回一视，语维垣曰：“毫无妖氛，殆仙也，吾术不能祛之。”竟辞去。维垣弗信，复与诸弟维城等，故牵猎犬数头，突入维藩之室。女坦然殊无惧色，惟笑曰；“伯伯亦甚无礼！”言已，下阶趋迎。犬见女反皆辟易，如有所追逐然。诸客无如之何，遂怀惭散去。逾年，女举一男，无异于常儿，群议方息。

    Explanation

    576

    The elder brother detained her until evening before she left. After a month, Wei Yuan returned from outside and heard about this, deeply considering it a problem. He advised his younger brother to send her away, but Wei Fan did not listen. Upon learning that a certain county had a strange person who was quite skilled in the techniques of the Chale, he invited him to drive her away. When the person arrived, he entered the woman's room and looked around. He told Wei Yuan, "There is no sign of evil, she must be a divine being; my techniques cannot drive her away." He then left. Wei Yuan did not believe him and, with his younger brothers Wei Cheng and others, intentionally led several hunting dogs into Wei Fan's room. The woman remained calm and showed no sign of fear, only smiling and saying, "Uncle, you are also very impolite!" After saying this, she stepped down from the stairs and rushed to greet them. The dogs saw her and all retreated as if chasing something. The guests were unable to do anything about it and left with a sense of shame. The following year, the woman gave birth to a son who was no different from any other child, and public opinion finally calmed down.

    577

    嗣遇御前某真人以事过陕，维垣终以女为虞，复卑礼延至其家。真人结坛行法，女在室中亦甚惶惧，方与维藩诀别，忽维垣等见有金甲神人，屹立天半，持黄绢丈余，披示真人。共视之，朱书五字，巨如斗，其文则“葛仙翁作伐”也，倏不见。真人即起谓众曰：“吾师命也，不可以遣。”遂亦别去。初嫂与女善，屡谏维垣，因是益力争之，族中始无异心。女连孕三子，而容色不衰。阅数年，维藩寄子于兄嫂，与女入室遂不出。众辟其户入，阒其无人。盖皆从赠符者仙去矣！

    Explanation

    577

    A Taoist sage who had previously appeared before the emperor passed through Shaanxi on some business. Wei Yuan, the woman's brother, remained skeptical of the woman and humbly invited the sage to his home. The sage set up an altar and performed rituals, and the woman, who was in the room, was extremely frightened. As she was about to bid farewell to Wei Fan, Wei Yuan and others suddenly saw a divine figure in golden armor standing high in the sky, holding a yellow silk banner over a meter long, which he displayed to the sage. They all looked at it, and on the banner were five red characters as large as a wine jar, which read "Ge Xuan Arranged the Marriage." The figure then vanished. The sage immediately stood up and told the crowd, "It is the command of my master, and she cannot be sent away." He then left as well. Initially, the woman's sister-in-law had been friendly with her and often advised Wei Yuan, and as a result, he became even more insistent in his efforts, and the family no longer harbored any doubts. The woman went on to bear three sons, but her appearance did not diminish. After several years, Wei Fan entrusted his son to his brother and sister-in-law, and he entered the room with the woman, never coming out again. When the others forced their way into the room, it was empty. They had all become immortals by following the one who gave them the talisman.

    578

    外史氏曰：有此硬媒人，何虑好事不就？葛仙翁夫妇多强主人世婚姻，于此又见一斑。且维藩亦甚豪粗，既迫之于途，复窘之于室。南山隐隐，翁几陨身；斗帐嘤嘤，女更受创；莽男儿绝不似温存娇婿矣！何更有助虐之师与煽恶之族哉？宜乎，女为之寒心，而以暴客目之也。

    ○　仙涛
    　　仙涛者，广陵贫家女。父杜某，素无赖，郡人咸不齿之。仙涛生而聪颖，及长，色更殊尤。维扬故以瘦马驰名，养女之家自中人以下，鲜不居为奇货。因而自揣薄命，悬知不免于画屏，情实不甘，遂日夕祷天以祈早夭，厥志良可哀也。年十七，斧柯盈门，咸为豪家作伐，知必无望于盩蘩。闻其父已首肯，不胜悲怨，几以白练自戕，而不忍舍其母。一夕，中夜独起，踌躇于疏篱之下。秋魄微明，见一物乌毛白喙，目啖闪如灼金，状似狸奴。仙涛素珍此畜，虽愤闷之余，不禁破涕。迎而就之，且甚驯。乃贮之以皀，抱归绣阁。

    Explanation

    578

    Final Translation:

    The historian remarked, "With such a strong matchmaker, why worry about good things not being accomplished? The couple of Ge Xian often compelled human marriages, and here we see another example. Wei Pan was also very rough and bold, pressuring her on the road and further troubling her at home. The distant mountains loomed, and the old man nearly died; in the small bed, the woman suffered more. A rough man is not like a gentle and tender husband! How could there be teachers who help in cruelty and clans that incite evil? It is appropriate that the woman was heartbroken and regarded him as a violent intruder."

    Xian Tao
    Xian Tao was a poor girl from Guangling. Her father, Du, was always a scoundrel, and the people of the county all looked down on him. Xian Tao was born with intelligence and, as she grew older, her beauty became even more exceptional. Weiyang was famous for its "slender horses," and families that raised daughters, especially those of middle-class status or lower, often treated them as rare goods. Thus, Xian Tao, feeling her fate was weak, knew she would inevitably end up on a painted screen, which she truly did not want. She prayed to heaven day and night, hoping for an early death, a wish that was truly pitiable. At seventeen, her door was filled with marriage proposals from wealthy families, knowing she had no hope of a good match. Hearing that her father had already agreed, she was overwhelmed with grief and almost took her own life with a white silk scarf, but could not bear to leave her mother. One night, in the middle of the night, she got up alone, pacing beneath the sparse fence. The faint moonlight revealed a creature with black feathers and a white beak, its eyes flashing like burning gold, resembling a house cat. Xian Tao had always cherished this pet, and even in her anger and frustration, she could not help but break into tears. She welcomed it, and it was very docile. She fed it with food, then carried it back to her embroidered chamber.

    579

    时已夜阑，顿然思睡，遂不及解衣，竟就枕。方转侧如寐，觉前物暴长，其巨如牛。惊而欲号，己身忽不自由，飞跨于其背。畜即咆哮作威，奔突出户，盖虎也。仙涛心胆俱碎，而又无如之何，因瞑目听其所之。有顷，虎忽蹲伏不前。少苏而展视，则非城非郭，半水半山，面溪有屋数椽，野花岸柳围绕柴门。曾几何时，而日已正午，仙涛恃有人家，娇嘶以乞救。虎竟踊身置之地上，掉尾自去。仙涛自庆幸免馋吻，存息尔许时，见此家绝无人出，乃强起而入之。其中草堂三楹，颇幽洁，而松阴当槛，竹影横窗，迥异人间俗境，疑为仙宅。无如升堂入室，不但无人，且无饔飧之具，心益大疑。有藤床，坐而偃息。

    Explanation

    579

    It was already deep into the night when he suddenly felt drowsy. He finally went to bed without undressing. As he turned over as if about to sleep, he suddenly realized that something in front of him had grown abnormally large, as big as a cow. Shocked and wanting to cry out, he suddenly became unable to control himself and leapt onto its back. The creature roared with authority, rushed out of the door, and it was a tiger. His heart and courage both shattered, yet he was helpless, so he closed his eyes and waited to see where it would go. After a while, the tiger suddenly crouched and stopped. After a brief rest and opening his eyes, he found himself not in a city or a town, but in a place half water and half mountain, with a few thatched cottages facing a stream, surrounded by wildflowers and willows by a thatched gate. In no time, it was already noon. Relying on the presence of the house, he cried out pitifully for help. The tiger jumped and placed him on the ground, wagged its tail, and left. He felt relieved to avoid its bite, and after resting for a while, he saw that there was no one coming out of the house, so he forced himself to get up and enter. Inside, there was a thatched cottage with three rooms, quiet and clean, with pine shade by the windows and bamboo shadows across the windows, completely different from the mundane world. He suspected it was a fairy's abode. However, when he entered the main hall and inner chambers, there was not only no one there, but also no food and drink supplies, making him even more suspicious. There was a rattan bed, and he sat and rested.

    580

    惊定不觉苦饥，而前虎复来，以首枕藉其爪，卧于树下。仙涛甫知其异，乃祝曰：“承若携我来，虽脱火坑，将为饿殍，可若何？”虎似解其言，即出旋归，背负鹿肉半肩，以遗之。仙涛笑曰：“此地无由举火，我岂能茹毛饮血者哉？盛意殊不敢拜登。”虎又知其意，又去许久，衔莲房数十枚，归而委诸槛外。仙涛剖食，味绝甘，竟以果腹。自此与虎渐稔，不复畏忌。虎暮出朝归，尽日不他适，若相伴然。且时觅佳果，以备糗粮，仙涛遂不绝口实。惟虑秋飚凛冽，莫具絮衣，而一榻宵眠，片毡罔有，将不啼饥而号寒。

    Explanation

    580

    After the shock, Xian Tao no longer felt the hunger, and the previous tiger came back, lying with its head resting on its paws under a tree. Just then, Xian Tao realized its strangeness and wished, "If you brought me here, although I escaped the fire pit, I will become a starving corpse, what then?" The tiger seemed to understand his words and immediately went out and returned, carrying half a shoulder of deer meat to give him. Xian Tao laughed and said, "There is no way to light a fire here. How can I be someone who eats raw meat and drinks blood? I truly dare not accept such kindness." The tiger understood his meaning and went away for a long time, carrying dozens of lotus pods, and returned to leave them outside the cage. Xian Tao cut and ate the lotus pods, which had an extremely sweet taste, and finally satisfied his hunger. From then on, Xian Tao gradually became familiar with the tiger and no longer feared or avoided it. The tiger would go out at dusk and return in the morning, staying all day without going elsewhere, as if accompanying him. Sometimes it would search for good fruits to prepare for food, so Xian Tao never went hungry. However, he only worried about the cold autumn wind, not having warm clothing, and sleeping on a bed at night, without even a small piece of felt, he would cry from hunger and shiver from the cold.

    581

    讵意世外桃源，别有天地，不第草木长春，亦从无阴雨凉盪之虞。习之既久，遂以相安。继而甚苦郁闷，初犹诓怯，仅倚荆扉，积渐远出，竟不时为骑虎之游。始知其地环山带水，只余一径相通，广袤约数百里。岚光溪影，树色花香，靡不绝佳，仙涛领略殆遍。且自辟谷以来，红颜若有羽翼，身轻于叶，虽不谙吐纳之术，已不啻地上游仙。暇辄扰虎为戏，役之如奴，少忤即挞之十数，虎亦伏而不动。居约五六年，虎忽出而不复。仙涛既自能遐举，亦不甚仰给于虎。惟遁迹已久，无由再觅衣襦，况乎出当秋初，尚衣单袷，既空山无以缝缀，布缕渐尽，遂无可衣。虽以深闺之丽质，竟不免裸以为饰，亦茫然不觉其苦。

    Explanation

    581

    Who would have thought that in this secluded paradise beyond the world, there is a unique realm, not only with evergreen plants and trees but also without the worry of cold, rainy weather. Having become accustomed to it over time, one comes to accept it. Later, she greatly suffered from depression; at first, she was still hesitant, leaning only on a simple, rustic door. Gradually, she ventured further out and even took risks by riding a tiger. She then realized that the place is surrounded by mountains and water, with only one path connecting it, covering an area of about several hundred miles. The shimmering light and reflections in the stream, the colors of trees and the fragrance of flowers, nothing is not excellent; the fairy waves have been thoroughly experienced. Moreover, since abstaining from food, the fair maiden seems to have wings, her body is lighter than a leaf. Although she does not know the techniques of breathing exercises, she is no different from a wandering immortal on earth. When idle, she would play with the tiger, treating it like a servant. If it disobeys, she would beat it several times, and the tiger would lie down and remain still. After living there for about five or six years, the tiger suddenly disappeared and did not return. The fairy waves, having the ability to ascend to the heavens, do not rely much on the tiger. However, having been hidden away for a long time, she could not find clothes again. Especially when she goes out in early autumn, still wearing thin and light clothing, with the empty mountain having no means to sew or patch, the threads of cloth are gradually running out, thus having nothing to wear. Despite her beautiful nature as a lady in her inner chambers, she is not immune to being naked as decoration, and she is unaware of her own discomfort.

    582

    一日，忽动乡思，念母綦甚，恃其不翼而飞，顿欲一归省视。乃甫经涉想，虎即自来，且作人言语之曰：“子前生实施厚德，出予于陷阱，纵予于山林，得以享年至千岁之久，予故化身，脱子之厄，成子之志，将与子同证地仙。今乃尘念顿生，孝思不匮，予不能更留子，请送子归。然自兹富贵可期，蓬瀛则不可望矣。”仙涛聆其言，意颇少悔，究迫于鞠育之恩，绝不系恋。兼因地无居人，性同野鹿，忘身无寸缕，坦然驭虎以行。时已昏夜，恍惚在风云中，不知几千百里。比及天曙，已抵江都，则山川如旧，风境依然，不禁凄然泪下。

    Explanation

    582

    One day, suddenly struck by homesickness, he missed his mother greatly, relying on the impossible hope of flying without wings, and immediately wanted to return home to visit her. Just as he began to think about it, the tiger came immediately, and spoke in human words, saying, "In your past life, you practiced great virtue, saving me from a trap and setting me free in the mountains. You allowed me to live for a thousand years, and I therefore transformed, freeing you from danger and helping you achieve your goals, intending to share with you the attainment of earthly immortality. Now, earthly thoughts have suddenly arisen, and your thoughts of filial piety are inexhaustible. I cannot keep you here anymore, so I will send you back home. But from now on, wealth and honor can be expected, while the immortal lands of Penglai and Yingzhou are no longer within reach." Xian Tao listened to his words, feeling a little regret, but was ultimately driven by the debt of being raised, and did not linger or hesitate. Because the place had no inhabitants, his nature was like that of a wild deer, forgetting his body and wearing no clothes, he calmly rode the tiger. It was already evening, and he felt as if in a whirlwind, not knowing how far they had traveled. By the time dawn broke, they had already arrived at Jiangdu, where the mountains and rivers were the same, and the scenery unchanged. He could not help but shed tears of sadness.

    583

    而千行夹郭，万雉笼烟，昔人所云“绿杨影里是扬州”者，又举目即见矣。仙涛方悲不自胜，虎忽大吼而跃。弹指之顷，早已陨越于下。晨光四照，身落郊坰，遥见行人纷如，不觉顿萌闺态，究以无衣自沮。幸有苇塘，藏娇终日，至夜，甫敢飞出。将返其家，又不甚记忆，黾勉而行。约里许，自谓已至，则非莫愁村中，实在辟疆园外。仓猝款扉，履声几几，有人出应，仙涛犹谓其母。及拔关觌面，冠服翩翩，竟属儒林吉士。银蟾皎洁之下，仙涛遂无地自容，因亟飞遁，知若人已睹之也。闻伊人诧叹良久，唾为妖魅，复自入。仙涛既路迷乡井，归忘云山，进退维谷之余，不禁因悔生痛，涕泗滂沱。猛悟曰：“身为目染，归亦安之？斯人殆我夫也。

    Explanation

    583

    Thousands of rows of houses flanked the city walls, and tens of thousands of buildings were shrouded in smoke. The ancient saying, "In the shade of green willows lies Yangzhou," was once again visible at a glance. Xiantao was overwhelmed with sorrow, unable to bear it, when suddenly a tiger roared and leaped. In the blink of an eye, it had already fallen to the ground. Morning light spread everywhere, and her body fell in the outskirts. She saw people moving about in the distance and suddenly developed a delicate, feminine demeanor, feeling discouraged due to lack of clothing. Fortunately, there was a reed pond where she could hide her beauty all day. At night, she dared to fly out. As she struggled to return home, she was not very clear in memory, so she walked with effort. About a mile away, she thought she had arrived, but it was not in Mochou Village; it was actually outside the Pijiang Garden. Hurriedly knocking on the door, footsteps could be heard. Someone came out to respond, and Xiantao still thought it was her mother. Upon opening the door and facing the person, he was dressed in elegant attire and turned out to be a scholar from the literati class. Under the bright moon, Xiantao could not bear to face the person and quickly flew away, knowing that the person had seen her. She heard the person exclaim for a long time, spat out as a demon, and then went back inside. Xiantao, having lost her way in the village and forgotten the mountains, found herself in a difficult situation with no way out. She could not help but feel pain from regret, and tears and snot flowed profusely. Suddenly realizing, she said, "My body is tainted by sight, even returning home would not be safe. This person might be my husband."

    584

    ”顿思就之，而谅其疑怪，并耻于自献其身。俄闻虎啸声，有布裹掷自空际。乘月启视，则女衣一袭，袍裤咸具。乃大喜，遂益德虎。身已就衣，深宵亦不他往，藉草眠至旦，踪迹至家。适父以贱业远行，惟母在，失女而得女，把臂号泣，仙涛亦失声。母言前失儿时，门户尽扃，不知焉往，疑儿不屑小星，毕命荒野，不谓犹在也。因缅询之，仙涛尽白其异。母殊未信，验之，闺体宛然，乃释厥疑。仙涛居家旬日，不复火食，日惟啖果数枚。兼慕白云乡，将出家为女冠。究以裸体之耻，白之母，使访其人。则许姓名靖，青衿中之秀出者，因更注念不置。母知共意，闻许方鳏，反倩冰以干之。

    Explanation

    584

    顿思就之，而谅其疑怪，并耻于自献其身。俄闻虎啸声，有布裹掷自空际。乘月启视，则女衣一袭，袍裤咸具。乃大喜，遂益德虎。身已就衣，深宵亦不他往，藉草眠至旦，踪迹至家。适父以贱业远行，惟母在，失女而得女，把臂号泣，仙涛亦失声。母言前失儿时，门户尽扃，不知焉往，疑儿不屑小星，毕命荒野，不谓犹在也。因缅询之，仙涛尽白其异。母殊未信，验之，闺体宛然，乃释厥疑。仙涛居家旬日，不复火食，日惟啖果数枚。兼慕白云乡，将出家为女冠。究以裸体之耻，白之母，使访其人。则许姓名靖，青衿中之秀出者，因更注念不置。母知共意，闻许方鳏，反倩冰以干之。

    Translation:
    He thought about it immediately and accepted her suspicions and strange thoughts, feeling ashamed to offer himself. Soon he heard the roar of a tiger, and a cloth-wrapped object was thrown from the sky. Under the moonlight, he opened and looked, and found a set of women's clothing, with a robe and trousers complete. He was greatly delighted and thus further honored the tiger. Having dressed, he did not go elsewhere even in the deep night, lying on the grass to sleep until dawn, tracing his way home. Just as his father was traveling far away for a humble occupation, only the mother was there. She had lost a daughter and gained a daughter, holding each other's arms and weeping, and Xiantao also lost his voice. The mother said that when the child was lost, all the doors were locked, and no one knew where she went. She suspected that the child had despised the small stars and died in the wilderness, did not expect her to still be alive. Therefore, he asked about it in detail, and Xiantao fully revealed his strange experience. The mother did not believe it at all, but examining it, her body was intact, thus he released his doubts. Xiantao stayed at home for ten days, not eating fire-cooked food, each day only eating a few fruits. He also admired the White Cloud Village and intended to become a nun. Finally, due to the shame of being naked, he told his mother and asked her to find the person. The person named Xu Jing, a distinguished scholar among the students, therefore, he continued to think about it without letting go. The mother knew his intention, hearing that Xu was a widower, she asked a matchmaker to arrange it.

    585

    初时许未诺，循得佳梦，合以吉卜，皆谓娶妇得若人，当游玉堂金马。而杜父不在家，杜母又不计聘资，许家綦贫，乃以金钗订盟。仙涛虑父返或有扞格，使媒妁怂恿之，匝月即涓吉成礼。比及杜父归来，早无及矣。燕婉克遂，鱼水倍欢。第仙涛之始末，惟母知之，他人皆莫之与闻，许益未由得知，绝不疑月下飞去者，即此床头人也。仙涛虑以怪异见薄于夫，始日食一餐，究亦见意而已。惟弱体轻盈，辄潜以距跃为乐，恒至数十寻不止。数月之后，渐以不能饮馔，竟复其故。期年，许以乡魁登桂榜。未及三载，又捷南宫。馆阁数年，出为某郡太守。仙涛忆虎言果验，绣像祀之，因并祀元坛神以掩其迹。

    Explanation

    585

    Initially, Xu had not agreed, but following a good dream and favorable divination, it was believed that marrying a woman like her would lead to a life of prestige and high office. However, Du's father was not at home, and Du's mother did not consider the dowry, as Xu's family was extremely poor. Thus, they used a gold hairpin as a token of engagement. Xiantao was concerned that her father's return might cause difficulties, so she had matchmakers encourage them, and after a month, they chose a favorable date and completed the wedding ceremony. By the time Du's father returned, it was already too late to change the situation. Their marriage was harmonious and happy, with a blissful relationship. However, the details of Xiantao's origins were known only to her mother; others were unaware, and Xu had no way of knowing, so he never suspected that the person who flew away under the moonlight was the same person at her bedside. Xiantao feared that her unusual nature might make her seem strange to her husband, so she initially ate only one meal a day, which was also an expression of her feelings. Her delicate and graceful body enjoyed jumping as a form of play, often leaping dozens of times in a row. After several months, she gradually could no longer eat and eventually returned to her original state. After a year, Xu passed the local examination and was listed on the imperial examination board. Within three years, he passed the higher-level examination at the imperial palace. After several years in the imperial library, he was appointed as the governor of a certain prefecture. Xiantao remembered the tiger's words and found them to be true, so she created embroidered images to worship and also worshipped the Yuan Tan deity to conceal her true nature.

    586

    外史氏曰：虎知报德，无足奇也。惟以刚猛之质，而软款温柔，绝无粗莽，寅使君何遽能办此？至仙涛以矢志之高，享洞天数年之乐，宜已！虽裸裎跨虎，颇失闺箴，而世外苎萝不同人间帷闼，或不妨少越范围。况念切春晖，即图归计，以世无不孝神仙也。杜陵云：“绝代佳人，幽居空谷。”吾犹得以此况之。

    Explanation

    586

    The historian says: It is not remarkable that the tiger knows to repay kindness. Yet, with its fierce and strong nature, it can be gentle and tender, completely without roughness. How could the Tiger Lord suddenly accomplish this? As for Xian Tao, with his lofty aspirations, he enjoys the bliss of the fairy realm for several years, which is fitting! Although he rides a tiger naked, it slightly violates the rules of propriety for women, but since the secluded Zhulu is different from the worldly chambers, it may not be inappropriate to slightly exceed the bounds. Moreover, deeply longing for maternal love, he plans to return home, as there is no filial immortality. Du Fu says, "The most beautiful woman of her time, living in seclusion in a remote valley." I can still use this to describe him.

    587

    ○　陆厨
    　　桐城张相国家有治庖之人，陆姓无名，呼之以厨而已。煎熬燔炙，滋味深谙，相国尤喜之。其家负郭，岁时始请假一归。其妻少艾，独处寂寥，而厨又嗜酒，归即酣卧，以是脱辐为常，遂出之。然厨欲藉此出游，秘不以告，府中人罕有知其鳏者。一日中秋节后，宴会少闲，厨又给假。归途中遇公之厮养某。两人素莫逆，因戏之曰：“陆大哥今始归视，阿嫂恐天上婵娥，不能为汝重圆也。”时厨已微醺，乃笑曰：“曩昔或如子言，今则不然矣。”某愕然，亟询其故。厨既失口，不能讳，遂具白其弃妻之由。某不信，又诘之，厨更正色以言，凿凿可据。

    Explanation

    587

    In Tongcheng, there was a high-ranking official's household that employed a man skilled in cooking, surnamed Lu but unnamed, who was simply called 'the cook.' He was adept at frying, boiling, roasting, and grilling, deeply understanding the flavors, and the official particularly appreciated his work. His home was located near the city walls, and he only took leave once a year during the seasonal festivals. His wife was a young and beautiful woman, living alone in loneliness, while the cook loved drinking and would get drunk and sleep upon returning, making it a common occurrence for him to become inebriated, eventually leading to their divorce. However, the cook used this as an excuse to travel, keeping it secret from the household, and few people knew that he was a widower. One day after the Mid-Autumn Festival, when the banquets were few and far between, the cook was given leave again. On his way back, he encountered a servant of the official. The two had been very close friends, and the servant joked with him, saying, "Brother Lu, you're just returning home now; your wife might be afraid that the celestial maidens can't reunite you." By then, the cook was slightly tipsy and laughed, saying, "In the past, perhaps as you said, but not now." The servant was surprised and immediately asked why. The cook, having already let slip, could not hide it and explained the reason for his divorce. The servant did not believe him and continued to question him, to which the cook responded more seriously, stating that his words were solidly based and verifiable.

    588

    某始笑曰：“兄亦太不情，奈何耽麴蘖而捐鸾凤？”因拉之如肆，与共饮。盖某从相国数年，薄有积蓄，思娶妇苦无佳者。向曾见厨之妻，心颇艳之，今闻其离异，欲浼厨为玉成，故假杯勺与之议。酒数巡，遂以言挑之曰：“阿嫂既被兄出，以渠姿容，早当再醮而去。兄已失计，纵欲复合，恐破镜未易圆矣。”厨侧首曰：“予固不屑于此，但前返敞村，风闻臭花奴以予为鉴，拣精择肥，犹未嫁也。”某大喜，亟起酌曰：“果尔，则弟有所恳，兄其毋辞。”厨饮而叩之，则曰：“非弟敢为陈平，但兄听弃者，人皆可取。况弟久无室家，兄必怜之，盍为予成此好事。”厨闻其言，有难色，良久乃曰：“妇既见逐，人尽可夫，弟娶之固无害于义。

    Explanation

    588

    I began to laugh and said, "Brother, you are also too uncaring. How can you indulge in alcohol and abandon your phoenix-like wife?" So I pulled him to the market and drank together. It was because I had served the Prime Minister for several years and had a little savings, wondering about marrying but finding no good candidate. Once I saw the wife of the cook, I thought her very beautiful. Now I hear that she has been divorced. I want to ask the cook to help arrange the marriage, so I pretended to drink with him to discuss it. After several rounds of drinks, I used words to hint at it, saying, "Your wife has already been divorced by you. Because of her appearance, she should have remarried and left. You have already made a mistake. Even if you want to reconcile, fear that the broken mirror is hard to mend." The cook turned his head and said, "I naturally do not care about this. But before returning to my village, I heard that the ugly flower slave takes me as a model, selects the best and the fattest, and still has not married." I was very pleased and quickly stood up to pour another drink, saying, "If so, then I have something to ask. You should not refuse." The cook drank and asked, "It is not that I dare to be like Chen Ping. But you have already abandoned her, anyone can take her. Especially since I have been without a wife for a long time, you must pity me. Why not help me with this good deed?" The cook heard my words, looked hesitant, and after a long time said, "The woman has already been driven away. Everyone can marry her. I marrying her would not harm righteousness."

    589

    第予与若人，不等陌路，何能言？”某又相强，且曰：“弟居城市，乡曲之人多未稔，若欲倩冰，非兄不可。否则，当泄兄事于主人，自兹不令兄归。”厨微笑，终不许。某乃顿生狡计，餂之曰：“兄与嫂离，今已数月，亦思胶续乎？”厨曰：“然。予年正壮，讵能久鳏？无如执役潭府，数旬甫一归，此心不免疑虑。今欲得妇，必在城中，而邑居者又复我憎，可若何？”某心暗喜，遂言曰：“兄意若此，正不必图其新。维彼旧者，固可耳。”厨不能解，诘其故。某嗫嚅久之，始曰：“弟家实居府侧，娶若人必置于此。虽是弟之新婚，实兄之旧偶，妇人水性，与之言当无不从。兄倘一时情动，不妨夜来，弟自义让。两雄一雌，永以为好，惟在兄之作合耳。

    Explanation

    589

    Since I and you are not strangers, how could I not speak?" The narrator then urged further, saying, "I live in the city, and I am not well acquainted with the people of the countryside. If you wish to find a matchmaker, you must be the one to do it. Otherwise, I will reveal your affairs to the master, and from now on, you will not be allowed to return." The cook smiled but still refused. The narrator then hatched a cunning plan and coaxed, "Brother, you have been separated from your wife for several months now. Do you also miss her?" The cook replied, "Yes. I am in my prime, how could I remain a widower for long? But since I serve in the deep palace, I only return home once every few weeks, so I cannot help but have doubts. Now, if I want to find a wife, it must be in the city, but those who live in the countryside I dislike. What can I do?" The narrator was secretly pleased and said, "If that is your intention, you need not seek a new wife. An old partner would suffice." The cook did not understand and asked why. The narrator hesitated for a while before saying, "My home is actually beside the mansion. If you marry her, she will be placed there. Although it is my new marriage, it is actually your old partner. Women are like water, and whatever you say to them, they will follow. If you feel a sudden urge, you can come at night, and I will yield out of propriety. Two males and one female, forever in harmony, it all depends on your arrangement."

    590

    ”语已，厨大笑曰：“弟欺予哉？世固无此理，且弟亦非其人。”某又曰：“再婚之佳人，不等深闺之处子，弟又何所吝惜，而以之诳兄？”厨见其实然，遂窃计曰：“予所得者，仅足杖头。况续一妇，非数十金不能，又无若故妻之美者。今果如此，是无妇而有妇也。渠自愿顶绿巾，于予何尤？”计画有顷，又阳为不可，必待某自誓方始允从，与之订约而别。归至家，即往谒妇之父，且措词曰：“某不才，身为贱役，不能时常归家，恐虚令爱青春，故以之返璧。而伉俪之情，则不忍忘也。窃虑令爱仍适匪人，不免甚为系念。兹有邑中某者，家累千金，颇殷富，可托终身。翁倘垂盼，某愿执伐。”言已又再拜。妇之父喜其委婉，姑颔之。厨退又浼人怂恿。

    Explanation

    590

    After speaking, the servant laughed and said, "Are you deceiving me, brother? There is no such thing in the world, and you are not the right person for it." The speaker replied, "A remarried beautiful woman is not the same as a maiden from a secluded room. What do you have to be stingy about, and to deceive me with it?" The servant, seeing that it was true, thought to himself, "What I have is barely enough for a walking stick. To marry again requires tens of gold coins, which I cannot afford. There is no one as beautiful as my former wife. If it is indeed so, then I am without a wife but have a wife. She voluntarily wears the green cap. What fault is there with me?" After some planning, he pretended to be unwilling and only agreed after the speaker swore to him. They made an agreement and parted ways. Upon returning home, he immediately went to visit the father of the woman and phrased his words as follows: "I am a lowly servant, unable to return home frequently, fearing that your daughter's youth will be wasted. So I return her as a jade, meaning to give her back. The affection between husband and wife cannot be forgotten. I secretly worry that your daughter will still marry an unworthy person, which cannot help but cause great concern. There is someone in the county with a fortune of thousands of gold coins, quite wealthy, and can be relied upon for life. If you would be so kind as to consider, I am willing to act as a matchmaker." After speaking, he bowed twice again. The father of the woman was pleased with his tactful manner and nodded slightly. Afterward, he asked someone to persuade him.

    591

    明日某潜来访厨，肥马鲜衣，风姿俊逸。厨留之与饮，妇之父母私往窥觇，皆大喜。遂从厨言，慨许之，妇故阴有弗愿也。某诹吉行聘，不日亲迎。毕姻后，某之年既甚少于厨，又从相国无他职役，朝夕与妇聚处，妇亦安之。惟厨以宿约，数向某言求一晤妇。某难于峻拒，初犹借端支延，盖不忍以既得之珠，复用以弹雀。厨不能平，每与侪辈言，詈某负心，闻者为之喷饭。某知之，乃大恚，指厨为诬己，厨益忿忿。阅数月，某从相国赴他处，夕未得归。有人逾垣入，毙妇于室，面被刀伤十数，几无完肤。某归即报县，验之，四体裸然，且系成奸后死者。某以厨有夙恨，必其所为，遂言于相国。

    Explanation

    591

    The next day, someone secretly visited the kitchen, arriving in a fine horse and dressed in fresh clothes, with a graceful and elegant appearance. The kitchen keeper kept him and drank with him, and the wife's parents went to secretly observe, all greatly pleased. They followed the kitchen keeper's words and readily agreed, though the wife secretly did not wish it. The visitor chose a favorable time for the betrothal and soon went to personally fetch her. After the marriage was completed, the visitor, who was much younger than the kitchen keeper, followed the chancellor and had no other duties, living day and night with his wife, who also settled in. However, the kitchen keeper, due to an old agreement, repeatedly asked the visitor to arrange a meeting with the wife. The visitor found it difficult to reject harshly and initially found excuses to delay, as he could not bear to waste a pearl already obtained by using it again to shoot sparrows. The kitchen keeper could not be calm and often spoke to his peers, cursing the visitor for being unfaithful, which made others laugh so hard they spit out their food. The visitor knew this and became very angry, pointing the finger at the kitchen keeper as accusing him falsely, which made the kitchen keeper even more angry. After several months, the visitor followed the chancellor to another place and did not return that evening. Someone climbed over the wall and entered, killing the wife in the room, leaving her face with dozens of knife wounds, almost none of her skin intact. The visitor returned immediately and reported to the county, where they examined the body, finding it completely naked and related to the murder after committing adultery. The visitor believed that the kitchen keeper, with an old grudge, must have done it and reported this to the chancellor.

    592

    闻是事者咸证之，执以送官，毒加拷掠。厨即有前隙，不能复辩，竟成招，其实诬也。某自妇亡，不敢复处其室。适有山东某客，因事淹留，不胜旅邸之费，缘与某素识，假以所居。初未知有怪异也，及夕客寝，闻有人啼曰：“予面虽毁，亦宜细检予身，奈何使淫人得志也？”言之数四，而不睹其形容，始疑厨为枉。翌日见某，为述所闻，某不听。僚仆有与立者，聆之骇然，以白相国。相国曰：“事果有冤，当复验。”乃致书于宰，宰亦疑厨不类，因与吏谋。召某与妇之父母，并出厨于狱，同至葬所。启冢出尸，竟未腐，俨然如生。宰心异之，先令某谛观，答曰：是。又令厨与妇之母视之，则皆云非。宰诘焉，厨先对曰：“渠虽某之妇，其先实囚之妻。

    592

    Those who heard the matter all testified to it, and he was seized and taken to the authorities, where he was subjected to torture. The cook had a previous grudge and could not defend himself, so in the end, he confessed, though it was actually a false accusation. After his wife's death, he dared not live in the same room again. Just then, there was a guest from Shandong who stayed due to circumstances and could not bear the cost of the inn. Because he had known him before, the guest lent him his residence. At first, he did not know of any strange occurrences, but when the guest went to sleep at night, he heard someone cry out saying, "My face though destroyed, should also be carefully examined, why should a licentious person achieve his desires?" He said it several times, but could not see his appearance, and began to suspect that the cook was wronged. The next day, he saw the cook and told him what he had heard, but the cook did not listen. There was a subordinate who was standing with him, who heard it and was greatly startled, and told the chancellor. The chancellor said, "If there is indeed a wrong, it should be re-examined." He then wrote a letter to the magistrate, who also doubted that the cook was unlike the victim, and conspired with the officials. They summoned the cook and the parents of the woman, along with the cook, they were taken to prison, and together to the burial place. They opened the tomb and brought out the body, which had not yet decayed and still looked as if alive. The magistrate was surprised by it and first instructed the cook to carefully observe, and he answered, "Yes." Then he instructed the cook and the mother of the woman to look at it, and both said it was not. The magistrate questioned him, and the cook first replied, "She was the wife of the cook, but originally, she was the wife of the prisoner."

    593

    寝处数年，隐微无不知。其左乳有疮疤，大如掌。私处有瘤，如指顶，今并无之。且肤色过白，亦不相似。”宰又以诘妇之母，所供佥同。宰大惊，姑掩其棺，逮众俱返。至署，先以刑拟妇之父，讯以家所往来，犹有何人。其父本乡愚，大惧吐实。则有远亲邢某，居某县，来即寓于其家。自妇未嫁，已先归，此外固无人也。宰知有因，乃系众于狱，关行邻邑，不旬日而邢与妇皆拘到。出，众识之，有泣者，有怒者，有失色者，遂皆目之为是。宰以严刑鞫邢，尽得其奸状。始知妇自被出，不能静处，遂与邢有私，订为婚嫁。邢故妇弟之叔岳，亲谊相悬，屡言之，妇之父母皆不许。甫得俯从，又责重聘，邢因遄归措办，而厨之说行，竟背前约。

    Explanation

    593

    After living together for several years, he knew every detail, even the subtlest ones. He had a scar on his left breast as large as a palm, a tumor in a private area as large as the top of a finger, which was now absent, and his skin color was too pale, not matching hers. The magistrate then questioned the woman's mother, who confirmed the same details. The magistrate was greatly shocked and temporarily covered the coffin, ordering everyone to return. Upon arriving at the magistrate's office, he first subjected the woman's father to severe punishment, interrogating him about who he had been visiting at home. The father, being a simple-minded person from the countryside, was greatly afraid and revealed the truth. There was a distant relative named Xing, who lived in a certain county and stayed at their house. Since the woman was not married, he had already returned, and there was no one else. Knowing there was a reason, the magistrate imprisoned everyone and sent a message to the neighboring county. Within a few days, Xing and the woman were both brought in. When they came out, the crowd recognized them; some wept, some were angry, some turned pale, and all pointed to them as the culprits. The magistrate interrogated Xing with severe punishment and uncovered all his illicit acts. It was then known that the woman, after being expelled, could not remain calm and had an affair with Xing, agreeing to marry him. Xing was the brother-in-law of the woman's younger brother, but their relationship was distant, and when he repeatedly mentioned it, the woman's parents did not agree. Only after they finally agreed, he demanded a large dowry, so Xing hurried back to prepare it, but the woman's story of the kitchen had already been spread, and she broke her previous promise.

    594

    及邢返，知其已嫁，乃大怒，不至妇家，且思以报复之。邢本无赖，素与穿窬善，因赂之，使为昆仑。窃妇已逃，而终虑其事之泄。邑中某妇，亦与之欢，其家故娼也。是夕，邢宿于某处，偷引妇至，即共醉彼妇，置于囊，荷之去。至妇室中，偷儿又共狎之。昧爽，始引刀断喉，恐其有不似，兼剥厥面，人因寂无知者。邢乘暗携妇远行，更置廿金于案，以赂彼妇之夫。其夫遂疑妇奔邢，未悉姓氏，莫能声言，亦携金他适。向微冤鬼自白，竟无人为之昭雪。不数日，拘偷儿至，与邢皆论抵，妇亦杖遣。厨与某，一以贪赂，一以诬告，皆杖之。通邑哄传为笑。此相国未入阁，以内阁丁艰时事也，洎乎大拜，厨复从相国入京。其足微跛，亦受刑重之故云。

    Explanation

    594

    When Xing returned, he learned that she had already married, and he became greatly angry, refusing to go to his wife's family, and thinking of retaliating against her. Xing was originally a rascal, and he had always been friendly with thieves. He bribed them, making them into "Kunlun," a term that may refer to a type of thief or criminal. The stolen woman had already fled, but he was still worried that the matter would be exposed. A woman in the town also had relations with him, and her family was originally prostitutes. That night, Xing stayed at a certain place, and the thief led the woman there. They immediately got the woman drunk, put her in a bag, and carried her away. When they reached the woman's room, the thief and his companions further molested her. At dawn, they began to cut her throat, afraid that she might not look like the original, and they also skinned her face. People remained silent and unaware. Xing took the woman away under the cover of darkness, leaving twenty gold pieces on the table to bribe the woman's husband. The husband then suspected that his wife had run off with Xing, not knowing her name, and could not speak up. He also took the gold and went elsewhere. If not for the ghost speaking up, there would have been no one to clear the case. Ultimately, no one cleared the case. After a few days, the thief was arrested, and both Xing and the thief were sentenced to death. The woman was also beaten and dismissed. The cook and the other person, one for accepting bribes, one for false accusation, were all beaten. The whole town spread the news and laughed about it. This occurred when the Prime Minister was not yet in office, during a period of mourning. When the Prime Minister was officially appointed, the cook followed him to the capital. His foot was slightly lame, due to the severe punishment.

    595

    外史氏曰：庖人不治，祝史皆起而越俎，固已。若陆厨者，既不治庖，又思代庖，舍其田而欲芸人之田，贪鄙无知，宜乎有奇祸中之也。邢之计，直可瞒天，而究莫逃于鬼，怨抑何补哉？至某无耻丧心，反复变诈，既许共有其宝，旋思独匿其琛，是又小人之尤，而无足论也。

    Explanation

    595

    外史氏曰：庖人不治，祝史皆起而越俎，固已。若陆厨者，既不治庖，又思代庖，舍其田而欲芸人之田，贪鄙无知，宜乎有奇祸中之也。邢之计，直可瞒天，而究莫逃于鬼，怨抑何补哉？至某无耻丧心，反复变诈，既许共有其宝，旋思独匿其琛，是又小人之尤，而无足论也。

    Translation:

    The Historian commented: The cook fails to manage the kitchen, and the historian steps in to overstep their role, which is already problematic. As for the person in charge of the kitchen, they not only neglect their own duties but also seek to take over others' responsibilities, abandoning their own field to till another's. Greedy, petty, and ignorant, it is fitting that they are struck by strange misfortune. The plan of the person named Xing can deceive the heavens, but ultimately cannot escape the judgment of the spirits. What use is resentment? As for the person who is shameless and heartless, constantly changing and deceitful, having agreed to share the treasure, they soon thought to hide it alone. This is yet another example of the worst of the wicked, and there is no need to discuss it further.

    596

    ○　艳梅
    　　滇南于伯玉，以三考起家，历官丞簿，宦于浙中有年矣。晚岁弄瓦，尤钟爱之。及长，貌姝丽且聪敏胜人。因生于红梅开日，遂字之曰艳梅。延师课读，内则既已成诵，将授毛诗。伯玉以女将可笄，不宜与诸男伍，思得女儒如大家，使卒其业，而难乎其人。一日，有秀士款关请见，自言：“有姊知书，兼通诸艺，因贫老，子又不肖，欲售其才为糊口计。窃闻女公子另聘闺师，不揣冒昧，代为蹇修，未识能中所选否？”伯玉视其人，年仅而立，姿容秀逸，谈吐风生，甚相钦重。秀士又出一卷曰：“此即媪之近作也。”伯玉览之，诗文大雅，卓矣名流。益大悦，与之订约而别。越两日，又来为下关书，询之，夫家茅姓，云系巨室中落者。伯玉故未及详核，届期扫除别室，铺设精洁，而杳不见至。心疑其罔，而又无从速之。已而，艳梅凝妆赍书，径赴新塾。

    Explanation

    596

    ○　艳梅

    In the southern region of Yunnan, Yu Boyu rose to prominence after passing the imperial examinations three times. He served as a minor official and had spent many years in Zhejiang. In his later years, he had a daughter, whom he particularly cherished. As she grew older, she was both beautiful and exceptionally intelligent. Because she was born on the day the red plum blossoms bloomed, he named her Yanyan. He hired a tutor to teach her, and she had already memorized the internal rules and was about to be taught the Book of Songs. Yu Boyu thought that as his daughter was approaching the age of coming-of-age, it was not appropriate for her to be educated alongside boys. He sought a female scholar of high caliber to complete her education, but found it difficult to find such a person. One day, a handsome scholar came to his door and requested an audience. He said, "I have an elder sister who is well-versed in literature and proficient in various arts. Due to poverty and old age, my son is not worthy, and I wish to use her talents to make a living. I heard that your daughter is looking for a female teacher. I dare to be bold and act as a matchmaker. Do you think she might be suitable for the role?" Yu Boyu saw the scholar, who was in his thirties, with an elegant and refined appearance, and spoke eloquently. He greatly respected and admired him. The scholar then presented a scroll and said, "This is her recent work." Yu Boyu read it and found the poetry and prose to be of high literary quality, indicating that she was a distinguished and eminent figure in literature. He was even more delighted and agreed to the arrangement, then parted ways with him. Two days later, the scholar returned to deliver a formal agreement. Upon inquiry, it was revealed that his family name was Mao, and he claimed they were a once-wealthy household that had declined. Yu Boyu had not yet had time to verify this, but on the agreed date, he prepared a separate room, meticulously and cleanly arranged. However, the tutor never arrived. He began to doubt the scholar's intentions and found no way to expedite the situation. Soon after, Yanyan, dressed in her best attire and carrying a letter, went directly to the new school.

    597

    怪而问之，对曰：“父为儿别设青毡，已开绛帐，敬往执贽，又何询焉？”伯玉大疑，心知有异，乃尾其后与之偕。甫及室外，异香遄发，馥郁氤氲，绝非人世所者。足未逾槛，即闻老夫人声逆而语曰：“东翁来乎？予恐重烦车从，故潜踪自来，幸勿以枉道见笑。”伯玉大骇，惊顾之，阒其无人，知为鬼狐之类。亟曳艳梅使速返，不听，径入。致礼喁喁然，对面接谈，似曾相识。又闻媪笑曰：“东翁不以予为衰朽，使备师席，似不宜见惑至此。”于是开卷，授关睢三章，音韵清越，训诂详明。艳梅遂受业自读，伯玉亦不得已，入与寒暄而后坐语。询以古今女史，历历如数家珍，不禁心折。虽未亲瞻道貌，而聆厥声音，则五十许人也。媪又谓伯玉曰：“予本涂山氏之裔，寄居贵治百有余年。因与令闺爱有缘，是以猥屈若此，决非为人祸者，幸勿疑讶。”伯玉唯唯，而私心实以为忧，退而命具馔入。媪与艳梅欣然举箸，虽未见其哺啜，而四簋空矣。

    Explanation

    597

    Bo Yu found it strange and asked, "What is this?" She replied, "My father has set up a special green mat for my son, and the red curtain is already open. I respectfully went to present a gift, so why ask further?" Bo Yu was greatly puzzled and knew in his heart that something was unusual. So he followed behind and went with her. As soon as they reached the outside room, a strange fragrance suddenly emitted, sweet and heavy, definitely not something burned in the world. His feet had not yet crossed the threshold when he immediately heard the voice of the old lady speaking from the other side: "Is Master Dong here? I was afraid to trouble you with a carriage, so I came secretly. Please do not laugh at me for taking a roundabout way." Bo Yu was greatly startled, looked around in surprise, and saw that there was no one there. He knew it was a ghost or fox spirit. He hurriedly pulled Yan Mei to return quickly, but she did not listen and went straight in. She bowed respectfully, and they talked face-to-face, as if they had met before. He heard the old lady laugh and say, "Master Dong does not consider me old and decrepit, to serve as a teacher. It seems inappropriate to be so confused." She then opened the book and taught the first three chapters of "The Ospreys," the sound was clear and resonant, and the explanations were detailed and clear. Yan Mei then accepted the lesson and read on her own. Bo Yu also had no choice but to enter, exchange pleasantries, and then sit and talk. He asked about ancient and modern female scholars, and she listed them as if counting family treasures, impressing him. Although he had not seen her appearance, but hearing her voice, she seemed to be around fifty years old. The old lady then said to Bo Yu, "I am a descendant of the Tu Shan clan, have lived in your jurisdiction for more than a hundred years. Because I have a connection with your daughter, that is why I have humbled myself to this extent. I am definitely not here to cause harm, please do not doubt or be surprised." Bo Yu nodded in agreement, but in his heart, he was actually worried. He then ordered the food to be prepared and brought in. The old lady and Yan Mei happily picked up their chopsticks. Although they did not see her eating, the four bowls were empty.

    598

    家人私以询艳梅，诘其见否，亦笑不肯言。伯玉将觅术士驱遣，艳梅闻而止之曰：“父所以特延明师者，俾儿学也。今既幸逢大匠，可以裁成，何更计及其他乎？且始以礼币而终以兵戎，亦大非风雅之道。”伯玉素爱女，遂从其言。艳梅朝而受业，昼而讲贯，夕而习复，不三月而即通一经。伯玉闻之喜，益丰其供帐，有加礼。媪感其意，又受艳梅以命卦诸书，语之曰：“儿命薄，多才反非厚福，习此可以相夫御穷。笔墨究非汝事也。”艳梅受教，精心学之，数旬而尽通其奥。媪忽辞去曰：“汝业有所就，当习女红，以执妇道。予教无所施矣，今将他往，十稔后再晤汝于邯郸道中，慎毋相忆。”艳梅不忍别，牵衣恋恋，粉泪纵横。媪为勉留一夕，诘朝遂失其所在。盖自媪下榻于此，衙眷侍从皆未之觏，独艳梅见之。至是亦茫然无睹，知其已逝，悲不自胜。始为人言其状貌，则少好如处女，唯鬓发颁白而已。

    Explanation

    598

    The family privately asked Yan Mei, inquiring whether she had seen anything, and she laughed and would not speak. Bo Yu planned to seek a sorcerer to drive it away, but Yan Mei heard and stopped him, saying, "Father specially invited a distinguished teacher so that I could learn. Now that I have fortunately encountered a master craftsman, I can be shaped and perfected. Why should we consider anything else? Moreover, starting with courtesy and ending with military force is also far from the refined way." Bo Yu, who always loved his daughter, followed her words. Yan Mei studied in the morning, practiced during the day, and reviewed in the evening. Within three months, she had mastered one classic. Bo Yu heard of this and was delighted, further enriching her provisions and showing even more respect. The old woman, moved by his intention, also taught Yan Mei the books of fate and divination, telling her, "Your destiny is thin, and having many talents is not a great blessing. Learning this can help you manage your husband and endure poverty. Writing is ultimately not your concern." Yan Mei accepted the instruction and studied it carefully, and within a few months, she fully understood its depths. The old woman suddenly bid farewell, saying, "Now that you have achieved something, you should learn needlework to fulfill your duties as a wife. My teaching has no further use for you; now I will go elsewhere. We will meet again in ten years on the road to Handan, so do not miss me." Yan Mei could not bear to part, clinging to her clothes, her tears streaming down her face. The old woman tried to keep her for one more night, but the next morning she was gone. Since the old woman arrived here, no one else in the household has seen her, only Yan Mei. Now, she also saw nothing, realizing that the old woman had passed away, and she could not bear the grief. When she first described her appearance, she said she was still young and beautiful like a maiden, but only her hair was white.

    599

    恒衣褐色衫练裙，寒暑不一更。暇则搦管为诗，若与人倡和者。吟成即有物攫去，其草渺不可得。然味其词旨，或天狐而暂谪人世者。又言自幼至长，梦寐辄见之，故相习熟，无所猜忌，曩日之乐于执经，实由于此。今忽不见，能无凄惨？言之每泣数行下，家人皆劝慰之。艳梅自媪去后，始稍稍衒其术，奇中如神。伯玉欲为择婿，辞曰：“父在此履任数年，政绩卓异，当有升乔之喜。置儿于此异乡，弱质保无有仳离之叹耶？”时伯玉虽耋，犹以邑篆为望，闻女言欣然。询之，则愀然不对。初亦不解其意，未几，伯玉忽遇寒疾，竟以不起，遂卒于官舍，家人始服其先见。殓毕，将扶榇归里，艳梅沮之曰：“父殁后始行厄运，恐有无妄之灾，不可以惊我桑梓。”诸兄皆嗤其诞，竟启行。舟至滇界，已有风信传扬，朝命下，将藉其家。盖当康熙初年，吴藩悖逆，伯玉之仲弟实在幕中，吴败漏网，至是获之。

    Explanation

    599

    She always wore brownish clothes and linen skirts, not changing with the seasons. In her spare time, she would hold the brush to write poetry, as if engaging in mutual poetic exchanges with others. Once she finished composing a poem, it would be snatched away by something, and the draft would be lost. However, savoring the meaning of her words, one might speculate that she was a celestial fox temporarily exiled to the human world. She also said that from childhood to adulthood, she often saw her in dreams and sleep, so they became familiar with each other, without any suspicion, and the joy she found in teaching and studying the classics was indeed due to this. Now that she suddenly disappeared, how could she not feel melancholy? Whenever she spoke of it, tears would stream down in rows, and her family would try to comfort her. After the old woman left, Yanmei began to show off her skills slightly, predicting accurately like a deity. Bo Yu wanted to find a husband for her, but she declined, saying, "My father has served in office for several years, with outstanding achievements in governance, and should have the joy of promotion to a higher position. Placing me in this foreign place, how can a weak body avoid the lament of separation?" At the time, Bo Yu, though old, still had ambitions for the county seal. Upon hearing his daughter's words, he was delighted. When he asked her further, she responded solemnly without speaking. Initially, he did not understand her meaning, but soon after, Bo Yu suddenly contracted a cold illness and ultimately did not recover, dying in the official residence. The family then recognized his foresight. After the funeral preparations were completed, they planned to return home with the coffin. Yanmei discouraged them, saying, "After my father's death, we will begin a period of misfortune, and there may be unjustified calamities. We cannot startle our hometown." Her brothers all laughed at her as superstitious, but they still set out. The boat reached the border of Yunnan, and news had already spread, and the imperial command was issued, intending to seize their family's property. It was during the early years of the Kangxi era, when the rebellion of Wu occurred, and Bo Yu's second brother was indeed in the entourage. Wu's defeat and escape allowed him to evade capture until now, when he was finally caught.

    600

    律以从叛，祸故延及于乃兄，伯玉若在，亦不免于死也。阖家闻此，不胜震惊，益兼程。比及故乡，装犹未卸，而缇骑已来，尽没其家产，禁锢眷属于宅中。州县又虑有挪移，株连根洵，波及戚族。诸兄始悔其遄归之谬。幸中丞某公，稔知伯玉醇谨，特疏代为辨明。嗣圣恩宽大，不尽加诛，仅流其三子于边，以示薄惩。而于氏遂家无立锥矣！艳梅之诸兄，欲奉母同往戍所，艳梅独不欲，曰：“暮年风烛，何堪远涉山川！且生子与女，同一罔极，妹虽闺阁，独不能菽水奉亲乎？”竟不听其行。诸兄亦以母老不任风霜，遂携眷自去。艳梅当藉没时，私匿百金，至是置田供母。县大夫又怜其茕独，少助薪水之需，家以粗给。明年，艳梅已十九，里中闻其贤，咸思聘之。母又继于远族得一子，颇称克家，使承伯玉之嗣，固劝艳梅择所夫。

    Explanation

    600

    Punished for treason, the misfortune extended to his elder brother. If Bo Yu were still alive, he too would not escape death. The whole family heard this and could not bear the shock; they hurried even faster. By the time they reached their hometown, they had not yet unpacked, when the imperial guards had arrived and seized all their property, confining their relatives in the house. The local officials were also worried about further disturbances, and the case spread to involve the entire clan and relatives. The brothers then regretted their hasty return. Fortunately, Governor-General Someone, who knew Bo Yu well for his integrity and caution, submitted a special petition to clarify the matter. Later, the emperor's mercy was lenient, and not all were punished; only three of his sons were exiled to the border, as a light punishment. And the Yu family was left with no place to stand! Yan Mei's brothers wanted to take their mother to the place of exile, but Yan Mei alone did not want to go, saying, "In her old age, how could she endure traveling across mountains and rivers? And raising children, whether sons or daughters, is equally demanding. Although I am a woman, can I not provide for my parents with simple fare?" She finally refused to go. The brothers also, seeing that their mother was too old to endure the cold, took their families and left on their own. During the confiscation, Yan Mei secretly hid a hundred taels of silver, and later she bought land to support her mother. The county magistrate, moved by her loneliness, provided some assistance for daily expenses, allowing her family to barely make ends meet.
