The following year, Yan Mei was nineteen, and the people in the village, hearing of her virtue, all wanted to marry her. Her mother, through a distant relative, obtained a son who was considered capable and was made to carry on Bo Yu's lineage, firmly advising Yan Mei to choose a husband.

    601

    艳梅初犹不愿，欲奉母终此余年，以践昔日之语，及有螟蛉之兄，出入扞格，乃首肯。然以年庚来者，一览辄屏弃之，曰：“此曹非贫即殀者，乌足以当雀屏选哉！”最后得一人，艳梅曰：“可矣！”母兄素信其数，遂不及详，竟字之。其人陆姓，名学洙，父本邑中豪士，已早亡，惟老母在，拥资钜万，富甲一乡，人皆为艳梅欣慰。及嫁，琴瑟甚调，美食锦衣，婢媪环侍，即艳梅亦窃讶其师御穷之言，以为弗验。

    Explanation

    601

    Yan Mei initially did not wish to, desiring to honor her mother and spend the remaining years with her, to fulfill the words spoken in the past. Upon having a foster brother, she encountered difficulties and conflicts, and finally agreed. However, those who came with their birth years, she glanced at them and immediately rejected them, saying, "These people are either poor or short-lived, how could they be worthy of the phoenix screen selection?" Finally, she found one man, and Yan Mei said, "This is acceptable!" Her mother and brother had always trusted the numbers, so they did not delve into details, and she was married to him. The man was surnamed Lu and named Xuezhou. His father was a wealthy and influential man in the county, but had died early, leaving only his old mother. He possessed immense wealth, being the richest in the village, and everyone was relieved for Yan Mei. Upon marriage, their relationship was harmonious, enjoying fine food and luxurious clothing, with maids and servants attending to them. Even Yan Mei secretly wondered about her teacher's prediction of poverty, thinking it was not proven true.

    602

    居无何，其姑诞日，贺者盈闾，戚族之妇莫不来。陆母酬酢已毕，乃命新妇代为娱宾。艳梅称觞至一媪，年颇高大，昏愦龙钟，忽笑而谓之曰：“新娘子亦太匆忙！明日又阿婿生辰，虽无贺客，中馈亦岂能无事？”语未已，座中多怒之以目，乃不复言。艳梅心疑，然亦未敢研诘。及夕入室，坚叩学洙。学洙以佳偶已谐，且因爱不能讳，微露其情。则媒氏以斧柯无成，徒劳跋涉，因窃与陆母计，重赂日者，择古人全福之造，伪为赝鼎以往耳。艳梅大戚，亟索其真者。

    Explanation

    602

    After a short while, it was the birthday of her mother-in-law, and the courtyard was filled with people coming to congratulate. No woman from the in-laws or relatives failed to come. Madam Lu had finished the toasts and responses, and then ordered the new bride to entertain the guests. Yan Mei presented a cup of wine to an old woman, who was quite advanced in age, confused and feeble. The old woman suddenly smiled and said to her, "Newlywed, you are also too hasty! Tomorrow is also the birthday of my son-in-law; even without guests, the kitchen still needs to be attended to." Before she finished speaking, many in the audience glared at her, and she did not speak again. Yan Mei was suspicious, but dared not inquire further. At dusk, she entered the room and firmly questioned Xuezhu. Xuezhu explained that the good match had been made, and because of his love, he could not hide it, so he revealed the situation slightly. The matchmaker, seeing that the match had not been successful, wasted his efforts, and secretly plotted with Madam Lu, offering a large bribe to a fortune-teller, choosing an ancient person with complete good fortune, and pretending to be a fake treasure to deceive them. Yan Mei became very sad and urgently demanded the truth.

    603

    学洙出之椟中，其实少艳梅三岁，而悬弧之斯，果在来朝，乃知媪言非谬。初犹冀其稍可，及就烛下推之，则视向之贫殀者，殆有甚焉。艳梅益泫然出涕，究亦无可如何。甫周岁，而学沫病矣。盖学洙体貌虽伟，实系童年，燕尔既已，孔嘉新婚，又值艳梅花月之态，尽足销魂，斧斤之伐，能无蚀骨？加以天资薄弱，血气未坚，遂患瘵疾，卧床不起。初艳梅既知夫命，首虑其殀，诸事撙节，兼劝姑使之出外就傅。虽不见听，家人皆称道其贤。至是危笃，乃窃自恫曰：“非我死夫子，夫子实由我而疾，我则何心而忍于坐视？”因自制疏文，乘夜密祷于上帝。其略曰：“宁为饿殍，同填沟壑于他年，不愿孀居，独守闺房于此日。”情词悱恻，约数十言。祝讫，遽引刃自刲其臂，罗袂尽殷，香饥零落，痛极，遂仆于地。迷惘中闻有人大呼曰：“上帝有命，已许汝易夭而贫矣！”声巨若雷，惊悸而苏，人尚寂无知者。

    Explanation

    603

    Xue Zhu emerged from the box, and in reality, he was three years younger than Yan Mei. The custom of hanging the bow for his birth was indeed to take place the next day, and thus it was known that the old woman's words were not false. Initially, he still hoped it would be somewhat acceptable, but when he examined her under the candlelight, he saw that the previously poor and frail one was even worse. Yan Mei wept even more tearfully, and in the end, there was nothing to be done. Just when he turned one year old, Xue Mo fell ill. Because Xue Zhu was physically strong, but he was still a child, the joy of marriage had already passed, and the marriage was very good. Also coinciding with Yan Mei's blooming and moonlit beauty, it was enough to make one's soul melt. The cutting of axes, how could it not eat into the bones? Furthermore, with weak natural talent and unfirm blood and qi, he then fell ill with consumption and could not get up from bed. Initially, Yan Mei, knowing her husband's fate, first worried about his death. She practiced frugality and also advised her mother-in-law to send him out to find a tutor, although not listened to. Family members all praised her virtue. At this point, he was seriously ill, and then secretly lamented, "Not because I died, but because of me, my husband fell ill. What heart do I have to sit by and watch?" Because of this, she wrote a petition and at night, secretly prayed to God. Its gist said, "Rather than be a starving corpse, to together fill the ditches in the future, do not want to be a widow, alone guard the inner room today." The words were deeply moving, about several dozen words. After finishing the prayer, immediately drew the blade, all the sleeves were dyed, fragrant blood scattered, extremely painful, then fell to the ground. In a daze, heard someone. "God has a command, has already allowed you to exchange early death for poverty!" The sound was as loud as thunder, startled and woke up, people were still silent, no one knew.

    604

    扶创起立，入室视夫，病势似觉少减。乃以肉和药，煎而进之，翌日竟小愈。浃旬大安，弥月遂扶杖而起。艳梅既不自言，夫婿姑嫜亦罔知出于新妇之力也。学沫既痊，益更束书不读，时或出外游荡，小试饮博之端。然赖其母约束綦严，尚不敢过于驰骋。又越二年，母已病卒，学沫乃肆无忌惮，日出与无赖者游，举手以千金，缠头有百贯。凡祖父之经营，萱亲之积蓄，干仆之生殖，藏镪近千，朽索论万，尽输外府，而家室为之一空。浸及田宅，人尽寒心，独闺中者漠不为意。所亲咸咎艳梅，以为同床秦越，痛痒弗关，岂异日竟能独富哉？艳梅闻而太息曰：“虽非世上金夫，幸免冢中枯骨，何劝为？”益听其浪游，且出奁具以供其挥霍。学洙以为贤，每向人津津乐道之，人以是亦薄艳梅。未及二稔，家无斗筲，夫妇遂徙居陋巷。桑户棬枢，竹屋低矮，学洙始杜门不出。乃自落魄以来，一衣一食学洙皆仰给于妇。

    Explanation

    604

    Xue Mo was supported and stood up, entering the room to see his husband, whose condition seemed to have slightly improved. He then mixed meat with medicine, boiled it, and gave it to him. The next day, his husband was slightly better. After ten days, he was greatly relieved, and by the end of the month, he could walk with a cane. Yan Mei did not speak about it herself, and her husband and in-laws did not know it was due to the efforts of the new wife. Xue Mo was fully recovered and increasingly stopped reading books, sometimes going out to wander and trying his hand at gambling and drinking. Relying on his mother's strict discipline, he still dared not go too far. After another two years, his mother had already fallen ill and died. Xue Mo then acted without restraint, going out daily to hang out with ruffians, spending a thousand gold coins at a stroke and giving a hundred strings of coins as a reward. All the efforts of his grandfather, the savings of his mother, and the earnings of his servants—nearly a thousand gold coins and tens of thousands of strings of coins—were all spent outside the home, leaving the household empty. Gradually affecting the land and houses, people were all disheartened, but only the woman in the inner room remained indifferent. Those close to him all blamed Yan Mei, thinking that they were like Qin and Yue, sharing neither pain nor pleasure, and how could she possibly become rich alone in the future? Yan Mei heard and sighed, saying, "Although he is not a gold husband in the world, I am lucky to avoid being a skeleton in the tomb, what is there to advise?" She allowed him to wander more and also provided her dowry items to support his extravagance. Xue Zhou thought she was virtuous and often spoke about it with great enthusiasm to others, but people therefore also looked down on Yan Mei. Before two years, the household had no more than a few measures of grain, and the couple then moved to a dilapidated alley. Living in a humble dwelling with a mulberry door and bent door frame, and a bamboo house that was low and small, Xue Zhou then closed the door and did not go out. Since his fall from grace, every piece of clothing and meal for Xue Zhou depended on his wife.

    605

    艳梅昼则补缀，夜则纺绩，日夕为常，毫无愠色。学洙生于富厚，身无寸长，惟供炊火而已。默然内愧，仰屋咨嗟，艳梅反温言慰藉之。闻者多美其安贫，而不意其知命也。如是三岁，滇忽大饥。当米珠薪桂之年，艳梅十指不足以赡二人，并日而营，浸以饥冻。适学洙之舅，以京秩出宰中州，闻甥家剥落，无以资生，欲助之。深虞其浪费，乃封舟车之资，驰书召之。学洙欣然欲往，谋与妇俱。艳梅始谏曰：“家散万金，依人四海，妾固未见其可也。况官署如邮亭，舅既过客，我辈复往投之，倘一朝相左，当更有旅人之忧矣。”学洙久困思苏，遂不纳妻言，觅舟治任，强以偕往。艳梅亦顿忆茅媪之约，或能邂逅他乡，随亦从之。而长途近万，跋涉维艰，比及汝蔡之间，月已三易。未入县境，舅处已有伻来，则又升任赵州牧矣。转折而西，又将千里。

    Explanation

    605

    Yan Mei would sew during the day and spin thread at night, making this her daily routine without any sign of resentment. Xuezhu was born into wealth but had no skills, only able to tend to the hearth. He felt silent inner shame and sighed at the ceiling, but Yan Mei comforted him with gentle words. Those who heard of their situation praised their contentment with poverty, but did not realize their acceptance of fate. For three years, they lived this way until a great famine struck Yunnan. In those days when rice was as precious as pearls and firewood as costly as cinnamon, Yan Mei's ten fingers were insufficient to feed the two of them, and they struggled to survive, gradually succumbing to hunger and cold. Just then, Xuezhu's uncle, a high-ranking official in the capital, was appointed to govern the central region. Hearing that his nephew's family had fallen into ruin and could not sustain themselves, he wished to help them. Worried that they might waste the money, he sealed the funds for travel expenses and sent a letter to summon them. Xuezhu was delighted to go and planned to take his wife with him. Yan Mei began to advise him, saying, "Our family has fallen into ruin, and we must rely on others across the world. I do not see this as a good idea. Moreover, an official's residence is like a post station, and since your uncle is a guest, and we are also going to visit him, if we happen to be at odds, we will have even more worries as travelers." Xuezhu, long confined and eager for change, ignored his wife's words, sought a boat and prepared their belongings, and insisted on taking her along. Yan Mei suddenly remembered the agreement with the old woman and hoped they might meet by chance in a foreign place, so she followed him. However, the journey was long and arduous, covering nearly ten thousand miles, and by the time they reached Ruzhou and Cai, three months had passed. Before they even entered the county, a messenger from Xuezhu's uncle had already arrived, informing them that he had been promoted to the position of magistrate in Zhaozhou. They turned westward, and there were still thousands of miles ahead.

    606

    艳梅素质荏弱，不耐驰驱，遂病于旅次，急未能前，舅之使者乃先返。夫妇耽延匝月，方始启行，抵州治，衙署一新，吏役将出迎刺史。惊询之，前任果其舅氏，下车未久，即遭揭参，以莅县多受暮金故也。舅解赴省，衙眷知有后灾，避向都门旧居。盖召甥之价未回，去赵之旌已远而莫可追矣。学洙至此，气绝心灰，始悔妇言之不用。既无所之，又不能返，资斧将尽，告助无门，不得已而谋诸妇曰：“悔不纳子言，今已至此，进退维谷，去死无几。然予亡子亦有不利也，盍筹其所以处之。”答曰：“君将奈之何？此地非如故乡，可以手爪养此口腹者。其他，则妾何能为？”学洙曰：“不然。闻子有妙术，昔在家园，良难轻售。今日失路于兹，胡不为予一试？不愈于束手坐困乎？”艳梅颇有难色，学洙再四强之，乃许诺，亦缘其师有相夫之谶也。择日设肆，出余金赁屋治具，且悬一幔于门，以蔽春色。艳梅默坐其中，学洙往来传送。

    Explanation

    606

    Yan Mei, with her delicate and weak constitution, could not withstand the rigors of travel and fell ill during their journey. Unable to proceed further, her uncle's messenger returned ahead of them. The couple was delayed for a full month before they finally set out, arriving at the state capital. The governor's residence was newly renovated, and the officials were about to welcome the new governor. In surprise, they inquired and learned that the former governor was indeed Yan Mei's maternal uncle. He had not been in office long before he was exposed for accepting bribes during his tenure as county magistrate. Her uncle was arrested and taken to the provincial capital, and the family, fearing further calamity, retreated to their old residence in the capital. The promised dowry for Yan Mei's son-in-law had not returned, and the honor of being a son-in-law to Zhao had been lost and could not be recovered. Xuezhou, at this point, felt utterly hopeless and regretted not heeding his wife's advice. With nowhere to go and unable to return, his funds were nearly exhausted, and he had no one to turn to for help. Desperate, he turned to his wife and said, "I regret not listening to your advice. Now we are in this predicament, caught between a rock and a hard place, with death not far away. Yet, my son's situation is also dire. What should we do?" She replied, "What can you do? This place is not like our hometown, where we could earn a living with our own hands. What else can I do?" Xuezhou said, "That is not true. I have heard that you possess a special skill. In our hometown, it was difficult to sell. Now that we are in this situation, why not try it once? Is it better to be helpless and trapped?" Yan Mei hesitated, but Xuezhou urged her repeatedly, and she finally agreed, partly because her master had predicted that she would help her husband. They chose a day to set up a stall, spent their remaining funds to rent a house and prepare the necessary tools, and hung a curtain at the door to conceal the view. Yan Mei sat silently inside, while Xuezhou came and went, delivering items.

    607

    就问者辄书片纸，批而答之，即欲闻柳外之莺而不可得，矧其他哉！赵人初犹观望，渐且云集，旬日而户外之屦满矣。艳梅一一批断，尽决所疑而去。由是喧传，名噪远近，幔中仙之誉倾动簪绅，盖不止士庶已也。乃艳梅安于天命，晨起坐幔中，以十人为率，余皆辞以翌日。又不多索命金，日惟青蚨百数，仪足饔飧，此外别无赢余。学洙疑之曰：“以子之能，富可立致。非予敢存奢望，何不多取锱铢，早还乡里，而乃自为限量如此？”艳梅不欲言其意，因权词以对曰：“命之理微，圣人且罕言之。以妾之神明，只足应此，多则不验，不亦虚人来意耶？”学洙实贪于得，乃笑曰：“此子之饰说耳！术苟甚神，安在不多多益善哉？”益固强之。艳梅亦思自验其命，乃增其数，且倍其值。次日占者塞门，悉为判决。自朝至于日昃，得钱近千，学洙乃大悦。诘旦，夫妇犹未起，暑雨滂沱。旁午，天始稍霁，来者甚稀。

    Explanation

    607

    Whenever someone sought advice, Yan Mei would write on a small piece of paper and answer with a brief note. They wished to hear the song of the oriole outside the willow but could not even obtain that, let alone other desires! Initially, the people of Zhao hesitated, but gradually they gathered like a crowd, and within ten days, the floor outside was full of shoes. Yan Mei would resolve doubts one by one, and everyone left with their questions answered. The news spread widely, and her fame grew both near and far. The reputation of the "woman in the curtain" caused a stir among the gentry, not just the common people. Yan Mei accepted her fate and would rise early to sit in the curtain, with ten people as a standard. The rest were told to come the next day. She did not charge a high fee, earning only a few hundred coins a day, enough to cover meals, with no extra profit. Xue Zhu questioned her, saying, "With your ability, wealth could be easily acquired. I do not dare to have excessive hopes, but why not take more, to return home sooner, and yet you limit yourself in this way?" Yan Mei did not wish to reveal her true intent and responded cautiously, saying, "The principle of fate is subtle, even sages rarely speak of it. With my divine insight, I can only handle this much; if there are too many, it will not be effective, would it be in vain for people to come?" Xue Zhu was truly greedy and laughed, saying, "This is just his embellished talk! If the art is truly divine, why not take more and more?" He insisted even more strongly. Yan Mei also thought to test her own fate, so she increased the number and doubled the fee. The next day, the door was blocked by those seeking advice, and she resolved all their queries. From morning until noon, she earned nearly a thousand coins, and Xue Zhu was greatly pleased. The next day, the couple had not yet risen, and it was pouring with summer rain. At midday, the sky began to clear slightly, and the number of visitors was very few.

    608

    至暮，重阴凝闭，遂成淫霖，十余日无少止息。溪流涨溢，市中水深数尺，人绝无乘槎问卜者。夫妇坐食其资，所得尽耗，又枵腹一朝夕，方始晴明。艳梅益信有定数，亟欲复其故辙。学洙不甘，仍强之，三日又获资数缗，夫妇欣幸。至夜高枕而眠，有偷儿潜入室中，席卷所有，且并其衣物，尽携而去。盖缘阴雨，坏垣未及修整，故宵人得以肆其志，究亦造化使然也。学洙大恸，愤不欲生。艳梅言笑自若，徐曰：“君不知命，宜其不免于贫。妾遭媒妁之误，至今犹深恫于心。诚以君之禀赋，非贫当殀，微妾祷于天帝，予今已少寡久矣！安得一寒彻骨，犹然倡随至是乎？君无怨焉可耳！”因述其梦中天语，学洙益恸，以为所造如斯，愈无首止之望。艳梅又解之曰：“君知善可回天乎？彼苍固任人转圜者也。诚能力行阴骘，天将别注禄籍。以妾之能，固可相君旋里，且还尔素封矣。”语本宽其忧，乃学洙即耸然请教。

    Explanation

    608

    Until dusk, thick clouds gathered and closed in, leading to continuous heavy rain that lasted for more than ten days without any cessation. The stream overflowed, and the water in the market was several feet deep, with no one venturing out to consult the stars or seek divination. The couple sat idle, consuming their resources, and all they had gained was exhausted. They went hungry for a day and night, and it finally cleared up. Yan Mei increasingly believed in fate and urgently wanted to return to her old ways. Xue Zhu was unwilling, but he still forced her. In three days, they gained several strings of cash, and the couple was happy. At night, they slept soundly, but a thief sneaked into the room, took everything, including their clothes, and carried it all away. Due to the rain, the broken walls had not been repaired, thus the thief could act on his intentions, ultimately, it was the will of fate. Xue Zhu was greatly distressed and felt like giving up on life. Yan Mei spoke and laughed as usual, calmly saying, "You don't understand fate, so you can't avoid poverty. I was misled by matchmakers, and I still deeply regret it. If your nature is not destined for poverty, you would not die young. If I hadn't prayed to the Heavenly Emperor, I would have been a widow for a long time! How could I have endured such coldness and still be with you to this day? You have no reason to complain!" She then recounted the words she heard in her dream, and Xue Zhu was even more distressed, believing that their fate was so bad, there was no hope left. Yan Mei then explained, "Do you know that good deeds can change fate? The heavens indeed allow people to change their fate. If you truly practice good deeds, heaven will grant you a new record of fortune. With my abilities, I can certainly help you return to your hometown and restore your former wealth." Her words were meant to ease his worries, and Xue Zhu was immediately attentive and sought her advice.

    609

    艳梅沉思良久，忽忆一事，言曰：“妾从君来时，渡一溪，猝不能过，水深湍急也。其地距此里许，别无桥梁。君倘立志，即于朝夕之暇，负土担石往营之。妾再以所得者助君，鸠工架木，终岁之间，人即永免衣襦之患。利济诚宏，善不在此乎？”学洙即慨然曰：“诺！”遂与艳梅谋，每日食余启肆，日晡便休，暇则前往营建。水浸胫骨，劳不知疲，严寒之龟手皴足，盛暑之雨笠烟蓑，举所不恤。人有乐助者，反揖而去之。夫妇又自兹茹素，口食而外，悉以庀材。物集而后召匠，攻木攻石，斧凿之声盈于路。通计经始至此，凡阅十月而桥成。初是溪虽深，有时亦仅没膝，而人恒不能涉，原其水性峻急，即盎盂之石，皆可激之使行。膑脚刖足，立尚颠仆，遂无有创此议者。自学洙之尽日立于水次，狂澜汹涌，究无损伤，人因以为神。

    Explanation

    609

    Yan Mei pondered for a long time, then suddenly recalled an incident and said, "When I came with you, we crossed a stream that I could not suddenly cross because the water was deep and swift. The place is about a mile from here, and there is no other bridge. If you set your mind to it, you can use your spare time in the morning and evening to carry earth and stones to build it. I will contribute what I have earned to help you, and we can assemble workers and build wooden structures. Within a year, people will forever be free from the suffering of cold. The benefit is truly great, isn't this good?" Xuezhu immediately said with a sigh, "Yes!" He then planned with Yan Mei, opening the shop every day after meals and resting at dusk. When free, they went to construct the bridge. The water soaked their shins, but they worked tirelessly, enduring cold hands and cracked feet in the severe cold, and rain caps and smoke cloaks in the hot summer. They endured all these hardships without concern. Those who were willing to help, they politely declined. The couple then began to eat vegetarian food, contributing all their resources except for what they ate. They gathered materials and then called in craftsmen, cutting wood and stone, with the sound of axes and chisels filling the road. Calculated from the start to now, the bridge was completed in ten months. At first, the stream was deep, sometimes only reaching the knees, but people always could not cross it because the water was steep and swift. Even small stones in a bowl could be stirred to move. Crippled feet and legs, standing still would still result in falling, so no one dared to propose this idea. Since Xuezhu stood by the water all day, wild waves surging, ultimately, no harm came to him, and people therefore considered him divine.

    610

    徒杠已就，行旅欢然，因号之以幔仙桥，纪其灵也。学洙亦由是小康，遂有薄积。又越两岁，致钱百缗，乃作旋归之计。市二蹇卫，载妻以行。艳梅自至赵地，深自韬晦，从未以半面示人。至是返辔，人甫识荆，虽年届三旬，而花貌俨然，举惊为神仙，不敢仰视。夫妇遂就道，不再徘徊。乃行未三日程，学洙忽得暴疾，昏睡无知，抚之若冰，竟卒于旅舍。艳梅恸甚，自恨其生之不辰，百计维持，究无望于偕老。且居异乡，只身谁倚？遂哭之呕血，绝而复生。旅邸主人又以其死之甚遽，不免干连，将首之于官而脱其累。正纷然聚讼，观者如堵，忽一人排众直入，以寸缄予艳梅曰：“前村一媪，闻娘子命理綦精，敬以此相浼。”众闻而大恚，以为当此抢攘，何来是不急之务，将拳石交下，以泄其不平。艳梅挥之曰：“是必有异。”展视焉则其师茅媪之造也。

    Explanation

    610

    The simple bridge was completed, and the travelers were joyful. They named it the Mantian Bridge to record its miraculous nature. Xuezhu also became relatively wealthy as a result and thus accumulated a small fortune. After another two years, he managed to save a hundred strings of cash and then planned to return home. He bought two old horses and carried his wife with him. Yan Mei had been in Zhao territory, deeply concealing herself, and had never shown her face to anyone. Upon returning, she reined in her horse, and people just began to recognize her. Though she was in her thirties, her appearance was still as beautiful as a flower, and everyone was surprised by her beauty, thinking she was a fairy, and dared not look up at her. The couple then set out on the road without hesitation. But they had only traveled for three days when Xuezhu suddenly fell ill with a severe illness, falling into a deep sleep, unconscious, and touching him felt like touching ice. He eventually died in the inn. Yan Mei was greatly grieved, regretting her unfortunate fate, and tried every possible way to sustain him, but there was no hope for them to grow old together. And being in a foreign land, alone, who could she rely on? She cried so much that she vomited blood, died but then revived. The inn owner was also alarmed by his sudden death and could not avoid getting involved, intending to report it to the authorities to clear himself of any responsibility. The situation was chaotic with many people arguing, and the spectators were as dense as a wall. Suddenly, a person pushed through the crowd and entered, gave her a short note and said, "An old woman from the village heard that you have a very precise understanding of fate, respectfully entrusting this to you." The crowd heard and became very angry, thinking that at such a chaotic time, why bring up such an urgent matter, and threw punches and stones to vent their anger. Yan Mei waved them off and said, "There must be something unusual about this." When she opened it, it was from her teacher, Mother Mao.

    611

    因雪涕急询曰：“媪安在？”答曰：“已与某偕来，渠行甚缓，是以在后耳！”艳梅乃趋出候之，曼立远视，见一人从容步履，仪状如昔媪，犹未至。艳梅遽伏地娇啼，宛如失乳之婴忽睹其母，呜咽不可名状。媪近前以手曳之曰：“儿勿悲。美玉泥沙亦其命也，宜先捄吾婿。”乃扶艳梅起，与之偕入视学洙。艳梅涕泗交颐，谓其已死。媪笑曰：“此特好姬缘，岂真长逝耶？”即索片纸，书数字焚之，学洙突然欠伸。有顷，蹶然起坐，若梦魇乍寤者。艳梅喜叩其由，答曰：“昨夕与子同梦，忽觉身体飘然独行，旋至一洞天，朱户丹楹，居甚赫奕。有主者年仅三十许，衣冠尊贵，仪从甚都。见予，降阶迎迓。又闻堂上有呼卿之小字者，其音甚细，曰：‘艳梅之婿至矣！’主者揖予坐，且谓曰：‘君以一桥免千万人之厉揭，厥功伟矣！适奉帝诏，以至宝赐君，君其拜受。’言讫缚一物至，周身苍毫，状若狸。口吐一珠，赤光四映，主者令予吞之，三咽而后下。

    Explanation

    611

    Yan Mei, weeping in the snow, urgently asked, "Where is the old woman?" The answer came, "She has already come with someone, but she walks very slowly, which is why she is behind us!" Yan Mei then hurriedly went out to wait for her, standing gracefully and looking into the distance. She saw a person walking calmly, whose appearance was like that of the old woman she remembered, but he had not yet arrived. Yan Mei suddenly fell to the ground and cried tenderly, as if an infant who had lost its mother suddenly saw her, wailing in a way that could not be described. The old woman approached and pulled her up by the hand, saying, "Do not grieve, child. Like a jade lost in mud and sand, it is also part of its fate. We should first rescue your husband." She then helped Yan Mei up and entered with her to see Xuezhu. Yan Mei wept tears that mingled on her cheeks, thinking he was dead. The old woman smiled and said, "This is just a good fate for a concubine, is it really gone forever?" She then fetched a piece of paper, wrote a few words on it, and burned it. Xuezhu suddenly yawned and stretched. After a while, he abruptly sat up, as if he had just awakened from a nightmare. Yan Mei was delighted and asked him about the reason. He replied, "Last night I had the same dream with you. Suddenly, I felt my body light and airy, walking alone, and soon arrived at a heavenly cave. There was a red door and vermilion pillars, and it was a magnificent place. There was a host who was only around thirty years old, dressed in dignified attire, with very splendid attendants. Seeing me, he descended the steps to welcome me. I also heard someone in the hall calling my pet name, the voice was very soft, saying, 'Yan Mei's husband has arrived!' The host bowed and gestured for me to sit, and said, 'You prevented thousands of people from facing dangers with a bridge. Your merit is great indeed! Just received an imperial decree, granting you the most precious treasure, bow and receive it.' After speaking, he bound an object and brought it. It was covered in grayish fur, appearing like a cat. It spat out a pearl, shining red light in all directions. The host ordered me to swallow it, and after swallowing three times, it went down."

    612

    脏腑如经炮烙，寸心火炽，刻不能安。历许时而后稍可，则神明一时洞达已。主者命置酒为予贺，又出二女郎为予捧觞，云是卿之姊妹行。曰：‘见越水茅媪，当自知之。’盘桓竟日，忽有一人持札至。主者阅之，笑曰：‘姊亦多虑乃尔！’因遣予归。出门顿觉，孰知死生俄顷哉？”艳梅乃引之拜媪，媪谓学洙曰：“予即茅姓是也。向曾荐予于乃岳，今又十龄矣。”学洙顿悟。媪又曰：“渠亦太惫懒。

    Explanation

    612

    The internal organs were as if being roasted, the heart burning like fire, unable to find peace even for a moment. After a while, it became somewhat bearable, and then the spirit suddenly became clear and enlightened. The host ordered wine to celebrate for me and brought out two young ladies to serve me with cups, saying they were my sisters. They said, "When you meet the old woman from the waters of Yue, you will know her." We spent the entire day together, and suddenly a person arrived with a letter. The host read it and laughed, saying, "My sister is overly concerned!" He then sent me back. As soon as I stepped out, I realized, who would have thought that life and death could be so fleeting?" Yan Mei then led me to bow to the old woman. The old woman told Xue Zhu, "I am indeed the surname Mao. I once recommended you to your father-in-law, and now it has been ten years." Xue Zhu suddenly understood. The old woman also said, "He is also too lazy."

    613

    使非予草数行，渠或夺艳梅之婚，妻汝以二女未可知也。”言次，旅邸主人与众皆心安，始各散去。学洙设具款媪，聚饮甚欢。媪忽诘陆夫妇曰：“仙乡乐乎？故乡乐乎？”二人同声俱以仙乡对。媪乃探囊中，取出刀圭之药，令艳梅服之，而少与学洙曰：“渠身故自有宝也。”更以余药饲二蹇，遂亦不烦刍秣。凌展同出逆旅，媪与艳梅叠跨其一，惟学洙另乘。媪叱之，冉冉上升，瞬息入云中，形若纸鸢，倏不见。男妇聚观何啻千百，行者居者咸下拜。因更里名为“三仙”，与黄梁之镇不朽矣！

    　　外史氏曰：信哉，人之可以造命也。艳梅以一念欲殉其夫，而殇子转为老彭矣！学洙以一事克从其妇，而饿骨不难羽化矣！二蹇并举，何异鸡犬同升？茅媪之事虽不经，然玉成终始，殊不愧为人师。独荐西席者，几夺东床，不免令人不满耳！

    Explanation

    613

    If it were not for me writing a few lines, he might have taken away Yan Mei's marriage, and it is not impossible that he could have married you with two daughters. During the conversation, the innkeeper and everyone else felt at ease, and they then dispersed. Xuezhou prepared food to entertain the old woman, and they gathered to drink and were very happy. The old woman suddenly questioned the couple Lu, saying, "Is the fairyland more enjoyable than the homeland?" Both answered in unison that the fairyland was more enjoyable. The old woman then reached into her bag and took out a small amount of medicine, ordered Yan Mei to take it, and spoke privately to Xuezhou, saying, "He indeed has a treasure within himself." She gave the remaining medicine to the two donkeys, and they no longer needed fodder. They all left the inn together, with the old woman and Yan Mei riding together on one, and only Xuezhou riding separately. The old woman scolded them, and they slowly rose, and in an instant entered the clouds, their form like a kite, and suddenly they were no longer visible. Hundreds or even thousands of men and women gathered to watch, and those who were walking and those who were staying all knelt down. Therefore, the village was renamed "Three Immortals," and it would be immortalized with the town of Huangliang.

    Indeed, people can shape their destiny. Yan Mei, with the intention to sacrifice herself for her husband, saw her deceased child transformed into the ancient sage Lao Peng! Xuezhou, by doing one thing to follow his wife, saw his starving bones easily transform into a feathered being! The two donkeys rising together, how different is it from chickens and dogs ascending together? The story of Old Woman Mao is somewhat unbelievable, yet in completing the task from start to finish, she was not ashamed to be a teacher. Only the one who recommended the teacher nearly took the bride, which cannot help but be unsatisfactory.

    614

    随园老人曰：此当与聊斋细柳传参看，彼成厥子，此相乃夫，皆蒙恶声而不辞，天壤间惟此二妇人耳！彼三家村蠢老媪，少有不白，便将指日矢天，涕泣分诉，是真所谓婆子气。

    ○　卷四
    　　袅烟
    　　高邮长生邓兆罴，侍卫公兆熊弟也。筑一精室，四壁图书，晨夕坐卧其中，非韵友罕得至。一日深秋，天空云敛，生诵读少暇，命小奚横长笛，自倚歌以和之。于是饮酒乐甚，不觉沉醺，乘醉命骑将出游，时盖求凤未遂也。恍惚出门，驰骋街市。过曲巷，似有人家，其外户饰以丹漆，不甚轩敞，大书一联曰：“舞罢云停岫，歌成柳啭莺。”字迹柔媚，语气酷似青楼。生遂驻马不行，忽一蓬头婢轧然启关而出，且自语曰：“予袅烟岂屑为此者？任汝百磨，傲骨终不可折。”生异之，睨其丰姿，俨然绝代，唯眉目问少存积垢。见其投东，将往从之，所乘忽中蹶。

    Explanation

    614

    The Old Man of Sui Garden said: This text should be read alongside the "Xiliu Zhuan" from the "Liaozhai Zhiyi." In that story, a woman arranges her son's marriage, while here, a woman arranges her husband's marriage. Both endure slander without complaint, and in the world, only these two women possess such virtue. In contrast, village women from three villages, who are stupid and ignorant, are quick to make dramatic accusations or defenses, weeping and dividing the account, which is truly the behavior of old women.

    Chapter 4

    Niao Yan

    Deng Zhaopi of Changsheng, Gaoyou, was the younger brother of the Imperial Guard official Deng Zhaoxiong. He built a fine study, with books and scrolls on all four walls, where he spent his days and nights reading and resting. Few could visit him unless they were refined companions. One day in deep autumn, the sky was clear and the clouds had gathered, and he had a little spare time from his reading. He ordered a young servant to play a long flute, and he sang along with it. Thus, he drank and was greatly delighted, unaware of his intoxication. Drunk, he commanded his horse to go out for a ride, as he had not yet found the woman he sought. He left in a daze, galloping through the streets. Passing a winding alley, he saw what seemed to be a house. Its outer door was decorated with red lacquer, not very grand, and a large couplet was written on it: "Dance ends and clouds pause on the peak; Song finishes and willows sing like birds." The handwriting was soft and charming, and the tone was very similar to that of a brothel. He stopped his horse and did not move. Suddenly, a ragged-headed maid opened the door and said to herself, "I, Niao Yan, would never stoop to such things! No matter how much you grind me, my proud spirit will never be broken." He was surprised by this, and looked at her graceful appearance, which seemed to be of an extraordinary beauty, though her eyes and eyebrows were slightly stained with grime. Seeing her head east, he intended to follow her, but his horse suddenly stumbled.

    615

    撒然惊觉，则在萧斋榻上，实一梦也，其心竟历历弗置。明年，太夫人为娶于巨室，贤淑而美，然时以袅烟为念。至秋，赴京省其兄，所寓在正阳门外。间行偶经一巷，仿佛梦中旧游。及过一门，又酷似，且双扉紧闭，依然赤幅乌阑，而十字联珠缀于其上，生因心讶。及询之人，则故名妓玉兰之家也。少时颇有盛名，今老矣，车马稀疏，畜一假女名袅烟，又为恶少诱与偕逋，迄今踪迹未获，以故深锁院门，不复见客。生既得其详，疑袅烟必以不屈而死，此皆鸨妓之饰说，人未及觉察耳。归见其兄，请语南城巡视者。公以事涉影响，不之听。生究不平，乃与其仆私计之，使仆冒为袅烟之兄，先往兰家索人，不与，遂讼之。两造具备，生乃自具公服，补牍申诉。

    Explanation

    615

    Suddenly awakened with a start, he found himself on the couch in his desolate study, realizing it was indeed a dream, yet his heart remained vividly clear. The following year, his mother arranged for him to marry into a wealthy family, a virtuous and beautiful woman, but he often thought of Niaoyan. By autumn, he traveled to the capital to visit his brother, staying outside the Zhengyang Gate. While wandering, he accidentally passed through a lane that seemed familiar, as if from a dream. Passing by a gate, it was even more similar, with two tightly closed doors, still adorned with red borders and black railings, and cross-shaped beads strung on top. He felt a surge of surprise in his heart. Upon asking someone, he learned that it was the former home of the famous courtesan Yu Lan. In her youth, she was quite renowned, but now she was old, with few visitors, and had taken in a girl named Niaoyan as a foster daughter. This girl had been lured away by a young rascal, and her whereabouts remained unknown. As a result, the gate to the courtyard was tightly locked, and they no longer received visitors. Having learned the details, he suspected that Niaoyan must have died of defiance, a belief he thought was merely the embellished stories of courtesans, and that people had not yet realized this. Returning home, he spoke to his brother, asking him to inform the official in charge of the southern city. The official, however, dismissed the matter as speculative. Still feeling unjust, he privately discussed the matter with his servant, instructing him to pretend to be Niaoyan's brother and go to Yu Lan's home to demand the return of Niaoyan. When they refused, he took legal action. With both parties present, he donned his official robes and submitted a supplementary petition for appeal.

    616

    内言：“有婢袅烟，服役数载，即是仆之女弟，为奸人拐掠，不知所往。仆至京师，以事过妓馆之门，见婢立户侧，瞥见乃兄即避入。其形容服饰，举凿凿可据。恳祈宪差搜捕”云云。时司南城侍卫为某公，捕奸发伏，甚著直名。又悉为寅执之弟，华胄英年，必无诞妄，因以严刑拟妓。兰惧，始吐真实。果缘袅烟不从，鞭挞至屡，一夕忽自缢而死。人命非轻，威逼有据，遂不敢声言，私瘗于客座院中。又惧事泄，托言逋逃，而不知犹有其兄也。今对乌台，情甘服罪。公命役往出女尸，则面色如生，犹未朽。一时观者如市，莫不啧啧叹息。忽一人高冠盛服自外入，抚尸大哭，众皆惊愕。役询之，则其女弟也。役乃大笑，争诘其详。

    Explanation

    616

    The report stated: "There was a maid named Niaoyan who had served for several years and was my younger sister. She was kidnapped by a villain, and her whereabouts are unknown. When I came to the capital, I passed by a brothel and saw her standing by the door. She caught a glimpse of her brother and immediately hid. Her appearance and attire were clearly identifiable. I pleaded with the legal officer to search and capture her." At that time, the commander of the southern city guards was a certain official, known for capturing villains and exposing hidden crimes, and he had a very upright reputation. He was also fully aware that he was the younger brother of a certain person, of noble lineage and young age, and thus would not fabricate falsehoods. Therefore, he imposed severe punishment on the courtesan. Lan, fearing the consequences, finally revealed the truth. It turned out that Niaoyan had refused to comply and was beaten repeatedly. One night, she suddenly hanged herself to death. Human life is not light, and there was evidence of coercion. Thus, they dared not speak and privately buried her in the guesthouse courtyard. Fearing the matter would be exposed, they pretended she had fled, not knowing that she still had a brother. Now, facing the imperial censorate, they willingly confessed guilt. The official ordered the body to be exhumed, and her face looked as if she were alive, still not decayed. At that time, the crowd gathered like a market, all murmuring in astonishment. Suddenly, a man wearing a tall hat and splendid attire entered from outside, embraced the body, and wept, causing everyone to be shocked. The official asked him, and he replied that she was his younger sister. The official then laughed loudly and asked for the details.

    617

    自言陆姓，名仲昇，曾掌案卷于某部。以吏员考授杂职，今已及选。有妹年十四，适陆远出，其妻性素悍，凌虐之。及夫将归，惧其愤诉，乃乘其寐卷以席，使人弃之于野。陆归知之，怒逐其妇，而其妹究不可得，事阅二年矣。是日陆仆往观，相验一目，即遄归告其主曰：“兰家缢者，当是我家袅姑。”陆大惊，趋往视之，果其妹，故哀恸如此。役乃驰白某公，公甚疑讶，命价延生来。婉叩之，生见真鼎已出，遂笑而缅述真由，而独隐遇梦一节，公为叹服。由是都门任侠者，咸慕生好义之名，愿得纳交。陆更称谢不置，往来契密如旧相知。生居京数月，辞兄旋里。行及郭外，墓田丛杂。适相署之役，以公命送生，乃指以新冢告生曰：“此即陆女也，乃兄为营此葬地，丧具甚盛。

    Explanation

    617

    He claimed to be surnamed Lu, named Zhongsheng, and had once managed case files in a certain department. He was promoted to a minor official position through an examination for clerical staff and is now eligible for promotion. He had a younger sister who was fourteen years old. When his husband was away on a journey, his wife, who was typically fierce and domineering, mistreated her. When the husband was about to return, fearing her anger and complaints, they suffocated her while she was sleeping by rolling her up in a mat and had someone throw her away in the wilderness. Upon returning, the husband learned of this and angrily expelled his wife. However, his sister could not be found, and the matter had been two years old. On that day, the husband's servant went to inspect and, upon examining one eye, hurried back to inform his master, saying, "The woman who hanged herself in the Lan family should be my sister Niao." The husband was greatly shocked and rushed to see for himself, and indeed it was his sister, which is why he was so grief-stricken. The servant then hurried to report to a certain official, who was very surprised and ordered the servant to bring the man in. The official gently questioned him, and the man saw that the true culprit had already been revealed, so he smiled and recounted the truth, but he deliberately omitted the part about meeting a dream. The official was greatly impressed. As a result, the chivalrous people in the capital all admired the man's reputation for virtue and wished to form friendships with him. The husband thanked him profusely and they became close friends, as if they had known each other for a long time. The man stayed in the capital for several months before bidding farewell to his brother and returning to his hometown. Walking outside the city walls, the cemetery area was overgrown and cluttered. A subordinate official of the magistrate, following the official's command to escort the man, pointed out a new grave and told him, "This is the grave of the Lu girl. Your brother arranged this burial ground, and the funeral arrangements were very elaborate."

    618

    ”生闻之心动，遂命仆于近村觅得杯酒，而自下骑酹之曰：“予为卿泄此沉冤，卿故漠然无知耶？”语甫已，觉襟裾有物，累累下垂，回顾之又无所见。乃复趋乘而前，至旅次，步履周旋辄如此。及寝，则伏于衾侧，扪之渺然。生心以为异，而不肯宣。凡数十程皆如之，亦习而未复介意。及归见母，室家相聚，因述其前事，太夫人与细君莫不称异。阅数日，细君坐蓐，生遂宿于精室。夜已过半，闻榻前窸窣有声，询之则曰：“袅烟也。”生素向慕，竟不之惧，惟笑曰：“暮夜无烛，安知非汝以绐我者？”语未终，灯光四射，烬者复燃，果见女立于灯下，花貌嫣然，容妆富丽，迥异梦中之惨淡。且敛衽再拜曰：“袅烟以薄命之身，初遭恶嫂，次遇淫娼，备极摧折之苦。窃虑有玷先人，缒梁自尽。

    618

    The youth, upon hearing this, was moved, and he ordered his servant to fetch a cup of wine from the nearby village. He then dismounted and poured the wine before it, saying, "I will avenge you of this deep injustice. Are you still indifferent and unaware?" As soon as he finished speaking, he felt something hanging from his clothes, heavy and drooping. He looked back but saw nothing. Then he hurried on again, and at the inn, every step was like that. When he slept, it lay beside his bedding, but when he touched it, it was insubstantial. The youth thought it strange, but he did not speak of it. For dozens of miles, it was the same, and he became accustomed to it, no longer paying attention. When he returned home and saw his mother, the family gathered, and he then recounted his earlier experiences. The mother and wife both called it strange. After several days, the wife gave birth, and the youth then stayed in the inner room. By midnight, he heard a rustling sound by the bed. He asked and replied, "It's Niaoyan." The youth had long admired her, yet he was not afraid, only laughing and saying, "In the dark of night, how can I know you're not deceiving me?" Before the words were finished, the lamp light spread, and the extinguished lamp relit. Indeed, he saw a woman standing by the lamp, with a beautiful face and richly adorned, completely different from the bleakness in the dream. She bowed twice, saying, "Niaoyan, with her unfortunate life, first encountered a wicked sister-in-law, then met a prostitute, suffering the utmost torment. Worried about dishonoring her ancestors, she hanged herself from a beam."

    619

    不意豪侠如君者，为妾大白此冤，久思图报，抱愧无媒。复辱吊临，是用忘耻，前自京师附骥，至今始敢露形，幸勿以阴浊为嫌，使妾得少酬高厚，则又不世之惠矣。”生闻言甚喜，微诘曰：“鬼固无害乎？”女腼然答曰：“害诚有之，亦视乎其人耳。以恩义而结绸缪，鬼亦人也；逞色欲而忘躯命，人亦鬼也。况妾以一念坚贞，久已超出鬼道，君何患焉？”生遂欣然纳之。及命之寝，女固羞缩不前。生强之，乃笑曰：“抱白璧于生前，而碎明珠子死后，微君之大德，妾亦等于淫奔矣。”于是解衣共枕。交合之际，退避逡巡，已而流丹浃席，无异生人。翌日晨起，遂以环堵之宫为藏娇之室。女虽白昼亦现形，与生聚处，惟不洗濯，不饮食，是其所异。他如倡随之乐，谈宴之欢，生亦亲身享之矣。

    Explanation

    619

    I did not expect someone as heroic as you to clear this injustice for me. I have long wished to repay you, but felt ashamed to find no way. To be honored by your repeated visits has made me forget my shame. Before, I traveled to the capital city to hitch a ride on someone else's success, and until now, I have dared to reveal my true form. Please do not嫌弃 my dark and impure nature. If I could repay your great kindness, it would be a rare and precious favor." The hero was very pleased to hear this and lightly questioned, "Is there really no harm in being a ghost?" The woman answered shyly, "There is indeed harm, but it depends on the person. If one binds oneself with bonds of love and loyalty, a ghost is also human; if one indulges in lust and forgets one's life, a human is also a ghost. Moreover, with my unwavering virtue, I have long surpassed the realm of ghosts. What do you have to fear?" The hero then happily accepted her. When he commanded her to sleep, she hesitated and retreated. The hero insisted, and she smiled, saying, "To hold a white jade before life and to break a pearl after death, without your great virtue, I would be no better than a runaway bride." They then undressed and lay together on the same bed. During their union, she hesitated and retreated, but soon after, there was a flow of blood that soaked the mat, no different from a living person. The next morning, he made the humble room a place to hide a beautiful woman. The woman appeared even during the day and lived with the hero, but she did not wash or eat, which was her difference. Other joys, such as the pleasure of following and the merriment of conversation and feasting, the hero also experienced firsthand.

    620

    然以女故，不与人通，僮仆亦无敢皔槛。幸生有静癖，旧常如此，故人亦不甚疑之。女不善歌，因生学之，一发声而响遏行云。女无他技，因生习之，一吹弹即臻精妙。清夜相对，颇不寂寥。生或微问之，对曰：“昔在妓家，虽未肄习，而声容节奏之妙，亦心领会，但不屑为之耳。今对知己，故不复藏拙，亦情之所使也。”生以是愈呢之。女故识字，生教之，即能通文。暇辄浼生为市金刚楞严诸经，跏趺坐诵，恒至子夜不休。及细君产后身健，女乃义不当夕，曰：“妾在此如尘栖弱草，而可夺人并蒂耶？”生不之听，女忽不见。生乃入内寝，然值三五之期，必宿于外，与女为欢。如是者年余，女忽笑谓生曰：“鬼亦生人，岂不大异？意者，天许妾以此酬德乎？然不可复居于是矣。

    Explanation

    620

    Yet because of the woman, he did not interact with others, and even the servants dared not [do something]. Fortunately, he had a fondness for solitude, and it was always like this, so people did not doubt him much. The woman was not skilled in singing, but because of him, she learned it, and when she sang, the sound stopped the passing clouds. The woman had no other skills, but because of him, she practiced them, and when she played, she reached a level of perfection. Spending clear nights together, they were not very lonely. He gently asked her, and she replied: "In the past, in the brothel, although I did not practice, the beauty of the voice, appearance, and rhythm also understood in the heart, but I considered it unworthy to do. Now, for a true friend, I no longer hide my clumsiness, it is also the emotion that drives me." He became even more attached to her because of this. The woman already knew how to read, he taught her, and she could understand the text. When he had free time, he would often ask him to buy the Diamond Sutra and the Lengyan Sutra, and they would sit in a cross-legged posture and recite, often until midnight without stopping. When the fine lady gave birth and recovered well, the woman felt morally should not share the bed, saying: "I am here like dust settling on weak grass, how can I take away someone's twin?" He did not listen to her, and the woman suddenly disappeared. He then entered the inner chamber, but when it was the third and fifth days of the month, he had to stay outside, spending time with the woman in pleasure. Like this for over a year, the woman suddenly smiled and said to him: "Ghosts can also be born, isn't that very strange? Perhaps, is it by heaven's will that I repay your kindness in this way? But I cannot continue to stay here anymore."

    621

    ”生亦骇然，而情不能舍，亟叩其所之。女曰：“妾赖佛经，洞彻本来。再生以前乃天妃之侍儿也，缘过失堕落至此。幸能矢志，不甘风尘，已为故主所鉴，将令仍还供职。只为君一脉在身，是以少留。明日君适近郭，白杨树下一襁褓物，是即君之遗体。君抱之归，如是云云，人必信之。君命中无令子，是儿尚能象贤，毋自误。”言讫，挥泪诀别，荏苒间化为淡烟而灭。生悲痛不胜，如其言，果得宁馨以返。诈云弃儿，命人乳哺，亦无疑及于此者。比长，耳目口鼻无不肖生，戚族始以为异。生乃稍稍述之，闻者骇叹。生后位至显宦，细君所生三儿，不胜克家，惟袅烟之儿名梦锡，能继书香，登上第。时仲昇宦游数年，致仕家居。生父子皆官居于京，乃率甥往谒其舅，并告以故。悲喜相交，梦锡始识其外家。自是邓陆往来数世，如姻娅云。

    Explanation

    621

    The man was also startled, but he could not let go of his feelings, and he immediately inquired about her destination. The woman said, "I rely on Buddhist scriptures, which have enlightened me to the true nature of things. Before rebirth, I was a servant of the Heavenly Consort, but due to my mistakes, I fell to this state. Fortunately, I was able to remain steadfast, unwilling to remain in the world of sin, and I have been recognized by my former master, who will allow me to return to my duties. Only because of your lineage within me, I am staying a little longer. Tomorrow, when you go near the city, there will be an infant in a cradle under a white poplar tree. That is your descendant. You should take it back, if you say such things, people will believe you. You do not have a worthy son in your destiny, but this child can emulate virtue. Do not deceive yourself." After speaking, she wiped away tears and bid farewell, gradually transforming into thin smoke and disappearing. The man was overwhelmed with grief and, as she had said, indeed found a precious one to return with. He falsely claimed it as an abandoned child and had someone nurse it, and no one doubted him. As the child grew, his ears, eyes, nose, and mouth all resembled the man, and his relatives began to find him strange. The man then began to recount the story, and those who heard it were astonished and sighed. Later, the man rose to a position of prominence, and his wife gave birth to three sons, but they were unable to manage the household. Only the child of the woman named Niaoyan, named Mengxi, could continue the scholarly tradition and achieve success in the imperial examinations. At that time, Zhongsheng had been traveling for several years and had retired to his home. The man and his son both held official positions in the capital, and he then took his nephew to visit his uncle, telling him the reason. A mix of sadness and joy filled the air, and Mengxi first met his maternal relatives. Since then, the Deng and Lu families have been connected for several generations, like relatives.

    622

    外史氏曰：女以不磨之贞，而独感生一语，不能复坚，情以义生故也。情可制，而义不可泯，故以鞭扑之威当之无惧色者，竟能温柔娇媚，呈身奏技于夫子之前，义之有所以致之，非初贞而后渝也。然无生之侠肠，生之义举，而徒思与鬼为好，不第贞魂不屑来，淫妖且立至矣，欲水免于鬼手得乎？而况居然生子也耶！

    ○　镜儿
    　　高僧邵本一，卓锡灵隐，戒行精严。初名某，陕人也。少时为邑庠生，慕吴楚人文之盛，游学江左。数年忽有所悟，竟削发逃禅，辗转至越，为释门尊宿。杭人仰其名，延至寺中，即今之所称定心大师者是已。其子曰续师，出时方始匍匐。及长，每以不识其父为恨，间关两江，数数求之，又往往相左。比闻其在钱塘，遂附舟南下。共载者为一少年，貌姣好如女子，自言龚姓，由京归，将返山阴。闻续之孝，甚礼敬之，两人颇相莫逆。迨至杭，续得父之所在，亟往谒焉。龚亦请从，许之，乃偕往。

    Explanation

    622

    The Historian said: A woman, endowed with unshakable chastity, was swayed by a single word, and could no longer maintain her resolve, for emotions arise from righteousness. Emotions can be controlled, but righteousness cannot be erased. Therefore, those who show no fear in the face of severe punishment can still display gentleness and charm, presenting themselves and performing their skills before their husband, motivated by a specific moral reason, not a change from initial chastity. However, the heroic spirit of the deceased and the righteous actions of the living, if one merely seeks to befriend a ghost, not only would the virtuous spirit not come, but a licentious demon would appear instead. Can desire be protected from the hands of a ghost? And yet, she gave birth to a child!

    ○ Mirror
    The high priest Shaoben, residing at Lingyin, was known for his strict adherence to monastic rules. Originally named something, he was from Shaanxi. In his youth, he was a student at a local school, admiring the cultural richness of Wu and Chu, and traveled to study in the east. After several years, he suddenly had an epiphany and shaved his head to escape the world, eventually reaching Yue, where he became a respected Buddhist elder. People in Hangzhou revered his name and invited him to stay at a temple, which is now known as the Temple of the Tranquil Mind. His son, named Xush, was born while crawling. As he grew older, he often regretted not knowing his father and traveled through the two rivers, seeking him repeatedly, but often failing to find him. Upon hearing that his father was in Qiantang, he boarded a boat and sailed south. Traveling with him was a young man, handsome as a girl, who said his name was Gong and was returning home from the capital to go back to Shanyin. Hearing of Xush's filial piety, he treated him with great respect, and the two became close friends. When they arrived in Hangzhou, Xush found his father's location and hurried to visit him. Gong also requested to go with him, and he agreed, so they went together.

    623

    甫及寺门，早有一僧逆之曰：“大师出定，已知法嗣远来，但不宜与镜儿俱，请止之。”续茫然，而龚忽变色。续觉其有异，而思亲念切，亦不暇诘，遂止龚于门外，独入见。及至法堂，师方趺坐，续本不能识，僧语之曰：“此即若翁也。”续乃嗷然大痛，哭拜于膝下。师麾之曰：“止！止！孽种勿尔。汝父甚安乐，汝宜生欢喜心，何作此态？”乃命之坐，略诘戚族之长者，并同学诸故人，续一一敬对。师忽攒眉曰：“汝跋涉不易，亦征孝心，第来则自来，如镜儿辈，何携之来溷老僧？”续亟起俯伏，跪白不知，兼请其故。

    Explanation

    623

    Just as he reached the temple gate, an early-arriving monk greeted him, saying, "Master, having emerged from meditation, I already knew that your disciple had come from afar, but it would not be suitable for you to bring 镜儿 along. Please leave him outside." The disciple was confused, and 龚 suddenly looked alarmed. The disciple sensed something was amiss, but his longing for his father was so strong that he had no time to question further. He then stopped 龚 outside the gate and went in alone to see the master. When he arrived at the Dharma hall, the master was just sitting cross-legged. The disciple originally could not recognize the master, but the monk told him, "This is your father." The disciple suddenly felt great sorrow, cried, and bowed at the master's knees. The master waved him away, saying, "Stop! Stop! Evil offspring, do not do that. Your father is very peaceful. You should have a joyful heart. Why do this?" The master then ordered him to sit, briefly questioned the elders of the family, and also asked about various old friends and classmates. The disciple respectfully answered each question one by one. The master suddenly furrowed his brows and said, "You traveled a long and difficult journey, which also demonstrates filial piety. If you came, you came. But why bring the likes of 镜儿 here to disturb the old monk?" The disciple quickly stood up, bowed down, knelt, and explained that he did not know, also asking for the reason.

    624

    师曰：“龚即镜儿之夫，镜儿乃其眷属，实一野狐也。爱欲牵缠，两不能舍，即藉汝之孝得以渡江。又思窥伺老蹭，冀吾片言以成其好。镜儿实在渠身边，以汝凡躯，乌能见之。”既而曰：“是狐颇风雅，且识孝子，吾固不惜此寸楮也。”命取黄纸一条，书数字付侍者，嘱曰：“持此予之，勿令渠久溷净地。”侍者将命以出，龚得之，即再拜而退。续居寺中匝月，师即遣之曰：“归奉汝母，犹之乎奉父也。此非尘境，可以久容俗迹。”续不欲旋返，师呵之，乃行。归见其母，康健犹昔。承欢数载，又念父，复诣浙江，则师已挂单而南，不知所往。

    Explanation

    624

    The master said, "Gong is the husband of Mirror, and Mirror is his relative, indeed a wild fox. Bound by love and desire, they cannot part with each other, and through your filial piety, they were able to cross the river. Mirror also thought of spying on the old monk, hoping that my words would help them form a relationship. Mirror is actually with him, and with your ordinary body, how could you see her?" He then said, "This fox is quite refined, and she recognizes the filial son, so I naturally wouldn't hesitate to write this small piece of paper." He ordered to take a strip of yellow paper, wrote a few characters, and handed them to the attendant, instructing, "Hold this and give it to him, do not let him stay long in the pure place." The attendant took the message and left, and Gong received it, immediately bowed twice, and withdrew. He then stayed in the temple for a full month, when the master sent him away, saying, "Return to serve your mother, just as you serve your father. This is not a mundane place, it cannot accommodate ordinary traces for long." Gong did not want to return immediately, but the master scolded him, and he then left. Upon returning, he saw his mother, who was still healthy as before. He spent several years in her company, then thinking of his father, he went to Zhejiang again, but the master had already left south, and no one knew where he went.

    625

    续因罔极恩深，遂亦南行。忽于山阴道上遇一人，裘马翩翩，驺从甚盛，视之，则故昔年同舟者也。龚见续，即下骑拜伏道周，曰：“孝子别来无恙？”续亟下拜，相扶曰：“故人何癰谦至此？”龚起曰：“乔梓之德，实同天地，每恨申报无由，而敢傲慢自尊乎？”因固请过其家。续之私心亦思深求其异，欣然从之。爰假骖乘，并舆而前。乃班荆之地去其居犹半日程，途次，续微叩之，龚亦不讳。始悉龚本浙人，其季父宦于京师，携以之任，肄业西北山中。结庐数楹，地绝幽雅。一日冬初雪盛，龚方携卷围炉盜唔攻苦，俄有一物如火团，其色正赤，烟焰尺余，自梁而下。

    Explanation

    625

    Due to the complexity and length of the text, I will provide the final translation in parts. Here is the first part:

    Xu, moved by the deep and boundless kindness of his parents, also set out on a journey south. Suddenly, on the road through the mountains and rivers, he encountered a man dressed in fine fur and riding a horse gracefully, with a large retinue. Upon closer inspection, it turned out to be his old companion from the previous year. Gong saw Xu and immediately dismounted, bowing deeply beside the road, and said, "How is the filial son since we last met?" Xu hurriedly bowed in return, and they helped each other up, saying, "Why is an old friend so humble?" Gong stood up and said, "The virtue of father and son is truly as vast as heaven and earth. I often regret not having a way to express my gratitude, and how could I be arrogant and self-important?" He then insisted on taking Xu to his home. Xu, who had also been curious about Gong's unusual abilities, gladly accepted the invitation. They borrowed a carriage, and Xu rode along with Gong. The place where they had met before was still half a day's journey from Gong's home. On the way, Xu asked Gong about his background, and Gong did not hide anything. Xu learned that Gong was originally from Zhejiang, and his paternal uncle had served in the capital, bringing Gong along with him. Gong had studied in the northwest mountains, building several small houses in a secluded and elegant location.

    Would you like me to continue with the rest of the translation?

    626

    就地旋转无休，一室生温，较前益炽。龚大怖，方疑祝融肆虐，将起奔避，其光忽顿敛，遽化为人，则一白发媪，衣裙朴素，拱立于前。益骇然，知为山间妖异，避之倍亟。媪直前止之曰：“郎君勿惶惧，予非为人祸者。见子读书岑寂，家有弱女曰镜儿，颇耽翰墨，意将遣奉箕帚，少领教益，故尔仓卒请见。未知肯容纳否？”龚更愕然，心滋忧惧，乃辞曰：“不才下士，学未有成，恐误媪之闺爱。且来踪诡秘，惕惕于怀，燕婉之求，益非所愿，惟媪怜而恕之。”媪性甚暴，似不见听，即怒曰：“我女天人也，以偶小子，自谓无可推拒矣。

    Explanation

    626

    Gong was greatly frightened and suspected that the fire god ZHU RONG was wreaking havoc, about to get up and flee. The light suddenly condensed and immediately transformed into a person, then into an elderly woman with white hair, simple clothing, and standing respectfully in front. He was even more startled, knowing it was a strange and eerie creature from the mountains, and avoided it even more urgently. The old woman stepped forward and stopped him, saying, "Young sir, do not be afraid. I am not here to cause harm to people. I saw you reading in solitude, and I have a young daughter named Jing'er, who is very fond of writing. I intend to send her to serve you and learn from you, that's why I hastily asked to meet you. I don't know if you would accept her." Gong was even more surprised and felt increasingly anxious, and replied, saying, "I am an unworthy and lowly scholar, my studies are not yet complete. I fear I might disappoint the old lady's affection. Moreover, my arrival is mysterious, and I am deeply troubled. A marriage proposal is even less desirable. I hope the old lady will pity and forgive me." The old woman's temper is very fierce, and it seems she will not listen, and then angrily said, "My daughter is a heavenly being, and I intend to marry her to you. I believe there is no way to refuse."

    627

    而乃喋喋若是，岂以适才之威，不能付汝于煨烬乎？”言次忤目相向，睒闪如牛，龚亦惴惴。正无如之何，又见一婢，艳妆冶容自外入，笑曰：“如此硬主婚，反损人伉俪之情，殊非善道。请即归，镜姑当自至。”又曰，“予固知媪之风厉，不克办此。”扶而去之，媪犹忿。出门数武，俱不见。龚心胆几碎，将下山而雪迷蹊径，马不能行。且仆之长者，奉命入都取薪米，寓中自龚而外，只一十二岁之童子，复何能为？不得已而静俟之，死生在天而已。及夕，雪少霁，故掩扉高卧，权度此宵，计将明日他徙。然当惊悸之余，寝不成寐，旋闻弹指声。

    Explanation

    627

    Incessantly chattering like that, could it be that the authority just exercised is not enough to leave you to the ashes?” As they spoke, they glared at each other, blinking like bulls, and Gong was also fearful. With no other recourse, another maid entered, dressed in bright colors and with a charming appearance, and smiled, saying, “To force such a marriage, it harms the affection between husband and wife, which is certainly not a good way. Please return; Mirror Maiden will come herself.” She added, “I certainly know that the old woman is strict, and unable to accomplish this.” She helped her away, but the old woman was still furious. After stepping out a few paces, no one was seen. Gong’s heart and courage almost shattered. He was about to descend the mountain, but the snow obscured the path, and the horse could not move. Moreover, the elder servant had been ordered to go to the capital to fetch firewood and rice, and in the inn, apart from Gong, there was only a twelve-year-old boy. What could be done? Unwillingly, he waited quietly, knowing that life and death were in the hands of heaven. By evening, the snow had slightly stopped, so he closed the door and lay down, pondering this night and planning to move somewhere else tomorrow. However, after being startled and frightened, he could not sleep, and soon heard the sound of snapping fingers.

    628

    有人叩窗作歌曰：“叹空闺兮掩孤檠，望伊人兮违素诚。伐柯伐柯兮其音丁丁，果得相随兮我愿卿卿。”声甚娇婉而余韵悠扬，俨有绕梁之致，龚知为镜儿，穴窗窥之，雪色明如皎月，则一小女子辫发垂鬟，盈盈然立于槛外。天寒翠袖，暮倚修竹，差可仿佛其一二。龚心窃怜之，但为前事所惊，不免首鼠，究不敢出一言。又闻女歌曰：“雪欲晴兮云微，乌不宿兮双飞。奈有人兮愿孤帏，我不见兮又空归。”歌阕退步欲行，意甚怅怅。

    Explanation

    628

    Someone knocked on the window and sang, saying, "Lamenting the empty chamber, I hide behind the solitary lamp; longing for that person, I find their faithfulness betrayed. Chopping wood, chopping wood, the sound is crisp and clear; if I could follow you, my wish would be fulfilled." Her voice was very gentle and sweet, with a lingering melody that seemed to circle the beams, as if it had the quality of a beautiful, enduring song. Gong recognized her as Jing'er, and peered through a hole in the window. The snow was bright as the moon, and there stood a young girl with braided hair and hanging tresses, gracefully standing outside the railing. It was cold, and she wore green sleeves, leaning against tall bamboo in the evening. She somewhat resembled her in some aspects. Gong secretly pitied her, but having been startled by previous events, he could not help but hesitate, and ultimately dared not speak a word. Then he heard her sing again, saying, "The snow is about to clear, the clouds are faint; the crows do not roost, they fly in pairs. Alas, there is someone who wishes for solitude, yet I cannot see them, and return empty-handed." After the song ended, she stepped back to walk away, her heart filled with deep disappointment.

    629

    龚遂不能自禁，大呼曰：“卿之所向者在此，将安归耶？”女乃中止，隔牖语之曰：“屡遭峻拒，妾实汗颜，故不得不归。岂真悻悻欲返者？”龚即披衣起榻，启扉延之，径携素手以入。时残烛未灭，灯下玩之，肌不丰而玉腻，貌不饰而花娇，一颦一笑，婉娩可人，闺中实罕其匹。因询之曰：“所谓镜儿殆卿耶？使非恶撮合，好事之成已久矣。”镜儿笑曰，“君固色胆巨者，非妾自来，好事真不可望。”龚遂挽之就枕，辞曰：“妾尚待年，君姑毋躁。”因出一卷曰：“此妾之涂鸦，愧无师授，乞君为我郢政之。三日后，妾来拜领，幸勿以谀碑见还。”言已再拜而退，旋失所在。龚阅所作，风流秀艳，语语醉心。诘朝早起，潘硃展卷，评骘端详，因是无迁乔之意。

    Explanation

    629

    龚遂不能自禁，大呼曰：“卿之所向者在此，将安归耶？”女乃中止，隔牖语之曰：“屡遭峻拒，妾实汗颜，故不得不归。岂真悻悻欲返者？”龚即披衣起榻，启扉延之，径携素手以入。时残烛未灭，灯下玩之，肌不丰而玉腻，貌不饰而花娇，一颦一笑，婉娩可人，闺中实罕其匹。因询之曰：“所谓镜儿殆卿耶？使非恶撮合，好事之成已久矣。”镜儿笑曰，“君固色胆巨者，非妾自来，好事真不可望。”龚遂挽之就枕，辞曰：“妾尚待年，君姑毋躁。”因出一卷曰：“此妾之涂鸦，愧无师授，乞君为我郢政之。三日后，妾来拜领，幸勿以谀碑见还。”言已再拜而退，旋失所在。龚阅所作，风流秀艳，语语醉心。诘朝早起，潘硃展卷，评骘端详，因是无迁乔之意。

    Translation:
    龚遂 could not restrain himself and shouted loudly, "You are heading this way, where else will you go?" The woman then stopped, and through the window, she said to him, "I have been repeatedly faced with harsh refusal, and I am truly ashamed, so I cannot help but return. Are you really leaving in a sullen mood?" Gong immediately got out of bed and dressed, opened the door to invite her in, and took her hand to lead her inside. At the time, the candle was still burning, and under the dim light, he observed her. Her skin was not plump but smooth like jade, her appearance unadorned yet charming as a flower. Every frown and smile were captivating and endearing, rarely matched among women. He asked her, "Is the so-called Mirror Lady you? If not for me coming myself, the good fortune would have been long achieved." The woman laughed and said, "You are indeed bold in matters of love. If not for me coming myself, good fortune would truly be impossible." Gong then led her to the bed, and she replied, "I am still young and unmarried, please be patient." She took out a scroll and said, "This is my scribbling, ashamed to have no teacher, I ask you to correct it. Three days later, I will come to receive it, please do not return it with flattery." After speaking, she bowed twice and left, disappearing immediately. Gong read her work, which was graceful and beautiful, every word captivating. Early the next morning, he spread out the scroll and read it carefully, evaluating and examining it thoroughly, and therefore had no intention of moving.

    630

    越二日，镜儿果夜至，龚出卷授之曰：“幸不辱命。但卿之珠玉，令人不谀不能耳！”镜几翻阅数四，冁然曰：“名下犹信无虚。”仍欲去，龚遮留之，代解衣带。镜儿乃頳颜笑曰：“人当十五即嫁，王昌岂得谓非孽事？”己而海棠枝上，得试新红。镜儿虽娇啼宛转，态若不胜，然已极人间之乐矣。欢好有间，龚因询媪为谁何，答曰：“此妾结义之母，渠自古姓，野人家也。”龚曰：“其威颇令人震怖，今言之，尚觉股慄。”镜儿哂曰：“以君今夕之威，较之恐犹相去远甚。”语次皆大笑，遂并头熟寐。

    Explanation

    630

    Two days later, Mirror Girl indeed arrived at night. Gong presented the scroll to her and said, "I have not disgraced your command. But your jade and pearls are so valuable that it is impossible not to praise them!" Mirror Girl read the scroll several times and laughed happily, saying, "As expected, there is no empty fame." She still wanted to leave, but Gong detained her and helped her untie her sash. Mirror Girl blushed and smiled, saying, "A woman should marry at fifteen; how can it be said that this is not an appropriate thing?" On the branches of the crabapple tree, the new red flowers bloomed, symbolizing the onset of puberty or the beginning of a new phase in life. Mirror Girl cried softly and turned around, appearing as if she could not bear it, but she had already experienced the greatest joy in the world. After a moment of enjoyment, Gong asked who the old woman was. She replied, "She is my mother who has taken a formal oath of brotherhood, and she comes from a humble background." Gong said, "Her presence is quite intimidating; even now, when I speak of it, I still feel a shiver of fear." Mirror Girl laughed and said, "With your current strength, it far surpasses hers." They all laughed together and fell asleep side by side.

    631

    侵晨，前婢叩门而入，龚因谢其解纷之德。婢笑曰：“痴老媪故不谙此，予固谓非镜姑自至不可。”俟女整衣，奉之以去。自此无夕不至，缱绻日深。镜儿既嗜诗文，兼饶韵致，以所居湫隘，且有僮仆，不获畅所欲为。往往枕上推敲，床头辨难，或采雅言以相谑，或取妙句而同吟。语笑有声，间至通宵不辍。时年长之仆已返，闻而讶之。昧爽伺于户外，则主人倦卧，门忽自启，且珊珊小步，裙际风生。窥之又茫然无睹，乃大惧，以为地无居人，此必鬼狐者流，遂力劝龚归。龚不纳，仆又入都，白之于龚之季父。知为妖惑，遣使召之。

    Explanation

    631

    At dawn, the previous maid knocked on the door and entered. Gong thanked her for resolving the dispute. The maid smiled and said, "The foolish old woman doesn't understand this; I firmly believed that only Mirror Maiden herself could come." She waited for the girl to dress and then left. From then on, she visited every night, and their affection grew deeper. Mirror Maiden was fond of poetry and prose, and she was also full of charm and elegance. However, her living space was small and cramped, and with servants around, she could not fully express herself or act as she wished. Often, she would ponder over her thoughts while lying in bed, debated difficult issues by the bedside, or used refined language to tease each other, or recited beautiful verses together. Their laughter and conversation were audible, and sometimes they continued until dawn without stopping. Meanwhile, the older servant had returned and was surprised by the situation. Just before dawn, he waited outside the door, but the owner was asleep. Suddenly, the door opened by itself, and a figure stepped out with graceful, light steps, with her skirt fluttering in the wind. When he looked again, he saw nothing, and he became greatly afraid, thinking that the place was empty and that this must be a ghost or fox spirit. He strongly urged Gong to return home. Gong refused, and the servant went back to the capital and reported the matter to Gong's uncle. The uncle recognized it as a case of bewitchment and sent an envoy to summon Gong.

    632

    未发而镜儿已知，是夕忽涕泣谓龚曰：“欢不克永矣，可奈何？”龚惊询之，对曰：“仆已泄吾事于主，使来召君。君归当不复来，将焉置妾？”龚亦悲叹，因要镜儿偕行。辞曰：“妾实不敢，身本狐也，吾侪各有畛域，况圣天子所都，无能擅往。君不弃妾，必也南旋而后可。”龚已深入温柔，亦难遽舍，惟问计将安出。镜儿曰：“妾有薄蓄，足任舟车。君但如妾所言，则故乡可返，何必恋恋于兹土？”龚竟毅然从之。乃与镜儿谋，不携一物，悄然夜行。仆本久留都中，童子又垂头而睡，遂无有挽留之者。行约里许，草际似有灯光，隐隐双炬，旋暗复明。

    Explanation

    632

    Before it was revealed, Jing'er already knew. That night, she suddenly wept and told Gong, "Our joy will not last forever, what can we do?" Gong was startled and asked her about it. She replied, "I have already revealed our affair to the master, who has sent someone to summon you. When you return, you will not come back again, so where will I be placed?" Gong also sighed with sorrow and urged Jing'er to go with him. She declined, saying, "I truly dare not. I am originally a fox, and we each have our own domains. Moreover, the capital, where the sage emperor resides, is a place I cannot go to擅自. If you do not abandon me, you must return to the south first." Gong, having deeply fallen in love, found it difficult to leave immediately and asked for a plan. Jing'er said, "I have some savings, enough to cover the cost of a boat and a carriage. If you follow my advice, we can return to our hometown, why must we linger here?" Gong ultimately decided to follow her. They planned together, taking nothing with them, and quietly traveled at night. The servant had been staying in the capital for a long time, and the child servant was sleeping with his head down, so there was no one to detain them. They walked about a mile, and at the edge of the grass, it seemed there were lights. Two faint torches appeared, dimming and then brightening again.

    633

    龚指之以为人家，镜儿笑曰：“君之所畏者至矣！亦太剥人面皮，何竟咆哮于此？”龚犹未解。俄闻虎啸，声震山谷，龚乃大惊，几坠崖下。镜儿掖之曰：“有妾在，何震恐乃尔？”乃呼曰，“儿与婿将他适，承母玉成，异日归当效反哺。”言未已，虎忽潜踪。龚心少定，反戏曰：“昔假虎威，今日威可伏虎矣。”及抵山麓，有村落，暂假朝饔。遂显称夫妇，人亦无自究诘。镜儿又出白金觅代步，置衣装，竟纡道由京而南，直抵通郡。不日解缆登舟，一帆南去。迨其季父之召来，龚早出山三日矣。舟中龚与镜儿志始得畅，或涤瓯茗战，或剪烛手谈，或举诗书为觞政，或纪风景于行笥。

    Explanation

    633

    Gong pointed it out as a place for people, and Mirror smiled and said, "The thing you fear has arrived! Also, it's too harsh, why are you roaring here?" Gong still did not understand. Soon after, he heard a tiger roar, the sound shaking the mountains and valleys, and Gong was greatly surprised, almost falling off the cliff. Mirror held him and said, "With me here, why are you so frightened?" Then she called out, "My child and her husband will go elsewhere, thanks to your mother's help, on the day they return, they will repay the favor like a bird feeding its parents." Before she finished speaking, the tiger suddenly disappeared. Gong's heart calmed slightly, and then he joked, "In the past, I pretended to have the tiger's power, today, my power can subdue the tiger." When they reached the foot of the mountain, there was a village, and they temporarily borrowed breakfast. They then openly called themselves husband and wife, and no one questioned them. Mirror then took out white gold to find a means of transportation, bought clothes and other items, and took a detour from the capital to the south, directly to Tong County. In no time, they untied the boat and set sail, sailing south with one sail. By the time his uncle called for him, Gong had already left the mountain three days earlier. On the boat, Gong and Mirror finally felt at ease, sometimes washing cups and playing chess with tea, sometimes trimming candles and playing chess by candlelight, sometimes using poetry and books as a drinking game, sometimes recording the scenery in their travel cases.

    634

    而分题限韵，更唱迭和，较前益为豪放。龚初犹强而后可，继且乐此不疲，虽良友之晦明无以异焉。行及下江，将入浙界，镜儿忽愀然曰：“此地水神颇作梗，妾不能渡。须俟大福之人，布帆乃能无恙。”龚诘其故，则曰：“伍相范伯，威灵素著，非他神可比，妾实惮之。”龚不之信，乃未及扬帆，巨浪坌起，阴霾匝地，翳不见人。龚大恐，为留五日。适以扁舟至，镜儿色喜曰：“有大德者至矣，犹愈于厚福者也。君能与之共济，虽百江神妾不惴也。”龚从其言，适续正值易舟，遂从中怂恿，使榜人招接之。

    Explanation

    634

    Dividing topics and limiting rhymes, alternating verses and responses, the style became even more bold and unrestrained than before. Gong was initially reluctant but eventually agreed, and later found himself enjoying it endlessly, finding no difference between brightness and darkness in the presence of his good friends. As they approached the lower reaches of the Jiang River, about to enter Zhejiang territory, Jing'er suddenly looked grave and said, "The river deities here are quite obstructive; I cannot cross. One must wait for someone of great fortune, only then can the sail be safe." Gong questioned her about this, and she replied, "Wu Zixu and Fan Li, their divine power is well-known and not to be compared with other deities; I truly fear them." Gong did not believe her, and before they could hoist the sail, huge waves surged up, dark clouds covered the sky, and it was so dark that one could not see anyone. Gong was greatly frightened and decided to stay for five days. Just then, a small boat arrived, and Jing'er's face brightened and said, "Someone of great virtue has arrived, even more so than someone of great fortune. If you can share the boat with him, even a hundred river deities will not dare to harm us." Gong followed her advice, and as it happened, the next person to board the boat was a good match, so Gong encouraged the boatman to invite him on board.

    635

    然自此镜儿不复昼见，语龚曰：“此人孝子，诸天拥护。妾若肆然无忌，恐有不虞，当谨避之。”故续与龚共载一舟，究不知其携眷而游也。比入大江之中，波平浪静，如履康庄，数日遂抵杭。续将登岸，镜儿先告龚曰：“若人之父高僧也。妾至贵邑，土神尚虑不容，得师一言，为吾缓颊，庶可与子偕老。”龚又唯唯。因力请于续，偕往谒师。镜儿又嘱曰：“君之素箑，妾所化也，务置袖中，面师时妾自能言，切记不可儳说。”龚亦领诺。乃未入寺，师早知之，龚固不胜惶悚。幸侍者赍送片纸出，视之有十字，则“一切水土诸神不得拦阻”，宛如官府之勘合。

    Explanation

    635

    After the mirror spirit ceased to appear during the day, she spoke to Gong, saying, "This person is a filial son, protected by the heavens. If I were to act recklessly without restraint, I fear there might be unforeseen consequences, so I should be cautious and avoid it." Therefore, the spirit continued to share a boat with Gong, though it was unknown whether she was traveling with her family. When they entered the great river, the waves were calm and the journey was as smooth as traveling on a broad, level road, and after several days, they arrived at Hangzhou. As the spirit was about to disembark, she first told Gong, "Your father is a high-ranking monk. I am traveling to your prestigious city, and even the local deities may not accept me. If you can speak on my behalf to the master, perhaps we can live together until old age." Gong nodded in agreement. He then pleaded earnestly with the spirit to accompany him to visit the master. The spirit instructed him again, "The fan you carry is transformed by me; make sure to keep it in your sleeve. When facing the master, I will be able to speak for myself, but remember not to interrupt." Gong also agreed. Before they entered the temple, the master had already known this, and Gong was deeply anxious. Fortunately, the attendant brought out a piece of paper with ten characters, which read "All local deities shall not obstruct," resembling an official permit.

    636

    龚乃大悦，亟与镜儿归舟，果无扞格。至其家，托言季父为娶于京，戚里莫不信之。龚故早失怙恃，于是以镜儿主内政，颇能持家。且出金钱数万，为营田宅，龚以骤富。向未见其携一文，今乃取之不竭，亦可异已。是日龚为续言，极尽其详。薄暮始抵龚处，则门庭巍焕，俨然素封。揖续入，盛筵相款。镜儿所诞之子，岁已三周，携出见客，眉目之秀丽，迥异恒儿，由是可想见其母。饮至夜分，龚始别去，供帐之丰美，益不待言。翌日辞行，龚亦不再援止，惟曰：“道路辽阔，尊翁恐不克相值。还时祈再过我一叙。”续唯唯。

    Explanation

    636

    龚乃大悦，亟与镜儿归舟，果无扞格。至其家，托言季父为娶于京，戚里莫不信之。龚故早失怙恃，于是以镜儿主内政，颇能持家。且出金钱数万，为营田宅，龚以骤富。向未见其携一文，今乃取之不竭，亦可异已。是日龚为续言，极尽其详。薄暮始抵龚处，则门庭巍焕，俨然素封。揖续入，盛筵相款。镜儿所诞之子，岁已三周，携出见客，眉目之秀丽，迥异恒儿，由是可想见其母。饮至夜分，龚始别去，供帐之丰美，益不待言。翌日辞行，龚亦不再援止，惟曰：“道路辽阔，尊翁恐不克相值。还时祈再过我一叙。”续唯唯。

    Translation:
    Gong was greatly pleased and hurriedly returned to the boat with Jing'er, and indeed there was no obstacle. Upon reaching his home, he claimed that his younger brother had arranged for him to marry in the capital, and no one in the family doubted this. Gong had lost his parents at an early age, so Jing'er took charge of the household affairs and was quite capable of managing the home. Moreover, she spent tens of thousands of taels of silver to purchase land and property, making Gong suddenly wealthy. Previously, he had never been seen carrying even a single coin, but now he could draw on resources endlessly, which was also quite remarkable. That day, Gong spoke at length to Xù, going into great detail. It was late in the evening when they finally arrived at Gong's residence, where the courtyard was grand and splendid, resembling a wealthy family. They were greeted with a formal bow, and then invited inside for a sumptuous banquet. Jing'er's son, who was already three years old, was brought out to meet the guests. His beautiful eyes and eyebrows were strikingly different from those of ordinary children, allowing one to imagine the beauty of his mother. They drank until late at night, and then Gong took his leave, the luxurious furnishings and decorations of the household being even more impressive than words could describe. The next day, as Xù prepared to depart, Gong did not try to stop him again, but merely said, "The road is long, and it may be difficult for your respected father to meet us. When you return, please come by to visit me again." Xù nodded in agreement.

    637

    龚送之郭外，赠以百金，续不获辞，乃拜授。南行至海，不遇其父，遂悒怏旋归。复至龚家，龚适他出，仆承主妇奉以一袭，出之雪色晶莹，则一越玉如意也。仆又致词曰：“藉此略酬高厚，且取白华之义。但君宜遄归，否则，太翁未遇，而反失太母，则抱终天之恨矣。”续闻言甚惊，不及俟龚，兼程而返。归至家，其母果在床褥，病已危笃。见续归，一笑而逝。续始服镜儿之先见，每向人称述其异，闻者咸骇异。后龚有书来言，因镜儿惮于津梁，遂不复求仕进，优游畎亩，以尽天年。独本一之信杳然，意者雪山成道，已归忉利之天。续虽至孝，竟无由偕之以昇，不亦人生一大憾事耶？

    　　外史氏曰：闲常观剧，至《雷峰塔》传奇，事虽不经，而每恨法海老髡败人清兴。及闻此事，老和尚甚谙人情，当是第一尊活佛出世。而究其源，本实出于儒，乃有此恻隐仁恕之心。不然，心既定矣，又乌知镜儿之为镜儿，为成此一段奇缘，使之流传千古也哉？

    Explanation

    637

    Gong sent him to the outskirts of the city and gave him a hundred gold pieces. Xu could not refuse, so he bowed and accepted. He traveled south to the sea but did not find his father, and thus returned in disappointment. When he returned to Gong's house, Gong had just left, and the servant received the lady's gift of a robe, which was snow-white and crystal-clear, and it turned out to be a jade如意 (auspicious object) from Yue. The servant then said, "This is a small token to repay your great kindness, and it also symbolizes the purity of white flowers. But you should return quickly, otherwise, if you do not meet your grandfather, you will lose your grandmother, and you will carry a lifelong regret." Xu was very surprised upon hearing this and did not wait for Gong, but hurried back. Upon returning home, he found his mother lying on the bed, already critically ill. She smiled and passed away when she saw Xu return. Xu then believed in Mirror's foresight and often told others about this extraordinary event, and those who heard it were all amazed. Later, Gong wrote a letter saying that because Mirror was afraid of crossing the river, he no longer sought official positions, but lived a peaceful life in the fields until the end of his days. Only the news of Benyi was mysterious; perhaps he had achieved enlightenment on Mount Snow and had ascended to the Trāyastriṃśa Heaven. Although Xu was filial, he could not accompany him to ascend, which is indeed a great regret in life.

    The historian said: When watching plays, I often come across the legend of the Leifeng Pagoda, which, though not factual, often leaves me annoyed by the old monk Faha spoiling the mood. Upon hearing this story, I found that the old monk was very understanding of human emotions, as if he were a living Buddha. But when we trace the origin, it is rooted in Confucianism, which has this heart of compassion, benevolence, and forgiveness. Otherwise, if his heart was already set, how would he know that Mirror was Mirror, to create this extraordinary bond and make it last for a thousand years?

    638

    随园老人曰：两事迥不相谋，而合成一片，几如无缝天衣。高僧孝子传中，乃得此旖旎文字，足称奇观，不独奇事。

    ○　翠微娘子
    　　时有跛叟，精医术，应手如神，所活者难更仆数。生二子，甲已授室，乙尚孑然。叟卒，甲纳妇言，逐其弟，不与同居，乙怒控之于官。甲之岳家又巨族，为之苞苴，官竟不直乙，斥为傲弟，笞之十数。乙愈不能平，夜挟白刃，将往杀兄而兼屠其嫂。行及闾侧，见其父策杖而来，厉声呵之曰：“畜产意欲何为？岂丈夫竟不克自立耶？汝父幸有薄积，汝等乃阋墙至此，倘无立锥，若又将如何？”乙见父大恸，哭拜于地，呜咽不能言。叟抚其背曰：“儿勿悲。西去数百里，有翠微娘子者，曾受我再生之恩。汝往依之，当不止于温饱也。”言已不见。乙乃挥泪而归，不再做拚生之想。明日即襆被而行，亦不往辞兄嫂，即兄嫂亦不意其有所之。

    Explanation

    638

    The Old Man of Su Yuan said: "Two matters that are completely unrelated yet form a single entity, almost like a seamless robe. In the biographies of high monks and filial sons, one finds such graceful and beautiful writing, which is indeed a marvel, not just a strange event."

    ○ The Lady Cuimui

    At that time, there was an old man who was lame but skilled in medicine, his hands working like magic, saving countless lives. He had two sons; the elder, Jia, had already married, while the younger, Yi, remained unmarried. After the old man's death, Jia, influenced by his wife's advice, expelled his younger brother and refused to live with him. Yi, filled with anger, took the matter to the authorities. Jia's in-laws were a powerful family, and they bribed officials, resulting in the judge siding against Yi, scolding him as an arrogant younger brother and flogging him several times. Yi became even more incensed, preparing to go at night with a sharp blade to kill his elder brother and his sister-in-law. As he walked up to the gate of the neighborhood, he saw his father coming with a walking stick, scolding him loudly, "What do you, a lowly creature, intend to do? How can a man not be able to stand on his own? Fortunately, I have a small savings, but you two have quarreled like this within the family. If we had no place to stand, what would you do then?" Yi, seeing his father, wept bitterly, fell to the ground in tears, and could not speak. The old man patted his back and said, "Do not grieve, my son. Go west for hundreds of miles, and there is a Lady Cuimui who once received my life-saving grace. You should go to her, and you will not just find warmth and food." After speaking, he disappeared. Yi then returned home with tears, no longer thinking of taking his own life. The next day, he packed his bedding and left, not bidding farewell to his brother and sister-in-law, and even they did not expect him to go anywhere.

    639

    乙行数日，询之人，咸不识翠微娘子为谁。乙亦自谓父诳己，天下必无是人，遂止于旅邸，不复前。然已资斧告竭，进退维谷矣。正仓皇间，闻旅人相语曰：“明夕宿处，当有剧可观，盍遄行。”因盛称其声容，洋洋盈耳。乙时方抱闷，顿思一往，以畅郁怀。乃复早旦启行，跋涉又将百里。及至其处，天尚未暝，亦不谋所栖止，先往观焉。适演《千金记》，霸王挥戈，韩侯命将，场上几无隙地。而钲鼓雷鸣，敖曹聒耳，颇可泄人幽郁。乙鹄立众中，看至终场。甫思往觅邮亭，忽一人揖而语之曰：“郎非跛翁之子乎？翠微娘子固命予相俟久矣！”乙大喜，实出望外。视其人，青衣矮帽，状若仆人。亦不暇细询，惟诘曰：“娘子安在？予实奉老父命敬来晋谒者。

    Explanation

    639

    After several days of travel, the protagonist (乙) asked people, but everyone did not know who Lady Cuiwei was. He also believed his father had deceived him, thinking there must be no such person in the world. So he stopped at an inn and did not continue forward. However, his funds were exhausted, and he was in a difficult situation, unable to advance or retreat. In the midst of his distress, he heard travelers talking to each other, saying, "Tomorrow night's lodging place will have something worth seeing. Why not hurry on?" They highly praised its appearance and performance, and the sound filled his ears. He was feeling depressed at the time, but suddenly thought of going to relieve his inner frustration. So he set out again early the next morning and traveled another hundred miles. When he arrived there, the sky had not yet darkened, and he also did not plan where to stay. He first went to watch.
