Just then, they were performing the Qianjin Ji. The Overlord (Xiang Yu) was wielding his halberd, and the Marquis of Han (Han Xin) was commanding his generals. There was hardly any empty space on the stage. The drums and gongs sounded like thunder, and the loud noise was deafening. It was quite good at relieving one's inner frustration. He stood like a crane among the crowd and watched until the end of the performance. Just as he thought of going to find a post station, suddenly a person bowed and said to him, "Are you not the son of the lame old man? Lady Cuiwei has indeed ordered me to wait for you for a long time!" He was greatly delighted, truly beyond his expectations. He looked at the person, who was wearing a blue robe and a short hat, looking like a servant. He also did not have time to ask in detail, but he asked, "Where is Lady Cuiwei? I am here to respectfully pay my respects on behalf of my old father."

    640

    ”其人亦大悦，请与偕行。由村后约行半里许，别有巨宅，闬闳高峻，栋宇巍峨。门外立健卒十余，皆披坚执锐，环立森严。又有小卤簿，幢幡杂色，云軿攒花，咸云娘子将往赴盛会。乙遂震竦不敢前，其人先入禀白。有顷复出，遽拜伏不起，谢曰：“娘子未有明命，我辈小人擅行侪伍礼，冒渎尊严，恕之幸甚。”乙愕然，罔知所措，姑颔之。其人又跪白曰：“娘子适将他往，乘舆已驾，不能因佳客弗行。请暂居别馆，即进晚餐。娘子去当亟返也。”遂导乙入门。曲折至一院，华屋严整，围以崇垣。其中灯火之辉煌，铺陈之富丽，非巨家莫能有。内设一绳床，锦裀累寸。其人延乙坐，乙觉足下温软，不类砖石。

    Explanation

    640

    The person was greatly pleased and asked to accompany him. After traveling about half a li from the village, they came upon a grand mansion with imposing gates and towering buildings. Outside the door stood more than ten armed guards, all equipped with weapons, standing in a strict and orderly formation. There was also a small entourage, with colorful flags and decorated carriages adorned with flowers. Everyone said that the lady was going to attend a grand gathering. The person became frightened and dared not proceed. He went in to report. After a short while, he came out again, prostrated himself, and did not rise, saying, "The lady has not given a clear command. We, mere commoners, have acted on our own, committing an act of disrespect. Please forgive us." The person was surprised and at a loss, nodding in response. The person then knelt and reported, "The lady is about to go elsewhere, and her carriage is already prepared. She cannot stay for the guest. Please stay in a separate hall and have dinner. The lady will return soon." He then led the person inside. They took a winding path to a courtyard, where elegant and well-organized buildings were surrounded by high walls. The brilliance of the lights and the opulence of the decorations were such that only a wealthy household could afford them. Inside, there was a rope bed with a thickly padded mat. The person invited the guest to sit, and the guest felt the warmth and softness of the floor, which was unlike stone.

    641

    俯视之，则以氍毹饰地，五色灿然，益惊诧不已。未几，车马喧嚣，辘辘远听，娘子已出游矣。乙坐未久，即有妖鬟十数，咸来叩参，而前之接引者，遂亦辞出。比及进食，侍婢益伙，且作乐于阶下，箫管悠扬，不知何曲。席前燃巨烛，每供一馔，婢辄报其名，为数甚多，备极珍错之品。乙至此反若无下箸处，在己亦不觉莞然。乃乙少从天戒，不善饮，略进一盫，即已微醺，遂命饭，食之。至饱，甫起离席，闻群婢哗曰：“娘子归矣！”又许时，有人来邀客曰：“娘子请相见。”乙从之行，由院落而东，曲廊宛转，花径萦回，良久始达内第。皆烛燃纱笼，而光明如昼，第中一花一木，无不瞭如观掌。己而深入闺阁，益更气象，香雾空濛，灯光辉映。巨室五楹，遍垂绣幌，其阶以美石为之。

    Explanation

    641

    Looking down, he saw that the ground was decorated with a colorful carpet, the five colors shining brightly, and he was increasingly astonished. Soon after, the sound of carriages and horses became noisy, the distant rumbling could be heard, and the lady had already gone out. Bǐ had not been sitting for long when a dozen beautiful maids came to pay respects, and the one who had introduced before then also left. By the time of eating, the number of maids increased, and music was played downstairs, the flutes and pipes were melodious, but he did not know what tune it was. A large candle was lit in front of the table, for each dish served, the maid would announce its name, and there were many dishes, all kinds of precious and rare delicacies. Bǐ felt as if there was nowhere to put the chopsticks, and he himself could not help but smile slightly. Bǐ slightly followed heavenly discipline, not being good at drinking, drank a small cup, and became slightly tipsy, then ordered food and ate it. Until full, he just got up and left the seat, hearing the maids shouting, "The lady has returned!" After a while, someone came to invite the guest, "The lady requests to meet." Bǐ followed along, from the courtyard to the east, through winding corridors and flower paths, after a long time, finally reached the inner quarters. All candles were lit in silk lanterns, and the lighting was bright as day, every flower and tree in the residence was clear as seeing one's palm. Deep into the inner chambers, even more impressive, fragrant mist, lights shining. A large room with five bays, decorated with embroidered curtains, the steps were made of beautiful stone.

    642

    朱阑旋绕，两庑尽挂珠灯，可以鉴人眉目。乙未入，娘子早逆于帘外，嘤咛而言曰：“前承阿翁不弃，以宝钗为聘，许主盬蘩。妾守此盟已久，而君竟未辱临，几令妾有白头之叹。今幸惠然肯来，愿无废阿翁成命。”乙茫然，都不解为何语。惟凝睇，娘子美如画图，冠五凤之冠，衣七宝之衣，装束一如仙子。且云翁意，吻合叟言，遂唯唯升阶，径入其室。呆立四望，铺设俱新，而光华奇异，举不能识其物。中楹设沉香小榻，娘子逊乙对坐，又谢曰：“适为本境诸官长以妙部见召，猝遇郎至，又不获辞，是以未即抠迎，亵慢之愆，谅亦鉴原。”乙复唯唯，莫措一词，侍儿皆掩口窃笑。居无何，自外传报曰：“土谷诸神咸集堂上，候见新贵人。

    Explanation

    642

    The red railing wound around, and the side halls were fully adorned with pearl lamps, which could clearly reflect people's facial features. When 乙未 entered, the lady was already waiting outside the curtain, speaking in a soft and gentle voice: "Before, my father-in-law did not disdain me, and with the betrothal gift of the hairpin, he promised me to you, entrusting me with household duties. I have been waiting for this alliance for a long time, but you have not yet honored me with your presence, almost causing me to lament my lifelong solitude. Now that you have kindly come, I hope you will not disappoint my father-in-law's wishes." 乙未 was bewildered and did not understand her words. He only stared intently, and the lady was as beautiful as a painting, wearing a crown adorned with five phoenixes and a robe embellished with seven treasures, dressed like a celestial being. She also mentioned that her father's intentions matched the old man's words, so he obediently ascended the steps and directly entered the room. He stood still and looked around, seeing that everything was newly arranged and adorned with unusual and radiant objects, none of which he could identify. In the center of the room, a small couch made of sandalwood was placed, and the lady invited 乙未 to sit opposite her. She then apologized: "Just now, I was summoned by the local officials to perform in a special department, and I unexpectedly encountered you, so I could not immediately greet you. I hope you will forgive any offense caused by my absence." 乙未 again nodded in agreement, unable to utter a word, as the maidservants all covered their mouths and laughed quietly. Soon after, a messenger announced from outside: "The various deities of earth and grain have gathered in the hall, waiting to meet the newly arrived noble person."

    643

    ”乙始大骇，娘子乃起曰：“君勿惊疑，妾因相礼无人，特语诸公，愿来相助。此皆翁之友执，君当以礼谒见。”即命诸婢中一齿长者曰：“汝引郎去，薰沐更衣，少习揖逊之节。予出前庭陪侍众宾，少顷即来相请。”婢领诺，导乙入屏后。转折至一室，排闼而入，香气蒸腾，兰汤馥郁。婢侍乙解衣，见其私，不禁胡卢曰：“乡间人果具雄材，但何不雅之甚？”乙亦为之失笑。浴竟，婢进衣履，皆华美异常。乙著之如负芒刺，久而始安。婢又引至内堂，俾乙习礼，口授而身示之。至再至三，然后步之促者微缓，腰之劲者稍柔，婢抚掌曰：“此亦可谓风流佳婿矣！”于是群婢观者皆笑不能仰。仪节既娴，漏下三鼓，娘子已命人速之数四，众婢乃簇乙出。

    Explanation

    643

    乙起初大为惊骇，娘子起身说道：“您不必惊慌，因为没有合适的人来主持礼仪，所以我特别告诉各位，希望他们能来帮忙。这些人都是您父亲的朋友，您应当按照礼仪向他们行礼。”随即命令众婢女中一位年长者说：“你带这位郎君去，让他沐浴更衣，稍加练习行礼的礼仪。我出去前厅陪侍各位宾客，一会儿就来请您。”婢女答应后，领着乙进入屏风后面。转过几道弯后进入一间房，打开门，香气蒸腾，兰汤的香味浓郁。婢女为乙脱衣，看到他的私处，不禁笑道：“乡下人果然有雄才，只是为何如此不雅？”乙也失笑。沐浴完毕，婢女拿来衣履，都非常华丽。乙穿上后感觉像背着芒刺，过了很久才适应。婢女又领着他来到内堂，让他练习礼仪，口授并亲自示范。练习了两次三次之后，步伐稍微放慢，腰身也显得柔和了些，婢女拍手称赞道：“这也可以算是个风流佳婿了！”于是众婢女观看的人都笑得前仰后合。礼仪娴熟后，漏下三鼓，娘子已经让人催促了好几次，众婢女才簇拥着乙出来。

    644

    厅事以上，红烛高烧，乙亦不遑谛视。娘子命与众宾相晤，其衣冠亦类时人，绝无所谓簪绅袍笏者。凡四人，娘子一一指示，一司农，一田祖，其二则社神山神也。众神少致款曲，即请乙与娘子并立，细乐迭奏，交拜成礼。宾即造退，娘子亦不款留，惟送至檐际曰：“暮夜不恭，诘朝再当申谢。”宾既尽去，娘子始偕乙入至向者相见之室。东侧一楹，早已肆筵相候。乙觉深邃胜前，而华缛尤准名状。帐以鲛绡，席以蜀锦。衾褥之香软，兰桂之氤氲，实属人间天上焉。少饮卺卮，婢即撤去，娘子忽娇羞无主。乙强之入帷，缓松绣带，既已云低，及解里裳，不禁玉软，乃匆匆先入衾内，乙亦去衣就枕。方将好合，俄闻群婢大噪曰：“妖将入室。

    Explanation

    644

    Above the main hall, red candles burned brightly, and Bǐ also did not have time to scrutinize closely. The lady ordered the guests to meet each other, and their attire was similar to contemporary people, with absolutely no scholars in official robes and hats. There were four people, and the lady pointed them out one by one: one was a granary official, one was a field god, and the other two were village gods and mountain gods. The gods briefly paid their respects, then asked Bǐ and the lady to stand together. Soft music played in succession, and they bowed to each other, completing the ceremony. The guests then left, and the lady did not detain them, only sending them to the eaves and saying, "It is disrespectful to meet at night; we will formally apologize tomorrow morning." Once the guests had all left, the lady then accompanied Bǐ into the room where they had met before. On the east side, a room had already been prepared with a spread. Bǐ felt that the place was deeper and more profound than before, and the luxury and splendor were even more fitting to describe. The canopy was made of鲛绡 (a fine silk), and the mat was made of蜀锦 (Sichuan brocade). The bedding was fragrant and soft, and the air was filled with the scent of orchids and cassia, truly a heavenly place on earth. They drank a little from the卺卮 (a ceremonial cup), and the maid immediately took it away. The lady suddenly became shy and helpless. Bǐ urged her into the curtains and slowly loosened the embroidered belt. Once the clouds had descended, when she undressed, she could not help but feel soft and delicate. She hurriedly got into bed first, and Bǐ also took off his clothes and lay down. Just as they were about to consummate, suddenly the maids began to shout loudly, saying, "A monster is entering the room."

    645

    ”一时啼声、号声、哀哭声、乞命声纷沓嘈杂，由远而近。乙大惊，将起奔逸，既不忍舍此丽姿，娘子又牢握其臂，战战无片言。乙不得已，白身负之而走。娘子始呼曰：“左之！”乙遂趋左。娘子又曰：“右之！”乙更趋右。幸室中皆有门户，湾环而出。至一园，别设双扉，乙启之出，则菁葱遍野，对面似有冈峦。娘子犹曰：“行！行！”乙乃力负娘子，升彼大阜而后止。回望第中，赤焰烛天，且格斗之声遥闻数里。娘子垂涕曰：“妮子辈皆为我死矣！”乙惊定，始诘其由。答曰：“妾非生人，实本省城隍之第三女也。父生时曾任贵邑，阖家病疫，而妾尤危笃，赖阿翁投以良剂，得以全活。此德亦时铭肺腑。及父升任某处，经过大江，遭水厄，舟覆俱溺。上帝念忠直，且殁于王事，俾得为神。

    Explanation

    645

    At first, there was a cacophony of cries, shouts, laments, and pleas for mercy, coming closer from afar. Man B was greatly alarmed and was about to flee, but he could not bear to leave this beautiful woman. The lady tightly held his arm, trembling without uttering a word. Man B had no choice but to carry her away on his back. The lady then called out, "Left!" Man B turned left. The lady said again, "Right!" Man B turned right. Fortunately, the house had many doors and passages, allowing them to escape in a winding manner. They reached a garden with a separate set of double doors, which Man B opened to exit. The garden was lush with greenery, and in the distance, there seemed to be hills. The lady urged, "Go! Go!" Man B then carried her up a hill and stopped. Looking back at the house, he saw flames lighting up the sky, and the sounds of fighting could be heard from several miles away. The lady shed tears and said, "My servants have all died for me!" Man B, after recovering from his shock, began to question her about the reason for this. She replied, "I am not a living person; I am the third daughter of the provincial city god. My father once served in your county, and the entire family was afflicted with an epidemic, with me being particularly gravely ill. My father saved me with his good medicine. This kindness is always etched in my heart. When my father was promoted to another position, he passed through a river and encountered a disaster where the boat capsized and everyone drowned. The gods, recognizing his loyalty and service, made him a deity after his death."

    646

    而妾又以生前好道，邂逅一女冠，授以伏狐诸术，因承父命，镇抚此方，使群妖不得逞。妾之俾御，皆狐也。其所供具，亦狐为妾致之，今竟为妖所据，情实不甘。”乙又询曰：“妖何为者？独不畏子乎？”答曰：“是亦一狐，无怪乎君子相诮也。此妖盘踞山谷，千有余龄，道根深重。妾至此，群小慑伏，而渠独强项，且大言欲求予父，附为婚姻。妾闻而益怒，治之以法，尚未帖然。适尊翁过北，以宝钗为君纳聘，言君不日当来。妾感生死之恩，顿忘忌讳，驰白父母，预治喜筵。不意此妖乃抵隙而来，大肆猖獗。然亦行当歼灭矣。”乙因询以己父之所在，娘子曰：“阿翁为某邑社神，前已赴任，无须思念。

    Explanation

    646

    The protagonist, speaking humbly, said, "I, before my death, was fond of the Dao and chanced upon a female Taoist priest who taught me various techniques for taming foxes. I then took on my father's command to pacify this region, preventing the demons from causing trouble. All that I control are foxes, and the provisions I receive are also provided by the foxes. Now, an evil spirit has taken over, and I truly feel aggrieved."

    The other character asked, "What is this evil spirit? Does it not fear you?" The protagonist replied, "It is also a fox. There is nothing surprising about this, as gentlemen might mock. This spirit has been entrenched in the mountains for over a thousand years, with deep roots in the Dao. When I arrived, all the minor spirits were subdued, but it alone was defiant and boldly declared its intention to seek my father and become part of our family."

    The protagonist continued, "Upon hearing this, I became even angrier and tried to deal with it according to the law, but it was not yet tamed. Coincidentally, my father passed through the north and used a hairpin to betroth you, saying that you would soon arrive. Moved by the gratitude of life and death, I forgot my reservations and hurriedly informed my parents, preparing a wedding feast. Unbeknownst to me, this evil spirit took advantage of the opportunity and acted wildly."

    The protagonist concluded, "However, it will also be annihilated soon." The other character then asked about the location of his father, and the protagonist replied, "My father is the local deity of a certain village and has already taken up his post. There is no need to worry about him."

    647

    ”语未已，忽闻空中言曰：“阿父知姊遭盗劫，业已委员擒治之。但阿姊既堕欲界，不合为神，可与郎同返乡井，创立家室，无负跛翁之雅。”语竟，有一袭凭虚掷下，且笑曰：“无衣无褐，何以卒岁？予不耐与裸裎者相对，亦从此逝矣。”乙大怖，叩之娘子，则其妹玉华小姑也。娘子因笑曰：“为郎絮絮聒人，浑忘却两无寸缕。今为妹见，异日相遭当惭赧死。”遂启袭出衣，分著之。天方昧爽，娘子谓乙曰：“去之！此地已无片瓦，不足相依，请与郎归故土。”乃把袂欲行。乙虽得妇：而悼其失家，并虑己之无家，勉从之行。未数武，娘子曰：“足力有几，恐来能达，非代步不可。”指顾田间，已有双卫奔突而出，控坐俱全。娘子命乙共乘之，其驰如风，转瞬且数百里，而邑门在望矣。

    Explanation

    647

    While the words were still being spoken, suddenly a voice from the air said, "Father knows that your sister has been robbed, and has already appointed officials to capture and punish them. But your sister has already fallen into the realm of desire and is not suitable to be a god. She can go back home with the young man and establish a family, without disappointing the noble intention of the lame old man." The voice then said, "Without clothes or furs, how can one survive the year? I cannot bear to be with someone who is naked, and so I will depart as well." B felt greatly terrified and asked the lady, who replied, "It is her younger sister, Yu Hua, the younger daughter-in-law." The lady then said, "Talking to the young man endlessly and annoyingly, I completely forgot that we have no clothes. Now that I have seen her, I will be ashamed to meet her again in the future." She then opened the bundle and took out the clothes, dividing them to wear. The sky was just beginning to lighten, and the lady said to B, "Go! This place has no roof left, and it is not a place we can rely on. Please go with the young man back to your ancestral home." She then took his arm and was about to leave. B, though he had gained a wife, mourned the loss of his home and worried about not having a home himself, so he reluctantly followed her. Not far down the road, the lady said, "How much stamina do you have? I fear we cannot reach our destination, and we will need a代步不可." She pointed to the fields, and two horses suddenly appeared, fully saddled and ready to ride. The lady instructed B to ride with her, and they galloped like the wind, covering hundreds of miles in an instant, with the city gate in sight.

    648

    乙甚骇然。及至外郭，娘子止之曰：“此地不城不村，尽可卜居，无须前。”因弃骑相与胥宇。路侧有一家屋，为雨坏，人尽他徙。娘子曰：“可矣。”入视之，却席地幕天，唯四壁岿然屹立，乙不觉大噱。娘子正色曰：“是可栖也。候吾婢来，固居然轮奂矣。”乃强乙售驴于市，得数金以供朝炊。比及乙归，其室倏改观。虽无昔之壮丽，亦修洁非故，乙乃悦。入则果有二婢，侍于娘子之侧，第皆缟衣綦巾，无复向之妖艳。询以往事，同对曰：“妖已殄灭，我辈不舍娘子，故跟寻至此。余人仍居故宅，幸无恙。”乙遂与娘子对食，笑语欢然。及夜定情，则床帐卧具，崭然新制，亦不解其从来。晨起，娘子语乙，使觅屋主，以百金易其产。

    Explanation

    648

    Yi was very startled. Upon reaching the outer city, the lady stopped him and said, "This place is neither a town nor a village; it is completely suitable for settling, and there is no need to go forward." So they gave up riding and looked for a place to live together. By the side of the road, there was a house that had been damaged by rain, and all the people had moved elsewhere. The lady said, "This will do." They entered and saw that it was only the four walls that remained, empty and bare. Yi couldn't help but laugh loudly. The lady said seriously, "This is suitable for living. Wait for my servant to come, and it will naturally become splendid." She then forced Yi to sell his donkey in the market, and they got some money to provide for the morning meal. By the time Yi returned, his room suddenly changed appearance. Although it lacked the former grandeur, it was also clean and tidy, unlike before, and Yi then became happy. Upon entering, there were indeed two servants serving beside the lady, but they were all dressed in plain clothes and headscarves, no longer as beautiful as before. When Yi asked about past events, they answered together, "The demons have been eliminated. We do not want to leave the lady, so we followed her here. The others still live in the original house, fortunately, nothing is wrong." Yi then ate with the lady, laughing and chatting happily. By night, when they decided their relationship, the bed, bedding, and other furnishings were all newly made, and Yi did not understand where they came from. In the morning, the lady told Yi to find the owner of the house, and they bought the property for a hundred gold coins.

    649

    屋主乐从，娘子乃鸠工建木，营造一新，凡匝月而就。然梓匠虽司其事，而结构之奇，落成之速，举莫能测其由。娘子出入无异恒人，始亦荆钗裙布，与乙指画于其间，人视之，故一田家少妇也。洎乎第宅已成，则深居邃处，美食华衣，仆婢以百计，食指日繁，而既不求田，又不问舍，乃取之而裕如，即乙亦窃神之。初甲知乙返，且闻其娶于外乡，与妇皆笑之。既而闻其置产，乃以为奇。数月阖邑喧传，咸称巨富，遂且信且疑。谋之于妇，因假馈问之名，使婢往探之。婢去竟日始归，告其主曰：“奉命往侦二娘子，比至其门，阍者禁不许入。乃白主人姓字，阍者恍然曰：‘主人之兄是也。’令婢子少候，往返再三，始得入。其屋宇之华，什倍于我，役使之众，亦什倍于我。

    Explanation

    649

    The house owner happily complied, and the lady gathered workers to build wooden structures, creating something entirely new, which took one full month to complete. However, though the carpenters were in charge of the task, the unusual structure and rapid completion were such that no one could understand how it was done. The lady went in and out without any difference from ordinary people; initially, she wore simple wooden hairpins and cloth skirts, and discussed plans with someone named "乙" during the process. People saw her as a young wife from a farming family. When the residence was completed, she lived in a secluded area, enjoying fine food and clothing, with hundreds of servants and an increasing number of dependents. She neither sought land nor asked about a place to stay, but took everything abundantly, and even "乙" secretly admired her. Initially, "甲" knew that "乙" had returned and heard that he had married a woman from another village, and they both laughed at him. Later, when they heard that "乙" had acquired property, they found it extraordinary. After several months, the whole town was abuzz with the news, and everyone called him a great wealthy man, and they both believed and doubted it. They discussed it with their wife and sent a maid to investigate under the pretext of delivering food. The maid went and did not return until the end of the day. She reported to her master: "I was sent to investigate the second lady. Upon arriving at her door, the gatekeeper forbade me from entering. I then told him the name of the owner, and he suddenly said, 'This is the master's brother.' He asked me to wait a moment, and after going back and forth several times, I was finally allowed in. The grandeur of her house was ten times greater than ours, and the number of servants she employed was also ten times greater than ours."

    650

    入见，二娘子方坐一榻，用绿帕引白雪盠儿，闲戏于红裀之上。见婢子即笑曰：‘阿姆亦太费心，远迢迢使将若来，殆思窥我夫妇也。’婢子固再拜，敬陈主人意，二娘子乃不复言。婢子窃窥玉貌，邑中实少其俦，而衣饰之艳，又目所未睹。忽失声曰：‘阿叔大好福，竟得偶天上人。若娶于桑梓，曾何有耶？’二娘子色似甚喜，因留婢子饮食。其肴馔之精美，举不识为何物。饭讫，叔自外来，迥非昔比。其容阔绰而丰美，其衣轻软而光华。从者皆小童，其年约十三四，鲜衣秀容，及至中门，俱散去。叔见婢子，若不相识，婢子乃亟起拜见。

    Explanation

    650

    Upon entering, the second lady was seated on a couch, using a green handkerchief to play with a young girl named Bai Xue, who was leisurely playing on a red mat. Seeing the maid, she smiled and said, "Aunt, you've been too considerate, sending her all the way here from afar, probably thinking of peeping into my husband and wife." The maid bowed twice and respectfully conveyed the master's intentions. The second lady then remained silent. The maid secretly admired her beauty, noting that there were few like her in the town, and her attire was so exquisite that it had never been seen before. She suddenly exclaimed, "Uncle has such great good fortune, to meet a heavenly being. If he were to marry someone from his hometown, what could compare to this?" The second lady seemed very pleased and invited the maid to join them for a meal. The delicacies served were so exquisite that none of them knew what they were. After finishing the meal, the uncle returned from outside, looking quite different from before. His appearance was spacious and beautiful, and his clothing was light, soft, and radiant. His companions were all young boys, around thirteen or fourteen years old, dressed in bright and colorful clothes. Upon reaching the main gate, they all dispersed. The uncle saw the maid as if he did not recognize her, and the maid quickly rose to greet him.

    651

    二娘又从旁语之，叔哂曰：‘兄富翁，嫂贵家，何劳惠顾于我？昔者数椽之屋，既不我容，今日盘飧之馈胡为见赐？岂谓苏季子终于贫贱耶？请即璧还，无劳驱斥。’言之色甚怒。二娘子止之曰：‘君勿尔尔！若论兄嫂之前情，理宜请绝，但阿翁之像在焉。君为人子，既婚而未告父母；妾为人妇，既嫁而未谒舅姑；此心不免歉然。方将随君庙见，以全大礼，今竟杜绝往来，能不令九泉心恫乎？’叔乃默然，其色亦少解。以婢子观之，亦如我家伉俪焉。叔始笑谓婢子曰：‘视余家所有者，较之兄嫂为何如？’婢子因极口赞羡，叔亦大悦，其留婢子盘桓，乃得遍览其居处。屋宇百余，有园亭足以娱目。至暮又赐酒食，始令婢子归。临行叔语曰：‘为告兄嫂，三日后当率新妇以往也。’”婢既缕缕述之，甲与妇皆惊异，意其必来，乃遍邀戚族，张乐设饮。

    Explanation

    651

    The younger wife again spoke to him from the side, and the younger brother smiled and said, "My elder brother is a wealthy man, and my elder sister-in-law comes from a noble family. Why should you bother to visit me? In the past, a small house could not accommodate me, so why am I given food today? Do you think Su Qin will end up poor and lowly? Please return it like a jade disc, and do not trouble me further." His words were spoken with a very angry expression. The younger wife stopped him and said, "Do not act like that! If we consider the past relationship between my brother and sister-in-law, it would be reasonable to sever ties, but the image of our father-in-law is still here. As a son, you have married but did not inform your parents; as a wife, I have married but did not visit my in-laws. This heart cannot help but feel guilty. We are about to follow you to the temple to complete the formalities. Now, if we completely cut off contact, how can it not make our ancestors in the netherworld grieve?" The younger brother then remained silent, and his expression also slightly relaxed. From the maid's perspective, it was also like our own couple. The younger brother began to smile and said to the maid, "Compare what my family has to what my brother and sister-in-law have." The maid then enthusiastically praised and envied it, and the younger brother was also very pleased. He kept the maid around so that she could see his entire residence. There were over a hundred buildings, with gardens and pavilions enough to please the eyes. Until evening, he was given wine and food again. Finally, he ordered the maid to return. On the way out, the younger brother said, "Tell my brother and sister-in-law that three days later, I will take my new wife there." The maid then recounted it in detail, and the man and his wife were both surprised, thinking he would definitely come. They then invited all relatives, set up music, and prepared a feast.

    652

    至日拱伺于门外，俄见骏马香车，倡随而至，男妇从者数十人。及门，娘子降舆先入，见者皆诧以为异，目为神仙。乙见兄，弃乘拜谒，甲不觉惭然，把手偕行。乙遂与妇参谒遗象。乙顿触心事，大痛无声，良久，始哭而起。娘子又参诸尊长，具贽见之仪，珠绣凡十余箧。又别馈兄嫂，甲与妇赧容受之。事已，娘子即告乙曰：“新妇素娇贵，不耐烦剧，愿先归去。”乙即听之。甲妇亦挽留不住，登车而去。乙亦不再流连，杯卮弗沾，辞别遗象，与妇俱返。因而里党之众，皆不直甲，议论汹汹，夫妇几置身无地。乙归，娘子即言曰：“今日之举，足慰阿翁之心，而吐郎君之气矣。然世人少见多怪，群疑满腹，妾与君不可复居于此。异日可致兄嫂来，以此区区者畀之。

    Explanation

    652

    On a certain day, one stood in respectful anticipation outside the door. Soon, a fine horse and a fragrant carriage appeared, followed by a procession of several dozen men and women. Upon reaching the door, the lady alighted from the carriage first and entered. Those who saw her were all astonished and thought it strange, regarding her as a fairy or immortal. Seeing his brother, the man abandoned his carriage and paid respects. Jia felt ashamed, took her hand, and walked with her. He then accompanied his wife to pay respects to the ancestral portraits. The man suddenly touched on a matter of the heart, greatly grieved in silence. For a long time, he finally cried and stood up. The lady then paid respects to the elders, presenting a formal offering with numerous chests of pearls and embroidered items. She also gave separate gifts to his brother and sister-in-law, whom Jia and his wife received with a flushed face. After the ceremony, the lady told the man, "I have always been delicate and noble, and cannot tolerate much activity. I wish to return first." The man agreed to her request. Jia's wife also could not persuade the lady to stay, and she mounted the carriage and left. The man did not linger, did not touch the cups, bid farewell to the ancestral portraits, and returned with his wife. Consequently, the people of the neighborhood all held a grudge against Jia, engaging in loud and heated discussion, leaving the couple feeling as if they had no place to stand. After returning home, the man said to the lady, "Today's action sufficiently comforted the old man's heart and vented the young master's frustration. However, the world is full of people who see little and are easily surprised, full of doubts. We cannot continue to live here. One day, you can bring your brother and sister-in-law here and give this small offering to them."

    653

    妾请与君遨游六合，效陶朱之事，君以为何如？”乙亦欣然愿从，遂折柬邀甲及其妇。果偕来，乙亦治具相款。酒酣，乙起曰：“新妇实仙人，不愿久居尘浊，弟将从之远游。敢以此中之所有，为兄嫂寿，幸勿固辞。”甲与妇遂愕然，因坚留之。乙遽出白刃，长尺许，掷地有声，慨然曰：“微父之慈，此刃已饱兄嫂之血。”因娓娓详言，悉其颠末。甲与妇俱惴惴不能出声。娘子即谓乙曰：“装束矣！”乙乃长笑与娘子同出，从以向者二婢，共四骑，南向而驰，不知所往。甲视其所居，虽无金贝，而仓箱之富，服物之华，犹不下千镒，遂徙而处之。乙之藏获，咸归于甲。及生子不肖，专务游荡，夫妇俱怄气而死。子遂售其宅于人，仍复业医，家以粗给。人以为跛翁之遗泽，犹未泯焉耳！

    Explanation

    653

    The wife said, "I propose we travel the world together, emulating the life of Tao Zhu Gong, what do you think?" Jia was delighted and agreed. He then sent an invitation to Jia and his wife. They indeed came, and Jia prepared a feast to entertain them. After drinking to their fill, Jia stood up and said, "My new wife is actually a fairy, she does not wish to remain in this mundane world for long. I will follow her on a distant journey. I dare to offer all that we have here as a gift to you, my elder brother and sister-in-law, please do not refuse it." Jia and his wife were both startled, and they tried to persuade him to stay. Jia suddenly drew a blade, about a foot long, and threw it to the ground with a loud sound. He said solemnly, "Without my father's kindness, this blade would already be soaked in the blood of my elder brother and sister-in-law." He then spoke in detail, recounting everything. Jia and his wife were both trembling and could not speak. The fairy said to Jia, "We are ready!" Jia then laughed heartily and left with the fairy, followed by the two maids who had come before. Together, they mounted four horses and galloped south, their destination unknown. Jia looked at the place where they had lived, though it lacked gold and silver, it was still rich in grain and fine clothing, valued at no less than a thousand taels. He moved in and settled there. Jia's servants and property all came under his control. Later, when he had a son who was dishonorable and devoted himself to aimless wandering, both husband and wife died of frustration. The son then sold the house to others and returned to practicing medicine, barely making ends meet. People believed that the legacy of the lame old man was still present.

    654

    外史氏曰：兄嫂忘亲，弱弟怀刃，叟之灵盖侧然矣。因为之远订姻盟，阴结眷属，俾孽子大平其不平。非正直为神，何能至此？而翠微娘子又女中之铮铮者也。不以粗鄙为嫌，唯以恩义为重，遂令天下之恶兄嫂，闻之莫不心灰而气绝。故虽乙之庸庸，曾见嗤于康成婢子，竟能拔剑斫地，慷慨于判袂之时，抑亦豪矣！余谓雁行中断不宜仍号以埙篪，故以甲乙别之，亦窃附于春秋正名之典云。

    Explanation

    654

    The Editor said: "When an older brother and sister-in-law neglect their kin, and a younger brother harbors a blade, the old man's spirit is moved with compassion. Therefore, he arranges distant marriage alliances for them, secretly uniting families, to make the wicked son right what is wrong. If not for righteousness as a deity, how could he achieve this? And the Lady Cuiwei is a resolute woman among women. She does not disdain coarseness and vulgarity, only valuing kindness and righteousness, thus causing all the evil older brothers and sister-in-laws in the world to lose heart and despair upon hearing of it. Thus, even though 'Yi' is mediocre, once mocked by Kang Cheng's maidservant, he can still draw a sword and strike the ground, being generous at the moment of parting, truly heroic indeed! I say that when the line of brothers is broken, it should not still be called the 'xun and chime' (a metaphor for harmonious brothers), therefore, to distinguish them as A and B, I also secretly align with the Spring and Autumn tradition of naming things correctly."

    655

    ○　徐之璧
    　　明末有徐之璧者，闽之巨商也，常贩生药于湖湘间。思宗十年，遭献忠之乱，囊橐如洗。窜迹荆南山中，攀藤附葛，忘其远近。偶见平坦之地，因惫少息。自念资本全亏，归乡无路，临风长叹者久之。遥闻木鱼声格格然，似有梵宇。时正苦饥，因思就而乞余，少延残喘，遂力疾而起，随其声响踪迹之。曲折半里许，路转峰回，似有佳境。视之，则荆扉半启，茅舍清幽，乃人家，非兰若也。谛听焉，声出于户内，因近而款之。即有小童启扃出，问客何为。徐语以故，随入而禀，复旋以壶飧来，语之曰：“主人云此非善地，饱宜远飏，不可遂止于是。

    Explanation

    655

    A Man Named Xu Zhibi

    In the late Ming period, there was a man named Xu Zhibi, a wealthy merchant from Fujian, who often traded medicinal herbs in the Hu-Xiang region. In the tenth year of the Chongzhen era, he encountered the chaos caused by Zhang Xianzhong, and his possessions were completely depleted. He fled into the mountains south of Jing, climbing vines and ivy, and forgot how far he had traveled. He stumbled upon a flat area and, exhausted, rested briefly. Reflecting to himself that his capital was completely lost and he had no way to return home, he sighed for a long time, facing the wind. He heard the sound of a wooden fish from afar, which sounded like 'gege,' and it seemed like there was a Buddhist temple. At that time, he was suffering from hunger and thought to go there and beg for some food to prolong his remaining breath. He then forced himself to get up despite his illness, followed the sound to find its source. After turning and twisting for about half a li, the road turned, and the mountain peaks receded, seeming like there was a beautiful place. When he looked, he saw that the thatched door was half open, and the thatched cottage was quiet and secluded. It was a house, not a temple. He listened carefully and heard the sound coming from inside the house. He approached and knocked on the door. A young boy immediately opened the door and came out, asking the guest what he wanted. Xu explained the reason, followed the boy inside, and explained his situation. Then the boy returned with a pot of food, telling him, "The master said this is not a good place. After eating, you should leave quickly. You should not stay here."

    656

    ”徐骇其言，且笑击木鱼诵经呗者竟出此语，则世途之险仄可知。食已，还其器，称谢而退。将返旧路，又苦无所栖，乃奋然曰：“与其饱于虎狼，毋宁犯乎魑魅。同一死也，且袪予心之惑，庶不至为迷惘之鬼。”竟止于树下，不即行。视童子，已阖扉入矣。天将向晦，木鱼之声益急，童子亦不再出。比及夜深，山风刺骨，徐正寒不可当，俄见火光荧煌，其巨如斗。及门忽化为异物，朱鬣赤毛，形状若豕，鸱张而入，徐不禁股栗，战战欲走。又有黑气若栲栳，自北而南，疾如奔马，及门，亦化为夜叉，电目血口，吽吽直入，徐益大怖。既而思，命之罔极与死为邻，复何所惴惴？更屏息以觇之，旋有白虹，长亘数十丈，极天际地，矗立而来。

    Explanation

    656

    Xu was startled by his words and laughed, for he had not expected the person reciting scriptures to say such a thing. Thus, the perilous and narrow path of life could be known. After eating, he returned the utensils, expressed his gratitude, and left. As he was about to return to his old path, he was distressed by having nowhere to stay. He then resolutely said, "It is better to be devoured by tigers and wolves than to encounter demons. It is the same death, but it will dispel my heart's confusion and hopefully not make me a bewildered ghost." He ended up stopping under a tree and did not immediately leave. Looking at the child, he saw that the door had already been closed and the child had entered. The sky was turning dark, and the sound of the wooden fish became more urgent; the child also did not come out again. By the time night deepened, the mountain wind was piercingly cold, and Xu was feeling the cold intensely. Suddenly, he saw flaring firelight, as large as a winnowing basket. At the gate, it suddenly transformed into a strange creature with a red mane and red fur, shaped like a pig, and entered with a fierce posture. Xu could not help but tremble and was trembling and wanted to run away. There was also a black vapor like a winnowing basket, moving from north to south, as fast as a galloping horse. At the gate, it also transformed into a night demon, with electric eyes and a bloody mouth, entering with a loud, fierce sound. Xu was even more terrified. After thinking, he realized that life is boundless and close to death, so what else should he be afraid of? He held his breath to watch, and suddenly there was a white rainbow, stretching for dozens of feet, from the sky to the ground, standing upright and coming.

    657

    至则缩小，化为美丈夫，白衣星冠，犹长丈余，俯躬而入。徐连睹数异，漠不动心，反若穆王鱼龙之戏，一时聊作场上观，亦奇人也。已而木鱼绝响，门忽洞开，遥聆笑语之声，宛似闺中弱质，心窃讶之。须臾小鬟四五辈，貌皆妖艳，以绛纱笼烛引丽人出自门中。睨之，年可十八九，云衣宝髻，装束若仙，色尤冠乎其众。甫出篱落，即闻一鬟语曰：“如斯良夜，奈无明月何？”丽人微笑有声，随掣小鬟所持之烛，举袂一掷，空际洞明，光辉如白昼。徐稔为真仙，将出拜求其拯救。

    Explanation

    657

    When it arrived, it shrank, transforming into a handsome man in white robes and a star-crowned hat, still over ten feet tall, who bowed his body and entered. I saw several strange occurrences but remained unmoved, as if they were the fish and dragon play of King Mu of Zhou, viewing them temporarily as a stage performance, which was also a peculiar person. Soon after, the sound of the wooden fish ceased, and the door suddenly opened wide. From afar, I heard the sound of laughter, which seemed like that of a delicate woman in the inner chambers, secretly surprising me. In a moment, four or five young maids appeared, all charming and beautiful, leading a beautiful woman out of the door with a red silk-covered candle. Glancing at her, she was about eighteen or nineteen years old, dressed in cloud-like clothes and a precious hairpin, dressed like a fairy, her beauty surpassing the others. Just as she stepped out of the fence, one of the maids said, "Such a good night, what to do without the bright moon?" The beautiful woman smiled with a sound, then took the candle held by the young maid, raised her sleeve and threw it, and the space became completely bright, the light as bright as daytime. Gradually realizing she was a true fairy, I was about to go out to bow and ask for her salvation.

    658

    而鬟有黠者，早瞥见之，亟白丽人曰：“有俗客在此，姑奈何遂炫其技？”丽人骇曰：“老父之占果应耶？不然，山中三异，阿谁见之不踉跄而遁哉？”遂敛其法，烛仍入小鬟手中，抽身亟返，门庭寂然。徐方懊恨缘悭，而向之小童早拔关而出，呼曰：“客固未去乎？主人相待，可亟入见也。”徐大喜，亟整衣冠随之入。草庭三楹，花竹森秀，木鱼经卷尚在案头。有顷，主人出，年可七旬。芝眉星目，神采焕发，扶杖而谢曰：“适作小法，窃恐震骇嘉宾，故命小童谢客。不意君胆自足包身，反令老夫惭赧无地。”徐亦拜伏曰：“逃窜余生，无可投止。感蒙一饭，恩戴二天。第以暮夜无归，故且瞑目待死，非敢自诩其胆也。过劳奖嘉，益切悚惶。”主人乃扶徐起，延之宾席，晤语甚欢。徐因请其姓氏，答曰：“老夫元时访道，居此不出已历三百余稔矣。岁久自忘其族，亦不复忆其名字。

    Explanation

    658

    A clever maid caught sight of the guest early and quickly reported to the lady, saying, "There is a vulgar guest here; how can you just display your skills?" The lady was startled and asked, "Did the old father's prediction come true? Otherwise, of the three strange things in the mountains, who would not stumble and flee upon seeing them?" She then put away her method, the candle was placed in the small maid's hand, she quickly withdrew, and the courtyard became silent. Xu was filled with regret and frustration, while the small boy from before had already unlocked the door and came out, calling, "The guest has not left yet? The host is waiting for you; you should come in quickly." Xu was greatly pleased and quickly tidied his clothes, following the boy inside. The grass courtyard had three halls, with flowers and bamboo arranged in a fine and elegant manner, and wooden fish and scriptures still on the table. After a while, the host came out, about seventy years old. With a lily eyebrow and starry eyes, radiant with spirit, he supported himself with a cane and said, "I just performed a small ritual, secretly fearing that it might shock the guests, so I ordered the small boy to apologize to the guests. I did not expect your courage to be enough to contain your body, instead, it made me feel ashamed and embarrassed." Xu also bowed and said, "A life of fleeing and hiding, with nowhere to turn. Grateful for a meal, indebted to the heavens. Only because of the dark night and no place to return, I was about to close my eyes and wait to die, not daring to boast of my courage. Overly praised and rewarded, I am even more anxious and fearful." The host then helped Xu up, invited him to the guest seat, and they talked very happily. Xu then asked for his name and surname, to which he replied, "During the Yuan period, I sought the Dao and have lived here without leaving for over three hundred years. After so many years, I have forgotten my clan and no longer remember my name."

    659

    近来龙钟特甚，人因号之曰懵懂公。老妻弱女，相偕同居，久无尘世之想。今晨偶起一数，主有婚嫁之喜。心窃不欲，因做术以魇之，孰意天定胜人，君竟流连不去，其命也夫！”徐闻言窃喜，而故诘之，公大笑曰：“君非懵懂者，而乃索解于老夫耶？此事当不言喻矣！”徐因含笑起谢，公命媪出见，年约四十余，容犹少好，而自云小公十龄，真奇人也。见讫，肆筵款婿。珍错之品数十器，而公犹自谦曰：“山家愧无兼味，重贻老夫羞。”觥筹甫交，媪即入妆女，遣之于归。悲泣之音，微达于外，公正色曰：“终不失为地仙，何戚戚如是？”因告徐曰：“本意留郎夫妇，但此非俗境，不可以设青庐，故遣小女随君去。

    Explanation

    659

    Lately, I have become very old and frail, and people thus called me Foolish Old Man. My old wife and young daughter live with me, and we have long since abandoned thoughts of the mundane world. This morning, I happened to cast a number, which indicated a marriage and wedding blessing for the master. In my heart, I secretly did not wish it, so I performed a spell to bewitch it. Who would have thought that fate is stronger than man? You stayed here, it seems, and it is his fate, indeed!" Mr. Xu secretly rejoiced upon hearing this, but deliberately questioned him. The old man laughed and said, "You are not a foolish person, and yet you seek an explanation from me? This matter should be self-evident!" Mr. Xu then smiled and thanked him. The old man ordered the old woman to come out to be seen. She was about forty years old, still looking youthful and beautiful, and she claimed to be ten years younger than the old man, truly a remarkable person. After they met, the old man spread out the banquet to welcome the son-in-law. Dozens of precious and exotic dishes were served, and the old man still humbly said, "The mountain home is ashamed to offer a variety of flavors, greatly causing shame to the old man." As the wine cups and game tokens were just being exchanged, the old woman immediately went to dress the daughter and sent her back home. The sound of weeping, barely reaching the outside, made the old man sternly say, "Ultimately, she will not fail to become a terrestrial immortal, why are you so sad?" He then explained to Mr. Xu, "My original intention was to keep the couple, but this is not a mundane place, it is not possible to set up a bridal chamber, so I sent my little daughter to go with you."

    660

    渠母子不舍，是以啼耳！”徐本意得此息肩，闻公言大失所望，亟起白曰：“婿已四海无家，愿侍晨昏，不欲返。”公摇首不许曰：“去！去！自有佳处。”无何，女妆已成，小鬟拥之出见徐，则即顷之丽人也。交拜成礼，公乃取巨觥置于中霤，谓女曰：“以此送若夫妇出山，家园汝自能创，予不屑代作马牛也。”女尚恋恋，公与媪皆促之，遂掩泪跃入，倏已无踪。徐大骇，公又遣婿，不得已而登之，恍惚中若坠重崖，惊怖欲号。张目视之，此身已履坦途，丽人早迎面而立。因笑谓徐曰：“君一龌龊商，何修乃作仙家婿耶？实与君言，予父母皆已千岁，妾之寿亦仅少廿余年。今以夙孽，不免配君，异日不得与二亲同证天仙者，此也。

    Explanation

    660

    "Her mother and son cannot part, that's why she cries!" Xu Benyi, who had been hoping to find a place to rest his weary soul, heard the father's words and was greatly disappointed. He quickly stood up and said, "I am now homeless in the world, I wish to serve my parents in both morning and evening, and I do not wish to return." The father shook his head and refused, saying, "Go! Go! There is a better place for you." Soon after, the daughter was dressed and a young maid led her out to meet Xu Benyi. She was the same beautiful woman they had seen earlier. They bowed to each other and the ceremony was completed. The father then took a large drinking vessel and placed it in the center of the house, saying to the daughter, "Use this to send you and your husband out of the mountain. You can create your own home, but I have no intention of working like a horse or ox for you." The daughter still clung to her husband, but the father and mother urged her to leave. She then shed tears and leaped into the vessel, and suddenly, she was gone. Xu Benyi was greatly startled, and the father sent him away as well. Reluctantly, Xu Benyi climbed into the vessel. In a daze, he felt as if he were falling from a great height, terrified and wanting to scream. When he opened his eyes, he found himself on a smooth path, and the beautiful woman was already standing in front of him. She smiled and said to Xu Benyi, "You are a lowly merchant, how did you become a husband of the immortals? Let me tell you the truth: my parents are both over a thousand years old, and my lifespan is only twenty years less than theirs. Now, because of our karmic bond, I must marry you. In the future, I cannot be together with both parents in the realm of immortals because of this."

    661

    ”言之又浩叹再三。徐逊谢，观其地，则已在汀漳间，盖去故乡伊迩矣。徐欲归里，丽人不可曰：“兵革未厌，即此亦非我辈所可居。”乃与徐东行数十里，择一山僻之区，丽人相视良久曰：“可矣！”拔鬓边小钗指挥之，旋成巨宅，无烦堂构。徐益信其仙，携腕同入，床帷皆若夙备。丽人一呼，仆婢坌集，亦不识所自来。命之供役，无不恭谨。有顷，喜卮既具，相对共酌，始悉其姓名，则陶氏，小字采春。至夜，欢好无异于常人，而交合之际，宛转不胜，固一葳蕤弱质也。徐自是不出户庭，日唯与丽人坐语，所谈皆黄老之术。徐习之既久，渐能吐纳，后遂辟谷不食。而自居此地，理乱不知，兵燹莫及，徐亦自忘其故我。

    661

    He spoke with deep sorrow and regret again and again. Xu humbly declined, but upon looking at the place, he found himself already in the region of Ting and Zhang, already close to his hometown. Xu wanted to return to his hometown, but the beautiful woman said, "The wars have not yet ceased, and even here it is not a place for us to live." She then traveled east with Xu for dozens of miles, choosing a secluded mountain area. After looking at it for a long time, she said, "This will do!" She used her hairpin to magically create a large house, without the need for traditional construction. Xu became even more convinced of her divine nature, and they entered together, the beds and curtains already prepared as if they had been there before. When the beautiful woman called, servants and maids gathered, though Xu did not know where they came from. She ordered them to serve, and they were all respectful and diligent. Soon, wine was poured, and they drank together, finally learning her name—she was from the Tao family, and her nickname was Cai Chun. By night, their joy and affection were no different from those of ordinary people, but during their intimate moments, she was delicate and graceful, truly a woman of soft and gentle nature. Xu never left the house after that, spending his days only in conversation with the beautiful woman, discussing the teachings of Huang-Lao. After practicing these teachings for a long time, Xu gradually learned to practice breathing exercises, and later he abstained from food altogether. In this place, he was untouched by political turmoil and war, and Xu himself forgot his former self.

    662

    约十余年，丽人忽谓徐曰：“沧桑大变，妾与君幸在壶中。今逢大圣人御世，天下雍熙，可出为盛世氓矣。”徐亦不解其言，相偕出门。行未数武，回顾之，屋宇渺然，侍从皆归于乌有。乃大诧，询之，丽人笑而弗答。比至通衢，辞徐欲返曰：“自侍巾栉，久虚定省。今请归宁父母，五年后再当相晤也。”徐时已见道，竟不挽留，唯叮嘱而别。归至故乡，桑柘尽废，亲故无从寻访。执途人而询之，则明鼎已革，海内归于圣朝已五易春秋矣。徐不胜叹惋，遂无意人世。结茅于九仙山，日坐一室，不食亦不出。人问之，颇不自隐，且尽其详。如是者五稔。一夕，居民皆闻木鱼声，侵晨迹之，其室则存，其人已杳。遂相与叹息，以为仙去。

    Explanation

    662

    About ten years later, the beautiful woman suddenly said to Xu, "Great changes have occurred in the world, and fortunately, I and you have remained secluded. Now that we have encountered a great sage ruling the world, the world is peaceful and prosperous, and it is possible to emerge as commoners in a prosperous age." Xu did not understand what she said and they left together. After walking just a few steps, he looked back and saw that the buildings had vanished and the attendants had disappeared without a trace. He was greatly surprised and asked her about it, but the woman smiled and did not answer. By the time they reached the main road, she bid farewell to Xu and said, "Since I have been serving you, I have long neglected the duty of visiting and showing respect to my parents. Now I request to return to my parents' home. We will meet again in five years." At that time, Xu had already seen the truth, so he did not try to keep her, only giving instructions and then parting. Upon returning to his hometown, he found that the mulberry and silk trees had all been destroyed, and he could not find any relatives or acquaintances. He asked passersby and learned that the Ming Dynasty had been overthrown, and the country had been under the rule of the new dynasty for five years. Xu could not help but sigh with regret, and thus had no interest in the world. He built a thatched hut on Nine Immortals Mountain, sat in a room every day, did not eat, and did not go out. When people asked him, he did not hide much and told all the details. In this way, he lived for five years. One evening, all the residents heard the sound of wooden fish. In the early morning, they traced it and found that his room still existed, but the person had disappeared. They all sighed together, thinking he had become a deity.

    663

    外史氏曰：世无懵懂神仙，其自谓懵懂者，皆不懵懂者也。徐以一时胆力，遂结仙缘。使见异而思迁，舍之径去，又安得有此一段佳话耶？自是君身有仙骨，世人那得知其故。徐之胆最巨，故亦得为懵懂神仙。
    　　随园老人曰：世外桃源，何咄咄逼人？此仙乎？鬼乎？吾不能无疑于是矣。

    ○　女南柯
    　　悟枕道人者，杭州诸生黄履诚之季女也。初名畹兰，幼聪慧，三岁即能辨之无。黄爱之若掌珠，躬亲教读。九龄授以杜诗，一日即朗诵无遗，若夙识者，其宿颖殆由天授。然善病，自春至冬，恒卧床蓐数次。即愈亦孱弱不支，行则如柳絮风中，坐亦若梨花雨后，父母咸忧之。及长，姿态益妍，脸艳朝霞，眉横远岫，遥而睇之，画中人也。且娴词章，善吟咏，有林下风致。黄为择婿，多未许可，年二九，摽梅已届，不免怅然。一日寒食，邻母杨夫人约游花港。诸女伴率皆闺秀，载以兰桡，从以箫鼓，见者俱眩目，诧为谪仙。至则画栏同倚，翠荇俯观。

    663

    Final Translation

    The historian remarked: In the world, there are no truly ignorant immortals; those who claim to be ignorant are actually not ignorant. Xu, through her courage at a certain moment, established a connection with the divine. If she had abandoned her path upon encountering something different and left immediately, how could there have been this beautiful story? Since then, she has had the divine essence, which ordinary people cannot understand. Xu's greatest courage allowed her to become a "foolish" yet wise and enlightened person.

    The old man of the Su Yuan said: The outside world's paradise, how overwhelming and intimidating! Is this a divine realm? Or a demonic one? I cannot help but have doubts about it.

    The Dream of Nanhua

    The person known as the "Enlightened Pillow Daoist" was the youngest daughter of Huang Lucheng, a scholar from Hangzhou. Initially named Wanlan, she was very intelligent from a young age, able to distinguish right from wrong at the age of three. Her father loved her as if she were a precious pearl, personally teaching her. At the age of nine, he taught her Du Fu's poetry, which she could recite without error, as if she had known it all along. Her innate talent seemed to be bestowed by heaven. However, she was prone to illness, often being bedridden several times from spring to winter. Even after recovering, she remained weak and frail, moving as lightly as willow catkins in the wind and sitting as fresh as pear blossoms after the rain, causing her parents great concern. As she grew older, her beauty increased, her face radiant like the morning sun, her eyebrows graceful like distant mountains. From afar, she appeared like a figure in a painting. She was also skilled in writing and poetry, possessing a natural and elegant demeanor. Huang tried to find a suitable husband for her, but most of the candidates were not acceptable. At the age of eighteen, the time for marriage had come, and he was inevitably disappointed. One day during the Cold Food Festival, a neighbor's mother, Lady Yang, invited them to visit the Flower Harbor. All the female companions were well-educated and refined, traveling in a boat decorated with orchids and accompanied by music. Those who saw her were all dazzled, marveling at her as a celestial being. Upon arrival, they leaned on the painted railings together, looking down at the green water plants.

    664

    旋见金鳞出没，五采缤纷，诸女或以香饵掷之，或以花片戏之，娇音嬉笑，依稀柳浪之莺。唯兰凝眸不语，遥视众鱼，倏则并鬣而去，倏则衔尾而还，与波上下，意甚恬适。不觉喟然曰：“鱼水之乐竟如此乎？”顿触情怀，不能自已。俄有一巨鱼，长近三尺，隐然若露头角，扬鬐鼓鬣，色似赤金，他鱼见之皆辟易。诸女方错愕间，鱼至兰前，昂首如有所睹，良久乃攸然而逝。诸女哗曰：“黄家姊为鱼相去矣！”兰赧然而心窃以为异也。已而兴阑，返乎舟中，张乐设饮，顺流而东。一时觥筹交错，箫管嗷嘈，不须臾而解语之花无不少酣春色。于是暂停杯斝，随意游玩。

    Explanation

    664

    Soon after seeing the golden-scaled fish appearing and disappearing, their multicolored and splendid forms, the women either threw fragrant bait at them or playfully tossed flower petals. Their charming and playful sounds echoed like the chirping of orioles in a willow grove. Only Lan gazed silently, her eyes fixed on the fish. She watched as they swam together in a coordinated manner, sometimes swimming side by side and sometimes following one another. They moved up and down with the waves, their movements peaceful and contented. Unconsciously, she sighed and said, "Is the joy of being in harmony with nature truly like this?" Her emotions were suddenly stirred, and she could not control herself. Soon, a large fish appeared, nearly three feet long, gently revealing its head and horns. It raised its fins and flapped its gills, its color resembling gold. The other fish all fled away in fear. The women were startled, and just as they were, the fish swam up to Lan, raising its head as if it had seen something. After a long while, it suddenly swam away. The women exclaimed, "The sister of the Yellow family has been taken away by the fish!" Lan blushed and secretly thought it was strange. Soon, the excitement waned, and they returned to the boat, setting up music and preparing a feast. They sailed downstream to the east. In no time, the cups and chopsticks intermingled, and the flutes and pipes played loudly. The flowers that could speak were all enjoying the spring scenery. They paused the cups and vessels and played around freely.

    665

    诸名媛有对两峰比黛者，有临一泓整妆者，有纤手搦管即景赋诗者，有素箑藏春凭舷笑客者。兰素质荏弱，三斝后即托故而起，小憩蓬窗。侍儿以绣被覆之，此时如海棠春睡，梦境沉沉。倏见二小鬟，一衣绯，一衣素，皆婉娩善迎人意，夹侍榻前，低呼曰：“君夫人醒乎，寡君相待久矣！”兰不自由推衾而坐。方将询所从来，衣绯者代为掠鬓，衣素者代为整衣，皆极其恭谨。既而敛衽启曰：“乘舆已驾，请即行。”兰起欲辞主人，而衣素者微晒曰：“何物老妪，亦劳贵人为礼耶？”兰默然，衣绯者以目怒之，似怪其失言。乃更词以对曰：“人正欢饮，小君去恐淹留也，能不重使人罪乎？”语甚温婉，兰乃欣然。

    Explanation

    665

    There were several distinguished ladies present. Some were admiring two peaks that resembled black eyebrows, while others were by a clear pool, adjusting their appearance. Some had delicate hands holding writing brushes and composing poetry on the spot, while others were holding white fans, hiding behind them, and laughing at the guests from the boat's side. Lady Lan had a delicate and weak nature. After three cups of wine, she pretended to have an excuse and stood up, taking a short rest by a window. A servant covered her with a brocade quilt, and at that moment, she was like a springtime hibiscus, deeply immersed in a dream. Suddenly, two young maids appeared, one wearing red and the other wearing white. Both were gentle and adept at pleasing others, standing on either side of the bed and speaking softly, "Lady, have you awakened? Our lord has been waiting for a long time." Lady Lan involuntarily pushed aside the quilt and sat up, about to ask where they came from. The maid in red helped to comb her hair, and the maid in white helped to tidy her clothes, both being extremely respectful and careful. Then, they bowed and said, "The carriage is ready, please depart immediately." Lady Lan stood up to bid farewell to the host, but the maid in white smiled slightly and said, "What kind of old woman is this, troubling you with such formalities?" Lady Lan remained silent. The maid in red glared at her, seeming to be angry at her for speaking out of turn. Lady Lan then changed her words and responded, "Everyone is enjoying themselves, if Lady leaves, it might cause a delay, and it could bring trouble to others." Her words were very gentle and courteous. Lady Lan then happily agreed.

    666

    甫出舱，衣素即呼曰：“倌人来！”即有金甲武士十余拥翟茀，上张鸾盖，向前迎迓。小鬟左右扶掖，搦衣捧履，俟兰升舆讫，乃自乘小川马随之。兰窃思，岂父母为我缔姻，今夕亲迎耶？第不知是何门楣，竟炫煌至此。正惝恍间，见所行皆非故道，恍惚如在云雾中耳。耳畔水声潺湲不已，不辨为何地。行许时，垂帘之外似有城郭，小鬟即搴帷禀曰：“诸大夫奉命郊迎内主。”兰茫然，旋闻唱名曰：“江湖大使臣某某，招文学士臣某某，敬谒小君。”兰不得已，以首颔之。小鬟即传曰：“诸卿远迎劳苦，请平身。”少顷，又禀曰：“诸勋戚承制候迓贵人。”又闻唱名曰：“骨鲠候臣某某，浪喷都尉臣某某，谨参君夫人。

    Explanation

    666

    刚刚走出船舱，一位穿着朴素的人立刻喊道：“船夫来了！”随即有十余位身穿金色盔甲的武士簇拥着一辆翟茀车，车顶上高高举起装饰着凤凰的华盖，向前迎接。左右两边有小丫鬟扶持，手中拿着衣服和鞋子，等待兰进入车厢后，才各自骑着小马跟随其后。兰心中暗想，难道是父母为我安排了婚姻，今晚就要迎亲吗？只是不知道对方是何等门第，竟然如此辉煌。正当她心神恍惚之际，发现所走的道路并非熟悉的路径，仿佛置身于云雾之中。耳边传来潺潺的水声，却无法辨别这是何处。行进了一段时间后，透过帘幕外似乎能看到城郭，小丫鬟立即掀开帘幕禀报道：“各位大夫奉命在郊外迎接新娘。”兰感到茫然，随即听到有人唱名道：“江湖大使臣某某，招文学士臣某某，敬谒小君。”兰不得已，点头示意。小丫鬟随即传达道：“各位大人远道而来辛苦了，请起立。”不久，又有人禀报：“各位勋贵按制候迎贵人。”又听到唱名道：“骨鲠候臣某某，浪喷都尉臣某某，谨参君夫人。”

    667

    ”小鬟即代劳曰：“将军跋涉艰辛，请即退。”其余则丙穴太守，枫叶令，不下数十员，小鬟仅以策麾之，不屑报也。兰从幔中窥觇，或衣飞鱼服，或披细鳞铠，簪绅俱分五色，亦有赤白相间者，印绶累若，皆鱼贯而退。又许时，闻呵殿声，则已行乎国中矣。有间，衣绯者启曰：“已至路门，请夫人面见寡君。”兰是时始觉羞涩。小鬟挽之降舆，历朱户凡数重，至一处，雕楹刻桷，堂陛巍峨。即闻殿上言曰：“不谷待子久矣，来何濡也？”小鬟命以俯伏，兰亦无敢仰视。殿上又言曰：“寡人依蒲国主也，适出游戏，得睹玉容。

    Explanation

    667

    The young maid immediately volunteered to say, "The general has traveled far and endured hardships, please withdraw immediately." The rest were the Governor of Bing Cave, the Commander of Maple Leaf, and dozens of others. The young maid merely waved her whip at them and did not bother to report back. Lan peeped from behind the curtain and saw some wearing flying fish robes, others wearing fine-scaled armor, with hairpins and sashes in five colors, some with red and white alternating patterns. The seals and ribbons were numerous, and they all withdrew in a single file. After some time, hearing the sound of a herald's shout, they had already reached the gate of the road. After a while, someone in red robes said, "We have already reached the gate of the road, please allow the lady to meet my humble lord." At that moment, Lan began to feel shy. The young maid pulled her down from the carriage, passing through several red doors, and arrived at a place with carved pillars and beams, and a majestic hall and steps. They heard the voice from the hall say, "I have been waiting for you for a long time, why did you come so slowly?" The young maid ordered her to bow, and Lan dared not look up. The voice from the hall said again, "I am the ruler of the state of Bo, just came out to play, and got to see your beautiful face."

    668

    窃欲以中宫之籀奉屈美人，未识肯许我乎？”兰赧颜，悚惶不能对。小鬟从旁赞曰：“君夫人诺矣。古诗不云乎，尽在不语中。”殿上即命平身。小鬟乃簇兰升阶，兰始展视。其君冠明月之冠，衣龙鳞之服，年约三旬，风姿潇洒，神仙中人也。左右皆小鬟，衣以五采，亦数十余人。王乃命酒合卺，设宴藻香殿，肴核纷陈，锦玉辉映。王左兰右，比目共筵，教坊奏乐，全爵牵红，交互而饮，嘉礼告成，对酌欢叙。又有梨园一队，以剧目呈上。王拣《南柯记》数折，梨园乃即席扮演。兰默然，王笑谓之曰：“我与卿今日亦同此奇遇者也。”兰不能解。无何，莲漏已催，霓裳罢舞，小鬟报曰：“三星在盭，可以寝矣。”乃以绛纱笼烛引王与兰归寝殿。

    Explanation

    668

    I wish to offer the precious script from the imperial palace to the beautiful Lady Qu, but I do not know if she would consent. Lady Lan blushed and was so nervous that she could not respond. A young maid stood by and said, "Your lady has agreed. As the ancient poem says, everything is in the silence." The emperor then ordered them to stand up. The maid led Lady Lan up the steps, and she began to look around. The emperor wore a crown shaped like the moon and a robe adorned with dragon scales. He was about thirty years old, with a graceful and elegant demeanor, like a being from the realm of the immortals. Around him were dozens of young maids, dressed in colorful attire. The emperor then ordered wine to be served, and they shared a cup in a wedding ritual. A banquet was set up in the fragrant hall, with a variety of dishes and luxurious decorations. The emperor and Lady Lan sat together, like two fish swimming side by side. The imperial music bureau performed music, and they exchanged cups and red ribbons, drinking together in celebration. The wedding ceremony was completed, and they drank and chatted happily. There was also a troupe of actors who performed a play. The emperor selected a few acts from the play Nanke Ji, and the actors performed them on the spot. Lady Lan remained silent, and the emperor smiled and said to her, "I and you today have also experienced this strange encounter." Lady Lan did not understand. Soon, the lotus clock signaled that it was time to rest, and the dancers stopped their performance. The maid reported, "The stars are in the constellation Li, and it is time to sleep." Red silk curtains and candles guided the emperor and Lady Lan to their sleeping chamber.

    669

    王执兰手曰：“卿慕鱼水之乐耶？寡人得子，亦如鱼得水耳。”因先解衣就枕，小鬟促兰卸妆。兰犹腼腆，众遂代宽衣缕，拥之入帐，与王勉成欢好。王因口占以赠曰：“艳自生前得，情从梦里来。早知鱼水乐，不羡楚阳台。”兰性敏捷，亦口占以酬之曰：“雨露花间过，恩波枕畔来。莫教纨扇冷，胜筑避风台。”吟讫，王大悦，益深眷爱，挽其项曰：“卿故今时之道韫也。”早起晨妆，小鬟进飞凤之冠，明珠之履，翠钿玉瑱，锦衣绣裳，妆束一如妃主，且藏其故者于笥曰：“敝帷不忘，况君夫人微时之服乎？”三朝王乃大饷群臣，号曰“鱼水宴”，贺者皆以诗。

    Explanation

    669

    The king took Lan's hand and said, "Do you desire the joy of mutual harmony like fish in water? I, like a fish in water, have found you." He then undressed and lay down on the bed, while the young maidservant urged Lan to take off her makeup. Lan was still shy, so the others helped her undress and carried her into the tent, where they had a harmonious and intimate encounter. The king composed and recited a poem to give her:

    "Beauty is naturally endowed, love comes from dreams. Had I known about the joy of being like fish in water, I would not envy the pleasure of the Cloud Terrace."

    Lan, being quick-witted, also composed and recited a poem in response:

    "Rain and dew pass through the flowers, the waves of kindness come to the pillow. Do not let the silk fan become cold, better than building a wind-avoiding platform."

    After reciting, the king was very pleased and became even more fond of her. He pulled her by the neck and said, "You are indeed the modern-day embodiment of wisdom." She got up early to do her morning makeup, and the young maidservant brought her a phoenix crown and pearl shoes, along with ornate headwear and accessories. Her attire was just like that of a queen or princess, and she put away her old clothes, saying, "Do not forget the old tent,何况 your wife's clothes from her humble beginnings?" On the third day, the king generously rewarded his ministers, and it was called the "Fish and Water Banquet." Those who congratulated him all wrote poems.

    670

    其中一律尤工丽，诗曰：“星轩降自木兰舟，鱼贯宫人咏好逑。水国旧传龙并戏，湖邦今喜风来游。虽欣在藻君臣乐，莫为忘筌伉俪忧。千古盬蘩羞煽处，禹门从此近河洲。”兰讽咏再四，深喜之，而未明所指。王命兰次其韵，遂援笔立成曰：“深宫每愧济川舟，须信干城亦好逑。彤管不堪劳柱史，卷阿何事拟仙游。漫言同梦无人戒，也解司晨有客忧。愿把脱簪风折槛，好将磐石固沧洲。”王览之赞曰：“诰诫得体，无愧乎古之贤妃矣。”因出之以示群臣，皆叹服，具表以贺。兰居宫中旬余，燕婉之求虽遂，毛里之爱难忘，闲时辄思忆父母。王每出巡，小鬟皆从去，掖庭阒其无人，益觉岑寂。阅数日，旧疾复作。王怜之，亲视汤药，遂荒国政，群臣咸有谏章。

    Explanation

    670

    In the midst of all this, there was a poem of exceptional elegance and beauty. It read:

    "The star-laden carriage descended from the magnificently decorated boat, palace ladies in orderly procession singing praises of a good match. The ancient kingdom by the lake had long passed down the tradition of dragons playing in the water, and the lake kingdom now rejoiced in the breeze visiting. Though delighted by the aquatic plants, the ruler and minister enjoyed themselves, but do not forget the fishing net when talking about a harmonious couple. The ancient customs and practices, which once brought shame, now open the gate of Yu, bringing us closer to the riverbank." Lan recited and pondered the poem, deeply appreciating its beauty, yet unsure of its deeper meaning. The king then commanded Lan to compose a poem in the same meter, and she quickly wrote:

    "In the deep palace, I often feel ashamed of the boat that crosses the river; one must believe that the city's defenders are also good matches. The red tube cannot bear the labor of the recorder, why should the gentle hill be compared to a fairy journey? It is idle to say that no one warns about the same dream, even understanding the role of the morning bird, there is still worry for guests. Wishing to remove the hairpin and break the railing in the wind, to firmly establish the base like a rock on the riverbank." The king read it and praised, saying, "This poem is well-crafted and serves as a fitting admonition, not unlike the wisdom of ancient virtuous consorts." He then showed it to the court officials, who all admired it and presented a memorial to congratulate him. Lan stayed in the palace for more than ten days, and though her desires for intimacy were fulfilled, her longing for her parents remained. When the king went on inspection tours, the maidservants followed him, leaving the inner palace deserted and even more desolate. After several days, her old illness returned. The king pitied her, personally attending to her medicine, and as a result, neglected state affairs, prompting many officials to submit memorials of advice.

    671

    兰乃力劝王出，且曰：“昔之诗王不复记忆耶？盍出视朝，勿使外廷之人，执我咎也。”王惟太息曰：“佳人难再得。”兰见王钟情实甚，恐贻宫阃羞，力疾而起。王乃出见臣僚，不崇朝而倾城之祸作矣。烽燧告警，上下忧虞，王袖其疏入告兰。时兰初病起，视小鬟涤药铛而自拈象牙管，铺乌丝阑，将和王《建宫词》第二首，即“树头树底”一绝也，见王急起。

    Explanation

    671

    The person strongly urged the king to go out and said, "Do you no longer remember the ancient poems? Why not go out to the court, so that the people outside the palace do not hold me responsible." The king sighed deeply and said, "A fine woman is hard to find again." Seeing that the king was truly deeply attached, the person feared bringing shame to the palace and struggled up despite illness. The king then went out to see the officials, and the disaster of the city began. The beacon fires signaled an alarm, and all levels of the government were worried. The person carried his petition to inform Lan. At that time, Lan had just recovered from illness and was watching a young servant clean the medicine pot while picking up an ivory pen himself and spreading out a black silk sheet, about to harmonize the second part of the king's 'Palace Building Poem,' which includes the phrase "at the top and bottom of the trees," when he got up quickly.

    672

    王颜色沮丧，出袖中疏与之曰：“我夫妇何缘之悭耶？”兰惊讶展视之，略曰：“湖壖守将骨鲠候臣某，为强邻压境，飞章请援事：前接吞舟国来书一缄，内言吾王新得丽人，擅汉皋游女之姿，具洛浦神人之美，邻封向慕，甚为垂涎。欲仿明妃远嫁故事，否则致动干戈。如是云云。臣因其不逊，叱去来使，罔敢以闻。今彼倾国兴师，恶等鲸鲵，大如鲂盰，鳣鲔督其后，鳅鳝舞其前，且有拥剑之士三千，兼饶升木之卒半万，安澜顿扰，喷沫成波，巨浸难容，暴腮起浪。唯唯而至，镜湖为之不明；唼唼而来，断桥因之复续。臣无任公子之智，既难遏彼凶锋；空怀史大夫之忠，只虑摇我宗祚。愿王速决奇策，奠国家于涸辙之时，万勿坐昧先几，索臣等于枯鱼之肆。”其表语多类此，不胜摇尾乞生之状。

    Explanation

    672

    The king's face was dejected, and he took a letter from his sleeve and gave it to her, saying, "Why are my husband and I so lacking in fortune?" The princess was surprised and read the letter, which briefly stated: "A loyal and upright general stationed at the lakeside, named 臣某, sent a hasty message asking for aid because a powerful neighbor is pressing against our borders. He received a letter from a country that can swallow boats, which said that our king has recently obtained a beautiful woman, possessing the grace of a beauty from Han Gao and the beauty of a divine being from Luo Pu. The neighboring country admires and covets us greatly, wanting to follow the story of a famous princess marrying far away, or else they will resort to war. They are as fierce as sharks, as large as carp and catfish, with eels and yellow catfish following behind and dancing in front, and have three thousand swordsmen and half a million soldiers who can climb trees. The calm waters are suddenly disturbed, foam forms waves, the vast sea cannot contain them, and they rise with foam. They come with a sound like 'wei wei,' making the Mirror Lake unclear; they arrive with a sound like 'sha sha,' and the Broken Bridge is restored. I lack the wisdom of Ren Gongzi, so I cannot stop their fierce advance; I only have the loyalty of Shi Daifu, but I fear it will shake our royal lineage. I hope the king will quickly decide on a brilliant strategy, to secure the state in times of drought, and never sit idly by, waiting for the first signs, and let us be reduced to the status of dried fish." The letter continued in a similar vein, expressing a sense of desperation and pleading for immediate action.

    673

    兰阅讫，霞晕于面，栗生于肌，泫然曰：“王将奈何？”王盳蹙曰：“我国实尺泽之鲵，安能与人量江海之大哉？然渠虽有挟而来，吾宁葬于江鱼腹中，不能割所爱以饵敌也。”兰沉思良久，毅然曰：“妃有一语，王请勿疑。王之视妃，与先王之宗庙孰重？”王曰：“庙重而妃之敌体亦不轻。”兰曰：“不然，妃在国中不过一妇人耳，非有子孙承祧血食千载之重也。为王计者，与其国破而妃掳，毋宁弃妃以存国。请为王却万乘之强敌，保一邦之黎庶。窃比王嫱，以报主知。留取冢草之青，更表贞风于不朽。王以为何如？”王大惨，色变，拂衣而出曰：“何来此不祥之语？”兰遂不敢言。

    Explanation

    673

    After reading, Lan's face turned red, and a chill ran through her body. She said tearfully, "What will the king do?" The king looked distressed and said, "Our country is like a tiny fish in a puddle; how can we measure the vastness of the ocean? Even though he has brought something with him, I would rather be buried in the belly of a fish than sacrifice what I love to lure the enemy." Lan thought deeply for a long time and then said resolutely, "The queen has one piece of advice for the king; please do not doubt it. Compared to the king, the queen is just a woman in the kingdom, without the weight of descendants to carry on her lineage and enjoy sacrifices for a thousand generations. For the king's sake, it is better to sacrifice the queen to save the kingdom rather than let the kingdom fall and the queen be captured. I will offer my advice to repel the powerful enemy and protect the people of the kingdom. I compare myself to Wang Qiang to repay the king's favor. Let the green grass on the grave remain, to forever represent my unwavering virtue. What do you think, Your Majesty?" The king was deeply saddened, his face turned pale, and he angrily left, saying, "Why are you saying such ominous words?" Lan then dared not speak further.

    674

    未几，群臣惶惶，交章请退，愿挂冠归里，以避釜鬵之危，其意固在兰也。王不得已，与兰计曰：“寇深矣，可若何？”对曰：“王实深之又若何？若纳妃言，割衽席之爱，寇早敛甲退矣！”王见其意决，乃允其行，驰书报敌。仍宴于藻香殿，以饯之。兰谓小鬟曰：“我不可以艳妆往。”因索其故衣，易之以出。王执斝呜咽曰：“妃行矣！欲如向者合欢之宴，岂可得乎？愿妃善事新主，勿以予为念。”言已泣下。兰正色曰：“王是何言也！岂犹不谅我心哉？小童为社稷有此一行，恨不如虞美人刎颈王前，以明己志。然而此心可表，异日自知。王勿以汉王好色语妾也。”王惭，谢兰，虽词色激烈，然已泪溢杯盈。左右皆涕泣不能仰，相对凄然。离樽莫罄，兰因起辞行。王将送之，兰沮之曰：“妃之往亵体已甚，不可以再辱国君。

    674

    Not long after, the officials were in a state of panic, submitting multiple petitions requesting to step down, wishing to hang their caps and return to their hometowns, in order to avoid the danger of boiling water in a pot, the true intention behind their actions being directed towards Lan. The king, having no choice but to, discussed with Lan, saying, "The enemy is deeply entrenched, what should be done?" Lan responded, "What difference does it make if the king is deeply involved? If the king accepts the advice of the concubine, sacrifices the love of the bedchamber, the enemy will quickly withdraw their armor and retreat!" The king saw that Lan's intention was firm and agreed with her plan, sending a letter to the enemy. A banquet was held in the Fragrant Lotus Hall to bid farewell. Lan told her maid, "I cannot go in a glamorous makeup." She then took out her old clothes and changed into them before leaving. The king, holding a cup and sobbing, said, "The concubine is leaving! To have the same joyful banquet as before, how could it be possible? I wish you to serve the new master well, do not think of me." Having spoken, tears fell. Lan, with a serious expression, said, "What is the king saying! Does he still not understand my heart? The young person is doing this for the sake of the state, regretfully, it is not as good as a Lady Yu cutting her neck in front of the king, to clarify one's own intentions. However, this heart can be expressed, in the future, it will be known. The king should not speak to me about the Han king's fondness for beauty." The king felt ashamed and thanked Lan. Although the words and expressions were intense, tears were already overflowing the cup. Those around were all weeping and could not look up, looking at each other in a sad manner. The farewell cup was not empty, and Lan stood up and took her leave. The king wanted to see her off, but Lan discouraged him by saying, "The concubine's departure has already been too intimate, it cannot be done again to disgrace the king."

    675

    ”王乃止，只命向者二小鬟相送出境，且曰：“别恨之深，方寸已乱。但恐鸿飞遵陆，雁字难凭。勉成一章，为妃作念。”遂吟曰：“一曲骊歌送画轮，鲛绡无复梦中春。龙宫亦有毛延寿，又把丹青误美人。”兰愀然曰：“昭君使人疑，妾不可不使人信也。”因口占以和其韵曰：“百结柔肠似转轮，罗衣难望汉宫春。君王只待香魂返，莫费黄金赎美人。”吟成俯伏于王前，涕泗交颐，曰：“妃不复生侍左右矣！”王亦把袂诀别，悲不自胜。兰强起再拜，辞王而行。甫出路门，武夫已驾舆相候，掩泪登之，不敢回顾。小鬟仍策驹相随，至来时所税之农郊，诸大夫早望尘拱俟，遂借驿亭小憩。

    Explanation

    675

    The king then stopped, only ordering the two young maidservants to escort her out of the border, and said, "The deep parting sorrow has made my heart in turmoil. I fear that the geese flying over land, the letters from the swans are hard to rely on. Strive to compose a poem, for the sake of the queen." He then recited, "A song of farewell sends the painted carriage away, the silken cloth no longer brings spring dreams. Even in the dragon palace, there is Mao Yanshou, again the brush misrepresents the beauty." Lan said with a solemn expression, "Zhaohuan makes people doubt, I cannot fail to make people believe." Then she recited a poem to harmonize with the rhyme, "My tender heart, full of knots, is like a turning wheel, the silk robe cannot hope for the spring of the Han palace. The king only waits for the fragrant soul to return, do not waste gold to redeem the beautiful woman." After finishing the poem, she knelt before the king, tears streaming down her face, and said, "I will no longer live to serve you!" The king also held her sleeves, bidding farewell, and was overwhelmed with sorrow. Lan forced herself to stand up and bow twice, bidding farewell to the king and leaving. As soon as she left the gate, the warriors had already driven the carriage to wait. She wiped her tears and climbed in, not daring to look back. The young maidservants still led the horses to follow. They arrived at the rural outskirts where they had stopped before, and the officials had already been waiting with respect. Then they borrowed a way station to rest briefly.

    676

    群臣匍匐而前，合词以谢曰：“臣等胸无鳞甲，腹少藏书。不能为王乘长风，破巨浪，致令逝梁之寇辱及君夫人。死罪！死罪！”兰亦权词慰劳之。因命笔大挥一律于壁曰：“故国辞雕辇，他乡祛舞衣。云深宫树远，木尽雁书稀。欲堕鲛人泪，羞随介士旗。惟留香草在，仿取汉明妃。”更附以一绝曰：“强将眉月渡沧波，肯负当年得宝歌。云雨若归别岫去，画图人面愧如何。”题毕，君臣争阅，皆有惭色。兰遂行，易车而骑，亦乘小骊驹，车仗驺从俱已返旆矣。凄怆就道，唯二小鬟相从。又行许时，似西湖放生池，衣绯者启曰：“不敢越境，请从此辞。”兰怅然，乃脱左手指环与之曰：“以贻王，无相忆也。

    Explanation

    676

    The officials crawled forward, joined in words of apology, saying, "We lack the armor and protection, and our knowledge is scant. We were unable to ride the strong wind and break through the great waves, allowing the enemy of the lost beam to humiliate Your Majesty's wife. Death upon us! Death upon us!" Lan also spoke soothing words to comfort them. Then he ordered the writing of a poem on the wall, which read: "Leaving the carved carriage behind, I discard the dancing attire in a foreign land. The distant palace trees are shrouded in mist, and the letters of the geese are rare. I wish to shed tears like those of the mermaid, but feel ashamed to follow the flag of the warrior. Only the fragrant grass remains, emulating the Bright Consort of Han." He added another verse: "Forcing the crescent moon to cross the vast waves, how could one betray the song of gaining treasures in the past. If the clouds and rain return to another mountain, how can one feel ashamed of the face in the painting?" After writing, the king and his officials read it together, all feeling a sense of shame. Lan then departed, changing from a carriage to a horse, riding a small black steed, with his entourage returning. He set off with a somber heart, accompanied only by two young maids. After traveling for some time, they seemed to reach the West Lake's release pond, where a person in red clothing said, "I dare not cross the border, please let me bid farewell here." Lan was filled with regret, and he removed the ring from his left hand, giving it to him, saying, "To give to the king, do not forget me."

    677

    ”俄有皂衣人巨口硕腹，凡数十辈，见兰踊跃曰：“妃主来矣！吾王固终日望之也。”兰此时义激于中，奋不顾己，叱之曰：“汝国不道，拆我鸾凤，犹妄冀绸缪耶？”因以骑授鬟曰：“不可使敌秣吾驹。”语未终，轻身一跃，遽赴清流。恍惚中犹闻小鬟号救声，凝睇四望，则枕藉乎舟中，已霍然寤矣。惊悸久之，香液湿襦，始知其梦。时众复欢晏，左右无人，兰因自叹曰：“薄命如斯，梦固使人觉也。”遂萌出世想。推枕而起，斜日盈窗，去卧时亦俄顷耳。侍儿入，见兰已醒，遽报主人，复强之就席。飞一觞来，不饮也；哺一箸来，不食也。诸女伴叩之寂然，嗤之漠然。筵未及终，适遇小艇，竟托疾乘之先返。归至家，卧而不起。

    Explanation

    677

    Soon there appeared black-clad people with large mouths and big bellies, several dozen in number, who, upon seeing Lan, exclaimed joyfully: "The princess is coming! Our king has been looking forward to her all day." At that moment, righteousness stirred within Lan, and she acted bravely without regard for herself, scolding them: "Your country is unjust, having torn apart my phoenix, yet you still foolishly hope for a reunion?" She handed over her horse to the maid and said: "I will not let the enemy feed my horse." Before she finished speaking, she hastily jumped into the clear stream. In a daze, she still heard the little maid's cries for help, and as she looked around, she found herself lying scattered on the boat, having suddenly woken up. She was shocked and frightened for a long time, and the fragrant liquid wet her robe, when she realized it was a dream. The gathering continued with merriment, but there was no one around her. Lan sighed to herself: "Such a wretched fate, dreams indeed make one wake up." She began to think of renouncing the world. She pushed aside her pillow and got up, and the slanting sunlight filled the window. It had only been a short while since she lay down. The servant girl entered and saw that Lan had woken up, and hurriedly reported to the host, who then forced her to sit down. She poured a cup of wine, but Lan did not drink; she served a piece of food, but Lan did not eat. The other girls knocked on her door but found her silent, and they laughed at her indifferently. Before the banquet was over, she happened to meet a small boat and pretended to be ill, returning home first. She returned home and lay down, not getting up.

    678

    父母询其故，腼然不言，惟请出家为女道士。父母怪之，固诘焉，竟以实告。父味其所言，遽诵毛诗二句曰：“‘鱼在于藻，依于其蒲。’儿游花港，殆有所思耶？此固颁首莘尾者也。”兰详其诗文，果似于渊之类，而靡他之志愈坚，仍固请之。父笑曰：“此梦也，几何鲢鲤不分？”兰曰：“然。斯固非真，抑安知真不似梦？且儿既为梦中罗敷，又可作使君新妇乎？”父仍坚执不许，遂负气绝粒。勉从之，始食。竟以女冠入栖霞观中，而自易其名曰‘悟枕’，言从枕上得悟也。起居一室，不见人，惟闺中良友得谒焉。钱塘令陈公莅任时，内子陆孺人亦闽中闺秀也，慕其名，时一过从。因得其梗概，为作《鱼水缘》传奇，至今犹脍炙人口焉。

    Explanation

    678

    Her parents inquired about the reason for her request, and she blushed and remained silent, only asking to leave home and become a female Taoist nun. Her parents were puzzled and pressed her further, until she finally revealed the truth. Her father, considering her words, quickly recited two lines from the Book of Songs: "The fish are in the algae, relying on the reeds. My daughter wanders through the flower-filled stream, perhaps she is deep in thought? This is truly a sign of wisdom." Lan, upon hearing this, realized that her poetry and writings indeed resembled the wisdom of the ancients, and her resolve to remain chaste grew even stronger. She still firmly requested to leave. Her father smiled and said, "This is a dream; how can one distinguish between carp and catfish?" Lan replied, "Indeed. This may not be real, but how can one know that reality is not like a dream? Moreover, since I am already the dream-like Luo Fu, can I also be the new wife of the magistrate?" Her father still refused, so she, filled with anger, decided to fast. Reluctantly, she accepted and began to eat again. Ultimately, she became a nun and entered the Qixia Monastery, changing her name to "Wuzhen," meaning "Enlightened by the Pillow," indicating that she gained enlightenment through a dream. She lived in solitude, rarely seeing others, with only close female friends allowed to visit. When the magistrate Chen of Qiantang took office, his wife, Lady Lu, a distinguished woman from Fujian, admired her reputation and occasionally visited her. Lady Lu gathered the general outline of her story and wrote the play Yu Shui Yuan, which remains widely popular to this day.

    679

    外史氏曰：尝读玉茗《南柯》，憎其似幻而不似真。盖凡人梦中啼笑，不可谓之无情，梦固由情而生也。女之始遇，则苎萝西子也，继则帐中李夫人也，一变而为王嫱，再变而为绿珠。梦中之忧乐，身实受之，犹可谓无情乎？惟其有情，故临池而感，推枕而悟，总不出情字之中。而乐则极其缠绵，忧亦不胜愤懑，梦中人既不自禁，醒时人亦不自解。于是超出情关，猛登道岸，岂真曰“鱼，我欲也”，而以其身殉之哉！

    Explanation

    679

    The Commentator said: I once read Tang Xianzu's "The Dream of South柯" and found it to be illusory yet lacking in authenticity. For all that, the crying and laughing in dreams cannot be called devoid of emotion, for dreams are born of emotion. When the woman first encountered the man, he was like Xi Shi of the Zhu Luo; later, he was like Lady Li in the tent; he then transformed into Wang Zhaojun, and finally into Lu Zhu. The joys and sorrows in the dream were experienced in reality, so can they truly be called devoid of emotion? Only because of the emotion did the dreamer feel moved by the sight of a pool and awaken with a sudden realization, all within the realm of emotion. The joy was deeply affectionate, and the sorrow was overwhelming with frustration. The dreamer could not control himself in the dream, and the waking person could not understand it either. Thus, he transcended the barrier of emotion and suddenly reached the shore of enlightenment. Is this truly to say, "Fish, I desire it," and to sacrifice oneself for it?

    680

    随园老人曰：昭君当殿请行，千古为之陨涕。今读此传，觉女荆卿之壮，远胜于雌子卿之悲？世固无此事，而闺阁不可无此人。

    ○　子都
    　　河南某邑宰，素有龙阳之癖。门役侍从多择美少年，内署经旬不入，人多病之。辛巳岁，奉宪檄饬委巡视河隄，凡有余桃爱者，无不携以随行。日惟学魏公子，多饮醇酒，但不近妇人，而比顽童。隄之溃与不溃，工之坚与不坚，弗问也。风闻于上官，督诫者屡矣，而不能夺其所好。将行参效，又事涉暧昧。乃檄守河南吴公，尽拘其厮养，而易以面目可憎者。宰迫于宪命，无如之何，强起视工，藉以排遣。

    Explanation

    680

    The Old Man of Sui Garden said: "Wang Zhaojun volunteered to go to the border, and for thousands of years, it has caused people to shed tears. Reading this account now, I feel that the bravery of a female Jing Ke far surpasses the sorrow of a female Zixi. The world certainly does not have such a thing, but the inner chambers cannot do without such a person."

    ○ Subordinate to Zi Du
    A certain magistrate of a county in Henan had always had a preference for male beauty. His attendants and servants mostly chose handsome young men, and he did not enter the inner chambers for ten days, which often led to criticism. In the year Xinsi, he was ordered by the government to inspect the river embankments. Anyone who had extra affection for him would accompany him on his journey. Every day he only learned from Wei's prince, drinking strong wine, but he did not approach women and compared himself to mischievous children. Whether the embankment would collapse or not, whether the work was strong or not, he did not inquire. He was repeatedly warned by his superiors, but they could not change his preferences. Before he was about to report to his superiors, the matter involved some ambiguity. The governor of Henan, Wu Gong, was then ordered to confiscate all his servants and replace them with those who were unattractive. The magistrate was forced by the imperial command and had no choice, so he reluctantly went to inspect the work, using it as a way to distract himself.

    681

    一退食，则黧而黑、麻而胡、籧篨戚施者，夹侍左右。较之曲眉丰颊，便体清声，其妍盵尚堪数计耶？于是一石始醉者，一斗亦醉，而潘河阳复为陶彭泽矣。一夕，新魄悬钩，屏人独步。有酒无欢，顿萌月白风清之感。忽闻丛竹中吃吃似有笑声，以其地为公廨，或有邮亭女子，虽无心之遇，亦可以舍后而趋前。及拨疏篁视之，则二童子隐身于檀栾深处，捋裤为欢，彼此相嬲，益觉喜动颜色。因悄然而前，思为一箭双雕之计。其一闻修箨簌簌，如有人来，早苍黄如脱兔，穿竹而遁。

    Explanation

    681

    After retiring from official duties, the person was surrounded by those with dusky and dark skin, coarse and Hui-like features, and hunched or deformed appearances, serving on either side. Compared to a beautiful woman with arched eyebrows and full cheeks, a graceful and clear voice, how can their beauty be counted? Thus, one who starts to get drunk after a stone (of wine) can also get drunk after a dou (a smaller unit of wine), and Pan Yue once again becomes Tao Yuanming.
