    Explanation

    721

    As the days passed, they became increasingly intimate. Occasionally, they discussed current affairs and ancient and modern classics, and both were extremely knowledgeable and well-versed. The old man had read little, but he was fond of doing good. They spoke at length and in detail, deeply loving and respecting each other. One day, the old man was slightly drunk, and the other person arrived. He then asked, "Looking at your appearance, you must be someone who has studied in the schools of the city. Recently, the city has held a grand festival to honor the gods, with dazzling jewels and silk, and deafening music. Everyone rushed to participate, fearing they would be left behind. But you, on the contrary, roam in the outskirts, buying and drinking cheap wine, and after these few days, you will be gone for another year. I am puzzled by this and dare to ask the reason." The other person heard the old man's words and sighed, "Is it only because of this? Indeed, there is a reason. I am not a human, but the city god. These few days of travel are a plan to avoid the noise, not to emulate the ten-day drinking of the plain!" The old man was surprised by his words and suspected he was joking. He then asked, "People, out of reverence for the god's spirit, rush to the temple halls, wasting resources and effort, so as to honor the god. But the god, instead, abandons this and goes on a journey. Is it that all those who come with incense are not sincere? Although humans and gods are different, they do not transcend reason. You cannot deceive this weak old man." The god laughed and said, "Why would I deceive you? The connection between humans and gods lies in sincerity, not in the mixing of grass roots and tree bark with sandalwood chips to call it fragrant."

    722

    即如翁之明虔，每值会日，辄于无人之处泥首者三，又何尝亲诣坛眀，予始降若以福哉！”翁闻神言至此，色然而骇。盖翁以肆事乏人，因感神庇，每如此而人未知也。因信其真神，将起跪拜。神止之曰：“居，吾当语翁。夫予之遁迹于斯，实有所不能耐也。来者既多托故至者，不尽攄诚。冥冥之中既已洞鉴，而此曹之嘈杂扰攘，大溃男女之防，并废公私之务，睹之更有所不忍。况村野之夫值炎暑之日，汗出如蒸，衣湿如浣，以经年不濯之垢，郁积而升，纵薰以荀令之香，闻者亦罔不掩鼻。兼之入喉三寸，酒蒜之臭犹存；果腹崇朝，谷食之性渐化。当稠众之中，口不能缄，脔不能掩，氤氲而来者，大抵皆是也。将何以堪？”言未毕，翁亦眃然曰：“是诚有之。”神曰：“未也，是犹可耐者。

    Explanation

    722

    As for the old man's devoutness, whenever he meets on the day of assembly, he bows three times with his head touching the ground in a place where no one is present, yet has he ever personally visited the sacred altar to receive my blessings? The old man, upon hearing the deity's words, turned pale with fright. It is because the old man lacks people to help with his affairs, and thus he feels the deity's protection, always acting this way without others knowing. Therefore, he believes the deity is a true spirit and is about to rise to kneel and bow. The deity stopped him and said, "Stay, I will tell you. My retreat here is indeed due to something I cannot endure. Those who come often do so under pretenses, not fully expressing their sincerity. In the unseen realm, I have already seen through everything, but the noise and disturbance of these people greatly break down the barriers between men and women, and waste both public and private affairs. Seeing this, I find it even more unbearable. Especially, when rural men are on a hot day, sweating profusely, their clothes as wet as if washed, with dirt accumulated over years without washing, rising and accumulating, even if perfumed with the fragrance of Xun Ling, those who hear it also cannot help but cover their noses. Moreover, the smell of wine and garlic still lingers three inches into the throat; after filling their stomachs in the morning, the nature of grains and food gradually transforms. In the midst of a dense crowd, they cannot keep their mouths closed, and the pieces of meat cannot be covered, all coming with a pervasive smell. How can one bear this?" The deity had not finished speaking when the old man also said, "This is truly the case." The deity replied, "Not yet, this is still bearable."

    723

    最恶妇人女子不守闺门之训，以焚香为神可媚，以顶礼为福可邀。涂粉施朱，庄严其外相，而适足以诲淫；靓妆袨服，蠲洁其仪容，而不免于藏垢。升阶入殿，瞻像蹇帷，以五漏之躯，隐难言之垢。白发之婆犹可恕，绿鬓之媛最堪憎。共度良宵，讵免春藏玉洞；未绝天癸，难保月浸鸿沟。神之所惧者，尤在于此。而村姑、田妇衣麻纻之裙衫，渍婴儿之溲便，骸垢莫浴，足茧不脱，其暗香之习习又与向之男子夹杂而迭作，盖惟木偶者能堪之。有像即有神，人且避之惟恐不亟，岂神独能安享哉！”因谓翁曰：“翁有善心，寿当再历十稔。念故旧之情，必令急脚相召，东庑一判，尔时当更易其中，即翁之坐地也。”言已，出白金一锭曰：“以此偿连日酒值，但为我宣播此语，予实受若之赐。且凡有庙而神者，亦锡若以福矣！”翁犹有干请，转瞬竟失其所在。

    Explanation

    723

    The most detestable women are those who do not adhere to the teachings of proper conduct within the home, burning incense to please the gods and prostrating themselves to invite blessings. They apply makeup and rouge to beautify their appearance, which only serves to incite lust; they dress up in fine clothes to appear clean and presentable, but cannot escape the dirt they hide. Ascending the steps into the temple, looking at the images and drawing back the curtain, they reveal the immature bodies and hidden impurities. Older women, with their gray hair, can be somewhat excused, but young women with green hair are particularly loathed. Spending a night together, they cannot avoid the act of spring hidden in the jade cave; if menstruation has not ceased, they cannot ensure the moon does not drench the great divide. The gods are especially fearful of this. Rural women and peasant women, dressed in rough hemp or ramie skirts and shirts, soiled with the waste of infants, their bodies unwashed and feet calloused, their subtle scents mingling with those of men, only statues could endure such conditions. Where there is an image, there is a deity; people avoid such places as if fleeing a fire, how could deities alone remain there? He then said to the old man, "You have a kind heart, your life will extend another ten years. Thinking of old acquaintances, I will surely send a messenger to summon you. When you are in the eastern hall, at that time, the place will be changed, and it will be your seat." After speaking, he took out a piece of white gold and said, "Use this to pay for the recent wine expenses, but spread this word for me, I truly accept your kindness. And all those with temples and deities, I will also bestow blessings upon you!" The old man still had something to ask, but in an instant, he vanished.

    724

    外史氏曰：偏是闺阁中人专好寻神入庙，而不虞神之攒眉已久也。至于举大会、赛大社，则呼姨唤姊引女随娘攘攘而来者，直不知其几千百辈。无论苎萝西子或蒙不洁，即此阴浊之气、妖冶之形，早令阳明正直之神屏息而避之。于以邀福，不綦难乎！昔京西有异僧，斫二石球。晨则登绝高之峰，自崖而推之，径达其麓。已乃下而掇石，再登再推，操纵终日。人因号之曰魔，而钦其道行者来且接踵。僧于丈夫犹见礼，独至巾帼者流则谩骂曰：“没家法！露面出头，直供妄男子看杀耳，岂真为老僧哉！”味僧之言，可知神之于此更有侧然于中者，不止臭之不堪相逐也已。

    Explanation

    724

    The historian remarks: It is particularly the women of the inner chambers who are fond of seeking divine intervention in temples, without realizing that the gods have been frowning for a long time. When it comes to grand gatherings and major communal festivals, there are countless women and girls bustling in, calling out to their aunts and sisters, coming together in droves. Whether it is a beauty like Xi Shi or someone defiled, the dark and turbid atmosphere and the alluring, seductive appearance have long caused the pure and upright deities to hold their breath and avoid them. To seek blessings, is it not extremely difficult? In the past, there was a strange monk in the western part of the capital. He carved two stone balls. Every morning, he would climb a very high peak, roll the stone balls down the cliff, and watch them reach the foot of the mountain. Then he would descend, pick up the stones, climb again, and push them again, controlling them all day long. People therefore called him a demon, and those who admired his spiritual practice came in a continuous stream. The monk treated men with respect, but when it came to women, he would curse them, saying: "No family discipline! Showing your face and head, you are just for the sake of being looked at by foolish men, not truly for the old monk!" From the monk's words, we can understand that the gods feel even more repulsed by such behavior, not just because of the foulness that makes them unwilling to be near.

    725

    ○　智媪
    　　旧传燕南有一媪，不知何许人，盖富家也。附近有剧盗，聚党十数，觊其资，夜入其家。时媪已就寝，夙闻人言盗首与己同乡，并且少失其父母，遂坦然无惧，披衣起，将亲逆之。聆其履声，知至户外，乃操土音呼其子曰：“儿曹何贪睡至此！若舅来，竟不一迎耶？”盗闻而骇，意犹未深信。媪已自出，涕泣而言曰：“数年不晤弟，已魁然丈夫，奈何不一念姊，今夕始来相视哉？”语竟，掩面欷歔，悲不自胜，俨若骨肉重聚者。

    725

    A Wise Old Woman

    According to old legends, there was an old woman in the southern part of Yan, whose background is unknown, but she was probably from a wealthy family. Nearby, there was a notorious band of robbers, consisting of about ten or more members, who coveted her wealth and broke into her house at night. At that time, the old woman had already gone to bed, but she had long heard from others that the leader of the robbers was from the same hometown as herself and had lost his parents at a young age. Thus, she remained calm and fearless. She got dressed and got up, intending to greet them personally. Hearing their footsteps, she knew they were at the door, so she spoke in a local dialect to call her children: "Why are my children sleeping so deeply? If my uncle has come, why didn't you greet him?" The robbers were startled and still did not fully believe it. The old woman then came out, crying and speaking: "We haven't seen each other in years. He is now a tall and strong man. Why didn't you think of your aunt once? Tonight you finally came to see me." After finishing speaking, she covered her face and wept, so sad that she could not bear it, as if they were reuniting as family members.

    726

    盗为之动容，因曰：“弟少不屑，早背椿萱，不知有姊，非敢恝然相忘。”媪又曰：“弟幼时予犹归宁，后从汝姊夫远出至此，遂不得返。岂意怙恃皆弃弟仙逝，弟已成立如此耶！”言已大恸。盗竟信为实然，劝慰至再，随媪入室。媪命婢烛，盗复出约束其众曰：“此予之姊家，非路人也，慎勿骚扰。”乃命尽屯于外。媪心窃喜，又呼其子出见舅。其子知为盗，举股栗，不得已而参谒之。盗笑曰：“甥皆长成，姊真厚福人也。”媪又命子妇出参舅翁。时媪之中子方授室，新妆出拜，恍若一家人。

    Explanation

    726

    The thief was moved by her words and said, "I was young and did not value family ties, leaving my parents at an early age. I did not know I had an older sister, and I would not dare to forget her indifferently." The old woman replied, "When I was young, I still visited my in-laws, but later I left with your brother-in-law to come here, and thus could not return. Who would have thought that my parents would all pass away, leaving me behind, and that you have grown up to be so accomplished!" She wept greatly upon saying this. The thief, believing her story, comforted her repeatedly and followed her into the room. The old woman ordered the maid to light the candle, and the thief went out again to discipline his followers, saying, "This is my sister's home, not a stranger's. Be careful not to disturb anyone." He then ordered them to camp outside. Secretly delighted, the old woman called her son to meet his uncle. Her son, knowing the thief was a bandit, trembled all over, but had no choice but to pay respects. The thief smiled and said, "Your nephews are all grown up, and your sister is truly a person of great fortune." The old woman then called her daughter-in-law to pay respects to her uncle-in-law. At that time, her middle son had just been married, and she appeared newly dressed, bowing as if they were one family.

    727

    盗亦自忘其盗，惟曰：“不知吾甥嘉礼，舅竟未备一芹，将如之何？”因呼从盗以一袭入，倾囊出珠十粒，以为答拜之仪。媪因辞而后命妇拜受，亟命其子治筵款舅，大犒其从者。子已心安，家又素裕，咄嗟数筵立办。妪与盗对酌，絮谈乡俗，咸中肯綮。盗益信为同胞，醉饱始去。濒行，妪曰：“姊家幸有余积，弟如乏用，数百金不妨将去。”盗大笑曰：“弟以白手游四海，反来耗姊家物耶？”径行。妪与子送之门外，呼啸而返。是日也，妪家自饮馔而外，未尝少失锱铢。举室庆幸，亦不敢以告人。

    Explanation

    727

    The thief also forgot he was a thief, and said, "I did not know about my nephew's wedding. The uncle failed to prepare even a small gift, what shall be done?" He then called his followers and took out ten pearls from his bag, intending to return a gift. After politely declining, the wife then bowed to receive. The family prepared a feast to entertain the uncle and greatly rewarded his followers. The son felt at ease, and the family was usually well-off, so they prepared several feasts in a short time. The woman chatted about local customs with the thief, and all the conversations hit the mark. The thief believed they were cousins, and after drinking and eating to his heart's content, he left. As he was about to depart, the woman said, "My sister's family is usually well-off. If you need anything, you can take up to a few hundred gold coins." The thief laughed and said, "I travel around the world as a thief, would I come to waste my sister's family's things?" He shouted and returned. The family was relieved and dared not tell anyone that except for the food and drink, not a single coin was lost.

    728

    越数日，盗又夜至，以千金置妪榻上曰：“以此为姊寿，略表弱弟之诚。”妪不复辞。盗又赠甥及甥妇金币钗钏，约值数百缗。厚赉仆婢，大肆挥霍，复与媪子欢饮达旦，而后去。自是习以为常，每至，辄有馈遗。媪家益富，人固不及知，抑且不及料也。媪又嘱盗为己芘荫，盗予以剑曰：“绿林之豪见此，当无敢犯者。”妪大悦。后年余，盗远出行掠，妪与子谋徙其家于晋，盖虑盗之贻累也。及迁于河东，植此剑于寝户。有小盗夜入，见之辄咋舌，不敢肆虐。自是数年无盗患。信乎，妪之智眅绝天壤，而非人所能及已。

    Explanation

    728

    After several days, the thief came again at night and placed a thousand gold pieces on the old woman's bed, saying, "I offer this as a gift to my sister, to slightly express the sincerity of a younger brother." The old woman did not refuse. The thief also gave gifts of gold coins, bracelets, and other valuables to her nephew and his wife, worth several hundred strings of cash. He generously rewarded the servants, spending lavishly, and then drank joyfully with the old woman's son until dawn before leaving. From then on, this became a regular occurrence, and every time the thief came, he always brought gifts. The old woman's family grew wealthier, but people were unaware of this and did not even suspect it. The old woman then asked the thief to protect her, and the thief gave her a sword, saying, "Those powerful figures in the Green Forest bandits will not dare to offend if they see this." The old woman was very pleased. After more than a year, the thief went far away to raid, and the old woman and her son planned to move their family to Jin, fearing that the thief might bring trouble. When they moved to Hedong, they placed the sword on the bedroom door. Whenever a small thief entered at night, he would see the sword and be taken aback, daring not to act wildly. Thus, for several years, there were no more bandit troubles. Indeed, the old woman's wisdom was unparalleled in the world, beyond the reach of ordinary people.

    729

    外史氏曰：指鹰隼为鹡鸰，化荆棘为棠棣，非凭此一副急泪，实以天性动之也。夫盗必甚黠，岂甘为妪所笼络。而妪之笼络，一本于人情，盗即自悟非亲，亦不忍放下面皮，况妪有以预知其家事而言之切中乎。故妪之智既不可及，而所以行其智者，尤不可及。

    ○　挑绣
    　　长州诸生邹大任，年仅弱冠，美风姿。貌白晳而性騃愚，咿唔一室中牝牡不知，时艺文字外无所晓，同胞咸笑之。一日如市，见有逆女者，箫鼓喧阗，宾从络绎，茫然莫解其故。以询友，友绐之曰：“兄不识耶？此郡中某家初得官，夸示于人也。”生信而不疑，且素以功名为念，欣然尾之。往至其门，见新婿奠雁御轮，仪节繁缛，心窃好之，久立而不去。已而彩舆复出，哄然启行，微闻第中有悲泣声。生乃鼓掌大噱曰：“此亦大好事，何戚为？”观者胥怪而相顾，生殊不自觉。

    Explanation

    729

    Translation

    The historian says: Turning eagles into skylarks, transforming thorns into peach trees, this is not achieved through a sudden burst of tears, but rather by stirring the innate feelings. Thieves must be very clever; how could they willingly fall into the old woman's trap? Yet, the old woman's method of ensnaring them is rooted in human nature. Even if the thief realizes they are not family, they cannot bear to lose their dignity, especially when the old woman had a way of knowing the family matters and speaking right to the heart of the matter. Therefore, the old woman's wisdom is unattainable, and the way she puts her wisdom into practice is even more so.

    挑绣

    Longzhou scholar Zou Daren, barely in his twenties, was handsome and graceful. He had fair skin but was simple-minded, knowing nothing of the world beyond his studies, and his fellow scholars all laughed at him. One day, he went to the market and saw a procession for a bride, with music and drums resounding, guests and attendants coming and going in a continuous stream. He was completely puzzled by what was happening. He asked a friend, who teased him, saying, "Don't you recognize? This is a family in our county that has just received an official position, showing off to others." The scholar believed him without doubt and, since he always valued fame and success, followed the procession happily. He went to the door and saw the new son-in-law performing the ritual of presenting a goose and driving the carriage, with elaborate ceremonies. He secretly admired it and stood there for a long time. Soon after, the colorful sedan chair came out again, and the procession started with a commotion. He faintly heard weeping inside the house. The scholar then clapped his hands and laughed loudly, saying, "This is also a great good thing, why be sad?" The onlookers all looked at each other in surprise, but the scholar was completely unaware.

    730

    翌日复出，遇送丧者，其状颇仿佛，而哭益哀。生愈不平，谓人曰：“当喜而戚，此行必近不祥。”闻者无不捧腹。其呆率多类此。庚午夏，读书于某寺。其地近山，素多魅，寺僧不胜其扰，皆徙去。同人以生痴，故怂恿使居之。生亦罔知所惧，入其室，蛛网罥户，蝠粪盈阶。生洒扫而处之，足不窥园，其读书益苦。居三日，殊无他异，同人以为痴福，亦不复劝徙。乃生自居寺中，每夜读，辄闻有笑声，生亦不顾。数夕后，天气炎蒸，生袒褐就月，执卷而读之，曼声长吟，深宵不辍。俄而砰然作响，院门辟矣。

    Explanation

    730

    The next day, the scholar went out again and encountered a funeral procession. The mourners' appearance was somewhat similar, but they cried even more sorrowfully. The scholar became even more resentful and told others, "They should be joyful but are sad; this behavior must be close to inauspicious." Those who heard this found it highly amusing. The scholar's simple-minded and straightforward nature often led him to such situations.

    In the summer of the year of the Yang Horse, the scholar studied at a certain temple. The place was close to the mountains and usually had many ghosts, which disturbed the temple monks so much that they all moved away. The fellow students thought the scholar was foolish, so they encouraged him to stay there. The scholar, ignorant of fear, entered his room, where spider webs hung from the door and bat droppings filled the steps. He swept and cleaned the room and settled in, his footsteps not venturing into the garden, and he studied even more diligently. After three days, there was no other abnormality, and the fellow students thought it was foolish luck, so they no longer advised him to move.

    The scholar, staying in the temple, read every night and often heard laughter, but he paid no attention. Several nights later, the weather was hot and humid, and the scholar unbuttoned his robe and leaned against the moonlight, holding the scroll and reading it softly and long, until late at night without stopping. Soon there was a loud noise, and the courtyard gate opened.

    731

    生愕然四顾，则二妇人新妆如画图，均衣薄縠，各执白纱小扇，飚然径入。生不以为意，诵如故。二妇逼近生身，戏以纤指抚其肌，笑曰：“个儿郎体固如玉哉！”言之似甚垂涎。生若不闻，读倍亟。二妇扪搎良久，见生漠然，反敛手抱惭而退。出门忽不见，生亦莫之讶，只曰：“山寺夜深，何来女娘，指爪且尖瘦，令人颇不耐。”言已，掩卷欲寐。旋又闻人声，其音娇细，笑曰：“予来视郎寝未？”入则一女子，年可二八，绿鬓微松，红腮带艳，白身无寸缕，掩笑立于生前。生视之，容如花绽，肤若脂凝，亦冥然不为动念。

    Explanation

    731

    The protagonist was startled and looked around, only to see two women freshly made up, their appearance as vivid as a painting. Both were dressed in thin, gauzy clothing and each held a small white silk fan, entering swiftly and directly. The protagonist paid no heed, continuing to recite as before. The two women approached him closely, playfully touching his skin with their slender fingers, smiling and saying, “This young man’s body is indeed as jade-like!” Their words seemed to imply great desire. The protagonist, as if not hearing, read even more urgently. The two women caressed him for a long time, seeing his indifference, and withdrew, hands folded in shame. Upon leaving, they suddenly vanished, and the protagonist was not surprised, merely remarking, “Deep in the mountain temple at night, what sort of young women are these, with sharp and slender fingernails, making me rather impatient.” After speaking, he closed the book and wanted to sleep. Soon, he heard a woman’s voice, soft and delicate, laughing and saying, “I come to see if my lord has fallen asleep.” Entering was a young woman, about sixteen years old, with slightly loose green hair and rosy cheeks tinged with beauty, her body bare with not a stitch of clothing, standing before him with a hidden smile. The protagonist looked at her, her face like a blooming flower, her skin like congealed fat, yet he remained unmoved in his mind.

    732

    但笑曰：“汝效祢衡，以清白之躯傲我乎？予体亦非不洁者。”因捋己裩，与之对立，皎然如双舞之鹤。女反羞缩，障面自返，细语曰：“若人只合偶痴鬼，当令挑绣来。”生言笑自若，徐徐著裤曰：“白雪之雪究不如白玉之白。”拂榻就枕，了无怖畏，其愚诚不可及。诘朝，无访戴者，生仍安于肄业。及昏，骤雨滂沱，砌下水深尺许。生方篝灯展卷，复闻笑语纷然曰：“予等送痴妇来伴痴郎，幸勿再劳璧谢。”生于灯下瞻视，则客夕二妇更偕数人，前女子亦在其中，则既衣裳楚楚矣。众拥一少艾者近生，曰：“以此为若妇，若愿之乎？”生亦莫之拒，反诘曰：“妇之说予殊不解。

    Explanation

    732

    He just laughed and said, "Are you emulating Mi Heng, using your pure and clean body to mock me? My body is not unclean either." Then he stroked his own pants and stood opposite her, his appearance as clear and graceful as two white cranes dancing. The woman became shy and retreated, covering her face and turning away, whispering, "This person should only be paired with a fool, let's come and play with me." The man spoke and laughed as usual, slowly putting on his pants and saying, "The snow in the White Snow is not as pure as the white jade." He smoothed the bed and lay down, completely fearless, his foolishness truly beyond comprehension. The next morning, no one came to visit, and the man continued his studies as usual. By evening, heavy rain poured down, and the water was deep enough to reach the feet. The man was just lighting a lamp and opening a book when he heard laughter and chatter, saying, "We are sending a foolish woman to accompany a foolish man, please don't trouble yourself to return the favor." Under the lamp, he looked up and saw that the two women from the previous night were accompanied by several more people, including the woman from before, who was now neatly dressed and attractive. They brought a young and beautiful woman close to him and said, "We will give this woman to you, do you wish for her?" The man did not refuse, but instead asked, "The woman's explanation to me is completely incomprehensible."

    733

    ”众曰：“达道之三，非夫妇乎？”生即取书翻阅一周，恍然曰：“是诚有之！予为夫而渠为妇，其此之谓欤？”众因哄堂大笑曰：“然。”生遽呼少艾为妇，绝无所虞，惟曰：“予正读书，欲究君臣之道，夫妇之义尚未暇及。学不躐等，汝曹可仍将妇去。”众不听，嘤咛一声，青衣数辈自外入，肆筵设席，强生与少艾并肩坐，饮以合卺之杯，绾以同心之带。生熟视少艾，容光如珠玉之润，体态如花柳之妍，心颇爱好。

    Explanation

    733

    The crowd said, "The three great principles, are they not the way of husband and wife?" The scholar immediately took a book and flipped through it, suddenly exclaiming, "Indeed, this is true! I am the husband and she is the wife, is this what you mean?" The crowd burst into laughter, saying, "Yes." The scholar then called the young lady his wife without any doubt, saying, "I am currently reading, and I intend to study the principles of ruler and subject, but the principles of husband and wife have not yet come to my attention. Learning does not skip levels, so you may take her away." However, the crowd did not listen, and with a soft cry, several officials in plain clothes entered from outside, spread out a banquet, and forced the scholar and the young lady to sit side by side, giving them wine from the marriage cup and tying them with a cord of unity. The scholar looked at the young lady closely, noting her radiant beauty like the luster of jade and pearls, and her graceful form like the beauty of flowers and willows, and he had a liking for her.

    734

    慨然曰：“得妇如此，于愿足矣！”少艾亦绝娇痴，毫无腼腆色，时以美目灼灼然顾生，且笑曰：“予夫忒无赖，予身几被看杀。”又语众曰：“予夫即若夫，胡不均此一杯羹，而使我一人独享耶？”众皆笑不能仰。大礼已成，众始列坐，浮白痛饮，谐谑甚欢。生与少艾独以白眼相看，四目交注，不饮亦不食，吃吃憨笑而已。众呼少艾为挑绣，生亦呼之。杯斝之侧犹以一卷自随，时复呫哔曰：“夫妇之义，予当与绣共参之。”须臾二鼓，众皆半酣，起曰：“新人夫妇当合欢，我辈且退。”遂纷纷俱出。旋有二人复返曰：“小儿女未谙情事，予得代为操持。

    Explanation

    734

    He said with a sigh, "To have a wife like this, my wishes are fully satisfied!" The young lady was also extremely playful and innocent, showing no signs of shyness. She often looked at him with bright, captivating eyes and laughed, saying, "My husband is really mischievous; my body is almost being killed by looking at him." She then addressed the crowd, "My husband is your husband too; why don't we share this bowl of soup instead of letting me enjoy it alone?" Everyone laughed so hard they couldn't look up. After the great ceremony was completed, everyone sat down, drinking to the point of intoxication, and engaged in lively and humorous banter. The husband and the young lady looked at each other with a blank stare, their eyes meeting and locked, neither drinking nor eating, just laughing shyly and innocently. The crowd called the young lady "Pick and Embroider," and the husband called her the same. She still had a scroll with her by the cups and would occasionally whisper, "The duties of husband and wife, I should share them with you, Pick and Embroider." After a while, when the second drumbeat sounded, everyone was half-drunk and stood up, saying, "The newlyweds should be happy together; let's retreat." They all left one by one. Shortly after, two people returned and said, "Young people don't understand matters of love; I can help manage the situation."

    735

    ”乃为生展衾缛，牵二人衣使皆登榻，以手抚其枕曰：“今夕共此，明年抱子矣！”语毕含笑而去，疾风烈雨，亦不知其安往。生与绣并头而眠，究未解床笫之事。生少卧即起曰：“夫妇有别，予不可以无礼。”乃东向而坐。绣闻生言，亦起西向而坐，瞑目存息，莫通片语。倦极思睡，倚璧鼾然，睫甫交而东方明矣。虫飞薨薨，众又早至。入见生绣垂头对坐，形如土木偶，不禁失笑曰：“蚩蚩者果无情耶？”绣见众入，亟起离榻，欲从之返，曰：“闷杀阿奴。与夫处固不若与姊戏也。”众又粲然曰：“婢子亦大憨生。妆已有夫，从予归将奚为？”绣乃垂涕不已，嘤嘤作小儿啼。

    Explanation

    735

    He then spread out the quilt and bedding for the young man, pulled both of their clothes and made them climb onto the bed, stroking the pillow with his hand and saying, "Tonight we share this, and next year we will hold a child!" After speaking, he smiled and left, in a fierce wind and heavy rain, one does not know where he went. The young man and the girl lay down with their heads together, but he did not understand the matters of the bed. Soon after lying down, he got up and said, "There is a distinction between husband and wife; I cannot be without propriety." He sat facing east. Upon hearing his words, the girl also got up and sat facing west, closing her eyes and conserving energy, not uttering a word. Exhausted and wanting to sleep, she leaned against the wall and snored, as soon as her eyelids closed, the east began to brighten. Insects flew in a noisy swarm, and the group arrived early again. Upon entering, they saw the young man and the girl sitting with heads down, their appearance was like wooden or clay statues, and they could not help but laugh and said, "Are those simple ones really without feeling?" Upon seeing the group enter, the girl quickly got up from the bed, wanting to follow them back, saying, "I am dying of boredom, little one. Being with a husband is certainly not as good as playing with a sister." The group then said with a bright smile, "The maid is also quite naive. You already have a husband; what would you do if you followed me back?" The girl then tears fell continuously, crying softly like a baby.

    736

    众匿笑为之整妆，事毕自去。绣自是在生室中，日为生缮衣履，主饔飧，瀹茗煮酒，迄无倦容。暇则掘土和泥，学为儿戏，绝不类闺阁成人。其所作多玩器，瓶彝樽鼎，巧擅人工，亦未卜其所用。生并不诘，诵读如常。而自与绣处，虽盛暑未尝露臂，相对如宾，绣亦如之。夜即共卧一榻，而东西异向，恒虚其中。如是者数夕，未及于私。生固寒士，家惟寡嫂，因僦居于外，旬日一归省视。出即遭友于途，询生近业，生遽曰：“兄宜为贺，学有长进，迩来又悟得‘夫妇也’一句意义。”友惊问之，历历敷陈，不少讳。友素与人忠，亟曰：“此系鬼狐，能为人祸者，君宜速避之。

    Explanation

    736

    The crowd secretly laughed and helped her tidy up her makeup, and she left after finishing the task. Xiu then stayed in the room of the scholar, daily sewing clothes and shoes for him, preparing meals, brewing tea, and cooking wine, without ever showing signs of fatigue. In her leisure time, she dug soil and mixed mud, learning to play childish games, which were unlike the behavior of a typical woman. The objects she created were mostly toys, such as bottles, vases, jugs, and tripods, skillfully crafted with human-like precision, though their purpose remained unknown. The scholar did not question her, continuing his studies as usual. When they were together, even in the heat of summer, he never exposed his arms, and they treated each other with the formality of guests, Xiu doing the same. At night, they slept on the same bed, facing opposite directions, always leaving a space between them. This arrangement continued for several nights without any intimate contact. The scholar, being a poor scholar, lived outside with only his widowed sister-in-law, returning home every ten days to visit. When he went out, he met a friend on the road, who asked about his recent studies. The scholar quickly replied, "You should congratulate me; there has been progress in my studies. Recently, I have understood the meaning of the phrase 'husband and wife.'" The friend was surprised and asked for details, to which the scholar explained in full, without any concealment. The friend, who was always honest with others, immediately said, "This is a ghost or fox spirit, capable of causing harm to people. You should quickly avoid it."

    737

    ”生犹未达，惟应曰：“诺。”遂不复往嫂处，遄归寺中。入室即谓绣曰：“友言妇为鬼狐，其信乎？”绣炯炯相视，无一言。生取书证之，至鬼神无形与声，大恚曰：“友误我！此固有形与声者。”又读至狐狸食之，益恚曰：“友欺我！此固不能啖人者。”因复不信友，与绣处如故。乃自此友皆与闻，相约偕来，以觇其异。入所居，绣适不在，因以诘生。生又详言之，友乃坚请一见。生曰：“适往后园移花，少顷即来矣。”未几果至，以红巾罥首，贮花于皀，珊珊而来。众视之，神仙中人也。

    Explanation

    737

    The protagonist, still uncertain, only replied, "Yes." He then ceased to visit his sister-in-law and hurried back to the temple. Upon entering the room, he immediately said to Xiù, "A friend said that my wife is a ghost fox. Is this true?" Xiù looked at him intently but remained silent. The protagonist took out books to prove his point, up to the point where it was mentioned that ghosts and spirits have no form or sound, and he became greatly angry, saying, "My friend misled me! This one indeed has form and sound." He then continued reading, and when he came across the part about the fox eating, he grew even angrier, saying, "My friend deceived me! This one indeed cannot eat people." Therefore, he no longer trusted his friend and lived with Xiù as before. From then on, all his friends heard about it and agreed to come together to observe its strangeness. When they entered the residence, Xiù happened to be absent, so they questioned the protagonist. He explained in detail, and his friends then firmly requested to meet her. The protagonist said, "I just went to the back garden to move some flowers. I will be back soon." Soon after, she indeed arrived, with a red scarf tied around her head, holding flowers in a basket, and coming gracefully. The crowd looked at her and exclaimed, "She is a person among immortals!"

    738

    绣见客绝不惊避，委花地上，踞坐而以手植之，旁若无人。众见其衣有缝，其行有影，且不匿迹销声，亦不敢臆定为异类。坐谈至夕，绣亦不时入斋，但不与客交言，中馈之事，固其所司也。客去，而两人相欢仍如往日。友有善谑者，一日谓生曰：“兄与尊嫂处，亦同衾处否？”答曰：“未也。”友笑曰：“奚为不同？”生曰：“予读内则，七年男女不同席。席且不同，况衾乎？”友冁然曰：“嘻！君误矣。夫妇非男女比也。诗不云乎，角枕粲兮，锦衾烂兮。不同衾，胡为怨其独旦耶？”生即庄容谢曰：“谨受教。

    Explanation

    738

    绣 saw the guest without showing any surprise or fear, casually placing flowers on the ground, sitting cross-legged, and planting them with her hands, as if no one were around. The guests noticed that she had clothes with seams and a shadow, and since she did not hide or make a fuss, they did not immediately assume she was an unusual being. They sat and talked until evening, and绣 also did not frequently enter the room, but she did not speak to the guest, as household affairs, especially those related to the kitchen, were naturally her responsibility. When the guest left, the two of them remained as happy as before. One of the friends, who was good at joking, said to the other one day, "Do you sleep in the same bed with your wife?" The other answered, "No." The friend laughed and said, "Why not?" The other replied, "I read in the Nei Ze that men and women should not sit on the same mat for seven years. If they can't even sit on the same mat, how can they expect to share the same bed?" The friend chuckled and said, "Oh, you're mistaken. Husband and wife are not just like any man and woman. The Shijing doesn't say so? 'A horned pillow shines brightly, a brocade quilt is splendid.' If they don't share the same quilt, why would they complain about being alone at dawn?" The other person then bowed respectfully and said, "I will take your advice."

    739

    ”归而谋诸妇曰：“友令我与子同衾，子其勿辞。”绣亦无难色，第询曰：“书有之乎？”生曰：“书不言而诗则有之。尽信书，固不若信诗也。”是夕遂合被而寝，而衣缕各未去身，转侧殊多不便。生晨起即诣友处，相告曰：“过听子言，使我永夕不能寐。”友询之，以状对。友又笑曰：“同衾而不去衣，犹异衾耳，子焉能寐？”生骇然曰：“衣可去乎？何据乎？未之前闻也。”友因绐之曰：“子不善读书，宜其愚也。孟子曰：‘尔为尔，我为我，袒裼裸裎于我侧，故由由然与之偕。’袒裼等字，皆去衣之谓。

    Explanation

    739

    Returning home, he consulted his wife, saying, "My friend has instructed me to share the same blanket with you, please do not decline." His wife, without any difficulty, merely asked, "Is this mentioned in the books?" He replied, "The books do not say so, but the poems do. To completely trust the books is less reliable than trusting the poems." That night, they slept together under the same blanket, but their clothes were still on their bodies, making it very inconvenient to turn over. The next morning, he went to see his friend and told him, "I took your words too seriously, which kept me awake all night." His friend inquired about the situation, and he described it. His friend then laughed and said, "Sharing the same blanket without removing clothes is still like sleeping alone, how could you sleep?" He was startled and asked, "Can we remove our clothes? What is the basis for this? I have never heard of such a thing before." His friend then explained, "You are not good at reading, so it is natural that you are ignorant. Mencius said, 'You are you, and I am I, naked and exposed by my side, so I would naturally go along with it.' The words '袒裼' all mean 'to remove clothing.'"

    740

    不如是，宁能由由偕寐乎？”生亦笑曰：“审是则‘虽’字与‘焉能’句皆衍文耶？”友亦忍笑答之曰：“是矣。”生遂深信不疑。适遇他事，归已昏瞑，不及阅书，又谋诸绣曰：“友令我去衣与子同衾，其可乎？”绣始有难色，又询以书言若何。生叹曰：“读书而不明句读，从前皆浪掷居诸。微友言，吾不知也。”乃强绣尽解其衣，登床覆衾而寐。腻肌交拥，神魂荡然，遂熟睡达旦。明日遇友，即谢曰：“君言不诬，黑甜乡予已近之矣！”友曰：“恐子未近温柔乡也。”生复讶曰：“温柔乡犹有异乎？”友乃缅陈居室之道，生闻之津津入耳。

    Explanation

    740

    Without this, how could we sleep peacefully together?" The student laughed and said, "If this is true, then are the '虽' character and the '焉能' phrase redundant?" The friend, with a restrained smile, replied, "Yes." The student then believed it without doubt. Later, encountering other matters, he returned home in the dark, unable to read the book, and consulted with his wife, saying, "The friend told me to take off my clothes and sleep with you under the same quilt, is that acceptable?" His wife initially hesitated, then asked what the book said about it. The student sighed, "Reading without understanding punctuation, all my efforts before were wasted. Without my friend's words, I wouldn't have known." He then insisted that his wife remove her clothes, and they slept together under the quilt. Their smooth skin intertwined, and their spirits were enchanted, and they slept soundly until dawn. The next day, meeting the friend, he thanked him, saying, "Your words were not false; I have already approached the land of sweet dreams!" The friend said, "I fear you have not yet approached the land of warmth and softness." The student was surprised, "Is there anything different about the land of warmth and softness?" The friend then described in detail the ways of living together, and the student listened with deep interest.

    741

    惟曰：“夫妇之大伦若此者，无乃亵甚？”友笑曰：“君又未读易矣。大传有云：‘夫妇媾精，儿女化生。’不然，子之先祀且几斩。”生惧然揖曰：“仆甚愚，见不及此。非仆也敢专承之，其自祖父以上，咸承吾友之赐。”言讫茫茫然归，友又为之绝倒。生入寺，天犹未夕，即又谋之妇，使共寝。绣曰：“昼可寐乎？”生曰：“昼眠夕寐，眠与寐同也。”绣从之。生如友教，甫一合而绣即呻吟欲起奔避，曰：“君今者殊不怀好意，予不再与夫处矣。”生又坚下之，绣不胜其创，泫然泪下。

    Explanation

    741

    The friend said, "Isn't it too intimate for a husband and wife to be in such a situation?" The friend smiled and said, "You haven't read the Yijing yet. It is said in the Yijing: 'When a husband and wife come together, children are born.' Otherwise, your ancestral line will be nearly cut off." The speaker, feeling afraid, bowed and said, "I am very foolish and lack insight. I do not dare to take responsibility alone. My ancestors and I have all benefited from your help." After saying this, he left in a daze, and the friend laughed heartily. The speaker entered the temple, and it was still not evening, so he immediately planned to act with his wife. The wife said, "Can one sleep during the day?" The speaker said, "Sleeping during the day is the same as sleeping at night." The wife agreed. The speaker followed the friend's advice, but as soon as they came together, the wife began to groan and wanted to get up and run away, saying, "You have a bad intention today. I will no longer stay with you." The speaker insisted, and the wife could not bear the pain, crying with tears.

    742

    生素未睹此状，悱恻不忍，力少纵，而绣逸，生裸逐之，倏忽不见。方呆立，而他友适来，见即大笑曰：“是何形状！”生正色答曰：“予欲与妇媾精，以延先人一脉，此亦伦常中要务，君何哂为？”友不禁鼓掌，强之入室，俟其衣而后坐谈，既晦乃去。生既失妇，不胜懊闷。比及漏下，前之众妇复来，拥绣入门，笑曰：“苦我家妮子，情实不甘。”生朗应曰：“昔未妇予，汝为政；今既妇予，我为政。不甘将如何？”妇皆喜曰：“痴儿亦甚强项。”乃并力纳绣于榻，顾谓生曰：“逋逃者既已还君，再遁勿予问也。”释手而退。生阖户解衣，欲与绣狎，绣退却不敢复近。

    Explanation

    742

    He had never seen such a state before, and deeply moved by it, he couldn't bear it. He released a little force, and the embroidered one became unrestrained; he chased after it naked, but it suddenly disappeared. Standing there dazed, another friend just arrived and, seeing the situation, burst out laughing, saying, "What kind of shape is this?" He replied seriously, "I want to copulate with my wife to continue the lineage of my ancestors, this is also a crucial duty in human relationships, why are you laughing?" The friend couldn't help clapping, forcefully took him into the room, waited for him to dress before sitting and talking, and left after nightfall. He had already lost his wife and was unable to bear the frustration. By the time the water clock dropped, the previous many women returned, carried the embroidered one into the door, and laughed, saying, "Sigh, my little girl, in reality, not willing." He replied loudly, "In the past, when she was not my wife, you were in charge; now that she is my wife, I am in charge. What will you do if you are not willing?" The women all smiled and said, "This foolish child is also quite stubborn." They then worked together to place the embroidered one on the bed and turned to him, saying, "The one who fled has already been returned to you; if she flees again, we will not interfere." They let go and left. He closed the door, undressed, and wanted to be intimate with the embroidered one, but she retreated and dared not come near again.

    743

    生强之，鹃血啼红，倍极呻楚。迨至芙蓉露下夫妇俱有味乎其中。生乃怡然曰：“予今日乃识此真乐。”自是无虚夕，绣亦渐入佳境，不再如向之推拒矣。生既与绣为欢，自谓佳趣，遇人辄道之，闻者皆目笑。偶诣嫂处，言之綦详，嫂之兄适在座，艴然色变曰：“是何言也！”生笑曰：“事无不可对人言，岂嫂独不可谈耶？”竟恬然不以为怪。后因绣有娠，谋徙于家。绣令生尽将所作者移归，嫂见之大粲曰：“子家宛若，奁具乃如是之丰？”绣亦不惭，嬉戏如故，而事嫂维谨。明年，绣举一子，家益赤贫。绣命生以泥器如市，索价甚昂，嫂又以为病狂。

    Explanation

    743

    Sheng insisted, and the cuckoo's blood-stained tears turned red, indicating extreme pain. When the lotus dew fell, both the husband and wife found pleasure in each other. Sheng then happily said, "Today I have come to know this true joy." From then on, there was no night without intimacy, and Xiu also gradually entered a better state, no longer rejecting as before. Sheng, having found joy with Xiu, considered it delightful and would often speak of it to others, who would all laugh at him. Once, when he visited his sister-in-law, he spoke of it in great detail, and his sister-in-law's brother happened to be present, turning pale and saying, "What are you saying!" Sheng laughed and said, "There is nothing that cannot be said to others; could it be that only your sister-in-law cannot be spoken to?" He remained unperturbed and did not consider it strange. Later, because Xiu became pregnant, they planned to move to her family home. Xiu ordered Sheng to take all his creations back, and when his sister-in-law saw them, she laughed heartily and said, "Your home is like a place of abundance, with such a rich array of items!" Xiu also did not feel ashamed and continued to play as usual, while serving her sister-in-law with utmost care. The following year, Xiu gave birth to a son, and the family became increasingly poor. Xiu then ordered Sheng to sell pottery in the market, setting the price very high, which his sister-in-law thought was another sign of madness.

    744

    生至暮竟携千金以归，而货犹未售其半，始大惊。视之，乃古铜也。嫂由是奇绣，而生与妇亦不再痴。连举三雄，家益巨富。处五年，绣忽辞去曰：“夙缘已满，当别矣。”生惊询其故，答曰：“妾非人，实鬼也。生前以痴故，见弃于人，郁郁以死。赖诸姊妹以术授予，渐觉聪颖，而痴情犹未尽绝，因以冥数与君配合。今将转轮往生富贵家，望君勿以妾为念。”生诘其何所，俯而不答，颦眉曰：“再来人似不必相识。”辗转而殁，化为淡烟以逝。生思之不置，乃以三子属其嫂，遨游于湖湘间，不复言娶。究不知为绣作合者为鬼为狐，每以未及问绣为憾。

    　　外史氏曰：痴者天全以其痴，绝无所欲也。故虽艳质当前，淫妖触目，而彼无见无闻。具此质地，可以仙，可以佛，并可以圣贤，所全不亦大哉！乃自燕朋失志，导以情关，后遂不能复痴。如此之友，最能坏人心术，切宜远之。在人以为救痴之药石，予以为祸生之疚疢，良不可不辨。

    Explanation

    744

    The husband returned home late at night, carrying a thousand gold pieces, and was greatly surprised to find that half of the goods he had brought still remained unsold. Upon closer inspection, he realized they were ancient bronze. His sister-in-law was now impressed by Xiu, and the husband and his wife no longer acted foolishly. They subsequently gave birth to three sons, and their family became increasingly wealthy. After five years, Xiu suddenly bid farewell, saying, "Our fated connection has been fulfilled; it is time to part ways." The husband, startled, asked her why she was leaving. She replied, "I am not a human but a ghost. Before my death, due to my foolishness, I was abandoned by people and died in sorrow. Thanks to my sisters who taught me with their skills, I gradually became more intelligent, but my foolish affection was still not entirely extinguished. Thus, our fated numbers aligned, and we were matched. Now I will be reborn into a wealthy family. I hope you do not think of me." The husband asked where she would go, but she bowed her head and did not answer, frowning and saying, "Those who come again may not need to know each other." She passed away after much hesitation, transforming into a faint mist and departing. The husband could not stop thinking about her and entrusted his three sons to his sister-in-law, wandering around the Hu-Xiang region and no longer speaking of marriage. He ultimately did not know whether it was a ghost or a fox that matched them, and he regretted not asking Xiu.

    The historian commented: The foolish are fully endowed with their foolishness, having absolutely no desires. Thus, even when beautiful women are before them and seductive demons are everywhere, they see and hear nothing. With this nature, they can become immortals, Buddhas, or sages, and isn't this a great fulfillment? However, losing one's resolve due to close companions and being guided by emotional entanglements, they cannot remain foolish. Such friends are most capable of corrupting one's heart and mind, and it is best to stay far away from them. What others consider a remedy for foolishness, I see as a cause of disaster and illness, and it is truly necessary to distinguish.

    745

    随园老人曰：刻画痴状笔笔如生，而一管写二痴人，尤擅绝技，吾每望而畏之。

    ○　田一桂
    　　田一桂，大梁人。父为洛中陶朱，至一桂始中衰，然犹拥万金之产，里中单寒之族，莫敢望。一桂幼失所怙，惟母在，为聘于同邑巨家。岳卢姓，女名四娘，天姿绰约人也。一桂年十七即行亲迎。四娘色既殊，尤兼饶机智，当新婚之夕，即心窃计曰：“富室之儿，骄傲性成，非有以缚束之，少纵即逝矣。”于是温柔旖旎之中，别寓峭厉棱角之意。拒之而不峻拒，予之而不轻予。腼腆之余，微加以色笑，柔顺之内，隐示以端严。而或推或拒，或送或迎，女儿情性，令人不可捉摸。只此一夕，丈夫之气既夺已。三朝庙见，娩婉倍于寻常。

    Explanation

    745

    The Old Man of Sui Garden said: "To depict the foolishness vividly, stroke by stroke as if alive, and to portray two foolish people with one brush, it is especially a masterful technique; I always look at it with awe."

    ○ Tian Yigui
    Tian Yigui was from Daliang. His father was a wealthy merchant in Luoyang, and it was only with Tian Yigui that the family began to decline. However, he still possessed a fortune of ten thousand gold. Among the poor families in the village, none dared to compare. Tian Yigui lost his father at a young age, and with only his mother left, he was married to a wealthy family in the same county. His mother-in-law's surname is Lu, and her daughter's name is Si Niang. Si Niang is a woman of natural grace and charm. At the age of seventeen, Tian Yigui went to fetch his bride. Si Niang is exceptionally beautiful and also very intelligent. On their wedding night, she secretly thought to herself, "The son of a wealthy family, naturally arrogant. Unless there is something to restrain him, he will quickly become reckless." In her gentle and charming demeanor, she subtly included sharp and angular elements. She refused without being harshly rejecting, and gave without being easily giving. In her bashfulness, she slightly added a smile. In her gentleness, she subtly showed a sense of dignity. Sometimes pushing, sometimes refusing, sometimes sending, sometimes welcoming, her daughterly nature is unpredictable. Just this one night, her husband's spirit was already taken. On the third day of the wedding, during the formal meeting, she was even more gentle and kind than usual.

    746

    且善窥姑意，候其喜怒，察其心情，视寒燠，洁饮食，孝妇之名以著。又善处戚族间，无纤毫失礼，人因贺一桂为得贤助。独是当闺帷之地，操纵良多。自花烛以后，昼遇一桂，从不以笑语相通。行必异路，居必异席，若反目者然。及夫寝门已闭，一室燕私，一桂言或涉邪，则必正色规戒，责其轻浮，甚至泣下沾襟，自嗟薄命。抑且裳衣无自解之时，粉泽无轻沾之日。皀席甫就，笑面即无。衾枕虽同，羞容时有。较之结褵之始，殆有甚焉。至一桂绝望欲寝，则又絮谈家事，故搅其眠，屡转腰肢，阴破其睡。洎乎蝶梦难成，花枝欲戏，又必强而后可。

    Explanation

    746

    She is adept at observing her mother-in-law's intentions, watching her moods, and examining her feelings, ensuring that the temperature is comfortable and the food and drink are clean, thus earning the reputation of a filial daughter-in-law. She also handles relations with relatives well, never being impolite in the slightest, and people congratulate her husband for gaining a wise helper. However, in the private quarters, there is a lot of manipulation and control. After the wedding, she never communicates with her husband through laughter and words during the day; they always take different paths and sit on different seats, like enemies. Once the bedroom door is closed, in the privacy of their room, if her husband's words might involve immorality, she will certainly scold him with a serious expression, blaming him for being frivolous, even to the point of crying so much that her sleeves are wet, lamenting her short life or bad fate. Moreover, she never undresses herself, never lightly applies makeup. The bed is just set up, and she immediately stops smiling. Although the bedding is the same, she sometimes has an embarrassed expression. Compared to the beginning of the marriage, it is even worse. When her husband is desperate and wants to sleep, she talks about family matters, disturbing his sleep, often turning her waist, secretly breaking his sleep. Until the butterfly dream is hard to achieve, the flowers want to play, and she must be forced to comply.

    747

    而缱绻之下，倍极绸缪，柳既宛转迎风，云更低徊恋雨。一颦一笑，尽态极妍，使一桂得之不易，舍之实难，其颠倒于情中不可言喻。四娘又善于修饰，每以晨省为名，盥濯绝早，恒呼一桂与之偕。既起，令坐于侧，观其梳裹。间或使之掠鬓，或役之簪花，虽不必代画眉妩，而朱粉之浓淡，钗钿之高低，无不含笑与商。及昏，复如之，益穷艳冶。髻则散挽浓云，更添膏泽；容则浅匀腻玉，另染铅华。虽不副笄六珈，从未慵妆草草。至此，必命一桂捧镜于前，己乃徘徊顾影。

    Explanation

    747

    In the midst of their intimate and affectionate relationship, their bond was exceptionally deep and tender. Willow, graceful and delicate, moved with the wind, while Cloud lingered and yearned, deeply attached to the rain. Every frown and smile revealed her full beauty and grace, making it difficult for Yi Gui to part with her, and even more so to leave her. The depth of their love was beyond words. Si Niang was also skilled in adorning herself. She often called Yi Gui to join her in the morning, claiming it was for the traditional practice of morning visits to show filial piety. She would rise early to wash and groom herself, always inviting Yi Gui to accompany her. Once they were up, she would have him sit beside her and watch her comb and style her hair. Sometimes, she would ask him to comb her hair, or to help her insert flowers into her hair. Although she didn't always need him to draw her eyebrows, she would discuss with him the varying levels of red and white makeup, the placement of hairpins and ornaments, and they would laugh together. By evening, she would repeat the same routine, even more elaborately. Her hair was loosely gathered and styled like a dense cloud, adding more luster. Her face was lightly and smoothly adorned, with a touch of cosmetics. Though she didn't wear the elaborate hair accessories and decorations of noblewomen, she never rushed through her grooming. Finally, she would command Yi Gui to hold a mirror in front of her, and she would pause to admire her reflection.

    748

    值深宵密室之中，当银烛金釭之侧，见此丽人，遇此韵事，即以宋广平当之，铁石心未能不动，况一桂乎？以故房帷之爱，惟恐不深，积渐而尾大不掉，四娘之威乃日肆。初一桂之迎娶也，契友祝希年曾戒之曰：“妇人女子所以挟制其夫者，以色故也。君少年，不免好色，但庄以莅之，正以率之，可以无患。若狎暱无忌，则妇纲将倒置矣。”一桂不能从。弥月后，以事触怒四娘，面有爪痕。祝见之，即笑曰：“好色者果不免此。向从予言，何至于是？”一桂殊不介意，归且以之语四娘，四娘甚恚。适祝来过访，一桂呼茶，四娘以饴和椒涂其盏，使婢馈客。祝不知，径啜之，吻若胶粘，且麻不可耐。及视其器，始悟四娘怪其言，将使学周庙金人三缄其口耳。

    Explanation

    748

    In the depths of the night, within a secret chamber, by the side of silver candles and golden lamps, seeing this beautiful woman and encountering this elegant affair, even a heart of iron and stone could not remain unmoved, let alone one of Gui. Therefore, the love in the bedroom, fearing it was not deep enough, gradually grew uncontrollable, and the authority of the fourth lady became increasingly bold. When Gui first married, his close friend Zhu Xiniang had warned him: "Women use their beauty to control their husbands. As a young man, you cannot help but be attracted to beauty. But govern with seriousness, lead with propriety, and you can avoid trouble. If you flirt without restraint, the hierarchy of women will be inverted." Gui did not follow his advice. After a month, he angered the fourth lady with some matter, and she left claw marks on his face. Zhu saw this and laughed, saying, "Those who love beauty indeed cannot avoid this. Had you followed my words, how could you have ended up like this?" Gui was not particularly bothered and, upon returning home, told the fourth lady about it. She was very angry. Just as Zhu came to visit, Gui called for tea. The fourth lady coated the cup with honey and pepper and had a servant bring it to the guest. Zhu did not know and drank it directly. His lips felt like they were glued together, and the numbness was unbearable. When he looked at the cup, he realized that the fourth lady was angry at his words and intended to make him learn from the golden man in the Zhou temple to keep his mouth shut.

    749

    大笑而去，后亦不再哓哓。未几，一桂之母卒，四娘执丧既毕，乃自言曰：“吾无蒸梨之小过，渠即欲出之，无名矣。”由是益横。一日，以烹调失节迁怒一桂，突以羹碗掷之，曰：“田舍郎乃以草具食我佳人耶？”一桂幸未被伤，嗣是庖人之职，恒亲临之。而稍有不洁不甘，四娘辄取以饲犬，兼碎其盛。且自此四娘之室，数夕不容鼾卧矣。一桂甚惧，惴惴然惟虑或撄其怒。然遇脱辐之顷，犹常反唇相诋，未遽至俯首帖耳也。四娘每不平，以为笼络未至。复借端与之争，自兹竟不再与言。及夕亦纳之，不至如前之闭门，一桂心窃喜。

    Explanation

    749

    She laughed and left, and later, she also stopped arguing or nagging. Not long after, the mother of Yi Gui passed away, and after completing the funeral rites, she said to herself, "I have no minor fault like steaming pears; if he wants to expel me, he has no reason." Therefore, she became even more domineering. One day, she blamed Yi Gui for a cooking mishap and suddenly threw a bowl of soup at him, saying, "A rustic peasant would feed a beautiful lady with such coarse food?" Fortunately, he was not injured, and thereafter, he personally oversaw the cooking duties. If there was any dirtiness or dissatisfaction, Si Niang would take it and feed it to the dog, along with breaking the container. From then on, he could not sleep soundly in Si Niang's room for several nights. Yi Gui was very afraid, trembling in fear of provoking her anger. When the opportunity arose, he still often retorted, but he did not immediately become submissive. Si Niang felt it was not enough, believing that he had not been sufficiently appeased. She found another excuse to argue with him, and from then on, she no longer spoke to him. At night, she let him in, but not as severely as before. Yi Gui secretly rejoiced in this change.

    750

    及入室中，早已别设一榻，以朱绳界之，遍悬和鸾，一触之即洋洋盈耳。复命婢值宿廊庑，有声则告警，不言则笞，婢亦莫敢不承。四娘既安置一桂讫，乃自明烛晚妆，倍加涂泽，膏兰之气，盈室袭人。及寝，复较前放旷，莲花换瓣不必灯昏，玉树流辉何须月皎。绣带有亲松之日，锦衾有缓覆之时，此皆数年伉俪未经阅历者，一旦遇之，当亦情不自禁。无如鸳鸯枕畔，白刃双横，凛不可犯。四娘又怒目言曰：“予既拚此弱质，倘无耻者钻穴逾墙，纵不戕其生命，亦必残其肌肤，誓不再与薄倖儿同眠此榻。”一桂备闻其言，不禁恇怯，虽摇目荡心，无敢妄动，随分独眠，转侧未安。

    Explanation

    750

    Upon entering the room, a separate bed had already been set up, separated by a red rope and adorned with harmonious bells. Upon touching it, the sound filled the ears abundantly. Yi Gui then ordered the maids to guard the corridors, instructing them to report any sound and to be beaten if they did not speak. The maids dared not disobey. After arranging for Yi Gui, she lit the candles and put on her evening makeup, applying it more lavishly. The fragrance of orchids filled the room and enveloped the person. Upon sleeping, she was even more unrestrained than before, the lotus petals changing without the need for dimming the lights, the jade tree radiating light without the need for a bright moon. The embroidered sashes were loose on the day, the brocade quilt loosely covered. These were experiences that had not been experienced in many years of marriage. Upon encountering it, one would also be unable to control their emotions. But on the pillow of the mandarin ducks, two white blades were crossed, it was stern and unassailable. Yi Gui spoke angrily with a stern look, "I have already decided to sacrifice my frail body. If someone without shame were to burrow through walls, even if they do not harm their life, they will certainly injure their skin. I swear never to sleep with a fickle person on this bed again." Yi Gui, having heard these words, could not help but feel fearful. Though his eyes were fluttering and his heart was stirred, he dared not act rashly. He slept alone, turning restlessly and unable to find peace.

    751

    四娘忽若惊觉，伏枕遍观曰：“铃似有故，予不可疏于防闲。”竟不索衣而起，绕室巡行。其所以射入卧者之目而隐以动之者，当非一肌一容已。一桂果大动，亟起而就之。视其腕，则森森霜锷犹然在握，遂惧不能复前。既而见其复卧，叹曰：“遭际狂夫，使我心恫。不然，值此良宵，斯时可以同梦矣。”言已，似有欷歔之声。一桂因以怨艾，反不咎四娘而自咎。无何，夜风入牖，铎声微敲。户外即群呼曰：“室中有寇！”一桂亦不觉失笑。四娘仍前周视，良久始眠。又戒群婢曰：“人行则铃必大震，小警固不必告也。但当醒睡勿偷安，犯之挞无赦。

    Explanation

    751

    Four Nanny suddenly as if awakened from a dream, lay on her pillow and looked around, saying, "The bell seems to have a reason, I cannot be negligent in vigilance." She finally got up without searching for clothes and walked around the room. The subtle movements that caught the eye of the one lying down must not be just a single muscle or expression. One Gui indeed made a big movement, hastily getting up to approach her. Looking at his wrist, the sharp blades were still firmly held, so he was afraid and could not move forward again. After a while, seeing her lying down again, he sighed, "To encounter a madman has made my heart ache. Otherwise, at this beautiful night, this moment could have been for sharing dreams." After speaking, there seemed to be sounds of sobbing. One Gui thus became resentful, blaming himself instead of Four Nanny. Soon, the night wind entered the window, faintly knocking the bell. Outside, a group shouted, "There is an intruder in the room!" One Gui also couldn't help but laugh. Four Nanny continued to inspect the room thoroughly, and after a long time, she finally fell asleep. She then warned the group of maids, "When someone walks, the bell must surely ring loudly. For minor alarms, it is certainly not necessary to report. But one should stay awake and not take advantage of the situation; those who violate it will be punished without mercy."

    752

    ”婢皆敬诺。四娘径灭烛熟寐，盖料其夫无斩关之勇也。如是三夕，一桂不能复耐，俟其巡视，即长跪于阑中，哀恳曰：“予知罪矣！盍贳我，使得同衾乎？”四娘不之顾，一桂俯伏不起，词益卑，渐至于流涕。四娘稔其诚服，乃数之曰：“若以男子而凌虐一女流，畴能与若抗衡也？如欲复合，非从予三事不可。”一桂请其说，四娘曰：“予性不耐繁言，但能无动手，无动足，且无动口而已，予岂多求耶？”一桂究不能解，四娘始为剖晰曰：“若夙昔亦能帖伏，独恨若怒即弗恤耳。嗣后小杖则受，大杖弗走，无挽戈夺戟之心；呼之即前，麾之不退，无突围破阵之念。

    Explanation

    752

    The maids all respectfully acknowledged. Four娘 extinguished the candle and fell into a deep sleep, having anticipated that her husband lacked the courage to break in forcibly. For three consecutive nights, One桂 could no longer bear it, and awaited his inspection, prostrating himself at the railing and pleading earnestly, "I know I have sinned! Why not pardon me, allowing me to share the same bed?" Four娘 did not look at him, and One桂 remained prostrate, his words becoming increasingly humble, gradually reaching the point of tears. Four娘, convinced of his sincerity, then listed the conditions, saying, "If a man were to bully a woman, who could stand up to you? If you wish to reconcile, it is not possible without following my three conditions." One桂 asked for the explanation, and Four娘 said, "My nature cannot tolerate much talk; just not to touch, not to move, and not to speak, am I asking for much?" One桂 still could not understand, and Four娘 began to explain in detail, "In the past, you could also be submissive, but hated that when you were angry you did not care. Afterward, if it is a light beating, accept it; if it is a heavy beating, do not run away, without the intention to seize weapons; when called, go forward; when dismissed, do not retreat, without the thought of breaking through encirclements or destroying formations."

    753

    至于牙齿之际，为害无多，唾面可以自干，恶声不容或反。果能兢业如斯，且老死而后已，予当不念前愆，收若同榻。否则，此生不必奢望。”一桂唯唯受命，坚以自矢。四娘乃语婢使退，尽撤其悬，引一桂与同寝处。是夜极意款洽，一桂乐甚。譬之枵腹之夫，忽遇屠门大嚼，呼尔蹴尔，又何能不受不屑哉？自此奉法惟谨，小有所犯，四娘即呼婢笞之，固强忍不嘶也。四娘或加意辱之，亦跬步弗移也。呼之为牛，不敢应之以马。詈之终日，不敢复以片言。而巾帼之势益张，衣冠之气尽短，其情极不可问矣。

    Explanation

    753

    Regarding the matter of teeth, it does not cause much harm, for one can let spittle on one's face dry on its own, and one cannot tolerate or retaliate against bad words. If one can be diligent and cautious in this manner, and commit to a lifelong endeavor, I will not remember past wrongs and accept you as a fellow sleeper. Otherwise, do not expect much in this life." Yi Gui accepted the command with a nod and firmly vowed to himself. Si Niang then told the maids to leave, removed all the decorations, and invited Yi Gui to sleep with her. That night, she was extremely friendly and hospitable, and Yi Gui was very happy. It was like a hungry man suddenly finding himself eating heartily at a butcher's shop, and being called out and kicked, how could one not be unworthy of consideration? From then on, Yi Gui strictly adhered to the law, and if there was a slight violation, Si Niang would call the maids to whip him, and he would forcibly hold back from crying out in pain. If Si Niang deliberately insulted him, he would not move even a small step. She might call him a cow, but he would not respond by calling her a horse. She might curse him all day, but he would not respond with even a single word. And the power of women was growing stronger, while the dignity of men was diminishing, the situation was so extreme that it could not be questioned.

    754

    一夕四娘以事怒一婢，缚之于楹，以雄狸置其裆中，而痛殴此狸。狸怒以爪伤婢，股间及私处，几无完肤。血流至足，褶裤尽殷，婢号呼惟求速死。一桂不忍，从旁解以婉言。四娘益忿，遽褫此婢之裩，罥于一桂之首，狼藉面目，余血沾濡。一桂弗能堪，究不敢与较，亦含笑受之。翌日出，遇其从弟，愤诉之。弟因戏曰：“嫂之性淫，兄必欲报复之，必也枕席而后可。”一桂遂赧然不语。四娘闻之，辄笑曰：“我固水也，阿奴火攻，真出下策矣。”是夕仍与一桂异榻，防御如前，己亦不再妆饰，洗粉涤朱，俨如嫠妇。及卧，恒以线密缝其衣，至旦而后去之。

    Explanation

    754

    One night, Lady Si became angry with a maid over an incident and tied her to a pillar. She then placed a male cat in her crotch and beat the cat severely. The cat, angry, scratched the maid from her thighs to her private parts, almost tearing off all her skin. Blood flowed down to her feet, and her folded pants were completely stained. The maid cried out, only wishing for a quick death. Yi Gui could not bear it and tried to stop her with gentle words. Lady Si became even more furious and quickly stripped the maid's underpants, hanging them around Yi Gui's head, making his face messy and covered in blood. Yi Gui could not endure it, but still dared not argue, and also smiled and accepted it. The next day, he went out and met his younger brother, angrily telling him about it. His younger brother then joked, "Sister-in-law's nature is promiscuous, if you want to take revenge, it must be in bed." Yi Gui then blushed and remained silent. Lady Si heard this and laughed, saying, "I am indeed water, and your fire attack is truly a last resort." That night, she still slept in a different bed from Yi Gui, as she had done before, and she also did not adorn herself again, washing off her makeup, looking like a widow. When she lay down, she always sewed her clothes tightly with thread, and only removed them the next morning.

    755

    且曰：“渠敢轻觑我，我岂若渠家小娘子，终夜而无餍足者？”守贞经月，一桂不能近身。而又密于防闲，昼近远游，夜虑潜逋，一桂遂大窘。托故少出，又以告弟，弟毅然曰：“兄无深虑，待弟亲往说之。”径诣嫂处。见四娘即太息，俄又长笑，四娘喻其意，佯问之曰：“叔何前悲而后愉也？”叔曰：“予闻兄嫂异处，宗祀将斩，心是以悲”。诘曰：“然则，何为而笑乎？”叔曰：“予不揣，欲为吾兄置妾，而虞阿嫂弗容。今嫂既不在当夕，可以成吾之志，能无喜也？”四娘忽笑谢曰：“得叔如此，田氏之幸也。但宜早为之，阿兄急不能待矣。”言已，径以他事相语，色甚温和。

    Explanation

    755

    And said, "How dare he look down on me? How could I be like his young wife, who would be unsatisfied even at night?" One Gui observed chastity for a month, and could not get close to her. Moreover, she was very cautious in guarding herself, being close during the day but far away at night, and worrying at night about being secretly avoided. One Gui thus found herself in great distress. She made an excuse to go out less often, and also told her younger brother. The younger brother resolutely said, "Brother, do not worry too much. I will go to speak to him." He went directly to his sister-in-law's place. When he saw Four-Niang, he sighed deeply, and then burst into laughter. Four-Niang understood his meaning and asked in a feigned manner, "Why were you sad before and happy now?" The younger brother replied, "I heard that my elder brother and sister-in-law are apart, and the family line will be cut off, so I was sad." Four-Niang asked further, "Then, why did you laugh?" The younger brother said, "I dare not presume, but I want to arrange a concubine for my brother, fearing that my sister-in-law would not allow it. Now that my sister-in-law is not present tonight, I can achieve my goal, and how could I not be happy?" Four-Niang suddenly smiled and thanked him, saying, "To have you like this, it is a blessing for the Tian family. But you should act soon, my elder brother cannot wait." After speaking, she directly talked about other matters, and her expression was very gentle.

    756

    弟退而大悦，即言于兄，且以重金为觅妾，旬日得之。一桂初犹首鼠，四娘反正言怂恿，以襄其成。及迎妾入门，四娘忽靓妆受礼，坚令侍坐于旁。一桂谛观，甚新孔嘉，究逊其旧远甚，独值饥渴之余，蜀不可望，而思陇得之，亦甚欣慰。乃四娘亲起，引妾入室，使更衣。周身皆易以新者，衾褥崭然，即命同居于内寝，不以侧室相处。一桂不可，则曰：“予以退闲，明日即以家政相属，使即居此。容予长斋绣佛，亦人生一大快事，若不必故为伪谦。”一桂虽疑之，而得其洗心向道，亦如去背上之芒，遂温语数四。四娘曰：“予倦矣！若不必在此絮絮。”径起下帷，解衣安寝，而故留烛不熄，以俟其至。

    Explanation

    756

    The younger brother stepped back and was greatly delighted, immediately telling his elder brother, and using a large sum of money to find a concubine, who he found within ten days. Yi Gui initially hesitated like a mouse, but Sister Si reversed her stance and encouraged him, assisting in the completion. When the concubine was welcomed into the house, Sister Si suddenly appeared in beautiful attire to receive greetings, firmly ordering her to sit beside him. Yi Gui carefully observed and found her very new and pleasing, but still falling far short of his previous concubine. Although she was not as desirable as before, thinking of her made him feel relieved, like finding relief in the west when one is thirsty in the east. Then Sister Si personally stood up and led the concubine into the room, to change clothes. She changed into all new clothes, and the bedding was brand new. She immediately ordered them to share the inner chamber, not treating her as a secondary wife. Yi Gui objected, and she said, "I am stepping back from my duties, tomorrow I will entrust household affairs to her, so she should stay here. Allow me to long-term fast and embroider Buddhas, it is also a great pleasure in life, if you don't have to pretend to be modest." Yi Gui, though skeptical, was pleased that she had turned her heart towards the path of righteousness, feeling as if the thorn on his back had been removed, and he spoke warmly several times. Sister Si said, "I am tired! You don't need to keep talking here." She quickly stood up, lowered the curtain, undressed, and went to bed, but deliberately left the candle burning, waiting for him to arrive.

    757

    夜未半，一桂果来。盖艰难纳妾，如获百朋，及缓其衣扣，气味如鲍鱼之腥，而下体尤甚。既不可堪，比入衾裯，又复如是。一桂不禁欲呕，亦不及询妾，裸体而奔。室内别无床，因就四娘，而独虑其醒也。披帷伺之，鼻息宛然，亟启其衾而入，觉温香如熏兰麝。抚其体，又柔腻绝无寸丝，情不能禁，将潜狎之。娘狎瞑若惊寤，大诧曰：“予有利剑，贼何胆巨如此？”一桂因白曰：“予。”四娘笑曰：“若何无厌至是？岂新花向雨，又肯觅此枯枝耶？殊令人莫解。”一桂乃以实告。四娘曰：“予既微闻之，但思熏以百合，蜣螂亦或可抱耳。”微哂而罢，亦不甚拒。

    Explanation

    757

    Midnight had not yet struck when one named Gui Guo arrived. It was a difficult endeavor to take a concubine, akin to gaining a hundred friends, but upon loosening her garment, the odor was as pungent as that of fish, with the lower part of her body being particularly so. Unable to endure it further, as he entered the bedding, the same unpleasantness persisted. Gui Guo could not bear it and, without time to question the concubine, fled naked. There was no other bed in the room, so he approached Si Niang, fearing only that she might wake up. He peeked through the curtain and saw that her breath was still steady. Quickly opening her bedding and entering, he found a warm fragrance like that of orchids and musk. Touching her body, he found it soft and smooth, without a single thread, and could not restrain his emotions, intending to secretly engage in intimacy. Si Niang woke up in a startled manner and was greatly surprised, saying, "I have a sharp sword, how dare you, thief, be so bold?" Gui Guo replied, "It is I." Si Niang smiled and said, "How could you be so insatiable? Are you new flowers facing the rain, yet seeking such a withered branch? It is truly perplexing." Gui Guo then revealed the truth. Si Niang said, "I have heard it faintly, but think of being scented with lilies; even a dung beetle might be able to embrace it." She smiled slightly and stopped, not strongly rejecting it.

    758

    第一桂欲为欢，则辞曰：“予以淫为人讥，万不敢再萌邪念。暮夜仓卒，容若半榻，亦过矣。他事决难奉命。”一桂不从，以力相强，四娘始容纳之。而久别新婚，不独一桂醉心，四娘亦情荡。惟所纳之妾，捱此长宵，亦觉床榻有秽气，知为正室所算，垂泣而已，无敢言也。晨起，一桂欲遣妾，四娘止之曰：“如斯秽物，乌能别嫁？将冻馁死矣。予家犹或可容耳！”因褫其华衣，命主涤厕之事。少惰，即以鞭扑随之，妾遂无复人面。四娘与一桂欢好如初，而法之峻厉犹昔。一桂失新，犹幸得旧，亦以天命自安。数日后，弟来省兄嫂，一桂适他出，四娘与叔语。

    Explanation

    758

    Gui desired intimacy with Si, but she replied, "I have been criticized for licentiousness, and I dare not again harbor any improper thoughts. An unexpected encounter at night, allowing for half a bed, was excessive. Other matters I absolutely cannot comply with." Gui refused to accept this and used force to compel her, and Si finally accepted him. The long separation followed by a new marriage left only Gui intoxicated, but Si was also emotionally stirred. The concubine that Gui had taken endured this long night, and she too felt an unpleasant smell on the bed, knowing she had been outsmarted by the principal wife. She could only weep and dared not speak. In the morning, Gui wanted to dismiss the concubine, but Si stopped him, saying, "Such a disgraceful person, how could she marry elsewhere? She will freeze and starve to death. My family might still be able to accommodate her!" She stripped her of her fine clothes and ordered her to clean the toilet. If she was lazy, she followed with beatings, and the concubine no longer had any human dignity. Si and Gui were as happy as before, and the strictness of her methods remained unchanged. Gui lost the new, but still fortunate to have the old, and found comfort in fate. Several days later, the younger brother came to visit his elder brother and sister-in-law, and Gui had just left, so Si spoke with her younger brother.

    759

    偶询及于妾，四娘即绐之曰：“承作合，新人颇得意。但思家不置，须叔一临慰之。”叔信之，应曰：“诺。”有顷，叔归。四娘随以黠婢诳指曰：“此即如君之室也。”叔乃顿忆嫂言，命婢导以入。中甚黑暗，忽有妇人自内出，面目黧黑，衣更褴缕，视之，即兄所纳之妾。骇而致询，妾未及答，四娘俄自外掩入，詈叔曰：“若以淫谤我，若何盗兄之妾耶？”叔大惭，趋出。四娘遂毒殴其妾，使招奸状。妾不能支，竟诬服。一桂返，四娘以为言，且命妾证之。一桂怒，竞绝其弟，妾亦转售于人。以是知四娘之留妾，正所以鱼肉其叔也。明年，四娘忽抱恙，荏苒床褥，竟以不起。

    Explanation

    759

    Once the narrator casually mentioned the concubine, Si Niang immediately deceived him, saying, "Thanks to your arrangement, the newlyweds are very pleased. But she misses her home terribly, and needs you to visit and comfort her." The uncle believed him and replied, "Alright." After a while, Uncle returned. Si Niang then used a cunning maid to deceive him by pointing and saying, "This is exactly like your room." Uncle then suddenly remembered what his wife had said, ordered the maid to lead him in. It was very dark inside. Suddenly, a woman came out from inside. Her face was sallow, and her clothes were tattered. Upon seeing her, he realized it was his brother's concubine. He was shocked and asked, but the concubine hadn't had time to answer. Si Niang suddenly rushed in from outside, scolded Uncle, saying, "You slander me with lewdness, how dare you steal my brother's concubine!" Uncle felt extremely ashamed and hurriedly left. Si Niang then severely beat the concubine, making her confess to adultery. The concubine couldn't bear it and finally confessed falsely. When Yi Gui returned, Si Niang reported this to him and ordered the concubine to testify. Yi Gui became furious and severed ties with his brother. The concubine was also sold to someone else. Thus, it is clear that Si Niang kept the concubine to abuse Uncle. The following year, Si Niang suddenly fell ill and lay bedridden for a long time, eventually dying.

    760

    而值属纩之时，犹力疾自起对镜。一桂在旁偶不谨，即以镜投之，呼曰：“天何不仁？何死我四娘，使懦男子得志也？”言未及终，呕血升余而卒。亡年只二十有六焉。婢妪之畏威者，甫敢言其隐事。盖四娘赐妾衣衾，皆以鱼腥曝干为细屑，散置绵中，而锦绣裆襦，倍加增益。侍者皆见其制作，第不敢告人耳。一桂既释重负，旋即续胶，亦里中巨家女，貌既远逊，悍又同然。一桂习于惧，又懦不能争，亦郁郁而死。希年之婢实出于田，每举其概以告其主。希年又曾分杯羹，知之最切，以故里人哄传，其详如此。噫！四娘其女中操莽乎？何弄夫于股掌之间，而不可测度也？”

    Explanation

    760

    At the moment of her death, when the breath was about to be drawn out, she still struggled against her illness to get up and look into the mirror. One of the maids, who happened to be careless, immediately threw the mirror at her, shouting, "Why is heaven so unkind? Why did it take my fourth daughter away, allowing a weak man to achieve his ambitions?" Before she could finish her words, she vomited blood and died. She passed away at the age of twenty-six. The maids and elderly women who feared her authority only dared to speak of the hidden matters after her death. It is said that the fourth daughter bestowed clothes and bedding on her slave, all of which were dried fish powder scattered in the cotton, while the brocade undergarments were greatly increased in value. The servants all saw her making these preparations, but dared not tell anyone. After the maid was relieved of her heavy burden, she immediately continued with glue, and another woman from the neighborhood, a daughter of a wealthy family, was less attractive and equally fierce. The maid, accustomed to fear and weak, could not contend, and also died in depression. The old maid, who actually came from the fields, often mentioned the general idea to her master. The old maid had once shared a bowl of soup and knew the details most intimately, so the story spread widely in the village, as described. Alas! Was the fourth daughter a powerful woman among women? Why did she play with her husband in such a way and be impossible to predict?

    761

    外史氏曰：天下好内之人，未有不惧内者。维其好之，故人得以中之也。汉成不纳歌者，何以燕啄皇孙？唐高不嬖才人，何以武移李皐？以天子之威，不能行子宫阃，要皆好之一念致之耳！况夫人生授室，非当情窦初开，即值寒窗久寡，其欲之也汲汲矣。故其始也，寤寐反侧，其既也，琴瑟鼓钟。由是妇不必才而以为才，妇不必美而以为美。即以孟光道韫语之，彼固笑而未信焉。既宠而未能不骄，既爱而未能不纵。妇必以敌体为词，夫又以如宾可法。其初小有言隐忍受之，其后大无礼度量包之，而狮子之吼可试，牝鸡之晨无妨已。时或难堪于众瞩之地，有触于羞恶之心，未尝不忿而思逞，少戢其咆哮之威。无如妇怨已深，夫情已动。黄昏人静，虽当粉界啼痕；黑夜灯残，讵敌簧生巧舌。

    Explanation

    761

    外史氏曰：天下好内之人，未有不惧内者。维其好之，故人得以中之也。汉成不纳歌者，何以燕啄皇孙？唐高不嬖才人，何以武移李皐？以天子之威，不能行子宫阃，要皆好之一念致之耳！况夫人生授室，非当情窦初开，即值寒窗久寡，其欲之也汲汲矣。故其始也，寤寐反侧，其既也，琴瑟鼓钟。由是妇不必才而以为才，妇不必美而以为美。即以孟光道韫语之，彼固笑而未信焉。既宠而未能不骄，既爱而未能不纵。妇必以敌体为词，夫又以如宾可法。其初小有言隐忍受之，其后大无礼度量包之，而狮子之吼可试，牝鸡之晨无妨已。时或难堪于众瞩之地，有触于羞恶之心，未尝不忿而思逞，少戢其咆哮之威。无如妇怨已深，夫情已动。黄昏人静，虽当粉界啼痕；黑夜灯残，讵敌簧生巧舌。

    Translation:

    The historian says: Throughout the world, those who are fond of women are always dominated by them. Because they are fond of women, they can be deceived by them. Why did Emperor Cheng of Han fail to recognize the danger of Zhao Feiyan and Zhao Hede, leading to the downfall of the Han dynasty? Why did Emperor Gaozong of Tang fail to control Wu Zetian, resulting in significant political changes? Even with the power of an emperor, one cannot control a wife, and it all comes down to one's fondness for her! Moreover, when a man marries, it is either during the early stages of romantic feelings or after a long period of loneliness and hardship, and his desire for her is intense. Thus, at the beginning, he tosses and turns in bed, unable to sleep, and after marriage, they enjoy marital harmony. Therefore, women are often perceived as talented and beautiful regardless of their actual qualities. Even if one were to discuss this with Ban Zhao and Wang Daojun, they would likely laugh and not fully believe it. Once a man is overly fond of his wife, he cannot avoid becoming arrogant and indulgent. The wife must treat him as an equal, and the husband must treat her as a guest, following Confucian ideals. At first, he endures minor complaints quietly, but later, he tolerates major acts of disrespect with a broad mind, and the wife's authoritative voice can be tested, and her taking on traditionally male roles is not seen as a problem. Sometimes, in public, he feels embarrassed or guilty, and he cannot help but become angry and want to act out, but he temporarily restrains his fierce and aggressive behavior. However, the wife's resentment has deepened, and the husband's feelings have been stirred. At night, when people are quiet, even if there are tears on her face, and at midnight, when the lights are dim, how can one resist the persuasive speech of her cunning tongue?

    762

    于斯时也，男儿之刚肠已软，能不折五斗之腰？女子之煞气频添，定使屈千金之膝。已而娇嗔少减，被底犹待割鸡；盛怒已回，枕边尚须歃血。谚云：“夫妇之恨不隔宿。非妇之无恨也，实夫之不敢恨也。其险阻艰难，有如此者，其孰敢轻于尝试也哉？是以诃责摈斥，不甘于父兄师长者，独忍于室内之佳人。姗笑怒骂，姑听诸乡党州闾者，专媚此闺中之少妇。苟具肺肝，何以至是？原其弊，惟好之深故爱之切，爱之切故惧之也亦深。不必玉软香温，惟虑迷桃源之路。不必花愁柳怨，只忧闭月夜之门。加以小信小忠，卿独怜我。更有挟贤挟贵，我敢违卿。迨至理绣窗前，小试鸠盘之毒手；渐且合欢床上，痛遭鬼母之沉椎。麟梦无征，庭坚不祀，螽斯有咏，周姥羞闻。全军既已尽覆，卷土讵敢重来？然使才美尽如四娘，惧之犹可解也。独奈何远逊四娘者，而亦爱之如一桂，惧之如一桂也耶？

    Explanation

    762

    At this time, the man's strong will has become weak, how can he not bend his five-dan waist? The woman's fierce spirit has increased, certainly causing her to lower her high-priced knee. Soon her playful anger has slightly decreased, still waiting to cut the chicken under the covers; her intense anger has subsided, still needing to swear an oath by blood beside the pillow. A proverb says: "The resentment between husband and wife does not last overnight. It is not that the wife has no resentment, but rather that the husband dares not resent her. The dangers and difficulties are as such, who would dare to lightly attempt it? Therefore, scolding and expelling, what one cannot bear from parents, brothers, teachers, and elders, one uniquely endures from the beautiful woman in the room. Mocking laughter and angry scolding, what one can only hear from fellow villagers and neighbors, one exclusively flatters in the young woman in the inner chamber. If one has a heart and liver, how could one come to this? The root of the problem is that deep affection leads to intense love, intense love leads to deep fear. Not necessarily needing softness and warmth, but only worrying about being lost on the path to Peach Blossom Spring. Not necessarily needing to worry about the sadness of flowers and willows, but only about the closed door of the moonlit night. With small trust and loyalty, you alone have pity on me. Furthermore, with the support of the wise and the powerful, how dare I disobey you? When it comes to the embroidered window, trying out the poisonous hand of the magpie's nest; gradually on the bed of joy, suffering the heavy blows of the ghost mother. The dream of the qilin has no sign, the sacrifice of Tingjian is not offered, the song of the grasshopper is sung, and the old woman of Zhou is ashamed to hear. The entire army has been completely defeated, how could one dare to return and rebuild? If all talents and beauty were like Si Niang, fear could still be resolved. How can those far inferior to Si Niang also love and fear her as much as one would a precious cassia tree?

    763

    ○　沈阳女子
    　　沈阳一女子，年甫及笄，貌美而见祟于狐。摄其精气，日渐羸尪，父母甚忧之。郡中有一赵三公，精于驱邪之术，自言得之仙传。其法，以银针五枚，以次刺病者手爪，甫及其拇，怪即哀号求生。与之约，而后纵之，无敢再犯，疾亦寻愈。赵故世家子，且乐于为人疗治，丝粒无所取。行之有年，人益重而趋之。女父耳其名，亦卑礼延之至家。乃赵未入室，怪即附女体，言笑自若，谓人曰：“久闻赵三爷，今日姑面试之。”赵闻而恚，排闼直入曰：“死老魅既知赵某，当速避。”女作狐言曰：“敬来观君绝技，何避为？”赵益怒。曾询家人，知怪之来，与女称姊妹，稔其为雌。遽出针刺女右手，贯其一指，血出如濡。

    Explanation

    763

    A Girl from Shenyang

    A girl from Shenyang, who had just reached the age of marriage, was beautiful but troubled by a fox spirit. The fox drained her vital energy, causing her to gradually become weak and emaciated, which greatly worried her parents. In the county, there was a man named Sāo Sān Gōng, who was skilled in exorcism, claiming his techniques were passed down from immortals. His method involved using five silver needles to pierce the hands of the afflicted person, one after another, until reaching the thumb. When the needles reached the thumb, the spirit would cry out in pain and plead for mercy. Once a pact was made with the spirit, it would be released, and it would not dare to trouble the person again, thus the illness would soon heal. Sāo Sān Gōng was a descendant of an aristocratic family and enjoyed helping people without any monetary gain. He had been practicing this for many years, and his reputation grew, attracting more people to seek his help. The girl's father heard of Sāo Sān Gōng's name and invited him to their home with humble respect. Before Sāo Sān Gōng entered the room, the spirit attached itself to the girl's body, speaking and laughing as usual, and said to others, "I have long heard of Sāo Sān Gōng. Today, let us test his skills." Upon hearing this, Sāo Sān Gōng became angry and burst into the room, saying, "Since you, the old demon, know of Sāo Sān Gōng, you should leave quickly!" The girl spoke in the voice of the fox, saying, "I have come to admire your extraordinary skills. Why should I avoid you?" Sāo Sān Gōng became even angrier. He asked the family and learned that the spirit claimed to be a sister of the girl and was known to be female. He immediately took out a needle and pierced the girl's right hand, piercing one of her fingers, and blood flowed out like water.

    764

    女犹呻吟而笑曰：“技止此乎？余固无恙也。”赵愕然，知其非常，复贯其二。女色变似不能堪，而盛气犹末相下。赵又取针将贯其三，不禁大嗥曰：“予与若无郄，今何故欲毙命？”赵笑曰：“若既惜命，何祟人不顾？”而针犹未贯革，女即宛转哀鸣曰：“予不敢矣！”赵曰：“不敢当去！”女敬诺，赵令设誓，始去其针。室中犹闻狐语曰：“可惜！可惜！”径去，女亦仆。赵语女父，使延医疗以药饵，己遂退。逾年，女既痊可，赵有子未婚，遍阅都城无一当意者。因于病室见女，以为佳妇，乃倩冰焉。女父衔其旧德，欣然许诺。亲迎成礼，伉俪甚欢，翁姑亦甚愉悦。未及旬日，女疾又作，且发狂倍于曩昔，啮赵子肌无完肤。

    Explanation

    764

    The woman moaned and laughed, saying, "Is that all there is to your skill? I am still well!" Zhao was startled and realized that something was not ordinary. He then pierced the second. The woman's face changed color as if she could not bear it, but her high spirit had not yet been subdued. Zhao took another needle to pierce the third, and she could not help but cry out loudly, "I have no grudge against you, why do you want to kill me now?" Zhao smiled and said, "If you value your life, why do you harm others without regard?" The needle had not yet pierced the skin, and the woman turned and cried out in distress, "I dare not anymore!" Zhao said, "If you dare not, then leave!" The woman respectfully agreed, and Zhao made her swear, then removed the needle. In the room, they still heard the fox say, "Too bad! Too bad!" The fox left directly, and the woman also fell. Zhao told the woman's father and had the woman treated by a doctor with medicine. Zhao then withdrew. After a year, the woman had recovered. Zhao had a son who was unmarried and searched the entire city but found no one to his liking. Because he saw the woman in the sickroom, he thought she was a good wife and asked an intermediary to arrange the marriage. The woman's father remembered his old kindness and happily agreed. The wedding was held with the groom coming to fetch the bride. The couple was very happy, and the parents-in-law were also very pleased. Not even ten days later, the woman's illness flared up again, and she became even more insane than before, biting Zhao's son's flesh until it was all torn.

    765

    赵适他往，家人莫可如何乃闭诸其室。及赵归，闻而笑曰：“日为人驱贼，今贼反入吾室矣。”亟往治之。女见赵怒目如炬，嫚骂曰：“死老畜，若遣我去，乃以女归汝家耶？予情实不甘，纵死不容汝得妇。”赵颇内惭，乃温言遣之曰：“汝有誓在，口血未干，何自背之？”女憨跳如故，且丑诋曰：“汝欲去釜底烬余耳！不然，天下多女子何独属意于若人？”赵因大恚，治之以术。女三指血殷，詈犹不辍。赵忿然作色，取针刺其无名指，女方敛戢曰：“予知汝矣幸宥我。”赵思怪绝悍，且患在室中，必翦灭而后已。遂叱曰“汝反复无信，今日决毙汝命，不少贷。”女百口求生，嘤嘤惟呼赵三爷，誓不再犯。赵毅然弗恤，又以针贯其五。

    Explanation

    765

    Zhao had gone somewhere else, and the household members had no choice but to confine the woman in the room.
