Upon Zhao's return, he heard about the situation and laughed, saying, "Every day I chased away thieves, but now a thief has entered my own room." He quickly went to deal with the situation. The woman saw Zhao and, with angry eyes, insulted him, saying, "Dead old beast, if you had sent me away, would you have brought a woman back to your house? My feelings are truly unwilling, and even if I die, I will not let you have a wife." Zhao felt somewhat ashamed inside and, with gentle words, sent her away, saying, "You made an oath, and the blood from your mouth is not yet dry, so why did you break it?" The woman acted foolishly and playfully as before and insulted him, saying, "You want to discard what's left at the bottom of the pot! Otherwise, there are many women in the world, why should you be particularly interested in this one?" Zhao then shouted, "You are unreliable and inconsistent, today I will surely kill you, without any mercy." The woman pleaded for life with many words and called out softly to Master Zhao, swearing not to do it again. Zhao remained resolute without pity and pierced her five fingers.

    766

    女发尽上竖张目直视，大诟曰：“五百年基业坏于崇朝，赵三真狠心哉？然为鬼亦不容汝。”言讫，三踊而踣。赵亟命人索之，得牝狐于积薪之处，巨如初生之犊，毛已苍黑，针在其右爪。口吻尽赤而死矣。赵令褫其革，以药医女，女乃复初。第赵自行法以来，未尝杀一物，今以子妇故而毙此狐，心实怏怏。未几遂病，惭无起色。狐又不时来牖外涕泣而詈，作鬼声，向赵索命。家人恐怖不敢宵行。赵愈危，狐亦愈纵，室中财贿先故自亡，笥内裳衣无故自毁，延及于所藏券契，概成煨烬，若以冯眎自居者。而狐既为鬼，鬼又为狐，赵术亦穷于所试矣。无何，赵卒。又祟其子子亦亡，家缘日以消耗。茕茕姑媳，至今犹存，则已贫无卓锥之地。人每述而叹息之。

    Explanation

    766

    The woman's hair stood up on end, her eyes wide open, and she shouted loudly, "A 500-year-old legacy was destroyed in a single morning, Zhao San, you truly have a heart of stone! Yet, even as a ghost, I will not forgive you." After speaking, she jumped three times and then collapsed. Zhao immediately ordered someone to search for her, and they found a female fox among the firewood. The fox was as large as a newborn calf, its fur already gray-black, with a needle in its right paw. Its mouth and nose were completely red, and it was dead. Zhao ordered the fox's skin to be stripped, and he treated the woman with medicine, and she returned to her original state. However, since Zhao had practiced his methods, he had never killed anything, but now, because of his son's wife, he killed this fox, and his heart was truly resentful. Soon after, he fell ill, and his condition did not improve. The fox would occasionally come to the window, crying and cursing, making ghostly sounds, and demanding Zhao's life. The family was terrified and dared not travel at night. As Zhao became more gravely ill, the fox became more unrestrained. The wealth in the house began to mysteriously disappear, and the clothes in the chest were mysteriously destroyed. Even the stored contracts and documents were reduced to ashes, as if the fox were taking possession of the house. The fox became a ghost, and the ghost became a fox, and Zhao's methods were exhausted in their application. Soon after, Zhao died. The fox then tormented his son, who also died. The family's resources were gradually depleted. The lonely grandmother and daughter-in-law still survive, but they are now so poor they have no place to stand a cone. People recount this and sigh over it.

    767

    外史氏曰：赵不为子纳妇，狐亦不敢复逞。妖由人兴实由心生也。以公始，以私终，而又恃法以祛之，狐不死固不甘心，狐即死宁可瞑目哉？泊然寡营，神钦鬼伏又何畏乎？如鬼之狐，更何惧夫死狐之鬼？

    ○　晋阳生
    　　晋阳生者，其始非即晋阳人也。乃翁宦游于太原，纳妾得子，取古人以地命名之意，而字之曰“晋阳生”。生既生于晋，育于晋，饮晋之泉，食晋之粟，声音笑貌遂无一非晋，即晋人亦或忘其非晋矣。年甫十二而父殁，以末秩且薄于宦囊，遂不能归，因家于晋之榆次而寄籍焉。生性佻达，不嗜书。及长，辄出与无赖游。母不能禁，乃出私蓄百金，使贩于他省。命之曰：“汝父官位虽卑，亦官也。汝今弱冠，未授有室，余心窃忧之。

    Explanation

    767

    The historian commented: Zhao did not marry his son, and the fox dared not act again. Evil spirits arise from human actions, and in reality, they are born from the heart. Beginning with public matters and ending with private ones, and relying on laws to eliminate them, the fox would not be content if it did not die, and if the fox did die, how could it close its eyes in peace? Calmly with few ambitions, gods respect and ghosts submit, what fear is there? A fox like a ghost, how much less fear would there be of a ghost that has killed a fox?

    ○ Jin Yangsheng
    Jin Yangsheng was not originally from Jin Yang. His father traveled for his official duties in Taiyuan and took a concubine who bore him a son. Taking the meaning of naming after places from ancient times, he named the child "Jin Yangsheng." Born and raised in Jin, drinking from Jin's springs and eating Jin's grain, his voice, appearance, and manner were all distinctly Jin, so much so that even the people of Jin might forget he was not truly from Jin. At the age of twelve, his father passed away. Due to his low rank and meager official salary, he could not return home, so he settled in Yuci, Jin, and registered there. Yangsheng was naturally lively and did not enjoy reading books. As he grew older, he often went out to associate with ruffians. His mother could not stop him, so she took out her private savings of a hundred gold pieces and sent him to trade in other provinces. She told him, "Your father's position was lowly, but he was still an official. You are now of age, but you have not been given a wife, and I secretly worry about you."

    768

    第我异乡人，此数恐不能得妇。今以与儿，权其子母，如贾三倍，可亟归为汝纳聘。不然，将鳏此一世矣！儿宜好为之。”言已，为之治装，遣之行。生承母命，欣然发轫。里人有顾二者，旧曾贸易于京，因与偕往。生意在得妇，每事敛束，无少纵。顾怪之，叩曰：“若素豪爽，今何拘拘若守财虏？岂真以慈命为重耶？”生腼然对曰：“否！否！予母将以此金为余娶妇，而虑其不敷，故遣余贾，以襄其好事。若出而浪费，致少赢余，则寒窗之寡，将不止于十年，吾故慎之。
    ”顾既得其详，陡生狡计。乃抚掌笑曰：“迂者，子也。吾未闻临渴掘井而能速饮者。以子之年，正当抚而有室，今乃长途跋涉，冀得蝇头以图鸳侣。予恐玄霜未捣，潘鬓将星，老去刘郎，谁能以桃花付汝乎？”生瞿然，亟请其说。顾曰：“予以微金往谋重利，纵使籴贱贩贵，不过什一而止耳。若欲倍之，必俟十稔而后可。

    Explanation

    768

    As a stranger in a foreign land, I fear that these numbers may not be enough to secure a wife. Now, I give them to you, treating them as a substitute for a dowry, like a merchant making a threefold profit. You can quickly return and arrange a marriage for me. Otherwise, I will remain unmarried for life! You should handle this well." After speaking, she prepared your travel arrangements and sent you off. You obeyed your mother's command and happily set out. A fellow villager named Gu had previously traded in the capital and so accompanied you on the journey. You were intent on securing a wife, and in everything you did, you were careful and restrained. Gu found this strange and asked, "You are usually generous and open-handed, but now why are you so cautious as if guarding treasure? Are you truly prioritizing your mother's command?" You blushed and replied, "No, no! My mother intends to use this money to arrange my marriage, but she fears it may not be enough, so she sent me to trade in order to secure a good match. If I spend it recklessly and end up with less, then my years of study and hardship will not be limited to ten years, and that is why I am being cautious."

    Gu, having learned the full details, suddenly devised a cunning plan. He clapped his hands and laughed, "You are the one who is being foolish. I have never heard of someone digging a well when thirsty and being able to drink quickly. At your age, you should be settling down and getting married, but now you are traveling long distances, hoping to gain a small profit in order to find a partner. I fear that your youth will not last, and your hair will turn gray before long. When you grow old, who will give you a rose to marry you?" You were startled and quickly asked for his explanation. Gu said, "I am going to make a small investment to seek great profits. Even if I buy cheap and sell expensive, I will only make a tenfold profit at most. If you want to double it, you must wait ten years."

    769

    子能待之乎？”生颦蹙曰：“不能！子将何以教我？”顾曰：“子之志在伉俪，原无望作富家翁，何为徒取劳瘁？距此数日程，其地为清风店，实多佳丽。且娶一妇只需数十金，衣饰一惟子命。子至彼处，择其可者而聘之，婚而后归，俨有家室，不愈于长途之役役耶？”生信其言，乃大喜曰：“早我十年婚嫁者，吾子也。若从母言，几误予事。”遂益与顾谋，终日津津不倦，齿颊非姻事不言。比至其地，顾止生于传舍，不令窥户，己乃与熟识者，聚饮于市。日晡始反命报生曰：“事谐矣！某家有女，色绝丽，予浼所亲与其父母言，约以六十金为聘，钗钏衣裙廿数在外。以子在客，即赘其家，弥月后偕返故里。

    Explanation

    769

    "Can you wait for it?" the man asked with a frown. "No, I can't!" the student replied. "Then what will you teach me?" the man inquired. "Your wish is to have a wife," he said, "but there is no expectation of becoming a wealthy husband. Why take on unnecessary labor and fatigue? A few days' journey from here is a place called Qingfeng Store, which is filled with beautiful women. And to marry one woman, only a few dozen gold pieces are needed. Clothing and accessories are entirely at your command. When you arrive there, choose one who is suitable and marry her. After marriage, return home, and you will have a household. Is it not better than being burdened by a long journey?" The student believed his words and was greatly pleased, saying, "If I had married ten years earlier, I would have been your son. If I had followed my mother's advice, I would have made a mistake in my affairs." So he became even more involved in planning with him, talking about it all day long without tiring, and talked about nothing but marriage matters. When they arrived at the place, he kept him in a lodging house and did not allow him to look out the window. He then went out with acquaintances to drink in the market. In the evening, he returned and reported to the student, "The matter is settled! There is a woman in a certain family who is extremely beautiful. I asked a friend to speak to her parents, and agreed to a dowry of sixty gold pieces, with twenty or so pieces of jewelry and clothing included. Since you are a guest, you will live in her family. After a month, you will return to your hometown together."

    770

    敬俟吾子金诺，佳期当订在明宵。”生喜甚，亟起拜谢，如数以予之，惟求一面，以安寸心。顾怫然曰：“谁家闺阁竟使路人轻窥哉？予以丽人许兄，岂其相诳者？”生遂不复言。顾怀金去，有顷，引一人来，年甫成童，貌极狡好，云是若人之弟。生熟睹之，心神飞越，与之欢然讲礼，订约而别。顾笑谓生曰：“摩厉以须。其姊之美，尤远胜其弟也。”生益信之，再拜而谢。翌日，顾又令生出数金，为市衾褥，崭然一新。生之所存，遂十无其一矣。

    Explanation

    770

    Awaiting your honorable promise, the auspicious time will be set for tomorrow night." The young man was greatly pleased, quickly rose to bow in gratitude, gave the agreed amount, and only asked for a brief meeting to calm his heart. The other person suddenly became displeased and said, "How can a lady of the house allow strangers to look in? I have promised a beautiful woman to you, how could I deceive you?" The young man then remained silent. The other person left with the money, and after a short while, brought a young boy, barely a child, who was exceptionally handsome, saying he was the younger brother of the lady. The young man recognized him, his heart and mind flew, and he happily exchanged pleasantries, making an appointment before parting. The other person smiled and told the young man, "Sharpen and wait. Her sister's beauty, especially, surpasses her brother." The young man believed him even more, bowed twice in gratitude. The next day, the other person asked the young man to spend several more coins to buy new bedding, which was brand new and shiny. What the young man had left, thus, only one-tenth remained.

    771

    至夕，顾先以卧具往，然后引生同行。既至视之，屋宇低矮而修整异常，生既初涉江湖，绝不知其为狎邪处。入门即有翁媪承递，呼生为贵客，而不以婿称。生以其土俗，不之疑。欲执子婿礼，公媪皆固辞。延入室中，酒胾罗案。顾少坐即行，谓生曰：“明晨敬求扶头，今夕尚未敢相溷。”生亦不解所云，挽留弗住。归即束装，乘夕北遁，杳不知其所之矣。生送顾反室，翁媪亦避去。旋有美妇人袨服靓妆，年可二旬余，掩笑自外入。生以为妻之姑姊，揖而后坐。

    Explanation

    771

    By evening, Con Xian brought bedding to the guest and then led him along. Upon arriving, they looked at the house, which was low but exceptionally neat and tidy. The protagonist, having just entered the world of the river and lakes, was completely unaware that it was a brothel. As soon as they entered, the elders received and served them, calling the guest a respected guest but not referring to him as a son-in-law. The protagonist, considering this a local custom, did not question it. He wanted to show the respect due to a son-in-law, but the elders all firmly declined. They invited him into the room, where meat and wine were arranged on the table. Con Xian stayed briefly and then left, saying, "Tomorrow morning, I will come to accompany you to bed, but tonight I do not want to disturb you." The protagonist did not understand what he meant and could not persuade him to stay. He immediately packed his belongings and fled northward, vanishing without a trace. The protagonist sent Con Xian back to the room, and the elders also left. Soon, a beautiful woman dressed in fine clothes and makeup, about twenty years old, entered with a smile. The protagonist thought she was his wife's aunt, and bowed before sitting down.

    772

    妇献酬自若，狂荡不类良家，生始疑。酒酣，妇以美目盼生，备极狎昵。生对之反觉汗颜，呆坐如木鸡，妇遂时时窃笑。已而漏下三鼓，妇不言归，生渐不能耐，遽起曰：“翁媪何在？敢请新娘子即此合欢。”妇笑曰：“家无姊妹，即妾是已，郎犹未知耶？”生惊愕曰：“若一妇人，齿且长，乌足以当此？”妇曰：“章台之柳，适兴为佳，君何过于苛求？”生大恚曰：“予以八十金娶妇，不可以此言相戏。

    Explanation

    772

    The woman served wine and food with ease and composure, her behavior reckless and unlike that of a woman from a good family, which made the man begin to suspect. When the wine was flowing freely, she gazed at him with her beautiful eyes, showing extreme familiarity and intimacy. In response, he felt ashamed and sat motionlessly like a wooden chicken, while she secretly laughed at him. Soon after, when the third watch of the night had passed, she did not mention returning home, and the man gradually became impatient. He suddenly stood up and said, "Where are the old man and woman? I dare ask that the bride join us here for our union." The woman smiled and said, "There are no sisters in the family, so I am the one you seek, sir. Do you not know yet?" The man was shocked and said, "If she is just a woman, and she is already grown, how can she be suitable for this?" The woman replied, "The willow by Chang'an Gate is best enjoyed as it comes. Why do you demand so much?" The man was greatly angered and said, "I spent eighty taels of silver to marry a wife. I cannot joke about this."

    773

    ”妇亦正色曰：“阚金五星，尚在予袖，君何以此言诬我？”生始悟其落局，厉呼翁媪，寂无应者。妇乃笑曰：“郎毋躁，事或有由。此地南北通衢，如妾等钱树子，不可枚举。今晨君友辱临，言君客途寂寞，无以为欢，命妾相伴一宵，明早即行，从无媒妁之约。况妾自有夫，又谁敢以绿巾移赠哉？”生又问翁媪何人，妇曰：“皆君友浼来承应者。君友既去，渠亦潜归，实不知其焉往。”生益惊，不再询，遂只身而奔。及归旅邸，视之，室中一空，顾不见。再趋妇所，门已下键，不独美人化为彩云，并己之衾枕余资，俱已携归别岫矣。

    Explanation

    773

    The woman spoke sternly, saying, "The five stars of gold are still in my sleeve; how dare you accuse me with such words?" The man then realized he had fallen into a trap and shouted angrily for the old men and women, but no one responded. The woman laughed and said, "Do not be so agitated, dear. There may be a reason for this. This place is a major thoroughfare, and there are countless women like me, who are often referred to as money trees. This morning, your friend came and said that you were lonely on your journey and had nothing to enjoy, so he asked me to accompany you for one night and leave early the next morning. There was no arrangement through a matchmaker. Besides, I already have a husband; who would dare to give away his green headwear to another?" The man asked again who the old men and women were. The woman replied, "They were all sent by your friend to attend to you. After your friend left, they also secretly returned, and I do not know where they went." The man was even more shocked and did not ask further. He fled alone. When he returned to his temporary lodging, he found everything empty and saw no one. He went back to the woman's place, but the door was already locked. Not only had the beautiful woman disappeared like a rainbow, but also his bedding, pillows, and remaining funds had all been taken away to another place.

    774

    生乃大恚，而诸人姓名举所未悉，因思顾必赴京，盍迹其纵追袭之，庶得而甘心焉。于是奋不顾身，宵行十数里。天已达曙，倦极小憩道旁，忽见一蹇叟，襆被蹀躞而来。瞥睹生貌，若甚惊疑者。遽顾之，言曰：“不肖子乃竟在此耶！”生闻之，怒甚，将饱以老拳，而念其耄不忍发，惟瞠目注之。叟操西音曰：“汝之横逆犹昔耶？虽然，予之肠几为汝断矣！”语已泣下。生觉有异，遂伪为悚惧，拱立不言。

    Explanation

    774

    The protagonist became greatly angry, but he did not know the names of all the people involved. Thinking that they must be going to the capital, he suggested tracing and pursuing them, hoping to satisfy his desire. He then rushed forward without regard for his own safety, traveling ten or more miles at night. By dawn, he was extremely tired and took a short rest by the roadside. Suddenly, he saw an elderly man with a limp, carrying a bundle of bedding and shuffling along. The old man glanced at the protagonist's appearance and seemed very surprised and doubtful. He quickly turned to look at the protagonist and said, "Unworthy son, you are here after all!" The protagonist heard this and became very angry, ready to beat the old man with a fist, but remembering that the old man was elderly, he refrained. He only stared at the old man with wide eyes. The old man, speaking in a Western accent, said, "Is your rude behavior still the same as before? Although, my heart has almost been torn apart by you!" After finishing his words, he shed tears. The protagonist felt something was different and pretended to be afraid, standing respectfully and silently.

    775

    叟又嫚骂曰：“畜产无须乔致，可从予赴省，代纪肆务。明岁与汝偕归。”生审为误认，乃委蛇从之，故为追悔之状，代叟控卫，徐步而前。叟心悦，于路絮谈家政，喁喁若父子。生本晋音，叟故不疑，而生遂谓他人父矣。迨至保定，止于北门之钱肆，生于此已得其详。盖叟亦顾姓，世为平阳富家。生子游荡，屡戒不悛，父逐之于外。三易春秋，思之綦切而竟弗归。前遭生于途，耳目口鼻无一不酷肖亡人，遂目为己子，而复收之。携归肆中，寝食与共。生亦狡狯，更迎机附合之，遂无人识为它姓子。

    Explanation

    775

    The old man then insulted rudely, saying, "You don't need to pretend to be refined. You can accompany me to the province and help with the business. Next year, I will bring you back with me." The young man realized it was a mistaken identification, so he reluctantly followed, acting as if he were regretting it, and helped the old man guard. They walked slowly forward. The old man was pleased, and on the way, they chatted about family matters, talking to each other like father and son. The young man originally had a Shanxi accent, and the old man naturally did not doubt him, so the young man then considered him as his father. When they arrived in Baoding, they stayed at a money shop near the north gate, and the young man had already learned the details there. It turned out the old man was also surnamed Gu, from a wealthy family in Pingyang. His son was a wanderer who repeatedly ignored warnings, and the father expelled him from the family. After three years, he missed him very much but he never returned. He encountered the young man on the road, and his ears, eyes, nose, and mouth were all very similar to his deceased son, so he regarded him as his own son and took him in again. He brought him back to the shop and shared meals and sleep. The young man was also cunning and adapted to the situation, so no one recognized him as a son of another family.

    776

    居半载，叟得家邮，读之色甚不平。阅日，呼生至寝室，与以三百金，语之曰：“汝岳近有信来，言汝数年不复，将以女他适。汝今可亟归，以金与汝母，毕汝姻事，俟明春再来经理。吾视汝醇谨胜前，慎勿再萌故态，则家门之厚幸也。”言讫，又出寸函曰：“榆次有汝从兄，行二，名某者，向因汝荡游，族中公议以渠承嗣。今汝既在膝下，可以此书复之，亦系要务。汝勿惮于驰驱。”生一一领诺，聆其从兄之名，实即所偕之顾二，私心窃喜。

    Explanation

    776

    After half a year, the old man received a letter from home. Upon reading it, his expression turned very angry. After a few days, he called the young man to his bedroom and gave him three hundred gold pieces. He said to him, "Your father-in-law recently sent a letter saying that you have not returned for several years and is planning to marry your daughter to someone else. You should return home immediately, give the money to your mother, and complete your marriage arrangements. Wait until next spring to come back to handle other matters. I see that you are now more sober and cautious than before. Be careful not to revert to your old ways again, as that would be a great fortune for our family." After finishing his words, he took out a small letter and said, "There is a cousin of yours in Yuci, the second son, named someone, who was adopted as a successor due to your wandering. Now that you are back, you should write a reply to him, as it is an important matter. Do not hesitate to travel." The young man nodded in agreement, and upon hearing the name of his cousin, he realized that it was indeed the same person he had been traveling with, and he secretly felt pleased.

    777

    又两日，辞叟启行，即策叟之前驴，仍就故道。途次自计，欲归叟家而不舍在金，且虑春光或泄，势难存身，因决意旋里。行及邑境，距家仅百里，雨骤驴不得前，乃止于村舍。主人出见客，衣冠朴实，面有怒容。及与生为礼，又极尽东道情，生心惑之。主人辞入，即闻闺中汹言曰：“渠既薄行，今得此人，尽可夫也。”有间，主人复出，谓生曰：“聆君土音，实吾桑梓。似此青年，曾谐凤卜否？”生不欲诣平阳，因答以无。主人大悦曰：“婿在是矣！”亟入，以一袭出。启之，衣虽韦布，皆新制甫成者。

    777

    After two more days, the guest bid farewell to the old man and set off, immediately mounting the old man's former donkey and continuing on the old path. Along the way, he reflected on his journey, wanting to return to the old man's home but not giving up on his wealth, and worrying that spring might pass, making it difficult to survive. He therefore decided to return home. Traveling to the outskirts of the town, he was only a hundred miles from home when heavy rain prevented the donkey from moving forward, so he stopped at a village inn. The host came out to see the guest, dressed in simple attire and with a face showing anger. When he greeted the guest with courtesy, he showed the utmost hospitality, which puzzled the guest. The host then went inside, and the guest immediately heard loud words from the inner chamber: "He has already acted lightly; now that he has this person, he can be satisfied." After a while, the host came out again and said to the guest, "Hearing your local accent, I realize you are from my hometown. Such a young man, have you already settled in marriage?" The guest did not want to go to Pingyang and so answered no. The host was very pleased and said, "The groom is here!" He quickly went in and came out with a set of clothes. He opened it, and although the clothes were made of coarse fabric, they were all newly made.

    778

    主人言曰：“家有息女，貌颇不陋，欲以侍奉巾栉，幸勿峻拒。”生惊，喜出望外，略为推逊，遂以婿礼见翁。主人曰：“本宜少缓，俟择吉期。但为匪人所绐，嘉礼濡迟至今，贻笑乡里，心所不甘。请即以今夕成好合。”遂以衣衣婿，生亦不辞。主人又驰召戚族，遍邀乡党，鼓乐旋集，花烛顿兴。生恍惚如梦，而究不得其所以见字之由。比入青庐，女有殊色，而齿微长于生。及夜定情，两相欢洽，女忽长叹，诣生曰：“予不意失顾而又得顾也。”时生犹冒叟姓，女固云然。

    Explanation

    778

    The host said, "I have a daughter who is not unattractive, and I wish for her to serve as a wife and manage household affairs. Please do not refuse harshly." The guest was surprised and delighted beyond expectation, slightly declining the offer, and was then received by the father-in-law as a son-in-law. The host continued, "Originally, it should have been delayed slightly, awaiting the selection of a favorable date. But I was deceived by someone unscrupulous, and the good ceremony has been delayed until now, bringing ridicule to the village. It is not pleasing to the heart. Please let us make a happy union tonight." Thus, he was dressed as a groom, and the guest also did not refuse. The host then hurriedly summoned relatives and friends, invited everyone in the village, and soon drums and music gathered, and the wedding lights were lit. The guest felt as if in a dream, but ultimately, he could not understand the reason for the letter. When he entered the bridal chamber, the woman was exceptionally beautiful, but her age was slightly older than the guest. When they consummated their marriage at night, they were both very happy together. The woman suddenly sighed deeply and approached the guest, saying, "I did not expect to lose your regard and then regain it." At that time, the guest was still using the host's surname, and the woman confirmed this.

    779

    生正怀疑，因诘之。女曰：“妾自弥岁已许字君之同姓行二者，其人久出为商。前岁一归，又不完娶。父促之，顾反怨怅。客夕有书来言，将在京娶妇，径绝妾婚。父甚怒，适君辱临，遂毅然成此好事。岂非天哉？”生遂叩顾居址，又即前所偕者。乃慨然曰：“冥漠中良有鬼神耶？何不爽若是也！”女亦惊询之，生以实告，遂相对叹惋不已。由是琴瑟甚调。弥月后，女请于父母，从生归拜姑嫜，皆许之。生遂以驴载妻，而自徒步以控之。不一日，径抵其家，率妻见其母，举室皆惊。

    Explanation

    779

    The protagonist was filled with doubt and questioned her. She said, "Since I was sixteen, I had already been betrothed to a man of the same surname as you, ranked second in lineage. He has been away for a long time as a merchant. Last year, he returned once, but did not complete the marriage. My father urged him, but he returned with regret and resentment. A letter came from him the other night, saying he will marry a woman in the capital and directly broke off my marriage. My father was very angry. Just when you humbly came, so decisively this good thing was accomplished. Isn't this fate?" The protagonist then asked about her residence and found that she was the same as the one he had previously been with. He sighed and said, "Indeed, there are spirits in the unseen world? Why not like this?" The woman was also surprised and asked, and the protagonist told the truth. They then kept sighing and lamenting. Thereafter, their marriage was very harmonious. After a month, the woman asked her parents and went with the protagonist to pay respects to his in-laws, all of whom agreed. The protagonist then carried his wife on a donkey and walked himself to control it. Not in a day, they directly arrived at his home, led his wife to see his mother, and the whole family was surprised.

    780

    生乃历述所遭，无不额手称庆。女事姑以孝，处室以和，且善自涂泽，年虽长而罔觉其长。生又以叟书致顾家，大失所望。后三年，生以事至太原，适遇顾于稠人中，见生负惭，即避去。生呼而止之，曰：“顾二兄，别来无恙？何遂无故人情耶？”顾不得已，与生见，谢其欺罔之罪。生握手大笑曰：“两憾俱有人代平，吾于君复何尤？”顾惊诘其故，生借市肆与之言，悉陈颠末。顾面赤汗淫，良久叹曰：“苍苍者诚不可欺。”因言其季父某贾于保定，有子失而复得，不谓即君也。

    Explanation

    780

    The man then recounted in detail his experiences, and everyone was overjoyed and celebrated. The woman served her mother-in-law with filial piety, lived harmoniously with her husband, and was also good at taking care of herself. Although she was old, no one could tell. The man also sent a letter from an elderly man to the family of Gu, greatly disappointing him. Three years later, the man arrived in Taiyuan on official business and just happened to meet Gu in a crowded place. Seeing the man, Gu felt ashamed and immediately turned away and left. The man called out to stop him, saying, "Brother Gu, how have you been? Why have you lost all sense of friendship?" Gu could not help but meet with the man and apologized for the deception. The man said, "The two regrets were both resolved by others. What more can I blame you for?" Gu asked in surprise the reason, and the man explained in the marketplace, detailing everything. Gu was flushed and sweating profusely, and after a long time, he exclaimed, "Indeed, the heavens cannot be deceived." He then said, "My younger uncle traded in Baoding and had a son who was lost and then found again. I did not expect it to be you."

    781

    客岁又寓书来言，逋者仍逋，且以重金潜逃，因以此致疾而死。向本议予为继，及得弟，遂毁成言。今岁叔亡，予适出外，乃以远族承其祀，此中殆有天意焉。叔所遗簿藉有云：‘白金三百，与子顾某。’君之所得，其即是欤？曩不识我负者叔偿，今又知予弃者子取矣。”生因为之鼓掌。既而念叟之恩，不觉流涕。乃与顾约，事毕迂道过平阳，哭拜于其冢，并谒顾媪，以母事之。家人环瞩，俨一顾叟之子也。及归，生又强顾至家，妆妻出见。顾大惭，遁去。生自是折节读书，后以选拔佐某县，夫妇竟以稀寿而终。

    Explanation

    781

    Last year, a letter came again stating that the debtor still failed to pay, and fled with a large sum of money, and thus died from the illness caused by this. Originally, it was planned for me to succeed, but upon getting a younger brother, the plan was abandoned. This year, my uncle died, and I happened to be out, so a distant relative took over the ancestral rites. There seems to be a divine will at play here. My uncle's will said, "Three hundred taels of silver, given to your son Guo." Is this what you received? I didn't know that my uncle, whom I owed, was repaid. Now I know that the one I abandoned took it. The man clapped his hands in approval. Later, thinking of the old man's kindness, he couldn't help but shed tears. He then made an agreement with Guo. After completing the matter, he took a detour through Pingyang, wept and bowed at his grave, and visited Guo's mother, treating her as a mother. The family looked at him, and he seemed like Guo's son. When he returned, he insisted on bringing Guo home. His wife came out to see, and Guo was greatly ashamed and fled. From then on, he became more diligent in his studies. Later, he was selected to assist in a county, and they both lived to an advanced age and died.

    782

    外史氏曰：顾之施不奇，而生之报则奇。然亦天实为之，非人之所能及也。向使以生见欺遽归，所损不过百金，而宵人终不免得志。今竟以此报复，使知失金得金，失妇得妇，而金即顾之金，妇即顾之妇，有不爽然自失者哉？局诈之风，其亦可以少息矣。亦以见天之报施，正自不爽。

    ○　春云
    　　沔阳之北有毕应霖者，少失怙恃，依其叔居。其性敏捷，不甚苦读，而词章诗赋绰有可观，人以为种灵使然。某年季秋，与人游菊圃。众皆对花命酒，惟毕素戒于天，乃设竹炉于花深处，探其落英，和以岕片，坐而品之。一时茶香花气，别具会心，醉翁故不及知也。毕正流览未已，吟兴欲发，忽一人短筇扶体，徐徐而来。迫视之，眉霜鬓雪，古朴衣冠，隐然高年逸士。知其非常人，起立拱俟。叟笑谓毕曰：“众人皆醉而独醒，郎君品必不俗。”乃以杖筑地曰：“龙钟之人不能为礼，请复居，聊当班荆，少领玉屑。”遂抠衣先坐，毕亦席地与谈。

    Explanation

    782

    外史氏曰：顾之施不奇，而生之报则奇。然亦天实为之，非人之所能及也。向使以生见欺遽归，所损不过百金，而宵人终不免得志。今竟以此报复，使知失金得金，失妇得妇，而金即顾之金，妇即顾之妇，有不爽然自失者哉？局诈之风，其亦可以少息矣。亦以见天之报施，正自不爽。

    The Commentator said: Gu's action was not unusual, but the reward of life was extraordinary. Indeed, it was Heaven that brought about this result, beyond the reach of human effort. If he had been deceived and returned promptly, the loss would not exceed a hundred gold coins, and the wicked person would still not fail to succeed. Now he finally avenged himself in this way, so that he understands the loss and gain of gold, and the loss and finding of a wife. The gold is Gu's gold, and the wife is Gu's wife. Who would not feel a sense of loss and disappointment? The风气 of deceit and cunning can also be somewhat alleviated. It also shows that the retribution and reward of Heaven are indeed without error.

    春云

    In the north of Mianyang, there was a man named Bi Yinglin who, in his youth, lost his parents and lived with his uncle. He was quick-witted and did not study very hard, yet his literary compositions and poetry were impressive, and people believed it was due to his innate talent. In the autumn of a certain year, he went to a chrysanthemum garden with others. Everyone was drinking wine while admiring the flowers, but Bi, who always abstained from alcohol, set up a bamboo stove deep among the flowers, picked fallen petals, and mixed them with tea leaves, sitting to savor them. At that moment, the fragrance of tea and flowers was uniquely refreshing, something the drunken man could not appreciate. As Bi was admiring the scenery and about to start composing, a man appeared, supporting himself with a short walking stick, coming slowly. Upon closer inspection, he had a beard and hair as white as snow, wore ancient and simple clothing, and was clearly an elderly recluse. Knowing he was not an ordinary person, Bi stood up and bowed to wait. The old man smiled and said to Bi, "Everyone is drunk except you; your taste must be refined." Then he tapped the ground with his staff and said, "An old and infirm person cannot perform proper etiquette, please sit again. Let us sit on the ground and share a little of this fine tea." He then adjusted his robe and sat down first, and Bi also sat on the ground to converse with him.

    783

    甫叩姓氏，叟即笑曰：“雅人相值，不宜絮絮俗事。鸡群之鹤，鸣必惊人，幸见赐观佳篇，以洗老耳，其他非所愿闻。”毕唯唯逊谢，而值技痒之余，不容自秘，因清命题。叟指畦边二种，目之曰：“此亦东篱之美人也。菊诗之作，代有名家，恐易落人窠臼。如此香艳，敢请各赋一律，何如？”毕亦微笑曰：“翁之意甚善，但隐逸当前，怜及脂粉，究虑来能免俗。”乃吟西施菊曰：“不共五湖游，偏逢三径秋。露凝归浣洗，烟笼捧心愁。吴苑香何在，庄园艳独留。近来添傲骨，无复舞腰柔。”又咏杨妃菊曰：“忽访陶彭泽，因惭李谪仙。亭中原眏酒，篱畔且偷眠。

    Explanation

    783
    ### Final Translation

The narrator asked the old man for his name and surname, and the old man smiled and said, "Meeting a cultured person, it's not right to talk about mundane matters. A crane among chickens, surely will astonish. Please show me your good works to cleanse my old ears, but I don't wish to hear anything else." The narrator bowed and expressed his gratitude, but due to the itch to show off his skills, he couldn't keep it to himself and politely asked for a topic to compose on. The old man pointed to two plants in the garden and said, "These are also the beautiful ladies of the east fence. Chrysanthemum poetry has been composed by many masters throughout the ages, and it's easy to fall into clichés. With such fragrance and beauty, dare I ask each of you to compose a regulated verse? How about that?" The narrator smiled and said, "The old man's intention is good, but with the current situation of seclusion, pitying the beauty of women, ultimately, it's hard to avoid the mundane."

The narrator then composed a poem about the West Beauty (Xishi) chrysanthemum:

"Unwilling to wander the five lakes, I meet the three paths in autumn. Dew forms on the flowers, returning to be washed, smoke envelops the flowers, holding a heart of sorrow. Where is the fragrance of the Wu garden? Only the beauty of the estate remains. Recently, I've gained a sense of pride, no longer with a graceful and flexible waist."

He then composed a poem about the Yang Fei chrysanthemum:

"Suddenly visiting the land of Tao, I feel ashamed in front of Li. In the pavilion, wine is reflected, by the fence, I sneak to sleep."
    784

    月映残妆懒，风回睡态偏。倘逢新雨露，绝似浴温泉。”诗成，叟大悦，遽起以掌扑毕肩曰：“真吾家快婿也。”言已，忽不见。毕大惊，自谓遇鬼，跄踉而归，茗碗茶铛狼藉于地，弗顾也。众方大嚼，见其皇仓失措，骇问之。喘汗以告，众犹笑而未信。毕又自诵所作，众始悚然，以为地近荒僻，且惧且疑，竟不及尽欢而散。毕归亦不敢言。越数日，毕之姊忽病，自邻邑使人来告，叔命毕往省之。至姊家，信宿淹留，俟姊小愈，始得归，则已旬余日矣。

    Explanation

    784

    The moonlight reflects on her half-applied makeup, and the wind turns to reveal her sleeping posture. If it encounters new rain and dew, it is absolutely like bathing in a hot spring." The poem was finished, and the old man was very pleased. He suddenly stood up and slapped Bi's shoulder, saying, "Truly a son-in-law of our household." After he spoke, he suddenly disappeared. Bi was greatly startled, believing he had encountered a ghost. He stumbled and hurriedly returned, leaving tea cups and kettles scattered on the ground, which he did not bother to pick up. The others were still enjoying their meal when they saw Bi in a state of panic. They were shocked and asked him what had happened. Bi, panting and sweating, told them, and the others still laughed but did not believe him. Bi then recited his own poem, and the others finally became alarmed, thinking the place was near a desolate area, and they were both afraid and suspicious, so they did not manage to finish their enjoyment before dispersing. Bi returned home and did not dare to speak of it. A few days later, Bi's sister suddenly fell ill, and someone from the neighboring town came to inform them. His uncle sent Bi to visit her. Upon arriving at his sister's house, he stayed for several days. After waiting until his sister was slightly better, he was finally able to return, and it had already been more than ten days.

    785

    及至家，其叔适在堂上，见即诃之曰：“牲畜少长，便不由老夫作主，婚嫁大事，竟不以闻，何复来面我耶？”遽操巨杖从之。毕惊而却走，其叔母自屏后出，劝止之。毕乃匍匐请命，叔犹恨恨怒詈不已。叔母因语毕曰：“自汝出门，期逝不返，汝叔颇疑之。昨者薄暮，有老妪送新妇至家，登堂展拜。予夫妇惊询其由，妪自言陆姓，其主翁居近村，悦汝聪颖，以女字汝，成婚已旬日。因汝思归，故先送新妇来。汝惧叔责，迟迟始至。迨言讫，遂自去。汝果今日还，足征不诬。新妇已留予室中，可证也。”毕愕然，乃力争之。叔母即呼曰：“若婿来矣！春云胡不出见耶？”俄见一女子，新妆炫目，亦出自屏后，匿笑含羞，立于叔母座侧。毕睨之，丽容稚齿，玉润花妍，目中实罕其匹，心大动。

    Explanation

    785

    Upon arriving home, Bi's uncle happened to be on the hall, and seeing him, he scolded and said, "You, being young, did not let me, the old man, make the decision. A major marriage affair, yet not informed, why come to see me again?" He immediately grabbed a big stick to chase him. Bi was startled and retreated. His aunt came out from behind the screen and advised and stopped him. Bi then crawled to beg for mercy, but his uncle still angrily scolded him without stopping. His aunt then told him, "Since you left, you did not return as promised, and your uncle suspected you. Yesterday evening, an old woman brought the new bride to the house, and she ascended the hall to pay respects. My husband and I were surprised and asked the reason. The old woman said her surname is Lu, and her master lives in a nearby village. He admired your intelligence and gave his daughter to you. The marriage has been for ten days. Because you wanted to return home, so she sent the new bride first. You were afraid of your uncle's blame, so you came late. After finishing speaking, she left. You indeed returned today, which proves it is not a lie. The new bride is already in my room, which can prove it." Bi was stunned and tried to argue. His aunt then called out, "Your husband has come! Why doesn't Chunyun come out to see him?" Soon, a woman appeared, dressed in new clothes that dazzled the eyes, also coming out from behind the screen, hiding a smile and blushing, standing beside his aunt's seat. Bi looked at her, with a beautiful appearance, young teeth, jade-like smoothness, and flower-like beauty. In his eyes, there is rarely a match, and his heart greatly moved.

    786

    窃念事必有由，若明言指证，叔性素暴，必拒绝而不容，是自弃其佳偶也，盍姑承之，至夕研诘可知。遂伏地自诬曰：“诚有是事。因翁命万不获辞，不告之罪，百口莫逭，惟叔念昔先人，宽儿重责，何敢置辩？”叔母乃大笑曰：“予固谓春云不我欺也。”于是向叔关说。叔究忿，怒命治东侧数小屋，析毕使居之，径掷杖于地，拂袖而出。毕不敢违命，叔母又怂恿之，令往粪除。阅时告竣，毕遂偕新妇移居东偏。叔母又遣一媪一婢，代供十指，一切器具，咸资焉。盖春云初至，叔母甚爱之，纳为己女，故呼以名而勷其事，殷殷然不能已。至暮，又馈晚眐。俟夫妇食讫，婢媪乃告白而去。

    Explanation

    786

    Secretly reflecting, I thought there must be a reason. If I were to speak openly and point out the evidence, my brother-in-law's character is typically violent, and he would surely refuse and not allow it, which would be to abandon my good match. Why not temporarily accept it, and by evening, you can investigate and find out. So, I fell to the ground and falsely confessed, saying, "Truly, there is such a thing. Because of my father-in-law's command, I cannot refuse. The crime of not informing, which no one can escape. Only the brother-in-law should remember the ancestors, forgive the child and impose a heavy responsibility, how dare I argue?" The sister-in-law then laughed greatly, saying, "I indeed thought that Chunyun did not deceive me." She then pleaded with the brother-in-law. The brother-in-law became extremely angry, angrily ordered to build several small houses on the east side, divided and let them live there, threw the staff on the ground, and waved the sleeves and left. Bi did not dare to disobey. The sister-in-law then urged him, ordering him to go and clean. After some time, it was completed, and Bi moved with the new wife to the east side. The sister-in-law then sent one old woman and one maid, serving as substitutes for the fingers, all utensils were provided. When Chunyun first arrived, the sister-in-law loved her greatly, taking her as her own daughter, calling her by name and assisting in the matter, with great earnestness and not stopping. By evening, she sent evening food. After the couple had finished eating, the old woman and maid then reported and left.

    787

    既夕人定，毕始诘女曰：“与若家素昧平生，未稔宅第所向，今忽矫称婚媾，加我以不白之冤，诚所不解。”女闻毕言，赧然者久之。徐曰：“此大人之命，妾实不能与知。”语已，背烛而坐，不复有言。毕青年未偶，颇不能自制，三问莫对，不复诘，径强之就榻下帷。欢好交合之际，流丹浃席，则固处子也。毕见女甚苦凿枘，乃戏曰：“岂有花烛经旬而犹葳蕤未启者？足征子之善诳矣！”女亦微笑，而终不肯言。晨起辟户，视院中什物，堆积几无隙地。盖即女家赠嫁者，竟莫解其所自来。毕以询女，仍不答，唯指挥婢媪，室中铺设一新。凡叔母所赐者，概令璧还，曰：“新妇家已自将来，无重烦慈颜僝僽也。

    Explanation

    787

    At dusk, when the people had settled down, Bi began to question the girl, saying, "I have never met your family before, and I do not know the direction of your home. How can you suddenly claim a marriage alliance, wronging me with an unexplained grievance? I truly do not understand." Upon hearing Bi's words, the girl blushed for a long time. She then replied, "This is the command of my elders; I truly do not know." After saying this, she turned her back to the candle and sat silently, saying nothing more. Bi, a young man who had not yet married, could not control himself and asked her three times without receiving an answer. He no longer questioned her and forced her to lie down on the bed. During their intimate encounter, blood stained the bed, indicating that she was indeed a virgin. Seeing the poor fit between them, Bi joked, "Is there anyone who, after a wedding celebration lasting several days, still remains closed like an unopened flower bud? This sufficiently demonstrates your skill in deception!" The girl smiled slightly but still refused to speak. In the morning, upon opening the door, Bi saw that the courtyard was filled with household items, piled up so much that there was almost no space left. These items were likely gifts from the girl's family, but he did not understand their origin. When he asked the girl, she still did not answer, only directing the maidservants to rearrange the room. She returned all the gifts given by Bi's mother-in-law, saying, "The new bride's family has already brought them over; do not trouble the kind face of my mother-in-law with unnecessary worry."

    788

    ”毕贪丽质，且年少罔知畏忌，反觉欣然。独其叔父母深忧之，疑为妖异。因走伻诣其姊家，并就近村侦访。姊家言毕稽迟之故，及攀留日期，与毕初言吻合。且近村亦无陆姓富家，益大惧，究亦无可如何。居数日，女白于毕，将归宁。毕诺之，兼欲偕往，女亦许之。诘朝，有肩舆二乘止于门前，曰：“前村陆翁来迎春娘子并新郎君者，幸即行。”毕欲告其叔父母，女力止，乃各乘一舆，悄然命驾。毕本思窃迹其异，而舆中严密，莫克窥观。不知行几里许，似抵其家，舆止而不前。毕始搴帷瞻之，则已嵯峨插天，巉岩俯地，已息于万山之中，不禁大骇。而谛观之下，鸟啼花落，林木蔚然，又似别有佳境，心甫少安。

    788

    毕贪丽质，且年少罔知畏忌，反觉欣然。独其叔父母深忧之，疑为妖异。因走伻诣其姊家，并就近村侦访。姊家言毕稽迟之故，及攀留日期，与毕初言吻合。且近村亦无陆姓富家，益大惧，究亦无可如何。居数日，女白于毕，将归宁。毕诺之，兼欲偕往，女亦许之。诘朝，有肩舆二乘止于门前，曰：“前村陆翁来迎春娘子并新郎君者，幸即行。”毕欲告其叔父母，女力止，乃各乘一舆，悄然命驾。毕本思窃迹其异，而舆中严密，莫克窥观。不知行几里许，似抵其家，舆止而不前。毕始搴帷瞻之，则已嵯峨插天，巉岩俯地，已息于万山之中，不禁大骇。而谛观之下，鸟啼花落，林木蔚然，又似别有佳境，心甫少安。

    789

    及视翁之门第，日蔽云连，壮丽不可名状。女先降舆，招毕同入，毕趦趄从之。应门无丈夫，惟童子数人，披发垂髫，年约舞象。见女即笑迎曰：“阿姊来矣！翁与诸姊皆凝望。”女令先入告，乃与毕握腕徐行。重门内外，茂树干霄，且蓊郁，日光莫入。再进，则疏竹倚墙，幽兰盈砌，奇花数百本，概莫能举其名。人未至，眠盠即惊起，隔花绕柳，吠声与铃声相杂。女因笑曰：“去几何时，狗子竟不我识耶？”及至厅庑，翁早扶杖出迎。视其状貌，即菊畦相遇之叟也，被服赫奕，迥殊畴昔。童子拥之降阶，且揖且笑曰：“吾家快婿果来乎？老夫唯虑若不至耳！”毕忆往事，顿觉震悚，勉以婿礼见，然已跼蹐不胜。

    Explanation

    789

    Upon seeing the father-in-law's house, it was grand and imposing, magnificent beyond description. The bride invited her husband to enter together, and he hesitated before following her. There were no adult men at the door, only a few young boys with long hair and braids, around the age of 15-20. They greeted her with smiles and said, "Big sister has come! The father and sisters are all looking forward to her arrival." The bride asked them to go inside and inform the family, and then she and her husband entered, holding hands. Inside, the doors and halls were adorned with tall, lush trees that reached the sky, and the interior was dense and shadowy, with no sunlight entering. Further inside, there were sparse bamboo plants leaning against the walls, and fragrant orchids growing in the courtyards, with hundreds of rare flowers, none of which could be named. Before they reached the hall, a dog named Mianli woke up and started barking, moving through the garden among the flowers and willows, its barks mingling with the sounds of bells. The bride laughed and said, "It's been so long, but the dog doesn't recognize me anymore!" When they finally reached the hall, the father-in-law had already come out to greet them, supporting himself with a cane. He looked just like the old man from the chrysanthemum field, but he was now dressed in splendid attire, quite different from before. The young boys escorted them down the steps, bowing and smiling, and said, "Our beloved son-in-law has finally come! I was only worried that you wouldn't come!" The groom remembered the past and felt a sudden shudder, but he managed to greet him as a son-in-law, though he felt constrained and uneasy.

    790

    忽闻画屏之侧，娇音群噪，似有人鼓掌曰：“姊昔谓市井小儿俗态可掬，今新婿竟如何耶？”言已，一堂粲然，毕颇有惭色。瞩之，丽人四五，貌皆亚于女，而妖冶过之。叟揖婿升阶，且谢曰：“邻家诸犹女，阿云之姊妹行也。素相嘲谑，幸勿怪。”又叱诸女曰：“嘉客初至而喧笑如斯，何不逊之甚？”众始敛容，拥女入内。叟与婿坐谈，毕视堂上，金玉交辉，而布置亦绝闲雅，第怀疑惧，终不自安。叟乃自白曰：“若勿骇异，老夫实狐仙，居此千五百年矣。爱女择婿，每难其人。遥望贵乡，其气森秀，应有吉士生其间。敬往访之，前见郎君啜茗临风，俨然芝兰玉树，翘然于荆棘之中。

    Explanation

    790

    Suddenly, from beside the painted screen, sweet voices erupted in a clamor, as if someone was clapping and saying, "Sister once said that the vulgar manners of common children were obvious. Now, what about the new son-in-law?" Upon hearing this, the entire hall was filled with smiles, but everyone had a somewhat embarrassed look. Looking around, several beautiful women were present, their appearances all inferior to the daughter's, yet more alluring than her. The old man bowed to the son-in-law and invited him to ascend the steps, saying, "Thank you." He then explained, "These are the daughters of the neighbors, the sisters of Ah Yun. We have always teased each other. Please don't be offended." He then reprimanded the women, "A good guest has just arrived, and you are laughing so loudly. How disrespectful is this?" The crowd began to compose themselves, and they escorted the daughter inside. The old man sat and talked with the son-in-law. Looking around the hall, gold and jade intermingled in brilliance, and the arrangement was also extremely elegant and refined. However, he was still suspicious and fearful, finally unable to feel at ease. The old man then revealed, "If you do not find it strange, I am indeed a fox spirit. I have lived here for fifteen hundred years. I love my daughter and choose her husband carefully, but it is always difficult to find the right person. From afar, I see your village, and its atmosphere is dense and beautiful. There should be a virtuous gentleman born there. I respectfully went to visit. I saw you drinking tea and facing the wind, like a fragrant orchid and a jade tree, standing out prominently among thorns."

    791

    更聆瑶章，字珠句玉，老夫心窃依慕。归与阿云谋，乃作狡狯，结此良姻，非有所祸于若也。”语竟，又谢欺罔。叟虽凿凿剖陈，毕仍忧怖于内，因强起申谢，力求先归。叟不怿，哂曰：“讵有远谒泰山而无杯勺之敬者？”言次，麝兰遄发，诸女复出。叟一一指曰：“此为艳云腻云等，皆吾族也。”又指一人曰：“此名春柳，本他族，逼处于此，既已拜予膝下矣。”毕稍稍目之，见其倜傥风流，别具丰韵，颇为之意释。有顷，诸童供馔，肴核杂陈。叟亲起觞婿，毕以不能饮固辞。乃别设醴酒，使与云偕坐，唯叟异席相酬。诸女俱列坐于云侧，粉香缭绕，口脂频吹，毕竟乐而忘返。间与诸女角采争胜，觥筹相交，竟罔顾叟之在坐。

    Explanation

    791

    Upon hearing the refined and exquisite writing, the old man secretly admired and longed for it. Returning home to discuss with Ah Yun, he acted cunningly to arrange this good marriage, saying, "There is no disaster for you." After finishing his speech, he apologized for the deception. Although the old man explained clearly and in detail, he was still anxious and fearful inside. Therefore, he forced himself to stand and express his gratitude, striving hard to return first. The old man was unhappy and sneered, "How can there be someone who visits the Eastern Peak without the slightest respect?" As he spoke, a sudden fragrance of musk and orchid was released, and all the women came out again. The old man pointed out one by one and said, "This is for Yan Yun, Ni Yun, etc., all of my family." He then pointed to another person and said, "This person's name is Chun Liu, originally from another family, forced to stay here, and has already been adopted by me." All of them gradually looked at him, seeing him dashing and elegant, possessing a unique charm, and were quite relieved for him. After a while, all the children served food, with delicacies arranged in a variety. The old man personally got up to toast the son-in-law, but all of them firmly declined because they were unable to drink. Then, sweet wine was set up separately, and he was made to sit with Yun, while the old man toasting each other from a different seat. All the women sat in a row beside Yun, with fragrance of powder winding around and lipstick frequently applied. In the end, they were happy and forgot to return. Sometimes competing with all the women in beauty and for victory, wine cups and counting sticks interacting, and finally ignoring the old man's presence.

    792

    正嬉笑问，似闻叟叹曰：“人不易知，此亦俗不可医者也。”遽起入屏后，不复终礼。须臾，杯盘狼藉，女与艳云等亦先去。毕不善饮，虽薄釂，早已沉醺，辄与春柳互相嘲戏。醉中见其貌，弥觉动人，乃以言挑之，笑而弗拒。窃视诸童不在，遂就堂侧合焉，较春去倍觉流动，毕益迷惑。柳因谓毕曰：“君固知渠为孤，亦闻妾非其类。但人与狐处。不三月当有死道，翁所言皆绐君也，君宜慎之。”毕本预怀首鼠，闻柳言，益大恐。乃诘曰：“渠固狐，非其类者岂亦人耶？”柳曰：“然！妾即居此山下，实人也。因屡为狐祟，勉从欢笑，岂真螟蛉于此哉？”毕大喜，谋与偕遁，柳亦首肯。

    Explanation

    792

    He jokingly asked, and it seemed as if the old man sighed and said, "People are not easily understood; this person is also someone whose habits cannot be changed." He suddenly got up and went behind the screen, leaving without finishing the ritual. Soon, the tableware was in disarray, and the woman and Yanyun also left first. Bi was not good at drinking; even though he only took a small sip, he was already heavily intoxicated. He would often tease and joke with Chunliu. In his drunken state, he found her appearance even more attractive, and he then tried to flirt with her, and she laughed but did not reject him. He secretly saw that the children were not there, and he then went to the side of the hall and did something intimate. Compared to when Chun went, he felt even more aroused, and Bi became even more confused. Liu then said to Bi, "You know that she is a fox spirit, and you also know that I am not of her kind. But if a human and a fox spirit live together, there will be a death within three months. What the old man said was all a trick on you; you should be cautious." Bi had already been hesitant, and upon hearing Liu's words, he became even more frightened. He then asked, "She is a fox, and those who are not of her kind, are they also humans?" Liu replied, "Yes! I live at the foot of this mountain, and I am a real person. Because I have been repeatedly tormented by fox spirits, I have reluctantly joined in their merriment, but I am not truly one of them." Bi was greatly pleased and planned to run away with her, and Liu also agreed.

    793

    遂同起整衣，相携潜出，叟家寂无知者。转折而下，约有里许，果至其家。茅屋数椽，围以短篱，虽远逊叟之所居，毕自乐之。柳将命酒，毕辞以量，遽展衾褥，觅其余欢。乃毕自与柳交，小腹隐痛，若以冰雪相沃。初亦不以介意，至是复作，而因爱之，不能遽舍。情事甫阕，觉冷逼丹田，径透脏腑，遂昏然不复人世。魂既离舍，缕缕如丝，仿佛闻柳笑曰：“妖婢不羞，竟能独擅佳婿耶？”毕心甚衔之。又有顷，火光大作，震电有声，毕乃恍然若梦寤。犹未欠伸，耳畔似有人娇泣曰：“予引夫子至此，非予死之，而谁咎？”又有人娇嗔曰：“薄情郎固不足恤也。”细语嘈杂。毕张目一视，则春云伏尸而啼，艳云等亦旋绕于侧。

    Explanation

    793

    They got up together, tidied their clothes, and crept out quietly, with no one at the old man's house knowing about it. Turning and going down, they covered about a few li, and indeed arrived at his home. It was a mud hut with a few rooms, surrounded by a short fence, far inferior to the old man's dwelling, but the character completely enjoyed it. Liu ordered wine, and the character refused on the grounds of capacity, quickly spreading out the bedding, and seeking the rest of the pleasure. Then the character had intercourse with Liu, feeling a dull pain in the lower abdomen, as if washed over by ice and snow. Initially, the character did not regard it as significant, but by then the pain reappeared, and the character loved it and could not let go quickly. The affairs just ended, and the character felt a cold pressure in the lower abdomen, penetrating the internal organs, becoming dazedly no longer feeling like a human being. The soul had already left the body, thread by thread like silk, and the character seemed to hear Liu laugh and say, "The demon maid is not ashamed, could she uniquely possess a fine husband?" The character resented it very much. Again, there was a moment, and the flame erupted, with thunderbolts and sounds. The character then suddenly awoke as if from a dream. Still not yawning and stretching, by the ear, someone seemed to tenderly weep and say, "I led the gentleman to this place, not I caused his death, who else could blame?" Again, someone tenderly scolded and said, "The heartless man is not worth concerning." The whispers were noisy. The character opened his eyes once and looked, and then saw Chunyun lying over the body and crying, and Yanyun and others also twirled beside.

    794

    毕不觉心怍，且四体裸然，遂复抱惭闭目。女见其更生，取衣与著。因微含愠容数之曰：“君以妾为异类，不念旧姻，亦当别求新特，何甘心与鬼为婿，中夜逋逃，自蹈死地。今日非妾父子，郎岂能生下此山乎？”毕益赧然。衣讫自起，微诘柳之所在，女指岩下白骨示之曰：“此即君之可人也。渠本故宋时淮南一名妓，因随商人至此，偶患心疾，死即藁葬于此岭之侧。历年久远，精魄不消，间出为行旅患。老父虑为妾玷，治之以术，渠乃极口求生，父不忍，命与妾雁行，朝夕相依。春柳之名，亦父之所字也。昨夕嘉会，本不宜令渠干预。但念郎君高雅，必不见惑于淫妖，故亦容之与席。

    Explanation

    794

    Bì did not feel ashamed and was completely naked, so he closed his eyes out of embarrassment. The woman saw him reviving and took clothes to dress him. She then slightly frowned and scolded him, saying, "You consider me an outsider, forget our old relationship, and should have sought a new partner. Why did you willingly marry a ghost, flee at midnight, and step into a place of death? Without my father and me, how could you have survived this mountain?" Bì felt even more embarrassed. After dressing himself, he asked about the location of the willow. The woman pointed to the white bones at the foot of the rock and said, "This is the person you favored. She was once a famous courtesan from Huainan during the Song Dynasty, who followed a merchant here. She fell ill, died, and was buried in a simple grave on the side of this hill. After many years, her spirit has not dissipated and occasionally becomes a threat to travelers. My father feared she would bring disgrace, so he treated her with a method. She loudly sought life, and my father could not bear it, so he ordered her to live with me, relying on each other daily. The name 'Spring Willow' was also given to her by my father. Last night's joyful meeting should not have involved her. But considering your high moral standards, you would not be swayed by her charms, so I allowed her to participate."

    795

    畴知竟为所诱哉？幸父上浼丰隆，击破其冢，更以丹药活郎。不然，弗可生也已。”语次，毕视其骸，髑髅如雪，益为胆寒心悸。因启于女，将复谒翁，以酬德而谢过。女摇腕曰：“翁谓郎有俗骨，不欲再相见，命妾伴君遄归，勿致叔父母滋惑。”言未已，前舆即自来，女与毕仍各乘之。女忽顾谓众云曰：“妹等少俟，姊当复来矣。”毕虽闻之，犹不料其有归心。舆行绝驰，俄抵里门。女又招毕同下，把袂而泣曰：“郎自此旋归，父有严命，不容再侍裳衣矣。幸自爱，无庸念妾。”毕闻惊绝，面无人色，悲咽曰：“赖子复活，方期相守白头，何忽生去志，岂犹以前郄为念耶？”女曰：“不然。老父薑桂之性，在昔已然。自生妾，即期以雅人相配。

    Explanation

    795

    Who would have known that I was ultimately lured? Fortunately, my father summoned the Wind Lord, who smashed the tomb and revived me with elixirs. Otherwise, I would not have survived." While talking, I looked at his bones, which were as white as snow, and I became even more terrified. I then told the woman that I would visit the old man again to repay his kindness and apologize for my mistake. The woman shook her hand and said, "The old man thinks you have common bones and does not want to see you again. He has ordered me to accompany you back quickly so that your uncles and aunts do not become confused." Before she finished speaking, the carriage came, and the woman and I each got in. The woman suddenly turned and told the others, "Wait a little while, I will come back." I heard it but did not expect her to have such a strong desire to return. The carriage sped along and soon reached the village gate. The woman called me down and held my sleeve, crying, "From now on, you must return home. My father has strict orders, and I cannot stay with you anymore. Take care of yourself, and don't think of me." I was so shocked that my face turned pale, and I sobbed, "Thanks to you, I was revived. I expected to spend my life with you. Why do you suddenly want to leave? Are you still thinking of our past misunderstandings?" The woman said, "No. My father has a sharp and spicy personality, which he has always had. Since I was born, he has always expected me to be paired with a refined person."

    796

    前见君子，一旦倾心，故不惜百计营求，成此好合。不谓贪俄顷之欢，折百年之偶，竟在此日也。”毕知不可挽，复以言激之曰：“如卿所论，诚予自贻伊戚。然必有风雅过我者，翁故以此败盟耳。”语未及终，女早色变曰：“何来此薄幸之言，岂反不谅我乎？妾虽迫于父命，终身或不自主。但自入君家，人情汹汹怪异，所可恃者，唯君耳！今君心又添疑块，不去，将祸起于皀席矣！前车可鉴，君不尝惑于邪鬼之谗耶？”毕语塞。女又叹曰：“天壤虽大，半皆未有之王郎。妾即归，实无他志。然以君之才貌，虽具俗肠，犹钟秀气，不可谓非佳配。

    Explanation

    796

    When I first saw the gentleman, I instantly fell in love, and thus spared no effort to pursue him, achieving this harmonious union. Who would have thought that indulging in a fleeting moment of pleasure would break a lifelong partnership, and it happened on this very day." Knowing that she could not change his mind, she tried to provoke him with words: "As you say, indeed, I have brought this misfortune upon myself. But there must be someone more refined than me, and my father-in-law used this as an excuse to break the engagement." Before she finished speaking, the woman's expression changed, and she said, "How can you say such unfaithful things, do you not understand me at all? I am forced by my father's command, and my life may not be my own. But since I came into your family, there has been much gossip and strange behavior, and the only one I can rely on is you! Now your heart is filled with doubt, and if you don't leave, disaster will arise from within the household! We can learn from past mistakes, haven't you been deceived by the slander of evil spirits before?" The man was speechless. The woman then sighed, "Though the world is vast, half of it consists of men like Wang Lang. If I return, I truly have no other intentions. But with your talent and appearance, even if you have worldly desires, you still possess a refined spirit, and you cannot be considered a poor match."

    797

    今既灭裂，其命也夫？”乃留玉钏为别，兼脱珊瑚指环一双，曰：“以献叔母，见物庶几相忆。”竟挥泪登舆，去如飘风，瞬息即渺。毕懊丧而返，步及所居，启扃而入，则室皆璧立，不知所自来，亦莫知所自往。惟束古书数卷于案头，上粘锦笺一幅，大书九字曰：“劝毕郎宜苦读，毋过俗。”毕因三叹。往告叔父母，反不为毕吊，而为毕庆。惟其叔母，见环犹深思忆，余人莫不色喜。女之言，良非无见也。其叔亟为议姻，毕不愿，而力不获辞。成婚之夕，忽一急足锤门曰：“春娘子以一缄致郎。”启读之，则七言一绝。其诗曰：“大雅从来绝世尘，奈何相见即相亲。知君俗骨应难换，莫对新人话旧人。”毕方盷悒，旋失急足所在。自此刻意求雅，谈吐襟怀顿异。故昔龚芝麓先生，盖尝称之。

    Explanation

    797

    Now, using the research and context we gathered above, produce a final, polished translation of the source text.

    Your goal is to deliver a final English translation that is both faithful to the original Classical Chinese and reads naturally in English. Apply the insights from the pre-drafting research directly — handle idiomatic expressions, cultural allusions, syntactic ambiguities, and proper nouns as you planned.

    Here are 5 examples showing the expected translation quality and style:

    Example 1

    Source: 爰及農商工賈，廝役奴隸，釣魚屠肉，飯牛牧羊，皆有先達，可為師表，博學求之，無不利於事也。

    Translation: As for farmers, merchants, artisans, and traders, as well as servants, laborers, and slaves, those engaged in fishing and butchering, feeding oxen and herding sheep—all have predecessors who can serve as role models. By seeking knowledge broadly from them, nothing is unhelpful in one's endeavors.

    Example 2

    Source: 兩頭四臂女

    靈帝時，洛陽女子生時兩頭四臂。

    Translation: A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    Example 3

    Source: 凡為文章，猶乘騏驥，雖有逸氣，當以銜勒制之，勿使流亂軌躅，放意填坑岸也。

    Translation: In all writing, it is like riding a swift steed — though it may possess a wild and unrestrained spirit, one must control it with bit and bridle, and must not let it run wild, straying from the proper path or indulging in aimless wanderings.

    Example 4

    Source: 自古明王聖帝，猶須勤學，況凡庶乎！此事遍於經史，吾亦不能鄭重，聊舉近世切要，以啟寤汝耳。

    Translation: From ancient times, wise kings and sage emperors still required diligent study; how much more so, then, for common people! This matter is found throughout the classics and histories. I cannot elaborate further, but I will briefly mention a few essential points of recent times in order to enlighten you.

    Example 5

    Source: 夫文章者，原出五經：詔命策檄，生於書者也；序述論議，生於易者也；歌詠賦頌，生於詩者也；祭祀哀誄，生於禮者也；書奏箴銘，生於春秋者也。朝廷憲章，軍旅誓誥，敷顯仁義，發明功德，牧民建國，不可暫無。至於陶冶性靈，從容諷諫，入其滋味，亦樂事也。行有餘力，則可習之。

    Translation: As for writings, their origin lies in the Five Classics: edicts, memorials, and military proclamations arise from the Shujing (Book of Documents); expository and argumentative writings arise from the Yijing (Book of Changes); songs, odes, and praises arise from the Shijing (Book of Poetry); ritual sacrifices, mourning, and elegies arise from the Liji (Book of Rites); memorials, admonitions, and inscriptions arise from the Chunqiu (Spring and Autumn Annals). Court regulations, military oaths and decrees, the proclamation of benevolence and righteousness, the elucidation of virtue and merit, governing the people and establishing the state—all are indispensable. As for cultivating the mind and spirit, offering gentle admonition in a relaxed manner, and savoring the flavor of such writings, these too are pleasant pursuits. When one has spare energy, then one may study them.

    Source text:
    今既灭裂，其命也夫？”乃留玉钏为别，兼脱珊瑚指环一双，曰：“以献叔母，见物庶几相忆。”竟挥泪登舆，去如飘风，瞬息即渺。毕懊丧而返，步及所居，启扃而入，则室皆璧立，不知所自来，亦莫知所自往。惟束古书数卷于案头，上粘锦笺一幅，大书九字曰：“劝毕郎宜苦读，毋过俗。”毕因三叹。往告叔父母，反不为毕吊，而为毕庆。惟其叔母，见环犹深思忆，余人莫不色喜。女之言，良非无见也。其叔

    798

    外史氏曰：雅人深致，近世颇难其人。叟欲于立谈间得之，谈何容易也？既已轻掷其珠，旋复亟怀其宝，狐之行事，究属迂疏。况当履交错，又不闲之以礼，翁先自失雅道矣，婿宁得有雅士乎？惟其女侃侃数语，不第得妇之正，抑且得雅之真。

    ○　折狱
    　　某进士少登黄甲，年只十八令，榜下即受某县令。虽圣朝有心吁俊，实重任不易仔肩。封君某，心窃忧之，偕以之任，薄书案牍靡不身亲，宰唯升座佥行而已。暇更与之讲求吏治，指陈弊端。封君固浙中宿儒，兼工刀笔，言之皆中肯綮。宰本素慧，亦积渐能通。莅任周岁，政声大著，自中丞以下，举不能以年少轻之。一日，因公出郭，适遇某大户之丧。执绋者约数百人，幡幢鼓乐，仪采甚都。旧例吉凶大故，虽上宪亦避其途，宰因止于道周，以俟其进。一时灵眑既过，其后有孝舆，娇泣嘤嘤，固即未亡人也。忽值暴风，素帏高揭，妇之衣尽露于外，则斩缞之下，别有红裳，且色甚鲜艳。

    Explanation

    798

    Translation

    The historian said: "A refined person with profound character is rare in recent times. The old man desires to attain such refinement in a brief conversation; how easy is that? Having casually discarded a pearl, he quickly cherishes a treasure again; the fox's actions are ultimately impractical. When social interactions are complex, and he does not treat them with propriety, the old man first loses the way of refinement, how can the son-in-law be a refined gentleman? Only the daughter speaks calmly and clearly, attaining not only the proper conduct of a wife but also true refinement."

    Case of Judicial Resolution

    A certain scholar-official, having passed the imperial examinations at a young age, was appointed as magistrate at eighteen. Though the sovereign court sought talent, this was a heavy responsibility not easily shouldered. His father-in-law, Mr. So-and-so, privately worried about this and accompanied him to his post. He personally attended to all official documents and records, while the magistrate merely ascended his seat and signed off on matters. In his spare time, he further instructed him in the administration of government and pointed out its flaws. The father-in-law was indeed a renowned scholar from Zhejiang, skilled in writing legal documents, and his words all hit the mark. The magistrate, naturally intelligent, gradually became adept. After a year in office, his reputation for good governance grew, and from the vice-governor down, none could look down on him because of his youth. One day, on an official errand, he happened to encounter the funeral procession of a prominent family. There were about several hundred people holding the funeral banners, with banners, drums, and music, all very grand. According to old custom, even high-ranking officials would avoid the path of a funeral procession, so the magistrate stopped at the roadside to wait for it to pass. Once the main funeral procession had gone by, a carriage followed, with a woman sobbing softly inside—she was the widow. Suddenly, a strong wind blew, lifting the white mourning cloth high, revealing the woman's clothes beneath the mourning attire. Underneath the mourning clothes, there was another red dress, and its color was very bright.

    799

    宰瞥见之，心颇疑讶，因命役咨访，哭于舆者何人，犹不意为其室也。反报，则某监生新逝，别无眷属，舆中人实惟其妻。宰乃大疑，知必有异，呼群役使沮其行，且命停榇于某寺，以候检验，究亦不言其故。乃亡者之戚族，半系巨绅，其次亦无白衣者，闻之愕然。亟面宰，哀恳至再，终不听，惟正色曰：“诸公与化者似非路人，讵忍其死不以命？如不从予相，予宁挂冠归，誓不再莅是邑。”众不得已，姑听之，且私议曰：“俟无实迹，再当反唇，看此乳臭官以何面目相见。”宰既力止其丧，遄归告父。封君侧首沉思曰：“汝能体察，吾心甚快。但系巨家，非齐民，不得玩视。倘验而无伤，便难收拾。

    Explanation

    799

    The magistrate caught sight of the carriage and was greatly suspicious and astonished. He then ordered the servants to inquire who was weeping in the carriage, still not suspecting it was the wife of the deceased. They reported back that a certain candidate for the imperial examinations had recently died, with no other relatives, and the person in the carriage was indeed his wife. The magistrate then became greatly suspicious, knowing there must be something unusual. He called out to the servants to stop their journey and ordered the coffin to be stopped at a certain temple to await inspection, but did not explain the reason.

    The relatives of the deceased, half of whom were great gentry and the rest not commoners, were shocked upon hearing this. They immediately confronted the magistrate, pleading with great sorrow repeatedly, but he finally did not listen. He only said sternly, "You gentlemen seem not to be strangers to the deceased. How could you not tell him about his death? If you do not follow my advice, I would rather resign and return home, swearing never to return to this county." The crowd had no choice but to listen to him, and privately discussed, "Wait until there is no evidence, then we will speak out against him. See how this young official will face you." The magistrate strongly stopped the funeral and rushed back to tell his father. The father nodded and thought deeply, "You can understand, my heart is very pleased. But it involves a great family, not ordinary people. It cannot be treated lightly. If the examination shows no injury, it will be difficult to handle."

    800

    必须先探本源，得有确证，然后一发破的。欲明此事，非予亲行不可。”宰时已有成见，窃谓不然，且不欲劳父，跽止之。封君笑曰：“予虽未登仕版，而为民跋履，亦犹为国驰驱，非一家之私也。汝何阻为？”于是易装为卜人，秘密出署。濒行，授宰以策，且戒曰：“事涉闺闼，勿以一衣之微而召祸也。”宰始悟，一一敬从。翌日遂托疾不出视事。诸绅闻而大粲，谓宰以儿戏阻丧，既而知悔，因埋首衙斋，不改孺子之故智。乃故具公牍，促其出验，宰竟置若罔闻。越数日，又叠催之，更冥然漠然。有棺不得葬，有穴不得掩，众皆含愤不平。即署中吏役，及里巷之人，莫不咎宰。

    Explanation

    800

    The high-ranking official said, "One must first explore the root cause, obtain solid evidence, and then strike the target accurately. To clarify this matter, I must personally go." The magistrate had already formed an opinion and privately thought otherwise, but he did not want to trouble his father and knelt to stop him. The high-ranking official smiled and said, "Although I have not yet entered the ranks of officials, to walk on behalf of the people is also like running for the state, not just for the benefit of one family. Why do you try to stop me?" Thus, he changed his attire to that of a fortune-teller and secretly left the office. Before leaving, he gave the magistrate a strategy and warned, "The matter involves the inner chambers; do not let a small matter like clothing invite disaster." The magistrate then realized the gravity of the situation and followed his instructions. The next day, he pretended to be ill and did not leave his office to handle affairs. The local elites heard this and laughed, thinking that the magistrate was stopping the funeral with trivial matters. Later, they regretted their hasty judgment and saw that the magistrate was deeply concerned. They prepared official documents to urge him to investigate, but the magistrate ignored them completely. After several days, they continued to pressure him, but he remained indifferent. With a coffin but unable to bury it, and a grave but unable to cover it, everyone was filled with anger and injustice. Even the officials and common people in the neighborhood blamed the magistrate.

    801

    事闻太守，不忍严檄，姑先驰书切责，欲其谢过于众绅。宰不引咎，惟禀复云云，以为人命至重，缓葬无妨，愿假旬日限，疾愈即出相验，如不得其致死之由，情甘伏此淹留之罪。语直而壮，太守亦解其意，而究以为忧。乃封君周行数日，绝无人讼某死之冤，心亦忐忑。一夕，孤踪郊外，无所栖身，因借中田之处小憩。旋有人来叱问之，封君起与为礼，伪称异乡失足，货卜糊口，路暗不能前进者，其人信之，慨然留宿。庐甚隘，不足以容二人，其人又田主雇倩为之守望者，亦不敢寐。相与絮谈，以消长夜。封君固有心，咨询不欲或遗一人。微以言挑之曰：“今岁田禾如此，脱遇贤长官，百姓可以无忧。”其人忽叹曰：“君勿言此，使我心戚。

    Explanation

    801

    The matter came to the attention of the prefect, who, unable to issue a stern edict, temporarily sent a letter with stern criticism, wanting the magistrate to admit his fault in front of the gentry. However, the magistrate did not admit fault and only replied as follows: "Believed that human life is of utmost importance, and delaying the burial would not be harmful. Wish to grant a ten-day limit. When he recovers, he will come out to verify. If the cause of death cannot be determined, is willing to accept the sin of delay." His words were straightforward and bold, and the prefect also understood his intention, but still considered it a concern. After the lord traveled for several days, no one came forward to complain about the injustice of someone's death, and he also felt uneasy in his heart. One night, alone in the outskirts, nowhere to rest, he took a rest in a middle field. Soon someone came and shouted at him. The lord stood up and greeted him, falsely claiming to have lost his footing in a foreign land, selling divination to make a living, and the road was dark and he could not proceed. The person believed him and generously offered to let him stay overnight. The hut was very narrow, not spacious enough for two people. The person was a hired guard for the landowner and dared not sleep. They chatted endlessly to pass the long night. The lord had a particular intention, inquiring without wanting to miss anyone, and subtly probed with words: "This year's crops are like this. If we encounter a wise official, the people can be free from worries." The person suddenly sighed and said, "Do not say this, it makes me feel sad."

    802

    敝邑数年来，颇遭悍吏之虐。今邑侯年虽甚少，独能体恤小民。昨入城市，闻将不能久任，后有来者，恐难克继美政也。”封君闻之，心喜，又故诘之。答曰：“聆君土音，似与邑侯相近，无敢泄。”封君佯答曰：“一贵一贱，何论乡情？予谒之且若登天，言亦何从而泄乎？”其人乃曰：“我辈皆在草野，言固无碍。某太学者，予之佃主也，甚强壮，闻其猝死，心颇疑之。及往职丧事，询其死由，其家人皆莫知，惟一小童深知之，私以告予。则太学之妻，夙与其内兄有染。内兄适新断弦，思毙其夫，因以嫁之。好事宜将成，不意为邑侯所疑，留尸侯验，又不即发。

    Explanation

    802

    My humble town has suffered greatly over the past few years from the tyranny of harsh officials. Now, our county magistrate, though young, is uniquely able to sympathize with and care for the common people. Yesterday, when I entered the city, I heard that he will not remain in office for long, and I fear that the new official who comes after him may not be able to continue good governance. The enfeoffed lord, upon hearing this, was pleased, but he deliberately questioned me further. I replied, "I can tell from your local accent that you are similar to the county magistrate, so I dare not reveal anything." The enfeoffed lord feigned a response, saying, "When one is rich and the other poor, why should we talk about hometown ties? Visiting him is like climbing to heaven; how could I possibly reveal anything?" The other person then said, "We are all in the countryside, so our words are not a concern. My landlord is a scholar, very strong, and I heard that he died suddenly, which made me quite suspicious. When I attended to his funeral, I asked about the cause of his death, but his family members knew nothing. Only a young boy deeply understood the situation and privately told me. It turns out that the scholar's wife had been intimate with her brother-in-law for a long time. Her brother-in-law had recently lost his wife and wanted to kill his husband in order to marry her. The good match was about to be arranged, but it was unexpectedly discovered by the county magistrate, who left the body for inspection and did not immediately release it."

    803

    族中觊其巨资，将群起与邑侯为难，事果上闻，欲不免官得乎？”封君闻至此，不胜私幸，又故为咨嗟，曰：“是真黎庶无福。但邑侯此举，究亦不免孟浪。”其人大言曰：“君误矣！以予论之，当断而疑，邑侯实属畏葸。若破棺出尸，独探隐处，则秦镜立照矣？”封君益固诘之。其人耳语数四，封君唯唯，径返内署。时宰以父冒星霜，又未决公务，寝食俱废。封君一见即笑曰：“痴儿欲作大好官，何太瘦生也！”因备告之。宰得父指，次日即出堂。延至旁午，始简精细仵人，随往检验。且嘱曰：“予云视，汝即视之，慎勿有误。”仵人领诺。既至，诸绅咸在，且不以笑面相迎。

    Explanation

    803

    The family members coveted the large fortune, and they would collectively cause trouble for the county magistrate. If this matter indeed reached the higher authorities, could the magistrate avoid being dismissed? Upon hearing this, the official could not help but feel secretly satisfied and intentionally sighed, saying, "Indeed, the common people are unfortunate. But this action by the county magistrate, after all, cannot help but be rash." The man spoke loudly, saying, "You are mistaken! According to my opinion, the magistrate should be decisive but is doubtful, and he indeed belongs to the timid. If the coffin is opened and the body is taken out, and the hidden places are investigated alone, then the mythical mirror will immediately reveal the truth!" The official further firmly questioned him. The man whispered several times, and the official nodded, directly returning to his inner office. The magistrate, having received his father's guidance, neglected both sleep and eating due to the unresolved official duties. Upon seeing his father, the official immediately laughed and said, "A foolish child wants to become a great good official, why so thin and emaciated!" He then fully explained it to him. The magistrate received his father's guidance and the next day immediately came out of the hall. The inspection extended until noon, and he began to select a careful coroner to follow and inspect. He also instructed, saying, "I say to look, you should look, carefully do not make a mistake." The coroner agreed. After arriving, all the gentry were present, and they did not greet with a smiling face.

    804

    宰微哂曰：“予为公家泄愤，而反仇予，岂金资不即瓜分耶？”语中隐微，众皆色变。宰坐后，始命启榇。尸已臭腐，不可近，其族中有泣下者，则其怨宰可知。宰亦弗恤，惟听仵人如法细检。迨至下部，宰遽指之曰：“视之！”仵人会意，应手而出，则银针五寸，血迹犹存，隐伏于厥具之内。众乃大哗，靡不匍伏称谢，至亲又号呼诉冤。宰笑曰：“诸公何前倨而后卑？幸无悬悬，凶人予已得之矣。”因问：“某亦来否？”同声以对，果在众中，则其内兄也。视其色如死灰，众始悟。宰命役拘执，即起出寺，且命殓尸候详。回署亟标一肉臂，往逮此童与它者之妻。薄暮咸集，宰乃当庭研讯。先以严刑拟童，童惧，罄吐其实。

    Explanation

    804

    The magistrate smiled slightly and said, "I acted to vent the public's anger, but instead they turned against me. Are they not dividing the wealth among themselves?" His words were subtle, and all present changed their expressions. The magistrate sat down, then ordered the coffin to be opened. The body was already rotten and could not be approached; some of the family members were weeping, indicating their resentment towards the magistrate. The magistrate showed no pity, only allowing the coroner to carefully examine it according to the law. When they reached the lower part, the magistrate suddenly pointed and said, "Look at it!" The coroner understood and immediately found a five-inch silver needle with blood still on it, hidden inside the genitalia. The crowd then made a great commotion, all prostrating themselves in thanks, while the closest relatives cried out in appeal. The magistrate smiled and said, "Why are you so arrogant before and so humble now? Fortunately, there is no longer any suspense, the villain has already been caught." He then asked, "Is he here too?" They answered in unison, and indeed, he was among the crowd, identified as the brother-in-law. His face was ashen, and the crowd finally realized. The magistrate ordered the arrest, and immediately left the temple, also ordering the body to be prepared for burial and details to be reported. Returning to the office, he quickly marked a piece of meat and went to arrest this child and the wife of another. By evening, they all gathered, and the magistrate then conducted a thorough interrogation in court. He first threatened the child with severe punishment, and fearing, the child confessed everything.

    805

    盖章故某之心腹，荐于亡者以遂其私，妇因与之同谋也。某日，亡者饮于某家，大醉而返。童扶掖入室，妇即命童缚以革带，然后自捋其裩，遽以针刺其具，深入无遗。亡者醉不能支，大吼而卒。童与妇始缓其缚，扶置榻上，以暴疾讣于人，人固未及料焉。童既凿凿供招，某与妇遂皆伏罪。宰大笑，命褫妇之麻衣，红裳宛在。诸绅时萃讼庭，罔不发指。宰又诘妇，则自其夫化后，深虑不祥，时时密著此裳于内，亦不自解何心，岂非天哉？宰更大怒，立命笞之，而后同械于狱。具谳上详，大吏皆喜，将飞章荐扬。宰叹曰：“辛苦一官，使老父心力俱瘁，殊不成人子。”即日以养告归，奉父旋里。今其人犹在故乡，年仅廿五六，而据事论断，老吏弗如。他年重履琴堂，又乌可限量耶？

    Explanation

    805

    A Man's Trusted Confidant, the Deceased, the Woman, and the Judge

    The trusted confidant of someone named 某 recommended the deceased to satisfy his private desires, and the woman joined the plot as a result. On a certain day, the deceased drank heavily at someone's house and returned in a drunken state. The child supported him into the room, and the woman then ordered the child to bind him with a leather belt. She then pulled down her undergarment and suddenly stabbed him with a needle, driving it deep into his body without leaving anything behind. The deceased, drunk and unable to bear the pain, shouted loudly and then died. The child and the woman then loosened the bindings, placed him on the bed, and reported his sudden illness to others, who had not anticipated such an outcome. The child provided a clear and detailed confession, and both 某 and the woman confessed their crimes. The judge laughed heartily and ordered the woman's mourning clothes to be removed, revealing that her red dress was still visible. The officials gathered in the courtroom, all of them horrified by the situation. The judge then questioned the woman, who explained that after her husband's death, she was deeply concerned about bad omens and often secretly wore the red dress, not understanding why she did so. The judge, greatly angered, immediately ordered her to be whipped and then chained her together with 某 in prison. The case report was prepared and submitted, and the high-ranking officials were pleased, planning to send a rapid report praising the judge. The judge sighed, saying, "How hardworking an official I have been, making my old father exhausted both mentally and physically. I am no good son." On the same day, he returned home to care for his father. Today, the person is still in his hometown, only twenty-five or twenty-six years old, yet he judges matters with such skill that even older officials are not as good. In the future, when he returns to his office, his achievements are limitless.

    806

    外史氏曰：此妇必深谙铜人之法，故以针奉其夫也。第妇之所悦者，孰愈于此？今乃去之而不惜，岂以其疲惫不职，因悬针以惩之欤？无如人琴俱亡，妇亦随殒，曾不得与所欢者仍效金针之暗度。则谓贤宰官眼底红云，直是阎摩王堂前孽镜，当无不可。
    　　随园老人曰：老成持重，年少聪察，乔梓均可以传。

    Explanation

    806

    The historian says: This woman must have deeply understood the method of the bronze figures, hence she offered the needle to her husband. As for what the woman delighted in, how could anything surpass this? Now she abandoned it without hesitation, could it be because she was tired and not fulfilling her duties, so she hung the needle as a form of punishment? Unfortunately, both the person and the instrument (the needle) were lost, and the woman also died, never to be able to continue the subtle transmission of the needle technique with her beloved. Thus, to say that the virtuous official's gaze is like a red cloud, or that it is like the mirror of Yama's judgment hall, would not be inappropriate. The elder scholar says: The elder is steady and responsible, the younger is quick-witted and discerning, both can be passed down as examples.

    807

    ○　隔江楼
    　　江南某县有医，刘姓，失其名。居近江岸，每赴病家诊视，独棹一叶之舟，横流北渡，恒系缆于隔江楼下，习以为常。楼故某姓宅后，其家有女曰大姑，此即其妆楼也。嗣因大姑缘忿自缢死，而其楼遂闭而不登。刘亦有戒心，不再泊于其地，将数月矣。一夕，留饮于病家，酩酊而归。归艇经由楼侧，望见江面月光，晶莹一色，不禁喜而长啸。俄闻楼上低呼曰：“刘先生暮夜始归哉！”刘醉中顿忘之，仰睇楼上，则大姑凭栏独立，风鬟雾鬓，绰约如生。刘故数视其疾，笑貌声音，无一不稔。且见楼有灯光，一时恍惚，不复忆其既死。停舟问讯，女以啜茗招之。刘正需此，遂舍水就陆，缘梯而登。女逊之入，闺室中香奁器具陈设如昔。少坐，即亲献茗盏，饮之芳馥异常，刘甚悦。女因启曰：“儿家夙费清心，时叨妙剂，既已没齿不忘。今因痞块不消，又成鬼病，一死不堪再死，敢烦良医为我疗之。”刘欣然许诺，而试把其腕，冷如冰。

    Explanation

    807

    ○　隔江楼
    　　In a certain county in Jiangnan, there was a physician surnamed Liu, whose given name is unknown. He lived near the riverbank and, whenever he visited patients, he would row a small boat alone, crossing the river northward. He would always tie his boat to the tower across the river, which had become a routine for him. The tower was located behind the residence of a family, and the family had a daughter named Dagu, who used to live in this tower. After Dagu died by suicide due to anger or resentment, the tower was closed off and no longer entered. Liu also became cautious and stopped anchoring his boat there for several months. One evening, after being invited to a patient's home for a drink, he returned home drunk. As his boat passed by the side of the tower, he saw the moonlight reflecting on the river, clear and bright, and could not help but let out a joyful cry. Suddenly, he heard a soft call from upstairs: “Mr. Liu, you finally return at night!” In his drunken state, Liu forgot about the tower and looked up, seeing Dagu standing alone by the railing, her hair disheveled and misty, appearing graceful and lifelike. Liu had visited her many times when she was ill, and he was familiar with her smile, appearance, and voice. Seeing the light in the room, he momentarily forgot that she had already died. He stopped his boat and asked for news, and the woman invited him to drink tea. Liu needed this, so he left the boat and climbed onto land, ascending the stairs to the tower. The woman led him inside, and the incense box and other household items were arranged as they were before. After sitting for a short while, she personally offered him a cup of tea, which was unusually fragrant, and Liu was very pleased. The woman then said, “Your family has always been careful with their health, and I have often benefited from your wonderful remedies, which I will never forget. Now, because of a stubborn swelling that hasn't gone away, I have also developed a spirit-related illness. I cannot bear to die again after already dying once. Would you please treat me, esteemed doctor?” Liu happily agreed, but when he tried to feel her pulse, it was cold as ice.

    808

    忽焉记其亡化，身在醉乡，竟不知惧，惟诘曰：“人死矣！何犹虑病乎？”女曰：“然鬼之病，正无异于人之病。第皆生前所积，非由死后所染。如妾以负气投缳，其气究盘踞于胸，即其症也。故虽以人医鬼，实属以人治人。”语次切视已周，刘为立方。因询：“冥中有药乎？”答曰：“地藏王广施慈惠，立局于枉死城，今已千年矣。”于是坐而闲叙，刘谈甚豪，遍及九幽风景。女一一敷陈，多异于人世所传。

    Explanation

    808

    Suddenly, he became aware of her death and transformation, as if in a drunken state, yet he did not feel fear. He only asked, "The person has died! Why are you still concerned about illness?" The woman replied, "Indeed, the illness of ghosts is no different from that of humans. But it is all accumulated from life, not contracted after death. For example, if I hanged myself due to anger, the qi (vital energy) remains stuck in my chest, which is the disease.
