Therefore, even though treating ghosts with human medicine, it is actually treating people with people." During the conversation, he carefully observed her, and Liu prescribed a formula. Then he asked, "Is there medicine in the underworld?" She replied, "King Dizang widely bestows compassion and kindness, establishing an office in the City of Wrongful Deaths, which has been there for a thousand years now." So they sat and chatted leisurely, with Liu talking very enthusiastically about the scenery of the nine depths. The woman explained one by one, and many things were different from what is commonly known in the human world.

    809

    刘忽戏曰：“闻缢死鬼颇可畏怖，今日见子，何殊不然？”女正容答曰：“恩义当前，劣形何敢顿现？”刘弗信，迫欲观之，女终不肯。刘适吸烟在口，遽喷之，且数喷之。女不能耐，呼曰：“迫妾惊君，非妾之罪也。”语未终，鬼声哀鸣。刘视之，发披愁云，舌垂惨血，帛上悬而腕下缒，目瞪睛突，丑状百端，不觉惊倒于地，而宿醒解矣。足软不能步，强起而奔，暗中似有人掖之，甫克下楼，竟不及登舟，卧于芦花深处。昧爽始苏，浼人操楫，始得渡江而归。自此隔江楼下，遂永无问津者。

    Explanation

    809

    Liu suddenly teased, saying, "Hearing that hanged ghosts are quite terrifying, seeing you today, how is it any different?" The woman replied formally, "With kindness and righteousness before us, how dare the ugly form suddenly appear?" Liu, however, forced to see it, and the woman finally refused. Liu suddenly spat at it, and spat several times. The woman, unable to bear it, called out, "Forcing me to scare you, it is not my fault." Before the words were finished, a ghostly wail of sorrow. Liu looked, and saw hair hanging like sorrowful clouds, tongue hanging with bloody tears, hanging from a cloth, wrists dangling below, eyes wide and staring, a hundred ugly forms, and was unconsciously thrown to the ground, and the previous day's drunkenness was dispelled. His feet were weak and unable to walk, so he forced himself to get up and run. In the darkness, it seemed someone supported him, and just managed to descend the stairs, but did not manage to board the boat. He lay down in the deep reeds. At dawn, he began to recover, and asked someone to row, and finally managed to cross the river and return home. From then on, the building across the river was never visited again.

    810

    外史氏曰：鬼者，积气也。宜乎生而气郁者，死犹不免于病。非若疮痍残疾长逝者，既损其形，夜台必无呻吟之状。然则气之为患若此，女虽数言，不可唤醒一切哉！

    ○　谈易狐
    　　天下学宫率多宽广，所以妥先师也。陕省某郡，庙倍宏敞，其后有楼屋数楹，栋接甍连，非常壮丽。有狐居之，时时游行殿庑。朔望，洒扫之人恒见其履迹，咸以为异。其地初无书院，太守某公始命诸生于习礼之暇，就此讲肄。捐俸构屋，使居于棂星门外，以便修养，亦崇儒之雅意也。一日，诸生会讲经义，列坐于奎楼下，互相辩难。惟至易理深微，罕通奥旨，相与攒眉者久之。俄闻有人鼓掌笑，众惊顾，则一杖者，褐衣草服，年近耄耋，立于诸生坐后，含笑谓曰：“诸君才质皆不凡，惜生于边地，颇乏师传。欲明周易，何弗下询衰朽？”众大喜，逊之中坐，亦不辞。即举诸生所疑者，一一剖析之，莫不披窍导眒，且证以名家诸说，滔滔如悬河，众皆心折。

    Explanation

    810

    The Historian says: Ghosts are formed from accumulated vital energy. It is fitting that those who, while alive, had a depressed vital energy, would still suffer from illness even after death. Unlike those who die from physical ailments, their bodies are already damaged, so in the afterlife, they would not suffer from groaning. Yet, given the severity of the problem with vital energy, can a few words not awaken everyone?

    ○ The Fox Who Talks About the I Ching

    Schools and academies throughout the land are generally spacious, serving to honor the sage teachers. In a certain county of Shaanxi Province, the temple is particularly grand, with several buildings in the rear, their rafters connected and roofs joined, forming an extremely magnificent structure. A fox resides there, wandering through the halls and corridors. During the first and last days of the lunar month, the cleaners would always see his footprints and all considered it strange. There was originally no academy in this place, but a local governor named a certain official ordered the students, during their leisure time after practicing rituals, to gather here to study. He donated funds to build a house, so that the students could reside outside the Lingxing Gate, to facilitate rest and study, which also reflected an elegant intention to honor Confucianism. One day, the students gathered to discuss the meaning of the classics, sitting in rows under the Kui Pavilion, and debated and questioned each other. However, when it came to the profound and subtle principles of the I Ching, few understood their profound meanings, and they frowned and puzzled for a long time. Suddenly, someone was heard clapping and laughing, and the students were startled and looked around, then saw an old man carrying a walking stick, wearing coarse clothing, nearly in his eighties, standing behind the students, smilingly saying, "All of you have extraordinary talent and character, but unfortunately, you were born in a remote area, lacking in proper instruction. To understand the I Ching, why not ask an old man like me?" The students were greatly pleased, and invited him to sit in the middle, and he did not refuse. He immediately addressed the students' doubts, analyzing and explaining them one by one, and no one failed to be enlightened and guided. He also cited the theories of famous scholars, speaking fluently like a flowing river, and all were deeply impressed.

    811

    间有不平者，又故以古今疑义与之相质，翁复响答如流。始各肃然起敬，愿请受为弟子。翁微哂曰：“子归而求之，有余师。”径起别去，众亦散归己室。自是数日辄一来，众又益之癰谦。翁甚悦，悉心指授，渐至晨夕与共矣。年余，六爻十翼，诸生无弗通晓。惟叩以他经，则辞不敏。偶遇诸生邀饮，必欣然赴招，沉醺始返。忽一夕，月光甚佳，赏心尽醉。欲去，众力挽之，良久，始得脱。绥绥径入殿后，月下微露其尾。众大骇，始悟其非人，狐尚不自觉。明日复晤，有尖嘴生请曰：“敢问小狐，汽济何谓也？”翁乃大惭，拂衣振杖而起，曰：“鸱枭不可育，育将啄其母睛，其诸君子之谓矣！”倏不见，后遂不复来。然而府庠诸生，每遇学使者下车，易经一艺，微奥宏深，犹能冠河陇诸郡。

    Explanation

    811

    There are those who feel aggrieved, and the old man intentionally used ancient and modern doubts to challenge them. He responded fluently and promptly. They all suddenly became respectful and wished to be accepted as his disciples. The old man smiled slightly and said, "Go back and seek further; you have more teachers." He rose and left without delay, and the others also dispersed back to their own rooms. From then on, they came every few days, and they grew even more humble and respectful. The old man was very pleased and instructed them carefully, gradually spending the mornings and evenings together. After a year, the students had no difficulty understanding the six lines and ten wings of the I Ching. However, when asked about other classics, he would say he was not skilled. Occasionally, he would be invited by the students to drink, and he would gladly accept and return drunk.

    One night, the moonlight was exceptionally beautiful. He quietly and directly entered behind the hall, and the moonlight revealed his tail. The others were greatly startled and realized he was not human; the fox was unaware. The next day, they met again, and a student with a pointed beak asked, "Dare I ask the little fox, what does '汽济' mean?" The old man was greatly ashamed, brushed his clothes, and stood up, saying, "Owls and crows cannot be raised; if raised, they will peck out their mother's eyes; this is what you gentlemen are like!" He suddenly disappeared and never came back.

    However, the students at the prefectural school, whenever the provincial examiner arrived, in the art of the Book of Changes, subtle and profound, they could still excel among the counties of the Hexi Corridor.

    812

    外史氏曰：易蕴至深，宣圣尝韦编三绝。区区一狐，何能竟通大意？要亦性灵不昧，能窥天地阃奥，故能识性命精微。盖犹修行家独能悟黄庭南华诸至理也。惜乎青衿佻达，甫见其尾而即相向鼓唇，以致易道有不东之叹。

    Explanation

    812

    The Commentator said: The Yi Jing (Book of Changes) contains profound meanings, and Confucius once wore out three sets of leather straps binding the bamboo strips while studying it. A mere fox, how could it fully grasp the deeper meanings? Yet, it possesses a clear spiritual insight that allows it to understand the profound secrets of heaven and earth, and thus recognize the subtle aspects of life and destiny. This is similar to how practitioners of spiritual cultivation can alone comprehend the profound truths found in the Huangting Jing and Nanhua Jing. Alas, scholars are often frivolous and undisciplined, seeing only a glimpse and immediately beginning to criticize, leading to the lament that the teachings of the Yi Jing have not spread widely.

    813

    ○　田再春
    　　闽商田再春者，滨死而复生，因号以自警，非其名也。丙子仲秋，余遭之于逆旅，为竟夕之谈，自述其易名之由，不少讳。再春本名某，贩于江湖，孑然一身，无妻孥，亦无昆仲，洒如也。因以传舍为居庐，以舟车为宅第，往来水陆之区，致资巨万，曾不一归。而生性佻达，貌复白晰，所至恒多穴隙行。且善房中术，从不以涓滴与所欢。人有劝之娶者，辄笑曰：“予负人债多矣！若计锱铢而偿，为予妻者，恐不任受也。”遂决意不复纳室。第其为人，轻财任侠，忧人之忧，急人之急，以故多得人欢心。癸酉孟春，商于吴郡，适罹疾疫之灾，卧于市廛，病甚危笃。梦一皂衣人，须毛如蝟，状若捕差，以巨链絷之去。飘然至一衙署，巍赫异常。初未疑为阴司，已而重门洞开，遥见公座，有紫衣官吏纷纷抱案牍从东西侧门入。未几自内传呼，不知何语。

    Explanation

    813

    ○　田再春
    　　The merchant from Fujian, surnamed Tian Zai Chun, nearly died but was revived, and thus he named himself as a self-warning, which was not his original name. In the second month of autumn in the year of the Fire Rat, I encountered him at an inn, and we had a conversation that lasted through the night. He freely recounted the reasons for his name change without any concealment. Tian Zai Chun's original name was something else; he traded on rivers and lakes, completely alone, without a wife or children, nor any brothers or sisters, living a carefree life. He used temporary lodgings as his home and boats and carriages as his residences, traveling through water and land regions, accumulating vast wealth without ever returning home. His nature was carefree and unrestrained, and he was fair-skinned. Wherever he went, he often engaged in extramarital affairs. Moreover, he was skilled in sexual techniques and never shared even a drop of affection with his lovers. When people advised him to marry, he would laugh and say, “I owe many debts to others! If I were to pay back every small amount, my wife might not be able to bear it.” Thus, he decided never to marry again. However, he was generous with his wealth and a friend to the underprivileged, sharing others' burdens and acting promptly in their needs, which earned him much goodwill. In the first month of spring in the year of the Water Rooster, he was trading in Wu County when he suddenly fell ill with a disease outbreak, lying in the bustling streets, gravely ill. He dreamed of a black-clothed man with hair like a hedgehog, resembling an arrest officer, who took him away with a large chain. He floated to a grand government office, which was majestic and unusual. At first, he did not suspect it was the underworld, but then the great doors opened wide, and he saw the public seat from afar, with purple-clad officials carrying official papers entering from both side doors. Soon after, an announcement came from inside, but he could not understand the words.

    814

    皂衣人遽牵再春如奔马，凡历门限三重，始达厅事。左右褫其链，令跪于庭阶。窃睨堂上，贵官十数，皆侧坐，好丑不一。中一道者，冠雷巾，衣电衣，状貌魁梧，势甚尊贵，每一言，则堂之上下皆耸然听之。回视己后，跪者已数十人，颇有熟识，亦未敢遽通片言。跪未久，西向一贵人，虬髯虎面，命检再春禄藉。即有吏以案进，贵人览之，愕然曰：“是未合绝。”因起白道者，道者颔之，命稽其生平。又有吏以卷呈阅，则盈堂传视，举艴然变色而作矣。堂上因大声呼田某，再春匍匐而前。道者怒目叱之曰：“汝纵一人之淫欲，致玷百十人之家声。彼妇之父母翁姑，靡不痛心疾首。汝又狡猾，无物可偿，将若何？”再春惴惴不能答，东向一贵人起曰：“是宜绝其禄，罚使为娼，庶合平允之意。”道者曰：“不然。凡世人淫债，必报于生前，方可惩一而儆百。若待转轮之后，则死者何知？生者何罪？虽娼不如无娼也。

    814

    A person dressed in black clothing swiftly pulled Zai Chun like a galloping horse, passing through three sets of thresholds before finally reaching the hall. The attendants removed the chains from him and ordered him to kneel on the steps of the hall. He stealthily glanced at the upper part of the hall, where several high-ranking officials were seated sideways, each with a different appearance. Among them was a central figure, wearing a hat like thunder and clothes like lightning, with a tall and robust appearance, exuding a very high status. Every word he spoke made everyone in the hall listen attentively. Looking back, he saw that there were already dozens of people kneeling behind him, some of whom he was familiar with, but they dared not speak a word. He had not been kneeling for long when a high-ranking official from the west, with a fierce appearance and a curly beard, ordered the inspection of Zai Chun's records. An official immediately brought the case forward, and the high-ranking official, upon reading it, expressed surprise and said, "This is not yet appropriate for a final decision." He then stood up and reported to the central figure, who nodded in agreement and ordered the investigation of Zai Chun's life history. Another official presented a scroll for review, and it was passed around the hall for everyone to see, causing many to change color and become angry. The central figure then loudly called out the name of a person named Tian, and Zai Chun crawled forward. The central figure glared at him and scolded, "You indulged in sexual desire, tarnishing the reputation of many families. The parents and in-laws of that woman were all deeply grieved. You are also cunning, with nothing to compensate for your wrongdoing, so what should be done?" Zai Chun, filled with anxiety, could not answer. A high-ranking official from the east then stood up and said, "This person should be deprived of their salary and punished by being made a prostitute, which would be fair." The central figure replied, "That is not the case. All debts of lust in the world must be repaid in this life, in order to punish one and warn many. If we wait until after the cycle of rebirth, then what do the dead know? What sin have the living committed? Even being a prostitute is better than not being a prostitute."

    815

    吾欲变通其间，诸君必有以处此。”众官皆唯唯，敬谢不敏。道者笑曰：“此固无难也。适接直北某城隍申详，某村有女，貌媸而志洁。因伊兽叔，售为土娼，气结不食而死，昨己藁葬于郊。吾今行炼形之法，即以田某代女之身，以旬为期，少偿前逋，然后仍令更生，不已足以垂戒乎？”众皆冁然曰：“真君之策，诚尽善。但恐污女名，可若何？”道者又笑曰：“予自有两全之术，且必使作恶者显然涉讼，不准立判其贞淫，宁至重为女累哉？”言讫，以黄纸书数字如符篆，命役火之，和水以噀再春。再春竦然，顿觉肤革充实。前者虚浮如影，今则固结有形矣。然在廊庑之下，窃闻众议，心甚张皇。未几，堂上又厉声呼令阉之，即有数役缚之于凳，反接而去其势。再春大痛无声，役又以水噀之，乃复眔。窃视私处，胪肉坟起，则俨然一雌矣。道者即命驱之去，限满复来。

    Explanation

    815

    I wish to make adjustments in this matter; all of you must have a way to handle this." The officials all nodded in agreement, respectfully declining due to lack of ability. The Taoist smiled and said, "This is not difficult at all. Just now, I received a report from a certain city god in the northern direction. There is a woman in a certain village, ugly in appearance but pure in heart. Because of her uncle's actions, she was sold into prostitution, died from grief, not eating, and was buried in the outskirts yesterday. I will now perform the method of refining form, using Mr. Tian's body as a substitute for the girl's, for a period of ten days, to partially repay the debt, and then I will allow her to be reborn, is it not sufficient to serve as a warning?" Everyone smiled and said, "The strategy of the True Lord is truly perfect. But I fear it will tarnish the girl's name, what can we do about it?" The Taoist smiled again and said, "I have my own way to achieve both ends, and I will ensure that the wrongdoers are clearly implicated in legal proceedings, without immediately judging her virtue or vice, how could it be worse for the girl?" After speaking, he wrote numbers on yellow paper like talismans, ordered the servants to burn it, and then sprinkled water on Mr. Tian. Mr. Tian suddenly felt a sense of solidity in his skin. Before it was as insubstantial as a shadow, now it is solid and tangible. However, while standing under the eaves, he secretly heard the discussion and was very anxious. Soon, the master on the dais loudly ordered him to be castrated, and several servants bound him to a bench and removed his sexual organs. Mr. Tian was in great pain but made no sound, the servants then sprinkled water on him, and he regained consciousness. He secretly looked at his private parts, and the flesh was swollen, making him appear female. The Taoist immediately ordered him to leave, to return after the ten days.

    816

    再春遂仍从皂衣人出，因询以真君果谁何。答曰：“许姓，旌阳真人，奉帝旨来主疫政耳。其左列者，皆瘟部之神；其右列则冥王也。”及出署外，仰视牌额，果榜曰“瘟眓之府。”益大惊，意不欲行。皂衣者又呼二人来，兽首人身，甚可怖畏，以巨椎迫之，乃前。欻急如风，瞬息至一处，茅屋低矮，围以短垣，聆之颇有喧声。似一老妪哗曰：“汝匿吾人，乃欲以死鬼诈我耶？”又有男子低声曰：“渠实不愿而死，抔土未干，予岂诳汝？”旋有数男子怒诟曰：“既无人，曷偿予金？”声乃大哄。皂衣人谛听，曰：“是矣！”乃以掌击再春背，心遂迷惘。突如径入，俄闻室中群噪曰：“钱树子故无恙耶！谎老龟当羞死矣！”皆有喜色，独一男子惊惶失措，连呼有鬼，形状如鬼，夺门而去。众不顾，竟拥再春以行。视之，男妇数人，举凶悍可惧。己欲言，则有口如喑，不得已而从之。约行数里，至一家，土室而茨墙，居甚湫隘。

    Explanation

    816

    Zaixun followed the man in black clothes and, upon exiting, asked who the True Lord truly was. The man replied, "He is surnamed Xu, the Immortal of Jingyang, who has come under imperial decree to oversee epidemic affairs. Those on his left are all gods of the plague department; those on his right are the King of Hell." Upon leaving the office, Zaixun looked up at the plaque and saw that it indeed read "Mansion of Plague and Pestilence." He was even more surprised and did not wish to proceed. The man in black clothes then called two more people, who had animal heads and human bodies, and they were very terrifying. They forced Zaixun forward with a large mallet. Suddenly, they moved as swiftly as the wind and instantly arrived at a place. There was a low, humble thatched house, surrounded by short walls, and there was quite a commotion inside. It seemed an old woman shouted, "You are hiding my people and want to deceive me with dead ghosts?" Another man whispered, "He was unwilling to die, and the soil is not yet dry; how could I deceive you?" Soon, several men angrily shouted, "Since there is no one, how can you repay me the money?" The noise grew louder. The man in black clothes listened carefully and said, "That's it!" He then struck Zaixun on the back with his hand, and Zaixun's heart became confused. Suddenly, they entered directly, and soon Zaixun heard a commotion in the room. Someone shouted, "Is the money tree still fine? The lying old turtle should be ashamed to death!" Everyone else had a look of joy, but one man was terrified and panicked, shouting that there were ghosts, looking like ghosts, and fled through the door. The others paid no heed and continued to push Zaixun forward. Looking around, there were several men and women, all fierce and terrifying. When Zaixun wanted to speak, his mouth was as if mute, and he was forced to follow. They walked about several miles and arrived at a house, a mud room with thatched walls, and the dwelling was very low and narrow.

    817

    甫入门，再春已能出声，谓众曰：“我闽之商人田某也，汝辈何人，胡为致我于此？”众俱惊愕。方将展询，惟妪嫚骂曰：“此皆若叔所预授，将以怪异赖吾金耳！世岂有女身而负贩者？”再春闻言自视，则上衣下裳，皆类巾帼。已而徘徊顾影，亦既云鬓蓬飞，俯首瞻莲，又复鸡距宛若，遂不复能置辩。妪因询其从违，再春终以为耻，默然弗答。妪诘之再四，乃怒曰：“是尚未知吾威也。”遽呼健男子折柳为箠，浸以盎水，将裸而挞之。再春始惧，且自计曰：“业遭冥谴，数无可逃，何为更试此毒手？”遂靦然而应之曰：“诺。”妪始欣然引之入闼，顾谓再春曰：“为汝贱妮子，群婢皆虚此一日矣。”再春方欲举趾，觉室内蒸腾如雾，且若鲍鱼之腥，惧不敢前。妪诃之，方始逾槛入。

    Explanation

    817

    As soon as he entered the door, Zaicun was already able to speak, addressing the crowd, "I am Tian, a merchant from Fujian. Who are you people, and why have you brought me here?" Everyone was startled. Just as they were about to ask further, the old woman began to scold harshly, "All of this was prearranged by your uncle, who will use some strange excuse to blame me for my money! Is there in the world a woman who carries goods?" Upon hearing this, Zaicun looked at himself and realized that his upper garment and lower skirt were like those of a woman. Soon after, he paused, looking at his reflection, and noticed that his hair was disheveled, his head bowed, and his feet resembling those of a woman. Thus, he could no longer argue. The old woman then asked if he would comply or resist, but Zaicun, feeling ashamed, remained silent and did not answer. After questioning him repeatedly, the old woman became angry and said, "This one has not yet known my authority." She immediately called a strong man to break a willow branch into a whip, soaked it in water from a bowl, and prepared to beat him while he was naked. Zaicun began to fear and thought to himself, "I have already suffered divine punishment, and my fate cannot be escaped. Why should I endure such a cruel hand?" He then bashfully responded, "Yes." The old woman, now joyful, led him into the inner room, turning to Zaicun and saying, "For you worthless girl, all the maids have wasted this day." As Zaicun was about to take a step, he felt the room steaming like mist and smelled the stench of stinky fish, and he feared to go forward. The old woman scolded him, and only then did he finally cross the threshold and enter.

    818

    见妇人四五辈，貌俱不扬，犹调粉涂脂，状如泥塑之鬼，周身惟著一短襦，自腰以下皓皓乎绝无寸丝，心益惊怖。妪又向外呼曰：“重劳推挽，无可以酬，今夕盍早来，当令小花奴先伴诸郎宿也。”健男等皆嬉笑而去。至夜果来，妪以威胁再春，衣带含泪而解，击刺之。初觉操刀而割者，又复及锋而试，其痛深创巨，不可胜言。已而渍丹浃席，血流漂杵。妪与众妇俱鼓掌曰：“黄花放矣。”继之以二，渐不能堪，妪恐有失，乃以他妇代之，甫得小憩。

    Explanation

    818

    Upon seeing several women, all of whom were unattractive, they were still applying powder and lipstick, looking like clay figures. Their entire bodies were only covered by a short jacket, and below the waist, they were completely bare, which made me even more terrified. The old woman called out again, saying, "You have greatly burdened us with your pushing and pulling, and there is nothing we can offer in return. Why not come early tonight? We will let the little flower slave accompany the young men to sleep first." The strong men all laughed and left. That night, they indeed came, and the old woman threatened Rechun. With tears in her eyes, he untied his belt and was subjected to violent actions. At first, it felt like being cut with a knife, and then the pain was tested on the edge, which was deep and severe, beyond words. Soon after, the blood stained the mat, and it flowed so much that it floated the pestle. The old woman and the other women clapped their hands and said, "The flowers have bloomed." Two more followed, and he gradually became unable to bear it. Fearing that something might go wrong, the old woman replaced him with another woman, and he finally managed to take a short rest.

    819

    漏下已三，晨起欲著衣缕。妪夺之，不许曰：“汝见他人被服耶？”并不予以短襦，四肢裸然，其羞愈不可耐。仍令盥栉，施以朱粉，日甫南而户外之履满矣。源源而来，率皆被褐顶笠之俦，绝无一文雅者。见再春，皆顾而笑曰：“其新孔嘉。”遂力争而群弃其旧，以故再春之所遇尤多，豷沓交欢，几无宁刻。初尚可以容受，迨至数人，小腹胀满，口喘目张。向不欲以涓滴与人，今则欲减其涓滴而不可，仰承甘露而饮之，瀼嚷者悔不挹此注彼矣。

    Explanation

    819

    The water clock had dripped three times, and in the morning, she wanted to put on her clothes. Her mother took the clothes away and refused to let her, saying, "Have you seen how others are dressed?" She did not give her a short jacket, and her limbs were exposed, her shame becoming increasingly unbearable. She still made her wash and comb her hair, applying rouge and powder to her. The sun had just reached the south, and the shoes outside were already full. People came in a continuous stream, mostly wearing coarse clothes and hats, and there was not a single refined person. Seeing Reicun, they all looked at her and said, "How beautiful she is now." They then competed to abandon their old ones, and therefore, Reicun met with many more people, crowded together in joy, almost no moment of peace. At first, she could still tolerate it, but until several people came, her small belly was full, and she was panting with wide eyes. Originally, she did not want to give even a drop to others, but now, she wanted to reduce even a drop, but could not. She had to drink the sweet dew from above, and the one who was full regretted not having distributed it.

    820

    赖妪以沸汤沃帨，代为按摩，花径微清，名园顿爽。无如吐陈纳新，踵门者又至，蹂躏之惨，不可名状。一日之内，狼狈者三。幸而夕阳西下，问渡者稀，再春方得安枕而卧。夜深辄与众妇言其情，咸笑而不信，明日复然。客至益伙，以其雏而喜之，恒有坐俟其旁，摩厉以须，此刃出而彼刃将斩者。再春以一人当之，其惫甚矣。况客皆壮夫，颇能持久，昔己以逗留为欢，今人亦以濡迟为乐，其报施可谓不爽。茹苦终朝，几死花下，屈指旬日，窃幸得生。乃于朝食之后，复来一猛丈夫，阳道伟岸，凿枘已不可当，又复兼擅所长，如以再春之矛刺再春之盾，坚锐莫比，自午至酉不少披靡。再春已目闭头眩，汗淫舌冷，奄然垂毙矣。瞀乱中，见前之皂衣人已至，径趋榻前，呼之曰：“冥限已满，盍竟行乎？”携之出，人亦寂无知者，但闻屋中作惊诧声而已。

    Explanation

    820

    The old woman used boiling water to pour over the handkerchief, and then massaged her in place of the usual treatment. The flower path became slightly cool, and the famous garden suddenly felt fresh. However, discarding the old and accepting the new, another visitor arrived, and the cruelty of trampling was indescribable. In one day, she was in a state of distress three times. Fortunately, as the sun set in the west, few people asked to cross, and Zaicun could finally sleep peacefully. At night, she often talked about her situation with the other women, and they all laughed but didn't believe her; the next day, it happened again. More guests arrived, and they were pleased with her youth, and there were always people sitting by her side, sharpening their knives to wait, one knife coming out, and another about to strike. Zaicun had to face one person at a time, and she was extremely exhausted. Moreover, the guests were all strong men, capable of enduring for a long time. In the past, they took delight in lingering, and now people also took pleasure in prolonging the encounter; their retribution was indeed not wrong. She endured suffering all day, nearly dying under the flowers, counting the days with her fingers, secretly hoping to survive. Then, after breakfast, another strong man came, his male organ was strong and imposing, the fit was so tight that it could not be resisted, and he also excelled in his specialty, like using Zaicun's spear to attack Zaicun's shield, unmatched in strength and sharpness, from noon until evening, he showed no signs of tiring. Zaicun had her eyes closed, her head dizzy, sweating profusely, her tongue cold, and she was suddenly on the brink of death. In a state of confusion, she saw the person dressed in black had arrived, rushing to the bed, calling out to her, "The limit of life has been reached, why not go on?" He took her out, and no one knew, but only heard sounds of surprise from the room.

    821

    又至向之衙署，则中只一贵人，面南据案坐，谓之曰：“风流之况味，汝已深尝之乎？本不欲贳汝死，但奉真君命，赐汝再生，当亟图自新，痛为改革。不然，将十世娼矣！”乃命以具还之，厥物复得出门，而自为按捺，未及释手，豁然顿苏。时已昏睡十日，不食亦不言。诸相好逻而守之，投之以药，冥然弗瘳。及寤而言其故，人皆骇汗，遂不药而痊。自是改行从善，不再涉邪。后以贩入燕，闻某县有土娼，其行事颇相类。就而访之，邑人曰：“向曾有此。因某家女为叔所卖，抱恨而卒。既葬而鸨至，告以故，不信，坐而索人。旋见亡者自外而入，强拥以归。为娼已数日，方与客接，音迹杳然。客与鸨互讦妖异，官鞫之，尽得其实。启棺视女，贞体宛然，知女之冤抑使然。遂重责其叔与鸨，而流之远方，今则绝无此人矣。”再春乃自述其梦，事迹吻合，闻者又莫不竦然。是夕为余言，悉其颠末。余以为深足警世，因笔其略于书。

    Explanation

    821

    He returned to the previous office, where he found only one noble person seated facing south on the desk. The noble person said to him, "Have you deeply experienced the taste of being fashionable and sophisticated? Originally, I did not intend to spare your life, but in obedience to the command of the True Lord, I have granted you a second chance at life. You should urgently seek to reform yourself and thoroughly change. Otherwise, you will be a prostitute for ten generations!" The noble person then ordered the object to be returned to him, and the object was able to go out again. He held it tightly, and before he could let go, he suddenly woke up. By that time, he had been unconscious for ten days, neither eating nor speaking. The officials guarded around him, administering medicine to him, but he remained unconscious and did not recover. When he woke up and told the reason, everyone was shocked and sweating. Thus, he recovered without medicine. From then on, he changed his ways and followed the good path, no longer involving himself in immoral activities. Later, he traveled to Yan and heard that a certain county had a local prostitute whose actions were quite similar. He went to inquire about it, and the locals said, "There used to be such a person. A girl from a certain family was sold by her uncle, died with a grudge. After her burial, the madam arrived, told the reason, but they did not believe, sat and demanded someone. Soon, they saw the deceased enter from outside, forcibly took her back. She had been a prostitute for several days, just about to meet with a guest, but her voice and trace vanished. The guest and the madam accused each other of strange occurrences. The official investigated and found out the truth. They opened the coffin and saw the girl's body remained pure, knowing that the girl's suffering caused this. They severely punished the uncle and the madam, exiling them to a distant place. Now, there is no such person anymore." Zai Chun then recounted his dream, and the details matched. Everyone who heard it was startled. That night, he told me all the details. I thought it deeply sufficient to warn the world, so I wrote a brief account in a book.

    822

    外史氏曰：花营柳队，容有漏网之夫；孽火罡风，亦非报淫之典。偿之妻妾，妻妾无辜；祸及子孙，子孙何罪？惟雄飞而雌伏，莫待来生；庶暮报于朝施，不昧天理。许旌阳准情定法，田再春革面洗心。不然，即令十世为娼，绥绥者未必能当前立悟也。读之汗下，可称贯顶金针矣。

    Explanation

    822

    The commentator said: "Even in places of vice and pleasure, there are those who escape punishment. Destructive forces are not necessarily a direct form of retribution for immoral behavior. The wives and concubines are innocent victims of the consequences, and future generations are not guilty of the sins of their ancestors. Only by taking action in this life and not waiting for a better life in the afterlife can one avoid the immediate consequences and adhere to the principles of heaven. Xu Jiyang set an example by his moral and spiritual teachings, and Tian Zaichun transformed his life. Otherwise, even if one is destined for a life of degradation, they might not realize their fate. Reading this, one might break out in a sweat, for it is a profound and critical insight."

    823

    ○　卷二
    　　宜织
    　　柳生名家宝，山阴人。其生也，祖父母年高，皆爱惜如珍，因以命名。比长，风神蕴藉，俊逸绝伦，且童年即游泮水，邑中巨家有女者，咸属意焉。宝父母苛于择妇，每曰：“吾儿人中鸾凤，岂可耦世上鸡鹜？”以故媒氏踵门，恒未许可。蹉跎将弱冠，犹虚琴瑟，心亦为怅然。一日，以父命往郭外省其姑。少叙家事，即与姑之子闲瞩门前。无何，婢来呼其弟，宝与偕入。则姑以事赴近村，命子随往，且留宝少待，归来犹有所言。宝不得行，而心颇怏怏。盖姑子年甫舞象，已定盟于某家，此行盖为姻事也。既见姑率其子欣喜自去，一时颇觉无聊，仍立闾间。遥望西南林壑，似有佳境，顿思前往观之，遂踽踽前行。阍者止焉，则曰：“予不耐此岑寂，少行当自归，勿惧也。”径去，莫能挽留。乃行来及半，至一溪，足力已疲，因憩于水次，俯视清流，意颇恬适。

    Explanation

    823

    ○　Volume Two
    　　Suitable Weaving
    　　Liusheng Mingjia Bao, a native of Shanyin, was born into a family where his grandparents, both of advanced age, cherished him as a treasure and thus named him. As he grew older, he possessed a refined and dignified demeanor, was exceptionally handsome and talented, and had already participated in the imperial examinations at a young age. Wealthy families in the county who had daughters all had their hearts set on him. Liusheng's parents were strict in choosing a wife for him, often saying, "Our son is a phoenix among men, how could he be paired with the common chickens and ducks of the world?" Therefore, matchmakers repeatedly visited their door but were never allowed. He had delayed marriage until approaching adulthood, still unmarried, and his heart was filled with sorrow. One day, he was ordered by his father to visit his mother-in-law outside the city. After briefly discussing family matters, he idly looked out the front door with his mother-in-law's son. Soon, a maid came to call his younger brother, and Liusheng accompanied him inside. It turned out that his mother-in-law had gone to a nearby village for a matter, ordering her son to accompany her, and left Liusheng to wait for a short time, planning to return and still have something to say. Liusheng could not go, and his heart was quite displeased. His mother-in-law's son, who had just come of age, had already arranged a marriage with a certain family, and this trip was likely for wedding-related matters. After seeing his mother-in-law leave happily with her son, he felt somewhat bored and remained standing in the village. Looking far into the southwest woods and valleys, it seemed to have a beautiful scenery, and he suddenly thought to go and see it, so he walked alone. The gatekeeper stopped him, and he said, "I cannot bear this loneliness, I will return soon, do not fear." He left straight away, and could not be persuaded to stay. He walked halfway and reached a stream, his feet were already tired, so he rested by the water's edge, gazing down at the clear stream, feeling quite peaceful.

    824

    俄闻隔溪娇语曰：“如此风姿，那得不令人看杀？”宝惊视之，则一女郎，约当及笄之年，玉容妩媚，花貌幽妍，将一片绛纱浣于溪内，指爪映水，雪色莹然，衣饰亦甚淡雅。宝不觉心醉，将欲通问而腼腆不容启吻，状甚嗫嚅。女郎见其木立，乃笑曰：“觑我何为？苎萝村女儿正恐未易胜汝也。”宝闻而心喜。女郎即招之曰：“盍渡此溪，当与尔言。”宝以首示之意，答以不能。女郎指曰：“西侧有红桥，痴郎何竟病涉耶？”宝远立而望，不数武果有徒杠，烂然赤色，遂欣趋就，蹑之以过。比及对岸，女郎早辍洗相俟。见其至前，欢然与语，谓之曰：“妾处闺中，颇以贞信自守。今见郎，竟不能复坚，此中固有天意。”因挽之共坐于柳下。绿莎茸茸，宛然锦席，绝胜班荆相对者。女郎因诘其里族，宝终以口讷不能言。女郎頳颜而起曰：“丈夫犹如是，妾辈复何堪？请从此辞，不敢再与郎见矣。”宝又揽其裾，强白姓氏，究以吃吃不能畅。

    Explanation

    824

    Suddenly, a sweet voice came from across the stream: "Such beauty, how could one not be captivated?" The man, startled, looked up and saw a young lady, around the age of marriage, with a jade-like face and a flower-like beauty. She was washing a piece of red silk in the stream, her fingers reflected in the water, snow-white and translucent, and her attire was simple and elegant. The man was enchanted and wanted to inquire but was too bashful to speak, looking hesitant and stuttering. The young lady saw him standing there and laughed, saying, "Why do you look at me? A girl from the village of Zhusuo probably cannot easily surpass you." The man was pleased to hear this. The young lady then invited him, saying, "Why not cross this stream, and we can talk." The man gestured with his head, indicating he could not. The young lady pointed and said, "There is a red bridge on the west side, why are you afraid to cross, foolish man?" The man stood far away and looked, and indeed, there was a simple bridge a short distance away, bright red, so he happily hurried over and stepped onto it. By the time he reached the other side, the young lady had already stopped washing and was waiting for him. When she saw him approach, she happily spoke to him, saying, "I live in the inner chambers, strictly adhering to chastity and trustworthiness. But seeing you, I could not remain firm, there must be a divine will at work." She then invited him to sit together under the willow. The green grass was soft and lush, like a brocade mat, far surpassing a humble meeting on the ground. The young lady then asked about his hometown and clan, but the man, being tongue-tied, could not answer. The young lady blushed and stood up, saying, "Men are like this, how can we endure it? Please part ways, I will not dare to meet you again." The man grabbed her skirt again and forcefully asked for her surname, but finally, stuttering, he could not speak fluently.

    825

    女不禁鼓掌曰：“艾艾果有几艾？”乃自陈曰：“妾家居此近村，父姓令狐，有女名宜织，即妾也。君如不弃，当造访焉。垂杨门巷，偏东一带疏篱，固无难识。”言已，即举所浣之沙相赠，曰：“此亦足以当绣幙之丝矣。”方将缱绻，上流似有笑声。女郎亟起曰：“女伴将至，不能复留。须记妾言，勿使人望穿此眸。”遂冉冉缘溪而去，尚以横波回顾，眷恋不胜。宝亦怃然如有所失，伫望移时，直至不见始能举趾而归。匆匆过桥，则夕阳在山矣。比至姑家，新月已上。时姑久已旋返，俟之不至，心以为忧，业遣仆僮遍觅之。宝至入见，姑怒诘其焉往，答以闲行。姑叱之曰：“孺子亦太不羁。邑门已闭，汝将安归？汝父母为汝倚闾。幸在予家，犹无虑，不然肠断矣。”宝亟谢罪，姑父亦力为排解，姑始霁颜，即命婢进食相款。是夜遂宿于姑家。明日辞归，假他事对其父母。父母素爱之，竟不穷究。宝至己室，始出纱玩之。

    Explanation

    825

    The girl couldn't help clapping her hands and said, "Indeed, 'Ai Ai' has many 'Ai's?" She then introduced herself, saying, "I live in a nearby village. My father's surname is Linghu, and I have a daughter named Yi Zhi, who is me. If you do not disdain me, you should come to visit. There is a lane with willow trees near the gate, slightly eastward, with a sparse fence; it should not be difficult to recognize." After speaking, she immediately presented the sand she had washed as a gift, saying, "This is also sufficient to serve as the silk for embroidery." Just as they were about to linger in affection, it seemed as if there was laughter coming from upstream. The young lady quickly stood up and said, "My companions are coming, and I cannot stay any longer. Remember my words, do not let anyone look through these eyes." She then slowly followed the stream away, still glancing back with a sideways glance, filled with lingering affection. The boy also felt disappointed as if he had lost something, standing and gazing for a while until he could not see her anymore before he could raise his foot and return home. Hurriedly crossing the bridge, the sun was already setting behind the mountains. By the time he arrived at his aunt's house, the new moon had already risen. At that time, the aunt had already returned, but she waited for him without success, and was worried, having already sent servants to search for him. The boy entered and saw his aunt, who angrily questioned where he had been. He replied that he had been wandering around. The aunt scolded him, saying, "You are too unruly, my child. The town gate is closed, where will you go? Your parents are standing by the gate, waiting for you. Fortunately, you are here at my house, so there is no need to worry, otherwise, they would be heartbroken." The boy quickly apologized, and his uncle also tried to mediate, and the aunt finally calmed down and ordered a maid to serve food to entertain him. That night, he stayed at his aunt's house. The next day, he bid farewell and returned home, telling a fabricated story to his parents. His parents loved him dearly and did not thoroughly investigate. When he returned to his room, he finally took out the silk and played with it.

    826

    阔仅数寸，长尺余，两端缀以金钮，缝纫已成者，状如妇人之诃子。然思蛮腰即细，不应如斯。及嗅之，虽经浣濯，娇香犹在，果即是物。宝乃惊喜如狂，虑为人见，秘之笥中。夜卧辄拥之衾底，如对丽人。自是每至姑处，必往踪迹。无如溪水泛滥，并无桥梁，宝因心窃讶之。屡不得渡，抱闷而返。数旬后，闻父母已为问名，则同邑陆弁之女，素以色美闻于乡，父母因聘之。宝心亦少安，而究思女郎不置。

    Explanation

    826

    A piece of cloth, a few inches wide and over a foot long, with golden buttons adorning both ends, was already sewn and shaped like a traditional undergarment for women. However, thinking of a woman's waist as slender, it should not be this narrow. Upon smelling it, even though it had been washed, the delicate fragrance still remained, confirming that it was indeed the object in question. The protagonist was overjoyed and almost wild with excitement, fearing that others might see it, he kept it secret in a bamboo box. At night, he would hug it under the quilt, as if in the presence of a beautiful woman. From then on, every time he went to his aunt's place, he would go in search of it. Unfortunately, the stream was flooding, and there was no bridge, so the protagonist secretly wondered about it. He failed to cross the stream repeatedly, returning frustrated. After several weeks, he heard that his parents had arranged for a marriage. It was the daughter of a local official, known in the village for her beauty, and his parents had arranged for her to be married. The protagonist's heart was somewhat at ease, but he could not stop thinking about the young lady.

    827

    一日，偶过陆家，适陆女出游，肩舆息于门外。盖陆故寒微，所居湫隘，舆中人之升降，途中人皆可旁观，宝故得以覸之。见其貌虽姣小，而丰肌啬骨，抹粉涂脂，不第视眕纱之艳，大有妍媸之分，即较掷果之容，尚有黔皙之别，私心遂窃有不愿。然迫于亲命，似亦无可如何。乃忿然出郭，仍至溪侧，虽故无苇可杭，幸其水势清浅，于是不暇顾恤，径去其履袜，白足而涉之。宝本素未习此，溪水森森，凉欲沁骨。彳亍及岸而登，衣裤尽湿。因笑曰：“褰裳涉溱，褰裳涉洧，予今乃反而用之也。

    Explanation

    827

    One day, passing by Lu's house by chance, I happened to see Lu's daughter going out for a walk, with her sedan chair resting outside the door. Because Lu's family was humble and lived in a narrow and low dwelling, the movement of the sedan chair could be observed by passersby, allowing me to peek at her. I saw that although her appearance was pretty and small, she had a plump figure with well-proportioned bones, and she had applied makeup. Not only did she look more beautiful than fine silk, there was a significant difference in her beauty compared to others, even when compared to the woman who threw fruit, there was a difference in skin tone. Secretly, I felt unwilling, but under pressure from family commands, it seemed there was no way out. Angered, I left the city and went to the side of a stream. Although there were no reeds to cross, fortunately, the water was clear and shallow. I had no time to worry, so I directly removed my shoes and socks and waded through with bare feet. I had never practiced this before, and the cold, clear water penetrated to the bone. I slowly walked to the shore and climbed up, with my clothes and pants completely wet. I laughed and said, "Lifting the skirt to cross the Zhen River, lifting the skirt to cross the Wei River, I am now using it in reverse."

    828

    ”整衣而前，约里许，果得一村。其中屋宇俨然，桑麻森秀，似不止一二人家，宝因徐步而入。东偏有小巷，绿阴垂荫，仿佛女郎所云。及入而望之，篱花堆艳，黄蝶粉飞，旋即得其门户。宝犹未至，见有杖者科头箕踞，独坐于篱边树下。视其年已古稀，而瑰玮奇特，不类田叟，疑即女之父也。直前趋谒，杖者颇傲慢，徐起为礼，询所自来。宝忽自觉唐突，呐呐有顷，先以姓字相告，而来意则未敢遽陈，杖者忽愕然曰：“是吾妻侄也！数年不晤，今成立矣。然果何由至此？”宝窃喜，疑其误认，而藉此或可入门。遂谬对曰：“久失音问，父颇思忆，故遣侄来省视耳！”杖者大笑曰：汝父讵能识予？此遁词也。虽然，有劳远涉，且系瓜葛，当非突如其来，请即入。”径揖之行。宝以失言故，其色頳然，勉随之登堂。其居亦甚幽雅，假山活水，极尽丘壑之致。而琴书潇洒，案无纤尘，其人之风韵可见一斑。

    Explanation

    828

    Baobao straightened his clothes and proceeded forward, traveling about a mile before finally reaching a village. The houses in the village were neatly arranged, with mulberry and hemp trees lush and tall, suggesting that there were more than just one or two households. Baobao slowly walked into the village. On the eastern side, there was a small alley with green shade hanging over, as the young lady had described. Upon entering and looking around, he saw flowers piled up in the fence, with yellow butterflies fluttering, and soon found the door. Baobao had not yet arrived, but he saw an old man with a staff, bareheaded and sitting cross-legged, alone under a tree by the fence. The old man was in his ancient age, yet magnificent and unique, unlike a farmer, leading Baobao to suspect that he might be the young lady's father. Baobao went forward to pay respects, but the old man was quite arrogant, slowly rising to greet him and asking where Baobao came from. Baobao suddenly felt awkward, stammering for a moment, and first told his name, but was hesitant to reveal his purpose. The old man suddenly exclaimed in surprise, "He is my wife's nephew! We haven't seen each other in several years, and he is now grown up. But how did he come here?" Baobao secretly rejoiced, suspecting that he might have been mistaken, but perhaps this could allow him to enter. So he replied falsely, "We haven't heard from each other for a long time, and my father often thinks of him, so I sent my nephew to visit him!" The old man burst into laughter, saying, "Your father could never recognize me. This is a fabricated excuse." Nevertheless, he acknowledged that Baobao had traveled far to visit, and they were related by blood, so it wasn't unexpected. "Please come in," he invited, bowing and gesturing for Baobao to enter. Embarrassed by his slip of the tongue, Baobao's face turned red, and he reluctantly followed and entered the hall. The residence was also very elegant, with artificial hills and flowing water, fully capturing the charm of mountains and valleys. Inside, the setup of musical instruments and books was simple and elegant, and the desk was spotless, revealing a glimpse of the old man's character.

    829

    因请以子侄礼见，杖者亦木辞，居然受之。始与坐谈，曰：“山妻为乃尊远房之姊，物故已久。遗一女，老夫携之，村居未入城邑，至今莫识其外家，揆其心窃以为恨。予既辱临，可使一面，俾知母族人物不同琐屑者，小妮子庶几无憾矣！”宝唯唯。适有双鬟捧茗出，杖者遽令呼之。茶次，又诘之曰：“侄幼时余至若家，曾见汝父，实未握手耳。前所云云，窃疑相绐，可明以告我。”宝不得已，起白曰：“父实未及作念，侄闻人言，令狐叟世之伟人，隐居于此，故愿望见以求教益，幸无疑。”杖者乃微笑，遂不复咨。无何，环珮璆然，则女郎盛妆至矣。宝睨之，衣饰已更，美艳倍胜于溪畔。回忆陆女，弥若霄壤。女郎低鬟伫立，凝睇无言，杖者语之曰：“汝之大兄来自邑内，即若从舅之子也。汝为妹，当以礼见。”女郎即向宝敛皀，宝亦致揖。而当觌面之间，女郎之色顿异，若羞若恨，如怨如怒，一似深憾其来迟者。

    Explanation

    829

    Because he asked to meet with the courtesy of a nephew, the man with the staff also did not refuse, and surprisingly accepted. They began to sit and talk, and he said, "My wife is your respected ancestor's distant aunt, who has passed away for a long time. She left behind one daughter, whom I have taken with me, living in the countryside and not entering the city. To this day, I do not know her maternal family, and considering her heart, she secretly feels regret. I have already been honored by your visit, and I can arrange for her to meet you, so that she may know that the people of her mother's family are not trivial, and the little girl might finally be without regrets!" Bao nodded in agreement. Just then, a woman with twin buns brought out tea, and the man with the staff quickly ordered her to be called. After the tea was served, he asked again, "When I was young and came to your home, I once saw your father, but we did not shake hands. What was said before, I secretly suspect is misleading. Please make it clear to me." Bao, unable to avoid it, stood up and said, "My father did not have the chance to think about it. I heard from others that Linghu Old Man is a great person of the world, living in seclusion here, so I wish to see him to seek his teachings and benefits. Please do not doubt." The man with the staff then smiled and did not ask further. Soon after, the sound of jade ornaments could be heard, and then the young lady arrived in full dress. Bao glanced at her, and her attire had changed, her beauty even more stunning than by the stream. Recalling the girl from the stream, the difference was even more profound than heaven and earth. The young lady lowered her hairpin and stood silently, gazing intently without speaking. The man with the staff spoke to her, "Your elder brother has come from the town, he is your maternal uncle's son. You are the younger sister, and you should greet him with courtesy." The young lady then bowed deeply to Bao, and Bao also returned the bow. However, when they met face to face, the young lady's expression suddenly changed, as if she were ashamed, resentful, like a complaint, like anger, as if deeply regretting that they came too late.

    830

    杖者又笑曰：“宜织与乃兄貌竟相似，使非育于二姓，尽足生一家之光。惜乎男不从姑，而女徒似舅也。”言次，数数目宝，意颇垂青。宝因无敢自媒，而恋女又不能去。荏苒间，阴霾陡起，骤雨滂沱，宝乃仓皇失措。杖者慰曰：“侄勿虑此，虽初遇，亦至戚也，即留宿于予家，当无不可。”宝大悦，益出望外。视女郎，以手拈带，默坐于父侧，眉目无复愠色。乃以言挑杖者曰：“妹年几何矣？”曰：“十七岁矣。”宝闻又曰：“只少侄二龄乎？”杖者似解其意，不复答。适值馔具，肴核杂陈，宝乃重致不安，言词爽朗。忽闻女郎低哂曰：“何对长者反无艾气？口舌亦因人利钝耶？”宝亦为之匿笑。食已，雨犹未霁，杖者命设榻于东堂，为客馆。且辞曰：“老夫髦矣！不能久陪晤语，侄自偃息，慎勿忆家。”径率女郎入屏后而去。宝私喜曰：“东床坦腹，予今亦俨然右军矣。”未几，双鬟以烛至，小语曰：“阿姑寄声，俟翁寝，当自来也。

    Explanation

    830

    The elder man laughed and said, "Your appearance is so similar to your elder brother's that if you were not raised by two different families, you would certainly bring honor to your family. Unfortunately, the boy did not follow his aunt's wishes, while the girl only resembles her uncle." As he spoke, he looked at Bao with favor. Bao, however, was too shy to propose himself and was also reluctant to leave the girl. Suddenly, dark clouds gathered and heavy rain began to pour down. Bao was in a state of panic. The elder man comforted him, saying, "Nephew, do not worry. Although we have just met, we are already related, and you can stay at my house overnight." Bao was delighted, feeling more than he had expected. He looked at the girl, who was quietly sitting beside her father, her hand playing with her sash, and her face no longer showing any signs of anger. The elder man then asked the girl, "How old are you?" She replied, "Seventeen years old." Bao, upon hearing this, said, "You are only two years younger than me, right?" The elder man seemed to understand his meaning and did not respond. Just then, the food was served, with a variety of dishes laid out. Bao felt uneasy, but his words were clear and straightforward. Suddenly, the girl whispered, "Why are you so polite to elders but so blunt with others? Is your speech sharper with some people?" Bao laughed softly. After the meal, the rain still had not stopped. The elder man ordered a bed to be set up in the east room for the guest. He then said, "I am old! I cannot stay and talk for long. Nephew, please rest here. Do not think of home." He led the girl into the inner room and left. Bao was secretly pleased, thinking, "I am now in a position of favor, just like Wang Xizhi." Soon, the maids brought candles and whispered, "Your aunt has sent a message, saying she will come when the old man falls asleep."

    831

    ”宝益欣悦，因取案头书翻阅之，不敢即寝。夜将半，女郎果出，则已残妆半卸，态愈动人。入见宝，即正色责之曰：“妾为一时柔情，不顾千秋笑柄，偶尔邂逅，即将近体之衣举以赠君，意固有所在也。乃君竟弃之如遗，一别三月，玉趾弗勤，令妾愧悔交集，忿且欲死。以君少年英发，不宜无信至此。今特见君，万祈还予故物，不必再有他言。”言讫，珠泪盈眶，潸然欲泣。宝知其怨已深，挽之就坐，自白其爽约之由，并陈徒涉之苦。女郎佯不信，宝又牵裾示知湿痕宛在，女郎始冁然回嗔，而犹絮絮不已，索取前纱。宝笑而出之于怀曰：“物则犹是也，然已近我肌肤，恐卿不可再束矣。”因缅述偎抱之状，女郎颜赤，不禁娇羞，亟起而避去。宝将止之，已不能及。迨过画屏，犹闻其语曰：“亦太无赖，几令人无地置身。”俄而人语嘈杂，其声忽出于堂后，有若忿詈，有若哀泣，又有若解纷者，宝心大疑。倾听之，苦不可辨。尔许时方寂然，宝即解衣安寝。

    Explanation

    831

    Baoyi was greatly pleased, and he took a book from his desk and read it, not daring to go to sleep immediately. It was nearly midnight when the young lady indeed appeared, and she had already partially undone her makeup, her appearance even more captivating. She entered and saw Baoyi, and she immediately spoke sternly to him, saying, "I acted out of a momentary tender feeling, without regard for being laughed at for a thousand years, and in a chance encounter, I gave you a piece of my undergarment, there was a specific reason for this. But you discarded it as if it were worthless, and we parted for three months, your footsteps were not frequent, it made me feel ashamed and regretful, I was so angry that I wanted to die. Considering your youth and brilliance, it is not fitting for you to be so untrustworthy. I have come to see you specifically, please return the old item to me, there is no need for further words." After she finished speaking, tears filled her eyes, and she was about to cry. Baoyi knew that her resentment was deep, and he pulled her to sit down, explaining the reasons for his breach of promise, and describing the hardships of traveling alone. The young lady pretended not to believe him, and Baoyi then pulled out the garment to show the wet stains. The young lady then smiled and calmed down, but she continued to talk incessantly, demanding the return of the previous garment. Baoyi smiled and said, "The object is still the same, but it has now touched my skin, I fear you cannot wear it again." He then described the embrace, and the young lady blushed, unable to help but feel shy, and she quickly got up and left. Baoyi tried to stop her, but he could not catch up to her. Until she passed the screen, he still heard her say, "You are too mischievous, it almost makes me have nowhere to stand." Soon there was a commotion of voices, the sounds suddenly came from behind the hall, some sounded like angry curses, some sounded like mournful cries, and some sounded like attempts to resolve conflicts. Baoyi was greatly puzzled and listened carefully, but it was difficult to discern. After a while, it became quiet again, and Baoyi then undressed and went to sleep.

    832

    晨起将见杖者陈谢，然后辞归，且少露求耦之意。忽女郎肌容憔悴，神色凄惶，疾趋而来。谓宝曰：“妾以衵衣在君处，势难挽回，不得已而告父，以冀俯从。不意家严震怒，大奋雷霆，将置妾于死地。幸婢之婉言代解，甫蒙俞允。限君以旬日，归告父母，即当亲来定议。否则，君不来之日，即妾毕命之期，须臾弗缓，惟君怜而许之。妾固无能自主也。”宝闻言大惊，且自晤女郎，早置旧姻于度外，一若未有其事者。今更睹女之状，深恫于心，惶急中益不暇顾虑，慨然曰：“诺。”女郎又要之，辄指皦日自矢，流连再三。女郎直送之门侧，方始挥泪而别。及至溪边，水已平添尺许，似不能涉。逡巡久之，前桥瞥现于波上，屈曲如虹。宝甚喜，乃指而笑曰：“世称无定诃，此独非无定桥耶？”因得径渡，遂登彼岸。

    Explanation

    832

    At dawn, about to meet the cane-bearer to apologize, he then bid farewell and returned, and slightly revealed his intention of seeking a partner. Suddenly, the young lady's appearance was emaciated, and her expression was sorrowful and anxious, rushing over to him. She said to him, "I have my inner garments in your possession, and it is difficult to retrieve them. Out of necessity, I informed my father, hoping for his approval. Unexpect
    |

    833

    于路忽自计曰：“陆女既已纳聘，且命于亲，此女未禀高堂，岂容暱就？前盟不可寒，后约必不能践，王魁李益之事，将见于予身，其若之何？”宝念及此，心始踌躇，而究无良策。行且至家，陡生谲计曰：“旧姻若就，新特难求。倘失此佳人，不如死。吾闻父母将諏日为予毕姻，盍重赂日者，诡称陆女之年庚实于翁姑不利，吾以孝义谏亲，誓死不娶。父母素怜我，必毁前盟，然后往就令狐不难矣。”筹思已定，归家以雨泞为词，云宿于姑所，父母亦不之疑。翌日遂行其计，邑中知命者，咸私其金。父因子媳俱长，果思择吉竣其事。宝知之，亦请随往。凡过数肆，皆攒眉曰：“谁令君缔此盟者？妇入而君之伉俪俱殆矣！”宝之父乃大骇，盖闻女美，急于遣冰，其初故未卜之也。然以成言难毁，强之定期而归。至暮，宝忽涕泣于母前曰：“生儿授室，虽出罔极之恩，实以尽奉养之道。

    Explanation

    833

    On the road, Bao suddenly thought to himself, "Lu's daughter has already been betrothed, and the marriage is arranged by the parents. This girl has not yet been approved by her parents. How can I be close to her? The previous promise cannot be broken, but the new one will surely not be fulfilled. The fate of Wang Kui and Li Yi will befall me. What should I do about it?" Bao reflected on this and hesitated, but ultimately found no good solution. As he was about to return home, he suddenly devised a cunning plan: "If the old marriage is consummated, the new one will be hard to find. If I lose this beautiful woman, I might as well die. I heard that my parents are going to choose a date to finalize the marriage. Why not bribe the fortune-tellers and falsely claim that Lu's daughter's birth year is unfavorable to my parents-in-law? I will advise my parents with filial piety and swear never to marry. My parents have always loved me, and they will surely break the previous engagement. Then it will not be difficult to go to Linghu." After thinking it through, he returned home and used rain and mud as an excuse, saying he stayed at his aunt's house, and his parents did not suspect him. The next day, he carried out his plan, and the fortune-tellers in the town all accepted his bribes. My father, seeing that both my fiancée and I are grown, indeed thought to choose a good day to finalize the marriage. Bao knew this and also asked to go along. Whenever he passed several shops, they all frowned and said, "Who made you make this promise? If the wife enters, your marriage will be in danger!" Bao's father was greatly shocked. Because he heard that the girl was beautiful, he was eager to send the matchmaker, but he did not initially know her fate. But because the promise was hard to break, he forced them to set a date and returned home. At dusk, Bao suddenly wept in front of his mother and said, "My son, you gave me a wife, although it was a boundless favor, it was actually to fulfill the duty of filial piety."

    834

    今新妇有碍于父母，而儿竟知而娶之，不孝莫大焉！纵令卜筮之言无验，此心既已不安，如其果验，儿不为名教之罪人乎？请罢此姻，昧死以告。”母闻之大惊，亟语其父。父不肯曰：“信荒诞之言，败已成之约，人其谓我何？事关名节，而儿戏至此，陆必不甘，势将搆讼。如之何其可哉？且予夫妇既羸老，荀得佳妇，配此佳儿，即死亦无所憾，况未必死乎？”坚不听。宝又长跽于父前，以死自誓，断不忍就此姻。且曰：“儿请往见陆翁索取前聘，倘有讼事，儿自当之，必不至贻罹父母也。”父终溺爱其子，虽不径许，亦姑颔之，不过安慰其心耳。诘朝，宝入邑庠，拉密友数人，径诣陆处，求退婚。陆讶之，宝与友皆侃侃正论，以纲常为言。又曰：“孝与义孰重？即令翁讼之于官，予亦死不敢就。”陆本粗鄙，莫能强词，且惮士林诸君，只唤原媒责让之，竟还其原聘，莫敢与争。此一举也，持论甚正，人反以宝为贤，而不知其计也。

    Explanation

    834

    Today, the new bride has an obstacle with her parents, yet the son knew about it and still married her; there is no greater filial impiety! Even if the divination is not proven, this heart is already uneasy. If it is indeed proven, wouldn't the son be a criminal against the norms and teachings? Please break off this marriage, I dare to report this at the risk of my life." The mother heard it and was greatly shocked, and immediately told her father. The father refused, saying, "Believing in absurd words, destroying an already established agreement, what will people say about me? This concerns one's reputation and integrity, and the son treats this as a game. Lu will certainly not be content, and there will be a lawsuit. How can this be allowed? Moreover, my husband and I are already old and weak. If we get a good wife, to match this fine son, even if we die, we have no regrets. Moreover, we might not even die." He firmly refused to listen. Bao knelt down long before his father, swore by his death, and absolutely cannot accept this marriage. He also said, "The son requests to go see Mr. Lu to reclaim the dowry. If there is a lawsuit, the son will bear it himself, and will not bring trouble to his parents." The father ultimately dotes on his son, although he did not directly agree, he also nodded slightly, it was just to comfort his heart. The next morning, Bao entered the local school, took several close friends, and went directly to Lu's place, requesting to break off the marriage. Lu was surprised, and Bao and his friends all spoke frankly and correctly, speaking about the norms and teachings. They also said, "Which is more important, filial piety or righteousness? Even if the old man sues him in court, I would die rather than accept it." Lu is originally crude and vulgar, and could not argue forcefully. Moreover, he fears the scholars and gentlemen, and only called the original matchmaker to scold him. He finally returned the original dowry, and no one dared to argue with him. This one act, the argument is very correct, people反而以为宝贤，而不知其计也。

    835

    宝志益得，而屈指浃旬，虑女有失，因思先往践言，然后归告父母劝使委禽，庶几周匝。遂复独行以往。幸溪桥尚在，跋履无难。乃甫至村中，即遭杖者于道，欢然握手，延之至家，遽曰：“侄来甚惬吾意，将有一事相浼。”宝叩之，答曰：“老夫故燕吏也，退休于此有年矣。昨承帝命，以幽蓟之众，供职者每私出，致为生民患，特简老夫前往统摄。今将远行而弱息断不能随，正以为忧，子乃适逢其会。眖在葭莩，敬以付托，妻之固可，嫁之亦唯命，老夫从此弗问矣。

    Explanation

    835

    宝志日益得志，但心中牵挂，屈指一算已过十天，担心那女孩会有不测，因此想到先去履行诺言，然后再回去告知父母，劝说他们促成这门亲事，希望一切都能圆满。于是他又独自上路前往。幸运的是，溪上的桥还在，行走并无困难。刚到村中，就遇到了手持拐杖的人在路上，对方热情地与他握手，并邀请他到家中。那人急切地说：“侄儿的到来让我非常高兴，我有一事相托。”宝志询问详情，那人回答道：“我曾是燕地的官员，退休多年了。昨天接到皇帝的命令，因为幽州和蓟州的民众中，那些在职的人经常私自外出，导致百姓遭受困扰，特地任命我前往管理。现在我要远行，而我的孩子还小，无法跟随，这正是我忧虑之处。你恰好在此时出现，我们是亲戚，我郑重地将此事托付给你。无论是娶她还是嫁她，都听凭你的安排，从此以后我就不再过问了。”

    836

    侄即携去，幸勿固辞。”宝因惊且喜，毅然受教。杖者旋起入内，促女束装，别离之惨，达于外。少顷，携女郎出，美目尚含余泪，对宝再拜曰：“妹今日惟兄是依矣！”色甚凄然。杖者又曰：“宜织好从兄去，钦限甚迫，宅已转售于人，不可复留也。”遂指箱簏数十，尽以赠宝。器具书玩，咸畀之。立命起行，不容再缓。宝乃与女郎泣拜于膝下。及出，则肩舆数乘，人百余，相候于外，亦不解其何以立办。女郎携二婢，各乘其一，宝亦乘一舆为引道，仗者目送于门。

    Explanation

    836

    The nephew should take it with him, and please do not refuse it." The nephew was both surprised and delighted, and he firmly accepted the instruction. The elderly man quickly rose and went inside to urge the girl to prepare herself. The sorrow of their separation was evident to those around them. Soon after, he led the girl out, her beautiful eyes still containing tears. She bowed twice to the nephew and said, "From today on, I will rely solely on my brother!" Her expression was very sorrowful. The elderly man added, "You should prepare well to accompany your brother, for the deadline is very strict, and the house has already been sold to someone else, so we cannot stay here anymore." He then pointed to dozens of chests and boxes, giving everything to the nephew. All the tools, books, and toys were given to him. He immediately commanded them to leave, leaving no room for delay. The nephew and the girl knelt and wept, bowing to the elderly man at his knees. When they finally went out, several sedan chairs and over a hundred people were waiting outside, and it was unclear how they had managed to gather so quickly. The girl took two maids, each riding a separate sedan chair, and the nephew also rode a sedan chair to lead the way. The elderly man watched them leave from the door.

    837

    女郎痛苦失声，杖者尉之曰：“儿勿自苦，父虽官守羁身，然欲相见，万里且无难，况仅数千里者。”宝更不解其语。行装既发，势难复停，一时络绎于道上，村人皆翘首以观。或叹曰：“令狐翁之富乃如此，何居乎未之前闻也？”既而渡溪，宝心自计曰：“骤携若人归家，父母将滋惧，予亦蹈不告之罪。盍往姑处暂居，使姑为我设策，当无不济。”遂麾舆从，直抵姑家。姑适与夫闲坐，谈及宝之辞婚，皆啧啧共赞其孝。宝忽偕丽人入，装束如仙，且辎重无算，举止于庭，乃骇然。诘问其故，宝始具言其实。姑忽惊曰：“是女吾姊所育耶？然实出于狐，非人也。”姑之夫亟询之，姑曰：“妾有从姊，未嫁而夭，其疾则为狐所祟也。病已沉顿，乃肯自言。云当十五岁时，即有美丈夫来同寝处，醉后每见形，实一狐耳。今既怀孕将产，死后勿即殓，恐狐来觅其子，阖家将不安。语终而殁，父母如其教。

    Explanation

    837

    The young woman cried out in pain, and the person with the staff consoled her, saying, "Do not distress yourself, my child. Although your father is bound by his official duties, he still wishes to see you. Even a journey of ten thousand miles would not be impossible, let alone a mere few thousand." Baobao did not understand her words. With their journey already underway, it was difficult to stop now. They moved along the road in a continuous stream, and the villagers all looked up to watch. Some remarked, "The wealth of the Linghu family is truly immense, how could we have not known of it before?" After crossing the stream, Baobao thought to himself, "If I suddenly bring this woman back home, my parents will become even more frightened, and I will also commit the sin of not informing them. Why not go stay with my aunt temporarily, and let her devise a plan for me? It will surely succeed." He then dismissed the carriage and attendants, and went directly to his aunt's house. His aunt happened to be sitting idly with her husband, and they were discussing Baobao's decision to break off his marriage. Everyone praised Baobao's filial piety. Suddenly, Baobao entered with a beautiful woman, dressed like a fairy, accompanied by an innumerable retinue. Her demeanor in the courtyard caused the aunt to be startled. When questioned about the matter, Baobao explained the truth. The aunt was suddenly shocked and asked, "Is this woman the daughter of my sister? But she is actually a fox spirit, not a human." Her husband urgently inquired, and she replied, "I have a deceased sister who died unmarried. She was possessed by a fox spirit. She was already severely ill and finally spoke up. She said that when she was fifteen, a handsome man came to share her bed. After getting drunk, she often saw his true form, which was actually a fox. Now that she is pregnant and about to give birth, do not bury her immediately after she dies, for fear that the fox will come to take her child, causing unrest in our entire family. She spoke these words before passing away, and her parents followed her advice.

    838

    是夜大风雨，家人有胆巨者私覸之，见狐来扶尸起坐，状如生人坐褥者。俄顷呱呱有声，竟抱之去，天复开霁。视姊，则血殷床席，依然僵卧，遂乃举而敛之于棺。姊生十七年而卒。今又十七稔矣。以年岁计之，是女尚二九未足。”姑既言详，室中人皆骇异，独女郎闻其母死之惨，泣不能仰。姑又熟视其貌，酷肖姊之仪容。因握其腕，同坐曰：“甥勿悲，予即汝之姨氏也。汝见予，不犹之乎见母耶？”即又笑曰：“予向以家宝为朴实，今乃知其狡狯矣。予曾亲见陆女，果去吾甥远甚，无怪乎以彼易此也。但以此为词，父母国人皆为所罔，其计不亦谲乎？”姑之夫亦大笑，宝色甚惭。姑命女郎与己处闺中，而贮其细软于内室，粗重者又另置之。且语宝曰：“予为汝成此美。不然，汝愿未遂，汝罪且莫逭矣。”因授以计。宝乃大喜，而奔抵家，告父曰：“儿往观姑，姑念儿母綦甚，不可不一行。”父果遣妻视其妹。

    838

    On that night, there was a great wind and rain. Those with great courage among the family secretly watched it, seeing a fox come to lift the body up and sit it on a cushion, looking like a living person sitting on a cushion. In a moment, there were sounds of crying, and they eventually took it away. The sky cleared up again. When looking at the sister, the bed and mat were stained with blood, and she was still lying stiffly. They then lifted her and placed her in the coffin. The sister lived for seventeen years and then died. Now it has been another seventeen years. Calculating by age, this girl was still short of eighteen. After the aunt spoke in detail, everyone in the room was shocked and amazed, but only the young lady, hearing the tragic death of her mother, cried and could not look up. The aunt then closely examined her appearance, finding it closely resembled her sister's. She took her hand and sat down with her, saying, "Do not grieve, I am your aunt. Seeing me, isn't it like seeing your mother?" She then laughed and said, "I once thought that the family treasure was simple and honest, but now I know it was cunning. I once personally saw the girl from Lu, and indeed, she was far superior to my niece, so it's no wonder she was replaced by her. But using this as an excuse, the parents and the nation were deceived, isn't her plan deceitful?" The aunt's husband also laughed, and the treasure's face turned very red with shame. The aunt ordered the young lady to stay in her inner chamber and stored her valuables in the inner room, placing the heavy items elsewhere. She then told the treasure, "I will help you achieve this beauty. Otherwise, your wish will not be fulfilled, and you will not escape punishment." She gave her a plan. The treasure was greatly delighted and rushed home, telling her father, "I went to see my aunt, and she greatly missed my mother, so I cannot not go." The father indeed sent his wife to see his sister.

    839

    既至，姑令女郎出见，言：“系邻人寄养者，乃父远宦不能携，故以之见属，聘嫁亦皆在予也。”宝之母谛观之，实远胜于陆女，因孜孜注目弗移。乘间请于姑，欲求为子妇。姑佯笑曰：“若家小郎君。二三其德，不可令此女又抱弃捐之恨。”宝之母又请，且索女之年庚。姑又笑曰：“不劳嫂虑，予已合之矣，决不至有妨于贤夫子也。”婚议遂谐。其母亟归，悉以语宝之父，父亦喜，择日即纳采于姑家，不半月而亲迎。

    Explanation

    839

    Upon arriving, the mother-in-law ordered the young lady to come out and be seen, saying, "She was a neighbor's child being raised by them; her father was far away on official business and could not bring her along, so he entrusted her to us, and her marriage arrangements were also entrusted to me." Bao's mother carefully observed her and found that she was actually far superior to Lu's daughter, so she kept her gaze fixed and unswerving. Taking the opportunity, she asked the mother-in-law, "I wish to request her as a daughter-in-law." The mother-in-law feigned a laugh and said, "Your son, the younger one, is unstable in character; it would not be right to let this girl suffer the pain of being abandoned again." Bao's mother asked again and also requested the girl's birth year and month. The mother-in-law laughed again and said, "Do not trouble yourself, I have already arranged it; it will certainly not interfere with your esteemed son-in-law." The marriage agreement was thus settled. Her mother hurried back home and told Bao's father everything. The father also agreed, and they chose a date to send a formal proposal to the mother-in-law's family. Within a month, the wedding took place.

    840

    合卺之夕，宝与女郎深感姑德。姑与女父所赠外，又复补其不足，衣饰奁具，虽贵家无以过之，宝之父母皆大悦。宝至夜始以红纱还女郎，坚令束之。女含羞解衣，著之于胸，犹宽然。因低笑曰：“妾为君浑消瘦矣！”宝乃知楚宫细腰非古人妄传者，愈觉得意，欢好倍深。三朝出见，戚党咸以为玉人有双，殊不负择婚之苛。女郎自此克供妇职，舅姑皆深喜。唯时时思父，夜寝即能相晤，随其所欲，暗中赠遗，女郎遂无所憾。间或为宝言，其数龄时，父始自山中相携至此。

    Explanation

    840

    The wedding night, Bao and the young woman deeply appreciated the kindness of the mother-in-law. Beyond what the mother-in-law had given to the young woman's father, she also supplemented what was lacking, and the clothing, adornments, and dowry items were so fine that even wealthy families could not surpass them. Bao's parents were greatly pleased. At night, Bao finally returned the red silk to the young woman, firmly instructing her to tie it around her waist. The young woman, blushing, undressed and tied it around her chest, but it was still somewhat loose. She then lowered her voice and laughed, saying, "I have become so thin for you!" Bao then realized that the slender waists of the women in the Chu palace were not merely a myth passed down by ancient people; he felt even more delighted, and their affection deepened even more. On the third day after the wedding, when she was formally introduced to her husband's family, relatives and friends all remarked that the couple were a pair of jade-like beauties, and the strict standards for choosing a spouse were fully met. From then on, the young woman fulfilled her duties as a wife, and her in-laws were deeply pleased. However, she often missed her father, and at night when she slept, she could meet with him in her dreams, giving him whatever he desired, and secretly offering him gifts. Thus, the young woman had no regrets. Occasionally, she would tell Bao about her childhood, when her father first brought her from the mountains to this place.

    841

    稍长，教以女红，兼授以书，督课如严师，无少间歇。父自居此地，不耕不织，衣食裕如。且闭门不与乡党通，人但知其姓为令狐而已。今岁之春，忽令妾日浣于溪畔，婢子相从，亦各任其游戏。妾所云女伴者即此，非他人也。每出，即予妾一红箸，嘱曰：“有少年郎欲渡者，汝须以此渡之。”遂授妾以口诀，妾以是少通神术。今在梦中相见，辄曰：“为汝夫妇跋涉良不易，然在吾，只取一日功，不甚劳苦。

    Explanation

    841

    When I was a bit older, I was taught embroidery and also given instruction in literature, with my studies supervised as rigorously as by a strict teacher, without the slightest break. My father, living here, did not till or weave, yet he provided food and clothing comfortably. Moreover, he kept himself shut off from the local community, and people only knew his surname as Linghu. This year in spring, he suddenly ordered me to wash clothes by the stream every day, with the maidservants following, each engaged in their own play. The female companions I mentioned are these maidservants, no others. Whenever I went out, he would give me a red stick and instruct me, saying, "If there is a young man who wants to cross, you must use this to help him cross." He then taught me a secret phrase, and through this, I slightly understood divine arts. Now, when we meet in dreams, he always says, "It is quite difficult for you two to travel, but for me, it only takes one day's effort, not very tiring."

    842

    ”因嘱妾善事翁姑，克相夫子，郎竟充耳不闻耶？宝遂叹息其奇，并悟桥之无定，总皆狐翁之术焉。初陆弁知宝另聘，以为邑中无出其女之右者，所娶必非绝色。及宜织归宁于姨氏，陆之族姓间有见者，莫不心折，以陆女实有弗如。后宝所贿之日者，稍稍漏言，人始知宝之本意，孝特其托辞。宝之名遂以少减，竟困于青衿，不克腾达，咸谓为弃妻所致。惟藉女郎之资，加以世家余蓄，迄今犹富甲一邑。其姑至事定后，时或语其兄嫂，亲族甫得女郎所自出。闺中宛若，相与嘲戏，恒以灵狐呼之。

    Explanation

    842

    Therefore, I entrusted my wife to serve her in-laws well and to assist her husband successfully, but the husband finally turned a deaf ear. Bao then sighed at the strangeness of the situation and realized that bridges are not always reliable, all of which were the tricks of the fox spirit. Initially, Lu Bie knew that Bao had another marriage arranged and thought that no one in the county was better than his daughter, so the person she married was certainly not exceptionally beautiful. When Yi Zhi returned to visit her aunt's family, some members of Lu's clan who saw her were all deeply impressed, as Lu's daughter was indeed not as good. Later, the astrologer bribed by Bao gradually revealed the truth, and people began to understand Bao's original intention, which was merely an excuse for filial piety. Bao's reputation was slightly diminished, and he was finally trapped in the ranks of scholars, unable to rise to prominence. Everyone said it was caused by Bao's abandoned wife. He only relied on the wealth of the young lady, added to which was the remaining savings of a noble family, and until now, he is still the wealthiest in the county. After the matter was settled, her mother-in-law sometimes spoke to her brothers-in-law and sisters-in-law, and the relatives just learned where the young lady came from. In the inner chambers, it seemed as if they mocked and played together, always calling her a spirit fox.

    843

    外史氏曰：浣纱西子，千百年后竟不一见，亦两间恨事也。不意柳于仓卒中遇之，且其人之美艳，既不少逊于夷光，而一室倡随，百年偕老，胜于吴之为沼蠡之泛湖多多矣。独狐翁以术饵其婿，柳生以智蔽其亲，冰清玉润者，曾如是乎？微姑之高义，好合虽可成，人言良可畏也。谋事在人，成事在天，两美之合，又无非苍天之意也夫！随园老人曰：武夷九曲，使人历尽方知，初见之，但有奇峰壁立耳，似无可转之境也。何物文心，竟与山灵争胜。吾于此，又得其不可解之一。

    Explanation

    843

    The Outer History Sage said: Xi Shi, the beauty who washed silk, is not seen even once after thousands of years, and it is also a regrettable matter in the world. Unexpectedly, Liu met her in a hurry, and her beauty was no less than that of Xi Shi. Living together in harmony, they grew old together for a hundred years, far surpassing the fate of Wu becoming a swamp and Xi Shi floating on a lake. Old Man Du used deceit to lure his son-in-law, and Liu used intelligence to deceive his parents. Is it like this for one who is pure and smooth like ice and jade? Without the high virtue of the aunt, even a good match could not be achieved, for people's words are indeed to be feared. Planning is up to man, but success is up to heaven. The union of two beauties is also nothing but the will of heaven. The Old Man of the Su Garden said: The nine bends of Wuyi Mountain can only be fully understood after experiencing them. At first glance, there are only towering peaks, it seems like there is no turning back. What kind of literary heart can compete with the spirit of the mountain? In this, I find another thing that is inexplicable.

    844

    ○　遗钩
    　　京师有逻卒高二者，性嗜酒。醉则仗一白梃，行歌于巷。以其端有铁，坚锐下曲，状如重乔之矛，谓之曰钩，夜巡者恃以捕盗之具也。一夕，夜甚深，高二又酕眗，持钩出。过一家，灯犹未烬，谛观之，则某人之室，新婚未久也。二心偶动，属耳于垣，闻妇与夫语，其声甚低，而狎昵之状可想。二不觉大动，筹思无可与者，遂挹怏而前。旋见一人，蠕蠕然行，来甚蹇缓。迫视之，衣装约略可辨，妇人也。窃意深夜独行，必非良家，似犹可挑。乃尾之，径入一巷，有荜门甚低矮，妇即侧身入内。二犹不敢肆，屏息伺之。俄闻群儿嬉戏声，见妇皆依依孺慕，似无壮夫，心乃安。窥有短垣，因逾焉。妇即叱问伊谁，二乃依钩于壁，突入其室，曰：“高二也。”妇惊询曰：“以钩来乎？”二笑曰：“钩诚有之，已植于墙下。”妇乃巧笑承迎，若不峻拒，惟日：“稚子辈未免扰攘，请君与妾升此屋。”二欣然从之。既登，果甚平坦。

    Explanation

    844

    A Lost Hook

    In the capital, there was a night patrol officer named Gao Er, who had a fondness for alcohol. When drunk, he would wield a white stick and sing as he walked through the alleys. The stick had an iron tip, making it sharp and curved, resembling a heavy spear, which was called a "hook." Night patrol officers relied on this hook as a tool for catching thieves. One night, when it was very late, Gao Er was again deeply intoxicated and left with his hook. Passing by a house, the lights were still burning, and he looked closely and saw that it was the home of a recently married couple. Suddenly, his heart stirred, and he listened closely to the wall, hearing the low, intimate conversation between the wife and her husband. Gao Er felt a sudden urge and thought there was no one else to satisfy it, so he moved forward with a sense of dissatisfaction. Soon, he saw a woman moving slowly, approaching very slowly. He looked more closely and could vaguely recognize her clothing; she was a woman. He thought that a woman walking alone at night must not be from a respectable family and seemed still approachable. So, he followed her, entering a lane directly. There was a low and humble gate, and the woman entered by sidling in. Gao Er still dared not act rashly and held his breath, waiting. Soon, he heard the sound of children playing, and saw the woman surrounded by affectionate children, as if there were no adult men present. His heart calmed, and he looked for a short wall and climbed over it. The woman shouted and asked who he was. Gao Er leaned the hook against the wall and suddenly entered the room, saying, "It's Gao Er." The woman was surprised and asked, "Did you come with the hook?" Gao Er smiled and said, "I do have a hook, but it's already planted at the foot of the wall." The woman smiled cleverly and welcomed him, as if not harshly refusing, and said, "The children are inevitably noisy, please come up to this house with me." Gao Er happily followed her. After they climbed up, the place was indeed very flat and spacious.

    845

    妇即解衣先卧，二抚其肌，绝丰腻。而近就之，若抱瓮，若拥肿，颇不可耐。然在饥渴之殷者，殊不计此也。为欢良久，甚惬素心，而宿酲未醒，遽拥之酣睡。及寤，似有人语曰：“此高二哥之物也，胡为乎在此？”又有人怒曰：“此偷儿欲盗予之小豝，故以钩致之。岂高二哥而亦为此？”既而哗曰：“是果高二哥矣！未见其人，反见其衣矣。”二乃大骇，张目，则旭日满天，己身卧于人家圈上，盖豢豕之牢也。

    Explanation

    845

    The woman undressed and lay down first. The man touched her skin, which was extremely smooth and soft. He approached her, feeling as if he were embracing a jar or a swollen object, finding it quite overwhelming. Despite the discomfort, driven by his intense desire, he did not care. They enjoyed themselves for a long time, feeling very fulfilled. However, still drowsy from a lingering hangover, he immediately embraced her and fell asleep. When he woke up, it seemed as if someone said, "These are the belongings of Gao Er Ge. Why are they here?" Another person angrily said, "This thief tried to steal my young pig, so he was caught with a hook. Could Gao Er Ge also be a thief?" Then they shouted, "He is indeed Gao Er Ge! We didn't see him, but we found his clothes." Gao Er Ge was greatly startled, opened his eyes, and saw the sun shining brightly in the sky. He realized he was lying on a pigsty, in a place where pigs were kept and raised.

    846

    俯而窥之，有彘母大且百斤，齁然假寐于墙角。己之钩，实在焉，不觉大惊。而身无寸缕，羞涩不胜。幸衣犹在侧，取著之，赧然而下。初主人见其为二，且形状如狂，亦愕然。呼而与语，其侪二人亦先在，争诘之，二实内怍，不能言。惟语主人曰：“是豕老矣！不烹之，将为妖。”众始悟其与豕为好，不禁大笑。二竟不顾其钩，匆遽而遁。翌日，主人果烹此豕。二梦前妇欣欣而来，谓之曰：“赖君片言解脱，今得转轮为人矣！”二惊觉，始举以告人。闻之者，莫不捧腹也。

    Explanation

    846

    Stooping to look, he saw a large sow, weighing over a hundred pounds, snoring and pretending to sleep in the corner of a wall. His own hook was indeed there, and he was unconsciously greatly startled. He was completely naked, feeling shame and embarrassment beyond endurance. Fortunately, his clothes were still nearby, so he put them on and descended, feeling ashamed and embarrassed. At first, the owner saw it as two, and the shape was like madness, also greatly startled. He called out and spoke with him, and the two companions were also already there, arguing and questioning him. The two were indeed internally ashamed and unable to speak. They only told the owner, "This pig is old! If not cooked, it will become a demon." The crowd finally realized that it was friendly with the pig and could not help but laugh. The two finally paid no attention to their hook and hurriedly and hastily fled. The next day, the owner indeed cooked this pig. The two dreamed of the previous woman coming happily, saying to them, "Relying on your few words to release, now has been reborn as a person!" They were startled awake and began to recount to others. Those who heard it, none did not laugh heartily.

    847

    外史氏曰：孟野之歌曰：“既定尔娄猪，盍归我艾豭。”二既以身为艾暇，则娄猪不难定矣。第恨主人当时误从一言，径投之鼎镬。不然，子朝方来，南子未老，其所生又必有异矣。惜哉！

    ○　奇遇
    　　余又闻之某公，言西陲荡定后，有军将秩且四品，部其众往戍回疆。兵弁百余人，临溪配渡，军将与其目长约十数，独乘一舟。有回人年且耄，为之操楫。偶闻军将乡音，忽操汉语咨询曰：“公等俱自华土来，风景近复何似？”阖舟闻而骇，竞诘之。乃泫然曰：“予虽居于此，种类实异，身本中州世族也。少年入伍，随征殊方，一时偶失利，遂陷于准噶尔部中。渠役之如奴隶，啮雪吞毡，苟延残喘。渠又售我于回部，遂习其俗，不能复变，今又数十年。窃幸皇威远震，复睹大邦人物，不禁感而失言，万勿见过。”众既悉其颠末，不觉恻然，有至泣下者。

    Explanation

    847

    外史氏曰：孟野之歌曰：“既定尔娄猪，盍归我艾豭。”二既以身为艾暇，则娄猪不难定矣。第恨主人当时误从一言，径投之鼎镬。不然，子朝方来，南子未老，其所生又必有异矣。惜哉！

    Commentator said: In Meng Ye's song it is said, "Having settled your lo pig, why not return to my boar?" If both have already taken themselves as boars, then it is not difficult to settle the lo pig. But I regret that the host at the time was misled by one word, and was immediately thrown into the cauldron. Otherwise, if Zi Chao had just arrived, and Nanzi was not yet old, what she bore would certainly be different. Alas!

    ○　奇遇
    　　余又闻之某公，言西陲荡定后，有军将秩且四品，部其众往戍回疆。兵弁百余人，临溪配渡，军将与其目长约十数，独乘一舟。有回人年且耄，为之操楫。偶闻军将乡音，忽操汉语咨询曰：“公等俱自华土来，风景近复何似？”阖舟闻而骇，竞诘之。乃泫然曰：“予虽居于此，种类实异，身本中州世族也。少年入伍，随征殊方，一时偶失利，遂陷于准噶尔部中。渠役之如奴隶，啮雪吞毡，苟延残喘。渠又售我于回部，遂习其俗，不能复变，今又数十年。窃幸皇威远震，复睹大邦人物，不禁感而失言，万勿见过。”众既悉其颠末，不觉恻然，有至泣下者。

    A Strange Encounter
    I heard from a certain official that after the western border was pacified, there was a military general of the fourth rank who led his troops to garrison the Hui region. There were over a hundred soldiers, and they were crossing a river near a stream. The general and about ten of his officers rode alone in a boat. There was an old Hui person who operated the paddle. He happened to hear the general's native accent and suddenly spoke Chinese and asked, "All of you are from the Central Plains, how is the scenery like these days?" Everyone on the boat was startled and questioned him. He then wept and said, "I have lived here, but my lineage is different. I am originally from a noble family in the Central Plains. I joined the army when I was young and followed the campaign to distant lands. At one time, I happened to suffer a defeat and was captured by the Dzungar tribe. They treated me like a slave, eating snow and swallowing felt, barely surviving. They then sold me to the Hui region, and I adopted their customs and could not change back. It has been many years. I secretly hope that the emperor's power is far-reaching, to see again the people of the great country. I cannot help but feel and speak out, please do not see it as a fault." After hearing his story, everyone felt deeply moved, and some even shed tears.

    848

    军将忽动念，复以里族叩之，则姓氏乡邦实与军将若合符节。及自言其名，军将瞿然甚惊，起立以询曰：“若去乡之日，曾授室否？”则曰：“娶某氏，琴瑟甚调。”又问：“抱子也未？”则曰：“年周岁，枣梨未觅。”再咨以其子之名，则言未及终，军将早噭然大恸，膝行而前矣。其人始愕然，亦释棹而跪，坚不敢承。同舟多有知者，又凿凿言之。更质以祖祢名讳，无不吻合，其人亦哭而失声，与军将相抱而泣。时已抵岸，军将白父，弃其所操之舟，出笥中衣冠，更易之，奉之同至戍所。具牒于上官，缕陈其实，兼缴官诰赎父罪。上官怜其情，喜其遇，亟为具奏。奉旨宥而弗问，兼听还其邻里，军将始命人送其父归。太夫人犹在堂，夫妇握手涕零，则皆年逾七十矣。

    Explanation

    848

    The soldier suddenly had a thought and again asked about his family and clan. The man’s surname, hometown, and place of origin were indeed as if they were made to fit together perfectly with the soldier’s. When the man mentioned his own name, the soldier was greatly startled and stood up to inquire, “Were you married when you left your hometown?” The man replied, “I married a woman from a certain family, and our marriage was very harmonious.” The soldier then asked, “Do you have a child?” The man answered, “My child is one year old, but I have not yet found dates and plums.” When the soldier asked for the name of his son, the man had not finished speaking when the soldier cried out in great sorrow and moved forward on his knees. The man was startled and let go of the paddle, kneeling down, and firmly refused to admit it. Many on the boat knew the truth and spoke firmly and clearly. When the soldier asked for the names of his ancestors, everything matched perfectly. The man also cried out in great sorrow, wept, and embraced the soldier, crying together. By then, they had reached the shore. The soldier told his father, who abandoned the boat he was operating, took out the clothes and hats from the chest, changed into them, and took the man to the garrison. They prepared a report for the higher authorities, detailed the facts, and submitted official documents to redeem the man’s father’s crime. The higher authorities pitied their situation, were pleased by their encounter, and quickly prepared a report. The emperor pardoned them without further inquiry and allowed the man to return to his community. The soldier then sent someone to escort his father back home. His mother was still alive, and the husband and wife held hands and wept, both over seventy years old.

    849

    外史氏曰：遇之奇者，惟在伦纪之地，益令人可泣、可歌、可哀、可喜，一时而七情具焉，诚莫知其所以然。夫以天涯之远，而聚天性之亲，其至乐乃出于至苦，其至苦愈有其至乐。异方之伯道，幸遇斑衣失怙之木兰，徒刻香木，苍苍者何巧耶？不然，秦越同舟，又何人迫之自言耶？

    ○　绣舄
    　　德安庄士玉，善女红，暇辄为细君缉履。落梅数瓣，妍艳如生。有窃闻其事者，每笑谓京兆眉妩，犹非钟情之至焉。一夕，就烛经营。夜已过半，乃置业履于牖上，同觅黑甜。翌日晨起，求之弗得，遂疑偷儿攫取。而室中都无所失，因而骇然，即亦不甚介意。及昏，与细君共语其异，忽梁上一物，疾如鸟隼，扑面飞来。亟视之，坠于榻上，则所绣之舄也。附以片纸，细楷簪花，庄阅之，则七言一绝，曰：“故抛象管弄银针，织尽文房几许心。自是深情怜一瓣，讵知寸趾价千金。”语意似讥细君之足下，而不知谁实为之者。

    Explanation

    849

    外史氏曰：遇之奇者，惟在伦纪之地，益令人可泣、可歌、可哀、可喜，一时而七情具焉，诚莫知其所以然。夫以天涯之远，而聚天性之亲，其至乐乃出于至苦，其至苦愈有其至乐。异方之伯道，幸遇斑衣失怙之木兰，徒刻香木，苍苍者何巧耶？不然，秦越同舟，又何人迫之自言耶？
