    The Historian says: Strange encounters occur only in the realm of family and social relationships, and they can evoke tears, songs, sorrow, and joy all at once, stirring all seven emotions. One truly cannot fathom the reasons behind such occurrences. When people who are separated by vast distances come together through the bonds of kinship, their greatest joy often arises from their greatest suffering, and their greatest suffering can paradoxically bring them even greater joy. A childless man from afar is fortunate to meet Mulan, the young woman who has lost her father, and he carves a wooden figure to serve as a substitute for a child. How skillful is this, when one has nothing but a wooden carving? Or, if it is like the case of Qin and Yue sharing a boat, who would force such a situation to come to light?

    ○　绣舄

    德安庄士玉，善女红，暇辄为细君缉履。落梅数瓣，妍艳如生。有窃闻其事者，每笑谓京兆眉妩，犹非钟情之至焉。一夕，就烛经营。夜已过半，乃置业履于牖上，同觅黑甜。翌日晨起，求之弗得，遂疑偷儿攫取。而室中都无所失，因而骇然，即亦不甚介意。及昏，与细君共语其异，忽梁上一物，疾如鸟隼，扑面飞来。亟视之，坠于榻上，则所绣之舄也。附以片纸，细楷簪花，庄阅之，则七言一绝，曰：“故抛象管弄银针，织尽文房几许心。自是深情怜一瓣，讵知寸趾价千金。”语意似讥细君之足下，而不知谁实为之者。

    Zhuang Shiyu of De'an was skilled in needlework and often made shoes for his beloved wife during his leisure time. The embroidery was so delicate and beautiful that it seemed to come to life. Some who heard of this would laugh and say that even the beauty of the governor's eyebrows paled in comparison to his deep affection. One evening, he worked by the light of a candle. By the time night had passed halfway, he placed his unfinished shoe on the window sill and went to sleep. The next morning, he could not find it and suspected that a thief had taken it. However, nothing else was missing from the room, which left him bewildered, though he did not dwell on it for long. Later that evening, he discussed the strange incident with his wife, and suddenly, an object flew down from the ceiling, swift as a falcon, and hit him in the face. He quickly looked and saw that it was the embroidered shoe he had been making. Attached to it was a small piece of paper with delicate calligraphy, and it contained a seven-character verse: "Thus, I abandon the brush to play with the needle, weaving the heart of the study room. It is only with deep affection that one cherishes a single petal, how could one know that a small foot is worth a thousand pieces of gold?" The words seemed to mock his wife's feet, but he did not know who had actually written them.

    850

    庄亦付之一笑，然心则窃窃然不满于闺中之履矣。乃细君固不觉，惟以怪异为惧。明日遂托故归宁，久而不复，务俟迁乔而后返，良亦有挟而然。庄不听，独处一室，辄祝曰：“寸趾者，肯容人一视乎？”言之数四，并闻梁上笑曰：“赤足婢似亦可好，奚用纤纤者为？”庄愕然，早见绣履半弯，缒梁而下，尖瘦如初生之月，束以鲛绡，缠以锦带，度之不及三寸，洵为尤物。且自下而上，褶裤俨然，皆以薄縠，愈足令人魂销。庄既得其一斑，益思窥乎全豹，又祝之。旋闻娇音哂曰：“措大亦浑不解事。此正动人情处也，奈何务睹其全耶？”庄益哀恳，转瞬径下，则一二八丽人。绿鬓双欹，玉容百媚，信非人世所易有。

    Explanation

    850

    庄亦付之一笑，然心则窃窃然不满于闺中之履矣。乃细君固不觉，惟以怪异为惧。明日遂托故归宁，久而不复，务俟迁乔而后返，良亦有挟而然。庄不听，独处一室，辄祝曰：“寸趾者，肯容人一视乎？”言之数四，并闻梁上笑曰：“赤足婢似亦可好，奚用纤纤者为？”庄愕然，早见绣履半弯，缒梁而下，尖瘦如初生之月，束以鲛绡，缠以锦带，度之不及三寸，洵为尤物。且自下而上，褶裤俨然，皆以薄縠，愈足令人魂销。庄既得其一斑，益思窥乎全豹，又祝之。旋闻娇音哂曰：“措大亦浑不解事。此正动人情处也，奈何务睹其全耶？”庄益哀恳，转瞬径下，则一二八丽人。绿鬓双欹，玉容百媚，信非人世所易有。

    Translation:

    Zhuang laughed it off, but secretly he was dissatisfied with the shoes worn by women inside the home. His wife, however, was unaware of his dissatisfaction and only feared the strange occurrences. The next day, she used an excuse to return to her parents' home and did not return for a long time, waiting until she had risen in social status before coming back, which was indeed a calculated move. Zhuang did not listen to her and stayed alone in a room, often making a wish: "Would the delicate feet allow someone to take a look?" He repeated this several times, and he heard laughter from above the beam: "A barefoot maid seems quite good too, why bother with the slender ones?" Zhuang was startled and soon saw embroidered shoes half-bent, descending from the beam. The toes were sharp and slender, like the new moon, tied with a fine, translucent silk fabric and wrapped with a brocade belt, measuring less than three inches, truly a rare beauty. From below upwards, the pleated trousers were clearly visible, all made of thin gauze, which only added to his enchantment. Having seen a glimpse of this, Zhuang wanted to see more, so he made another wish. Soon, he heard a playful voice say: "This scholar doesn't understand anything. This is the most touching part, why must you see the whole?" Zhuang became even more desperate and, in a moment, a beautiful woman descended. Her green hair was slightly disheveled, her face was charming in many ways, truly not easily found in the world.

    851

    回念其妻，不啻藁矢矣。因逊之坐，微叩由来。丽人低鬟不答，徐曰：“君只合与蠢东施捉足捧履耳，又乌知天上西子哉？”庄亦笑曰：“西子果肯枉屈，亦愿效圯上之故事。”遂相与鼓掌。款洽许时，丽人绝不羞涩，经投庄怀，任其缓带解衣，扶入衾底。一时好合之乐，有倍胜于伉俪之欢者。事讫，丽人解所御之履，以赠庄曰：“留此好样，倘值笔墨微暇，可为妾一掺针黹也。”言已，奋身而上，倏不见。庄玩其履，则以五纹织成者，虽纤嫋如锥，亦邻于敝屣矣。庄会其意，为更制之，且备极工巧。甫成而果至，出以与之，欣然有喜色。是日欢好倍初，天明易之以去。

    Explanation

    851

    Thinking back to his wife, he felt no different from a straw arrow. He invited her to sit down and asked about her background in a subtle manner. The beautiful woman lowered her head without answering, then slowly said, "You are only fit for menial tasks like Dongshi, how could you know the heavenly Xi Shi?" The man also laughed and said, "If Xi Shi were to deign to lower herself, I would also be willing to follow the story of Zhang Liang." They laughed and clapped hands together. They spent a considerable time in friendly conversation, and the woman showed no shyness. She threw herself into his arms, let him loosen his belt and undress, and helped him into the bed. The joy of their union was far surpassing the joy of a married couple. After the deed was done, the woman unfastened the shoes she was wearing and gave them to him, saying, "Keep this as a good example. If you have a moment to spare with your writing, you can embroider for me a little." She leaped up and disappeared suddenly. The man examined her shoes, which were made of five-colored silk, though thin and delicate like a needle, yet close to being a worn-out shoe. Understanding her intention, he made new ones for her, and made them with great skill. As soon as they were finished, she returned, presented them to him, and was delighted with a happy expression. That day, their joy was even greater than before, and at dawn, she exchanged them and left.

    852

    复嘱曰：“妾为君跋涉不易，每来即纳履踵决。君能时易以新，则朝夕可以相晤已。”庄诺之。由是屏绝外务，并日而营，惟恐其不逮。旬日之间，丽人之所得已不止五眘之多。而卜宅之举益杳，细君不能待，乃旋归。见庄，忽大惊曰：“别未匝月，何形神之困悴一至于此！”讳之，坚不肯言。细君搜诸床，得只履，迥非故物。试之，方枘圆凿，果不相入，盖即丽人所遗者。不禁勃然，遂至脱辐。细君投此履于火，且以死要庄，始移居。乃庄自此抱恙，渐以不起，未半年而卒。后邑人误开古圹，有牝狐疾驰而遁。众视之，其中衣奁备具，敝簏存屦数双，制度精妙。好事者袖归示人，则皆庄之手制也。

    Explanation

    852

    Repeatedly instructed, she said, "It has not been easy for me to travel for you. Each time you come, my shoes fall apart at the heels. If you can change them to new ones regularly, then we can meet every day." Zhuang agreed. Therefore, he cut himself off from external affairs and worked day and night, fearing that he would not succeed. Within ten days, the beautiful woman's gains were already more than five times the original amount. However, the plan to choose a house became even more elusive, and Fine Lady could not wait, so she returned home. When she saw Zhuang, she was suddenly greatly surprised and said, "Since we parted, it has not been a full month, and how could your body and spirit be so exhausted?" He avoided the topic and firmly refused to speak about it. Fine Lady searched the bed and found one shoe, which was not the old one. When tested, it was like a square peg in a round hole and did not fit. It was indeed the one left behind by the beautiful woman. She could not help but become very angry and became furious. Fine Lady threw the shoe into the fire and threatened to die if he did not comply. He finally moved to a new residence. Zhuang then fell ill and gradually could not get up, dying within half a year. Later, a villager accidentally opened an ancient tomb, and a female fox fled quickly. The crowd looked at it, and inside, there were complete sets of clothing and accessories, an old basket containing several pairs of shoes with exquisite designs. A curious person secretly took them and showed them to others, revealing that they were all made by Zhuang's hand.

    853

    外史氏曰：代闺人制履，韵事也，而卒罹非常之祸者，岂以巨屦小屦亦有利害之殊，而纤纤莲炬方在掌中，渺渺幽魂旋归泉下，曾不如大脚阿婆屦，为之而无患耶？东昏为玉儿做步步金莲，竟以之亡国，况其下焉者乎？人之沾沾于大小肥瘦之分者，其亦庄生之续也已。

    Explanation

    853

    The historian said: Making shoes for a woman is an elegant affair, yet those who ultimately suffer extraordinary calamities, is it because there is a significant difference in the consequences of making large and small shoes, while the delicate feet are still in her hands, the faint, distant spirit returns to the grave, is it not better to make shoes for an older woman with larger feet, without any risk? Emperor Donghun made gold lotus shoes for his concubine, and ultimately, it led to the downfall of the state. How much more so for those below him? Those who are preoccupied with distinctions of size and shape, they are also continuing the legacy of Zhuangzi.

    854

    ○　舆中人
    　　京师轮蹄络绎，行者多以舆，故虽曲巷筚门，皆有车辙马迹。而绣阁之姝，画屏之艳，其出也，香车宝马固不外需，下此者则非赁不能矣。有某公子者，性佻达，尤好冶游，遇色如集膻之蝇，辄营营不去。父死，益无赖。窃虑美人之隔重帷，嚬笑不能亲炙，乃与所交恶少谋，变服为人执鞭。凡少艾之登车降舆，纤腰弱体，眉黛唇朱，无不以目淫之。而貌之妍媸，妆之浓淡，又不时津津齿颊，僦车者固不及知也。丙子夏，又乘人之车如市。方将觅缘以饱盞目，俄有一老妪蹀躞而来，口中且絮语曰：“地将廿里，青蚨只百文，谁肯载之往耶？”瞥见公子之车，即呼曰：“赁舆！”公子询以所之，答曰：“八里庄去上新冢，往返皆汝车，只载一小娘子，盍行乎？”公子笑曰：“个老姥止叙家常，值果如何耶？”妪曰：“不亏汝，整百黄边，可肯一往？”公子少之。妪颦蹙转身，色似重有忧者。

    Explanation

    854

    车中人

    京城中车马络绎不绝，行人都以乘坐马车为常见，因此即便是狭窄的小巷和简陋的门户，也都有车辙马蹄的痕迹。而那些住在绣阁中的美人，画屏中的艳丽女子，出行时自然需要香车宝马，而那些地位较低的人则只能租用马车。有一位公子，性格轻浮，尤其喜欢寻欢作乐，见到美女就像苍蝇见到腐肉，总是不停地追逐。父亲去世后，他更加放荡不羁。他私下里担心美人被重重帷幕隔绝，无法亲自见到她们的颦笑，于是与他交往的几个恶少商量，改换服装去为人赶车。凡是年轻美貌的女子登上车或下车时，他都会用眼睛流连忘返，对她们的美貌、妆容浓淡无不津津乐道，而租车的人自然不会知道他的心思。丙子年夏天，他又像往常一样随意乘坐别人的车。正当他寻找机会以满足自己的色欲时，突然有一位老妇人蹒跚而来，口中絮絮叨叨地说：“路程有二十里，只收一百文钱，谁愿意载我去呢？”他瞥见公子的车，立刻喊道：“租我的车！”公子询问她要去哪里，老妇人回答：“我要去八里庄上新坟，往返都由你的车，只载一个年轻女子，为何不去呢？”公子笑道：“这位老妇人只是闲聊，价钱是多少呢？”老妇人说：“不亏待你，一百文钱，愿意去吗？”公子觉得价钱太低，老妇人皱着眉头转身离开，脸上似乎带着深深的忧虑。

    855

    公子自思，小娘子必新寡未久者，予虽阅人多，究未能通笑语，今遇此弗可失也。乃呼曰：“姥转来，车赁矣！”妪始欣然引之往。曲折数巷，方抵其家，亦未辨为何里。窥之，墙卑室浅，娇语嘤嘤，不免时达于外，盖不过二旬人也。妪入有顷，以楮锭出，又复施裀车内。蹒跚往来，而珊珊之步已至矣。睨之，容如桃绽，肤似脂凝，缟衣玄裳，果一新孀佳丽，益窃喜。妇流盼，谓妪曰：“好看家，慎启闭，日晡即归矣！”妪笑诺，阖扉自入。

    Explanation

    855

    The young master thought to himself, "This young lady must be newly widowed for a short time. Though I have seen many people, I still cannot understand their laughter and conversation. Now that I have met this, I cannot miss this opportunity." He then called out, "Grandma, please come back, the carriage is rented!" The old woman happily led him there. They turned down several alleys before finally arriving at her home, though he could not tell which lane it was. Looking around, he saw low walls and shallow rooms, with soft and tender whispers that could not help but reach the outside, indicating that the young lady was no more than twenty years old. After a while, the old woman returned, gave out paper money, and laid down a mat in the carriage. She stumbled back and forth, and the graceful steps of the young lady had arrived. Glancing at her, he saw her face was like a blooming peach, her skin like solidified fat, wearing white clothes and black robes, indeed a newly widowed beautiful lady, and he secretly rejoiced even more. The woman cast her eyes around and said to the old woman, "Watch the house, be careful with opening and closing, I will return at dusk!" The old woman smiled and agreed, then closed the door and entered.

    856

    妇始登舆，且谓公子曰：“若勿驰骋，予质荏弱，不任颠播也。”其音娇脆如新莺，公子心益大动。私计曰：“如此孑然，谅非大家闺闼。若至野劫之以力，事当可成。”因即辕端小憩，微叩之曰：“娘子所往者，先人之丘墓乎？”答曰：“亡夫之冢耳！”又问曰：“殁几时矣？”答曰：“百日犹未周也。”语次，渐以亲狎，口脂之香，咫尺飞越。未出皃阇，公子之情已荡矣。比及郊垌，道路岐出，公子故熟识幽径，乃策驷就之。妇在车中睹认良久，骇曰：“误矣！此非予往来之故道也。

    Explanation

    856

    The woman had just mounted the carriage and said to the young man, "Please do not gallop; my body is frail and weak, and I cannot bear the jolting and shaking." Her voice was delicate and crisp like a young oriole, which made the young man's heart even more excited. He thought to himself, "Such a solitary person surely does not come from a noble family. If we reach the countryside, I could force her, and the matter would be successful." He then leaned against the front of the carriage to rest slightly and gently asked her, "Where is the lady going? To the graves of one's ancestors?" She replied, "Only to my deceased husband's grave!" He asked again, "How long has he been dead?" She answered, "Not even a hundred days have passed." During the conversation, they gradually became more intimate, and the fragrance of her lip balm spread within arm's length. Before they had left the outskirts, the young man's heart was already agitated. By the time they reached the outskirts, the road branched off. The young man knew a secluded path well and drove the carriage along it. The woman in the carriage recognized it after a long time and was startled, saying, "It's a mistake! This is not the usual path I take."

    857

    ”公子答曰：“若勿言，此固捷且便者，若何由知之？”仍共语，言渐涉邪。妇亦不拒，惟以微笑答之。已而潜索其手，则以纤腕承迎；戏牵其衣，则以轻裾暱就。公子之欲盛炽，正思请间为欢，妇忽颦黛自语曰：“可若何？”有顷又云。公子询之，妇笑曰：“此事不可令人知，然而不得不告。予匆遽出门，未遑如厕，今小腹胀痛矣。若视有僻地可停车。”公子喜适其愿，笑而颔之。

    Explanation

    857

    The young nobleman replied, "If you don't speak of it, this method is indeed quick and convenient. How could I know?" They continued to converse, and their words gradually became more intimate. The woman did not resist, only responding with a smile. Soon, she subtly reached out for his hand, and he gently took it in her slender wrist. When he playfully tugged at her clothes, she responded by moving closer, her light skirt brushing against him. The young nobleman's desire burned intensely, and he was just thinking of seeking a private moment for intimacy. The woman suddenly frowned and spoke to herself, "What should we do?" After a short while, she said something else. The young nobleman asked her what she meant. She laughed and said, "This matter should not be known by others, but I have to tell you. I rushed out without time to use the bathroom and now feel abdominal pain. If you see a secluded place where we can stop the carriage, that would be good." The young nobleman was pleased and nodded in agreement.

    858

    须臾，至一林，密叶周匝，四无人迹，遂叱控而止，顾曰：“娘子请往，予不能从也。”妇下车，周围审视，谓曰：“地颇险恶，予心惴惴然。若可偕往，远立似亦无妨。”因以横波示意，公子大悦，径从之。妇至茂树下，忽变声，谓公子曰：“汝视我犹为可憎耶？”亟睹其面，则巨口劖牙，目光如炬，固一生夜叉也。骇极而奔，夜叉奋臂击之，应手而仆。犹是柔荑，已不啻巨灵之掌。夜叉搏之如匹雏，缚之于树，叱之曰：“汝父一生宦达，生汝不肖，目不睹诗书，惟覸人闺闼，律应眇。

    Explanation

    858

    In a moment, they arrived at a forest, with dense foliage surrounding all around, and no signs of human presence. He then shouted and stopped, turning and saying, "Please go ahead, Lady; I cannot follow." The woman got off the carriage, carefully examining the surroundings, and said, "The place is quite dangerous, and my heart is filled with anxiety. If you can go together, standing far away seems also harmless." She indicated with a sideways glance, and the young master was very pleased, going straight with her. When she reached the dense tree, she suddenly changed her voice and said to the young master, "Do you still find me repulsive?" He quickly saw her face, which had a large mouth with sharp teeth and eyes like torches—it was indeed a demon. Frightened to the extreme, he ran away, but the demon swung his arm and hit him, causing him to fall to the ground immediately. Her hands, still gentle, had transformed into something no less than the hand of a giant. The demon grabbed him like a young bird, tied him to the tree, and shouted at him, "Your father was successful in his career all his life, but you are unworthy. You do not read poetry or books, and you only peek into women's chambers. The law should blind you."

    859

    ”于是出腰间白刃，刳其左眶，乌珠随手而出。夜叉啖之如食蔗，公子嘶鸣。夜叉又叱之曰：“汝既目染，又复口污，律当哑。但留舌为人言，宜去唇以代。”因割其吻，公子益号。夜叉再叱之曰：“汝有手，文不能握管，武不能张弓，而甘心为之仆御，辱已甚矣，律合去其一。看若能扬鞭得意耶？”乃断其右腕，公子之袖尽殷。夜叉乃鼓掌笑曰：“闺人之耻，予今为之一洒矣。”言已，复化为美妇，敛衽而拜曰：“累君相载，媿无以酬。

    Explanation

    859

    "Then, drawing a blade from his waist, he cut open the left eye socket, and the eyeball came out immediately. The night demon ate it as if savoring sugarcane, and the young man cried out in pain. The night demon then scolded him, saying, 'Since you have stained your eyes, and further defiled your mouth, by law you should be made mute. But to leave your tongue for speaking, your lips should be removed as a substitute.' He then cut off his lips, and the young man cried out even more. The night demon scolded him again, saying, 'You have hands, yet you cannot hold a writing brush, nor draw a bow. And you willingly accept being a servant, disgracing yourself to an extreme degree. By law, one of your limbs must be removed. Can you whip with satisfaction?' He then cut off his right wrist, and the young man's sleeve was stained with blood. The night demon clapped and laughed, saying, 'The shame of women, I have now avenged for you.' Having spoken, he transformed back into a beautiful woman and bowed, saying, 'I have troubled you with my transport, and I am ashamed that I have nothing to repay you.'"

    860

    但归途迢递，孱弱难行，车马可暂假也。”竟出林升舆，控纵而去，莫知其所往。公子痛深创鉅，号救无人，始自悔所为之谬。既而剥肤日烈，枵腹雷鸣，愈知自艾。幸有行客数人至，疾声呼之。众至其前，咸惊怪。公子缅述颠末，靡不骇然，乃解其缚束，送之归。公子自是以疾废，然而狂态尽敛，醇谨老诚，非复向之轻薄子矣！后二年，又行遇一妇，仿佛共载者，而未敢复视也。闻妇遥语曰：“我舆中人也，君能改行从善，寿亦增长矣！”公子大惊，视之竟茫无所见。

    　　外史氏曰：狂且之恶，王法所不能禁。非不禁也，私皒之辱，身受者且不能知，又乌可绳以国家之典哉？幸活菩萨现生夜叉之身，刳之割之，卒能使之回心向善，不终于荡检逾闲，真具大慈悲者矣。舆中人乎，闺中人咸当绣象以祀之。

    Explanation

    860

    But the return journey was long and arduous, making it difficult to travel due to weakness," the young nobleman said. He finally emerged from the forest, mounted the carriage, and drove away, no one knowing where he went. The young nobleman suffered greatly, called for help in vain, and began to regret his mistake. Soon after, his skin was stripped off daily, his stomach growled like thunder, and he became more aware of his self-pity. Fortunately, several travelers arrived and shouted loudly for him. When they reached him, they were all surprised and puzzled. The young nobleman recounted his story in detail, and no one was not shocked. They then untied him and sent him back home. From then on, the young nobleman became ill and was unable to move, but his wild behavior was completely reined in, and he became sober, cautious, and honest, no longer the same frivolous person. Two years later, he met a woman again, who seemed to be the same one he had traveled with, but he dared not look at her again. He heard the woman say from afar, "I am the woman in the carriage. If you can change your ways and do good, your life will be extended!" The young nobleman was greatly surprised, but when he looked around, he saw nothing.

    The historian says: The wickedness of the reckless and wild cannot be stopped by the law. Not that it cannot be stopped, but the shame of private disgrace, even those who suffer it may not know, how can it be punished according to the state's laws? Fortunately, the living Buddha appeared in the form of a demon, cutting and slicing, eventually making him turn his heart to good, not ending in unrestrained behavior, truly a great and compassionate being. The woman in the carriage, the women in the inner chambers should all embroider images to worship her.

    861

    ○　庞眉叟
    　　闽臬陈公，治绩素著。有幕下生曰卢某，实左右之，公亦敬礼弗衰，时时与同寝食。卢年未三旬，断狱精严，令人无从置喙。公之僚属，罔不诚服，卢遂傲睨一世。适邵武来一异人，庞姓芝名，字眉叟，寿登七秩而少好如弱冠。挟有异术，能与鬼神言，悉生人去来事。某太守荐之于公，遂驿送来省。其言如响，公甚尊信之，亦致之幕中。庞既入署，得与卢遇，辄数数目之，色若愀然。公疑焉，然以卢素倨慢，待庞无加礼，故如此，亦不甚介意。一夕与庞夜坐，闻鬼声甚惨，似在左近。公与侍者皆间，因骇然以询庞。庞笑曰：“此卢生之夙孽耳，于公固无害也。”公叩之，初不肯言，诘之再三，乃曰：“公忆蒲葵扇之已事乎？”公愕然曰：“颇忆之，而非予任内事也。”庞曰：“公虽未逮，卢生实司其事。

    Explanation

    861

    Translation:

    The Elder with Thick Eyebrows

    The Governor of Fujian, Mr. Chen, was renowned for his administrative achievements. Among his entourage was a scholar named Lu, who actually assisted him closely. Mr. Chen showed him respect without diminishing, often sharing meals and lodging with him. Lu, not yet thirty years old, judged cases with precision and strictness, leaving no room for comment. All of Mr. Chen's subordinates were sincerely convinced, and Lu thus looked down on the world. Just then, a remarkable individual came from Shaowu, surnamed Pang, given name Zhi, courtesy name Mousuo. Though he had reached the age of seventy, he still looked like a young man in his twenties. He possessed unusual skills, could communicate with spirits and deities, and knew all about the comings and goings of living people. A certain governor recommended him to Mr. Chen, who then sent him by post to the provincial capital. His words were as if echoing, and Mr. Chen very respected and trusted him, also bringing him into the entourage. After Pang entered the office, he met with Lu, and always looked at him repeatedly, his expression as if serious or solemn. Mr. Chen doubted it, but because Lu was usually arrogant and disrespectful, treated Pang without extra respect, so he did so, and was also not very concerned. One night, while sitting up late at night with Pang, he heard very sad ghostly sounds, seeming nearby. Mr. Chen and his attendants were all startled, and then startled and asked Pang. Pang smiled and said, "This is Lu's past karma; certainly no harm to the official." Mr. Chen asked him, and at first he would not speak. Questioned him repeatedly, then said, "Do you remember the past event of the palm and osmanthus fan?" Mr. Chen was startled and said, "I vaguely remember it, but it was not within my jurisdiction." Pang said, "Although you did not handle it, Lu was actually in charge of the matter."

    862

    渠以片言致戕二命，昨阎摩既已发勘，祸将作矣。”公询其期，答以三日。公遂默然不乐，然犹将信将疑也。侍人有善卢者，闻而惊，转以相告。时卢正因恶警，神思弗宁，闻之，益更怵惕。遂治具邀庞，敬求良策。庞已觉侍者漏言，坚辞不赴。卢乃移樽过庞，仪节谦恭，笑语款洽，迥非向之倨侮者，庞亦窃笑不言。酒酣，卢以言挑之曰：“君居闽地，亦知蒲葵扇一狱谁实断之乎？”庞佯笑曰：“此前司某公事耳！君言及之，当必有故。”卢因忾然曰：“予时已司刀笔，就事论情，推见至隐，乃人或以为口实，心殊不解。”庞闻此言，遂变色而作曰：“君至今日尚犹以为精当耶？二屈吁冤，九阍震怒，君之危若朝露，顾乃诩诩如此哉？”卢大怖，离席而伏，一时侍者皆惊愕。盖闽中素矜男色，诗礼之家生子而美，其防闲尤甚于闺人。某县一巨绅，生子女各一，皆有殊色。绅故崇尚名教，闲之维则。

    Explanation

    862

    He caused the deaths of two lives with a single word, and yesterday, Yama, the god of death, had already issued a judgment, disaster is about to occur." The official asked for the time, and he answered with three days. The official then became silent and unhappy, but still hesitated between belief and doubt. There was a servant who was skilled in Lu, who heard it in surprise and relayed it to others. At that time, Lu was troubled by bad news, and his mind was unsettled; upon hearing it, he became even more fearful and vigilant. He then prepared a feast to invite Pang, respectfully seeking good advice. Pang had already sensed that the servant had leaked the information, and firmly declined to attend. Lu then moved the wine cup to visit Pang, with respectful etiquette, smiling and chatting warmly, quite different from the previous arrogant and contemptuous behavior, and Pang also secretly smiled without speaking. When the wine was flowing freely, Lu provoked him with words saying, "You who live in Fujian, do you know who actually judged the case of the palm leaf fan?" Pang feigned a laugh and said, "It was a matter handled by a certain official before! You mention it, there must be a reason." Lu then said with a sigh, "At that time, I was responsible for the writing of legal documents, discussing matters based on circumstances, and inferring the most hidden details, but people sometimes used it as an excuse, which I found particularly incomprehensible." When Pang heard these words, he immediately changed color and said, "Do you still think it is precise and appropriate today? Two wronged people cried out for justice, and the nine gates of heaven were enraged, your danger is like morning dew, yet you still act so boastfully!" Lu was greatly frightened, left his seat and crouched down, at that moment, all the servants were shocked. In Fujian, people have always been proud of male beauty, in families that value poetry and propriety, the prevention of male beauty is even stricter than for women. A prominent gentleman in a certain county had one son and one daughter, both of whom were exceptionally beautiful. The gentleman naturally respected the teachings of morality and propriety, and strictly controlled them.

    863

    男女既及冠笄，尚犹中门未出，家之僮仆并未少识乌衣，而红颜者更无论已。一日绅他出，见仆执蒲葵小扇乘凉于门侧，绅亦漠不关心。阅数日，过女闺中，案头适有此物。取视之，上题五言绝，墨迹犹新，而诗鄙俚可笑。绅心犹未甚疑，及诘其女，则曰：“弟适携来，云系某仆者，不知何人所书，读之令人喷饭。父亦曾见之耶？”绅微颔之，而疑乃顿起。时内外隔绝，仆之物无因而至，致以为讶。然思仆之妇服役门中，物或有所自来，遂不复根究。乃父出而子入，姊弟又以为笑柄，评眛许时，女因语弟，使更之。弟初不欲，既而念少年章甫忽等巾帼女流，亦深闭而不得出，不觉抑郁。乃以清水涤其墨，取笔大书一绝曰：“雄飞原有志，雌伏固无妨。倘借春风力，飘摇出画堂。”吟成，姊弟又笑语良久。而惧为父见，遂并是扇藏于中，即绅亦不甚记忆矣。

    863

    Once the young men and women had reached the age of adulthood, they still had not left the inner gate. The household servants had not yet seen many young men in black robes, and the young women were even less likely to have seen such men. One day, the scholar-official went out and saw a servant holding a small fan made of palm leaves cooling off by the gate. The scholar-official was indifferent to this. After several days, he passed by the young woman's inner room and there happened to be this object on the desk. He took it to look at it. The fan had a five-character quatrain written on it, with fresh ink, but the poem was crude and laughable. The scholar-official was still not very suspicious, but when he asked the young woman, she said, "My younger brother just brought it, claiming it belonged to a certain servant. I don't know who wrote it, but reading it makes one spit out one's food. Has Father seen it too?" The scholar-official nodded slightly, but his suspicion suddenly arose. At that time, there was a strict separation between the inner and outer parts of the house, and the servant's object had no reason to appear, which caused surprise. But considering that the servant's wife served in the inner quarters, the object might have come from somewhere, so he did not pursue the matter further. Then the father went out and the son came in, and the siblings took it as a laughing matter, discussing it for a while. The young woman then spoke to her younger brother, asking him to change the poem. The younger brother initially did not want to, but then he thought that young scholars suddenly being equal to women were also confined and could not go out, and he could not help but feel depressed. He then used clear water to wash off the ink and took a brush to write a quatrain saying, "A man's ambition to fly is natural, and a woman's submission is not a hindrance. If he borrows the power of the spring breeze, he can flutter out of the painted hall." When he finished reciting it, the siblings laughed and talked for a long time. But they were afraid the father would see it, so they hid the fan together in the inner quarters. Even the scholar-official did not remember it very clearly.

    864

    明年，绅将有远行，以门客某综理外事，即馆于家，其素所亲暱者也。时值溽暑，蚊聚成雷，客索一物为驱逐，乞之于内。绅子无以应，偶见此扇，即以付之，亦顿忘扇头所书矣。客挥箑竟夜，晨起，仆瞥见之，诧为己物。及读诗则又非是，遂置之。乃客当未冠，时实以色宠于宦，故今犹以家寄托。是日见仆错愕，取扇观之，不觉大惭，遂疑绅子为嘲己，思以报。及绅归，故以扇置其前，且言公子所赠者。绅本有疑，于是见之大恚。客又言公子每夜出，未知焉往，忝在腹心，不得不告。绅益怒，入内呼其子，将施鞭扑。幸女锐身自任，极力辩白，书扇有时，与扇有据，宵行又莫须有之说，绅乃释然，反下令逐客。客遂无颜，鼠窜而去。又逾年，绅遂为其子缔姻于某宦。既纳采矣，客知之，因衔旧怨，携扇造宦，凿凿言之。宦又迂腐异常者，乃以乞书为名，向婿丐字数行。

    Explanation

    864

    Next year, the gentleman would be embarking on a long journey, and he entrusted a certain retainer to manage external affairs, who stayed at his home as a close and familiar companion. It was during the hot and humid season when mosquitoes gathered in such large numbers that their buzzing sounded like thunder. The guest requested something to drive them away and asked for it from inside the house. The gentleman's son had no suitable item to offer, so he happened to see this fan and gave it to the guest, also forgetting what was written on the fan head. The guest waved the fan all night, and in the morning, the servant caught a glimpse of it and was astonished, thinking it was his own. Upon reading the poem on it, he realized it was not his, and so he put it aside. The guest, being unmarried at the time, was favored by officials for his appearance, and thus he was still trusted with the family’s affairs. That day, he saw the servant in a state of shock, took the fan and looked at it, and unconsciously felt deeply ashamed, suspecting that the gentleman’s son was mocking him, and thought to retaliate. When the gentleman returned, the guest placed the fan in front of him and said that it was a gift from the young master. The gentleman originally had doubts, and upon seeing it, he became very angry. The guest also said that the young master went out every night, and no one knew where he went, and since he was trusted as a close confidant, he could not help but report. The gentleman became even angrier, entered the house and called his son, was about to punish him with a whip. Fortunately, the daughter took responsibility for herself, tried her best to defend herself, explained that the poem on the fan was written at a certain time, and there was evidence linking the fan to her, and denied the claim that he goes out at night, which was unfounded. The gentleman then felt relieved and instead ordered the guest to be dismissed. The guest, feeling ashamed, scrambled away like a rat. After a year, the gentleman arranged a marriage for his son with a certain official. After the betrothal gifts had been exchanged, the guest knew about it and, harboring old resentment, carried the fan to the official and stated it firmly. The official was unusually rigid and conservative, and used the excuse of asking for a poem, to ask the son-in-law to write a few lines.

    865

    绅不知，命子与之。宦比观，字迹吻合，竟遣媒妁绝其婚。绅不能平，争论数四，遂涉讼。然在主斯狱者，犹以诗有可解，事有可疑，闻之中丞，藩臬亦命为之调停。而卢适入幕，见之即笑曰：“此地素有此风，已不可长，况绅家而亦为之耶？”因命取扇入署，草书一行于上曰：“既甘雌伏，何必雄飞？其人之品从可知，其人之婚礼宜绝。但存宦体，仰即断离”，云云。绅得此，惭赧无地，归即痛挞其子，逼使成招。子竟无以明，刎脰而死。女恫曰：“予实使弟为之。今若此，是予杀之也。”女遂亦投环。绅救之弗及，气忿成疾，竟以病废。而人犹喧传其丑，罕有识其冤者。是狱已隔数年，卢忽梦己持蒲葵扇，将有所书，身侧一女鬼，项拥素帛，哀哀而哭。及寤，寸心惊悸。又闻人言，故折节于庞，以冀悻免。

    Explanation

    865

    A Scholar-Official's Disgrace

    The scholar-official did not know, and ordered his son to give it to him. The official compared and observed, finding the handwriting to be a perfect match, and ultimately sent matchmakers to break off the marriage. The scholar-official could not accept this, and after arguing several times, the matter ended up in litigation. However, the judge handling the case still believed that the poem had a plausible explanation and that there were suspicious aspects to the matter, and upon hearing this, the Censor-General and the provincial officials also ordered mediation. At this time, Lu happened to enter the office and saw the situation, smiling and saying, "This place has always had this custom, and it cannot be allowed to continue, how much more so for a scholar-official's family to do it?" He then ordered someone to take a fan into the office and wrote a line of cursive script on it, saying, "Since he has already accepted a subordinate position, why bother to strive for a higher position? His character can be judged, and his marriage should be broken off. But maintain the official's dignity, please immediately break off the relationship." Upon receiving this, the scholar-official was so ashamed and embarrassed that he had no place to hide, and upon returning home, he severely beat his son, forcing him to confess. The son could not prove his innocence and committed suicide by cutting his throat. The daughter, in fear, said, "I truly made my brother do it. Now like this, it is as if I killed him." The daughter then also threw herself into a loop. The scholar-official could not save her, and became ill with anger, eventually becoming incapacitated by illness. People still spread the scandal, and rarely anyone understood his injustice. This case had been settled for several years, and Lu suddenly dreamed he was holding a palm-leaf fan, about to write something, when a female ghost appeared beside him, holding a white silk cloth around her neck, crying pitifully. When he woke up, his heart was filled with fear. He also heard people say that he had humbled himself before Pang, in the hope of escaping.

    866

    庞既责之，更力辞曰：“此狱既已定案，亦如君之铁笔，不能动摇。但因造言之客，时近大贵之人，鬼不得前，故君亦少延残喘。今渠已南下，覆舟于江，命无可逃，岂君尚能独缓乎？”言已太息。卢又流涕以请，兼以老母为言。庞不觉侧然曰：“只余一线，君自为之。”乃屏去左右，耳语曰：“某客为鬼所迫，因投于相国门下，乞为亲随，朝暮侍侧，故得苟免至今。刻闻阎君将转生人世，地府缺员，限以三日之期尽结旧案，君能得我公卵翼，同宿数宵，或藉以免亦未可料。此仆泄天地之秘，深有罪愆，明晨亦将他适矣。”卢闻此语，深信弗疑。及陈公退食，即号泣乞救。公诘之，乃具以告。公以咨庞，对曰：“以公之福德，不难庇护此一人，济与弗济，请从之。”公乃慨允，命人移卧具于卢室，与之对奕，直至夜分始寝。果无一事，即鬼声亦不复闻。达旦而起，阶头砌畔俨有绳索痕，侍者皆以为异。

    Explanation

    866

    庞既责之，更力辞曰：“此狱既已定案，亦如君之铁笔，不能动摇。但因造言之客，时近大贵之人，鬼不得前，故君亦少延残喘。今渠已南下，覆舟于江，命无可逃，岂君尚能独缓乎？”言已太息。卢又流涕以请，兼以老母为言。庞不觉侧然曰：“只余一线，君自为之。”乃屏去左右，耳语曰：“某客为鬼所迫，因投于相国门下，乞为亲随，朝暮侍侧，故得苟免至今。刻闻阎君将转生人世，地府缺员，限以三日之期尽结旧案，君能得我公卵翼，同宿数宵，或藉以免亦未可料。此仆泄天地之秘，深有罪愆，明晨亦将他适矣。”卢闻此语，深信弗疑。及陈公退食，即号泣乞救。公诘之，乃具以告。公以咨庞，对曰：“以公之福德，不难庇护此一人，济与弗济，请从之。”公乃慨允，命人移卧具于卢室，与之对奕，直至夜分始寝。果无一事，即鬼声亦不复闻。达旦而起，阶头砌畔俨有绳索痕，侍者皆以为异。

    Translation:
    The judge reprimanded him, and he more vigorously denied, saying, "This case has already been decided, just like your iron pen, and cannot be changed. But because of the guest who spread rumors, who was near a very powerful person, the ghost could not approach, so you also barely managed to survive. Now he has gone south and wrecked his boat on the river, fate cannot be escaped; could you still be spared alone?" After speaking, he sighed. Lu wept and pleaded, also mentioning his elderly mother. The judge unconsciously felt pity and said, "Only a thread is left, do it yourself." He then sent away the attendants and whispered, "A guest was forced by a ghost, so he sought refuge under the Chancellor's protection, asked to be a close attendant, serving day and night at his side, so he managed to avoid trouble until now. Now I hear that the Lord of the Underworld will be reborn in the human world, there is a vacancy in the underworld, with a deadline of three days to settle all old cases. If you can get under my Lord's protective wing and stay together for several nights, perhaps you can also be spared, but it's hard to say. This servant has revealed the secrets of heaven and earth, deeply guilty, by tomorrow morning, I will be elsewhere." Lu heard these words and deeply believed them without doubt. When Chancellor Chen took a break after meals, he immediately cried and begged for help. The Chancellor questioned him, and he fully reported it. The Chancellor consulted the judge, who replied, "Given your Lord's virtue and fortune, it is not difficult to protect this one person. Whether to help or not, please decide." The Chancellor generously agreed, ordered someone to move the bedding to Lu's room, played chess with him, and did not sleep until midnight. Indeed, nothing happened, and even ghostly sounds were no longer heard. Rising at dawn, there were clear rope marks on the steps and paths, and the attendants all found it strange.

    867

    及公出，庞即承迎，将辞归邵武。公留之，坚不受命，不得已而饯之衙斋。临别，庞语卢曰：“剩此两夕，君亦切勿自误。”卢敬诺，庞遂遄发。乃公至次夕，手谈既倦，因与卢坐语。夜半将眠，侍者亦多偷安，俄闻帘钩微动。公固有戒心，亟视之，则黑气两团恍若淡雾，森森然直冲卧内，竖人毛发，视卢已木坐如痴，公骇然，厉声呵逐，其气顿敛，仿佛如人，侍者皆见之。则一男一女，年约十六七，分跪于公之坐侧。公未及询，女即禀白曰：“蒲葵扇一案，公亦当洞鉴其冤。今卢某已罪无可逭，请公出此户，勿庇凶人，反使震公左右。”公时已气馁，强诘其名，则某绅子女者是也。公乃徐曰：“亡之，其命矣夫！”遂起而趋出。卢虽迷惘，犹思挽留，公乃以更衣辞之，亟返内署。

    Explanation

    867

    When the official left, Pang immediately received him, intending to bid farewell and return to Shaowu. The official tried to detain him, but Pang resolutely refused the command. Reluctantly, the official had to see him off at the office. At parting, Pang told Lu, "There are only two more nights left. You too must not make a mistake." Lu respectfully agreed, and Pang set off quickly. Upon returning the next evening, after playing chess became tiring, the official sat and talked with Lu. At midnight, about to sleep, the attendants also took advantage of the situation to rest. Suddenly, the curtain hook slightly moved. The official had a sense of caution and quickly looked at it. Two black masses that seemed like faint mist appeared, straight into the bedroom, making the hair stand on end. Seeing Lu sitting woodenly like a fool, the official was startled and shouted loudly to drive it away. The aura suddenly condensed, seeming like a person. The attendants all saw it. They were kneeling separately on the official's side. Before the official could ask, the female being reported, "The case of the palm leaf fan, the official should also see through its injustice." Now, Lu has committed a crime that cannot be avoided. Please let this person out of the house. Do not shelter a wicked person, otherwise, it will cause trouble for the official's surroundings. At that time, the official was already feeling discouraged and strongly asked for their names. They are the children of a certain gentry family. It is gone, that is its fate. The official got up and hurried out. Even though Lu was confused, he still thought to detain him. The official used the excuse of changing clothes to dismiss him and quickly returned to the inner office.

    868

    少顷，使人睹之，则报曰：“卢先生已长逝矣！”公益震眜，不敢复履其室，惟命纪纲以衣衾敛之。已而询其死状，则长跪中庭，宛如向人乞命者，且口鼻有血痕，及殁而膝犹未伸，筋骸拘挛，遂拳曲而纳于榇。驰书浙右，召其眷属，厚赙之，令载以归。然在公已如失左右手矣。幸即报升方伯，仓卒离任。复遭庞于途，公知其异，挽之晤言。因询卢之所在，并两夕顿异之由，颇加怨怅。庞正色曰：“初某以公之德政绰有可观，故以节钺卜之，谓不妨庇兹小丑。讵意庭训弗严，卢殁之日，公之长君受人暮夜之金，诬一良妇永沉囹圄，上帝因减公之禄籍，以父子至亲故也。以是匿迹之鬼因而现形，且冒眝而不顾。微公知几，亦将抱恙。非某误公，实公之自误耳！今卢已遍历冥罚，转眞人世，不再如向之得意已！”语竟辞去，公甚惄然。时公之冢子已莅任某州，因走伻以书询之，坚讳曰无，公因此郁郁弗快。无何，以公务降补盐道，未任而卒。

    Explanation

    868

    After a short while, the official sent someone to inspect the situation, and they reported back, "Mr. Lu has passed away!" The official was deeply shocked and could not enter the room again. He only ordered a servant to wrap the body in clothes and shroud it. Later, when he inquired about the circumstances of Mr. Lu's death, he found that Mr. Lu was kneeling in the middle of the courtyard, as if he were pleading for his life, with blood marks on his mouth and nose. By the time he had died, his knees were still bent, and his limbs were stiff and contracted, so he was placed in the coffin in a curled position. The official quickly sent a letter to Zhejiang, summoning Mr. Lu's family, and gave them a generous funeral gift, ordering them to carry his body back. However, for the official, it felt as if he had lost a trusted aide or advisor. Fortunately, he was soon promoted to the position of governor and left his previous post in a hurry. On the road, he encountered a mysterious figure named Pang, whom he recognized as unusual, and invited him to speak. He asked about Mr. Lu's whereabouts and the sudden changes in the situation, expressing frustration and disappointment. Pang spoke sternly, "At first, I thought your virtue and governance were admirable, so I predicted that you would not be harmed by such a lowly person. But I did not expect that your eldest son, on the day Mr. Lu died, accepted a bribe at night, falsely accusing an innocent woman and imprisoning her forever. Because of this, God reduced your official rank, as a punishment for your close relationship with him. That is why the ghost, who had hidden his identity, appeared, and did not care about the consequences. If you had not known the signs, you would have fallen ill too. It was not me who misled you, but you who made a mistake yourself! Now, Mr. Lu has endured all punishments in the afterlife and has been reborn in the world, and he will no longer be as successful as before!" After finishing his speech, Pang left, and the official was deeply distressed. At that time, the official's eldest son had already taken up a post in a certain state, so the official sent a messenger with a letter to inquire about the matter, but his son stubbornly denied it, saying there was nothing. The official was thus deeply depressed and unhappy. Soon after, he was demoted to a lower position and died before taking up the new role.

    869

    外史氏曰：甚哉，福必以德，而后能神钦而鬼伏也。陈公为子减禄，鬼即相逼而来。德者福之基，可不务乎？至蒲葵一扇，虽可招风，苟非卢专主，其事亦未必无由昭雪。任性失出尚当显罹阳刑，仅受冥罚，犹为侥佯。惟阎君亦转生人世，当亦授何职，殊属不经。每欲起眉叟而问之，又虑九天十天莫定所居，亦徒怀索解之意而已。

    Explanation

    869

    The historian says: How great is the truth that blessings must come from virtue, and only then can the gods revere and the ghosts submit. When Chen reduced his own salary for his son, the ghosts immediately came to press their demands. Virtue is the foundation of good fortune; how can one neglect it? As for a fan made of the leaves of the Pithaya tree, though it can bring cooling, if it is not handled by the proper authority, the matter may still not be cleared without reason. Even if a decision was made out of personal whim and should have been punished in the human world, it was only met with afterlife punishment, which is still a侥幸 (侥幸 means侥幸, which is a lucky escape). Only the Lord of the Underworld was reborn into the human world; what position would he be given? This is truly extraordinary. I often wish to ask the wise old man for an explanation, but I fear that the heavens and the highest levels of heaven are uncertain, so I can only harbor the desire to seek understanding.

    870

    ○　诗妖
    　　济南汤敬一，素业诗，所作有杜陵风致。一时苦吟之士，皆慕其名，每获佳篇，如逢拱璧。汶上李子，藏其稿百余首，非契友罕得一观。然李之所为，不第绝不似杜，抑且绝不似汤。呓语盲词，读者欲呕。而李辄大言曰：“汤以杜为师，予以汤为师。古今诗学之传，只此一线，区区元白，直将奴隶视之矣！”同袍者无不窃笑。一日诵汤吟草，方且狂呼，闻有人笑曰：“汤居杜下，子实高出杜上，何为击节如此？”李大喜。视之，见一物挺立于前，巨角劖牙，高逼屋梁，形状丑恶，骇极欲仆。强叱之，物忽不见。乃由是李益自负，舍汤与杜，别成一家言。且大书于门曰：“子美若生应下拜，敬一虽在敢齐驱。”见者益嗤其狂妄。忽于深夜有二八少女款李之斋，李启户谛观，貌绝妖冶，而态更骀荡不群。

    Explanation

    870

    Poetic Monster

    Tang Jingyi, who has always been devoted to poetry, possesses a style reminiscent of Du Fu's Du Ling style. Those who toil over their poetry all admire his name. Whenever they obtain a fine poem, it is as precious as a jade disc. A man from Weishang named Li collected over a hundred of Tang's drafts, but only close friends could see them. However, what Li did was not only unlike Du Fu, but also unlike Tang. His nonsense and blind words were nauseating. Yet Li would boast, saying, "Tang took Du Fu as his teacher, and I took Tang as my teacher. The tradition of poetic learning has only this one line, and Yuan Zhen and Bai Juyi are nothing more than slaves to it!" Those who knew him well would secretly laugh. One day, while reciting Tang's poetry, Li was about to shout in approval when he heard someone laugh and say, "Tang is below Du Fu, but you are actually above Du Fu. Why are you applauding so much?" Li was overjoyed. Looking around, he saw a creature standing before him, with huge horns and sharp teeth, towering almost to the ceiling, its appearance ugly and terrifying. He was so frightened that he nearly fainted. He tried to shout it away, and the creature suddenly disappeared. From then on, Li became even more arrogant, abandoning Tang and Du Fu to create his own style. He boldly wrote on his door, "If Du Fu were alive, he would have to bow down; even though Tang is present, he dares to compete with him." Those who saw this only laughed at his arrogance and foolishness. Suddenly, one night, a sixteen-year-old girl knocked on Li's study door. Li opened the door and looked closely, and she was incredibly beautiful and charming, with an air of grace that was beyond compare.

    871

    私心爱慕，延之入，叩所自来，答曰：“儿家居浣花溪畔，与草堂为邻，昨自蜀中流寓山左。生平酷嗜杜诗，欲得仿佛者而事之。闻君子又高出杜上，倘赐一律，愿侍终身。”言已，敛衽而拜。李愈大悦，遽请命题。女袖出红巾，色甚娇艳，随铺于几上曰：“以此代楮，即景可赋也。”李不自揣，信笔涂鸦。甫一起，女即颦蹙，连唤奈何。次句复然。竟不俟其终篇，揽巾入袖，曰：“坏儿家罗帕矣！此等劣句，只宜以厕中败纸书之。所谓翘然杜上者，竟如此耶？”李大惭。心虽忿忿，然因爱不暇为嗔，反笑而谢过。女意似流连，徐曰：“君欲学步篇章，盍与妾共处三五夕，或可成诗。否则，遗矢地上，犬且置之矣。”李愈抱愧，而幸其下榻，遂无一言，惟促之解衣，两相欢好。及寝，李忽顿忆前事，因举鬼物所赞者，聊以解嘲。女乃戏握其具，曰：“君不知耶？渠言高出肚上者，殆即此耳！”李亦大悟，不觉失笑。女虽鄙李之才，而情意浃洽，备极缱绻。

    Explanation

    871

    Secretly admiring her, he invited her in and asked where she came from. She replied, "I live by the banks of the Huaxia Creek, next to a thatched cottage. I came here from Sichuan, a refugee in the east. I have always been deeply fond of Du Fu's poetry and wish to serve someone who resembles him. I have heard that you claim to be superior to Du Fu. If you could grant me a poem, I would be willing to serve you for life." Having spoken, she bowed with clasped hands. Li was greatly pleased and immediately asked for a topic. The woman took out a red handkerchief from her sleeve, which was very beautiful, and laid it on the table, saying, "Use this to write on; you can compose a poem based on the scene." Li, without hesitation, began to write carelessly. Just as he started the first line, the woman frowned and called out in distress. The second line was the same. She did not wait for him to finish the entire poem, took the handkerchief and put it in her sleeve, saying, "You've ruined my handkerchief! Such poor lines should only be written on toilet paper. Those who claim to be superior to Du Fu, are they really like this?" Li was deeply embarrassed. Though he was angry in his heart, he did not have the time to be angry due to his affection for her, and instead laughed and apologized. The woman seemed to linger, slowly saying, "If you want to learn how to compose poetry, why don't you stay with me for three or five nights? Perhaps you can write a good poem. Otherwise, you might end up defecating on the ground, and even dogs would discard you." Li felt even more guilty but was glad that she had agreed to stay overnight. Without a word, he urged her to undress, and they enjoyed each other's company. When they went to sleep, Li suddenly remembered past events and, to ease the tension, mentioned what the ghost had praised. The woman then playfully held his member and said, "Don't you know? What he said about being superior to Du Fu, it's probably this!" Li also realized it and couldn't help but laugh. Though the woman looked down on Li's talent, their feelings were deeply intertwined, and they were extremely affectionate.

    872

    因嘱曰：“君无诗肠，但有妾在，杜老诚不能及，温、李尚不足道也。万勿轻泄，泄则不祥。”李亦姑颔之。晨起，旋失女之所在，李犹似信复疑。及有所触，将欲挥毫，恍惚中如女在侧。吟成则句新语隽，非复吴下阿蒙，自视亦觉刮目。又出以示人，咸惊曰：“子今者虽仍杜下，已俨然居夫汤上矣。”李乃知女言非罔，自此与女寝处，几无虚夕。后值同人高会，汤适远来，亦与席。主人出一轴求众题咏，展视之，则美人春睡图也。汤乃逊李，李亦固辞，众议令各赋一章，先成者书之，李竟一挥而就，曰：“遮莫春愁重，终宵有醒时。却因香梦远，故向画图欹。百啭莺难唤，三眠柳不移。但憎舒又卷，睡损海棠枝。”稿甫脱，众皆抚掌，汤遂为之阁笔。既题复饮，汤故有心，乃以言挑之曰：“君才敏捷，夙所服膺，第不能工稳如是。今日之事，仆窃有疑，惟君其教之。

    Explanation

    872

    She admonished, "You lack poetic talent, but if I am here, even Du Fu could not surpass me, and Wen and Li would be even less worthy of mention. Do not lightly reveal this; if you do, it will bring misfortune." Li nodded in agreement. In the morning, she suddenly disappeared, and Li still seemed to believe and doubt. When something stirred him, he was about to pick up his brush, and in a daze, it felt as if she were by his side. When he finished composing, the lines were fresh and the language was elegant, no longer the simpleton from Wu, and he also felt a sense of self-improvement. He showed it to others, and all were amazed and said, "You, although still following Du Fu's style, have already surpassed Tang in elegance." Li then realized that the woman's words were not false, and from then on, he lived with the woman, almost every night. Later, when there was a gathering of friends, Tang happened to come from afar and joined the gathering. The host brought out a scroll and asked everyone to compose poetry. When they looked at it, it was a painting of a beautiful woman sleeping in spring. Tang then yielded to Li, and Li also firmly declined. The group decided that everyone should compose a poem, and the first to finish would write it down. Li finished it in one stroke and said, "Even if spring sorrow is heavy, there will be a time to wake up in the middle of the night. But because the fragrant dream is distant, it leans toward the painting. The hundred-voiced oriole is hard to call, and the three-sleeping willow does not move. But I hate that it stretches and rolls, damaging the branches of the sleeping hawthorn." As soon as the draft was finished, everyone clapped, and Tang then put down his brush. After writing, they drank again. Tang deliberately had a plan and said, "You are quick-witted, I have always admired you, but I doubt you could be so precise. I have a suspicion about today's matter, please instruct me."

    873

    ”李时已半酣，乃笑曰：“君亦故为癰谦，岂落霞孤骛遂足辍子章之管哉？虽然，仆以前所为亦殊梦梦。近有奇遇，始觉挥洒自如。”因述女之踪迹，且极其详，众咸骇异。座中一达者，忽愀然曰：“君之祸不旋踵矣！此女必诗妖也，夜慑君之精气，昼复炫君之灵明。英华尽泄，津液将枯，冀享中寿犹难，矧夫长年厚福哉！”李乃大恐，敬求良策。达者与众皆曰：“远之自可以免。”李为首肯，归即移卧具于内，不敢复宿萧斋，盖不入闺中者已三月矣。细君大悦，剪烛晤言。女忽现形而来，吽吽然戟指而数之曰：“妾何负于君，而泄吾事于广众，使迂儒目妾为妖。妾诚妖，诗人畴不赖予，竟相与鄙薄如此耶？”语次，面目顿更。李视之，则故巨角劖牙，形状丑恶，即谓己高出杜上之怪也。大骇，与妻俱仆，家人力救之。有顷始苏，犹呕血数升，病极危殆。延医投以参苓，半载始愈。然有时一觞一咏，又依然向之覆瓿物矣。李遂抱恨终身，绝口不言一杜字。

    Explanation

    873

    "Li was already half-drunk, and then he laughed and said, 'You are also deliberately being modest, is the lone goose of the setting sun enough to stop the brush of Zizhang? Although, what I have done before was extremely confused. Recently, I had a strange encounter, and only then did I feel free and unrestrained.' He then described the woman's whereabouts in great detail, and everyone was shocked and amazed. A wise person in the audience suddenly looked solemn and said, 'Your misfortune will not be far off! This woman must be a poetry demon, who seizes your vital energy at night and dazzles your clarity of mind by day. All your brilliance has been drained, your body fluids are drying up, even hoping for a middle-aged lifespan is difficult, how much more so for a long life and great fortune?' Li was greatly frightened and respectfully sought advice. The wise person and the others all said, 'By distancing yourself, you can avoid it.' Li agreed, and upon returning, he moved his bed into the inner room, no longer daring to stay in the desolate study, for he had not entered the inner room for three months. His wife was greatly pleased and they talked by the light of a candle. The woman suddenly appeared in her true form, with a loud, angry sound, she pointed and scolded, 'What have I done wrong to you, to reveal my affairs to a large audience, making the narrow-minded scholars view me as a demon. I am indeed a demon, who among poets does not rely on me, yet they all despise me like this?' As she spoke, her appearance suddenly changed. Li looked at her and saw that she had a large horn and sharp teeth, with an ugly and repulsive shape, exactly like the monster that surpasses Du. He was greatly frightened, and both he and his wife fell to the ground. The family members rushed to save him. After a while, he regained consciousness, still vomiting blood in large amounts, and his illness was extremely dangerous. He called in a doctor and was given ginseng and atractylodes, and after half a year, he recovered. However, sometimes when he drank and recited poetry, he was still the same as before, fit only to be covered in a jar. Li carried regret for life and never mentioned the word 'Du' again."

    874

    外史氏曰：东施效颦，竟掩西子，非笔下有神，腕底有鬼，将必不能如是。但女以才色惑人，庸免斥之为妖。不然，得一日之名，乌用享期颐之寿？达者具旷世之识，当不以危言相耸动也。

    Explanation

    874

    外史氏曰：East Beauty imitated the frown, and in the end, she overshadowed Xi Zi. Without divine inspiration and supernatural skill, one cannot achieve such results. However, if a woman uses her talent and beauty to bewitch others, how can one avoid being called a demon or monster? Otherwise, gaining temporary fame, how can one expect to live to the age of 100? Those who are enlightened possess wisdom beyond their time and will not be intimidated by alarming words.

    875

    ○　变鬼
    　　黔楚故多妖术，其事皆肇于苗。以人化虎，以木易足，变幻不可名状。故明天启年间，荆南有无赖子十余，奉一苗人为师。其术则曰变鬼，奇诡异常，毒流闺闼。初小试于某县，幸黄岗李如龙道士识其踪迹，白之官，擒而治之，遂未至于大肆。及究其变鬼之法，则风声鹤唳，人实自惊，初非鬼之能事也。某县有富家，居近城市，有壮男三人，皆已授室。乃翁早丧其偶，惟携二少妾别居一院。奴仆之健者十数人，素矜拳勇，剧盗犹望风思遁。一日值翁诞辰，子媳皆称觞介寿。苗人因乘其夜宴启闭微舒，乃聚其类，择四人各授以符，与己皆变鬼以往。及抵翁家，夜将三鼓。时深秋，天气暴寒，颇不可耐。翁与子陪待贺客，倦而思眠，遂各归寝室。鬼亦分袂尾之，始则窸窣作响，继乃呜咽发声，阖门已战夕靡宁。

    Explanation

    875

    ○　变鬼
    　　The regions of Guizhou and Hunan have long been known for their many magical arts, all of which originated among the Miao people. These practices include transforming a person into a tiger, replacing a human foot with a wooden one, and other transformations that are too bizarre to describe. During the Tianqi period of the Ming Dynasty, in southern Hubei, there were over ten ruffians who followed a Miao person as their teacher. Their art was called "transforming into ghosts," and it was extremely strange and dangerous, affecting the private lives of women. Initially, they conducted a minor test in a certain county, fortunately, the Taoist Li Rugong from Huanggang recognized their tracks and reported them to the authorities, who captured and punished them, preventing the situation from escalating. Upon investigating their method of transforming into ghosts, it became clear that the initial fear was due to human imagination rather than actual supernatural causes. In a certain county, there was a wealthy family living near the city, with three strong adult sons who had all married. Their father had lost his wife early and lived with two younger concubines in a separate part of the house. They had several strong and brave servants, who were capable of intimidating even notorious bandits. One day, it was the father's birthday, and his sons and daughters-in-law all gathered to celebrate. The Miao person took advantage of the slightly open doors and windows during the night banquet, gathered his followers, and selected four people to give them talismans, transforming themselves into ghosts and entering the house. By the third watch of the night, it was late autumn, and the weather was extremely cold and harsh. The father and his sons were tired and wanted to sleep, so they all returned to their bedrooms. The ghosts followed them, making subtle sounds at first, then wailing, causing the entire household to be in a state of fear and unrest.

    876

    翁胆独巨，犹大言语其妾曰：“鬼阴质也，当畏火光，可明烛以待之。我辈三人为众，阳气盛炽，复何所畏？”其论若甚侃侃。乃闻长啸者相逼而来，白髭早簌簌自动，如风际之竹矣。鬼亦不禁窃笑。及鬼入户，形果骇人。其一斩衣凉冠，面无血色。其一乌珠双努，体无寸丝。灯下视之，翁与妾俱亦倒地，鬼乃恣其所为。天明，始吼叫而去。翁少醒，烛犹未烬，周览室内，箱簏尽开，始悟鬼而实盗。初未虞其妾也，迨观榻上，两美裸然，尚犹白身偃卧。疑而诘之，则泣曰：“始以为鬼，妾等遂昏然。比苏，实二少年，褫予衣而相嬲，其辱不可胜言。直至数度方起榻，妾辈遂狼籍不堪矣！”翁因愧忿不已，亟扶杖往呼其长子。虑妇未起，自牖召之，闻其冢妇娇泣曰：“予今者复何可为人耶？”翁遂大疑，呼之益亟。子甫启门以出，则面目黧黑如漆炭，恍即深夜所见者。

    Explanation

    876

    The Old Man's Courage and the Ghostly Intrusion

    The old man had an unusually large courage, and he still spoke grandly to his concubine, saying, "Ghosts are of yin nature and should fear firelight. We can use a bright candle to await them. The three of us are a group, and our yang energy is strong. What else should we fear?" His words seemed very confident. Soon, they heard a long howl approaching, and the old man's white beard trembled as if moved by the wind, like bamboo in the breeze. The ghost could not help but secretly laugh. When the ghost entered the house, its form was indeed terrifying. One of them wore a cut-off robe and a cool hat, with a pale face, while the other had wide-open eyes and a body without a single thread of clothing. Under the lamp, the old man and his concubine both fell to the ground, and the ghost then acted as it pleased. In the morning, the ghost roared and left. The old man slowly woke up, and the candle was still not extinguished. He looked around the room and saw that all the boxes and chests were opened. He then realized that the ghost was actually a thief. Initially, he had not suspected his concubine, but upon seeing the bed, he found two young men naked and lying on it. He questioned them, and they wept, saying, "We first thought it was a ghost, so we became dazed. When we came to, it was actually two young men who took our clothes and teased us. The shame was too great to describe. They only left after several attempts, and we were in a state of disorder and disgrace!" The old man felt ashamed and angry without end, and quickly supported himself with a cane to call his eldest son. Worried that his wife had not yet woken up, he called out to him through the window. He heard his eldest wife crying softly, saying, "I now have no face to be a person!" The old man became even more suspicious and called out to him more urgently. The son just opened the door and came out, with a face dark and black like charcoal, seeming like the one seen at midnight.

    877

    翁几欲奔，强询之，则有蓬首厉鬼，直履其闼，子甚怖，适有无火之灶，因埋首其间，理乱固不知也。翁遂不诘其详，惟叹曰：“壮夫如此，固无怪乎老夫矣！”又往视其仲子，妇已着缕而起，延翁入，涕泗而言。亦有一鬼，血污周身，突入其室，妇惊仆。及寤，则床头有鬼，身上无衣，因哭诉不愿复生。翁抚慰之。及询其子，则指曰在榻下。翁唤之至屡，始匍匐而前，许时甫见其面，盖已污垢深渍，无复人状矣。翁益太息而去。比出，见其季子裸裎狂走而来，忿然曰：“空畜多人，鬼来即瑟缩不起。脱使遇盗，何如乎？”翁壮其言，亟叩之。对曰：“鬼至时，儿与妇已就寝。闻其声甚可怖，乃以衾冒首而卧，不敢出息。讵意鬼竟弗容，以爪捽香衾。儿一睹其面，鬼发赤眉幽，冥劣相，乃恐惧避席，不及著裤而奔。迨出寝户，顿思人众可援，往呼仆辈，辄皆托醉未醒。

    Explanation

    877

    The old man almost wanted to run away, but he forced himself to inquire. Then, there was a ghost with disheveled hair and a fierce appearance, who walked directly into the door. The son was very frightened. Just then, there happened to be a stove without fire, so he buried his head in it. Whether it was in order or disorder, he didn't know. The old man did not press for details, but sighed and said, "A strong man like this, it's no wonder for an old man like me!" He then went to see his second son. The wife had already dressed and stood up, inviting the old man in and speaking with tears and snot. There was also a ghost, blood-stained all over its body, who suddenly entered the room, causing the wife to faint. When she woke up, there was a ghost at the head of the bed, and there was no clothing on the body. She cried and pleaded not to be reborn. The old man comforted her. When he asked his son about it, the son pointed and said it was under the bed. The old man called him many times, and he finally crawled forward. After a while, he finally saw his face, which was already deeply stained with dirt and grime, with no longer any human appearance. The old man sighed deeply and left. When he came out, he saw his youngest son, naked and wildly running towards him. The son angrily said, "Having so many people, when the ghost came, they all cowered and didn't dare to move. What if they met a thief? What would happen then?" The old man approved of his words and quickly asked him. He replied, "When the ghost arrived, my son and wife were already asleep. They heard a very terrifying sound, so they covered their heads with a quilt and lay down, daring not to breathe. They didn't expect the ghost to not tolerate it, so it used claws to tear the fragrant quilt. My son saw its face, which had red hair and deep-set eyebrows, a demonic and inferior appearance, and out of fear, he avoided the seat, without putting on pants, he ran away. Until he came out of the bedroom door, he suddenly thought that a crowd could help, so he went to call the servants, but they all claimed to be drunk and not awake."

    878

    间有寤者，闻儿言反各觳觫乞命，俨若亲见鬼形。儿不得已，徘徊于外，幸而顿忘其寒。将曙，见前鬼肩荷巨囊，累累然不知何物，徐徐而行。儿亦莫敢究诘，故急奔来告兄，不图先已遇父也。”翁因慨曰：“孺子少有志，汝妇今竟何如？”答曰：“儿行时，妇故无恙。”翁曰：“亟往视之，鬼殆窃妇而逃矣！”其子趋而入闺，果失其妇，乃大号。翁亦仓皇命仆物色之，至午始得于郭外，盛以麻袋，气息将绝。负之归，向晦始活。则鬼喜其少艾，自榻而贮之于囊，荷之去，将以永好。苗人惧泄其事，叱令弃之，故得以珠还合浦焉。翁心耻其事，反重赉臧获，使无漏言，仅以盗劫首诸官。及鬼事泄，历供前案，凡攫取数家，皆惊之使避，以快其胠箧之私，惟翁家畏鬼殊甚，且室多丽质，故不免于包羞。案定，人遂喧传。翁病之，命子各出其妇，又皆不从，乃徙于乡。未及十余稔，而献忠之乱作，粉黛从军，为祸尤酷。变鬼者或其兆之先见乎？

    Explanation

    878

    Occasionally, there were those who suddenly became aware and, upon hearing the child's words, all trembled and begged for their lives, as if they had personally seen the ghost's form. The child had no choice but to wander outside, fortunately forgetting about his cold. As dawn approached, he saw the ghost carrying a large sack on its shoulder, heavy and unknown what it contained, moving slowly. The child dared not ask and so hurried to tell his brother, not expecting to have already met his father. The old man then sighed and said, "The young man has shown ambition. How is your wife now?" He replied, "When I left, my wife was fine." The old man said, "Go see her quickly; the ghost has likely taken your wife and fled!" His son rushed into the room and indeed found his wife missing, then let out a loud cry. The old man also panicked and ordered the servants to search for her. They found her outside the city walls at noon, in a burlap sack, nearly dead. They carried her back, and she revived at dusk. The ghost liked her youth and beauty, took her from the bed and put her in the sack, carrying her away to enjoy her forever. The locals feared the secret would be revealed and ordered her to be abandoned, thus she was returned like a pearl to its original place. The old man was ashamed of the incident and heavily rewarded the servants to keep the secret, reporting it only as a robbery to the authorities. When the ghost's involvement was revealed, he recounted all previous incidents, having taken several families, frightening them away to satisfy his own hidden desires. Only the old man's family feared the ghost especially, and their home had many beautiful women, thus they could not escape the shame. When the case was settled, people began to spread the news. The old man was distressed by it and ordered his sons to send away their wives, but they all refused, so he moved to a village. Before ten years had passed, the rebellion of Xianzong began, women joined the army, and the disaster was especially severe. Was the ghost a harbinger of the disaster?

    879

    外史氏曰：人而鬼乃其常也，鬼而实人则其变矣。夫以如是之鬼，见之者固应动色，至于搜囊箧辱闺人，则虽狞恶当前，谁能堪此？忍不起而与之争乎？乃一老无论三子，皆壮夫，何竟伈伈皔皔低首下心，是可忍孰不可忍耶？迨夫受侮不少，子且迁怒于仆，父且命逐其妇，亦徒贻笑柄耳，抑何补哉？抑何补哉？

    Explanation

    879

    The historian says: Being a person and becoming a ghost is the norm, but a ghost that actually behaves like a person is a change. When one encounters such a ghost, they should naturally be moved. However, when the ghost searches bags and boxes and humiliates women, how can one endure it, even if the ghost is fearsome and evil? How can one not stand up and fight against it? Yet, one old man and his three sons, all strong men, why do they still tremble and cower, bowing their heads and submitting? If this can be tolerated, what cannot be tolerated? When they have been insulted enough, the son will transfer his anger to the servant, and the father will order the expulsion of his wife. It will only become a laughing stock; what benefit is there?

    880

    ○　续念秧
    　　《聊斋》言念秧之事，隐括其奸，既已如犀照怪。迄今行旅所传，又有数事，亦足寒跋涉者心。因择其尤奇者，用以当禹鼎之一足，俾客子知所趋避，欲窥全豹犹未也。浙东某方伯以引年致仕，时已簪绅济济，诸公子皆宦于京。惟一幼者留奉晨昏，已弱冠有余矣。方伯以功名为重，遣之赴都，与其诸兄谋，将以求仕。濒行，予以千金，仅资路费，以京中所需自足。犹虑其少未更事，嘱之曰：“途次念秧者颇多，汝此行不淫不赌不多事，可以无患。切宜慎之。”公子谨受教，纪纲数人，载以巨舰，非如一介之土，从以老仆，乘以匹马，可以垂钩而钓者也。

    Explanation

    880

    ○　续念秧
    　　The Liaozhai speaks of the deceit known as "念秧," binding its wickedness, much like a rhinoceros horn revealing monsters. To this day, travelers have passed down several such stories, which are also sufficient to make those who journey shudder. Therefore, I have selected the most extraordinary ones to serve as one leg of the great bronze tripod of Yu, so that travelers may know how to avoid danger, though one cannot yet see the whole leopard. A certain Prefect of Zhejiang East retired due to age, and at that time, his household was filled with officials and gentlemen, with all his sons serving in the capital. Only one young one remained to attend to his daily needs, having already surpassed the age of twenty. The Prefect prioritized official career and reputation, sending him to the capital to consult with his elder brothers, with the intention of seeking an official position. On the eve of departure, he gave him a thousand gold pieces, only to cover travel expenses, to be sufficient for what is needed in the capital. Still worried that he was young and inexperienced, he told him, "There are many such scams on the road. You will not engage in debauchery, gambling, or trouble, so you can avoid trouble. You must be very careful." The young man respectfully accepted the advice, accompanied by a few attendants, traveling by a large ship, not like a lone traveler, with an old servant and riding a single horse, someone who can fish at will.

    881

    行及汉口，弃舟而就舆，又已数十程。公子既恪守家训，繁费无多，而公又挂冠未久，门生故吏几遍中途，其饱猪肝而赠缟带者，不一而足。即从者亦益润行色，于是慢藏之戒倍严。将抵京，已使人先往驰报，公子与其仆驾轻车跨骏骑，辎重络绎，驰驱于畿南道中。虽非朝发夕至，亦既信宿匪遥，心皆渐放。是夕宿于安肃，小邑也。仆夫卸装，公子散步于旅邸，因与邮亭主人闲诘道里之远近，约数日可达。主人未及对，旁一人华服鲜衣，貌颇修伟，代答日：“明夕宿涿州，二日可抵都门。贵客按程前进，道亦不远矣！”公子以首颔之。微诘其姓，曰田某。审其音亦浙，公子颇动乡情。

    Explanation

    881

    Traveling to Hankou, they abandoned their boats and boarded carriages, covering another dozens of miles. The young master, adhering to his family's teachings, kept expenses to a minimum. Additionally, the gentleman had recently resigned from his post, and his former students and officials were spread along the route, offering generous gifts of pig liver and white sashes, which were not uncommon. Even the attendants added to the splendor of the journey, thus making the caution against carelessness even more stringent. As they neared the capital, messengers were already sent ahead to report their arrival. The young master and his servant drove a light carriage and rode fine horses, with a continuous flow of baggage and supplies, speeding through the southern part of the capital's jurisdiction. Although they did not reach the destination in a single day, they were close enough that the journey seemed almost within reach, and their spirits began to relax. That evening, they stayed in Anshu, a small county. The servant dismounted and unloaded, while the young master strolled around the inn, casually inquiring about the distance to the capital from the innkeeper. Before the innkeeper could respond, a man dressed in splendid and bright clothing, with a handsome and dignified appearance, answered on his behalf: "Tomorrow night, you will stay in Zhuozhou, and in two days, you will reach the capital. Your honorable guest is proceeding according to schedule, and the road is not far anymore!" The young master nodded in agreement. He lightly inquired about the man's surname, who replied, "Tian." Judging from his accent, he was also from Zhejiang, which stirred the young master's sense of hometown.

    882

    方将细询，适仆来请盥濯，遂入室。及昏，田忽以盛馔入浼仆禀白，言系公子长兄现莅某部斥革之吏，以故闲居在此。倘公子肯为缓颊，仍复厥役，则不啻再造之恩矣，因敬以盘飧上献。公子颇疑，召入问之，凿凿叙述，则其事甚细，其情可原。及诘其何以出京，又对曰：“吏之兄实亦小吏，现掌案卷于县，故来依之。”公子又私以询仆，则逆旅内外，人多识之。因亦坦然不疑，三辞而后受之，更加慰劳，许为排解。田色似甚喜，顿首至地，感激不胜。公子遂独酌于旅舍，田与群仆呼卢浮白，亦乐而未央。酒将半，又有客至。人骑喧嘶，遽问公子宿处。

    Explanation

    882

    As he was about to inquire in detail, the servant arrived to request washing, and he then entered the room. By evening, Tian suddenly brought in a sumptuous feast and asked the servant to report, stating that it concerned the eldest brother of the young master, who was currently stationed at a certain department and had been dismissed from his post, and therefore was staying here in leisure. If the young master would intercede on his behalf, restoring his position would be no less than a second life, so he respectfully offered a feast as a gift. The young master was quite suspicious and called him in to question him. Tian stated clearly and convincingly, and the matter was quite detailed, his situation deserving of leniency. When asked why he had left the capital, he replied again: "The brother of the official is also a minor official, currently managing records in the county, so he came to rely on him." The young master then privately inquired of the servant, and within and outside the inn, many people recognized him. Therefore, he became completely at ease and no longer doubted, and after three refusals, he finally accepted it, further comforting and rewarding him, and promising to mediate. Tian's expression seemed very pleased, kneeling down with his head touching the ground, overwhelmed with gratitude. The young master then drank alone in the inn, while Tian and the group of servants played dice and drank freely, also enjoying themselves without end. When the wine was half consumed, another guest arrived, people and horses making loud noises, and quickly asked about the young master's lodging.

    883

    主人导以入，其一人须髯如戟，服冠不类齐民，其一则形躯短小，年仅成童，美好如处女。公子私心计曰：“念秧者至矣！”强起询之，髯者操浙音笑曰：“弟与兄同一桑梓，君竟不识我耶？某太常为予季父，现任都中，与诸大兄杯酒盘桓，弟不才亦常叨陪待。至于浙中故乡，先人之敝庐与尊府相距只一衣带水耳。弟虽未获识荆，昨闻大兄言，知已北上，不意果得相遭，实出三生之幸。”公子闻其言语，仍涉疑忌，而依稀记忆故里若有其人。因叩其官位，并询以所知，则曰：“弟一武夫，叨登乡榜，因丁艰未经部选。客岁服阕入京，守候至今，始准保府试用，宦途可谓蹭蹬。然藉此遇兄，颇觉大快人意。”语甚洽比。

    Explanation

    883

    The host led them inside. One of them had a beard like a halberd, dressed and capped in a manner unlike that of a commoner, while the other was short in stature, barely an adolescent, yet beautiful like a maiden. The young master privately thought to himself, "The cheat has arrived!" He forced himself to inquire, and the bearded one, speaking with a Zhejiang accent, laughed and said, "Brother and I share the same hometown. How can you not recognize me? My uncle, the Grand Master of Sacrifices, is currently in the capital, and I often accompany my elder brothers in drinking and revelry. As for our hometown in Zhejiang, the humble abode of my ancestors is only a narrow strip of water away from your esteemed residence. Although I have not had the privilege of meeting you, I heard from my elder brother yesterday that you had traveled north. I did not expect to meet you here, and it truly feels like a great fortune in three lifetimes." The young master, upon hearing his words, still harbored doubts, but he vaguely remembered someone from his hometown. He then asked about his official position and inquired about what he knew. The bearded one replied, "I am a mere warrior, and I passed the local examination, but due to a period of mourning, I did not go through the selection process. Last year, after completing the mourning period, I came to the capital and waited until now. I was finally selected for a temporary position in the provincial government. My career has been quite difficult. However, thanks to this, I have met my elder brother, and it feels very pleasing." His words were very agreeable and harmonious.

    884

    公子因恍然曰：“君非武孝廉某耶？景慕久矣！”竟与之欢然讲礼，无所疑。盖公子邑中有武科，居虽窎远，曾耳其名，即太常之犹子也。所言吻合，方深信之，且揖以入席。某笑曰：“易云不速之客，殆弟之谓矣！”迳就坐。公子又诘少年伊谁，某耳语曰：“此京中一妙龄旦也，弟以数百金致之，故不使离左右。兄前放胆，愿赐一坐，何如？”公子不拂其意，果命侍坐。少年亦不谢，岸然即席，公子颇怪之。既而睨其貌，较敷粉而增光，视涂脂而益洁，俯仰娇羞，宛一闺中弱质，公子遂信其为优。未几，某之厮养亦入，具白旅中人满，无地可容，谋将他徙。

    Explanation

    884

    The young nobleman suddenly realized and said, "Are you not the martial scholar Mr. So-and-so? I have long admired you from afar!" He then happily engaged in formal conversation with him, without any doubt. It turned out that the nobleman's hometown had a martial examination, and though he lived far away, he had heard of his name before, and he was the nephew of the Grand Master of Rites. The conversation matched perfectly, and the nobleman finally believed him, and invited him to sit at the table. Mr. So-and-so smiled and said, "The Yijing says, 'an unexpected guest,' which must refer to me!" He immediately sat down. The nobleman then asked who the young man was, and Mr. So-and-so whispered, "This is a young actor from the capital, whom I brought here with several hundred gold pieces, so he does not leave my side. Please be bold, Brother, and grant him a seat, how about it?" The nobleman did not refuse his request and indeed ordered him to sit beside him. The young man also did not thank him, but sat haughtily, which made the nobleman quite surprised. Soon, he glanced at the young man's appearance, finding that he was even more radiant than if he had been powdered, and even cleaner than if he had been rouged. His demeanor was shy and delicate, like a young woman in her boudoir, and the nobleman thus believed him to be an actor. Shortly after, Mr. So-and-so's servant also entered and reported that the inn was full and there was no room to accommodate them, and they were planning to move to another place.

    885

    某对公子笑曰：“弟自北来，适遭兄之前驱，知今夕当宿此地，故跟寻而至，冀得一席之地，可以少领清谈。今竟不能，殊觉怅怅。”言讫，作别欲行，公子不禁动念。窃计同乡，又登仕版，既承物色之殷，更荷流连之雅，一时遂顿忘远虑，慨然曰：“弟不图兄至，先占枝头，致兄无地下榻，殊为负罪。然不以亵尊为嫌，弟一人正苦寂寞，何不共居此室，抵足谈心耶？”某大喜，谢曰：“暮夜良难转移。但弟武人鲁莽，或不便于兄，反取罪戾。既蒙盛谊挽留，弟亦不敢自外，谨受命。”即呼从人将衣装尽卸室内，竟不复迁。公子覸其豪华，迥殊寒素，与己亦不甚相悬，愈无所猜忌，乃洗盏更酌。又有顷，田入行酒，公子之侍从踵至。

    Explanation

    885

    The person smiled at the young master and said, "I came from the north and happened to meet the young master's advance, knowing that tonight you would stay here, so I followed and arrived, hoping to get a place to sit and have a chance to engage in a bit of refined conversation. Now I cannot, and I feel very disappointed." After finishing speaking, he said goodbye and wanted to leave. The young master could not help but think. He secretly thought that they were from the same hometown, and that the speaker was an official. Having received both the earnest search and the grace of lingering, he suddenly forgot his distant concerns and graciously said, "I did not expect the young brother to come first and take up the branch, causing the younger brother to have no place to stay, truly feeling guilty. But I do not consider it disrespectful to share the room and talks heart-to-heart. The younger brother is truly suffering from loneliness, why not share this room and talk heart-to-heart?" The person was very happy and thanked the young master, saying, "It is indeed difficult to move during the late night. But the younger brother is a rough military man, perhaps inconvenient for the older brother, and will instead incur blame. Having received great favor and the command, the younger brother也不敢 exclude himself, respectfully accept the command." Immediately, he called his attendants to unload all the clothes inside the room, and did not move again. The young master saw his luxury, very different from the poor, not much different from himself, even more without suspicion, then washed the cups and poured more wine. After a while, the wine steward served the wine, and the young master's attendants followed.

    886

    公子命款某之仆，某又起而谢。忽见田骇曰：“二兄何得在此？”公子诘其相识之由，则某之母党远族也。公子推某之爱，亦令与席。田三辞，始敢隅坐。某与之闲叙家事，剌剌不休。公子得注视少年，竟不复忆庭训。少年又时时流盼，倍觉销魂。四目交瞩，渐有不可解之势。知已上饵，故飞一觞来曰：“兄须尽此爵，是儿有绝技，尚未及一售也。”公子竟满饮之。某以箸代板，命少年清讴。初犹腼腆，辞以喉哑，强之而后歌。乃一发声，梁尘簌簌欲动。于是四人豪饮，均已酕醄，邑城早漏下三鼓。田乃辞出，公子与某皆命仆布衾，各据一榻。少年果随某同卧，俨然伉俪，公子因窃笑之。

    Explanation

    886

    The prince commanded his servant to greet me, and I rose to thank him. Suddenly seeing Tian, he was startled and asked, "How did you two brothers end up here?" The prince questioned the reason for their acquaintance, and I explained that it was a distant relative of my mother's family. Considering his affection for me, the prince also invited him to join the gathering. Tian declined three times before he dared to sit in a corner. I chatted with him about family matters, talking incessantly. The prince focused on the young man and forgot his father's teachings. The young man glanced around frequently, making it even more enchanting. Their eyes met, and there was an increasingly inexplicable tension. Knowing they had been lured, he brought a cup of wine and said, "You must finish this cup, for this boy has extraordinary skills that haven't been fully displayed yet." The prince finished the cup. I used chopsticks as a substitute for a board and commanded the young man to sing. He was initially shy and excused himself by saying his voice was hoarse, but he sang only after being urged. When he first sang, the dust on the beams seemed to tremble. Thus, the four of them drank heartily, all becoming tipsy. Early in the morning, the town clock struck three. Tian then bid farewell and left. The prince and I both ordered our servants to spread quilts, each taking a bed. The young man indeed slept with me, appearing like a married couple. The prince secretly laughed at this.

    887

    及寝，某忽发恶欲呕，做诸醉态，故搅清眠。公子本不惯此，加以宿酒在胸，遂不能寐。遥闻骰声清越，唤采夺雉，公子知仆辈共博，亦属长途恒事，漠不关心。而伺彼二人，则已共入睡乡矣！公子转侧亦渐朦胧，闻某又欠伸，仿佛病酒不能即安者。少顷，遽以昵语唤少年，少年弗应。又许时，方觉。微闻之，某低曰：“转背向予，奈何带亦弗缓？”旋闻少年曰：“卧榻有人，何又做此态耶？”某笑曰：“渠被酒甚深，此时早应熟寐，何能知？若乃故为阻我。”少年遂不言。须臾，榻上窸窣作响，枕席亦有声，鼻之呼吸，口之呕哑，皆不止。约略间，公子之情大动矣，惟恨不舍彼而就此。无何，齁声聒耳，某似熟眠。

    Explanation

    887

    When he lay down to sleep, the servant suddenly felt nauseous and wanted to vomit, exhibiting various drunken behaviors, thus disturbing his peaceful sleep. The young master was not accustomed to this, and the previous night's drinking further compounded his discomfort, so he could not sleep. He heard the clear sound of dice being thrown from afar, with calls for the roll and competition for the winning number. The young master knew that the servants were gambling, which was also a common occurrence during long journeys, and he was indifferent to it. However, watching the other two, they had already fallen asleep! The young master turned and gradually became drowsy, hearing the servant yawn again, as if suffering from a hangover and unable to settle down. A short while later, he suddenly called the young man in a low voice, but the young man did not respond. After a while, he finally woke up, faintly hearing the servant whisper, "Turn your back to me, why don't you loosen your belt too?" He then heard the young man say, "There's someone on the bed, why are you acting like this?" The servant laughed and said, "He was very drunk, at this time, he should already be sound asleep, how could he know? If he is deliberately trying to stop me." The young man remained silent. A moment later, there was a rustling sound on the bed, with sounds from the pillow and mat, the breathing and snoring sounds, all continued. In the meantime, the young master's desire was greatly aroused, but he regretted not leaving the other two to pursue this. Soon after, the loud snoring filled his ears, and the servant seemed to be sound asleep.

    888

    且闻少年笑曰：“误人黑甜，旋又醉梦，为欢几何？”公子顿思招之，忽忆父命，遂复强忍。铃柝已四敲，因假寐思睡。俄而耳畔有人笑语曰：“君盍少寤，敬来答垂盼之情。”语未及竟，身已入衾。公子觉肤香袭人，若薰兰麝。及抚其下体，则又如脂如韦，温膀堆绵。值火灾袄庙之时，宁复有舟返剡溪之事？未及片言，山僧早叩门而入矣。少年又极活泼，百倍闺人，公子初尝试之，能不神魂颠倒哉？事已，共枕交吻。少年始言曰：“仆以一时狂瞽，误从此赳赳者，使酒尚气，殊不留人面皮。兼之武勇可惮，倘违逆之，敲扑不远矣。

    Explanation

    888

    And hearing the young man laugh and say, "Depriving one of sweet sleep, soon again in a drunken dream, how much pleasure is there?" The young master suddenly thought of summoning him, but then remembered his father's command, and thus forced himself to endure. The bell and drum had struck four times, so he pretended to sleep, thinking of sleeping. Soon after, someone laughed and spoke by his ear, saying, "Why not wake up a little? To respectfully come and answer the longing gaze." Before a single word was spoken, the mountain monk had already knocked on the door and entered. The young man was extremely lively, a hundred times more lively than a woman, and the young master, experiencing it for the first time, could not help but feel dizzy with excitement. The mountain monk then said, "I, in a moment of foolishness, mistakenly came here. That man, drunk and arrogant, is not one to be trifled with. If you disobey him, whipping and beating are not far off."

    889

    宁得如君辈儒雅，使人乍领而已为之醉心。”公子已有意于此，乃以言挑之曰：“某兄亦甚爱吾子，何遂不满如是？”少年又曰：“若人清醒亦极能温存，无如性耽麯蘖，醉后益豪，虽当仆役之前，亦强人为此丑态。君知我辈，岂遂无耻至是？即如今夕，与君共室，何可复生欲心？致以猥亵悉入君耳，即此可见一般。”公子因笑曰：“胡不舍之而相随远出？子亦自有所乐者。”少年乃低诉曰：“渠止二百金为予偿债，遂诱予相伴，至任再酬十镒。予年幼误信之，于今深悔于心，然亦无可如何矣。宵来晤君，不胜仰企，故敢窃以豚馈君。倘留意付渠原金，仆自从君返京，朝夕侍从。

    Explanation

    889

    Would it not be better to have the elegance of your kind, to make one momentarily understand and then be deeply captivated? The young man had already taken a liking to this, and he gently probed the matter by saying, "My elder brother also greatly loves your son, so why are you so dissatisfied?" The youth replied, "If he is sober, he is also very gentle and considerate, but he has a fondness for alcohol, and when drunk, he becomes even more unrestrained. Even in front of servants, he forces himself into this shameful behavior. Do you know us? Could we truly be so shameless? Even now, sharing a room with you, how could I possibly have any desire? To the point where indecent acts have entered your ears, this is evident. The young man then laughed and said, "Why not leave him behind and travel far away with me? You must have your own pleasures." The youth then whispered, "He only gave me 200 taels of silver to pay off my debt, and then lured me to accompany him, promising to pay me ten times that amount. I was young and mistakenly trusted him, and now I deeply regret it in my heart, but there is nothing I can do about it. Meeting you tonight, I am overwhelmed with admiration, so I dare to secretly offer a piglet as a gift to you.
