If you take care to return the original sum to him, I will follow you back to the capital and serve you day and night.

    890

    我辈中尤有妙者，则汲引同来，不难尽入彀中，岂止仆之一脔哉？”公于见其滑稽，益更适宜，径许之。少年亦不复起，竟任公子拥背高眠，不觉东方之已白。诘朝，公子犹寝，忽闻某诟谇声。及醒，少年已为捽去，将饱老拳。公子心实侧然，乃著衣下榻，赧颜而排解之。某益忿忿，兼詈公子曰：“以若文人华胄，且系乡谊，倍致殷勤，何倚势夺人所爱耶？斯事无足涉讼，予只扑杀此獠。”公子实内怍。某即奋臂毒殴，少年号救，势甚汹汹。邮亭主及两家藏获皆排闼直入，正难解纷，田忽自外来，遽止某曰：“弟母躁率，公子实予恩主，有言宜缓商。”遂力劝某出，某犹作色，田乃把臂同去。

    Explanation

    890

    Among us there are particularly wonderful individuals, and if they are drawn along with us, it is not difficult to bring them all within the net; how much less would it be just a piece of my flesh?" The gentleman saw the humor in the situation and agreed to it immediately. The young man no longer got up, allowing the gentleman to sleep with his back supported, and did not notice the dawn. The next morning, the gentleman was still asleep when he suddenly heard the sound of scolding. Upon waking, the young man had already been dragged away and was about to be beaten. The gentleman truly felt pity, so he got up, dressed, and tried to mediate with a blush. The person became even angrier and cursed the gentleman, saying, "Given your status as a cultured nobleman and our shared hometown ties, you showed extra kindness and hospitality, but how could you use your power to take what I love? This matter is not worth going to court over; I will just beat this person to death." The gentleman felt ashamed inside. The person then punched violently, the young man cried out for help, and the situation became very tense. The innkeeper and the servants of both parties pushed open the door and entered, making it difficult to resolve the dispute. Tian suddenly came from outside, stopped the person, and said, "Do not be rash and hasty. The gentleman is truly my benefactor; let's discuss this calmly." He then strongly persuaded the person to leave, but the person still looked angry. Tian took the person by the arm and left together.

    891

    旋复公子曰：“渠实不甘，可若何？”少年又涕泗横流，不愿从某。田因为之居间，劝公子出金相易，公子亦心肯。田与某言，初犹不许，及言之再三，始允从。但索少年衣履，并饮食鞍马之需，公子亦靳之。直至日中，甫能定议。以二百四十金付某，某犹恨恨不绝口。方将束装启行，忽又闻斗声，哄且倍前。公子怪而出视，邮亭主人早颠踬奔入，为公子言：“贵价与某弁之仆博，负二百金，偿未及半，因而忿争。迄今犹詈于市上，倘被逻卒见之，功令森严，予将不能业此矣。惟公子怜之！”公子亟呼仆呵叱之。果有一仆，负重不能偿，而众仆怒某，又不肯代还，以致相殴。及公子垂询，皆无言，而索逋者益急。公子素慈祥悯下，又出百金命主人代偿之，某与其仆始扬镳而去。

    Explanation

    891

    The young master replied, "He truly does not wish to, what can be done about it?" The youth was again overcome with tears, unwilling to follow the unnamed person. The intermediary, named Tian, stepped in to mediate, urging the young master to offer gold in exchange. The young master agreed inwardly. Tian spoke to the unnamed person, who initially refused, but after being urged repeatedly, finally consented. However, the unnamed person demanded the youth's clothes, shoes, and the necessities for food, drink, and horse equipment, which the young master was stingy with. It was only at noon that they could finally reach an agreement. The young master paid two hundred and forty gold coins to the unnamed person, who still complained bitterly. Just as they were about to pack up and start on their journey, they suddenly heard fighting, which was even more intense than before. The young master was surprised and went out to see, and the innkeeper had already stumbled and rushed in, saying to the young master, "The high-ranking official gambled with the attendant's servant, owed two hundred gold coins, and had not yet paid half, leading to a fierce quarrel. They are still cursing in the marketplace. If the patrol officers see them, the laws are strict, and I will no longer be able to continue this business. Only you can show compassion!" The young master quickly called his servants to scold them. Indeed, one of the servants owed a large sum and could not pay, and the other servants were angry with the unnamed person and unwilling to pay on their behalf, leading to a fight. When the young master inquired, no one spoke, and those demanding payment became even more urgent. The young master, always kind and compassionate toward his subordinates, gave one hundred gold coins to the innkeeper to pay on their behalf. The unnamed person and his servant then rode away together.

    892

    公子虽失数百金，然得少年，甚惬所愿，故亦不以为憾。仆又因耗其主资，更无敢面诤而腹诽，乃就道。田亦策蹇相送，公子固辞不获，姑听其行。至某镇，日色将晡，始治飧。田又以盛馔入，公子深感其意，与少年对食于室中，田与仆皆在外也。忽又数人徘徊庭际，皆青衣，状类应捕，噪杂细语，良久始出。旋见田奔走张皇入室，即言曰：“敝亲诚不肖，又负累公子矣！”因指少年曰：“是儿非即某王府之旦也？受王身值若干，俱已浪费，故从敝亲远行。王怒，仰京畿追捕，急如星火。仆误劝公子纳之，今被京差窥见，以公子为逋逃主，将逮去见王。公子宜速为计。”言未已，汹汹者尽入，已至庑下。

    Explanation

    892

    Though the young master lost several hundred gold pieces, he was very much satisfied with obtaining the young man, so he did not consider it a regret. The servant, having wasted the master's funds, dared not openly criticize or secretly disapprove, and thus set off. Tian, the servant, also rode a slow donkey to see him off, but the young master firmly declined and could not be persuaded, so he reluctantly allowed Tian to accompany them. Upon reaching a certain town, as the sun was setting, they began to prepare dinner. Tian then brought in a lavish feast, and the young master deeply appreciated his intention, eating with the young man in the room while Tian and the servant remained outside. Suddenly, several people were seen wandering around the courtyard, all dressed in green, resembling constables, speaking in hushed, noisy tones, and after a long while, they finally left. Shortly after, Tian rushed in, looking panicked, and immediately said, "My humble relative is truly unworthy, and has burdened the young master again!" He then pointed to the young man and said, "This boy is indeed the旦 (Dàn) from a certain prince's mansion. He received a certain amount from the prince, which has all been wasted, so he followed my humble relative on a distant journey. The prince is furious and has ordered the capital region to pursue him, as urgently as a shooting star. I mistakenly advised the young master to accept him, and now the officials from the capital have seen this, considering the young master as the fugitive's leader, and will take him to see the prince. The young master should quickly make a plan." Before he finished speaking, the noisy ones all entered and reached the eaves.

    893

    公子闻王大怖，神色尽失。田又出与众语，安慰之。已有二人牵少年出室，加以肘锁如重囚。公子益骇，呼田与谋，思所以免祸。田又难色曰：“此曹眼孔大，区区者何能济事？姑试之。”出果遭捕人挥斥，且掌其颊，田不敢言。公子又浼之，田往复至三，捕人始有允意。然索重赂，金且论千。公子虽震恐，力实不能。田又为之关说，耐尽叱辱，议至八百，方首肯。公子所携之数，半耗于舟车，前事既已告罄。因以馈赆为苞苴，不敷者更质其衣物，囊橐半空，甫能如数。捕人犹争执不已，田恳之，乃絷少年北行。在公子则已人琴俱往矣！郁郁不快，而天已薄暝，即宿于此地。翌日起程，田早不辞而去，公子惑焉。

    Explanation

    893

    The prince heard the king was greatly terrified, and his expression completely lost. Tian went out to speak with others and comforted him. There were already two people dragging the young man out of the room and putting on elbow shackles like a serious prisoner. The prince became even more frightened, called out to Tian to discuss, and thought of ways to avoid disaster. Tian again showed a difficult expression and said, "These people have big eyes; how can such a small person help? Let's try it." When he went out, he was scolded by the captors and slapped his cheek, and Tian dared not speak. The prince again pleaded with him, and Tian went back and forth three times, and the captors finally showed a willingness to agree. But they demanded a large bribe, the amount was nearly a thousand coins. The prince was indeed terrified but was actually unable to pay. Tian again tried to mediate, enduring all the insults and scolding, and the price was reduced to eight hundred coins, and they finally agreed. The prince's available funds had half been spent on travel expenses, and the previous matters had already been exhausted. So he used gifts and travel expenses as bribes, and for what was insufficient, he pawned his clothes. His bags were half empty, just barely able to meet the amount. The captors continued to argue, and Tian pleaded with them, and finally tied the young man and took him north. For the prince, it was as if both the person and the instrument had gone away; feeling depressed and unhappy, and as dusk approached, he stayed at this place. The next day, he set out, and Tian left early without saying goodbye, and the prince was puzzled.

    894

    视仆之行装，俱若轻减。亟叩其故，多不敢置对，惟一仆答曰：“前夕与某之仆博，独田所负不貲。及田寝，吾侪始落下风。夜间宿此，因共计取偿于田，以报公子。不意局势忽翻，田竟大获，约资数百金。又不敢闻之公子，遂各卸其装，畀之令去。渠实饱载而归，公子幸无责。”公子闻此，怃然有间曰，“噫！予知之矣！此真念秧者也。”乃不复怒，反戒其仆曰：“予奉大人训，不能恪遵，致为宵小所愚。今我辈实犯淫赌，倘大人知之，罪亦莫逭，可慎言。予苟腾达，此数亦无难立致。”从者皆喜，徐请曰：“倘诸公子垂询，将何以应？”公子曰：“托于盗劫，犹较愈。”仆皆敬诺。抵都，其诸兄已命价相俟。

    Explanation

    894

    Upon inspecting the servants' luggage, it appeared to be lighter than before. They urgently inquired about the reason, but most were hesitant to respond. Only one servant answered, saying, "Last night, we played a game of chance with the servant of someone else, and only I lost an incalculable amount. After I went to sleep, we began to lose more. That night, while staying here, we planned to seek compensation from him to repay the young master. Unexpectedly, the situation turned, and he ended up winning a substantial sum, approximately several hundred gold pieces. We dared not tell the young master, so we each unloaded our belongings and gave them to him to take away. He returned fully loaded, and the young master was fortunate not to be blamed." Upon hearing this, the young master sighed for a moment and said, "Ah! I understand now! This is truly a trickster." He then calmed down and warned his servants, "I followed the instructions of the superior but failed to adhere to them, leading to being deceived by small people. Now we have indeed committed excessive gambling. If the superior finds out, we will be guilty, so be careful with your words. If I rise to prominence, this money will not be difficult to obtain." The followers were all relieved and slowly asked, "What should we say if the young masters ask?" The young master replied, "Attribute it to theft or robbery, which is still better." The servants respectfully agreed. Upon arriving in the capital, his brothers had already arranged for a price to be paid.

    895

    疑之曰：“约于某日可至，何濡滞若此？”公子不言。而先行之仆又讶其萧索，顿改昔观，后至者即伪以遇盗相告，众咸惊叹。公子至寓，见其诸兄，亦以被劫为言。诸兄欲穷治之，则又沮止曰：“吾兄位皆清秩，捕者乌肯尽心？且所失无多，奚必为是介介。”诸兄纳其言，反矜其量，而不知其别具隐衷也。公子遂洗心寂处，而使同病之仆密为咨访。其大兄署内，并无田姓之吏。某太常之犹子，现居京邸，未闻授职。诸王府班中，又无少年其人。知其皆妄，益不敢宣播，即仆亦无有泄者。居二年，以助饷授晋省州悴，始出京。再过其地，传舍已更三主，前事不问可知，乃叹息而去。念秧之诡谲如是，宜乎坠其云雾者，不仅若聊斋所云也。

    Explanation

    895

    He doubted and said, "We agreed that he would arrive on a certain day, why is he so delayed?" The young master did not speak. The servant who went ahead was surprised by his desolate appearance, suddenly changed from his previous demeanor. Those who arrived later pretended to have encountered robbers and reported it, and everyone was amazed. When the young master reached the inn, he saw his elder brothers and also claimed to have been robbed. The elder brothers wanted to thoroughly investigate it, but then discouraged and stopped, saying, "Our brothers all hold clean and honorable positions; would the pursuers really take it seriously? Besides, what was lost was not much. Why be so concerned about it?" The elder brothers accepted his words and instead praised his magnanimity, but did not know that he had a secret motive. The young master then purified his mind and lived in seclusion, and sent his similarly afflicted servant to secretly inquire. In the office of his eldest brother, there was no official surnamed Tian. A certain nephew of the Grand Master of Ceremonies, currently residing in the capital, has not been heard to have been appointed to a post. Among the ranks of the various princes' households, there was no such young man. Knowing that they were all false, he even more so dared not spread it, and even the servants did not dare to reveal it. After two years, he was appointed as a state official in Shanxi province due to his contribution to the military expenses, and finally left the capital. When passing through that place again, the inn had changed hands three times, and the previous events were well known without asking. Then he sighed and left. The cunning and deceitfulness of the plan were like this; it is appropriate that those who fall into its confusion are not just as described in Liaozhai Zhiyi.

    896

    外史氏曰：以藩宣之子，买笑千金，曾无所大损。独群小为此区区，费尽心力，殊无异于拔牛一毛，不禁令人失笑。虽然，汲水西江，挹流行潦。狮子搏象用全力，搏兔亦用全力，称此而求，溪壑可满。苟如公子固无伤，稍逊公子即可虑。吾因特著其奸，而以戒夫世之行迈者。

    Explanation

    896

    The commentator says: A son of a feudal lord, spending a thousand gold pieces for laughter, has suffered no great harm. But these petty individuals, for such trivial matters, have expended all their effort, which is no different from plucking a single hair from an ox, and cannot help but make one laugh. Although, drawing water from the West River and scooping up the flowing rainwater. A lion uses all its strength to fight an elephant, and also uses all its strength to fight a rabbit; seeking according to this standard, the ravine can be filled. If like the young master, it would be no harm; if slightly inferior to the young master, it is something to worry about. I specially point out their deceit, and to warn the world's ambitious people.

    897

    ○　生生袋
    　　京都一妇，以痨疾病入阴司。见一山堆积小儿外肾，不啻恒河沙数。旁有老妪百余人，踞地纺线，而不知所用。妇问之，一妪答曰：“此名生生袋。凡转轮者，皆于此领取，故如山而不厌其多。吾曹家贫，苦无衣，冥主怜之，俾得在此缝纫。每枚三钱，藉以资生。其中寿天彭殇，则以单线双线别之。汝归验之小儿，即可知也。”言已，妇犹有所问，忽一人飞马而过，见妇即叱曰：“汝不应死，盍去诸。”提其领若捉鸡雏，惊悸而苏。其病若失，因举以告人。始悟世俗所传，婴儿肾囊有缉边锁边之异，证之妇言，似犹可信。

    　　外史氏曰：人多以呵脬为活鬼，又以缝肾资生。生生一袋，直令人间地下无不营营于是矣！第不识此等老妪亦有更代与否？不然所缝者不啻恒河沙，其钱又可数计耶？惜此妇未及问之。

    Explanation

    897

    A Bag of Life

    In the capital city, a woman fell ill with tuberculosis and was taken to the underworld. There, she saw a mountain piled high with child's testicles, numbering as countless as the sands of the Ganges River. Beside the mountain, over a hundred old women were sitting on the ground spinning yarn, though she did not know what they were doing. The woman asked one of the old women, who replied, "This is called the Bag of Life. All those who are reborn receive their fate from here, hence the mountain is not厌 its multitude. We are poor and lack clothing, but the ruler of the underworld has pity on us and allows us to sew here. Each one earns three coins, which sustains our lives. Whether one lives long or short, it is determined by single or double threads. You can verify this by examining the child's testicles when you return." After the old woman finished speaking, the woman still had some questions, but suddenly a rider on a horse galloped past and saw the woman. He shouted, "You should not die; go away!" He grabbed her by the collar as if catching a chick, and she was startled awake. Her illness seemed to vanish, and she told others about her experience. People began to understand the common belief that the testicles of newborns have different stitching patterns, which the woman's words seemed to confirm.

    The commentator said: Many people make a living by preparing animal bladders, and others sustain themselves by sewing. The Bag of Life truly makes both the living and the dead busy with it! However, it is unclear whether these old women are replaced over time. Otherwise, the number of items they sew would be as countless as the sands of the Ganges, and the coins they earn would be too numerous to count. It is regrettable that this woman did not have the chance to ask more.

    898

    ○　窥井
    　　京西易州有古刹曰兴国，元所建也。三世尊皆梵象，高约二丈。以故殿宇崇峻，屺废后无力能修。然当每岁重阳，邑之人士，犹于节前十日瓣香奉佛，陈百货焉。一日盛夏，有老圃数人，与寺僧乘凉殿上。忽闻人呼声，四顾都无所见，骇极欲奔。其呼倍急，一人胆微巨，仰观焉。则天花之板本皆外方而内圆，一处脱落如井，有人俯首其间，且呼曰：“渴病难医，井中大哥盍予我一勺水，以济残喘。”众又大骇，而视其面貌似曾相识，盖左近佃人某也。于是共计首诸官，令人重梯攀援，良久始上。破殿瓦以入，取其人出，则已形状如呆。官命静摄一昼夜，始取讯之。供言：“某日入寺中，遇美妇人延至楼上。其居绝华美，饲以肥甘，与同寝处。每出必诫曰：‘院中有井，慎勿窥，窥则有祸。’昨因渴试一窥之，乃见人而不见水，是以呼，而不知何以竟在殿上也。”官知为狐所惑，召保逐出。至今郡人犹传以为异。

    Explanation

    898

    A Glimpse into the Well

    In the western part of the capital, there was an ancient temple called Xingguo, built during the Yuan Dynasty. The three main Buddhas were all depicted in foreign style, standing about two zhang tall. Due to the grand and towering halls, the temple fell into disrepair after its abandonment and no one had the means to restore it. However, every year on the Double Ninth Festival, the local people would still offer incense to Buddha and display various goods ten days before the festival. One hot summer day, several elderly gardeners were sitting in the temple's hall with the monks to cool off. Suddenly, they heard a human voice, but when they looked around, they saw nothing. They were so frightened that they wanted to run away. The voice grew even more urgent, and one of them, slightly braver, looked up. Then he saw that the ceiling boards, which were square on the outside and round on the inside, had one section that had fallen off like a well, and there was someone looking down into it, calling out: "My thirst is hard to cure. Well brother, could you give me a scoop of water to save my remaining breath?" Everyone was even more terrified, and when they looked at the person's face, they recognized him as a nearby tenant. They then gathered and reported the matter to the officials, who sent people with ladders to climb up. After a long time, they finally reached the top, broke through the temple's roof tiles to enter, and took the person out. By then, he was in a dazed state. The official ordered him to rest for a full day and night, and then questioned him. He said: "On a certain day, I entered the temple and met a beautiful woman who invited me to her upper room. Her residence was extremely luxurious, and she fed me rich and delicious food, and we slept together. Every time she left, she warned me: 'There is a well in the courtyard; be careful not to look into it, for if you do, you will bring misfortune.' Yesterday, because of thirst, I tried to look into it, and instead of seeing water, I saw a person, which is why I called out, but I don't know how I ended up on the temple's roof." The official realized that he had been bewitched by a fox spirit and summoned a spiritual protector to drive it out. To this day, the people of the region still talk about it as a strange event.

    899

    外史氏曰：古云“坐井观天”，此人竟窥并知地矣！夫以如是之狐，其伎俩正自有限。然使乡人得此，奚啻刘阮天台。乃因文园之渴，仍为眢井之蛙，良亦人生一可恨事。

    ○　巨蝎
    　　蓟郡一石桥，传言下有毒物，行旅相戒，莫敢休息。一日有贩生椒者，驱二蹇卫驮椒远来。时正清和月杪，苦热，小憩于桥梁。卸其椒笼，置诸石栏，驴亦散龁于草际。初不知其有毒也，披襟偃息，倦极熟眠。梦中似有风声，又窸窣作响，疑有人攘其椒，而猝不能醒。良久始寤，起视之，椒故依然。有巨物悬于栏侧，状如琵琶，灰青色，乃一蝎也，大骇欲奔。既而见其不动，近之谛观，则已为椒麻毙矣！其人异之，归并其椒，以一驴载蝎而行，首尾皆拂地焉。

    Explanation

    899

    Commentary by the Historian

    The historian says: Anciently it was said, "To look at the sky from the bottom of a well," yet this person, despite his limited perspective, knows more than he should! As for such a fox, its tricks are naturally limited. Yet, if a local person were to gain this knowledge, it would be no less miraculous than the story of Liu and Ruan at TianTai. However, because of Sima Xiangru's unrequited love, he remains a frog in a dry well, indeed a regrettable thing in life.

    The Giant Scorpion

    In the region of Jixian (蓟郡), there is a stone bridge, and it is rumored that there are poisonous creatures beneath it, so travelers warn each other and dare not rest there. One day, a man selling live peppers arrived, driving two donkeys to carry the peppers from afar. It was late in the month of Qinghe, and the weather was hot, so he took a break on the bridge. He unloaded the pepper baskets and placed them on the stone railing, and the donkeys grazed on the grass nearby. He did not know that the place was dangerous, so he unbuttoned his robe and lay down, exhausted and fell into a deep sleep. In his dream, he heard the sound of the wind, and there was a rustling noise, making him suspect that someone was stealing his peppers, but he suddenly could not wake up. After a long time, he woke up and looked around, and the peppers were still there. There was a huge creature hanging from the side of the railing, shaped like a lute, with an ashy gray color—it was a scorpion. He was greatly frightened and wanted to run away. But when he got closer and looked carefully, he saw that the scorpion had already been killed by the peppers and spices! The man was amazed, and on his way back, he gathered the peppers and placed the scorpion on a donkey to carry it, with its head and tail both touching the ground.

    900

    外史氏曰：传称椒房，谓甚能辟除恶物。然惟皇后居之，岂他人独无所畏耶？惟是椒之为物，其气甚烈，蝎巨如此，当之辄毙，况小于是蝎者乎？审是则蕃衍盈皕，蕃衍盈升，竟为家不可少者矣！随园老人曰：此一段简洁可喜。

    ○　梅异
    　　吴楚为灵秀之区，人文蔚起，而扫眉才子亦丛生其间，亦山川之气所钟也。吴郡一广文林姓，失其名，无锡人。家本儒素，学亦迂疏，青衿三十载，以拔贡补授斯职，时已耆艾之际矣。内人名娴，吴氏女，少从毘陵吴太母受学。诗成谢絮，文绍班香，一时闺秀罕出其右者。曾咏送春一绝曰：“预烦小玉为留春，倦倚飞花饯故人。此去莫教莺语老，再来好啭柳条新。”其风韵可见一斑。父亦邑庠，因林补官有日，适虚中馈，求婚于吴，父以其将宦，竟许之。花烛之夕，妇尚摽梅不足，夫早知命有余。从任数稔，署冷官闲，而林又庸腐异常，制艺而外无所解，倡随绝少，益觉无聊。

    Explanation

    900

    The commentator said: According to tradition, the "pepper room" is said to be very effective at repelling evil things. However, only the empress resides there; why should others be afraid? Only the nature of pepper is so strong that even a scorpion, when it encounters it, will be killed instantly. How much more so for those smaller than scorpions? If it is so prolific as to overflow, it ultimately becomes indispensable for the household. The Elder of Suigarden said: This passage is concise and delightful.

    ○　梅异
    　　Wu and Chu are regions of exceptional beauty and talent, and among them, talented women also flourish, reflecting the essence of the land. A scholar named Lin, from Wuxi, served as a local official. His family was scholarly, but he himself was somewhat rustic and unworldly, having spent thirty years in study before being appointed to this position, by which time he was already quite elderly. His wife, named Xian, was a daughter of the Wu family, who had learned under the tutelage of Lady Wu of Piling. She excelled in poetry and prose, surpassing most of her contemporaries. She once wrote a poem bidding farewell to spring: "Please ask Xiao Yu to hold back spring, as I grow weary of leaning on falling flowers to bid farewell to old friends. Do not let the orioles' song grow old on this departure, for when they return, they will sing anew on the fresh willow branches." Her grace and elegance can be seen in this verse. Her father, also a local scholar, sought a match for her because Lin was about to take up his official duties. Seeing that the position was vacant, he agreed to the marriage. On the night of their wedding, the wife still felt that her youth was not fully spent, while her husband knew that his days were numbered. After several years of service, Lin found himself in a cold and idle office, and he himself was unusually dull and uninterested in anything beyond the literary arts. With little companionship, he felt increasingly bored.

    901

    幸姑苏诸名媛耳聆其名者，咸备珠绣之仪前往执贽，而明伦堂后，又增一班玉筍，吴之心始少慰。林以官守无多，亦任其酬酢，不之禁。癸未仲春，林已年逼耳顺，因年老癃病乞休。上官亦无留之者，择吉遂行。诸女伴闻之，情不能舍，举携酒饯之江皋。是日也，香舆隘岸，采缆盈堤，珠翠迷离，桂兰四射，较之二疏解组时，反以妍艳胜之，林亦与有荣施矣！离樽甫罄，祖帐未收，正当握手言别，吴忽昏绝倒地。众大惊，围绕呼号，竟莫能醒，舟遂因之不发。乃吴方与众申谢，俄见一老妪蹒跚伛偻自蓬底而来，貌甚丑恶。突前以气相嘘，其寒如觱栗之风，吴遂莫知人事。迷惘中妪犹在目，且把其袂曰：“孺人无惧，我奉诸姊命，相屈一叙耳，岂真以鲁莽相祸哉？”言已，曳之使行。其力甚猛，吴不得已而从之。

    Explanation

    901

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    902

    因复登岸，又有妖婢二人，姿容娟秀，拥一五色云軿，向前迎迓。见妪即笑曰：“如此速客，亦太恶。”妪亦笑曰：“老妇固善于劝驾者，若儿曹辈敛皀百拜，未必能屈玉趾也。”即促吴登车。吴心惴惴不敢前，妪又强之，然后挽绥以上。犹未安席，舆忽飘然，仿佛若在半天。吴大骇欲堕，妪与婢左右挟持之，且笑曰：“小胆如此，岂廿年以前未尝御风而行耶？幸少安，行且至矣！”瞬息果至一处，岛屿潆洄，楼阁重叠，绝似虎阜之西。而山林花卉之胜，栋宇轮奂之奇，若别有佳境，吴心始少定。仰见云居轩敞，其牌额皆古篆，文不可识辨。其车停于闾侧，更有小鬟状若司阍，貌尤妖冶，见吴皆含笑相迎，扶策使下，一如夙昔所亲狎者。

    Explanation

    902

    He then climbed back onto the shore, and there were two female attendants with a beautiful and graceful appearance, carrying a colorful cloud chariot. They greeted him with a smile and said, "Such a fast guest, you're also too bad." The old woman also smiled and said, "I am indeed skilled at persuasion. If the younger generation would bow a hundred times, perhaps they couldn't make you step down." She then urged Wu to get into the chariot. Wu was trembling with fear and did not dare to move forward, but the old woman insisted, and he was finally helped up. Just as he had not settled into his seat, the carriage suddenly moved lightly, as if floating in the middle of the sky. Wu was greatly frightened and about to fall, but the old woman and the attendants held him on both sides and laughed, saying, "Such a small heart, how could you not have flown on the wind twenty years ago? Please be patient, we will soon arrive!" In an instant, they arrived at a place with winding islands and overlapping pavilions and towers, absolutely similar to the west of Hu Fu. The beauty of the mountains, forests, and flowers, and the extraordinary splendor of the buildings, as if there were another beautiful place, made Wu's heart begin to feel a little more at ease. Looking up, he saw the spacious and open cloud dwelling, with plaques all in ancient seal script, but the text could not be recognized. The carriages stopped by the gate, and there were also young maidservants who looked like gatekeepers, with particularly alluring appearances. They all greeted Wu with smiles, helped him down, and treated him just like those he had been close to in the past.

    903

    吴方降舆，异香遄发，馥郁清芬，直达于门屏之外。及入而觇之，则老梅数百株合抱参天，花繁干密，杂植垣墉之内，始悟香所由来。益入而深，树且益伙。中一堂，连亘十数楹，鸟革翚飞，朱甍画栋。吴未及阶，妪早入报。倏见湘帘启处，有丽人十余，接裾而出，笑语曰：“婆子草草，几惊予妹，我辈尚须负荆。”吴睨之，衣素者十之六，衣绿者十之三，衣绯则一人而已。其衣愈淡，其貌愈妍，皆非目中所及觏。众既降阶，逊吴先登。其室远胜芝兰，而古雅绝伦，一琴一书，亦自有孤标别韵。而丽人之妆束，又皆不浓不淡，风格天然。至于衣之肥瘦，袖之长短，无不体裁尽善，信非慧心莫办焉。吴于此自觉形秽，愈加谦卑。众推之宾席，三让而后坐。

    Explanation

    903

    Wu was just descending from his carriage when a strange fragrance suddenly burst forth, rich and fragrant, reaching directly to the gate and screen. Upon entering to take a look, he saw hundreds of ancient plum trees, their trunks embracing the sky, with dense and abundant flowers, scattered within the walls and fences. He finally realized where the fragrance came from. Going deeper and deeper, the trees also became more numerous. In the middle was a hall, extending for ten or more bays, with ornate and colorful architectural features. Wu had not even reached the steps when the old woman entered to report. Suddenly, he saw the silk curtain being drawn aside, and more than ten beautiful women emerged one after another, laughing and speaking: "The old woman was so hasty, almost alarming my sister. We still need to carry firewood." Wu glanced at them: six out of ten wore white, three out of ten wore green, and only one wore red. The more subdued their clothing, the more beautiful they appeared, none of whom he had ever seen before. The group descended the steps, yielding to Wu to enter first. Their room far surpassed the fragrance of orchids and lilies, and was uniquely ancient and elegant, with one zither and one book, also having a unique and distinct charm. The women's attire was neither too heavy nor too light, with a natural style. As for the thickness and thinness of their clothes, the length and shortness of their sleeves, all were perfectly tailored, truly, only a wise heart could accomplish this. Wu felt inferior in comparison, becoming even more humble. The group pushed him to the seat of honor, and after three refusals, he finally sat down.

    904

    方将展询，一衣碧绡者遽启曰：“适游馆娃宫，见诸妮子为妹折柳，张筵设饮，意气甚都，大为闺中人生色。回忆畴昔，顿思良晤，因遣孟婆奉屈，幸勿以唐突见罪。”语竟起谢。吴本具林下风，不作儿女子态，亦逊曰：“郢中三曲，惟下里和者为多，白雪阳春反未之逮。姊言之，能勿令人颜汗？”遂亦答拜。众闻其言，相顾而言曰：“谈吐自是我辈个中人，固不昧其本来也。”因笑曰：“卿二人勿甚癰谦，我辈当以情告。昨天姊自钱塘邮寄赋题一纸，我辈短才，竟莫能就。闻妹以妙文屈抑群美，故乘行旌未发，邀来为我操刀，幸勿峻拒。”语次，婢早捧砚而前。吴起辞曰：“深闺倦绣，短咏尤艰。

    Explanation

    904

    As Wu was about to inquire, a person dressed in blue silk quickly began to say, "Just now I visited the Gua Wa Palace and saw the young ladies breaking off willow branches for the sister, laying out a banquet and setting up drinks, the atmosphere was very elegant, greatly enhancing the reputation of the women in the inner chambers. Recalling the past, I suddenly thought of a good meeting, therefore sent the spirit guide to invite you, please do not consider this sudden invitation as an offense." After finishing speaking, she bowed in gratitude. Wu naturally possesses the manner of a recluse, does not act like a child or a woman, and humbly said, "The 'Three Songs of Ying,' only the simple and common people can harmonize with it, but the 'White Snow and Yang Chun' have not yet been reached. What the sister says, can one not feel embarrassed?" And also replied with a bow. The others heard what he said and looked at each other and said, "The way we speak is naturally that of our kind, naturally does not obscure our true nature." They then laughed and said, "You two should not be too modest, we should tell you the truth. Yesterday, the sister from Qiantang mailed a sheet of assignment, our limited talent, were unable to complete it. Heard that you wrote a wonderful piece that overshadowed everyone else's beauty, therefore, taking advantage of the departure not yet happening, invited you to come and write for us, please do not refuse harshly." While speaking, the maid quickly brought the inkstone forward. Wu politely declined and said, "Tired of embroidery in the deep inner chambers, short poems are especially difficult."

    905

    若属以大斧长矛鲜克奏绩，况古人十年不就者而欲妹一朝成之，不亦邻于苛耶？”众笑曰：“妹自敏捷过我辈。脱遇女左思，则不敢以此相强。”于是布白玉之几，进绿沉之管。碧衣者又曰：“盍少为作者润笔，勿使人手劲肠枯。”遂以金叵罗进酒，大可数升，其酒绀碧色，香清且腴，众劝曰：“此梅花之精，即妹在罗浮以之饮赵君者，今还奉觞予妹矣。”吴始恍然，知前身亦在暗香疏影中也。乃欣然强尽其半，索题视之。众便出锦笺，细楷端研，有序曰：“客夕对月，偶有所思，昔先征君在日，值此蟾魄清莹，彼咏我笑，恒至子夜不休。今征君往矣，复觏此境，几令孤山三百树一时欲化杜鹃枝，伤何如也！故予亦不能遽睡，致效海棠婢子长恋黑甜，因取旧句为赋。

    Explanation

    905

    "If you all rarely succeed with great axes and long spears, how can we expect your sister to achieve it in one day, let alone ancient people who did not achieve it in ten years? Is it not also close to being harsh?" The others laughed and said, "Your sister is naturally more agile than us. If you meet a woman like Zuo Si, we would not dare to force her to do it." Then they set up a table of white jade, brought out green inkstones, and a jade-wrapped brush. The one in green clothes said, "Why don't you write a little to moisten the brush, don't let people's hands become stiff and their hearts dry out." They then offered a golden cup of wine, which was large enough to hold several liters. The wine was a deep blue color, fragrant and rich. They encouraged, "This is the essence of the plum blossom, the one your sister drank with Zhao Jun in Luofu. Now we offer it to your sister again." Wu suddenly realized, knowing that his previous life was also in the shadow of faint fragrance. He then happily drank half of it, asked to see the inscription. The others then took out brocade paper, fine calligraphy, and ink, and wrote an introduction saying, "One evening, facing the moon, I happened to think of something. When my late father-in-law was alive, when the moon was bright and clear, he would recite poetry and I would laugh, continuing until midnight. Now my late father-in-law is gone, when I see this scene again, it almost made the 300 plum trees on Solitary Mountain turn into azalea branches. How sad it is! So I also couldn't sleep immediately, to emulate the maid of the crabapple who longs for sweet dreams, so I took old verses to compose a poem."

    906

    犹未操觚，即遣鹤儿衔与诸妹，倘有清兴，请先挥毫。庶几世外佳人，又添一段佳话耳！”尾署“愚姊林门梅氏敛衽拜”。其赋题则“爱月夜眠迟”也。吴已技痒，遂不复辞，染翰构思，摛华掞藻，不半日而赋成。众叠肩诵之，其中警绝者有曰：“纵高洁以自怜，亦团圞之可爱。”又曰：“蕊珠宫外，误香梦于凭栏；群玉峰头，骋花魂而入月。”又曰：“月姊可怜人，须念今夕之眷眷；素娥真好我，必无来日之迟迟。”约二三百言，比偶工整。众皆诚服，赞曰：“洗刷殆尽，掷地金声，我辈宜拜下风。”因复命酒酬谢，且将肆筵相款。吴亟止之曰：“荷蒙宠召，聊为塞责。顷领玉液，已醉矣！扬帆者实不能再待，请即遣归，是所切望。

    Explanation

    906

    Not yet having taken up writing, she immediately sent a crane child to carry the message to her sisters, requesting that if they had the mood or inspiration, they should start writing first. Perhaps this will add another beautiful story to the tales of the world’s finest women!” At the end, she signed, “Silly Sister Linmen Mei, respectfully bowing.” The topic of the poem was “Love Moon Night Sleeps Late.” Wu, already eager to write, did not decline further. She took up her brush and began to think and plan, spreading out the brilliance and displaying the elegance, and within a short time, the poem was finished. The crowd pressed together to recite it, and among the most striking phrases were these: “Even in self-admiration for one’s purity and nobility, there is still the loveliness of being whole and complete.” Another phrase said: “Outside the Palace of Pearl and Jade, one mistakes fragrant dreams for leaning on the railing; at the peak of the Jade Mountain, one gallops with the spirit of flowers into the moon.” Yet another phrase said: “The Moon Sister pities the person, must remember the affectionate feelings of tonight; the White Lady truly loves me, there will be no delay in the future.” The poem was about two or three hundred words long, with well-arranged paired lines. All were truly convinced and praised, saying: “Thoroughly cleansed, like gold when thrown to the ground, we should bow to the wind.” They then ordered wine to thank her, and prepared a feast to entertain her. Wu quickly stopped them, saying: “Grateful for the honored invitation, I just fulfilled the duty. Just now, I drank the jade liquor and am already drunk! The one who sails really cannot wait any longer, please send me back immediately, this is what I deeply desire.”

    907

    ”众笑曰：“妹犹怜冬烘夫子哉！虽然，既已久羁，不宜复留。俟明岁梅花国中，再当相晤。”遂唤前妪并二婢，仍送之还。众亦相送之门外，且嘱曰：“妹以师雍之故，有涉凡情，因遭帝谴，堕落人间。虽不得意于痴翁，亦既旋归有日。努力自爱，慎勿过于僝僽。”嘱讫，俟吴登舆，始退入。车行较前倍速，俄顷至舟。吴将摄衣自下，忽飓风大作，所乘顿覆。吴大号，恍若梦魇。及寤而视之，祖饯者还在左右，谓吴已死，将与之更衣受含。吴乃缅述其故，更诵其赋如流水，人皆惊异。盖死已阅三时矣。诸女始告归，林乃入舱慰视。明日解维，遂尔长往。期年，锡山人至，好事者争询之，则吴果彩云散矣！所谓一岁之约，不爽若是。但恨无驿使来自岭头，竟不知群花相见，更自何著作，足以脍炙人口也！

    Explanation

    907

    The crowd laughed and said, "Does the sister still pity the foolish scholar? Although she has already been detained for a long time, it is not right to leave her any longer. Wait until next year in the land of plum blossoms, and we will meet again." They then called the old woman and the two maidservants to return her home. The crowd also saw her off at the door and said, "Sister, because of Master Shiyong, you became involved in worldly emotions, and as a result, you were punished by the emperor and fell into the human world. Although you are not pleased with the foolish old man, you will return soon. Try to take care of yourself and be careful not to be too hard on yourself." After giving these instructions, they waited until Wu got into the carriage and then retreated inside. The carriage traveled much faster than before, and soon they reached the boat. Wu was about to get out of the carriage, but suddenly a strong wind arose, and the carriage overturned. Wu cried out in fear, as if she were in a nightmare. When she woke up, she saw that the people who had seen her off were still around her, and they told her that she had already died and would be prepared for burial. Wu then recounted her story in detail and recited the poem as smoothly as flowing water. Everyone was amazed. It turned out that she had been dead for three hours. The women then returned home, and Lin went into the cabin to comfort her. The next day, the boat set sail, and Wu was gone forever. A year later, someone from Xishan arrived, and the curious people asked about her. It turned out that Wu had indeed dispersed like a cloud. The one-year promise was fulfilled. However, they regretted that there was no messenger from the mountains to tell them what the flowers did when they met, and what works they produced that were widely praised and popular.

    908

    外史氏曰：梅花赋出自广平，今竟自欲赋矣，且倩人赋矣。究之所倩之人，即属广平赋中人，而又不屑自赋。且借爱月以为赋，其赋遂香艳千古，其人亦香艳千古，其人之事更香艳千古矣！然非梅之香艳，其人虽足传，其赋虽足诵，其事究不足异也。惟孟婆一举，似乎孟浪，而因落梅之风，少缓山阳之笛，亦既有功于吴。况为梅之所畏者，一旦见役于梅，又不足为群芳吐气也耶？随园老人曰：梅之含酸，宜其薄命。然自罗浮梦后，纪载无闻，得此犹堪补入赋科。

    Explanation

    908

    The historian said: The ode to the plum blossom originated from Guangping, but now it is being composed by someone else, and even asked to be written by another. Upon closer examination, the person commissioned to write it is actually the one from the original ode of Guangping, yet they disdain to compose it themselves. Instead, they borrow the love of the moon as the theme for their ode, and thus the ode becomes fragrant and beautiful for all time, the person becomes fragrant and beautiful for all time, and the person's story becomes even more fragrant and beautiful for all time! However, it is not the fragrance and beauty of the plum blossom that makes the person worthy of remembrance, the ode worthy of recitation, and the story worthy of note. Only Meng Po's single act, which seems rash, but because of the gentle breeze of falling plum blossoms, slightly delays the melancholic flute of Shanyang, has also benefited Wu. Moreover, those who fear the plum blossom, once subjected to its influence, cannot be considered as contributing to the fragrance of all flowers? The old man from Sui Yuan said: The plum blossom's bitterness is fitting for its unfortunate fate. However, since the dream on Luofu Mountain, it has not been recorded in history, and this ode can still be added to the category of literary works.

    909

    ○　童之杰
    　　武生童之杰，滦州人。尝蓄一利剑，自云能斩鬼狐，人固未之信。某年秋，负之行山东道上，旅人有询者，辄津津道之。且曰：“吾持此刃，虽不能学万人敌，然遇魔鬼邪妖，不难一一断之。彼区区诛军斩将者，犹是人间钝器耳！”言之数数，即有好奇者欲试其能。因先与结交，行止皆共。时济上有巨家，所居宅第为妖所据，旷之而迁于邑中。其人深知之，一日合宿其地，乃绐童曰：“溽暑犹蒸，旅邸人满，有一清凉国，君能从我游乎？”童询焉，则曰：“知交有别墅，实在于是。

    Explanation

    909

    Wusheng Tang Zhijie, a martial artist from Luanzhou, once owned a sharp sword, claiming he could cut down ghosts and fox spirits, though people did not believe him. One autumn, he was traveling on the road in Shandong, carrying the sword. When travelers asked about it, he would eagerly recount its power. He said, “I wield this blade, and though I cannot match the prowess of a warrior who can face ten thousand enemies, when I encounter demons, devils, and evil spirits, I can easily sever them one by one. Those who merely kill soldiers and generals are still just dull tools of the world!” He spoke of it often, and soon there were those curious to test his abilities. They first befriended him, sharing the same movements and actions. At the time, there was a wealthy family in Jishi whose residence had been occupied by a demon and was abandoned, relocated to the county town. The person knew this well, and one day, they shared a lodging there and tricked Tang Zhijie, saying, “The intense heat still lingers, and the inn is full of people. There is a cool and refreshing place; would you like to travel with me?” Tang Zhijie asked, and they replied, “A close friend has a summer house, and it is actually here.”

    910

    倘偕访之，今夕东道主有所在矣！”童欣然从之，并迹而前。至则天已向晦，其人导以往，户犹未闭，阒其无人。盖巨家缘有怪异，知无敢入者，遂不慎管钥也。两人襆被径入，甫及中门，其人佯惊曰：“何洒扫亦无一二？君姑处此，待予往左近觅之。”童视第中颇修整，故亦不疑。旦恃其武勇，慨然曰，“诺。”其人即趋出。至外，即阖其扉，且以革带系双环，谅童无能飞越，喜笑而去。童久俟不至，心窃讶之，欲出，则门已扃。顿悟曰：“若人殆欲试我术也！幸有剑在，予何惧焉？”于是即庭侧一室，拂其尘榻而处。时已昏暝，亦不遑深入，按剑良久，寂无见闻，遂倦而思寝。

    Explanation

    910

    If we go to visit together, the host for tonight is now available!” The child happily followed and proceeded. Upon arrival, it was already dusk, and the person led them inside, where the door was still not closed, and the place was quiet and empty. It seemed that because of some strange occurrences, the wealthy family knew no one dared to enter, so they were careless about locking the doors and gates. The two of them went inside with their bedding. As soon as they reached the central gate, the person pretended to be surprised and said, “How there is no cleaning or tidying up at all? You please stay here; I will go nearby to find some.” The child saw that the house was quite tidy, so he did not suspect anything. In the morning, relying on his martial courage, he confidently said, “Okay.” The person then quickly left. Outside, he immediately closed the door and tied the door ring with a leather belt, believing that the child could not fly over, and laughed and left. The child waited for a long time without seeing him return, and secretly felt surprised. When he wanted to leave, the door was already locked. Suddenly, he realized and said, “This person probably wants to test my skills! Fortunately, I have a sword, so what do I fear?” So he went to a room on the side of the courtyard, cleared the dusty mat and sat down. It was already dark, and there was no time to explore further. He held the sword for a long time, silently and without seeing or hearing anything, and became tired and wanted to sleep.

    911

    甫及安枕，窸窣有声，窗牖故无片纸，伏而窥觇，淡月之下，见一人长仅尺余，侏儒肥矮，往来巡阶而走，状似狐。因厉声叱之，倏不见。童遂以为无足虞，弛然复卧。俄而火光顿炽，可鉴须眉。有异物高与檐等，面瓜色，双眸如盌，灼灼然，火光即从此出。周身皆绿毛，约长数寸，甚可怖畏。童不觉股栗，强掣其锋，虚声相向。物笑曰：“此剑尽足割鸡，奈何大言欺人耶？”其音如鸦，响震屋宇，童之剑早已坠地。正仓皇间，忽闻环珮之声，物竟敛迹。童因定性，视之，遥见纱笼数对，出自庭后。比及所卧室前，则一中年小妇，靓妆炫服，导以小鬟十数，咸衣红紫。童心诧妖异，屏息无言。

    Explanation

    911

    Just as he was about to rest his head on the pillow, there was a rustling sound. There was no paper at all in the windows and doors. He crouched down and peeped out cautiously. Under the dim moonlight, he saw a person no taller than a foot, a short and fat dwarf, wandering back and forth on the steps, looking like a fox. He shouted loudly at it, and it suddenly disappeared. The boy then thought there was nothing to worry about and relaxed, lying down again. Soon, the firelight suddenly became intense, bright enough to see one's eyebrows and beard. There was a strange creature as tall as the eaves, with a pumpkin-colored face, eyes like bowls, glowing brightly, and the firelight came from this spot. The creature was covered in green fur, about an inch long, very frightening. The boy felt a chill in his legs and bravely brandished his sword, shouting loudly in its face. The creature laughed and said, "This sword is more than capable of cutting a chicken, how can you make such grandiose claims?" Its voice sounded like a crow, shaking the house, and the boy's sword had already fallen to the ground. Just as he was in a panic, he suddenly heard the sound of jade pendants, and the creature suddenly disappeared. The boy then calmed down and looked around, seeing pairs of gauze lanterns coming from behind the courtyard. As they approached his bedroom, a middle-aged woman appeared, elaborately dressed and adorned, led by a dozen or so young maids, all dressed in red and purple. The boy was amazed at the strange sight and held his breath, remaining silent.

    912

    妇人排闼入室，命小鬟以烛照之，微哂曰：“看竹亦问主人，何擅作入幕之宾？无怪厮养不容也。”言讫，北面就坐，呼童以礼相见乙童既无能逋，强起而揖之。潜睨其容，黛绿粉白，犹存风韵。因窃计，巨鬼畏此，此必其中巨擘。予受此剑，本未一试，顷因气馁，致为鬼所揶揄，今值孱弱，犹不用武，岂真束手待毙乎？见妇人指挥从婢，似将不利于己，意殊莫测，乃俯掇其剑，挺而刺之。妇人忽回眸一笑曰：“汝犹以剑侠自居耶？予以好生，不戕汝命，且欲讲济世之术。汝乃包藏祸心，反以予试此刃，是诚不可救药者矣。汝既有利器，请断予首。倘不能效，弗可活也。”言次，侧身向童，命其尝试。

    Explanation

    912

    A woman pushed open the door and entered the room, ordering a young maid to light a candle. With a slight smile, she said, "Looking at bamboo, you also ask the owner, how dare you act as a close confidant? No wonder the servants cannot tolerate you." After speaking, she sat facing north and called the child to greet him formally. The child, unable to escape, reluctantly bowed to her. He discreetly glanced at her appearance, noting her makeup of dark green and pale white, which still retained a graceful charm. He thought to himself, "The powerful ghost fears this woman, so she must be a leading figure in this realm. I received this sword but had not yet tested it, and my lack of confidence led to being mocked by the ghost. Now, despite my weakness, I still do not use it; am I truly waiting to die? Seeing the woman commanding her maids, it seems she might harm me, and her intentions are utterly mysterious. So, he picked up the sword and stabbed her. The woman suddenly turned her head and smiled, saying, "Do you still consider yourself a swordswoman? I value life and do not want to kill you; instead, I wish to teach you a method to save the world. You have hidden malicious intentions and tested me with this blade, truly beyond redemption. You have a sharp weapon; please cut off my head. If you cannot, you will not survive." As she spoke, she turned sideways toward the child and ordered him to try.

    913

    童惧，复弃其剑，伏地曰：“予不敢矣！”妇人又笑曰：“孺子尚知分量，似可教也。”乃令起坐，与语曰：“予实红线之流，神于剑术，非若子之碌碌者。因此地鬼狐为祟，特来居之，怪皆远遁。向之试子者，皆予之仆隶为之，非真妖也。而主人不知予仙，犹以为怪，遂亦久无人居。适在后庭，闻仆言子有重宝而其气不扬，故宵行而来，将授子以异求，岂有他哉！”童闻言大喜，长跽请教，欲求为师。妇人命取所掷之剑，拂拭再三，谓之曰：“此道家荡魔剑也，非吾辈所用者，故须人力，始克奏功。若吾剑之飞腾变化，则行之无阻矣。虽然，子实负此剑，非剑之负子。

    913

    The child was fearful, discarded his sword, and knelt on the ground, saying, "I dare not!" The woman laughed and said, "The child still knows his limits, seems to be teachable." She then ordered him to sit up and spoke to him, "I am truly of the lineage of the Red Thread, skilled in swordsmanship, unlike you who are mediocre. Because spirits and foxes were causing trouble here, I came to reside and all the strange phenomena fled. Those who tested you were my servants, not true demons. But the master did not know I was a spirit, still thinking it was a strange phenomenon, so no one has lived here for a long time. Just now in the back garden, I heard the servant say you have valuable treasures but your aura is not prominent, so I came at night to offer you something special, what else could there be!" The child, upon hearing this, was greatly delighted and knelt long to ask for instruction, wanting to seek her as his teacher. The woman commanded the sword that had been thrown to be retrieved, wiped it clean multiple times, and said to him, "This is a Daoist sword for dispelling demons, not the kind we use, so it requires human effort to be effective. If my sword could fly and change, it would be unimpeded. Although, you are carrying this sword, not the sword carrying you."

    914

    吾授子口诀，再以符水煮是剑，则天下之魔，不难尽荡矣。”童益踊跃请教，妇人遂言曰：“天心正大，吾法正直，荡涤邪秽，肃清一世。”授讫，即令小鬟持剑去，以某符和某水煮之，以光起为度。且谓童曰：“剑非不利，但遭世尘埃，亦不免钝矣。”因与童坐语，历言剑侠行事。并戒童以正心济物，不然，剑虽通灵，其为宝也几何？童一一敬诺。又许时，婢以剑出，则精光进露，非复向之暗淡无华，童遂再拜祗受。妇人又叮嘱数言，始返内，时已漏下五鼓。童少寐，天已侵晨，亟起整衣。将诣庭辞谢，而小鬟已出，又以一革囊相付曰：“夫人有命，人神路殊，不宜数数相见。

    Explanation

    914

    I will provide the final translation of the source text, applying the insights from the pre-drafting research to ensure it is both faithful to the original and reads naturally in English.

    "I will teach you the secret formula, and then boil this sword with talisman water. Thus, the demons of the world will not be difficult to completely eliminate." The child eagerly sought to learn, and the woman then said, "The will of heaven is just and vast, my method is upright and direct, cleansing impurities and evil, clearing the world of corruption." After the teaching was completed, she immediately ordered the young maid to take the sword and boil it with a certain talisman and water, until the light rises. She also told the child, "The sword is not unsharp, but it has been affected by the dust of the world, and thus it cannot help but become dull." She then sat with the child and spoke at length about the ways of a swordsmen. She also warned the child to cultivate a pure heart and help others, otherwise, even though the sword is spiritually aware, how much is it worth as a treasure? The child respectfully agreed to each point. After a while, the maid returned with the sword, and its brilliance was now fully revealed, no longer dark and dull as before. The child then bowed twice and respectfully received the sword. The woman gave further instructions and then returned inside, by which time it was already past the fifth watch of the night. The child had little sleep, and by dawn, he quickly got up and dressed. He was about to go to the courtyard to bid farewell and thank her, but the young maid had already come out and handed him a leather bag, saying, "The lady has given an order, the paths of humans and spirits are different, and it is not appropriate to meet frequently."

    915

    君持此囊去，尽收天壤魔妖，十年后始可寻晤于武当山，彼时再倾囊细叙也。”童又拜受，小鬟即入。童甫出，绐之者早俟于门，笑而询之曰：“此中颇安乐否？”童心甚恚，然遵正直之教，不敢讳，为缅述之。末曰：“子实杀我，而予之剑赖子以神，恩怨可以两忘矣！”遂别去，不再与言，其人亦疑信参半。嗣闻童在江右颇著神奇，且出家为道士，代人驱遣，不受一钱，始为之骇异。余在邗江时，曾闻童之神术奇迹甚多，姑举其一二即足慴人。扬州有一妇，初患瘵疾，既而梦与鬼交。其鬼面目臃肿，毫无血色，且体冷如冰。每至，妇即沉顿，颇不可当。

    Explanation

    915

    The gentleman took this bag and went to gather all the evil spirits in the world. Ten years later, he would be able to meet again at Wudang Mountain, where he would then reveal the contents of the bag in detail. The child bowed and accepted the command, and the young maid entered. As soon as the child left, the one who had deceived him was already waiting at the door, smiling and asking, "Was it very pleasant inside?" The child was very angry, but he followed the teachings of integrity and did not hide anything, recounting the experience in detail. At the end, he said, "You actually killed me, and my sword became powerful because of you. Gratitude and resentment can both be forgotten!" He then left without speaking further, and the person was half-believing and half-doubting. Later, it was heard that the child was very famous for his magical abilities in Jiangxi, and he became a Taoist priest, helping others without accepting any payment, which initially surprised and amazed people. When I was in Yangzhou, I heard many miraculous stories about the child. Even a few examples would be enough to scare people. There was a woman in Yangzhou who initially suffered from a wasting disease. She then dreamed of having intercourse with a ghost. The ghost had a swollen face, no color, and a body as cold as ice. Every time the ghost appeared, the woman became extremely weak and could not withstand it.

    916

    其家延童祛之，童诘其形状，笑曰：“此一覆没之鬼也。”乃不入妇室，携众直至江干。夜深鬼果出，童突前擒之，删剁剖剥，流水满地，其臭不可闻，相偕者皆掩鼻。童遂纳之入囊，鬼犹啧啧作响，荷之而行。妇始安，半年而愈。又某县富家，有女亦为狐祟。昼则白身卧闺中，夜则凝妆以起。侍儿窥之，都无所见，惟衾枕时有长毫数十茎，则狐之所遗也。童闻之，佩剑而往。至即于富家门侧，拔剑挥之，应手而倒。得一狐，长近三尺，血殷然已垂毙。童乃剖狐之心，用以疗女，病良已。仍以囊贮此狐，徐徐而去。人视其囊约二尺，盛诸异物，无不包容，不禁咋舌骇叹。童既为黄冠，不归故里。至戊辰岁，作武当之游，遂不复返。

    Explanation

    916

    The family invited a young boy to perform an exorcism. The boy asked about the ghost's appearance and laughed, saying, "This is a drowned ghost." He did not enter the woman's room but led a group to the riverbank. Late at night, the ghost indeed appeared, and the boy rushed forward to capture it. He hacked, chopped, dissected, and peeled the ghost, causing water to run all over the ground, and the smell was unbearable. Those who accompanied him all covered their noses. The boy then put the ghost into a bag, and the ghost still made a sound. He carried it and went. The woman began to feel safe and recovered after half a year. Another wealthy family in a certain county had a daughter who was also plagued by a fox spirit. During the day, she lay naked in her inner room, and at night, she applied makeup and got up. A maid peeked at her but saw nothing, only occasionally finding long hairs on the bedding, dozens of strands, which were left by the fox. The boy heard about it and went wearing a sword. He arrived and immediately went to the side of the wealthy family's door, drew his sword, and swung it. With a single movement, it fell. He obtained one fox, nearly three feet long, which was bloodied and already nearly dead. The boy then dissected the fox's heart and used it to treat the woman, and her illness was greatly cured. He still stored this fox in a bag and left slowly. People saw his bag, about two feet, holding various strange things, nothing excluded, and could not help being speechless and shocked. The boy already became a Taoist priest and did not return to his hometown. Until the year of Wu Chen, he went on a trip to Mount Wudang and did not return again.

    917

    ○　外史氏曰：或疑授童之诀寥寥数言，必非兰亭真本。余谓即正直一语，可当百千符篆，并剑亦可无庸，况诀乎！何则？胆之巨细，由于气之盈虚。而气之盈虚，原于心之邪正。正而以直行之，即孟子所谓“浩然”者也。仙佛人神，不外此二字，剑侠更无论矣。独是同一剑也，懦则试辄不利，勇则所向无前，剑固灵以人也？故夫天下有大勇者，不必有剑，而亦神钦鬼伏矣。

    Explanation

    917

    The Historian says: Some doubt that the brief method for teaching children, consisting of just a few words, cannot be the original or authentic version of the Lanting Xu. I say that the concept of integrity alone can replace a multitude of instructions and even a sword. How so? The size of one's courage depends on the fullness or emptiness of one's energy. And the fullness or emptiness of one's energy originates from the righteousness or wickedness of one's heart. If one is upright and acts with integrity, that is what Mencius called "浩然" (boundless and magnificent). Immortals, Buddhas, humans, and deities are no exception to this principle, and heroic swordsmen are even more so. Indeed, the same sword can be ineffective if wielded by a timid person, but it becomes unstoppable when wielded by a brave one. The sword is indeed responsive to the person wielding it. Therefore, a person of great courage does not need a sword, and they can still impress and subdue even gods and spirits.

    918

    ○　卷三
    　　杨秋娥
    　　晋省有书院，未询其名，盖司牧者所建，以振铎之地也。邑诸生朱燮，年仅弱冠，肄业其中，谆谨嗜学，主是院者，举刮目焉。丁巳冬杪，将归度岁，院中人咸束其书，朱亦自理残编。忽得诗笺，大如掌，草书若簪花，诵之，其句曰：“莲房留莲子，莲子不肯住。一旦入金盘，空房泣秋露。”语极哀艳，颇类古乐府。朱因爱玩，不释手。及抵家，粘之壁上，暇即吟哦，初不知为闺人之作也。时值除夕，酬酢颇繁，迨至人日以后，渐能约伴出游。唐魏素勤俭，妇人女子间或荆布微行。所云如云如荼者，得未曾有。

    Explanation

    918

    Volume Three

    Yang Qiuhua

    In the province of Jin, there is a school, the name of which is unknown, as it was built by officials to serve as a place of learning. A local student named Zhu Xie, who was just twenty years old, was studying there. He was known for his diligence and love of learning, and the head of the academy was impressed by him. At the end of winter in the year Ding Si, he was preparing to return home for the New Year. The people in the academy were packing their books, and Zhu was also organizing his old writings. Suddenly, he found a poem note, large as a palm, written in cursive script like flowers. When he read it, the poem described a lotus pod holding lotus seeds, which were unwilling to stay, and once placed in a golden dish, the empty pod wept autumn dew. The poem was extremely sorrowful and beautiful, similar to ancient folk songs. Zhu loved the poem so much that he could not put it down. When he returned home, he stuck it on the wall and often recited it when he had free time, not realizing that it was written by a woman. During the New Year's Eve, there were many social obligations, and after the seventh day of the first lunar month, he gradually started to go out with friends. Women and girls sometimes traveled in plain clothes, often used to describe a person disguising themselves. What is described as a large number of people, like clouds and thistles, is something that has never been seen before.

    919

    朱逐队游邀，偶过一巷，忽见平屋之上，有妇人以面巾罥首，荏苒而行，双钩彳亍，似甚纤细，不觉属目。及近，则练裙布衣俱甚精洁，而素丝朱履益更动人。但未望见颜色，颇以为憾。乃积雪虽消，春冰微滑，妇竟捉足不定，延至众前，竟如片云飞坠矣。众大哗，妇适落朱怀抱，朱亟以两腕夹持，幸无损伤。而蹉跌之余，面巾自揭，众眺之，蝉鬓玉颜，天然绝艳，则女子也。众瞩之地，不胜羞涩，赖去其家伊迩，如青鸾垂翅而遁。众既为之醉心，不暇瞻顾，惟朱见红笺一幅委于地上，知女子所遗，亟拾而袖之，同人又皆茫然。女去已远，互相嘲戏，以朱未有室，争谑之。朱惟含笑不答，意中亦颇觉自得。洎乎与众分袂，不俟至家，即出笺于袖。

    Explanation

    919

    Zhu was wandering in a group, and by chance passed through an alley. Suddenly, he saw a woman on a flat house, wearing a face towel, walking leisurely. Her steps were slow and hesitant, appearing very slender. Unconsciously, he was drawn to her. As he approached, he noticed that her plain dress and simple clothes were very neat and clean, and her white silk shoes with red soles were even more captivating. However, he had not seen her face, which he found somewhat disappointing. The accumulated snow had melted, but the early spring ice was slightly slippery, causing the woman to lose her footing and fall towards the crowd, like a piece of cloud falling gracefully. The crowd exclaimed in surprise, and the woman happened to fall into Zhu's arms. Zhu quickly held her with both arms, fortunately without causing any injury. After the fall, her face towel slipped off, revealing her delicate hair and beautiful face, naturally stunningly beautiful, confirming she was a woman. The crowd, now able to see her clearly, felt extremely shy. Fortunately, her home was nearby, and she fled like a blue phoenix with drooping wings. The crowd was captivated by her beauty, too engrossed to look around. Only Zhu noticed a red note lying on the ground, knowing it was left by the woman, he quickly picked it up and put it in his sleeve. The others were all at a loss. The woman had already run far away, and they began to tease each other, mocking Zhu for not having a wife. Zhu merely smiled without answering, feeling quite pleased inside. When it came time to part ways with the group, he did not wait to get home, immediately taking out the note from his sleeve.

    920

    展视之，墨迹犹新，则故莲房一咏也，朱甚骇异。及返室中，合诸壁上，又复笔仗宛然，益为错愕不置。因而顿起痴情，更思女子态度，竟至终夜不寐。晨起潜往其处物色之，尽得其实。女，杨姓，生于桂月，父母因字以秋娃。其舅为山右通儒，以娃字虽近古，而于今则俗，因易之曰娥。及长，貌姝丽，性且聪颖，即从其舅受学。工书能诗，所作有晋唐风味，其舅或因而阁笔。但产于寒素，且韬晦其光，遂绝无知者，年十七守字闺中。岁前因读古有怀，拟作此绝，今往就正于舅，因颠遂返，惭赧不胜。初不意稿之遗落；更不知旬日之前何以先在朱手也。

    Explanation

    920

    Unfolding it, the ink was still fresh. It was an old poem about the lotus chamber, which greatly startled and amazed Zhu. When he returned to his room and hung it on the wall, the brush and inkstone were still as they were, and he was even more startled and could not stop being amazed. And thus he suddenly developed a foolish affection, thinking more about the girl's demeanor, so engrossed that he could not sleep all night. In the morning, he secretly went to the place to look for her, and fully discovered the truth. The girl, surnamed Yang, was born in the month of August, and her parents gave her the name Qiuwa. Her uncle is a renowned scholar from Shanxi, who changed her name to Ao because the name Qiuwa, though close to ancient usage, is now considered vulgar. When she grew up, she was beautiful and intelligent, studied under her uncle, and was skilled in calligraphy and poetry, with works that had the flavor of the Jin and Tang dynasties. Her uncle sometimes put down his brush because of her, but she was born into a poor family and kept her talents hidden, so no one knew about her. At seventeen, she remained unmarried and lived in the inner quarters. Last year, while reading ancient texts, she felt inspired and wrote this poem. Today, she went to her uncle to seek his advice, but due to some misfortune, she returned, too ashamed to face him. She did not expect the draft to be left behind, and she did not know how it could have been in Zhu's hands ten days ago.

    921

    然朱亦仅得其姓字，且出自乡邻之口，亦未尽悉其才。惟慕色之念，日攒于胸，遂忽忽如有所失。上元已度，势将入院肄习。朱之形状如狂，只默诵女诗，不绝于口，且颠首顿足，曰：“是耶？非耶？果真所作耶？”砚席之同者，莫不闻而讶之。诘以其故，坚不肯言。阅数日，院师开讲，众皆肃听，朱独漠然不闻，时时如与人偶语。师怪而诘之，乃缄口木立，少间复然。有与之同日游者，因陈其事于师，师叹曰：“此子殆病狂失心也，可扶归其家，延医施治，不然当疯痫。”因此辍讲，命人伴送使归，备述其详。父母皆垂泣，朱独摇首止之，曰：“儿无疾，特冰上人约我为父母撮合佳妇耳。”言罢，瞑目如睡，竟夕鼾然，閤家益惝怳不定。

    Explanation

    921

    Yet Zhu could only obtain her surname and name, and this information came from the mouths of neighbors, though he did not fully know her talents. Only the desire for her beauty accumulated daily in his heart, and thus he became distracted as if something were lost. After the Lantern Festival, it was time to enter the academy to study. Zhu's appearance was like that of a madman, as he silently recited her poems, never stopping, and stamped his feet, saying, "Is it? Is it not? Is it truly written by her?" Those who studied with him could not help but hear and be astonished. When questioned about the reason, he firmly refused to speak. After several days, the academy teacher began to lecture, and everyone listened respectfully, but Zhu alone was indifferent and did not hear, often behaving as if talking to someone. The teacher was surprised and questioned him, but he remained silent and stood like a wooden statue, and soon after, he acted the same way again. Someone who studied with him on the same day reported the matter to the teacher. The teacher sighed and said, "This boy is likely suffering from madness and losing his mind. He should be carried home and treated by a doctor, otherwise he will go insane." Therefore, the lecture was stopped, and someone was ordered to accompany him home, and they reported the details in full. Both parents shed tears, but Zhu alone shook his head to stop them, saying, "I have no illness; it is just that someone from the ice (a metaphor for a matchmaker) has invited me to arrange a good wife for my parents." After saying this, he closed his eyes as if to sleep, snoring all night, and the whole family became even more confused and unsettled.

    922

    殊不知朱自入院即祝曰：“畴以诗笺畀我，即当以月老自居，何累人相思而身处局外乎？”盖书院素有灵狐，朱曾闻之，疑笺为其所致，故祷之如此。已而寂然，遂心口作念，有类于痴，实则性灵未昧也。当在讲席之侧，有一人高冠盛服，年约五旬余，揖而与言曰：“前仿御沟流水，预以红叶属君，非无意也。良缘具在君，宜自图，奚为怨怅於仆。”朱知其为狐，大喜，诘以诗所自来，并所以图之之策，絮絮不休。维时人不见狐，只觉朱有所语，且未闻所云云，是以疑而且骇。乃狐谓朱曰，“女虽育于杨，其前生实我族类，犹有母在。盍以婿礼往谒之，此姻良可望。

    Explanation

    922

    Zhu entered the academy and immediately prayed, saying, "Who gave me this poem note? If so, I shall consider myself as the matchmaker, why trouble someone with yearning while being outside the situation?" The academy has always had a spiritual fox, and Zhu had heard of it. He suspected that the note was caused by it, so he prayed in this manner. Afterward, it was silent, and Zhu began to think in his heart and mouth, similar to being obsessed, but in fact, his nature and spirit were not obscured. When he was beside the lecture hall, a person appeared with a tall hat and splendid attire, around fifty years old. He bowed and spoke with Zhu, saying, "Before, I imitated the flowing water of the imperial canal, in advance, the red leaf belonged to you, not without intention. The good match is all in you, you should plan for yourself, why resent me?" Zhu knew that the person was a fox, and he was greatly delighted. He questioned the fox about the origin of the poem and the strategy to achieve it, speaking incessantly. At that time, no one saw the fox, only feeling that Zhu was talking, and not hearing what he was saying, so they were both puzzled and frightened. Then, the fox told Zhu, "Although she was raised by Yang, her previous life was indeed of our kind, still has a mother. Why not go to visit her with the bridegroom's etiquette, this marriage can be hoped for."

    923

    ”朱心已失所主，欣然曰：“诺。”及朱归家，狐从之，相俟于庭户之外。至是，朱觉身轻如叶，悄然出门，与狐偕行于市上。须臾至一巨宅，灯火荧荧，锦衣花帽之人司其启闭者，不止一二。见狐即声喏，曰：“丁员外来杨家，阿姊之事当有济。”狐以首颔之，其人即入禀白。少顷出肃客，朱从狐入。厅事五楹，极高敞，中设白玉屏风，镂牡丹宛然如生。下置胡床，朱茵累寸，旁有绣杌四，光华炫目。周视室内，金玉之彝器映带左右，举为朱所希觏。因窃念曰：“得为渠家婿，当不虑富贵矣。”乃宾已履阈，主人犹未出，谒者告狐曰：“太孺人以老疾，不能疾趋，恐慢客，请坐以相待。”言已自出。

    Explanation

    923

    Zhu's heart had lost its master, and he responded happily, saying, "Yes." When Zhu returned home, the fox followed him, and they waited outside the courtyard. Soon, Zhu felt as light as a leaf, quietly left the house, and walked with the fox on the street. In no time, they arrived at a grand mansion, where the lights were dimly lit, and people in fine clothes and hats managed the opening and closing, more than one or two. Upon seeing the fox, they bowed respectfully and said, "Ding, the employee from the Yang family, is here, and my sister's matter will be resolved." The fox nodded in agreement, and the person entered to report. After a short while, they came out to greet the guests formally, and Zhu followed the fox inside. The hall was five bays wide and very high and spacious, with a white jade screen in the center, carved with peonies that seemed alive. Below, there were trunks with red cushions stacked in layers, and four embroidered stools that were dazzling. Looking around the room, precious ritual vessels made of gold and jade were displayed on both sides, all of which Zhu had never seen before. He thought to himself, "If I become a son-in-law of this family, I won't worry about wealth and status." The guest had already stepped into the threshold, but the host had not yet appeared. The usher informed the fox, "The elderly lady is too old and ill to move quickly, so we ask the guests to wait." After saying this, he left on his own.

    924

    狐引朱耳语曰：“此媪性皉，亦不轻于许可人。以君之才，深虑未能入彀。予有拙稿，君录之，则雀屏不难中矣。”因以一纸授朱，朱益喜。俄而异香遄发，纱笼四引一媪冠帔而出。侍婢十数，妖冶绝伦。狐导朱执子婿礼，媪止之曰：“否！否！婚嫁非细故，无敢鲁莽。”言次，竟据中坐，亦不少逊，狐与朱反北向而坐。朱以色为重，亦不以礼为怪。媪语狐曰：“年来亦大匆忙，竟不一顾。”狐起谢过。又问朱以姓氏及所业，朱一一敬对。媪笑曰：“泮水中自是化龙之地，玉堂金马俱基于此。郎君以青年采芹，将来良不可限。”狐亦从旁赞扬，谓与朱比邻，咕哔之声无少息，故敢于先容。

    Explanation

    924

    The fox drew Zhu's ear close and said, "This old woman is strict and does not lightly give her approval to others. With your talent, you deeply fear you may not be within her favor. I have a humble draft; if you copy it, it will not be difficult to win her favor." He then handed Zhu a piece of paper, and Zhu became even more joyful. Soon, a strange fragrance swiftly emanated, and a silk lantern led out an old woman in formal attire from all directions. She was accompanied by dozens of attendants, all exceedingly beautiful and alluring. The fox guided Zhu to perform the duties of a son-in-law, but the old woman stopped him, saying, "No! No! Marriage is not a trivial matter; one dare not act rashly." As she spoke, she ended up sitting in the central seat, showing no deference, while the fox and Zhu sat facing north. Zhu placed value on appearance and did not find the lack of propriety strange. The old woman said to the fox, "This year has been very busy, and I have not even glanced at you once." The fox apologized. She then asked Zhu about his name and occupation, and Zhu answered respectfully. The old woman smiled and said, "The pond in front of the Confucian temple is indeed a place where one transforms into a dragon, and all great achievements originate here. You, young sir, have already passed the imperial exams at a young age; your future is certainly limitless." The fox also praised Zhu from the side, saying that he lived nearby, and the sound of gossip never ceased, so he dared to introduce Zhu.

    925

    媪之色似甚喜。既而曰：“老妇龙钟，耳目之聪明大逊往日，儿女姻事无敢自作主张。俟小妮子来，与郎君旗鼓相当，渠之意中，则老妇之意亦中矣。”狐因请曰：“姊安在？”媪曰：“已遣婢，想当即至。”于是命婢进茗。啜之，味甚香冽。无何，婢报曰，“阿姑至。”朱与狐皆避坐，远立屏后；又见纱笼，有女子绣衣锦带。翩翩而来，见媪即敛衽再拜。朱睨之，装饰虽异，姿态如初，实即女也。媪握其腕，并坐于床，且抚其肩曰：“儿近来颇念母否？”答曰：“云胡不思，但隔世已久，非母于梦中见示，儿何能知？”媪又曰：“既念予，则予之命亦若父母之命也。母虑儿家綦贫，或轻许蚩蚩者流，误儿一生。

    Explanation

    925

    The old woman's expression seemed very happy. Afterward, she said, "I am old and feeble, and my hearing and eyesight are greatly diminished compared to the past. I dare not make decisions on my own regarding my children's marriage matters. Await the arrival of the young lady, and if she is a match for the young man, and if it pleases her, it also pleases the old woman." The fox then asked, "Where is the sister?" The old woman replied, "She has sent a maid, and she should arrive shortly." She then ordered the maid to bring tea. They sipped it, and it was very fragrant and refreshing. Soon, the maid reported, "The aunt has arrived." Zhu and the fox both stepped aside and stood far behind the screen; they also saw a screen, behind which was a woman in embroidered clothing and brocade sash. She entered gracefully and, upon seeing the old woman, bowed twice with her sleeves. Zhu glanced at her, noting that though her attire was different, her demeanor was the same, and it was indeed her daughter. The old woman took her hand, sat beside her on the bed, and patted her shoulder, saying, "Have you been thinking of your mother lately?" The daughter replied, "Why would you not think? But it has been so long since the other world; if not for your mother showing me in a dream, how could I know?" The old woman continued, "Since you think of me, my wishes are like those of your parents. Your mother is concerned that your family is extremely poor, or lightly agreeing with foolish people, misleading you for life."

    926

    今丁员外来，为一朱公子执伐，其人颇才俊，可许乎？”女闻媪言，俯首不答，色虽赧然，而意似重有忧者。媪笑曰：“母不强儿，故召儿来自试之。臧否在儿，月旦瞳子当不误人，奚为踌躇若此？”女色少解，而不语如故。媪又强之，卒默然。媪因谓狐曰：“妮子忒怕羞。题自老妇出，诗乃畀之自择，或可也。”狐即怂恿曰：“然。”媪指屏间花，令赋七言一律，且曰：“雀屏犹未若花屏之艳也。”于是群婢设几于柱下，进水晶之砚，铺浣花之笺。朱既有所恃，坦然就坐，以狐稿置纸下，挥兔毫，运象管，俄顷而就。倩婢转呈于媪，媪命女诵之。其律曰：“国艳依稀落笔端，玉山添媚彩云团。

    Explanation

    926

    Ding, the scholar-official, has come to act as a matchmaker for a young man of the Zhu family, who is quite talented and handsome. The girl, upon hearing the old woman's words, lowers her head and does not answer, blushing and appearing to have serious concerns. The old woman smiles and says, "The mother does not force the child, so she called the child to test them. The approval or disapproval is up to the child, and the judgment of the child should not be mistaken. Why are you hesitating so?" The girl's expression slightly relaxes, but she remains silent. The old woman tries to persuade her further, but the girl remains silent. The old woman then tells the fox, "The girl is too shy. The topic is chosen by me, and the poem is given to her to choose, or perhaps it can be done." The fox encourages her by saying, "Yes." The old woman points to a flower screen and asks the girl to write a seven-character regulated verse, saying, "A peacock screen is not as beautiful as a flower screen." The maids set up a table under the pillar, bring a crystal inkstone, and lay out fine paper. The young man, feeling supported, sits down confidently, places the fox's draft under the paper, and quickly writes the poem. The maids present the poem to the old woman, who asks the girl to read it. The poem reads: "National beauty faintly falls at the tip of the brush, jade mountain adds charm to the cluster of colorful clouds."

    927

    图成信有千金价，张去还宜百宝栏。云母开颜堆绣被，花王笑日依琅歼。蒹葭果入黄荃画，也许当筵学风鸾。”媪聆之，意先首肯。女亦微盳曰：“道在是矣。”乃起，而先入屏后。媪始延朱复其故处，曰：“郎信天才，与吾家秋娥可称匹敌。归宜亟觅执柯，好事固可谐也。”狐又起白曰：“微媪有命，凤卜固属无征，然亦何能使人必从乎？仆意择吉即招朱郎入赘，婚而后告，庶无扦格。媪以为然否？”媪又侧首沉思，徐曰：“本不当强预人事，但欲得快婿，老妇亦不恤人言。”因顾婢曰：“速取十丈红，先为大媒光采，异日再治筵相酬。”狐令朱起谢，媪始含笑受之。狐径披红与朱出，直送朱至其家，乃别去。

    Explanation

    927

    The scene is truly worth a thousand gold, and the arrangement should be adorned with a hundred treasures. The mica smiles as it piles up like embroidered quilts, and the king of flowers laughs under the sun, leaning against the jade. The reeds, indeed, are as beautiful as Huang Quan's paintings, and perhaps one could learn to mimic the wind and phoenix at the feast. The old woman listened, and her mind was already inclined to agree. The girl also smiled faintly and said, "The way lies here." She then rose and went into the screen. The old woman then invited Zhu back to his original place and said, "You are indeed a genius, and you can be compared to my daughter, Autumn Beauty. You should quickly find a matchmaker, and the good fortune is sure to come to pass." The fox then stood up and said, "Without the old woman's command, the phoenix divination would certainly be unproven, but how could one force someone to follow? I think we should choose a good day to invite Zhu to come and marry, and then inform you after the marriage, so as to avoid any obstacles. Do you think this is right?" The old woman nodded her head and thought deeply, then slowly said, "I should not interfere in human affairs, but if I want a good son-in-law, I will not care what others say." She then looked at the maid and said, "Quickly fetch the ten-zhang red cloth, to first bring glory to the matchmaker, and I will prepare a feast to repay them another day." The fox ordered Zhu to rise and thank the old woman, and she finally accepted with a smile. The fox then led Zhu out, wearing the red cloth, and escorted him directly to his home before parting ways.

    928

    曰：“俟订吉期，再当敬以相报。”朱入门而觉身在榻上，父母犹环守其侧。朱俱以告，阖室俱以为颠，谨进汤药以饮之。翌日，朱起，神气如旧，且赴院谢其师。仍其讲贯，以非儒者所乐闻，遂不置之齿颊。旬余，忽失朱之所在，院师与其家俱仓皇，浼人追寻，究无迹兆。而杨家亦于是日户窗未启失其掌珠，阖邑始传以为异。年余，有朱之从叔，家巨富，为贾于京，始致书并朱之书来。则朱已纳粟坐监，且登乡榜矣。盖朱在院数日，又见狐来奔告曰：“媪家已以舆来，予以院中人甚伙，故止之于予家。新郎可往矣！”朱欲归告父母，而以前事为鉴，惧其弗信，将不得娶，乃键户从狐行。

    Explanation

    928

    Zhu said, "Awaiting the fixing of a favorable date, I will respectfully report back." Upon entering the house, Zhu felt himself lying on a bed, with his parents still guarding him on either side. Zhu informed them of everything, and the entire household thought he was crazy. They carefully offered him medicinal soup to drink. The next day, Zhu got up, with his vitality as before, and went to the academy to thank his teacher. He continued to attend the lectures, but since these were not topics that Confucian scholars found enjoyable, he did not bring them up in conversation. After ten days, Zhu suddenly disappeared, and both the academy teacher and his family were in a state of panic. They asked someone to search for him, but no trace or sign was found. Meanwhile, the Yang family also on that day, with the doors and windows not opened, lost their beloved daughter. The entire county began to regard this as strange. After a year, Zhu's cousin, who was wealthy and traveling as a merchant in the capital, finally sent a letter along with Zhu's letter. It turned out that Zhu had already become a student in the Imperial College through a grain tax payment and was listed on the local examination list. It is said that Zhu, while in the academy for several days, saw a fox come and urgently report, saying, "The old lady's family has already arrived in a carriage. I told them that there were many people in the academy, so I stopped them at my house. The groom can go now!" Zhu wanted to return home to inform his parents, but fearing that they might not believe him, and that he would not be able to marry, he locked the door and followed the fox.

    929

    及抵狐处，门庭潇洒，绝无纤尘，果有舆止于外。狐揖朱入，朱因谢其玉成之德。狐笑曰：“子非他，前生亦一女也，遭吾祟而死，恩情惓惓。子纵忘之，予不能忘也，故百计图，维成子佳偶，亦聊以为报耳，何谢为？”朱顿悟。狐又出衣衣之，华美异常。朱虽无羊车之貌，亦既神采不群矣。乘舆启行，天已向晦；箫鼓敖曹，里人竟如充耳。既至，傧相仆从蜂拥而入，堂前大宾肃立，约数人，冠服不类于今，貌皆奇伟。媪又出，扶杖候之。朱降舆，礼入鸣赞，婢始拥女出，装束如仙，以帕重遮娇面，生忽疑非杨女。迨及交拜成礼，启袱视之，花貌俨然，则故秋娥者是，朱乃大悦。及寝，燕婉之求既遂，而鱼水之乐因以靡加。

    Explanation

    929

    Upon arriving at the fox's residence, the courtyard was elegant and spotless, with no trace of dust, and indeed, a carriage was waiting outside. The fox bowed and invited Zhu inside, and Zhu thanked the fox for its kind assistance. The fox smiled and said, "You are not someone else; in a past life, you were also a woman, who died after being tormented by me. The feelings of gratitude were deep and persistent. Even if you have forgotten, I cannot forget. Therefore, I have gone to great lengths to arrange a good match for you, and I do this as a way of repayment; why thank me?" Zhu suddenly realized. The fox then dressed Zhu in attire that was exceptionally beautiful. Although Zhu did not have the appearance of a sheep cart, he now had a unique and distinguished aura. They set off in the carriage, and the sky was already turning dark; the sound of flutes and drums was loud, and the villagers seemed to be deaf to the noise. When they arrived, the attendants and servants swarmed in, and the main guests stood respectfully in the hall, about a dozen in number, their attire unlike that of today, and they all had extraordinary appearances. The old woman then came out, leaning on a cane to await them. Zhu stepped down from the carriage, and the attendant announced the ritual. The maid then escorted the woman out, who was dressed like a fairy, covering her face with a veil. Zhu suddenly doubted that it was Yang's daughter. Upon completing the exchange of bows and the ceremony, he uncovered the veil to see her, and she had a beautiful and dignified appearance—it was indeed the former Autumn Ode, and Zhu was greatly pleased. When they retired to bed, their desires were fulfilled, and the joy of being like fish and water was even greater.

    930

    浃洽之余，朱又疑身在梦境，且以杨氏之姝何以聘嫁由媪？细询之，女初惭怍不言，徐乃自白曰：“妾生有夙慧，襁褓即洞见本来，但惧惑人，不敢妄语。比长，亦渐昧前因。客岁忽梦媪，自云是妾前生之母，妾缘母病出觅仙芝，为猎犬所咋而死，盖一狐也。母因痛念妾，适父来游于晋，携母至此，始得根寻相见，不胜劬劳。衣食珍异时自暗中馈遗，妾亦在睡乡日来省视，今春语妾曰：‘若父母与乃舅，谋以儿适刘郎子。此人文名诚藉甚，而福泽纂薄。吾当为儿觅一佳婿。’曩日，因丁公得读佳什，母与妾皆心肯。检君禄籍，又仕版中人，故以妾奉箕帚。

    Explanation

    930

    After a deep and harmonious relationship, Zhu began to doubt that he was in a dream. He also wondered why the beautiful woman from the Yang family was betrothed by the old woman. Upon closer questioning, the woman initially blushed and remained silent, but after a while, she confessed: "I was born with innate wisdom and, even as an infant, I had a profound understanding of my true nature. However, I was afraid of misleading others, so I did not speak rashly. As I grew older, I gradually forgot my past. Last year, I had a dream where the old woman appeared and said she was my mother from a previous life. I had gone out to search for the magical herb, but was killed by a hunting dog. I was actually a fox. My mother, deeply grieving my loss, came to this place with my father, who had traveled to Jin, and we were finally reunited. She secretly provided me with food and rare treasures, and I would visit her in my sleep. This spring, she told me: 'Your parents and your maternal uncle have decided to betroth you to Liu Langzi. This man is indeed famous for his literary talent, but his fortune is limited. I will find you a good match.' In the past, because of Ding Gong, I read some excellent poetry, and both my mother and I were pleased. Checking your official register, you are a person in the officialdom, so I am willing to fulfill my domestic duties."

    931

    ”朱曰：“然则卿今至此，其犹是漆园之蝶耶？且两次未瞻泰岱，系何衔名，而媪遽称太孺人？”女曰：“妾亦不自解。日前母云以妾字君，妾心甚觉犹豫，以为未奉父母之命，再生之说，究属不经，遂婉言以辞。母甚不怿。昨夕妾在深闺，忽二婢入室，夹持妾行，若在云雾中。至此，母始告妾，则已不能归矣。又闻妾前世有兄，俱在帝京供役，授有微秩，仆婢因以此尊母。至于父，亦往都中，久出来返。其大概如此，妾实不得其详。”朱既得女叙述，心下霍然，亦自言两诗之异，并邂逅相遭，遂致怀思之苦。因戏曰：“卿即坠楼人也，非予虽不玉碎，恐难瓦全矣。”女亦得悉其颠末，笑曰：“睹君壮貌，似曾相识，想以妾失足之故。”言已交颈而眠。明日早起，入谢妪。

    Explanation

    931

    “Zhu said, ‘Then, have you come here, still like the butterfly from the lacquer garden? And how can you have two times without seeing Mount Tai, what rank do you hold, and why is the old lady hastily calling you ‘Tai Ruhen’?’ The woman replied, ‘I also don’t understand myself. Before, my mother said she would betroth me to you, and I felt very hesitant, thinking that since I had not received my parents’ permission, the idea of rebirth seemed too far-fetched, so I politely declined. My mother was very displeased. Last night, while I was in my inner chambers, suddenly two maids entered the room, carried me away, as if I were in a dream. By then, my mother finally told me that I could no longer return home. I also heard that in my previous life, I had a brother who served in the imperial capital and was granted a minor rank, so the servants and maids honored my mother with respect. As for my father, he also went to the capital and came back after a long time. That’s roughly it; I don’t know the details.’ Zhu, having heard the woman’s account, felt a sudden clarity, and he also spoke of the differences between the two poems and how they had met by chance, leading to his longing. He then joked, ‘You are the woman who fell from the tower, and if not for me, though I wouldn’t crumble like jade, I might not be able to remain intact as pottery.’ The woman also learned the full story and smiled, saying, ‘Seeing your strong appearance, it seems familiar, perhaps because of my mistake.’ After speaking, they embraced and fell asleep. The next morning, they woke up early and went to thank the old lady.”

    932

    媪忽盲曰：“婿与女究非吾道中人，不能久居于此。”乃以辎拼一乘，駃騠数骑，婢二人，仆三人，送女与朱俱北上。濒行，授以千金，曰：“婿自能腾达，厚黄反以相误。况乃父若兄俱在京邸，薪水固无虞不给也。”朱与女泣拜膝下，勉强发辆。途中，女与朱倡和，朱靦颜辞以未谙。女怪之，朱以实告。女叹曰：“丁公为所爱误人闺嫒，恶得无罪？”既而见朱所为文，始冁然曰：“妾虽未解为此，然诗文一也。英锐之气，自宜早发，惜未斟酌尽善耳。”朱甚服其论。抵京，婢仆皆辞去，朱乃以金自觅十指以代之。阅数日，翁至，议论渊博，绝类老儒。朱遂执经受业，学乃大进。

    Explanation

    932

    The elderly woman suddenly said, "Your son-in-law and daughter are indeed not of my family's way, and cannot stay here for long." She then gave them a carriage, several fast horses, two maids, and three servants, and sent the daughter and Zhu northward together. On the verge of departure, she gave them a thousand gold coins and said, "Your son-in-law will surely rise to prominence, but wealth will instead mislead him. Your father and elder brother are both in the capital, so daily necessities will certainly not be lacking." Zhu and the daughter cried and bowed at her knees, reluctantly setting off. On the journey, the daughter and Zhu sang and responded, but Zhu blushed and excused himself by saying he did not know. The daughter found this strange, and Zhu told the truth. She sighed, "Ding, because of his love for her, has misled a young lady—how can he be without sin?" Afterward, when she saw what Zhu had written, she smiled and said, "Although I don't understand this, poetry and prose are one.
