How dare I disobey? However, since the establishment of the harem, ancient sage kings listed it among the three vices, leaving a lasting warning for future generations. Later kings and officials were criticized for riding in four-horse chariots or condemned for the leftover peach incident. What does the Dragon King gain from this? Moreover, considering the Dragon King's palace, with beautiful women filling the lower ranks and dogs and horses filling the outer stables, it is already sufficient for self-entertainment. I hope you will rescind your decision, so as not to bring shame to the Dragon King or ridicule from the sea's subjects. It would be a great favor." Upon finishing his words, the Dragon King remained silent in displeasure. The attendant continued, "This argument is very correct. As I have heard, the Dragon King's attendant once wept over a fish, how could he not wish for the continuation by future generations? Now he is purifying his soul, seeking to completely reform the remaining customs. The intention is also very noble."

    977

    况龙主之职，只宜霖雨仓生，而耽耽于顽童之比，九阍闻之，能无怒乎？即某亦窃为龙主滋惧矣。”龙主又默然。黎遽按剑而起，谓之曰：“君有三大罪，其知之乎？”龙主亦毅然曰：“不知也。”黎曰：“君虽蛙尊水府，实已南面称王，竟不顾大亵体制，而宴然争此娈童，一大罪。龙阳君受封于楚，曾为贵臣，君乃以威势压之，倚众暴寡，恃强凌弱，二大罪。龙阳君尽收陆海，虑人宣淫，而君强竞南风，导人纵欲，非三大罪而何？”语至此，须磔目张，剑已离室。

    Explanation

    977

    Considering the role of the dragon king, it is only fitting that he brings timely rain to nourish the crops. Yet, he acts greedily like a mischievous child, and the nine officials, upon hearing this, could not help but be angry. Even I am secretly growing increasingly fearful for the dragon king. The dragon king remained silent. Li suddenly drew his sword and stood up, saying to him, "You have committed three great crimes. Do you know them?" The dragon king also firmly replied, "I do not know." Li said, "Although you are a frog in the water palace, you have effectively become a king. You disregard the great violation of the system and calmly pursue this young boy, which is your first great crime. Lord Longyang was once a high-ranking official in Chu, and you use your power to oppress the weak and bully the few, which is your second great crime. Lord Longyang gathered all the lands and seas and was concerned about people engaging in promiscuity, but you forcibly compete for the southern wind and lead people to indulge in lust. What else could these be but three great crimes?" By the time he finished speaking, his eyes were wide with anger, and his sword had already left its sheath.

    978

    厉声曰：“某受朝廷之职，奉幕府之命，虽居末秩，实仗天威。凡有梗化者，无论鬼神，皆得问罪。矧今日之举，将以扶弱锄强，诛淫去暴，即以三尺之钢，溅汝项血，谅不为过也。”挺剑而前，龙主色挠，长揖而谢之曰：“将军坐！何致于此？寡人知过矣！如再思断袖之欢，当遭醢身之祸。所不应者，有如此烛。”黎乃掷剑大笑曰：“吾固谓龙主豪杰，必无不断之事也。”龙阳君又请为盟。黎笑而麾之曰：“以盟为有益乎？吾恐口血未干，即有龙战之事矣。

    Explanation

    978

    Loudly he said, "I have been appointed by the court and tasked by the military office, though in a low rank, I rely on the majesty of heaven. Anyone who obstructs the order of the world, whether human or divine, may be held accountable. Especially today's action, it is to support the weak and eradicate the strong, to eliminate debauchery and remove violence. With a three-foot-long sword, I shall splatter your neck with blood, and it would not be excessive." Advancing with a drawn sword, the dragon lord's face turned pale, and he bowed deeply and apologized, saying, "General, please sit! How did you come to this? I know I have erred! If I consider the pleasure of a lover's embrace again, I will suffer the fate of being minced like meat. Let this candle bear witness to my words." Li then threw the sword and laughed, saying, "I always believed the dragon lord was a hero, there is nothing he will not cut through." The Dragon Lord asked to take an oath. Li laughed and waved him away, saying, "Is taking an oath beneficial? I fear the blood on our lips has not yet dried, and there will be a dragon battle."

    979

    盟既如此，不如无盟。”龙主亦辞而不歃。遂复坐饮，相对甚欢，直至鸡鸣。龙主先辞去，龙阳君谢黎曰：“非借将军神勇，此事正未易决也。”因献明珠一柈，黎坚辞不受，依然乘马而归。比及舟中，天已昧旦，从人皆不知黎出，见始骇然。黎询之，对曰：“我等闻公命烛，及燃之，公复偃卧，竟未见公他往也。”黎亦笑而不言。诘朝扬帆，见一蛟蜿蜒海面，从以小鱼约数百，且稽首作谢状。黎知为龙阳君，温言慰之，旋失所在。黎后位至协镇，恒向人述及其异如此。

    　　外史氏曰：蛟字从交，毛诗以狂童为狡，孟氏以艳丽为姣，音虽殊，而字形相类，则龙阳君之化蛟也，宜矣！但无虎豹之威，不免蛟龙之斗。黎将军侃侃正论，立解其纷，何其豪也？至以中山之皘为南溟之鲲，似乎寓言。不然，众维鱼矣，何复有漏网者献笑争妍，致短须眉之气耶？

    Explanation

    979

    The alliance being as it is, it might as well not exist," said the Dragon Lord, also refusing to take a sip. They then sat down to drink, enjoying each other's company until the rooster crowed. The Dragon Lord left first, and the Dragon Lord thanked Li, saying, "I did not rely on the general's divine courage; this matter was not easy to decide." He then offered a tray of pearls, but Li firmly refused to accept, mounting his horse and returning. By the time he reached the boat, the sky was already dim, and the attendants did not know Li had left, being startled when they saw him. Li asked them, and they replied, "We heard you order the candle, and when it was lit, you lay down again, and we did not see you go anywhere else." Li smiled but said nothing. The next morning, as they set sail, they saw a dolphin winding through the sea, followed by about a hundred small fish, bowing and making a gesture of thanks. Li knew it was the Dragon Lord and spoke kindly to comfort him, and then it disappeared. Later, Li reached the position of Assistant Governor and always told people about the strange events like this.

    The commentator said: The character for "jiao" (dolphin) comes from "jiao" (to join). The Book of Songs calls a wild child "jiao," and Mengzi calls beauty "jiao." Though the sounds are different, the characters look similar, so the transformation of the Dragon Lord into a dolphin is appropriate! But lacking the ferocity of tigers and leopards, he cannot escape the fighting of dragons and dolphins. Li's general, with clear and correct discourse, immediately resolved the dispute, how heroic it is! To the transformation of the cicada of Zhongshan into the whale of the Southern Sea seems like an allegory. Otherwise, the many are fish, how can there be those who escape the net and vie for beauty, to the detriment of the dignity of men?

    980

    ○　苑公
    　　巨珰苑公，直省人，谈吐隽妙，有文士风。王公大人恒以其阉而惜之。然苑公之阉，非犹蚕室也。苑公家巨富，父亦仕至别驾。苑公之生，锦襁绣褓，岂等于贫苦小民净其身以求显达哉？其父年届六旬，犹无子，戚族劝之，始纳一妾。期年生苑公，其父甚悦，亟觅乳媪，使字之。彼妇吕姓，少寡，性妖淫。其夫之死不可知，孀两岁，又举一男，因不见容于姑嫜，遣归母家。母家又贫，劝之嫁，又恋所私，不肯听。乃以其乳售于人，意盖借此以自便，庶不至如再醮者，受夫箝制也。公父不及察，竟以十金质其身，约以三周，俟子口食而后去。妇敬诺，视公亦甚爱惜。初公父缘有少妾，治家綦严，虽五尺之童罔敢入中门。及见乳媪少艾，且妖冶异常，防闲益密。而性复刚暴，婢妾小忤，即行敲扑，而余人可知。妇既入樊笼，所欢者三秋莫见，即通一讯而无从。又惧官势，不敢自断其乳，因而由悔生恨，积恨成怒，思毙其所哺之儿，而后可脱然事外。

    Explanation

    980

    Translation:

    The Eunuch Yuan

    The eunuch Yuan, a man from a direct province, was known for his eloquent and refined speech, possessing the demeanor of a literatus. High-ranking officials and nobles often pitied him for his castration. However, Yuan's castration was not the result of being castrated in a prison. Yuan came from a wealthy family, and his father had risen to the position of deputy governor. Yuan was born in fine silk swaddling clothes, far removed from the desperate measures taken by poor people to gain status. His father, who was nearly sixty years old, still had no son. His relatives persuaded him to take a concubine, and after a year, Yuan was born. His father was very pleased and quickly sought a wet nurse to raise him. The woman was a young widow named Lu, known for her seductive and alluring nature. Her husband's death was unknown, but two years later, she gave birth to another son. She was not accepted by her in-laws and was sent back to her mother's home. Her mother's family was also poor, and they encouraged her to remarry, but she refused, still longing for her lover. She decided to sell her services as a wet nurse, hoping to use this opportunity to be free and avoid the constraints of a second marriage. Yuan's father did not investigate further and hired her for ten gold pieces, agreeing that she would serve for three years until the child could feed himself. The woman agreed and treated Yuan with great care. Initially, Yuan's father, who had a concubine, governed his household with great strictness, and even young boys were not allowed to enter the main gate. When he saw the young and beautiful wet nurse, who was exceptionally seductive, he increased his surveillance and control. He was harsh and violent, and even minor disobedience by servants or concubines would result in severe punishment. The woman, once in this household, was isolated and unable to see her lover for a long time, and communication was impossible. She also feared the power of the officials and dared not stop nursing. Consequently, her regret turned into hatred, which accumulated into anger, and she thought of killing the child she was nursing, in order to escape from her situation.

    981

    无如苑公幼即健壮，曾无纤微疾痛，妇益计窘。适公父远出，公之嫡母生母，均以时疫病在床，妇乃得逞。阴以生丝一线，束儿外肾，日以加紧。儿痛楚啼号，人亦莫测其故。妇又以醴酒一盏置枕畔，乘儿呱呱，即濡指潜抹其吻。儿醉，辄熟寐，妇又移易其扣。如是者兼旬，儿之母病始痊，而儿之人道已随手而坠。妇初冀儿死，而儿痛则啼，啼已复食，妇于此不觉智尽能索矣。既而闻公父将归，心更大惧。

    Explanation

    981

    There was no way to help the young Yuan, who was already strong and healthy, never having suffered even the slightest illness or pain. The woman, finding herself in a tight spot, took advantage of the situation when the father of the man was away. Both the legitimate mother and the biological mother of the man were sick with a seasonal epidemic and in bed. The woman then succeeded in carrying out her plan, secretly tying the boy's external genitalia with a single thread of raw silk, tightening it more and more each day. The boy cried out in pain, but no one could understand the reason. The woman would place a cup of sweet wine beside the pillow, while the boy was crying, and secretly rub her moistened finger on his lips. The boy would fall asleep immediately, and the woman would then change the lock. In this manner, for two weeks, the boy's mother's illness finally healed, but the boy's masculinity had already fallen away as if by the hand. The woman initially hoped the boy would die, but the boy cried in pain and would eat again after crying. Without realizing it, the woman had exhausted all her wits and resources. Afterward, she heard that the father of the man was returning, and her heart became even more fearful.

    982

    一夕伺儿寝，自缢室中，比及人知，早已救而弗及。举家震骇，幸公父旋返，虽未悉其致死之由，而缄书邑宰，检验无伤，事遂寝。阅数日，其妾视儿便溺，忽大惊曰：“予所诞者，岂女也耶？”嫡室闻而趋视，则非女非男，在二者之间。阖室皆变色，闻之公父，乃大戚，始悟妇死之故。重责婢妪，而兼及其妻妾，究亦无如之何，惟时时陨涕而已。比长，耻使为阉，亦教之诵读。年十二而父卒，犹学为文。及应童子试，邑人攻之，竟不得考。公乃忿忿束装入都，渐致通显。每言之，犹欷献不已。

    Explanation

    982

    One night, while the child was sleeping, the mother hanged herself in the room. By the time others knew, it was already too late to save her. The whole family was shocked and terrified. Fortunately, the father returned soon after, though he did not fully understand the reason for her death. He sealed a letter to the local magistrate, who examined the body and found no injury, and the matter was then dropped. After several days, her concubine observed the child's excretion and suddenly exclaimed in surprise, "What I gave birth to, could it be a girl?" The principal wife heard and hurried to see, and found that the child was neither a girl nor a boy, but somewhere in between. Everyone in the house changed color. When the father heard about it, he was greatly saddened and realized the reason for the woman's death. He severely punished the maidservants and also implicated his concubines. Ultimately, he could do nothing about it, and he could only shed tears occasionally. As he grew older, he was ashamed to have him castrated, so he also taught him to read and write. At the age of twelve, when his father died, he still continued to learn how to write. When he took the examination for young scholars, the townspeople attacked him, and he ultimately failed to pass the exam. The man then angrily packed his belongings and went to the capital, and gradually he became well-known and influential. Every time he spoke of it, he still sighed and wept.

    983

    外史氏曰：谓他人母，亦莫我有，儿固以乳为母者。乃不第不有其子，且并其所有而去之。穷凶极恶，要之皆淫之一字，实为厉阶。淫则必阴，阴则必毒，吾因以告夫天下之为父母者。

    ○　银筝
    　　故明鼎革时，天下遭流氛之乱，烽烟匝地，羁旅者多不能归。甲申以降，本朝大圣人出，海内永清，民始得返其乡井。鸿雁哀鸣，旋集安宅，甚盛事也。泾阳商人李元燮，久困于吴楚间，亦将复其邦族。策一蹇卫，徐驱于邯郸道上，重睹山川风日之胜，不禁快然。薄暮，宿某县旅舍。主人，其乡人也，挽留信宿，不忍遽别。旦起，闲观市上，往来络绎，依然盛世之氓，心益欣悦。立未久，闻市人哗曰：“秽物来矣！”多奔避，唯恐不及。李愕然以问，主人笑曰：“行且至，子试觇之。”李因伫俟。

    Explanation

    983

    Translation

    The historian commented: "To call someone else's mother one's own, one has no right to do so, for a child is naturally bound to its mother through nursing. Yet not only did they not have their own children, but they also took away what belonged to others. Their extreme wickedness and cruelty can ultimately be traced back to the word 'lust,' which is truly the root of disaster. Lust inevitably leads to darkness, and darkness inevitably leads to poison. Therefore, I hereby warn all parents in the world."

    ○ Silver Zither

    During the time of the Ming dynasty's collapse and the establishment of the Qing dynasty, the world suffered from the chaos of wandering bandits, with war raging everywhere, and many travelers were unable to return home. After the year 1644, a great sage emerged in our dynasty, bringing lasting peace to the land, and the people began to return to their hometowns. Like geese returning to their nests after a mournful cry, this was a great event. Li Yuanxie, a merchant from Jingyang, had long been trapped in the Wu and Chu regions and was also returning to his hometown. He rode a donkey slowly along the road to Handan, seeing the beautiful landscapes again and feeling a sense of joy. Late in the afternoon, he stayed at an inn in a certain county. The innkeeper was from his hometown and invited him to stay for two nights, not wanting to see him leave immediately. In the morning, he casually observed the bustling market, where people were moving about as if it were a prosperous era, and his heart became even more joyful. He had not been standing for long when he heard the people in the market shout, "The dirty thing is coming!" Many people ran away, fearing they might not escape in time. Li was surprised and asked the innkeeper, who smiled and said, "It will be here soon. You can go and see for yourself." Li then waited.

    984

    俄见一人，身无半缕，仅以尺幅蔽其私，竟体污秽，若负涂泥。迫视之，发毡面鬼，气如新遗之矢，十步之内，渐不可耐。人有不及避者，辄掩鼻而趋。李强忍而观，双钩荏弱，一妇人也。乃大骇，徐察其异，容虽不洁，其目则灼然如秋水之波，其腰则溺然如春风之柳，低鬟敛恨，俯仰生姿，苟具深心者，莫不知为尤物。李谛视良久，惊曰：“此固非常品，奈何污辱至此？”因不避其讳，潜尾之。女至人家，即呼曰：“银筝来。”人随以敝器置食，委之地而与之。女手一小竹眥，即倾于其中，再诣他处乞。约足一人之食，返其故道，不再履入门户，飘然自去。

    Explanation

    984

    Suddenly, Li saw a person who was completely naked, covering only her private parts with a small piece of cloth. Her entire body was dirty, as if covered in mud. Approaching to look at her closely, he saw that she had a ghost-like face and smelled like a freshly defecated arrow. Within ten steps, the smell became increasingly unbearable. Those who did not have time to avoid her would cover their noses and hurry away. Li endured it and continued to observe, noting that she had weak and thin hands, and realized she was a woman. He was greatly startled and slowly observed her peculiarities. Although her appearance was dirty, her eyes were bright like the waves of autumn water, and her waist was soft and graceful like a willow in the spring breeze. With a lowered head and a look of resentment, she had a charming presence when looking up and down. Anyone with a deep heart would know she was a rare beauty. Li observed her for a long time and said in surprise, "This is certainly not an ordinary person, why is she treated so poorly?" Therefore, without avoiding her, he followed her secretly. When the woman went to a house, she called out, "Silver Zhen, come." People placed food in a worn-out container and left it on the ground for her. She took a small bamboo scoop and poured the food into it, then went to another place to beg. She gathered enough food for one person and returned to her usual path, not entering any doors and leaving gracefully.

    985

    李蹑迹相从，女至一废宅，人遂不出。李默识其处，然后归。及见主人，亦不复齿。至夜，始往伺之，闻破壁中，似有人吟咏，音甚娇细。倾听之，则七言律也。其诗曰：“黄巾满地翠娥羞，愧向风流作楚囚。吞炭不缘仇未雪，文身只为美堪忧。敢辞泥滓十分涴，略避纶竿一旦钩。幸遇安澜还净俗，阿谁刮目到沧州。”诗虽不工，语甚明显。已而又吟曰：“故乡咫尺似天涯，遗臭流芳念不差。玉骨纵甘埋粪壤，翠眉宁忍映荒沙。石中自韫无瑕璧，树底谁怜薄命花。试向灯前欣把臂，守宫依旧色如霞。”李素知书，聆其音韵清浏，不禁雀跃。

    Explanation

    985

    Li followed her stealthily, and when the woman arrived at an abandoned house, she did not leave. Li silently remembered the place and then returned home. When he met the master, he was no longer spoken to. Until night, he began to go and watch her, hearing from behind a broken wall as if someone was reciting poetry, with a voice that was very delicate and soft. Upon listening closely, it turned out to be a seven-character regulated verse. The poem said: "The green women blush amidst the chaos of yellow banners, ashamed to be among the gallant men as a captive of Chu. I swallow coal not because my grudge has not been avenged, but to bear the scars for the sake of beauty's sake. I dare not shun the mud that stains me utterly, just to avoid the hook of the fishing line once. Fortunately, I encounter a calm sea and return to a pure world, who will look at me with new eyes in Cangzhou?" Though the poem may not be skillful, the words are very clear. Then he recited again: "Home is close yet feels like it's far away, the thoughts of leaving a bad name or a good name are not mistaken. Even if the pure bones are willing to be buried in filth, how can the green eyebrows bear to reflect on the desolate sand? A flawless jade hidden in a stone, who will pity the weak flower under the tree? Try to hold hands by the lamp, the color of the palace remains as red as the evening glow." Li, who had always known literature, listened to the clear and melodious tones and could not help but leap with joy.

    986

    大呼曰：“刮目者至矣！守宫砂可容一验乎？”女闻声悬知为日尾己者，乃隔堵墙与语曰：“君真有心人也，不为俗目所拘，能识妾于污浊之内，洵为独具只眼者。但暮夜之间，幽僻之地，嫌疑易涉，不敢呈身，窃自恕。”李笑曰：“百体无丝，提防尽溃，卿此言无乃娇乎？”女曰：“不然。妾虽裸裎人侧，人实不以人视妾，妾遂不以女自居。今者君既知妾，妾犹以此相见，是人与人相值而无男女之别也。妾即衣履不全，犹不敢见君，矧无礼之若此哉？”李因诘曰：“然则终已不见乎？”答曰：“妾望豪杰如望岁焉，讵忍交臂而失之？曩者见：君垂盼，知必辱临，故吟拙律以见志。

    Explanation

    986

    Shouting loudly, he said, "The one who will change his perspective has arrived! Can the vermilion mark be used for a test?" The woman, hearing the voice, instinctively knew it was someone closely associated with the sun. She spoke to him through the wall, saying, "You are truly a person with a heart, not constrained by conventional views. You can recognize me within this filth, and indeed, you are a person with unique insight. But in the dark of night, in a secluded and remote place, it is easy to involve in suspicion. I dare not present myself, so I secretly forgive myself." Li laughed and said, "Without any threads, all defenses have collapsed. Isn't this statement somewhat playful?" The woman replied, "Not so. Even if I stand naked among others, people do not see me as a person, so I no longer see myself as a woman. Now that you know me, I still present myself in this way, which means people meet each other without the distinction of gender. Even if my clothes and shoes are incomplete, I still dare not see you, how much more so in such a disrespectful manner?" Li then asked, "Then will you never see me again?" The woman answered, "I look forward to heroes as eagerly as one looks forward to the new year. How could I bear to lose you like this? Before, when I saw you looking down, I knew you would surely come, so I recited clumsy verses to express my feelings."

    987

    君果剖石取玉，披沙拣金，不以污辱而收之，妾请俟于前途，永执箕帚，君以为何如？”李大悦曰，“是予所愿也。”遂叮嘱而别。诘朝李起如市，潜置女衣一袭，袍祷咸具，归即束装。主人留之不住，匆匆辞去。约行半里许，闻丛莽中小语曰：“郎来乎？妾固料无爽约也。”窸窣而出。李视之，泥垢犹昔，熏莸已少异，益喜其有心。乃即驴背取衣与之著，女止之曰：“不可。三年积垢，一旦逢君，当还我庐山面目矣。西僻有小溪，尚可涤灌，倩君随予一往。”李从之，携手同行，绝无惫恶。女深感其情，因自述其颠末曰：“妾名银筝，邻邑绅家女也。年甫及笄，即以色美闻于人。

    Explanation

    987

    If you can indeed dig out jade from stones and sift gold from sand, not accepting it because of its contamination or disgrace, I will wait for you in the future, always holding the broom and dustpan. What do you think of this? Li was greatly pleased and said, "This is what I desire." Then he gave instructions and parted. The next morning Li went to the market, secretly placed a set of women's clothes, all the necessary items for a prayer or ritual were prepared, returned home and immediately packed. The host could not keep him, hurriedly bid farewell. After walking about half a mile, he heard a small voice from the bushes saying, "Is the gentleman coming? I had already expected no breach of promise." She emerged quietly. Li looked at her, the mud and dirt were still the same, but the smell of incense and garlic had slightly changed, becoming even more pleased that she had thought of this. Then he took the clothes from the donkey's back and dressed her, the woman stopped him and said, "No. The three years of dirt and grime, once met with you, should return me to my true self. There is a small stream to the west, which can still be used for washing, please accompany me there." Li followed her, holding hands and walking together, completely free of fatigue or discomfort. The woman deeply felt his affection and then recounted her entire story, "My name is Yin Zhen, I am the daughter of a scholar from a neighboring town. I was just coming of age when I became known for my beauty."

    988

    适遇流寇兵起，父母深以为忧，逆知不免于包羞，将置妾于死地。妾悲父母乏嗣，乃跽而言曰：‘贼之所悦者，色也。儿有毁容之术，使贼不可以近身，不愈于舍亲而就死乎？’父母亦不忍予，遂听之。妾预储人犬之矢及秽污等物，闻城将陷，先以炭漆其身，继以垢泥重以溲便，径扶父母出奔。虽乱军之际，白刃交挥，见妾以为狂，曾不一盼。嗣是父母失其产，又染痼疾，尝在床蓐。妾以此亲往贼营求乞以养。贼恒怜妾，而从无邪心，呼妾曰“癫人”，时以口食与之。如是者半岁，贼退而父亡。妾负母丐于四方，深惧宵小，因以不更。今春，母又见背，孑然只影，愈不敢稍露其形。

    Explanation

    988

    恰好遇到流寇作乱，父母非常担忧，预料到无法避免羞辱，打算把妾置于死地。妾为父母无后嗣而悲伤，于是跪下说：“贼人所喜欢的是美色。我有毁容的方法，让贼人无法靠近，这难道比放弃亲人去死更好吗？”父母也不忍心让我去死，于是同意了。妾预先储存人和狗的粪便及污秽之物，听说城池即将陷落，先用炭和漆涂身，接着用污泥和尿液涂抹，直接扶着父母逃出。即使在乱军之中，刀光剑影交错，见到我误以为疯狂，连一眼都不看。此后父母失去了财产，又染上了顽疾，常常卧床不起。因此亲自前往贼营乞讨以养活他们，贼人总是怜悯我，而且从未有邪念，称呼我为‘癫人’，时常给我口粮。这样过了半年，贼人退走后父亲去世，我背着母亲四处乞讨，非常害怕小偷，因此不再改变。今年春天，母亲又去世了，我孤身一人，更加不敢稍微显露自己的形貌。

    989

    脱非有心如君，妾亦不欲轻泄。”李因赞之曰：“卿固曹娥一流人也。但值栗烈，将何以堪？”女曰：“是亦有故，妾少时遘一女尼，授以异术，日饮冷水半升，运气三刻，虽盛暑无忧其热，隆冬无虑其寒。日行风雪中，体常温暖。人以是目妾为仙，而不复狎视，职此之由。”李弗信，试以手抚其肌，时当秋尽，其燠不类无衣，乃奇之。语次，已至溪边，女笑谓李曰：“丑形将露，愧实难禁，君姑少避三舍。”李故弗肯，女不得已始跃入溪中。沉浸许时，方加湔洗。李从岸旁睨之，尘浊尽去，肤亚新霜，掩映清波，不觉心醉。既而掬水沐发，发虽短而如漆，挹注盥容，容不满而似月。

    Explanation

    989

    "If not for the kindness of someone like you, I would not wish to reveal this lightly." Li praised her, saying, "Indeed, you are of the same caliber as Cao E. But when faced with severe cold, how will you endure?" The woman replied, "There is also a reason for this. When I was young, I encountered a nun who taught me mysterious techniques. I drink half a sheng of cold water daily and circulate qi for three kē. Even in intense heat, I am not worried about the heat, and in severe winter, I am not concerned about the cold. I travel daily in wind and snow, and my body remains warm. People regard me as a fairy because of this, and they no longer look at me closely, which is the reason for this." Li did not believe her, so he tried to touch her skin with his hand. At the time, it was the end of autumn, and her warmth did not resemble being without clothing, which amazed him. As they talked, they had already reached the edge of the stream. The woman smiled and said to Li, "My ugly form will be revealed, and it is truly difficult to hide my shame. Please step back a bit." Li refused, so the woman had no choice but to leap into the stream. After immersing herself for a while, she began to wash herself. Li watched from the bank and saw that the dust and turbidity were all gone, her skin was as fresh as new frost, and she was reflected in the clear water, which captivated him. Then she scooped water to wash her hair, which, though short, was black like lacquer. She poured water to cleanse her face, which was not fully filled but resembled the moon.

    990

    芙蕖出水，华彩焕然，李于斯时，难免见猎之喜矣。女既浴讫，逡巡不敢登岸，李促之，甫腼颜露其半身。笑曰：“日裸逐于市上，反不如郎前之可羞也。”李至水边，戏牵其臂，女始登。全体已现，倍觉魂销。李将拥之欢好，女坚拒之曰：“野合非礼，君岂不知？必相逼，妾宁怀沙而死，不敢从君。”李乃止。以衣畀之，女著讫曰：“使妾复得为人，君之惠也。”李遂以骑载女，而自控以行。至夕，宿于村舍，两始定情，则犹然处子也。相偕至家，李之妻乱后已不知所往，因以女为室。女善居积，尤多智慧，相扶立业，家以稍丰。某县数日不见银筝，疑其仙去，犹喷喷称异之。是真不知浊水之珠，而妄以为延津之剑也。噫！

    Explanation

    990

    The lotus flower emerges from the water, its radiant colors shining brightly. At this moment, Li could not help but feel a thrill of excitement. After bathing, the woman hesitated and did not dare to climb onto the shore. Li urged her, and she blushed, revealing only half of her body. She laughed and said, "Being naked in the marketplace is less shameful than being in front of you, sir." Li approached the water's edge, playfully pulling her arm, and she finally climbed out. Her entire body was revealed, and he was struck by a profound sense of enchantment. Li intended to embrace her in a romantic encounter, but she firmly refused, saying, "An illicit union is improper, sir. How could you not know? If you force me, I would rather commit suicide by drowning than follow you." Li stopped. He gave her clothes, and she put them on, saying, "If I could be reborn as a human, it would be because of your kindness." Li then rode with her, controlling the horse as they went. By evening, they stayed overnight in a village house, and they finally established their romantic relationship, remaining virgins. Together, they returned home, and Li's wife, after the chaos, had disappeared. He then took the woman as his wife. She was skilled in managing savings and had much wisdom, helping him build their household, which gradually prospered. In a certain county, people had not seen Yin Zheng for several days and suspected she had ascended to heaven, marveling at the strange event. Indeed, they failed to recognize a pearl in muddy water and mistakenly thought it was a famous sword. Alas!

    991

    外史氏曰：闯逆跳梁，人民涂炭，闺阁之被祸尤烈。历览明末已事，不胜惨然。女独有洁身之智，反以不洁为洁，抑亦奇矣。使当日恶其不洁，必以冶容为洁，其不洁犹可浣乎？惟能达变，可与守经。秽污既除，依然故我，以视深衣护玉，华服藏姣，一旦受美面之辱者，其相悬又何啻万万耶？又曰：女子之义，当处常时，虽尺寸之肤亦不可令人见。至于患难，则与其为人污，毋宁使人见。见可言，污不可言也。达哉，银筝庶几箕子之亚矣！

    Explanation

    991

    The external historian said: The rebel Li Zicheng caused chaos, leading to widespread suffering among the people, with women in the inner quarters suffering particularly severely. Reviewing the events of the late Ming dynasty, one cannot help but feel deeply saddened. This woman had the wisdom to maintain her purity, yet she embraced what appeared to be unclean, which is truly remarkable. If she had rejected what seemed unclean at the time, she would have resorted to beautifying herself to appear pure, but could her impurity still be washed away? Only by being adaptable can one truly uphold principles. After removing apparent impurity, she remained her true self. In contrast to those who concealed their beauty under elaborate clothing, only to be shamed by having their faces exposed, the difference is vast indeed. Moreover, it is said that the duty of a woman is, in times of peace, to keep even the smallest part of her body hidden from view. However, in times of hardship, it is better to be seen as unclean than to be defiled by others. Being seen is acceptable, but being defiled is not. How wise is this silver zither (a metaphor for the woman), who is indeed comparable to Ji Zi, a figure of high moral standing.

    992

    随园老人曰：苟延性命，所以为亲，倘遭微瑕，适以辱之矣。女之可贵者，以此；可传者，亦以此。

    ○　董文遇
    　　齐东董文遇，粗鄙不文，而近狎声色。往往游乎康，使酒尚气，以致躏柳蹂花。曲中以其世家，且丰于资，贪得缠头，反畏之无敢怒也。一日初冬，将饮于妓馆。闻有胡媪，携二女卖歌于市，色艺皆妙选，齐之勾阑无其匹，人因从之如蚁。董甚歆羡，走价召之。迟久弗至，遂勃然，怒不可遏，盛气以待。及媪至，布衣练裳，引其女，亦绝无华饰，被服綦缟，而态动神流，容妍骨秀，早如彩霞入座矣。董不觉降心，威严顿霁，惟作色以询之。媪不答，二女从容应对，若流莺之巧啭。董遂无从置喙，反大喜，命座共饮。一时妙语娇歌，得未曾有，因更为之颠倒。欢然引满，不禁酰酌。

    Explanation

    992

    随园老人说：苟且地延续生命，是为了孝敬父母；如果遇到小小的瑕疵，反而会给他们带来羞辱。女人的价值在于此；值得传承的，也在于此。

    ○　董文遇
    　　齐东的董文遇，粗鲁鄙俗，不学无术，却接近声色犬马。他常常在康地游荡，酗酒傲气，以至于欺凌妇女。在曲巷中，因为他出身名门，而且富有，贪图得到赏钱，反而没有人敢对他发怒。一天初冬，他打算去妓馆饮酒。听说有一位胡姓老妇，带着两个女儿在市集上卖歌，她们的容貌和技艺都非常出众，齐地的妓院中没有能与之匹敌的，人们因此像蚂蚁一样蜂拥而至。董文遇非常羡慕，派人去召她们。等了很久她们没有来，他顿时勃然大怒，怒不可遏，带着盛气以待。等到老妇来了，穿着朴素的衣服，带着她的女儿，也没有任何华丽的装饰，穿着简陋的衣服，但姿态动人，容颜秀美，宛如彩霞入座。董文遇不由得心生敬意，威严顿时消散，只是脸色严肃地询问她们。老妇没有回答，两个女儿从容应对，声音如同夜莺般婉转动听。董文遇于是无话可说，反而大喜，邀请她们坐下一起饮酒。一时之间，妙语连珠，娇歌动听，前所未见，因此更加为之倾倒。他高兴地满饮一杯，不禁继续畅饮。

    993

    又以事怒妓家，举盏掷之，误中一女额角，血流如注。媪早变色而作曰：“村人不可与处，乃至是耶？”麾二女去之。出户即飞跃登屋，鸳瓦无声，而彩云顿逝。妓家及董之仆从，罔不咋舌。视董已大吐狼藉，倒于席上。醉乡侯之故态，人所习睹，惟扶之使卧于榻。越夕，即自归。乃自此媪与二女不再见于市，知之者莫不咎董。无何，董与所亲邹姓，同赴近郭，裘马翩翩，意气甚都。忽县役挟刺相迎，敬曰：“大尹命，将有要语相嘱。”董虽衿贵，究不免于趋承，亟诘以大尹所在。答曰：“在某家园亭公宴。”役又语同行者曰：“邹相公非他人，请亦偕往。”两人皆大喜，从役疾驰，若恐不及。既至，果系邑中别墅。

    Explanation

    993

    Dong became angry with the madam of the brothel over some matter and threw a cup at her, accidentally hitting one of the women on the forehead, causing blood to flow profusely. The old woman's face changed color, and she said, "Country people cannot be trusted, and it has come to this?" She waved her hand to send the two women away. Stepping out, Dong immediately leaped onto the roof, the roof tiles made no sound, and the colorful clouds vanished suddenly. The madam of the brothel, Dong's servants, and all others present were amazed. They saw Dong vomiting in a disheveled state and collapsing on the floor. His old habit of the Drunken Lord, which people had grown accustomed to seeing, was evident. They helped him lie down on the bed. The next day, he returned home on his own. From then on, the old woman and the two women no longer appeared in the market, and those who knew about it all blamed Dong. Soon, Dong and his friend named Zou went to the nearby town, wearing fine clothes and riding gracefully, full of spirit and vigor. Suddenly, a county official escorted them with a letter and said respectfully, "The magistrate has important words to impart." Despite Dong's high status, he could not avoid showing deference. He hurriedly asked where the magistrate was. The official replied, "There is a public banquet at a garden pavilion of someone's home." The official also said to the person traveling with him, "Zou is not an ordinary person. Please go along too." Both were very happy and followed the official at a gallop, as if afraid of missing out. When they arrived, it turned out to be a villa in the city.

    994

    役请先入禀白，久而始出相延，董与邹絷驹而入。历门两重，罔见大尹形影，即从役亦无一二，邹与董皆疑。询役，俯而不答。须臾得一亭，始曰：“止此相候，大尹当自出也。”遂亦踱去。二人拱候良久，绝无迹兆，乃就栏槛小憩。日将西下，枵腹雷鸣，董欲去，而邹反留之，邹将行，而董又止之。辗转昏瞑，甫闻人声嘈杂，如在左近。静聆之，笑语娇婉，又若妇人女子，始大骇。方思趋出，忽前役引二人来，素衣白冠，高几与园树等，面目狞恶，状如世之所绘无常者，大惊而踣。二人出巨练絷其项，若犬羊。行不数武，有公廨，绝宽敞，红裙翠袖者，几盈厅事，见董与邹之形状，莫不掩口而笑。

    Explanation

    994

    The servant requested to enter first to report, and after some time, he came out to invite them. Dong and Zou tied their horses and entered. They passed through two doors, but saw no sign of the magistrate, and there were hardly any servants either. Both Zou and Dong were suspicious. When they asked the servant, he lowered his head without answering. After a short while, they reached a pavilion, where he said, "Wait here, the magistrate will come out himself." They then walked away. The two men bowed and waited for a long time, but there was no sign of the magistrate. They then leaned on the railing and took a short rest. As the sun was setting, their stomachs were growling, and Dong wanted to leave, but Zou tried to persuade him to stay. As Zou was about to leave, Dong stopped him. They were in a state of confusion and drowsiness when they suddenly heard loud and chaotic voices nearby. Listening carefully, they heard soft and playful laughter, like that of women. They were greatly alarmed. Just as they thought of hurrying out, the servant suddenly led two men to them. The two men were dressed in white robes and white caps, standing as tall as the garden trees, with fierce and ugly faces, resembling the depiction of the god of death in the world. They were greatly shocked and fell to the ground. The two men produced a large rope and bound their necks, treating them like dogs and sheep. They walked only a few steps and reached a public hall, which was extremely spacious. Women in red skirts and green sleeves filled the hall. Seeing the shapes of Dong and Zou, no one could help but laugh.

    995

    强视其堂，珠箔四匝，饰以金玉，而雕题绣柱，轮焉奂焉，遂迷惘不知所为。又有顷，蟾魄高照，纱笼遍燃，厅上大呼曰：“速牵酗酒贼来。”白衣人驱董以前。厅中设高座，皤然者坐于其上，翠冠而锦衣，则故市上之媪也。董亦不甚记忆，威焰之下，仅俯伏听命。媪数其罪曰：“汝以白丁纨裤，而妄思依翠偎红。一事拂心，早闻狗吠。青楼中固皆薄命，复何堪汝之践踏耶？且因恃醉伤吾掌珠，诚为擢发难数。今既絷来，罪应莫逭。”董闻媪言，始悟前事，不得已而谢过，豪气尽靡。媪又宣言曰：“此曹不足污吾刃，婢子可为吾击之。”语未已，早有垂鬟数人，揽彩袂，舒柔荑，以掌加董面上。董犹觳觫畏避，顿觉襟袖中香气流溢，且不甚痛。

    Explanation

    995

    Forcing his eyes open, he looked into the hall, where jade curtains encircled all around, adorned with gold and jade. Intricate carvings and embroidered pillars stood magnificently, leaving him bewildered and unsure of what to do. Soon after, the bright moonlight shone high, and lanterns lit all around. From the hall came a loud call: "Quickly bring the drunkard here." A white-robed man dragged Dong forward. In the hall, a high seat was set up, and an old woman, wearing a green crown and a brocade robe, sat upon it. She was the old woman from the market. Dong did not remember much, but under her stern gaze, he could only bow down and obey. The old woman listed his offenses: "You, a commoner, recklessly thought of clinging to beauty. One thing displeased you, and you heard barking early on. In the brothel, all are destined for a short life; how can they bear your trampling? And because of your drunkenness, you injured my precious jewel, truly, the offenses are countless. Now that you are here, your guilt cannot be avoided." Hearing the old woman's words, Dong began to realize what had happened before, reluctantly apologized, and his bravado was completely gone. The old woman then declared, "These people are not worth staining my blade; the maids can strike him for me." Before she finished speaking, several maidens with hanging hair appeared, grasping their colorful sleeves, extending their delicate fingers, and striking Dong on the face. Dong still trembled and avoided, but suddenly felt the fragrance flowing from his sleeves, and it was not very painful.

    996

    腻指着肌，柔若无骨，虽当惊惧之余，尚觉形神俱软。旋见一婢，以媪命酌以卮酒，曰：“此酖也，可饮之自死。”董又大怖，不欲下咽，众复夹持而灌之。芬馥袭鼻，绝无他异，而入喉以后，如冰雪沃心，精气顿爽。董私念媪非恶意，方自喜，又闻群婢鼓掌曰：“自兹不再作高阳酒徒矣！”众正哄然，欻自屏后有笼烛照二人出，新妆如画，霞佩云衣。谛观之，则即二女者是。甫至几前，媪即命以董退，询曰：“邹君安在？”白衣人又复驱邹。

    Explanation

    996

    Her touch was soft and gentle, as if she had no bones, and even in the midst of fear, one still felt physically and mentally weak. Soon, a maid came, ordered by the old woman to serve a cup of wine, saying, "This is poison, you can drink it and die." Dong was greatly terrified and reluctant to swallow it, but the crowd forced him to drink it. The fragrance filled his nose, and there was no other unusual sensation, but after it entered his throat, it felt like ice water had been poured into his heart, leaving him suddenly refreshed. Dong secretly thought that the old woman did not have ill intentions and was just about to feel relieved when he heard the maids clapping and saying, "From now on, no more will you be a drunkard of Gaoyang!" Just as the crowd was making a commotion, suddenly from behind the screen, two people emerged with lanterns, newly dressed, their attire as beautiful as a painting, adorned with patterns resembling clouds and red clouds. Upon closer inspection, they were indeed the two women. As soon as they reached the table, the old woman ordered Dong to step back and asked, "Where is Mr. Zou?" The white-clad person then drove away Zou again.

    997

    媪熟视，怒曰：“蠢仆真不谙事！何恩怨不明，而亦絷累以来耶？”叱使去练，复下座逆之，且谢曰：“老妇龙钟，指挥不克明爽，以致开罪于君，恕之幸甚！”乃逊邹宾席，二女亦敛椎相见，色似赧然。既而媪命张筵，俄顷，水陆毕具。邹因为董缓颊曰：“同造仙坛，而独为阶下之囚，渠即罪所应得，在某不胜跼蹐，请宥之，庶不使风月笑人。”媪似未首肯，二女又言之，语细不可闻。媪始冁然，命去董缚，亦令入座。白衣人遂皆不见，于是邹董偕坐，媪之母女亦以主席相陪。酒香肴味，盘盎蒸腾，媪亲起劝酬。独至董则曰：“醉态不堪承教，请代之以茗。”董亦似不胜酒力，嗅之即恶作欲呕。惟邹欢然大釂，且与二女互相谐谑，若旧相识。酒数行，媪即命止曰：“夜阑矣，不可以再迟好合。”邹遂起席，与二女同入屏后，如有夙约。

    Explanation

    997

    The old woman looked intently and angrily said, "You foolish servant truly do not understand the matter! Why are you not clear about grudges and grievances, and also brought me here in chains?" She scolded him to remove the ropes, then got up from her seat to meet him, and apologized, saying, "An old woman with frail limbs, unable to give clear and sharp instructions, which led to offending you. I beg your forgiveness!" She then yielded her seat to Zou, and the two girls also bowed their heads to greet him, their faces seeming to blush. Shortly after, she ordered the table to be set, and in a short while, all kinds of dishes were ready. Zou interceded for Dong, saying, "We are together on the platform of immortals, but you are alone as a prisoner at the foot of the steps. He is indeed deserving of punishment, and I cannot bear to stand thus. Please forgive him, so as not to let the wind and moon mock us." The old woman seemed to hesitate, but the two girls spoke again, their words too soft to hear. Finally, the old woman smiled, ordered the ropes to be removed from Dong, and also ordered him to sit down. The white-clad people then all disappeared, and Zou and Dong sat together, with the old woman and her daughters also taking the main seats. The wine was fragrant, and the dishes were delicious, with steam rising from the plates. The old woman personally got up to toast them. When it came to Dong, she said, "Your drunken state is not fit to be taught, please substitute it with tea." Dong also seemed unable to handle the wine, and when he smelled it, he felt nauseous and almost vomited. Only Zou happily drank deeply, and exchanged playful jokes with the two girls, as if they were old acquaintances. After several rounds of wine, the old woman ordered the meal to stop, saying, "It is late at night, we cannot delay any longer." Zou then got up from the table, and with the two girls, they entered behind the screen, as if they had an appointment.

    998

    媪与婢亦不少留，但见怪鹗啼树，寒月照怀，厅上既未留烛，鬼嶙时时辉映。值清醒之时，无卧席之地，其苦又难言状。幸而良宵易短，早巳月落星稀。见邹自厅后出，笑容可掬，拱曰：“误登乐国，致君独享清福，贪花人诚不免有罪。”董亦不敢有言，默默俱出，马犹系于柳下，乘之遂返。途间始询邹以见厚之故，邹犹嗫嚅。董叩之倍亟，始肯吐露。则媪之母女，挟技来齐东，已匝月。邹故酷好狭邪，因以居室送之，并资其薪水，靡不周至。媪以是德邹，二女亦与之相得，颇有自荐之意。自媪携女遁去，经数旬，邹犹思忆不置。故是夕独留邹，枕席之间，倍极缱绻。邹亦以踪迹诡异，宛转诘之，二女皆不讳。

    Explanation

    998

    The old woman and the maid did not linger much, but saw an unusual owl calling from the tree, the cold moon shining on their hearts. The hall had no candles left, and the ghostly figures were often illuminated. When they were awake, there was no place to lie down, and their suffering was difficult to describe. Fortunately, the good night was short, and soon the moon set and the stars became sparse. They saw Zou coming out from behind the hall, with a smile on his face, and he bowed and said, "I mistakenly entered the land of pleasure, causing you to enjoy your peace alone, a lover of flowers truly cannot escape blame." Dong dared not speak either, and they all left silently. The horse was still tied under the willow tree, and they rode it back. On the way, Dong began to ask Zou about the reason for his kindness, but Zou hesitated and stammered. Dong pressed him repeatedly, and finally, he was willing to reveal. Then the old woman and her daughter came to Qi East with their skills, having been there for a month. Zou was deeply fond of the underbelly of society, and he therefore gave them a place to stay and provided for their living expenses, taking care of everything. The old woman was grateful to Zou for this, and the two daughters also got along with him and had some intention of offering themselves. After the old woman took her daughter away, after several weeks, Zou still could not forget her. Therefore, that night he kept Zou alone, and in the intimacy of the bed, they were extremely affectionate. Zou also questioned them about their strange movements, and the two daughters did not hide anything.

    999

    自言其身实狐，适间伎俩亦其母之小试道术耳。语已，董始惊愕，益为愧悔。归至家，方各分袂。董故不欲宣播，邹亦秘密，他人绝无知者。乃自此董之于酒，向也亲之若命，今也疾之若仇，杯勺不复入口。强饮之，涓滴落腹，不啻烈焰炽心，必卧病经旬，危而后安，遂不敢少为尝试。且于花柳之乡，足迹不至，犹可以面目向人，苟一涉其藩篱，则眉棱眼角，自生粉墨，拭之终不可去，人见之，皆笑不可仰。又必数日，始反其故吾，而豪情顿减，不再履章台咫尺地，反以此得为醇谨之土。而人之异之者，不免博加咨访。经历岁月，董始为人道之，闻者又靡不捧腹。余闻此事时，董年已强仕，躯貌伟然，恒自述其少壮豪举。盖既阅十余稔，青楼翠馆之中，久无文遇之迹矣。

    Explanation

    999

    He claimed that his own body was a fox, and the tricks he just performed were merely a minor test of his mother's magical arts. After hearing this, Dong was initially startled and even more ashamed and regretful. Upon returning home, they were about to part ways. Dong did not wish to publicize this, and Zou kept it secret; no one else knew about it. From then on, Dong's attitude towards alcohol changed dramatically. Previously, he had treated it as a necessity, but now he hated it like an enemy, refusing to even put a cup or spoon to his mouth. Forcing himself to drink, every drop that fell into his stomach felt no different from a burning flame in his heart. He would inevitably fall ill for a month, barely recovering before feeling better, and thus dared not try it again. Moreover, he no longer ventured into the brothels and teahouses, and he could still face people with a clear conscience. However, if he even stepped into the vicinity of such places, his eyebrows and eyes would spontaneously become smeared with makeup, which could not be wiped off, and people who saw him would laugh so hard they could hardly stand upright. It would take several days for him to return to his usual self, and his boldness and enthusiasm diminished significantly. He no longer frequented the Zhaotai Pavilion, a famous brothel district, and instead became a person of pure and cautious character. Those who found him strange could not help but ask about his transformation. After many years, Dong finally shared this story with others, and everyone who heard it could not help but laugh. When I heard this story, Dong was already in his prime, with an imposing physique and appearance. He often recounted his youthful exploits. By then, he had been living this way for more than ten years, and he had long ceased to have any literary encounters in the brothels and teahouses.

    1000

    外史氏日：酒为狂药，岂不信然？大则以之贾祸灭身，小则以之招憎市怨。至若买笑追欢之地，虽以纵酒为豪，而数斗沉醺，实杀风景。奈何叫号怒詈，几以温柔乡为解酲之具，是真村牛之不若矣。旨哉，醉态不堪承教，一言直可作宾筵全诗读，而晓风残月之旁，又可书为座右铭。媪固解人，其言亦甚解颐。

    Explanation

    1000

    The historian says: Alcohol is a drug that induces madness, is it not so? On a grand scale, it can bring disaster and ruin one's life; on a minor scale, it can provoke hatred and resentment. As for places of entertainment and pleasure, even though indulging in drinking is considered brave, drinking heavily to the point of intoxication truly spoils the mood. How can one shout, curse, and abuse, almost using the place of pleasure and indulgence as a remedy for drunkenness, which is truly less civilized than a village ox. Indeed, the state of drunkenness is not worthy of instruction; a single statement can serve as the entire poem for a banquet, and in the serene setting of dawn and the moon, it can also be written as a motto. The old woman is truly wise, and her words are also very amusing.

    1001

    ○　马元芳
    　　马元芳，淮右人，太史介庵公之犹子也。介庵疾笃，元芳为祷于岳祠，既归，遇一急足隶，貌颇狞恶，逆而语之曰：“郎君勿旋返，奉公命来相召，请即同行。”元芳茫然，疑其季父所使，遂从之去。出城东门至一处，绝类邮亭。官役百数，见元芳皆声诺曰：“公子来！”隶导之，见一紫衣吏，貌亦奇伟，谓元芳曰：“尊大人相俟已久，亟从予入。”时元芳之父犹在堂，为邑庠生，名位俱来显，心甚讶之。及入，从者数十人，皆披绣铠，手眧戟，或衣锦袍捧文书，分列阶下。堂上巍然高座者，果其父也。旁有二八姝丽，状如画图，捧符剑而夹侍之。元芳顿悟父卒，哭拜于地。父语之曰：“儿勿悲！帝命汝叔为济南府城隍，方伯巨任也。天符已下，因其典试楚中有颠倒优劣一事，为文昌所劾。

    Explanation

    1001

    Ma Yuanfang, a man from the right side of the Huai River, was the nephew of the Grand Historian Jie'an. When Jie'an fell gravely ill, Ma Yuanfang prayed at the temple of the Five Great Mountains. After returning, he met a swift runner who looked fierce and ugly. The runner approached and said, "Young master, do not return immediately. On the master's command, I have come to summon you. Please follow me." Ma Yuanfang was puzzled but followed the runner. They went out of the east gate to a place that resembled a post station. There were many officials and servants who greeted Ma Yuanfang and said, "The young master has arrived!" The runner led Ma Yuanfang to a purple-robed official who looked very distinguished and said, "Your father has been waiting for you for a long time. Hurry and follow me inside." Ma Yuanfang's father was still alive and was a student in a local school, but his position and status were now elevated. Ma Yuanfang was surprised. Upon entering, he saw dozens of attendants, all wearing embroidered armor and holding spears, or dressed in brocade robes carrying documents, arranged in rows below the hall. On the high seat, sitting majestically, was indeed his father. Beside him were two beautiful women, looking like figures from a painting, holding swords and standing on either side. Ma Yuanfang suddenly realized that his father had passed away and fell to the ground in tears. His father said to him, "Do not grieve, son. The Emperor has appointed your uncle as the City God of Jinan, a great responsibility for a governor. The heavenly decree has been issued. This is because there was an incident of reversing merits and demerits during the examination in Chu, which led to the impeachment by the Celestial Master of Literature."

    1002

    查予生平不履公门，不谈隐恶，颇为上帝所器，因以予代之。仓卒起行，未遑与汝别，故召儿来一面。功名皆身外物，阴德勿或缺，勉之，慎之。归语尔母尔妻，此行甚安乐，毋须悲悼。”元芳闻父言益大恸，伏地不起。父命人扶之出，又曰：“告尔叔父，好改革，相见有日矣！”元芳作儿啼，霍然顿觉。视之，日方卓午，身实卧于天齐殿前，大惊而起。甫出祠门，家人已竭蹶来报，则其父果殁。颠踬而归，尸犹未冷，因抚之大哭。嗣闻母，妻言，其父晨起犹扶杖游小园，呼童汲水，躬亲灌溉，身体毫无疴痒。既而往视厥弟，及归，忽语家人曰：“速觅元芳来，天帝有命，以我代阿定之任矣。”盖即太史小字也。有顷又曰：“候送者多，予不能复待，盍行乎！”言讫索衣，更毕而殁。元芳亦述其所梦，阖室骇异。往视介庵，则已汗出如蒸，不旬日而疾愈。

    Explanation

    1002

    Upon reviewing my life, I have never entered the public gate nor discussed hidden faults, and thus I was greatly favored by the Deity, who has entrusted me with a new role. I departed in haste, without time to bid you farewell, so I called my son to meet me. Honor and fame are external to the self, so do not neglect hidden virtue, and be diligent and cautious. Go tell your mother and wife, this journey is very peaceful and pleasant, there is no need to grieve. Yuan Fang heard his father's words and wept even more, laying on the ground and not rising. The father ordered someone to help him up, and said, "Tell your uncle, reform well, we will meet again soon!" Yuan Fang cried like a child, suddenly waking up. Looking around, it was noon, and he realized he was lying at the front of the Tianqi Hall, jumping up in surprise. As soon as he stepped out of the temple gate, the family members had rushed to report, and indeed his father had passed away. Returning stumbling, the body was not yet cold, so he cried while touching him. Later hearing from his mother and wife, his father in the morning still supported himself with a cane and walked in the small garden, called a child to fetch water and personally irrigated the plants, his body had no illness or discomfort. When he returned, he suddenly spoke to the family, "Quickly find Yuan Fang, the Heavenly Emperor has a command, to replace me in the position of A Ding." That is, the short name of the historian. After a while, he said, "Many are waiting to see me off, I cannot wait any longer, why don't we go?" After speaking, he asked for his clothes, and after dressing, he passed away. Yuan Fang also described his dream, the whole household was shocked and amazed. Going to see Jie An, he was already sweating profusely, and within ten days, the illness was cured.

    1003

    外史氏曰：旨哉，马公之言，曰功名皆身外物，阴德不可或缺。初味之甚属平平，及读孟氏天爵人爵之论，始叹功名之浮而不实，诚不若阴德之实而可据也。马太史一生耿介，徇为不愧厥宇，而究以一事倒置，让天爵于乃兄。不可或缺一语，良亦有所见而云。此康熙戊子事也。余闻之太史令嗣，其伯父讳龢，字立斋，先太史五年卒。所云相见有日者，殆不负其属望乎。又曰：正直为神，固不限于资格，然亦有殁后晋阶者。旧闻某府一通判，舟行赴其任所。行及江中，日有一巨舰与之偕，昼隐晦现，其笼灯牌额，则亦某郡分府也。

    Explanation

    1003

    The historian said: "How profound are the words of Ma Gong, saying that honor and fame are all external things, and filial piety and virtue cannot be dispensed with. At first, they seem quite ordinary, but after reading Mencius' distinction between heavenly rank (inner virtue) and human rank (external status), one begins to marvel at the superficiality and lack of substance in honor and fame, truly inferior to the substantial and reliable nature of filial piety and virtue.

    Ma Taishi lived an upright life, acting in such a way as not to disgrace his position. Yet, due to one incident, he was wronged and allowed his elder brother to receive the heavenly rank. The phrase 'cannot be dispensed with' is indeed based on deep insight.

    This is the story of the year 1718 during the Kangxi era. I heard this from the son or descendant of the historian, whose elder brother, named Hé and with the courtesy name Lìzhāi, died five years before the historian. The people he spoke of, those who will meet again someday, did not disappoint his expectations.

    Uprightness is divine, not limited by qualifications, yet there are also those who are promoted in rank after death. There is a known story about a magistrate in a certain prefecture who traveled by boat to take up his post. When he reached the middle of the river, there was a large ship traveling alongside him every day. It would appear and disappear during the day, and its lanterns and signboards indicated that it was also the governor of a certain prefecture."

    1004

    乃大惊，疑为奸人冒赝，而行藏都又不类。至夜泊舟，因具官服往拜之，亦欣然延入。视其人，年届六旬，岸然道貌，舟中亦载细弱，遂不敢疑其他。坐次，微叩之曰：“公之所之者，某府之倅乎？”答曰：“樗栎之才，实忝此任。”曰：“然则将焉置予？”答曰：“不知也。”遂不胜愤懑，亟索其凭。慨然相视，阅之与己无殊，而所钤者则东岳大帝之篆也。骇然询之，始自白曰：“公所莅者人，予所莅者鬼耳。生前为某邑教职，以清介得擢斯任，何竟不相容耶？”疑虽尽释，心倍悚然，亟告退。强留之饮，欢然竟夕。次夜径来答拜，由是往还莫逆，直至江干，帆影乃没。

    Explanation

    1004

    He was greatly alarmed, suspecting that the person was an impostor, and their behavior and appearance were unlike what he had expected. At night, when they docked the boat, he dressed in official attire to pay respects, and the other person gladly invited him in. Upon seeing the person, he noticed they were in their sixties, with a dignified and upright appearance. The boat also carried women and children, so he dared not suspect anything else. As they sat, he asked indirectly, "Are you the deputy of a certain prefecture?" The person replied, "My talent is like that of a useless tree, and I am truly unworthy of this position." He said, "Then where will you place me?" The person replied, "I do not know." Filled with frustration, he quickly asked for proof. The other person looked at him with a sense of resignation, and upon reviewing it, found it to be no different from his own, but the seal was that of the Eastern Emperor. Startled, he asked, and the person finally confessed, "You are here in the human world, while I am here in the spirit world. Before my death, I was a teacher in a certain county and was promoted to this position due to my integrity. Why, then, could we not coexist?" His doubts were finally dispelled, but his heart was even more filled with awe. He quickly took his leave. The other person insisted on keeping him for a drink, and they spent the entire night in joyful conversation. The next night, the other person came to return the visit, and from then on, they became close friends, until they reached the riverbank, where the sail disappeared.

    1005

    ○　瓢下贼
    　　晋人王某为余言，其邑中一积贼，则故偷儿中之黠者也。觊某村一妇，家小康而夫适他出，遂宵行以往，冀饱其囊。既至，径皔其垣，兼辟其牖，仡然入其室中，放手榻前而立。时妇犹清醒，灯故未熄，见贼乃大惊。强诘曰：“若何为者？”答曰：“予欲资。”妇度不能禁，乃曰：“由若取之，予家亦仅温饱者。”贼不肤箧，而易其可欺，复戏曰：“予欲眠。”妇大恚，不应。贼出短刃，长尺许，雪色与孤檠相映，晃耀一室。

    Explanation

    1005

    A Thief from the Region of Piaoxia

    A man from the state of Jin named Wang told me that in his village, there was a habitual thief, a particularly clever one among the bandits. This thief coveted a woman from a nearby village, whose family was moderately well-off and whose husband had gone out. Thus, he traveled at night to the village, hoping to fill his pockets. Upon arriving, he climbed over the wall and opened a window, entering the room boldly. He stood by the bed. At that time, the woman was still awake, and the lamp had not been extinguished. Seeing the thief, she was greatly startled. She forcefully asked, "What are you doing here?" The thief replied, "I want to rob." The woman thought that she could not stop him and said, "Take whatever you want; my family is only moderately well-off." The thief did not search the chest but found it easy to deceive her. He then jokingly said, "I want to sleep." The woman became very angry and remained silent. The thief drew a short blade, about a foot long, which gleamed white in the light of the lone candle, shining brightly throughout the room.

    1006

    妇大怖，方觳觫无能自免，而贼因枵腹适苦饥，忽谓妇曰：“予欲食。汝为予炊，饱或即行。”妇大喜，顿得良策。以庖厨之在异室也，亟衣而起，且笑谓贼曰：“饥者无能久俟，予藏斗酒，原以备不时之需。子姑自酌，予往执爨，不一时而腹可果矣。”贼闻有家酿，亦大悦，而虑妇叵测，携酒同往，就炊火酾而饮之。妇揣其意，亦不遽发，殷勤为供具。及贼将食，突出扃其户，键以巨锁，且大号。邻佑有未寐，闻声咸惊起，竞持短梃，聚者十数人。妇启外户迎之入，众亟曰：“盗安在？”妇指之答曰：“即在此中。

    Explanation

    1006

    The woman was greatly terrified, trembling with fear and unable to escape, while the thief, being very hungry, suddenly said to her, "I want to eat. You prepare food for me, and if I am full, I will leave immediately." The woman was greatly pleased, having suddenly found a good plan. Because the kitchen was in a different room, she quickly dressed and got up, and smilingly told the thief, "One who is hungry cannot wait long. I have hidden a dou of wine, originally to prepare for unexpected needs. You may drink by yourself. I will go and cook, and in no time, my stomach will be satisfied." The thief, hearing there was home-brewed wine, was also very happy, but he was wary of the woman's intentions, so he took the wine with him and went to the kitchen. Sitting by the cooking fire, he drank the wine. The woman understood his intention and did not immediately act, diligently preparing the utensils. When the thief was about to eat, she suddenly locked the door with a large lock and shouted loudly. Some neighbors who had not yet fallen asleep heard the noise and were all startled awake, holding short sticks and gathering a dozen or so people. The woman opened the outer door to welcome them in, and the crowd quickly asked, "Where is the thief?" The woman pointed to him and replied, "He is right here."

    1007

    方餔啜，予防其遁，业以葳蕤守之矣。”因贼无能逋，遂缕陈其状，意颇自矜。众视其室，黑暗都无所见。先以二人守户，防贼冲突，甫命妇取钥，排闼拥入。乃贼闻妇号，初无惧色，亦不奔窜，尽以其食物匿之灶底，而熄其火，盖已得藏身之固矣。及众入室以炬烛之，室中物俱安堵。凡妇所云式饮式食者，举无迹象。惟瓮中一瓢，漂摇水面而已，众固疑不至此。而屋仅一楹，举目可见，亦无待于冥搜，因反以妇为举烽，相顾微哂，默然散去。

    Explanation

    1007

    As they were eating, I prevented him from escaping and had already used a dense barrier to guard against it. Since the thief could not flee, he then described his state in detail, feeling quite proud. The people looked into his room, which was dark and revealed nothing. First, two people were stationed to guard the door, to prevent the thief from breaking out. As soon as the woman was ordered to fetch the key, they pushed open the door and rushed in. The thief, upon hearing the woman's cries, showed no fear and did not run away. He hid all his food at the bottom of the stove and extinguished the fire, having already found a secure hiding place. When the crowd entered the room with a torch, everything inside was undisturbed. All the things the woman had said about his eating and drinking were completely untraceable. Only a single ladle was floating on the water surface. The people, already skeptical, found no evidence of the thief's presence. The room was small, with everything in plain sight, so there was no need for a thorough search. As a result, they began to suspect that the woman had falsely reported the thief's presence, and they looked at each other and smiled slightly before quietly dispersing.

    1008

    妇竟无以自明，众去乃大疑，曰：“予岂梦耶？炊具犹温，所炊者亦安往耶？”语未终，瓮中震响，眪然瓦解，见一人淋漓遍体，衣水而出，且谩骂曰：“予不汝戕，汝反戕予，真犬彘之不若矣。”众去时室复掌火，妇见贼大惊，口未见启，匕首早陷其胸，妇遂倒。贼复审视，径断其头，然后履其闺闼，席卷所有，并觅乃夫之衣，易其湿者，而后去。平明，众知妇死，乃大骇。及见破瓮，始悟水面漂摇者，其下实即盗薮也。因以其状首之官，而贼究不获。后数年，贼遂以他案被逮，方拷掠间，忽昏愦自供如此，亦妇之幽魄不散也夫！

    　　外史氏曰：此事凡三易局，固不仅贼之急智足奇也。方贼之入室，妇已为在槛之猿。及妇之出号，贼又如入樊之鸟。究之请公入瓮，众竟茫然，妇遂奄然，而贼反翩然。一纤细之事，不可逆料若此，不亦足以借鉴耶？

    Explanation

    1008

    The woman was unable to clear herself, and the villagers, upon leaving, became greatly suspicious. They said, "Am I dreaming? The cooking utensils are still warm, and where has the cooked food gone?" Before their words had finished, there was a sudden rumble from inside the jar, and it shattered, revealing a man soaked all over, emerging dressed in water, and scolding, "I did not harm you, but you harmed me, truly worse than dogs and pigs!" When the villagers left, the room was lit again, and the woman, upon seeing the thief, was greatly shocked. Before her mouth could open, the dagger had already pierced her chest, and the woman then fell down. The thief then inspected her and immediately severed her head, after which he stepped into her room, took everything, and also searched for her husband’s clothes, replacing the wet ones, and then left. At dawn, the villagers learned of the woman’s death and were greatly shocked. When they saw the broken jar, they finally realized that the floating object on the water was actually the thief’s hiding place. They reported the incident to the authorities, but the thief was never caught. Several years later, the thief was arrested for another case, and during the interrogation, he suddenly became confused and confessed in this manner, it was also the lingering spirit of the woman, it seems.

    　　The Historian remarked: This incident underwent three twists, not just due to the thief’s quick wit. When the thief entered the room, the woman had already become like a caged ape. When the woman cried out, the thief was like a bird trapped in a net. In the end, when the villagers were asked to enter the jar, they were utterly baffled, the woman died suddenly, and the thief escaped unharmed. Such a trivial matter could not be predicted in this way, is it not a lesson to be learned?

    1009

    ○　卷四

    ○　蛇媒
    　　蛇媒者，媚术也。儿时窃闻先大父言，辽东某县，有一执鞭，其人固不谙风月者。一日驱虚车，偶过大泽之侧。时届夏秋交，草深禾茂，忽见双蛇长尺余，纠缠若饴，牢不可开。彼固不知其媾也，戏以长策挥之，蛇即分头而去，其人亦不为意。暮归，遇比邻之孀，偶扬其策，孀忽夜奔其家，百计求合。其人遂纳之，事讫各散。私为庆幸，而不以为策故。乃孀素持雅操，既归而夙夜自思，惺然曰：“我何以为此？”因愧而泣，顿欲自经。翁姑闻声亟拯之，坚叩其由，具以实告。所亲有知者曰：“此必蛇媒也。”伪以他故假策，其人竟慨然与之。携至家，沸油于镬，断而投诸其中。其人遂叫号竟夜，肢体糜烂而死。孀因赍恨亦寻卒。

    Explanation

    1009

    Snake Matchmaker

    A snake matchmaker is a form of seduction. When I was a child, I secretly heard my grandfather tell a story: In a certain county in Liaodong, there was a carriage driver who was not familiar with romantic affairs. One day, he was driving an empty carriage and happened to pass by the side of a large marsh. It was the transition between summer and autumn, with tall grass and lush crops. Suddenly, he saw two snakes, each about a foot long, entwined together like candy, firmly bound and unable to separate. He did not realize that they were mating and playfully struck them with a long whip. The snakes immediately separated and left, and the man did not pay much attention to it. At dusk, he returned home and encountered a nearby widow. She happened to see the whip he was holding and suddenly ran to his house at night, trying every means to consummate the union. The man then accepted her, and after the matter was completed, they parted ways. He privately felt fortunate and did not think it was due to the whip. However, the widow had always maintained a high moral standard. After returning home, she thought about it day and night and realized, "Why did I do this?" She felt ashamed and cried, immediately wanting to hang herself. Her husband and wife heard the sound and quickly rescued her. They firmly questioned the reason, and she told them everything in detail. Someone who knew the situation said, "This must be the work of a snake matchmaker." They pretended to have another reason and gave him a whip. The man then reluctantly accepted it. They took him home, boiled oil in a pot, and cut the whip and threw it into the boiling oil. The man screamed all night, and his limbs melted away, leading to his death. The widow, carrying her grudge, soon died as well.

    1010

    外史氏曰：呜呼！不知而误用，犹亡其身，况知而故用者哉！故此事先大父谆谆言之，用以戒人。予小子弗敢忘也。慎之！慎之！其勿以余为导淫而妄思一试，则幸矣！

    ○　续五通
    　　五通邪神，《聊斋》及诸书言之详矣。近今奉祀者少减，然而旧闻犹有可嗤者，因并附录于左。故明天顺中，钱塘民戴小一，鄙而有力。其室某氏，虽村女颇具冶容，齿且甚稚。小一防闲綦密，人固无能问鼎，即妇亦未敢自炫其玉。一夕，夫妇已就寝，忽闻窗外呵殿声。即有人叱曰：“戴小一何物小人，神圣过而犹然拥妻酣卧也！”小一大惊，穴牖窥之，纱笼十数对，卤薄纷沓中拥一贵人，紫衣而金冠，跨小骊驹，盖村中所祀五郎，即神之二也。村人素畏此神，小一蹶然起视。

    Explanation

    1010

    外史氏曰：呜呼！不知而误用，犹亡其身，况知而故用者哉！故此事先大父谆谆言之，用以戒人。予小子弗敢忘也。慎之！慎之！其勿以余为导淫而妄思一试，则幸矣！

    The Historian said: Alas! To use something without knowing it, one still risks losing oneself; how much more so if one knowingly and deliberately uses it! Therefore, my grandfather repeatedly spoke of this matter, to warn others. I, the younger one, dare not forget. Be cautious! Be cautious! Do not think that I am leading you into debauchery and rashly try it, then you will be fortunate!

    ○　续五通
    五通邪神，《聊斋》及诸书言之详矣。近今奉祀者少减，然而旧闻犹有可嗤者，因并附录于左。故明天顺中，钱塘民戴小一，鄙而有力。其室某氏，虽村女颇具冶容，齿且甚稚。小一防闲綦密，人固无能问鼎，即妇亦未敢自炫其玉。一夕，夫妇已就寝，忽闻窗外呵殿声。即有人叱曰：“戴小一何物小人，神圣过而犹然拥妻酣卧也！”小一大惊，穴牖窥之，纱笼十数对，卤薄纷沓中拥一贵人，紫衣而金冠，跨小骊驹，盖村中所祀五郎，即神之二也。村人素畏此神，小一蹶然起视。

    Continuation of the Five Tong

    The Five Tong is a malevolent deity, and the Liaozhai and other books have spoken of it in detail. In recent times, those who worship it have decreased, but old stories still have some laughable elements, so I will append them below. During the Tian Shun period of the Ming dynasty, there was a man named Dai Xiaoyi from Qiantang, who was humble but strong. His wife, though a village girl, had a very attractive appearance and was very young. Dai Xiaoyi kept a very tight watch, and no one could possibly interfere, not even his wife daring to flaunt her beauty. One night, after the couple had gone to bed, they suddenly heard the sound of a carriage outside the window. Then someone shouted, "Dai Xiaoyi, what kind of lowly person are you, allowing a holy being to pass by while you are still sleeping with your wife!" Dai Xiaoyi was greatly startled and peeped through a hole in the window. There were several pairs of lanterns, and in the midst of the entourage, a noble person was being carried, wearing purple clothes and a golden crown, riding a small black horse. It was the Fifth Lord worshipped in the village, who is the second of the Wu Tong deities. The villagers always feared this god, and Dai Xiaoyi jumped up and looked.

    1011

    妇则已熟寐，将呼之醒同往礼拜，神忽隔窗止之曰：“勿惊丽人，予之来亦正为此。”小一性素妒，闻神言颇不能平。且知近村之妇，多有为神蹂者，因奋然不顾，曰：“淫鬼耳，何足为神？其真能加祸于予哉？”径弛然复卧，若不知有神者。外又呼之，小一漫应之曰：“予已寝矣，神何为者？予妇恐不若他妇之易致也”。语未竟，神即哂曰：“予固谓渠倔强，非可以理化者，汝且姑待之。”乃叱其从者，行如风雨，疾驰而去。小一始撼妇醒，语之，妇大怖。小二笑曰：“予有力如虎，神即能亦无如我何，子勿以为忧。

    Explanation

    1011

    The wife was already sound asleep, and he intended to call her awake to go and worship together. Suddenly, the deity stopped them from the window and said, "Do not startle the beautiful woman; I have come for this very reason." Xiao Yi, who had always been jealous, was greatly unsettled by the deity's words. He also knew that many women in the nearby village had been trampled by the deity. Bravely without hesitation, he said, "It's just a lustful ghost, what kind of deity is it? Could it really bring disaster upon me?" He lay down relaxed and went back to sleep, as if he did not know there was a deity. Someone else called him, and Xiao Yi replied carelessly, "I am already asleep, what does the deity want? My wife is probably not as easy to seduce as other women." Before he finished speaking, the deity smiled and said, "I had indeed thought he was stubborn and could not be reasoned with. You may wait for him for now." Then he shouted at his followers, who left as fast as wind and rain. Xiao Yi finally shook his wife awake, told her, and the wife was greatly frightened. Xiao Er laughed and said, "I have strength like a tiger. Even if the deity could, it would be of no use against me. Do not worry about it."

    1012

    ”明日小一出田间，而心系其妇。往返数四，妇故无恙也。里人莫知其由，咸戏曰：“若今日足趾何勤也？岂阿嫂亦紧待播种耶？”小一怍不能言。及昏而归，与妇谋所以备御之策。乃以巨石镇其户，且牢鏁其牖。令妇戒严而卧，衣裤皆亲以密线缀之。室中不燃炬，而自荷一锹，摩厉以须，其防维可谓密已。如是者三夕，而竟杳然。妇亦渐厌其为，唾曰：“岂若梦魇而妄有所见耶？脱令有之，宁以神之威灵，而遂惮此不来者？”小一终不释然，仍前戒备。夜未深而神果至，其来也声势远逊于前。

    Explanation

    1012

    The next day, Xiao Yi went to the fields, but his mind was preoccupied with his wife. He went back and forth several times, and his wife was still well. The villagers did not know the reason, and all joked and said, "Why are you so diligent today? Is your sister-in-law also eagerly waiting for sowing?" Xiao Yi was embarrassed and could not speak. Until evening when he returned, he discussed with his wife strategies to prepare for defense. They placed a large stone to block the door and securely locked the windows. They ordered the wife to be on high alert and sleep, and clothes and pants were all sewn tightly with fine threads. There was no light in the room, and he carried a shovel himself, sharpened it in preparation. Their defense measures can be said to be very thorough. Like this for three nights, but still nothing happened. The wife also gradually grew tired of it, spat out and said, "Could it be that I am having a nightmare and seeing things that aren't real? If there is indeed something, even with the power of the spirits, they would still be afraid to come?" Xiao Yi was still not at ease, still maintained vigilance. Before midnight, the spirit indeed arrived, the sound and appearance were much less than before.

    1013

    惟闻篱落间有踯躅之状，盖其所乘之驹也。小一心知有异，蹴妇便起曰：“来矣！卧必不免。”妇悚然，毛发尽矗，手足亦无所措。未几，而猛风大作矣，瓦砾纷飞矣，应门者自移而司闭者自启矣。一时户牖洞开，向所为闭关谢客者，又俨若开门揖盗。然小一心亦惊，木立而视，顿忘利器在身，反束手以待之。俄而烛光自外而内，衾枕易故以新，室中之什物须臾一空，锹固不可得而知也。神犹未入，捭者复阖。灯火之下，小一自视其妇，则衣之弭缝者既以不解解之，裤之联络者又以不断断之。俄顷为无叶之花，转盼非在椟之玉。

    Explanation

    1013

    Only heard between the fences was a hesitant gait, it was his horse that he was riding. Xiao Yi knew there was something unusual, kicked his wife to get up and said, "Here it comes! We can't escape if we stay here." His wife was startled, her hair stood on end, and her hands and feet had no place to go. Soon after, a strong wind suddenly arose, tiles and debris flew everywhere, the person who answers the door moved on their own, and the person who locks the door opened it on their own. In a moment, doors and windows were wide open, what was once closed off to visitors now looked like it's inviting thieves. Yet Xiao Yi was also shocked, stood rooted in place and looked, suddenly forgot the sharp tools he had on him, instead of taking action, he stood with his hands tied behind his back. Soon after, the candlelight came from outside to inside, the bedclothes and pillows were replaced with new ones, the household items in the room disappeared in an instant, the spade, however, cannot be known. The spirit has not yet entered, the one who opens closes again. Under the candlelight, Xiao Yi looked at his wife, the seams of the clothes were undone, the connections of the pants were broken. In a moment, it became a flower without leaves, in a blink, it was no longer a jade in the box.

    1014

    小一于此，不禁心灰气绝已！又有顷，神始含笑入室，裘带翩翩，非复向之严肃，而温雅过之。顾谓小一曰：“若妇诚不易致也。”因叱曰：“卧榻之侧不宜有此癱人，盍牵去之”。果若有物径驱小一行，足不踏尘，旋出户外，而双扉早閛然复扃。小一立檐际，他无所睹，惟见青燐隐隐而已，胆力益怯，足不能移。旋闻窗中调笑声、狎昵声，妇默然，神则欢然。移时，零云断雨之声渐达于外，妇亦不再缄口，则其荡然情状可想矣！小一惊魂少定，忿气复激，思以报复之，而茫无善策。

    Explanation

    1014

    Small One here, cannot help but feel utterly hopeless! After a while, the deity entered the room with a smile, his robes and sashes flowing gracefully, no longer the stern demeanor of before, but even more warm and elegant. Turning to Small One, he said, "This woman is indeed difficult to attain." Then, he commanded, "It is inappropriate for such a person to be near the bed. Why don't you take her away?" Indeed, something seemed to drive Small One out, her feet not touching the ground, and she soon emerged outside, the double doors closing with a loud thud. Small One stood at the eaves, seeing nothing else, only faint blue light, and her courage and strength diminished further, unable to move. Soon, she heard the sounds of laughter and intimacy from the window, the woman silent, while the deity was joyful. After a while, the sounds of lovemaking gradually reached the outside, and the woman no longer remained silent, indicating a state of unrestrained passion. Small One's heart calmed slightly, but her anger flared again, and she thought of seeking revenge, yet had no good strategy.

    1015

    幸燐火尽息，妖物稍远，因筹所以同谋者。而邻比皆素畏此神，惟左侧一老教授颇饶于胆，且居恒时诋五通，或可借箸于彼。然不敢由门出，惧为神知，遂皔垣而就之。适犹来寝，款关而入，方孑然一身坐于短檠下，整理残编。见其突如，不禁诧异，亟起而询之。小一呐呐以告，且言其详。教授色然怒曰：“以神而行秽，其若人何？予固恶之久矣。请与子偕往，为子面叱而退之。”小一犹豫未信，教授据操戒方欲行，曰：“子勿虑，不从则打之，谅渠亦无能相抗”。小一不得已，遂偕来，仍骑墙而过。甫及户侧，已闻室中神语曰：“此老至，吾当谨避之。

    Explanation

    1015

    Fortunately, the phosphorus fire had finally gone out, and the strange creatures had slightly retreated, prompting him to think about those who had conspired with him. All the neighbors had long been afraid of this god, but there was an elderly professor on the left side who was quite bold. Moreover, he often criticized the Wutong, so perhaps they could rely on him. However, he dared not go out through the door, fearing that the god would know, so he climbed over the wall to approach him. Just as he reached the side of the house, he heard the god speaking inside, saying, "This old man is coming, I will carefully avoid him."

    He happened to be sleeping, knocked on the door and entered, finding him sitting alone under a short lamp, tidying up the remnants of a manuscript. Seeing him suddenly, he could not help but be surprised and quickly stood up to ask him. Little One hesitantly reported and explained in detail. The professor angrily said, "If a god acts so foully, how can it be tolerated? I have hated it for a long time. I will go with you and confront and drive it away." Little One hesitated and did not believe, but the professor took his weapons and was about to leave, saying, "Do not worry, if it does not comply, we will beat it, it cannot resist." Little One had no choice but to accompany him, and they still climbed over the wall. Just as they reached the side of the house, they heard the god speaking inside, saying, "This old man is coming, I will carefully avoid him."

    1016

    不然，血食胥不得享矣！”教授闻言大呼曰：“二郎速出见我，汝亦人头畜鸣者耶？”内寂然，外更厉声相召。良久，神始逡巡自出，匍匐于教授前，若请罪者。小一心异之，以为何倨我而恭彼也？教授责数之曰：“汝为一方保障，而擅污部民之妻，怙势宣淫，无所忌惮，岂谓我笔刀不利耶？予将上诉九阍，下控十地，使汝尊荣不享，庙貌无存，除神藉而落鬼道。汝谓我能否？”神不敢辩，顿首至地，唯唯而已。教授又曰：“不重警必将复逞，予无杖可以叩胫，只恁此夏楚，聊以示威可也”。神又伏地乞免，教授不从。打之数十，神亦不敢拒，仅嘶痛而无片言。

    Explanation

    1016

    If not, those who offer sacrifices will not be able to partake!" The professor, upon hearing this, shouted loudly, "Second Brother, come out and see me! Are you also a person with the head of a human but the voice of an animal?" There was silence inside, and he called out even more sternly from the outside. After a long time, the deity hesitantly emerged, crawling before the professor as if asking for forgiveness. Xiao Yi was surprised and wondered why the deity was so respectful to the professor and yet so arrogant towards him. The professor rebuked the deity, saying, "You are the protector of one region, yet you defile the wife of a commoner, abusing your power to indulge in lust without any fear or shame. Do you think my pen and knife are not sharp? I will appeal to the nine gates of heaven and accuse you in all ten directions, making you lose your honor and respect, destroying your temple and image, and demoting you from a deity to a ghost. Do you think I cannot do this?" The deity dared not argue, bowing repeatedly until touching the ground, nodding in agreement. The professor then said, "Without a serious warning, you will surely continue to act recklessly. I have no stick to tap your shins, so I will just use this cane to show my authority." The deity again fell to the ground, begging for mercy, but the professor did not relent. He beat the deity dozens of times, and the deity dared not resist, only groaning in pain without uttering a single word.

    1017

    教授杖讫，谓之曰：“念汝居位，稍从轻刑，此亦周官议贵之典也。可速去，再犯当不宥。”神又唯唯，忽遂不见。小一究不解其故，请于教授。微晒曰：“此非子所能知也。渠岂畏我老书生？畏我浩然之气耳！子可入视尊阃，事非得已，琴瑟仍宜如前，毋以一眚而弃永好。予亦行矣！”遂别去。小一入室，诸物又皆珠还。惟妇白身偃卧，形状如痴，以汤灌之始甦。晨起视，砌下泥皮盈地，即神所受杖处也。往谢教授，生徒对曰：“师于昧爽即束装启行，我辈后至者犹未得见，实不知其焉往。”小一叹惋，疑其为仙。复趋五通祠，私入覸之，其次坐者自腰以下颜色剥落数片，余俱如旧。

    Explanation

    1017

    The professor finished punishing him and said, "Considering your position, I have slightly reduced the punishment. This is also in line with the Zhou official's regulations for nobles. You can leave quickly, but if you commit the offense again, there will be no forgiveness." The spirit nodded again and suddenly disappeared. Xiao Yi could not understand the reason and asked the professor. The professor smiled slightly and said, "This is not something you can know. Does he fear me, an old scholar? He fears my righteous energy! You can go inside and see your wife. It is not by choice, so the music and harmony should remain as before. Do not abandon the eternal good because of one mistake. I am also leaving!" Then he left. Xiao Yi entered the room, and all the objects returned as if by magic. Only the wife lay motionless, her shape like that of a fool, and she only woke up after being bathed with warm water. In the morning, he saw that the ground was covered with mud, which was where the spirit was struck. He went to thank the professor, and the students replied, "The teacher left early in the morning, already packed and ready to go. We who arrived later still did not see him, and we do not know where he went." Xiao Yi sighed and wondered if the professor was a deity. He then hurried to the Five-Passage Temple and secretly entered to see. The one sitting next to him had several patches of his lower body peeling off, while the rest remained unchanged.

    1018

    外史氏曰：小一一壮夫，且素以力闻，而究不免气馁于神。使当时奇梃以击，未必遂为所污，何其临事而惧也？及观教授侃侃正言，其勇也大矣！于此见勇怯实由于养，而老壮弗与焉。第传者欲神其事，故言教授仿丈人之行，其实可以无庸。独惜薄责不足示惩，遂复有罹其荼毒者。

    Explanation

    1018

    The Chronicler said: There was a young, strong man who was known for his physical prowess, yet he ultimately could not avoid being intimidated by the supernatural. If he had struck with a large stick at the time, he might not have been defiled, how fearful he was in the face of the situation! When we observe the teacher speaking confidently and clearly, his courage is truly great! From this, we see that courage and cowardice truly depend on cultivation, and are not determined by age or physical strength. The storyteller wanted to make the event seem more miraculous, so they said the teacher imitated the actions of a respected elder, but in reality, this was unnecessary. Alas, light punishment is insufficient as a deterrent, and thus others suffered under the same harsh treatment.

    1019

    ○　又
    　　康熙初年，吴县一民妇美而寡，将议所适，犹未得其人也。其叔知嫂有异志，以幼侄非其所出，携之他徙。妇独居负郭，只一小婢执爨，故改醮之谋益亟。一日归宁其母家，实欲速竣其事，留婢守家，喁喁独往，以相距不过里许故也。路经五通祠时，为淫雨倾圮，神象亦都破坏，里中方鸠工，尚未营造。妇过之，以事关半生，将入而祷焉。足甫及门，见一丐者，敝衣褴褛，且眇而跛一足，自祠内出，相视而笑，意颇涉邪。妇遂不敢入，疾趋径过。行数武，闻丐鼓掌曰：“美哉人也！”妇大恚，辄欲反唇，而虞己只身，隐忍自去。至家语其诸兄，使往觅之，将饱以老拳，则已不见。妇居母家二日，心系其产，及暮复归。诸兄以农忙，妇遂仍独行。重过其地，则丐已先在，且不止于一，其数有五，率皆鹑衣百结之徒。妇大恐，苦无岐路可避，又自恃白昼，无敢谁何，不得已趋而过之。

    Explanation

    1019

    In the early years of the Kangxi era, a beautiful widow from Wu County was considering remarriage but had yet to find a suitable match. Her brother-in-law, aware of her intentions, took his young nephew, who was not of her bloodline, and moved him elsewhere. The widow lived alone near the city walls, with only a small maid to cook for her, which hastened her plans for remarriage. One day, she returned to her mother's home, intending to quickly finalize her plans, leaving the maid to watch over the house while she went alone, as the distance was no more than a mile. On her way, she passed by the Five Lords Shrine, which had been destroyed by heavy rain, and the statues of the deities were also in ruins. The villagers were currently gathering workers to rebuild it, but the work had not yet begun. As she approached the shrine, thinking about the significant matter at hand, she intended to enter and pray. Just as she reached the door, she saw a beggar, dressed in tattered and worn-out clothes, who was blind in one eye and lame in one foot. He emerged from the shrine and exchanged a glance with her, smiling in a way that seemed to carry a sleazy intent. The widow, feeling uneasy, did not dare to enter and quickly passed by. After walking a few steps, she heard the beggar clap his hands and say, "What a beautiful woman!" The widow was greatly angered and wanted to retort, but considering she was alone, she restrained herself and left. Upon returning home, she told her brothers about the encounter and asked them to go find the beggar, threatening to beat him severely. However, the beggar was nowhere to be found. The widow stayed at her mother's home for two days, but her thoughts were still on her property, and by evening, she decided to return. Her brothers, occupied with farming, left her to travel alone. As she passed by the same place again, she found that the beggars were already there, and there were not just one but five of them, all dressed in extremely ragged clothes. The widow was greatly frightened, but there was no alternative path to escape. Relying on the safety of daylight, she believed no one would dare to harm her, and thus she had no choice but to quickly pass by.

    1020

    迨及其前，丐者皆群睨不舍，大有轻薄之色。妇益恐，幸犹未措其手足，只以唇舌相嘲笑，亦听之而已。归至家门，天已向晦，妇以见戏于群丐，郁郁弗舒，命小婢阖户早寝。乃妇方假寐，恍闻床头似有人聚语者。一人曰：“我辈衣服不备，恐见笑于玉人，请俟之他日。”又一人曰：“渠思嫁颇亟，倘吏有夫，未识道里远迩。盎据之于未嫁之先，渠固无能再嫁。”众似曰“善”，其音微细，才可辨识。妇察其非人，心大惊。俄而声且孔扬，欢呼曰：“今夕姑逊大兄，弟辈以齿为序，自此美人之良宵不虑虚度矣！”语讫，如有飞隼一一破窗而去。妇心旌动摇，肢体俱软，亟呼婢，则已深入黑甜。强起燃烛，室中阒其无人，自以为梦境，或心虚所致耳，因更整衣而卧。眠席未暖，早入睡乡。梦寐中顿觉下体有异，惊寤而视之，独烛未息，向人眇而跛者，己裸然累乎其上。妇大惭，自视其身，不第一丝不挂，早且百体俱呈，益腼腆无以自容。

    Explanation

    1020

    As she approached, the beggars all stared intently without moving away, with a very disrespectful attitude. The woman became even more frightened, fortunately they had not yet touched her, only mocking her with words and laughter, which she listened to passively. When she returned home, the sky was already darkening. The woman was deeply troubled by the beggars' mockery, unable to relax, and ordered the young maid to close the door and go to bed early. As the woman was just dozing off, she vaguely heard people talking near the bed. One said, "Our clothes are not ready, we might be laughed at by the lady, let us wait for another day." Another said, "She wants to marry very urgently. If the official has a husband, I don't know whether he lives far or near. We might take advantage of her before she marries, she certainly cannot remarry." The crowd seemed to say "good," the sound was faint and barely distinguishable. The woman realized it was not human, and was greatly shocked. Soon the sound became very loud, and they shouted joyfully, "Tonight we will yield to the eldest brother, the younger ones will follow in order of age, from now on, the beautiful lady's good night will not be wasted!" After they finished speaking, as if a flock of falcons flew through the window and left. The woman's heart was shaken, her body became weak, she quickly called the maid, but she had already fallen into deep sleep. She forced herself to wake up and light a candle, the room was empty, she thought it was a dream, or it was caused by her nervousness, so she tidied her clothes and lay down again. The sleeping mat was still warm, but she soon fell back asleep. In her dream, she suddenly felt something unusual in her lower body, she woke up in shock and looked around, the candle was still burning, a person with one eye and a limp was lying on top of her. The woman was greatly ashamed, she looked at herself and saw that she was completely naked, she was already fully exposed, she felt even more embarrassed and couldn't bear to face the situation.

    1021

    旋闻丐者附耳语曰：“予实本境之福神也，昨于祠前睹卿玉貌，不觉倾倒。愿假一夕之欢，卿其勿拒！”妇疑而未信，然窃窥门窗，实无所启，始决其为五通。已而念垢污可忍，倘五者迭来，则身同传舍，将何以堪？若能以计制之，退其一，余者或不复逞。第鲜良图，庸讵能免？妇正存想间，神又伏轼撙衔，极其驰骋，且具又甚伟，妇颇不能当。忽忆人言，仙神皆惧秽物，虽此神淫邪不类，聊以试之，不成只博一笑，谅不至遂撄其怒也。适小婢天癸初至，落红狼藉，因就寝，见其纳污荐底，曾怒呵之，犹未遑移去，正可备不时之需。乃于暗中索得之，腥犹污手，心暗喜。神方豷沓交欢，不暇瞻顾，即举而置诸其颠。神果暴惊，连呼曰：“何恶作剧！”便将遁去。妇忿极，不复虑祸，思以重警之，极力以纤指深入其眶，乌珠随手而出，毫不见血，而彼目亦眇。神乃力挣而奔，出门如仆堵墙，卧不能起。

    Explanation

    1021

    Soon after, a beggar whispered in her ear, saying, "I am indeed the god of fortune of this place. Yesterday, at the temple, I saw your beautiful appearance and couldn't help but fall in love.
