I wish to share one night of pleasure with you; please do not refuse!" The woman was suspicious but not entirely convinced. She secretly peered through the windows and doors, finding no signs of entry, and finally decided that the beggar must be one of the Five Immortals. Then she thought, "Even if I can tolerate the filth, if the Five Immortals come in succession, my body will become a relay station, how can I bear it? If I can use a strategy to drive away one, the others may not continue." But she doubted that she had a good plan, and how could she avoid the consequences? While she was pondering, the spirit once again lowered the carriage and held the reins, riding with all speed, and its attributes were even more impressive, which the woman could hardly handle. Suddenly, she remembered what people say: gods and immortals all fear impurities. Although this spirit was lewd and unclassifiable, she decided to try it. If it doesn't work, it will only be a joke, and surely it won't incite its anger. Just then, a young servant girl had just started her menstruation, with blood stains everywhere. Because of this, the woman went to bed, saw the spirit absorb the blood on the bedding, scolded it angrily, but didn't have time to move it, just right for unexpected needs. She then found it in the dark, the blood still stained her hands, and she secretly rejoiced. The spirit was in the midst of passionate intercourse, not having time to look around, and she immediately picked it up and placed it on its head. The spirit was indeed startled, shouting repeatedly, "What a prank!" and would flee. The woman was extremely angry, no longer considering the consequences, and thought to give a severe warning. With great effort, she inserted her slender fingers deeply into its eye socket, and the black pupil came out with a touch, without any blood, and its eye became blind. The spirit then struggled and ran, ran out like hitting a wall, and lay down unable to get up.

    1022

    妇白身自起，以烛验之，则一土木偶人，即祠中所塑之大郎神，而绣象凋残，宜其衣履不整也。返视榻间，厥目犹在，宛如弹雀之丸，亦迥殊人之眸子。妇因大快，复其衣缕。呼婢起，罄其所有，分布床第，而后就枕，心究惕惕不宁，深思远虑，惟惧神之相仇。忽自悔曰：“此殆予负夫子之报也！弃幼子而他图，要皆慾心所致，故神得以乘之。世岂有坚贞之妇而见祟于邪鬼者乎？”于是矢志不嫁，顿易初心。诘朝使婢召其叔，并其诸兄。

    Explanation

    1022

    The woman herself got up and examined it with a candle, and found it to be a clay statue of the Great Lord in the temple, with its embroidered image faded, which explained why its clothes and shoes were disheveled. She looked back at the bed and saw that its eyes were still present, as if they were pellets for shooting birds, and very different from human eyes. The woman was greatly relieved and restored its clothing. She called the maid to wake up and used up all of it, distributing it on the bed and mat, and then went to bed. Her heart still felt uneasy, and she thought deeply and far ahead, only fearing that the god would take revenge. Suddenly she regretted and said, "This is probably my punishment for betraying my husband! Abandoning the young child to seek elsewhere, all due to desire, so the god could take advantage of it. Is there a loyal woman who would be plagued by evil spirits?" Thus she vowed never to marry again, and suddenly changed her initial intention. The next morning she sent the maid to summon her uncle and her brothers.

    1023

    众至，见象咸大骇。询之，妇匿其污，只陈其大概，且告之悔，誓以靡他。众俱服其智，更重其节，乃号召乡人，舁象入祠。里中亦怒神之淫，尽毁旧象，以其地为土谷之祠。惟妇犹惧神来，以清水渍其秽污，如红泉赤浪，贮于溺器，以备不虞，而神竟绝迹。后里人于祠侧，昏夜闻人相与语曰：“绝予祭饷，良可恨！但渠家近有节义神相护佑，将不克复报此仇也，奈何！奈何！”里人惊视之，则无所见。妇年至八十乃终，属纩时，犹命以月水置棺中，盖其戒心犹未忘云。

    　　外史氏曰：神何能为？人灵之则灵矣，神固因人而灵者也。第妇欲改节而神即至，妇一矢志而神不来。甚矣哉，节义之重也。不然，以神之五，岂不敌妇之一？安在蒙不洁遂足以保全西子也？可怪者，神依于象。无此象即无此神，人亦奚为设此象，反令邪神得志哉？余故曰：神何能为？其灵者人实灵之也。

    Explanation

    1023

    When the crowd arrived, they were all greatly startled by the sight of the elephant. Upon inquiry, the woman revealed that she had concealed her menstrual blood, only mentioning the general situation and confessing her regret, swearing never to repeat her actions. The crowd admired her intelligence and valued her virtue even more, and they called upon the villagers to carry the elephant into the temple. The villagers were also angry at the god's lustful behavior and completely destroyed the old statue, using the land to build a temple dedicated to the earth and grain gods. However, the woman still feared that the god would return, so she soaked her menstrual blood in clear water, which appeared like a red spring or crimson wave, and stored it in a receptacle to prepare for any unexpected events. As a result, the god eventually disappeared. Later, the villagers near the temple heard people speaking together at dusk, saying, "To stop offering sacrifices is truly regrettable! But their family has recently been blessed by a deity of chastity and righteousness, and we will not be able to avenge this grudge, what can we do? What can we do?" The villagers were startled and looked around, but saw nothing. The woman lived until she was eighty years old, and at the moment of her death, she still ordered that her menstrual blood be placed in her coffin, as her caution had not yet been forgotten.

    The historian remarked: What can the god do? It becomes powerful when people believe in it, but the god's power is dependent on human belief. When the woman wanted to change her ways, the god appeared; when she firmly resolved to maintain her virtue, the god did not come. How significant is the value of chastity and righteousness! Otherwise, with the god's five attributes, how could it not match the woman's one virtue? How can one remain unclean and yet be able to preserve the purity of Xi Shi? What is strange is that the god depended on the elephant. Without this elephant, there would be no god, and why would people set up this elephant, allowing the evil god to prevail? Therefore, I say: What can the god do? Its power is actually derived from human belief.

    1024

    ○　玉洞珠经
    　　闵人杜景行，壮而佞佛。尝独处一室，不与妻子俱，严戒荤酒，日惟淡饭一盂而已。戚族皆力诤，终莫之听，持斋三年，自谓道可以成，将不日拔宅上升，虽鸡犬亦同登极乐。谆嘱家人，皆沐浴以待，咸匿笑诺之。杜趺坐至暮，目不交睫，倦极而少寐。梦至一洞天，中有鱼尾冠者数人，见杜至，即欢然起逆曰：“来矣乎？吾曹待之亦甚苦。”因延之坐，授以一束书，使自披阅，视其额则“玉洞珠经”也。杜览之，开卷第一义即曰：“不生亦不灭，不灭何以生？轮回自有理，强汉与天争。谓空即色，空何以是色？谓色即空，色何以是空？当夫色空后，渺然无此形。如幻如泡，如电如露，不久留于世上，何以长住于山中？”反复数百言，皆辟释教之谬。

    Explanation

    1024

    Translation:

    The Jade Cave Pearl Scripture

    Dong Jinghang of Min was a devout Buddhist from his youth. He once lived alone in a room, abstaining from meat and alcohol, and subsisting on a simple bowl of plain rice each day. His relatives and clan members all tried to dissuade him, but he would not listen. After three years of fasting, he believed that he had attained enlightenment and was on the verge of ascending with his entire household to the Pure Land, even the chickens and dogs would accompany him. He earnestly instructed his family to bathe and prepare themselves, and they all secretly laughed and agreed. Dong sat in meditation until dusk, his eyes never closing, exhausted but barely able to sleep. In his dream, he found himself in a heavenly realm where several men wearing fish-tail hats greeted him joyfully upon his arrival, saying, “Have you come at last? We have been waiting for you for a long time.” They invited him to sit and handed him a bundle of books, instructing him to read them. The title of the book was “The Jade Cave Pearl Scripture.” Dong began reading, and the first passage stated: “There is neither birth nor death; if there is no death, how can there be birth? The cycle of rebirth has its own logic; the strong contend with the heavens. To say that emptiness is form, how can emptiness be form? To say that form is emptiness, how can form be emptiness? When form and emptiness cease, there is no trace of this body. It is like a dream, a bubble, a flash of lightning, or a dewdrop—how can it linger in the world for long, let alone remain in the mountains?” The text went on for hundreds of words, all refuting the errors of Buddhist teachings.

    1025

    杜素厌闻此言，一览即掷之曰：“此邪说也。若辈必非个中人，故言之绌缪如此。”即起辞欲行。众笑曰：“个中人至矣！何遽别也？”言未已，有丽人年可十七八，明眸皓齿，锦衣绣裳，自外入。笑语承迎曰：“妾来为君解此经，胡不少待？”径就杜并坐，以柔躯偎倚，妖脸熨贴，兼以腻指把杜腕，相与翻阅，一肌着肤，骨节欲酥，而剖析言之，口脂之芬咫尺飞越，杜神魂紊乱，意在女而不在经。且因爱生怜，惴惴然恐拂其意女遂去，因唯唯于口，莫敢出一词。忽闻众大粲曰：“心未死灰，那得成佛？”俄自经中出火光，撒手惊觉，始知其梦。亟呼妻闭户同寝，人皆莫测。至旦始言其故，闻者皆笑。自是饮食男女，一仍其旧，今且有子数人。每言及佛，则赧然不答。

    Explanation

    1025

    Residing in the mountains, Du Su was tired of hearing these words, so he glanced at them and threw them away, saying, "These are heretical teachings. If you are not truly enlightened, then your words are so inadequate and incorrect." He immediately stood up and said he was leaving. The others laughed and said, "The truly enlightened one has arrived! Why leave so soon?"

    Just then, a beautiful woman, about seventeen or eighteen years old, with bright eyes and white teeth, dressed in luxurious and ornate clothing, entered from outside. She greeted him with a smile and said, "I have come to help you understand this scripture. Why not wait a little longer?"

    She sat down next to Du Su, gently leaning against him, her face touching his, and holding his wrist with her soft fingers. They flipped through the book together, and the warmth of her skin against his made his bones feel as if they were melting. As she explained the text, the fragrance of her lip balm spread in the air, and Du Su's spirit and soul became disordered. His thoughts were no longer on the scripture but on the woman.

    Moreover, because of his love, he felt a sense of tenderness and was anxious not to offend her. She left, and he could only nod in agreement, not daring to speak a word. Suddenly, he heard the others laugh and say, "If your heart is not extinguished like ashes, how can you become a Buddha?" Instantly, a flash of light appeared from the scripture, and he woke up in shock, realizing it had all been a dream.

    He quickly called his wife to close the door and go to bed together. Everyone was puzzled. The next morning, he explained what had happened, and everyone laughed. From then on, he returned to his normal life, eating and drinking with his wife, and they now have several children. Whenever he mentions Buddhism, he blushes and does not answer.

    1026

    外史氏曰：心未尽死，佛便难成。一旦入纷华而靡，由其脚跟不牢耳。虽然，为杜之室者，必感此一番棒喝，不至长斋没世，徒为佛座之莲，梦之有益于人，竟若此。

    ○　阿玉
    　　蓟郡有薛端者，被褐之士也。家贫好友，而苦无其资。因书数语于壁曰：“淡交无酒，卿须怜我之贫；深语惟茶，予亦知君之馁。果腹而来，枵腹而去，固无妨也。麦饭一盂，葱汤一盏，乌敢荐焉。”如是云云，人恒诵而笑之。然其人绝风雅，缙绅大夫咸乐交之，以故其门若市，未尝以贫而减也。一日，行于郊，天寒雪霁，见枯莽中有物，毛苍黄，伏而不动。视之，乃一狐，为猎者所中，流血殷然，垂毙矣！薛忽自念曰：“闻狐能致富，足以供人之求。盍携之归，倘能活，即使佐我，樽中宁愁无酒乎？”竟直前裹以绨袍，且祝曰：“予非食肉寝皮利于之有者，可无惧。”径袺之以衽，归其家。

    Explanation

    1026

    Translation:

    The historian remarked: "If the heart is not fully devoted, enlightenment is difficult to achieve. Once one enters the world of luxury and indulgence, it is because their resolve is not firm. However, those who seek refuge under the protection of a certain person will surely be struck by a sudden realization, and they will not end up living a life of perpetual asceticism, merely being a lotus flower on the Buddha's seat, a dream that seems beneficial but ultimately is not."

    ○ Arhat Jade

    In the state of Jichuan, there was a man named Xue Duan, a scholar of humble attire. Though his family was poor, he had many friends, but he struggled with a lack of resources. He wrote several words on the wall: "In a casual friendship without wine, you must pity my poverty; in deep conversation with only tea, I also know your hunger. If you come with a full stomach and leave with an empty one, it is no harm. A bowl of millet porridge and a cup of leek soup, how could I dare to offer more?" Such words were often recited and laughed at by people. Yet, he was extremely refined and elegant, and scholars and officials all enjoyed his company, so his door was always bustling, and his poverty never diminished. One day, while walking in the countryside, it was cold and the snow had just stopped, and he saw something in the withered grass, its fur a dull yellow, lying motionless. Upon closer inspection, it was a fox, wounded by a hunter, bleeding profusely, on the verge of death! Xue suddenly thought to himself, "I have heard that foxes can bring wealth, enough to satisfy people's needs. Why not take it home? If it can be saved, it will certainly assist me, and I will never worry about lacking wine in my cup." He then approached and wrapped it in a silk robe, and said, "I am not one who would benefit from eating its flesh or sleeping on its skin, so there is no need to fear." He carried it home by its robe.

    1027

    人见而问之，亦笑而不答。时薛已失偶，室无二人，置狐于榻，抚之犹温，亟覆之以絮被。其邻适有医，托言雪中失足，乞得活血药一刀圭，研而灌之。狐微微转侧，似有生气。薛大喜，明灯相俟，以观其变。夜将半，神思少疲，乃甫一交睫，狐忽化为丽人，素面嫣然，衣裳楚楚，方将离榻欲下矣。薛故无所惊诧，已而闻狐笑曰：“妾系邻女也，君何致我于此？得无为穴隙行耶？”薛始骇然曰：“端虽不仁，免子于厄，何子诬我之甚也？”狐又笑曰：“妾名阿玉，与君同里，君自不相识耳。偶出嬉游，误中流矢，奔窜十数里，幸免韩卢之口。然非道力不能也。蒙君拯救，恩戴二天，窃思侍君房帷，以报厚德，故出此言以相戏。君慎勿恐。

    Explanation

    1027

    People saw him and asked about it, but he just smiled and did not answer. At that time, Xue had lost his partner and was alone in his home. He placed the fox on the bed, feeling its warmth, and quickly covered it with a cotton quilt. His neighbor happened to be a doctor, and he pretended to have fallen and broken his foot in the snow, asking for a small dose of a blood-activating medicine. After grinding it and giving it to the fox, the fox moved slightly, showing signs of life. Xue was greatly pleased and kept the lamp lit, waiting to see what would happen. As night approached its midpoint, his mind began to grow tired, and just as he closed his eyes, the fox suddenly transformed into a beautiful woman with a serene face and elegant attire, ready to get off the bed. Xue was not surprised, but then he heard the fox laugh and say, "I am your neighbor's daughter. Why did you bring me here? Did you hide me through a crack in the wall?" Xue was startled and replied, "Even if I am unkind, I saved you from danger. How can you accuse me so harshly?" The fox laughed again and said, "My name is A Yu, and I am from the same village as you. You just don't recognize me. I went out to play and accidentally got hit by an arrow. I ran for several miles and luckily avoided the hounds. But it was not by chance that I survived. I am grateful to you for saving me. I thought of serving you in your household to repay your kindness, so I said this to play a joke on you. Please do not be afraid."

    1028

    ”薛又愕然曰：“闻狐蛊人必死，卿之此举，毋乃仿中山狼，欲饱予之一脔乎？”阿玉颊遽赤，因曰：“狐岂恩怨不分，而必欲祸人为利者？且君实有所为，幸得与闻。”薛遂欣然曰：“予生平最喜交游，而因贫莫能治具，往往剧谈至暮，使客忍饥而归，心甚歉然。卿能为我平此憾，是即所以报德矣。”阿玉乃大笑曰：“审是，则中馈之事尤为妾所宜司也。但恐震骇邻佑，必得显然婚嫁，然后君有伉俪之名，妾与酒食之议，当得尽如君意。至于衾枕之同与否，其权在君，妾亦不敢相强。”薛益大悦，即与之谋。阿玉曰：“君可扬言于众，言聘某村女，假人仆舆亲往迎妾。至彼，妾自有家，第见红灯悬户者，即是也。

    Explanation

    1028

    薛 was startled and said, "I've heard that being enchanted by a fox spirit leads to death. Is your action perhaps imitating the Wolf of Mount Zhongshan, wanting to harm me for your own gain?" Aru's cheeks turned red, and she replied, "Fox spirits do not indiscriminately harm people for their own benefit. Moreover, you indeed have a purpose, and I am glad to be informed." Xue then happily said, "I have always enjoyed making friends, but because of my poverty, I cannot provide a feast. Often, lively conversations continue until late at night, leaving guests hungry and returning home. I feel deeply regretful. If you can help me alleviate this regret, that is how I will repay your kindness." Aru then laughed heartily and said, "If that is the case, then managing household affairs, particularly those related to food and sustenance, is especially what I should be in charge of. However, I fear alarming the neighbors, so a formal marriage is necessary. Only then will you have the name of a married couple, and we can discuss matters of food and drink as you wish. As for sharing the same bed, the decision is yours, and I dare not force you." Xue was even more delighted and immediately agreed to her proposal. Aru said, "You can make an announcement to the public, saying you are hiring a girl from a certain village. Borrow someone's carriage and personally come to welcome me. Upon arrival, I will have my own home, and the house with a red lantern hanging at the door will be the one."

    1029

    背创尤甚，妾不能久留。君不使妾辜恩，愿速为之是幸。”言已，忽不见。薛信此语，果如所教而行，辄言于知交曰：“仆已觅聚窟之胶，特以拮据，不克成礼。诸君盍助我乎？”众闻而窃叹，以为谁家季女，行将不免于斯饥，皆笑而许之。至期，薛以一舆一马前往迎亲。从者尽豪仆，踊跃争先，将以觇其岳家为何等。乃薛忆悬灯之约，日晡始出郡城。豪仆初以甚迩也，曲折十余里，比及村中，天将初鼓，皆讶曰：“门阖矣！夜将何归？岂区区数椽屋，能容此耶？”怨怅不已，薛亦默然。既而得其门，皘閎高大，悬纱笼数对，表里洞赤，俨然巨家。未几，僮仆欢迎，亲宾延接，华衣盛服，人亦甚伙。豪仆窥其居，重屋复阁，备极富丽，遂不敢相轻。

    Explanation

    1029

    The wound on her back was especially severe, and she could not stay for long. If you do not let me repay your kindness, I hope you will do it quickly for my benefit.” After she spoke, she suddenly disappeared. Xue believed her words and, following her instructions, proceeded as he was told. He then told his acquaintances, “I have already sought the mythical adhesive from the Juku, but due to financial difficulties, I cannot fulfill the proper rites. Why don’t you help me?” Upon hearing this, everyone quietly sighed, thinking that it must be the youngest daughter of some family who was about to be affected by this hunger, and they all laughed and agreed. On the appointed day, Xue went to welcome his bride with a carriage and a horse. His attendants were all bold servants, eager to lead the way, hoping to see what kind of family his father-in-law was. However, Xue remembered the agreement to hang lanterns and only left the county town at dusk. The bold servants initially thought it was very close, but the winding path took them over ten li. By the time they reached the village, it was early in the night, and they were all surprised, saying, “The gate is closed! Where will we go now? How could such a small house accommodate all of this?” They were filled with regret and disappointment, and Xue remained silent. After a while, they found the gate, which was tall and grand, with several pairs of lanterns hanging, the interior brightly lit, and the house appeared to be a grand estate. Soon, the servants and attendants welcomed them, and the relatives and guests greeted them warmly, dressed in fine clothes, and there were many people. The bold servants looked at their residence, which had multiple layers of houses and pavilions, extremely luxurious and magnificent, and they were no longer willing to look down on it.

    1030

    其家肆筵于庭，从人亦承厚款。且因鱼钥已下，留婿欢饮，五鼓，新妇始登舆。薛导之，平明始入邑门。及至己家，贺者早已骈集。阿玉降车，迳入室内，袖出千金与薛，曰：“以此犒驺从。宾客辱临，统俟酬诸异日。”薛喜，出而俵分之，豪仆俱欢欣而去。薛又谢客曰：“蒙咏车牵，愧乏吉筵，俟新妇少谙羹汤，当有以报。仆未敢悬定也。”宾亦含笑而散。薛入与阿玉晤言，见其貌愈妖艳，而荆钗练裙，装束朴素若贫家。因询曰：“视卿之居，当非淡泊者。其中亦有术乎？”阿玉笑曰：“君真慧人也。

    Explanation

    1030

    The host spread a banquet in the courtyard, and the attendants also received generous hospitality. Since the wedding key had been lowered, the groom was kept to enjoy the feast, and at the fifth drumbeat, the bride finally boarded the carriage. Xue escorted her, and at dawn, they entered the village gate. Upon reaching her own home, the guests had already gathered in large numbers. Ayu got off the carriage, directly entered the room, and took out a thousand gold pieces to give to Xue, saying, "Use this to reward the attendants. We are honored by your presence, and I will repay you at another time." Xue was pleased, distributed the money among the attendants, and they all left happily. Xue then thanked the guests, saying, "We are honored by your carriage being pulled, but we feel ashamed of lacking a celebratory banquet. We will wait until the new wife is somewhat familiar with the household, and then we will have something to repay. I dare not set a definite date." The guests smiled and dispersed. Xue entered and conversed with Ayu, finding her appearance even more alluring, though she wore a simple wooden hairpin and a plain white skirt, dressed in a way that resembled a poor family. He asked, "To see your dwelling, must not be someone who is unambitious or content with simplicity. Is there also a skill or method in it?" Ayu smiled and said, "You are truly a wise person."

    1031

    妾辈岩栖穴处，乌能轮奂如人？特为君少祛群疑，故作此狡狯耳！”薛又诘曰：“然则胡为乎易装？”答曰：“居君之室，自当以俭约示人，讵可任妾之意耶？”因亦诘薛曰：“君今者对妾，果无燕婉之思，而只为代庖之计乎？”薛不觉大笑曰：“情不自制，亦思两兼之矣！”阿玉鼓掌曰：“妾固知君犹然假惺惺耳！”于是沽酒交杯，欢然笑语。至夕，阿玉语薛曰：“衣饰可以从俭，衾褥不得不丰，勿使人笑贫家夫妇仅能藉草为欢也。”遂出户外，携卧具数事入，皆以锦绮为之。铺设一新，且极华软，始与薛解衣就枕。好合之际，固犹处子焉。薛抚其背，创痕宛然，因笑曰：“非予则卿真几上肉矣！”阿玉亦笑曰：“非予则卿非涸泽鱼耶？”相与吃吃被底，嬉笑不绝。三朝，大酬贺客，阿玉为治庖，凡十数筵，靡不丰盛，人因疑薛得婚于富家。

    Explanation

    1031

    "We live in caves and rock shelters, how could we be as splendid as others? I especially did this trick to dispel your doubts a little!" Xue then asked, "Then why change clothes?" She replied, "Living in your house, I should naturally show frugality, how could I follow my own wishes?" She then asked Xue, "Are you really not thinking of intimacy, but only of taking over the cooking?" Xue couldn't help but laugh and said, "Emotions cannot be controlled, and I also want to have both!" Ah Yu clapped her hands and said, "I knew you were still pretending!" So they bought wine and toasted, laughing and chatting happily. By evening, Ah Yu told Xue, "Clothing can be simple, but bedding must be luxurious, don't let people laugh at a poor couple who can only make do with grass for their joy." She then went outside and brought in several items of bedding, all made of brocade and silk. They were laid out freshly and extremely luxurious and soft, and then Xue undressed and went to bed with her. At the moment of intimacy, she was still a virgin. Xue touched her back, and the scars were still visible, and he laughed and said, "Without me, you would be like meat on a table!" Ah Yu also laughed and said, "Without me, you would be like a fish in a dried-up pond!" They laughed and joked under the covers, never stopping. On the third day, a grand feast for guests was held, and Ah Yu prepared the feast, with dozens of tables, all extremely abundant, and people began to suspect that Xue had married into a wealthy family."

    1032

    然而行炙乏人，仍以诸家臧获代之。内室障以布幔，外设一几，至则肴核早陈于上，应手裕如，从无立俟者，咸以为异。自是阿玉以烹饪相其夫，客至辄留饮，饮已复饭，斗酒八簋，无不咄嗟立办。即薛亦罔知所自来，顾而乐之，其心乃畅。但阿玉深虑人疑，每晨必浼邻儿为市鱼肉少许，其余珍错，率皆取诸室中。虽座上客满，无不醉饱而去。一夕，薛与客饮，忽思鱼餐，入而谋之与妇。阿玉笑曰：“此时何能得？幸妾预储于井中，君须自往钓之”。因授薛以短竿，上亦悬丈尺之纶。薛故笑而弗信，强垂其钩，引之甚重，尽力而致之，甃拨刺有声。则一赤鲤，长三尺，巨口细鳞，状如松江之鲈，目犹睒闪也。

    Explanation

    1032

    However, there was a shortage of people to serve the hot dishes, so they were still served by the servants of various households. The inner room was screened off with a cloth curtain, and outside, a table was set up. When guests arrived, the dishes and fruits were already laid out on it, and they were served promptly and abundantly. There was never anyone standing around waiting, and all thought this was extraordinary. From then on, Ah Yu cooked for her husband. Whenever guests arrived, they were invited to stay for a drink, and after drinking, they were served another meal. A large amount of wine and eight bowls of food were always prepared instantly. Even Xue had no idea where it came from, but he was pleased with it, and his heart was at ease. But Ah Yu was deeply concerned that people might suspect, so every morning, she would ask the neighbor's child to buy a little fish and meat. The rest of the delicacies were all taken from the house. Even when the room was full of guests, they all left drunk and full. One evening, Xue and his guests were drinking, and suddenly he thought of fish. He went inside to discuss it with his wife. Ah Yu smiled and said, "How could we get it now? Fortunately, I had stored it in the well ahead of time. You must go and fish it out yourself." She then gave Xue a short pole, with a line about a yard long. Xue laughed and did not believe it, but he forced himself to lower the hook. He pulled it with great effort and managed to bring it up, and there was a sound of scraping against the well wall. Then a red carp, three feet long, with a large mouth and fine scales, resembling the perch of Songjiang, its eyes still blinking.

    1033

    持归庖厨，有顷，即呼曰：“鱼具矣！”取以飨客，莫不称美，薛亦惊叹其奇。如是者有日，薛既好客，一时名士又喜与之纳交，因而客至益伙，薛之名誉遂益彰，即薛之学业亦益进。俄而登桂榜，旋又捷南宫，皆阿玉治庖之力也。薛无他眷属，携之都中，将授职，忽辞去曰：“君之大德已酬，妾之事毕矣！请放妾归山谷，再修本真。不然，碌碌红尘，日以汩没，行将与草木同腐，岂能尚有所为哉？”薛闻其去，乃大惊。挽之曰：“赖卿得有今日，正思报我糟糠，何卿言别之速也？”阿玉坚执不留，薛强之，忽言有病，至暮奄然逝矣，其面目固依然人也。薛大悔，遂具衣衾。至夜忽失其尸，家人皆骇，惟薛知其故，置棺槨，纳衣履于中，以礼殡葬。同人闻其空榇，咸来诘问，薛始娓娓为众道之。

    Explanation

    1033

    He took the fish back to the kitchen, and after a while, he called out, "The fish is ready!" He then served it to the guests, and no one failed to praise it; Xue also marveled at its rarity. For several days, Xue, being a good host, and at the time, famous scholars also liked to associate with him, thus the number of guests increased, and Xue's reputation became even more famous, and Xue's studies also progressed further. Soon he passed the imperial examination, and then he succeeded in the palace exams, all due to A Yu's cooking skills. Xue had no other family members, so he took her to the capital, about to be appointed, when suddenly she left, saying, "Your great kindness has been repaid, my duty is complete! Please let me return to the mountains, to restore my true self. Otherwise, in the mundane world, day by day, being overwhelmed, soon to rot like grass and trees, how can there still be any purpose?" Xue was deeply surprised by her departure and tried to persuade her, saying, "Thanks to you, I have reached this point, I am thinking of repaying your kindness, why are you leaving so quickly?" A Yu firmly refused to stay, and when Xue tried to force her, she suddenly said she was ill, and by evening, she had quietly passed away, her face remaining human. Xue deeply regretted and then prepared her clothes and bedding. At night, her body suddenly disappeared, shocking all the family members, but only Xue knew the reason, so he placed her in a coffin, put her clothes and shoes inside, and buried her according to ritual. The others heard that her coffin was empty, and all came to ask, and Xue then explained to everyone in detail.

    1034

    外史氏曰：四公子致客数千，以其富也。岂贫无立锥者，可以仿佛一二哉？乃竟致之，且多以致之，享荣名，膺厚实，飞黄腾达，胥根于此。非其一念之慈祥，并其无穷之痴想，有以作其基耶？阿玉之严密，自是妙人。若徒羡其烹炰，则犹是口腹之辈，而非深知阿玉者矣。

    ○　斗蟋蟀
    　　促织之戏，肇自宋明，沿及近世以来，遂流为赌具。日出为市，好事者多从之，醵钱合斗，恒至数十缗不止。都人有杨姓者，专其利十余稔矣。生子颇聪慧，貌且秀美，杨固习于市井，不使读书，日惟携资从己游。因而虫之材力，虫之性情，靡不知。耳湍而目染，童而习之，较乃父为尤胜焉。年弱冠，适有宦于杭者，亦酷好此戏，以杨之豢虫有法，募令从行。杨以老辞，遂令其子从之。往居杭，年余，杨子无他技能，所获无几，颇失所望。一日，闻净慈灵隐等处所产颇佳，乃白于主人，往觅之。从以二隶，携罩持筒，踪迹于茂林芳草问，迄无所得。

    Explanation

    1034

    The historian says: The Four Princes attracted thousands of guests, due to their wealth. How could those who are poor and have no place to stand even come close to achieving this? They actually managed to attract them, and even more so, enjoying a good reputation, possessing substantial wealth, and rising to prominence—all rooted in this. Was it not their one thought of kindness and their boundless foolish dreams that laid the foundation for this? 阿玉's strictness, truly a remarkable person. If one merely admires their cooking skills, they are still just someone who cares only about food, rather than truly understanding 阿玉.

    The Game of Cricket Fighting

    The game of cricket fighting originated during the Song and Ming dynasties and has since evolved into a form of gambling. Every day, the market opens for this activity, and many enthusiasts gather to participate, pooling their money to fight the crickets, often accumulating tens of thousands of coins in a single session. There was a man surnamed Yang in the capital who had been profiting from this for over a decade. His son was quite intelligent and handsome. Yang, accustomed to the bustling market, did not encourage his son to study; instead, he would take his son with him every day, carrying money for their travels. Thus, the son learned everything about the crickets' abilities and temperaments. Through constant exposure and practice from a young age, he surpassed his father in skill. By the time he was in his twenties, he accompanied a man who had been appointed to Hangzhou and was also a great enthusiast of the game. Impressed by Yang's expertise in breeding crickets, the man invited him to join his entourage. Yang, citing his old age, declined, but his son was sent instead. After moving to Hangzhou, after a year, the son had no other skills and earned little, which greatly disappointed him. One day, he heard that the crickets from the Jingci Temple and Lingyin Temple were of particularly high quality. He reported this to his master and went to find them. Accompanied by two servants, he carried nets and containers, searching through lush forests and fragrant grass, but found none.

    1035

    薄暮将归，缓步于白沙堤上，忽见一肩舆，后有二婢从之，其行如驶。及至杨子前，舆中人突以素手搴其幔，小语曰：“虫伯乐竟在此耶？”杨子故莫解其语，然睨之，微波横溜，媚脸半呈，迥非目中所习见者。神魂顿失，蚩然木立，舆早疾驰而过。乃行未数武，一婢忽折身旋返，迎而语曰：“清波门外顾家娘，蓄有佳虫，颇健斗，请往一观。”杨子会其意，遂绐二隶曰：“汝等亟归禀白，渠处虫果佳，吾当售以报命。”隶不敢挠，竟自去。杨子从婢行，因诘其家官阀，答曰：“主人亦大好官，殁已多年矣。”无何，抵其处，天已向晦。杨子视之，高门华屋，虽不甚修整，而宽广异常。

    Explanation

    1035

    As twilight approached, Yangzi was walking leisurely along the White Sand Causeway when he suddenly saw a sedan chair, followed by two maidservants, moving swiftly. As it reached Yangzi, the person inside the sedan chair suddenly lifted the curtain with a white hand and whispered softly, "Is the Bug Matchmaker here after all?" Yangzi could not understand her words, but he glanced at her. She had a subtle, flowing gaze, and a charming face partially revealed, far from what he had ever seen before. His spirit and soul were suddenly lost, and he stood motionless like a statue. The sedan chair quickly passed by. After walking a few steps, one of the maids suddenly turned back and greeted him, saying, "There is a woman from the Gu family outside the Clear Wave Gate who has a fine insect, quite strong in battle. Please go and see it." Yangzi understood her intention and then deceived the two servants, saying, "You both quickly return and report. The insect at her place is indeed excellent. I will buy it to fulfill my duty." The servants dared not resist and left on their own. Yangzi followed the maid. He asked about her family's status, and she replied, "Her master was also a great official, but he has been dead for many years." Soon, they arrived at the place, and the sky was already darkening. Yangzi looked at it. The gates were grand, and the houses were splendid, although not very well maintained, but unusually spacious and broad.

    1036

    阍者数人，皆圆帽青衣，绝不类时世装。见婢亦莫交片语，杨子心颇骇异，不得已而随婢入。至于庭除，婢未及禀覆，其人早降舆而出，命婢肃宾。全身已露，则烟鬟云鬓，霞脸蛾眉，年仅二旬余，绰约人也。杨子习为人役，睹此垣赫，不免震惊，乃直前牛跪。妇顾之微笑，止之曰：“勿作此态。子有绝技，奈何以厮养自卑”！遂敛衽逊之入，杨子几措身无处，腼然而行。庭后高屋五楹，花竹绕砌，遥闻喓喓之声，清如戛玉，即所蓄之大蟋蟀焉。履未及阶，湘帘高挑，又有美婢四人哄然出迎。妇延杨子入室，处以宾席，杨子更跼蹐不安。

    Explanation

    1036

    A number of gatekeepers stood there, all wearing round hats and blue robes, completely unlike the fashion of the time. They did not exchange a single word with the maid, and Yangzi's heart was greatly startled and surprised, unwillingly following the maid inside. Upon reaching the courtyard, the maid had not yet reported back when the woman descended from her carriage and came out, ordering the maid to welcome the guest. Her entire appearance was revealed—hair like smoke and clouds, face like a sunset, eyebrows like silkworms—and she was only in her early twenties, a graceful and elegant person. Yangzi, accustomed to serving as a servant, was overwhelmed by the grandeur and could not help but be shocked, bowing deeply like a cow. The woman looked at him and smiled, stopping him and saying, "Do not do this. You have exceptional skills, why belittle yourself as a servant!" She then bowed and invited him in, and Yangzi almost didn't know where to stand, shyly walking. Behind the courtyard stood a high house with five rooms, surrounded by flowers and bamboo. Far away, the sound of cicadas could be heard, as clear as a jade chime, which were the large crickets she kept. Before his feet reached the steps, the silk curtains were raised high, and there were four beautiful maids who rushed out to greet him. The woman invited Yangzi into the room, seating him at the guest's seat, and Yangzi felt even more uneasy.

    1037

    妇因谓曰：“闻子之于促织，颇得家传。且来自帝都，见闻应广，故特屈来与子戏，消此永夜。”言未已，遍室皆燃巨烛，辉煌如昼。杨子窃窥，四壁皆以文楠为架，异锦装璜，上列细泥陶器数百具，制绝精工，皆虫也。妇一颐指，侍婢即施红毹于地上，设锦裀，置斗盆，以瓦为质而范金于外，刻镂雕文，更极匠巧。妇先起语杨子曰：“子之于虫，不第鸣之而通其意，当必聆之而识其材。架上物唯子自择，予亦以一虫当之，姑先尝试，可乎？”杨子欣然从之。于是婢执烛，妇导以行。盆盈于架，皆嵌小牙牌凿银为字，杨子故不识之无，疑即虫之名号焉。斯时彩色炫目，兰麝薰心，杨子即善于品题，亦无能月旦。

    1037

    The woman then said to him, "I have heard that you are skilled in crickets and have inherited this knowledge from your family. Moreover, you come from the imperial capital, so your knowledge and experiences should be extensive. Therefore, I have specially come to play with you and pass this long night." Before she finished speaking, the entire room was lit with large candles, shining as brightly as daylight. Yangzi secretly peered around and saw that the four walls were lined with fine navelwood shelves, adorned with exotic brocades, on which were arranged hundreds of finely crafted clay pots, all of which contained crickets. At the woman's nod, the maidservant spread a red carpet on the floor, laid down a silk mat, and placed a fighting pot, which was made of clay with a metal exterior, intricately carved with designs, showcasing the utmost craftsmanship. The woman then said to Yangzi, "When it comes to crickets, you not only understand their chirping but also recognize their qualities by listening. The items on the shelves are for you to choose, and I will also select a cricket to match. Let's try it first, shall we?" Yangzi happily agreed. The maidservant then took a candle, and the woman led the way. The pots were full on the shelves, all with small ivory plaques engraved with silver letters. Yangzi, who didn't know how to read, guessed that they were the names of the crickets. At that moment, the colors were dazzling, and the fragrance of orchids and musk filled the air. Even if Yangzi was good at evaluating, he could not assess them.

    1038

    流览数四，罔知所从，遂冒然指一盆曰：“得此足矣！”妇微哂，亦命婢取其一，偕返中庭。两人席地坐，明炬张灯，婢又进金丝罩、玉筒诸物。杨启奁见虫，早已气沮。及斗，妇之虫三跃，杨之虫果已披靡，妇与婢皆鼓掌，哄然盈室。杨子少年负气，起请再易一虫，妇亦笑而许之。杨子乃自往物色，得之于西北隅。屏息良久，侧耳倾听，大喜，自捧之来。及烛下，启视之，锐喙劲足，铁背金头，昂昂然真英物也。妇一谛观，即掩其盆曰：“此非凡质，不可以空言斗，愿以一物为采。”杨子慨然请命，妇曰：“予虫若负，当以盆罩赠子，无所吝。

    Explanation

    1038

    After glancing at the insects several times, Yang was at a loss about which to choose, so he impulsively pointed to one box and said, "This one is enough!" The woman smiled slightly and also ordered a servant to take one, and they returned together to the central courtyard. The two sat on the ground, lit torches and lanterns, and the servant brought in decorative items like gold-threaded covers and jade tubes. When Yang opened the box to see the insects, he had already lost heart. When they fought, the woman's insect jumped three times, and Yang's insect was indeed defeated. The woman and her servant clapped their hands, and the room was filled with laughter. Yang, being a young man with a strong sense of pride, stood up and asked to exchange another insect. The woman smiled and agreed. Yang then went to find another insect and found it in the northwest corner. He held his breath for a long time, listened intently, and was greatly pleased before bringing the insect. When they lit the candles, he opened the box to see it, noting its sharp beak and strong legs, iron back and golden head, and concluded that it was truly a magnificent creature. The woman took a close look, covered the box, and said, "This is not an ordinary quality; we cannot settle the matter with empty words. I propose to settle it with a token." Yang agreed, and the woman said, "If my insect loses, I will give you the box without hesitation."

    1039

    子虫若负，其如之何？”杨子顿悟，己实罄身无所携，遂不敢答。旁一婢笑曰：“昔主君与娘子较，娘子负即得当夕。今反其道而用之，杨子之身，岂其一无所有乎？”妇闻言面赧，而意似首肯。杨子亦解其指，心窃喜，即起曰：“敬诺！”遂复斗。甫入盆中，新虫鼓鬣一鸣，故虫早趯足而遁。杨子亦大笑，辄取盆罩以自与。妇哂曰：“牧竖儿唯利是视，何竟如此？汝既转负为胜，予当大败请复。”语次，曰婢令别取一虫来。虫至，斗于盆，踔厉良久，相持不下。妇乃顿更庄态，始而掠鬓支颐，指顾笑语，继乃軃身移坐，媚态纷呈。癷以螓首枕杨子之膝，旋以柔荑抚杨子之腕。杨子慾焰巳炽，遂不暇视虫。

    Explanation

    1039

    If the young insect loses, what can be done about it?" Yangzi suddenly realized that he actually had nothing to offer, so he dared not respond. A maid standing by smiled and said, "In the past, when the master and the mistress competed, if the mistress lost, she would get the night. Now, reversing the method, Yangzi's body, how could it be absolutely empty?" The wife blushed upon hearing this, but her expression seemed to indicate agreement. Yangzi also understood her hint, secretly happy, and immediately stood up, saying, "I respectfully agree!" So they resumed the contest. As soon as they entered the basin, the new insect flapped its legs and cried once, while the old insect jumped and fled early. Yangzi also laughed heartily, immediately taking the basin to cover himself. The wife sneered, "Shepherd boy, you only care about profit, why are you like this? Since you have turned a loss into a win, I will greatly defeat you and request to continue." While speaking, she ordered the maid to fetch another insect. When the insect arrived, they fought in the basin, struggling fiercely for a long time, neither side giving in. The wife then suddenly changed to a serious demeanor, first stroking her hair and resting her cheek on her hand, pointing and looking, laughing and talking, then leaning her body and moving her seat, displaying flirtatious manners. She leaned her beautiful head on Yangzi's knee and then stroked his wrist with her soft hands. Yangzi's desire was already burning, so he had no time to look at the insect.

    1040

    未几，闻婢群噪曰：“杨虫北矣！”杨子视焉，虫果出于盆外。则婢之黠者，乘其意之不在，竟以纤手出之，而杨实未及知也。众因请曰：“天缘可卜，虫实良媒，杨子固无辞，娘子亦幸无固辞。”妇不言，手撚裙带，而首之枕杨如故。婢遂不再言，亟撤斗具，促妇起，拥之以行。屋侧另有卧室，衾褥綦华，杨子与妇，解衣就枕，婢乃以烛去。妇既柔媚，而枕席之情又倍加恣肆，杨子遂颠倒于情中，卧以为不世之遇矣。倦极方眠，群婢复至，启曰：“明星煌煌，固难俾昼作夜也。”妇乃携杨子起，垂泪诀别曰：“一夕新欢，三生深幸。但妾非生人，实故宋平章贾似道之宠姬也。

    Explanation

    1040

    Not long after, the maidservants cried out in unison, "The Yang insect has gone north!" Yang looked up, and sure enough, the insect had indeed escaped from the pot. It turned out that the clever maid had taken advantage of the moment when Yang's mind was not focused, and stealthily released the insect with her delicate hand, while Yang was unaware of it. The group then urged, "The heavens have shown a favorable omen, and the insect is indeed a good matchmaker. Yang, you have no reason to refuse, and Madam, you should also not decline." The woman remained silent, twirling the hem of her skirt, but continued to rest her head on Yang as before. The maid said no more, quickly removing the equipment for the insect fight, and urging the woman to get up. She escorted her away. There was another bedroom on the side of the house, where the bedding was exceptionally fine. Yang and the woman undressed and lay down together, and the maid then left with the candles. The woman was both gentle and alluring, and the passion between them grew even more unrestrained. Yang was utterly lost in the moment, believing it to be a once-in-a-lifetime encounter. Exhausted, he fell asleep, only for the maids to return and say, "The stars are shining brightly, it is indeed difficult to make the day into night." The woman then took Yang by the hand and, with tears in her eyes, said, "One night of new joy, a deep fortune for three lifetimes. But I am not a living person, I am the beloved concubine of the former Song Chancellor, Jia Sidao."

    1041

    生时以色专房，缘渠好斗蟋蟀，妾辈亦以此逢迎之。幸先平章而天，遂并戏具纳之窀穸。昨见子风流玉立，不觉醉心，因借秋虫复沾春色。惟不敢久留吾子，谨将盆罩二事少志欢悰，幸勿弃捐为嘱。”言讫，取以相赠，乃命婢导之以出。杨子虽不通文，然知身在鬼蜮，不胜战兢。甫出门，略一回顾，则荒坟三处，屋宇杳然，益股栗不能举趾。踉跄而前，幸逢堤畔，约半日始返本署。乃主人得隶回报，既已怀疑，及询之，又都类诞妄。杨子无以自明，始出盆罩以献主人。细玩之，土色斑然，果非今世物，方释厥惑。居数日，杨子患河鱼之疾，弥留匝月。主人虑焉，畀以百金，命仆送之北上，其物遂留于越。余在钱塘，曾见之于某巨家，则已珍为玩器矣。

    Explanation

    1041

    A Woman with Exclusive Beauty and Cricket Fights

    During her lifetime, she monopolized the room with her beauty. Because he loved to fight crickets, the women in the household also flattered him with this interest. Fortunately, he passed away before the court could judge him, and thus, the toys were buried together in the grave. Yesterday, I saw you, graceful and tall, and unconsciously, my heart was intoxicated. Therefore, borrowing the autumn insects to touch upon spring colors. But I dare not keep you for too long, carefully giving you the two items to slightly record our joy, please do not discard them as a reminder. After saying this, she took them to give to him and then ordered a maid to guide him out. Yangzi, though not well-versed in literature, knew he was in a realm of ghosts and spirits, and could not help but tremble with fear. As soon as he went out, he glanced back, and there were three desolate graves, the houses vanished, even more trembling, unable to lift his feet. Stumbling forward, fortunately encountering the embankment, after about half a day, he returned to his office. The master received a report from the servant and was already suspicious, when he asked, it seemed like nonsense. Yangzi had no way to prove himself, and then took out the pot and cover to present to the master. Carefully examining it, the soil color was speckled, indeed not from this world, only then did he dispel his confusion. After several days, Yangzi fell ill with the disease of the river fish, dying for a month. The master was concerned and gave him a hundred gold coins, ordered the servant to send him north, the items remained in Yue. I was in Qiantang and once saw them in a great family, they were already treasured as playthings.

    1042

    外史氏曰：蟋蟀微物，以似道之奸雄，何竟嗜好如此？及闻是事，乃悟记云：似道在半闲堂，与姬妾为促织之戏，亦明皇蝶幸之遗意，为色荒之所致欤？独惜杨子不文，且胆落于鬼语，不克详询其梗概。然窥此一斑，亦可助风流之谈柄，而脍炙人口已。

    Explanation

    1042

    The Commentator said: Crickets are insignificant creatures, yet they are compared to the corrupt officials like Jia Sidao, who indulged in such peculiar pastimes. How could they have such unusual preferences? Upon hearing this, I realized that the record states: Jia Sidao, in his leisurely residence, engaged in cricket fighting with his concubines, much like the legendary Tang Emperor Xuanzong's indulgence in butterflies, suggesting a similar decadence, perhaps caused by excessive indulgence in pleasure. I regret that Yang Zizhi lacked literary talent and was so frightened by ghostly words that he could not inquire into the details. However, even a glimpse of this story can serve as a topic for elegant conversation and is widely praised and enjoyed.

    1043

    ○　狐判官
    　　新城杜梧，少时习刀笔，为县吏。宿于公廨，每值雨夜，即有美女来与其寝处，却之不能，久而虺羸。一日，昏然如死，梦至一衙署，绝类县治。谛观之，则邑中之城隍祠也。出入皆公役，亦多熟识，但忽忽不忆其姓字。继见一老吏，侏儒短小，须发浩然，乃其同案某，以老疾而卒，殁又未久，颇能识之。因就而咨询，老吏骇曰：“若正少壮，何由而来此？”杜以情告，老吏曰：“此狐判官之所司也，盍往谒之。”亟引杜就东庑，见一人须毛如蝟，形状极丑恶。老吏代白其由，且为之缓颊。判似有难色，老吏又曰：“人鬼虽殊，实同桑梓。况案牍是司，彼此均属吏道，君可不念乎？”判无以辞，乃引杜入室，亲检簿箱。甫一目，即慨然曰：“予以少年色狂，思污一孀妇，狐因抵隙而来。病虽可为，但狐无能力祛，可奈何？”杜自揣并无是事，遂力争之。

    Explanation

    1043

    ○　狐判官
    　　Du Wu of Xincheng, young and skilled in clerical work, served as a county clerk. He lodged in the government office, and every rainy night, a beautiful woman would come to share his bed. He could not refuse her, and over time, he fell ill and weakened. One day, he lay in a state of near-death, and in a dream, he arrived at an administrative building that resembled the county seat. Upon closer inspection, he realized it was the temple of the city god in his town. Those who entered and exited were all government officials, many of whom he recognized, but he could not recall their names. Later, he saw an elderly clerk, short and stout, with a full beard and hair. It was his former colleague, who had died of old age and illness, and had passed away not long ago. The elderly clerk could still recognize him. Du approached and asked him about the situation, and the elderly clerk was startled and said, "You are still young and strong, why are you here?" Du explained his situation, and the elderly clerk replied, "This is the domain of the fox spirit judge. Why don't you go and pay him a visit?" The elderly clerk quickly led Du to the east wing, where they saw a man with hair like a porcupine, whose appearance was extremely ugly. The elderly clerk explained the situation on Du's behalf and interceded for him. The judge seemed reluctant, but the elderly clerk said, "Though humans and ghosts are different, we are truly of the same hometown. Besides, we both handle official documents and are part of the same bureaucratic system. Shouldn't you consider this?" The judge had no excuse to refuse and led Du into his room, where he personally examined the records. Upon a quick glance, he sighed and said, "I was young and lustful, and intended to defile a widow. The fox took advantage of the opportunity and came. The illness can be treated, but the fox has no power to cure it. What can be done?" Du thought he had done nothing of the sort and vigorously defended himself.

    1044

    判取籍与观，则大书曰：“某月日，杜晤见邻妇王氏，心窃念曰：‘渠夫新卒，倘逾墙而搂之，为欢可期矣！’”杜始悚然。判因曰：“斯时予幸以差出，遂寝此念。不然，祸且不止于此。今受某兄重托，更念道之相同，当为召狐，以礼责之，或可以免。”于是以片纸书数字，顾室中一人曰：“速召东城破庙狐来！”持帖去。有顷，果见一狐，大于犬，徐徐而来。判引之与语，狐似桀骜不驯。判麾之使退，复语杜曰：“狐固无礼，宜以法惩。

    Explanation

    1044

    The judge took the register and examined it, then wrote in large characters: "On a certain day of the month, Du Wu saw his neighbor's wife, Wang, and silently thought to himself, 'Her husband has recently died; if I were to climb over the wall and embrace her, we could have a good time together!'" At this, Du began to feel alarmed. The judge then said, "At that time, I was fortunate to be on leave, so I suppressed this thought. Otherwise, the disaster would not stop there. Now, I have been entrusted by a certain brother, and because our ways are the same, I will summon the fox, reprimand it with propriety, and perhaps it can be avoided." He then wrote a few characters on a slip of paper and told one person in the room, "Quickly summon the fox from the broken temple in the east city!" The person took the note and left. After a short while, indeed a fox appeared, larger than a dog, coming slowly. The judge led it into conversation, but the fox seemed defiant and unruly. The judge waved it away to retreat and then spoke to Du again, "The fox is indeed disrespectful; it should be punished according to the law."

    1045

    然妖由人兴，幸子广有余算，今归，正心以祛之，且延医治疗，病尚可已。至于意念之恶，尤所兢兢宜慎也。”老吏亦以此相嘱，相送出署。未及半途而寝，则家人将躄踊矣。自此杜以义理自闲，又延某名医，投以妙剂，疾果愈。后值独宿，狐辄来与之调笑，备极款洽，渐且以白身昵就。杜惟诵“妖由人兴”一语，不为少动。数夕，狐亦渐厌，因自语曰：“三日不见，非复吴下阿蒙。”遂去不复来。杜又弃吏读书，以明经入泮。迄今嘉言懿行，邑中犹推宿儒云。

    　　外史氏曰：意念之恶，人所未知，以故受谴尤重，古人言之屡矣。奈人不能诚意，遂不免妄念。讵知狐之伺之，且将乘间窃发乎？旨哉，狐判之言，不必惩狐，惟期正己。非明于圣贤之道者，不克臻此。冥司慎选吏椽，所得皆正人。如老吏之侃侃，已可想见，况聪明正直如狐判者，讵可以须毛如蝟而少之哉？

    Explanation

    1045

    Yet, demons arise from human actions, fortunately, Du had ample foresight, and now he has returned, correcting his heart to dispel them, and inviting a doctor for treatment, the illness can still be cured. As for evil thoughts, one must be especially cautious and vigilant." The old official also advised him in this manner, seeing him off at the office. Before they had reached halfway, Du fell ill, and his family would have been greatly distressed. From then on, Du disciplined himself with moral principles, and again invited a renowned doctor, who administered a miraculous remedy, and indeed, the illness was cured. Later, when he was alone at night, the fox would come to tease him, and they grew increasingly intimate. Du only recited the phrase "demons arise from human actions," remaining unmoved. After several nights, the fox also gradually grew tired, and said to itself, "After three days, he is no longer the same person." Then it left and did not return. Du gave up his official duties to study, entering the school through the study of Confucian classics. To this day, he is still regarded in the county as an old scholar, known for his noble words and virtuous deeds.

    The historian says: "Evil thoughts, which people do not know, are thus punished especially heavily, as ancient people have said many times. How can people not be sincere, thus they cannot avoid false thoughts? How could they know that the fox was watching, and might take advantage of an opportunity to secretly act? How wise are the words of the fox judge, there is no need to punish the fox, but only to correct oneself. Not those who understand the way of the sages, cannot reach this level. The underworld carefully selects officials, all of whom are upright people. Like the old official who spoke confidently, one can already imagine, how much more so, then, for the wise and upright fox judge, how can they be dismissed because they have coarse hair like a hedgehog?"

    1046

    ○　钟鼐
    　　宁波袁太守，故明之循吏也。有二客昆季，长曰钟鼒，次曰钟鼐，举司衙务，人皆以大小钟别之。方公为某县令，大钟即从公。及公历任丞牧，小钟适来省兄，见其醇谨，亦留之。公任之如左右手，两人事公以忠，待人以礼，苞苴不行于冷面，关说莫移其刚肠。公虽未以宾礼待之，而置腹推心，不啻骨肉，僚友咸庆公得人。时郡中有疑狱，久而不决，公将揭宰而不忍，因谋之于大钟。对曰：“土人攻苦十年，始克出宰百里。某令非无才者，但事属暖昧，仓卒难明。请公予鼒旬日限，或可以白。”公知其侠且才，笑而许之。大钟故多髭，虑人识之，乃截须拔眉，衣敝衣，乘昏出署，变姓名为人佣。未及期，果得其奸状。盖邑有豪家，素习强横。

    Explanation

    1046

    ○　钟鼐
    　　宁波袁太守，故明之循吏也。有二客昆季，长曰钟鼒，次曰钟鼐，举司衙务，人皆以大小钟别之。方公为某县令，大钟即从公。及公历任丞牧，小钟适来省兄，见其醇谨，亦留之。公任之如左右手，两人事公以忠，待人以礼，苞苴不行于冷面，关说莫移其刚肠。公虽未以宾礼待之，而置腹推心，不啻骨肉，僚友咸庆公得人。时郡中有疑狱，久而不决，公将揭宰而不忍，因谋之于大钟。对曰：“土人攻苦十年，始克出宰百里。某令非无才者，但事属暖昧，仓卒难明。请公予鼒旬日限，或可以白。”公知其侠且才，笑而许之。大钟故多髭，虑人识之，乃截须拔眉，衣敝衣，乘昏出署，变姓名为人佣。未及期，果得其奸状。盖邑有豪家，素习强横。

    Translation:
    ○　Zhong Nai
    　　Mr. Yuan, the governor of Ningbo, was a virtuous official of the Ming dynasty. He had two guest brothers, the elder named Zhong Zui and the younger named Zhong Nai, who handled the affairs of the office, and people distinguished them by calling them "Big Clock" and "Small Clock." When Mr. Fang was a county magistrate, Big Clock followed him. When Mr. Fang served as a prefect or governor, Small Clock came to visit his brother and saw that he was sincere and cautious, so he also stayed. Mr. Fang trusted them as if they were his own hands, and the two brothers served him with loyalty, treating others with courtesy. Gifts and bribes did not work on his stern face, and no one could sway his strong will through intermediaries. Although Mr. Fang did not treat them with the courtesy due to guests, he trusted them deeply, as if they were family. His colleagues and friends all rejoiced that Mr. Fang had found good men. At that time, there was a doubtful case in the prefecture that had been unresolved for a long time. Mr. Fang was about to report it to the governor but hesitated, so he consulted Big Clock. Big Clock replied, "The locals have endured hardship for ten years to finally become magistrates of a hundred li. The magistrate is not without talent, but the matter is obscure, and it is difficult to clarify in a hurry. Please give me ten days to investigate, and perhaps I can clear it up." Mr. Fang knew that Big Clock was both brave and talented, so he smiled and agreed. Big Clock had many mustaches, so he feared being recognized. He cut off his mustache and eyebrows, wore tattered clothes, and left the office under cover of darkness, changing his name and working as a hired laborer. Before the deadline, he indeed discovered the criminal behavior. It turned out that there was a powerful family in the county who had always been arrogant and domineering.

    1047

    以其居近清溪，托言凿池，引水入囿，凡奴仆及佃人有不适意者，辄生而纳之沟中。既毙而后弃之溪内，急流迅下，瞬息数十里，人因莫测其致死之由，毙者且不一人矣。一日，主私美婢，主妻见而怒。乘主外出，挞之无算，亦如其法淹毙之，遽投诸清流。邑人见尸，投首于宰。宰验之有伤，无敢判为自溺。且系外乡人，绝无尸亲，因榜于通衢，而行路皆知。于是前所受害诸家，咸疑死者之不明，群起赴诉。又不知婢出豪家，莫敢指为怨府，豪益自得，所为愈暴，而狱更经年不决。幸大钟见及于此，出署即投豪家，以金赂其左右，为之先容，使为粪除之隶。暇与豪家群儿戏，诱之使言，遂如犀烛，并悉婢之姓氏由来。遁归告公，公以他事命役驰召婢家。既至，躬亲听讼。婢之冤雪，众之死遂可类推，豪因尽伏其罪。

    Explanation

    1047

    Because it was near the Clear Stream, they pretended to dig a pond and divert water into the garden. Any servant or tenant who was dissatisfied was immediately drowned in the ditch. After killing them, they were thrown into the stream, where the swift current carried them away, covering dozens of miles in an instant. People could not determine the cause of death, and the number of dead was not just one. One day, the master privately favored a beautiful maid. The master's wife saw this and became angry. While the master was out, she beat her mercilessly and drowned her in the same way, then immediately threw her into the clear stream. The townspeople found the body and reported it to the magistrate. The magistrate examined the body and found injuries, but dared not rule it as suicide. Since she was from another town, there were no relatives to identify the body, so the case was posted in public, and everyone knew about it. Thus, the families of previous victims all suspected that the cause of death was unclear and came forward to file complaints. But they did not know that the maid came from a wealthy family, so no one dared to accuse them. The wealthy man became even more arrogant, and his actions became even more brutal, and the case remained unresolved for years. Fortunately, someone named Da Zhong noticed this. He went straight to the wealthy man's home, bribed his attendants with money, and introduced himself, making him a laborer in the garden. In his spare time, he played with the wealthy man's children and persuaded them to speak, and they spoke as clearly as a torch. He learned the maid's surname and origins and secretly returned to report to the official. The official summoned the maid's family under the pretext of another matter. When they arrived, the official personally heard the case. The maid's innocence was proven, and the deaths of others could be inferred. The wealthy man confessed to all his crimes.

    1048

    案定，公以功归宰。宰得无患，深感大钟之德，馈以百金，义不受。乃自大钟以此案外出，小钟代纪其事。署中人深嫉其宠，思以中伤，遂伪为私书渎陈公，许有重赇。乘公将出，遽然投之于小钟，盖冀公见而讯之，使之无从置喙也。讵小钟少年持重，见公出，辄以献之。公果一览而笑曰：“此跖之妄思污惠也。”既而熟睹其名，若不释然，则其牍尾所署者，为某县大尹。公素薄其人，因疑其有所干求，强以赂请，一时微有怒容。小钟见公色动，谓公疑己，遂亦不敢有言。及公退休，即长跪力辞，请以他人更易。公乃笑而曳之曰：“若勿尔尔，予之所惑者非在若。

    Explanation

    1048

    The case was settled, and the public official attributed the success to the Chief Magistrate. The Chief Magistrate, relieved of any trouble, deeply appreciated the virtue of the great bell and offered a hundred gold pieces as a gift, but the public official refused out of righteousness. The great bell then took the case outside, and the lesser bell recorded the matter. The officials in the office deeply resented the public official's favor and thought to slander him, so they forged a private letter to accuse the public official, promising a heavy bribe. Seizing the opportunity when the public official was about to leave, they suddenly threw the letter into the lesser bell, hoping that the public official would see and interrogate it, thereby preventing him from having a say. But the lesser bell, a young person who was responsible, saw the public official leaving and promptly presented the letter to him. The public official, upon seeing it, smiled and said, "This is the bandit's foolish attempt to defile the benefaction." After carefully examining the name, he seemed unconvinced, noting that the signature at the bottom was that of the Chief Magistrate of a certain county. The public official had always had a low opinion of this person and suspected that he might be petitioning for something, forcing a request for a bribe, and he had a slight look of anger. The lesser bell, seeing the public official's expression change, thought he was being suspected and dared not speak up. When the public official retired, the lesser bell knelt for a long time and strongly declined, asking for someone else to take his place. The public official then laughed and pulled him aside, saying, "You need not worry, my confusion is not with you."

    1049

    某贪滥素著，今竟以不义之物妄思尝试耶？”小钟始悟公意，复跪白曰：“鬼蜮之奸既已难逃洞鉴，岂能污鼐者，独不能污宰乎？盍以宰之禀牍比而观之，当悉其非出一手矣！”公如其言，验之信然，更思穷治其事。小钟又力求寝息，惟曰：“隽不疑且不自辩，公乃代为辩之，是益树鼐之敌也。”公解其意，遂不问及。大钟返署，公乃专任两人，而郡中大治。如是者数年，而祸作。初公以久任不迁，颇怀观望。时值政在严氏，父子擅权，黜陟在其掌握。适浙西某令以卓荐入都，公之故吏，实严氏之私人也，过郡谒公，公见之。语次，颇以瓜期为慊，令即以夤缘说公，谓得万金，可以谋迁一秩。公时已热中，将欲曲从，而大钟已侧闻其论，不胜勃然。及令出，即面叱之曰：“诱吾主与不义者，汝也。东楼夫子，走肉行尸，若辈恃此冰山，赤日一出，势将压覆。何更思煽惑正人耶？”令闻言大惭，抑亦无如之何，抱恨而去。

    Explanation

    1049

    A certain person, notorious for greed and corruption, now dares to recklessly attempt to use unjust gains? Xiao Zhong finally understood the official’s intention and knelt to explain: "The deceitful and treacherous acts of evil cannot escape thorough scrutiny. If they cannot defile the official’s reputation, can they not defile the main official? Why not compare the main official’s report to see that it is not from a single hand?" The official followed his suggestion, verified it, and indeed found it to be true, then considered further investigation. Xiao Zhong again insisted on resting, saying only: "Even if Jun Buqi does not defend himself, the official is defending him, which will only strengthen the enemies of the official." The official understood his intention and did not pursue the matter further. When Da Zhong returned to the office, the official then relied on the two of them, and the administration of the prefecture was well-ordered. Thus, for several years, there was no trouble. Initially, the official was hesitant about promotion due to his long tenure. At the time, the powerful family of the strict faction controlled promotions and demotions. A certain magistrate from Zhe West, recommended for promotion based on merit, visited the official, who was actually a close associate of the strict faction. During the conversation, the magistrate expressed dissatisfaction with the term of office and suggested currying favor, offering a bribe for promotion. The official, eager for promotion, was considering complying, but Da Zhong had overheard and reacted angrily. When the magistrate left, Da Zhong rebuked him: "It is you who are tempting our master with injustice. The esteemed official from Donglou, a lifeless and spiritless person, you rely on this unstable support, but when the truth is revealed, it will be crushed. How dare you think of inciting the virtuous?" The magistrate was deeply ashamed, but could do nothing, and left with regret. Thus, for several years, there was no trouble, but eventually, disaster struck.

    1050

    大钟既斥某令，又与弟力诤于公，公始悔，遂不致饩于令。令亦无颜再谒，扬帆北上，而心并衔公。至京，藉严力竟登乌台。闲与世蕃述此言，世蕃大恚，致书浙抚，以阴事劾公，奉诏解京，并逮大钟兄弟。阖署仓皇，罔知所措，惟大钟笑曰：“予已预知有此祸。然使吾公钟椒山之后，不致人目为严党，不亦荣乎？”因与小钟谋曰：“今使吾两人与公同入虎阱，固足以报公。但吾公老矣，而夫人年少，两地皆需人，不可以徒蹈汤火，竟置大事于不问也。”遂乘缇骑未来，悄然夜出，不知所往。公觅之不得，既已切齿痛心，而上而僚佐，下而吏卒，亦莫不发指。至于署以内之唾其负恩，郡以外之詈其卖主，又复难更仆数矣。公因俯首就逮，囚服赴京。

    Explanation

    1050

    大钟已经罢免了某位县令，又与弟弟在公面前激烈争辩，公这才感到后悔，于是不再向县令送礼。县令也因无颜再见公，扬帆北上，心中却对公怀恨在心。到了京城，县令依靠严氏的力量最终进入了乌台。闲暇时，县令向世蕃讲述了这件事，世蕃大怒，写信给浙江巡抚，以秘密罪名弹劾公，奉诏将公解送京城，并逮捕了大钟兄弟。整个衙署陷入恐慌，不知所措，只有大钟笑着说：“我已经预见到会有这样的灾祸。然而，如果我的主人钟椒山的后人，不至于被人视为严党的成员，那不是也是一件荣耀的事吗？”于是与小钟商量说：“现在让我们两人与公一同进入虎穴，这足以报答公的恩情。但是，我们的主人已经老了，而他的夫人还年轻，两地都需要人，我们不能只顾冒险，竟然置大事于不顾。”于是趁着缇骑未到，悄然夜出，不知去了何处。公找不到他们，既已切齿痛心，而上级官员和下级吏卒也都怒不可遏。至于衙署内部的人唾骂他忘恩负义，郡外的人则咒骂他出卖主子，这样的事又难以数清。公于是低头就擒，穿着囚服前往京城。

    1051

    夫人亦幽于任所，奇惨深痛，莫可形容。公至，锦衣毒加拷掠，亦不俟其诬服，辄以狱上。公论弃市，妻子流徙，敕下胥如吏议。幸值大醮，公得缓死须臾，而夫人早已签名远配矣！惟时大钟兄弟虑人侦获，匿迹山谷间。数日，大钟谓弟曰：“公事灭裂，予职其咎。向特留此身，以为外援，非可埋首不出也。今日之事与弟分任之，其可乎？”小钟曰：“诺。”因请其良策。大钟曰：“公之靳重者在身命，而其次即在嗣续。吾将北行，以图全吾公。而夫人势将南去，实所隐忧。倘公幸脱天牢，而不能复圆鸾镜，其如之何？”小钟即慨然曰：“此实重务，弟不敏，敢请当之。’大钟不语，惟熟视小钟，色似愀然。小钟疑而询之，则曰：“非兄疑弟，夫人少艾，而吾弟又在青年。

    Explanation

    1051

    The wife was also imprisoned in her official residence, suffering extraordinary torment and deep pain beyond description. When the official arrived, the Jinyiwei subjected him to severe torture, and without waiting for him to falsely confess, they immediately sent the case to court. The official was sentenced to public execution, his wife and children were exiled, and the imperial decree followed the advice of the officials. Fortunately, there was a grand ritual taking place, which granted the official a brief reprieve from death, while the wife had already been formally sentenced to distant exile! At that time, the brothers Dazhong and Xiaozhong feared being discovered and hid in the mountains and valleys. After several days, Dazhong told his younger brother, "The official business has been mishandled, and it is my responsibility. I left myself here as an external support, not to remain passive and inactive. Today, we must share the responsibility, can we do it?" Xiaozhong replied, "Yes." He then asked for a good plan. Dazhong said, "The most precious thing for the official is his life, and secondly, his descendants. I will go north to try to save my lord. The wife is likely to go south, which is a hidden worry. If the official is fortunate enough to escape from the heavenly prison but cannot restore the broken mirror, what then?" Xiaozhong immediately said, "This is indeed a serious matter. I am not clever, but I dare to take on this responsibility." Dazhong remained silent, only looking intently at Xiaozhong, his expression appearing troubled. Xiaozhong, suspecting something, asked him, "It is not that you doubt me, but the lady is young and beautiful, and I am young."

    1052

    倘成不世之勋，反抱不白之怨，弟亦何辜！吾将往从夫人，而公之事非吾莫办，是以不禁僝僽。”盖袁公久亡原配，夫人以闺嫒新续鸾胶，是时仅廿二三耳。小钟闻兄言及此，意亦踌躇。良久，忽忿然作色，诘兄曰：“父母与公孰重？”答曰：“父母生我，公固不啻生我也。”又诘曰：“公与身孰重？”答曰：“身有重轻，于公则身亦可轻也。”小钟乃崛然起，自拔佩刀，且以手捋其衣曰：“弟常涉猎传记，窃见豫让聂政，皆能以身报主。彼非无父母也，况弟幸有兄在，父母血食可以无斩。今请以此报公矣！”于是自捋其裩，以左手执势，以右手举刀，尽力割下。义激而力猛，血出如泉，小钟早昏绝于地。大钟悲且喜，急掬土为之按捺，且祝曰：“天不殄吾公祀，则吾弟可生。否则从此逝矣！”言未绝，小钟已呻吟复苏。

    Explanation

    1052

    If one achieves an unparalleled merit, instead bears an unjust grudge, what wrong has the younger brother done? I will go with the lady, and the affairs of the lord cannot be managed without me, therefore, I cannot help but complain." It was said that Lord Yuan had long lost his original wife, and the lady, as a young maiden, newly joined in a marital bond, and at that time, she was only around twenty-two or twenty-three. Younger brother Zhong, upon hearing his elder brother speak of this, hesitated. After a long time, he suddenly became angry and changed color, questioning his elder brother, "Which is more important, parents or the lord?" The elder brother replied, "Parents gave birth to me, and the lord is no less than a parent to me." He then asked, "Which is more important, the lord or one's own life?" The elder brother answered, "Life has its value, but for the lord, life can also be light." Younger brother Zhong then stood up abruptly, drew his sword, and with his hand, pulled down his pants, holding his genitals with his left hand and with his right hand raised the knife, cutting with all his might. Driven by righteousness and with great force, blood flowed like a spring, and younger brother Zhong fainted and fell to the ground. Elder brother Zhong was both sad and happy, and quickly scooped up earth to press down on him, and prayed, "If heaven does not destroy our lord's lineage, then my younger brother can live. Otherwise, he will die here!" Before he finished speaking, younger brother Zhong had already moaned and regained consciousness.

    1053

    语大钟曰：“适见一白衣人，以柳枝醮水，遍洒吾身。意者其观音大士乎？”因视其私处，既已结痂，亦绝无痛楚。遂自起同兄顶礼，大钟始授以策。且曰：“公之狱若成，势不能庇妻子，夫人将遣戍，计时亦启行。弟自北而南，即得遭之于途。后事好为之，兄固不烦多嘱也。”遂分其囊橐，分袂遄征，绝无恋恋之容，其义烈概可想见。小钟已留髭，至此皆自落，缉者既未易辨识，况某令之意在钟，而严氏之怒独在袁，公既身陷大辟，法网遂亦少疏，小钟因得径行无惧。直抵黔楚之交，始闻袁公之产已籍没，眷属解配，不日可止荆南，遂佣于传舍以俟之。夫人惟随一妪，果于旬日颠沛而来。暮宿逆旅，适与小钟遇，幸解者数人饮于外肆，乘间进谒，哭拜于地。夫人故不识，惟妪尚识之，而讶其无髭。小钟一一缅诉，并请从行。

    Explanation

    1053

    The White-Clothed Figure

    Da Zhong said, "I just now saw a white-clad figure, who used a willow branch dipped in water to sprinkle all over my body. Could it be the Bodhisattva Guanyin?" He then looked at his private parts, which had already formed a scab and were completely free of pain. He got up and knelt with his brother in reverence, and Da Zhong then gave him a staff. He said, "If your case is finalized, it will be impossible to protect your wife and children; your wife will be exiled, and it is also time to set out. I am traveling from north to south and will meet you on the road. Take good care of the future; you don't need to worry too much, brother." They then divided their belongings, parted ways, and hurriedly set out, showing no signs of attachment or reluctance. One can imagine the righteousness and bravery of their actions.

    Xiao Zhong had grown a beard, but it had all fallen off by itself. Those who were searching found it difficult to identify him, especially since the magistrate's intention was on Zhong, while the anger of the Yan family was solely directed at Yuan. Since Zhong had already gotten caught in a serious legal case, the legal net became somewhat relaxed, allowing Xiao Zhong to walk straight ahead without fear. He reached the border of Qian and Chu, where he first heard that Yuan's property had been confiscated, his family members had been separated, and they would soon reach Jingnan. He then worked at an inn waiting for them. The woman was accompanied only by an old woman, who arrived after ten days of hardship. They stayed at an inn at night, and Xiao Zhong happened to meet her. Fortunately, several of the guards were drinking at an outside tavern, so he took the opportunity to pay respects, crying and kneeling on the ground. The woman did not recognize him, but the old woman still recognized him and was surprised that he had no beard. Xiao Zhong explained everything in detail and asked to accompany them.

    1054

    夫人疑而固辞，妪重其义，代言于夫人，请自验之。遂与小钟如侧室，去衣一视，痂犹未脱，妪为之太息。亟白于夫人，亦为感动。始与小钟谋，以重金啖差，谓是母家，因有远行遣来服役者。差因受公僚友托，莫之敢拒，遂许以偕行，然犹仅司外事也。及抵配所，未三日，妪因暮年重染烟瘴，以疾亡。居惟斗室，四无垣墙，小钟露宿于外。夫人怜之曰：“汝犹婢也，何妨共此室处？”小钟初犹力辞，乃昼出入山斫薪，至夜复重冒风雨，渐不能堪，始肯入。然必夫人先卧，而后以束草置地，枕藉而眠。且事夫人如母，下气柔声，承颜顺志，惴惴焉惟恐少拂其意。于时夫人幸有余资，衣食粗给，无虞匮乏。

    Explanation

    1054

    The lady hesitated and firmly declined. The old woman valued her righteousness and spoke on behalf of the lady, asking her to verify it herself. They then took Xiao Zhong into the side room, where she removed her clothes for a quick look. The scab had not yet fallen off, and the old woman sighed deeply. She quickly reported this to the lady, who was also moved. The lady then began to plot with Xiao Zhong, offering a large sum of money to the official, claiming that Xiao Zhong was from her mother's family and had been sent to serve after a long journey. The official, due to a request from a public official friend, dared not refuse and agreed to accompany her, but still only handled external affairs. When they arrived at the assigned place, not even three days later, the old woman, due to her advanced age, was severely affected by the smoky environment and died of illness. They lived in a small room surrounded by no walls, and Xiao Zhong slept outdoors. The lady felt pity for her and said, "You are still a servant. Why not share this room?" Initially, Xiao Zhong still firmly declined, but then during the day, she went out to cut firewood in the mountains, and at night, again braved the wind and rain. Gradually, she could not bear it anymore and finally agreed to enter. But only after the lady had already gone to bed, she then placed a bundle of straw on the ground and lay down to sleep. She served the lady like a mother, with a gentle voice and deferential manner, obeying her wishes and following her will, anxiously afraid of slightly displeasing her. At that time, the lady fortunately had some extra funds, and food and clothing were just barely sufficient, with no worry of scarcity.

    1055

    无何而岁饥，岁饥则盗起，滇南六诏以次称兵，居民遂不能安枕。小钟白于夫人，将徙以避之。未及而寇来，人咸奔窜。夫人素娇弱，弗能举趾，小钟乃负之以行。北走崇山，日百余里，足踵尽裂。夜宿空室中，夫人安寝，小钟虑有不肖，持挺徼巡，目不交睫者浃旬。幸获乐土，小钟乃伐竹营室，草创数椽，奉夫人而居之。夫人悯其劳，呼使同榻，则辞曰：“形躯虽异，然在上下之义犹不可废。”明日之县，具言避寇僦居之故，盖恐袁公幸生，或有赦来，无从侦访故也。乃自此夫人之所携既荡，小钟又无敢远出，惟有昼织蒲，夜捆屦，以供饔眐。抑且涤釜炊食，一切自任。夫人倘助之，即跪而谢曰：“有鼐在而主母尸饔，不免罪戾。”究不使夫人劳。居近三年，只如一日。夫人得小钟侍养，虽甚念公，幸犹安佚。

    Explanation

    1055

    Soon after, there was a famine, and when there was famine, bandits rose. The six chieftaincies of southern Yunnan successively raised arms, and the residents could no longer sleep soundly. Xiao Zhong informed the lady and planned to move away to avoid it. Before he could move, the bandits arrived, and people all fled. The lady, being usually delicate and weak, could not walk, so Xiao Zhong then carried her. They went north to a high mountain, traveling over a hundred miles a day, and their feet were all cracked. At night, they stayed in an empty room, and the lady slept soundly. Xiao Zhong was concerned about the presence of the wicked, so he held a stick and patrolled, not closing his eyes for ten days. Fortunately, they found a peaceful place, and Xiao Zhong then cut bamboo to build a house, constructing a small house with several rooms and living there with the lady. The lady pitied his labor and called him to share the bed, but he declined, saying, "Although our bodies are different, the hierarchical relationship should not be abandoned." The next day, he went to the county office and explained the reason for moving to avoid the bandits, out of fear that Yuan Gong might survive, or there might be a pardon, and there would be no way to find out. From then on, the lady's belongings were gone, and Xiao Zhong dared not go far out. He only had the daytime weaving of rush and nighttime bundling of straw shoes to provide food. Moreover, he washed the pot and cooked food, taking on everything himself. If the lady helped him, he would kneel and thank her, saying, "With me here, the lady would be taking over the cooking, which would incur sin." Ultimately, he did not allow the lady to work. They lived there for nearly three years, as if it were just one day. The lady, being cared for by Xiao Zhong, though she greatly missed her husband, was fortunate to be safe and at ease.

    1056

    惟公自抵京师，棘圜深锢，桎梏缠身，而且百忧煎其中，四肢伤于外，汹汹传播，不日即明正典刑，亦既心灰气绝矣。忽一日，狱吏来视公，引与密语曰：“某公主使人传语，谓君与粉侯为中表之戚，嘱余善视君，幸自宽慰。”公茫然无以应。徐诘粉侯姓氏，狱吏乃自耳而白之，则亦实无瓜葛。然处急难之时，遂伪应曰：“是诚葭莩，初不意犹念及予也。”狱吏大悦，重与公为礼。因曰：“此地秽污，不足以处。”遂命隶粪除一室，盛其铺陈，状若上宾之馆，移公而居之。至夕狱吏又来，具酒馔饷公，因与对饮。微酣，复屏人语公曰：“君之事似有可为。公主欲为君乞恩，而碍于严老。昨已浼朝天宫法师，为上言星象不吉，宜缓刑狱。其人实上所亲信，已敕法司，各加复勘矣。”公心窃喜，亦姑颔之。

    Explanation

    1056

    The gentleman had arrived in the capital, where he was deeply confined within the prison, shackles binding his body, and his heart boiled with a hundred worries, while his limbs were injured externally. Rumors spread noisily, and within days, he would face official punishment, leaving him feeling utterly defeated. Suddenly one day, a prison guard came to visit him, leading him aside to speak in secret: "A certain princess has sent a message, saying that you and the Marquis of Powder are related by marriage, and she has entrusted me to treat you well, so please comfort yourself." The gentleman was bewildered and did not know how to respond. He slowly inquired about the Marquis of Powder's surname, and the prison guard then told him what he had heard: there was indeed no relation. However, in this urgent and difficult situation, he pretended to reply, "Indeed, we are distant relatives, and I never expected her to think of me still." The prison guard was very pleased and performed another courtesy to the gentleman. He then said, "This place is filthy and unsuitable for you." He ordered the servants to clean a room, fill it with furnishings, and make it look like a guesthouse for a distinguished guest, moving the gentleman to live there. That evening, the prison guard came again, prepared wine and food for the gentleman, and they drank together. After a slight intoxication, the prison guard secluded himself from others and told the gentleman, "Your case seems to have a way out. The princess wants to beg for mercy for you, but there is an obstacle with Elder Yan. Yesterday, I asked the Taoist priest from the Chao Tian Palace to inform the emperor that the celestial omens are unfavorable, and it is advisable to delay the punishment. That person is indeed trusted by the emperor, and he has already ordered the judicial officials to re-examine the case." The gentleman secretly rejoiced and nodded in agreement.

    1057

    阅数日，狱吏复来报公，则公主已嘱法司，令将公罪末减。且某侍御有忤严府，严府顿疑其言，故稍稍忘情于公。惟原参甚重，宽典难于遽邀，缓留诏狱，再当徐图。今已定议白严相，严相无词，法司且往覆公主。吏因此为公贺，公益弛然于心。然自徙居以来，日恒有人以酒食馈，亦不言其所自。公问之，则以狱吏对，公乃深德狱吏，遂成莫逆交。迨事局既定，又有人以成衣进公，长短直如身度，公颇讶之。由是鲜衣美食，逍遥狱邸，虽不克拨云睹日，亦已身逸心安。囹圄数年，而严氏忽栋折榱崩。籍其家，得所与浙抚各书，始悟公冤，以原秩出诸狱中，既已五阅寒暑矣。乃公甫出狱，即有人乘坚策肥，舁一舆迎公，且直前俯伏，持公足而号泣。公视之，其人虽眇一目，跛一足，而形状宛然，实大钟也。

    Explanation

    1057

    After several days, the prison official returned to report to the gentleman, informing him that the princess had instructed the legal officials to reduce his crime to a lesser offense. Additionally, a certain censor had offended the strict household, which suddenly began to doubt his words, and thus they gradually became less hostile toward the gentleman. However, the original charge was very serious, and it was difficult to immediately obtain leniency. They decided to delay his release from the imperial prison and plan carefully again. The plan had been decided and reported to the strict chancellor, who had no objections. The legal officials would then go to confirm with the princess. The officials therefore congratulated the gentleman, and he thus relaxed in his mind. Since moving to the prison, every day there were people who sent him wine and food, but they did not say who sent them. When the gentleman asked, they said it was the prison official. The gentleman then deeply appreciated the prison official and they became close friends. When the situation had settled, there were people who brought him ready-made clothes that fit him perfectly. The gentleman was somewhat surprised. Wearing fine clothes and eating delicious food, he was leisurely in the prison, although he could not see the sun through the clouds, he was at ease both in body and mind. After several years in prison, the strict household suddenly collapsed like a house with broken beams and rafters. When their family was searched, letters between them and the governor of Zhejiang were found. They then realized the gentleman was wronged and released him from prison with his original rank. Five years had passed. When the gentleman just came out of prison, there were people who came riding in a sturdy carriage with fat horses, carrying a sedan chair to welcome him, bowing deeply, and holding his feet while weeping. The gentleman looked at them, and although the person was blind in one eye and lame in one foot, his appearance was recognizable. It was actually Da Zhong.

    1058

    公在昔从未斥其名，今值盛怒，因叱之曰：“鼒，尔犹来见予乎？”泣而对曰：“此地不敢禀白，请公至鼒家再当上告。”公时无所归，勉从之，而犹怅恨不已。初不知回天之力，举皆出自大钟也。盖大钟有内兄，服役于公主府中，历有年所，其妻又代主乳哺，积有勤劳，公主故厚遇之。夫妇每盛称二钟才智，公主以府事废弛，每恨不得二钟，而为之代纪。其内兄曾以书召之，二钟因从公久，不忍舍之遽去。此其本末，大钟已历历在胸，故当日毅然北行，实有所恃也。比至途中，深虑人识，乃以灰瞽一目，石损一足，亦已备尝奇苦。及至府中，见其内兄，又仿秦庭之痛，涕泣通宵，勺水不入于口。其内兄乃命妻白主，悉公冤状，并述二钟自残之苦。公主素重其能，且怜其义，遂许为经画。

    Explanation

    1058

    The lord had never before mentioned his name, but now, in a fit of anger, he scolded Zi and said, "Zi, do you still dare to see me?" Zi wept and replied, "I dare not report here; please let me take you to my home, where I can then formally report." The lord, having nowhere else to go, reluctantly followed, but still felt deeply resentful. Initially unaware of the power to change the situation, all of it came from Da Zhong. It was said that Da Zhong had a brother-in-law who served in the princess's household for many years, and his wife also served as a wet nurse, accumulating much hard work, and thus was treated well by the princess. The couple often highly praised the two bells' intelligence, and the princess felt that the household affairs were neglected, often regretting not having the two bells to manage them. His brother-in-law had once written to summon them, and the two bells had been with the lord for a long time and were unwilling to leave suddenly. This is the beginning and end of it, and Da Zhong had it all clearly in mind, thus he resolutely set off northward that day, indeed having something to rely on. By the time they reached the road, they deeply feared being recognized, so they blinded one eye with ash and damaged one foot with stone, and had also endured great hardships. When they reached the household, they saw their brother-in-law, and again imitated the pain of the Qin court, weeping all night, not even drinking a single drop of water. His brother-in-law then ordered his wife to report to the princess, fully reporting the lord's grievances, and also describing the two bells' self-inflicted suffering. The princess always valued their abilities and also pitied their loyalty, and thus agreed to help them plan.

    1059

    大钟始肯为主用，所有诸务一切井然，益蒙公主宠任。公主本世宗胞姊，少而寡，晚年以郡主晋封。世宗素敦手足，公主遂乘间用事，朝绅多出其门。大钟假主威力，营谋斡旋半由己出，公主亦知之不问，故得出公于陷阱，公故无由得知。迨至其家，大钟正位再拜，始泣诉其原委。公由悟生感，亦持之大恸。继视其屋宇，都类素封。大钟久无室，公主以侍儿赉之，孪生二子，是年已周岁矣。大钟张筵贺公，公感主德，欲往谢。大钟曰：“祁奚不见叔向，叔向独可见祁奚乎？”公乃止。钟又言弟事，久已奉夫人南行，迄今鱼雁隔绝，尚犹未遑存问。公闻小钟自阉，倍甚感激，而意中犹未遽信。大钟又以致仕劝公，公从其说，遂以病状赴部，竟得冠带锦旋。公之事既已年湮，虽奉还产之诏，所偿者百不及一。

    Explanation

    1059

    Da Zhong finally agreed to serve as a minister, and all his affairs were in order, gaining him more favor and trust from the princess. The princess was originally Emperor Shizong's elder sister, who became a widow at a young age and was later enfeoffed as a princess. Emperor Shizong always valued his siblings, and the princess then took advantage of the situation to wield power, with many officials coming from her door. Da Zhong leveraged the princess's power, and much of his planning and maneuvering was done by him. The princess also knew about it but did not inquire, thus putting the official into a trap, which the official had no way of knowing. Upon arriving at his home, Da Zhong bowed again and began to cry, recounting the details. The official felt moved upon understanding and held him in great sorrow. He then observed the house and buildings, which were like those of a wealthy family. Da Zhong had long been without a wife, and the princess gave her maidservants to him. They had twin sons who were already one year old. Da Zhong held a banquet to congratulate the official, who was grateful for the princess's kindness and wanted to go thank her. Da Zhong said, "If Qi Xi doesn't see Shu Xiang, can Shu Xiang see Qi Xi alone?" The official then stopped. Da Zhong then mentioned his brother's affairs, who had already accompanied his wife southward, and they had been separated by distance and time, still unable to inquire about each other. The official heard that Xiao Zhong had castrated himself, and was even more grateful, though he still did not fully believe it. Da Zhong then advised the official to retire, and the official followed his advice, using illness as an excuse to resign, and finally returned home in honor. The official's story has been long forgotten, and even though he received an imperial edict to return property, only a fraction was returned.

    1060

    大钟以千金命人为公治第，更以数百镒润公行色。临行送公河干，跪而言曰：“分宜从公，再效犬马。但为公事，主之恩，尚有未酬，今请暂留为借臣。抑更有请者，鼒之弟形体既残，筋力亦瘁，愿公放之北还。渠已不克诞育，鼒以一子嗣之，绕膝牵裙，庶可以少慰其意。”言已，哭不能仰。公诺之，感触心事，益更流涕不止。盖公年六旬，因值多难，尚未诞有令嗣也。挥泪扬帆，至家，夫人虽经遇赦，以路远未至，又数月，始达本籍。小钟先驱入见公，公一睹其形状，面媪声雌，俨然宦监，始信大钟非谬言。心乃大动，返迎而拜之曰：“袁氏倘有嗣，实若钟氏之赐也。”小钟亦泥首辞逊。及夫人至，少叙离悰，即极表小钟之忠荩。公更服膺，呼之以弟。小钟究不敢承，尽礼事公。

    Explanation

    1060

    Da Zhong gave someone a thousand gold coins to build a mansion for Gong, and then gave hundreds of ingots to smooth Gong's departure. He saw Gong off at the riverbank, knelt, and said, "Fenyi will follow Gong again and serve him like a dog or horse. But for Gong's sake, the master's grace still has not been fully repaid. I ask to stay temporarily as a borrowed servant. There is also another request: my brother's body is already damaged, and his strength is also exhausted. I wish Gong would allow him to return north. He cannot have children anymore. I will have one son as his successor, to be around his knees and pull at his skirt, perhaps it can slightly comfort his heart." After he finished speaking, he cried so much that he could not look up. Gong agreed, moved by these feelings, and tears flowed even more. Gong was sixty years old, having encountered many difficulties, and still had not yet had a worthy heir. With tears, he raised the sail and arrived home. Although his wife had been pardoned, because the journey was long and she had not arrived yet, after several months, she finally reached her hometown. Xiao Zhong went ahead and met Gong. Gong saw his appearance for the first time, and his face was old, and his voice was weak, looking like a eunuch. Only then did Gong believe that Da Zhong was not lying.
