His heart was greatly moved, and he returned and bowed to him, saying, "If the Yuan family has an heir, it is truly a gift from the Zhong family." Xiao Zhong also prostrated himself and humbly declined. When his wife arrived, they briefly talked about their separation, and Gong highly praised Xiao Zhong's loyalty. Gong was even more convinced and called him brother. Xiao Zhong finally dared not accept and served Gong with utmost respect.

    1061

    公忆大钟言，为之整装，使其北上。辞曰：“所以历艰险负羁絏以从夫人者，实为公也。以公盛德，必将有后。今夫人已归，鼐请俟公子诞生，少啖汤饼，即自行。”公因听之。期年，夫人举一子。公大悦，名之曰鼐锡，以志小钟之德。欢宴经旬，小钟始束装就道。及至京，见兄，引谒公主。公主知其阉，命为都监，总握邸中事。大钟乃以家授弟，忾然曰：“公之厉阶实启于予，而累弟至于亏体，予又安享妻子之奉，于今数年，弟所未有也。今汝旋返，吾子即若子，叔嫂其共处，教以义方。吾将往从吾公，图报其未尽之德，幸勿我念。”言讫欲行。小钟挽留之，究不肯听，遂往袁公处。公以老友待之，杖履追随，益征相德。惟小钟抚育两男，及长，教以躬耕。诫曰：“慎勿干预人事，汝父与若叔，良可鉴也。”迄今数传子孙，皆以农夫没世焉。

    Explanation

    1061

    The official recalled the words of Da Zhong and prepared himself, sending him northward. Da Zhong declined, saying, "I endured hardships and servitude to follow the lady, truly for your sake. With your great virtue, you will surely have descendants. Now that the lady has returned, I request to wait until the prince is born, to eat a little porridge in celebration, and then depart on my own." The official agreed. After a year, the lady gave birth to a son. The official was greatly pleased and named him Nai Xi, in memory of the virtue of the small bell. For ten days, they celebrated with feasting and music, and then Xiao Zhong began to pack his belongings and set off. Upon arriving in the capital, he met his brother, who introduced him to the princess. The princess, knowing he was eunuch, appointed him as the chief supervisor of the mansion, entrusting him with all matters within the household. Da Zhong then handed over the family affairs to his younger brother, sighing, "Your difficulties were indeed initiated by me, and I have burdened you to the point of disfigurement. Yet I have enjoyed the support of my wife and children for several years, which you have not yet experienced. Now that you return, my son will be as if he were yours, and you and I will live together as brother-in-law and sister-in-law, teaching him the proper path. I will go to join my lord and repay the unfulfilled virtue, so please do not miss me." Having said this, he was about to leave. Xiao Zhong tried to persuade him to stay, but he would not listen, so he went to Yuan Gong. Yuan Gong treated him as an old friend, walking with him and showing respect for his virtue. Xiao Zhong raised two sons, and when they grew up, he taught them to till the land. He warned them, "Be cautious not to interfere in human affairs; your father and uncle are good examples to follow." To this day, their descendants have continued to live as farmers until the end of their lives.

    1062

    外史氏曰：观大钟行事光明，自是伟丈夫。且能营脱袁萤窗公，岂其辜恩不报者比。但叱座宾，顿忘鼠器，实为公之祸异基，似不可训。独小钟温柔敦厚，绝少圭角。即伪书一节，草三已足征其气度。而自宫从戍，奋不顾身；茹苦事人，毫无倦编卷色；凡其所处，又较乃兄为尤难。予故为小钟立传，并附大四钟于其间。

    Explanation

    1062

    The historian remarks: Observing the elder brother's actions, they are bright and upright, truly a great man. Moreover, he was able to rescue the scholar named Yuan from a difficult situation, unlike those who betray and do not repay kindness. However, scolding the guest and immediately forgetting the mouse trap were the root of his misfortune, seeming not to be a good lesson. In contrast, the younger brother is gentle, kind, and has few sharp edges. Even the incident of the false book, three attempts were enough to show his character. He volunteered for military service, risking his life; endured hardships while serving others, showing no signs of fatigue or weariness. All his circumstances were even more challenging than those of his elder brother. Therefore, I wrote a biography of the younger brother, including the elder brother in it.

    1063

    随园老人曰：熟读此传，余颇疑之。盖目中固未见鼐，亦并未见鼒。浩歌子犹不满于大钟，谈何容易也。

    ○　鬼无颏
    　　宛平谢紫庵有坟庄，距都门十里有奇，甚迩也。其犹子数人，率多游荡，以其地产鹑，冬月辄往觅之，恒数夕不归。比闾又多其类，亦以墓田在彼，兼有居停，皆游而忘返，故萃处每十391数人。晨则踏雪罗禽，夜则围炉饮酒。即漠不相识者，其情同，其事同，遂言笑同，亦纨裤之积习使然也。一夕，又群居，藏醅既罄，鸷材未得，相对无聊，乃剧谈以消长夜。言涉怪异，乐且不疲。时夜色已深，众中有胆怯者已毛发微竖。

    Explanation

    1063

    The Old Man of Sui Garden said: "Having thoroughly read this biography, I greatly doubt it. I have never seen Nai, nor have I seen Zi. Hao Gezi is still dissatisfied with the great bell, how easy is it to talk about it?"

    ○ Ghosts Have No Chins
    Xie Zian'an of Wanping has a tomb estate, about ten miles from the city gate, which is very close. Several of his nephews often wander around, because their land produces pheasants, and in winter they always go to look for them, often staying out for several nights. There are many of their kind in the neighborhood, also because the tomb fields are there, and they have places to stay, all of them wandering and forgetting to return, so they gather in groups of dozens. In the morning, they step through the snow to catch birds, and at night, they gather around a stove to drink. Even those who do not know each other, their feelings are the same, their actions are the same, so they laugh and talk together, it is also due to the accumulated habits of the wealthy and idle. One night, they gathered together again, the stored wine has run out, no good materials have been found, they were bored with each other, so they engaged in lively conversation to pass the long night. Their conversation involved strange and unusual topics, they were happy and not tired. By then, it was late at night, among the group, those who were timid had their hair standing on end.

    1064

    忽一人曰：“旧传谓鬼无颏，究亦凭虚之论，畴曾见之？”又一人曰：“予试验之，我辈中有无颏者，即鬼是已。”因试以己手遍探座客。至一人转背匿笑，坚不许摸。众共强之，突以厥首反向，短檠之侧，众逼视焉，则自唇以下无所有，绝类小儿面具。不禁大骇，狂噪而奔，举室不及俟屦。甫出屋门，颓然皆仆，犹闻鬼声噭然，益大怖而号。村人闻喧出视，则累累如贯珠，叠卧地上。笑而扶之，其下有物，尚狺狺不绝。烛之非鬼，一徼夜之犬耳，不觉大噱。众惊定，亦为粲然，始详言之。翌日皆归，盖自是无复弋鹑之志矣。

    Explanation

    1064

    Suddenly, someone said, "The old saying claims that ghosts have no chins; it is ultimately a baseless claim, for who has ever seen one?" Another person replied, "I have tested this. Among us, there is someone without a chin, and that person is a ghost." He then tried to feel everyone in the room with his hand. When he reached one person who turned away and laughed secretly, they firmly refused to let him touch them. The crowd forced them to turn around, and suddenly, they turned their head around, facing the short lampstand. Everyone crowded around to look closely, and they saw that from the lips downward, there was nothing, resembling a child's mask. Unable to help being greatly frightened, they screamed and ran away, and everyone in the room did not even wait for their shoes. As soon as they left the house, they all fell down weakly, still hearing the ghostly cries, and became even more terrified, screaming. The villagers heard the commotion and came out to see, and they saw one after another, like beads on a string, stacked up lying on the ground. They laughed and helped them up, and there was something below, still barking incessantly. When they lit it, it was not a ghost, but just a dog that had wandered in at night. They could not help laughing loudly. The crowd calmed down, and they also laughed heartily, then explained in detail. The next day, everyone returned home, and from then on, there was no more interest in hunting quails.

    1065

    外史氏曰：青灯黑夜，谈鬼为佳，而不知鬼即在其中也。当诸人快谈之顷，鬼必不能默然。奈何胆落于无颏，竟使说鬼之鬼不获罄吐其说，能不詈噉猪肠儿败人清兴？

    ○　秋露纤云
    　　郁生名琥，号秋轩。毗邻绅家子，折节读书，通五经，能文章，郡中衿士举不以膏梁少之。然试辄不利，三踬棘闱，于是学益苦。邻有废圃，买而居之，少置花木，草创数楹，盖为藏修之所，并非作窥园之计者也。寝于斯，食于斯，定省而外莫入中堂。即时遇细君于寝门，亦不少通言笑。在琴瑟之素睦者且如是，而燕游之朋，谈宴之举，更一切屏绝。居数月，时值新秋，槐黄期迫。生已科试有名，计日将赴省，因攻苦倍亟。俄闻身后有人小语曰：“如此用功，那得不中？”惊顾之，寂无所见，乃大骇。

    Explanation

    1065

    外史氏曰：在青灯照耀的黑夜中，谈论鬼怪是最为惬意的，却不知鬼怪其实就在其中。当众人畅谈之时，鬼怪必定不会沉默。为何胆小得连下巴都掉了，竟让说鬼的鬼怪无法畅所欲言，难道不会因此而咒骂那些败坏人兴致的猪肠儿吗？

    ○ 秋露纤云
    　　郁生，字琥，号秋轩。他住在一位绅士家的隔壁，谦逊地读书，精通五经，擅长文章，郡中的士人都不因他出身富贵而轻视他。然而，每次考试都不顺利，三次在棘闱中受挫，于是更加刻苦学习。邻近有一块废弃的园子，他买下并住在那里，稍微布置了一些花草树木，建了几间简陋的房屋，作为读书和修身的地方，并非为了闲逛。他在这里睡觉、吃饭，除了早晚的问候，不进入中堂。即使在寝门遇到妻子，也不多言笑。对于那些原本和睦如琴瑟的夫妻来说，尚且如此，更何况是那些喜欢游山玩水的朋友和宴饮的聚会，他更是完全断绝了。住了几个月，正值新秋，槐树黄叶飘落的季节即将来临。他已经因科举考试有名声，计算着日子即将赴省，因此更加刻苦用功。忽然听到身后有人小声说：“如此用功，怎能不中？”他惊回头，却什么也没看到，于是大为惊骇。

    1066

    犹以为耳音之误，及复读，又语如前，不禁悚然。而业不忍辍，因移坐默诵，意盖静以伺之。无何复语，杂以笑声，则不当生前，仍居生后，生因毛发俱竖。亟呼仆，适值他出，不得已而纳屦以奔。比出户外，又闻室中言曰：“不识秋露纤云，那得便中？”其言绝娇脆而清，如佩玉之鸣。心忽少动，且行且思曰：“飞琼绿华二传，古人果不我欺乎？”遂踌躇不欲归。及门，又将旋踵，仆适自外来，遂相偕返室。岑寂依然，生讳之，竟不告仆，仍前肄业。至夕，将归内寝，恐致父母疑，且虑仆婢笑，遂复下榻萧斋。幸而竟夜无扰，心乃安。生故五夜起读，是夕因悸不能寐，起倍恒时。

    Explanation

    1066
    1. Still thinking it was a mistake in hearing, when he repeated it, the words were as before, and he could not help but feel alarmed. Yet the work could not be stopped, so he moved to sit and silently recite, intending to remain calm and wait for it. Soon after, he heard words spoken again, mixed with laughter, which were not in front of him, but still behind him. The student's hairs stood on end. He quickly called the servant, who just happened to be out, so he had no choice but to put on shoes and run. By the time he was outside the door, he heard words spoken inside the room: "Do not know if autumn dew and fine clouds can be convenient?" The words were extremely delicate and clear, like the sound of jade being struck. The heart suddenly felt a bit moved, walking and thinking at the same time, he said: "The legends of Feiqiong and Lüehua, did the ancients not deceive me after all?" Thus hesitated and did not want to return. By the time he reached the door, almost turned around, the servant just happened to come from outside, thus returned to the room together. Still as silent and desolate, the student concealed it, still did not tell the servant, continued his studies as before. Until evening, was about to return to the inner chamber, feared causing suspicion in parents, also feared the servants and maids would laugh, thus again lay down in the quiet study. Fortunately, there was no disturbance all night, the heart finally calmed. The student usually got up at five in the morning to read, that night, due to fear, could not sleep, got up twice the usual time.
    1067

    仆惟侍主盥濯，别无事事，覸其展卷，亦潜就睡乡。生颇有戒心，诵不能畅久之。甫复其故，而娇韵嘤嘤在耳厢大作矣。生不胜震恐，辍业静听，其音实在几之左右，近且咫尺，恍若两人对赓者，调协声一，绝无抑扬高下之殊。初犹骇而呼仆，嗔其弗觉，及徐聆之，则又别更一艺，迥非已所习者。义奥笔妍，金科玉律，其题则唐棣之一章也。生本嗜文如命，遂以不惧，竟舍己业而从之。共案咿唔，俨然益友，惟是衣香袭鼻，口脂频吹，使人颇不可耐。然而生具夙慧，数遍即能熟一艺。比及成诵，冥冥者又顿易新篇。迨至漏尽声息，生已得佳文五首，不禁忭跃。

    Explanation

    1067

    I was merely attending to the master's washing, with nothing else to do, when I saw him open the book and slip into sleep. The young man had some reservations and could not recite it smoothly for long. Just as he resumed his original task, a delicate and charming sound began to fill the air near the table, close to an arm's length. It was as if two people were responding to each other, with harmonious and unified tones, showing no difference in pitch or volume. At first, he was startled and called out to the servant, rebuking him for not noticing. Upon listening more closely, he realized it was a different art or skill, completely different from what he had learned. The text was profound in meaning and elegant in writing, with strict or unchangeable rules, and the topic was a chapter from the Tang Di. The young man loves literature as if it were life itself, so he was not afraid and finally abandoned his own task to follow the sound. Together at the table, they recited softly, as if they were good friends, but the fragrance of clothes and the scent of lip balm kept wafting, making it quite unbearable. However, the young man possesses innate wisdom and after a few rounds, he could master one skill. By the time he had memorized it, the unseen force suddenly changed to a new piece. Until the sound ceased and the night was silent, he had already obtained five excellent texts and could not help but be joyful.

    1068

    揖而谢之，只闻笑声吃吃，俱入壁中而去。生至晨遂不复读，折纸濡毫，尽录其作。于时久不窥户，因此复出访友，举以相示，见者皆咋舌，谓是飘飘欲仙，取元魁直如拾芥。生益喜，归斋不忍释手，益更熟读，至夜分不寝。且为诘朝计，托言晨饥，命仆具果饵，盖将以酬其金针暗度也。乃生方恬吟，吻边忽有茗一杯，香冽异常，且笑而语之曰：“那便如此苦读，解元郎宁不患口渴耶？”生惊视之，未见其人，先睹其手，纤瘦而洁白，俨如玉盫。而蝉鬓花腮，云衣月态，早已逼近其侧矣！生虽熟识，不免愕然。洎乎举首，又有一美人以朱盘贮果数枚，笑置案头曰：“啖此当连中三元。

    Explanation

    1068

    He bowed and thanked them, and only heard the sound of light laughter before they all disappeared into the wall. The next morning, he no longer returned to his studies, instead folding paper and wetting his brush, fully copying their work. For a long time, he did not leave his room, and thus went out again to visit friends, showing them the work. Those who saw it were all amazed, saying it was as if it could make one float and become a deity, and winning the top prize was as easy as picking up a mustard seed. The student was even more delighted, returning to his study room and unable to let go of the work, reading it even more thoroughly, staying awake until midnight. And for the next day’s plan, he made an excuse of being hungry in the morning, ordering a servant to prepare fruits and snacks, intending to repay them for their secret passing of valuable knowledge. Just as he was leisurely reciting, suddenly a cup of tea appeared near his mouth, unusually fragrant and refreshing, and smiling, she said to him, “Why are you studying so hard? Wouldn’t the top scholar worry about thirst?” The student was startled and looked around, not seeing the person, but first noticing her hands, slender and white, beautiful like a jade basin. Her delicate hair, rosy cheeks, and graceful, ethereal appearance had already approached his side! Though he was familiar with her, he could not help but be surprised. When he lifted his head, another beautiful woman appeared, holding a red tray with several fruits, smiling and placing it on the table, saying, “Eat these, and you will surely pass all three imperial examinations.”

    1069

    ”生因释卷起谢，而深虑其妖，乃整衣以礼相见。两美不答，惟相顾而哂曰：“迂儒故态犹昨。”生复逊之坐，询及姓氏，一美人笑答曰：“昨因不平，已将小字入君耳。渠即纤云，而妾实秋露，本书仙侍儿，谪居人世，已近百年。以冥数当奉巾栉，故根寻至此，幸勿却。”生故解其来意，闻言而倍怀首鼠。惟谢曰：“鲰生不才，敢有仙眷？况抛金闺少妇，实因月桂一枝。冥数难知，盛爱似不敢领。只以文字交，不致堕于欲界，差为厚幸。

    Explanation

    1069

    The scholar laid down his book and rose to thank them, deeply worried about their strange appearance. He then dressed himself and greeted them with proper etiquette. The two beauties did not respond, but looked at each other and smiled, saying, "The old ways of the pedantic scholar are still the same as yesterday." The scholar again invited them to sit and asked about their names. One of the beauties smiled and replied, "Yesterday, because of some injustice, I already put my nickname into your ear. He is Xianyun, and I am Qiulu. We were originally fairies' servants, exiled to live in the human world for nearly a century. By fate, we are destined to serve as concubines, so we came here, please do not refuse." The scholar understood their intentions and became even more hesitant upon hearing this. He only thanked them, saying, "I am a humble and unworthy scholar, dare I have a fairy's favor? Besides, I abandoned a noble wife, actually because of a branch of the moon orchid. Fate is hard to know, such great love seems too much to accept. Only through literary friendship, will not fall into the realm of desire, it is a great fortune."

    1070

    ”语未已，纤云即从旁赞曰：“使乎！使乎！束锦而行，可谓词林妙品矣！虽然，不慕裴航，何以顿怀张硕？迟迟门外，欲去仍留，司马昭之心岂路人竟未之见耶？”生语塞。秋露乃剖析曰：“君固有志图南，惜乎重染腐习，将不免为斥鷃笑。夫欲成不世之名，原不关数行闱墨，矧务举子业以冀首肯于朱衣。即今晨五艺，已如操券，文心患不活泼耳。一以贯之，违道不远，奚必青灯独宿，皓首穷经，始能居孙山以内哉？”生尚犹豫，纤云复笑曰：“三科康了，一枕孤眠。倘潦倒场屋，势将鳏此半世，文章真误人不浅。”生亦不觉捧腹。秋露即就案上取前文为生指迷，一一提其要领，因曰：“君之积习沉锢，断不能联捷甲科。然从此贯通，纵场期伊迩，今秋可以获隽。切不可亟赴南宫，自觅刘蕡之耻。

    Explanation

    1070

    The conversation has ended.

    1071

    归来与妾等处，不出三年，玉堂金马可以立致。但当去此陋室，别构名园，快目爽心，自足伐毛洗髓，幸勿以妾言为谬。”语竟，与纤云俱起曰：“君犹疑忌，俟鹿鸣宴后，敬当以衽席奉贺。”遂就烛下冉冉而没，倏不见。生时已司空见惯，不以为怪，亦就枕。自此日惟即秋露所言者殚心揣摩，少通阃奥。及为文，顿异故吾，同人亦莫不称许。因而变其拘迂，出而与人酬酢，虽未接杯酒之欢，而逐队随行，亦不甚埋头于书本下矣。迨赴省垣，将入闱，忽有小童以漆盒至。询之则曰：“纤娘子之所馈也。”生心知为云，启视焉，则桂圆百枚。

    Explanation

    1071

    Upon returning to live with me and my sisters, within three years, you can achieve a high position in the imperial court. However, you must leave this humble abode and build a different, more splendid garden. This will please the eyes and refresh the mind, and it will be enough to rejuvenate yourself thoroughly. Do not consider my words mistaken. She then slowly disappeared from the candlelight, and suddenly vanished. The protagonist was so familiar with these events that he did not find them strange, and he also went to bed. From then on, he devoted himself to studying and imitating what the woman had said, and he slightly understood the deeper principles. When he wrote, he suddenly became different from before, and everyone else also praised him. As a result, he changed his rigid and narrow-minded ways and began to engage socially with others. Although he did not join in the drinking and merrymaking, he followed the crowd and participated in activities, and he was no longer deeply engrossed in books. When he went to the provincial capital and was about to enter the examination hall, a messenger suddenly arrived with a lacquered box. Upon inquiry, he was told, "This is a gift from Lady Xian." The protagonist realized it was from the woman and opened the box to find a hundred dried longans.

    1072

    初以为嫦娥之代祝而已，将语小童，旋失所在，及分其半而携之。搜者以果结于树，绝无可疑，竟听其将入，即生亦罔知其有异。次日昧爽，题纸飞下，顿思以圆佐茗。剖之，与恒果迥殊。生就烛谛观，实以绵纸团成者，细楷如蚊翼，极目力始能见，乃时下才人之预构也。生大喜，乘暗破丸，得文数百首，兼有目次。就题物色，则首场书艺，虽未吻合，亦既不中不远。生有此可恃，遂就卷挥毫，无殊宿构，固不俟于脱稿，而三作立成。沿及经艺，顺流扬帆，瞬息直下。明晨首先交纳而出，同考者就而索观，又靡不气沮。二场又瓜分其果，携入矮屋，表判既莫不工丽，三场五策出之丸中，又典核超群。是役也，生未少费心力，已可望巍科。

    Explanation

    1072

    Initially, he thought it was only a substitute for Chang'e's blessing. Soon, it lost its location, and when half was taken away, it was carried away. Searchers found the fruit attached to the tree, leaving no doubt, and even the master did not know it was different. The next morning at dawn, a paper with writing flew down, and he immediately thought to use the round object to assist with tea. Upon opening it, he found it vastly different from ordinary fruit. The master examined it closely by the candlelight and discovered it was actually made of soft paper rolled into a ball, with the fine script as thin as a mosquito's wing. Only with great effort could one see it, and it was actually a pre-written work by contemporary talented people. The master was greatly pleased and secretly broke the ball, obtaining hundreds of poems, along with an index. To examine the topic and its characteristics, the first session's book art, although not perfectly matched, was still close enough. The master had this to rely on, thus he began writing without hesitation, as if it were pre-planned, and there was no need to finish the draft; three works were completed instantly. Then, he proceeded to the classics and arts, like sailing downstream with the wind, it was completed in an instant. The next morning, he was the first to submit his work and leave, and those who were taking the same exam came to see it, all were disheartened. In the second session, they divided the fruit again and carried it into a small room, the judgments were all skillful and beautiful. In the third session, five strategies came out of the ball, and they were even more authoritative and outstanding. In this task, the master did not spend much effort but could already hope for a high rank.

    1073

    甚感云德，恨不一晤，且不解其果中界限，何以判然不淆，益信为真仙。惟虑不得为眷属，而狐疑因以尽祛。已榜下，则以文有讹字，置之亚元。主司尤为之扼腕，惜其全璧微瑕，实则冠场之作也。生益感云玉成，暇即祝于斗室，冀得一见颜色，而竟杳然。一日，忽于书中得小柬。启之，字迹柔媚，盖即二美之札，特以之申贺者。其意惟劝生勿北上，行且不吉。更愿得郡中名园，以为藏娇之所。纸尾数行，则曰：“君夫人久虚鸳被，今幸折桂人仍返床头，妾等不敢以陋质溷。俟燕婉少伸，再当抱衾裯以事君子，今尚非其时也”，云云。

    Explanation

    1073

    Deeply grateful for your virtue, I lament that I have not had the chance to meet you. I do not understand how you have such a clear and distinct nature, but I believe you to be a true immortal. I was concerned that we could not be family, and my doubts were completely dispelled. After the examination results were announced, it was because of a mistake in my writing that I was placed second. The chief examiner was especially frustrated, lamenting that the work was almost perfect but for a small flaw, which was in fact the best in the competition. I am even more grateful to you for your success, and in my spare time, I would pray in a small room, hoping to see you, but you vanished without a trace. One day, I suddenly found a small note in my book. Opening it, the handwriting was soft and graceful, it was a letter from the two beauties, specially sent to congratulate me. The message simply advised me not to travel north, as it would be unlucky. They also wished to obtain a famous garden in the county as a place to hide their beauty. At the end of the letter, it said: "Your wife has been waiting in vain for you to return to the bed. Now that the successful scholar has returned, we dare not mix our humble qualities with you. Wait until our affection has developed a bit more, then we will hold the quilt and pillow to serve the gentleman. It is not the right time yet," and so on.

    1074

    生乃倍钦其贤。然以少年高捷，不忍韬晦，竟不听所言，束装赴都。乃行未数程，从人沈犹狂恃主新贵，殴某县隶役。令怒，作牒通详，置之于法。以生为疏纵，削其科名，生遂垂翅复返。深悔不听秋露，而服其先知，思晤愈亟。且当新黜之后，愧见戚族，闻无锡董氏，有别墅，花木森繁，地更幽邃，乃白于父母，以重价僦而居之。入园之初，适当季春上巳，生薰沐更衣，若有所俟，僮仆皆窃笑。且束书不读，惟云露二字不绝于口，亦多所不解。居甫定，尽驱从者就外宿，非奉使不令入园，一切添香烹茗之事，皆自任之，意盖有所为也。

    Explanation

    1074

    The young man greatly admired his virtue. However, because he was a young and successful official, he could not bear to hide his talent and ignored the advice given to him, packing up and heading to the capital. After traveling only a short distance, one of his followers, Shen, became arrogant due to his new status and beat a county official. The official was furious, issued a report detailing the incident, and had Shen punished by law. Because the young man was seen as lenient, his official position was revoked, and he returned defeated. He deeply regretted not heeding the warning and began to believe in the foresight of the advice, thinking about meeting the person who gave it more urgently. After being demoted, he felt ashamed to face his relatives and heard that the Dong family in Wuxi had a villa with lush gardens and a secluded location. He informed his parents and rented the place at a high price to live in. On the first day he entered the garden, it was the festival of the third month of the lunar calendar. He bathed, changed clothes, and seemed to be waiting for something, causing his servants to secretly laugh. He stopped reading books and kept repeating the words "cloud" and "dew," though he did not understand their meaning. As soon as he settled in, he drove all his followers to stay outside, allowing only those on official business to enter the garden. He took on all tasks related to preparing tea and incense himself, indicating that he had a specific purpose in mind.

    1075

    阅二夕，生正对月怀思，而环佩璆然，两美早联袂而至。生大喜，下阶迎之。纤云先贺而后吊，秋露独先吊而后贺，生亦不甚介意。相携入斋，生因向纤云申谢。云曰：“自是妾强作解事，致君得而复失，不然君纵不中，亦不至于白衣，何谢为？”秋露因微哂曰：“婢子诱人怀挟，论罪尚宜连坐。”生与云皆粲然。生复向秋露自咎，并服其灼见。秋露正色曰：“初以科名许君，妾犹盽怳不定。乃君竟掇高魁，且重负妾约，行将看上苑之花，不复念梁园之旧，果遭蹉跌，铩羽归来，此其所以宜吊而不宜贺也。今幸深绎妾言，别开生面，既不坐枯寂之禅，自宜有生花之笔。

    Explanation

    1075

    After two nights, the protagonist was gazing at the moon, lost in thought, when the sound of jade pendants tinkling came from two beautiful women who arrived together. The protagonist was greatly pleased and went down the steps to greet them. Fengyun first congratulated and then expressed condolences, while Qiulu first expressed condolences and then congratulated. The protagonist did not take it too seriously. They went into the study together, and the protagonist thanked Fengyun. Fengyun said, "It was I who acted presumptuously, causing you to gain and then lose something. Even if you had failed, you would not have become a commoner, so there is no need to thank me." Qiulu smiled slightly and said, "You tempted the protagonist, and you should be held responsible." The protagonist and Fengyun both laughed. The protagonist again apologized to Qiulu and acknowledged her sharp insight. Qiulu said sternly, "I initially promised you success, but you achieved high distinction and broke your promise, leading to a setback. I now congratulate you for starting afresh and producing creative and productive writing."

    1076

    从兹扶摇直上，九万鹏程，不难一蹴而至，则又宜贺而不必吊矣。况君家声赫奕，当抱王参元之忧。纵使纤妹计行，亿则屡中，将流言四起，重累清名，实亦妾所逆虑。”生闻其言侃侃，益觉心折，乃悟先吊后贺之故。于是相对叙阔，绝无畏忌。已而剪烛促坐，置酒合欢，云露皆善清歌，兼工谐谑，每一曲梁尘欲动，每一语玉屑纷飞。语笑声音，时达户外，而仆辈究如充耳，亦可异已。夜将半，生微醺，托醉先起。云又飞一觥来，谓生曰：“今夕何夕，情难自已，请以一语为觞政。能则先占枝头，不能则退之殿后，倘中原并驱，则一箭双雕。君亦慎勿峻拒。”生亦不禁大笑，因亦酌一卮，属云出令。

    Explanation

    1076

    From here, one can rise straight up like a whirlwind, covering a journey of nine thousand miles, and achieve success with one step. Thus, one should be congratulated rather than mourned. Moreover, your family's reputation is grand and illustrious, and you should bear the concerns of Wang Can and Yuan Shao. Even if a younger sister's plan succeeds, and some guesses hit the mark, rumors will spread everywhere, burdening a good reputation, which is indeed something I have anticipated negatively. The speaker listened to the words with composure, feeling deeply impressed, and realized the reason for the change from mourning to congratulating. They then conversed freely without fear or hesitation. Soon, they trimmed the candle and sat close together, setting up wine and celebrating joyfully. Both Cloud and Dew were skilled in pure singing and humorous and witty conversation. Each song caused the dust on the beam to seem to move, and each word produced a sparkling effect like jade chips flying. Their laughter and conversation reached the outdoors, but the servants pretended not to hear, which was also strange. By midnight, the speaker was slightly intoxicated and pretended to be drunk to leave early. Cloud brought another cup of wine and said to the speaker, "What a night it is tonight, emotions are hard to control. Let us play a drinking game with one word. If one can, they take the top position; if not, they come last. If we compete equally in the middle, then one arrow will kill two birds. Please do not firmly reject." The speaker couldn't help but laugh and also poured a cup, asking Cloud to make the rules.

    1077

    云即一饮而尽曰：“烟云满纸，君宜先赋行云。”露亦朗然曰：“月露盈帘，郎岂迟挥垂露。”一时大噱，几翻酒盏。遂同起。拂榻解衣共寝，左珠右玉，拂柳穿花。晨起，生颇虑为仆见。讵意奔走服事，若役瞽矇，怅怅然莫能觏止。露与云亦只默坐而已。及仆出，欢然萃处。露以手谈教生，云亦以白描之技进，生皆悉心学之。而且酒兵茗战斗于宵，锄花种竹娱于昼。生室中故无乐器，惟古琴一张，露抚之，听者忘倦。云自携有筝笛，时一弹弄，又复妙绝人寰。至于诗文典籍，无时不共相涉猎。探奇索异，辨难析疑，盖不闻诵声而生之业已大进。

    Explanation

    1077

    The cloud drank it all at once and said, "The paper is full of mist and clouds; you should first compose flowing clouds." The dew also spoke clearly, "The moon and dew fill the curtain; will you delay in writing your drooping dew?" Everyone burst into laughter, and they passed the wine cups several times. They then got up together, swept the bed, undressed, and slept together, with jewels on the left and jade on the right, sweeping willows and passing through flowers. In the morning, the student was somewhat worried about being seen by the servant. Who would have thought that the servant ran around serving, like blind and deaf servants, sorrowfully unable to meet. The dew and the cloud only sat silently. When the servant left, they gathered joyfully. The dew taught the student how to play chess, and the cloud also taught the student the skill of white description, which the student learned with great care. They also engaged in intellectual and social activities late into the night, and dug flowers and planted bamboo for fun during the day. The student's room originally had no musical instruments, only an ancient zither. The dew played it, and listeners forgot their fatigue. The cloud brought a zither and flute, sometimes playing them, which was again marvelous beyond the world. As for poetry, literature, and classics, they never ceased to explore them together. They explored the strange and sought the unusual, debated difficulties and analyzed doubts, and without hearing the sound of recitation, the student's studies had greatly advanced.

    1078

    生虽深入佳境，久而忘归，然至三朝，露必勗之归省，至旬日，云必劝之家居。而床笫之私，露与云举不甚重。每曰：“郎君将飞黄腾达，忍死于妇人女子之手耶？”其贤淑类如此。若是者二稔，既无人觉其形声，且两人俱不火食，唯哺佳果数枚，绝无所需于生。而美醖佳茗，又皆有术以致之，以故内藏丽质而外静浮言。生既博享韵事，集益闺房，因之心体流通，不啻脱胎换骨。初犹待露为之削，云为之斫，相习已久，不费斧斤已伟然清庙明堂之器矣。露又劝生以文稿行世，广致揄扬，人益信其前非幸中，名誉益彰。是岁，改籍游泮，再入秋闱，遂冠南省，中式第一人。

    Explanation

    1078

    The young man, though deeply immersed in a beautiful realm and forgetting to return home, would be reminded by Lu to visit his family on the third day after birth, and by Yun to stay at home after ten days. However, their intimate relations were not highly valued by Lu and Yun. They often said, "My young master will rise to prominence; how can he die at the hands of women?" Such was their virtue and gentleness. For two years, no one noticed his presence, and both Lu and Yun did not eat cooked food, living solely on a few good fruits. They had no other needs. Fine wine and excellent tea were obtained through their methods, so they kept their inner beauty hidden while remaining calm and quiet on the outside. The young man enjoyed elegant and refined matters, benefiting his home, and thus his mind and body flowed smoothly, not just transforming but completely changing. Initially, he relied on Lu to guide him and Yun to support him, but after becoming accustomed to it, he became a majestic figure, a vessel of great importance. Lu then advised him to publish his writings, which gained widespread praise and commendation. People increasingly believed that his previous misfortune was a lucky turn of events, and his reputation grew even more. In that year, he changed his household registration to study at a local school, entered the imperial examination again, and won the top prize in the southern province, becoming the first to pass the examination.

    1079

    明春将赴京取应，云与露忽辞去，曰：“久旷职司，将归天上，不得再捧君砚，可奈何？”生大惊，强之复留，继以涕泣，露不从，而云颇恋恋。露曰：“纤妹不能忘情，已堕色界，可以无返。郎君宜善处之，俾得出以见人。匿迹藏声，究非长策也。”生乃喜。犹将留露，露即趋出，涌身向上，转瞬直达重霄，倏不见。云将继之，甫去地丈许，雷声殷然，乃惧而复下，喟曰：“孽缘自作，书仙良不可欺。”生虽失露，喜复得云，乃少慰。因与云计，使先行，俟之中途。生至维扬，托言乏嗣置妾，纳为如君。仆人莫知其诈，云自此饮食无异恒人，相随入都，赞襄场务。

    Explanation

    1079

    In the coming spring, I will go to the capital to take the imperial examination. Suddenly, Yun and Lu bid farewell, saying, "We have long neglected our duties and will return to heaven. We cannot continue to hold your brush. What can be done?" The protagonist was greatly startled and tried to persuade them to stay, followed by tears. Lu refused, but Yun seemed reluctant. Lu said, "My younger sister cannot forget her feelings; she has fallen into the realm of desire and can no longer return. You should handle this well so that she can appear before people. Hiding one's tracks and voice is not a long-term strategy." The protagonist was then happy. He still tried to keep Lu, but she rushed out, leaped upward, and in an instant reached the highest heavens, suddenly disappearing. Yun intended to follow her, but just a few feet above the ground, a loud thunderclap frightened him back down. He sighed and said, "Self-made karma, the book spirits cannot be deceived." Although the protagonist lost Lu, he was happy to regain Yun and felt a little comfort. He then made a plan with Yun, letting him go first and waiting for him in the middle. The protagonist arrived in Yangzhou, claiming to be childless and buying a concubine, taking her as a wife. The servants did not know it was a deception. From then on, Yun's eating and drinking were no different from ordinary people; he accompanied the protagonist to the capital and assisted in the examination affairs.

    1080

    生因戏之曰：“蜡丸密函，卿当为我预办矣。”答曰：“今日之事，君事也，妾不敢以鱼目混珠。”生竟联捷成进士，殿试二甲第一。将受职，云谓生曰：“人子魁名金榜，所以悦亲也。大人以家累，实不克北来，君可无念乎？”生曰：“然。”即乞假南旋。抵里，云始庙见舅姑，谒嫡室。细君妬其色，颇苛遇之。生因私述其默助之德，并白其异，细君以为诞。一日，生与云皆在细君处，忽细君之弟来省姊，意盖覸云。云亦绝不惊避，迨客及阶，云乃以身就生，旋合为一。侍婢环瞩，皆见生而不见云，举室骇然。及客去，云故在生侧跬步未移，细君始信生言，惧而修好。及生限满，携眷来都，闺中之雍睦不啻尹邢之相遇焉。

    Explanation

    1080

    The protagonist jestingly said to her, "Prepare a secret message in a wax-sealed container for me." She replied, "This matter is your affair, and I dare not mix pearls with fish eyes." The protagonist eventually succeeded in the imperial examinations and became a Jinshi, ranking first in the second class in the palace examination. As he was about to take office, she told him, "Achieving the top rank on the imperial list brings joy to one's parents. Your father, burdened with family responsibilities, could not come north, so you need not worry." The protagonist said, "Indeed." He then requested leave to return south. Upon arriving home, she began by paying respects to his parents-in-law and visiting his original wife. The original wife, jealous of her beauty, treated her harshly. The protagonist privately recounted her silent assistance and explained the strange occurrences, but the original wife dismissed them as fantastical. One day, while both the protagonist and she were in the presence of the original wife, the latter's brother came to visit his sister, likely with an eye on her. She showed no surprise or evasion. As the guest reached the threshold, she approached the protagonist, and they quickly merged into one. The maidservants looked around and saw only the protagonist, not her. The household was greatly astonished. After the guest left, she remained by the protagonist's side, having moved not a step. The original wife then believed the protagonist's words, feared, and sought to reconcile. When the protagonist's term ended, he brought his family to the capital, and their harmonious relationship was no less than that of the virtuous women in the story of Yin and Xing.

    1081

    外史氏曰：适有巨家延师课子，年余而学未增益。主人亦知名之士，因叩其故于师。师曰：“此子材器诚不凡，然使之困守寒毡，所就只可如此。若能予我千金，携之远游，三年当一鸣惊人。”主人服其论，慨然从之，资以舟车，丰其囊橐，听其携子出行，戚友皆窃笑。乃师导其弟搜奇览胜，南尽闽粤，北极燕齐，足迹遍历数省。每值通都，则购异书供读，而参考胜迹，晋谒名流，又无虚日。期年，弟倦于游，请于师欲返故里。

    Explanation

    1081

    The Historian said: There was once a wealthy family who hired a teacher to educate their son. After more than a year, the boy's learning had not increased. The master was also a well-known scholar, and he asked the teacher about the reason. The teacher replied, "This boy truly possesses exceptional talent and ability, but if he is confined to a life of poverty and hardship, his accomplishments will be limited to this extent. If you were to give me a thousand gold pieces, I could take him on a long journey. In three years, he will surely achieve something remarkable." The master was convinced by his argument and readily agreed, providing him with boats and carriages, filling his bag with money, and allowing him to take his son on the trip, which made his relatives and friends secretly laugh. Thus, the teacher guided the boy to seek out the strange and unusual, and enjoy scenic views, traveling as far south as Fujian and Guangdong, and as far north as Yan and Qi, covering several provinces. Whenever they reached a major city, they would buy rare books to read, consult famous sites, and visit celebrities, never taking a day off. After a year, the boy became tired of traveling and asked the teacher if they could return to their hometown.

    1082

    师曰：“游兴阑乎？书可读矣！”即就舟中指授，聊当下帷。及归，键户肄业。出其所作，先达皆为称赏，因以成名，竟登高第。从可知坐破蒲团未必即悟上乘。文人之脏腑，务须剔透玲珑，始可以笔墨为如意珠，题名雁塔，走马曲江，但不宜为浮薄者别开蹊径耳。故如云露之踪迹诡异，洵为理所应无。而化钝为灵，化板为活，颇得此中三昧。至于赝鼎终成虚话，贪痴竟列小星，则云之去露不啻径庭。书仙之侧尚有人，吾愿闭户潜修者，皆当馨香以祝之。

    Explanation

    1082

    The teacher said, "Has your interest in wandering come to an end? It is time to read books!" Immediately, he began to teach from the boat, and they temporarily closed the curtain. Upon returning home, they locked the door and engaged in studies. When they presented their work, all the respected scholars praised them, and thus they became famous, eventually achieving high honors. From this, we can see that sitting on a broken rush mat does not necessarily lead to enlightenment. The inner workings of a writer must be clear and delicate, only then can writing be valued as a precious pearl, inscribed on the Big Wild Goose Pagoda, and enjoyed in the Qujiang Pool, but one should not open a new path for superficial people. Therefore, the mysterious traces of clouds and dew are indeed beyond reason. Transforming dullness into brilliance, transforming rigidity into liveliness, one has grasped the essence of this. As for a fake tripod ultimately becoming an empty story, greed and delusion ending up as small stars, the difference between clouds and dew is not just a little. There are still people by the side of the book god. I wish to offer incense and pray for those who close their doors and engage in secluded study.

    1083

    ○　萧翠楼
    　　苑之缙者，燕之诸生也。有邻人子萧姓，不言其名，性佻达，颇多穴隙行。里之荡妇咸与之私，邑人因以切齿。以其垣居妓馆，遂私以翠楼号之。苑少未更事，间与之游，章台杨柳，攀折外则无所染焉。无何，萧以瘵疾卒，苑因鉴于前车，少加撙节。忽梦萧来召之曰：“仆案未结，须兄一往对质，幸勿惮于跋涉。”苑寐中顿忘其死，疑其以花事搆讼，词涉于己，勉从之。行出门约数里，过一溪，水黑色，腥秽扑鼻。苑欲止，萧固强之，乃褰裳以渡。又里许，始见屋宇，则兰若，绝非衙署，苑心颇疑。久之，阒其无人。

    Explanation

    1083

    Yuan Jìn, a student from Yan, had a neighbor's son surnamed Xiao, whose name was not mentioned. By nature, Xiao was carefree and unrestrained, engaging in many secretive and illicit activities. Women in the neighborhood, known for their promiscuity, all had secret affairs with him, and the townspeople were so angry that they bit their teeth in frustration. Because Xiao lived near a brothel, he was privately nicknamed "Cui Lou" (Green Tower). Yuan, being young and inexperienced, occasionally associated with Xiao, visiting places like the Changtai Willow, but did not engage in any illicit activities beyond that. Soon after, Xiao died of tuberculosis, and Yuan, learning from Xiao's fate, restrained himself a little. Suddenly, Yuan dreamed that Xiao called him, saying, "My case is still unresolved. I need you to come and face the evidence. Please do not hesitate due to the distance." Yuan momentarily forgot that Xiao was dead, thinking that Xiao was involved in a lawsuit related to "flower matters" (a euphemism for prostitution or illicit affairs) that implicated him. Reluctantly, he agreed to go. He walked out about several miles, passing a stream with black, foul-smelling water. Yuan wanted to stop, but Xiao insisted, so he lifted his clothes to cross. After another mile, he finally saw buildings, which turned out to be a Buddhist temple, not an official government building. Yuan was very suspicious. After a long time, the place was completely deserted.

    1084

    有巨像当门而立，几与檐等，赤金色，面目绝不可见。萧即跪拜于地，苑亦俯伏。像忽发声曰：“此案结之已久，老僧以婆心度世，故待苑生来。今渠至矣，汝盍行乎！”萧犹嗫嚅，似有所言，像严声叱之，萧忽不见，像亦归于乌有。苑大骇，不敢深入，反身却退。比出时，则行者肩摩，往来如蚁，非复向之寥寥者矣。苑茫不解其故，逐队而前。忽一人短衣陋貌，状若鸨奴，拱之曰：“苑相公故多豪兴，何竟来此一游？”苑以萧事语之。其人笑曰：“大好大好！请从我去，不第可以释惑，且可快观。”苑喜，径从之偕往。旋至一处，粉垣四围，有侧门。其人导以入，且语曰：“欲窥隐秘，当非正路可由也。

    Explanation

    1084

    There was a giant statue standing in front of the door, almost as tall as the eaves, with a reddish-golden color, and its face was completely invisible. Xiao immediately knelt and bowed to the ground, and Yuan also prostrated himself. Suddenly, the statue spoke, saying, "This case has been settled for a long time. The old monk uses his compassionate heart to guide the world, so I waited for Yuan to come. Now he has arrived, why don't you go?" Xiao still stammered, as if he had something to say. The statue sternly rebuked him, and Xiao suddenly vanished, and the statue also disappeared into nothingness. Yuan was greatly frightened, dared not go deeper, turned around and retreated. By the time they left, the crowd was pressing against each other, moving like ants, no longer the sparse scene as before. Yuan was bewildered and did not understand the reason, followed the crowd forward. Suddenly, a person with shabby clothes and a disreputable appearance, looking like a courtesan's servant, bowed to him and said, "Mr. Yuan, you usually have so much enthusiasm, why did you come here for a visit?" Yuan told him about Xiao's matter. The person laughed and said, "Very good, very good! Please follow me, not only can it dispel doubts, but also can be enjoyed." Yuan was happy and followed him directly. Soon, they arrived at a place surrounded by white walls, with a side door. The person led him in and said, "If you want to see secrets, you cannot go through the main path."

    1085

    ”及入而视之，华屋数楹，皆内向。旁开月窗，障以纱，烛光外射，顿悟以昼为夜。俄闻笑音，室中似有数人，语杂猥亵。苑故习于此，心微动。其人使苑伏牖而窥之，内方肆筵豪饮，一长髯者据中坐，旁四客皆甚粗鄙，袒裼露臂，相与大釂。侍者惟一美少年，执壶而立，只可十四五，姿容娟秀，腼腆向人，美目中恍含余泪，时时潜拭以巾。及视其衣，绿襦红棍，当是娈童。苑方静坐壁上观，忽见长髯者顾谓客曰：“翠楼尚多做作，今宵当试以毒手，庶快素心。”众皆笑诺。苑闻之愕然，熟视少年，虽妍媸不类，而眉目态度果肖萧，心始怀疑。

    Explanation

    1085

    Upon entering and seeing it, there was a grand house with several rooms, all facing inward. Beside the house were moon-shaped windows, covered with gauze, and the candlelight shone outward, making one suddenly realize that day was mistaken for night. Soon, laughter was heard, and it seemed there were several people in the room, speaking in coarse and obscene terms. The young man was accustomed to this environment and his heart slightly stirred. The person made the young man crouch by the window and peek inside. Inside, people were drinking heavily, with a man with a long beard sitting in the center. Four guests beside him were very crude and vulgar, naked and bare-armed, drinking together in large quantities. There was only one handsome young servant, holding a pot and standing, probably around fourteen or fifteen years old. His appearance was graceful and delicate, shyly facing people, with beautiful eyes that seemed to contain lingering tears, frequently wiping his eyes with a handkerchief. When he looked at his clothes, they were a green tunic and red trousers, must be a young male servant. The young man was quietly sitting on the wall watching, when he suddenly saw the man with the long beard look at the guests and say, "The Green Tower still has many pretenses, tonight we will try a harsh method, to satisfy our long-held intentions." Everyone laughed and agreed. The young man was startled by what he heard, and looked carefully at the young boy, though their beauty and ugliness were not similar, but their eyebrows, eyes, and demeanor truly resembled Xiao, and he began to doubt.

    1086

    既而烛烬酒阑，坐客皆起，有二人以事辞去，长髯者复命易烛，辉煌如昼。三人作醉态，皆嬲少年，或挽其颈，或交其吻，少年娇羞无主，莫知所为。未几，客皆自解亵衣，擂垂盈掬，俱甚伟岸。盖不独室中者寒心，即室外者亦为咋舌。视少年大有惧色，众捉之，置诸床榻，捋裩狎玩，莫不鼓掌曰：“美哉臀也，翠楼乃以之馈我。”闻者不禁竦然，而方凿圆枘，漂杵血流，嘤嘤受创之声与击刺之音间杂而作。三虎啖羊，其无余肉可知已。众方背水夹击，欻一壮男子排闼直入，横提白刃，怒不可遏。众惊避，惟长髯者挽之以力，状若解纷。坚不听，以刃拟少年。少年觳觫不胜，壮男竟决其首，血喷屋隅。苑在恍惚之中，见所杀者非少年，实即萧也。

    Explanation

    1086

    After a while, the candles burned out and the wine ran out, and the guests all stood up. Two of them left, citing some matter, and the long-haired man ordered the replacement of the candles, which then shone as bright as day. The three men acted drunk, all teasing the young man, pulling his neck or kissing him. The young man was shy and flustered, not knowing what to do. Shortly after, the guests all removed their undergarments, revealing their impressive physiques. Not only those inside the room were appalled, but those outside were also astonished. The young man showed great fear, and the group grabbed him, placing him on the bed, fondling and playing with him. They all clapped and said, "How beautiful his buttocks are, the Green Tower has offered this to us." Those who heard this could not help but be startled, and the square peg in a round hole, the pestle floating, blood flowing, the sound of someone being injured and the sound of striking and stabbing were heard. Three tigers eating a lamb, it is clear that nothing is left. The group was attacking from both sides with their backs to the water, and suddenly a strong man burst in through the door, carrying a sword horizontally, furious and uncontrollable. The group was startled and retreated, but only the long-haired man pulled him back with force, as if trying to mediate. The man firmly refused to listen and brandished the sword at the young man. The young man trembled with fear, and the strong man finally decapitated him, blood splattering to the corner of the room. In a state of confusion, it was revealed that the one killed was not the young man, but actually Xiao.

    1087

    惊悸如寤，耳畔有人语曰：“何竟睡去？”张目，则即前所导引者，己身仍伏于牖下。静伺室中，鼻息齁然，已灭烛寝矣。其人曰：“去之，君亦当自寻佳处。”遂仍从侧门中出。天忽开朗，似有曙光，其人谓苑曰：“闻君颇好狭邪游，予家近得丽者，盍往观乎？”苑已惊定，见猎心喜，又许之。其人引之至一门，垂帘低下，朱户盈盈，物色果类妓家，苑乃径入。及返顾，萧亦自外来，面容惨淡，见苑甚有愧色。苑伫立俟之，呼而与语，竟不答，疾趋而过。苑怪之，亦随入。萧入内房，即有一垂髫婢自侧屋出，呼曰：“阿姊来末？客来矣！”内遽应曰：“予适易衣，若可延客坐。

    Explanation

    1087

    Startled and trembling as if waking from a dream, someone spoke by his ear, asking, "Why did you fall asleep?" Opening his eyes, he saw it was the person who had guided him before, and he found himself still crouched under the window sill. Quietly waiting in the room, he heard heavy breathing, and the candle had already been extinguished, signaling that they had fallen asleep. The person said, "Go, you should also find a good place yourself." Then he still went out through the side door. The sky suddenly became bright, as if there was a hint of dawn. The person told Yuan, "I heard you like to visit brothels; my family has recently acquired a beautiful woman. Why don't you come and see her?" Yuan was startled but calmed down, seeing the opportunity, he was happy and agreed. The person led him to a door, with a hanging curtain lowered, red doors, and a graceful appearance. The appearance was indeed like a brothel, so Yuan then entered directly. When he turned back, Xiao also came out from outside, with a pale face, seeing Yuan, he looked very guilty. Yuan stood still waiting for him, called out to speak with him, but he did not answer, quickly passed by. Yuan found it strange, also followed in. Xiao entered the inner room, immediately a young girl with braids came out from the side room, calling out, "Has Sister come? The guest has arrived!" The person inside quickly replied, "I just changed clothes, you can invite the guest to sit."

    1088

    ”婢即搴箔邀苑入。苑视室内，颇精洁，但无美人雅态。四壁所悬，惟琵琶筝笛而已，因疑萧。适婢取茗，乃自帘隙窥觇之，见萧果白身立于内，以手举一物，肉色莹白。徐视之，绿黛朱樱，乳阴毕具，则妇人之驱壳也。骇极，更穷其异，萧振衣著之，如蝉入蜕，转瞬即更女身。苑始大怖，亟奔出。行及外户，有数人欣欣而来，操土音，都类已，相与语曰：“不费半文，得阚美妓，亦甚快人意。”又曰：“感煞佛力，使我辈一洗此辱，不愈于生啖其肉耶？”遂相率入门而去。苑始顿悟，身在冥途，所遇者皆鬼也。正盽怳迷路，急不能归，俄见其亡祖扶杖而来。苑即匍匐直前，涕泣请命。

    Explanation

    1088

    The servant immediately drew aside the curtain and invited Mr. Yuan in. Mr. Yuan looked around the room, which was quite neat and clean, but there was no grace or beauty of a lady. The walls hung only with pipa, zither, and flute. Therefore, he suspected Xiao. Just as the servant fetched tea, Mr. Yuan peeped through the curtain gap and saw Xiao indeed standing inside, holding something with his hand, the flesh color lustrous white. Upon closer inspection, it was green eyebrows, red lips, breasts, and vulva, all present, indicating a woman's body. He was terrified and further investigated the strange occurrence. Xiao shook off his clothes, like a cicada shedding its skin, and instantly transformed into a woman. Mr. Yuan was greatly frightened and rushed out. When he reached the outer door, several people came happily, speaking in local dialects, all resembling himself. They said to each other, "Without spending a single coin, we got a beautiful courtesan, which is very pleasing." They also said, "Thanks to the Buddha's power, we have washed away this humiliation, isn't it better than eating his flesh?" They then entered the house together and left. Mr. Yuan suddenly realized that he was in the underworld, and those he met were all ghosts. He was bewildered and lost, unable to find his way back. Soon, he saw his deceased grandfather coming with a cane. Mr. Yuan immediately crawled forward, weeping and begging for mercy.

    1089

    其祖曰：“畜生！汝见冥罚亦已有恫于心乎？”苑因悚惧，不敢对。其祖太息良久，命之曰：“从予行尚可以生，然宜洗心改过。否则，寿既易夭，报亦无可逭也。”已乃携苑入一夹弄，初黑暗，渐次朗然。而行不数武，即闻哀嘶之音，痛入心髓。及近，则两旁皆崇廊，高近二丈，男妇无算，倒悬于庑下，举裸无寸缕。由胯间如抽丝，肠出丈余，束其双足而吊之。间有僧尼，遇之倍酷，顶下自生烈焰，烂额焦头，故号声尤惨于众。苑以问其祖，答曰：“此即屠肠狱也。汝之罪不至于为娈为娼，此中恐所不免耳。”言之三叹。苑亦潜然，悔且愧。及将出弄，其祖又嘱之曰：“归宜好自为。汝前所见巨像，乃净秽金刚，故不惜时临秽地。

    Explanation

    1089

    His ancestor said, "Beast! Have you already felt the pain of the afterlife's punishment in your heart?" The youth was filled with fear and could not reply. His ancestor sighed deeply for a long time, then commanded, "If you follow me, you may still live, but you should cleanse your heart and reform your ways. Otherwise, your life will be easily cut short, and the retribution cannot be avoided." He then took the youth into a narrow alley, which was initially dark but gradually became clear. After walking just a few steps, they heard cries of anguish that pierced the heart. As they approached, they saw on both sides tall corridors, nearly two zhang high, with countless men and women hanging upside down, completely naked. From their hips, their intestines were being pulled out like silk, over a zhang long, and their feet were bound and hung. Occasionally, there were monks and nuns who met them with even greater cruelty, with flames spontaneously erupting from their heads, burning their faces and heads, so their cries were even more pitiful than the others. The youth asked his ancestor, who replied, "This is the Hell of the Sliced Intestines. Your sins do not warrant becoming a courtesan or a prostitute, but you may not escape this place." He sighed three times. The youth also felt ashamed and regretful. As they were about to leave the alley, his ancestor warned him, "When you return, you should conduct yourself well. The huge figure you saw before is the Pure and Impure Vajra, so do not hesitate to face impure places."

    1090

    亟求是经奉诵之，尚可幸脱法网。”苑欲叩其详，旋失其祖之所在。一时顿触孺慕，大恸失声。撒手而觉，则身在榻上，漏下已五鼓矣。苑因历历记忆，自誓改行从善。明日起即购金刚经，薰沐奉持。月余又梦美妇人，靓妆艳服，贸然而来，谓苑曰：“淫人一妇，其伯叔戚族莫不予毒。幸得孽债少清，以淫行，仍将为娼。子如有意，廿年后可访我于吴山楚水间。”苑知其为萧，挽之晤言，不及而寤。于是斋心益诚。又闻萧内外兼好，每诱人子弟，更以断袖之丑，致其人之兄赍恨而亡，乃悟操刀而割者，亦非无故也。苑既易辙，不敢再履青楼，学行以优闻，竟得食饩。年五旬，闻江淮有名妓，亦号翠楼，颇啧啧于人口。拟欲访之，以寻夙约，究以道远而未果。

    Explanation

    1090

    Urgently seek this sutra and recite it devoutly, and you may still escape the net of law." Yuan wanted to ask for details, but suddenly lost the location of his ancestor. Suddenly feeling filial affection, he wept greatly and lost his voice. He awoke by letting go, then found himself on the bed, and the time had passed five drums. Yuan remembered clearly and vowed to change his ways and do good. The next day, he immediately bought the Diamond Sutra, washed and purified himself to hold it reverently. After a month, he dreamed of a beautiful woman, dressed in fine clothes, who suddenly appeared and said to Yuan, "Seducing one woman, her uncles, aunts, and relatives will not forgive you. Fortunately, fewer karmic debts, but due to sexual misconduct, will still become a prostitute. If you have the intention, twenty years later, you can visit me in the Wu and Chu mountains and waters." Yuan knew it was Xiao, tried to converse with her, but did not manage before waking up. Therefore, he purified his heart and increased sincerity. He also heard that Xiao was good both inside and out, often tempted young men, and due to the disgrace of cutting the sleeve, caused the brother of the person to die with resentment. He realized that those who wield the knife also have reasons. Yuan changed direction, dare not step into the brothel again, gained a reputation for excellent learning and conduct, and finally obtained a stipend. At fifty years old, he heard of a famous courtesan in Jiangsu and Anhui, also known as the Green Tower, widely talked about, planned to visit to fulfill an old agreement, but ultimately, due to the distance, it was not achieved.

    1091

    外史氏曰：余前于田再春传中，已三复于孽报当身之义矣。及闻是事，倍叹金刚之棒喝更捷于旌阳。盖凡死者受报，非生者莫与之传，见者惊心，谅闻者亦当悚念。况夫牝牡转圜，均遭贯革之射；人鬼同趣，得施反戈之攻。甚至百体莫赎一夕之欢。纵下愚不移，难禁变色，则在中材如苑者，有不翻然自新也哉？

    ○　卢京
    　　卢京，本名京儿，以妙龄绝色为优于都中，名噪一时。秀水某孝廉，以候选在都，见而悦之，恒流连不去。孝廉綦贫，囊空如洗，不能出缠头费，惟于演剧处所，携百钱日往一游，驾言观场，实则意有所为也。京师名园数十处，每以班名揭于市。孝廉侦之，得其所在，辄竭杖头物，奔赴恐后，虽远弗辞。至则息虑凝神，木坐于场侧，卢出则翘首以观，卢入则曲肱以卧。且于其来也，若睹名画，注目弗移；其去也，若送飞鸿，神往不已。

    Explanation

    1091

    Final Translation

    外史氏曰：余前于田再春传中，已三复于孽报当身之义矣。及闻是事，倍叹金刚之棒喝更捷于旌阳。盖凡死者受报，非生者莫与之传，见者惊心，谅闻者亦当悚念。况夫牝牡转圜，均遭贯革之射；人鬼同趣，得施反戈之攻。甚至百体莫赎一夕之欢。纵下愚不移，难禁变色，则在中材如苑者，有不翻然自新也哉？

    The historian says: In my previous account of the story of Tian Zaichun, I have already emphasized three times the principle that retribution occurs in this life. Upon hearing this story, I am even more struck by the immediacy of the "diamond fist and shout" compared to the traditional form of retribution symbolized by Zhang Ling. For all who die must receive their retribution, and only the living can pass on the story. Those who see it are shocked, and surely those who hear it should also be filled with dread. Even more so, both men and women, whether human or ghost, are subject to the same fate, and are met with the same retribution. Indeed, no amount of effort can make up for one night of pleasure. Even the most morally corrupt cannot be changed, and it is difficult to remain unchanged in the face of such consequences. Therefore, even those of average ability, like Mr. Yuan, will surely be moved to reform themselves.

    ○ 卢京

    Lu Jing, whose original name was Jing'er, was renowned in the capital for her beauty and youth, achieving fame during her time. A certain scholar-official from Xiushui, who was a candidate for office in the capital, saw her and was captivated by her. He often lingered and did not want to leave. The scholar-official was extremely poor, with his purse as empty as a washbasin, unable to pay for her company. Instead, he would carry a hundred coins each day to visit the theater, claiming to watch the performance, but in reality, he had a specific purpose in mind. There were dozens of famous gardens in the capital, each named after a performance troupe and advertised in the market. The scholar-official would investigate and find out where she was, then spend all his money to rush there, fearing he would be too late. When he arrived, he would calm his thoughts and concentrate, sitting woodenly on the side of the stage. When she appeared, he would look up eagerly to watch her; when she disappeared, he would lie down with his elbow propped up. And when she came, it was as if he were viewing a famous painting, his gaze fixed and unblinking; when she left, it was as if he were sending off a flying goose, his spirit following her endlessly.

    1092

    场上一盳，孝廉亦为之一盳；场上一笑，孝廉亦为之一笑。虽诸伶纷沓盈场，而精神有所专主。耳之所闻，非卢若无闻也；目之所见，非卢若无见也。或问所演何剧，则答曰：“予乌能知？”于是戏痴之名，同乡人咸传为笑柄。乃卢以色艺擅长，自矜得意，目中初未有此一人。比及年余，竟无虚日，其坐则尺寸不移，其态则初终不易，目孜孜而神恋恋，只专注于己身，卢亦微有所觉。始犹窃笑，久而以为奇。益阴伺之，孝廉之若送若迎，直将性命之不恤，卢亦不禁感动。

    Explanation

    1092

    On the stage, when the performer gave a glance, the scholar-official also gave a glance; when the performer smiled, the scholar-official also smiled. Although the stage was crowded with performers, the scholar-official's spirit was focused on something specific. What he heard was not as if he were deaf, and what he saw was not as if he were blind. When someone asked what play was being performed, he replied, "How could I possibly know?" Thus, the name "play enthusiast" became a laughing stock among his fellow townspeople. The performer, who was skilled in appearance and artistry, was proud and pleased with himself and did not initially consider the scholar-official at all. After a year or so, there was no day without the scholar-official's presence. His posture remained unchanged, and his demeanor was consistent throughout. His eyes were intently focused, and his spirit was deeply attached, solely focused on himself. The performer also gradually became aware of this. At first, he secretly laughed, but after a while, he found it strange. He began to secretly observe, and the scholar-official seemed to be sending and receiving, so engrossed that he did not care about his life. The performer could not help but be moved.

    1093

    既而不得其名，叩之园主，则笑曰：“此戏痴也，随子有年矣，子故未之知耶？”卢深以为异，更物色之，得其详。则虽登桂榜，实守寒毡，非能向梨园中买笑者，心益怜之。踌躇数日，竟弃其业，席卷所有归孝廉。见即泣拜于地，请为仆。孝廉虽钟情有素，初不虞其自来，不禁骇然。力辞之，而并诘其故，对曰：“非敢有他意，感君之青盼，使人不能自已耳。”继以号泣，卒不肯去，孝廉因留之。卢昼则青衣若厮养，代孝廉执炊；夜则市斗酒，易女妆，歌舞氍毹之上，以悦其意。

    Explanation

    1093

    After a while, Lu did not know the name of the person, and he asked the garden master, who laughed and said, "This is a playful fool, he has been with you for years, and you still do not know him?" Lu found it strange and searched further for more information, obtaining the details. It turned out that although he had passed the imperial examination, he actually held a lowly position and was not someone who could seek amusement in the imperial theater. Lu increasingly pitied him. After hesitating for several days, he finally abandoned his profession, packed up all his belongings, and returned to the scholar-official. Upon seeing him, the person wept and bowed on the ground, requesting to serve as a servant. Although the scholar-official had been deeply attached to him from the beginning, he did not expect that he would come on his own and was startled. He strongly refused, but also questioned the reason. The person replied, "I do not dare to have other intentions; I feel your favor, which makes me unable to control myself." He then wailed, but finally would not leave, so the scholar-official kept him. During the day, Lu dressed in a blue robe like a servant and replaced the scholar-official in cooking; at night, he bought a measure of wine, changed into women's makeup, and danced and sang on the carpet to please his intention.

    1094

    及孝廉将寝，则辞出曰：“非爱此残躯，深虑损公盛德。”孝廉习之已久，亦爱而重之。而赋性聪敏，倚之如左右手。迨选期将届，卢又出己资数百金，为孝廉营干，得铨大邑。孝廉素鲜积蓄，一切赴官之需举出于卢，倍甚感激。抵任，命总衙务，辄辞不谙，曰：“从公本以酬知，若如此，是以为奇货可居也。况优人用事，上宪将为之寒心。”竟不受。故从宦十年，反不若绮筵数夕。及孝廉卒于官，代纪其家，扶柩归里。临穴一恸，始辞归。晚年至京，贫且老，以教歌为活。有浙人知其事者，或以孝廉语之，辄流涕不止，以为失生平第一知己。

    Explanation

    1094

    When the Filial and Honest Official was about to sleep, Lu said and left, saying, "I do not love this remaining body; I am deeply concerned about damaging the public's great virtue." The Filial and Honest Official had been accustomed to this for a long time and loved and respected him. Lu was naturally intelligent and depended on him as if he were a right and left hand. When the selection period was approaching, Lu again contributed several hundred gold coins from his own resources to assist the Filial and Honest Official, who obtained a major county post. The Filial and Honest Official had little savings, and all the expenses for taking office were provided by Lu, making him extremely grateful. Upon arriving at the post, he was ordered to oversee the administrative affairs but refused, claiming unfamiliarity, saying, "Following the official's orders is to repay his knowledge; if so, it would be treating him as a rare commodity to be hoarded. Moreover, if a performer were to take charge, the higher authorities would be disheartened." He finally refused. Therefore, serving as an official for ten years was less than the few nights of luxury. When the Filial and Honest Official died in office, Lu represented the family in mourning and carried the coffin back to the hometown. Weeping at the grave, he finally returned home. In old age, he came to the capital, poor and old, living by teaching songs. There was a person from Zhejiang who knew the story, and someone told him about the Filial and Honest Official, causing him to weep uncontrollably, thinking it was the loss of his lifelong best friend.

    1095

    外史氏曰：人谓孝廉为情痴，而不知卢之情痴视孝廉为尤甚。何则？辞纷华之境，甘淡泊之天，惟读书明理者能之，此曹何望焉？乃因一顾之知，从以十年之久。事出优伶，殊为可异。若孝廉者，以青白眼待旦，未闻以青白眼待人，尚不免见笑于步兵。抑余闻之，有陶公名某，以甲科授张掖令，下车之始，谨饬时闻。且年逼耳顺，簿书而外只以一卷自随。终岁始召优演剧，盖亦周旋僚友之故也。甘郡某班有旦，名悦生，陆姓，貌颇秀媚。一日奏技于署中，公瞥见之，情不能遏，竟留为近侍，朝夕弗离，所赏赉无算。悦生又媚诸公子，中冓喧争，人为掩口。后公将离任，悦生以万金兔脱而遁，公亦因色致疾，儿不起。呜呼！不见可欲，使心不乱。如陶公者犹不免于陆沉，岂孝廉之佳遇可以幸致也耶？

    Explanation

    1095

    The historian remarked: People say that the filial and upright scholar-official is infatuated, but they do not know that Lu's infatuation surpasses that of the filial and upright scholar-official. Why is this so? To reject the luxurious world and embrace a simple life, only those who read and understand the principles of life can do this; what hope do such people have? Yet, due to a brief encounter, he followed her for ten years. The matter involves actors, which is quite unusual. As for the filial and upright scholar-official, he treated others with indifference from dawn until dusk, never having treated anyone with favor or disdain, yet he was still laughed at by Wu Jun. I have also heard that there was a person named Tao Gong, who was appointed as the magistrate of Zhangye through the jiake examination. At the beginning of his term, he was careful and upright in his conduct. Moreover, as he approached the age of sixty, outside of official documents, he only carried a single scroll with him. Only at the end of the year did he summon actors to perform plays, for the sake of entertaining his colleagues. In a certain troupe in Gan Prefecture, there was a female lead actor named Yue Sheng, surnamed Lu, who was quite charming in appearance. One day, she performed at the magistrate's office, and he saw her and could not control his emotions, so he kept her as a close attendant, never leaving her side, and gave her countless rewards. Yue Sheng also flattered the magistrate's sons, leading to internal family disputes, and people would cover their mouths to hide their laughter. Later, when the magistrate was about to leave his post, Yue Sheng fled with ten thousand gold coins, and the magistrate also became ill due to indulgence in pleasure, and his son did not recover. Alas! Do not see what you desire, so that your heart does not become confused. Even Tao Gong was not spared from sinking into obscurity; how can the filial and upright scholar-official's fortunate opportunity be achieved by chance?

    1096

    ○　苏瑁
    　　郡人苏瑁，弃儒而医，颇谙于岐黄之术，延请者接踵而来。其家居负郭，暮不能归，即借栖于邑内，习以为常。多宿于上元观中，焚修者其莫逆也。一夕，又值下榻，道士置酒与饮，谈晏甚欢。因为苏言，其地近多祟，东庑某真人位下一执拂女子，颇出惑人。又阎君殿内，有赤身女囚，夜间闻嘻笑声，深以为忧。且谓苏曰：“君出旋溺，切自慎。”苏乃戏之曰：“法师纵无符，亦真无福，此诚佳遇也。”言已，大笑而罢。及苏就枕，即闻窗前作弹指声。骇而问之，音绝娇婉，答曰：“侬。”苏每至，辄独居一室，披衣起坐，连叱之，外竟寂然。苏因此竟夜不寐。

    Explanation

    1096

    A Native of the County, Su Mao

    Su Mao, a native of the county, abandoned his studies in Confucianism and became a physician, quite proficient in the art of medicine, and those who invited him came one after another. His home was located near the city walls, and at dusk, he could not return home, so he would temporarily reside within the town, which had become a habit for him. He often stayed at the Shangyuan Temple, where those who practiced religious rituals were his close friends. One night, he stayed overnight again, and a Taoist monk prepared wine for him to drink, and they enjoyed a casual conversation. The monk told Su that the area was close to many spirits, and under the statue of a certain Taoist deity in the eastern wing, there was a woman holding a whisk who often bewitched people. Inside the hall of the God of the Underworld, there was a naked female prisoner, and at night, they heard laughter, which deeply worried the monk. He also advised Su, "When you go out and return, be very careful." Su jokingly replied, "Even if the monk has no talismans, also truly has no luck, this is indeed a good opportunity." After speaking, he laughed loudly and ended the conversation. When Su went to bed, he immediately heard a snapping sound in front of the window. Startled, he asked, and the voice cut off, soft and gentle, answered, "I." Whenever Su arrived, he would always live alone in a room, wear clothes and sit up, shout at repeatedly, but outside, it was quietly. Su therefore could not sleep all night.

    1097

    晨起耻之，不以告道士，惟致谢而去，遂不敢复宿观中。乃奔走诊视，履无定踪，阅数日，又为重关阻隔，即留宿于病者家。寂处无聊，顿思所亲居左近，欲往探之，竟忘其与观为邻。悄然独出，恐主家迎送，主家亦浑未及觉。及晤所亲，盘桓情话，漏下二鼓始旋归。所亲命小奚伴送，力辞之，自持碗灯，孑然而行。至观前，忽忆往事，心已不免忐忑。将过观门，瞥见一物，如积雪立于檐下。惊而注视，仿佛如人，渐近而形状宛然，不觉股栗战战，不能趋。强以所持之炬烛之，闻其笑语曰：“痴男子胆既芥子大，何复以孜孜视侬，将吓杀矣。”挺身而立，则妇人四体裸然，披发垂手，相逼而来。苏因大怖失声，弃炬狂奔。妇复追蹑其后，苏惧而号，通衢皆闻。

    Explanation

    1097

    Upon rising in the morning, he felt ashamed and did not tell the Taoist about it, but instead expressed his gratitude and left, vowing never to stay in the temple again. He then rushed around for diagnosis and treatment, wandering aimlessly without a fixed path. After several days, he was once again blocked by heavy gates, so he decided to stay with a sick person's family. Feeling lonely and bored in his solitude, he suddenly remembered a loved one who lived nearby and wanted to visit them, but he forgot that they lived near the temple. He stealthily left alone, afraid that the host would greet or send him off, but the host was unaware of his departure. When he met his loved one, they chatted and lingered until the second drum strike before returning home. His loved one ordered a young servant to accompany him, but he strongly refused, holding a bowl lamp and walking alone. As he approached the temple, he suddenly remembered past events, and his heart could not help but feel anxious. As he was about to pass the temple gate, he briefly saw something like a pile of snow standing under the eaves. Surprised and looking closely, it seemed like a person, and as it got closer, its shape became clear. He shook with fear and could not move forward. Forcing himself to light the object with the held torch, he heard its laughter: "You foolish man, your courage is as small as a mustard seed. Why continue to look at me intently, you will scare yourself to death." It stood upright, revealing a woman's body completely naked, with hair loose and hands hanging, coming close to him. Su became extremely frightened and lost his voice, throwing away the torch and running wildly. The woman followed closely behind, and Su was afraid and shouted, loud enough for everyone in the street to hear.

    1098

    邑人有未寝者，出视之，颇多识荆。见其状如疯癫，止而与语。苏犹反首顾瞻，绝不见鬼，方始坌息以告。众既密迩于观，咸耳其异，亦骇然。因曰：“先生既知此，似不宜踽踽夜行。”乃相与伴送，直至主家而返。主人闻其遇鬼，亦出问视，苏时已惊定，少话别去。苏甚困惫，亦遂灭烛解衣。乃甫探手入衾，早有人偎枕而卧，肤香入鼻，盖鬼已先据床头矣。苏不胜惶惧，亟欲返身，势将拔关出屋，号呼求救。讵臂为所牵，猝不能脱，因向之乞命。妇笑曰：“何遂无香火情？侬实慕君高雅，忘耻相就，岂能为君祟者，而竟觳觫如此？”苏仍哀之。妇因嗤曰：“君误矣！岂阎摩当殿，竟有潜出之鬼哉？侬本仙人，时出游戏。

    Explanation

    1098

    A person from the village who had not yet gone to bed came out to see him, recognizing him as someone familiar. Seeing that he appeared as if mad, they stopped to speak with him. Su still turned his head to look around, but could not see any ghosts, so he finally sighed with relief and told them. The crowd was close by and watching, all hearing of the strange occurrence, and were startled. They said, "Since you know this, it seems you shouldn't walk alone at night." So they accompanied him until they reached the host's home and returned. The host heard that he had encountered a ghost and came out to see. By then, Su had calmed down and briefly chatted before leaving. Su was very tired and also extinguished the candle and undressed. Just as he reached into the quilt, there was someone already lying beside him, and the fragrance of skin filled his nose. It seemed the ghost had already taken up residence at the head of the bed. Overwhelmed with fear, Su wanted to turn around immediately, about to open the door and leave the room, shouting for help. But his arm was held back, and he could not escape suddenly, so he begged for mercy. The woman laughed and said, "Why have you no affection for me? I truly admire your elegance. I have forgotten my shame and come to you. How could I harm you? Yet you tremble like this." Su was mistaken, she explained, "Is it possible that the Lord of Death is in the hall? Could there be a ghost sneaking out? I am actually a fairy, occasionally coming out to play."

    1099

    前所云执拂儿，亦即是侬。人以楚楚衣裳，判而为二，而不知其实一也。”苏故未深信，而腻脂着肌，媚言入耳，且半窥皓体，暗室流辉，一时顿释忌惮。遂去衣与妇共枕，其肌骨之柔靡，意态之颠狂，举非身所经历。苏因深入彀中，反恨相见之晚。侵晨始起，反致叮嘱于妇。妇笑而诺之，裸裎自去。时已赤驭当天，苏益信其非真鬼。越二日，苏宿于家，其妻率子往省母。苏孤眠，颇涉冥想。俄而室门自辟，有人悄然入。初以为盗，亟起视，残檠未剔，兰气遥吹，其人早立于榻侧。苏又意其为妇，观之，则一垂髫少女，衣销金之衣，冠翡翠之冠，容色倾城，嫣然微笑，视妇相去远甚。而麈尾轻挥，衣香肆溢，顿忆道士所云某真人位下者，此其是已。

    Explanation

    1099

    What was mentioned before, the one holding the fly whisk, is also me. People, seeing the neatly dressed figure, divide it into two, but do not realize it is actually one. Su did not fully believe it at first, but the soft fat on her skin, the flattering words in his ear, and the glimpse of her white body in the dark room, which shone with light, suddenly dispelled all his fears. So he took off his clothes and lay down with the woman. The softness of her flesh and bones, the wildness of her demeanor, was all beyond what he had ever experienced. Su, having fallen into the trap, regretted meeting her too late. At dawn, he got up and gave instructions to the woman. The woman laughed and agreed, then left naked. By then, the sun was high in the sky, and Su became even more convinced that she was not a real ghost. Two days later, Su stayed at home, and his wife took the children to visit her mother. Su slept alone, deeply in thought. Suddenly, the door opened by itself, and someone quietly entered. At first, he thought it was a thief and quickly got up to look. The dim lamp had not been trimmed, and the fragrance of orchids wafted in from afar. The person had already stood by the bed. Su thought it was the woman again, but upon closer inspection, he saw a young girl with a braid, wearing a gold-embroidered dress and a jade hairpin, with a beauty that could charm the city, and she smiled sweetly, far surpassing the woman he had encountered before. While lightly waving a fly whisk, the fragrance of her clothes spread widely. Suddenly, he remembered what the Taoist had said about a certain True Person, and realized this was the one.

    1100

    苏既习于见怪，蹶然离榻，身固未及着缕。女即以袂障面，曰：“羞杀阿奴！耳可洗目不可濯，深悔多此一来。”意将反身，苏径揽其绣带，俾不得行，且拥而置之于脸。叩其所从来，女低鬟蹙黛，羞若不胜曰：“作尽诸丑态，夫复何言？惟有委身事君而已。”因置拂几上，连衣入衾。苏近之，见其蜷曲如小儿，怜而抚摩，加以软款。须臾，罗襦衿解，芗泽近闻。及合，火齐渥丹，猩红渍席，而呻吟啮被，宛转随人，温柔乡别有佳处，觉前之淫妖，又直将粪土视之矣。

    Explanation

    1100

    The male character, accustomed to seeing strange things, leapt up from the bed, his body still not fully dressed. The female character immediately covered her face with her sleeve and said, "Shameful for such a man! Ears can be washed but eyes cannot be cleansed, deeply regrets coming here." Intending to leave, the male character suddenly grabbed her embroidered belt, preventing her from moving, and embraced her, placing her on his face. He asked where she came from, and she lowered her head, furrowed her brows, and said with shame, "Have done all kinds of ugly things, what more can I say? Only to submit myself to serve you." She then placed the brush on the table, put on her clothes, and got into bed. The male character approached her, saw her curled up like a child, pitied and caressed her, adding gentle affection. Soon, the silk robe and collar were undone, and the fragrance was nearby. When they joined together, bright red like fire, crimson stains the mat, and she moaned and nibbled the bedding, twisting and turning with the person. The gentle and tender place has its own delights, regarding the previous lewd and妖 as worthless.

    1101

    欢已，女始自白来意，曰：“初闻骚狐冒妾，以为二即是一，使君谓我辈露体宵奔，且将勾阑之钱树子不若，因而腼颜夜至，分诉其伪。孰意遭君狂暴，古云‘尤而效之’，则又妾之谓也。”苏乃悟前疑为鬼者非鬼实狐，遂疑今之言狐者非狐即鬼。稍涉猜虞，女即觉之，因笑曰：“君亦有疑于妾耶？妾辈诚多鬼仙，渠实狐之丑类，试留拂为质，君姑访妾于观中，益验予言之非谬。”言讫，披衣起曰：“君心未坚，请以三日为约，再当诣君。爱欲所牵，妾今亦无能自主矣。”甫下床，倏然无踪，而余香未歇，衾褥犹芬，苏早信其为真仙。翌日，袖拂诣观，为道士言，欲穷前之二异，道士欣然导之。先游十殿，果有一女囚，无裤无衣，俯身地上。旁立一巨鬼，以钢义拟之，将入油镬。视之，果肖所遇之妇。

    Explanation

    1101

    After the joy, the woman began to reveal her true intentions, saying, "At first, I heard that the fox spirit had deceived me, thinking that the two were one and the same. You accused us of running out at night with our bodies exposed, and you even said that we were not as valuable as the money in the brothel. Therefore, I came to you with a guilty conscience to explain the falsehood. Who would have thought that I would be subjected to your violent behavior? As the saying goes, 'To imitate what one has criticized,' it seems to apply to me as well." Su then realized that the previous doubt about the ghost was not a ghost but a fox, and he began to doubt whether the current claim about the fox was a fox or a ghost. As soon as he started to suspect, the woman sensed it and laughed, saying, "Do you also doubt me, sir? We are indeed many ghost spirits, but he is actually a member of the ugly fox kind. Please leave the broom as a token, and visit me at the temple. It will further prove that my words are not mistaken." After speaking, she got up, put on her clothes, and said, "Your heart is not yet firm. Let us agree to a three-day period, and I will come to you again. Tied by love and desire, I can no longer control myself now." As soon as she got out of bed, she suddenly disappeared, but the lingering fragrance remained, and the bedding was still fragrant. Su quickly believed that she was a true fairy. The next day, he took the broom and went to the temple, telling the道士 (Daoist priest) about his desire to explore the previous two anomalies. The Daoist priest gladly guided him. They first visited the ten hells, and indeed, there was a female prisoner without pants or clothes, bent over on the ground. Standing beside her was a giant ghost, threatening her with a steel weapon, about to throw her into the oil pit. Upon seeing her, he realized that she was exactly like the woman he had met before.

    1102

    方注目间，道士已闻其遇鬼，忽笑曰：“迫君于途者殆此耶？然非木偶者之能为，亦非冥王之疏纵也。”因言某月扫殿，见有狐迹，又于深夜，逐一白狐，入此即不见。始知向之嘻嗃者，狐实为之，于鬼乎无与。苏闻此益信女言。道士又引至一殿，有真人踞狮而坐，侍以二女，貌皆殊尤。苏因凝睇，细观其左者，面容衣饰与若人无少异，心窃骇然。俄闻道士诧曰：“执拂者拂竟安在？”苏不觉捧腹，出之袖中，置诸女手，竟宛然。遂笑语道士曰：“李药师敬来返璧。但惜此真人，独无杨越公之雅度耳。”道士愕然，固诘之，苏不肯言，径笑而趋出，亦不辞道士。而及抵家，其妻已归，方将湔制其衾裯，见苏勃有怒色。苏叩之，则泣而请去。

    Explanation

    1102

    At the moment he was looking, the Taoist master had already heard of his encounter with a ghost. He suddenly laughed and said, "Was this the one who was chasing you on the road? But it was not something a puppet could do, nor was it due to the negligence of the King of Hell." He then explained that during a certain month when he was sweeping the hall, he saw fox tracks. At night, he chased a white fox, but it disappeared as soon as it entered here. Then he realized that the one who was laughing and shouting before was actually the fox, and it had nothing to do with ghosts. Su became even more convinced of the woman's words. The Taoist master then led him to a hall where a true person was sitting on a lion, accompanied by two women, both of whom had extraordinary appearances. Su stared intently and closely observed the one on the left. Her face and clothing were almost identical to someone else's, which secretly startled him. Soon, he heard the Taoist master exclaim in surprise, "Where is the person holding the whisk?" Su unconsciously laughed and took it out of his sleeve, placing it in the woman's hand. It turned out to be exactly the same. He then laughed and said to the Taoist master, "Li Yishou respectfully comes to return the jade. Only this true person lacks the elegance of Yang Yuegong." The Taoist master was startled and persistently questioned him, but Su refused to say anything. He laughed and left directly, also not bidding farewell to the Taoist master. When he arrived home, his wife had already returned and was just about to wash and prepare her bedding. She saw Su with an angry look and asked him. He cried and asked to leave.

    1103

    盖妇归宁母家，尚未思复，忽有一妇人素衣綦巾，突至女所，语之曰：“若夫太不自重，诱良家处子相与为欢。子归而视之，杜鹃枝头赤痕且宛在矣。”妇闻而疑，果遄归。展其卧具，艳迹信然，且香犹未息。因忿忿裂被出绵，将以浣濯，而良人固未之知也。妇既讪之云云，苏乃悟，力为分解，并述其相遇之奇。妇不信，诟之倍亟，因以脱辐，竟至数日之久，而琴瑟方调。旬余，苏以诊视出，暮归遇女于委巷，迎而言曰：“狐构吾衅，使不得终事君子。虽然，君家娘子当亦是醋炙者，何竟悻悻如是耶？”苏欲挽之晤言，旋失其所在。自是不再遇女，而闺训綦严，不能复宿于邑内，遂亦绝不遇妇。此苏壮年事也，至其裔孙始举以告人。而上元观倾圮已久，蔓草荒烟，竟无有征其异之所在者。

    Explanation

    1103

    When a woman returned to her mother's home, she had not yet thought of returning, when suddenly a woman in plain clothes and a simple scarf appeared at her place and said to her, "Your husband is not self-respecting, he has enticed a virtuous maiden to engage in intimacy. Go back and see it; the red mark on the rhododendron branch is still there." The woman, upon hearing this, became suspicious and indeed hurried back. Unfolding her bedding, she found the traces of the affair to be true, and the fragrance still lingered. Filled with anger, she tore the quilt to reveal the cotton, intending to wash and clean it, though her husband remained unaware of it. The wife then scolded her as such, and Su then understood, striving to explain and describing the strange encounter. The wife did not believe, scolding her even more harshly, and as a result, she left, lasting for several days, and the relationship was finally harmonized. After ten days, Su went out to attend to medical matters and returned in the evening, meeting the woman in a narrow alley. Approaching and saying, "A fox caused me trouble, preventing me from completing my duties to the gentleman. Although, your wife must also be a jealous one, why is she so resentful?" Su wanted to engage her in conversation, but she suddenly lost her whereabouts. From then on, no longer met the woman, but the wife's discipline became extremely strict, and she could no longer stay in the town, thus never encountering the woman again. This was Su's story in his prime, and it was only until his descendant recounted it to others. The Shangyuan Temple had been in ruins for a long time, overgrown with weeds and shrouded in mist, there was no trace of where the strange events occurred.

    1104

    外史氏曰：不有雅俗之分，几使洛阳好女儿与河间荡妇同讥。夫以狐之行类媚猪，所惑者当非一二，而仅传其嘻笑。女之婉丽如此，乃以煽处特闻，又不独醋炙娘子怒目于衾底之流芳，而忘情于床头之作俑也。苏子瞻竟失朝云，当亦生平第一恨事。

    Explanation

    1104

    The historian remarked: "Without the distinction between refined and vulgar, the virtuous women of Luoyang would be criticized alongside the carefree women of Hejian. For a woman who behaves like a seductive pig, the number of those she charms would not be just one or two, yet only her playful behavior is remembered. A woman so graceful and beautiful, yet known for stirring up trouble, is not only a jealous woman who releases fragrance from under the quilt with angry eyes, but also a woman who forgets emotion at the foot of the bed. Su Shi, the great poet, lost his beloved concubine, and this must surely be his greatest regret."
